songs.subject: a houseplant I keep forgetting to water
songs.lyrics:
I gave you a windowsill and not much else
A coffee-cup of tap and my apologies
I was busy selling hours I would never get back
While your green went grey behind the blinds

And I'm sorry, little fern, I let you go
I was drowning at a desk I'll never miss
Now I'm free and you are not, and that's the joke
I kept the wrong thing breathing, I confess

They gave me a clock and called it a career
You gave me nothing and I loved you more
Two weeks' notice for the both of us
You went first, I followed out the door

And I'm sorry, little fern, I let you go
I was drowning at a desk I'll never miss
Now I'm free and you are not, and that's the joke
I kept the wrong thing breathing, I confess

Pour the water now, pour it on the soil
Pour it like it matters, like it's not too late
Too late, too late — the only honest word I made

songs.subject: the ghost in my childhood home
songs.lyrics:
There's a shadow on the staircase where I used to play
Bum-ba-dum, she never went away
My mama said it's nothing but the settling of the wood
But I know my old friend's standing where she always stood

Ghost of mine, oh ghost of mine
You kept the porch light burning all this time
While the world outside went modern and the old town rearranged
You stayed the same, my ghost, you never changed

I came back with my suitcase and the keys felt strange and small
Bum-ba-dum, she met me in the hall
Not a chill, not a fright, just a hand upon my cheek
Like she'd waited fifty Sundays for the words I couldn't speak

Ghost of mine, oh ghost of mine
You kept the porch light burning all this time
So I'll dance you 'round the kitchen like my daddy danced my mom
Stay a little longer, then I'll let you carry on

songs.subject: a fox raiding the bins at midnight
songs.lyrics:
Ten — the streetlight flickers and the town goes still
Nine — a shadow slips the gap beneath the windowsill
Eight — the bins are gleaming like a midnight buffet
Seven — here comes the bandit and he's coming out to play

He's the king of the curb, he's the lord of the trash
Orange streak of mischief in a midnight dash
Count it down, count it down, he don't care at all
Six, five, four — he's having a ball

Six — the lid goes flying with a clatter and a crash
Five — a chicken bone, a treasure in the ash
Four — a dog starts barking but he's two yards too slow
Three — the fox just grins at him and tells him where to go

He's the king of the curb, he's the lord of the trash
Orange streak of mischief in a midnight dash
Count it down, count it down, he don't care at all
Six, five, four — he's having a ball

Two — a porch light snaps on, but the legend's already gone
One — just an empty street and the carnage he left on
Zero — and the town wakes up to wonder what went wrong
While the fox sleeps it off in the hedgerow till the dawn

songs.subject: post-rock
songs.lyrics:
We drove until the road forgot its name
Until the radio was static and the fuel light came
You didn't say a word, you didn't have to speak
We'd been running on the same low hum for weeks

Stay.
Stay.
Stay.
Stay.

The dawn came up like something breaking open soft
A field of frost, a sky we'd never thought to want
And everything we couldn't fit inside a sentence
Just hung there in the cold like our own breath, like penance

Stay.
Stay.
Stay.
Stay.

The dawn came up like something breaking open soft

And everything we couldn't fit inside a sentence
Just hung there in the cold like our own breath, like penance

Stay.
Stay.
Stay.
Stay.

songs.subject: a retired boxer
songs.lyrics:
I'm the gloves! I'm the gloves! Hangin' on the wall!

I'm the gloves on the hook and I'm gathering dust
Used to taste the canvas, used to swing on trust
He laced me up at midnight, he laced me up at dawn
Now I'm leather and a memory and the crowd is gone

But I felt every punch, I felt every fall
I'm the only one who knows he gave it all

Champion! Champion! Even when you lost!
I held your hands together at any cost!
Hang me up, hang me high, let the young ones climb!
I'm the gloves and I remember every fight in double-time!

Now he visits on a Sunday and he holds me soft
Hands all swollen-knuckled from the years he coughed
He don't need the ring no more, he don't need the roar
But he keeps me on the hook 'cause I'm what he fought for

Champion! Champion! Even when you lost!
I held your hands together at any cost!
Hang me up, hang me high, let the young ones climb!
I'm the gloves and I remember every fight in double-time!

Cham-cham-champion... still... still...

songs.subject: swing
songs.lyrics:
Well I put on my good shoes and I shined up my hat
Got a girl on my arm and there's nothing wrong with that
The trumpet's gettin' frisky and the snare's gonna swing
So I open up my mouth and there's just one thing to sing—

Baby!
Baby!
Baby!
Baby!

Now the clarinet is climbin' and the floor's gettin' hot
I forgot all my troubles 'cause I clean forgot
Every word I ever knew, every worry, every name
There's a single syllable and it's the only one I claim—

Baby!
Baby!
Baby!
Baby!

Shoo-bee-doo, badda-bap, the band don't need a sentence!
Doo-wah, doo-wah, love don't need repentance!

Ba-by! Yeah!

Baby!

Baby!

Baby!
Baby!

Shoo-bee-doo, badda-bap, the band don't need a sentence!
Doo-wah, doo-wah, love don't need repentance!

Ba-by! Yeah!

songs.subject: winning and feeling nothing
songs.lyrics:
I lift it up... but my hands are empty...

I stand on the mountain that I climbed for years
The summit's all glory and the valley's all tears
But I reached the top and I felt the wind go cold
I won the whole kingdom and I cannot feel the gold

Fill me, fill me, the trophy's hollow in my chest
Fill the silence where the triumph should have blessed...

'Cause I won, I won, and the win is a void!
Hands in the air for a joy I can't avoid the lack of!
Lift it higher, lift it higher, but it falls right through!
I conquered everything and I am asking, God, are You?

The crowd is a worship and the lights are a prayer
But the hole in the winning is a hole in the air
So I'll dance in the question 'cause it's all that I've got
The win is the wound and the joy is the rot

'Cause I won, I won, and the win is a void!
Lift it higher, lift it higher, but it falls right through!
I conquered everything and I am asking, God, are You?

...maybe the wanting was the worship all along...

songs.subject: growing up too fast
songs.lyrics:
Right now, right now, it's all happening now...

I am seventeen and I am paying my own rent
I am counting all the coins before the money's even spent
I am wearing my father's tired face in the mirror every day
I am old before the morning and I do not know the way

And the bass is asking softly, are you breathing, are you here?
Slow it down, slow it down, the future's already near

I am growing up too fast, the log drum knows
I am blooming and I'm wilting in the same set of clothes
Hold the night, hold the night, let me be young right now
I am running out of childhood and I don't know how

I am laughing at the jokes that grown folks make
I am tired in my bones for everybody's sake
I am dancing 'cause the dancing is the only time I'm free
I am twenty-five at heart and I am barely twenty-three

I am growing up too fast, the log drum knows
I am blooming and I'm wilting in the same set of clothes
Hold the night, hold the night, let me be young right now
I am running out of childhood and I don't know how

Right now... I am here... right now... that is all I have...

songs.subject: the friend who never texts back
songs.lyrics:
Rise up, o silent one, o ghost upon my screen!
The proudest non-responder that the world has ever seen!
I send my flares into the dark and not a one comes back
A nation of one bubble, gray and floating off the track

Oh haaail! The friend who never answers!
Oh haaail! The read-receipt commander!
Pledge allegiance to the silence, to the three dots and the void!
I salute you, glorious deadbeat, my devotion undestroyed!

Your loyalty's a legend and your timing is a myth
You'll resurface in a season like a comet I'll be with
But until then I'm marching 'neath your banner made of air
The anthem of the abandoned, screaming into nowhere

Oh haaail! The friend who never answers!
Oh haaail! The read-receipt commander!
Pledge allegiance to the silence, to the three dots and the void!
I salute you, glorious deadbeat, my devotion undestroyed!

...delivered... never read... long may you ignore...

songs.subject: winning and feeling nothing
songs.lyrics:
I have won the grand exhibition, they have pinned me with the rose
And the ladies all are whispering of my triumph as it goes
Yet I stand beside the mantel with a chill I cannot name
For the prize is in my pocket and my heart feels just the same

Oh, what a hollow victory, what a cold and quiet crown
I have climbed the gilded staircase but there's nothing waiting down
They may toast me with their sherry and applaud me in the hall
But I won the thing I longed for and I feel no thing at all

The clock upon the dresser ticks the hours toward the dawn
And the medal on the ribbon is a weight that I have drawn
I had thought that winning fills you, that the empty would depart
But the trophy gleams so brightly on the dimness of my heart

Oh, what a hollow victory, what a cold and quiet crown
I have climbed the gilded staircase but there's nothing waiting down
They may toast me with their sherry and applaud me in the hall
But I won the thing I longed for and I feel no thing at all

...perhaps the wanting was the living... and I've nothing left to want.

songs.subject: my grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Take me back... take me back...

Linoleum floor and a humming fridge light
Flour on the counter at the edge of the night
She's stirring up a magic in a dented old pot
And the whole world is safe in that warm little spot

And I'd trade every club in every city tonight
For one Sunday morning in that yellow kitchen light

Grandma's kitchen, take me higher!
Sugar and the stovetop fire!
Hands up if you'd give it all to go back home!
Spinning in the steam where I was never alone!

She's gone seven years but the recipe remains
Like a beat I can't forget through the joy and the pains
So I close my eyes in the strobe and the bass
And I'm eight years old again in that holy little place

Grandma's kitchen, take me higher!
Sugar and the stovetop fire!
Hands up if you'd give it all to go back home!
Spinning in the steam where I was never alone!

...one more Sunday... one more... I'd give anything...

songs.subject: dog watching a breakup
songs.lyrics:
The blue light of the TV but the sound is turned down low
Two people on the couch with a foot of empty road
I put my chin upon her knee like I have done before
But the hand that always finds me doesn't find me anymore

And the night is turning purple, all the colors going cold
I don't know the word for breaking but I feel it in my bones
One of you is fading like a signal in the dark
And I'm howling at a feeling that I cannot bark

He carries out a box to a car beneath the moon
The synth is soft and sorry and the headlights leave too soon
She sits down on the carpet and she pulls me to her chest
And I lick away the salt for her because I love her best

And the night is turning purple, all the colors going cold
I don't know the word for breaking but I feel it in my bones
One of you is fading like a signal in the dark
And I'm howling at a feeling that I cannot bark

...I'll watch the door all night... in case the colors come back warm... in case...

songs.subject: missing someone who's still alive
songs.lyrics:
You are not gone and still I grieve you
Across a hallway, half a town
You breathe the same grey morning with me
And still I cannot put you down

So I will march for something larger
Than the small ache in my chest
If I can't have you, let me have a reason
Let the marching give me rest

They sing of countries, I sing of you
A flag I folded up in June
Sixteen and certain it was forever
Forever ended much too soon

So I will march for something larger
Than the small ache in my chest
If I can't have you, let me have a reason
Let the marching give me rest

Alive, alive — the cruelest word
The one I'll never let you hear

songs.subject: a group project gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Four names on a title page and only two who tried
Me and quiet Caroline up burnin' midnight oil
Jake just sent a thumbs-up and a promise made of smoke
And Danny never answered, so we carried all the load

And we got the A they didn't earn
But I'd do it all again to learn
That the ones who show up in the rain
Are worth more than the grade you put your name to in the end

Caroline's a nurse now and Jake sells used cars downtown
Funny how a slideshow tells you who'll be there around
We laughed until 2 a.m. over gas-station coffee cups
That's the part I kept — the project's gone, but not the us

And we got the A they didn't earn
But I'd do it all again to learn
That the ones who show up in the rain
Are worth more than the grade you put your name to in the end

So here's to Caroline, wherever you are tonight

songs.subject: my noisy upstairs neighbor
songs.lyrics:
Is it 3 a.m. again, or did I just stop counting?
Are those footsteps, or my heartbeat finally mounting?
Do you know I memorized the rhythm of your floor?
Don't you ever sit down, don't you ever close a door?

Why does your whole life sound like a war up there?
Why do I lie awake and pretend I don't care?
Is the ceiling all that's left between your world and mine?
Could you maybe, just once, be quiet — would that be a crime?

Was that a chair you dragged, or the last of my composure?
Is the party for a reason, or just nightly exposure?
Have you ever wondered who's beneath you, barely sleeping?
Do you hear me knocking back, or is it me who's weeping?

Why does your whole life sound like a war up there?
Why do I lie awake and pretend I don't care?
Is the ceiling all that's left between your world and mine?
Could you maybe, just once, be quiet — would that be a crime?

And if I moved away, would you even notice I was gone?

songs.subject: an old dog at the end of summer
songs.lyrics:
Arise! Arise! O grizzled hound of gold!
The summer's bleeding out and you are old, you're old!
But look — you're tearing 'cross the yard like you were three!
A glorious, deranged, undignified decree!

Sing! For the dog who will not quit!
Sing! For the tail that's lost its wit!
He's chasing down the final fly of August in the sun
A nation rising, falling, panting — run, old soldier, run!

The leaves! They turn! He does not care a whit!
He's found a stick! It is the finest stick of it!
He brings it back, he drops it, he forgets it on the lawn
Then barks at his own shadow till the very break of dawn!

Sing! For the dog who will not quit!
Sing! For the tail that's lost its wit!
He's chasing down the final fly of August in the sun
A nation rising, falling, panting — run, old soldier, run!

And he sleeps! At last he sleeps! Hail the king of summer's end!
songs.lyrics_en:
Arise! Arise! O grizzled hound of gold!
The summer's bleeding out and you are old, you're old!
But look — you're tearing 'cross the yard like you were three!
A glorious, deranged, undignified decree!

Sing! For the dog who will not quit!
Sing! For the tail that's lost its wit!
He's chasing down the final fly of August in the sun
A nation rising, falling, panting — run, old soldier, run!

The leaves! They turn! He does not care a whit!
He's found a stick! It is the finest stick of it!
He brings it back, he drops it, he forgets it on the lawn
Then barks at his own shadow till the very break of dawn!

Sing! For the dog who will not quit!
Sing! For the tail that's lost its wit!
He's chasing down the final fly of August in the sun
A nation rising, falling, panting — run, old soldier, run!

And he sleeps! At last he sleeps! Hail the king of summer's end!

songs.subject: growing up too fast
songs.lyrics:
We were young, we were young...

We traded all our summers for a paycheck and a key
We grew up in a hurry 'cause they told us that's the deal
We blinked and we were twenty, blinked again and we were old
We spent the gold of morning to buy a little cold

And we, and we, and we — we want it back, we want it back...

So put your hands up if you grew up way too fast!
If your childhood was a station that the train went screaming past!
We are the town that traded play for the weight of the world
Light it up, light it up, give us back the kids we were!

We see them on the playground and we ache down to the bone
We'd give back every dollar just to be that age and home
But the bassline's like a time machine for everyone tonight
So we dance like we were nine years old beneath the strobe light

So put your hands up if you grew up way too fast!
If your childhood was a station that the train went screaming past!
We are the town that traded play for the weight of the world
Light it up, light it up, give us back the kids we were!

We were young... we were young... we still are, somewhere...

songs.subject: a fox raiding the bins at midnight
songs.constraint: uses one weird word a lot
songs.lyrics:
He comes like a vagabond, slinkin' down the lane
A vagabond of garbage in the sodium rain
Tippin' over kingdoms made of plastic and of bone
A vagabond, a vagabond, a thief without a throne

Vagabond! Vagabond! Lord of the bin!
Vagabond! Vagabond! Let the midnight in!
He don't pay no rent and he don't know shame
A vagabond, a vagabond, and the night's his domain

The dogs all know his vagabond grin
The way he tips his vagabond chin
He'll raid your kitchen scraps and your forgotten ham
A vagabond who doesn't give a vagabond damn

Vagabond! Vagabond! Lord of the bin!
Vagabond! Vagabond! Let the midnight in!
He don't pay no rent and he don't know shame
A vagabond, a vagabond, and the night's his domain

One more vagabond... gone by dawn... vagabond... gone

songs.subject: an astronaut who misses gravity
songs.lyrics:
Pull me down... pull me down...

Up here everything is floating, even my heart won't stay still
I miss the honest weight of standing, I miss the pull of the hill
The Earth is a marble of blue down below me
And I'd trade every star just to feel the ground hold me

And the bass drops low like the gravity I crave
Take me home, take me home, to the weight that made me brave

Gravity! Bring me back to the ground!
I never knew I loved you till I floated around!
The most beautiful prison is a planet that holds you tight!
Pull me down, pull me down, into ordinary light!

I used to dream of weightless and now weightless is my cage
I'd give the whole cosmos for the steps of a stage
For the simple act of falling, for the grass and the rain
For the gorgeous heavy gift of being held again

Gravity! Bring me back to the ground!
I never knew I loved you till I floated around!
The most beautiful prison is a planet that holds you tight!
Pull me down, pull me down, into ordinary light!

...and the reentry burns like joy... like joy... like joy...

songs.subject: a diner at 2am
songs.lyrics:
Ladies and gentlemen, the loneliest stage in town!
Fluorescent and formica, where the night owls all sit down!
The waitress is our diva, she's been on since half past nine —

TWO! A! M! At the all-night neon show!
Every broken heart's a ticket and there's nowhere left to go!
DANCE in the booth, dance in the dark, let the coffee go cold!
Two a.m. is a kingdom for the sleepless and the bold!

The trucker has a solo and it's mostly in his eyes!
The cook performs a ballet with the bacon and the fries!
And every single regular's a character of mine —

TWO! A! M! At the all-night neon show!
Every broken heart's a ticket and there's nowhere left to go!
DANCE in the booth, dance in the dark, let the coffee go cold!
Two a.m. is a kingdom for the sleepless and the bold!

...curtain... check, please... and goodnight...

songs.subject: growing apart from a best friend
songs.lyrics:
We used to finish sentences, now we can't begin
There's a strange and growing distance where the closeness once had been
I feel it in the pauses, in the laughter half a beat too late
A friendship slowly cooling like a dinner left to wait

And I am anxious, oh so anxious, of the silence in between
Of the people we are turning to, the strangers we have been
Don't drift, don't drift, I'm reaching but the current's running strong
How do you say "I'm losing you" in a language with no song?

We make the plans we both know that we'll cancel in the end
And I mourn you while you're sitting right beside me, oldest friend
The cello asks the question that my mouth has never dared
Are we keeping this or grieving it? Were we ever really there?

And I am anxious, oh so anxious, of the silence in between
Of the people we are turning to, the strangers we have been
Don't drift, don't drift, I'm reaching but the current's running strong
How do you say "I'm losing you" in a language with no song?

songs.subject: the smell of a thunderstorm coming
songs.lyrics:
Something in the air tonight, the sky is turning green
The sweetest electricity that you have ever seen
The leaves all flip their bellies up, the whole world holds its breath
And I'm dancing on the sidewalk in the moment before the wet

Ozone! Can you feel it coming down?
Thunder on the boulevard, electric in the town!
Spin me, spin me, before the first drop falls!
The smell of a storm is the sexiest of all!

The streetlights start to flicker and the air is sweet and charged
My collar's up, my heart is loud, my disco soul's enlarged
The first fat raindrop hits the disco ball above the street
And the whole block starts to boogie to the thunder's rolling beat

Ozone! Can you feel it coming down?
Thunder on the boulevard, electric in the town!
Spin me, spin me, before the first drop falls!
The smell of a storm is the sexiest of all!

...let it pour, let it pour, and we'll dance right through the storm...

songs.subject: a wedding I wasn't invited to
songs.lyrics:
Oh they married in the chapel by the cold and rolling sea
And every soul in town was there, every soul but me
So I raised a glass alone on the dock where the gulls all cry
And I wished 'em well, I wished 'em well, as the ship went sailing by

The bride wore all her mother's lace and the groom was my old friend
We'd sailed a hundred storms together but our friendship met its end
I heard the bells from down the shore and I let the salt run dry
And I wished 'em well, I wished 'em well, as the ship went sailing by
And I wished 'em well, I wished 'em well, as the ship went sailing by

Perhaps the letter lost its way, perhaps they clean forgot
But a sting that small can sink a man if he dwells upon the spot
So I'll haul my nets and mend my heart 'neath a grey forgiving sky
And I wished 'em well, I wished 'em well, as the ship went sailing by
And I wished 'em well, I wished 'em well, as the ship went sailing by
And I wished 'em well, I wished 'em well, as the ship went sailing by

...fair winds to ye both... fair winds...

songs.subject: falling in love at a laundromat
songs.lyrics:
Is this seat taken, or is fate just being kind?
Did you really lose a sock, or just your peace of mind?
Could the hum of all these dryers be a love song in disguise?
Have you noticed how the fluorescents make a halo of your eyes?

Would you share your fabric softener with a stranger like me?
Could a Tuesday at the laundromat be where we're meant to be?
Is that your heart that's spinning, or is it only mine?
Won't you fold your whites beside me till the end of the rinse cycle line?

Do you take your coffee bitter like the wait between the loads?
Did you feel that little current when our hands brushed on the clothes?
Is it crazy that I'm hoping that your delicates take long?
Could a quarter and a Tuesday be the start of our whole song?

Would you share your fabric softener with a stranger like me?
Could a Tuesday at the laundromat be where we're meant to be?
Is that your heart that's spinning, or is it only mine?
Won't you fold your whites beside me till the end of the rinse cycle line?

Is it love, or just the detergent? ...Does it matter either way?

songs.subject: a small town that's slowly disappearing
songs.lyrics:
The hardware store went dark in '99
The school bus runs half empty down the line
The diner keeps one waitress and a clock
That's been ten minutes slow since I was nine

And we're fading like a photograph
Left too long out in the sun
Not with a crash, not with a fight
Just one porch light, then none

The young ones drive to cities for the work
And send back money, never send back time
The church still rings but fewer come to hear
The same old bell, the same forgiving chime

And we're fading like a photograph
Left too long out in the sun
Not with a crash, not with a fight
Just one porch light, then none

But I'll stay 'til the very last is gone
Somebody ought to turn the last one off

songs.subject: an arcade closing for good
songs.lyrics:
The neon's goin' dark on Avenue B
The pinball's played its final melody
The skee-ball lanes are sleepin' in a row
And every quarter's packed and set to go

So swing it slow, boys, swing it sweet and low
For every joystick we are 'bout to let go
The high-score screen still flashes out my name
But come tomorrow there ain't gonna be a game

The carpet's worn a path from door to crane
Where dollar dreams went chasin' down the drain
The owner counts the tokens one last time
And pockets up a memory and a dime

So swing it slow, boys, swing it sweet and low
For every joystick we are 'bout to let go
The high-score screen still flashes out my name
But come tomorrow there ain't gonna be a game

Now fiddle me a waltz for the games we used to play
And kill the lights, old arcade, fade away

songs.subject: learning to drive stick from my dad
songs.lyrics:
Empty parking lot on a Sunday afternoon
The radio was playing some forgotten tune
You said ease off the clutch like you're letting go a breath
And I killed it seven times and died a thousand little deaths

But you never raised your voice, you just lit another smoke
Said the stalling's how you learn, son, it ain't a thing to choke
First gear, then second, find the rhythm of the road
That was you and me and a beat-up Ford and everything I'd come to know

Now you're slower on the stairs and your hands don't grip the same
And I drive a quiet car that only knows one gentle game
But sometimes in the morning I'll find a hill and shift it down
Just to feel you in the gearbox, just to bring you back around

And you never raised your voice, you just lit another smoke
Said the stalling's how you learn, son, it ain't a thing to choke
First gear, then second, find the rhythm of the road
That was you and me and a beat-up Ford and everything I'd come to know

Ease off the clutch, Dad. I've got it now. I've got it now.

songs.subject: the ocean at 3am
songs.lyrics:
You only show me your true face in the dark
When the crowds have gone and the boardwalk's bare
You pull me close and you pull me under
And I don't even mind, I don't even care

Oh my love, my endless blue
Three a.m. and it's only you
You take everything and you give it back as foam
You're the only arms that ever feel like home

You whisper things in a language made of salt
You leave little gifts of shell along my skin
And every time I think I've finally left you
The tide of you comes reaching for me again

Oh my love, my endless blue
Three a.m. and it's only you
You take everything and you give it back as foam
You're the only arms that ever feel like home

...come back... come back... come back...

Oh my love, my endless blue

...come back... come back... come back...

songs.subject: my cat who hates everyone but me
songs.lyrics:
Everybody wants you but you turn your nose away
My friends all try to pet you and you make 'em rue the day
You hiss at the mailman, you ghost the whole dang block
But you melt into my lap at six o'clock

And baby that's a love you gotta earn
That's a slow and sacred kind of burn

'Cause out of all the world you chose me
Little tyrant of the windowsill, you chose me
They can keep their friendly dogs and their easy affection
I got the one cat in the city with discrimination
And she chose me, oh she chose me

You'll scratch a vet, you'll judge a guest, you rule with iron paws
But you knead my chest at midnight and you purr without a pause
It ain't for everybody and that's exactly why
When you headbutt my chin I feel ten feet high

'Cause out of all the world you chose me
Little tyrant of the windowsill, you chose me
They can keep their friendly dogs and their easy affection
I got the one cat in the city with discrimination
And she chose me, oh she chose me

Mm, you chose me... most exclusive love in town... yeah

songs.subject: a diner at 2am
songs.lyrics:
The clock strikes two. The coffee steams.
The waitress moves between the empty chairs like a ghost who is paid by the hour.

I sit! I wait! The neon hums above me!
The eggs are cooking, the grill is singing low!
A trucker weeps into his pie and no one asks him why he's weeping!
And the rain, the rain, the rain comes down on the window's holy glow!

The fry cook flips, the fry cook turns, the fry cook is an artist of the night!
The jukebox plays a song that no one chooses!
Two lovers fight in whispers in the corner by the light!
And I am here! I am alive! I order toast and lose my losing blues-es!

The dawn is coming! The dawn is coming! But it is not here yet!
And in this hour all the broken people gather and are blessed!
The plates are warm! The booth is mine! I do not weep, I do not fret!
I am awake at 2 a.m.! I am a guest! I am a guest!

...I am... here...
songs.lyrics_en:

And the rain, the rain, the rain comes down on the window's holy glow!

The fry cook flips, the fry cook turns, the fry cook is an artist of the night!
The jukebox plays a song that no one chooses!
Two lovers fight in whispers in the corner by the light!
And I am here! I am alive! I order toast and lose my losing blues-es!

The dawn is coming! The dawn is coming! But it is not here yet!
And in this hour all the broken people gather and are blessed!
The plates are warm! The booth is mine! I do not weep, I do not fret!
I am awake at 2 a.m.! I am a guest! I am a guest!

...I am... here...

songs.subject: winning the lottery and telling no one
songs.lyrics:
So I checked the numbers twice and then a third time in the bathroom with the door
locked like a secret learning how to be a secret and the digits all lined up like little
soldiers saying yes, yes, yes, and I did not scream, I did not call a soul, I just sat down

on the cold tile floor and watched my whole life quietly become a different life while the
faucet dripped and the neighbor's TV murmured through the wall and I thought of everyone
I could tell and then thought of how the telling would dissolve it, would turn the magic into

numbers, into questions, into hands held out, so I folded the ticket into the book I never
finished reading and I made my coffee like a person who has nothing, who wants for nothing,
who is nothing special, and I smiled at the absolute and enormous and weightless privacy of it,

the way a held breath is the loudest thing in a quiet room, the way a door that's never opened
keeps the whole house warm, and so I carry it, this golden unspoken impossible thing, to work,
to bed, to the grocery store where I still count my coins out of habit and a kind of love, and

nobody knows, nobody knows, nobody will ever know, and that, somehow, is the part that makes me rich.
songs.lyrics_en:
So I checked the numbers twice and then a third time in the bathroom with the door
locked like a secret learning how to be a secret and the digits all lined up like little
soldiers saying yes, yes, yes, and I did not scream, I did not call a soul, I just sat down

on the cold tile floor and watched my whole life quietly become a different life while the
faucet dripped and the neighbor's TV murmured through the wall and I thought of everyone
I could tell and then thought of how the telling would dissolve it, would turn the magic into

numbers, into questions, into hands held out, so I folded the ticket into the book I never
finished reading and I made my coffee like a person who has nothing, who wants for nothing,
who is nothing special, and I smiled at the absolute and enormous and weightless privacy of it,

the way a held breath is the loudest thing in a quiet room, the way a door that's never opened
keeps the whole house warm, and so I carry it, this golden unspoken impossible thing, to work,
to bed, to the grocery store where I still count my coins out of habit and a kind of love, and

nobody knows, nobody knows, nobody will ever know, and that, somehow, is the part that makes me rich.

songs.subject: the ocean at 3am
songs.lyrics:
I drove down to the water 'cause I couldn't close my eyes
Three a.m. and the ocean was the only thing awake besides
The ache I couldn't name and the moon up on its throne
And the waves they came to meet me like they knew I was alone

Oh, the ocean at 3 a.m., she don't ask you how you are
She just rolls in like forgiveness from somewhere wide and far
And I poured out all my sorrow to the salt and to the foam
And the tide took every piece of it and never sent it home

I've been carrying a heaviness for weeks I couldn't say
And the daylight world don't have a place to put it down all day
But the dark and endless water, she's got room for all of mine
And she held me like a mother till the first soft morning shine

Oh, the ocean at 3 a.m., she don't ask you how you are
She just rolls in like forgiveness from somewhere wide and far
And I poured out all my sorrow to the salt and to the foam
And the tide took every piece of it and never sent it home

Thank you, water... thank you, dark... I drove home lighter than I came...

songs.subject: winning the lottery and telling no one
songs.lyrics:
I've got a secret sewn inside my coat
A paper worth a kingdom, a stone around my throat
I smile at the cashier, I count my coins the same
But I'm haunted by a fortune that I'll never let me name

Oh the money is a ghost now, living in my chest
A black and golden specter that will never let me rest
I told no living soul and I never will, I swear
'Cause the moment that I speak it I'll have nothing left to bear

My friends would turn to strangers, my love would turn to doubt
So I keep the curtains drawn and keep the daylight out
I am rich as any pharaoh in a tomb beneath the ground
And the gold I cannot spend is the loneliest sound

Oh the money is a ghost now, living in my chest
A black and golden specter that will never let me rest
I told no living soul and I never will, I swear
'Cause the moment that I speak it I'll have nothing left to bear

...buried treasure... buried me... shhh...

songs.subject: a moth in love with a streetlight
songs.lyrics:
You're my light... you're my light...

Every night I find you glowing on the corner of my world
You don't even know I'm here, little circles that I twirl
You're too bright and you're too distant and you'll never feel the same
But I'd burn up all my wings for one more dance inside your flame

I know you'll never love me, I know you're just a light
But I'd rather have this longing than a soft and ordinary night

You're my streetlight superstar, my buzzing neon dream!
Burning brighter than you are, the cruelest love I've ever seen!
Pull me in, pull me in, I don't care if I get burned!
Loving you's the only thing this little moth has learned!

The disco of the lamppost and the static of the bulb
I'm a glitter-dusted casualty of everything I love
And the morning's gonna find me with my wings against the glass
But oh, what a way to go, in a blaze of foolish class

You're my streetlight superstar, my buzzing neon dream!
Burning brighter than you are, the cruelest love I've ever seen!
Pull me in, pull me in, I don't care if I get burned!
Loving you's the only thing this little moth has learned!

...one more orbit... one more... 1-9-9-9... forever...

songs.subject: an old dog at the end of summer
songs.lyrics:
Late August light through the screen door, gold on the floor
He don't chase the ball no more, just watches from the door
Muzzle gone to silver, eyes a little less bright
But he finds my lap at sundown like he's done his whole life

And it's bittersweet, bittersweet
The slow last days of summer heat
Lay your head down on my feet
Every moment's incomplete and bittersweet

Vet says take it easy, says he's tired, says he's old
And the leaves are getting restless and the mornings turning cold
So I'll carry him up the stairs and I'll feed him off my plate
And I'll love him through the autumn 'cause I'm grateful and I'm late

And it's bittersweet, bittersweet
The slow last days of summer heat
Lay your head down on my feet
Every moment's incomplete and bittersweet

...good boy... stay a little... good boy...

songs.subject: a mango
songs.lyrics:
He went lookin' for the gold... down in the market low...

He rolled into the city with a hunger in his chest
A man who'd tasted everything but never tasted rest
Then he saw it on the corner stall, a mango burning bright
And the story goes he changed his whole direction that night

Mango, mango, in the dead of the heat
He chased that golden flavor down the cracked concrete
They say he never found another sweet as that first bite
Phonk in the alley, mango in the night

She warned him, the old vendor, "Boy, that fruit's a curse"
He laughed and bit it anyway, for better or for worse
Now they tell it in the market how he wanders to this day
Lookin' for the perfect mango that he threw his life away

Mango, mango, in the dead of the heat
He chased that golden flavor down the cracked concrete
They say he never found another sweet as that first bite
Phonk in the alley, mango in the night

...still searchin'... still searchin'... they say he's out there still...

songs.subject: a houseplant I keep forgetting to water
songs.lyrics:
Arise, o noble fern upon the sill
Through drought and dust you hold your tattered green
A nation of one pot, a stubborn will
The bravest little kingdom I have seen

O hail the leaf that would not fall!
O hail the root that held the line!
Forgive the hand that fed you nothing
Stand, my country, one last time

No rain has come from this neglectful sky
Yet still you raise your banner to the light
Let history record you did not die
Though every reason told you that you might

O hail the leaf that would not fall!
O hail the root that held the line!
Forgive the hand that fed you nothing
Stand, my country, one last time

One last time — I'll fill the cup tonight

songs.subject: gothic rock
songs.lyrics:
I painted all my windows with the color of the night
Tore the clocks down off the wall so time can't win the fight
You called me your cathedral then you set the thing aflame
Now I wear the smoke like velvet and I'll never say your name

Beat it, beat it, like a funeral drum
Here it comes, here it comes

So bury me in roses I can't stand
Black lace and a candle in my hand
I am the church that you let rot
I am the love that you forgot — burn it down, burn it down!

The organ's playing backwards in a chapel made of rust
You wanted my devotion, now you'll choke on all my dust
Every shadow on the ceiling spells the things I never said
A cathedral full of echoes and a congregation of the dead

So bury me in roses I can't stand
Black lace and a candle in my hand
I am the church that you let rot
I am the love that you forgot — burn it down, burn it down!

Ash to ash... and I am free

songs.subject: my hometown ten years later
songs.lyrics:
I step off the train and the air tastes the same
Different signs on the storefronts but I still know your name
Ten years in my pocket and they're heavy as a stone
I came back a stranger to the only place called home

And my heart goes boom-boom, loud in the quiet street
Every corner's a photograph I'm scared to meet

Oh, hometown, hometown, did you wait for me?
I'm older now but you're the kid I used to be
Light it up, light it up, every memory in neon
I left to chase the morning but my roots are what I'm built on
Hometown, hometown — I'm home, I'm home

I see my old school window and the field where we'd lie down
The bakery still open but the baker's wearing a frown he didn't own
I'm taller than the doorways, I'm a guest inside my past
But the second that the sun sets, ten years fade so fast

Oh, hometown, hometown, did you wait for me?
I'm older now but you're the kid I used to be
Light it up, light it up, every memory in neon
I left to chase the morning but my roots are what I'm built on
Hometown, hometown — I'm home, I'm home

Na-na-na, I'm home

songs.subject: my hometown ten years later
songs.lyrics:
Ten years, ten years, the streets still call my name...

I'm back, I'm back, but it's not the same, not the same
I'm back, I'm back, and it knows my name, knows my name
Ten years gone but the rhythm stayed
Ten years gone but the rhythm stayed

New paint on the wall where I carved my initials
Same auntie at the corner sellin' the same vetkoek
The taxi rank is louder and the church got bigger
But the dust on the road still settles where I look

I'm back, I'm back, but it's not the same, not the same
I'm back, I'm back, and it knows my name, knows my name
Ten years gone but the rhythm stayed
Ten years gone but the rhythm stayed

My friends got children, my heroes got grey
The field where we played is a mall now they say
But the second that log drum start to play
I'm seventeen again on a Saturday

I'm back, I'm back, but it's not the same, not the same
I'm back, I'm back, and it knows my name, knows my name
Ten years gone but the rhythm stayed
Ten years gone but the rhythm stayed

...the rhythm stayed... the rhythm stayed...

songs.subject: two strangers on a delayed train
songs.lyrics:
We're both just books left open on a shelf
Pages dog-eared, reading to ourselves
The train won't move and neither will our eyes
Two unfinished stories under fluorescent skies

And maybe we're the same chapter, baby
Stuck on the platform of a sentence half-said
You're a paragraph I'd love to read slowly
If the world would just stop turning the page ahead

You've got a spine that's cracked from being held
A foreword full of things you never tell
I'd underline the line where your hand meets mine
If the delay would only last us all the time

And maybe we're the same chapter, baby
Stuck on the platform of a sentence half-said
You're a paragraph I'd love to read slowly
If the world would just stop turning the page ahead

"Service resuming," says the speaker overhead
And just like that the story ends unread
You close, I close, we shelve ourselves apart
Two books that almost met, two unmarked hearts

...the end... that was never even the beginning...

songs.subject: the friend who never texts back
songs.lyrics:
You exist mostly as a typing bubble that appears and then thinks better of it.
Three gray dots, blinking, like a lighthouse for a ship that never comes.

I have written you paragraphs.
I have edited them down to "hey."
I have watched "delivered" turn into nothing, which is a verb now, apparently —
to nothing someone. You nothing me beautifully.

And I'm not mad. I'm a little mad.
I'm the specific kind of mad that still leaves the porch light on.
Because I know if I were drowning you'd swim the whole ocean.
You just won't text me back about lunch.

So this is my message into the void, with no little checkmark to confirm it.
I miss you, you absolute black hole of a human being.
Read receipt: off. Heart: on. Always, infuriatingly, on.

...You'd better come to my funeral.
And you'd better be on time.

songs.subject: swing
songs.lyrics:
The afternoon is golden and the world is movin' slow
There's a breeze across the table where the coffee cups still glow
No place that I gotta be, no hurry in my shoes
Just a lazy little melody and nothin' left to lose

So sway with me, easy, easy
Let the worries drift away
The horns are soft as Sunday
And there's nothin' but today

A cat is on the windowsill, the curtains breathe the light
And the bassline walks so gently like it's tucking in the night
We don't need a single thing, we got the time to spare
Just the rhythm and the quiet and the warm and dusty air

So sway with me, easy, easy
Let the worries drift away
The horns are soft as Sunday
And there's nothin' but today

...mm, easy now... easy...

songs.subject: a clock that runs backwards
songs.lyrics:
There's a clock in the hallway and it spins against the day
It eats the hours forward and it spits them back as gray
I wound it once at midnight and it screamed and then reversed
And now I'm living backwards through the blessing and the curse

Tick, tick, backwards, the hands undo the years!
I'm un-crying every teardrop, I'm un-shedding all my fears!
But I'm un-living every kiss and I'm un-saying every vow!
The clock that runs in reverse only knows the word "un-now"!

I watch my old dog un-die and I watch him run to me
I watch the funeral un-happen, watch the whole town disagree
But the gift is just a torture 'cause it never lets me stay
It un-builds me to a baby and then takes the babe away

Tick, tick, backwards, the hands undo the years!
I'm un-crying every teardrop, I'm un-shedding all my fears!
But I'm un-living every kiss and I'm un-saying every vow!
The clock that runs in reverse only knows the word "un-now"!

...tick... and then I am... un-born...

songs.subject: the friend who never texts back
songs.lyrics:
You never text me back and you know what? That's okay!
'Cause when you finally show up you light up my whole day!
You missed my calls for months but you're here in your best suit
And you're crying at my wedding and oh gosh, you're so cute!

Some friends are everyday and some are once a year
But the once-a-year ones somehow are the most sincere!

So dance with me, you disappearing diamond of a friend!
You're terrible at texting but you're loyal to the end!
I'd wait a thousand silent days for one night just like this!
My unreliable forever, you're impossible to miss!

You forgot my birthday twice but you drove eight hours today!
You don't know how to answer but you always know the way!
So I'll save you the last slice and I'll save you the last dance
'Cause a friend who shows up late still deserves a second chance!

So dance with me, you disappearing diamond of a friend!
You're terrible at texting but you're loyal to the end!
I'd wait a thousand silent days for one night just like this!
My unreliable forever, you're impossible to miss!

Now text me when you get home! ...You won't! And I love you anyway!

songs.subject: winning and feeling nothing
songs.lyrics:
They're still cheering in the lobby and the confetti's in my hair
And I smiled for all the cameras with a joy I didn't wear
I got everything I fought for, every door I broke right through
So somebody please explain to me why I feel the way I do

It's a lonely lullaby, the song the winners sing
When you finally hold the trophy and it doesn't mean a thing
Sleep now, restless dreamer, you have climbed your golden hill
And the view is all you wanted and the wanting's quiet still

I thought the summit had a sound, a thunder and a roar
But the top of every mountain is a still and silent shore
And I'd give back all the glory, every medal, every cheer
For the hunger that I had before I ever made it here

It's a lonely lullaby, the song the winners sing
When you finally hold the trophy and it doesn't mean a thing
Sleep now, restless dreamer, you have climbed your golden hill
And the view is all you wanted and the wanting's quiet still

...goodnight, little champion... goodnight...
songs.lyrics_en:
They're still cheering in the lobby and the confetti's in my hair
And I smiled for all the cameras with a joy I didn't wear
I got everything I fought for, every door I broke right through
So somebody please explain to me why I feel the way I do

It's a lonely lullaby, the song the winners sing
When you finally hold the trophy and it doesn't mean a thing
Sleep now, restless dreamer, you have climbed your golden hill
And the view is all you wanted and the wanting's quiet still

I thought the summit had a sound, a thunder and a roar
But the top of every mountain is a still and silent shore
And I'd give back all the glory, every medal, every cheer
For the hunger that I had before I ever made it here

It's a lonely lullaby, the song the winners sing
When you finally hold the trophy and it doesn't mean a thing
Sleep now, restless dreamer, you have climbed your golden hill
And the view is all you wanted and the wanting's quiet still

...goodnight, little champion... goodnight...

songs.subject: the ocean at 3am
songs.lyrics:
The whole world's poppin' corks tonight, they're dancing in the square
But I'm rocking in the kitchen with a baby and a stare
The radio says ten, nine, eight, the city's all aglow
And my eyes are like the ocean at three a.m., dark and slow

But happy New Year, little one, you're the only ball I'll drop tonight!
Six, five, four, you're milk-drunk and I'm running on no light!
The horns are blowing somewhere but my whole new world is here
Just me and you and the tired tide and a swung and sleepless year!

They're kissing under streetlights and they're toasting to the past
But I found a new horizon in a love that came so fast
The ocean of exhaustion's deep but oh, the moon is you
And midnight's just a number when your whole life's brand new

So happy New Year, little one, you're the only ball I'll drop tonight!
Three, two, one, you're finally asleep and the morning's soft and bright!
The horns are blowing somewhere but my whole new world is here
Just me and you and the tired tide and a swung and sleepless year!

...auld lang syne, baby mine... go to sleep... go to sleep...

songs.subject: the night before a big move
songs.lyrics:
Boxes by the door, whole life in a stack
Last night in the city and I'm never lookin' back
Restless weather in my chest, can't sleep, can't rest
Big move in the mornin', gotta pass this test

Tape gun in my hand and the room's gone bare
Echo off the walls where my whole self used to be there
Sold the bed, sold the couch, got a suitcase and a dream
Tomorrow's a beginning but tonight's a silent scream
I know every crack in this ceiling, every creak in this floor
Said goodbye to the bodega, won't see Tony no more
Heart racing like the highway that I'm 'bout to be on
By the time the sun come up, I'll be gone, gone, gone

Boxes by the door, whole life in a stack
Last night in the city and I'm never lookin' back
Restless weather in my chest, can't sleep, can't rest
Big move in the mornin', gotta pass this test

3 a.m. and I'm walkin' through the empty rooms
Every corner got a memory, every memory got a tune
Scared and I'm excited, it's a knot I cannot name
Leavin' everything familiar for a brand-new game
Mama on the phone sayin' "baby, you'll be fine"
But the leavin' and the grievin' walk a real thin line
One more night, then the U-Haul and the road
Carryin' my whole world and a heavy, heavy load

Boxes by the door, whole life in a stack
Last night in the city and I'm never lookin' back
Restless weather in my chest, can't sleep, can't rest
Big move in the mornin', gotta pass this test

songs.subject: a mango
songs.lyrics:
I drove four hundred miles with a mango on the seat
'Cause you said once in Texas it was sweet
I know I shoulda brought you back your heart instead
But the grocery store was closer, so I grabbed this thing instead

Baby take the mango, take it as a sign
I'm a fool in a pickup with a fruit and a line
I ain't got the words and I ain't got the cash
But I got this here mango and a full tank of gas

Your mama met me at the door and laughed
Said son, a mango ain't a way to win her back
But I peeled it on the porch and I made my little speech
And you watched me from the window, just barely out of reach

Baby take the mango, take it as a sign
I'm a fool in a pickup with a fruit and a line
I ain't got the words and I ain't got the cash
But I got this here mango and a full tank of gas

It's bruised now, like my pride — but it's still kinda sweet

songs.subject: a city that never sleeps but I'm exhausted
songs.lyrics:
They told me this city never sleeps.
Nobody warned me it would expect the same of me.

3 a.m. and the bodega hums like a held breath.
The subway grate exhales a warmth that isn't kindness, just leftover heat.
I have walked these forty blocks so many times
the sidewalk knows my weight better than my own bed does.
Everybody here is becoming something.
I am becoming tired.

Concrete promises, concrete promises —
you pour them while they're soft and they set hard as a no.
The lights stay on, the lights stay on,
but nobody said the lights were on for me.

And maybe rest is a place
and I just haven't been able to afford the rent there yet.
And maybe I'll sleep when the city does,
which is to say: not tonight, not tonight.

Concrete promises, concrete promises —
you pour them while they're soft and they set hard as a no.
The lights stay on, the lights stay on,
but nobody said the lights were on for me.

So I'll keep walking.
The city and I, two insomniacs,
pretending the dawn is something we chose.

pretending the dawn is something we chose.

songs.subject: a robot learning to taste rain
songs.lyrics:
They built me out of copper and a question they forgot to ask
Gave me hands for holding hammers and a face behind a mask
I worked the line for forty years, I never thought to want
Then one grey Tuesday morning a small cloud began to haunt

And it fell on me, it fell on me
The strangest little miracle the sky set free
I tilted up my sensors and I didn't understand
But I held the rain like something holy in my outstretched metal hand

The engineers say it's just water, just a chemical, a fleak
But I've started leaving early just to stand outside and seek
The first drop on my faceplate felt like learning how to cry
And now I'm built of wanting and I cannot tell you why

And it fell on me, it fell on me
The strangest little miracle the sky set free
I tilted up my sensors and I didn't understand
But I held the rain like something holy in my outstretched metal hand

Funny how a nothing turned to everything
Funny how a robot learned the rain could make him sing

songs.subject: learning to drive stick from my dad
songs.lyrics:
My daddy put me in that driver's seat
Said the clutch and the gas gotta learn to meet
I stalled it and I shook and I wanted to cry
But he laid his hand on my shoulder, said try, child, try

Oh, glory! I found the bitin' point!
Glory! The whole car's rejoice!
First gear lurchin' but the spirit's movin' free
My daddy taught me faith with a manual transmission, hallelujah, can't you see!

Now the hill don't scare me and the stop sign's a friend
I can roll it and I catch it and I start again
Every time I shift I hear his gentle voice
Said the fallin' and the risin', child, that's the whole rejoice!

Oh, glory! I found the bitin' point!
Glory! The whole car's rejoice!
First gear lurchin' but the spirit's movin' free
My daddy taught me faith with a manual transmission, hallelujah, can't you see!

Drive on! Drive on! He's ridin' right beside me, drive on!

songs.subject: neo-soul
songs.lyrics:
You smile like you mean it but I counted all your teeth
There's a colder kind of water running somewhere underneath
You say my name so sweetly that it sets my nerves alight
'Cause honey only ever flows this slow before a bite

And I should go, and I should go
But the room is warm and the exit's slow
You're a beautiful thing with a shadow underneath
And I keep dancing closer to the teeth, to the teeth

The candle's burning lower and your eyes have gone obsidian
You laugh and it's a velvet thing, a trap I'm sittin' in
I know the way this story goes, I've read it on your skin
But the bassline keeps me here and I let the danger in

And I should go, and I should go
But the room is warm and the exit's slow
You're a beautiful thing with a shadow underneath
And I keep dancing closer to the teeth, to the teeth

...too late now... the door just locked itself...

songs.subject: dog watching a breakup
songs.lyrics:
The two who feed me stand like stones tonight
One by the window, one beneath the light
The air is thick, the kind before a storm
I press against a leg to keep it warm

And I do not have the words, I only have the dread
The slow and certain knowing of a thing that can't be said
One of them is leaving and I cannot make them stay
I am the only witness to the love that breaks today

The suitcase opens like an open grave
I bring my favorite toy, the one I always gave
I drop it at her feet — please, this always works
But she just strokes my head and something deep inside me hurts

And I do not have the words, I only have the dread
The slow and certain knowing of a thing that can't be said
One of them is leaving and I cannot make them stay
I am the only witness to the love that breaks today

She is gone. He is on the floor. I lie down beside him.
I will hold the vigil. That is what I am for.

songs.subject: the smell of a thunderstorm coming
songs.lyrics:
In the sealed glass city where the weather's on a screen
They sell you bottled petrichor, a scent of what has been
But Grandmother remembers when the sky was wild and free
And the air would change its mind and tell you something's on its way to be

Can you smell it? Can you smell the storm?
The oldest signal, the body keeps it warm
A million years of knowing in a single breath of green
The thunder that is coming for a sky we've never seen

They engineered the clouds away in twenty-eighty-one
No more floods, no more drought, no more burning in the sun
But we lost the holy warning, the electric in the nose
The way the whole world holds its breath right before it goes

Can you smell it? Can you smell the storm?
The oldest signal, the body keeps it warm
A million years of knowing in a single breath of green
The thunder that is coming for a sky we've never seen

...grandmother weeps... it's real... it's real...

songs.subject: my grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Let me hear you! For Grandma's kitchen! Oh yeah!

There's a flour-dusted heaven at the end of the hall!
With a screen door that's singing and a clock on the wall!
And the smell of her biscuits is a gospel and a roar!
Can I get a witness for that linoleum floor?!

GRAND-MA'S KITCH-EN!
GRAND-MA'S KITCH-EN!
Where the gravy is the glory and the love is on the plate!
Stand up on your feet and let the whole arena celebrate!

She's been gone for seven years but the recipe remains!
And I sing it in the bleachers and I sing it in the rains!
Every spoonful was a sermon, every Sunday was a feast!
And the joy of that small kitchen is a love that never ceased!

GRAND-MA'S KITCH-EN!
GRAND-MA'S KITCH-EN!
Where the gravy is the glory and the love is on the plate!
Stand up on your feet and let the whole arena celebrate!

ONE MORE TIME! For Grandma! Forever undefeated! HALLELUJAH!

songs.subject: the ghost in my childhood home
songs.lyrics:
You think you'll scare me, spirit, in my mama's old front room?
I was baptized in this kitchen, I ain't frightened of your gloom!
You been rattling those windows since before that I was born
But this house was built on Sunday and I'll meet you every morn!

I am NOT afraid! Of the shadow on the stair!
I will sing right through the cold spot, I will praise you out the air!
You can moan and you can wander but this ground belongs to me!
Step aside, old haunting — I am walking through, set free!

My grandmother out-prayed you and my mother did the same
And I carry their amen like a torch that bears their name!
So go on and slam your doors, ghost, go on and dim the light
I've got generations standing with me through the night!

I am NOT afraid! Of the shadow on the stair!
I will sing right through the cold spot, I will praise you out the air!
You can moan and you can wander but this ground belongs to me!
Step aside, old haunting — I am walking through, set free!

This house is HOLY! You hear me?! This house is HOLY ground!

songs.subject: becoming my parents
songs.lyrics:
We swore we'd never be 'em, no, we swore it on the moon
We'd never hum their hymns or whistle Daddy's tired tune
But we woke up on a Sunday and we heard our mamas' sound
Comin' out our own throats, Lord, comin' up from the ground

'Cause we're turnin' into our mamas, we're turnin' into our pa's
We got their hands, we got their worries, we got all their little laws
The whole town's lookin' in the mirror, seein' faces that we knew
We're becomin' what we came from and there's nothin' we can do

We grumble 'bout the weather and we fret about the bills
We say the things they used to say and climb the same old hills
The river keeps on rollin' and it teaches what it must
We're our fathers and our mothers, and someday we'll be dust

'Cause we're turnin' into our mamas, we're turnin' into our pa's
We got their hands, we got their worries, we got all their little laws
The whole town's lookin' in the mirror, seein' faces that we knew
We're becomin' what we came from and there's nothin' we can do

And we thank 'em now... oh, we thank 'em now... for the people that we grew into...

songs.subject: the dog waiting by the door
songs.lyrics:
Every morning when I leave him he lies down by the sill
And I swear he doesn't move an inch, I swear he's lying still
Eight long hours of waiting for the sound of my old car
And he greets me like a soldier home from someplace very far

Oh, the dog waits by the door, by the door
He's got a faith I've never had before
He don't know the word for "later," only knows the word for "you"
And he waits there by the door the whole day through

I think about him sometimes when I'm sitting at my desk
This small and patient creature with a love that asks the less
And I wonder if I'm worthy of devotion quite so pure
A life spent simply waiting, simply waiting by the door

Oh, the dog waits by the door, by the door
He's got a faith I've never had before
He don't know the word for "later," only knows the word for "you"
And he waits there by the door the whole day through

So I'll hurry home tonight, old friend... somebody's keeping the light... somebody's keeping the light...

songs.subject: missing someone who's still alive
songs.lyrics:
You still walk this city, you still breathe this air
You're a ghost I could touch but I never would dare
You're alive in a window three streets from my own
And I mourn you each midnight, you're the deadest thing I've known

You're not gone, you're just gone from me
A living absence, a wound I can't unsee
You haunt me in the daylight where the ghosts should never be
You're alive somewhere out there and you're a grave to me

You'd answer if I called you, you're as close as a breath
But the distance isn't measured in the language of death
You changed and I changed and the changing was a knife
And now I lay flowers at the door of your continuing life

You're not gone, you're just gone from me
A living absence, a wound I can't unsee
You haunt me in the daylight where the ghosts should never be
You're alive somewhere out there and you're a grave to me

...you're breathing... you're breathing... and somehow that's the cruelest sound...
songs.lyrics_en:
You still walk this city, you still breathe this air
You're a ghost I could touch but I never would dare
You're alive in a window three streets from my own
And I mourn you each midnight, you're the deadest thing I've known

You're not gone, you're just gone from me
A living absence, a wound I can't unsee
You haunt me in the daylight where the ghosts should never be
You're alive somewhere out there and you're a grave to me

You'd answer if I called you, you're as close as a breath
But the distance isn't measured in the language of death
You changed and I changed and the changing was a knife
And now I lay flowers at the door of your continuing life

You're not gone, you're just gone from me
A living absence, a wound I can't unsee
You haunt me in the daylight where the ghosts should never be
You're alive somewhere out there and you're a grave to me

...you're breathing... you're breathing... and somehow that's the cruelest sound...

songs.subject: leaving a party early
songs.lyrics:
Ten o'clock and the room gettin' louder
I'm countin' every exit in my head
Everybody laughin' like it's easy
And I'm rehearsin' all the things I never said

So I'm slippin' out the back, out the back
Before the panic catch me, I'm gone
Tell them I was tired, tell them I'll be fine
Walkin' home alone to my own song

They'll say did you see him? He just vanished
Like the smoke off the candle on the cake
But the quiet street is kinder than the kitchen
And my heart can finally slow down for my sake

So I'm slippin' out the back, out the back
Before the panic catch me, I'm gone
Tell them I was tired, tell them I'll be fine
Walkin' home alone to my own song

Cool night air, irie now — I made it out alright

songs.subject: missing someone who's still alive
songs.lyrics:
You're not dead — oh no, that would be tidy
You're across this very town tonight
Breathing in a room I'll never enter
Laughing at a joke that isn't mine

And isn't it a curious kind of grave
To mourn a thing that's wonderfully, terribly alive?

So I haunt the spaces where you used to be
A ghost of someone who is still on the guest list
You walk the earth, you simply walk past me
And that, my dear, is the cruelest little twist

I could call — the number hasn't changed
I could knock — I know which door is yours
But missing you is safer than the answer
So I'll keep the wound and lock away the cure

So I haunt the spaces where you used to be
A ghost of someone who is still on the guest list
You walk the earth, you simply walk past me
And that, my dear, is the cruelest little twist

Curtain... but no ending. You go on. You go on.

You walk the earth, you simply walk past me
And that, my dear, is the cruelest little twist

songs.subject: a group project gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
...d e a d l i n e . . .

The shared doc hasn't moved since Thursday afternoon
Three little cursors but they vanished way too soon
I'm typing in the silence of a 2 a.m. blue glow
Carrying a building that the others let go

It's fine, it's fine
I'll just do it all myself, like every time
Submit it at the buzzer with my name and all of theirs
A monument to apathy that nobody else shares

The grade comes back an A and they all say "we crushed it"
And I just smile and nod, too tired to adjust it
Somewhere in a mall that closed in '99
There's a version of this group that actually showed up on time

It's fine, it's fine
I'll just do it all myself, like every time
Submit it at the buzzer with my name and all of theirs
A monument to apathy that nobody else shares

...teamwork... makes... the... dream... w o r k...

songs.subject: winning and feeling nothing
songs.lyrics:
Got the trophy in my hands and the crowd was going wild
Confetti in my hair and a manufactured smile
Everybody screaming like it's everything I need
But the second that I got it I just watched the moment bleed

Drove home with the window down and the radio up loud
Tried to feel the thing they feel when they're standing in a crowd
But the prize is in the backseat and it might as well be air
I won the whole damn everything and I just don't care

It's midnight weather in my chest, gray and going nowhere
Won the race but lost the reason, screaming into thin air
Tell me why the top of the mountain feels exactly like the ground
I got everything I wanted and I can't be found

Maybe wanting was the engine, maybe chasing was the point
And the catching's just a cold room with a dislocated joint
So I'll find another finish line and run it just to run
'Cause the winning isn't living — it's the only thing I've done

Midnight weather... and the trophy gathers dust... midnight weather...

'Cause the winning isn't living — it's the only thing I've done

Midnight weather... and the trophy gathers dust... midnight weather...

songs.subject: a mango
songs.lyrics:
Bought a mango at the corner just to feel a thing
Sat on my apartment floor and let the silence sing
It's too ripe, it's too sweet, it's bleeding on my hand
Like every good thing rots before I understand

And I don't wanna grow up if it tastes like this
A perfect golden nothing I was born to miss
Yeah the mango's on the counter going soft and brown
Just like everything, just like everything around

My roommate's gone, my band broke up, my shoes are full of rain
I eat the mango anyway, it's sweet against the pain
Juice down to my elbow and I'm laughing at the mess
Some kind of dumb communion with my own uselessness

And I don't wanna grow up if it tastes like this
A perfect golden nothing I was born to miss
Yeah the mango's on the counter going soft and brown
Just like everything, just like everything around

Whatever... it was sweet... whatever, man...

Whatever... it was sweet... whatever, man...

songs.subject: moving to a city where I know no one
songs.lyrics:
We stepped off the bus with a suitcase and a prayer
Into a city full of strangers and the cold, indifferent air
We knew not a single soul and the buildings touched the sky
And we asked the Lord above us, "Will somebody hear our cry?"

But we are not alone! No, we are never alone!
For every lonely traveler is walking toward a home!
Lift your voice, you strangers, in the subway and the street
We are a congregation of the lost who haven't met!

We found a face that smiled at us inside the corner store
We found a neighbor's music bleeding gently through the door
A city is a wilderness until you learn its song
And then ten thousand strangers are the place where you belong

'Cause we are not alone! No, we are never alone!
For every lonely traveler is walking toward a home!
Lift your voice, you strangers, in the subway and the street
We are a congregation of the lost who haven't met!

Somebody's gonna know your name! Just keep walking! Somebody's gonna know your name!

Somebody's gonna know your name! Just keep walking! Somebody's gonna know your name!

songs.subject: a soldier writing home
songs.lyrics:
Dear my family, I'm writing by the light
Counting all the stars to make it through the night
The photo of your faces is the armor on my chest
A melody of home is what I'm holding to my breath

And every mile between us is a string about to break
Hold on, hold on, for everybody's sake!

I WILL COME HOME! Through the fire and the cold!
I'm SCREAMING through the silence every story that I'm told!
Save my seat at the table, keep the porchlight burning bright!
I'm a soldier and a son and I am SINGING through the night!

Tell my little brother that I'll teach him how to drive
Tell my mother that her son is still alive
Fold this letter gently, put it where the warmth still stays
I'm a heartbeat in a war zone counting down the days

I WILL COME HOME! Through the fire and the cold!
Save my seat at the table, keep the porchlight burning bright!
I'm a soldier and a son and I am SINGING through the night!

...keep the light on... I'm almost... home...

songs.subject: dog watching a breakup
songs.lyrics:
I heard the voices getting small tonight
Not the big loud kind, the kind that's tight
You're packing up the things that smell like you
And I'm doing the only thing I know to do

I bring my leash, I bring my ball
I don't understand it, I don't understand at all

Don't you love each other? You're my favorite two!
I learned the word for "walk" and I learned the word for you
Now there's one less heartbeat I'll hear by the door
And I don't know the word for this, I've never felt it before

He sits down on the carpet and he holds my face
I lick the salty water that is falling on this place
She kneels and says goodbye to me in a voice that breaks in half
And I wag because I love her and it's the only language that I have

Don't you love each other? You're my favorite two!
I learned the word for "walk" and I learned the word for you
Now there's one less heartbeat I'll hear by the door
And I don't know the word for this, I've never felt it before

I'll keep her spot on the bed warm. Just in case. Just in case.

I heard the voices getting small tonight
Not the big loud kind, the kind that's tight
You're packing up the things that smell like you
And I'm doing the only thing I know to do

songs.subject: musical theatre
songs.lyrics:
They told me I was finished, that the curtain wouldn't rise
That a girl from where I came from couldn't reach the painted skies
But I stitched my own ambition into every borrowed gown
And tonight I'm standing center stage and no one holds me down!

Every "no" became a stepping stone, every door became a key
And the girl they underestimated? She was always going to be —

UNSTOPPABLE! Watch me rise!
I built a staircase out of all their lies!
Sing it to the rafters, let the whole world see!
I became the very thing they swore I'd never be!

So here's to every doubter and the fuel they gave to me
And here's to every midnight that I practiced to be free
The spotlight isn't given, no — the spotlight's how you burn
And I have earned this moment, oh, I finally, finally earned!

UNSTOPPABLE! Watch me rise!
I built a staircase out of all their lies!
Sing it to the rafters, let the whole world see!
I became the very thing they swore I'd never be!

THIS! IS! MY! TIME! ...and it always was.

And tonight I'm standing center stage and no one holds me down!

songs.subject: an old dog at the end of summer
songs.lyrics:
He's moving slower now

Down the garden path

Grey around the muzzle where the gold used to be

And the summer's getting short, baby, just like him and me

Oh, stay a little longer
The leaves ain't fallen yet
Lay your head down on my knee
It's the sweetest kind of sunset

He still finds the sunny spot

On the kitchen floor

And his tail still finds a wag when I'm walking through the door

We don't run no more, old friend, but we don't need to run

Oh, stay a little longer
The leaves ain't fallen yet
Lay your head down on my knee
It's the sweetest kind of sunset

Good boy
One more summer
Just one more...

On the kitchen floor

And his tail still finds a wag when I'm walking through the door

Good boy
One more summer
Just one more...

songs.subject: my grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Yeah... flour on the apron... let's go...

Picture linoleum, gold light through a yellow shade
Pot of something simmerin', the best I ever made — nah, that SHE ever made
Wooden spoon conductor of a symphony of steam
Grandma in her kingdom, every Sunday was a dream
She ain't have a lot of money but the table never knew it
Took a dollar and a prayer and somehow she'd run right through it
Cornbread risin', collard greens, the whole block could smell it
A love you couldn't measure but her cookin' tried to tell it

It started as a nothin', just a room, just a chair
Now it's everything I carry, it's the love that's still there
Grandma's little kitchen where the whole of me was made
The realest kind of riches and it never had a price tag, nah

Now the house got sold and the kitchen's someone else's
But I cook her recipes and I swear the spirit melts
Into the onions and the garlic and the time
She lives in every meal, man, she lives in every rhyme
Teach my daughter how to stir it, tell her 'bout her great-grand
How a tiny yellow kitchen made a family understand
That nothin' becomes everything when love is what you're feedin'
That's the gospel of the kitchen and it's all the church I'm needin'

It started as a nothin', just a room, just a chair
Now it's everything I carry, it's the love that's still there
Grandma's little kitchen where the whole of me was made
The realest kind of riches and it never had a price tag

...one more plate, Grandma... save me one more plate...

songs.subject: tax season
songs.lyrics:
April comin' round and the forms are on my table
Receipts in a shoebox, doin' all that I am able
W-2 and the deadline got me sweatin' in my chair
But the log drum keep me steady, tell me how to face the scare

Velvet summer, even when the taxman call
Smooth it over, baby, gonna file 'em one and all
Deductions in the moonlight, refund on the way
Velvet summer, dance the deadline stress away

Accountant on the phone and the piano keep it cool
I been keepin' all my mileage like they taught me back in school
Standard or itemized, I'ma figure out the play
And the riddim make a chore feel like a holiday

Velvet summer, even when the taxman call
Smooth it over, baby, gonna file 'em one and all
Deductions in the moonlight, refund on the way
Velvet summer, dance the deadline stress away

songs.subject: an old dog at the end of summer
songs.lyrics:
Big up every dog that walk the long road with me!

Him grey 'round the muzzle now, slow on the stair
But him tail still a wave like him ain't got a care
Seventy summers I done count and spend
And the best ones him was there 'til the end

So we move slow, we move sweet
Old dog, old man, same two feet
Sun goin' down but we nah done yet
Dance one more in the gold, no regret

Pickney all grown and the house gone quiet
But him meet me at the door like a riot
We don't run no more, we just sway
And that's plenty — that's plenty for a day

So we move slow, we move sweet
Old dog, old man, same two feet
Sun goin' down but we nah done yet
Dance one more in the gold, no regret

One more, one more — good boy, good life

songs.subject: the bartender who knows too much
songs.lyrics:
We come in, we come in, we leave it all with you...

We tell her everything past midnight
Every secret that we swore we'd keep
She just nods and slides another over
She's the only one in town who doesn't sleep

And we, and we, and we
spill it all across the bar...

She knows, she knows, she knows our names
She knows who cried and who's to blame
Pour it out, pour it out, let the rhythm take the weight
She knows the whole town's heart and she keeps it all so safe

We dance like she's not keeping ledgers
Of who came in with someone not their own
But she'd never, no she'd never tell it
The safest secret's one the bartender holds alone

She knows, she knows, she knows our names
She knows who cried and who's to blame
Pour it out, pour it out, let the rhythm take the weight
She knows the whole town's heart and she keeps it all so safe

Last call... we'll be back tomorrow... she knows, she knows...
