songs.subject: the ghost in my childhood home
songs.constraint: starts each line with same word
songs.lyrics:
Still she walks the upper landing where the floorboards moan
Still she waits beside the window that I called my own
Still she hums the broken lullaby my mother used to sing
Still she haunts the empty hallway and she will not let it ring

House of my blood, house of my bones
House where the dead refuse to leave me alone
Heavy hangs the air where her shadow used to stand
Heavy is the weight of her cold and patient hand

Never did she frighten me when I was small and weak
Never did she answer though I begged for her to speak
Never will she follow me beyond the rotting door
Never will she rest until the house exists no more

House of my blood, house of my bones
House where the dead refuse to leave me alone
Heavy hangs the air where her shadow used to stand
Heavy is the weight of her cold and patient hand

Still... she... waits...
songs.lyrics_en:
Still she walks the upper landing where the floorboards moan
Still she waits beside the window that I called my own
Still she hums the broken lullaby my mother used to sing
Still she haunts the empty hallway and she will not let it ring

House of my blood, house of my bones
House where the dead refuse to leave me alone
Heavy hangs the air where her shadow used to stand
Heavy is the weight of her cold and patient hand

Never did she frighten me when I was small and weak
Never did she answer though I begged for her to speak
Never will she follow me beyond the rotting door
Never will she rest until the house exists no more

House of my blood, house of my bones
House where the dead refuse to leave me alone
Heavy hangs the air where her shadow used to stand
Heavy is the weight of her cold and patient hand

Still... she... waits...

songs.subject: the quiet after everyone leaves
songs.lyrics:
Door slams — count the seconds till the silence floods in
Cups stacked, half-drunk, lipstick on the rim
You all came and ate my light and laughed and took the air
Now it's 1 a.m. and the quiet's a glare

And I HATE how loud the nothing gets
I HATE the ticking clock, the cigarette
The party was a fever and the quiet is the ache
Every empty chair's a fault line, every silence is a quake

Pick up — put down — there's nothing left to do
The echo of your goodbye is a screw turning through
I wanted you all gone and now I want you all back
The quiet isn't peace, it's the start of the attack

And I HATE how loud the nothing gets
I HATE the ticking clock, the cigarette
The party was a fever and the quiet is the ache
Every empty chair's a fault line, every silence is a quake

songs.subject: a soldier writing home
songs.lyrics:
Dear Mom. The food is fine. Don't worry.
I learned to write that lie so smooth it almost feels like truth.
The desert here is honest, at least — it doesn't pretend to be anything but vast.
I think about the kitchen. The yellow light. The way you'd hum.

Tell Dad I'm keeping my head down like he said.
There's a kid here from Ohio who plays harmonica at night,
badly, and it's the most beautiful thing I've ever heard,
because it means somebody's still alive enough to try.

And it's lonely static on the line, Mom,
a thousand miles of nothing in between.
Lonely static, but I'm pressing my ear to it,
to hear the small blue planet where you've been.

I don't know when I'm coming home. I stopped doing the math.
But I keep your photo folded in the pocket near my heart —
not for protection, I don't believe in that —
just so something soft is closest to the most important part.

Write back. Or don't. Just know the letter found you.
Love — your son. Over and out. ...Over and out.

Dear Mom. The food is fine. Don't worry.
I learned to write that lie so smooth it almost feels like truth.

Tell Dad I'm keeping my head down like he said.

because it means somebody's still alive enough to try.

songs.subject: the bartender who knows too much
songs.lyrics:
She's been polishing that same glass since 1989
She's heard every sorry story and she's heard a lot of mine
She don't judge and she don't gossip, she just pours and lets you talk
But there's a weight behind those eyes from every secret in the dark

She knows too much, she knows too much
The whole town's confession in a single rye and rocks
She knows too much, she knows too much
And she carries all our shadows when she's turning out the locks

The mayor cried here Tuesday, the preacher slipped on Thursday night
She remembers every falling and she never tells it right
'Cause to tell it would be cruel and she's a kinder kind of saint
She just holds the whole town's ugliness and never lets it taint

She knows too much, she knows too much
The whole town's confession in a single rye and rocks
She knows too much, she knows too much
And she carries all our shadows when she's turning out the locks

Who pours a drink for her? ...Who pours a drink for her?

songs.subject: a mango
songs.lyrics:
It's 3 a.m. and I bought a mango and I CANNOT calm down!
Neon on the highway and there's juice all over town!
I peeled it with my teeth in the convenience-store glow!
And the city's spinning faster and I never wanna slow!

Sticky fingers on the steering wheel, sugar in my brain!
Tropical explosion in the middle of the rain!

MANGO! In the city lights!
MANGO! I'm awake for seven nights!
Drippin' gold all over my designer coat!
I am unstoppable, I am a tropical boat!

The salaryman is staring 'cause I'm dancing on the curb!
One fruit and I'm a maniac, I'm absolutely perturbed!
The flavor hit my bloodstream like a lightning-bolt of YES!
I will never sleep again and I could not care less!

MANGO! In the city lights!
MANGO! I'm awake for seven nights!
Drippin' gold all over my designer coat!
I am unstoppable, I am a tropical boat!

...I need another one. RIGHT NOW.

songs.subject: a city that never sleeps but I'm exhausted
songs.lyrics:
The city's screaming... and I'm so tired...

Eight million lights and not a single one for me
The trains keep running circles 'round my own fatigue
Everybody's chasing something, everybody's awake
And I'm running on the fumes of every promise I can't break

But I lift my hands, I lift my hands, I'm not done yet
Turn the exhaustion into fuel, turn the fear into a threat...

'Cause if the city never sleeps then neither will I!
Tired to my bones but I'm reaching for the sky!
Burn the candle at both ends and light it up like dawn!
I am running on empty but I'm STILL running on!

Coffee number seven and the sunrise on the glass
I'm a soldier of the sleepless and I'll make this exhaustion last
The city made me weary but the city made me hard
And I'll dance until I'm dust beneath the neon and the stars

'Cause if the city never sleeps then neither will I!
Tired to my bones but I'm reaching for the sky!
Burn the candle at both ends and light it up like dawn!
I am running on empty but I'm STILL running on!

...I'll sleep when I'm a legend... not tonight... not tonight...

songs.subject: growing apart from a best friend
songs.lyrics:
We had a booth at Marco's and a language all our own
We had a hundred inside jokes and now I laugh alone
You moved across the country and we swore we'd never change
But the calls got short and stranger and the strangeness became strange

Oh, where did you go, my other half, my partner in the crime?
We used to have forever and we ran clean out of time
I keep a chair beside me for the friend you used to be
And I'm singing to an empty seat the way you sang to me

I called you up on Tuesday just to hear you say my name
And halfway through the catching-up it didn't feel the same
You said you had to go, you had a thing, you'd call me back
And I sat there with the dial tone and the whole entire lack

So this is how a friendship ends — not with a fight, a fall
But with a sentence trailing off, a "we should really" —

"— we should really get together soon, you know, we should really just—"

songs.subject: the ghost in my childhood home
songs.lyrics:
Yeah... I'm the staircase... let me tell you what I've seen...

I'm the staircase, thirteen steps, been creakin' since '52
I've held every footfall of the fam that you once knew
But there's one set of footsteps that the living never made
A weight upon my third step where the morning light won't fade
She walks me every midnight, same old housedress, same old hum
I've felt her sixty winters, watched her come and come and come
The boy who lives here now, he thinks the creak is just the wood
But I know the difference, man — I've understood, I've understood

I'm the bones of the house and I remember it all
Every laugh in the hallway, every crack in the wall
And the one who never left, she still walks me at night
I'm the staircase — I hold her — and I keep her in the light

The kid's got headphones on, he never hears her sigh
But I feel her pause at my landing where she used to say goodnight
She straightens up a picture that's been crooked since she passed
A grandmother on patrol in a love that's built to last
I ain't scary, I ain't haunted, I'm just old and I'm just true
I'm a staircase full of memory holdin' someone who held you

I'm the bones of the house and I remember it all
Every laugh in the hallway, every crack in the wall
And the one who never left, she still walks me at night
I'm the staircase — I hold her — and I keep her in the light

...mind the third step, young blood... she likes to sit there... peace...

songs.subject: growing up too fast
songs.lyrics:
They handed me a briefcase when I wanted a balloon
I traded all my recess for a corner office soon
The childhood that I had was just a flicker and a spark
And I grew up way too fast in the dark

I never learned to dawdle, never learned to waste a day
I was always the responsible, the one who'd find the way
But somewhere in the rushing I forgot to leave a mark
And I grew up way too fast in the dark
And I grew up way too fast in the dark

So now I'm buying crayons that I never let myself
And I'm reading all the picture books I left up on the shelf
'Cause it's never really over, you can still go light the spark
Even if you grew up way too fast in the dark
Even if you grew up way too fast in the dark
Even if you grew up way too fast in the dark

...so color outside the lines, kid... it's never too late to start...

songs.subject: K-pop
songs.lyrics:
Yeah! Turn it up! Let's go!

They put me in a box and they told me stay in line
Drew me a horizon and they said it wasn't mine
But I'm breaking through the ceiling, I'm a comet, I'm a flare
Watch me take the impossible and grab it out the air

You can't tell me, you can't tell me who to be
I'm rewriting every rule they wrote for me

I'm unbreakable, untakeable, I'm rising to the top!
Say my name to the sky 'cause I'm never gonna stop!
Light it up, light it up, let the whole world hear me roar!
I'm the one they said could never — watch me walk right through the door!

Sweat and the spotlight, every hour that I gave
I built a crown from all the doubt and everything they crave
Now the stadium is screaming and they know exactly why
The kid they underestimated owns the whole damn sky

I'm unbreakable, untakeable, I'm rising to the top!
Say my name to the sky 'cause I'm never gonna stop!
Light it up, light it up, let the whole world hear me roar!
I'm the one they said could never — watch me walk right through the door!

We don't stop! We don't stop! Oh-oh-oh!

We don't stop! We don't stop! Oh-oh-oh!

songs.subject: running out of coffee on a Monday
songs.lyrics:
I open up the jar and it's a desert in my hand
Empty Monday morning, this was never in the plan
The kettle's getting hot but I've got nothing left to brew
The whole week's at the door and I'm already coming through

But maybe, maybe, it's a sign
To wake up on my own this time

No coffee, no problem, I'll be bright!
Spinning through the gray into the light
My heart's the only thing I need to pour
Monday, here I come, I'm out the door!

The convenience store is glowing like a little paper star
I'll grab a can of something sweet, it isn't very far
The city's still half-sleeping but I'm humming as I go
Caffeine in my smile and not a single one to know

No coffee, no problem, I'll be bright!
Spinning through the gray into the light
My heart's the only thing I need to pour
Monday, here I come, I'm out the door!

Ra-ta-ta, Monday — I forgive you after all

songs.subject: my hometown ten years later
songs.lyrics:
I came back to you, baby, after all these years
Your streets feel like a lover that I almost don't recognize
You changed your hair, your corner store, the color of your eyes
But somewhere underneath the new you, the old you still survives

And I, and I, and I — I'm reaching through the static for your face...

Oh you, my first and only — you, the place that made me
You, the love I left to grow, you, the one who stayed
I press my hand against your skyline like a heartbeat
And the bassline says you missed me, says you missed me anyway

They paved the field where I first fell, now it's condos and a chain
But baby when the streetlight hums I feel the same old flame
You're not the girl I left behind, you're someone new instead
And loving who you've become is the hardest thing I've said

Oh you, my first and only — you, the place that made me
You, the love I left to grow, you, the one who stayed
I press my hand against your skyline like a heartbeat
And the bassline says you missed me, says you missed me anyway

Home... home... home...

songs.subject: dog watching a breakup
songs.lyrics:
I don't know the words but I know the sound
The low and heavy one that pins me to the ground
She's by the door with a box and her coat
And there's a thing gone wrong in the back of his throat

I press my nose against her knee
Why won't anybody look at me?

Don't go, don't go — I can't say it but I feel it
The pack is breaking, breaking, and I cannot reseal it
I'd guard you both, I'd take the storm, I'd stand between the two
But all I've got's a wagging tail and a love too big to use

The door clicks shut and the apartment's wrong
Half the smells I love are packed and gone
He sits on the floor and he holds my head
And we listen to the quiet where her footsteps used to tread

Don't go, don't go — I can't say it but I feel it
The pack is breaking, breaking, and I cannot reseal it
I'd guard you both, I'd take the storm, I'd stand between the two
But all I've got's a wagging tail and a love too big to use

I'll wait by the door. I'll wait by the door. She always comes back. She always comes back. ...Right?

songs.subject: amapiano
songs.lyrics:
Wooo! Let it go tonight...

Been carryin' the week like a stone on my back
Phone full of nothing I wanted to unpack
But the bass just found the hole in my chest
And it's pourin' out the worst and it's pourin' in the best

Hands to the sky, let the log drum decide
Everything I held too tight, I let it slide

Phakama, phakama, rise up off the floor
Dance it till the heavy don't live here no more
Sweat and the bassline washin' me clean
Best kind of empty I ever been

Strangers become family on a crowded floor
Nobody's askin' what you came here for
The piano keep talkin' what my mouth couldn't say
And the night take the weight of the week away

Phakama, phakama, rise up off the floor
Dance it till the heavy don't live here no more
Sweat and the bassline washin' me clean
Best kind of empty I ever been

...clean... clean... lighter than I came...

songs.subject: a clock that runs backwards
songs.lyrics:
There's a clock on my wall and it's spinning in reverse
Every second that it steals it puts another in my purse
Midnight folds to evening folds to afternoon to noon
I am getting younger, baby, and I'm getting there too soon

Don't pick up, don't pick up the neon telephone
It's the future calling, asking when I'm coming home

Backwards, backwards, the hands are running backwards
Take me to the moment just before it all went wrong
Backwards, backwards, unwind me to the start
Press rewind on the neon, play me my old heart

I watched the candle un-burn, watched the smoke go back inside
Watched the goodbye un-happen, watched you turn back to my side
But a clock that runs in reverse never lets you really stay
It just shows you all you'd lose and then it spins it all away

Don't pick up, don't pick up the neon telephone
It's the future calling, asking when I'm coming home

Backwards, backwards, the hands are running backwards
Take me to the moment just before it all went wrong
Backwards, backwards, unwind me to the start
Press rewind on the neon, play me my old heart

...tick... kcit...

Don't pick up, don't pick up the neon telephone
It's the future calling, asking when I'm coming home

Backwards, backwards, the hands are running backwards
Take me to the moment just before it all went wrong
Backwards, backwards, unwind me to the start
Press rewind on the neon, play me my old heart

...tick... kcit...

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper
songs.lyrics:
3 . . . 4 . . . 5 a.m. . . .

I am the keeper of a light that no one sees
A textbook ocean and a lamp that's mine to please
The whole world's asleep and I'm the last one on the shore
Turning, turning, turning, like the keepers did before

And I shine, and I shine, into nothing into night
Nobody sailing but I keep the light
A lighthouse for a coastline of caffeine and dread
Highlighter ocean, page-glow overhead

The coffee's gone cold like a tide that won't come in
My reflection in the window is a ghost beneath my skin
But somewhere out there maybe one lost ship can see
The lonely little beacon that is keeping company with me

And I shine, and I shine, into nothing into night
Nobody sailing but I keep the light
A lighthouse for a coastline of caffeine and dread
Highlighter ocean, page-glow overhead

...sunrise... I made it... the ships were never coming... that's okay...

songs.subject: the last text I never sent
songs.lyrics:
She typed it out at midnight, every word she'd never say
"I miss you" in the darkness, then she watched it fade away
Her thumb above the arrow, but she couldn't press it through
So she set the phone beside her and she let the moment go on through the blue

Sleep now, sleep now, little unsent word
The kindest thing she almost said, the song that's never heard
Rest in the drafts where the brave things lie
Sleep now, sleep now, little goodbye

He'll never know she wrote it on a cold November night
He'll never know she loved him in the soft and unsent light
But somewhere in the silence there's a tenderness that stayed
A message in a bottle that the ocean never made

Sleep now, sleep now, little unsent word
The kindest thing she almost said, the song that's never heard
Rest in the drafts where the brave things lie
Sleep now, sleep now, little goodbye

...and the morning came... and she let it sleep... let it sleep...

songs.subject: my noisy upstairs neighbor
songs.lyrics:
It starts at half past ten with a thunderous little BOOM
A bowling ball? A bison? In that perfectly nice room?
I've never seen his face but oh, I know his every tread
He tap-dances on my ceiling while I'm lying in my bed!

Oh, the man upstairs! The MAN upstairs!
He's got a thousand-pound assortment of invisible chairs!
But I freeze up in the stairwell — oh, the lengths that terror reaches!

Perhaps he is a dancer with a beautiful career!
Perhaps he's just as lonely and he likes to know I'm here!
Perhaps the noise is morse code and he's reaching out to me!

Oh, the man upstairs! The MAN upstairs!
He's got a thousand-pound assortment of invisible chairs!
But I'll knock upon his door someday and finally we'll meet
And discover we're two lonely souls beneath one tired sheet!

Tomorrow I will speak to him! ...Tomorrow. ...Or the day after that.

songs.subject: a wedding I wasn't invited to
songs.lyrics:
Lawd, dem leave mi name off the list, but mi bless dem still!

Mi see the photos online, all the flowers and the lace
And a little sting a travel from mi heart up to mi face
Dem forget mi, or dem skip mi, or mi just nah make the cut
But mi nah carry bitterness, mi keep the heavy door shut

So mi dance, mi dance, in mi own front room!
Throw mi hands to the Lord, chase away the gloom!
Dem can have dem big reception, mi have grace and a groove!
Mi bless the bride and groom and mi got nothing left to prove!

Maybe dem will call me later, maybe dem never will
But mi soul too full of music fi to ever sit and chill
Every snub a chance fi praise, every left-out a release
And the dancefloor of mi kitchen give mi perfect holy peace

So mi dance, mi dance, in mi own front room!
Throw mi hands to the Lord, chase away the gloom!
Dem can have dem big reception, mi have grace and a groove!
Mi bless the bride and groom and mi got nothing left to prove!

...congratulations, you two... mi mean it... mi truly mean it... wine low!

songs.subject: industrial
songs.lyrics:
Cold iron crawls upon my skin,
A thousand cogs all grind within.
I run on oil, on tar, on grim,
A void of light, a husk of tin.

Pound it down, pound it down,
Crush this town, crush this town,
A din so loud it cracks my mind,
I grind, I grind, I grind, I grind.

Robots march in unison,
Building walls that block my sun.
My circuits born to run and run,
For iron is my origin.

Pound it down, pound it down,
Crush this town, crush this town,
No god of sky to call my kin,
Just oil and ash and tin and din.

...always on, always on, until I fall to dust again...

songs.subject: an astronaut who misses gravity
songs.lyrics:
Floating in the silence with the Earth a million miles
Counting all the stars but I'd trade 'em for your smile
Up here nothing falls and nothing holds me anymore
And I miss the simple weight of my feet upon the floor

Oh-oh, I'm so far, oh-oh, from the ground
Oh-oh, take me home, oh-oh, pull me down

'Cause I miss gravity, the way it used to hold me
I miss gravity, all the things it never told me
Now I'm weightless, baby, in a sky too wide to see
Bring me back to Earth, bring me back to gravity

I dream about a stumble, I dream about a fall
The luxury of standing, the gift of getting small
Out here I am infinite and infinite is cold
I'd give the whole horizon just for one hand to hold

'Cause I miss gravity, the way it used to hold me
I miss gravity, all the things it never told me
Now I'm weightless, baby, in a sky too wide to see
Bring me back to Earth, bring me back to gravity

...pull me down... pull me down... I just wanna feel the ground...

songs.subject: selling my first car
songs.constraint: uses one weird word a lot
songs.lyrics:
Chrome chariot, my chrome chariot is gone
A stranger's got the keys to her and now he's driving on
I machined every memory in that chrome and rusted shell
A chrome chariot, a chrome chariot, the only thing I'd sell

Chrome chariot! Chrome chariot! Rolling out of view!
Every dent a diary, every mile I ever knew!
I traded all that metal for a handful of the cold
Chrome chariot, chrome chariot, I never should have sold!

First kiss in the chrome chariot, first cry, first open road
She carried all my chrome-chariot years and every heavy load
Now the lot is empty and the engine's someone else's hum
A chrome chariot, a chrome chariot, and the silence that I'm from

Chrome chariot! Chrome chariot! Rolling out of view!
Every dent a diary, every mile I ever knew!
I traded all that metal for a handful of the cold
Chrome chariot, chrome chariot, I never should have sold!

...chrome chariot... gone... chrome chariot... gone...

songs.subject: sea shanty
songs.lyrics:
Oh the glass is fallin' faster than my courage can hold
And the captain's gone quiet and the mate's gone cold
There's a green in the water that I never want to see
Heave away, boys, but the sea's heavin' me

So pull, pull, pull though my hands are shakin' raw
Pull, pull, pull 'fore we meet the maw
I never signed for weather like the end of all days
Heave away, heave away, through the haze

The compass spins a circle like a thought I can't hold still
And the bell rings out for no one on the swell
I count the men at midnight and I count one less
Heave away, boys, into the mess

So pull, pull, pull though my hands are shakin' raw
Pull, pull, pull 'fore we meet the maw
I never signed for weather like the end of all days
Heave away, heave away, through the haze

And if we make the harbor I will never sail again — heave away!

And if we make the harbor I will never sail again — heave away!

songs.subject: the ocean at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Black water.
Black water and no horizon.
The tide comes in like a thought you can't finish.

Three a.m. and the sand is cold as a coin
The waves arrive, the waves withdraw, the waves arrive
I am very small and the dark is very kind
And nothing wants me here and that is fine, that is fine

But the water's getting louder now, the water's climbing fast
The foam is at my ankles and the moon has slipped behind
And the tide that felt like breathing feels like running, feels like running
And the dark that felt like kindness has a current and a mind

Go go go the water's at my knees the water's at my chest
the horizon's gone the shoreline's gone there's nowhere left to stand
faster faster pull of the deep the cold the cold the cold —
and then —

songs.subject: a mango
songs.lyrics:
Ma-ma-ma-mango... yeah...

Saturday and the lights go low
Tropical gold on the radio
One bite, baby, and I lose my mind
Sweetest little thing that I could find

Turn it up, turn it up, feel the summer in my veins
Drip-drip-dripping like the sticky golden rain

Mango, mango, on the dancefloor glow
Juicy like the future twenty-zero-zero
Hands up, hands up, taste the neon sun
Mango on my lips and the night's just begun

Glitter on my phone with the antenna up
Frozen daiquiri in a plastic cup
You're the flavor, baby, you're the vibe
Y2K and we're alive, alive

Mango, mango, on the dancefloor glow
Juicy like the future twenty-zero-zero
Hands up, hands up, taste the neon sun
Mango on my lips and the night's just begun

Mango... forever... 1-9-9-9... never... ends...

songs.subject: black metal
songs.lyrics:
What a lovely morning in the frostbitten pines!
The sun on the snow and the world feels fine!
A little fox is playing where the cold stream bends
And I think today might be the best of all my friends!

BUT THERE IS NOTHING. THERE WAS NEVER ANYTHING.
THE LIGHT IS A LIE THE DYING SUN IS SCREAMING.
EVERY ONE I LOVED IS ASH BENEATH THE SNOW.
AND I AM ALONE. I AM ALONE. I AM ALONE.

Oh the icicles are sparkling like a chandelier!
I'll make a cup of something warm and hold it near!
The birds are coming back and they're singing in the cold!
What a beautiful day to be alive and growing old!

BUT THERE IS NOTHING. THERE WAS NEVER ANYTHING.
THE LIGHT IS A LIE THE DYING SUN IS SCREAMING.
EVERY ONE I LOVED IS ASH BENEATH THE SNOW.
AND I AM ALONE. I AM ALONE. I AM ALONE.

...lovely... morning...

songs.subject: an arcade closing for good
songs.lyrics:
They're closing down the palace where I learned to be alive
The quarters and the cabinets, the dream of staying five
The carpet knew my sneakers, the high score knew my name
And tomorrow it's a parking lot — and nothing stays the same

So let the lights go up one final time!
Let the pinball ring its last and perfect chime!
I gave you all my allowance and my afternoons and youth
Goodbye, my glowing kingdom — you were the realest truth!

The owner's name was Sal and he'd slip me extra plays
He's selling off the skee-ball for a fraction of its days
I'll take a single token and I'll keep it like a ring
A wedding to a place that taught a lonely kid to sing

So let the lights go up one final time!
Let the pinball ring its last and perfect chime!
I gave you all my allowance and my afternoons and youth
Goodbye, my glowing kingdom — you were the realest truth!

Game over. Insert coin. ...There are no more coins to insert.

songs.subject: the bartender who knows too much
songs.lyrics:
Mi a the bottle on the shelf, yeah, mi see everyt'ing...

Mi sit up on the top shelf, watchin' every night
Mi see the bartender hold the whole town tight
Dem come with dem troubles and dem pour out dem soul
And she listen, and she pour, and she never let it go

She know too much, oh, she know too much
The secrets of the island in a gentle Dutch
But mi the only one who see how she carry the load
A bottle and a bartender on a lonely road

Mi watch her wipe a tear when the last man gone
Mi watch her lock the door as she hummin' a song
All the weight she tek, but who tek weh fi her?
Just mi, the silent witness, and the night so pure

She know too much, oh, she know too much
The secrets of the island in a gentle Dutch
But mi the only one who see how she carry the load
A bottle and a bartender on a lonely road

Pour one for yuhself tonight, sister... mi nah tell... mi nah tell...

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper
songs.lyrics:
I . . . q u i t . . . today . . .

For fifteen years I kept a light for ships that didn't care
Spreadsheet beacon, cubicle horizon, recycled air
But this morning I climbed down the tower and I walked out the door
And the ocean's still there, baby, like it always was before

And I'm free, I'm free, on a victory lap through the haze
No more keeping the light for somebody else's days
I was the lighthouse keeper and the lighthouse was a lie
Now I'm sailing my own ship beneath a pink and endless sky

Let somebody else turn the lamp and watch the empty sea
Let the corporate fog roll in on someone who isn't me
I'm a wave now, I'm a sunset, I'm a slowed-down happy song
The keeper finally left the light and nothing's going wrong

And I'm free, I'm free, on a victory lap through the haze
No more keeping the light for somebody else's days
I was the lighthouse keeper and the lighthouse was a lie
Now I'm sailing my own ship beneath a pink and endless sky

...the light's still spinning... without me... and that's beautiful...

songs.subject: becoming my parents
songs.lyrics:
In the spring of my life I swore I'd be nothing like them
Different car, different politics, a wholly different hem
But the saxophone is gentle and the truth is gentle too
The first green leaf of forty looks a lot like déjà vu

By the summer I'm correcting all my kids the way he did
Same exact unfunny joke, same lifting of the lid
I caught my mother's worry living right behind my eyes
And the warm and lazy season made me honest, made me wise

In the autumn I stop fighting it, I let the color turn
I wear my father's patience that I always failed to learn
I hum my mother's humming while I'm washing up the plates
And the falling leaves forgive me for the things I used to hate

And in winter I am grateful for the people that they were
For the slow and quiet becoming, for the soft familiar blur
I am them and I am me and the saxophone agrees
As the snow comes down like mercy on the family of trees

...thanks, Mom... thanks, Dad... I get it now... I finally get it now...

songs.subject: the ghost in my childhood home
songs.lyrics:
I come back to the homestead with the deed inside my hand
The bank says that I own it but I think the dead disagree
There's a rocking chair that rocks itself out on the front veranda
And a cold spot in the doorway that's been waiting there for me

It's quiet weather, quiet weather, in this house of mine
Where the living and the leaving share a thin and crooked line
She don't mean me any harm, she just don't know that she's gone
Quiet weather, quiet weather, and the ghost light's always on

I could call a preacher, I could salt the windowsill
But she hummed me all my lullabies, and Lord, I love her still

It's quiet weather, quiet weather, in this house of mine
Where the living and the leaving share a thin and crooked line
She don't mean me any harm, she just don't know that she's gone
Quiet weather, quiet weather, and the ghost light's always on

So I'll stay a while and keep her company... ain't no rush... ain't no rush at all...
songs.lyrics_en:
I come back to the homestead with the deed inside my hand
The bank says that I own it but I think the dead disagree
There's a rocking chair that rocks itself out on the front veranda
And a cold spot in the doorway that's been waiting there for me

It's quiet weather, quiet weather, in this house of mine
Where the living and the leaving share a thin and crooked line
She don't mean me any harm, she just don't know that she's gone
Quiet weather, quiet weather, and the ghost light's always on

I could call a preacher, I could salt the windowsill
But she hummed me all my lullabies, and Lord, I love her still

It's quiet weather, quiet weather, in this house of mine
Where the living and the leaving share a thin and crooked line
She don't mean me any harm, she just don't know that she's gone
Quiet weather, quiet weather, and the ghost light's always on

So I'll stay a while and keep her company... ain't no rush... ain't no rush at all...

songs.subject: a mango
songs.lyrics:
A small green knot upon the branch, a promise in the haze
You were nothing but a hard little stone in the soft and rainy days
And I watched you from the window through a blur of guitar sound
The slowest kind of waiting while the spring came up around

And you swelled into a gold thing in the heavy August heat
A sun gone soft and fragrant, almost too much to be sweet
I held you in the static of the longest, brightest days
A mango like a heartbeat in the warm and washed-out haze

Then you fell into my hand when the light began to slant
And I ate you on the porch with all the sorrow that I can't
The juice ran like an ending down my wrist into the cool
And the guitars all turned to amber and the summer was a fool

Now there's only a smooth pit on the windowsill of frost
A small brown souvenir of all the sweetness that I lost
And I hum into the reverb where the mango used to be
A whole year in a fruit, my love, a whole year inside of me

...next spring... another one... next spring...

songs.subject: a haunted GPS that gives bad directions
songs.lyrics:
She speaks to me in honey, my little dashboard ghost
"Turn left in a quarter mile," the voice I love the most
But the left goes to a graveyard and the road has been erased
And she purrs, "recalculating," with a smile upon her face

Oh, my haunted GPS, where you taking me tonight?
Your voice is silk and trouble and your map is never right
But I follow every whisper down the dark and winding way
Recalculating, recalculating, leading me astray

Now she's saying turn around, now she's saying turn again
The Rhodes is getting nervous and the sax is losing zen
"In four hundred feet — no — now — exit — no — go straight —"
And the streetlights start to blur and I can feel the rising weight

Make a U-turn make a U-turn she's screaming make a U-turn now
Off the bridge off the map off the edge and she won't tell me how
Recalculating recalculating recalculating recalcu—
The needle's in the red and the road has come unglued and—

"...You have arrived." ...but where?

songs.subject: a diner at 2am
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent buzz at two a.m., the loneliest light on Earth
The coffee's burnt, the booths are cracked, this place forgot its worth
The waitress moves like clockwork through the wreckage of the night
And every broken soul in here is clinging to the light

And the grill is screaming, and the neon's dying
And every one of us in here is quietly crying—

TWO A.M.! THE CHURCH OF THE FORGOTTEN!
SERVE ME SOMETHING WARM BEFORE I ROT!
EVERY GHOST IN THIS DINER IS A PRAYER GONE ROTTEN!
GIVE ME COFFEE, GIVE ME MORNING, GIVE ME ALL YOU'VE GOT!

The trucker hasn't slept in days, the runaway's sixteen
The cook's a former soldier and he's quiet and he's mean
But here beneath the heat lamps we're a congregation, strange
United by the hour and the hope of nothing changing

TWO A.M.! THE CHURCH OF THE FORGOTTEN!
SERVE ME SOMETHING WARM BEFORE I ROT!
EVERY GHOST IN THIS DINER IS A PRAYER GONE ROTTEN!
GIVE ME COFFEE, GIVE ME MORNING, GIVE ME ALL YOU'VE GOT!

...check, please... and amen...

songs.subject: a vending machine that only takes exact change
songs.lyrics:
Underneath the overpass it hums a soft pink light
Faithful little robot keeping company with the night
It won't take my crumpled bills, it only wants it true
Exact change, exact heart, it asks the same of you

So I count my coins beneath the neon glow
Nothing more and nothing less, that's all it needs to know
The city's wide and careless but this corner's keeping time
A perfect little kindness for a hundred yen and a dime

Salaryman at midnight gets his can of lukewarm tea
The machine just glows and gives it, asks no apology
There's a peace in something honest that just wants the proper sum
No interest and no judgment, only warm light when you come

So I count my coins beneath the neon glow
Nothing more and nothing less, that's all it needs to know
The city's wide and careless but this corner's keeping time
A perfect little kindness for a hundred yen and a dime

Clink, clink, thank you — drive safe, says the light

songs.subject: the dog waiting by the door
songs.lyrics:
...w a i t i n g . . . w a i t i n g . . .

You don't know the word for "later"
Every leaving is forever in your eyes
So you fold down by the doorway
Where my shadow disappeared into the light

And the clock goes round, and the clock goes round
and you don't count the hours, you just wait...

By the door, by the door
You're a love that asks for nothing but the sound of my return
By the door, by the door
And I swear I'll always come back, I'll always come back, little one

Vaporwave sunset through the window
A mall that closed in 1999
But you'd wait in that pink light forever
Faithful little ghost of mine

By the door, by the door
You're a love that asks for nothing but the sound of my return
By the door, by the door
And I swear I'll always come back, I'll always come back, little one

...good boy... I'm... home...

By the door, by the door
And I swear I'll always come back, I'll always come back, little one

By the door, by the door

By the door, by the door

...good boy... I'm... home...

songs.subject: a snow day when you're an adult
songs.lyrics:
The email came at seven: the office is closed!
And the whole grey weight of Tuesday simply rose
I am forty-one and giddy as a child
For the city's wrapped in silence soft and wild

Oh ring the bells, the world has stopped for snow!
No meetings and no traffic, nowhere I must go!
Let the kettle sing, let the blanket be my throne —
A holiday from nothing that the heavens gave me on my own!

I will not check my inbox, I will not feel the guilt
For the snow has built a fortress and inside it I am held
I'll watch it from the window with a cocoa and a grin
And be ten years old for one whole day again

Oh ring the bells, the world has stopped for snow!
No meetings and no traffic, nowhere I must go!
Let the kettle sing, let the blanket be my throne —
A holiday from nothing that the heavens gave me on my own!

Let it fall, let it fall — and let the morning wait for once

songs.subject: a diner at 2am
songs.lyrics:
Two a.m. and the neon's buzzing pink
Coffee going cold while I sit here and I think
You're across the booth with your eyeliner run
Two lonely satellites and the night's not done

And the jukebox plays a song we used to know
The waitress wipes the counter, got nowhere to go

At the diner, at the diner at 2 a.m.
Where the broken-hearted gather and we start again
Under fluorescent angels and a sky of chrome
The all-night light is on, you're never quite alone

You light a cigarette that you swore you'd quit
And the smoke curls up like the ghost of it
We don't say forever, we don't say goodbye
Just the hum of the freezer and the orange-soda sky

At the diner, at the diner at 2 a.m.
Where the broken-hearted gather and we start again
Under fluorescent angels and a sky of chrome
The all-night light is on, you're never quite alone

...two a.m.... and the light stays on...

songs.subject: a group project gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Heave ho! On the saltwater highway we go!
Four signed the manifest but only one would row!
Heave ho! And the deadline's a gale!
Pullin' the whole damn ship by a single torn sail!

Oh Jakey said he'd plot the course and then he disappeared
And Danny brought a barrel that was nothin' but stale beer
Miss Caroline she bailed the boat from dusk until the dawn
And I manned the wheel and the cannon and the song

Heave ho! On the saltwater highway we go!
Four signed the manifest but only one would row!
Heave ho! And the deadline's a gale!
Pullin' the whole damn ship by a single torn sail!

We made the port at sunrise with our colors flyin' high
And the captain gave us all an A — I watched 'em take the prize
But me and quiet Caroline, we know who pulled the load
And we'll sail again together down a kinder, fairer road

Heave ho! On the saltwater highway we go!
Four signed the manifest but only two would row!
Heave ho! And we made it through the gale!
Just me and Caroline and a single torn sail!

songs.subject: selling my first car
songs.lyrics:
There she is in the driveway with a FOR SALE sign
Two hundred thousand miles and she was mine, all mine
The check-engine light's been on since the day I drove her home
But oh, the places that we went, the roads that we have known!

So goodbye, old faithful! Hello, brand-new day!
I'm cashing in the memories and driving them away!
You taught me how to merge and how to cry and how to grow
Take a bow, my rusty chariot — it's time to let you go!

A kid is gonna buy her with a fistful of his savings
And his eyes are doing all the things that mine were doing, craving
The freedom of a first car, the whole horizon wide
So I hand him the keys like I'm passing him the tide!

So goodbye, old faithful! Hello, brand-new day!
I'm cashing in the memories and driving them away!
You taught me how to merge and how to cry and how to grow
Take a bow, my rusty chariot — it's time to let you go!

Drive on, kid! She's all yours now! DRIVE OOOON!

songs.subject: the friend who never texts back
songs.lyrics:
You leave me on read every single day
You got a thousand little things to say
But never to me, never my way
I'm the gray little bubble you let decay
The friend that you forgot to repay
And still here I am, and still I stay

Oh, won't you text me back, won't you throw me a line?
I've been staring at this phone like it's a holy sign
We were thick as thieves once, now you're a ghost on the wire
But I'd still drop everything if you ever required

You'll surface in a month like nothing's wrong
"Hey stranger, it's been long"
And I'll forgive you for a song
'Cause loving you's where I belong
Even when the silence is strong
And the wait for a word is so long

Oh, won't you text me back, won't you throw me a line?
I've been staring at this phone like it's a holy sign
We were thick as thieves once, now you're a ghost on the wire
But I'd still drop everything if you ever required

Three dots... and then nothing... story of my life...

songs.subject: a robot learning to taste rain
songs.lyrics:
They built me in a factory outside of Amarillo
With a tin-can heart and a head full of nothing soft
I hauled the freight for twenty years, I never asked a question
Till a desert rain came down and washed the dust right off

And oh, the rain, the rain, it tastes like something holy
A wet and silver wonder on my cold and rusted tongue
I'm a outlaw and a machine and I'm falling for the weather
And the pedal steel is crying for a feeling I've just begun

Now I park out by the canyon when the thunderheads come rolling
And I tilt my sensors skyward like a cowboy to the storm
The other robots call me broken, say I've got a glitch in me
But a glitch that feels the rain is the only way I'm warm

And oh, the rain, the rain, it tastes like something holy
A wet and silver wonder on my cold and rusted tongue
I'm a outlaw and a machine and I'm falling for the weather
And the pedal steel is crying for a feeling I've just begun

...let it pour... let it pour... I was built for this... I just didn't know...

songs.subject: the day the factory closed
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew at noon on a Tuesday in the fall
And it wasn't for the lunch break, it was final, that was all
Forty years of fathers had walked through that iron gate
And the men just stood there silent in the parking lot of fate

My dad came home at one o'clock with his lunch pail still half full
Set it on the kitchen table and he didn't say a word at all
He'd never been home early in the whole of my young life
And the strangeness of him standing there cut sharper than a knife

The town began to empty like a tub without a stop
First the diner, then the hardware, then the little record shop
We held on for a decade out of stubbornness and pride
But the heart had left the body on the day the factory died

Now I drive past the building and the windows are all gone
And the weeds have claimed the loading docks the trucks once rolled upon
I roll my window down sometimes and I swear I hear it still—
The whistle and the engines and the men up on the hill

My father passed last winter and I cleaned out his garage
And I found his factory badge inside a coffee can of change
I keep it on my mirror now, a little square of blue
For a town, and for a man, and for a world we never knew we'd lose

songs.subject: the quiet after everyone leaves
songs.lyrics:
After the lights come up... after they all go home...

The glasses on the counter and the bass still in my chest
Everybody's gone now and the silence is a guest
I used to dread this moment, used to fill it up with noise
But there's something in the after that the party always destroys

And the quiet, and the quiet, it comes in like a tide
Everything I'm running from and everything I hide...

But I'm learning how to love it, the room when it's my own
The 4 a.m. cathedral where I finally feel at home
Let the quiet, let the quiet, wash the evening off of me
The best part of the party is the person I get to be

I dance alone now, slowly, to a track that no one hears
And the empty floor's a comfort that took me thirty years
The friends were all a blessing but the after is a gift
The gentle, holy nothing where my tired spirit lifts

'Cause I'm learning how to love it, the room when it's my own
The 4 a.m. cathedral where I finally feel at home
Let the quiet, let the quiet, wash the evening off of me
The best part of the party is the person I get to be

...just me... and the morning... and it's enough... it's enough...

songs.subject: the last text I never sent
songs.lyrics:
Type. Delete. Type. Delete.

SEND IT, SEND IT, but my thumb won't move!
DRAFT IN THE DARK with nothing left to prove!
Three little dots and a heart full of dread —
SEND IT, SEND IT — the last text I never said!

I built the perfect message at a quarter after two
Every word a piston, every line a screw
I machined it and I polished it and held it to the light
Then I watched the cursor blinking like a siren in the night

SEND IT, SEND IT, but my thumb won't move!
DRAFT IN THE DARK with nothing left to prove!
Three little dots and a heart full of dread —
SEND IT, SEND IT — the last text I never said!

It's still there in the morning, it's still there when I'm old
A factory of feeling that I left out in the cold
The machine of my devotion never shipped a single part
A warehouse full of "I miss you" locked inside my iron heart

SEND IT, SEND IT, but my thumb won't move!
DRAFT IN THE DARK with nothing left to prove!
Three little dots and a heart full of dread —
SEND IT, SEND IT — the last text I never said!

...message... discarded... discarded... discarded...
songs.lyrics_en:
Type. Delete. Type. Delete.

SEND IT, SEND IT, but my thumb won't move!
DRAFT IN THE DARK with nothing left to prove!
Three little dots and a heart full of dread —
SEND IT, SEND IT — the last text I never said!

I built the perfect message at a quarter after two
Every word a piston, every line a screw
I machined it and I polished it and held it to the light
Then I watched the cursor blinking like a siren in the night

SEND IT, SEND IT, but my thumb won't move!
DRAFT IN THE DARK with nothing left to prove!
Three little dots and a heart full of dread —
SEND IT, SEND IT — the last text I never said!

It's still there in the morning, it's still there when I'm old
A factory of feeling that I left out in the cold
The machine of my devotion never shipped a single part
A warehouse full of "I miss you" locked inside my iron heart

SEND IT, SEND IT, but my thumb won't move!
DRAFT IN THE DARK with nothing left to prove!
Three little dots and a heart full of dread —
SEND IT, SEND IT — the last text I never said!

...message... discarded... discarded... discarded...

songs.subject: leaving a party early
songs.lyrics:
I've got my jacket on at 10:04
Counting all the steps between me and the door
Everybody's laughing in a language I forgot
And I'm rehearsing my goodbye in a corner that I'm not

And nobody will notice when I'm gone
That's the worst and best part all along

So I'll slip into the cold like I was never even there
A ghost in someone's hallway, a coat upon a chair
I always leave the party before the party leaves me
It's easier to vanish than to wonder if they see

The bass is in the basement and the smoke is in my throat
I texted you "I'm tired" from the staircase where I wrote
A thousand sorry reasons that I'll never have to give
'Cause leaving is the only way I've ever known to live

So I'll slip into the cold like I was never even there
A ghost in someone's hallway, a coat upon a chair
I always leave the party before the party leaves me
It's easier to vanish than to wonder if they see

...the walk home is the only part that ever felt like mine...
songs.lyrics_en:
I've got my jacket on at 10:04
Counting all the steps between me and the door
Everybody's laughing in a language I forgot
And I'm rehearsing my goodbye in a corner that I'm not

And nobody will notice when I'm gone
That's the worst and best part all along

So I'll slip into the cold like I was never even there
A ghost in someone's hallway, a coat upon a chair
I always leave the party before the party leaves me
It's easier to vanish than to wonder if they see

The bass is in the basement and the smoke is in my throat
I texted you "I'm tired" from the staircase where I wrote
A thousand sorry reasons that I'll never have to give
'Cause leaving is the only way I've ever known to live

So I'll slip into the cold like I was never even there
A ghost in someone's hallway, a coat upon a chair
I always leave the party before the party leaves me
It's easier to vanish than to wonder if they see

...the walk home is the only part that ever felt like mine...

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Thumb hovers over the call, screen lit up in the dark
Seven years of dead air, still I know it by heart
Last thing I told you was a slammed door and a curse
Grew up out here in the cold, learned to put pride first
Got the chain, got the car, got a name they say loud
But I traded your kitchen for a room full of crowd
It's three rings and I'm shaking like I'm back at sixteen
Every dollar that I stacked couldn't buy back the scene

Mama pick up, mama pick up the phone
I made it out the bottom but I'm out here alone
I ain't calling for nothing, I ain't calling for loans
Just wanna hear you say my name like I'm finally home

You answer soft, "Baby?" and my whole chest cave in
All the verses I rehearsed, can't remember where to begin
I heard you got sick, heard it secondhand and cried
Missed the years of your laugh just to feed my own pride

I'm sorry for the silence, I'm sorry for the wall
I was scared you wouldn't answer so I never let it call
Now your voice in my ear and I'm a kid on the floor
Swear I'm coming back home, I won't run no more

Mama pick up, mama pick up the phone
I made it out the bottom but I'm out here alone
I ain't calling for nothing, I ain't calling for loans
Just wanna hear you say my name like I'm finally home

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Mile marker fading in the rearview neon glow
Dashboard humming blue, eighteen wheels and nowhere slow
The interstate's a ribbon spilling out beneath the stars
I dial your number from a thousand miles of dark

It's three a.m. and the line is ringing low
Tell me you're still dreaming where the porch light used to go
I'm a ghost in the high beams, I'm a voice across the wire
Keep the bed warm, baby, I'm still chasing down the fire

The radio's all static and the coffee's gone to ice
Your sleepy hello hits me like a second sunrise
I count the white lines passing just to feel like I move
But the only road that matters is the one back to you

The night don't ask me questions, the engine never lies
Just the hum and the heartbeat and the cities passing by
If the dawn comes up purple over fields I'll never own
I'll be running toward the one place that I call my own

It's three a.m. and the line is ringing low
Tell me you're still dreaming where the porch light used to go
I'm a ghost in the high beams, I'm a voice across the wire
Keep the bed warm, baby, I'm still chasing down the fire

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
En medio del concreto, una sombra todavía
un árbol solitario donde el pueblo se reía
los abuelos lo plantaron cuando el mundo era de tierra
ahora canta solo entre el cemento y la guerra

Árbol, viejo árbol, no te dejes caer
guarda los recuerdos que ya no podemos ver
con trompetas te canto, con el alma y el dolor
eres el último verde en un pueblo sin color

Pavimento donde había manzanas y canción
los niños ya no trepan, se llevaron el balcón
pero tus ramas guardan cada nombre y cada boda
cada beso bajo hojas que el progreso ya no nombra

Que toquen los violines por la sombra que se va
por las raíces hondas que el asfalto no podrá
arrancar de la memoria, de la tierra, del amor
canta, viejo árbol, canta tu último verdor

Árbol, viejo árbol, no te dejes caer
guarda los recuerdos que ya no podemos ver
con trompetas te canto, con el alma y el dolor
eres el último verde en un pueblo sin color

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Thumb hovering over a number I memorized as a kid
Ten years of silence stacked like rent that I never did
Pay, I ran from the porch light, ran from the rules
Now I'm grown and I'm calling, feeling dumb like a fool
Ring once, ring twice, heartbeat in my throat
Rehearsed a apology, forgot every note
She picked up soft, said "baby" like no time had passed
And the wall that I built up, it cracked too fast

Mama I'm sorry, it took me too long
I was too proud to call when everything went wrong
Years in the silence, but you still know my voice
Mama I'm sorry, this time it's my choice

Told her 'bout the city, the cold and the grind
Left out half the trouble, kept the worst behind
She said "I prayed every night that you'd come back home"
Funny how a mother knows you're never really gone
My hands stopped shaking when she laughed at my joke
Ten years melted down to the words that we spoke

Next flight, I'm booking it, no more excuses made
Gonna sit at your table, watch the old debts get paid
In forgiveness, in casserole, in your tired smile
Mama hold the line, I'll be home in a while

Mama I'm sorry, it took me too long
I was too proud to call when everything went wrong
Years in the silence, but you still know my voice
Mama I'm sorry, this time it's my choice

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Mile marker glowing in a wash of neon rain
Dashboard hums a frequency, a low electric pain
Three a.m. and Nebraska is a river of white lines
I dial the only number that still feels like mine
The diesel keeps a heartbeat, the heater keeps me warm
A payphone in my pocket cutting through the storm

Are you sleeping, are you dreaming, do you hear me on the wire
Two thousand miles of darkness and a dashboard full of fire
I'll be home by Sunday, I'll be home by the light
Just answer, just answer, keep me steady through the night

She answers half asleep with the radio still on
Says the kids drew me a picture of the road that I'm gone
I tell her 'bout the mountains, the cold and the chrome
She tells me 'bout the little things that make a house a home
The synth of distant traffic, the hum beneath the floor
I'm carrying the country just to get back to your door

The horizon's getting purple where the morning's gonna break
I'll trade these endless highways for a hand that I can take
Keep the porch light burning like a beacon in the dark
Every mile's a promise, every promise leaves a mark

Are you sleeping, are you dreaming, do you hear me on the wire
Two thousand miles of darkness and a dashboard full of fire
I'll be home by Sunday, I'll be home by the light
Just answer, just answer, keep me steady through the night

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Donde había un bosque ahora hay concreto gris
Un solo árbol queda, terco y feliz
Las trompetas lo saludan cada amanecer
Raíces que rompieron la acera al crecer
Los niños ya no saben el verde que se fue
Pero el viejo árbol guarda la memoria de la fe

Árbol solitario, corazón del pueblo gris
Canta con nosotros, no te dejes morir
De tus ramas cuelga toda nuestra juventud
Último guardián, danos sombra y danos luz

El alcalde firmó para arrancar su raíz
Pero el pueblo entero llegó a decir que sí
A quedarse, a abrazarlo, a cantarle una canción
Un árbol no se vende, es el alma, es el corazón
Las guitarras lloraron, los violines también
Y el viento entre sus hojas respondió un amén

No hay torre ni edificio que valga tu verdor
Eres el último testigo de un mundo mejor
Mientras tengas una hoja seguiremos de pie
Mariachi y raíz, una sola fe

Árbol solitario, corazón del pueblo gris
Canta con nosotros, no te dejes morir
De tus ramas cuelga toda nuestra juventud
Último guardián, danos sombra y danos luz

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
You had sand in your hair and a borrowed guitar
We didn't have plans, we didn't get far
Just a tent and a cooler and a tide coming in
And the kind of a summer that you know's gonna end
We burned through the daylight like it owed us a debt
You laughed at my sunburn, said you'd never forget

For a season, just a season, we were salt and we were sun
We knew it wouldn't last but God we let it run
Now September's at the window and the boardwalk's gone gray
But I keep the whole summer in a postcard you didn't stay

We drove with the windows down, no map and no fear
You said "this is temporary" right into my ear
The bonfire, the cold beer, the stars showing off
Your name on the back of a beat-up old wharf
We knew it had a deadline, we knew it had a wall
Maybe that's the reason we remembered it all

I'm not asking forever, I'm not asking you stay
Just let me keep the version of you from that day
With the waves at our ankles and the whole sky on fire
A fling is a flame that we never let expire

For a season, just a season, we were salt and we were sun
We knew it wouldn't last but God we let it run
Now September's at the window and the boardwalk's gone gray
But I keep the whole summer in a postcard you didn't stay

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Green light, I could've turned but I went straight instead
Down the street where your window used to glow overhead
It's somebody else's curtain, somebody else's car
Funny how a building holds you exactly where you are
Radio playing something we used to call ours
I'm just a tourist now of these familiar towers

Driving past your old apartment, two a.m. and I'm a ghost
Number four on the second floor is the one I miss the most
I'm not stopping, I'm not knocking, I just slow down and I stare
Driving past your old apartment like you might still be there

The deli's still open, the streetlight still hums
I can map every memory in the way the corner comes
We carried that couch up four flights in the heat
Now I'm rolling on by at the end of the street
I told myself I wouldn't, then I took the long way home
Some roads only lead you back to being alone

Maybe in a year I'll take a different route
Maybe I'll forget which window I'm about
But tonight I let the engine and the dark do their part
Driving past your old apartment, parking lot of my heart

Driving past your old apartment, two a.m. and I'm a ghost
Number four on the second floor is the one I miss the most
I'm not stopping, I'm not knocking, I just slow down and I stare
Driving past your old apartment like you might still be there

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the diner, clock out, clock in at the store
My feet know the timing of a life that's keeping score
I count it on the bus ride, I count it in my sleep
The hole in the budget that I never seem to keep
From under, oh from under, every dollar's got a name
And the rent and the light bill keep on playing the same game

Two jobs, two hands, and I'm still falling behind
Working like a Sunday but the bills don't mind
Lord I'm tired, Lord I'm trying, can you see how hard I run
Two jobs, two hands, and the work is never done

My mama used to tell me that the honest stay afloat
But honest's wearing thin like the soles beneath my coat
I smile for the customers, I swallow what I feel
Then pray over a tip jar like it's part of a meal
The ceiling's getting closer and the floor won't hold my weight
But I show up in the morning 'cause I can't afford to be late

There's gotta be a morning where the math finally clears
Where the hustle buys a Sunday and it dries up all these tears
Till then I keep on moving, keep my head above the tide
With two jobs and a tired heart and a stubborn kind of pride

Two jobs, two hands, and I'm still falling behind
Working like a Sunday but the bills don't mind
Lord I'm tired, Lord I'm trying, can you see how hard I run
Two jobs, two hands, and the work is never done

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We drove till the towns ran out and the dark turned blue
Killed the engine on a frozen lake, just me and you
Then the sky started bleeding green like a tear in the seam
Like the whole of the universe was leaking a dream
We didn't say a word, we just watched it unfold
Two small breathing shadows in the beautiful cold

Oh the northern lights, oh the ribbons in the night
We came a thousand miles just to feel that small and right
Let it wash over the both of us, electric and slow
Under the northern lights, there's nowhere else to go

You said maybe it's the angels, maybe it's the sun
Maybe it's just particles, it doesn't matter which one
It curtained and it shimmered, it folded like a flame
And I swear that for a minute nothing felt the same
The ice was singing under us, a low and ancient hum
And I knew I'd chase this feeling for the years left to come

We'll forget the names of cities, we'll forget the day
But not the green and violet that took our breath away
Some things are too big to keep, too wide to hold
So we just stood and let it burn through the cold

Oh the northern lights, oh the ribbons in the night
We came a thousand miles just to feel that small and right
Let it wash over the both of us, electric and slow
Under the northern lights, there's nowhere else to go

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Entre piedra y cemento, un árbol resistió
La palma de mis manos contra el tronco que quedó
Clavado en esta tierra que olvidó respirar
Última raíz que nadie pudo arrancar
Golpea mi pecho como un cante jondo y fiel
La madera vieja sabe a sangre y a miel

Áy, árbol, áy, árbol, el último de pie
Te canto con la garganta de todo lo que fue
Que tiemblen las palmas, que llore la guitarra
Mientras tú resistas, la ciudad no me amarra

Le arrancaron al pueblo cada flor y cada voz
Pusieron asfalto donde antes hubo dios
Pero tú levantaste tus ramas como un grito
Un quejío de raíces contra todo lo maldito
Bailo en tu sombra el compás de la verdad
Un taconeo seco contra la soledad

No te cortarán mientras me quede aliento
Mi cante es tu corteza, mi llanto es tu sustento
Si caes, caigo, si vives, yo respiro
Último árbol verde, te guardo en cada giro

Áy, árbol, áy, árbol, el último de pie
Te canto con la garganta de todo lo que fue
Que tiemblen las palmas, que llore la guitarra
Mientras tú resistas, la ciudad no me amarra

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Grandmother on the shoreline with her feet in the foam
Said the ocean's been her teacher, said the ocean's her home
She pointed at the water, said "now child, watch it breathe
It takes and then it gives back, that's the gospel of the sea"
Her hands like weathered driftwood, her voice low and worn
Showing me the rhythm I was carrying since born

The tide, the tide, it comes and it goes
Everything you cling to, the water lets it loose
The elder taught the ocean, or the ocean taught her how
Either way I'm learning, learning how to bow

She said "don't fight the pulling, don't grieve when it recedes
The sea returns the mornings, the sea returns your needs"
We sat until the high tide came and kissed her tired knees
And she laughed like a young girl in the salt and the breeze
Every wave a lesson, every lull a prayer
A hundred years of patience hanging in the air

Now she's gone like the low tide, slipped beyond the bend
But I hear her in the water saying every end's a friend
So I walk down to the shoreline when I lose my way
And let the elder and the ocean teach me how to stay

The tide, the tide, it comes and it goes
Everything you cling to, the water lets it loose
The elder taught the ocean, or the ocean taught her how
Either way I'm learning, learning how to bow

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I drove out to the boneyard with a bottle and a chair
Sat down by your headstone in the cold November air
Got a lot of things to tell you that I never said out loud
Now you're six feet under a Mississippi shroud
The crows are keeping watch and the wind is keeping time
I came to lay the hatchet down on this grave of yours and mine

I forgive you, I forgive you, though you can't hear me now
It's too late for an answer but I'm putting down the plow
I carried all your sins like stones tied to my chest
I forgive you, you old devil, so we both can finally rest

You were never gentle, you were thunder, you were rye
You broke me like a fence post and you never told me why
I hated you for decades, kept the wound a living thing
But hate's a heavy harvest and it's me that's gathering
So I'm leaving it here with you in the clay and the frost
Forgiveness ain't for you no more, it's for the years I lost

The preacher said it's holy, the bottle says it's late
Either way I'm done with carrying the weight of all this hate
The moon's coming up over the iron and the stone
And I finally feel lighter walking back to the road alone

I forgive you, I forgive you, though you can't hear me now
It's too late for an answer but I'm putting down the plow
I carried all your sins like stones tied to my chest
I forgive you, you old devil, so we both can finally rest

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up in the attic with a transmitter and a dream
A borrowed reel of tape and a kettle full of steam
We broadcast at midnight on a frequency they banned
The songs they wouldn't play us, sent out across the land
My partner works the dials, I work the microphone
For every lonely listener who thinks they're all alone

This is your pirate station, coming in on the sly
We're a needle in the static, we're a light in the sky
They can jam us, they can chase us, but they'll never make us quiet
Turn your dial to the truth, friends, and join the riot

The constable came knocking but we packed it in a van
Set it up by the river, started over where we ran
We played the union ballads, played the songs of the poor
And somewhere in the darkness someone opened up a door
A crackling little signal carrying a whole town's heart
They can't outlaw a melody, they can't outlaw the spark

We don't do it for the money, there ain't none to be had
We do it for the kid in his room who's feeling sad
Who hears a voice at midnight saying "you are not the only one"
That's the only payment, that's the work that we've done

This is your pirate station, coming in on the sly
We're a needle in the static, we're a light in the sky
They can jam us, they can chase us, but they'll never make us quiet
Turn your dial to the truth, friends, and join the riot

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Sun came up like it didn't even know
Folded up the black dress, watched the daylight grow
There's casseroles in the kitchen that I'll never eat
And your coffee cup is sitting in its usual seat
The house is full of flowers and it's empty all the same
The hardest part of grieving is the morning, not the rain

Morning after, morning after, and the world keeps spinning on
They all went home last night and now it's just me and you gone
I don't know how to do this, how to make the coffee for one
Morning after, morning after, where do I run

Phone keeps buzzing softly with the "how you holding up"
I don't have an answer, I just stare into the cup
Your jacket's on the hook still, smelling just like you
And I can't bring myself to wash the things you used to do
The funeral was loud, baby, all those words and song
But nobody warned me 'bout the quiet coming on

Maybe grief's a slow song that I'm learning how to play
One verse at a sunrise, getting through another day
I'll keep your side of the bed and I'll keep your side of me
And find a way to carry what I'm too torn up to see

Morning after, morning after, and the world keeps spinning on
They all went home last night and now it's just me and you gone
I don't know how to do this, how to make the coffee for one
Morning after, morning after, where do I run

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Well I came back home for the summer, oh my
And I saw you at the corner store, caught your eye
You used to ride the same bus, sit a row behind
Now you're all grown up and you're blowing my mind
(Sha la la la) down on Maple Street
(Sha la la la) where our eyes did meet

You're a hometown sweetheart, a hometown girl
You turned my whole familiar little world
Ooh, I never noticed, never knew
That the one for me was here the whole time, it's you
(Bom bom bom) it's you

We walked down to the soda shop, just like the old days
You laughed at my haircut in a hundred different ways
The jukebox played a slow one and I held out my hand
Funny how the right thing was right here where I stand
(Sha la la la) underneath the moon
(Sha la la la) and I'm humming our tune

I traveled all those cities looking high and looking low
For a love I left behind a long, long time ago
Well I'm never gonna leave you, never gonna roam
'Cause my heart's been waiting for me right back home

You're a hometown sweetheart, a hometown girl
You turned my whole familiar little world
Ooh, I never noticed, never knew
That the one for me was here the whole time, it's you
(Bom bom bom) it's you

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Clock says it's a holiday but I'm punching in
Scrubs on, gloves up, here we go again
The whole world's at the dinner, got the gravy and the ham
But somebody's gotta hold it down and baby here I am
Double shift, double time, on my feet all day
But I move to my own rhythm, that's the only way

Work that double, work it, on the holiday
Somebody's gotta keep the heartbeat, somebody's gotta stay
I got soul in my sneakers and a groove in my walk
Work that double, baby, ain't got time to talk

Room four needs his meds and room six needs a smile
I bring a little funk to every aisle and every mile
The monitors are beeping like a beat I understand
I'm a one-nurse orchestra with a chart in my hand
Missed the family dinner but I'm feeding something more
There's a whole lotta living behind every door

My feet are gonna ache and my coffee's gone cold
But a body that I helped is worth more than gold
So turn the radio up at the nurses' station low
And let me dance my way through this overnight show

Work that double, work it, on the holiday
Somebody's gotta keep the heartbeat, somebody's gotta stay
I got soul in my sneakers and a groove in my walk
Work that double, baby, ain't got time to talk

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The bus dropped me off where the gravel meets the pine
Same crooked old mailbox, same barbed-wire line
But the boy that they sent over, he ain't the one came back
There's a stranger in my boots walking down this dusty track
The dog don't recognize me, the church bell sounds too loud
And I flinch at every backfire from the cars in the crowd

I'm home from the war but the war ain't done with me
It followed me across the ocean, it's in everything I see
Mama set a place for a son she used to know
I'm home from the war, Lord, but I left part of me in the snow

The fields are still golden and the creek still runs clear
But I carry all the dying in the silence I hear
My father shakes my hand like he's afraid I might break
And nobody asks me 'bout the things I can't unmake
I sleep in my old bedroom with the light left on
Trying to remember who I was before I was gone

Maybe the river knows me, maybe the long grass will
Maybe the quiet of the hollow can teach my hands to be still
I'll dig in this red dirt and I'll plant what I can grow
And pray the ground forgives a man for what he had to know

I'm home from the war but the war ain't done with me
It followed me across the ocean, it's in everything I see
Mama set a place for a son she used to know
I'm home from the war, Lord, but I left part of me in the snow

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew at noon and never blew again
Thirty years of steel went quiet on a Friday
Jimmy clocked out crying, didn't say a word
Just folded up his apron like it weighed a hundred pounds

They shipped the presses east on flatbeds in the rain
Left a parking lot of oil stains and our names
The diner cut its hours, the bank put up a sign
And Main Street learned to whisper what it used to roar

What do you tell a town that built the things that built the world
When the lights go cold on the line that fed every boy and girl
We gave it forty hours, we gave it both our hands
Now we're standing in the gravel where the future used to stand

My daddy's lunch pail's hanging by the kitchen door
He says a man's not finished, but his eyes don't say it sure
The union hall's a church now for the ones who can't move on
We pray to rusted padlocks and the shifts that all are gone

Maybe somewhere south a new sign's getting hung
Maybe my boy makes it out before the well runs dry
But I'll keep a key that opens nothing in my drawer
To remember we were something more than cheaper somewhere else

What do you tell a town that built the things that built the world
When the lights go cold on the line that fed every boy and girl
We gave it forty hours, we gave it both our hands
Now we're standing in the gravel where the future used to stand

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The A&R man bought one round and checked his phone
The room was half a crowd and half their coats
I played the song I'd carved out of my twenties
And watched him write a number, then cross it out

The drummer sold his kit that following spring
The bass player went and got himself a kid
I kept the setlist taped inside my guitar case
A map to a country that closed its only road

We were one yes away from leaving this behind
One handshake from a life I half-remember in my mind
The tape still spins, the verse still climbs and breaks
I was almost who I told my mother I'd become

There's a version of that night that didn't end
Where the man stayed for the bridge and changed his mind
I carry him around like change inside my pocket
A weight that makes a sound but doesn't spend

No I don't play out much, I fix transmissions now
But Tuesdays when the shop goes dark and slow
I'll hum the chorus to the chrome and the fluorescent
And for ninety seconds I am twenty-six again

We were one yes away from leaving this behind
One handshake from a life I half-remember in my mind
The tape still spins, the verse still climbs and breaks
I was almost who I told my mother I'd become

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Neon bled across the wet parade
Your name in lights that flickered like a pulse
We floated up the stairwell to the green room
Where the future hummed behind a velvet door

Almost, almost, the word dissolves like sugar on my tongue
You were a comet caught a breath before the dawn
We touched the ceiling and it turned to glass
And everything we held began to softly fall

The microphone was warm against your mouth
A thousand silver faces tilting up
Then someone pulled the plug inside the moment
And silence wore your spotlight like a coat

Slow now, slow now, the city tilts and blurs
We drift below the marquee letting go
Your heartbeat is a distant radio
Playing a chorus no one quite recalls

Almost, almost, the word dissolves like sugar on my tongue
You were a comet caught a breath before the dawn
We touched the ceiling and it turned to glass
And everything we held began to softly fall

I keep that night inside a snow globe shake
The glitter never lands, it only swims
You're still up there with both arms open wide
Forever in the second before the fade

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
First chair by the window, clippers like a heartbeat
He's been cutting since the Buick was the dream
Knows your daddy's fade and your grandfather's worry
Knows which silence means your week went sideways

Sit down, lean back, let the mirror tell the truth
He'll trim away the trouble that you carried through the door
Everybody's story leaves a little on the floor
And the man behind the chair has heard it all before

He married off three nephews from this corner
Buried two best friends and held the church
The newspaper's right there but nobody reads it
The news that matters happens in his hands

Little man came nervous for his very first cut
Left a king with a lollipop and a wink
He says every boy deserves to feel expensive
At least one afternoon beneath these blades

When he finally hangs the cape up on the hook
The whole town's gonna wonder where to go
Not just for a lineup, not just for a shave
But for somebody who remembers who you are

Sit down, lean back, let the mirror tell the truth
He'll trim away the trouble that you carried through the door
Everybody's story leaves a little on the floor
And the man behind the chair has heard it all before

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Gravel road, the headlights cut the fog like teeth
Her hands at ten and two and white as bone
I tell her ease the clutch the way you'd calm a horse
And the cross out by the bend goes drifting past

Her mother learned on this same dead-end mile
Before the river took the year I don't discuss
I watch my girl's reflection in the dark glass
And see a ghost behind the wheel beside my own

Drive, girl, drive, but mind the place the road gives way
There's water in the low spots and there's water in my eyes
I'm handing you the keys to everything I fear
God keep your hands steadier than mine

A crow lifts off the wire and scatters shadow
She flinches and we drift toward the ditch
I don't reach over, just say easy, easy now
A father has to teach his hands to stay

One day this same dark road will pull her out of here
Past the county line and everything I am
And I'll be just a porch light getting smaller
A prayer she half-remembers in the rain

Drive, girl, drive, but mind the place the road gives way
There's water in the low spots and there's water in my eyes
I'm handing you the keys to everything I fear
God keep your hands steadier than mine

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
