songs.lyrics:
The dial tone hum like a long road home
My thumb been hovering for a thousand nights
Four years of pride sitting heavy in my chest
And one ringtone undoing all of it

Mama pick up, Mama pick up the phone
I been gone too long, let me hear you sing my name
The city took my voice but it could not take the line
That run from your kitchen straight into my bones

She answer soft, she answer like no time had passed
Like I never slammed the gate and caught that plane
Na my name she calling, the small one she used to use
And the whole of my hard heart just dey melt

She ask if I dey eat, she ask about the cold
She no ask about the money or the why
Just tell me say the mango tree still standing
And my room dey wait, the bed still made for me

E be like say the years were just a held breath
And love no expire, e just dey wait
I book one ticket while she still dey talking
Going home, going home, going home

Mama pick up, Mama pick up the phone
I been gone too long, let me hear you sing my name
The city took my voice but it could not take the line
That run from your kitchen straight into my bones

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Third floor window still got that amber glow
Somebody else is dancing where we used to fall
I slow the wheels down, let the engine purr
The streetlight catches all the years I tried to lose

Drive on by, baby, drive on by
The mirrorball is spinning in a room that isn't mine
Every light I pass is a heartbeat in my chest
Go on and turn it up, I'm dancing through the past

The corner store still humming that same blue
Where we bought our cheap champagne and called it gold
I swear I see us laughing through the window glass
Two silhouettes still moving to a fading song

The light goes green but I don't want to leave
Just one more loop around this glittered block of ghosts
The radio is playing what we used to play
And the city wears our memory like a sequined coat

I could ring the buzzer, no I never will
Some rooms you only visit from the street
So I keep my foot light and my window down
And let the warm night carry what we were away

Drive on by, baby, drive on by
The mirrorball is spinning in a room that isn't mine
Every light I pass is a heartbeat in my chest
Go on and turn it up, I'm dancing through the past

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. and the diesel's gone to sleep
(ooh, ooh, the highway moans)
A phone booth glowing lonely by the weigh
(sha-la-la, so far from home)

Hello darling, did I wake you, are you there
I just wanted to hear breathing on the other end
The road is long, the moon is low, and Wyoming's wide
So sing me back to sleep, my love, before I drive

The coffee's cold, the radio's all static now
(ooh, ooh, the white lines blur)
I keep your picture taped above the wheel
(sha-la-la, it's all a smear)

You tell me Tommy lost a tooth and laughed it off
(ooh, ooh, my little man)
And I'm a thousand miles of yellow paint from that
(sha-la-la, hold his hand)

When I get the rig home Sunday I will hold you slow
And I won't let the engine call me back so soon
But tonight it's just this cord and this old booth
And your sleepy voice, the sweetest thing I own

Hello darling, did I wake you, are you there
I just wanted to hear breathing on the other end
The road is long, the moon is low, and Wyoming's wide
So sing me back to sleep, my love, before I drive

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five dollars says you can't outlast the clock
He slaps the timer like it owes him rent
Forty years of pigeons and cheap cigars
Have taught his hands a language no school sells

Sit down, son, the board don't care who's rich
The king falls just as hard in any coat
He'll take your money slow and call you friend
And teach you something costing more than what you lost

They say he played a grandmaster to a draw
In a prison yard in nineteen sixty-nine
Now he reigns over a folding table kingdom
Where students think their college makes them safe

A banker in a suit sits down to prove a point
Gets mated in eleven, leaves his lunch
The old man just resets the pawns and grins
The pigeons scatter, then they settle back

The winter's coming, fewer marks each day
His fingers stiffen, but the eyes stay quick
He says when they bury me just leave a board
So death has got to earn me move by move

Sit down, son, the board don't care who's rich
The king falls just as hard in any coat
He'll take your money slow and call you friend
And teach you something costing more than what you lost

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The bus drop me where the red road bend
Same dust, same goats, same church bell in the heat
But I dey carry something heavy in my walk
Things these quiet streets no fit understand

I dey home, I dey home, but the home no feel the same
The boy wey left no be the man wey come
So hold me, hold me, till the gunfire leave my sleep
And teach my hands to plant instead of break

My mother run, she drop the basin for the ground
Her arms around a soldier she half-know
My brother taller now, he salute me like a joke
But I see him flinch when I no laugh enough

The drum start up, the neighbors bring the food
They dance for me like say I win some prize
I smile, I move, but my eye dey scan the dark
For a danger wey no dey, but no go leave

Slowly, slowly, the morning learn my name
The rooster, not the siren, wake me up
One day I go hold my own child for this yard
And the war go be a story, not a ghost

I dey home, I dey home, but the home no feel the same
The boy wey left no be the man wey come
So hold me, hold me, till the gunfire leave my sleep
And teach my hands to plant instead of break

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Woke up to a little gray bubble on the screen
Thirty-one words to end up everything
No door slammed shut, no rain against the pane
Just a buzz against the table and a quiet I can't name

Lord, she left me in a text message
Didn't even spell my whole name out
Three years of my life in a half a second
Blue light fading and the phone face down

I read it forty times like it might rearrange
Like the letters might get sorry and come home
But a screen don't blush and a screen don't shake
It just holds her cold goodbye and won't let go

I miss the way a fight used to have a sound
The slam, the shout, the making up at dawn
Now heartbreak comes in silence and in pixels
And I'm crying at a rectangle of glass

I threw that phone across the kitchen floor
It landed face up, still showing me the words
Even broken, baby, you found a way to leave me
Glowing in the dark like a wound that won't go dark

Lord, she left me in a text message
Didn't even spell my whole name out
Three years of my life in a half a second
Blue light fading and the phone face down

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
They carry Mr. Theo down the avenue
The tuba walking slow behind the box
Handkerchiefs like white birds in the heat
And grief that knows it's gonna learn to dance

Second line, second line, let the saints come marching slow
We cry him to the corner then we set the sorrow free
Lift your knees, lift your knees, the man would want the noise
A New Orleans goodbye is a body learning joy

The snare hold back its sticks until the church
Then the bass drum kick the sadness off its feet
Auntie spin her parasol up toward the sun
And a sob become a holler become a song

The whole ward leaning off the porches now
The children running barefoot in the brass
We bury love but we refuse to bury swing
The sweat and the salt mixing on our face

So when my own day come, don't weep me down
Find me a trumpet and a hot July
Walk me past the corner store one final time
And let the city shake me up into the sky

Second line, second line, let the saints come marching slow
We cry him to the corner then we set the sorrow free
Lift your knees, lift your knees, the man would want the noise
A New Orleans goodbye is a body learning joy

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Forty years of dishes and the same cracked floor
The stereo still works, the needle still drops down
You take my ruined hands like it's the wedding hall
And the kitchen turns to ballroom under one bare bulb

ONE MORE DANCE before the dark comes in
ONE MORE SONG with the body that I wrecked
I'd burn down every year just to stay in this
Your heartbeat and my heartbeat refusing to forget

The doctors got their charts and their countdown clocks
But they don't get a vote inside this room tonight
Your bones against my bones, we sway and we hold on
And I scream into your shoulder that I won't let go

We fought like dropping anvils, we forgave like rain
We raised three kids on nothing but our gritted teeth
And now the last verse's coming and I'm not afraid
Long as your weight is leaning right against my chest

WHEN THE SONG ENDS — don't you pull away
WE'LL JUST STAND HERE in the silence, breath to breath
The needle hits the runout and it crackles low
And I will hold this slow dance till there's nothing left

ONE MORE DANCE before the dark comes in
ONE MORE SONG with the body that I wrecked
I'd burn down every year just to stay in this
Your heartbeat and my heartbeat refusing to forget

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The field is gold with trampled grass and dawn
We traded names for wristbands and for songs
The main stage breathing out its final low
And strangers turned to family in the dust

Don't let the houselights find us yet
Keep the bassline running soft beneath my skin
We got one more sunrise we can borrow from the dark
So hold my hand and let the morning come in slow

Somebody's selling coffee out a van
A guitar passing hand to tired hand
Mascara and the glitter all run down
And nobody looks more beautiful than now

Three days we lived a life without a clock
No phones, no Mondays, just the open sound
Now the tents are folding like a closing flower
And I'm already missing what I haven't lost

Take a picture with your eyes and not your phone
So the field can stay forever this alive
We'll go back to the gray and to the grind
But a part of me is staying in this light

Don't let the houselights find us yet
Keep the bassline running soft beneath my skin
We got one more sunrise we can borrow from the dark
So hold my hand and let the morning come in slow

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
The clarinet remembers what I tried to play
A shaking voice, a stool, a coffeehouse in March
My knees were doing freylekhs underneath the table
While my heart was davening for the crowd to stay

Oy, that first night, how the violin would laugh
At the boy who clutched his page like it could save his life
Now play it minor, now play it bright
The room was nearly empty but the room was everything

My zayde said go make a little noise
So I dragged his old guitar into the light
Four strangers and a barista wiping cups
And I sang like the whole shtetl filled the chairs

I flubbed the second verse, my throat went dry
A woman in the back called out keep going
And something in that kindness lit the wick
That's burned through every stage I've stood on since

So lift the horns and let the rhythm spin
Faster, faster, like the years that ran away
The boy still trembles somewhere in my chest
And I would not trade his terror for the world

Oy, that first night, how the violin would laugh
At the boy who clutched his page like it could save his life
Now play it minor, now play it bright
The room was nearly empty but the room was everything

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The walls still ring with everything we said
Broken glass of words we can't take back
I'm standing in the silence you have left
Drowning in the dark, the sudden black

Your shadow on the doorway, turning slow
The storm has passed but I am still afraid
The loudest thing I've ever come to know
Is the quiet after every word we made

In the quiet, in the after
I can hear my own heart break
Scream the silence, scream it louder
For the love we couldn't save
In the quiet, in the after
Still I reach for you again

The echo of the door we never closed
A battlefield of cold and empty chairs
I'd rather face the rage than face the cold
Of knowing that you're gone and still you're there

No thunder now, no lightning in your eyes
Just the ringing of a war we both have lost
I'd burn the world to hear you say goodbye
Instead the silence holds me at the cost

In the quiet, in the after
I can hear my own heart break
Scream the silence, scream it louder
For the love we couldn't save

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nadie creía en nosotros, dale
Desde abajo subimos, mira el barrio
Último partido, esta noche es la nuestra
Manos pa'l cielo, suena la orquesta

Nos contaron afuera, nos dieron por perdidos
Pero el corazón late, escucha ese latido

Dale, dale, esta es nuestra final
Nadie nos puede parar, vamo' a ganar
Dale, dale, el sueño es real
Sube las manos, suena, no para de sonar

Noventa minutos, deja' lo en la cancha
Sudor en la cara, el alma no se mancha
Del último puesto subimos pa' la cima
Este es el momento, ahora o nunca, encima

Nos contaron afuera, nos dieron por perdidos
Pero el corazón late, escucha ese latido

Dale, dale, esta es nuestra final
Nadie nos puede parar, vamo' a ganar
Dale, dale, el sueño es real
Sube las manos, suena, no para de sonar

Uno, dos, tres, salta el barrio entero
Desde la nada hasta ser el primero
Esta copa la levanto contigo
Los que no creían ahora están conmigo

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Under the lights of a borrowed romance
A stranger reached out and offered her hand
I didn't know your name, I didn't care
We burned through the night in the smoke and the glare

The bass like a heartbeat, the floor like a fire
Two strangers colliding, no fear, no desire
Just the pull of the moment, the chaos, the sound
Two comets that crashed and then spun back around

Dance with me, stranger, into the flame
We'll never remember, we'll never explain
A night with no future, a love with no name
Dance with me, stranger, before the light came

The band played on louder, the world fell away
We were nameless, we were faceless, we were young for a day
Your eyes held a riot, your hands held a war
And I knew when it ended I'd want nothing more

When the music dies and the morning is grey
You'll vanish like thunder, you'll fade and you'll stray
But for one burning hour the wildness was true
A stranger, a moment, a fire, and you

Dance with me, stranger, into the flame
We'll never remember, we'll never explain
A night with no future, a love with no name
Dance with me, stranger, before the light came

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
En el café de la esquina escribo mi verdad
Con tinta de la sangre y hambre de verdad
Me ofrecen oro falso por callar mi voz
Pero el verso que no vendo me lo dio mi Dios

Bailan los billetes, me tientan en la mesa
Visten de promesa lo que es una bajeza
Prefiero el bolsillo vacío y el alma entera
Que ser un payaso de su primavera

No me vendo, no me callo, no me rindo, corazón
Que mi pluma sea pobre pero nunca sea traición
Baila el tango de los necios que escogieron la razón
No me vendo, no me callo, soy poeta, soy canción

La ciudad me da la espalda, las luces me desprecian
Pero en cada barrio pobre mis palabras se aprecian
No escribo pa' los ricos ni pa' su salón
Escribo pa' los rotos que no tienen voz

Que me llamen el bohemio, el loco, el perdedor
Moriré con el orgullo de no haber sido vendedor
De mi propia dignidad, de mi propia canción
Un poeta no se compra, no se rinde el corazón

No me vendo, no me callo, no me rindo, corazón
Que mi pluma sea pobre pero nunca sea traición

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine humming low beneath the neon haze
One more turn and then I'm done for good
The city blurs to colors I can't name
Washed out like a photograph of childhood

They said one final run and then we're free
The rearview swallows everything I knew
The headlights drown in fog I cannot see
And somewhere in the static, so do you

Drive away, fade away
Into the white and the blue
Drive away, fade away
This is the last thing I'll do

The radio dissolves into a wave
A wall of sound that swallows up my name
I'm floating in the distance that I gave
To every empty road that looked the same

If I make it past the bridge I'll disappear
Into the hush of morning, into grey
No one's gonna find me, no one's near
Just the hum, the haze, the drifting away

Drive away, fade away
Into the white and the blue
Drive away, fade away
This is the last thing I'll do

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Last night, last ride, baby I'm gone
Engine dey purr, we move before the dawn
One more job and then I disappear
Money in the bag, no time for fear

Dem dey wait for me for corner, dem dey wait
But my tyre dey fly, I no fit be late

Drive away, drive away, this na the last one o
Foot on the pedal, we dey go, we dey go
Drive away, drive away, no looking back no more
After today I go rest, I go close the door

City lights dey shine like say dem know my name
But after this night nothing go remain the same
Girl I promise you say this na the end
No more running, baby, this na the last bend

Dem dey wait for me for corner, dem dey wait
But my tyre dey fly, I no fit be late

Drive away, drive away, this na the last one o
Foot on the pedal, we dey go, we dey go
Drive away, drive away, no looking back no more
After today I go rest, I go close the door

Oya wave goodbye to the streets wey raise me
Freedom dey call and the road no fit hold me
When the sun go rise I go be far away
Last job, last ride, na new life today

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Tape gun screaming, last box sealed
Empty rooms, man, I can't feel
Echo off the walls where the memories lived
Everything I had, now it's packed in a grid

Dust on the floor where the couch used to be
Last night in a place that used to be me
Keys on the counter, gon' leave 'em behind
New chapter loading, leave the old one behind

Tape it up, box it up, time to let it go
Everything I'm carrying, everything I know
Close the door behind me, don't look back no more
Last box on my shoulder walking out the door

Marker on the cardboard, label every part
Can't fit in a box what I'm holding in my heart
Shadows on the paint where the pictures used to hang
Four years in this place and it ends with a bang

One last look around, yeah, the silence so loud
Nothing left but footprints in the dust on the ground
Moving on, moving up, gotta carry the weight
Close the chapter, lock the past, walk out through the gate

Tape it up, box it up, time to let it go
Everything I'm carrying, everything I know
Close the door behind me, don't look back no more
Last box on my shoulder walking out the door

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Desde la mañana con la aguja y el hilo
Visto todo el barrio, le doy estilo
Del quinceañero al traje del velorio
Mi máquina canta, ese es mi territorio

Tráeme la tela que yo te lo arreglo
Del pobre al rico, a todos los visto, lo juro

El sastre del barrio, dale, dale
Viste la cuadra entera, no hay nadie
Corta y cose, baila la tijera
Todo el bloque elegante donde quiera
El sastre del barrio, dale, dale
De la cabeza a los pies, no hay nadie

La doña del frente, el bobo de la esquina
Todos pasan por mi puerta, mi vitrina
Un botón, un dobladillo, una bastilla
Mis manos hacen magia con la maravilla

Tráeme la tela que yo te lo arreglo
Del pobre al rico, a todos los visto, lo juro

El sastre del barrio, dale, dale
Viste la cuadra entera, no hay nadie
Corta y cose, baila la tijera
Todo el bloque elegante donde quiera

No tengo diploma pero tengo el don
Visto a mi gente con todo el corazón
Desde la boda hasta el día del adiós
El sastre los acompaña, los visto a los dos

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The water tower's fading in my mirror
The church bell rings a song I'll soon forget
I packed my whole life in a rusted suitcase
And everything I loved I'm leaving yet

My mama's porch light burning in the evening
The gravel road that knew me as a boy
I gave this town my laughter and my growing
Now I'm trading all I had for something more

So goodbye, little town, goodbye
I'm chasing something out beyond the sky
I'll carry you in every song I sing
But I can't stay here waiting for the spring

The diner where we used to count our pennies
The river where I learned to be alone
There's nothing here but ghosts of who I used to be
And roads that only ever led back home

Maybe I'll come back when I am older
With stories of the cities and the sea
Or maybe this is just the final chapter
Of the boy this little town will never see

So goodbye, little town, goodbye
I'm chasing something out beyond the sky
I'll carry you in every song I sing
But I can't stay here waiting for the spring

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle through the morning, thread of fading gold
A hundred quiet stories that the fabric holds
The whir of the machine beneath the streetlight glow
Stitching all the lives of everyone I know

The wedding dress, the funeral suit, the school uniform
Through my hands the seasons of the city are born
I dress the block in silence, I dress the block in care
A tailor and his window and the cloth laid bare

And it builds, and it builds, and it rises like the dawn
Every thread a heartbeat of the people I have sewn
And it builds, and it builds, till the whole street glows
In the garments of a love that only the tailor knows

The years collect like buttons in a wooden drawer
The faces come and go but I am here once more
A quiet kind of giving, a humble kind of art
Dressing every body, holding every heart

Let the strings rise slow, let the cymbals swell
For the unsung hands that dress the lives they cannot tell
The block walks by in beauty and they never know
The man behind the window who loved them so

And it builds, and it builds, and it rises like the dawn
Every thread a heartbeat of the people I have sewn
And it builds, and it builds, till the whole street glows
In the garments of a love that only the tailor knows

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
They were rough as the bark on the old oak tree
Soft as the quilt that she laid over me
Flour on her fingers, a ring worn thin
Those hands held the whole world I was livin' in

They snapped the green beans on the back porch swing
They waved me off to school every spring
They wiped away my tears when I skinned my knee
Grandmother's hands, they were holdin' me

Oh, those hands, those weathered hands
Built a home from a hard, hard land
Every wrinkle was a prayer, every line was a plan
I'd give anything to hold my grandmother's hands

They turned the pages of the family Bible worn
They rocked every baby that was ever born
They planted the garden, they pulled every weed
Those hands gave me everything I'd ever need

Now they're folded soft beneath the willow shade
But I feel 'em every time that I kneel and pray
And when I hold my own child close at night
I swear I feel her fingers holdin' mine just right

Oh, those hands, those weathered hands
Built a home from a hard, hard land
Every wrinkle was a prayer, every line was a plan
I'd give anything to hold my grandmother's hands

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
A borrowed guitar and a six-string out of tune
A half-empty barroom on a Tuesday afternoon
My hands were shaking, voice was thin and small
But I stepped up to that microphone and gave 'em my all

Three chords and a story that I barely knew
A crowd of empty chairs and a friend or two
The spotlight felt like fire, the silence felt like stone
But something started burning that I'd never known

Oh, I was young and I was scared and I was free
With nothing but a song and a dream of who I'd be
I'd give it all to feel that fear again tonight
The first time that I stood up in the light

I flubbed the second verse, forgot the bridge
My voice cracked open like a falling ridge
But when the last note faded, somebody clapped
And right then and there my whole heart was trapped

They're tearing down that barroom where it all began
But I can still remember the trembling man
Who found out who he was beneath that single light
The boy who became a singer on that ordinary night

Oh, I was young and I was scared and I was free
With nothing but a song and a dream of who I'd be
I'd give it all to feel that fear again tonight
The first time that I stood up in the light

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raise my hand but the room rolls on
My words dissolve before they're gone
A wall of voices, louder, near
And everything I say disappears

I had it right there on my tongue
The sentence faded, came undone
They speak across me, through the haze
I'm fading into static, into grey

Talk over me, talk over me
I'm drowning in a sea I cannot see
Talk over me, talk over me
I'm vanishing, I'm vanishing, set me free

The fluorescent hum, the same old room
My silence blooming into gloom
I mouth the words but no one hears
The distance growing year by year

Maybe if I scream beneath the noise
The walls would shake and find my voice
But I dissolve into the wash
A whisper underneath the loss

Talk over me, talk over me
I'm drowning in a sea I cannot see
Talk over me, talk over me
I'm vanishing, I'm vanishing, set me free

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete horizon, a graveyard of grey
They poured out the future and buried the day
One crooked survivor still claws at the sky
The last living thing they forgot how to kill

Roots crack the asphalt, a fist through the floor
A green scream of rage at the boot and the war
They took every forest and called it a gain
But this one defiant won't bow to their chains

ThE LAST TREE STANDING, WON'T BACK DOWN
ROOTS IN THE RUIN OF A PAVED-OVER TOWN
CUT IT, CHOKE IT, IT STILL FIGHTS BACK
THE LAST GREEN HEARTBEAT IN A WORLD OF BLACK

They idle their engines, they sharpen the blade
But the bark holds the names of the dead unafraid
A monument standing where forests once grew
A middle-finger branch pointing straight up at you

TEAR IT DOWN! — IT GROWS BACK
PAVE IT OVER! — IT FIGHTS BACK
This is the last of the wild, the last of the free
And I'll chain myself to it before they take my last tree

THE LAST TREE STANDING, WON'T BACK DOWN
ROOTS IN THE RUIN OF A PAVED-OVER TOWN
CUT IT, CHOKE IT, IT STILL FIGHTS BACK
THE LAST GREEN HEARTBEAT IN A WORLD OF BLACK

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Me acuerdo de esa noche, micrófono en la mano
Las piernas temblando, el corazón en vano
Un bar medio vacío, las luces en mi cara
Primera vez cantando, na' me preparaba

Era un nadie con un sueño y una rima
Mírame ahora, llegué hasta la cima

Dale, mírame atrás, ¿quién lo diría?
De ese micrófono roto a la melodía
Dale, dale, así fue que empezó
Esa primera noche en que todo cambió

Se me olvidó la letra, la voz me falló
Pero algo allá adentro esa noche nació
Dos personas aplaudiendo, na' más en el salón
Pero yo sentí el fuego prender mi corazón

Era un nadie con un sueño y una rima
Mírame ahora, llegué hasta la cima

Dale, mírame atrás, ¿quién lo diría?
De ese micrófono roto a la melodía
Dale, dale, así fue que empezó
Esa primera noche en que todo cambió

Ahora lleno estadios, pero nunca olvido
Ese bar de la esquina donde fui un atrevido
Levanto mi copa por el nene asustado
Que subió a ese escenario y nunca se ha bajado

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
It's a quarter past midnight, phone glowing blue
Her name on the screen and I know what to do
Delete it, don't send it, just put the thing down
But my thumb's got a mind and it's spinning around

There's a ring on my finger, a truck in the drive
A good woman sleeping, the reason I'm alive
But you said you'd be passing back through in the fall
And now here I am answering an old ghost's call

I'm texting someone I shouldn't
Saying words that I couldn't take back
One little spark off a fire I thought I'd put out
Now it's burning a hole through the dark
Lord, I'm texting someone I shouldn't tonight

Three dots keep on dancing then stopping again
Like we both know better and don't give a damn
The coffee's gone cold and the dog won't lie down
And the whole sleeping house don't hear a sound

I'm texting someone I shouldn't
Saying words that I couldn't take back
One little spark off a fire I thought I'd put out
Now it's burning a hole through the dark
Lord, I'm texting someone I shouldn't tonight

There's a trash can right here for the words I should bury
And a chain on the door of the man I should be
But the screen lights my face like a sin in a church
And I press the green arrow and I let it go free

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I packed up the kitchen in two cardboard boxes
Left the third for the neighbor and the cat
My mother stood small at the end of the platform
Waving a hand I'll remember her by

The harbor was grey as a coin in a fountain
The gulls didn't care that I'd gone
I counted the lights of the town going under
Like candles somebody blew out one by one

And the sea, the sea, it don't ask where you're from
It just rolls and it rolls till the old world is gone
I'm a name in a ledger, a stamp in a book
With my whole life behind me, too scared to look
Oh, the sea don't ask where you're from

There's a girl on the deck speaking words I can't follow
We trade cigarettes, point at the moon
We're all of us strangers rehearsing a future
In a language we'll stumble through soon

And the sea, the sea, it don't ask where you're from
It just rolls and it rolls till the old world is gone
I'm a name in a ledger, a stamp in a book
With my whole life behind me, too scared to look
Oh, the sea don't ask where you're from

If I make it, I'll write you
I'll send back the cold of this water in ink
And I'll learn how to miss you in somebody's accent
And I'll build a new window to stand at and think

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Iron skillet screaming, black with ancient grease
Flour like a snowfall, war that smells of peace
Garlic in the marrow, onion in the air
Smoke against the ceiling, ghosts upon the stair

She ruled that little kingdom with a wooden blade
Every pot a relic, every meal she made
Burned into my memory, branded in my skull
The holy reek of bread rising, beautiful and full

The smell still drags me under
Down through years like rusted chain
Grandmother's kitchen burning bright
A cathedral made of steam and flame
I breathe it in, I breathe it in
And the dead come home again

Now the house is silent, the stove gone cold as stone
Strangers in the doorway where the cinnamon was thrown
But close my eyes and there it is, that thick and savage air
And I'm six years old and starving and she's standing there

The smell still drags me under
Down through years like rusted chain
Grandmother's kitchen burning bright
A cathedral made of steam and flame
I breathe it in, I breathe it in
And the dead come home again

No grave could ever hold her
No silence kill the sound
Of a spoon against a kettle
Where the whole world once spun round
One breath and she is risen
One breath and I am found

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Clock hit two, my willpower checked out
Phone in my hand doing what it's about
I said behave but my fingers said nah
Typing out trouble in a smiley and a comma

Got the bassline thumping in my guilty little chest
Know I should stop but I'm doing my best
Which ain't much, baby, when the groove gets greasy
Sliding into trouble 'cause you make it look easy

Texting somebody I shouldn't
Ooh, my thumbs got a will of they own
Said I wouldn't, said I couldn't
Now I'm grinning at a glow in the dark all alone
Texting somebody I shouldn't tonight

Three dots bubbling like a pot on the stove
Every little buzz got me losing control
This a slippery pocket, this a one-way street
And the rhythm of the wrong thing is too sweet

Texting somebody I shouldn't
Ooh, my thumbs got a will of they own
Said I wouldn't, said I couldn't
Now I'm grinning at a glow in the dark all alone
Texting somebody I shouldn't tonight

Put it down, put it down (I can't)
Turn it off, walk away (no chance)
The horns are saying leave, the drums are saying stay
And the funk in my chest got the last little say

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the whole world into one flat grey
A parking lot sunrise, another loading bay
But up through a crack where the concrete gave
There's one stubborn green thing nobody could pave

The crows hold their meetings on the only branch in town
The kids carve their names and the old men sit down
The mayor wants it gone, got a permit and a plan
But the roots got a grip like a fist in the sand

One tree, one tree, throwing shade on the street
Last little lungful of the green and the sweet
Pick up your feet, pick up your feet and dance
Under the last leaf left standing, give the old tree a chance
One tree, one tree, one tree

They brought out the chainsaws on a Tuesday at noon
But the whole neighborhood came out humming this tune
Tied themselves round it, brought their kids and their dogs
And the bulldozer choked on a chorus of frogs

One tree, one tree, throwing shade on the street
Last little lungful of the green and the sweet
Pick up your feet, pick up your feet and dance
Under the last leaf left standing, give the old tree a chance
One tree, one tree, one tree

You can pave every garden, pave over the sea
But you'll have to come through us to get to that tree
Pickup, horns up, skanking in the lot
One green flag flying over all that we've got

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The screen door's quit its slamming and the dust has settled down
Your boots are on the porch step but you're nowhere to be found
The words we threw are hanging like the smoke above the field
And the silence in this kitchen is a wound that will not heal

I can hear the kettle ticking as it's cooling on the stand
I can hear the clock you hated and the tremble in my hand
There's a plate set for two but only one of us will eat
And the quiet's gotten louder than the shouting in the street

Oh the quiet, oh the quiet after the storm
When the heart's still beating angry but the bed's gone cold and warm
I'd take back every hard word for the softness of your name
In the quiet, in the quiet, I'm the one to blame

The banjo's in the corner where you set it down to yell
And the dog won't leave the doorway 'cause he knows the house too well
I keep rehearsing apologies I'm too proud to say out loud
While the quiet wraps around me like a low and lonesome cloud

Oh the quiet, oh the quiet after the storm
When the heart's still beating angry but the bed's gone cold and warm
I'd take back every hard word for the softness of your name
In the quiet, in the quiet, I'm the one to blame

Then I hear you in the hallway, hear you set your anger down
Hear two floorboards come together like the last folks left in town
And you reach for me so gently that the quiet starts to mend
And we don't say that we're sorry, we just hold on to the end

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Chair bolted to the floor since nineteen sixty-three
Mirror full of faces that this town used to be
Clippers never stopping, scissors going fast
Every head a chapter, every cut a past

He knows who's getting married, knows who's going down
Knows the kid that ran, knows who burned the warehouse down
Keeps it all in silence, sweeps it on the floor
The confessional with a candy-striped door

He knows! He knows the whole damn town!
Every secret, every scar, every house gone down!
Sit in the chair, look him in the eye
He's seen 'em all come up and he's seen 'em all die!
HE KNOWS! HE KNOWS!

Forty years of fathers, forty years of sons
First haircut to the last one before the dirt comes
He don't read the paper, he don't have to look
The whole town's history's written in his book

He knows! He knows the whole damn town!
Every secret, every scar, every house gone down!
Sit in the chair, look him in the eye
He's seen 'em all come up and he's seen 'em all die!
HE KNOWS! HE KNOWS!

When he finally closes, when the lights go black
The whole town's memory ain't coming back
So sit down, shut up, let the old man cut
He's the last one standing and he keeps it shut

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the first foundation on a ridge that softly breathed
Called it solid bedrock, swore by all they believed
But the old ones in the valley said don't build upon that hill
That ain't a mountain, stranger, that's a thing that's lying still

The streetlights flicker double when the deep ground turns at night
The church bells ring untouched and the dogs won't quit their fright
We paved across his shoulder, put a market on his spine
And we sip our morning coffee on a fault line dressed in vine

There's a giant down below us and he's dreaming, dreaming deep
And this whole city's just a blanket on the body of his sleep
We sing and sin and bury our dead in his hide
And we pray he don't roll over, pray he stays on his side

The preacher takes our offering, the banker takes the rest
And nobody asks the question that's been buried in our chest
What happens to the faithful and the wicked all the same
The morning that the mountain wakes and finally learns our name

There's a giant down below us and he's dreaming, dreaming deep
And this whole city's just a blanket on the body of his sleep
We sing and sin and bury our dead in his hide
And we pray he don't roll over, pray he stays on his side

I felt him shift this morning when the coffee left its cup
And I knew it in my marrow that the giant's waking up
So ring the bells, you sinners, dance upon his chest
We built our whole damnation here and here we'll take our rest

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
String lights and slow air, somebody's cousin's vow
I didn't know your name an hour ago and I don't need it now
The band found a soft one, the floor went half-empty and gold
You held out a hand like a question I was too tired to fold

So we turn, so we turn in the low amber light
Two strangers, one song, holding still in the night
No before, no after, just the hum of the room
And the slow turning world and your hand and the bloom

We don't talk, we just sway where the music goes thin
Your breath on my collar, the warmth of your skin
Somewhere a glass clinks, somewhere a toast
But here it's just gravity, the nearest and most

So we turn, so we turn in the low amber light
Two strangers, one song, holding still in the night
No before, no after, just the hum of the room
And the slow turning world and your hand and the bloom

The song's going to end and we'll let our hands fall
Go back to the tables, the strangers, the hall
But for these three minutes the whole world stood true
And I danced past my life in the arms of a you

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights of the city were close enough to taste
My tank read empty and my heart read waste
I had a guitar in the back and a name on a card
And a hundred miles of nothing and a road running hard

The man said come Monday, said the deal was as good as signed
I drove all that Sunday with the future on my mind
The radio preacher said the Lord rewards the bold
And the rain came down like silver turning slowly into gold

Oh, that was the night, the night I almost made it
Close enough to touch it, Lord, close enough to break it
The door swung open and the wind blew it shut
And I've lived ever after in that almost, in that cut
The night, the night I almost made it

The bridge went out at midnight in the river black and wide
And the man, he didn't wait, found another by my side
I sat there in the headlights watching everything go past
The one shot that I waited for, too far and going fast

Oh, that was the night, the night I almost made it
Close enough to touch it, Lord, close enough to break it
The door swung open and the wind blew it shut
And I've lived ever after in that almost, in that cut
The night, the night I almost made it

Now I play the same six songs in a bar that no one knows
And I tell the young ones leaving how the cold wind blows
Don't wait on a Monday, son, don't trust the bridge to hold
The night you almost make it is the night that turns you old

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon humming pink at a quarter to three
Vinyl booth, bottomless coffee, just you and me
The cook's got the radio low on a station from '99
And the whole sleeping city's outside but we're doing fine

Nowhere to be, nobody to please
Just pancakes and laughter and skinned-up knees

At the all-night diner, the lights stay on
Where the lonely and the lovers wait out the dawn
Pour another, baby, let the jukebox play
The all-night diner won't turn us away
Won't turn us away, no, never

The waitress knows your order and she calls me hon
There's a trucker telling stories that go on and on
And somewhere out the window the sky's turning blue
But I'd stay in this booth for a year with you

At the all-night diner, the lights stay on
Where the lonely and the lovers wait out the dawn
Pour another, baby, let the jukebox play
The all-night diner won't turn us away
Won't turn us away, no, never

When the world locks its doors and the last bus is gone
There's a window still glowing to carry you on
So whatever you're running from, whatever you lack
There's a stool and a sunrise and they'll always take you back

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I drove out to the cemetery, sat down in the wet grass
Brought all the things I never said, finally let 'em pass
You can't hear me now, and maybe that's the only way
I ever could've told you all the things I'm here to say

I carried you like a stone in my coat for all these years
The weight of every wrong you did, the salt of all my tears
But a heavy thing gets heavier the longer that you hold
So I'm setting it down here in the morning, in the cold

I forgive you, oh, I forgive you
Even though you're already gone
There's nobody here to receive it but the wind and the dawn
Still I forgive you, and the lock comes undone
And I feel myself loosen, feel the rising of the sun

It was never gonna come from you, that sorry, that release
So I had to learn to give myself the gift of my own peace
And the strangest thing is loving you got easy once you died
'Cause now there's only memory and the softer, kinder side

I forgive you, oh, I forgive you
Even though you're already gone
There's nobody here to receive it but the wind and the dawn
Still I forgive you, and the lock comes undone
And I feel myself loosen, feel the rising of the sun

This ain't for you, it's for me
This is how I finally breathe
I lay it in the ground beside you and I turn and walk away
Lighter than I came here, into the rest of all my days

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Same streets we grew up running, same names on the wall
I knew your face before I knew that I could fall
We were just two kids on bicycles, dust on our shoes
Now you're standing in the kitchen and I got nothing to lose

You know the church I cried in, know my mama's maiden name
You knew me at my ugliest and loved me just the same
There's a comfort in your knowing that the city couldn't give
You're the answer to a question I've been asking how to live

Falling for somebody from my hometown
Somebody who was always around
We planted these roots when we were too young to know
And look how the love had the whole time to grow
Falling, falling for my hometown girl

We drive the same back roads we did at seventeen
But now your hand is in my hand and I know what it means
Every mile is a memory, every corner's a kiss
How'd I leave for all those years and somehow miss all this

Falling for somebody from my hometown
Somebody who was always around
We planted these roots when we were too young to know
And look how the love had the whole time to grow
Falling, falling for my hometown girl

Maybe home was never the place, it was you all along
The face in every photograph, the hum in every song
So I'm staying, I'm staying where the start and ending meet
In the only town that knows me, dancing barefoot in the street

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Dusty vinyl crackle, rain against the pane
Grew up two blocks over, knew your middle name
We were just background extras in each other's old scenes
Now you're texting me at midnight and I'm fixing up my dreams

Same corner store, same flicker of the sign
Same cracked basketball court where we used to waste time
I moved away, came back, and there you were again
Like a verse I forgot I wrote back when I was ten

Falling for somebody from my hometown
Slow, like the snow when it's settling down
No rush, no pressure, just the hum and the ease
Falling for the somebody who's always been these streets
yeah, these streets

We split a pair of headphones on your mama's front step
You know all my history, every promise I kept
Ain't gotta explain me, ain't gotta perform
Just a beat and your shoulder keeping each other warm

Falling for somebody from my hometown
Slow, like the snow when it's settling down
No rush, no pressure, just the hum and the ease
Falling for the somebody who's always been these streets
yeah, these streets

Long drives, low lights, nowhere we gotta be
The town that raised us both is the town that set us free
Press record on the quiet, let the tape softly spin
The oldest thing around me is the newest love I'm in

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Oh, there's one little tree on the corner of Main
Where the parking lots stretch in the sun and the rain
The last bit of green in a world made of grey
And I sit in her shade at the end of the day
(shoo-bop, sha-na-na)

They cut down the orchard, they paved down the park
Left her standing alone, singing low in the dark
But the birds still come home to her arms every night
And she shivers her leaves in the soft yellow light
(shoo-bop, sha-na-na)

Last tree, oh, last tree, won't you stay, stay, stay
Don't let them come take all your branches away
You're the heart of this town made of concrete and stone
Last tree, oh, my last tree, don't leave me alone
(my last tree, ooh-wah)

I carved out our names when I was just a boy
Me and my baby, our secret, our joy
Now my baby is gone but the tree's standing tall
And the letters we cut are still there on the wall
(shoo-bop, sha-na-na)

Last tree, oh, last tree, won't you stay, stay, stay
Don't let them come take all your branches away
You're the heart of this town made of concrete and stone
Last tree, oh, my last tree, don't leave me alone
(my last tree, ooh-wah)

So I'll sit here beside you and I'll hold the line
With my back to your bark and your hand in mine
Let 'em pave the whole world, let 'em pour the cement
We're the last bit of green and we won't relent
(ooh-wah, ooh-wah, my last tree)

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down like it owed the street something
You were standing at the edge of nothing
I tilted the handle, you stepped underneath
Two wet shoulders learning how to breathe

One umbrella, two hearts unknown
Neither one of us wanted to go home
Let the city flood, let the gray turn gold
Stay a little closer, the night's still cold

You laughed at a joke I forgot to tell
We traded names like we knew them well
Your arm against mine, accidental warm
A little dry circle in the middle of the storm

One umbrella, two hearts unknown
Neither one of us wanted to go home
Let the city flood, let the gray turn gold
Stay a little closer, the night's still cold

When the clouds break and the sky goes clear
Will you still be standing here
Or just a stranger I dried beside
While the whole sky tried to push us outside

One umbrella, two hearts unknown
Don't make me walk these streets alone
Let the city flood, let the gray turn gold
Stay a little closer, the night's still cold

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Soft engine humming under streetlight rain
One more easy turn down the quiet lane
The bag in the back, the deal is done
This is the last one, baby, the very last one

Drive slow, drive smooth, don't let them see
The ocean's waiting somewhere south for me
No more red lights flashing in my eyes
Just palm trees swaying under sleepy skies

She hums a melody I half recall
A samba spinning slow against the wall
I promised her a beach and a brand new name
After tonight I'll never drive again

Drive slow, drive smooth, don't let them see
The ocean's waiting somewhere south for me
No more red lights flashing in my eyes
Just palm trees swaying under sleepy skies

Funny how a heart can keep its time
While sirens fade behind a yellow line
I'll trade the throttle for a quiet shore
And never check the mirror anymore

Drive slow, drive smooth, the road is free
The morning sun is coming up for me
No more red lights flashing in my eyes
Just your soft laughter and the open skies

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We trained for the dark in a Texas field
Promised we'd hold on, never yield
Now the tether snapped on a routine day
And the cold black water's pulling you away

Drifting apart, drifting apart
A million miles between two hearts
I'd give every star in this empty sky
For one more minute before goodbye

Your voice on the radio thin and small
Fading out like the last leaf in the fall
I reach through the glass but there's nothing there
Just the long slow turn and the silver air

Drifting apart, drifting apart
A million miles between two hearts
I'd give every star in this empty sky
For one more minute before goodbye

Back home there's a porch and a screen door
A dog that won't know you anymore
Tell mama I tried, tell her I held tight
To the rope that let go in the endless night

Drifting apart, drifting apart
The distance grows but I keep your heart
When they find me cold in this empty sky
I'll still be reaching for your goodbye

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The needle drops on a worn-out hymn
The parlor light is burning dim
Forty years of dust on the floor
We'll waltz across it once more

One last dance before the grave
A slow turn for the vows we gave
Your hand in mine, cold as the rain
We'll dance until there's nothing to remain

The ring's gone thin, the gold worn through
Like everything I gave to you
The preacher's words have turned to bone
But I won't dance this last alone

One last dance before the grave
A slow turn for the vows we gave
Your hand in mine, cold as the rain
We'll dance until there's nothing to remain

The candles gutter, the shadows sway
Death is humming, come away
But hold me tighter, don't let go
We knew this song so long ago

One last dance before the dark
A slow turn round the dying spark
Your hand in mine, cold as the rain
We'll dance until there's nothing left to name

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the concrete river wide
Killed the green on every side
But one old oak still stands alone
Roots breaking through the asphalt stone

Last tree standing, last tree blue
Nothing left to talk to but you
They took the forest, took the shade
But you're still here in the mess they made

The crows come back though there's nowhere to land
They circle the lot where the woods used to stand
I lean my back on your dying bark
And we hum the blues till the falling dark

Last tree standing, last tree blue
Nothing left to talk to but you
They took the forest, took the shade
But you're still here in the mess they made

They'll bring the saw come Monday morning
No permit posted, no real warning
But tonight your leaves still catch the moon
And I'll sit and sing this old worn tune

Last tree falling, last tree blue
Whole town paved and I'm losing you
They took the forest, took the shade
Now even you go the way of the made

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Morning bus before the sun wakes up
Night shift coffee in a paper cup
I clock in here, I clock out there
Money like water, slipping through air

Two jobs, no rest, still I'm running behind
Chasing a dollar I can never find
But I move my feet, I keep the beat
Even when the rent eats my whole receipt

Mama calls, she needs a little more
Landlord knocking on my tired door
I smile for the customers, smile for the boss
Counting up the hours, counting up the cost

Two jobs, no rest, still I'm running behind
Chasing a dollar I can never find
But I move my feet, I keep the beat
Even when the rent eats my whole receipt

One day this grind gonna pay me back
One day I'll rest on a different track
Till then I dance through the weary night
Keep my head up, keep my dreaming bright

Two jobs, no rest, but I won't lie down
Gonna lift my city, gonna lift my town
Move my feet, I keep the beat
Even when the world keeps moving the street

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle and thread under neon glow
Every soul on the avenue knows
Walk in tired, walk out brand new
The tailor's hands gonna carry you

Dress the block, dress the night
Make the whole street feel alright
Satin, denim, gold and lace
Everybody dancing in their second skin grace

The corner kids in their Sunday best
The dancer queen with the sequined chest
He measures dreams from heel to crown
Stitches up the whole downtown

Dress the block, dress the night
Make the whole street feel alright
Satin, denim, gold and lace
Everybody dancing in their second skin grace

When the bassline drops and the lights go low
You can see his work in every glow
A thousand bodies catching shine
Every seam a steady line

Dress the block, dress the night
Make the whole street feel alright
Satin, denim, gold and lace
Everybody dancing in their second skin grace

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We rose together past the morning cloud
Two voices singing soft and proud
But the line went slack and the dark grew wide
And you're drifting out on the silver tide

Though we drift apart in the endless black
The Lord's own light will guide you back
No distance deeper than His grace
He'll hold you close in the empty space

I cannot reach you with these hands
But faith can cross what no rope spans
Your breath is fading on the wire
But the angels carry you higher and higher

Though we drift apart in the endless black
The Lord's own light will guide you back
No distance deeper than His grace
He'll hold you close in the empty space

Oh glory, glory, the stars all sing
There's a hand far bigger than anything
When the tether breaks and the cold comes near
You are never lost, you are never here alone, my dear

Though we drift apart in the endless black
The Lord's own light will guide you back
No distance deeper than His grace
We'll meet again in a brighter place

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole life in a careful line
A house, a name, a date, a sign
I watched it bloom on someone else's wall
A garden I never planted at all

So I'm leaving the future they made for me
Fading out like static on the sea
I don't know where the open road will lead
But it's mine, it's mine, it's mine to bleed

The wash of guitars drowns out their voice
The hum says maybe you still have a choice
I close the door on the gilded plan
And dissolve into the static I am

So I'm leaving the future they made for me
Fading out like static on the sea
I don't know where the open road will lead
But it's mine, it's mine, it's mine to bleed

Blurred lights bleeding through the rain
No name to answer, no old chain
I let the noise become a kind of prayer
A loud and shapeless open air

So I'm leaving the future they planned for me
Washing out like color in the sea
I don't know where the open road will lead
But it's mine, at last it's mine to need

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The ferris wheel groans to a stop
The carnival lights begin to drop
Popcorn ghosts on the trampled ground
And a fiddle plays a dying sound

Last night of the fair, the lanterns fade
Gather up the dreams that the summer made
The tents come down, the music's thin
And the dark rolls over where the lights have been

The fortune teller packs her cards
The horses sleep in the empty yards
A child's balloon floats up and gone
Into the black where the rides shut down

Last night of the fair, the lanterns fade
Gather up the dreams that the summer made
The tents come down, the music's thin
And the dark rolls over where the lights have been

Come dance with me on the breaking boards
Before they haul away the cords
One final spin on the rusted ground
Before the silence drinks the sound

Last night of the fair, the lanterns die
A cold wind moving through an empty sky
The tents come down, the music's done
And the dark rolls over everyone

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The door stopped slamming an hour ago
Now there's just the kettle and the radio
We're sitting in the wreck of what we said
Counting all the cracks above the bed

It's so quiet now, too quiet now
The kind of silence I don't know how
To fill with anything but I love you still
Across the cold half of the windowsill

Your back is turned but you haven't gone
The lamp's still humming, the heat's still on
I trace the outline of the things I meant
Under all the words I didn't intend

It's so quiet now, too quiet now
The kind of silence I don't know how
To fill with anything but I love you still
Across the cold half of the windowsill

Maybe in a minute one of us will move
Reach across the gap we've got to prove
That the quiet isn't where we end
Just the slow long breath before we mend

It's so quiet now, but I'm still here
Drawing a little closer through the fear
I'll fill this silence with I love you still
And cross the cold half of the windowsill

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Thirty years we built this little home
Now the kids are grown and the nest is our own
The radio playing that old slow song
The one we swayed to all along

Come dance with me one more time, my love
Under the kitchen light from above
We done so much, we come so far
Let's sway slow and easy like we always are

Your hair gone grey but your eyes still shine
Still the sweetest hand I ever did find
The floorboards creak and the night is warm
We'll rock together through every storm

Come dance with me one more time, my love
Under the kitchen light from above
We done so much, we come so far
Let's sway slow and easy like we always are

If this the last dance the good Lord gives
I'll spend it close to the one I live
Irie and gentle, the bassline low
Hold me steady, don't let go

Come dance with me one more time, my love
Under the kitchen light from above
We done so much, we come so far
Let's sway slow and easy like we always are

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Raise my hand, got a point to make
Voice cut short for somebody's sake
Mid-sentence and the room moves on
My half a thought already gone

Talked over, talked over again
Swallowing words I can't begin
Low hum static under the room
My mute button stuck on assume

Good idea fading to the floor
Somebody louder grabs it for sure
Repeats it back like it's something new
And the whole table nods it through

Talked over, talked over again
Swallowing words I can't begin
Low hum static under the room
My mute button stuck on assume

One day I'm gonna let it ride
Let the slow beat carry my pride
Write it down if they won't hear
My quiet truth gonna still ring clear

Talked over, talked over again
But I keep my notebook and my pen
Low hum static under the room
My turn's gonna come, I'm built to bloom

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They paved the park, they killed the green
Built a lot for a parking machine
One tree left and they want it dead
Gonna chain myself to it instead

Last tree standing, won't back down
In this gray and gutted town
Bring your saws, bring your suits
You ain't tearing out these roots

Concrete dreams and a council vote
Signed away with a phony note
But I'm standing here in the morning chill
And the only thing green is my will

Last tree standing, won't back down
In this gray and gutted town
Bring your saws, bring your suits
You ain't tearing out these roots

They think we forgot how to fight
Think we'll sleep through the dying light
But every leaf is a fist held high
We ain't watching this last one die

Last tree standing, won't back down
We're taking back this rotten town
Bring your saws, bring your suits
You ain't tearing out these roots

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Frío en la noche pero tu calor
Green fire dancing en el cielo, mi amor
We drove so far past the city glow
Just to watch the heavens put on a show

Luces del norte, baila conmigo
Green and purple, you're all I need-o
Under the sky we move so slow
Northern lights got the whole world aglow

Manos frías but the moment's hot
Never seen nothing like what we got
The sky's a club and the stars are loud
Two little dancers under a glowing cloud

Luces del norte, baila conmigo
Green and purple, you're all I need-o
Under the sky we move so slow
Northern lights got the whole world aglow

Levanta las manos al fuego del cielo
Dance with the colors, no fear, no recelo
The whole horizon a ribbon of light
Y tú y yo dancing through the arctic night

Luces del norte, baila conmigo
Green and purple, you're all I need-o
Under the sky we move so slow
Northern lights got the whole world aglow

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The stage lights bit the dust like copper coins
My heels spoke faster than my throat could pray
The manager leaned in with golden eyes
Then turned to take another call away

So close I tasted salt on the brass door
So close the curtain breathed against my hand
One inch of velvet kept me from the floor
Where they remember every name but mine

Guitar strings still warm beneath my fingernails
The crowd had thinned to ashtrays and to chairs
I danced for no one in the empty hall
And no one is the hardest crowd of all

Madrugada keeps the secret of my feet
The cobblestones still hum the missing beat
I did not fall, I did not win the night
I hovered in the gap between two lights

So close I tasted salt on the brass door
So close the curtain breathed against my hand
I keep the almost folded in my shawl
And dance it out again, again, again

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The candles in the window are not mine to light
I traded eight more hours for a stranger's quiet night
The clarinet of monitors keeps wailing down the hall
And I am the only dancer left to answer when they call

Oy, the holiday is bleeding into morning
My feet have learned the freylekhs of the ward
I carry someone's grandfather his water and his warning
And hum the songs my mother sang to God

Room four is asking if his daughter ever came
I hold his paper wrist and tell a kindness like a name
The vending machine is dinner and the chapel light is dim
The holiday belongs to him, the holiday belongs to him

Spin me, tired feet, one circle round the desk
The horns inside my chest still know the test
There is no table set, no wine, no chair
But someone here is breathing 'cause I'm there

Oy, the holiday is bleeding into morning
My feet have learned the freylekhs of the ward
I dance the double shift without a warning
And hum the songs my mother sang to God

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night the rooftops let me go
The chimney smoke becomes a set of stairs
My grandfather is laughing from below
With both feet planted in his rocking chair

Up, up, the violin pulls the thread
The town gets small as buttons on a coat
I fly above the living and the dead
Where every minor key becomes a boat

I never learned the trick of coming down
The market square is bread crumbs in the dark
The river wears the moon like a wet crown
And dogs forget to fear me when I'm sparked

Then always, always comes the morning bell
My shoulders heavy, ordinary, plain
I walk the cobblestones I knew too well
And wait for night to lend me sky again

Up, up, the clarinet wants to climb
My arms remember more than my own feet
I fly the same dream every single time
And land inside a bed that's still too sweet

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I drove out to the cemetery with a list in my coat
All the things you did to me, all the things I wrote
The stone don't read, the stone don't beg, the stone don't say it's sorry
So I read it to the grass and let the wind take half the story

I'm forgiving you a little late, baby
You can't hear a word I say
But this anger's gotten heavy
And I'm setting it down today

You never said the word I needed, never once looked back
Now the only conversation left is me against the black
I used to keep the score in fire, now I keep it in the rain
Forgiveness ain't for you no more, it's me, undoing chains

Funny how the dead can hold a door you can't get through
Funny how I had to lose you 'fore I quit on losing you

I'm forgiving you a little late, baby
You can't hear a word I say
But this anger's gotten heavy
And I'm walking light away

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Antenna on the water tower, signal in the rust
Three a.m. transmission, in the static we trust
No license, no commercials, just a frequency I stole
Broadcasting from a basement with a heater and a soul
FCC van circling, I can see 'em on the cam
Flip the dial, ghost the channel, disappear like I'm a scam

We on the air, we off the grid
Tell 'em what the silence hid
Tune me in or tune me out
This the frequency they doubt

Kids in the projects got my numbers on the wall
When the city cuts the power I'm the loudest in the squall
Mixtape requests come hummin' through the burner line
Every song's a middle finger to the corporate design

They can't bill the airwaves, can't fence in the sky
Every station's a mouth they wanna sew up and deny
But I'm a ghost in the wire, I'm a voice in the night
Kill the broadcast, I'ma build it back by light

We on the air, we off the grid
Tell 'em what the silence hid
Tune me in or tune me out
This the frequency they doubt

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The water tower's got my high school painted blue
The diner closes early like it's always gonna do
I loaded up the Chevy with a duffel and a dog
And watched the only stoplight blinking yellow through the fog

I'm leaving for good this time, no Sunday coming back
The county line's a river and I'm halfway down the track
Tell my mama that I love her, tell the church I learned to pray
Somewhere that ain't got my whole life carved into the clay

I know the name of every road and who they used to be
The Hendersons still farm the field that swallowed up my keys
There's a comfort in the knowing and a coffin in it too
A town this small remembers every wrong thing that you do

My daddy never left here, he just settled like the dust
Got old beside the hardware store and rusted like the trucks
I won't be a photograph they point to with a sigh
I'd rather be a question than an answer left to die

I'm leaving for good this time, no Sunday coming back
The county line's a river and I'm halfway down the track
The radio is static and the gas tank's running clean
Goodbye to every face that knew exactly what I'd been

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
She walks the shoreline barefoot in the haze
Her voice a low and salt-soft lullaby
She tells the water how to count the days
And folds the moon into her cloudy eye

Come in, come in, then learn to let it go
The ocean is her oldest, slowest child
She teaches it the only thing to know
Return, return, and never stay too wild

Her hands are maps of every coming wave
The foam remembers everything she said
She gives the tide a rhythm, not a grave
And combs the kelp like ribbons through its head

When she is gone the water still will breathe
In, out, the lesson written in the sand
The sea will wear her patience like a sleeve
And reach, each dusk, toward an empty hand

Come in, come in, then learn to let it go
The ocean is her oldest, slowest child
She teaches it the only thing to know
Return, return, and never stay too wild

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Flour in the creases, river in the veins
A wedding ring grown thin from peeling years
These hands have buried gardens, carried rain
And wiped a hundred children's quiet tears

Hold still, hold still, the soft machine of love
Each knuckle is a country I have known
The roadmap of a woman made of dough and dove
The warmest, oldest language ever sewn

They shake a little now above the tea
The steam writes letters none of us can read
They braided me, they bandaged me, set free
The smallest broken bird I'd ever need

When they go still beneath the folded sheet
I'll press my palm to glass and find them there
The heat of every loaf, the kneaded wheat
Still rising somewhere in the kitchen air

Hold still, hold still, the soft machine of love
Each knuckle is a country I have known
The warmest, oldest language ever sewn
Now stitched inside the hands I call my own

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They came with golden contracts and a velvet guillotine
Said soften up the language, make the chorus nice and clean
They want my teeth defanged, my ink diluted into wine
But every word I bleed is mine, is mine, is mine

I WON'T SELL OUT, I'LL BURN THE PAGE FIRST
LET THE MARKET CHOKE ON ALL MY THIRST
MY VERSE IS NOT A PRODUCT ON YOUR SHELF
I'D RATHER STARVE AND STILL BE FULL OF MYSELF

The suits have hollow microphones where their honest mouths should sit
They'd sand a mountain to a coin and call the rubble hit
I'll scream into the granite till the granite screams it back
No polish on my fury, no apology, no slack

You can buy the singer, you can never buy the song
You can break the printing press, the verse goes marching on
Bury me with empty pockets and a fist around my pen
I'll claw my truth from underground and howl it once again

I WON'T SELL OUT, I'LL BURN THE PAGE FIRST
LET THE MARKET CHOKE ON ALL MY THIRST
MY VERSE IS NOT A PRODUCT ON YOUR SHELF
I'D RATHER STARVE AND STILL BE FULL OF MYSELF

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raised my hand at the long oak table
The coffee had gone cold and so had I
My sentence found a door but not a window
The louder voices folded up the sky

Talk over me, talk over me
I'm the verse you never let the chorus see
I brought the answer in my coat
But nobody wrote down a word I wrote

A man repeated everything I'd offered
They nodded like the moon had finally spoke
My good idea came home in someone's pocket
And I just smoothed the ashes of the smoke

I used to fill the silence like a river
Now I count the times I almost tried
There's a quiet kind of leaving in a meeting
Where the smallest part of you just steps aside

But the kettle hears me, and the morning hears me
The dog beneath the table knows my name
And one day I will say it past the talking
Or build a smaller room without the game

Talk over me, talk over me
I'm the verse you never let the chorus see
But I am writing down each word
And someday, somewhere, I will be heard

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Woke up in the same suit, tie still on the chair
Casseroles on the counter, his cologne still in the air
Phone won't stop buzzin' but I left it face down
Last night I was a pallbearer, today I'm just around
Folded up the program, can't throw the picture out
This the kind of empty that you never talk about

Morning after, morning after
Where'd they go, where'd they go
Hall full of laughter
Now it's just the radio

Fridge still got the leftovers from the night he came through
I keep settin' two cups when the coffee's almost due
The flowers gonna die soon, the cards gonna fade
But grief don't read a calendar, it sleeps right where you laid

They say it gets lighter, I'm waiting on the proof
Right now it's a hole in the middle of the roof
Rain comin' in slow on the place where you stood
I'd trade everything I got just to hear you say it's good

Morning after, morning after
Where'd they go, where'd they go
Hall full of laughter
Now it's just the radio

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole life out before I learned to walk
The wedding and the business and the script for how I talk
Gold rings in a velvet box, a stranger they call mine
A factory of a future and they handed me the line
Packed a bag at midnight while the prayers were on the wall
My name was on a contract that I never signed at all

Not the son they ordered, not the man they planned
Writing my own ending with my own two hands
Leave the gold, leave the gate, leave the made-up me
First time in my life I get to choose what I'm gonna be

Mother's gonna cry and call it shame upon the door
Father's gonna swear I'm everything he warned me for
But a cage of good intentions is a cage just the same
I'd rather be a stranger than a portrait in a frame

Maybe I come back one day with a life that I built true
Maybe they don't open, maybe love don't make it through
But I can't wear a future that was sewn before my breath
I'd rather risk the living than perform a careful death

Not the son they ordered, not the man they planned
Writing my own ending with my own two hands
Leave the gold, leave the gate, leave the made-up me
First time in my life I get to choose what I'm gonna be

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Forty years I stood in gallery nine
Watching strangers shuffle down the line
But there's a girl in oil they hung in eighteen-ten
And I have been her only friend

Good morning, painted lady, it's just me
The rest of 'em walk past and never see
You never aged a day and I went gray
But I'll be here to guard you anyway

She holds a withered letter and a rose
What it says, well, only heaven knows
The artist's been dust two hundred years
But her half-smile still survives the tears

The schoolkids snap their pictures and they leave
The scholars argue what the brushstrokes weave
But me, I memorized the way light falls
And how she leans toward the eastern walls

They're retiring me come the first of May
Handing me a watch and sent away
Who's gonna talk to her when I am gone
Who's gonna make sure that the light stays on

Goodbye, my painted lady, it's just me
The last of all the eyes that really see
You never aged a day and I grew old
But I'll remember you when nights are cold

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Close my eyes and I'm a child again
Cinnamon and Sunday on the windowpane
Butter on the iron skillet, gospel on the air
And Grandma humming low like she ain't got a care

That smell, that smell
Takes me somewhere I can't buy
Greens and smoke and brown sugar
Got me weeping, don't know why
Ooh, that kitchen, that kitchen
Where love had a recipe and a name
I'd give back every dollar
Just to stand there once again

She never measured nothing, kept it in her hands
A pinch of this, a little faith, the rest she understands
The whole house held its breath until the cornbread came around
And everybody softer once they sat that table down

They sold the house, they took the stove, they painted over time
But the smell still lives behind my ribs, it's stubborn and it's mine
So when the world gets too much salt and not a drop of sweet
I find a kitchen, light a flame, and bring her to her feet

That smell, that smell
Takes me somewhere I can't buy
Greens and smoke and brown sugar
Got me weeping, don't know why

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
The trumpet weeps before it learns to laugh
The parasol is black, then turns to gold
We carry him down Rampart, half and half
A grief that knows the secret of the bold

Dance, you mourners, dance him to the sky
The drum is breaking open, the saints come marching by
We cry into the brass, then let the brass reply
The only way to bury love is teach it how to fly

My heels keep flamenco time against the stone
The second line is clapping like the sea
Two worlds inside one rhythm, blood and bone
He taught me how to live, now he taught me to be free

Duende in the river, duende in the horn
The shadow and the sunlight both were born
I stamp the grief into the burning ground
And lift my arms until the sorrow turns to sound

Dance, you mourners, dance him to the sky
The drum is breaking open, the saints come marching by
We cry into the brass, then let the brass reply
The only way to bury love is teach it how to fly

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The record spins, a quiet, fading sound
Forty years of footsteps worn into the ground
Your hand finds mine like it always knew the way
We don't need the words we used to say

The walls remember every song we played
Every storm we weathered, every promise made
The light is going golden through the door
One more turn across this hardwood floor

So dance with me, slow, beneath the dying light
Hold me like the very first night
The music swells and then it lets us go
This is the last dance, I want you to know

And if the silence comes to take the song away
I'll carry every step until my final day

So dance with me, slow, beneath the dying light
Hold me like the very first night
The music swells and then it lets us go
This is the last dance, I want you to know

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the concrete over Maple Street
Where I learned to ride and felt the summer heat
But there she stands, one oak against the gray
Roots that wouldn't let them haul her away

The diner's gone, the church became a lot
Everything we loved is everything they bought
But every evening when the whistle blows
That old tree's the only green this town still knows

She's the last tree standing in a paved-over town
Arms still reaching when the rest came down
A little piece of yesterday they couldn't tear away
The last tree standing, and she's here to stay

My daddy carved our names there long ago
Now my children climb the limbs I used to know

She's the last tree standing in a paved-over town
Arms still reaching when the rest came down
A little piece of yesterday they couldn't tear away
The last tree standing, and she's here to stay

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
La lluvia cae sobre la avenida
Two strangers caught beneath one falling sky
You held the only shelter in the city
And offered half to a passing stranger by

We walked in step, our shoulders almost touching
The trumpets of the rain played soft and low
I did not know your name, you did not know mine
But neither one of us was quick to go

Ay, bajo un paraguas, dos corazones
Sharing one small circle from the storm
The rain may fall forever on the city
But here beside you, mi amor, I'm warm

The clouds will break, the sun will dry the street
But I will pray for rain so we can meet

Ay, bajo un paraguas, dos corazones
Sharing one small circle from the storm
The rain may fall forever on the city
But here beside you, mi amor, I'm warm

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
The clarinet is crying to the snow
Across the frozen river, soft and slow
My grandfather once told me of a sky
That dances when the winter spirits fly
