And there above the village, green and gold
The heavens spin the stories that he told
The fiddle climbs to meet the burning air
A prayer of light, a wonder hanging there

Oh, dance, dance beneath the northern flame
Where even sorrow learns to sing again
The sky is ours tonight, we are not small
The lights are dancing, dancing for us all

Hey! Lift your glass and stamp the frozen ground
The whole world's spinning to a holy sound

Oh, dance, dance beneath the northern flame
Where even sorrow learns to sing again
The sky is ours tonight, we are not small
The lights are dancing, dancing for us all

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
The hands that built the barn now shake with rain
The oak that never bent is bowed with pain
I watch him from the doorway, growing gray
The mountain of my childhood worn away

He used to lift the world up with his arms
Now he forgets the names of all his farms
The whiskey sits untouched beside his chair
And something in his eyes is no longer there

Oh Father, you are fading like the day
The strongest man I knew is turning gray
I'd carry all your years if that were true
But all I have is time to lose with you

The river takes the mountain, grain by grain
And I am just a shadow in the rain

Oh Father, you are fading like the day
The strongest man I knew is turning gray
I'd carry all your years if that were true
But all I have is time to lose with you

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the trunk at a quarter past three
Sugar-white lightning, the good company
Groove in the engine, a rumble in the night
Headed down the county with the headlights bright

But the sheriff had a tip and a brand new car
Red and blue flashing, didn't get too far
Jugs in the river, smoke on the road
Slippin' and slidin' with a heavy load

Moonshine run gone wrong, wrong, wrong
Gotta keep it movin' to the bottom of the song
Lay it down low, let the bass guitar moan
Moonshine run, ain't makin' it home

Throw your hands up, get down on the floor
The revenue man's a-knockin' at the door

Moonshine run gone wrong, wrong, wrong
Gotta keep it movin' to the bottom of the song
Lay it down low, let the bass guitar moan
Moonshine run, ain't makin' it home

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Finger on the contact, heart up in my throat
Years of words unspoken, every burning note
The phone rang twice, felt a lifetime in between
Then I heard your voice and I was seventeen
All the pride I carried turned to dust and shame
Couldn't even speak, I just whispered out your name
You said, baby, baby, where you been so long
And the dam I built inside just came undone

I'm calling you, Mama, after all this time
Forgive the silence, forgive the pride that's mine
It's been years, too many years, I know
But I'm on the line now, Mama, please don't go

We talked till morning, every wall came down
All the bridges burned were somehow turning round
Life is short and pride don't pay the rent
Should've called you sooner, should've never went

No distance and no anger's worth a single day
So I'm picking up the phone before it slips away

I'm calling you, Mama, after all this time
Forgive the silence, forgive the pride that's mine
It's been years, too many years, I know
But I'm on the line now, Mama, please don't go

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lamplight flickered on the cobbled street
My fiddle and a dream that felt complete
So close I could taste it on the air
The whole world waiting, glowing everywhere

The clarinet was laughing through the door
My name was almost written on the floor
One more step and I'd have touched the sky
But almost is a thin and crooked lie

Oh, the night I almost made it, almost shone
So near the golden city, then alone
Dance away the sorrow, lift it high
Almost is a star that fills the sky

Hey! The fiddle weeps but still the feet must go
For almost is a road, not the end, you know

Oh, the night I almost made it, almost shone
So near the golden city, then alone
Dance away the sorrow, lift it high
Almost is a star that fills the sky

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night the same, the ground falls away
Chains of the waking world cannot make me stay
Wings of fire tearing through the screaming black
I rise above the ashes and I won't look back

The city burns below me like a dying star
I taste the freezing heaven, I have come so far
No gravity can hold me, no cage can contain
The thunder in my chest, the lightning in my brain

I'm flying, flying, breaking through the night
Screaming at the heavens, drowning in the light
This dream that keeps returning sets my soul on fire
I'm flying, flying, ever higher, higher

Then I wake to chains again, the cold and bitter dawn
But every night I rise once more, the dream is never gone

I'm flying, flying, breaking through the night
Screaming at the heavens, drowning in the light
This dream that keeps returning sets my soul on fire
I'm flying, flying, ever higher, higher

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Low light, smoke curling in the room
Laid my last chip down against the gloom
You smiled across the table, oh so cool
And I forgot that I was nobody's fool

One more hand, you whispered, just one more
I bet the things I never bet before
The night went deep, the stakes began to climb
And somewhere in the shuffle I lost track of time

We played too far, baby, played too long
Folded up my heart in a losing song
The cards don't lie but oh, the players do
I went all in and lost it all to you

The dealer never warned me where this game would lead
Now I'm counting up the cost of my own greed

We played too far, baby, played too long
Folded up my heart in a losing song
The cards don't lie but oh, the players do
I went all in and lost it all to you

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
He sits beneath the dying autumn tree
A board of black and white, a five-dollar fee
His coat is thin, his eyes are cold as stone
He's played a thousand kings and won them all alone

They come with money and they leave with none
He never smiles, not even when he's won
The pigeons gather like a jury in the gray
And every soul that sits will lose and walk away

Checkmate, mister, lay your money down
The devil plays for keeps in this old town
He moves the queen like he was moving fate
There ain't no mercy, son, there's only checkmate

They say he lost a war that no one ever saw
Now every game's a sermon and the board's his law

Checkmate, mister, lay your money down
The devil plays for keeps in this old town
He moves the queen like he was moving fate
There ain't no mercy, son, there's only checkmate

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Headlights soft against the empty street
The hum of tires, a slow and steady beat
I didn't mean to take this road tonight
But there's your window, and the pale blue light

Someone else is living where we used to be
A different shadow moving, no longer me
I slow the car but I do not stop
Just let the memory rise and let it drop

Driving past, driving past, in the dark
The ghost of us still glowing like a spark
I won't go in, I'll never knock again
Just drive on by, and let the night begin

The red light turns to green, I let it go
Some doors stay closed so quiet hearts can grow

Driving past, driving past, in the dark
The ghost of us still glowing like a spark
I won't go in, I'll never knock again
Just drive on by, and let the night begin

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete river running down my street
Gray on gray where the old roots used to meet
But there she stands, ooh, in the morning haze
One lonely tree from my childhood days

They built their towers and they paved the green
The quietest beauty that I've ever seen
She drinks the rain through a crack in the stone
The last little wonder this town's ever known

So grow, grow, baby, reach for the sun
The last tree standing when the cutting's done
You're the soul of the city, the breath in the air
Grow, grow, somebody still cares

And if they come to take you, I'll be there
With my arms around your branches, this I swear

So grow, grow, baby, reach for the sun
The last tree standing when the cutting's done
You're the soul of the city, the breath in the air
Grow, grow, somebody still cares

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I walked away from the light one day
Thought I knew a better way
The nights got long, the road got cold
My heart grew tired, my spirit old

But something called me in the rain
A gentle whisper through the pain
I fell down low, then I looked above
And found again the endless love

Oh, I lost it, then I found it, yeah
The fire that I thought was gone
I lost it, then I found it, yeah
Now I'm dancing, I'm dancing on and on

Eh eh, lift your hands up to the sky
Every broken heart can fly
Eh eh, the morning's gonna come
We are healed, we are one

Oh, I lost it, then I found it, yeah
The fire that I thought was gone
I lost it, then I found it, yeah
Now I'm dancing, I'm dancing on and on

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They offered me the gold and the crown
Said change your words and we'll take you downtown
But my pen don't bend for a price
My truth don't fold, I won't roll the dice

They want the sweet, they don't want the real
They want the mask, they don't want how I feel
But every line I write is mine to own
I'd rather walk this road alone

I won't sell out, no no, I won't sell out
My words are free, that's what it's all about
Dance to the truth, let the rhythm shout
I won't sell out, no no, I won't sell out

Eh, money come and money go
But the poet's heart, it has to glow
Eh, sing it loud and sing it proud
The truth will rise above the crowd

I won't sell out, no no, I won't sell out
My words are free, that's what it's all about
Dance to the truth, let the rhythm shout
I won't sell out, no no, I won't sell out

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. and the payphone's froze
Diesel ghost in my truck-stop clothes
I dialed the number I know by heart
Nebraska dark where the dead roads start

You answered slow with a sleeping voice
Said the kids are fine, I had no choice
The rig out back is a coffin of steel
And the miles between us are all I feel

So hold the line, don't hang up yet
The night is long and the highway's wet
I'm a thousand miles from your front door
A ghost in the glass of a liquor store
Hold the line till the sun comes home
So I don't have to ride this dark alone

There's a black dog running the median strip
A hymn on the radio, a coffee sip
I told you lies about coming back soon
I been chasing taillights since last June

If I die on the eastbound lane
Bury me low where you can't see the rain
Keep the porch light burning gold
And tell 'em the trucker just got too old

So hold the line, don't hang up yet
The night is long and the highway's wet
I'm a thousand miles from your front door
A ghost in the glass of a liquor store

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Maleta en la mano, no miro atrás
El pueblo dormido no me da más
La misma esquina, la misma luz
Me llevo el corazón pero suelto la cruz

Dile a mi madre que voy a llamar
Que la ciudad me viene a buscar
Guardo tu foto en el bolsillo azul
Mañana soy humo, ya no estoy aquí, tú

Me voy, me voy, no vuelvo más
El bus de la una me lleva al compás
Adiós barrio, adiós portal
La vida me llama, no me hace mal
Me voy, me voy, búscame en la luz
Del otro lado donde brilla el bus

Dejé las llaves bajo el felpudo
Un beso al perro, al cielo desnudo
Suena el motor como un tambor
Y el pueblo se queda con todo el dolor

No es que te odie, tierra de sal
Es que aquí no cabe lo que quiero lograr
Un día regreso con oro en la voz
Pero hoy me despido, hoy me voy con Dios

Me voy, me voy, no vuelvo más
El bus de la una me lleva al compás
Adiós barrio, adiós portal
La vida me llama, no me hace mal

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody picked us when the season start
Dusty pitch and a borrowed heart
They laughed at the badge on our second-hand shirt
Now ninety minutes between us and the dirt

Oya, run it, run it down the wing
Let the whole stadium hear us sing
Underdog dey come, underdog dey shine
We waited all year for this one time
Oya, run it, run it to the net
This na the night we no go forget

The captain dey pray on the halfway line
Mama dey dance with the whole front nine
Down by one but the clock still talk
Final whistle never stop our walk

Cross am, cross am, jump for the sky
The keeper too small and the ball too high
Net dey shake like a Lagos rain
We carry the cup, we forget the pain

For every door wey close for our face
For every coach wey doubt our pace
We dey here now, we dey rise, we dey fly
Look us for the top, look us touch the sky

Oya, run it, run it down the wing
Let the whole stadium hear us sing
Underdog dey come, underdog dey shine
We waited all year for this one time

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
I loaded the jars in the dead of the night
Daddy's old still and the headlamps off white
The county was sleeping, the river ran low
I told the Lord I had nowhere to go

Have mercy, mercy, on a running man
My hands are shaking with the jar in my hand
I took the wrong road for a little bit of gold
Lord, buy back this sinner before the night gets cold
Have mercy, mercy, let the morning come
Carry me home when the running is done

The blue light came up over Caney Creek hill
I cut through the timber but the engine went still
Glass in the gravel and a prayer on my breath
I bargained with Heaven on the edge of my death

They pulled me from the water, half drowned and ashamed
The deacon was there and he called out my name
He said son, that liquor never carried you free
But the grace in this river just might set you be

No more midnight, no more still
No more racing down that crooked hill
I poured the last jar in the cold black tide
And I let the good Lord take the wheel and ride

Have mercy, mercy, on a running man
My hands are shaking with the jar in my hand
I took the wrong road for a little bit of gold
Lord, buy back this sinner before the night gets cold

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Clippers humming at a quarter to eight
Mr. Earl unlock the same old gate
Forty years of fades on the avenue
He know your daddy and your granddaddy too
Mirror full of stories, walls full of grins
High school photos where the legend begins
You sit in the chair, you don't gotta talk
He already heard the whole block walk

He know the town, every name on the street
Every wedding, every hustle, every heartbeat
Line it up clean, brush it off the cape
Mr. Earl keep the whole neighborhood in shape
He know the town, from the cradle to the grave
Most honest hour that a man ever gave

Kid come in nervous for his first day cut
Earl say sit down, close the door, no buts
Give him game with the grease and the comb
Send him out a little more ready for home
Politician, preacher, the dope boy too
All get the same straight razor, same true blue

When Earl finally close that swinging door
The whole town gonna feel it to the core
Who gonna hold the secrets, hold the lore
Who gonna keep the corner like before

He know the town, every name on the street
Every wedding, every hustle, every heartbeat
Line it up clean, brush it off the cape
Mr. Earl keep the whole neighborhood in shape

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Packed my suitcase with a rusty key
Waved at the harbor, the harbor waved at me
One-way ticket and a borrowed coat
Climbed up the plank of the morning boat

Woah-oh, across the sea
A brand new country waiting for me
Pick up your feet, skank to the rail
Leave the old town in the steamer's tail
Woah-oh, across the sea
Wherever I land I'm gonna be free

Mama she cried at the customs door
Said don't forget where you come from no more
I got the rhythm in my carry-on case
Gonna teach the new world how to find the pace

Salt on my lips and the engine roar
Never gonna see that old shore no more
Horns up high when the seasick fade
I'm building a home out of songs I made

Funny how a goodbye can feel like a start
Got two flags stitched in a single heart
One for the island and one for the new
Both of 'em flying when the sky turns blue

Woah-oh, across the sea
A brand new country waiting for me
Pick up your feet, skank to the rail
Leave the old town in the steamer's tail

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Slow Saturday, the warm light through the blinds
Incense and pomade, an old radio rewinds
Miss Renata working magic with the shears
She been cutting in this chair for thirty years
She remember when you couldn't reach the seat
Now you bring your own son in off the street

She knows the town, mmm, every soul that walks the door
Every heartbreak that you carried in before
She lay it down so gentle, smooth and slow
The truest kind of love this town will ever know
She knows the town, oh, and the town knows her name
Nothing in this neighborhood will ever feel the same

She hum a little tune while she shape you up right
Ask about your mama, ask about last night
No judgment in the mirror, just the honest glow
A confession booth with a steady hand to hold

When the day get heavy and the world won't slow
There's one warm chair where the whole town go
She pour you something easy, let you breathe again
Then she dust you off and call you back, my friend

She knows the town, mmm, every soul that walks the door
Every heartbreak that you carried in before
She lay it down so gentle, smooth and slow
The truest kind of love this town will ever know

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Blue light glowing in a midnight room
Three little words and they spelled my doom
No perfume lingered, no door to slam
Just a quiet buzzing telling me who I am

You ended it all in a paragraph
No final dance, no parting laugh
Seven years folded into gray
And a little gray bubble fading away
You ended it all on a tiny screen
The coldest goodbye that I've ever seen

I poured a brandy and I read it twice
The words were warm but the heart was ice
The saxophone wept from the radio
Singing songs for the ones who let love go

If you'd only called I could hear you break
A trembling voice for an old love's sake
But you typed it neat and you hit the send
And that's how the music had to end

You ended it all in a paragraph
No final dance, no parting laugh
Seven years folded into gray
And a little gray bubble fading away

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Última noche, la luna en sol
Los altavoces escupen calor
Manos arriba, la arena en mi piel
No quiero que acabe, no quiero perder

Una noche más, una noche más
Que no salga el sol, que no se vaya el compás
Baila conmigo hasta el final
Que el mundo se apague pero no este local
Una noche más, dale, no pares ya
Que mañana es lunes pero hoy no llega más

Luces de neón en tu cara y mi voz
Dos desconocidos bailando feroz
La pulsera brilla, se acaba el papel
La última copa sabe a miel

El DJ levanta la mano final
La multitud grita, no quiere el final
Sudor y bocinas, la tierra temblar
Un último beso antes de cerrar

Mañana las carpas se van a caer
El campo vacío al amanecer
Pero guardo esta noche en mi corazón
Como el último drop de la última canción

Una noche más, una noche más
Que no salga el sol, que no se vaya el compás
Baila conmigo hasta el final
Que el mundo se apague pero no este local

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Golden hour on the old porch swing
Boxes by the door and a packed-up thing
Mama in the kitchen humming low
Tomorrow morning is the day I go

Oh, carry me, carry me on
From this little porch where my heart was born
One more sunset, one more song
Then the road gonna call and I'll be gone
Carry me, carry me, Lord, hold my hand
Walk me out to that promised land

We drove to the lake where we always go
Watched the same water, watched it slow
Friends since six on the same dirt lane
Nothing gonna be the same again

Daddy put his hand on my shaking shoulder
Said the world gets wide as you grow older
But a praying family and a hometown sky
Gonna find you again by and by

Thank you for the summers, thank you for the years
Thank you for the laughter, thank you for the tears
I'm scared and I'm ready, I'm leaving and I'm whole
With my mama's God still keeping my soul

Oh, carry me, carry me on
From this little porch where my heart was born
One more sunset, one more song
Then the road gonna call and I'll be gone

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Dim dash light, two a.m. haze
Trunk full of jars in a backwood maze
Static on the AM, crickets in the trees
Rolled the windows down, felt the cold creek breeze
Supposed to be easy, just a quiet little run
Gravel turning slow underneath the gun

Slow down, the night caught up to me
Lights in the mirror where I used to be free
Drip of the leak and the engine cough
Watch the whole thing slowly drift off
Slow down, the morning's getting near
Nothin' left to do but disappear

Hit a soft shoulder and the back wheel slid
Jars start breaking like a secret I hid
Smell of the corn liquor soaking the seat
Siren in the distance keeping perfect beat

Sometimes the easy money got a hidden cost
Sometimes you don't notice till the whole thing's lost
I just sat there breathing in the broken glass
Watching the blue light, letting it pass

Slow down, the night caught up to me
Lights in the mirror where I used to be free
Drip of the leak and the engine cough
Watch the whole thing slowly drift off

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Soft little lights on the warm night air
Guitars going quiet by the river there
The last samba fading into blue
And I'm holding the last of the night with you

Stay a little longer, the morning can wait
Let the lanterns burn low by the garden gate
One more song before the dawn
One more dance before the crowd is gone
Stay a little longer, my love, with me
While the last of the music drifts out to the sea

The vendors are folding their painted stalls
A saxophone sighs down the empty halls
Your head on my shoulder, your eyes half-closed
The sweetest goodbye that the evening knows

Tomorrow the field will be only grass
And the lights and the laughter will softly pass
But tonight is a glass that we sip so slow
Let's drink every drop before we go

Stay a little longer, the morning can wait
Let the lanterns burn low by the garden gate
One more song before the dawn
One more dance before the crowd is gone

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Seventeen and a borrowed guitar
Three wrong chords in a basement bar
My hands were shaking, my voice cracked twice
Four people clapping, that was the price

But I was alive, I was loud, I was free
That broken little stage made a person of me
Sweaty and scared with a fire in my chest
First open mic and I gave it my best
I was alive, I was loud, I was free
That night still burns like it's burning in me

The mic kept feeding back a screaming wail
Forgot my lyrics, watched my whole plan fail
But the kid in the corner with the spiked-up hair
Screamed louder than me like he actually cared

Now I play arenas and the lights are bright
But nothing hits like that first dumb night
No money, no fame, just a beat-up amp
And a dream too big for that tiny lamp

So here's to the losers with a song to scream
Here's to the basements and the three-chord dream
Don't wait till you're ready, you never will be
Just plug it in loud and set it all free

But I was alive, I was loud, I was free
That broken little stage made a person of me
Sweaty and scared with a fire in my chest
First open mic and I gave it my best

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Door slammed shut and the echo dies
Nothing left but the static and lies
My throat is raw from the things I said
Now the silence is screaming inside my head

This quiet is the loudest sound
Two feet apart and we can't be found
I'd take the shouting over this
The cold dead air where there used to be a kiss
This quiet is the loudest sound
Tearing me down, tearing me down

You're in the next room, I can hear you breathe
Both too stubborn and too proud to leave
The words still hang like a broken wire
Smoke in the house but there's no more fire

I don't even remember what it was about
Just the way your eyes went hollow and went out
I'm standing at the door with my hand on the wood
Say something, say anything, God, if I could

This quiet is the loudest sound
Two feet apart and we can't be found
I'd take the shouting over this
The cold dead air where there used to be a kiss

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Radar lit up like a beating heart
Threw my bags in before the dawn could start
She begged me stay but the sky turned green
I'm gone again toward the in-between

Chasing, chasing, can't slow down
Never gonna live in a quiet little town
The wall cloud calling and I follow the roar
I was never built for a closing door
Chasing, chasing into the gray
The storm is the only home I'll ever stay

Mile markers blur and the hail comes down
Funnel touching dirt outside of town
My hands on the wheel and my pulse in my ears
I feel most alive at the edge of my fears

She said you love the wind more than me
And maybe she's right, I was born to be free
But every quiet night in a still warm bed
The thunder keeps rolling around in my head

One day the sky gonna swallow me whole
A price I been paying with my restless soul
But I'd rather burn out in the funnel's eye
Than live my whole life under clear blue sky

Chasing, chasing, can't slow down
Never gonna live in a quiet little town
The wall cloud calling and I follow the roar
I was never built for a closing door

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I been gone for fifteen years, came back to bury my old man
Saw you down at the diner with that coffee in your hand
You said you stayed right here, never had a mind to roam
And Lord, my heart went runnin' for somebody from my hometown

Oh, somebody from my hometown
Knew me before I let myself down
You seen my whole sorry story, every up and every down
And I'm fallin', I'm fallin' for somebody from my hometown

We walked the same dirt road we walked when we were young
You still laugh at the trouble that left these old streets stung
I got a suitcase full of cities, but none of 'em felt like home
Till I sat across the table from somebody from my hometown

Oh, somebody from my hometown
Knew me before I let myself down
You seen my whole sorry story, every up and every down
And I'm fallin', I'm fallin' for somebody from my hometown

Maybe I been searchin' all the wrong dark rooms
For a love I left here breathin' under that same old moon
So pour me one more, baby, tell me I ain't alone
Let me lose myself forever in somebody from my hometown

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Two plates on the counter, but I only need the one
Your recipes in pencil, faded by the morning sun
I measure out the saffron like you taught me in the heat
Now the kitchen holds an echo where you used to stand and eat

For one, my love, for one I learn to cook
Your name still in the margins of the book
The table set for shadows, a candle and a chair
I dance with the silence, your perfume in the air

The oil it spits and stutters like a heart that lost its beat
I salt it like you scolded me, too little or too sweet
The steam rises slow and lonely, a partner in the dark
And I lead it through the doorway, every step a missing spark

For one, my love, for one I learn to cook
Your name still in the margins of the book
The table set for shadows, a candle and a chair
I dance with the silence, your perfume in the air

They say grief is just love that has nowhere left to pour
So I pour it in the saucepan and I taste it on the floor
One portion, one fork, one glass of bitter wine
I cook for one, my darling, but I'll always set out mine

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine breathing low, two seconds on the clock
Four bodies in the back, I can hear the safety lock
This is the last one, I swear it on the road
Then I disappear with everything I'm owed

Foot down, lights out, we don't slow
Last job, last run, here we go
Sirens in the rearview, bleeding red and blue
One more mile of midnight and I'm done with you

They said it would be clean, they said it would be quick
Now the cargo's screaming and the steering's gone slick
I take the river bridge, I take the broken line
Every turn I'm carving is a stolen piece of time

Foot down, lights out, we don't slow
Last job, last run, here we go
Sirens in the rearview, bleeding red and blue
One more mile of midnight and I'm done with you

There's a girl who waits, there's a door I'll never knock
There's a tank near empty and a city full of cops
If I make the harbor, if the dawn don't catch me first
I'll bury all these engines and I'll lift this driver's curse

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Neon on the wallpaper, cinnamon and chrome
A cassette of her humming in the rooms I used to roam
Flour on the counter glowing soft electric blue
Every memory a frequency that leads me back to you

The smell of her kitchen, a synth in the dark
Butter and lightning, a pilot-light spark
I drive through the static, I follow the steam
Grandmother's kitchen on the edge of a dream

VHS of Sundays, the oven's amber haze
Her apron pixelated in the warm cathode blaze
Gingerbread and ozone, a tape rewound too far
I'm chasing down a feeling in a long-abandoned car

The smell of her kitchen, a synth in the dark
Butter and lightning, a pilot-light spark
I drive through the static, I follow the steam
Grandmother's kitchen on the edge of a dream

The city's all forgotten, the highways gone to rust
But a single bowl of sugar can resurrect the dust
So play the tape one more time, let the warm distortion swell
And take me to her kitchen by the magic of a smell

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Volví al barrio donde aprendí a caminar
Y te vi en la esquina, no te puedo olvidar
Me conoces de antes, de los días sin plan
Desde el mismo bloque, desde el mismo lugar

Yo me enamoré de mi gente, de mi pueblo, de ti
La misma calle donde corría, ahora corre por mí
Dale, baby, dale, que tú eres de aquí
Me enamoré del barrio cuando me enamoré de ti

Mismo sol, misma plaza, mismo ritmo y sudor
Tú bailabas conmigo cuando no había reloj
Me fui pa' la ciudad pero el corazón se quedó
En la esquina contigo, en el mismo rincón

Yo me enamoré de mi gente, de mi pueblo, de ti
La misma calle donde corría, ahora corre por mí
Dale, baby, dale, que tú eres de aquí
Me enamoré del barrio cuando me enamoré de ti

No necesito mapa para llegar a tu piel
Tú eres mi código postal, mi calle, mi cartel
Vente conmigo lento, que la noche es de los dos
Del mismo pueblo vinimos, al mismo cielo vamos

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up on a corner where the streetlights barely shone
Mama said baby someday you gonna find your own zone
I shined my only shoes and I straightened up my tie
Caught the uptown rhythm and I kissed that block goodbye

I'm swingin', I'm swingin' out the door
Ain't gonna live on a dead end no more
Brass section blowin' like a ticket on a train
Made it out the alley and I'm dancin' in the rain

They said you'll never leave here, son, this block is all you'll know
But my feet had other plans and the band began to blow
I two-stepped past the corner store, I jitterbugged the line
And the city opened up for me, oh honey it was mine

I'm swingin', I'm swingin' out the door
Ain't gonna live on a dead end no more
Brass section blowin' like a ticket on a train
Made it out the alley and I'm dancin' in the rain

I'll send a little something to the friends I left behind
Tell 'em there's a doorway if they're willing just to climb
The trumpet hits the high note and the whole sky starts to rock
'Cause a kid with happy feet just made it off a dead-end block

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Tinted up, engine cold, sittin' low in the night
Three minutes on the meter, keep it out of the light
Last run, last bag, then I'm ghost from the scene
New name, new coast, wipe the slate of the machine

Last job, foot on the gas, I'm gone
Rearview full of red, I been runnin' too long
Drop the load, hit the road, never lookin' back
This the last time you ever gonna see this Cadillac

They came out heavy, sirens screamin' down the strip
Grip the leather, hold the line, I don't ever let it slip
Dodge the spikes, kill the lights, take the tunnel underground
Count the seconds in my chest like the bass in this sound

Last job, foot on the gas, I'm gone
Rearview full of red, I been runnin' too long
Drop the load, hit the road, never lookin' back
This the last time you ever gonna see this Cadillac

Got a daughter I ain't seen since she learned how to crawl
This the money that I need so I can answer when she calls
If I make it past the bridge, if the dawn don't drop the net
I'll park this life forever, pay the only honest debt

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I wrote it all down on a napkin in a bar
Everything I hated, every nasty little scar
Then the phone went off at 3 a.m. and somebody said you died
Now I'm stuck here with a list and nobody on the other side

I forgive you, you're gone, too late, so what
Screaming at a headstone with my stupid heart shut
I let it go, I let it go, but you can't even hear
Forgiving someone gone is just shouting at the air

I practiced all the speeches, every line I'd throw your way
Now they rot inside my throat, I got nobody left to say
The anger's got no target and the silence got no end
So I'll bury the grenade and call a ghost my friend

I forgive you, you're gone, too late, so what
Screaming at a headstone with my stupid heart shut
I let it go, I let it go, but you can't even hear
Forgiving someone gone is just shouting at the air

Maybe forgiveness was never even meant for you
Maybe it's the only way I drag my own self through
So I'll tear the napkin up and let the pieces hit the floor
And I won't carry your name like a weight no more

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Antenna on the rooftop, wired to a beating heart
We broadcast from the shadows where the silence comes apart
A hum beneath the city, a frequency they fear
We send it through the static for the ones who need to hear

We are the signal they can't shut down
A ghost in the wires above the town
Turn your dial to the edge of the night
We burn through the dark with a borrowed light

Three of us and a transmitter held together with tape
Every song a small rebellion, every chord a way to escape
They triangulate the towers, they come kicking down the door
But the music's already flying where they cannot reach it anymore

We are the signal they can't shut down
A ghost in the wires above the town
Turn your dial to the edge of the night
We burn through the dark with a borrowed light

Let it swell, let it rise, let the feedback climb the walls
Let it crack the quiet open till the whole sky calls
We were never meant to whisper, we were never meant to wait
So we rise into the airwaves and we levitate

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the tundra, the sky begins to breathe
A ribbon of green velvet that the cold can't quite believe
A muted trumpet shivers where the horizon used to be
And the colors start to swing across an endless frozen sea

Northern lights, take it slow, brush the snow
A blue note in the heavens, let the soft glow grow
Dance for me, aurora, in your shimmer and your sway
You improvise the silence into colors I can't say

A walking bass of glaciers, a piano made of frost
The melody keeps moving like a wanderer who's lost
Violet bends to emerald, a chord no hand can play
And the whole cold world is listening till the night dissolves to day

Northern lights, take it slow, brush the snow
A blue note in the heavens, let the soft glow grow
Dance for me, aurora, in your shimmer and your sway
You improvise the silence into colors I can't say

There's no sheet music written for a sky that comes alive
Just the rhythm of the cosmos and the way the embers thrive
So I'll sit here on the snowbank with my collar turned up high
And let the universe play standards on the canvas of the sky

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raise my hand, mic check, but the room don't pause
Mid-sentence, somebody else just steamrolls my cause
Got the data on the slide, got the plan in my chest
But my voice hit a wall while they applaud the rest
Same idea I dropped, ten minutes, now it's gold
When it came outta his mouth, suddenly bold
I'm takin' notes on the patience that they never extend
Got a folder full of points that don't make it to the end

Talked over, talked over, every single time
My sentence cut to pieces while they finish the line
But I'm patient, I'm a vault, I keep the receipts clean
One day they gonna hear me when I run the whole scene

Interrupted on the call, muted by design
They 'circle back' to nothing, then they cross the line
But I'm buildin' in the quiet, I'm a slow-burn flame
Writing down the credit they keep stealin' from my name
Next quarter, next room, watch the table turn
Gonna speak so clear they got no choice but to learn

Talked over, talked over, every single time
My sentence cut to pieces while they finish the line
But I'm patient, I'm a vault, I keep the receipts clean
One day they gonna hear me when I run the whole scene

Give me three seconds, that's all that I ask
A full breath of silence to finish the task
'Cause the loudest in the room ain't the smartest in the fight
And the one they kept ignorin' gonna own the spotlight

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up the trunk with the jars from Cooper's still
Back roads after midnight, headlights off on Mercer Hill
Danny said he knew a shortcut, swore the law was fast asleep
But the gravel turned to water and the ditch was running deep

Oh the moonshine's in the river and the engine's in the mud
We traded all our luck tonight for a taste of something good
Run, run, the whole thing's gone wrong
We'll be laughing about this if we live to sing the song

Blue lights in the treeline, Danny took off on his feet
I hid behind the gas station, heart bangin' like a drum beat
Lost the whole month's money in a creek that ran too high
And the smell of spilled corn liquor hung like smoke against the sky

Oh the moonshine's in the river and the engine's in the mud
We traded all our luck tonight for a taste of something good
Run, run, the whole thing's gone wrong
We'll be laughing about this if we live to sing the song

Maybe we were stupid, maybe young's the same as dumb
Maybe Cooper's gonna kill us for the cash that never come
But the stars were spinning crazy and the night felt half alive
And I'd do it all again just for the feeling of survive

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Sun going down on the avenue slow
Tomorrow I'm packing, tomorrow I go
We dey for the rooftop, the speaker still loud
One more golden hour with my whole little crowd

Last day of summer, hold me, don't let go
Dance one more time before the morning show
We go remember this, oya feel the glow
Last day of summer before I gotta grow

Jollof on the table, laughter in the air
My brother braiding stories, sunlight in her hair
We took the same shortcut a thousand times before
Tomorrow it's a memory I'm walking out the door

Last day of summer, hold me, don't let go
Dance one more time before the morning show
We go remember this, oya feel the glow
Last day of summer before I gotta grow

No cry for me, my people, we go link again
When the holidays come calling we go meet at the bend
But tonight make we move, make the bassline carry me
Last dance of the summer, set my young heart free

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The church bell rings for nobody, the fields are turning grey
I packed my daddy's pistol and the debts I couldn't pay
The crows are on the power line, they watch me leave my door
This dust has had my blood too long, it won't have any more

I'm leaving for good, leaving for good
Burning the crossroads, the chapel, the wood
The devil can have it, the dirt and the stone
I'm taking the long road and I'm taking it alone

My mother's in the cemetery, my name's upon the wall
This town's a hungry shadow and it wants to watch me fall
I seen the way it eats its own, I heard the freight train cry
So I'll follow that black ribbon till the day I'm meant to die

I'm leaving for good, leaving for good
Burning the crossroads, the chapel, the wood
The devil can have it, the dirt and the stone
I'm taking the long road and I'm taking it alone

Don't send no preacher after me, don't light no candle flame
The man who's walking out tonight won't answer to that name
There's nothing here but rust and ghosts and one long dying sun
And I'd rather meet the darkness than stay here and come undone

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Keys in her hand and the morning's looking bright
She's gripping that wheel like she's holding on for life
Mirror, signal, breathe now, ease it off the brake
My little girl's a driver for goodness sake

Pick it up, slow it down, watch the road
One foot on the future, kid, you got the load
Horns up, top down, here we go
Teaching my daughter everything I know

She stalled it on the hillside, laughed until she cried
I'm clutching the dashboard but I'm beaming with pride
Green means go, baby, red means stay
And love means I'm nervous in the passenger way

Pick it up, slow it down, watch the road
One foot on the future, kid, you got the load
Horns up, top down, here we go
Teaching my daughter everything I know

Soon enough you'll drive away to places I can't see
And I'll be in the driveway just a speck in your memory
So take the wheel and take the world, but take it nice and slow
There's a whole brass band inside me every time I let you go

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Clippers humming, six a.m., the shutter rolls up loud
Mirror fogged with yesterday, a fresh and waiting crowd
He knows your father's haircut and the year your mother left
Keeps the secrets in the talc, the gossip in his breath
Every fade a confession, every trim a quiet booth
The chair becomes a courtroom and the barber holds the truth

Buzz it down, low to the ground, the bassline shakes the floor
He's cut the mayor and the thief and everybody more
Faster, faster, blade and chatter, scissors snap the beat
The whole town walks out lighter, leaves their weight beneath the seat

He lent the kid a twenty when the kid was too ashamed
Remembers when the corner shop still had another name
The broken still come crawling for a reason to feel new
He never charges extra for the listening he'll do

Roll the rhythm, snare on snare, the city's pulse runs through
A thousand heads of stories that he combs and shapes anew
When they finally close his doors the avenue will mourn
The one man who could read the town the moment they were born

Buzz it down, low to the ground, the bassline shakes the floor
He's cut the mayor and the thief and everybody more
Faster, faster, blade and chatter, scissors snap the beat
The whole town walks out lighter, leaves their weight beneath the seat

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent hum at midnight, the hallway smells of bleach
My phone's lit up with photos of a table I can't reach
They're carving up the turkey while I'm charting room nine's pain
My coffee's gone to ashes, but I pour another cup again

Second shift, same scrubs, holding somebody's whole world
While the fireworks bloom outside for some other family's girl
I'll trade my holiday for the breath beneath my hands
Somebody's gotta stay when the rest of it disbands

Room four's been alone all year, no visitor, no call
So I sit and hold her wrinkled hand against the empty wall
She says I have my mother's eyes, I let her think it's true
There's a kind of love that only shows at 2 a.m. for you

My feet have stopped complaining, they went numb three hours ago
The monitor's a lullaby, slow green and steady glow
I'll miss the pie, I'll miss the kids, I'll miss the front-door kiss
But nobody dies alone tonight, and I can live with this

Second shift, same scrubs, holding somebody's whole world
While the fireworks bloom outside for some other family's girl
I'll trade my holiday for the breath beneath my hands
Somebody's gotta stay when the rest of it disbands

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
The candle wax has memorized the shape of all my prayers
I knelt here as a boy beneath the saints upon the stairs
The organ knows my grandmother, the pews recall my name
But something in the rafters doesn't answer me the same

So I'll slip out through the side door while the evening hymn still rings
No letter and no quarrel, just the closing of some things
The steeple keeps its shadow, the bells will toll the same
And I'll carry what was holy in a quieter, plainer name

The stone is cold and certain, it has held a thousand years
It held my christened forehead and it caught my mother's tears
I loved the way the morning came through glass in colored rain
But a window isn't doorway and I cannot kneel in vain

Don't think I leave in anger, there's no thunder in my chest
Just a tide that turned in silence and a soul that needs to rest
The river doesn't argue with the bank it leaves behind
It only knows the ocean is a wider thing to find

So I'll slip out through the side door while the evening hymn still rings
No letter and no quarrel, just the closing of some things
The steeple keeps its shadow, the bells will toll the same
And I'll carry what was holy in a quieter, plainer name

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I memorized the ceiling back when I was counting tiles
Learned to fold my hands so small and practice all the smiles
The songs are stuck inside me like a tattoo on my tongue
But I don't believe the ending of the story I was sung

So I'm walking out so quiet that the doors don't even creak
Not a scene, not a sermon, just a goodbye I won't speak
I loved you like a childhood that I'm finally letting grow
I'm not slamming any windows, I just thought you'd want to know

My mother's in the second row, she doesn't see me leave
I'll text her in the morning all the things I can't believe
The parking lot is empty and the sky is finally mine
The first breath out the doorway feels like crossing some thin line

It isn't that I hate it, it's that I outgrew the size
Like a coat that fit at twelve but now it's pinching at my eyes
I can keep the parts that loved me, leave the parts that made me hide
There's a whole and open evening and it's waiting just outside

So I'm walking out so quiet that the doors don't even creak
Not a scene, not a sermon, just a goodbye I won't speak
I loved you like a childhood that I'm finally letting grow
I'm not slamming any windows, I just thought you'd want to know

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
We started just for nickels and the laughter on the porch
By midnight it was rent money and somebody lit a torch
The whiskey did the talking and the deck began to lie
I watched my best friend's jawline turn to something I'd deny

Oh, we played it too far, way past the line
Lost more than money, lost what was mine
The cards don't remember the faces they break
But I'll carry that table for the rest of my days

He pushed in his grandfather's watch, I shoulda told him no
But greed's a quiet preacher and I let the river flow
The ace fell like a verdict and the room went cold and still
There's a silence after winning that no winning ever fills

I gave the watch back twice and twice he wouldn't take my hand
Some debts ain't paid in dollars, some lines you cross can't stand
The porch is empty now and the deck's still in the drawer
Fifty-two reminders of a friend I don't see anymore

Oh, we played it too far, way past the line
Lost more than money, lost what was mine
The cards don't remember the faces they break
But I'll carry that table for the rest of my days

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
The engine breathes like a bull behind the gate
My hands are wet but my eyes are stone
Three minutes counted in the throat of fate
The city sleeps and I drive alone

¡Una vez más! one turn of the key
The last of the nights that will set me free
The guitars are crying down every street
My heart keeps time with the gunshot beat

She begged me to leave it, to plant something green
To trade the asphalt for a quiet name
I swore this would be the last engine I'd clean
One more dance with the headlight flame

The doors fly open, the bags hit the floor
Foot to the metal, the alley screams
But a child crosses slow where I gun for the door
And I see in his face the end of my dreams

¡Una vez más! but the wheel turns cold
I braked for a boy and I broke the hold
The sirens are roses that bloom in red
My last job ended where the guitar bled

I sit in the silence with my hands raised high
The last engine ticking as it dies
She was right all along, and I watch the sky
Trade the speed of the world for her eyes

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the sawmill when the rooster starts to crow
Clock out and tie my apron where the diner lights still glow
My hands have got the splinters and my feet have got the burns
And the dollar I'm chasing is a dollar that never returns

Two jobs, no rest, and the bills keep on climbing the wall
Run twice as fast just to watch myself fall
The banjo rolls quicker but the ground gives no more
I'm working myself thin and I'm still keeping poor

The young'uns need their shoes and the truck's got a cough
The landlord don't care how many hours I clocked off
I sold my daddy's fiddle just to make it through July
And I played it one last time with a fellow's watered eye

They tell me pull the bootstraps, son, the harder that you toil
But you can't grow a fortune in another rich man's soil
Still I'll wake before the morning and I'll do it all again
For the ones who call me daddy and the hope of who-knows-when

Two jobs, no rest, and the bills keep on climbing the wall
Run twice as fast just to watch myself fall
The banjo rolls quicker but the ground gives no more
I'm working myself thin and I'm still keeping poor

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They came with their gold for the words on my tongue
Wanted my fire in a bottle to sell
To soften the verses, to sweeten the sung
To trade my cathedral for a coin in a well

¡No, no vendo! my voice is not for hire
The truth in my throat is a stubborn fire
Let the palms keep their rhythm, let the heel strike the wood
I'd starve with my poems before I'd be good

The café is empty, the landlord is loud
My pockets are silent, my coat's getting thin
But the verse that I bleed for won't bow to a crowd
That only wants echoes of where they have been

They'll forget all the singers who sang what was paid
The smooth and the pretty, the easy, the bought
But deep in the alley where the real ones are made
The guitar keeps the name of the man who would not

¡No, no vendo! my voice is not for hire
The truth in my throat is a stubborn fire
Let the palms keep their rhythm, let the heel strike the wood
I'd starve with my poems before I'd be good

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Knuckles like the roots of an ancient burning oak
Scarred from every winter and the gospel that she spoke
They pulled a husband from the war and buried two of three
They kneaded bread from nothing while the whole world tried to flee

These hands! they built a fortress out of bone and stubborn will
These hands! they held the line when every other army fell still
Calloused, cracked, and crowned with the wars she never named
A cathedral made of fingers and an iron, holy flame

They slapped the boys for lying, then they stitched their wounds with thread
They carried generations on the strength they never said
A map of all her labor carved in rivers on her skin
The softest thing she ever touched was the grandchild she'd take in

Now they fold so slow and quiet on a quilt she'll never finish
But the grip that raised an empire will never once diminish
When they lower her to silence and the chapel bells run cold
I'll feel those hands upon my back, unbreakable and old

These hands! they built a fortress out of bone and stubborn will
These hands! they held the line when every other army fell still
Calloused, cracked, and crowned with the wars she never named
A cathedral made of fingers and an iron, holy flame

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
La sirena last screamed at the end of the line
The gates that fed the barrio are shutting for all time
Mi papá gave thirty years to the grease and the steel
Now the parking lot is empty and the silence is real

Dale, the lights go dark on the block tonight
The machines stopped breathing and we lost the fight
But we move, we move, we won't go down
The heartbeat keeps living when they close the town

The corner store is hurting, the cantina's half-asleep
They shipped the jobs to nowhere for a dollar they could keep
But the kids still bounce the rhythm where the trucks used to roll
You can lock up all the buildings, you can't padlock the soul

Señor in the suit, you don't know what you took
The lunches, the weddings, the years that it shook
But listen to the bassline rising up from the ground
We were here before the factory and we're here when it's down

Dale, the lights go dark on the block tonight
The machines stopped breathing and we lost the fight
But we move, we move, we won't go down
The heartbeat keeps living when they close the town

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Sun comin' up over the harbor town
Engine idle low, brim pull down
Them inside doin' what them came to do
Me just a wheel and a heart beatin' through

One more run, then me done, me swear
Lay down the keys, breathe the morning air
Jah know me tired of the runnin' life
One last drive, then me home to me wife

Me used to think the speed was a kind of free
Now the rearview only show what's chasin' me
Every green light feel like a borrowed grace
And me cyaan outrun the lines upon me face

The bag in the back, it heavy like sin
Me look at the road and the road look in
So me ease off the gas where the river run wide
Leave the money on the seat, let the morning decide

One more run, then me done, me swear
Lay down the keys, breathe the morning air
Jah know me tired of the runnin' life
That last drive, it carry me home to me wife

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The rooms repeat my footsteps now that everything is gone
The walls wear pale rectangles where the photographs once shone
The tape screams as I pull it, the loudest sound all day
One box left in the middle of a life I'm folding away

¡La última caja! I press it down tight
Sealing the years in a square of cardboard and light
The heel of my shoe on the bare wooden floor
Echo of a home that won't be home anymore

The kitchen knew my mother and the doorframe knows my height
The pencil marks of childhood climbing slowly out of sight
I run my palm along the wall like touching someone's face
A goodbye to the geometry of one beloved place

I could leave the marks, I could scrub the wall to white
But some other family's morning needs the room to be light
So I tape the final corner and I lift it to my chest
And the empty house exhales like it's finally at rest

¡La última caja! I press it down tight
Sealing the years in a square of cardboard and light
The heel of my shoe on the bare wooden floor
Echo of a home that won't be home anymore

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Saturday morning, empty lot, the sun just learning to rise
Her hands at ten and two o'clock, the worry inside her eyes
I say breathe, my girl, the road don't bite, just ease it off the brake
The whole world's gonna open up for every turn you take

Go slow, go slow, but go, my darling, go
I'm beside you in the passenger, but the wheel is yours to know
Green light, your light, the future's down the street
I taught you how to walk and now I'm teaching you to keep the beat

She stalls it twice and laughs it off, the engine coughs and dies
Then something clicks, she rolls it smooth, surprise across her eyes
I see her mother in the mirror and her grandmother's strong jaw
Three generations rolling forward, steady, sure, and raw

One day this seat'll be empty and she'll drive off on her own
To a city, to a love, to a life I've never known
So I'll teach her every signal, every yield and every merge
Then pray the roads be gentle when she follows that urge

Go slow, go slow, but go, my darling, go
I'm beside you in the passenger, but the wheel is yours to know
Green light, your light, the future's down the street
I taught you how to walk and now I'm teaching you to keep the beat

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Last train platform, neon on the rain
You turned around once and I froze in the lane
The doors slid closed on a word I never said
Now you live in the four a.m. inside my head

The one that got away, away, away
Still dancing in the strobe where I let you stay
Every beat drops you back into the room
The one that got away in the purple gloom

I see your shadow when the bassline swells
In every stranger's perfume, every story it tells
We had a summer that we never let begin
A door left open to a room I won't go in

Lift your hands, let the kick drum erase
The almost and the maybe and the look on your face
But when the lights come up and the floor turns gray
You're the silence, you're the one that got away

The one that got away, away, away
Still dancing in the strobe where I let you stay
Every beat drops you back into the room
The one that got away in the purple gloom

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The bar was half a hundred folks, the stage was made of crates
I tuned my daddy's old guitar against the smell of cheap and great
A man in back with a city card said son, I like your sound
I played like heaven opened up and angels gathered round

That was the night I almost made it, almost touched the gold
The spotlight on my shaking hands, the whole world mine to hold
Then the rain came down on the interstate and the city man drove away
And I went back to the morning shift, but I had that one good day

He said he'd call on Monday, I waited by the phone
Till Monday turned to autumn and the autumn left me alone
But I still hear that barroom roar when the night gets soft and old
For a minute I was lightning, for a minute I was bold

My boy asked did I ever dream of something more than here
I poured a little coffee and I wiped away a tear
I said the dream's not in the making it, it's standing in the light
And letting it pour through you for one electric night

That was the night I almost made it, almost touched the gold
The spotlight on my shaking hands, the whole world mine to hold
Then the rain came down on the interstate and the city man drove away
And I went back to the morning shift, but I had that one good day

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
We stood on opposite ends of the hall
Two names carved deep in the same old wall
The fiddle struck up and the floor pulled us near
My hand in your hand, my old enemy dear

Years of bad blood in the turn of a heel
Nobody asked us how we'd come to feel
The piper kept time like he didn't care
Who hated who underneath the candle's glare

So dance with me, dance with me, here in the round
Let the old grudges fall down to the ground
Your eyes are still fire but your steps are still true
And God help me now, I am dancing with you

Three turns of the reel and the anger ran thin
Somewhere between the out and the in
The village all watched as the music grew wild
Two bitter rivals were dancing and smiled

Maybe it's whiskey, maybe it's fate
Maybe the river forgave its own weight
The song doesn't ask who was wrong, who was right
It only asks who you will hold through the night

So dance with me, dance with me, here in the round
Let the old grudges fall down to the ground
Your eyes are still fire but your steps are still true
And God help me now, I am dancing with you

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Concrete cradle, broken glass crown
They said this corner's where you'd lay down
Same four walls and the same dead light
Grinding my teeth through another long night

I'm getting out, I'm getting out
Scream it loud, I'm getting out
This block don't own me, this block don't win
I'm breaking the chain that they locked me in

Mama prayed hard at the kitchen sink
Father just gave me a reason to think
Every siren a lullaby here
I turned my rage into something like fear into gear

I'm getting out, I'm getting out
Scream it loud, I'm getting out
This block don't own me, this block don't win
I'm breaking the chain that they locked me in

You can bury the seed but it claws for the sun
You can cage up the kid but the kid's gonna run
Every scar on my back is a map to the door
I ain't dying on the floor of this dead-end no more

I'm getting out, I'm getting out
Scream it loud, I'm getting out
This block don't own me, this block don't win
Never coming back to where I've been

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
It started friendly with a six-pack and a deck
Just me and old Earl and a hundred-dollar check
The ceiling fan turning over smoke in the air
Nobody figured we'd lose more than we'd dare

Oh the cards kept falling and the stakes kept climbing
The whiskey kept pouring and the clock stopped timing
We bet the truck, then we bet the farm
A friendly little game that did somebody harm

Earl put his wedding ring down on the felt
I saw the whole story in the hand he was dealt
I should've folded, I should've walked away
But pride's an awful thing on a losing day

Now the sun's coming up on an empty chair
Earl drove off mad and he left it all there
A deck of cards and a debt I can't pay
Some games you win and still throw it away

Oh the cards kept falling and the stakes kept climbing
The whiskey kept pouring and the clock stopped timing
We bet the truck, then we bet the farm
A friendly little game that did somebody harm

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The rooms all echo now, so strange and wide
The afternoon is gold where the couch used to hide
I run my hand along the empty wall
This is the last one, this is all

Tape it up slow, let the light go soft
Everything we were is packed and lofted
Close the lid on the years we made
I'm folding up the home before it fades

There's a mark on the floor where the plant once stood
A ghost of a life that was almost good
The dust drifts down like a quiet snow
It's time, it's time, but I don't want to go

Maybe somewhere keeps the shape of us
In the hum of the wires, in the settling dust
A box can't hold what a heart still knows
But I'll seal it anyway and let it close

Tape it up slow, let the light go soft
Everything we were is packed and lofted
Close the lid on the years we made
I'm folding up the home before it fades

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Rain on the window, beat on the phone
A half-finished verse and I'm sitting alone
That night the crowd almost knew my name
So close to the fire, so close to the flame

Almost, almost, the lights came up
Almost held the whole thing in my cup
Then the morning came and washed it gray
The night I almost made it slipped away

They said come back when you got a bigger sound
Packed up my dreams in a duffel on the ground
Bus ticket home with a head full of maybe
Still hum that hook like it could save me

It ain't a sad song, it's a quiet one
A candle still burning when the show is done
Almost is a seed that you bury and wait
Maybe next time I don't almost, I make it

Almost, almost, the lights came up
Almost held the whole thing in my cup
Then the morning came and washed it gray
The night I almost made it slipped away

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
We carried him down through the Tremé heat
A hundred slow shoes on the cobblestone street
The trombone cried and the bass drum sighed
We weep on the way but we dance on the side

Oh when the saints go marching slow
We send him off where the spirits go
Then pick it up, let the brass run wild
Grieve like a man, then dance like a child

Umbrellas spinning, handkerchiefs high
We laugh through the tears and we wave goodbye
The second line snaking through the Quarter's gold
Death came knocking but the joy got bold

You don't bury a soul, you set it free
Let the tuba teach you how mourning can be
A celebration, a holy parade
For every cent of love that the dear man paid

Oh when the saints go marching slow
We send him off where the spirits go
Then pick it up, let the brass run wild
Grieve like a man, then dance like a child

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The field is breathing, slow and low
Ten thousand candles starting to go
The last bass note dissolves in the dark
We are the embers of a fading spark

Hold this night, hold it gently
Let it blur, let it empty
We were here, we were light
Don't let go of the last night

Strangers asleep on the trampled grass
Morning is coming and it will not pass us by
The speakers hum a final prayer
Dust and stars in the cooling air

Somewhere a tent flap moves in the wind
This is the end, this is the end, my friend
But the silence is full, the silence is kind
We leave a little of ourselves behind

Hold this night, hold it gently
Let it blur, let it empty
We were here, we were light
Don't let go of the last night

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The water tower's got my whole name carved
The diner where I worked and the church where I starved
for something bigger than these two-lane roads
Now my suitcase is full and the bus engine glows

Goodbye little town, goodbye
I gave you my childhood, now I gotta fly
You raised me up but you held me down
I'm leaving you for good, my little town

Mama's on the porch and she's trying not to cry
Daddy just nods, he was always that kind of guy
Every face on Main Street knew me by heart
That's the sweetest reason and the hardest part

I ain't running from you, I'm running toward
A door I can't see but I can't ignore
Maybe I'll come back when my hands are full
But tonight the highway's got a stronger pull

Goodbye little town, goodbye
I gave you my childhood, now I gotta fly
You raised me up but you held me down
I'm leaving you for good, my little town

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I drove out to the hillside where they laid you to rest
With twenty years of anger still hard in my chest
The wind through the cedars and the mockingbird's song
I came here to forgive you but I came here too long

So I lay it down, lay it down by the stone
All the years I carried it walking alone
You can't hear me say it but I'll say it anyhow
I forgive you, father, I forgive you now

The banjo of the morning rolls over the field
There's a peace in the letting and a strength in the yield
No letter can reach you, no phone line, no door
Just a man and a grave and a debt that's no more

They say the dead can't change but the living surely can
And I'm tired of being half of an unforgiving man
The grudge was a stone I was carrying for two
Now I'm setting it down here on the grass over you

So I lay it down, lay it down by the stone
All the years I carried it walking alone
You can't hear me say it but I'll say it anyhow
I forgive you, father, I forgive you now

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Midnight, low beams on a familiar street
I told myself I wouldn't but here come my feet
on the gas, taking that long way around
Past the window where I used to be found

Driving past your place again
The light's still gold like it was back then
Different curtains, different name
But my foolish heart still drives the same

Third floor corner where we burned the night
Now somebody else is reading by that light
I slow it down to ten, then I let it ride
With all of my history locked up inside

I'm not trying to knock, I'm not trying to stay
Just trying to feel it one more time and drive away
Memory's a city I can't leave behind
So I circle the block in my own troubled mind

Driving past your place again
The light's still gold like it was back then
Different curtains, different name
But my foolish heart still drives the same

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Doppler on the dash, sky turning green
Fastest thing on the highway you ever seen
They want me to slow down, buy a house, settle in
But I'm married to the funnel and the howling wind

I chase the storm, I chase the storm
Where the sky split open and the clouds get torn
Can't love nobody when I'm gone with the rain
I'm a storm chaser, I can't stay, I can't stay

She left a note on the fridge, said come home soon
I was already gone by the light of the moon
Grey wall rising up over Kansas wheat
My heart only beats when the thunder repeats

Maybe I'm running, maybe I'm called
Maybe I'm scared of four quiet walls
The calmest place is the eye of it all
That's the only stillness I ever recall

I chase the storm, I chase the storm
Where the sky split open and the clouds get torn
Can't love nobody when I'm gone with the rain
I'm a storm chaser, I can't stay, I can't stay

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I packed up my mother's old Bible and shawl
Left the only green hills that I knew at all
The harbor was crying, the ship gave a moan
Lord, I am crossing this water alone

Carry me over, carry me through
Great rolling ocean, I'm trusting in You
A new land is waiting on the far other shore
Lord, carry me over, I'm Yours evermore

The salt and the seasick, the cold steerage night
A hundred souls praying for a glimpse of the light
We sang the old hymns till the dawn broke the gray
And faith was the only thing keeping the fear at bay

What lies on the far side I cannot yet see
But the God of my fathers is the God of the sea
He walked on the water, He calmed the wild foam
And He'll walk me on into a new kind of home

Carry me over, carry me through
Great rolling ocean, I'm trusting in You
A new land is waiting on the far other shore
Lord, carry me over, I'm Yours evermore

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Neon flicker on the stairwell wall
Footsteps racing down the concrete hall
They built these towers like they built a cage
But I got fire and I got rage

Run, run, faster than the fall
Run til the block is a memory small
Break through the dark where the bassline flows
I'm out, I'm out, and I never look back, here I go

Seventeen and I drew up a plan
Get myself gone any way that I can
Every streetlight a countdown clock
Every beat a hammer on the dead-end lock

The city is breathing, the night is alive
I'm not just surviving, I'm gonna arrive
Let the tempo carry what my legs can't hold
A kid from the bottom turning grey into gold

Run, run, faster than the fall
Run til the block is a memory small
Break through the dark where the bassline flows
I'm out, I'm out, and I never look back, here I go

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
The kettle still whistles the same as before
But there's one cup now and not two anymore
She knew where the salt was, she knew the right heat
I'm burning the porridge she'd make sweet

One plate, one chair, one candle lit low
Learning the things that she used to know
Forty-one years she fed me with grace
Now I set one place, I set one place

Her handwriting fades on the recipe card
Boiling an egg shouldn't be this hard
I talk to the kitchen, I talk to the air
And somehow I feel that she's still standing there

They say grief is a soup that you stir very slow
A little less bitter the longer you go
So I'll chop the onions and I'll let them sting
It's the closest thing to her that the evening brings

One plate, one chair, one candle lit low
Learning the things that she used to know
Forty-one years she fed me with grace
Now I set one place, I set one place

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five bucks a game on the worn stone table
Talk all you want if you think you're able
Clock slamming down like a fist on the wood
I been out here since the world misunderstood

Move, make your move, the clock is alive
Only the sharpest are gonna survive
King in the corner, knight on the run
I'll take your money before the day's done

Suits come through thinking they're so refined
I trap their queen and I poison their mind
The park is my office, the board is my fist
Every pawn is a soldier on a list

They see an old man on a fold-out chair
They don't see the war I am running there
Thirty years deep in the lines I have known
The street made me ruthless, the game made me stone

Move, make your move, the clock is alive
Only the sharpest are gonna survive
King in the corner, knight on the run
I'll take your money before the day's done

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I sold the mule and burned the deed
And kissed the dirt that wouldn't feed
The preacher said the Lord goes too
But the Lord don't book no steerage crew

My mother's hair is in a locket
A cold black coin sleeps in my pocket
The gangway groans, the gulls all scream
I'm waking up inside a dream

So carry me over, dark water carry me
Bury my old name beneath the sea
There's a new world hungry and it don't know me
Lord, carry me over, carry me

The waves come up like open graves
And every soul down here's a slave
To some far shore we've never seen
A promise dressed in borrowed green

So carry me over, dark water carry me
Bury my old name beneath the sea
There's a new world hungry and it don't know me
Lord, carry me over, carry me

If I should drown, don't mark the spot
Let the salt forget what the land forgot
But if I land, I'll plant a tree
Where no man owns the man or me

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Clock hits seven, lights stay loud
Fireworks crack outside the crowd
Third floor humming, monitors blink
No time to breathe, no time to think
Coffee gone cold on the windowsill
Somebody coding down the hall, run

Double shift, double time
Holding the night on the front line
Everyone's home, everyone's gone
But the heartbeat needs me, so I run on
Double shift, double time
Give me the pulse, I'll keep it alive

Room four-oh-two won't make it home
I hold a hand that's not my own
The city's dancing, glasses raised
I'm counting drops through the IV haze

Double shift, double time
Holding the night on the front line
Everyone's home, everyone's gone
But the heartbeat needs me, so I run on

Let the bass drop low, let the morning come slow
When the sun comes up I'll finally let go
Sixteen hours, two cups of light
I gave the holiday back its night

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew for the very last time
The conveyor stopped on the assembly line
We punched the clock, we punched our pride
Forty years on the factory side

But tonight we dance on the killing floor
Gonna shake these walls one evening more
Turn the work lights into a disco glow
If they're taking it down, then let it go, let it go

Manager crying by the loading dock
Somebody jammed a quarter in the clock
The boombox spinning where the engines died
We sweat together one more ride

So tonight we dance on the killing floor
Gonna shake these walls one evening more
Turn the work lights into a disco glow
If they're taking it down, then let it go, let it go

The pension's gone but the rhythm's free
This floor remembers you and me
They can sell the steel, they can sell the name
But they'll never own the way we came

So tonight we dance on the killing floor
Gonna shake these walls one evening more
Turn the work lights into a disco glow
If they're taking it down, then let it go, let it go

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Close my eyes, I'm back on Grandma's floor
Cinnamon ghost slipping under the door
Pot been simmering since before the sun
She stir it slow, tell me everything come
Flour on her apron, gold on her hand
Feed the whole block, never asked for a brand

That smell, that smell, take me home
No matter how far I roam
Grandma's kitchen, holy smoke
Everything she ever spoke
That smell, that smell, take me home

Now the house is sold, the stove went cold
But I caught the recipe before she got old
Same brown sugar, same iron pan
Try to bring her back the only way I can

That smell, that smell, take me home
No matter how far I roam
Grandma's kitchen, holy smoke
Everything she ever spoke
That smell, that smell, take me home

Steam on the window, photo on the wall
I swear I hear her humming down the hall
Bless the food, bless the years
Grandma cooked away my fears

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
The screen lit up the ceiling blue
Three gray dots, then nothing new
I read it once, I read it twice
The words went soft, the words went ice

And the whole room hums like a dial tone
I'm a ghost in a hallway, glowing alone
You ended us in a thumbprint's space
No voice, no door, no face

I typed a paragraph and let it drown
Deleted every letter down
What is there left for me to say
To a little bubble fading gray

And the whole room hums like a dial tone
I'm a ghost in a hallway, glowing alone
You ended us in a thumbprint's space
No voice, no door, no face

Feedback swells, the reverb cries
I hold the phone against my eyes
Seven words and then you're gone
The notification carries on

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Soft on the back of a giant we sleep
Streets like a blanket, gentle and deep
He dreams of the ocean, he dreams of the rain
We pour our coffee and forget his name

So hush, little city, sway and stay
Don't wake the mountain beneath the bay
Dance very softly, love very slow
On the chest of a giant, breathing below

Lovers on balconies, lights in a line
His heartbeat keeps the trolleys in time
The fountains, the markets, the lazy guitars
All rocking gently under the stars

So hush, little city, sway and stay
Don't wake the mountain beneath the bay
Dance very softly, love very slow
On the chest of a giant, breathing below

One day he'll stretch and the towers will fall
But tonight he is dreaming, so dream for us all
Kiss me, my darling, before the dawn
While the giant goes on sleeping on

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I folded the country into my chest
A paper boat where the swallows nest
The harbor lights turned into stars
And everything I knew got far

Over the water, into the white
I'm a feather lost in the endless night
Goodbye, goodbye, to the shore I knew
The whole world's blue, the whole world's new

My language melts on the salty air
I wear my mother like underwear
Close to the skin where no one sees
The small warm hum of the people I please

Over the water, into the white
I'm a feather lost in the endless night
Goodbye, goodbye, to the shore I knew
The whole world's blue, the whole world's new

Will they have a word for the way I miss
