A street, a song, my grandmother's kiss
The sea keeps humming, soft and wide
I'll learn to love the other side

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up where the streetlight flickered out
Sirens for a lullaby, that's what it's about
Mama working doubles, daddy out of frame
Every corner whispering my name

But I'm gone, baby, gone
Got the groove and I'm moving on
Dead-end block in the rearview now
Watch me get up, watch me get out, ow!

Saved every dollar in a coffee can
Learned to play bass, became a man
They said you'll never leave, you're stuck like glue
But the bassline told me what to do

Now I'm gone, baby, gone
Got the groove and I'm moving on
Dead-end block in the rearview now
Watch me get up, watch me get out, ow!

I ain't forget where the pavement cracked
Gonna send it all back, gonna bring it back
For every kid on a busted stoop
Get up off the ground, find your group, woo!

I'm gone, baby, gone
Got the groove and I'm moving on
Dead-end block in the rearview now
Watch me get up, watch me get out, ow!

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Measuring rice for two out of habit again
Scrape half back in the jar, sigh, count to ten
Her apron still hangs on the kitchen door
I don't move it, I just stare at the floor

Cooking for one, low flame, slow
Learning the things that she used to know
One plate, one fork, one quiet chair
I set the table like she's still there

She knew the salt by the flick of her wrist
A pinch, a taste, a list I never kissed
Now I burn the onions and I let it sting
So I got a reason for the tears they bring

Cooking for one, low flame, slow
Learning the things that she used to know
One plate, one fork, one quiet chair
I set the table like she's still there

Leftovers Tuesday, leftovers Wednesday too
Forty-one years, I never had to
But I'm learning her recipes, line by line
Keeping her warm in this kitchen of mine

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Block so dead they ain't fix the lights
Boarded windows, gunshot nights
I was sketching dreams on the back of a test
While they sold the corner, I stayed obsessed
Mama said baby don't end up a name
On a candle and a t-shirt, photograph frame

I made it out, I made it out
Whole hood watching what I'm about
Dead end turned to a runway now
Look at me, look at me, made it out

First one in my family with a key to a door
That ain't got somebody's name on the floor
They doubted me, now they quoting me
Put the whole zip code on my hoodie, see

I made it out, I made it out
Whole hood watching what I'm about
Dead end turned to a runway now
Look at me, look at me, made it out

But a part of me's still on that curb
Nineteen, hungry, biting my words
I go back, I don't flex, I build
Empty lot, gonna get it filled

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The water tower wears our name in rust
The gravel road is breathing dust
I loaded everything I own
Into a car that hums alone

The diner sign still flickers slow
The places that I'll never know
Are waiting somewhere past the line
Where this town ends and starts the wide

So let it go, let it rise
The whole horizon's in my eyes
I'm not coming back this time
The quiet's mine, the quiet's mine

My father waves but doesn't speak
The railroad's silent for a week
The fields go gold then turn to gray
And everything I am gives way

So let it go, let it rise
The whole horizon's in my eyes
I'm not coming back this time
The quiet's mine, the quiet's mine

And the sky cracks open, white and vast
The town I was becomes the past
I drive into the swelling sound
The ground gives way, the ground gives way

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone in my hand like a stone, like a knife
Three years of nothing carved out of my life
I dial the numbers I never forgot
Each ring is a wound, each ring is a knot

Madre, it's me, it's your blood on the line
Forgive the silence, forgive the time
My pride built a wall I could not climb
Madre, it's me, it's your child, it's mine

The pause when she answers, the catch in her breath
We danced around love till it looked like death
But her voice is a guitar I have always known
Every string of it calling me home

Madre, it's me, it's your blood on the line
Forgive the silence, forgive the time
My pride built a wall I could not climb
Madre, it's me, it's your child, it's mine

Clap your hands, let the heels strike the ground
We wasted the years, but the years can be found
She's crying, I'm crying, the line crackles warm
Three years burn off in the eye of the storm

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
Ooh, she walks by every day
(Sha-la-la) and looks the other way
I got a heart that's beating loud
But she's a star up in the crowd

Why won't you see me, darling, see me
(Ooh-wah, ooh-wah)
I'd give the moon if you would be with me
(Ooh-wah, ooh-wah)
But you don't know my name at all
While I'm the fool against the wall

I shine my shoes and comb my hair
(Sha-la-la) like maybe you would care
I practice all the words to say
Then watch them melt and float away

Why won't you see me, darling, see me
(Ooh-wah, ooh-wah)
I'd give the moon if you would be with me
(Ooh-wah, ooh-wah)
But you don't know my name at all
While I'm the fool against the wall

Maybe someday, maybe somehow
(Bom-bom-bom)
But it ain't gonna be right now
So I'll just love you from afar
My unrequited shooting star

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Midnight glow on a screen so bright
Fingers hover in the neon night
I know the rules, I know the cost
One little message and I'm lost

But I'm typing it anyway
Neon burning the dark away
Someone I shouldn't, someone I crave
Driving too fast on a one-way wave
Typing it anyway

The city pulses pink and blue
Every streetlight points to you
Delete, rewrite, delete again
Then press the button, count to ten

But I'm typing it anyway
Neon burning the dark away
Someone I shouldn't, someone I crave
Driving too fast on a one-way wave
Typing it anyway

Three dots blinking back at me
A future I'm not supposed to see
The synth swells up, the heartbeat climbs
I'm crossing a line, I'm crossing a line

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The roll of tape screams down the seam
The empty room begins to gleam
With light it never had before
When there was furniture and more

And I press the cardboard flat and slow
The last of everything I know
Sealed in a box, a label, a name
Nothing in here will feel the same

A nail hole where a picture hung
A hundred mornings, stairs I'd run
The echo's louder than it was
The silence hums, the silence buzzes

And I press the cardboard flat and slow
The last of everything I know
Sealed in a box, a label, a name
Nothing in here will feel the same

I stand here in the swelling haze
The reverb of forgotten days
Then turn the key and let it drown
The whole life folded, taped, and down

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night the ceiling melts away
A window opens where the walls once stayed
My shoulders lift before I understand
The floor lets go and lets me leave the land

Up, and the city is a quiet sea of light
Up, and I'm breathing in the soft of the night
Nothing holds me, nothing tells me down
I am the hush above the sleeping town

The air is water, slow and warm and blue
I lean and turn the way the swallows do
No destination, only open air
The dream forgets that gravity was there

Up, and the city is a quiet sea of light
Up, and I'm breathing in the soft of the night
Nothing holds me, nothing tells me down
I am the hush above the sleeping town

Then morning hums and pulls me by the spine
The sky folds shut along a fading line
But something in my chest still knows the way
And waits all day to fly again someday

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Brass against the iron sky
A second line to say goodbye
Drums like thunder split the street
Bone and bourbon, drag your feet
The coffin sways on shoulders proud
A dirge to drown the weeping crowd

March us down to the river of the dead
Let the trumpets scream what the preacher said
We bury slow but we rise in fire
Dance on the grave, climb the funeral pyre

Tombs above the flooded ground
The living shake the saints around
Umbrellas black then handkerchief white
Grief by day and joy by night

March us down to the river of the dead
Let the trumpets scream what the preacher said
We bury slow but we rise in fire
Dance on the grave, climb the funeral pyre

When the dirge turns into a roar
The dead don't sleep here anymore
They strut the cobbles, swing the brass
And laugh at every hour that passed

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Well I close my eyes and the bed lets go
I'm tippin' my hat to the world below
The rooftops wave and the streetlamps grin
Come on up, baby, the air's drawin' me in

I'm flyin', flyin', light as a tune
Dancin' a two-step around the moon
Every night I take to the sky
And I never once learned how or why

The clouds are a ballroom, the stars are the band
I'm cuttin' a rug where there's no place to stand
Gravity sat this one out tonight
So I'm spinnin' my partner in pale blue light

I'm flyin', flyin', light as a tune
Dancin' a two-step around the moon
Every night I take to the sky
And I never once learned how or why

Then the rooster crows and the dream comes down
My two good shoes hit the cold hard ground
But I'm grinnin' all day with a secret to keep
There's a whole open sky on the far side of sleep

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded it at midnight, no one signed the log
Iron crates and a smell like rot beneath the fog
The captain wouldn't look us in the eye
We set our course beneath a bruised and silent sky

And the sea keeps the secret that we carry in the hold
Deeper than the water, older than the cold
We are sailing, we are sailing, but we'll never reach the shore
Something in the cargo wants the sea forevermore

The compass spins, the lanterns dim to blue
The men who touched the crates are down to two
The waves go still the way the dead go still
And something hums against our every will

Throw it over, throw it over, someone cried into the dark
But the ropes had grown like roots around the heart of the ship's hull
And the ocean opened slow

And the sea keeps the secret that we carry in the hold
Deeper than the water, older than the cold
We are sailing, we are sailing, but we'll never reach the shore
Something in the cargo wants the sea forevermore

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Empty parking lot, eight a.m. on Sunday
White knuckles on the wheel, I'm sweating in the gun lane
You stalled it out three times, you're laughing, I'm not
Get your foot off the gas, kid, this ain't a parking lot— oh wait it is

Go, go, go, just don't hit the curb
Go, go, go, you're doing better than I deserve
My little wrecking ball, my reckless shooting star
God help the neighborhood, my daughter's got a car

Signal left, you turned the wipers on
The radio's too loud and now the mirror's gone
But your eyes are on the road and your hands have got the feel
Someday soon you're gone, baby, gripping your own wheel

Go, go, go, just don't hit the curb
Go, go, go, you're doing better than I deserve
My little wrecking ball, my reckless shooting star
God help the neighborhood, my daughter's got a car

One day I'll hand the keys and watch you drive away
With everything I taught you and the rest you'll learn the hard way
So grind the gears and floor it, leave a little rubber too
The whole dumb beautiful road is waiting up ahead for you

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Phone lit up at a quarter to two
Three little dots, then a paragraph from you
No phone call, baby, no knock on the door
Just a buzz in my pocket and we ain't us no more

You broke my heart in a text, oh
Didn't even use your voice, oh
Left me read at 2 a.m.
Now I'm dancin' through it 'cause it's all I've got
Groovin' on the wreckage of a love gone cold

I typed and I deleted, I typed it again
What do you say to the screen of an ex-best-friend
The cursor kept on blinkin' like it knew the truth
Ain't no emoji big enough to hold the proof

You broke my heart in a text, oh
Didn't even use your voice, oh
Left me read at 2 a.m.
Now I'm dancin' through it 'cause it's all I've got
Groovin' on the wreckage of a love gone cold

So I put the phone face down and I turn the bassline up
Let the one-drop heal the part of me you interrupt
You can keep your little message, keep your blue and gray
My hips already know how to move the hurt away

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Las campanas que sonaron en mi niñez
still ring in a語 I no longer speak
I knelt on these tiles when my knees were small
Now I stand in the doorway, my back to the wall

Adiós, adiós, to the saints in their gold
Adiós to the hymns that my mother still holds
I'm slipping out quiet while the candles still burn
No trumpet, no thunder, no promise to return

The Virgin still watches from her painted blue veil
The incense, the velvet, the bell and the rail
My faith got too heavy to carry alone
So I leave it here gently and walk out on my own

Adiós, adiós, to the saints in their gold
Adiós to the hymns that my mother still holds
I'm slipping out quiet while the candles still burn
No trumpet, no thunder, no promise to return

No guardo rencor, solo cierro la puerta
The God of my grandmother stays where she prayed
And maybe out there in the wide morning air
I'll find my own way to be holy and bare

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
The clarinet is weeping low
The way I wept three weeks ago
You didn't come, you didn't call
A little glowing screen said all

Oy, the message came at night
Gray bubble in the candlelight
We danced at weddings, hand in hand
Now it ends with a rubber band of words I don't understand

The fiddle laughs to keep from tears
We loved across a thousand years
And all of it could not outlast
The little buzz, the words you cast

Oy, the message came at night
Gray bubble in the candlelight
We danced at weddings, hand in hand
Now it ends with a rubber band of words I don't understand

So play it faster, spin me round
Let grief become a joyful sound
If love must die, then let it die with violins
And stamping feet and where the next dance begins

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle in the window, light stays on past nine
Mr. Okonkwo making everybody shine
From the corner-store hustle to the church on the hill
If you steppin' out clean, then you stepped through his bill
Chalk on the fabric, tape around the neck
Turns a hand-me-down suit into a sign of respect
Wedding, funeral, graduation gown
He's the thread holding up the whole downtown

He dress the block, head to toe
Every stitch tells the story that the people already know
From the cuff to the crown, he the man you gotta know
Dress the whole block, watch the neighborhood glow

Kid came in nervous for his first job day
Walked out lookin' like he owned the runway
No charge for the widow, no charge for the broke
"Pay me when you can," that's the gospel he spoke
Forty years pressing creases into pride
The whole avenue walking with their heads held high

He dress the block, head to toe
Every stitch tells the story that the people already know
From the cuff to the crown, he the man you gotta know
Dress the whole block, watch the neighborhood glow

When he finally hang the scissors and he lock the door
The block gon' wear his name like the suits he made before
'Cause you can't measure a man by the money in his hand
Measure him in the people he taught how to stand

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
One suitcase and a folded photograph
The harbor lights are fading at my back
A borrowed coat, a name they'll mispronounce
And everything I was, I leave for an ounce of maybe

Across the sea, across the sea
Where the man I'll be is waiting there for me
Salt on the railing, salt in my eyes
I'm trading every certain thing for a stranger's sky

The deck is cold, the engines hum a blues
A hundred souls all running from the news
We share a language only in our sighs
And every face is somebody's goodbye

Across the sea, across the sea
Where the man I'll be is waiting there for me
Salt on the railing, salt in my eyes
I'm trading every certain thing for a stranger's sky

Mama, I'll write you when I find a street with my new name on the door
I'll send back what I can and the rest I'll keep ashore
In the marrow, in the dark, in the part of me that's yours
The horn cries low and the whole boat leans toward shore

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Stage lights hot and the crowd was wide
Manager said the label's gonna be inside
We hit that pocket, we were tighter than a drum
Felt the whole room shakin' like the future finally come

That was the night, the night we almost made it
So close to the dream we could taste it, we could shake it
One more break, one more yes away
So close to forever on a Saturday

They shook our hands and they said they'd call
We drank to the ceiling, we owned it all
But the phone stayed quiet through the cold of June
And the band drifted off like a borrowed tune

That was the night, the night we almost made it
So close to the dream we could taste it, we could shake it
One more break, one more yes away
So close to forever on a Saturday

But I'd take that almost over never any day
The way the bassline lifted me, the way the crowd would sway
'Cause for one shining hour underneath those colored lights
We were the baddest band alive, and we owned the night

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The roll of tape screams one long final sound
The last brown box upon the empty ground
The echo of the room repeats my name
Four walls that watched us, never quite the same

Tape it up, tape it up, seal the years inside
The apartment of our love where we laughed and where we lied
One last look across the floor where we used to dance so slow
Now it's just a square of dust and a door I have to close

The nail still holds the ghost of where you hung
The photograph, the days when we were young
I peel my key from off the silver ring
And hand it to the silence like a wedding band, a sting

Tape it up, tape it up, seal the years inside
The apartment of our love where we laughed and where we lied
One last look across the floor where we used to dance so slow
Now it's just a square of dust and a door I have to close

The taxi's running, the meter's ticking down
I carry all my life in cardboard through this town
And somewhere in box number nine I taped your laugh away
To open in another room on some forgiving day

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Close my eyes on the dancefloor
And I'm seven years old at her kitchen door
Butter and cinnamon, Sunday rain
The whole world humming through the windowpane

Take me back, take me back
To the smell of her kitchen, the warm and the sweet
Take me back, take me back
Where love was a pot on a low slow heat

Flour on her apron, sugar on the floor
A hum of a song I don't know anymore
The kettle, the spices, the bread on the rise
The whole of my childhood alive in my eyes

Take me back, take me back
To the smell of her kitchen, the warm and the sweet
Take me back, take me back
Where love was a pot on a low slow heat

Feel the bassline like her hands upon my face
Every beat's a memory I'm trying to retrace
She's been gone for years now but she's here when the music's deep
In the smell of her kitchen that I carry in my sleep

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Mile marker eighty on a road with no name
The coffee's gone cold and the radio's just rain
I pull to the shoulder and I dial up the line
Three a.m. in Kansas, two a.m. your time

Hey baby, it's me, I know it's late
I just wanted to hear you before the morning freight
Kiss the kids for me, tell 'em Daddy's halfway home
A thousand miles of dark and a dollar on the phone

The diesel's still tickin', the big rig's gone quiet
There's a moth on the windshield and a star to fly it
You whisper so soft so the little ones don't wake
And I hold that whisper for the long road's sake

Hey baby, it's me, I know it's late
I just wanted to hear you before the morning freight
Kiss the kids for me, tell 'em Daddy's halfway home
A thousand miles of dark and a dollar on the phone

Three more days and a sunrise and I'll roll into the drive
With a truck full of nothin' but the will to stay alive
Don't wait up, but leave the porch light burnin' low
It's the brightest thing for a hundred miles in all this dark I go

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the gray over everything green
Glass and concrete where the gardens had been
But down on the corner where the old church stood
There's one stubborn oak still remembering wood

Last tree standing in a paved-over town
Roots in the asphalt, refusing to lie down
Throw your shade on me, baby, let me breathe a while
Last green thing for a concrete mile

The developers measured, the city signed the deed
But the birds still come back to the one place they need
A grandmother planted it in nineteen-fifty-two
Now it's holding up the sky for the whole avenue

Last tree standing in a paved-over town
Roots in the asphalt, refusing to lie down
Throw your shade on me, baby, let me breathe a while
Last green thing for a concrete mile

So I'll chain myself beside you when the bulldozers come
We'll be two old soldiers and we won't be overcome
'Cause a city needs a heartbeat, a city needs a lung
And you're the only song the morning still gets sung

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
We came in with patched-up shoes and a borrowed coach's whistle
Mud on every jersey, knees as gray as winter thistle
The town said save your bus fare, save your breath, go home
But the fiddle in my chest kept playing all alone

First whistle and the favorites grin like wolves behind a fence
We trip, we fall, we laugh, we rise, our pride our last defense
Clarinet of the heartbeat climbing higher with the score
A goal, a gasp, the bleachers shake and beg us back for more

Oh play it loud, the violin, and stamp the frozen ground
We are the patched, the laughed-at, the last ones to be found
One final game, one final dance, let the cymbal crash and ring
Even if we lose tonight, by heaven we will sing

Clock is bleeding seconds, accordion holds its breath
We push the ball like pushing back the cold gray hand of death
The favorite's keeper stumbles on a root grown through the field
And everything we never had is what we won't now yield

Dance, dance, you hopeless ones, your grandmothers are watching
Every shtetl ghost is up and clapping, no one's stopping
Win or lose the trumpet weeps the same triumphant tune
We were the underdogs who howled back at the moon

Oh play it loud, the violin, and stamp the frozen ground
We are the patched, the laughed-at, the last ones to be found
Whistle blows, we're tied, we're tired, but God we're still alive
The underdogs don't die tonight, the underdogs survive

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raise my hand, the room goes on
My sentence cracks, then it is gone
Glass walls and coffee going cold
My good ideas left on hold

He says my point in a deeper voice
They nod like he just made a choice
I typed it first inside the chat
Nobody saw, imagine that

Let me finish, let me finish
Turn the volume up on me
Let me finish, let me finish
My voice is the frequency
Four to the floor, I take the floor
I'm not waiting anymore

The mute light blinks, a tiny sun
I unmute slow, the talk's begun
I'll say it once, I'll say it loud
I'll be the kick drum in this crowd

Clap, clap, hear the room go quiet
Clap, clap, this is the riot
No more sorry, no more please
I move the air, I move the keys

Let me finish, let me finish
Turn the volume up on me
Let me finish, let me finish
My voice is the frequency
Four to the floor, I take the floor
I'm not waiting anymore

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I drove out to the county where they laid your stubborn bones
The grass had grown past reading, and the marble swallowed stones
I brought the words I sharpened over twenty years of nights
And set them down beside you in the thin November light

You never said you're sorry, and I waited like a fool
Kept the anger like a lantern, kept it burning low and cruel
But a lantern's heavy lifting when there's no one left to blame
So I let the wick go quiet and I whispered out your name

I forgive you, though you'll never hear me say it
I forgive you, laid the old debt down to rest
There's no hand to shake on it, no door I get to walk through
Just the wind across the wheat and a weight off of my chest

Funny how the hating kept you living in my head
I was sweeping out a house where only memory still bled
Now I'm leaving you the silence that you left for me to keep
And I'm taking back the morning, and I'm finally gonna sleep

Maybe up there, maybe nowhere, maybe just inside my mind
But I needed it spoken, and I couldn't leave it behind

I forgive you, though the rain has worn your letters
I forgive you, every crooked thing you said
There's no hand to shake on it, no door I get to walk through
Just the wind across the wheat and the peace of going home

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five dollars on the table and a smile like broken glass
He lets you take his bishop just to watch the moment pass
He's been here since the pigeons, gold tooth catching morning sun
Kings have fallen on this concrete, every game already won

Clock is ticking, slap it down
He owns this corner, owns this town

Checkmate, baby, count your money twice
He plays the fool and then he plays you nice
Four moves ahead while you're still saying hi
King of the park with the danger in his eye
Checkmate, baby, you should've walked away
Nobody beats him, not today

Tourists think they've got him when he fumbles with his queen
Then the knights come out of nowhere like a trap you've never seen
He talks the whole time, laughing, telling stories of his youth
While he's quietly dismantling every plan you thought was truth

Clock is ticking, slap it down
He owns this corner, owns this town

Checkmate, baby, count your money twice
He plays the fool and then he plays you nice
Four moves ahead while you're still saying hi
King of the park with the danger in his eye

Thirty years of winters, he could play it in his sleep
The board is just an ocean and he taught himself the deep

Checkmate, baby, count your money twice
He plays the fool and then he plays you nice
Four moves ahead while you're still saying hi
King of the park with the danger in his eye

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Door still shaking from the slam, now the silence got a sound
Phone face-down on the counter, both of us too proud to turn around
Heard your footsteps in the hallway, then they stopped behind the wall
We said too much, we said too loud, now we ain't saying nothing at all

TV's playing to nobody, blue light on an empty chair
I can hear you breathing slow, I know you know that I'm still there
Counting cracks up in the ceiling, counting reasons I was wrong
The quiet hit me harder than the screaming all along

It's too quiet, it's too quiet
Wish you'd yell so I could find you
It's too quiet, it's too quiet
Let me say I'm right behind you
No more thunder, just the after
Miss the noise, I miss your laughter
It's too quiet, it's too quiet
Come back through the door

I rehearse the words to fix it, then I swallow every one
Pride is heavy in the dark when all the shouting's done
Your light flicks off across the hall, but you don't fall asleep
And neither one of us crosses the inches that we keep

One text, three dots, then nothing
I start to type, then stop
The loudest thing in this whole house
Is the silence that won't drop

It's too quiet, it's too quiet
Wish you'd yell so I could find you
It's too quiet, it's too quiet
Let me say I'm right behind you

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Gold afternoon dissolving on the squares of black and white
He moves a pawn like moving smoke, dissolving into light
The pigeons blur, the city hums a low and distant tide
His fingers ghost above the queen, the whole park holds inside

I sat down for a minute, I forgot to leave again
The clock became a heartbeat and the world went soft and dim
He whispers something kind and cruel, the trees lean in to hear
A bishop slides through golden air and disappears

Fall into the board, the board, falling slow
Watching all my soldiers learn to go
He is just a shadow in the sun
And I have already lost before I begun
Drifting, drifting, checkmate like a dream
Nothing is ever quite the way it seems

Five dollars and an afternoon, a small and gentle theft
He takes my king so tenderly there's nothing to regret
The haze comes down like velvet on the players in the green
The most beautiful losing I have ever seen

Who is winning, who is dreaming
Half the moves were never there
Just the glimmer and the leaning
And his laughter in the air

Fall into the board, the board, falling slow
Watching all my soldiers learn to go
He is just a shadow in the sun
And I have already lost before I begun

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up at midnight from a man who wouldn't speak
Wooden crates all nailed and silent, hold them low beneath the deck
The captain took the silver, said don't open, just deliver
But something started knocking 'fore we'd even crossed the river

Lay down, lay down, cursed and heavy cargo
We should've never carried what we carry on this run
Lay down, lay down, the river's turning over
And we won't see the harbor 'fore it's done, before it's done

First the dog went missing, then the cook lost half his mind
He swore he heard his mother in a tongue he'd left behind
The banjo wouldn't tune itself, the fiddle wept off-key
And the water 'round the hull turned black as anything could be

Lay down, lay down, cursed and heavy cargo
We should've never carried what we carry on this run
Lay down, lay down, the river's turning over
And we won't see the harbor 'fore it's done, before it's done

The captain pried a crate apart, we never saw inside
But his hair went white as cotton and that night the first man died
We rowed toward any shoreline, but the shore kept moving back
And the moon hung like a lantern over everything we lack

Nail it shut and say a prayer, don't look and don't ask why
Some freight is just a debt you pay in others' lives
Throw it overboard, boys, let the deep dark have its share
But the knocking's in the timber now, the knocking's everywhere

Lay down, lay down, cursed and heavy cargo
We should've never carried what we carry on this run
Lay down, lay down, the river's turning over
And we won't see the harbor 'fore it's done, before it's done

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Reb Mendel's got a window full of buttons made of bone
He stitches up the morning before the sun is fully grown
The baker comes in flour, leaves in linen pressed and proud
The shoemaker, the rabbi, every soul in this whole crowd

Dance in your new jacket, turn around and let me see
The whole block wears the magic of his needle running free
Ai-ai-ai, the thread don't break, the seam will never tear
Reb Mendel cuts the cloth of God and stitches us with care

A bride comes in on Tuesday with a dress her mother wore
He lets the seams out gently, makes it more than it was before
He never charges widows, and he always finds the chalk
The man who can't afford a thing leaves dressed to walk the walk

His fingers learned in hunger in a country far away
The stitches hold the stories that he never has to say
A pin between his teeth, a fiddle playing down the street
He hems the trembling trousers of the poor man on his feet

When they bury old Reb Mendel, what a suit the man will wear
The finest cloth he ever cut, his own two hands laid bare
And every back upon this block will stand a little tall
The tailor who, with thread and love, once dressed us one and all

Dance in your new jacket, turn around and let me see
The whole block wears the magic of his needle running free
Ai-ai-ai, the thread don't break, the seam will never tear
Reb Mendel cuts the cloth of God and stitches us with care

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Thumb above the number that I memorized as a child
Four years in the silence, four years running wild
The ringtone like a heartbeat, like a door I never knocked
Three rings and then her voice, and everything unlocked

Ma, it's me, it's me, don't hang up the line
I've been gone, I've been wrong, give me one more time

Pick up, pick up, I'm calling you tonight
Across the years, across the static, into the light
Pick up, pick up, I never stopped, I swear
Let the silence break wide open, Mama, are you there

She says my name so quiet that it nearly breaks the sound
Four years she kept the porch light on, four years I wasn't around
The pride that built the wall between us crumbles into dust
A dial tone became a bridge, a leap of brand-new trust

Ma, it's me, it's me, I'm coming home
No more lost, no more cost, no more alone

Pick up, pick up, I'm calling you tonight
Across the years, across the static, into the light
Pick up, pick up, I never stopped, I swear
Let the silence break wide open, Mama, are you there

She's crying, I'm crying, the line is full of rain
Four years melt to nothing in the sound of my own name
Raise your hands, the wall is falling, dance until the dawn
The call I should have made before, finally going on

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up where the streetlight flickered like it couldn't make up its mind
Mama working doubles, daddy's dreams we left behind
Same ten faces on the corner, same dog barking at the moon
Everybody said you'd stay here, said you'd never leave so soon

I taped my boots together, kept a notebook in my coat
Wrote down every reason that I'd someday hit the road
The block, it had a gravity, it pulled on everyone
But I kept my eyes on the highway like a runner on the gun

I made it out, I made it out
Past the chain-link and the doubt
Got dust on my old shoes but I'm finally on the route
I made it out, I made it out
Waved goodbye to the dead-end, found the road I dreamed about

I still see it in my mirror, getting smaller down the line
The porch, the lot, the liquor store, the friends I left behind
I carry every one of them inside my beating chest
I didn't leave to lose them, I just left to find the rest

For the ones still on that corner, I'll leave a light on too
Making it out ain't forgetting, it's just finding something new
And if you're standing where I stood, with the whole world saying no
There's a road out past the dead-end, and I'm proof that you can go

I made it out, I made it out
Past the chain-link and the doubt
Got dust on my old shoes but I'm finally on the route
I made it out, I made it out
Waved goodbye to the dead-end, found the road I dreamed about

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Lluvia on the avenue, the sky came down tonight
One broken umbrella underneath a buzzing light
She stepped under without asking, shoulder close to mine
Two strangers, one small circle, in the falling line

No sé tu nombre, but I know your eyes
The rain keeps falling, baby, no goodbyes

Bajo la lluvia, just you and me
One umbrella, where we gonna be
Dale, dale, don't run from the storm
Under this little sky, I'll keep you warm
Bajo la lluvia, the city's ours
Two strangers dancing in between the showers

Her perfume mixed with petrichor, the puddles caught the neon
We missed three buses on purpose just to keep on movin' on
She laughed and tipped the handle and the water ran on me
But I'd stay soaked forever if she'd stay here, can't you see

No sé tu nombre, but I know your eyes
The rain keeps falling, baby, no goodbyes

Bajo la lluvia, just you and me
One umbrella, where we gonna be
Dale, dale, don't run from the storm
Under this little sky, I'll keep you warm

When the rain stops, what happens to us two
Give me your number 'fore the gray sky turns to blue
Otra cuadra, one more block, don't let the morning come
Under one umbrella, baby, we are only one

Bajo la lluvia, just you and me
One umbrella, where we gonna be
Dale, dale, don't run from the storm
Under this little sky, I'll keep you warm

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine idling like a held breath in the alley out of sight
I told her this would be the last one, told her every other night
Three minutes on the counter, fingers drumming on the wheel
The city's full of strangers who will never know how this feels

The radio is talking soft about a town I'll never see
Got a map to somewhere quiet folded up for her and me
They said in, out, and clean, kid, but the clock's gone past the line
And the door I'm watching isn't opening this time

One more turn of the key, one more mile to the sea
I swear this is the last, the last, the last job for me
Headlights cutting through the dark, heart going like a drum
I'm not the man I used to be, I'm the man I might become

Then the door explodes wide open and I floor it through the rain
Sirens bloom like flowers in the mirror's broken frame
I'm singing to the windshield, I'm praying to the night
Just let me reach the county line, just let me make it right

She's waiting at the diner with the coffee growing cold
A wedding band, a clean ID, a story to be told
I never wanted any of this, I only wanted out
And the engine's the only thing that's never had a doubt

One more turn of the key, one more mile to the sea
I swear this is the last, the last, the last job for me
Headlights cutting through the dark, heart going like a drum
I'm not the man I used to be, I'm the man I might become

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Ooh, do-wop, do-wah
I was seventeen with a six-string and a shake in both my knees
The coffee shop was half asleep, the mic stand caught the breeze
My voice came out a whisper, then it cracked and then it flew
And somewhere in that little room a dreamer's dream came true

That first night, oh that first night (do-wop)
Under that one shaky light (sha-la-la)
My heart was pounding out of time
But oh, that crowd, they made it mine
That first night, I'll never forget
The best mistake I haven't made yet

There were maybe nine folks listening and the barista clapped the most
I forgot the second verse and made the third one up, almost
But a girl up in the corner smiled and tapped her little spoon
And I floated out the door beneath a kind and crooked moon

Now the years have come and gone (do-wah)
But I still can hum that song (sha-la-la)
Every stage I ever played
Started with that scared kid, unafraid

That first night, oh that first night (do-wop)
Under that one shaky light (sha-la-la)
My heart was pounding out of time
But oh, that crowd, they made it mine
That first night, I'll always know
That's the night I learned to glow

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Five a.m. alarm, I'm already tired before I rise
Warehouse till the afternoon, then the diner till sunrise
Clock in, clock out, the numbers never seem to add
Work the hardest that I can and still come up short and sad
Rent went up again, they call it market, call it fair
I call it watching every dollar vanish into air
Got calluses for trophies and a back that won't forget
Two jobs on my shoulders and I'm deeper into debt

Running, running, and the finish keeps on moving
Working, working, but I'm never really proving
That the harder that you grind it pays you back somehow
Got two jobs and one life and I'm still falling now
But I'm still standing, still standing
Whatever this thing's demanding
I'ma give it, I'ma give it, take the little that's left
Keep my head up to the sky even short of breath

My daughter draws me pictures of a house we'll never own
I tape 'em to the fridge in our apartment built of stone
She says, Daddy, you're a hero, and it cracks me right in two
'Cause a hero shouldn't have to choose the rent or buy her shoes
I'm not asking for a yacht, just a minute I can breathe
A day off in the sunlight, a small thing to believe
The system's built a ladder where the rungs keep breaking off
But I climb it every morning 'cause my baby girl looks up

They say pull yourself up, but my boots are coming apart
I got everything but money, I got muscle, I got heart
So I'll work another double, watch the city wake and yawn
Falling behind, but I refuse, I refuse to not go on

Running, running, and the finish keeps on moving
Working, working, but I'm never really proving
That the harder that you grind it pays you back somehow
Got two jobs and one life and I'm still falling now

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. and the payphone's broke, so I'm screaming through the cell
Mile marker nowhere, gas station fluorescent hell
Black coffee and a heartbeat and a thousand miles to go
I just need to hear you breathing 'fore I lose it on this road

Pick up the phone, pick up the phone
I'm a long way gone and I'm all alone
Three in the morning and I'm wired and wrecked
Just wanna hear you, that's all I expect
Pick up the phone, pick up the phone
This cab's a coffin and you're my home

The diesel's screaming murder and the white lines turn to snakes
I been driving thirteen hours on cigarettes and shakes
The kids are surely sleeping and I'm sorry that I'm gone
But the freight don't care 'bout fathers, so I'm pushing on and on

You answer half asleep and you don't even sound annoyed
You say come home in one piece, fill the silence in the void
And that's the only church I got, your voice across the dark
A tired god of asphalt with your number in my heart

I don't need no sermon, I don't need no rest
Just two minutes of your voice to keep me at my best
Four hundred miles of nothing and a sky that won't turn gray
Keep talking, baby, keep me here, don't let me drift away

Pick up the phone, pick up the phone
I'm a long way gone and I'm all alone
Three in the morning and I'm wired and wrecked
Just wanna hear you, that's all I expect
Pick up the phone, pick up the phone
This cab's a coffin and you're my home

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the gray over everything green
Last one standing where the orchard had been
Roots crack the concrete, reaching for the light
A stubborn little soldier in the dead of night

Oh, you're the last tree, the last tree standing
In a world of asphalt, you keep on demanding
A little bit of sky, a little bit of rain
The last tree standing through the growing pains

The cars drive past you, they don't even see
A hundred rings of stories in your memory
They built their towers, they paved their dreams
But you hold the secret of what living means

Oh, you're the last tree, the last tree standing
In a world of asphalt, you keep on demanding
A little bit of sky, a little bit of rain
The last tree standing through the growing pains

And I'll be there beside you when the bulldozers come
We'll be the final heartbeat, the last beating drum

Oh, you're the last tree, the last tree standing
Still reaching for the heavens, still understanding
A little bit of sky, a little bit of rain
The last tree standing through the growing pains

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
I remember how you used to hold my hand
Walking down that aisle of a promised land
But I let you slip right through my fingers, oh
Now I'm on my knees and the tears still flow

You were the one, the one that got away
Lord, I'm praying that you'll come back someday
I'll lift my hands and I will testify
For the love I lost beneath the open sky

Every Sunday morning I can hear your name
In the choir singing, in the candle flame
I was blind, oh Lord, but now I see
The greatest blessing you had sent to me

You were the one, the one that got away
Lord, I'm praying that you'll come back someday
I'll lift my hands and I will testify
For the love I lost beneath the open sky

Glory, glory, hallelujah
Even in my sorrow I can feel you
Glory, glory, set me free
Bring my lost love back to me

You were the one, the one that got away
But I'll keep the faith and I will pray
I'll lift my hands and I will testify
For the love I lost beneath the open sky

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every morning I unlock the heavy door
Walking down the hall on the marble floor
There she is, hanging on the wall
The most beautiful woman of them all

You're my masterpiece, my painted dream
Living in a world I've never seen
Dance with me, baby, in the gallery light
I'll be your guardian through the night

The tourists come, the tourists go
But they don't see you like I know
I talk to you when the lights go down
My silent queen in a golden gown

You're my masterpiece, my painted dream
Living in a world I've never seen
Dance with me, baby, in the gallery light
I'll be your guardian through the night

Oh-oh-oh, frozen in the frame
Oh-oh-oh, you'll never know my name
But I'll keep you safe till the morning comes
While the city sleeps and the night drums

You're my masterpiece, my painted dream
Living in a world I've never seen
Dance with me, baby, in the gallery light
I'll be your guardian through the night

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slid shut and the air got thin
My heart was pounding like a drum within
The walls were closing, I could not breathe
In the underground I began to plead

Lord, hold me now, I'm calling out your name
In this crowded car I feel the same
Reach down and steady my trembling hand
Give me the peace I don't understand

The strangers staring, the lights so bright
I closed my eyes and I found the light
A still small voice said you're not alone
Every stop is leading you back home

Lord, hold me now, I'm calling out your name
In this crowded car I feel the same
Reach down and steady my trembling hand
Give me the peace I don't understand

Breathe in, breathe out, the storm will pass
This moment, child, it will not last
Glory hallelujah, I am free
The spirit moving over me

Lord, hold me now, I'm calling out your name
Through the tunnel dark I'm not the same
You took my burden, you held my hand
And gave me peace I understand

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Two plates on the table, now I only need one
Your recipe book sitting out in the sun
I'm measuring the rice the way you taught me to
Every little spoonful tastes like missing you

Cooking for one, oh, it's a lonely art
Filling up the kitchen with a broken heart
But I hear you whisper, baby, take your time
A table set for one and a glass of wine

The steam on the window where you used to stand
I can almost feel you reaching for my hand
A little too much salt, a little too much tears
Learning how to live through all these empty years

Cooking for one, oh, it's a lonely art
Filling up the kitchen with a broken heart
But I hear you whisper, baby, take your time
A table set for one and a glass of wine

And maybe one day I'll invite a friend
Maybe this old heart will learn to mend
But tonight it's just your apron and me
And the love you left in the recipe

Cooking for one, oh, it's a lonely art
Filling up the kitchen with a broken heart
But I hear you whisper, baby, take your time
A table set for one and a glass of wine

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up on the rooftop with my antenna high
Sending out the riddim to the city sky
The government can't catch me on the frequency
Broadcasting freedom, spreading unity

Pirate radio, playing all night long
Give the people music, give the people song
Turn your dial, baby, find me in the static
Love and revolution, irie and emphatic

They shut me down but I rise again
A hidden transmitter and a faithful friend
From the ghetto to the hills they hear my sound
Underground forever, never going down

Pirate radio, playing all night long
Give the people music, give the people song
Turn your dial, baby, find me in the static
Love and revolution, irie and emphatic

Jah guide the signal through the midnight air
Music is a weapon and the message is care
No license, no master, just a heart that's true
This one, this one is going out to you

Pirate radio, playing all night long
Give the people music, give the people song
Turn your dial, baby, find me in the static
Love and revolution, irie and emphatic

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I close the wooden door behind me one last time
The stained glass casting colors I can't call mine
The hymns still echo somewhere in my chest
But I'm walking out to find my own way to the rest

So I'm letting go, letting go
Of everything I thought I'd know
The steeple fades behind the trees
And I am finally finally free

The pews remember every prayer I ever made
The candles flicker low on the faith that started to fade
No anger in my heart, just a quiet kind of peace
A gentle understanding, a long-awaited release

So I'm letting go, letting go
Of everything I thought I'd know
The steeple fades behind the trees
And I am finally finally free

And the morning light is breaking through
A wider sky, a different blue
No one is following, no one will know
Just the sound of my footsteps as I go

So I'm letting go, letting go
Of everything I thought I'd know
The steeple fades behind the trees
And I am finally finally free

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We were tethered to each other in the cold
Floating through the silence growing old
But the line between us started to fray
And I watched you slowly drifting away

We're two astronauts, lost in the dark
A million miles between every spark
Reaching out my hand but I can't hold you near
Falling through the void, my dear, my dear

The radio is static, your voice is fading out
The oxygen of love is running out
I see your helmet shining like a distant star
So close to me yet you're so far

We're two astronauts, lost in the dark
A million miles between every spark
Reaching out my hand but I can't hold you near
Falling through the void, my dear, my dear

Ooh, gravity won't bring you home
Ooh, I'm spinning here all alone
The earth is just a marble far below
And I gotta finally let you go

We're two astronauts, lost in the dark
A million miles between every spark
Reaching out my hand but I can't hold you near
Falling through the void, my dear, my dear

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
From a cottage on the cliffside where the wild winds blow
I send the songs of olden days the world should know
With a fiddle and a whisper through the salty air
A secret little signal that the fishermen share

Oh, the pirate radio sings across the sea
A melody of freedom, a melody of me
They cannot still the music, they cannot stop the tide
The spirit of the islands on the airwaves rides

The constable comes searching but he'll never find
The little hidden aerial I keep in my mind
From Galway to the Hebrides they hear the reel
The ancient songs of sorrow that the heart can feel

Oh, the pirate radio sings across the sea
A melody of freedom, a melody of me
They cannot still the music, they cannot stop the tide
The spirit of the islands on the airwaves rides

So gather round the wireless when the moon is high
And let the tin whistle carry you up to the sky
No king nor crown can silence what the people love
The music of the angels coming from above

Oh, the pirate radio sings across the sea
A melody of freedom, a melody of me
They cannot still the music, they cannot stop the tide
The spirit of the islands on the airwaves rides

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
The whole town said we'd never make it here
But we showed up anyway despite the fear
The clock is ticking down on the final play
Everything we are comes down to today

So rise, rise, we were born to fight
Underdogs burning in the stadium light
Nobody believed but we believed
This is the moment that we'll never leave

The scoreboard's against us but we won't lie down
The roar of the crowd is the only sound
Every drop of sweat and every fall
Led us to this one, the biggest of all

So rise, rise, we were born to fight
Underdogs burning in the stadium light
Nobody believed but we believed
This is the moment that we'll never leave

And whatever happens when the whistle blows
We gave it everything, and everybody knows
This is our story, this is our song
We were the ones who proved them wrong

So rise, rise, we were born to fight
Underdogs burning in the stadium light
Nobody believed but we believed
This is the moment that we'll never leave

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Fifty years of mornings and the kettle on the fire
Fifty years of holding you, my only heart's desire
Now the music's playing soft, the candle burning low
One more turn around the floor before it's time to go

So dance with me, my darling, one last time
Let the fiddle and the flute keep the rhythm and the rhyme
Though our steps are slow and our hair is gray
I'll hold you close until the break of day

We weathered every winter and we danced through every spring
Through the laughter and the heartache that the years would bring
Your hand still fits in mine the way it always did
The love we made together cannot be undid

So dance with me, my darling, one last time
Let the fiddle and the flute keep the rhythm and the rhyme
Though our steps are slow and our hair is gray
I'll hold you close until the break of day

And if the morning takes you from my side
I'll carry every dance until I die
The melody will linger in the air
And I will always find you waiting there

So dance with me, my darling, one last time
Let the fiddle and the flute keep the rhythm and the rhyme
Though our steps are slow and our hair is gray
I'll hold you close until the break of day

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Well I never touched a skillet in my whole long life
That was always the department of my darling wife
Now she's resting on the hillside underneath the pine
And I'm standing in the kitchen at suppertime

Cooking for one, Lord, cooking for one
Burnin' up the biscuits till the day is done
I'll set one plate down where there used to be two
And I'll say a little grace and I'll think of you

I found her handwrote recipes inside a drawer
Buttermilk and bacon grease and so much more
I follow every line the way she wrote it down
The best darn cook in all the county round

Cooking for one, Lord, cooking for one
Burnin' up the biscuits till the day is done
I'll set one plate down where there used to be two
And I'll say a little grace and I'll think of you

The banjo's playing lonesome on the radio
The coffee's getting cold and the fire's burning low
But I'm learning how to live and I'm learning how to try
With her apron on my shoulders and a tear in my eye

Cooking for one, Lord, cooking for one
Burnin' up the biscuits till the day is done
I'll set one plate down where there used to be two
And I'll say a little grace and I'll think of you

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Soft the evening falls on the ocean and the sand
Quietly I take your fragile hand
The gentle waves are keeping time tonight
As we sway beneath the golden light

One last dance, my love, one last dance
For all the years of our sweet romance
Hold me close as the music plays
In the warm and tender bossa haze

The samba of our lifetime, the rhythm of our days
Every little sorrow, every loving phrase
Your smile still moves me like the very first time
A melody of ours, a perfect rhyme

One last dance, my love, one last dance
For all the years of our sweet romance
Hold me close as the music plays
In the warm and tender bossa haze

And the stars come out so slow and so bright
We'll dance until the morning, we'll dance through the night
No need for words, just the sway of the sea
Forever in this moment, you and me

One last dance, my love, one last dance
For all the years of our sweet romance
Hold me close as the music plays
In the warm and tender bossa haze

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the cobblestones on his ancient chest
Built their cathedrals where the titan takes his rest
We drink our coffee on the rising and the fall
Of the breathing of the giant beneath it all

Oh, don't wake him up, don't wake him up
We're dancing on the back of a sleeping god
Oh, don't wake him up, don't wake him up
Every step we take is a little too loud

The tremors in the morning are just his beating heart
The legends say the city and the giant won't part
We build a little higher and we hope he won't stir
A fragile little kingdom on a dreaming blur

Oh, don't wake him up, don't wake him up
We're dancing on the back of a sleeping god
Oh, don't wake him up, don't wake him up
Every step we take is a little too loud

And one day he'll open up his ancient eyes
And shake the towers down beneath the skies
But until then we'll live and we'll love and we'll cry
On the chest of the giant where the centuries lie

Oh, don't wake him up, don't wake him up
We're dancing on the back of a sleeping god
Oh, don't wake him up, don't wake him up
Every step we take is a little too loud

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning and the black suit's on the chair
The casseroles are stacking up but you're not there
The sun came up like nothing even changed
My whole entire world got rearranged

But I'm pick-pick-picking up the pieces today
The horns are gonna play my grief away
One foot forward, gotta keep on moving on
Even when the one I love is gone

The relatives went home and the flowers start to fade
I'm drinking cold coffee and the bed's still made
The trumpet and the trombone, they help me cope
A little upbeat rhythm and a little bit of hope

But I'm pick-pick-picking up the pieces today
The horns are gonna play my grief away
One foot forward, gotta keep on moving on
Even when the one I love is gone

Pick it up, pick it up, dance through the pain
They wouldn't want me crying in the rain
So skank a little harder, lift my head up high
Gotta celebrate a life and say goodbye

So I'm pick-pick-picking up the pieces today
The horns are gonna play my grief away
One foot forward, gotta keep on moving on
Even when the one I love is gone

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The lamp burns low on a kitchen chair
Coffee gone cold and I don't much care
Books piled high like a mountain range
My daddy's clock keeps countin' strange

The whole town's sleepin' under the hill
Just me and the moon and a window sill
My pencil's worn down to a nub
Chasin' an answer in the dark and the mud

Two in the mornin', the lonesome hour
Nobody clappin', ain't no bright flower
Just a tired girl and a flickerin' light
Buildin' a road with the seeds of the night

Fiddle of the crickets out past the pane
Singing me on through the worry and strain
I'll trade this hush for a diploma day
If the mornin' bird don't carry me away

Momma said hard things make a soul
Like a creek cuts deep through the solid coal
So I'll wear this dark like a workin' glove
And carry the weight of the ones I love

Two in the mornin', the lonesome hour
Nobody clappin', ain't no bright flower
Just a tired girl and a flickerin' light
Buildin' a road with the seeds of the night

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded the crates by the lamplight low
The captain he laughed but his eyes said no
The harbor wind it whispered a name
We sailed at dawn and the sea's never the same

The clarinet cries on the rolling deck
A coin in the hold and a curse on our neck
The sailors don't sleep, they pray and they sway
The cargo it hums in the dark of the bay

Oy, the long voyage, the salt and the shroud
The fiddle keeps weeping, the thunder is loud
We carry the burden no man can name
Dance till the morning or drown in the flame

The cook saw a shadow climb out of the grain
The first mate's gone quiet, gone soft in the brain
We danced a wild hora to keep off the cold
But something is breathing down deep in the hold

Throw it overboard, the old men cried
But the chains wouldn't break though we wept and we tried
So play, little clarinet, sweet and so high
And carry our souls to the merciful sky

Oy, the long voyage, the salt and the shroud
The fiddle keeps weeping, the thunder is loud
We carry the burden no man can name
Dance till the morning or drown in the flame

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Neon bleeds on the rain-slick street
Second shift and my dragging feet
Clock out here, clock in over there
The city hums but it doesn't care

Numbers glow on the overdue page
A paper cage and a minimum wage
I chase the dawn in a borrowed car
The finish line keeps moving far

Two jobs, two names on a plastic tag
Still the red letters in the mailbox drag
I'm running through static, electric and blind
Working so hard and still falling behind

Midnight drive on an empty tank
Dream of a number in a healthy bank
The synth of the freeway sings me low
Somewhere a light I will never know

They told me grind and the world would bend
But the wheel just spins and it never ends
Still I keep the engine warm tonight
For the version of me in a kinder light

Two jobs, two names on a plastic tag
Still the red letters in the mailbox drag
I'm running through static, electric and blind
Working so hard and still falling behind

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up on the rooftop we hid the wire
Antenna humming like a city on fire
Bootleg frequency, midnight gold
Spinning the records the suits never sold

Turn it up, we're the voice in the static
Underground groove getting wild and dramatic
They can't catch us, we're a ghost on the dial
Dancing illegal in the strobe for a while

The van rolls slow with a government light
We move the transmitter to the roof every night
The people are calling from the kitchens and bars
We play them the funk that belongs to the stars

Turn it up, we're the voice in the static
Underground groove getting wild and dramatic
They can't catch us, we're a ghost on the dial
Dancing illegal in the strobe for a while

Mirror ball spinning in a basement room
A hundred thousand listeners in the velvet gloom
We never say our names, just the beat and the bass
A secret little kingdom they can never erase

When the signal cuts out you can feel the whole town
Holding its breath for the sound to come round
So find us again at the edge of the night
Where the needle drops down and the city ignites

Turn it up, we're the voice in the static
Underground groove getting wild and dramatic
They can't catch us, we're a ghost on the dial
Dancing illegal in the strobe for a while

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Ooh, the screen lit up so blue
Three little words but they weren't I love you
I read it twice on the corner alone
My whole heart breaking in a little glass phone

Shoo-bop, you left me with a text, my dear
No last dance, no whispered goodbye in my ear
Just a buzz and a blink and a fade to gray
Oh, my baby typed my heart away

We used to slow dance by the soda machine
Now it's pixels and dots on a tiny screen
I typed you back but you'd gone offline
The last little ghost of a love of mine

Shoo-bop, you left me with a text, my dear
No last dance, no whispered goodbye in my ear
Just a buzz and a blink and a fade to gray
Oh, my baby typed my heart away

Remember the malt shop, the jukebox glow?
Now even the dots have stopped, baby, don't you know
I'd give all my dimes for a face-to-face
Instead of this cold little empty space

Shoo-bop, you left me with a text, my dear
No last dance, no whispered goodbye in my ear
Just a buzz and a blink and a fade to gray
Oh, my baby typed my heart away

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Third floor walk-up, needle in his hand
Old man Vito, the king of this land
No store, no sign, just a buzzer and a thread
Dresses the living and he dresses the dead

HE CUTS THE CLOTH FOR THE WHOLE DAMN BLOCK
FROM THE WEDDING SUIT TO THE WORKING SMOCK
NEEDLE AND BLOOD AND A PROMISE KEPT
IN A ROOM WHERE NOBODY EVER SLEPT

The boxer's robe and the widow's black
The kid's first jacket off the very same rack
He knows your shoulders, he knows your name
Stitches your story in the seams of the frame

HE CUTS THE CLOTH FOR THE WHOLE DAMN BLOCK
FROM THE WEDDING SUIT TO THE WORKING SMOCK
NEEDLE AND BLOOD AND A PROMISE KEPT
IN A ROOM WHERE NOBODY EVER SLEPT

When they bury him they'll wear his hands
Every thread he pulled through these tired lands
YOU CAN'T BUY THIS, YOU CAN'T MASS-PRODUCE
A LIFE SEWN TIGHT WITH NO EXCUSE

Last light burning, the machine still hums
He's dressing tomorrow before it comes
HANDS ON THE FABRIC TILL THE VERY LAST BREATH
A TAILOR OF LIFE AND A TAILOR OF DEATH

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Cracked concrete and a busted swing
The same gray sky over everything
We carved our names in the overpass
Swore we'd be gone before the summer passed

I made it out, I made it out
Past the chain-link and the dead-end doubt
I took the long road, I took the shot
Left my heart on the corner lot

The bus came once and I let it go
Too scared to leave what I used to know
But the walls were closing a half-inch a day
So I packed a bag and I drove away

I made it out, I made it out
Past the chain-link and the dead-end doubt
I took the long road, I took the shot
Left my heart on the corner lot

They said nobody leaves, that's just how it goes
But I kept the key to the door that closed
Sometimes I drive back just to see it small
The block that swore it was the whole world and all

I made it out, I made it out
Past the chain-link and the dead-end doubt
I took the long road, I took the shot
Left my heart on the corner lot

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I shut the prayer book, I turned out the light
The words went silent and they offered no fight
The candles burned low and I let them all die
And I asked the empty and cavernous sky

Oy, the long winter, the doubt and the cold
The melody fading, the story half-told
But a fiddle came calling from a wedding next door
And it carried me home to the One I once swore

For years I walked with my collar pulled tight
No song in my chest and no spark in the night
Then I heard an old niggun, a wordless refrain
And it broke down the door I had bolted in pain

Oy, the long winter, the doubt and the cold
The melody fading, the story half-told
But a fiddle came calling from a wedding next door
And it carried me home to the One I once swore

Not the rules, not the building, not the fear and the rod
Just the dance and the weeping, the human and God
I light one small candle in the shaking dark
And the flame remembers my forgotten spark

Oy, the long winter, the doubt and the cold
The melody fading, the story half-told
But a fiddle came calling from a wedding next door
And it carried me home to the One I once swore

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
La casa se quedó callada
The slammed door echo, then nothing at all
You're on the couch and I'm in the hall
Dembow heartbeat against the wall

El silencio after the storm
Two bodies cold where it used to be warm
I hear you breathing on the other side
Too proud to cross, too in love to hide

The phone face down and the TV mute
The words we threw still hang in the route
I wanna text you from the same little room
Baby, this quiet is a heavy perfume

El silencio after the storm
Two bodies cold where it used to be warm
I hear you breathing on the other side
Too proud to cross, too in love to hide

Tra, the beat goes soft, no fuego, no grito
Just the hum of the fridge and the night so quieto
Then your hand on my back in the dark, despacito
And the silence forgives us, mami, te necesito

El silencio after the storm
Two bodies cold where it used to be warm
I hear you breathing on the other side
Too proud to cross, too in love to hide

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Smoke in the lamplight, drowning slow
We dealt the cards and we let it go
The stakes kept climbing through the haze
Nobody folded, nobody prayed

We played too far, we played too long
The wall of sound where the night went wrong
I bet the things I could not lose
And watched them wash away in blue

He put his keys and his ring on the felt
I saw the river of the cards he'd dealt
The room dissolved in a wave of gray
We couldn't stop, couldn't walk away

We played too far, we played too long
The wall of sound where the night went wrong
I bet the things I could not lose
And watched them wash away in blue

Dawn through the curtain like a drowning light
Nobody won in the end of the night
The last card turning, soft and slow
A tide of nothing in the undertow

We played too far, we played too long
The wall of sound where the night went wrong
I bet the things I could not lose
And watched them wash away in blue

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent hum in the empty hall
Snow on the window, the year grows small
My second shift in a sea of white
The whole world's home and I'm here tonight

Double shift, the holiday glow
Far away through the falling snow
I hold a hand that the family forgot
Drowning soft in the things I've got

The monitors blur into a wash of light
Coffee gone cold by the nursing site
Somebody's grandma calls me her own
So neither of us is quite alone

Double shift, the holiday glow
Far away through the falling snow
I hold a hand that the family forgot
Drowning soft in the things I've got

My phone lights up with a distant cheer
My kids on a screen so far and near
I wave through the static and the gauze
And melt back into the holy cause

Double shift, the holiday glow
Far away through the falling snow
I hold a hand that the family forgot
Drowning soft in the things I've got

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They picked my house and they picked my wife
They drew a map of my whole damn life
Good job, gray suit, a steady track
But I left my future in a paper sack

Pick it up, pick it up, I'm running today
From the life they built and the price they'd pay
Upbeat skanking down a different road
Dropping the weight of a borrowed load

The horns kick in and I jump the train
No more of their careful, careful chain
My uncle's frowning, my mother cries
But I gotta find me my own surprise

Pick it up, pick it up, I'm running today
From the life they built and the price they'd pay
Upbeat skanking down a different road
Dropping the weight of a borrowed load

Maybe I'm a fool, maybe I'll fall
But a planned-out life ain't a life at all
So I'll dance off-beat to a song they hate
And write my own unwritten fate

Pick it up, pick it up, I'm running today
From the life they built and the price they'd pay
Upbeat skanking down a different road
Dropping the weight of a borrowed load

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights were low and the crowd was thin
One more song and we might have got in
The scout was there in the back of the bar
So close, so close to the shining star

Oh, the night we almost made it
Held the dream and we almost saved it
So near the gate but it closed too soon
We sang to the rafters and the rising moon

The amp blew out on the final song
The magic moment came and went along
We laughed it off in the parking lot
Swallowing all of the things we forgot

Oh, the night we almost made it
Held the dream and we almost saved it
So near the gate but it closed too soon
We sang to the rafters and the rising moon

But every almost is a seed in the ground
Growing slow to a deeper sound
We don't regret what we couldn't hold
We lived one night that was purer than gold

Oh, the night we almost made it
Held the dream and we almost saved it
So near the gate but it closed too soon
We sang to the rafters and the rising moon

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Last tank of gas at the edge of town
Rearview shrinking the whole thing down
The water tower with my name still sprayed
Every promise that I never made

Running, running, the bassline calls
Tearing me out of these same four walls
No looking back at the fading lights
I'm gone for good in the rush of the night

The motorway opens like a beating heart
Breakbeat hammer as I tear apart
From the diner job and the dead-end street
The city ahead with a faster beat

Running, running, the bassline calls
Tearing me out of these same four walls
No looking back at the fading lights
I'm gone for good in the rush of the night

My phone goes quiet, the signal drops
The only sound is the engine's pulse that never stops
Goodbye to the faces that wanted me small
I'm a frequency rising above it all

Running, running, the bassline calls
Tearing me out of these same four walls
No looking back at the fading lights
I'm gone for good in the rush of the night

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night when the city sleeps
Softly into the dream I creep
My feet leave the warm São Paulo floor
And I drift through the open window door

I am flying, gentle and slow
Over the rooftops and the sea below
The same sweet dream comes back to me
Weightless, easy, finally free

The samba of the stars below my feet
A bossa breeze on a moonlit street
I never wonder how or why
I only spread my arms and fly

I am flying, gentle and slow
Over the rooftops and the sea below
The same sweet dream comes back to me
Weightless, easy, finally free

Then the morning calls with its yellow light
And it folds away my wings so light
But I carry the soft of the sky all day
And I wait for the dusk to fly away

I am flying, gentle and slow
Over the rooftops and the sea below
The same sweet dream comes back to me
Weightless, easy, finally free

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Concrete sky comes crashing down
Neon bleeding through the gray
I was running for the underpass
When you stepped into my way
No words, just a tilted nylon dome
A borrowed inch of dry
Two soldiers in a downpour war
Beneath one breaking sky

Hold the line against the flood
Shoulder pressed to shoulder, stranger
We are armor made of nothing
Standing still inside the danger
One umbrella, two unknowns
Thunder screaming overhead
We survive the storm together
Then we part without a thread

Your knuckles white around the shaft
Mine wrapped beside your own
The rain is throwing fists of steel
But I am not alone
Lightning carves the boulevard
In silver, jagged scars
Two silhouettes refuse to drown
Beneath the drowning stars

We never traded names
We never traded fears
Just held the sky from falling
For a hundred screaming years
And when the clouds surrender
And the gutters finally still
You'll walk your way, I'll walk my own
But I'll remember still

Hold the line against the flood
Shoulder pressed to shoulder, stranger
We are armor made of nothing
Standing still inside the danger

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
String lights flicker, half of them gone dim
Trampled grass, the cups are caving in
Speaker's tired, dropping one last beat
Sandals dragging dust across the street
Got a paper bracelet I won't cut
Memory taped onto my wrist, stays shut
Somebody's laughing by the lemonade
Last of the warmth before the colors fade

One more night, let it ride out slow
Watch the smoke from the bonfire go
We don't talk about the morning yet
Last night of the summer, no regret

Tent stakes loosen, somebody's saying bye
Numbers traded that we'll never try
Vendor packing up the painted signs
Moon hung low between the power lines
Got the bass still humming in my chest
Folding up the day like it's the best
Clock don't matter when the night's this kind
Leave the noise but keep the glow behind

Ash in the air, soft and slow
Nobody really wants to go
Hold the quiet, let it stretch
One more breath before we catch

One more night, let it ride out slow
Watch the smoke from the bonfire go
We don't talk about the morning yet
Last night of the summer, no regret

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the hold in the dead of night
Crate without a name, sealed too tight
Captain said don't ask, just set the course
Feel the engine humming with a darker force
Miles of black water, no land in sight
Something in the cargo breathing, taking flight

Don't open the box, don't break the chain
We ride the curse across the main
Four to the floor, the engines pound
We can't go back, we're outward bound
Don't open the box, don't say its name
The sea remembers, the sea's to blame

Compass spinning circles, stars gone wrong
Crew's been counting down a fevered song
Knock from the belly when the moon goes red
Whispers in the manifest of all the dead
Miles of black water, no return
Something in the cargo starting to burn

Let the bassline carry what the body fears
We've been sailing nowhere for a thousand years
Hands up in the dark of the lower deck
We dance off the weight of the wreck

Don't open the box, don't break the chain
We ride the curse across the main
Four to the floor, the engines pound
We can't go back, we're outward bound

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
La lámpara tiembla, the page is a wall
My coffee's gone cold in the dark of the hall
The clock strikes its two with a heartbroken hand
And the words on the paper are shifting like sand
My fingers are tired, my eyes are on fire
But the dawn is a promise I cannot acquire

Óle, the silence, the ache and the strain
Alone with my candle, alone with my pain
The rhythm of study, the clap and the cry
I'll dance with these pages till morning is nigh

The street is asleep but my mind is a flame
Reciting the verses, repeating each name
The heel of my longing strikes hard on the floor
One hour, then one more, then one hour more
My shadow's my partner, my pencil my rose
This duende of duty that nobody knows

Let the guitar weep low for the lonely and proud
For the ones who keep burning when nobody's loud
The night is a cantor, the lamp is a friend
And I'll see this through to the bitter, sweet end

Óle, the silence, the ache and the strain
Alone with my candle, alone with my pain
The rhythm of study, the clap and the cry
I'll dance with these pages till morning is nigh

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors slam shut and the lights go white
My chest is a fist that won't unclench tonight
Forty strangers all stacked in a tin
And the air's getting thin, getting thin, getting thin
I count the ads, I count the floor
I count to ten and then I count some more

I'm okay, I'm okay, I tell the glass
Breathe in slow and let the tunnel pass
It's just a train, it's just a train
It's just a storm inside my brain

The map's a snake of red and blue
I can't remember which one's getting through
My heart's a drummer who forgot the beat
My hands are ice, there's water in my feet
A woman reads, a kid just yawns
And no one knows that I am holding on

Next stop, next stop, the speaker hums
I grip the pole until the feeling comes
Down to my fingers, back into my name
The doors slide open, I am not the same

I'm okay, I'm okay, I tell the glass
Breathe in slow and let the tunnel pass
It's just a train, it's just a train
It's just a storm inside my brain
