songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Desk lamp humming, soft and amber low
Stack of pages with a long way to go
Rain on the window keeping easy time
Highlighter cap between my teeth, I'm fine
Clock says two, the city's gone to sleep
Just me and the notes and the silence I keep

Two a.m. glow, pencil moving slow
Nobody knows the hours that I owe
Just breathe it in, let the quiet stay
I'll find the answer before the gray

Coffee ring stains on a borrowed book
Forgot what the question even took
Lean my chair back, stare up at the cracks
Load another lo-fi, ease the back
Flashcards scattered like a sleepy hand
Learning how to wait, learning how to stand

No one's awake to see me try
Just the hum and the dark and the open sky
Small little victory, page by page
Quiet kind of growing up, off the stage

Two a.m. glow, pencil moving slow
Nobody knows the hours that I owe
Just breathe it in, let the quiet stay
I'll find the answer before the gray

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The bus let me off where the gravel road bends
Same crooked fence, same crows, same wind
My duffel's half empty, my boots are worn through
And the porch light is burning the way that it knew
I carried a country, I carried a name
But the boy who left here, he ain't the same

I'm coming home, coming home at last
Dragging the weight of a heavy past
The fields don't ask and the river won't tell
Lord, let me lay it down by the well

My mother's hands shook when she opened the door
My father just nodded, said nothing more
The table's still set with the same chipped plate
And the dog that I left learned how to wait
The medals don't fit in the dresser drawer
With the things I believed in before the war

There's a ghost in the mirror who wears my face
Who still hears the thunder in a quiet place
But the wheat's turning gold and the sun's coming up
And my hands remember how to lift a cup

I'm coming home, coming home at last
Dragging the weight of a heavy past
The fields don't ask and the river won't tell
Lord, let me lay it down by the well

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Under the mirror ball, the memory glows
She taught me to dance in her kitchen of gold
Flour on her fingers, a ring worn so thin
The map of a lifetime tucked under the skin
She'd snap to the radio, twirl me around
Those hands made a heaven of one little town

Grandma's hands, oh they move me still
Up on the floor with a soft little thrill
They stitched and they sang and they held me tight
Now they're dancing in the disco light

They braided my hair and they wiped every tear
They clapped on the one and the two through the years
Knuckles like rivers, palms warm as the sun
They worked till the setting and never were done
She'd say baby just feel it, let the rhythm be true
The love's in the motion, it'll carry you through

Now the strings rise up and the bassline swells
And I feel her beside me, I know it so well
Every turn that I make, every shine, every glide
Is a prayer of her hands keeping time at my side

Grandma's hands, oh they move me still
Up on the floor with a soft little thrill
They stitched and they sang and they held me tight
Now they're dancing in the disco light

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The clarinet wails as the wheels start to scream
The carriage is shaking, I'm caught in a dream
My heart plays a freylekhs too fast and too loud
A dance with no exit, alone in the crowd
Oy, the walls they are leaning, the lights start to spin
The tunnel's a throat and it's swallowing me in

Dance, little heart, though you tremble and race
The fiddle will carry you out of this place
Up from the dark, let the melody climb
We'll laugh and we'll weep and we'll keep the old time

The accordion gasps with the breath I can't take
My hands grip the rail till the knuckles all ache
A bride and a funeral packed in one car
The nearest of stations feels terribly far
But the trumpet remembers the joy in the pain
And it pulls me back up from the floor of the train

Breathe with the bow, with the trill and the turn
The spinning will slow and the lamps cease to burn
The doors open wide on a platform of air
And the music walks with me back up to the stair

Dance, little heart, though you tremble and race
The fiddle will carry you out of this place
Up from the dark, let the melody climb
We'll laugh and we'll weep and we'll keep the old time

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Duct tape on the dashboard, maps gone soft and gray
Radar humming static, chasing skies all day
Motel keys collected like a string of broken hearts
Never learned to stay still, that's the trouble where it starts
Supercell on the horizon, green and low and wide
I feel more at home in the funnel than inside

Can't settle down, can't settle down
There's a storm in my chest the size of a town
I chase what I fear and I fear what I miss
There's no quiet road that ends like this

She asked me to stay through one mild blue afternoon
But a calm sky feels like a too-empty room
Thunderheads stacking like the years I let go
The car's always packed, I just love it that slow
Wipers keeping rhythm with the doubt in my head
Another county line, another rented bed

Maybe one day the sky'll go still and so will I
Lay the camera down and let a sunset just go by
But not tonight, no, there's a wall cloud forming west
And the chase is the only thing that's ever felt like rest

Can't settle down, can't settle down
There's a storm in my chest the size of a town
I chase what I fear and I fear what I miss
There's no quiet road that ends like this

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Wheels rolling slow down a too-familiar street
The clave in my chest got a stuttering beat
Third floor window, still that same cheap shade
But the curtains are new and the light's been remade
I ain't gonna stop, no I'm just passing through
But the bassline of memory keeps pulling at you

Driving past, driving past your door
A ghost in the gear shift wanting more
The groove of the old days riding shotgun tonight
Driving past your window light

We used to dance barefoot on that warped wood floor
Spilled wine on the carpet, laughed and spilled some more
Now somebody else throws their keys in the bowl
Living in the rooms where I left half my soul
Green light's a green light, I keep on the gas
But my foot wants to linger, wants the moment to last

Horns hit a sting like a sweet little ache
Every block that I pass is a turn I won't take
The city keeps funking, the night keeps its cool
And I drive like a fool past the home of a fool

Driving past, driving past your door
A ghost in the gear shift wanting more
The groove of the old days riding shotgun tonight
Driving past your window light

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
The steeple went silent, the candles burned low
I prayed to a ceiling that had nothing to show
The words turned to ashes, the hymns turned to noise
And I buried my certainty deep with the boys
Mile after mile of an empty gray dawn
I walked out of everything I leaned upon

And the sky cracked open, slow and wide
Something ancient turning over deep inside
Not the god that I lost but a quieter flame
Learning how to believe in a different name

For years I was hollow, a bell with no ring
A winter that swallowed the promise of spring
But a stranger's small kindness, a child's open hand
Drew a line in me softly I came to understand
The roar of the guitars rising up like a tide
Washing over the doubt I had carried inside

It builds and it builds like a wave on the shore
Every cymbal a sunrise I'd not seen before
No answers, just wonder, no doctrine, just light
A cathedral of sound breaking open the night

And the sky cracked open, slow and wide
Something ancient turning over deep inside
Not the god that I lost but a quieter flame
Learning how to believe in a different name

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We sailed from the harbor on a cold autumn morn
With a hold full of cargo that no man should mourn
The priest wouldn't bless it, the widow she wept
And a black-feathered secret below decks was kept
Heave away, heave away, the wind's turning wrong
And the sea's growing hungry for the souls of our song

Oh the cargo is cursed and the captain is blind
We've left all the saints and the shoreline behind
The fiddle plays low and the lantern burns dim
And the deep dark water keeps calling us in

The first mate went missing on the seventh long night
His boots by the railing, no struggle, no fight
The whisper from the hold grew a terrible voice
And the men crossed themselves for we had no choice
Heave away, heave away, but the stars all have fled
And the figurehead weeps for the living and dead

Throw it over, cried the bosun, let the cursed thing drown
But the chains would not loosen and the box would not down
So we drink to the morning we'll most likely not see
With a song for the bottomless, beautiful sea

Oh the cargo is cursed and the captain is blind
We've left all the saints and the shoreline behind
The fiddle plays low and the lantern burns dim
And the deep dark water keeps calling us in

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
City lights are smearing in the driving rain
Bassline of the downpour on the windowpane
You stepped to my shoulder at the crossing sign
Half your coat soaked through, half of yours, half mine
No names in the chaos, just a nod and a grin
One small dome of shelter, let the stranger in

Run with me, run through the flooded street
Two hearts riding on a borrowed beat
One umbrella, one second of grace
Two strangers and the rain we chase

Green man flashing, and the puddles explode
We're dodging the rivers on the steaming road
You laugh at the thunder, I laugh at the noise
Two specks in the city finding fragile poise
Drums rolling faster like the racing pulse
A moment that the morning will surely dissolve

The beat drops out, just the hiss of the storm
Your breath and my breath keeping each other warm
Then it kicks back in and we split at the light
Two strangers, one second, gone into the night

Run with me, run through the flooded street
Two hearts riding on a borrowed beat
One umbrella, one second of grace
Two strangers and the rain we chase

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I climb the iron stairway as the evening turns to slate
And I light the lamp that loves you, my impossible, my fate
Forty years I've kept this candle for a bride who has no face
Just the gray and rolling shoulders of your cold and restless grace

So I write you, oh I write you, on the paper of the foam
Every wave's a word I send you, every tide that wanders home
You will never write me back, love, but I'll never need you to
For my whole life is a letter, and the letter's all for you

When the gulls bring me the morning I will walk along your hem
Gather stones you left as kisses, slip them in my coat and then
I will tell you of the ships I saved, the nights I held the line
And you'll answer with the thunder of your green eternal brine

Some say a man should marry, should have children, should grow old
With a woman who can hold him, not a lover made of cold
But they've never heard you singing when the storm has scrubbed the sky
And I'd rather have your roaring than a hundred years gone dry

So I write you, oh I write you, on the paper of the foam
Every wave's a word I send you, every tide that wanders home
You will never write me back, love, but I'll never need you to
For my whole life is a letter, and the letter's all for you

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Mason jars sweating in the bed of the truck
Gravel road grinning where the headlights cut
Daddy taught me how to ride the curve light
Fifty gallons rattling in the belly of the night

The still was up the holler past the broken oak
Corn and copper coil and a ribbon of smoke
I counted out the bottles, kissed my mama's hand
Swore I'd buy her shoes she wouldn't have to mend

But the deputy was waiting by the Sycamore bend
Red light spinning like the world was gonna end
I cut the wheel and prayed to a God I never knew
The river took the cargo and it nearly took me too

Now the catfish drink my fortune in the cold and the dark
And the smell of burning sugar still clings to the bark
Mama wears her old shoes, holes worn through the sole
And I carry the ditch water down inside my soul

Don't run what you can't lose, son, that's the only creed
The mountain gives you whiskey but it'll take the rest you need

So the deputy still waits by the Sycamore bend
And I dream of those bottles tumbling round the bend
I cut the wheel and prayed to a God I never knew
The river took the cargo and it nearly took me too

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Four men and a bottle in the back of Delray's bar
Smoke hung low like a verdict, neon bleeding through the jar
I had two pair and a feeling, Earl had nothing in his eyes
But a man who's lost his pension will gamble on his lies

We pushed it past the money, pushed it past the deed
Past the wedding ring he slid across the felt like a seed
There's a line you cross at midnight you can't uncross at dawn
We were still there counting losses when the morning came on

He bet the truck, I called it, he bet the house, I stayed
Then he reached behind the counter for the price a coward paid
The table flipped, the chips rained down like teeth across the floor
And the click of Earl's revolver was a sound we'd heard before

Nobody won that hand, boys, nobody walked out clean
The sheriff took the photographs, I took the in-between

We pushed it past the money, pushed it past the deed
Past the wedding ring he slid across the felt like a seed
There's a line you cross at midnight you can't uncross at dawn
And Earl's still in the county and the table's still withdrawn

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the rowboat when the harbor moon was high
White rum in the barrels and a prayer up to the sky
My cousin at the oars and me a-watching for the light
The coast guard cutter humming somewhere out there in the night

Oh, the run gone wrong, gone wrong, mi brother
Tide turn against we and we couldn't find no other
Spilled the sweet fire in the salt and the foam
Now we swimming, only swimming, trying to make it home

The engine choked and sputtered and the searchlight found we plain
We dumped the precious cargo just to spare we from the chain
Silver bubbles rising where a fortune used to be
And the whole month's labor giving up itself to the sea

Greed it have a weight, you know, it sink you like a stone
Better come back empty-handed than you never come back home

Oh, the run gone wrong, gone wrong, mi brother
Tide turn against we and we couldn't find no other
Spilled the sweet fire in the salt and the foam
Now we swimming, only swimming, trying to make it home

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
The man from the city came knocking with a pen
Said son, sand off the corners, write it simple for them
Put a brand in the second line, a smile in the third
And I'll turn your hungry winters into golden words

But I keep my crooked verses where the firelight shows
I'd rather eat the cold soup than wear a borrowed rose
They can buy the printing presses, they can buy the crowd
They can't buy the quiet thing that says a poem out loud

My boots have got no shine on them, my coat is patched and thin
But every line I leave you is the truth I'm standing in
The landlord wants his money and the baker wants his due
And the muse, she only whispers when you're broke enough to listen true

Let the famous ones go gilded, let them rhyme for the king
I'll keep my candle burning over the unprofitable thing

So I keep my crooked verses where the firelight shows
I'd rather eat the cold soup than wear a borrowed rose
They can buy the printing presses, they can buy the crowd
They can't buy the quiet thing that says a poem out loud

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Slick man rolled up in a Cadillac of gold
Said baby, drop the truth talk, do what you been told
Lay a hook on the money, put a price on the rhyme
I said keep your shiny contract, my words ain't yours to sign

I won't sell it, won't sell it, no
Groove so deep you can't buy what I owe
Bass in my backbone, fire in my mouth
I'd rather walk it broke than walk it sold out

They want the clean-cut version with the edges sanded down
A jingle for the jewelry, a grin for the crown
But my pen got a heartbeat, my page got a spine
And the funk in the feeling don't bend for a dime

Get up, get down, but don't get bought
The realest thing you got is the thing that can't be caught
They'll dress it up in diamonds, they'll auction off the sound
But the soul keep on stepping when the price comes around

I won't sell it, won't sell it, no
Groove so deep you can't buy what I owe
Bass in my backbone, fire in my mouth
I'd rather walk it broke than walk it sold out

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
Empty pews and a streetlight, two a.m. on the train
I prayed until the words wore thin and nothing answered back again
Left my Sunday on the platform, left the hymn beneath the seat
Walked into the city humming to the rhythm of my feet

Then the bassline found me on the floor
Lifted up the doubt I couldn't carry anymore
Hands up in the dark, every stranger like a sign
I lost it in the silence, found it in the four-four time

Sweat and light and the kick drum knocking on the chest like a door
A thousand bodies breathing what I came here looking for
It wasn't in the steeple, it was here all along
The holy in the heartbeat, the gospel in the song

Raise your hands, raise your hands, let it move through
Whatever you let go of is coming back to you
Let it move, let it move, through the night

Then the bassline found me on the floor
Lifted up the doubt I couldn't carry anymore
Hands up in the dark, every stranger like a sign
I lost it in the silence, found it in the four-four time

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Folded paper in my pocket and a tremble in my knees
Sixteen and the spotlight turned my whisper to a breeze
The coffee machine was hissing and the room was barely there
But I stepped into the circle and I left it in the air

Now the night comes back in waves, in waves
That shaking little kid is who the music saved
Turn the bassline up and let the memory ride
The first time I was nobody and somebody inside

Three people and a barista and a flickering little sign
I forgot the second verse and made the rest of it up fine
They clapped like it was Carnegie, they clapped like I was real
And I've been chasing down that feeling for the way it made me feel

Close your eyes and the room is full again
Every brave and stupid thing you started back then
Let it ride, let it ride, through the years

Now the night comes back in waves, in waves
That shaking little kid is who the music saved
Turn the bassline up and let the memory ride
The first time I was nobody and somebody inside

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew its last today, the gates are chained and still
Forty years of clatter gone to quiet on the hill
My father pressed the steel here, his father pressed it too
Now the windows watch the snowfall with a dull and empty blue

So play, clarinet, play, make the sorrow turn to dance
The Lord gives and the Lord takes and leaves us half a chance
We'll cry and we'll spin, we'll cry and we'll spin
For the work that's walking out the door and won't be coming in

The foreman shook our hands and looked away to hide his eyes
A pension like a paper boat beneath the winter skies
The machines stand like old men who forgot the songs they knew
And the dust is laying blankets where the workers used to brew

What the violin remembers, let the dancing not forget
We survived the worse than this, and we are standing yet

So play, clarinet, play, make the sorrow turn to dance
The Lord gives and the Lord takes and leaves us half a chance
We'll cry and we'll spin, we'll cry and we'll spin
For the work that's walking out the door and won't be coming in

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Chain-link and the same old corner, glass like fallen stars
The streetlight knew my shadow longer than it knew the passing cars
They said the block would keep me, said the gate was painted shut
But I counted every crack in it and waited for the cut

And it builds, and it builds, and it breaks
Everything I swallowed for the leaving that it takes
One road out and a sky too wide to name
I walked until the dead end couldn't call me by my name

The bus came at the morning like a door I'd never tried
My whole life in a duffel bag, the silence at my side
The towers shrank behind me into something I could hold
A fist that used to grip me letting go of its hold

Louder now, louder now, let the whole sky open wide
Everything I came from is the engine, not the tide
Rise, rise, the horizon's not a wall

And it builds, and it builds, and it breaks
Everything I swallowed for the leaving that it takes
One road out and a sky too wide to name
I walked until the dead end couldn't call me by my name

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Last pew, back row, watching the candles bend
Mouthing the words I memorized but couldn't feel again
The organ pulled the ceiling up, the saints looked down so kind
I buttoned up my coat and left the certainty behind

No slamming door, no thunder, just a click and then the cold
Walking out the quiet way they never told
The city took my breathing and it sped it up to mine
I'm running on a faster, truer line

They didn't see me go, and maybe that's the gentlest part
No argument, no fire, just a softening in the heart
I keep the songs, I lose the cage, I keep the better half
The rest dissolving in the rain along the underpass

Faster, faster, the pulse of the night
Nobody's watching, nobody's right
Just the rhythm and the dark and the breaking of the light

No slamming door, no thunder, just a click and then the cold
Walking out the quiet way they never told
The city took my breathing and it sped it up to mine
I'm running on a faster, truer line

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night about the same time the ceiling starts to fade
And my shoulders feel the loosening of the weight the day had made
The rooftops drop beneath me, the wires let me through
And the moon don't ask my business as I'm climbing toward the blue

I'm flying, oh, I'm flying in my sleep
Over all the promises this tired world won't keep
Don't wake me, no, don't wake me from the height
Where the only thing that's holding me is light

I seen my mama's kitchen from a thousand feet of air
I seen the boy I used to be still waiting on the stair
My arms become two open wings, my fear becomes a song
And for once in all my living I am right where I belong

Then the morning comes and pins me to the bed
With the gravity of everything I left unsaid
But I know come tomorrow when I close my heavy eyes
I'll go climbing, I'll go climbing through the skies

I'm flying, oh, I'm flying in my sleep
Over all the promises this tired world won't keep
Don't wake me, no, don't wake me from the height
Where the only thing that's holding me is light

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They poured the concrete soft and slow over the breathing ground
Laid the avenues like ribbons where the old one's heart still pound
We drink our coffee on his ribcage, hang our laundry on his spine
And pretend the tremors in the teacup are just passing time

Sleep, giant, sleep beneath the lights
We built our little glittering thing on your endless nights
Don't open up your ancient eyes, don't shake us from your skin
We're only just now learning how to let a little wonder in

The subway hums a lullaby through tunnels in his bones
The streetlights are the dreaming of a thing that sleeps alone
Grandmother says she felt him stir the year the river dried
And the whole of downtown shivered like a secret it can't hide

What we build on borrowed slumber we had better build with grace
For a giant only loans you for a while his resting place

Sleep, giant, sleep beneath the lights
We built our little glittering thing on your endless nights
Don't open up your ancient eyes, don't shake us from your skin
We're only just now learning how to let a little wonder in

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the diner when the dark is still around
Clock out and drive the forklift till the second shift winds down
The coffee's gone to water and the days have lost their seams
And somewhere in the overtime I lost the room for dreams

Two jobs and the rent still climbing like a hill I cannot crest
I'm running just to stay in place, I never get to rest
The numbers never balance no matter how I try
I'm working twice as hard as ever, falling twice behind

My daughter draws me pictures of a daddy that she missed
I tape them to the dashboard where the morning sun gets kissed
The bank sends me its letters in their cold official blue
And I fold them in my pocket with the things I cannot do

They tell me pull the bootstraps, tell me grind a little more
But the bootstraps come apart in hands already raw and sore

Two jobs and the rent still climbing like a hill I cannot crest
I'm running just to stay in place, I never get to rest
The numbers never balance no matter how I try
I'm working twice as hard as ever, falling twice behind

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
There's a light on Route 9 that never learned to sleep
Bottomless coffee, vinyl booths, and secrets it will keep
The trucker and the heartbroke and the kid who missed the bus
All find a kind of mercy in the hum and the fuss

The neon says we're open, we are open all night
Where the lonely come for pie and a little bit of light
Nobody asks your story but they'll pour another cup
The diner doesn't judge you and it never closes up

Sheila's worked the counter since the year the mill went dry
She knows your order's trouble by the looking in your eye
The jukebox plays the slow ones for the souls who can't go home
And two a.m. feels gentler when you're not the only one alone

The world out there is closing every door against the cold
But here the booth is warm and here the coffee's never old

The neon says we're open, we are open all night
Where the lonely come for pie and a little bit of light
Nobody asks your story but they'll pour another cup
The diner doesn't judge you and it never closes up

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Well the big shot waved a contract and a fat cigar
Said kid, you'll be a household name, you'll be a shining star
Just round off all the angry parts and sweeten up the line
I tipped my hat and told him, friend, these verses ain't for sale, they're mine

So swing it, boys, I'd rather croon the truth and stay flat broke
Than dress my words in dollar signs and choke on my own smoke
I'll take the smoky basement and the two-buck door
The poet that they purchased ain't a poet anymore

The critics in their fancy coats said honey, learn to bend
A little less of bite and they will make you their best friend
But I clinked my glass of water in the corner of the room
And let my crooked stanzas knock the silence off the gloom

You can gild a cage in glory but a bird still wants the sky
And a rhyme that's bought and bottled is a rhyme that's gone to die

So swing it, boys, I'd rather croon the truth and stay flat broke
Than dress my words in dollar signs and choke on my own smoke
I'll take the smoky basement and the two-buck door
The poet that they purchased ain't a poet anymore

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Neon static on the ballroom floor
I don't know your name and I don't need it anymore
The walls are melting into velvet sound
Your hand finds mine and the whole room slows down

Strangers in the haze of borrowed light
Two satellites colliding in the night
The band is just a rumor in my chest
I lean into the noise and let it press

So dance with me, dissolve with me
We're nothing and we're everything tonight
Dance with me, revolve with me
A stranger and a flicker of the light

The cake goes stale, the toasts all fade away
But here inside this blur we'll always stay
I'll forget your face by morning, that's the cost
But for one song we're beautifully lost

Ohh, the reverb swallows everything we are
Ohh, we're spinning like a half-remembered star

So dance with me, dissolve with me
We're nothing and we're everything tonight
Dance with me, revolve with me
A stranger and a flicker of the light

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
We met where the tide kissed the sand
You wrote your number in my palm with your hand
The sun hung low like a copper coin
And summer was a road we couldn't help but join

We shared a peach and a borrowed guitar
You said the ocean knows just who we are
We didn't talk of autumn or the cold
We were young and the night was made of gold

Oh, summer girl, oh, summer day
We knew that you'd be leaving anyway
But the sea remembers, the sea won't tell
The way you said my name and the way I fell

The boardwalk lights, the salt on your skin
The season ends the way it always begins
You caught the morning bus and waved goodbye
And I let the seagulls carry off my sigh

And if I'm ever lost out on the foam
I'll think of you and the sand we called a home

Oh, summer girl, oh, summer day
We knew that you'd be leaving anyway
But the sea remembers, the sea won't tell
The way you said my name and the way I fell

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Ay, mi corazón, the phone lit up at dawn
Three little lines and then your love was gone
No serenade, no roses at your door
Just a gray little bubble and nothing more

The trumpets cry the way I cannot say
You wrote goodbye and threw my heart away
No final dance beneath the plaza light
Just a screen that flickered out the night

Ay, ay, ay, you texted me adiós
Ay, ay, ay, the cruelest of the blows
The mariachi plays but you don't hear
A love undone by words upon a screen, my dear

I'll drink my tequila to the empty chair
And sing to the moon that doesn't care
You could have called, you could have come to see
But a message was all you gave to me

Guitarrón, weep low, violines, weep high
For a love that ended with a typed goodbye

Ay, ay, ay, you texted me adiós
Ay, ay, ay, the cruelest of the blows
The mariachi plays but you don't hear
A love undone by words upon a screen, my dear

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Midnight engine, diamonds on the dash
One more run before I make my dash
The city's glittering, the meter's running hot
This is the only life I ever got

Drive, drive, one last time tonight
Under the neon and the streetlamp light
Drive, drive, don't look behind
Leave the whole world and the past behind

They're in the back, the cash is in the bag
The sirens wail like a disco red flag
I hit the gas and the strings come alive
This is the rhythm that keeps me alive

Drive, drive, one last time tonight
Under the neon and the streetlamp light
Drive, drive, don't look behind
Leave the whole world and the past behind

Just one more corner, baby, one more mile
Then I'll hang up the keys and rest a while
The mirrorball is spinning like a wheel
And freedom is the only thing I feel

The sun comes up on an empty road
I dropped the weight, I dropped the load
No more running, no more night
The getaway driver fades into the light

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle and thread in the amber light
He stitches the dreams of the block tonight
A lapel for the banker, a hem for the bride
The whole neighborhood wears his pride

The bandoneón sighs as he measures the cloth
Every seam a vow, every cut an oath
He dresses the poor like they're dressed for a king
And the city walks taller in everything

Oh, the tailor, the tailor, the thread never breaks
He sews up the sorrow, whatever it takes
From velvet to denim, from cradle to grave
He fits us in dignity, gentle and brave

The widow in mourning, the boy in his suit
The dancer in scarlet, the man in pursuit
They all climb his stairway and ring the small bell
And leave with a secret only fabric can tell

Dance, dance, in the clothes that he made
Every stitch a kindness that will never fade

Oh, the tailor, the tailor, the thread never breaks
He sews up the sorrow, whatever it takes
From velvet to denim, from cradle to grave
He fits us in dignity, gentle and brave

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
The candle burns low on the felt-green table
Aces and shadows, as long as I'm able
Guitar strings tremble like a thief in the dark
Each card I turn is a spark, is a spark

I wagered my house, I wagered my name
The heat of the night, the fever, the flame
He smiled like a serpent across the divide
And the queen of spades was on his side

¡Olé! the cards have turned to fire
¡Olé! I've climbed too high a wire
I bet my soul on a single hand
And lost it all in this burning land

The clapping hands, the stomping heel
The blood runs hot, the danger's real
He took my coins, he took my pride
Now there's nowhere left to hide

The palmas thunder, the night won't end
I'd give it all to deal again

¡Olé! the cards have turned to fire
¡Olé! I've climbed too high a wire
I bet my soul on a single hand
And lost it all in this burning land

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Doppler on the dashboard, coffee going cold
Chasing supercells, never doing what I'm told
Funnel on the skyline, I floor it through the rain
Adrenaline's the only thing that keeps me sane
Mama said settle, find a girl, buy a home
But the sky keeps calling and I gotta roam
Green clouds rolling, hail like stones
I live for the moment the wind makes its groan

I can't settle down, I'm chasing the storm
Where the wind is wild and the air gets warm
Got a tank full of gas and a heart full of thunder
I'll never stay still, I'll never go under

Wall cloud spinning, EF-4 on the rise
I got the lightning reflected in my eyes
She wanted forever, I gave her goodbye
Can't love a man who's married to the sky
Motel rooms and radar screens
Living in between the in-betweens
A quiet life ain't built for me
I chase the chaos to feel free

The sirens scream and the people hide
But I run toward the other side
One day the storm gon' take me whole
But until then it owns my soul

I can't settle down, I'm chasing the storm
Where the wind is wild and the air gets warm
Got a tank full of gas and a heart full of thunder
I'll never stay still, I'll never go under

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down on a Tuesday street
You had the cover and I had the beat
We didn't speak but we shared the sky
One little umbrella, you and I

Your shoulder touched mine at the crosswalk light
Two strangers stitched by the storm that night
The buses passed and the puddles grew
And somehow I was falling for you

Under one umbrella, under one gray cloud
We found a little quiet in the middle of the crowd
I don't even know you but I don't want to go
Under one umbrella in the rain and the glow

We walked three blocks that felt like years
The city blurred and disappeared
You turned to leave at the subway stair
And I held the umbrella to the empty air

Maybe the rain knew something we didn't
Maybe forever was a moment hidden

Under one umbrella, under one gray cloud
We found a little quiet in the middle of the crowd
I don't even know you but I don't want to go
Under one umbrella in the rain and the glow

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The door clicks shut and the silence swells
A tide of static and unspoken bells
Your shadow's still warm on the other side
A wall of sound where the words have died

I hear the hum of the empty room
The fluorescent buzz, the gentle gloom
We said too much and we said too little
Now we're stranded in the brittle middle

And it's quiet, so quiet, after the storm
The kind of quiet that can't keep you warm
I'd take the shouting over this hush
The deafening silence, the empty crush

My heart's a feedback loop tonight
Drowning slowly in the dimming light
I'll knock again when the noise dissolves
And hope the quiet is what love resolves

Ohh, the reverb of a slamm-shut door
Ohh, I don't want to fight anymore

And it's quiet, so quiet, after the storm
The kind of quiet that can't keep you warm
I'd take the shouting over this hush
The deafening silence, the empty crush

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete river where the meadow used to flow
One green soldier in a long gray row
They paved the garden, they paved the stream
But the last little tree still holds the dream

Stand up, stand tall, oh last tree standing
Roots in the rubble, branches expanding
They took the forest but they left you here
A green little flag in a world of fear

The children gather in your gentle shade
The only cool in the trade they made
The bulldozers rumble but you won't fall
The last green heartbeat over the wall

Stand up, stand tall, oh last tree standing
Roots in the rubble, branches expanding
They took the forest but they left you here
A green little flag in a world of fear

One seed, one root, one drop of rain
Can bring the whole green world again
So water the cracks in the broken ground
And grow a forest in this paved-down town

Jah see the green and Jah see the gray
And the last little tree gonna find a way
Sing it loud and sing it free
There's a whole new world in one small tree

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Well I shined up my shoes and I combed back my hair
Walked into the club like I owned the air
The band struck up and the spotlight grew
And for one shining minute the dream came true

The crowd leaned in and the trumpets blew
I hit every note like I always knew
The manager nodded, the ladies sighed
I was one short step from the other side

Oh, I almost made it, almost touched the sky
One more break and I'd have learned to fly
The night was golden, the band was hot
I almost made it but I almost's all I got

Then the mic went dead and the lights went low
The big shot left before the final show
I bowed to a room that was halfway gone
But baby, for a moment, I was the song

Close don't pay the rent, they say
But I had my night, come what may

Oh, I almost made it, almost touched the sky
One more break and I'd have learned to fly
The night was golden, the band was hot
I almost made it but I almost's all I got

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Soft is the evening on Ipanema sand
I still feel the warmth of your slipping hand
The waves keep a secret they'll never tell
The name of the girl I loved so well

You laughed like the breeze on a quiet sea
And you took the best part of me
A cafezinho, a samba slow
And then you were gone like the undertow

You're the one, the one that got away
A gentle song I hum each day
The tide rolls in, the tide rolls out
And you're the love I dream about

I watch the sailboats drift and gleam
And carry off a faded dream
If I could turn the years around
I'd hold the love I never found

Saudade, oh, the sweetest pain
Loving a ghost in the soft warm rain

You're the one, the one that got away
A gentle song I hum each day
The tide rolls in, the tide rolls out
And you're the love I dream about

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Well I packed up my bags and I tuned my radio
There's a twister out in Kansas and I gotta go
My baby said honey, won't you stay in town
But I can't sit still when the sky comes down

Got a hop in my step and a swing in my soul
Chasing the thunder is my only goal
The band plays hot while the wind plays cold
A rolling stone don't ever grow old

I'm a storm-chasin' daddy and I can't slow down
I follow the funnels from town to town
You can't put a ring on a man like me
I'm married to the wind and the wind runs free

The clouds roll black and the trumpets blare
There's lightning and rhythm in the electric air
She wanted a house with a picket gate
But the storm's my dance and I just can't wait

So blow, wind, blow, let the brass section wail
I'll chase you through the hills and the hail
One day I'll settle, but darling, not now
The sky's got a swing and I gotta know how

I'm a storm-chasin' daddy and I can't slow down
I follow the funnels from town to town
You can't put a ring on a man like me
I'm married to the wind and the wind runs free

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Dejé mi tierra con el sol cayendo
Una maleta y un corazón rompiendo
The ocean wide and the future blind
Leaving everything and everyone behind

Cruzando el mar, cruzando el mar
I'm chasing a dream to a faraway shore
Cruzando el mar, cruzando el mar
I'll carry my home in my heart forevermore

Mamá me dijo, mijo, sé valiente
The waves are high but my faith is fervent
New city lights and a language I don't know
But I came too far, I can't let go

Cruzando el mar, cruzando el mar
I'm chasing a dream to a faraway shore
Cruzando el mar, cruzando el mar
I'll carry my home in my heart forevermore

Dale, dale, no pares de soñar
Every wave I cross is one step to my star
The sea is wide but my spirit is wider
A stranger in the morning, a survivor

Un día volveré con la frente en alto
With pockets of gold and stories I've brought
But tonight I sail with the moon as my guide
My roots in my blood and the world open wide

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
We grew up under the same gray sky
The water tower and the lullaby
I never saw you till you came back home
Now the whole town glitters like a chrome

The diner lights and the empty square
You brushed the dust right out of the air
We walked the streets that we used to know
And everything was soft and slow

Falling, falling, for a hometown face
A familiar heart in a familiar place
We came from nowhere, now we're floating high
Falling for you under the same old sky

The corner store and the schoolyard fence
It all makes a sudden kind of sense
You were here before I knew to look
A half-remembered open book

Ooh, the streetlights hum a melody
Ooh, you're the home I couldn't see

Falling, falling, for a hometown face
A familiar heart in a familiar place
We came from nowhere, now we're floating high
Falling for you under the same old sky

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew, the train pulled in at nine
My duffel slung, these boots no longer mine
The platform smell of coffee and of rain
I'm trading mud for Mama's window pane

The band downtown still swings that same old tune
The soda fountain glowing like the moon
A girl I left is waving down the line
Her lipstick red, her dancing shoes divine

So strike up the horns and let the brass ring loud
I'm home, I'm home, kick your heels off the ground
No more marching, no more cannon sound
Just two-step me dizzy and spin me around

The fellas back home buy my drinks all night
They toast to the ones who never won the fight
I raise my glass and hum a quiet hymn
Then take her hand before the lights go dim

The years can rust a rifle but not a song
The city's still here, baby, all along
So hold me close and let the trumpets cry
We danced through hell and lived to say goodbye

So strike up the horns and let the brass ring loud
I'm home, I'm home, kick your heels off the ground
No more marching, no more cannon sound
Just two-step me dizzy and spin me around

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
There's a little red chair where the whole town goes
He'll trim up your fade and he'll tell you who knows
Who's getting married, who lost the big game
Who moved away and who came back the same

Clippers humming like a small parade
Every secret in this town gets made

Frankie knows your name, Frankie knows your story
Every scar and every shot at glory
Sit down in the chair, the mirror's gonna shine
He's the heartbeat of the whole town in a line
Frankie knows, oh, Frankie knows

Kids get their first cut and the old men stay late
He sweeps up the hair like he's sweeping up fate
A photo of the mayor when the mayor was nine
A jar full of stories and a half-empty wine

Frankie knows your name, Frankie knows your story
Every scar and every shot at glory
Sit down in the chair, the mirror's gonna shine
He's the heartbeat of the whole town in a line
Frankie knows, oh, Frankie knows

When the streetlights buzz and he locks up the door
He knows every house behind every floor
He carries this town like a comb in his hand
The quietest king of a one-stoplight land

Frankie knows your name, Frankie knows your story
Every scar and every shot at glory
Sit down in the chair, the mirror's gonna shine
He's the heartbeat of the whole town in a line

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the avenue, headlights low
Same cracked window where I used to glow
Third floor light is somebody else's now
I slow the wheel down, I don't know how

The city hums a frequency I know
My hands on the wheel won't let it go

Drive past, drive past, don't you stop
Let the bassline carry what I lost
Drive past, drive past, neon rain
Every streetlight knows my name again

I could park right here and just remember
Summer turning slowly into ember
We danced in that kitchen 'til the morning
Now it's just a ghost without a warning

Drive past, drive past, don't you stop
Let the bassline carry what I lost
Drive past, drive past, neon rain
Every streetlight knows my name again

Four-on-the-floor like my heartbeat then
I'll loop this block just once and then
Let the green light pull me on
By the time the chorus drops you're gone

Drive past, drive past, don't you stop
Let the bassline carry what I lost
Drive past, drive past, neon rain
Every streetlight knows my name again

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Each night when the candle burns down to the floor
I rise like the mist off the cold mountain moor
My shoulders grow feathers, my feet leave the stone
And over the heather I'm flying alone

The loch lies below me a mirror of grey
The gulls call my name as I carry away
The chimneys, the steeples, the green and the gold
A country in miniature, ancient and old

Fly, fly, over the glen
I'll never come down to the village again
The wind is my fiddle, the cloud is my song
And I have been flying my whole life long

But dawn breaks the spell with its grey honest light
And I wake to the weight of the world and its plight
My boots by the door and the kettle to fill
Yet somewhere inside me I'm soaring there still

They say it's a longing, a soul stretching free
A bird in the ribcage that's pining for sea
So bury me high where the curlews still wheel
And maybe, just maybe, the dream will be real

Fly, fly, over the glen
I'll never come down to the village again
The wind is my fiddle, the cloud is my song
And I have been flying my whole life long

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
(Sha-la-la) The bus leaves at a quarter to nine
(Sha-la-la) I packed up this old heart of mine
Goodbye to the diner, goodbye to the square
I'm taking my dreams to the city out there

So long, so long, my little hometown
(Bom-bom) I'm laying my old life down
The stars over Main Street can shine without me
I'm gone, I'm gone, where the bright lights be
(Sha-la-la-la-la)

(Sha-la-la) Mary Jean waved from her porch
(Sha-la-la) Mr. Dell turned off his torch
The water tower painted with somebody's name
This place stays the same, but I can't stay the same

So long, so long, my little hometown
(Bom-bom) I'm laying my old life down
The stars over Main Street can shine without me
I'm gone, I'm gone, where the bright lights be
(Sha-la-la-la-la)

Maybe I'll cry by the time we hit the line
Maybe I'll miss every porch light and pine
But a boy's gotta go where his future is found
(Bom-bom-bom) so long, hometown

So long, so long, my little hometown
(Bom-bom) I'm laying my old life down
The stars over Main Street can shine without me
I'm gone, I'm gone, where the bright lights be

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The lamp is a halo, the pages all swim
The radiator ticking, the window grown dim
My coffee's gone cold in the cup by my hand
And the words on the textbook are wet shifting sand

The whole building sleeping, the hallway a hum
I'm chasing a meaning that won't ever come
The clock on the wall is a slow drowning sound
And two in the morning is holy and drowned

Wash over me, wash over me
The static, the longing, the half-things I see
I'm dissolving in fluorescent light
Just me and the night and the night and the night

My reflection is ghosting the glass where I sit
The future's a fog and I'm lost inside it
But there's something so soft in this lonely and late
Like the world holds its breath at the edge of a gate

Fade, fade, into the page
A blur of a body, a haze of an age
Nobody's awake but the hum in my chest
And the quiet is louder than all of the rest

Wash over me, wash over me
The static, the longing, the half-things I see
I'm dissolving in fluorescent light
Just me and the night and the night and the night

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
Le hablo a una silla vacía esta noche
The words that I never could say, ya no importan
I carried the anger like stones in my chest
Now I'm laying them down so my soul can get rest

Ya no estás, but I'm letting you go
The weight that I held, now I'm letting it flow

Te perdono, te perdono
Aunque ya no estés aquí
Dejo todo en el viento
Forgiving you finally sets me free
Te perdono, baila el corazón
Letting go is the only song

I talk to your photo, I talk to the rain
The grudge that I held only doubled my pain
Wherever you are now, I hope that you hear
I'm dropping the weight that I carried for years

Te perdono, te perdono
Aunque ya no estés aquí
Dejo todo en el viento
Forgiving you finally sets me free
Te perdono, baila el corazón
Letting go is the only song

Dembow steady like my breathing now
No guardo rencor, I released it somehow
Gone is gone but my heart can be light
Te perdono, mami, te perdono tonight

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine running, idling out back
Three minutes promised, then they're coming back
My hands are steady, my heart's a snare
Last job, last job, I swear I swear

Got a girl in Tucson and a clean ID
One more run and then they'll never find me
Sirens in the distance like a broken clock
Foot on the pedal, hand on the lock

GO! GO! Burn the rubber off the line
GO! GO! This is the last time
No more waiting in the dark for thieves
Drop the clutch, baby, and never leave a trace
GO! GO! GO!

Doors slam, screaming, we're peeling away
Backseat full of somebody else's pay
Redline screaming up the interstate
Last job, last job, don't tempt your fate

GO! GO! Burn the rubber off the line
GO! GO! This is the last time
No more waiting in the dark for thieves
Drop the clutch, baby, and never leave a trace
GO! GO! GO!

The last job always goes to hell
That's how all the dead men fell
But I'm not dead, I'm just gone
Foot on the floor and the radio on
GO!

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Thirty-eight people and a busted stop sign
Dead-end diner serving dead-end time
I scratched my name in the water tower steel
Now I'm gone, baby, and you know how I feel

Same three streets and the same dead bar
Same guys bragging 'bout their broken car
I'm not gonna die where my granddad died
Foot on the gas and the windows wide

I'M GONE! I'M GONE! Don't you wait for me
This town's a coffin, I'm a felony
Tear the rearview mirror off the glass
This dead little town can kiss my exhaust
I'M GONE! I'M GONE!

Mama said stay and Daddy said pray
The preacher said the city'll rot you away
But I'd rather rot than stand and rust
In a one-horse town turning all to dust

I'M GONE! I'M GONE! Don't you wait for me
This town's a coffin, I'm a felony
Tear the rearview mirror off the glass
This dead little town can kiss my exhaust
I'M GONE! I'M GONE!

No goodbye notes, no last parade
Just a slammed screen door and a debt unpaid
Watch the welcome sign shrink in the night
I'M GONE FOR GOOD AND IT FEELS ALRIGHT

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Trunk full of mason jars, clear as the dawn
Back roads only, headlights gone
Uncle Ray's recipe, hundred and ten
Run it to the county line, do it again
But tonight the moon hung low and red
Shoulda stayed home is what my grandma said
Gravel kicking, dust in the air
Headlights flash — somebody's there

Run, run, the whiskey's gonna spill
Flashing blue lights coming over the hill
Run, run, but the road runs out
This is what a bad run's all about

Hairpin turn and the back end slid
Glass to the floor like a busted bid
Liquor on the asphalt, sweet and strong
Every jar I packed now totally gone
Deputy laughing, cuffs come out
That's a felony charge with a country drawl shout
Grandma's gonna kill me 'fore the judge can try
Moonshine running's how good men die

Learned my lesson on a dead dirt road
Never carry more than your heart can hold
The still's still bubbling but I'm sitting in a cell
Moonshine money buys a one-way trip to hell

Run, run, the whiskey's gonna spill
Flashing blue lights coming over the hill
Run, run, but the road runs out
This is what a bad run's all about

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
El sol salió pero el cuarto sigue gris
Black suit on the chair where I left it like this
Café getting cold and the flowers still here
La casa is louder with nobody near

La mañana después, the world keeps on turning
My chest is a candle that won't stop burning

Mañana después, mañana después
La vida sigue pero el alma no sabe qué hacer
Dembow slow like a heartbeat in pain
Waking up to the silence and the rain
Mañana después, te llevo en la piel

Phone full of mensajes I don't wanna read
Somebody brought food that I'm too tired to need
I button the cuffs and I stare at the door
The sun says go on but I can't anymore

Mañana después, mañana después
La vida sigue pero el alma no sabe qué hacer
Dembow slow like a heartbeat in pain
Waking up to the silence and the rain
Mañana después, te llevo en la piel

Pongo tu canción y bailo solo en el cuarto
Grief is a rhythm I never asked to start
But you'd want me dancing, así que lo haré
Mañana después, otra vez de pie

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
The clarinet cries down the alley so tight
Where the brick meets the brick and there's barely a light
My zayde sold buttons, my papa sold shoes
And the block held us close like a debt you can't lose

But I packed up a fiddle and a name they can't break
And I danced past the gutters for my own sweet sake
The whole street was watching from the fire escape
As I two-stepped my way through the iron gate

Oy, I made it out, I made it out
Lift up your feet, let the trumpets shout
From a dead-end block to an open sky
Freylekh, freylekh, watch me fly
Oy, I made it out at last

The fruit cart, the pushcart, the rent always due
The whole crooked block knew my name and yours too
But the music kept calling from somewhere out far
And I followed the clarinet past every bar

So play it in a minor that turns into a grin
The sorrow and the joy are the same violin
I'll send for my mother, I'll send for my own
But I danced off the block and I'm never coming home

Oy, I made it out, I made it out
Lift up your feet, let the trumpets shout
From a dead-end block to an open sky
Freylekh, freylekh, watch me fly
Oy, I made it out at last

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Neon bleeding on the rain-slick street
Midnight downpour, nowhere dry to meet
You stepped beneath my little nylon sky
Two strangers and the storm rolling by

Your shoulder touching mine in the pink and blue
I don't know your name and you don't know mine too
The traffic lights are smearing into gold
And the night is electric and the rain is cold

Under one umbrella, under one machine
Two glowing strangers in a synth-lit dream
Don't ask me your name, just stay till the dawn
Under neon rain before the moment's gone
Under one umbrella, you and me

We walked for a block and we walked for a year
The arcade hummed and the future felt near
You laughed at the puddles, I laughed at the sky
Two cassette-tape hearts on a loop running high

The rain's gonna stop and you'll fold into night
A stranger again under different light
But for one falling synth and one shimmering street
We were the whole world that the storm couldn't beat

Under one umbrella, under one machine
Two glowing strangers in a synth-lit dream
Don't ask me your name, just stay till the dawn
Under neon rain before the moment's gone

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
The café is quiet, the rain falls slow
You stir your coffee, you don't even know
I memorized the way you tuck your hair
While you talk of someone who isn't there

The samba is soft and the evening is blue
And every soft note is a letter to you
But you smile at the window, you smile at the sea
And you never once turn that smile to me

So I'll love you in silence, gentle and low
Like the tide loves a shore it can never quite hold
You'll never quite hear it, you'll never quite see
This quiet little bossa I'm humming for thee

We dance once at parties, your hand light as air
Then you drift to another and leave me right there
I play it so cool with my drink and my song
While inside this small heart has been breaking so long

Maybe in some other softer blue night
You'd hear what I'm playing and turn to the light
But the bossa just whispers and lets the love be
A wave that returns to the patient slow sea

So I'll love you in silence, gentle and low
Like the tide loves a shore it can never quite hold
You'll never quite hear it, you'll never quite see
This quiet little bossa I'm humming for thee

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
(Bom-bom) Hands at ten and two, baby girl
(Sha-la-la) Slow and easy, give it a whirl
The seat's too far and the mirror's all wrong
But we got an empty lot and a radio song

Drive, drive, my little one
The road's wide open and the day's just begun
Ease off the brake, let the engine purr
(Sha-la-la) I'm right here, baby, right here, sir
Drive, drive, my little one

(Bom-bom) You stalled it twice by the old gas sign
(Sha-la-la) But you laughed and that laugh of mine
Is the same as your mother's the day that we met
In a borrowed old Ford that I'll never forget

Drive, drive, my little one
The road's wide open and the day's just begun
Ease off the brake, let the engine purr
(Sha-la-la) I'm right here, baby, right here, sir
Drive, drive, my little one

One day you'll drive clean out of my sight
Down some highway and into the night
But today you're still mine in the passenger glow
(Bom-bom) so take it slow, baby, take it slow

Drive, drive, my little one
The road's wide open and the day's just begun
Ease off the brake, let the engine purr
(Sha-la-la) I'm right here, baby, right here, sir
Drive, drive, my little one

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Folding table, duct-taped queen
Five bucks a game, I'll wipe you clean
Pigeons fighting over crust
Clock slams down, your knight is dust
Suit guy thinks he's got the brains
I traded sleep for these gambit chains

Mate in three, hand me the cash
Your ego's kindling, light the match
King's in the corner, nowhere to run
The park don't sleep and neither does the con

Cardboard sign says GENIUS HERE
Smells like hot dogs, weed, and fear
I sac the rook just to watch you grin
Then sac your wallet, that's the spin

Nobody taught me, the asphalt did
Learned the Sicilian from a homeless kid
They call it hustle, I call it art
Thirty-two pieces, one stone heart

Mate in three, hand me the cash
Your ego's kindling, light the match
King's in the corner, nowhere to run
The park don't sleep and neither does the con

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I been talking to an empty chair
Saying words I should've said
Your coffee cup still in the sink
A whole apology unsaid
The doctor's voice, the quiet hall
I never got to make it right
Now I'm holding all this softness
With nowhere to put it tonight

I forgive you, baby, I forgive you
Even though you cannot hear
I'm setting down this heavy stone I carried
So it don't follow me from here

It ain't for you, it's for my chest
That clenches every time it rains
I'm pulling all your splinters out
And blessing what is left of pain

Maybe somewhere in the static
You can feel me let it go
The grudge was just a tightrope
And I'm finally letting go

I forgive you, baby, I forgive you
Even though you cannot hear
I'm setting down this heavy stone I carried
So it don't follow me from here

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Flour on the counter, biscuit dough
Butter in a tin from years ago
Woodstove ticking, bacon hiss
There ain't a perfume sweeter than this
Apron strings and cinnamon
A screen door slappin' in the wind

Oh that kitchen, warm as June
Fiddle of the kettle, an old hymn tune
She stirred the pot and stirred my soul
Smell of home, it keeps me whole

Coffee bitter, peaches sweet
Lard and salt and Sunday meat
Her hands were maps of all her years
Kneaded out the worry and the tears

She's gone up yonder, kitchen's bare
But close my eyes, I'm standing there
The yeast still rising, soft and slow
In the only church that I would know

Oh that kitchen, warm as June
Fiddle of the kettle, an old hymn tune
She stirred the pot and stirred my soul
Smell of home, it keeps me whole

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Sold the mule and sold the plow
Mother's locket round my neck somehow
Green hills shrinking on the rail
Salt wind cracking the steamer's sail
Three weeks rolling, gray on gray
Everything I loved a world away

Carry me over, deep dark water
I'm a poor man's son, I'm a poor man's daughter
Leave the famine, leave the stone
God willing I will find a home

Below the deck the children cough
A fiddle plays to ward it off
We trade our names for foreign sound
For a fistful of unbroken ground

I'll write you letters I can't spell
Of the city and its iron bell
Keep my chair beside the fire
Though the sea has made us liars

Carry me over, deep dark water
I'm a poor man's son, I'm a poor man's daughter
Leave the famine, leave the stone
God willing I will find a home

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. and the floor still moves
I'm dancing out the things I lose
Your number's dead but the bassline lives
I close my eyes and I forgive
Lights are fractals on my skin
Letting all the old ghosts in

Let it go, let it go, into the sound
Wherever you are now, I'm setting you down
Let it go, let it go, into the night
I forgive you, I forgive you, and I'm alright

The synth swells up like the tide
Washing out the words we cried
No more replaying that last call
The drop arrives and I let it fall

Four to the floor, a steady heart
This is where the healing starts
Hands up high beneath the strobe
Releasing you across the globe

Let it go, let it go, into the sound
Wherever you are now, I'm setting you down
Let it go, let it go, into the night
I forgive you, I forgive you, and I'm alright

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The roll of tape screams one last time
Across a box of someone's life
The walls have rectangles of clean
Where all our posters used to be
Echo bouncing off the floor
Keys I'm sliding under the door

Seal it up, seal it up, mark it KITCHEN
Four years folded into a system
The light hits weird in an empty room
I'm taping up the last of you

Found a hair tie by the sink
A grocery list, your messy ink
The radiator's final clank
I'm grateful and I'm running blank

The Sharpie squeaks, the cardboard bends
This is how a chapter ends
Not with thunder, not with rain
Just packing tape and a U-Haul lane

Seal it up, seal it up, mark it KITCHEN
Four years folded into a system
The light hits weird in an empty room
I'm taping up the last of you

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Finger hovers over a ten-year number
Thumbnail bitten down to nothing
The ringtone sounds like a closing door
My whole chest is jackhammering
Swallowed all my stupid pride
With a payphone taste inside

Ma, it's me, don't hang up the line
I know I left you a hundred times
I'm older now and the anger's thin
Ma, it's me, will you let me in

Last we spoke I slammed the screen
Called you everything I didn't mean
Now I count the wrinkles I missed
In the silence of a clenched-up fist

Three rings, four rings, then you breathe
And say my name like you believe
The years collapse into the sound
Of your voice still hanging around

Ma, it's me, don't hang up the line
I know I left you a hundred times
I'm older now and the anger's thin
Ma, it's me, will you let me in

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Green sky humming over the wheat
I roll the windows, I love the heat
A woman waits in a quiet town
But the funnel calls and I can't slow down
Hail like diamonds on the hood
I'd stay for love if I only could

Soft thunder, take me away
I was never built to stay
The radar glows, my restless heart
Goes spinning, spinning, falling apart

She folds my shirts, she sets the table
I'm gone before the morning's able
A postcard from a Kansas rain
Is all the love I can sustain

Maybe one day the wind will tire
Maybe one day I'll dim this fire
Lay my head where the porch light glows
But tonight the wild barometer goes

Soft thunder, take me away
I was never built to stay
The radar glows, my restless heart
Goes spinning, spinning, falling apart

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
He used to lift me to the sky
Now I hold the cup to steady his hand
The newspaper trembles, gentle and slow
In the chair where the afternoon sun lands
His stories loop like a soft refrain
I nod and love him through it again

Time is a tide that takes the shore
The father I knew is here, and more
I'm learning the rhythm of letting go
While the bossa of his breathing goes low

He names the birds outside the glass
Forgets the year, remembers the past
His shoes by the door no longer worn
And I become the parent now, reborn

We dance a little in the hall
His weight against the bedroom wall
A waltz of all the years gone by
I hum the tune so he won't cry

Time is a tide that takes the shore
The father I knew is here, and more
I'm learning the rhythm of letting go
While the bossa of his breathing goes low

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Old brass bell above the door
Strips of hair across the floor
He's cut the mayor and the priest
And the boy who's now deceased
Scissors flashing, mirror wide
Every secret kept inside

Snip and tell, snip and tell
He knows the town and knows it well
From christening curls to widow's gray
He trims the years and trims the day

He knew your father by his cowlick
Knew the limp before the heart got sick
A dram of whiskey under the till
For the ones whose chairs are empty still

The striped pole turns through war and peace
The town confides, he grants release
No tower bell, no church, no crown
Just the barber who knows the whole town

Snip and tell, snip and tell
He knows the town and knows it well
From christening curls to widow's gray
He trims the years and trims the day

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
GREASE ON THE STOVE, SMOKE IN MY LUNGS
The only safe room when I was young
Burnt sugar, onions, a boiling pot
The one warm thing this cold world's got
Now it's drywall and a FOR SALE sign
They gutted the heart that was mine

I CAN STILL SMELL IT
THROUGH THE CONCRETE AND THE RAGE
I CAN STILL SMELL IT
BURNING OFF THE PAGE
DON'T YOU TELL ME IT'S GONE
IT LIVES IN MY BONES

They paved her garden, sold the plates
Auctioned off the cast-iron grates
But the smell of her bread is a riot in me
A fist against forgetting, can't you see

Every slammed door, every fight
She'd feed me whole and make it right
NOW I CARRY THAT FIRE, I CARRY THAT HEAT
A KITCHEN GHOST on every street

I CAN STILL SMELL IT
THROUGH THE CONCRETE AND THE RAGE
I CAN STILL SMELL IT
BURNING OFF THE PAGE
DON'T YOU TELL ME IT'S GONE
IT LIVES IN MY BONES

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
MUD AND BEER AND A DYING SUN
Three days of war, this is the last one
The field's a graveyard of broken tents
My voice is shot, my whole body spent
ONE MORE BAND, ONE MORE WALL
WE GIVE IT EVERYTHING OR NOTHING AT ALL

LAST NIGHT, LAST CHANCE
BLEED INTO THE FINAL DANCE
LAST NIGHT, LAST SCREAM
TEAR THE STITCHES OUT THE SEAM

Strangers' arms around my neck
A city built and left a wreck
The headliner counts it in
AND THE WHOLE FIELD ROARS, LET THE END BEGIN

Tomorrow it's just trash and rain
The long drive home, the dull gray pain
But RIGHT NOW we are infinite and loud
A hundred thousand thunderclouds

LAST NIGHT, LAST CHANCE
BLEED INTO THE FINAL DANCE
LAST NIGHT, LAST SCREAM
TEAR THE STITCHES OUT THE SEAM

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Got a tank of gas and a radar app
A cracked windshield and a fold-out map
You asked me to stay for the summer
But the sky turned the color of a bruise
And I chose the thunder

I'm a chaser, chaser, can't be still
Following the funnel over every hill
You want a porch and a steady name
But my heart only beats in the eye of the rain
Oh-oh, I'm a chaser, can't slow down
Love me at a hundred miles, I won't touch the ground

Lightning's tattooed in my eyes
I fall for the wreckage in the skies
You texted come home, I'm still here
But home is a word that just disappears

Maybe I'm scared of the quiet, babe
Maybe the calm is what I can't brave
So I drive into the dark gray wide
With your photo on the dash for the ride

I'm a chaser, chaser, can't be still
Following the funnel over every hill
You want a porch and a steady name
But my heart only beats in the eye of the rain
Oh-oh, I'm a chaser, can't slow down
Love me at a hundred miles, I won't touch the ground

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Antena en la azotea, cables y sudor
Frequency stolen, baby, dialed to the corner
The cops want my signal, they triangulate slow
But the barrio's listening from the rooftops below
Microphone hot, my voice in the night
Broadcasting the truth that they tried to delete

Dale, dale, turn the dial to me
Pirata en la noche, the people are free
They can't shut me down, I move every week
From a closet, a basement, the underground beat

No license, no logo, no boss on my back
Just vinyl and static and a beat-up four-track
I play what they banned, the songs that ignite
A hundred thousand watts of stolen light

They raid the address, the room is bare
The transmitter's smoke, but the sound's everywhere
You can't arrest a frequency, mano
It lives in the ears of the people who know

Dale, dale, turn the dial to me
Pirata en la noche, the people are free
They can't shut me down, I move every week
From a closet, a basement, the underground beat

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Cloudburst caught us on the avenue
One torn umbrella, me and you
Shoulders touching, soaking through
Strangers laughing at the sky so blue gone gray
You held the handle, I held the frame
Neither one of us knew the other's name

Under one roof of cotton and wire
Two lonesome hearts and a small dry fire
The rain came down and the whole world shrank
To a strip of sidewalk and the words we drank

You said you'd missed your bus by a hair
I said I had nowhere to be there
The gutters sang, the awnings dripped
And somewhere a future quietly slipped

The storm moved on, the sun broke loose
The puddles shone like an old excuse
You closed the umbrella, turned to go
And I asked your name 'fore I'd let you know

Under one roof of cotton and wire
Two lonesome hearts and a small dry fire
The rain came down and the whole world shrank
To a strip of sidewalk and the words we drank

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They came with gold and silver chains
To buy the words I bleed in pain
But every line is sealed in fire
I will not trade my soul for hire

They offered me the crowded stage
To tear the truth out of the page
But I will keep my candle low
And let the Holy Spirit glow

I won't sell out, I won't sell out
My pen belongs to grace alone
I won't sell out, I won't sell out
These words are seeds the Lord has sown

The market sings a hollow tune
It fades away by afternoon
But what is written from above
Will echo on in endless love

Let the angels hold my song
Keep me faithful, keep me strong
Though the world may turn away
I will not sell my heart today

I won't sell out, I won't sell out
My pen belongs to grace alone
I won't sell out, I won't sell out
These words are seeds the Lord has sown

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I light the lamp at fall of night
And write my words by candlelight
I fold them up and let them go
Out to the waves that rock so slow

Oh sea, my darling, cold and gray
You take my letters every day
You never answer, never call
But you're the only love at all

I'm writing letters to the sea
The only love that's left for me
The salt, the foam, the lonesome moan
A keeper writing all alone

The seagulls cry above the tide
My ink runs out, my hands have dried
But still I scratch your name in blue
There ain't no woman quite like you

The storm rolls in, the windows shake
I wonder if my heart will break
But every wave that meets the shore
Is one more letter, one love more

I'm writing letters to the sea
The only love that's left for me
The salt, the foam, the lonesome moan
A keeper writing all alone

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Burning sand beneath our feet
A reckless heart, a fever beat
Your eyes like fire in the sun
A war began, a love undone

We crashed like waves against the night
Two bodies forged in blinding light
No tomorrow, no goodbye
Just salt and thunder in the sky

Summer fling, ignite the flame
Scream my heart, forget your name
Burn it down beneath the moon
This fire dies away too soon

The tide will take what we have made
A broken vow, a debt unpaid
But for one night we ruled the shore
Two savage hearts and nothing more

The season ends, the embers fade
The price of passion must be paid
I'll carry scars of golden sand
The ghost of you upon my hand

Summer fling, ignite the flame
Scream my heart, forget your name
Burn it down beneath the moon
This fire dies away too soon

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Yo te vi tan fuerte ayer
Levantando todo el mundo en tus manos
Y ahora veo tu caminar
Más despacio por los años

Papá, no te vayas todavía
Dame un poco más de vida
Baila conmigo una vez más
Antes que el tiempo se nos va

Tus manos que me sostuvieron
Ahora tiemblan al café
Las canas cuentan las historias
De todo lo que tú me dé

Papá, no te vayas todavía
Dame un poco más de vida
Baila conmigo una vez más
Antes que el tiempo se nos va

El reloj no para, no, no, no
Pero tu amor me queda dentro
Cada arruga es una lección
Que guardaré en mi corazón

Papá, no te vayas todavía
Dame un poco más de vida
Baila conmigo una vez más
Antes que el tiempo se nos va

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
The brass begins to softly cry
Beneath a heavy August sky
A second line of weary feet
Moves slow along the cobbled street

We carry sorrow, carry song
The drums will keep us moving on
A handkerchief, a trembling hand
The slowest march across the land

Dance us home, oh let us go
Through the streets so soft and slow
Mourning turns to golden sound
We lay our beloved in the ground

Then something lifts inside the air
The tuba breaks the heavy prayer
The tempo climbs, the spirits rise
We celebrate beneath the skies

From grief to joy the city sways
We honor death with all our praise
The river hums, the candles burn
And everything we love returns

Dance us home, oh let us go
Through the streets so soft and slow
Mourning turns to golden sound
We lay our beloved in the ground

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I watch you laughing from across the room
You never see me standing here
I memorize the way you say my name
Though you don't know that I'm so near

And every time you walk away
I lose the words I meant to say

I'm in love with someone who don't love me
Caught up in a dream that'll never be
I give my heart but you don't see
I'm in love with someone who don't love me

I saved a seat for you that stayed empty
I played our song but it was mine
I keep on hoping that you'll turn around
But I've been waiting all this time

And every time you walk away
I lose the words I meant to say

I'm in love with someone who don't love me
Caught up in a dream that'll never be
I give my heart but you don't see
I'm in love with someone who don't love me

Maybe someday I'll let you go
Maybe someday my heart will know
But for tonight I'll hold on tight
To a love that'll never be right

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Ooh, the sand was warm in June
(Sha la la la)
We danced beneath the moon
(Sha la la la)
You held my hand so tight
Underneath the boardwalk light

Summer love, my summer girl
(Doo wop, doo wop)
You set my heart awhirl
(Doo wop, doo wop)
A kiss beside the sea
Oh, what you do to me

We shared a soda for two
(Sha la la la)
The sky was bright and blue
(Sha la la la)
The radio played our song
We knew it wouldn't last too long

Summer love, my summer girl
(Doo wop, doo wop)
You set my heart awhirl
(Doo wop, doo wop)
A kiss beside the sea
Oh, what you do to me

But September came around
And you left this little town
Still I hear the ocean roar
And I'm yours forevermore

Summer love, my summer girl
(Doo wop, doo wop)
You set my heart awhirl
(Doo wop, doo wop)
A kiss beside the sea
Oh, what you do to me

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
I didn't come here looking for love
Just a glass of wine and a quiet night
Then you crossed the floor and the lights went down
And somehow everything felt right

I don't even know your name
But baby, hold me anyway

Dancing with a stranger
Under chandelier light
Two hearts, no names, no danger
Just the rhythm of tonight
Dancing with a stranger
Won't you stay with me tonight

The band is playing something slow
Your hand is resting on my waist
We move like we have always known
This moment we don't want to waste

I don't even know your name
But baby, hold me anyway

Dancing with a stranger
Under chandelier light
Two hearts, no names, no danger
Just the rhythm of tonight
Dancing with a stranger
Won't you stay with me tonight

When the morning comes we'll say goodbye
But right now you're all mine
So let the music carry us away
One more song, just give me time

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Got a rusty old tower out back of the barn
And a microphone humming with a homemade charm
The FCC keeps knocking but they'll never find
The little bit of freedom in this heart of mine

I'm broadcasting outlaw on the midnight dial
Playing real country songs with a rebel smile
They can't shut me down, no they never will
Pirate radio coming from the top of the hill

Mr. Johnson tunes in from his pickup truck
Miss Sally calls requests and wishes me luck
We ain't got no sponsors, we ain't got no rules
Just the songs they don't play for the working fools
