I'm broadcasting outlaw on the midnight dial
Playing real country songs with a rebel smile
They can't shut me down, no they never will
Pirate radio coming from the top of the hill

So turn your dial to ninety-nine
Where the music's free and the signal's mine
The big-shot stations sold their soul
But out here, baby, we rock and roll

I'm broadcasting outlaw on the midnight dial
Playing real country songs with a rebel smile
They can't shut me down, no they never will
Pirate radio coming from the top of the hill

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
We been fighting since the day we met
Every word a battle, no regret
But the DJ called us to the floor
Now we moving like we did before

Oh oh, I don't wanna like it
Oh oh, but my body can't fight it

Dance with my rival under the light
Enemy lines blurring tonight
Move your body close to mine
Maybe hate is love in disguise
Wine for me, wine for me, oh

You step left and I step right
We been enemies, now we unite
The rhythm catch us, can't let go
The whole party watching the show

Oh oh, I don't wanna like it
Oh oh, but my body can't fight it

Dance with my rival under the light
Enemy lines blurring tonight
Move your body close to mine
Maybe hate is love in disguise
Wine for me, wine for me, oh

Maybe we were never meant to fight
Maybe this was always right
Drums are beating, hearts align
My rival turning into mine

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
The sun was setting on the bay
Your skin still warm from the salty spray
We didn't talk about the fall
Just let the ocean take it all

Baby, this is just for now
But I'll remember you somehow

Summertime, just you and me
Tangled up beside the sea
A love that's only meant to last
Until the season's gone too fast
Summertime, oh baby please
Give me one more night like these

We danced barefoot in the tide
Nothing left for us to hide
Your lips taste like the lemonade
In the shadows that we made

Baby, this is just for now
But I'll remember you somehow

Summertime, just you and me
Tangled up beside the sea
A love that's only meant to last
Until the season's gone too fast
Summertime, oh baby please
Give me one more night like these

When September steals the heat
And the waves erase our feet
I'll still feel you in the breeze
My summer love, my memory

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Well I strung a wire from a hickory tree
And I'm pickin' out a signal that the law can't see
With a banjo and a transmitter humming low
I'm the captain of a station that the world don't know

Pirate of the airwaves, fast and free
Playin' mountain music for you and me
They can chase the signal but they'll never win
Turn it up, the pickin' radio's comin' in

My granddaddy's fiddle on the late-night air
A hundred lonesome souls a-listenin' there
No commercials, no permission, just the strings
And the whippoorwill harmony the mountain sings

Pirate of the airwaves, fast and free
Playin' mountain music for you and me
They can chase the signal but they'll never win
Turn it up, the pickin' radio's comin' in

When the marshals come a-knockin' on my door
I'll pack the rig and move it down the holler more
You can't kill a tune that's runnin' through the pines
So keep your dial right here on the rebel lines

Pirate of the airwaves, fast and free
Playin' mountain music for you and me
They can chase the signal but they'll never win
Turn it up, the pickin' radio's comin' in

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The coffee's cold, the lamp burns dim
Textbooks scattered to the brim
The whole town's sleeping, not a sound
Just me and these four walls around

Two in the morning, all alone
Chasing dreams I've never known
One more page and one more cup
Lord, please help me make it up
Two in the morning, holding on
To a future I ain't found yet, but I'm strong

Momma said it's worth the fight
That's why I'm up here every night
My eyes are heavy, hands are sore
But I keep reaching for the door

Two in the morning, all alone
Chasing dreams I've never known
One more page and one more cup
Lord, please help me make it up
Two in the morning, holding on
To a future I ain't found yet, but I'm strong

Someday I'll look back and see
That all these nights were building me
The sun's gonna rise on the other side
Of every tear that I have cried

Two in the morning, all alone
Chasing dreams I've never known
One more page and one more cup
Lord, please help me make it up

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I dialed the number I still knew by heart
(Ring a ling a ling)
After all these years apart
(Ring a ling a ling)
My hands were shaking, voice was low
There was so much she didn't know

Mama, it's me, won't you talk to me
(Doo wop, doo wop)
I've been gone too long, can't you see
(Doo wop, doo wop)
I'm sorry for the silent years
Come and wash away my tears

She answered soft and said my name
(Ring a ling a ling)
And nothing felt the same
(Ring a ling a ling)
The years just melted on the line
Like no time passed between this time

Mama, it's me, won't you talk to me
(Doo wop, doo wop)
I've been gone too long, can't you see
(Doo wop, doo wop)
I'm sorry for the silent years
Come and wash away my tears

Pride kept me away so long
But a mother's love is never gone
She said come home, the door's still wide
And I just broke right down and cried

Mama, it's me, won't you talk to me
(Doo wop, doo wop)
I've been gone too long, can't you see
(Doo wop, doo wop)
I'm sorry for the silent years
Come and wash away my tears

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
We parked the truck out by the lake
One last day before we'd break
Your bags are packed, mine are too
Tomorrow splits the world in two

One last summer, one last sun
Before the rest of life's begun
Hold my hand and don't let go
The end of everything we know
One last summer, golden light
Let's stay young just one more night

We wrote our names in the old oak tree
Made some promises we couldn't keep
The cornfields swaying in the heat
The end of childhood, bittersweet

One last summer, one last sun
Before the rest of life's begun
Hold my hand and don't let go
The end of everything we know
One last summer, golden light
Let's stay young just one more night

Different towns and different roads
Different stories yet untold
But I'll remember who we were
Before the world became a blur

One last summer, one last sun
Before the rest of life's begun
Hold my hand and don't let go
The end of everything we know

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
He writes in a notebook the color of rust
In a one-room apartment that smells like the dust
They offered him silver, they offered him gold
For a jingle, a slogan, a story half-told

The man in the suit said the world wants it clean
Cut out the ache and the in-between
He poured out his coffee, he buttoned his coat
And handed back every last word that they wrote

He won't sell out, he won't sign the line
He'd rather be hungry and call the words mine
Let the radio glitter, let the billboards shout
He's got his own truth and he won't sell out

Now the rent's coming due and the lamp's burning low
But the lines on the page have a fire and a glow
He reads to a barroom of seven or eight
And they listen like Sunday, they listen like fate

Somewhere a younger man's chasing the gleam
Trading his marrow for somebody's dream
But here in the half-light, the pen never stops
And the only crown's the one that he's got

He won't sell out, he won't sign the line
He'd rather be hungry and call the words mine
Let the radio glitter, let the billboards shout
He's got his own truth and he won't sell out

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The A&R guy nodded through the second song
A whiskey in his hand, his attention half gone
We played like the ceiling was about to cave in
Like the rest of our lives hung on where we'd been

The green room was concrete, the beer was warm
We traded a cigarette, we weathered the storm
He said call me Monday, we believed every word
It was the loudest silence we ever heard

We almost made it, we touched the edge
Stood on the rooftop, leaned off the ledge
The lights were so bright I could taste the gold
We almost made it, then the night got cold

Monday came quiet, the phone never rang
The amps in the basement, the same old clang
But for one electric hour we were kings of the room
Before the fade-out, before the gloom

And I'd do it again for that thirty-foot stage
The sweat and the feedback, the beautiful rage
Even knowing the ending, even knowing it breaks
I'd chase that almost for as long as it takes

We almost made it, we touched the edge
Stood on the rooftop, leaned off the ledge
The lights were so bright I could taste the gold
We almost made it, then the night got cold

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon humming red at a quarter to three
Vinyl booths shining where the lonely come free
The griddle's still sizzling, the coffee's still hot
Under the mirrorball glow of the parking lot

All night, all night, the diner never sleeps
Spin me a record while the city counts sheep
Eggs over easy and a love on the side
All night, all night, come on in for the ride

The waitress named Dolores in her roller-skate shoes
Glides past the jukebox playing somebody's blues
A cabbie, a dreamer, and a girl in sequins bright
All find a home in the chrome and the light

All night, all night, the diner never sleeps
Spin me a record while the city counts sheep
Eggs over easy and a love on the side
All night, all night, come on in for the ride

When the bars all close and the streets go gray
The broken-hearted boogie till the break of day
There's a stool and a story and a pie to share
A disco of the desperate, and nobody cares

All night, all night, the diner never sleeps
Spin me a record while the city counts sheep
Eggs over easy and a love on the side
All night, all night, come on in for the ride

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Slow brass over Rampart, a handkerchief sky
The trumpet bends gentle as we're saying goodbye
A soft samba shuffle down Basin Street
Where the sorrow walks light on a second-line beat

Dance me home easy, my love, dance me slow
From the church to the river where the magnolias grow
There's grief in the rhythm, there's joy in the sway
We bury you laughing in a New Orleans way

The parasols open, the white horses wait
The drummer keeps brushing a soft, swinging gait
We mourned in the morning, now we let the horns rise
A bossa for an angel beneath Creole skies

The cemetery gates, they swing open wide
But the music turns tender, it carries the tide
No wailing, just whispering, smooth and serene
The saddest sweet samba this city has seen

Dance me home easy, my love, dance me slow
From the church to the river where the magnolias grow
There's grief in the rhythm, there's joy in the sway
We bury you laughing in a New Orleans way

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The county fair stage and a borrowed guitar
A scout from the city had come from afar
I sang every word like it cost me my breath
With the crickets and the floodlights and the smell of the wet

He leaned on the railing, he tapped out the time
He said son, that's a voice, that's a beautiful line
He handed a card with a number to call
And I held it like gospel against the nightfall

Oh the night I almost made it, how the river ran gold
I was twenty and burning and afraid to grow old
The card's in a drawer now, the ink's gone to gray
But I touched it, I touched it, for one shining day

The number went nowhere, the line just rang dead
The city forgot me like a thing it had said
But I went back to playing on the porch in the rain
For a wife and a dog and the sweet hurricane

And maybe the almost is all that we get
A close-enough heaven we don't quite forget
I'd not trade that evening for the comfort of doubt
For one perfect moment of nearly throughout

Oh the night I almost made it, how the river ran gold
I was twenty and burning and afraid to grow old
The card's in a drawer now, the ink's gone to gray
But I touched it, I touched it, for one shining day

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Green ribbons unspooling on a velvet night
A quiet aurora, a soft satin light
We stood on the ice with our coffee gone cold
And watched the sky shimmer in emerald and gold

Dance, aurora, dance for me slow
Over the fjord where the cold rivers flow
A samba of starlight, a hush and a hum
The northern lights whisper the night has begun

Your hand in my pocket, your breath in the air
The violet curtains came down from nowhere
No orchestra needed, no rhythm but this
The pulse of the heavens, the warmth of your kiss

They say it's just particles, just solar wind
But tonight it's a love song the universe pinned
To the dome of the darkness, so tender, so far
A bossa nova played on the strings of a star

Dance, aurora, dance for me slow
Over the fjord where the cold rivers flow
A samba of starlight, a hush and a hum
The northern lights whisper the night has begun

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
We laid him down Friday in a sharp white suit
Then the bass dropped heavy and the horns went toot
No crying on Bourbon when the spirit's set free
We turned the whole graveyard to a dance floor of glee

Second-line, second-line, shake it for the dead
Four on the floor and a feather on your head
He's gone to the glory but he left us the groove
So move your body, baby, that's the way that we move

The tambourines glitter, the trombones ignite
Grandmama's shimmying under the streetlight
A mirrorball moon over Saint Louis Three
Where we boogie the sorrow right out to the sea

Second-line, second-line, shake it for the dead
Four on the floor and a feather on your head
He's gone to the glory but he left us the groove
So move your body, baby, that's the way that we move

Don't you weep for the casket, don't you mourn for the gone
The party's the prayer and it carries us on
When my time comes calling, oh promise me this
You'll bury me dancing in disco-lit bliss

Second-line, second-line, shake it for the dead
Four on the floor and a feather on your head
He's gone to the glory but he left us the groove
So move your body, baby, that's the way that we move

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Stained-glass evenings, I knew every hymn
The velvet of the kneeler, the low light dim
I mouthed all the answers, I folded my hands
But something kept drifting like the shifting of sands

I'm slipping out softly through the side chapel door
No thunder, no lightning, no slammed-shut roar
Just a girl and her shadow on the cold stone floor
Leaving so quiet they won't know anymore

The candles still flicker for somebody's sins
The organ still swells where the worship begins
I loved you, old steeple, I did, all the same
But I can't keep on whispering a borrowed name

The sky didn't open, no fire came down
Just the soft hush of streetlight on the edge of town
Maybe God's in the leaving, maybe grace lets you go
Maybe heaven is wider than the things that I know

I'm slipping out softly through the side chapel door
No thunder, no lightning, no slammed-shut roar
Just a girl and her shadow on the cold stone floor
Leaving so quiet they won't know anymore

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I memorized the way you say my name
Like it's nothing, like it's ordinary, the same
While I'm burning down inside every time you walk near
And you're talking about someone who's not standing here

I love you, I love you, but you'll never know
It's a fire in a room where the windows don't show
I'd give you my heartbeat, my breath and my name
But you smile and you leave, and I'm still in the flame

You call me your good friend, you lean on my door
And you cry over someone who hurt you before
And I hold all the pieces and I help you stand tall
While the one who would love you is no one at all

Oh, how long can a heart keep on knocking that door
That opens for everyone but never for more
I'll carry this quiet, I'll carry it true
A love that's all mine 'cause it's never been you

I love you, I love you, but you'll never know
It's a fire in a room where the windows don't show
I'd give you my heartbeat, my breath and my name
But you smile and you leave, and I'm still in the flame

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raise up my hand in the boardroom light
Got a good idea, gonna make it right
But before the first word leave out my mouth
Big man cut in and he turn it about

Talk over, talk over, every time the same
They borrow my fire and they forget my name
But I keep my riddim, I keep my cool
One day they gonna learn, I'm nobody's fool

Miss Jackson agree but they call it Dave's plan
Same words, different mouth, now they understand
I sip my water and I watch the show
Writing down the truth that they'll never know

So I find my people, we build our own table
Where a voice is a voice and a hand is able
No talking over, no stealing the sun
Every one a leader, every word gets done

Talk over, talk over, every time the same
They borrow my fire and they forget my name
But I keep my riddim, I keep my cool
One day they gonna learn, I'm nobody's fool

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slid closed and the dark rushed in
A hundred strangers pressed against my skin
The lights started flashing, the rails gave a scream
And my heart was a drum in a runaway dream

I gripped the cold pole and I counted to ten
The air got thin and I lost it again
No one could see it, no one could tell
I was riding the F-train through a private hell

Breathe in, breathe out, the station's not far
You are not your terror, you are not where you are
The tunnel will open, the daylight will come
Just breathe through the thunder till the thunder is done

A woman beside me, she met my eye
She whispered real soft, you're okay, it's alright
And something inside me unclenched its fist
As the train pulled in through the morning mist

There's a kindness in cities you don't always see
A stranger's quiet mercy that set me free
I stepped to the platform on shaking knees
And thanked the wide morning and the open breeze

Breathe in, breathe out, the station's not far
You are not your terror, you are not where you are
The tunnel will open, the daylight will come
Just breathe through the thunder till the thunder is done

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Got my hand in the air, got my mouth open wide
Then Brad starts yelling and my words go and die
Same agenda, same suits, same parade
My idea comes back with a different name and a raise

Talk over me, talk over me, go on and try
I'll scream through the ceiling, I'll spit in your eye
You can't steal a riot, you can't mute a flame
I'm done being polite, I'm done playing the game

Nod, nod, nod, like I never spoke
While the boys pat the back of the guy who just broke
My exact same point in his lower-voiced drawl
Well I'm flipping the table and the table's gonna fall

No more raising my hand, no more waiting my turn
Watch the whole stupid meeting agenda burn
I'll say it out loud and I'll say it again
LOUDER, LOUDER, till you let me in

Talk over me, talk over me, go on and try
I'll scream through the ceiling, I'll spit in your eye
You can't steal a riot, you can't mute a flame
I'm done being polite, I'm done playing the game

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Old Mr. Abano on the corner of Vine
Has a needle and a thimble and a chalk-marking line
He's stitched every wedding, he's hemmed every grief
From the banker's fine collar to the busboy's relief

He dresses the whole block, every shape, every size
A suit for the funeral, a gown for the bride's
With a tape round his neck and a pin in his teeth
He measures the living and clothes the deceased

The widow's black dress and the graduate's pride
The nervous young groom that he calmed down inside
He knows all our secrets in the seams that he sews
The weight that we've gained and the love that still grows

When his hands get to shaking and his eyes start to dim
The whole street will gather and we'll sew one for him
A coat made of thank-yous, every stitch a sweet debt
For the man who made sure that we'd never forget

He dresses the whole block, every shape, every size
A suit for the funeral, a gown for the bride's
With a tape round his neck and a pin in his teeth
He measures the living and clothes the deceased

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Daddy shook hands with the man down the road
Traded my tomorrow for forty acres sowed
A ring in a box and a date on the wall
And a life laid out neat for a girl standing tall

So I'm taking the truck and the dust and the dawn
Leaving the future they wrote me upon
There's a road that ain't promised, there's a name that's my own
And I'd rather be lost than be safe and alone

He's a fine enough fella with a fine enough farm
Never raised me a hand, never wished me a harm
But I can't say I do to a thing I didn't choose
So I'm kissing it goodbye with these worn-out shoes

Mama, don't cry at the gate when I go
There's a fire in your daughter that you never let show
You stayed for your reasons, and I love you, it's true
But I'm chasing the life that you never could do

So I'm taking the truck and the dust and the dawn
Leaving the future they wrote me upon
There's a road that ain't promised, there's a name that's my own
And I'd rather be lost than be safe and alone

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
Two crews on the floor and the air's gone tight
We been throwing down shade since the start of the night
Then the DJ calls out, put your enemy's hand
In the grip of your rival, now move on command

Step, two, step, we don't break the chain
Locked in the rhythm, locked in the pain
Forced to move together when we'd rather fight
Two rivals on fire in the strobe-lit night

Your eyes full of venom, mine burning the same
But the bassline don't care about your stupid name
We spin and we counter, we duck and we weave
And the hate starts to blur into something we believe

Somewhere round the drop when the sub-bass quakes
I forgot what it was that I thought was at stake
Your rhythm met my rhythm and the war fell apart
Two enemies syncing to a single heartbeat's start

Step, two, step, we don't break the chain
Locked in the rhythm, locked in the pain
Forced to move together when we'd rather fight
Two rivals on fire in the strobe-lit night

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Clock said midnight when the second shift began
Fireworks outside, IV pole in my hand
The break room smells like coffee burned an hour ago
I got a paper hat and forty rooms to go

Pick it up, pick it up, the monitor's a beat
Dancing down the hallway on my blistered feet
Holiday, holiday, the whole world's at the party
But the call light's flashing so I'm moving double-hearty

Mrs. Lee in 12 just wants somebody near
So I held her hand and told her morning's almost here
My phone's a graveyard, all the texts I didn't send
Someone's gotta watch the night flip into the end

Pick it up, pick it up, the monitor's a beat
Dancing down the hallway on my blistered feet
Holiday, holiday, the whole world's at the party
But the call light's flashing so I'm moving double-hearty

Upstroke on the guitar, upstroke on my soul
Twenty hours deep and I have lost control
But a baby got born at a quarter to four
So I'll dance this skank right out the exit door

Pick it up, pick it up, we made it through the dark
Sun's a yellow ribbon hanging over the park
Holiday, holiday, I'm clocking out at seven
Trade my scrubs for sleep and call my pillow heaven

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
We gathered at the Boar's Head when the rain came down like lead
Four men and a tallow flame and the things we never said
The cards were dealt in candlelight, the whiskey poured for free
And Tom MacBride wagered more than any man should be

Oh lay it down, lay it down, the river and the queen
The coins go round, the coins go round, on the felt of green
But a man can lose his cottage and a man can lose his name
And some will lose their very souls before the morning came

He bet the field, he bet the cow, he bet his father's ring
He bet the bed his wife still slept, he bet most everything
And when the final hand was turned the room went cold and still
For Tom had nothing left to lose but standing on the hill

Oh lay it down, lay it down, the river and the queen
The coins go round, the coins go round, on the felt of green
But a man can lose his cottage and a man can lose his name
And some will lose their very souls before the morning came

They found the door swung open and the lantern guttered out
No one speaks of Tom MacBride or what the night was about
But the boys still meet on Fridays and they leave one chair alone
With a single card upon it, face-down beside the throne

So lay it down, lay it down, but know the price you pay
The coins go round, the coins go round, till they take your light away

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Flour on the apron, butter in the pan
A Sunday kind of morning that the radio began
There's cinnamon and coffee dancing in the air
And Grandma's humming low like nobody's a care

Oh that smell, that smell, it's swinging through the door
Got my heart doing a two-step on the linoleum floor
Gravy on the burner and a song upon the breeze
Take me back, take me back, to my grandmother's keys

The screen door used to whistle and the kettle used to croon
She'd waltz me round the table by the light of afternoon
The biscuits rose like Sunday and the bacon kept the beat
And everyone who entered found a reason to stay sweet

Oh that smell, that smell, it's swinging through the door
Got my heart doing a two-step on the linoleum floor
Gravy on the burner and a song upon the breeze
Take me back, take me back, to my grandmother's keys

The house is sold, the clock is still, the apron's in a box
But sometimes in a stranger's hall I catch it and it knocks
A little hit of nutmeg and I'm seven years old again
Snapping beans beside her with a grin from way back when

Oh that smell, that smell, it never really goes
It lives inside a memory that everybody knows
So pour another coffee and let the trumpet sing
My grandmother's kitchen, it'll swing through everything

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
My mama begged me settle, find a girl and find a town
But the sky out west was bruising and I had to chase it down
I got a tank of gasoline and a camera on the seat
And a heart that only beats right when the thunder's at my feet

I'm chasing, I'm chasing, where the wall cloud meets the plain
Got Oklahoma dirt road and a windshield full of rain
She asked me would I stay, boys, and I told her honestly
The only thing that holds me is the storm that runs me free

The banjo's in the back seat and the radar's in my hand
I've outrun forty twisters across this hollow land
The quiet kills me slower than a funnel ever could
A porch and a rocking chair never did me any good

I'm chasing, I'm chasing, where the wall cloud meets the plain
Got Oklahoma dirt road and a windshield full of rain
She asked me would I stay, boys, and I told her honestly
The only thing that holds me is the storm that runs me free

Maybe one day I'll get caught in the thing I love to find
And they'll bury me in Kansas with the wind upon my mind
But till the green sky's calling and the sirens start to wail
You'll find me on the highway with my fiddle and the gale

I'm chasing, I'm chasing, till the good Lord calls me home
Some men need a fire and some men need to roam
She asked me would I stay, boys, but the answer's in the breeze
The only thing that holds me is the storm that runs me free

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete to the skyline, every lot a parking space
Glass and steel and silence in a gray and humming place
But down on Seventh corner where the gutters meet the grate
One green crown is breathing and it will not abdicate

Run it, run it, roots beneath the road
Last one standing where the river used to flow
Break it down, break it down, but it won't go down
The last tree dancing in a paved-over town

They poured around it years ago, too stubborn to remove
Now the rhythm of the city moves around its quiet groove
The pigeons know its branches and the kids who skip the bus
It holds a hundred summers that were stolen out from us

Run it, run it, roots beneath the road
Last one standing where the river used to flow
Break it down, break it down, but it won't go down
The last tree dancing in a paved-over town

Subwoofer heartbeat, sap inside the wire
Green against the data, low against the higher
If they cut it tomorrow then the air goes still
So we circle it at midnight and we guard it with our will

Run it, run it, roots beneath the road
Last one standing where the river used to flow
Break it down, break it down, but it won't go down
The last tree dancing in a paved-over town

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Neon on the dashboard, midnight FM low
Took a wrong turn on purpose down a street I used to know
Your window's lit up amber but it isn't yours no more
Some stranger's silhouette is moving past the second floor

Driving past, driving past, with the headlights in the rain
Every streetlight is a heartbeat and your name is in the lane
I won't stop, I won't slow, I just let the engine moan
Driving past your old apartment all alone

The deli's still on the corner, the same crack in the curb
Where you told me that you loved me in a half-forgotten word
The synth is in my chest now, a pulse of purple light
And the past is just a building that I'm passing in the night

Driving past, driving past, with the headlights in the rain
Every streetlight is a heartbeat and your name is in the lane
I won't stop, I won't slow, I just let the engine moan
Driving past your old apartment all alone

Four more times around the block and then I'll let it die
The arpeggio is fading like a tail light in the sky
I knew you in another life behind that glowing pane
Now I'm just a chrome reflection slipping back into the rain

Driving past, driving past, one last time and then I'm gone
Every memory's a marker that the night is built upon
I won't stop, I won't slow, I just let the engine moan
Driving past your old apartment all alone

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
An empty lot on Sunday and your hands upon the wheel
Ten and two, my darling, feel the pavement, feel it real
The clutch is like a partner, you must listen, you must lead
Releasing slow and steady is the dance that you will need

Easy, easy, let the engine find the beat
Mirror, signal, breathe with me, the whole world's at your feet
I am holding nothing but the air above your sleeve
For the hardest step of any dance is learning how to leave

You stalled it on the first try and you laughed until you cried
I remember holding you the day they brought you home to ride
Now your foot is on the gas and you are reaching for the light
And I'm gripping at the door but I am letting go tonight

Easy, easy, let the engine find the beat
Mirror, signal, breathe with me, the whole world's at your feet
I am holding nothing but the air above your sleeve
For the hardest step of any dance is learning how to leave

One day you'll pull from the driveway and you won't look in the glass
And I'll stand there in the doorway as the taillights softly pass
The tango always ends the same, two dancers, then just one
But oh, the way you take the curve, my brave and only sun

Easy, easy, let the highway be your floor
I taught you all the steps I know, you do not need me more
So drive, my love, drive steady, take the world that I believe
For the hardest step of any dance is learning how to leave

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Nine to five in gallery three, just me and these old walls
But there's a woman in a frame and every evening when she calls
Her eyes follow the daylight as it fades across the floor
And I swear she leans a little closer than she did the day before

Oh, you're oil and you're canvas, you're a hundred years of paint
But you're realer than the living and you never make me wait
Every night I lock the doors and it's only me and you
Falling for a painting, what's a lonely guard to do

The tourists never see you, they just snap and move along
They don't catch the way you're listening when I hum a quiet song
I told you all my secrets in the hush of closing time
And that little Mona smile of yours, I swear to God it's mine

Oh, you're oil and you're canvas, you're a hundred years of paint
But you're realer than the living and you never make me wait
Every night I lock the doors and it's only me and you
Falling for a painting, what's a lonely guard to do

They're moving you on Friday to a wing across the sea
And I can't follow, baby, they don't make a room for me
So tonight I'll stand a little longer, let the moonlight touch your face
And memorize forever every brushstroke of this place

Oh, you're oil and you're canvas, but you taught me how to feel
In a world of moving strangers you're the only thing that's real
When they take you in the morning I'll still work the empty room
Loving a painting and the light it left to bloom

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
The sign out front is buzzing and it never says we're closed
There's coffee in the kettle for the weary and the froze
The trucker and the dreamer and the soul who lost their way
All find a vinyl booth to hold them till the break of day

There's a light, there's a light, that's a-burning through the night
Come on in, weary traveler, everything will be alright
There's a seat at the counter and a plate for everyone
In the diner of the Lord it's morning long before the sun

Miss Ruby works the griddle and she knows you by your eyes
She'll feed you when you're empty and she'll never ask you why
The jukebox plays a hymn that no one ever put a dime in
And grace comes with the biscuits in the booth where you've been cryin'

There's a light, there's a light, that's a-burning through the night
Come on in, weary traveler, everything will be alright
There's a seat at the counter and a plate for everyone
In the diner of the Lord it's morning long before the sun

When the whole world shuts its windows and the streets are cold and bare
There's a place that keeps the porch light on and meets you with a prayer
You don't need a reservation and you don't need to be clean
Just push the door, the bell will ring, you'll see what mercy means

There's a light, there's a light, hallelujah, it stays on
Come on in, weary traveler, you were never really gone
There's a seat at the counter and a plate for everyone
In the diner of the Lord it's morning long before the sun

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up the crates at dawn, the captain wouldn't say
What was knocking in the hold or why the gulls all flew away
The compass started spinning and the rum went sour too
There's something down below, y'all, and it's coming for the crew

That cargo's cursed, that cargo's funky in a bad bad way
Groove so heavy on the water that we cannot make it pay
Throw it over, throw it over, but it crawls right back aboard
Got a cursed cargo riding and we can't afford the lord

The bass line's in the timbers and the ship begins to sway
The cook done seen a shadow walk the galley yesterday
We signed up for the money but we're sailing for our souls
With a beat beneath the floorboards keeping time with what it stole

That cargo's cursed, that cargo's funky in a bad bad way
Groove so heavy on the water that we cannot make it pay
Throw it over, throw it over, but it crawls right back aboard
Got a cursed cargo riding and we can't afford the lord

Horns are blowing warning as the storm begins to grin
The wheel turns by itself now and the night is closing in
But if we gotta sink, boys, then we sinking with the groove
Let the cursed thing keep the rhythm while we make our final move

That cargo's cursed, that cargo's funky in a bad bad way
Groove so heavy on the water that we cannot make it pay
Land ahead, land ahead, but the harbor turns to gray
Got a cursed cargo riding and it never sails away

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Dos años en el polvo, ahora el avión baja al fin
Mi corazón golpeando como el bombo en el motín
La misma calle, el mismo sol, pero yo no soy igual
Traigo el ruido de la noche y el silencio del final

Vengo a casa, vengo a casa, suena el dembow en mi pecho
La bandera, los abrazos, todo el barrio satisfecho
Pero adentro hay una guerra que conmigo se quedó
Vengo a casa, mami, vengo, pero no el que se marchó

Mi madre en la ventana con un cirio y con un rezo
Mi hermano ya está grande, casi no lo reconozco
La música en la esquina, el perreo en el portal
Intento bailar libre pero el eco es militar

Vengo a casa, vengo a casa, suena el dembow en mi pecho
La bandera, los abrazos, todo el barrio satisfecho
Pero adentro hay una guerra que conmigo se quedó
Vengo a casa, mami, vengo, pero no el que se marchó

Dame tiempo, dame noche, dame el ritmo pa' sanar
Que las luces de la fiesta me recuerden a brillar
No soy héroe, soy un hombre que aprendió a sobrevivir
Y esta calle me recibe y me enseña a revivir

Vengo a casa, vengo a casa, y la luna me perdona
Paso a paso, beat a beat, vuelvo a ser de mi persona
La guerra se quedó allá, aquí me curo en el calor
Vengo a casa, mi gente, vengo, vengo lleno de su amor

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man on the gray rock, been there ninety years or so
He says the ocean's stubborn and it's awful slow to know
He come down every morning with a bucket and a cane
And he talk to all that water like a teacher with the rain

Oh, you can't teach the tide, son, no, the tide gon' do its thing
It'll take your daddy's fishing boat and everything you bring
But the old man keeps on talking to that cold and salty blue
Lord, you can't teach the tide nothing, but the tide sure teaches you

I asked him why he bothered with a thing that wouldn't hear
He just smiled with all three teeth and said come a little near
The tide ain't here for learning, boy, the tide is here to stay
And a man who fights the water gonna wash himself away

Oh, you can't teach the tide, son, no, the tide gon' do its thing
It'll take your daddy's fishing boat and everything you bring
But the old man keeps on talking to that cold and salty blue
Lord, you can't teach the tide nothing, but the tide sure teaches you

They found his cane on Tuesday but the old man, he was gone
The tide had come to fetch him and it carried him along
And I swear I heard him laughing in the rolling of the foam
Saying patience, patience, child, the water always brings you home

No, you can't teach the tide, son, but I go down just the same
With a bucket and his old cane and I whisper out his name
The tide don't learn a lesson, but it taught me what is true
You can't teach the tide nothing, but the tide sure teaches you

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent hum at midnight, the ward is glowing pale
My second shift is rising like a neon-painted veil
Outside the city's celebrating, fireworks in the dark
While I trace the monitor's horizon, every beat a little spark

Double shift, double shift, in the hum of the machine
I'm the only steady heartbeat on this holiday between
The dawn is just a pixel on a far electric line
But I'll hold the night together till the morning realigns

The coffee's gone synthetic, the clock's a melting screen
Room by room I'm gliding through the glow of in-between
A pulse, a chart, a whisper, a hand I gently hold
The holiday's a rumor and the corridor is cold

Double shift, double shift, in the hum of the machine
I'm the only steady heartbeat on this holiday between
The dawn is just a pixel on a far electric line
But I'll hold the night together till the morning realigns

Arpeggios of monitors are singing low and slow
And somewhere past the window all the colored rockets go
I traded fireworks for this, the quiet sacred kind
A hundred fragile heartbeats and the watch I keep behind

Double shift, double shift, and the sun begins to bleed
Through the blinds in rose and violet, every patient that I freed
The dawn is more than pixels now, it's warm against my eyes
I held the night together and I watched the morning rise

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The desk lamp's the only sun I've seen in seven hours
The coffee's gone to bitter and the textbook's lost its powers
The whole town's gone to sleeping but the highway still complains
And I'm tracing all these numbers like the rivers trace the plains

It's two in the morning and the page is a long dirt road
I'm carrying a dream that's a heavy kind of load
Nobody sees the quiet hours, nobody hears the fight
Just me and a yellow light and the country of the night

My mama worked the diner so I'd never have to choose
So I owe this empty kitchen and these stubborn study blues
The radio plays static and a song from long ago
And I write until my hand cramps up and the candle's burning low

It's two in the morning and the page is a long dirt road
I'm carrying a dream that's a heavy kind of load
Nobody sees the quiet hours, nobody hears the fight
Just me and a yellow light and the country of the night

One day this'll be a story that I tell across a fire
How I built myself in silence with a pencil and desire
The dawn is coming slowly like a train across the wheat
And I'll meet it wide awake, boys, with the lesson still complete

It's two in the morning but the page is finally turned
Every letter is a mile and every mile was earned
Nobody saw the quiet hours but the road ahead is bright
Just me and a fading lamp and the breaking of the night

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
There was a Sunday morning when I walked right out the door
Didn't feel the spirit moving like it used to move before
I threw away my praying and I traded it for doubt
Kept the bass line in my body but I let the gospel out

I lost it, lost it, on a cold and empty street
Set the rhythm down and walked away without the beat
But funk don't leave you lonely and the groove it finds you back
I lost my faith and found it on a downbeat and a crack

For years I played it hollow, all the motion, none the soul
A pocket with no preacher and a song without a goal
Then a stranger in the subway started clapping out a time
And something old and holy came back climbing up my spine

I lost it, lost it, on a cold and empty street
Set the rhythm down and walked away without the beat
But funk don't leave you lonely and the groove it finds you back
I lost my faith and found it on a downbeat and a crack

It wasn't in a steeple and it wasn't in a book
It was in the way the whole train moved together when I shook
The one is where the spirit lives, the pocket where it stays
I didn't lose my faith at all, I just changed up the praise

I found it, found it, in the sweat and in the shout
Put the rhythm back together and I turned my whole self out
Funk don't leave you lonely and the groove it brings you home
I lost my faith and found it and I'll never walk alone

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
Your hand in mine like a wire stripped raw
Gritted teeth and the count of four
We trained to break each other's jaw
Now we're locked on the same floor
Every step is a swallowed war
Sweat and rosin, salt and score
I hate the way you keep the time
I hate that mine lines up with yours

Spin me, hit me, hold the line
Two loaded fists in perfect time
We don't bow, we just collide
Enemies on the same beat, dancing tied

The drill sergeant claps the count
We move or we both get thrown out
Your grip's a threat I can't deny
My pulse keeps tempo with your spite
Clenched jaw learning your weight
Leaning in on the thing I hate

If I fall you fall with me
Chained at the wrist, set us free
The music's the only referee
Dance, or we both bleed

Spin me, hit me, hold the line
Two loaded fists in perfect time
We don't bow, we just collide
Enemies on the same beat, dancing tied

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Debajo de las plazas duerme un corazón
The cobblestones are ribs of a buried son
We poured our wine where his shoulder lies
And built our towers between his sighs
The fountains run from his quiet breath
Every cathedral a charm against his death

Duerme, gigante, beneath my street
Let the trumpets cradle your heartbeat
We dance on the chest of a giant asleep
Pray he never wakes from the dream he keeps

The vendors sell oranges over his spine
Grandmothers light candles, ask him for time
When the church bells swing the whole hill leans
As if he turned inside a dream
The river is the line of his open palm
We drink to the storm and we drink to the calm

If he stretches once the steeples fall
So we sing him soft, we sing him small
Guitars like fingers across his brow
Stay down, gigante, stay down for now

Duerme, gigante, beneath my street
Let the trumpets cradle your heartbeat
We dance on the chest of a giant asleep
Pray he never wakes from the dream he keeps

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Four seconds left and a county that doubts
The scoreboard frowning, the favored ones loud
We came up from the gravel and the chain-link yard
No banners on our wall, just the days that were hard
Coach on his knees in the dust of the line
Saying son, this whole season was building this time

Lift it up, lift it up, let it fly
The last shot rising like a prayer to the sky
We were never supposed to be here at all
So glory, glory, glory — watch it fall

Mama in the bleachers with her hands held high
The whole town hushing, holding one breath, one cry
We shouldn't have made it, the brackets all said
But grace put a fire where the doubt should have spread
The buzzer is coming like the day of the Lord
We leave it all sweating on this worn-down board

Win or we lose, we already rose
From the bottom of the standings nobody chose
Clap your hands, the underdogs sing
We walked through the valley to get to this ring

Lift it up, lift it up, let it fly
The last shot rising like a prayer to the sky
We were never supposed to be here at all
So glory, glory, glory — watch it fall

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Last row of the showcase, A&R in the cut
My hands cold as the rail, my collar buttoned up
Three minutes on the clock and a beat I built broke
Every bar I rehearsed in the bathroom mirror smoke
The lights hit me amber, the room held its breath
One verse from a deal, one cough from regret
I heard my own heart in the gap of the snare
Close enough to taste it, close enough to swear

So close I could feel the door give
So close to the life I don't live
Got the napkin with the number, never got the call
Almost made it — and almost is the longest word of all

He nodded twice, said kid, I'll be in touch
Left me holding a moment that was almost enough
Walked the avenue counting the cars that don't stop
Replaying the line where I rushed and I dropped
The city kept moving like it didn't just shift
Tucked the maybe in my pocket like a folded-up gift

Mornings I still hear the crowd half-cheer
That fork in the road still living right here
But I learned the night you almost arrive
Is the night that teaches you to stay alive

So close I could feel the door give
So close to the life I don't live
Got the napkin with the number, never got the call
Almost made it — and almost is the longest word of all

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slam shut and the air turns to stone
El vagón lleno y me siento solo
A hundred shoulders but I can't find the floor
My heart's a trumpet nobody asked for
The tunnel screams black past the window glass
I count the stops but the breath won't pass

Ay, corazón, slow down, slow down
Don't bury me here underground
The rails are shaking, the world tips and sways
Guide me, Virgencita, up into the day

The lights flicker out, the steel starts to lean
Sudor en la frente, the longest fifteen
A stranger's perfume and the squeal of the brake
My ribs like a cage that the violins shake
I grip the cold pole like a candle, a cross
Whispering home, home, before I'm lost

Then the doors breathe open, the platform, the air
The sky through the stairwell, the city right there
My hands stop trembling, the giant lets go
I walk up the steps like a man learning to grow

Ay, corazón, slow down, slow down
Don't bury me here underground
The rails are shaking, the world tips and sways
Guide me, Virgencita, up into the day

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
You were the green light I treated like yellow
Slowed down too cautious, played it too mellow
We used to split a coffee on the fire escape
Now it's just steam where your laugh used to shape
I had the words ready, kept them in a drawer
Told myself tomorrow, but tomorrow shut the door
Saw you on the platform with a ring that ain't mine
Gave a nod like a stranger, kept it cool, kept it kind

You're the one that got away
The right thing I said on the wrong day
I still keep your number like a scar I won't trade
The one, the one, the one that got away

Funny how the city keeps the ghost of your route
Same bodega, same song, same neon to boot
I rehearse old conversations in the back of the cab
Every what-if a coin that I never can grab
Maturity is knowing it was timing, not us
But timing's just heartbreak you can't even cuss

If I had a do-over I'd reach for your hand
Before the train pulled and you slipped through my plan
But you're somebody's morning, somebody's stay
And I'm just the verse about the one that got away

You're the one that got away
The right thing I said on the wrong day
I still keep your number like a scar I won't trade
The one, the one, the one that got away

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
La sirena calls but no shift answers back
Forty years of iron and a chain on the track
My father bent steel here, his hands turned to leather
Three generations clocked in through the weather
Now the windows go dark like extinguished saints
And the parking lot rusts where the future once waited

Adiós, fábrica, the whistle won't blow
You fed our table, now where do we go
The trumpets play low for the men in the line
Who gave you their backs and gave you their time

The foreman shakes hands like he's burying a friend
Hands out the last check and the word that it's the end
María packs her locker, a photo, a glove
Thirty years of mornings, thirty years of love
The machines stand frozen like a row of old men
Who worked till they couldn't and won't wake again

What becomes of a town when the heart stops its beat
When the gates lock for good on a hollowed-out street
We'll sing in the cantina, we'll lift up our glass
To the smoke and the sweat and the years that won't pass

Adiós, fábrica, the whistle won't blow
You fed our table, now where do we go
The trumpets play low for the men in the line
Who gave you their backs and gave you their time

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Drove past the tree line where the signal goes dead
Green fire spilling over the dark like it bled
Killed the engine, killed the lights, let the cold settle in
Sky doing things that the city never lets us begin
Ribbons of neon that no studio could mix
Nature flexing colors that don't come in a six
My breath turning ghost while the heavens go loud
God hit the strobe on the underside of the cloud

Lights up, lights up, dancing in the freeze
Purple and the green moving slow through the trees
No flash, no crowd, just the cold and the glow
Northern lights got me quiet, never felt so low-key, so whole

Phone in my pocket 'cause a lens couldn't hold it
Some things you just witness, you ain't meant to post it
Up here the hustle and the noise lose their grip
Just me and the sky on a frozen little trip
The aurora curl like smoke off a flame
Reminding a hustler that he's small, and that's okay, mane

Far from the trap, far from the stress
The universe glowing in a quiet address
I came for the silence, I left with a sign
That there's something way bigger than the grind on the line

Lights up, lights up, dancing in the freeze
Purple and the green moving slow through the trees
No flash, no crowd, just the cold and the glow
Northern lights got me quiet, never felt so low-key, so whole

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The coffee's gone cold in your favorite chair
Your coat on the hook still holds the shape of you there
The house is too loud in its terrible hush
Last night we buried you, this morning I brush
The crumbs from a table set for one less plate
The sun came up anyway, cruel and ornate

Dance with me once in the empty front room
One last slow turn through the dust and the gloom
The accordion sighs what the tongue cannot say
Goodbye is a tango I learn the next day

The flowers are wilting, the casseroles wait
The phone rings with sorrow I can't translate
I iron the black shirt I no longer need
And find your old letter, and start to read
The bandoneón breathes in the wall of the past
Each step a memory I can't make last

Grief is a partner who won't let me sit
Who pulls me up sharp, who insists, who insists
So I bow to the silence, I lead and I sway
With the ghost of your hand on this terrible day

Dance with me once in the empty front room
One last slow turn through the dust and the gloom
The accordion sighs what the tongue cannot say
Goodbye is a tango I learn the next day

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The parking lot's empty save the snow coming down
My kids open presents on a phone screen across town
I traded the turkey for the buzz of the floor
Twelve hours behind me and twelve hours more
Room 9 has no family, so I held his hand
Hummed him a carol he could barely understand

And it's holy in here under fluorescent light
Where the strangers and the dying make it through the night
I'll be home by morning if the morning will come
A nurse on a holiday, the work's never done

The coffee's burnt bitter, the vending machine's bare
I ate a cold sandwich on the stairs to nowhere
A baby got born just down the hall around two
So I cried in the supply room, then I pulled myself through
There's a paper-hat Santa that somebody taped
To the nurses' station where the long hours scrape

My mother did this and her mother did too
Women in white shoes that the linoleum knew
There's no medal, no parade, no song on the air
Just the call light blinking and somebody there

And it's holy in here under fluorescent light
Where the strangers and the dying make it through the night
I'll be home by morning if the morning will come
A nurse on a holiday, the work's never done

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Got the brown roll screaming as I seal up the lid
Last box in the hallway where my whole life is hid
The walls show squares where my posters used to glow
Echo in the kitchen 'cause there's nowhere left to go
Keys on the counter, deposit's gonna bounce
Four years in this apartment, every dent and every ounce

Tape it up, tape it up, that's the last one
Whistle while the moving truck idles in the sun
Goodbye to the leaky tap and the neighbor's loud TV
Tape it up, I'm packing up the person I used to be

Found a ticket stub stuck behind the old stove
A receipt and a hairpin and a single sad clove
The horns kick in as I sweep up the floor
Spinning round the living room, won't live here no more
The screech of the packing tape, the bounce in my chest
Leaving's just arriving if you squint at it best

New city, new keys, new ceiling to stare
But a part of me's living in the dust hanging there
Pick up the box, give the door one last knock
Then step into nothing as I reset the clock

Tape it up, tape it up, that's the last one
Whistle while the moving truck idles in the sun
Goodbye to the leaky tap and the neighbor's loud TV
Tape it up, I'm packing up the person I used to be

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Knees doing the rhythm I did not rehearse
Clutching crumpled paper with a half-finished verse
The lights were too hot and the mic smelled like beer
Three people clapping and two leaving, I swear
I cracked on the high note, I rushed the whole groove
But something in my hips finally started to move

Oh, that first night, sweaty and green
Worst little set that this town's ever seen
But I caught the bug under that cheap purple glow
Look how far, look how far, look how far we go

The bass player smirked and the barback yawned wide
I forgot my own bridge so I just improvised
Now I headline the room where I once couldn't speak
But I keep that old setlist taped up by the sink
That shaky little kid with the trembling hand
Is the reason I'm standing here fronting the band

So if you're in the back with your palms all wet
Clutching your courage and your first cigarette
Get up on the stage, let the nerves do their thing
The worst night you'll have is the night you'll one day sing

Oh, that first night, sweaty and green
Worst little set that this town's ever seen
But I caught the bug under that cheap purple glow
Look how far, look how far, look how far we go

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
They carried him slow down Rampart and Dumaine
Umbrellas turning circles in the heavy gray rain
The tuba moaned low like a heart in the ground
While the preacher walked silent and the family came around
Lord, we lost a good man, we lost him too soon
Gonna lay him down gentle by the light of the moon

Oh, when the saints come marching, baby, save me a place
They cried on the way there, now they dance with grace
Death took my brother but it can't take the song
New Orleans buries you slow, then it carries you on

First line weeping with a handkerchief raised
Then the snare drum cracking and the second line blazed
The trombone got happy, the trumpet caught fire
Grief turned to glory and the whole street climbed higher
Feet on the cobbles where the sorrow had been
They danced him to heaven, they let the joy in

That's how we do it down here by the river
Weep for the body, then we praise the giver
Black suits twirling like a parasol storm
Keep the spirit, keep it dancing, keep it warm

Oh, when the saints come marching, baby, save me a place
They cried on the way there, now they dance with grace
Death took my brother but it can't take the song
New Orleans buries you slow, then it carries you on

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They picked out the husband and they picked out the dress
Drew me a whole life I never said yes
The in-laws were chosen, the venue was booked
A cage made of gold but a cage when I looked
My suitcase was packed by the side of the bed
While they planned out the future I keep in my head

I'm gone, I'm gone, watch me walk out the door
The life that you built me, I don't want it no more
Funk in my footsteps, freedom in my feet
I'm writing my own future down a different street

Mother in the doorway with her arms folded tight
Father won't look at me, but I'm taking my flight
Left the ring on the table where the tea service shined
Kept nothing they gave me but the strength in my spine
The bassline is calling and it's calling my name
Nobody picks my tomorrow but the woman I became

Maybe I'll be lonely, maybe I'll be broke
But every breath is mine now and that ain't no joke
They can keep the inheritance, the silver, the plan
I'd rather be free than be somebody's pawn, understand

I'm gone, I'm gone, watch me walk out the door
The life that you built me, I don't want it no more
Funk in my footsteps, freedom in my feet
I'm writing my own future down a different street

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
The tether snapped quiet, no bang, just a creak
I watched your gold visor get smaller each week
You tumbled toward Venus, I drifted toward Mars
Two little specks in an ocean of stars
Radio crackling, your voice running thin
Counting the silence as the static rolled in

Drifting apart in the dark, in the dark
Two lonely sparks losing sight of the spark
I'd swim back to reach you but there's nothing to push
Just the cold and the quiet and the endless hush

We trained for the void but they never told us this
How a wave goodbye could turn into the abyss
You said keep on breathing, you said don't lose your head
Then your channel went dead and the panel glowed red
The horns in my helmet, the upbeat won't die
I dance in my suit as I drift through the sky

Maybe in a thousand years some comet will see
Two skeletons grinning where we used to be
Still aimed at each other across all that black
Reaching, forever reaching, but never reaching back

Drifting apart in the dark, in the dark
Two lonely sparks losing sight of the spark
I'd swim back to reach you but there's nothing to push
Just the cold and the quiet and the endless hush

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Thirty years of stepping on these worn-out kitchen tiles
We danced through every paycheck, every river full of miles
The band's packing up the horns now, the lights are running low
But you reach out your hand to me like all those years ago

So dance with me one more time, baby
Let the trumpets carry us slow
We gave it everything we had to give
Now it's time to let it go

The upbeat kept us moving when the world was tumbling down
We skanked through all the heartache in this little river town
Your shoes are tired, my knees are sore, the morning's coming fast
But every step's a memory and every memory's gonna last

No regrets in the offbeat, no sorrow in the brass
Just two old hearts still swinging till the final whistle passed

So dance with me one more time, baby
Let the trumpets carry us slow
We gave it everything we had to give
Now it's time to let it go

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The sun is going down on the final beat
Dust on every body, fire under our feet
We came here as strangers, we're leaving as one
One more drop before the morning sun

Tonight we are alive, tonight we are alive
Hands up to the sky, we never say goodbye
Tonight we are alive, tonight we are alive
Let the bassline take us higher, higher, higher

A thousand neon heartbeats in the open air
Glitter on your shoulders, electric in your hair
The DJ holds the moment in the palm of her hand
Forever in a second, never gonna land

Don't let the music stop
Don't let the feeling drop
We'll dance until the dawn
Long after it is gone

Tonight we are alive, tonight we are alive
Hands up to the sky, we never say goodbye

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
My father's hands were mountains when I was just a boy
They carried all my troubles, they fixed my every toy
Now I watch them tremble as he sips his evening tea
The strongest man I ever knew now leans his weight on me

Oh, father, take it easy now
Let the river carry you slow
You gave me all your sunshine
Now it's my turn to help you grow old, grow old

He used to know the answers to every single thing
Now he asks me twice my name and the songs he used to sing
But the love it never fades, no, it only grows more deep
Some memories are too precious for the years to ever keep

Irie, irie, the seasons come and go
The wise old tree still standing even as the cold winds blow

Oh, father, take it easy now
Let the river carry you slow

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Debajo de la calle duerme un gigante de piedra
The towers rise up high but the ground remembers everything
We dance above his heartbeat, feel the rhythm in the night
Un temblor en la cadera, baby, hold me tight

La ciudad no duerme, no, no
El gigante puede despertar
Dale, dale, move your body
Before the ground begins to shake, oh-oh

Neon on the skyline, the bass beneath our feet
Every step's a warning written in the heat
He's been sleeping for a thousand years below the concrete dream
We build our lives on shoulders that could split right at the seam

Desperta, gigante, no
Let us dance a little more
Una noche, una vida
Before you settle the score

La ciudad no duerme, no, no
El gigante puede despertar

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Key in the door but the lock don't feel the same
Mama on the porch, tears falling when she calls my name
Left a boy with a duffel bag, came back something else
Ghosts in my dreams that I can't tell nobody else
The streets look smaller, the sky looks too wide
Carrying brothers that I left on the other side

I'm home, but home don't feel like home no more
Walked through the fire, made it back to my front door
Lay my boots down, try to find my peace
Praying that the war inside me finally cease

Flinch at the fireworks, freeze up at the sound
Trying to plant my feet back on familiar ground
They shake my hand and call me hero in the street
But a hero's just a man still counting friends he couldn't keep

So I hold my little daughter, feel her heartbeat slow
Maybe that's the reason that I made it home, I know

I'm home, but home don't feel like home no more
Walked through the fire, made it back to my front door

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I carried all this anger like a stone inside my chest
For all the words you said to me, for all the rest
Now I'm standing at your graveside with the rain upon my face
Finally letting go of all the hurt I can't replace

So I forgive you, oh I forgive you
Even though you'll never hear me say
I forgive you, yes I forgive you
And I'm setting both our souls free today

I waited for an apology that never came around
Now the only voice that answers is the wind across the ground
But holding all this bitterness was killing me inside
So I'm laying down the burden that I carried till you died

It's too late for you to hear it, but it's right on time for me
The heaviest of chains can break with one small turning key

So I forgive you, oh I forgive you
Even though you'll never hear me say

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They built these glass towers on a heartbeat down below
A giant in the dark that only the old streets know
I feel him when I'm dancing, feel him when I sleep
A secret that this city's always trying hard to keep

We're living on a giant, baby, breathing slow
One day he's gonna rise from down below
So love me like the ground could shake tonight
Hold me in the neon, in the city light

Every skyscraper's a whisper, every subway is a sigh
We're walking on a wonder while we're staring at the sky
They say he dreams our future in the marrow of the stone
A million little lives and not a one of us alone

So if the morning brings the trembling
And the towers start to fall
I'll be holding onto you
Through the rising of it all

We're living on a giant, baby, breathing slow
One day he's gonna rise from down below

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody thought we'd make it, wrote us off from the start
But we carried every doubt like fuel inside the heart
Forty-eight minutes left and the whole world's holding tight
We were born for this moment, we were made to fight

Run it, run it, give it everything you've got
This is the only shot we'll ever got
Faster, harder, don't you stop the beat
Underdogs don't ever know defeat

The clock is bleeding seconds and the crowd is screaming loud
Every name they laughed at now is roaring from the crowd
One more push, one more breath, one more line to cross
We got nothing left to lose and everything to toss

Break it down, break it down
The bassline's pounding like our hearts
We rise, we rise, we rise
This is where the legend starts

Run it, run it, give it everything you've got
This is the only shot we'll ever got

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
The dust hangs low on the dying field tonight
A crooked moon above us casting borrowed light
They buried us in whispers, said we'd never stand
Now we're walking toward the reckoning with dirt upon our hands

Oh, ring the bell, the final game is here
We got nothing but our scars and all our fear
Lord, let us fall like thunder, let us rise like flame
Win or lose, they'll carve our name upon the same old frame

The preacher in the bleachers says a prayer for the lost
We counted up our blessings and we tallied up the cost
Every broken bone a verse, every bruise a holy psalm
Walking through the valley with a quiet kind of calm

No angels gonna save us, no, the devil's in the score
But we'll meet him in the dirt tonight and settle it for sure

Oh, ring the bell, the final game is here
We got nothing but our scars and all our fear

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Saltwater kisses underneath the August moon
You came in like the tide and you'll be leaving soon
We danced on the boardwalk till the stars began to fade
A fleeting little summer of the love that we made

Oh, summer baby, hold me by the sea
Just for a season you belong to me
When the leaves start falling you'll be on your way
But tonight, oh tonight, let's let the music play

Your laughter on the ocean breeze, a melody so sweet
Sandy toes and lemonade and shadows in the heat
We knew it wouldn't last, baby, we knew right from the start
But summer love still leaves a mark upon the heart

So pour another glass of gold and watch the sunset burn
Some flames are meant to flicker out, some loves were never meant to return

Oh, summer baby, hold me by the sea
Just for a season you belong to me

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Neon highway humming, mile marker glow
Three a.m. and I'm a thousand miles from home
The phone rings soft against the engine's lullaby
Just to hear you breathing underneath the sky

Are you sleeping, are you dreaming
Somewhere warm and far away
I'm just a voice across the darkness
Telling you I'm on my way

The white lines blur like memories I can't quite hold
Coffee gone to ashes and the cab is getting cold
Your whisper through the static is the only light I see
A little piece of heaven reaching out to me

Don't hang up, don't hang up
Just stay with me a while
I'll drive into the morning
On the memory of your smile

Are you sleeping, are you dreaming
Somewhere warm and far away

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Floating in the silence where the stars don't make a sound
Your tether came undone and now there's nothing holding ground
I reach my hand out slowly but you're farther than before
Watching you get smaller, can't do nothing anymore

We're drifting, drifting, slow and far
Two little lights between the stars
I'd give the whole world just to pull you near
But space don't care about a falling tear

Radio is crackling with the things we never said
The Earth's a marble spinning in the quiet of my head
We trained for every danger but they never taught us this
How to say goodbye across an endless dark abyss

So I'll watch you fade like static
Into the velvet black
Knowing every orbit
Won't bring you back

We're drifting, drifting, slow and far
Two little lights between the stars

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I left this little town with dreams too big to hold
Chasing all the city lights and streets paved up in gold
But I came back this summer and I saw you on the square
The girl I never noticed with the sunlight in her hair

And I'm falling, falling for my hometown
For the love that was always around
Didn't have to travel all those miles
It was right here in your smile

We walked the same old sidewalks where we played as kids
Now my heart is doing things it never did
Funny how you search the whole wide world to find
The sweetest thing was waiting just a step behind

So I'm staying, I'm staying right here
Where the rivers run slow and the love is clear
No more running, no more roam
I found my heaven here at home

And I'm falling, falling for my hometown
For the love that was always around

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
The music starts, we lock our eyes like swords across the floor
A hundred nights of bitterness, a hundred years of war
They put our hands together when we swore we'd never bend
Now every step's a battle that we cannot seem to end

So we move, we move, against our will
Two storms that cannot stay still
But somewhere in the turning we begin to understand
The enemy was nothing but a stranger's outstretched hand

The rhythm pulls us closer and the walls begin to fall
I see myself reflected in the one I hate the most of all
The crescendo's rising slowly like a tide we can't outrun
Two rivals on the dancefloor slowly turning into one

Louder now, the strings ignite
We let go of the fight
All the thunder breaks to light
As we surrender to the night

So we move, we move, against our will
Two storms that cannot stay still

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
Neon crosses flicker in the rain-soaked city night
I threw away my prayers when the world stopped feeling right
Walked the empty highways with an emptiness inside
A hollow where the heavens used to ride

But I'm finding it again, oh, finding it again
A light inside the static, a beginning in the end
I lost it in the darkness but it never lost me
Now I'm finding it again, setting my soul free

The years rolled by like engines on a fading neon street
I searched in every shadow for a heart that wouldn't beat
Then a stranger's kindness lit a fire in the cold
And I felt the something sacred I had sold

So let the synths rise like a hymn
Let the darkness start to dim
I was lost but now I see
The faith was always inside me

But I'm finding it again, oh, finding it again
A light inside the static, a beginning in the end

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
I was steppin' light in my Sunday shoes
Waitin' on a kiss, not the breakin' news
Then my pocket buzzed at a quarter past two
Three little lines and the whole thing's through

No trumpet blew, no slow goodbye
Just a gray little bubble and a reason why
You couldn't say it close, couldn't say it sweet
So you typed it out with the radio beat

Oh baby, baby, you let me down soft as a screen
Never danced me out the door, never came between
A hello and a so-long with a hand in mine
You ended all our music in a single line

Well the band plays on though my heart don't swing
I'm a one-step short of the everything
I keep my chin up high and my collar clean
But I'm dancin' alone with a telephone screen

If you'd looked me in the eye, I could let it go
But you killed the band before the final show
Now I tip my hat to the empty floor
And I won't read my messages anymore

Oh baby, baby, you let me down soft as a screen
Never danced me out the door, never came between
A hello and a so-long with a hand in mine
You ended all our music in a single line

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Got one bag packed and a tank of gas
Said goodbye to the church and the cuttin' grass
The water tower's got my name in paint
But this town's got everything I ain't

Mama on the porch with her arms crossed tight
Daddy didn't come out to wave goodnight
I been dyin' slow on this two-lane road
Gonna shake this dust and lighten my load

I'm leavin', leavin', and I ain't comin' back
Followin' the headlights down a long black track
Every dog I knew, every door I closed
Goodbye little town where nothin' growed

The diner's dark, the mill shut down
Ain't nothin' left but a one-light town
I loved a girl on the river bend
But love don't pay and the road don't end

Maybe I'll cry when the county line
Disappears in the mirror, fades like wine
But a man's gotta run when his heart's gone cold
So I'm leavin' this little town for good and for old

I'm leavin', leavin', and I ain't comin' back
Followin' the headlights down a long black track
Every dog I knew, every door I closed
Goodbye little town where nothin' growed

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Three dots blinkin', then they disappear
Last thing you sent me, "I can't do this here"
Phone on the dresser, screen lightin' up the dark
You ain't even call me, left it cold like a park
I was countin' on forever, you was countin' on the door
Typed it out so quiet, didn't say it no more
Read receipt, yeah, I saw it go through
Whole thing crumbled in a message or two

You broke my heart through a screen, no warnin' (no warnin')
Sent the text at 4 in the mornin' (mornin')
Now I'm scrollin' up, readin' how it used to be
You ended us in a bubble, set me free, set me free

Delete the thread but it's burned in my head
Every word you typed, every word you said
Icon goes gray, you ain't online no more
Closed it like a app, left me out on the floor

Could've called me, could've looked me in the eye
Instead it's a notification, then goodbye
Thumb on the keyboard, that's how love can die
Now I'm starin' at the ceiling askin' why, askin' why

You broke my heart through a screen, no warnin' (no warnin')
Sent the text at 4 in the mornin' (mornin')
Now I'm scrollin' up, readin' how it used to be
You ended us in a bubble, set me free, set me free

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Grandfather walks where the water meets the sand
Reads the morning like a line across his hand
He says the ocean's got a rhythm, child, just learn to feel the pull
The moon will tell you when it's empty, when it's full

So I watch him point to where the waves begin
Countin' every breath the sea is takin' in

He's teachin' the tide, teachin' me how to rise
With the salt in the air and the sun in his eyes
In and out, in and out, like a heartbeat slow
The oldest song the water ever known

He's got stories in the lines around his face
Every storm he weathered left a quiet grace
He says don't fight the current, baby, let it carry you home
The sea was here before us and it never feels alone

He's teachin' the tide, teachin' me how to rise
With the salt in the air and the sun in his eyes
In and out, in and out, like a heartbeat slow
The oldest song the water ever known

One day he'll be a wave that I can't see
But the ocean's gonna whisper him to me
And I'll stand right here where the two worlds meet
And feel his footsteps in the foam beneath my feet

He's teachin' the tide, teachin' me how to rise
With the salt in the air and the sun in his eyes
In and out, in and out, like a heartbeat slow
The oldest song the water ever known

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The coffee's gone cold in a borrowed cup
The black coat's still on, I can't take it up
Light through the curtains in a bruise of gray
The house keeps breathing like you'd stay

There's casseroles stacked that we'll never eat
Your shoes by the door in the morning heat
The phone doesn't ring, the clock won't move
I'm wearing the silence like it has to prove

And the day came anyway, soft and wide
Without asking me if I was alright
The sun rose careless over everything
And I forgot for a second, then remembered the sting

Washed all the glasses, folded all the chairs
The flowers already turning brown downstairs
I keep setting two plates out of habit and ache
Then taking one back for the quiet's sake

How does the kettle still know how to sing
How do the birds not feel a single thing
The world's so loud in how it carries on
And I'm just standing here now that you're gone

And the day came anyway, soft and wide
Without asking me if I was alright
