The sun rose careless over everything
And I forgot for a second, then remembered the sting

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Neon humming over felt and chrome
Midnight table, nobody goes home
Four kings glowing in the smoke and light
We said one more hand and we played all night

Started with quarters, then it climbed so high
The stakes turned to things that money can't buy
He bet his watch, then he bet his name
Then the room went cold and it stopped being a game

Deal me out, deal me out of the dark
The river card's bleeding a violet spark
We pushed it too far past the point of return
Watch the whole deck and the city burn

The dealer's eyes were a digital blue
She smiled like a screen when she knew you were through
I folded too late, lost more than my chips
There's a bet you can't take back once it leaves your lips

The clock on the wall blinking 3 AM red
Every face at the table like the recently dead
I cashed out nothing, walked into the rain
With a chrome king of hearts and a permanent stain

Deal me out, deal me out of the dark
The river card's bleeding a violet spark
We pushed it too far past the point of return
Watch the whole deck and the city burn

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Sun goin' down on the cul-de-sac
Boxes in the car and there's no turnin' back
We cranked up the radio one more time
Squeezed the whole season into a dime

Sprinklers still runnin' on the neighbor's lawn
We stayed up to watch the August dawn
Greasy paper plates and a melting cone
Last night together 'fore we're all alone

Get up, get down, it's the last of the heat
Funkin' on the corner, dancin' in the street
Tomorrow we scatter, but tonight we're whole
Summer in our pocket and fire in our soul

Jimmy's headed east and Maria's goin' west
We wore out the season like a favorite vest
Gonna miss the way the porch light glows
Where everybody's somebody that everybody knows

So slap the bass for the days we burned
For the lessons that the schoolbooks never learned
Hold the groove 'til the morning comes
And the engines start and the future hums

Get up, get down, it's the last of the heat
Funkin' on the corner, dancin' in the street
Tomorrow we scatter, but tonight we're whole
Summer in our pocket and fire in our soul

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Waved at the harbor 'til the land turned blue
Everything I knew in a trunk or two
My mother's photograph against my chest
Sailin' for a country I ain't met yet

Salt in the wind and the gulls cryin' low
Don't know the language where this boat gonna go
Left my brother standin' on the broken pier
Gonna build a life from the hope and the fear

Lord, carry me over the deep dark sea
Far from the hills that made a man of me
I'm tradin' my homeland for a chance to grow
And I'm cryin' for the world I'll never know

Third-class bunk and a stranger's snore
Dreamin' of the harbor on the other shore
They say there's work and they say there's gold
But a heart left behind don't grow back old

If I make it, Mama, I'll send for you
With the first few dollars that I sweat into
Keep my chair at the table, keep the lantern lit
A part of me is stayin', I can feel it sit

Lord, carry me over the deep dark sea
Far from the hills that made a man of me
I'm tradin' my homeland for a chance to grow
And I'm cryin' for the world I'll never know

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
We drove to the quarry with the windows down
Last tank of gas in this nothing town
You carved our names where the water meets the stone
Tomorrow morning we'll be on our own

Got a duffel bag and a half-charged phone
A playlist made for the great unknown
We lay on the hood 'til the stars came out
Nobody saying what it's all about

So here's to the end of the endless days
The paper-thin gold of the August haze
We were everything and now we're almost gone
Hold on to the night before the world moves on

You're going to the coast and I'm staying near
We both know it changes after this year
The streetlights flicker like they're saying bye
To a couple of kids and a summer sky

Maybe we'll text, maybe we'll fade
Like the chalk on the road and the plans we made
But for one more hour let's pretend it stays
This perfect ordinary blur of days

So here's to the end of the endless days
The paper-thin gold of the August haze
We were everything and now we're almost gone
Hold on to the night before the world moves on

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man on the seawall, eighty years of rust
Screamin' at the ocean 'cause the ocean's all he trusts
They tore down his harbor, put a parking lot in
But he's down there every morning where the waves begin

He spits at the moon and he counts the swell
Got a lifetime of salt and stories to tell
The kids on their phones never look his way
But he's teachin' the tide how to crash anyway

Go! Old man against the sea
Last one standing where the wild things be
The water don't listen but he yells it loud
One stubborn heart in a drownin' crowd

They said retire, said sit down, said quit
He said the ocean and me ain't done with it
Every wave's a verse and the foam's the beat
He conducts the chaos from his broken seat

When he's gone the tide's gonna keep on coming
But nobody left to hear the ocean drumming
So I'll take his spot on the crumbling stone
And teach the tide so it's never alone

Go! Old man against the sea
Last one standing where the wild things be
The water don't listen but he yells it loud
One stubborn heart in a drownin' crowd

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The roll of tape screams in the empty hall
Nail holes left like stars on the bedroom wall
Kitchen's just an echo, the fridge unplugged
Ten years of a life in a cardboard hug

Found a ticket stub from our second date
Under the couch where the dust accumulates
I almost left it but I tucked it in
With the coffee mugs and the cracked rolling pin

So I tape up the last box, write the kitchen in pen
Take one slow look at where we've been
The key on the counter, the door swinging wide
Everything that mattered fits inside

The light comes different through the curtainless glass
Funny how a home becomes a thing of the past
The stairs still creak on the third one down
Gonna miss that song when I'm out of town

Walls don't hold the laughter, walls don't keep
The quiet of a Sunday or a baby's sleep
We carry it with us, that's the only way
So I press down the tape and I drive away

So I tape up the last box, write the kitchen in pen
Take one slow look at where we've been
The key on the counter, the door swinging wide
Everything that mattered fits inside

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Graveyard shift in the marble hall
One dim light and a single wall
She's been hangin' there three hundred years
Gold in her eyes that never disappears
I walk my rounds, but I always stop
Where the painted girl and the silence drop

Tick of my radio, hum of the floor
I swear she breathes a little more

Run, run, the night runs deep
While the city and the cameras sleep
Just me and her in the rolling dark
A guard and a ghost and a single spark

They lock the doors and they kill the sound
Footsteps echo when there's no one around
I tell her my secrets, she don't say a word
But I feel her listen, it's absurd

When the dawn comes in and the crowds return
They'll never know how the long nights burn
They'll glance and move on down the line
But every midnight, she is mine

Run, run, the night runs deep
While the city and the cameras sleep
Just me and her in the rolling dark
A guard and a ghost and a single spark

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Volví al pueblo donde aprendí a caminar
The dusty plaza and the bells that call
I thought I'd seen every face in this town
Then you crossed the square in your faded brown

You grew up three streets from my mother's door
How did I never see you here before
Now the trumpets rise and my heart's a thief
For a hometown love and the sweet relief

Ay, ay, ay, mi corazón
Fell in the place where I was born
Under the same old steeple and sky
I found my whole world in the corner of my eye

We danced where the river runs slow and wide
With the mountains keeping watch like an old town pride
You know my grandmother, you know my name
And nothing in my life will be the same

I traveled the world to find what I lacked
And it was here at home, waiting, all the way back
So strike up the band by the fountain stone
For the sweetest love is the one back home

Ay, ay, ay, mi corazón
Fell in the place where I was born
Under the same old steeple and sky
I found my whole world in the corner of my eye

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The fluorescent hum on the Christmas ward
A tinsel string and a monitor's chord
I clocked in at six when the sky was black
It's black again and I ain't gone back

My babies opened their gifts on a screen
While I held a stranger's hand in between
The machines all sing their electric hymns
And the angel of mercy got tired limbs

Lord, it's a holy night on the dying floor
I'll work 'til the morning and then work some more
There's no silent night when the bells won't ring
Just the beep of a heart and the songs I sing

Room four passed quiet 'bout a quarter to ten
I closed her eyes and I breathed again
Then I dried my face and I straightened my coat
And I went next door with a hope in my throat

Who watches over the ones who watch
Who holds the hand at the final notch
I'll trade my Christmas for a stranger's breath
Standing guard at the door of death

Lord, it's a holy night on the dying floor
I'll work 'til the morning and then work some more
There's no silent night when the bells won't ring
Just the beep of a heart and the songs I sing

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
We loaded the cart 'neath a thin gray moon
Father's old still and a fiddle tune
The jars all rattled on the mountain track
With the law somewhere on our hard luck back

My brother held the reins and he sang so low
A song our mother taught us long ago
The creek was rising and the bridge was thin
And a lantern swung where they took us in

Oh, run, boys, run through the heather and the rain
The whiskey's spilled and the horse is lame
We gambled with the devil for a bag of coin
And the night came down on the moonshine line

They fired one shot and the cart turned over
Glass in the mud and the spilled-out clover
I dragged my brother to the cold creek shore
But the songs we sang, we'll sing no more

Tell our mother that we did our best
We ran the dark hills with fire in our chest
Lay me down where the wild thyme grows
With a jar in my hand where the cold wind blows

Oh, run, boys, run through the heather and the rain
The whiskey's spilled and the horse is lame
We gambled with the devil for a bag of coin
And the night came down on the moonshine line

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the hold in the dead of night
Captain wouldn't tell us what we carried
The crates was cold and they hummed with a light
And three good men we already buried

Oh, this cargo's cursed, Lord, this cargo's cursed
We sailed out blessed but we came back worse
The water won't take it, the land won't either
This cargo's cursed and I'm carrying the fever

Compass spinning like a drunk man's eye
Stars done left us out here alone
Every gull that follows is a soul gone by
And I can hear them crying in the cargo's moan

Oh, this cargo's cursed, Lord, this cargo's cursed
We sailed out blessed but we came back worse
The water won't take it, the land won't either
This cargo's cursed and I'm carrying the fever

Throw it overboard, the first mate said
But the chains grew tight 'round his sleeping head
Now he walks the deck though we know he's dead
And the crates keep humming till the morning's red

If I make it home I'll never sail again
I'll bury my boots in the dry red clay
But the harbor lights look a long way then
And this cursed old cargo wants me to stay

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody picked us, nobody believed
Wrote us off in the morning paper
But we laced 'em up and we rolled up our sleeves
Gonna make 'em eat that label later

This is the last game, baby, this is the last call
Four quarters left in us, gonna give 'em all
We ain't got the money, we ain't got the name
But we got each other in this final game

Coach got a voice like a gravel road
Said leave it all out on the floor
We been carrying more than this season's load
Ain't a one of us walking out that door

This is the last game, baby, this is the last call
Four quarters left in us, gonna give 'em all
We ain't got the money, we ain't got the name
But we got each other in this final game

Clock's running down, scoreboard tight
My legs are gone but my heart's all right
Whatever happens when they cut the light
They'll remember the dogs that came to fight

Win or lose when the buzzer's done
We walked out taller than we walked in
They'll say we never should've come this far
But underdogs, baby, that's where we begin

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Shoo-bop, shoo-bop, the harbor's calling low
Mama packed a suitcase, said it's time we go
I kissed the only street I'd ever known
And waved at all the windows of my home

Oh, across the sea (across the sea)
There's a brand new life waiting for me
With a sky so wide (so wide, so wide)
And my sweetheart standing by my side

The ocean rocked us slow, doo-wop, doo-wop
I counted every wave until they'd stop
A stranger with a fiddle played a tune
And we danced beneath a foreign moon

Oh, across the sea (across the sea)
There's a brand new life waiting for me
With a sky so wide (so wide, so wide)
And my sweetheart standing by my side

Will they speak my name the same out there?
Will the rain still smell of salt and prayer?
I hold a photograph and a folded coat
And all my hopes are sailing on this boat

When the city lights came up like falling stars
Shoo-bop, I knew that this new world was ours
So wave goodbye, my darling, dry your eyes
We're crossing to the morning, you and I

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The city is dancing, the bells are alight
But I trade my red dress for the white of the night
The corridor hums and the monitors glow
While somewhere the fireworks open and throw

Dance with me, midnight, I've no one but you
The second shift bleeding the first one straight through
My feet keep the rhythm the long hours keep
I'll waltz with the dying and dance them to sleep

Room seven is frightened, room nine is alone
I hold every hand like it's somebody's own
The coffee's gone cold and the holiday's spent
But the heart on the screen keeps its sharp accent

Dance with me, midnight, I've no one but you
The second shift bleeding the first one straight through
My feet keep the rhythm the long hours keep
I'll waltz with the dying and dance them to sleep

My children are sleeping beneath a lit tree
They'll open their gifts before they see me
But here is a fever that no one can hold
So I give them my warmth in this corridor cold

When the dawn slides its key in the door of the ward
I'll loosen my collar, unclench, and reward
Myself with a moment, a breath, and a chair
Then the next double calls and I'm already there

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Neon on the dashboard, midnight empty lot
Your hands are on the wheel, ten and two, don't stop
The engine's a heartbeat, the city's a glow
I'm buckled beside you, just let it go slow

Drive, drive into the violet light
I'll be your passenger tonight
You're faster than I ever knew
The whole horizon's made for you

Yesterday I carried you home in my arms
Now you're checking mirrors and reading the alarms
First gear is trembling, the clutch finds its place
And I see your mother in the line of your face

Drive, drive into the violet light
I'll be your passenger tonight
You're faster than I ever knew
The whole horizon's made for you

Green light, your foot finds the floor
One day you'll drive right out my door
So I'll memorize this synth-lit street
The quiet hum, your heartbeat's beat

Pull it to the curb now, kill the radio shine
You did it, you did it, the road's gonna be fine
We sit in the silence of the cooling machine
And I'm prouder than anything I've ever been

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
My daddy shook hands over me one spring
Traded my life like a bartered thing
A ring and a deed and a name not mine
A fence built tall down the property line

So I'm gone down the holler 'fore the rooster crows
With a fiddle and a satchel and the clothes that I chose
They can keep the arrangement, the land, and the vow
I'm marrying the morning and I'm choosing it now

Mama she wept but she pressed in my hand
A biscuit, a dollar, and a map of the land
Go on, child, go, 'fore they bind you tight
There's a freight train calling at the edge of the night

So I'm gone down the holler 'fore the rooster crows
With a fiddle and a satchel and the clothes that I chose
They can keep the arrangement, the land, and the vow
I'm marrying the morning and I'm choosing it now

Let the banjo ring on the ridgeline high
Let 'em wonder where and let 'em wonder why
A promised life ain't a life at all
When it's somebody else who made the call

I'll work my own acre, I'll sing my own song
I'll answer to no one for where I belong
And if love ever finds me it'll find me free
Not a deal in a parlor decided for me

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the diner 'fore the sun comes up
Pour a hundred coffees in a hundred cups
Clock out, drive across town, clock in again
Stocking shelves at midnight, where do I begin

Two jobs, no sleep, and the bills still climb
Running twice as fast and losing every time
They say hard work pays, well I'm working it blind
Working two jobs and still falling behind

Kids are getting taller in the photos I keep
I kiss 'em on the forehead while they're fast asleep
The rent went up again, the truck needs a part
I'm rich in good intentions and broke in the heart

Two jobs, no sleep, and the bills still climb
Running twice as fast and losing every time
They say hard work pays, well I'm working it blind
Working two jobs and still falling behind

I ain't asking for a mansion or a name in lights
Just a little breathing room on a Friday night
A chance to catch my breath, a chance to get ahead
Before the worry follows me right into bed

But I'll lace 'em up tomorrow 'cause that's what we do
For the ones we love we push our whole lives through
Maybe someday this mountain's gonna level out
Till then I keep on climbing, that's what love's about

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Tether snapped clean in the silent black
Your visor's a mirror, there's no turning back
I'm reaching out slow but the gap keeps wide
Two little lights on opposite tides

Drifting, drifting, lose the signal in the dark
Your voice breaking up like a dying spark
I count every meter that the void puts between
Two astronauts falling out of the scene

Radio crackle, your breath in my ear
Thirty more seconds till you disappear
The Earth turns below us, a marble of blue
And I burn all my air just to stay close to you

Drifting, drifting, lose the signal in the dark
Your voice breaking up like a dying spark
I count every meter that the void puts between
Two astronauts falling out of the scene

No gravity left to pull us home
Just the hum of the suit and the cold and the chrome
Say it once more 'fore the static wins
Say you remember where the love begins

Then silence, the deepest I've ever known
A single white point in an ocean of stone
I close my eyes and I let the dark take hold
Two astronauts, two stories never told

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slam shut and the dark swallows whole
The lights flicker fast like they're reading my soul
My heart finds a rhythm too quick for the song
And the tunnel keeps stretching the whole ride long

Breathe, breathe, count the stations slow
The train holds its tempo, won't let me go
Four beats of terror, two beats of air
I'm dancing alone with a ghost that's not there

The stranger beside me won't notice my hands
How they grip the cold pole like a drowning man's
The map on the wall is a maze of red
And the next stop is spinning inside of my head

Breathe, breathe, count the stations slow
The train holds its tempo, won't let me go
Four beats of terror, two beats of air
I'm dancing alone with a ghost that's not there

Name five things I can see in the car
The ad, the rail, the door left ajar
The floor underfoot, the gray of my coat
And slowly the panic unclenches my throat

The doors open up to a platform of light
I step out and steady, I made it tonight
The city receives me, the air tastes like rain
And I'll learn how to waltz with this fear again

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Alone on the rock where the gray waves break
I tend to the flame for the drowned ones' sake
Each night I write words that no man will read
And cast them to you in the salt and the screed

Oh sea, my devourer, my merciless bride
I've given my years to your terrible tide
Take these letters of fire, take this heart on the stone
You're the only one, sea, that I've ever known

The storm is a chorus that shatters the glass
The foghorn a hymn for the ships that won't pass
I scream out my love and the gale screams it back
A covenant carved on the cliff and the black

Oh sea, my devourer, my merciless bride
I've given my years to your terrible tide
Take these letters of fire, take this heart on the stone
You're the only one, sea, that I've ever known

You drowned all the sailors, you'll drown me as well
And I'll sink with a love that no language can tell
My lantern burns on though the keeper grows cold
Wed to the water, the dark, and the bold

When they find this tower with no man at the light
Let them read of the keeper who married the night
My ashes the foam and my bones the deep reef
Forever your lover, your light, and your grief

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
There's a corner cafe where we used to meet
I still slow my steps when I cross that street
You ordered black coffee and a slice of pie
And I let you walk out without saying goodbye

You're the one that got away, the one I let go
The deepest regret that a fool's gonna know
I had the whole world in the palm of my hand
And I watched it pour out like a fistful of sand

I heard you got married in a town up north
A good man, a garden, and a kid running forth
I tip back my whiskey and I wish you the best
Then I lie wide awake with this hole in my chest

You're the one that got away, the one I let go
The deepest regret that a fool's gonna know
I had the whole world in the palm of my hand
And I watched it pour out like a fistful of sand

If I could go back to that rain-slicked door
I'd take your two hands and I'd ask for more
But time only runs in the one mean way
And you'll always be the one that got away

So I sing to the bottle and I sing to the night
A song for the love I held too light
Somewhere you're laughing in the morning sun
And I'm here counting all I should've done

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
We pulled to the lot where the gravel meets grace
I saw all the years in the light on your face
Your hands on the wheel like a prayer just begun
Lord, watch over this child and the road she'll run

Drive on, drive on, the Lord's at your side
He'll steady your hands on this wonderful ride
I'm teaching you turns but He's teaching you more
There's a faithful old hand on the wheel and the door

First time you stalled it you laughed till you cried
I said baby just breathe, keep your eyes open wide
Every mile is a blessing, every stop sign a psalm
And the good Lord is riding in the cradle of calm

Drive on, drive on, the Lord's at your side
He'll steady your hands on this wonderful ride
I'm teaching you turns but He's teaching you more
There's a faithful old hand on the wheel and the door

One day this road's gonna carry you far
Past the reach of my arms, past the porch and the star
But I trust in the One who goes where you go
So I'll let you drive on and I'll let the faith grow

When you pulled back in with that smile so bright
I raised up my hands to the giver of light
Thank You for the daughter, the road, and the day
Guide her home, sweet Jesus, every mile of the way

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man on the shoreline, salt in his beard
Been reading these waters for sixty-odd years
He say, "Listen up, young blood, the ocean got rules,
More than they ever gon' teach you in schools"
Moon pull the water like it's pulling a string
He knows every season, every shift, every swing
Where the current runs cold and the riptide hide
Grandfather standing, he's teaching the tide

Tide come in, tide go out
That's what the elder be talking about
Patience and rhythm, you move when it's right
Learn from the water, you'll be all right

He点 a finger at the foam where the fish gonna run
Say the sea give plenty but you take only some
Leave the rest for tomorrow, leave the rest for the young
That's a wisdom older than any song sung
I was racing the waves, he said slow down your stride
The ocean don't hurry, learn to walk with the tide

Tide come in, tide go out
That's what the elder be talking about
Patience and rhythm, you move when it's right
Learn from the water, you'll be all right

Now he's gone with the gulls but his lessons remain
I feel him in the spray, I hear him in the rain
So I bring my own grandson down to the sand
Put the knowledge of the water right back in his hand

Tide it keep teaching long after we pass
Writing its wisdom in the foam on the glass
So I stand where he stood with the salt in my eyes
And I teach the next current how the old tide rise

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent hum and a coffee gone cold
The waitress knows everyone, no one gets old
The window's a mirror of the rain coming down
The last lit-up booth on the edge of the town

Stay open, stay open, all through the night
A washed-out cathedral in the kitchen's pale light
We're the lost and the lonely, the wired and the worn
Drifting in fog till the gray of the morn

The trucker, the dreamer, the heartbroke, the lost
We order the silence and we pay any cost
The jukebox is murmuring under the buzz
A song about everything love ever was

Stay open, stay open, all through the night
A washed-out cathedral in the kitchen's pale light
We're the lost and the lonely, the wired and the worn
Drifting in fog till the gray of the morn

Sugar dissolving in a slow turning spoon
The neon outside like a flickering moon
Nobody asks where you came from or why
Just refills your cup as the hours float by

When dawn comes to bleach out the booths and the floor
The bell softly rings as I push through the door
The diner exhales and it lets the day in
But tonight I'll come back to the haze once again

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Thirty years walking the same marble hall
My boots know every crack in the wall
But I always slow down by the gallery's end
Where a girl in a blue dress became my friend

She's been waiting on a wall since 1893
With her eyes turned soft like she's looking at me
The whole world walks by and they never stay
But I close up the lights and I tip my hat her way

They say she's a duchess from a faraway land
With a letter half-folded in her painted hand
I've made up a hundred sweet stories at night
Of the love she's still keeping in that amber light

She's been waiting on a wall since 1893
With her eyes turned soft like she's looking at me
The whole world walks by and they never stay
But I close up the lights and I tip my hat her way

The tourists take pictures and they hurry along
They don't hear the quiet, they don't feel the song
But a lonesome old guard and a girl made of paint
We keep one another from going faint

When I hang up my badge for the very last time
I'll stand there a while in the closing-bell chime
And I'll whisper goodbye to the only one
Who stayed through the years till my watch was done

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon buzzing pink against the wet black street
Coffee going cold but the booth keeps my seat
Grease on the window, clock that never sleeps
Waitress knows my order before I take a seat
Three in the morning and the city's holding breath
Kitchen light is humming like it's cheating death

Luces encendidas, never close the door
Dame un cafecito, baby, one more, one more
The griddle keeps singing till the sun comes up
Everybody's lonely with a half-full cup

Trucker in the corner with a map he can't fold
Teenagers laughing, hiding from the cold
A cook flipping eggs to a radio that's old
Every broken story finds a stool to hold
We pay in loose change and we pay in time
The jukebox eats a quarter, plays the same old rhyme

Luces encendidas, never close the door
Dame un cafecito, baby, one more, one more
The griddle keeps singing till the sun comes up
Everybody's lonely with a half-full cup

When the bars throw you out and the buses don't run
This little island glows like a second sun
No questions, no closing, just a plate and a friend
Dance with me here where the long nights end

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Cuarenta años he caminado este salón
Polished shoes on marble, the slow patrol I own
The crowds come and vanish like the morning rain
But she stays on the wall in her golden frame
A woman painted weeping by a hand long dead
And every night I bow to her instead

Ay, mi pintura, you never grow old
While my hair turns to silver and my hands turn cold
The trumpets in my chest play a song for you
Guarding what I love is all I know to do

They lock the heavy doors and they kill the lights
Just her oil-painted eyes and me through the nights
I tell her of my daughter, of the wife I lost
She listens like a saint and never counts the cost
The violins of memory keep playing low
In a hall full of strangers she's the only soul I know

When I retire they'll hang a younger man
To watch her quiet sorrow as only he can
But tell her, when I'm gone and the museum's still
That a guard once loved her more than any will

Ay, mi pintura, you never grow old
While my hair turns to silver and my hands turn cold
The trumpets in my chest play a song for you
Guarding what I love is all I know to do

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
The street is the same but the windows are wrong
Someone else's curtains where my heart used to belong
I kill the headlights, let the engine idle low
Third floor on the left, a light I used to know
My hands on the wheel like they're learning how to pray
I told myself I'd never drive this way

Que dolor, que dolor, the asphalt remembers your name
Every brick of that building is a coal of the same old flame
I clap once for the years and once for the lie
Then I let the red light hold me till it's time to drive

A shadow at the glass, but it isn't you
Just a stranger living rooms we walked into
The guitar in my ribs is striking minor and cruel
I'm a grown man crying like a lovesick fool
The city keeps spinning, doesn't care that I'm here
Bleeding in a parking space for one more year

Vamos, foot on the gas, leave the ghost on the stair
The night swallows your window, swallows the prayer
But at the next corner I will turn around again
Flamenco of the broken who keep loving past the end

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Born on a corner where the streetlights die young
Sirens for a lullaby, that's the only song I'd sung
Mama worked her knuckles down to bone and to red
Gravel in the gutter, that's the only road they said

But I made it out, Lord, I made it out the block
With holes in my pockets and a stone in my sock
They said I'd never leave, said the wall was too tall
Now I'm standing on the outside, looking over it all

My brother caught a bullet, my best friend caught a cell
I caught the last bus out of that little patch of hell
I buried all my heroes 'fore I turned twenty-three
Every name on that headstone was a piece of me

But I made it out, Lord, I made it out the block
With holes in my pockets and a stone in my sock
They said I'd never leave, said the wall was too tall
Now I'm standing on the outside, looking over it all

Don't you call me lucky, call me stubborn and worn
I dragged my whole bloodline through the eye of that thorn
And I'll send back a ladder for the ones still inside
'Cause the block don't lose me, the block just multiplied

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Mile marker ghosts in the dashboard glow
Nebraska's a black sea with nowhere to go
I dial the number, watch it ring and ring
Three in the morning, hear the answering machine

Don't hang up, don't hang up
Let me hear you breathe through the static and the dust

I'm calling home, calling home
Forty tons of steel and I'm so alone
Tell the kids I love them, tell them I'm not far
Just a voice on the wire and a moving star
Calling home, calling home
Keep the porch light burning till I'm not gone

She picks up sleepy, says the baby's okay
I close my eyes and I'm a thousand miles away
The diesel keeps humming a four-on-the-floor
Love is a phone line and an all-night door

I'm calling home, calling home
Forty tons of steel and I'm so alone
Tell the kids I love them, tell them I'm not far
Just a voice on the wire and a moving star

The sun's gonna crack the horizon soon
Another delivery, another empty room
But for these four minutes the highway can wait
While her voice keeps me steady and the engine keeps time

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the warehouse when the sky is still grey
Clock out, drive crosstown, do it all again by day
I count out the hours like I count out the bills
The stack on the table only grows and never spills

Two jobs and a tired back, still I'm falling behind
Running up a downhill, leaving sleep behind
They tell me work harder and the road will come clear
But the rent climbs a ladder every single year

My daughter drew a picture of a man she barely sees
A stick figure father with his hat on his knees
I taped it to the dashboard of the second-shift car
The only place I keep her, no matter how far

Two jobs and a tired back, still I'm falling behind
Running up a downhill, leaving sleep behind
They tell me work harder and the road will come clear
But the rent climbs a ladder every single year

I ain't asking for a fortune, ain't asking for gold
Just an evening with my family before I get old
A day where the math finally lands in the black
And a chair by the window when I'm not coming back

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I walked the same harbor a thousand grey morns
Knew every fisherman, every gull that was born
Then you turned a corner with the salt in your hair
And the village I'd memorized went strange in the air

Oh, the same little town, but it's lit up brand new
Every cobblestone golden since it's leading to you
The fiddle and the whistle play a reel in my chest
I fell for the one place I thought I knew best

We grew up two streets and a churchyard apart
Never once did I notice the door of my heart
Now I find every excuse to wander your lane
Like a tide that keeps turning back home again

Oh, the same little town, but it's lit up brand new
Every cobblestone golden since it's leading to you
The fiddle and the whistle play a reel in my chest
I fell for the one place I thought I knew best

Let them all leave for cities of glass and of stone
I'll stay where the heather and your laughter have grown
For I crossed all the oceans inside of my mind
And the love of my life was two doorways behind

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The screen lights my face at a quarter past two
A ring on my finger and a message to you
My thumb hovers heavy on a word I should kill
But the room is so quiet and the house is so still

Three little dots, then they vanish, then they start
Little blue ghosts running over my heart
I know it's a fire and I know it's a sin
But I'm typing your name in the dark again

We trade in our nothings like they're something to hide
A harmless hello with an ocean inside
Delete the whole thread when I hear footsteps near
Love turned to a secret I keep close and dear

Three little dots, then they vanish, then they start
Little blue ghosts running over my heart
I know it's a fire and I know it's a sin
But I'm typing your name in the dark again

One day I'll set the phone face-down for good
And love who I promised the way that I should
But the night isn't honest and the hour isn't wise
So I send it, then watch for the dots to rise

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Ten years of static on a line gone dead
Every word I owed you screaming in my head
I dial the old number with a shaking hand
Not sure if you're living, not sure you'll understand

The ringtone is a hammer, the silence is a blade
Every sin between us is a debt unpaid

Mother, can you hear me through the years of stone?
I'm a wreck of a man and I'm coming home
Forgive me, forgive me, before the line goes cold
I buried all my pride in the grave it was sold

You pick up so softly it tears me apart
That fragile little 'hello' detonates my heart
No screaming, no reckoning, just tears in the wire
Years of frozen distance going up in fire

Mother, can you hear me through the years of stone?
I'm a wreck of a man and I'm coming home
Forgive me, forgive me, before the line goes cold
I buried all my pride in the grave it was sold

We don't get the years back, we don't get the days
But you whisper my name like it was never erased
So I'll drive through the night with the phone on the seat
To hold what is left before the silence is complete

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete swallowed the orchard, swallowed the green
Now one crooked elm stands where a forest had been
It cracks through the asphalt with a stubborn old root
A widow in the city, still bearing its fruit

Dance with me, viejo, in your circle of shade
The last living dancer the bulldozers made
They'll come with their saws and their permits and steel
But tonight you still sway and tonight you still feel

The pigeons still court you, the children still climb
You keep a slow rhythm against the gray crime
A hundred summers folded inside of your rings
While the gray city forgets all the old growing things

Dance with me, viejo, in your circle of shade
The last living dancer the bulldozers made
They'll come with their saws and their permits and steel
But tonight you still sway and tonight you still feel

When they finally fell you the whole block will sigh
And a square of pale sunlight will go cold and dry
But a seed in some gutter will remember the song
And dance up through the cracks when the city is gone

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain come down on Kingston like it never gonna cease
I'm soaked to the bone with no shelter, no peace
Then a hand lift a canopy over my head
A stranger with a smile and a 'come walk' instead

One umbrella, two strangers, one rain
Walking down the road, easing all the pain
We don't know each other but we know the storm
Under one little sky we are keeping warm

We talk about nothing, we talk about all
Where the bus stop runs and how the mango trees fall
The puddles make rivers and we step over slow
Two lives for a moment going the same way to go

One umbrella, two strangers, one rain
Walking down the road, easing all the pain
We don't know each other but we know the storm
Under one little sky we are keeping warm

At the corner we part and the clouds break apart
Never learn your name but you live in my heart
Sometimes love is a minute and a shelter from the grey
Give thanks for the strangers who walk us part way

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
A basement that stank of stale smoke and beer
A mic taped together, my throat tight with fear
Four drunks and a bartender countin' the till
My hands on the strings and the whole room gone still

I was eighteen and starving, a riff and a dream
Screaming into darkness, learning how to scream

That first open mic, that holy little hell
Where I cracked on the high note and nobody could tell
I'd give back the stadiums, the lights and the roar
To stand terrified again on that splintering floor

Now the arenas are roaring my name like a flood
But none of it pumps like that first rush of blood
The kid in the basement still lives in my chest
Reminding the legend he started a mess

That first open mic, that holy little hell
Where I cracked on the high note and nobody could tell
I'd give back the stadiums, the lights and the roar
To stand terrified again on that splintering floor

So here's to the nobodies clutching a chord
The trembling and tone-deaf, the brave and ignored
Every thunder I conjure, every wall that I shake
Was born in a basement from one terrified ache

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
The man who once lifted me up to the stars
Now grips at the railing, now counts all his scars
His hands used to hammer, used to fix and to build
Now they tremble at coffee that he's careful not to spill

Time is a neon road running backward through the rain
My father in the rearview growing smaller in the lane
I'd reverse all the mileage, I'd unwind every year
But the dashboard keeps glowing and the exit's coming near

He forgets where he parked, forgets what he said
Then remembers a summer when his hair was still red
I become the strong one, I become the wall
The boy holding the father who once held them all

Time is a neon road running backward through the rain
My father in the rearview growing smaller in the lane
I'd reverse all the mileage, I'd unwind every year
But the dashboard keeps glowing and the exit's coming near

So I'll memorize his laughter while the engine still turns
The pink and purple sunset over everything that burns
And when the road runs out and the dashboard goes dark
I'll carry his headlights, I'll carry the spark

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Five AM alarm and the city won't wait
First shift loading boxes through the chain-link gate
Clock out, hit the train, count the change in my hand
Second shift waiting like a stamp on a brand

Faster, faster, the rent's at my heels
Faster, faster, you don't know how it feels

Two jobs, no sleep, and I'm still going under
Bassline of my heartbeat splitting like thunder
Run, run, run, but the floor keeps tilting
Work till I'm hollow and the lights keep wilting

Numbers on the envelope all bleeding into red
I do the math at midnight with a fog in my head
The more that I hustle the more that they take
A treadmill of pavement and I can't hit the brake

Two jobs, no sleep, and I'm still going under
Bassline of my heartbeat splitting like thunder
Run, run, run, but the floor keeps tilting
Work till I'm hollow and the lights keep wilting

But somewhere in the breakdown there's a breath I can take
A half-second of quiet before the next break
And I tell my tired reflection in the dark window glass
Keep running, keep running, this night's gonna pass

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We climbed the cold hillside when the village was asleep
The loch lying silver and the silence running deep
Then the sky tore open in a ribbon of green
The grandest old wonder these eyes have ever seen

Dance, you spirits, dance across the frozen night
Veils of green and rose in the curtain of light
The old ones say they're souls who have gone on before
Waltzing on the heavens, knocking at the door

My grandmother told me when I sat on her knee
That the lights were her mother come to dance over the sea
Now I stand on the heather with my own child's hand
And we watch the dead dancing over our quiet land

Dance, you spirits, dance across the frozen night
Veils of green and rose in the curtain of light
The old ones say they're souls who have gone on before
Waltzing on the heavens, knocking at the door

So when my own time comes and they lay me in the ground
Don't weep by the gravestone, don't make a sad sound
Just climb the cold hillside on a clear winter's eve
And I'll dance in the colours for the ones I had to leave

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I got gravel in my boots and thunder in my chest
Radio crackling, can't sit down and rest
The sky turns green and baby that's my song
A picket-fence life never held me long

I'm chasing, chasing, chasing down the rain
Lightning in my mirror, I do it all again
You want me steady but I'm built to roam
The eye of the storm is the closest thing to home

Funky little wind sock spinning on the porch
You packed me dinner, I packed up my torch
Every time the pressure drops I'm gone
Leaving you a postcard from a roadside dawn

I'm chasing, chasing, chasing down the rain
Lightning in my mirror, I do it all again
You want me steady but I'm built to roam
The eye of the storm is the closest thing to home

I know, I know I let you down
But a man like me can't wear a crown
When the funnel calls I turn around
And I never, never settle down

I'm chasing, chasing, chasing down the rain
Lightning in my mirror, I do it all again

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The casseroles are cold, the company is gone
The sun came up regardless, and I had to carry on
I laid your coat across the chair the way you used to do
And the morning light came pouring through

Oh, mercy in the morning, mercy in the dawn
He gives me strength to rise up even when my heart is gone
Weeping for the evening but the joy will surely come
Thank You for the mercy in the morning sun

I folded up the program, set it by the bread
I heard the choir singing every word the preacher said
My knees are on the kitchen floor, my hands are lifted high
I'm learning how to live and say goodbye

Oh, mercy in the morning, mercy in the dawn
He gives me strength to rise up even when my heart is gone
Weeping for the evening but the joy will surely come
Thank You for the mercy in the morning sun

I will lift my eyes, I will lift my voice
In the valley of the shadow I will still rejoice
For You walk beside me, and You wipe my tears
And You hold my every year

Oh, mercy in the morning, mercy in the dawn
Thank You for the mercy in the morning sun

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Cinnamon and Sunday hanging in the air
Flour on the apron, sunlight in her hair
A copper kettle humming low and slow
A warmth I didn't know I'd miss until you go

And I float on the smell of your kitchen
Float on a memory of gold
Butter and the morning, I'm a child again
In a house that never gets old

The yellow curtains breathing by the screen
The softest little world I've ever seen
You hummed a hymn while something baked away
And I would give the world for one more day

And I float on the smell of your kitchen
Float on a memory of gold
Butter and the morning, I'm a child again
In a house that never gets old

Ooh, the doorway, ooh, the light
Everything was tender, everything was right
Ooh, I close my eyes
And I'm home for a little while

And I float on the smell of your kitchen
Float on a memory of gold

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Mason jars are rattling in the back
Moonlight spilling silver on the cracked old track
Headlights off and breathing kind of slow
There's a county line I'm praying I can go

Oh the run went wrong, oh the night went long
Blue lights bleeding through a whiskey song
I was floating like the mist above the creek
Now I'm running out of road and out of week

Daddy taught me how to ride the curve
Keep it steady, keep it quiet, keep your nerve
But the engine started coughing on the hill
And the air went sweet and dangerous and still

Oh the run went wrong, oh the night went long
Blue lights bleeding through a whiskey song
I was floating like the mist above the creek
Now I'm running out of road and out of week

Glass on the highway, stars in the trees
Everything's dissolving in the breeze
I can hear the river calling out my name
And I'll never run this road again

Oh the run went wrong, oh the night went long
Blue lights bleeding through a whiskey song

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two a.m. and the city's gone to sleep
Just me and the highlighter, secrets I gotta keep
Coffee going cold, the pages start to blur
But I keep on turning, keep on turning sure

Up all night, up all night
Chasing down the morning light
Up all night, gotta get it right
Me, my books, and the city lights

Neon on the window, rain against the glass
Gonna make it through, gonna make it pass
Every little word is a step toward the sun
I won't stop, I won't stop until it's done

Up all night, up all night
Chasing down the morning light
Up all night, gotta get it right
Me, my books, and the city lights

Feel the bassline carry me through
Everything I'm learning, everything I do
When the morning breaks I'll be ready to fly
Just me and the silence and the two a.m. sky

Up all night, up all night
Chasing down the morning light

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Headlights carve the asphalt where the memories bleed
Third floor window dark, a ghost I used to need
I swore I'd never come back to this street
But the engine and the heartbreak share the same dead heat

Driving past the ruins of a life I used to own
Screaming at a window of a house that isn't home
The city's burning red behind the wreckage in my chest
I'm driving, I'm driving, and I'll never get to rest

The streetlight still is flickering the way it always did
The weight of every promise that we broke and that we hid
I grip the wheel like penance, knuckles white as bone
The loudest kind of silence is the sound of driving home

Driving past the ruins of a life I used to own
Screaming at a window of a house that isn't home
The city's burning red behind the wreckage in my chest
I'm driving, I'm driving, and I'll never get to rest

Tear it down, tear it down, let the engine roar
There's nothing left to bury at that broken door
Tear it down, tear it down, set the night on fire
Burn away the wreckage of a dead desire

Driving past the ruins of a life I used to own
Screaming at a window of a house that isn't home

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
You laugh at someone else's joke across the room
And I'm just furniture you're walking through
I memorized the way you tie your shoes
While you forget the color of my eyes

And I love you in a language you don't speak
I'm a Tuesday and you're somebody's weekend
I'll be fine, I'll be fine, that's a lie I keep
Loving you in a language you don't speak

We split a coffee and I called it a date
You called it killing time before your train
I'm a paragraph you skim and never read
A song that's always stuck inside my head

And I love you in a language you don't speak
I'm a Tuesday and you're somebody's weekend
I'll be fine, I'll be fine, that's a lie I keep
Loving you in a language you don't speak

Maybe in another life I'm brave enough to say
The things I rehearse and then throw away
But here I am again, just watching you leave
With my hands in my pockets and my heart on my sleeve

And I love you in a language you don't speak
Loving you in a language you don't speak

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
A trumpet on the corner, a slow and golden moan
White handkerchiefs are waving down the cobblestone
We cried on the way there, we'll dance on the way back
The sun is coming sideways through the live oak black

Second line, second line, marching through the haze
Death is just a doorway in a brass and shimmer blaze
We carry you in music down the avenue
And every step is mourning and is loving you

Magnolia and sorrow drifting in the air
The drummer keeps you living in the rhythm there
The river's moving slowly like it always will
And somewhere in the dazzle you are with us still

Second line, second line, marching through the haze
Death is just a doorway in a brass and shimmer blaze
We carry you in music down the avenue
And every step is mourning and is loving you

Wave the white above your head
Dance until the grief is bled
Ooh, the saints go drifting by
Under a Louisiana sky

Second line, second line, marching through the haze
We carry you in music down the avenue

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the diner, clock out, clock in again
My feet are keeping count of all the miles I've been
The envelope is red, the numbers never lie
I'm running out of month before I run out of try

Two jobs, one dream, and a tired pair of hands
Working every hour but I'm sinking in the sand
I keep my head up high while the bills keep piling on
I'm holding on, holding on, till the breaking of the dawn

The bus is always early or my body's always late
I count my tips in quarters at the kitchen table gate
They tell me work is honest, and I know that it's true
But honest don't add up to what an honest day is due

Two jobs, one dream, and a tired pair of hands
Working every hour but I'm sinking in the sand
I keep my head up high while the bills keep piling on
I'm holding on, holding on, till the breaking of the dawn

But I won't let it break me, no, I won't let it bend
I'll find a little daylight at the bottom of the end
One day I'll stop the running, one day I'll catch my breath
Till then I keep on giving everything I got left

Two jobs, one dream, and a tired pair of hands
Working every hour but I'm sinking in the sand

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up where the streetlights barely glow
Dreams stacked higher than the rent we owe
They said this corner's all you'll ever see
But I had a fire they couldn't take from me

I'm getting out, I'm getting out tonight
Watch me turn the darkness into light
From a dead-end block to the open sky
I was born to fly, I was born to fly

Packed a bag and a heart full of doubt
Everybody told me there's no way out
But every locked door was a song I'd write
Every broken window let in a little light

I'm getting out, I'm getting out tonight
Watch me turn the darkness into light
From a dead-end block to the open sky
I was born to fly, I was born to fly

For everyone still standing on that street
With concrete dreams beneath their feet
I'm proof that you can break the chain
And dance your way right out of the rain

I'm getting out, I'm getting out tonight
From a dead-end block to the open sky

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle in the window underneath the neon glow
Everybody coming to the man that they all know
He'll take your secondhand and make you brand new
A little bit of magic and a perfect seam or two

Stitch it up, stitch it up, make me shine tonight
The tailor on the corner got the whole block right
From the Sunday suit to the dancing dress
He dresses up the city in its Saturday best

Mister Johnny needs a hem, Miss Rosa needs a seam
He pins them all together like a disco dream
The sewing machine is humming like a bassline groove
And everybody's stepping out with something to prove

Stitch it up, stitch it up, make me shine tonight
The tailor on the corner got the whole block right
From the Sunday suit to the dancing dress
He dresses up the city in its Saturday best

Spin around, spin around, look at what he made
Everybody glittering in the light parade
Thread of gold and a heart of grace
He sews a little dignity on every face

Stitch it up, stitch it up, make me shine tonight
The tailor on the corner got the whole block right

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Same cracked sidewalks, same dead-end signs
Grew up forty feet apart on parallel lines
I never saw you coming till you knocked me flat
In the town I swore that I would never look back at

YOU AND ME AND THIS BURIED TOWN
EVERYTHING I RAN FROM PULLING ME BACK DOWN
I WAS GONNA LEAVE BUT YOU MADE ME STAY
FALLING FOR THE ONE THING I COULDN'T THROW AWAY

We drank warm beer in the parking lot dark
Carved our initials in the dying park
Every scar this place gave me started to make sense
When you held my hand against the chain-link fence

YOU AND ME AND THIS BURIED TOWN
EVERYTHING I RAN FROM PULLING ME BACK DOWN
I WAS GONNA LEAVE BUT YOU MADE ME STAY
FALLING FOR THE ONE THING I COULDN'T THROW AWAY

I hate this place
I hate this place
But I'd burn down every road if it meant your face
Stay
Stay
Stay

YOU AND ME AND THIS BURIED TOWN
FALLING FOR THE ONE THING I COULDN'T THROW AWAY

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
(Ooh, ooh) I still remember your name
(Sha-la-la) but nothing here's the same
You were my summer, my sweetheart, my song
I blinked and the years were gone

Why-y-y did you go, my darling
The one, the one that got away
I'd give my whole tomorrow
Just to hold you yesterday

(Ooh, ooh) the soda shop is closed
(Sha-la-la) and nobody knows
The way that you danced underneath the moon
Gone away much too soon

Why-y-y did you go, my darling
The one, the one that got away
I'd give my whole tomorrow
Just to hold you yesterday

Doo-doo-doo, I was a fool
Let you walk right out the school
Now I'm dancing here alone
With a memory of my own

Why-y-y did you go, my darling
The one, the one that got away

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Concrete cradle where my whole life began
Learned to count my blessings on a calloused hand
The corner had a story and it wasn't kind
But I kept a little light burning in my mind

I made it out, I made it out the block
With my mama's prayers and the ticking of the clock
They said I'd never leave, said I'd never grow
But look at me now, baby, look how far I go

Midnight on the stoop where the dreamers used to sit
Half of us got swallowed and the other half just quit
I carry every name that the streets took away
In the rhythm and the soul of the songs I play

I made it out, I made it out the block
With my mama's prayers and the ticking of the clock
They said I'd never leave, said I'd never grow
But look at me now, baby, look how far I go

And I won't forget the ground that made me
The broken roads, the hands that saved me
Every time I rise, I rise for all of them
From the dead-end block, I bloom again

I made it out, I made it out the block
With my mama's prayers and the ticking of the clock

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Fifteen years on the same hardwood floor
Watching her hang quiet by the eastern door
A girl in a blue dress they painted long ago
The only company a lonesome guard would know

And I talk to her at closing when the lights go low
She's the only one who never has to go
Oh, my painted darling on the gallery wall
You're the truest thing I've ever loved at all

The tourists come and snap and walk away
But I'm the one who's with her every day
The morning sun comes through and lights her hair
And for a breath it almost seems she's there

And I talk to her at closing when the lights go low
She's the only one who never has to go
Oh, my painted darling on the gallery wall
You're the truest thing I've ever loved at all

They say one day they'll move her down the hall
Sell the building, take her off the wall
And I'll hang my keys up, walk into the cold
Leaving half my heart in a frame of gold

And I talk to her at closing when the lights go low
She's the only one who never has to go

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The bus dropped me at the Conoco at half past one
Duffel on my shoulder, old dog tags and a setting sun
I walked the county road I used to race my brother down
Every fencepost looked the same, every porch light in this town

Mama left the kitchen light on like she did back then
Daddy's truck still wouldn't start, some things don't ever mend
I saw my name still carved inside the doorframe by the stair
A little lower than I stand now, but the boy's still there

I'm home, I'm home, put my boots beside the door
I ain't the same kid who shipped out, but I ain't asking for more
Just the smell of coffee Sunday, just the river running slow
Lord, I made it back to Georgia, I'm done with letting go

My old chair was waiting empty at the supper table still
Grace was said for those who didn't, voices going still
I held my baby sister, now she's grown and standing tall
She whispered welcome home to me, and that undid it all

There's names I'll carry quiet, there's nights I'll fight to sleep
But the oak tree by the mailbox kept the promises I keep

I'm home, I'm home, put my boots beside the door
I ain't the same kid who shipped out, but I ain't asking for more
Just the smell of coffee Sunday, just the river running slow
Lord, I made it back to Georgia, I'm done with letting go

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man on the warm sand, feet against the foam
He's been counting waves since before the town was home
He says, hush now, listen, don't you tell the sea
Let it come and find you, that is how to be

Morning paints the water in a lazy shade of gold
He shows me how the current keeps the stories that it's told
Don't go fighting backward when the pull begins to take
Float a little softer, child, and feel the morning break

Let the tide, let the tide carry what it will
Let the tide, let the tide and the rest goes still
He smiles like the harbor, slow and never wrong
The ocean keeps the rhythm, and we hum along

His hands have known the nets and every rope they've tied
Now he only teaches gulls and small ones on the tide
We lie back in the shallows where the light begins to fold
And he laughs that he is younger than the sea is old

When I am gone, he tells me, you will hear me in the swell
The water keeps the lessons that the living cannot tell

Let the tide, let the tide carry what it will
Let the tide, let the tide and the rest goes still
He smiles like the harbor, slow and never wrong
The ocean keeps the rhythm, and we hum along

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
The exit sign still glows the way it did
I told myself I wouldn't, then I did
Four floors of dark, one window burning low
Somebody new is living where we used to grow

I slowed the car down to a crawl of breath
The streetlight hummed a chord I can't forget
The parking lot where we said it was done
Is empty now except the ghost of someone

And the city keeps the heat of all our summers
In the glass, in the brick, in the rain
I am only passing, only passing through
But the windows still remember your name

I didn't stop, I didn't even pause
Just let the engine carry all the cause
The radio went quiet on the bridge
And everything I buried rose to the ridge

Let it build, let it build, let the headlights climb
Let it crest and break and fall behind the line
There is nothing left to knock on, nothing left to keep
Just the long road home and the city talking in its sleep

And the city keeps the heat of all our summers
In the glass, in the brick, in the rain
I am only passing, only passing through
But the windows still remember your name

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They offered me a billboard and a softer kind of voice
Said trim away the truth a bit, that's just a business choice
I've got ink under my fingernails and rent I cannot pay
But I'd rather mean a whisper than be loud the easy way

I won't sell it, I won't sell it, no
Not the line that made my mother cry
You can buy the room, you can buy the lights
But you'll never buy the reason why
I won't sell it, I won't sell it, no

A suit said put a hook on it and lose the second verse
The one about my father, said it only makes it worse
But that verse is where I live, that verse is where I bleed
And a song without its bleeding is a flower with no seed

I won't sell it, I won't sell it, no
Not the line that made my mother cry
You can buy the room, you can buy the lights
But you'll never buy the reason why
I won't sell it, I won't sell it, no

Maybe I stay broke, maybe I stay small
Singing to the corner of a half-filled hall
But every word is mine and every word is real
And that's a kind of rich you'll never get to steal

I won't sell it, I won't sell it, no
Not the line that made my mother cry
You can buy the room, you can buy the lights
But you'll never buy the reason why

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Down on Delancey there's a little lit-up door
Where old man Sammy's pressing what he pressed the day before
He's got chalk behind his ear and a tape around his neck
And everybody on the block comes in to get a check

He'll cuff you and he'll button you and send you out in style
A stitch in time, a perfect line, a hand-me-down with a smile
From the banker to the busboy, every shoulder fits just right
Sammy dresses up the whole darn block on Saturday night

The bride came in on Tuesday with her mother and a tear
He took it in, he let it out, he made the whole thing clear
The boxer needs a Sunday suit, the cantor needs a hem
And Sammy's got a needle and a kind word for all of them

He'll cuff you and he'll button you and send you out in style
A stitch in time, a perfect line, a hand-me-down with a smile
From the banker to the busboy, every shoulder fits just right
Sammy dresses up the whole darn block on Saturday night

He never charged the widow, never shamed a fraying sleeve
He says a man stands taller in the things that he believes

He'll cuff you and he'll button you and send you out in style
A stitch in time, a perfect line, a hand-me-down with a smile
From the banker to the busboy, every shoulder fits just right
Sammy dresses up the whole darn block on Saturday night

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the cobblestones across his shoulders wide
Built the courthouse on his collar, put the docks along his side
The mayor never mentions it, the maps don't draw him in
But every steeple's standing on the rising of his chin

So step light, step light on the avenue
The ground you're dancing on is dreaming too
Keep the music low and the trumpets sweet
There's a giant fast asleep beneath the street

The trolley hums a lullaby across his sleeping spine
The bakers time the ovens so the smell stays soft and fine
The lovers on the rooftop never raise their voice too high
For fear they'd wake the mountain that they're living on tonight

So step light, step light on the avenue
The ground you're dancing on is dreaming too
Keep the music low and the trumpets sweet
There's a giant fast asleep beneath the street

One night the band got carried, blew the brass clean off the wall
The whole town felt him stretching, felt the chandeliers and all
They hushed and held their breath and let the final note go thin
And the giant turned and settled, and the city breathed again

So step light, step light on the avenue
The ground you're dancing on is dreaming too
Keep the music low and the trumpets sweet
There's a giant fast asleep beneath the street

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The lanterns in the plaza are burning down to gold
The wine is nearly finished and the night is growing old
One more turn, one more clap, the dust is in the air
The morning's going to take it all, so leave it everywhere

Baila, baila, the last of the fire
Let the heel strike the stone, let it lift you higher
When the sun comes the streets will be bare
So give me your hands, give me everything there

The guitar man's fingers bleed but he will not put it down
The oldest woman rises, lets her shawl fall to the ground
She dances like she's nineteen and the boys forget to breathe
For she has buried husbands and she's earned the right to leave

Baila, baila, the last of the fire
Let the heel strike the stone, let it lift you higher
When the sun comes the streets will be bare
So give me your hands, give me everything there

Tomorrow we are strangers, we are debts and we are chores
But tonight we are a single heart pounding on the floors

Baila, baila, the last of the fire
Let the heel strike the stone, let it lift you higher
When the sun comes the streets will be bare
So give me your hands, give me everything there

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
We parked out at the water tower like a hundred times before
Your boxes in the back seat, my whole life out the door
We split a warm root beer and watched the fireflies catch fire
Knowing come tomorrow morning we'd be highways and a wire

So let it be August one more hour
Let the corn stand tall and the radio loud
We got the whole rest of our lives to be grown
But tonight we're seventeen and we're not going home

You're going east for engineering, I'm headed for the coast
We made a list of promises, you held the ones that matter most
We carved the year into the rail with your daddy's pocket knife
A little proof that for one summer we were the loves of each other's life

So let it be August one more hour
Let the corn stand tall and the radio loud
We got the whole rest of our lives to be grown
But tonight we're seventeen and we're not going home

Maybe we'll come back changed, maybe we won't come back at all
But I'll keep this dusty county and the way you laughed by the wall

So let it be August one more hour
Let the corn stand tall and the radio loud
We got the whole rest of our lives to be grown
But tonight we're seventeen and we're not going home

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The last train out is quarter after nine
I've got one bag packed and a borrowed dime
The diner's closing, Ruthie waved goodbye
This little town and I are out of why

I'm gone, I'm gone, with the whistle and the smoke
Gone where nobody knows the names of the folks
Don't you wait up, don't you keep the light
I'm trading every morning for a bigger night

I knew these streets the way you know a scar
Every porch and pothole, every parked-up car
But a girl can't grow inside a coffee cup
So I'm pouring myself out and lifting up

I'm gone, I'm gone, with the whistle and the smoke
Gone where nobody knows the names of the folks
Don't you wait up, don't you keep the light
I'm trading every morning for a bigger night

There's a piano in a city I have never seen
Playing all the chords this town has never been
And it's calling low and blue across the wire
So I'll follow till my shoes are full of fire

I'm gone, I'm gone, with the whistle and the smoke
Gone where nobody knows the names of the folks
Don't you wait up, don't you keep the light
I'm trading every morning for a bigger night

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Foot on the gas, low beams on the block
Used to have a key, now I just watch the clock
Third floor window, somebody changed the blinds
Ghost in the rearview, can't leave it behind
We came up broke in that two-bedroom hall
Now I'm wheels on chrome but I still feel small

Ridin' slow past the place that we stayed
Everything we built, everything we paid
Lights in the window but it ain't for me
I just drive, I just drive, let it be
Ridin' slow, ridin' slow through the rain
Got the whole city but I'm circling the same

Roaches on the counter, dreams on the wall
Now the dreams came true and I lost it all
Text on my phone that I'm never gonna send
Told myself this corner's where the old me ends
Engine keep runnin', heart on a loop
Past the same stairwell, past the same stoop

Ridin' slow past the place that we stayed
Everything we built, everything we paid
Lights in the window but it ain't for me
I just drive, I just drive, let it be

They say money change a man, nah, the man just learn
That you can't buy back a corner or a bridge you burn
Green light, green light, I let it turn red
Sit with all the things that I never said

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Roll the tape across the cardboard, hear it tearing slow
Every wall is bare now, just the nails got left to show
Kitchen where we cooked the rice, the radio that played
Goin' in a final box, and the rent is finally paid

Seal it up, seal it up, one more box and gone
Everything we lived in is a memory now, mon
Don't you look back sorry at an empty little room
We carry all the sunshine, leave the dust and leave the gloom

There's a crayon mark the baby made before she learned to walk
And the spot beside the window where we'd sit and talk and talk
I leave it for the next one, leave the light and leave the love
Say a little blessing for the family movin' up

Seal it up, seal it up, one more box and gone
Everything we lived in is a memory now, mon
Don't you look back sorry at an empty little room
We carry all the sunshine, leave the dust and leave the gloom

A house is just a body, but the home is in the heart
And we pack that one already, gonna take it where we start

Seal it up, seal it up, one more box and gone
Everything we lived in is a memory now, mon
Don't you look back sorry at an empty little room
We carry all the sunshine, leave the dust and leave the gloom

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every night at closing when the crowds have gone away
Old Henry tips his cap to her, the lady in the frame
A hundred years of painted blue, a smile that never fades
And Henry hums a little tune to her down empty colonnades

Good night, my painted darling, keep the moon out of your eyes
I'll be back at nine tomorrow, same old guard, same old skies
They can hang you in a palace, they can lock you in a vault
But you're the only girl who ever loved me without fault

The curators don't notice and the tourists never stay
They snap a flash and shuffle off and never hear her say
But Henry knows her secrets in the cracks across the paint
The way the light goes tender on the colors going faint

Good night, my painted darling, keep the moon out of your eyes
I'll be back at nine tomorrow, same old guard, same old skies
They can hang you in a palace, they can lock you in a vault
But you're the only girl who ever loved me without fault

When they finally let him go and gave the gold watch and the cake
He asked just one last favor, just an hour for old time's sake
And he stood there in the moonlight, said goodbye and turned to leave
And I swear the painted lady raised a hand inside her sleeve

Good night, my painted darling, keep the moon out of your eyes
Somebody else will guard you now, same old paint, same old skies

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The tape gun in my hand, it's the last sound in the place
Echo off the empty where the sofa left its trace
I run my palm along the wall, feel the years up in the grain
Seal it slow, baby, seal it slow, this the end of our domain

One more box, one more strip of tape
Fold the corners of the life we made
I'm not crying, no, it's just the dust
In an empty room there's nothing left to trust but us
One more box, then I lock the door

There's the dent where we slow-danced and the lamp came crashing down
There's the kitchen where you fed me when I couldn't leave this town
I thought a place could hold us, thought these walls could keep us tight
But a home was just the two of us, and we're packing up tonight

One more box, one more strip of tape
Fold the corners of the life we made
I'm not crying, no, it's just the dust
In an empty room there's nothing left to trust but us
One more box, then I lock the door

Key in the envelope, slide it under, let it go
The light through naked windows hits the floor we used to know
We were never the apartment, we were always just the two
So wherever the next door opens, I am moving in with you

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Up where the cold air bites and the night runs long and deep
The sky put on a color show while the whole town fell asleep
Green ribbon over the mountain, pink and a silver thread
Like the heavens got a sound system playin' over your head

Northern lights, dancin' in the freezin' sky
Northern lights, give I and I a reason why
No borders up there, no soldiers, no walls
Just the spirit of creation paintin' over us all

Me granny said it's ancestors come walkin' down the air
Come to check on all the children, come to show us that they care
So me wrap up in me blanket, watch the colors rise and bend
And I feel the whole wide universe sayin' easy now, my friend

Northern lights, dancin' in the freezin' sky
Northern lights, give I and I a reason why
No borders up there, no soldiers, no walls
Just the spirit of creation paintin' over us all

From the islands to the ice cap it's the same one shining face
We so small beneath the glory, but it hold us in its grace

Northern lights, dancin' in the freezin' sky
Northern lights, give I and I a reason why
No borders up there, no soldiers, no walls
Just the spirit of creation paintin' over us all

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The vending machine glow and a highlighter cap
Dead in my teeth while the whole world naps
Three empty mugs and a chapter to go
The streetlight's the only friend I know

My phone's on do not disturb, nobody's there
Just the hum of the fridge and the buzz of the air
I read the same line for the hundredth time
It's two in the morning and I'm losing my mind

But I'll make it, I'll make it, just one more page
Just me and the night and the words on the stage
Nobody's watching, nobody knows
This is where the quiet kind of bravery grows
I'll make it, I'll make it till the morning shows

Somewhere there's a future where this all pays off
Where I'm not just tired and I'm not just lost
So I underline harder and I drink it black
And I promise the dawn that I'm not turning back

The heater clicks off and the silence gets loud
I am small at this desk under no kind of crowd
But the city keeps sleeping and I keep awake
And that's got to be worth something for somebody's sake

So I'll make it, I'll make it, just one more page
Just me and the night and the words on the stage
Nobody's watching, nobody knows
This is where the quiet kind of bravery grows

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
The morning sun is soft on Ipanema sand
My coffee going cold inside my hand
The phone lit up, three little words in gray
And just like that you took the warmth away

No trembling voice, no door that slammed in two
Just pixels saying what we used to do
The ocean keeps its slow forgiving sway
While I reread the line you didn't stay

So softly, softly, love goes out the screen
No melody, no in-between
A whisper typed and sent and read
The sweetest things now left unsaid

I tap a heart and then I tap it back
The cursor blinks along a one-way track
The samba drum still patient in my chest
Keeps time for two and lets the rhythm rest

If you had only come and watched the bay
Maybe the tide would teach us how to stay
But love that ends in light upon a glass
Dissolves like foam and lets the evening pass

So softly, softly, love goes out the screen
No melody, no in-between
A whisper typed and sent and read
The sweetest things now left unsaid

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the avenue, low beams on the glass
Rolling through the old block watching memories pass
Third floor window, used to glow a kitchen gold
Now it's somebody else's warm, somebody else's cold
Gripping on the wheel, I tell myself don't slow
But my foot got a mind and the engine moving low
Same cracked curb where I used to park and wait
