Same buzzer, same number, different name on the gate

Driving past your old place when the city's asleep
Ghosts in the headlights, promises I couldn't keep
Green light, red light, I keep rolling through
Every street in this town got a piece of me and you

I could circle it forever, never knock the door
Learned that lesson hard, ain't gonna learn it no more
Radio playing something that we used to hum
Fingers on the volume but I let it come
The man I am tonight wave bye to the man I was
Not 'cause I stopped loving but because, because
The block don't owe me nothing, the block don't care
It just remembers somebody who used to be there

One more red light, one more breath
Let the past be the past, give the rest a rest
Mirror full of taillights, road wide and free
That apartment ain't home, it's just history

Driving past your old place when the city's asleep
Ghosts in the headlights, promises I couldn't keep
Green light, red light, I keep rolling through
Every street in this town got a piece of me and you

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning, the diesel's running warm
A payphone glowing yellow in the rain before the storm
I dropped my quarters in and prayed you'd hear it ring
A thousand miles of asphalt and your voice the only thing

Hello darling, it's your rambling man
Calling from a county that I can't understand
The banjo of the highway plays a lonesome midnight song
Keep the porch light burning, I won't be gone too long

The little ones are sleeping, don't you wake them up for me
Just tell them daddy's hauling toward the morning that they'll see
The mountains turned to flatland and the flatland turned to night
And every white line passing pulls me back into your light

My coffee's gone to bitter and my radio's gone dim
The trucks roll by like cattle and I'm rolling right with them
But I'd trade this whole horizon and the freedom of the road
For one more cup of morning and to help you bear the load

Fiddle plays a wandering tune, the kind that fades to gray
Three more states behind me and three more on the way
But love's a piece of twine that runs from here to your front door
And I'll follow it back homeward when this last long haul is o'er

Hello darling, it's your rambling man
Calling from a county that I can't understand
The banjo of the highway plays a lonesome midnight song
Keep the porch light burning, I won't be gone too long

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Neon spills like brandy on the wet motel glass
Three a.m., a saxophone of headlights drifting past
I feed the line a dime and let the dial tone hum
Waiting on your sleepy voice to tell me morning's come

Hello from the blue hour, baby, half a country wide
The road's a smoky ballad and I'm riding on the ride
The blue note in my chest is just the missing of your name
So hum a little softer, love, and call me home again

The rest stop's playing standards on a station out of tune
A trumpet made of taillights underneath a paper moon
I light another cigarette I swore I wouldn't smoke
And tell you 'bout the desert and the lonesome things I spoke

The brushes on the snare are just the rain against the cab
The walking bass a heartbeat through the static and the grab
We improvise these distances, we swing them till they bend
And every phrase resolves back to your doorway in the end

Hello from the blue hour, baby, half a country wide
The road's a smoky ballad and I'm riding on the ride
The blue note in my chest is just the missing of your name
So hum a little softer, love, and call me home again

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I walk the cobbled streets where your perfume once burned
A candle in the window for a face that never turned
I hold you in the music though you never held me close
A partner made of shadow, a romance made of ghost

Dance with me in silence, you who never knew my name
Every step a question and your answer just the same
I burn for you in two-four time, I bleed in minor key
My love, you are the only one who never dances me

The bandoneon is weeping what my mouth could never say
I built a thousand evenings of the things you'd never stay
Your laughter for another cuts me sharper than a knife
And still I'd give the dagger and the blood and all my life

The rose between my teeth has turned to ash upon my tongue
The songs I made to win you are the saddest ones I've sung
So let the violins go cold, let every lantern dim
I'll love you in the darkness where you'll never see me in

Dance with me in silence, you who never knew my name
Every step a question and your answer just the same
I burn for you in two-four time, I bleed in minor key
My love, you are the only one who never dances me

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The door stopped slamming, now the kitchen's holding still
The clock's the only thing still talking, ticking off the chill
I hear the fridge hum lonely and the faucet drip alone
Two people in one quiet make the loudest kind of unknown

It's the quiet, oh, the quiet after
No bassline, baby, no laughter
Just the space between the words we threw
And the silence sounding way too true

I'm sitting on the bottom step, you're somewhere down the hall
The groove we used to ride together hit a brick wall
I want to crack a joke and break this funkless frozen room
But pride's a heavy pocket and the night's a empty tomb

Somebody gotta start the rhythm, somebody hit the one
Somebody gotta say I'm sorry 'fore the morning sun
I feel your footsteps coming soft across the wooden floor
And the quiet breaks wide open when you crack the bedroom door

It's the quiet, oh, the quiet after
No bassline, baby, no laughter
Just the space between the words we threw
And the silence sounding way too true

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent lights are screaming on the underground line
The map above is melting, every station looks like mine
My heartbeat's in my earbuds, faster than the wheels
Neon doors keep closing on the nothing that I feel

Breathe in the static, breathe out the dark
The tunnel's a synthline, my chest is a spark
Hold on, hold on through the violet glow
The next stop is coming, just don't let go

The strangers turn to mannequins, their faces blur to chrome
The train becomes a heartbeat dragging me away from home
My hands are made of voltage, my lungs are made of glass
I count the flickering pillars as the dark keeps rushing past

Four, three, two, one, the arpeggio descends
I tell my body it's a wave and waves will always end
The platform breathes a cold air and the doors slide open wide
And I step into the white light with the storm still locked inside

Breathe in the static, breathe out the dark
The tunnel's a synthline, my chest is a spark
Hold on, hold on through the violet glow
The next stop is coming, just don't let go

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You took my hand like taking up a blade
The floor between us cold as debts unpaid
The orchestra commands and we obey
Two enemies who have to dance today

Step for step, we wage a sweeter war
Your eyes say hate but your body says far more
The drum of pride, the violin of spite
We burn each other down in two-four time tonight

You lead me sharp, I answer with a turn
A challenge in the candle as it burns
My palm against your back, a borrowed truce
The music is the chain and we're the noose

And somewhere in the spinning, something breaks
The line between the loathing and the ache
We pull apart, we crash, we hold, we glare
And no one in the room can breathe the air

Step for step, we wage a sweeter war
Your eyes say hate but your body says far more
The drum of pride, the violin of spite
We burn each other down in two-four time tonight

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The bus let me off where the cornfield meets the road
My duffel bag is lighter than the weight I never showed
The mailbox still leans crooked, the porch light's still the same
But I'm a different alphabet trying to spell my name

I'm coming home, I'm coming home
Through the screen door, through the dial tone
The war is over but it lives in me
So hold me till I learn how to be free

My mother's at the window with a dishcloth in her hand
My brother's grown a foot since I last saw the land
They set a plate of supper for the boy who went away
But a quieter man came walking up the gravel drive today

The fireworks on Sunday made me hit the kitchen floor
They didn't say a word, just helped me up and shut the door
I'm learning how the morning doesn't have to be a fight
And sleep can come without the sound of something in the night

The fields don't ask me questions, the river doesn't stare
The old dog just remembers me and lays beside my chair
Maybe healing's just the slow long way of getting found
One ordinary morning at a time on solid ground

I'm coming home, I'm coming home
Through the screen door, through the dial tone
The war is over but it lives in me
So hold me till I learn how to be free

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Every evening I climb the iron stair
Light the wick and comb my fingers through the salty air
I write you letters, ocean, in the spinning of the beam
A lover made of distance, a romance made of dream

Oh sea, my dark and shining bride
I keep this flame to keep you by my side
You never answer, never stay
But I'll burn for you the same way every day

You bring me broken ships and you take my words away
You kiss the rocks at midnight, then you leave before the day
I fold my devotion into bottles, set them free
And watch you swallow every one and never write to me

They say I'm married to a thing that cannot love
The gulls, the moon, the heavy gray above
But every wave that breaks against my stone
Is the closest thing I have to not alone

Oh sea, my dark and shining bride
I keep this flame to keep you by my side
You never answer, never stay
But I'll burn for you the same way every day

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
There's a map upon her palms of every year she gave
Rivers of the working and the loving and the brave
She braided all our mornings, she folded up our fears
Those hands have held the family through a hundred laughing years

Grandmother's hands, oh, soft and worn and brown
The gentlest kind of strong this world has found
They carried more than any hands should bear
And never once forgot to smooth my hair

She rolled the dough on Sunday with a rhythm all her own
She pressed a coin into my palm and called it seeds I'd grown
Those fingers knew the Bible and the needle and the spoon
And how to hush a crying child beneath a kitchen moon

Now they rest upon the quilt, slow and light as prayer
I hold them in my own and feel the whole life living there
Every scar a story, every wrinkle is a song
These are the hands that taught my hands how to carry on

Grandmother's hands, oh, soft and worn and brown
The gentlest kind of strong this world has found
They carried more than any hands should bear
And never once forgot to smooth my hair

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Dusty tape hiss, streetlight on the curb
I was just a kid with a dream and a verb
Dead-end block where the corner was a cage
Wrote my way out one page at a page
Mama working doubles, I could hear the alarm
Learned to keep my head down and my heart on my arm
They said where you from is exactly where you stay
But I packed up my future in a beat-up Chevrolet

Made it out, made it out the block
Slow drums humming while I beat the clock
Look back at the corner getting small in the glass
Never forget it but I'm done with the past

Vinyl crackle memory, the homies on the stoop
Some of them still circling that same old loop
I send a little something back when the checks come through
'Cause making it alone ain't really making it true
The block ain't a prison, it's a part of my song
It's the snare in my story, it's the reason I'm strong
Mixed the pain in the pot till it cooked to a gold
Now the dead end's a doorway and the door is wide open

Lo-fi sunset over rooftops I knew
Every cracked sidewalk got me on through
Raise a cup to the ones who couldn't ride
I carry all your names on the long way wide

Made it out, made it out the block
Slow drums humming while I beat the clock
Look back at the corner getting small in the glass
Never forget it but I'm done with the past

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The train moves slow through a watercolor haze
My uniform a stranger from some other person's days
The fields go soft and golden, the windows breathe with light
I'm floating somewhere distant between the day and night

Come home, come home through the silver air
Dissolve the years like smoke, like prayer
The war is a echo, the world is a sigh
And I'm drifting back to you under an endless sky

Your face is half a memory, half a wash of blue
I reach across the distance for the shape I thought I knew
The platform shimmers gently, the crowd is underwater slow
And everything is gauzy, glowing, far away and low

Maybe I'm still over there, maybe I never left
Maybe coming home's a dream the living are bereft
But your hand on my shoulder feels warmer than the sun
And the guns fade out to nothing and the long night comes undone

Come home, come home through the silver air
Dissolve the years like smoke, like prayer
The war is a echo, the world is a sigh
And I'm drifting back to you under an endless sky

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Silence over snowfields, the world holds its breath
A stillness wide as oceans, soft as the edge of sleep
And then the sky remembers a color it once knew
And spills it slow in ribbons of impossible blue

Green light, slow light, falling like a hymn
The heavens turning over at the planet's icy rim
No words for this, no melody can hold
Just the cold and the wonder and the silver and the gold

The stars step back in reverence, the mountains barely speak
A curtain made of starlight drifts across the peak
It breathes the way the tide breathes, it bends the way time bends
A cathedral with no ceiling and an aisle that never ends

I am very small here, and that is a kind of peace
The sky undoes my worry and grants me slow release
We are only borrowed moments under something vast and bright
So I let myself dissolve into the moving northern light

Green light, slow light, falling like a hymn
The heavens turning over at the planet's icy rim
No words for this, no melody can hold
Just the cold and the wonder and the silver and the gold

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Vinyl hiss and a rainy window pane
Thinking 'bout your name like a soft refrain
We were almost everything, almost on time
A half a beat off in the same old rhyme
You wanted the ocean, I was building a wall
Never caught you falling so we both let it fall
Now I stir my coffee to a mellow snare
And I picture you somewhere in the cooler air

The one that got away, soft static in my chest
The melody we never finished, never confessed
I loop the same memory, faded and slow
The one that got away, the one I let go

Saw your photo somewhere, you look happy and free
Good, that's good, even if it's not next to me
Maybe timing's just a beat we couldn't keep
Maybe some loves are meant to stay asleep
I don't want you back, I just want to know
If you ever hear our song on the radio
And think about the kid with the headphones on
Who loved you in lo-fi before you were gone

Dusty piano, a tape running thin
Some doors close gentle, some never let you in
I'm okay, I'm okay, just the rain talking low
About the one that got away a long time ago

The one that got away, soft static in my chest
The melody we never finished, never confessed
I loop the same memory, faded and slow
The one that got away, the one I let go

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The fiddle's tuned a quarter flat tonight
Like everything we owned that almost worked
Your shoes still scuff the same warped pine
We laid down by the church

Fifty winters froze the well twice over
Fifty springtimes thawed it back to mud
Your hand still finds the small of my back
Like water finds the flood

So turn me slow before the lamp goes cold
One more reel before the porch light dies
I memorized your weight against my chest
Long before I memorized goodbye

The banjo man don't know our names
He only knows the songs we used to keep
We danced this floor with kids on our hips
Now they're grown and we're half asleep

The doctor counts your heartbeats out
Like change he's afraid to spend
But I have spent a lifetime here
And I'd spend it all again

So turn me slow before the lamp goes cold
One more reel before the porch light dies
I memorized your weight against my chest
Long before I memorized goodbye

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Three A.M. and the grill still spits
Neon buzzing like a busted tooth
Waitress named after a dead saint
Pours us coffee like it's the truth

We don't close! We don't close!
While the rich kids lock their doors
We got pie and we got rage
And a jukebox stuck on four

Cab drivers, ghosts, and the night-shift nurse
Split a booth with the kid who ran
From a house that smelled like a closing fist
Nobody asks who you am

We don't close! We don't close!
While the rich kids lock their doors
We got pie and we got rage
And a jukebox stuck on four

They'll knock it flat for a parking lot
Glass and chrome in a dumpster grin
But tonight the bottomless cup is full
And the whole damn city's in

We don't close! We don't close!
Let the morning find us here
Grease and gospel, salt and smoke
The last warm light this year

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
You wore the same gray sky I did
We grew up underneath that wool
The gas station hum, the dead-end road
The river running slow and full

And I fell where the streetlights drown
In the amber of this nothing town
You were always almost mine
A channel I could never tune in time

We knew the same forgotten names
The boarded mill, the burned-out store
Your voice arrives all soft and blurred
Like a song heard through a floor

And I fell where the streetlights drown
In the amber of this nothing town
You were always almost mine
A channel I could never tune in time

We could leave or we could stay
Dissolve into the same gray air
I don't know the difference now
Between loving you and being there

And I fell where the streetlights drown
In the amber of this nothing town
Stay a while, go blurry slow
We are everyone we used to know

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Mama Bisi runs the needle hot
Thread between her teeth like a song
Every wedding on this dusty street
Walk in her color, walk in strong

She dress the block, she dress the block
From the baby to the old man's cane
Cut the cloth, let the body talk
Nobody leave her door the same

Gold and indigo, a river of wax print
Dancing on the line in the noon
She measure your shoulders, measure your dreams
Say come back Friday afternoon

She dress the block, she dress the block
From the baby to the old man's cane
Cut the cloth, let the body talk
Nobody leave her door the same

When the funeral comes she sew in white
When the new life comes she sew in green
Every thread a little prayer
The finest hands this town has seen

She dress the block, she dress the block
From the baby to the old man's cane
Cut the cloth, let the body talk
Nobody leave her door the same

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
We pushed the couch against the wall
Like we did when we were twenty-three
The record skips on the second verse
The way it always used to be

So dance me through the empty room
Where all the furniture used to fight
We spent our whole lives keeping time
Let's lose it for one final night

The lawyer's papers on the counter
Folded like a flag for the dead
But your hand still knows my crooked spine
The argument we never said

So dance me through the empty room
Where all the furniture used to fight
We spent our whole lives keeping time
Let's lose it for one final night

Nobody's wrong, nobody's right
We just ran out of the same old song
But God, you still move like the girl
I followed home, I followed home

So dance me through the empty room
One slow turn before the moving van
We were never good at letting go
Let's be terrible one last time again

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Suitcase packed with my grandmother's name
A photograph going soft at the fold
The coastline shrinks to a pencil line
The water's loud, the water's cold

Run, run, the engines climb
Leaving the harbor lights behind
I carry my country in my chest
A pulse the ocean can't outrun

New alphabet on a stranger's tongue
New weather pressing on my skin
I mailed my heart to a place that's gone
And the tide won't take it in

Run, run, the engines climb
Leaving the harbor lights behind
I carry my country in my chest
A pulse the ocean can't outrun

Three generations in a single bag
The spices, the songs, the broken clock
I'm not the man who left the dock
I'm not yet the man who'll walk the block

Run, run, the engines climb
Forward into the breaking light
I'll plant my mother's garden seeds
In soil that doesn't know her right

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I knew the third pew's every creak
The hymnal's water-wrinkled page
The stained glass throwing colored light
Across my small forgiven age

I'm slipping out the side door, Lord
While the choir hits the final swell
I'm not angry, I'm just tired
Of knocking where no answer fell

My mother's voice still rings the high notes
My father's hands still pass the plate
I love them more than I can carry
And I cannot love this weight

I'm slipping out the side door, Lord
While the choir hits the final swell
I'm not angry, I'm just tired
Of knocking where no answer fell

Maybe grace was always in the singing
The coffee and the funeral hams
Not the rules they hung above me
Like a price upon the lamb

So I'll close the heavy door real soft
And let the gravel say goodbye
If there's a God out past these walls
I'll meet Him under open sky

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
The second floor still wears that light
A yellow square inside the dark
Somebody else is making coffee now
In the kitchen where we used to spark

I drive past slow, I drive past slow
The engine hushed, the radio low
The window keeps a stranger's glow
Where all my younger summers go

The jacaranda dropped its purple
On the same cracked square of street
The city hums a softer samba now
Of rain and tires and August heat

I drive past slow, I drive past slow
The engine hushed, the radio low
The window keeps a stranger's glow
Where all my younger summers go

I won't park, I won't look up too long
The past is just a borrowed room
You rent it back for half a song
Then drive into the dark perfume

So goodnight window, goodnight light
Goodnight the girl I held so tight
The green light turns, I let it go
The city carries me below

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The building's quiet as a held breath
Just me and the vending machine's low hum
A highlighter bleeding through the page
Fingers cold and going numb

Two in the morning, hold the line
The whole world sleeping, but this is mine
No one's watching me become
The person I'm reaching for in the dark

My mother cleaned three floors a night
So I could sit beneath this lamp
Every sentence that I memorize
Is a letter home, a borrowed stamp

Two in the morning, hold the line
The whole world sleeping, but this is mine
No one's watching me become
The person I'm reaching for in the dark

The coffee's bitter, the clock won't move
My eyes are stones, my back's a bow
But somewhere down this paper road
There's a door, and I'll be the one to know

So two in the morning, hold the line
One more chapter, one more climb
The sun don't owe me anything
I'll meet it with my name underlined

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded crates we couldn't read
Sealed in wax and a foreign tongue
The captain laughed and the compass spun
And the church bells onshore went unrung

Oh, the cargo glows, the cargo glows
Under the deck where the cold wind blows
We're dancing on a floating grave
And nobody told the radio

The rats jumped ship at the second port
The parrot screamed and would not stop
The mirror in the captain's room
Shows a wave that's about to drop

Oh, the cargo glows, the cargo glows
Under the deck where the cold wind blows
We're dancing on a floating grave
And nobody told the radio

So crank the lights, let the strobe go wild
If we're hexed then let us shine
The horizon's just a hungry mouth
But the band still keeps the time

Oh, the cargo glows, the cargo glows
We'll boogie till the ocean knows
If this ship goes down it goes down loud
With a glitterball and a final bow

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They put your hand in mine onstage
Under lights too hot to hide
Every week you stole my shine
Now we're stuck on the same slow tide

So lead, or follow, I don't care
Just don't let the audience see
That the closest I've ever stood to you
Is the closest you've been to me

Your cologne's the smell of every loss
The scoreboard living in my head
But your pulse is loud against my wrist
And it's saying what we never said

So lead, or follow, I don't care
Just don't let the audience see
That the closest I've ever stood to you
Is the closest you've been to me

Four, five, six, and turn me in
Hate's just love that lost its name
We trained our whole lives to win
Now we're moving as one same flame

So lead, or follow, hold me near
Let the rivalry dissolve
The music ends in thirty counts
Let's lose the war we never solved

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Greyhound idling at the dollar store
My whole life in a duffel zipped
Mama crying on the gravel lot
Daddy's wave already half-slipped

I'm gone, I'm gone, watch the water tower shrink
Ten years staring at the same red light
I loved you, town, more than you think
But I gotta chase the city's light

Everybody here already knows
How my story's supposed to end
Graduate, get small, get old
Marry the bottle, lose a friend

I'm gone, I'm gone, watch the water tower shrink
Ten years staring at the same red light
I loved you, town, more than you think
But I gotta chase the city's light

I'll keep your dirt under my nails
I'll keep the church, the field, the train
But a boy can't grow inside a jar
With his name already on the pane

So I'm gone, I'm gone, let the cornfields blur
Let the county line turn to a thread
I'll send back money, send back word
But never send back where I'm headed instead

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I dialed the number I never forgot
Though my thumb has shaken for a year
Three rings hung in the kitchen air
Then your voice, so small, so near

It's me, Mama, it's me
The boy you raised and the man who ran
I don't know how to start so late
So I'll start with: here I am

The last we spoke we both said knives
Dressed as truths we couldn't hold
Four winters went without a word
And I watched your photo growing old

It's me, Mama, it's me
The boy you raised and the man who ran
I don't know how to start so late
So I'll start with: here I am

You didn't ask me where I'd been
You just said you'd kept my room
The quilt still folded at the foot
And the window open for the moon

So it's me, Mama, it's me
Let the silence fall apart
I've got years to give you back
And a hole the shape of your heart

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Downpour caught us at the same red sign
You had black, I had none
You tilted it without a word
And the cold rain turned to one

Share it, share it, two heads under one
Strangers tucked from the falling sky
We don't know each other's names
But we're dry, we're dry, we're dry

Your shoulder soaking so mine stayed warm
My arm angled so yours stayed in
We walked four blocks at a stranger's pace
Like we'd done it a hundred times again

Share it, share it, two heads under one
Strangers tucked from the falling sky
We don't know each other's names
But we're dry, we're dry, we're dry

The rain let up at the station mouth
You folded it, gave a nod
I watched you melt into the crowd
Like a kindness sent from God

So share it, share it, while the storm comes down
We were one for a city block
Never met and never will
But I still feel where our shoulders locked

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole life on a page
The wife, the shop, the family name
A suit pressed flat before I was grown
A road already lit with shame

But I and I will choose my way
Down the river, into the day
No man hangs my future for me
I cut the rope and I walk free

My father means it as a gift
The path his father gave to him
But a borrowed life don't fit my bones
It hangs off loose, it hangs off grim

But I and I will choose my way
Down the river, into the day
No man hangs my future for me
I cut the rope and I walk free

I'll honor you in my own tongue
I'll send my love on the morning breeze
But the man you planned, he never grew
He was just a shadow in the trees

So I and I will choose my way
Let the elders frown and pray
One life given, one to spend
I'll meet it as myself in the end

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine humming low beneath the neon rain
One more turn of the key and I'm free again
Gloves on the wheel, heartbeat in my throat
This is the night I finally float

One last ride, one last light
Fading taillights into the night
Hold my breath, let it go
This is the only road I know

They said the money's waiting on the other side
But the city's holding secrets I can't hide
Mirror full of shadows chasing me
Just a little further and I'll be free

One last ride, one last light
Fading taillights into the night
Hold my breath, let it go
This is the only road I know

No more running, no more chains
Washing all my sins away in the rain
The horizon's calling out my name
Nothing left to lose, nothing left to blame

One last ride, one last light
Fading taillights into the night
Hold my breath, let it go
This is the only road I know

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Wrinkled like the pages of an old love song
These are the hands that held me all along
Flour on her fingers, gold upon her ring
Softly she would hum the songs that angels sing

Grandmother's hands, telling me a story
Grandmother's hands, holding all the glory
Every line a memory, every fold a prayer
Grandmother's hands still holding me there

She stitched the broken pieces of my heart
Taught me how to love right from the start
Warm as Sunday morning, sweet as honey tea
Those gentle hands kept reaching out for me

Grandmother's hands, telling me a story
Grandmother's hands, holding all the glory
Every line a memory, every fold a prayer
Grandmother's hands still holding me there

Now the chair is empty by the window light
But I feel her fingers wrap around me tight
In every loaf of bread, in every quiet grace
I still can see her smile, I still can feel her face

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Fresh fade, lining sharp, I run the chair
Everybody in the city knows I'm here
Clippers buzzing, stories spilling on the floor
Mayor to the trap boys all walk through my door

I know the whole town, yeah I know it all
From the corner store to the city hall
Sit down in my chair, let me line you up
I know all the secrets in this city, what's up

Every little whisper, every little deal
Goes right past my mirror, tell me what's real
Grandmas and the goons, the preacher and the cop
Everybody's equal when they sit in my shop

I know the whole town, yeah I know it all
From the corner store to the city hall
Sit down in my chair, let me line you up
I know all the secrets in this city, what's up

Thirty years of clippers, thirty years of trust
This chair built a kingdom out of dust
They come for the haircut, stay for the truth
I'm the heartbeat of the city, living proof

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We set sail at midnight with a hold full of sin
The captain wouldn't tell us what was locked within
The water turned to black and the moon went red
And every man aboard could hear the whispers of the dead

Oh the cargo's cursed, boys, and the sea won't let us go
We're sailing toward the devil down below
No wind to fill the sails, no harbor light ahead
A cursed cargo carries nothing but the dead

The first mate lost his mind 'fore the second night
Clawing at the crates by the lantern light
Whatever's in that hold ain't meant for mortal eyes
And one by one we vanish 'neath the cold and starless skies

Oh the cargo's cursed, boys, and the sea won't let us go
We're sailing toward the devil down below
No wind to fill the sails, no harbor light ahead
A cursed cargo carries nothing but the dead

Lord have mercy on this rotting, drifting tomb
We sealed our fate the moment we left the room
The ocean is our coffin, the night will be our shroud
And the cargo keeps on calling, low and loud

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Boots on the tarmac, dust still in my lungs
The songs of the fallen left forever unsung
They told me I'm a hero but I don't feel the same
Carrying the weight of every single name

I'M HOME BUT I'M NOT HOME
The war is in my bones
I MADE IT BACK ALIVE
But part of me still dies

My mother's at the door but I can't meet her eyes
Every slamming door becomes a thousand fireflies
The streets are too quiet, the silence is a scream
I'm walking through a country that feels just like a dream

I'M HOME BUT I'M NOT HOME
The war is in my bones
I MADE IT BACK ALIVE
But part of me still dies

DON'T CALL ME A HERO
DON'T HANG UP NO FLAG
I LEFT MY BROTHERS BLEEDING
IN A WORLD THAT WENT BLACK

I'M HOME BUT I'M NOT HOME
The war is in my bones
I MADE IT BACK ALIVE
But part of me still dies

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The words are hanging heavy in the air
We said too much and now there's nothing there
Just the ticking of the clock upon the wall
And the space between us feels a mile tall

In the quiet after, baby, I still love you
In the silence I can hear my heart break through
We don't have to say a word, just stay
Let the quiet wash the hurt away

Your shoulders turned away beneath the light
I'd give up being wrong to make it right
The coffee's getting cold between our hands
And somewhere in the stillness love still stands

In the quiet after, baby, I still love you
In the silence I can hear my heart break through
We don't have to say a word, just stay
Let the quiet wash the hurt away

Reach for me, I'm reaching out for you
This love is bigger than the things we do
Let the silence be the bridge we cross
Nothing in this room is really lost

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I knelt in empty churches with a hollow chest
Prayers like falling feathers laid to rest
The sky was just a ceiling, gray and far
I lost the light, I lost my guiding star

But I'm falling back into the glow
Finding everything I used to know
Lost my faith and found it in the rain
The light is coming back to me again

I wandered through the darkness, cold and blind
Looking for a god I left behind
Then a stranger smiled and held the door
And I felt the thing I'd been searching for

But I'm falling back into the glow
Finding everything I used to know
Lost my faith and found it in the rain
The light is coming back to me again

Maybe heaven's not a place up in the sky
Maybe it's the moment I stopped asking why
The doubt was just a door I had to crawl
And love was waiting underneath it all

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Packed my bags in the back of a rusted car
Watched my whole life shrinking down to a star
The water tower fading in the rearview glass
Every little memory becoming the past

Leaving for good, no looking back
Dust on the road, my dreams on the track
Small town, goodbye, I gotta grow
There's a whole big world I gotta go know

Mama on the porch wiping tears from her face
This town was a hug but it felt like a cage
The diner, the bleachers, the old Friday lights
Fading like a dream into the city nights

Leaving for good, no looking back
Dust on the road, my dreams on the track
Small town, goodbye, I gotta grow
There's a whole big world I gotta go know

I'll carry you with me wherever I roam
A piece of these backroads inside of my bones
But the highway is calling, I can't stay the same
I'm chasing a future that don't know my name

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Garlic in the air and the radio loud
Grease on the stove like a thundercloud
She never read a recipe a day in her life
Just a wooden spoon and a beat-up knife

IT SMELLS LIKE HOME, IT SMELLS LIKE WAR
GRANDMA'S KITCHEN, GIMME MORE
BURNING SAUCE AND BROKEN PLATES
THAT'S THE SMELL THAT NEVER FADES

She'd scream in the doorway and feed you with rage
Love like a riot, sweet as the sage
Nobody left hungry, nobody left clean
Loudest little kitchen you ever seen

IT SMELLS LIKE HOME, IT SMELLS LIKE WAR
GRANDMA'S KITCHEN, GIMME MORE
BURNING SAUCE AND BROKEN PLATES
THAT'S THE SMELL THAT NEVER FADES

Now the stove is cold and the house is sold
But I smell that garlic when the nights get old
She's gone but the kitchen lives in me
Loud and burning, wild and free

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle in his teeth and a chalk-lined sleeve
He's been here since before I could believe
Every suit a story, every hem a vow
He stitched up the whole neighborhood somehow

He dresses the whole block, one thread at a time
From the weddings to the funerals, he makes us all shine
Old man with the scissors and the kindest eyes
Stitching up our city underneath the skies

The groom got his jacket, the boy got his first
The widow got her black dress for the worst
He knows all our measures, knows all our names
He knows that underneath we're all the same

He dresses the whole block, one thread at a time
From the weddings to the funerals, he makes us all shine
Old man with the scissors and the kindest eyes
Stitching up our city underneath the skies

The shop might close someday, the lights go dim
But every coat we wear's a piece of him
Woven in the seams of everything we are
The tailor of the block, our quiet little star

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raised my hand again, Lord, but nobody saw
Opened up my mouth and they talked right over the law
Got a good idea but it died upon my lips
They took the words right from me, let 'em sail away like ships

Talked over again, in every single room
My voice is just a whisper in somebody else's tune
I got something to say but it never sees the light
Talked over again, from the morning to the night

They said my name wrong then they stole my plan
Gave it to the fella who could shout louder than a man
I sat there in the corner with the coffee getting cold
Watching all my good ideas get sold for someone's gold

Talked over again, in every single room
My voice is just a whisper in somebody else's tune
I got something to say but it never sees the light
Talked over again, from the morning to the night

But one of these days I'm gonna stand right up
Gonna fill the whole darn room, gonna overflow the cup
They're gonna hear me coming like a freight train down the line
The quiet one is roaring and this meeting's gonna be mine

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Well I loaded up the Ford at the quarter to three
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
With a hundred gallons riding right behind of me
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
The moon was hanging low and the road was mine
Just a country boy a-running that sweet moonshine

Oh the run went wrong, baby, wrong wrong wrong
The sheriff's on my tail and the night's too long
Whoa-oh-oh, my darling don't you cry
I'm a moonshine runner and I'm doomed to fly

The blue lights flashing in my rearview glass
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
I hit the gas a-praying that this curve would pass
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
But the bottles started breaking and the smoke rolled in
Lord have mercy on a moonshine sin

Oh the run went wrong, baby, wrong wrong wrong
The sheriff's on my tail and the night's too long
Whoa-oh-oh, my darling don't you cry
I'm a moonshine runner and I'm doomed to fly

So if I don't make it home by the break of day
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
Just remember that I loved you all the way
Whoa-oh, whoa-oh, one more time
A broken-hearted runner and his last moonshine

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The candles are burned down, the mourners gone home
The kettle's still warm but I'm sitting alone
The fields are all silver with the dew of the dawn
And the world keeps on turning though my love is gone

Oh the morning comes soft on the mist and the moor
Though my heart is a stone by the cold cottage door
The larks they still sing and the river runs on
The morning after, and you're truly gone

I'll fold up your coat and I'll put out the fire
I'll walk down the lane past the old churchyard spire
The whole town was weeping, now the whole town will rise
While I carry your shadow in the green of my eyes

Oh the morning comes soft on the mist and the moor
Though my heart is a stone by the cold cottage door
The larks they still sing and the river runs on
The morning after, and you're truly gone

So I'll lift up me head and I'll greet the gray sky
For you wouldn't want me to lay down and die
There's bread to be baked and there's children to feed
And a love that lives on in the hearts that you freed

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
He used to lift the world up on his back
Now his hands are shaking just to hold a coffee black
The man who was a mountain's getting small
And I don't know how to catch him if he falls

MY OLD MAN IS GETTING OLD
The story's almost told
The strongest man I ever knew
Is fading out of view

He forgets my name some days but not the song
We used to scream together loud and wrong
The wrinkles on his face are battle scars
From a working-class war beneath the stars

MY OLD MAN IS GETTING OLD
The story's almost told
The strongest man I ever knew
Is fading out of view

I'LL HOLD YOUR HAND THE WAY YOU HELD MINE
I'LL WALK YOU SLOW THROUGH FATHER TIME
YOU TAUGHT ME HOW TO STAND AND FIGHT
SO I'LL BE HERE WITH YOU TONIGHT

MY OLD MAN IS GETTING OLD
The story's almost told
The strongest man I ever knew
Is fading out of view

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Ten and two, little darling, keep it slow
The whole wide world is waiting down the road
Your hands are shaking just like mine once did
Seems like yesterday you were my little kid

Easy on the gas now, easy on the brake
This old road we're driving's gonna be your own to take
I'm right here beside you, but I'm letting go
Teaching you to drive is teaching you to grow

You stalled it at the corner and you laughed out loud
Second gear and suddenly you're driving proud
The radio is playing all the songs you love
And I thank the stars above for the gift of

Easy on the gas now, easy on the brake
This old road we're driving's gonna be your own to take
I'm right here beside you, but I'm letting go
Teaching you to drive is teaching you to grow

One day you'll be driving down a road I've never seen
Chasing all the places that you've only ever dreamed
So keep your eyes wide open and your heart upon the wheel
And know your daddy loves you more than any words can feel

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Lamp burning low, the city gone to bed
Pages and pages spinning in my head
Kettle whistle soft so I don't wake the street
Four walls and a clock and the rhythm of my feet

No soundsystem now, just a fan in the dark
Highlighter yellow leaving its mark
Tomorrow there's a test gonna measure what I know
but tonight it's just me and the radio low

Two in the morning, I and I alone
building up a future out of paper and bone
No riddim but my heartbeat, no light but the page
gonna carry all this knowing when I walk off the stage

Mama said the hard road make the strongest man
so I plant every lesson like seed in the land
Eyes getting heavy but I push through the wall
one more chapter then I answer the call

When the rooster start crowing and the sky turn grey
all this midnight sweetness gonna carry me through the day
What you learn in the silence, no one can erase
so I bless the lonely hour and I keep my pace

Two in the morning, I and I alone
building up a future out of paper and bone
No riddim but my heartbeat, no light but the page
gonna carry all this knowing when I walk off the stage

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
One plate on the table where there used to be two
The kettle still hums the way it always knew
Her apron on the hook, I leave it on the nail
Measuring for half now, learning by the scale

Slow it down, slow it down
salt and water, no one around
The steam rises soft, the window goes pale
I'm cooking for one and I'm learning to sail

She knew where everything lived in this room
Now I find the cinnamon, I find the broom
A recipe card in her looping hand
grease on the corner, I start to understand

The radio low, the evening so wide
I set out one fork and the quiet's my guide
Every small thing she did without a sound
I feel her now in the warmth all around

Slow it down, slow it down
salt and water, no one around
The steam rises soft, the window goes pale
I'm cooking for one and I'm learning to sail

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Every night I climb the spiral stair
write your name in salt and the cold sea air
The lamp turns slow, a halo on the foam
I seal each letter, send it out from home

Oh my ocean, oh my endless blue
every wave's a word I wrote to you
I never need an answer to be true
the tide comes in and that's enough, that's you

Gulls take my paper out past the reef
a hundred lonely pages of belief
The fog rolls velvet over the bay
I love you in the dark and at break of day

They say a keeper's married to the light
but I gave my heart to the water at night
No ring, no vow, just the spray on glass
and the whisper of the green waves as they pass

Oh my ocean, oh my endless blue
every wave's a word I wrote to you
I never need an answer to be true
the tide comes in and that's enough, that's you

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Mama packed the photographs, the spices in a jar
I memorized the streetlights, every scar
The harbor swallowed morning as the engine pulled away
I watched my whole tomorrow turn to grey

Across the water, across the wide unknown
carrying a country in my bones
I'll plant my mother's garden in a colder kind of clay
and call it home someday, someday

New tongue in my mouth, it don't taste like mine
I fold my old name careful, keep it in line
The city hums a language I am learning how to sing
between the man I was and what I'll bring

Don't you cry for me, I'm not lost out here
every mile of ocean made the picture clear
I'm a seed the wind decided it would throw
and baby watch me grow, watch me grow

Across the water, across the wide unknown
carrying a country in my bones
I'll plant my mother's garden in a colder kind of clay
and call it home someday, someday

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Tres puntos en la pantalla, then they disappear
I'm reading it again, but the words are clear
No llamada, no goodbye, just a little grey line
You ended what we had in a buzz at 2:09

Un mensaje, that's all that it took
you closed the whole story, didn't even look
Dale, vibrate one more time on the floor
but I'm not gonna read you anymore, no more

We danced through the summer, perreo till the light
now you fit our forever in a screen so bright
Typing, then nothing, then you typing again
the cruelest little rhythm of the modern men

I threw the phone facedown, turned the music loud
let the bassline lift me up above the crowd
If love can die in pixels then it wasn't deep
so I'm dancing out the door, no tears to keep

Un mensaje, that's all that it took
you closed the whole story, didn't even look
Dale, vibrate one more time on the floor
but I'm not gonna read you anymore, no more

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
Them put us on the floor under the very same light
two fighting roosters told to move just right
Your hand in my hand and we can't pull away
the one-drop pulling us into the sway

Step for step, enemy of mine
the riddim don't care 'bout your side or the line
We could war all night or we could move as one
so wind to the bass till the bad blood done

You stepped on my pride, I stepped on your toes
but the drum keep talking and the tension goes
I catch you almost smiling when the skank come round
hard to hold a grudge to a sweeter sound

Funny how the music make a truce of its own
two stubborn fighters and we're not alone
The crowd start clapping and the feud feel small
when Jah riddim move the both of us all

Step for step, enemy of mine
the riddim don't care 'bout your side or the line
We could war all night or we could move as one
so wind to the bass till the bad blood done

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
They carried him slow down Rampart Street
black umbrellas in the heavy heat
The tuba sighing low, the trumpet hung its head
we walked behind the box where our brother lay dead

When the saints come marching, set my sorrow free
turn the dirge to glory, let the second line be
We cry on the way there, we dance on the way home
'cause nobody walks to heaven all alone

Then the snare cracked open and the tempo grew
handkerchiefs to parasols, the whole street flew
Grief became a holler, became a holy shout
that's how the city lets a good man out

Don't you bury the music, don't you bury the man
lift him on the rhythm, raise him while you can
From the church to the river, from the dirt to the sky
New Orleans don't say goodbye, she says fly

When the saints come marching, set my sorrow free
turn the dirge to glory, let the second line be
We cry on the way there, we dance on the way home
'cause nobody walks to heaven all alone

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Levanto la mano but they look right through
I start with an idea and somebody talks too
My sentence cut in half by a louder voice
dressed up in a suit like he's got no choice

Déjame hablar, let me finish the line
you stole my idea then you called it fine
I been holding the rhythm, holding the floor
so muévete, I'm not silent anymore

Mute on, mute off, I been waiting all day
then they repeat my words and they get the okay
Same point, his mouth, and suddenly it's gold
tired of the story always getting told

So I turn up the bass in the back of my chest
next time I open up I won't second-guess
Dale, dale, the boardroom's a club
and I came here to dance, not to get shoved

Déjame hablar, let me finish the line
you stole my idea then you called it fine
I been holding the rhythm, holding the floor
so muévete, I'm not silent anymore

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent cage, the table's set
I raise my voice but they forget
A wall of mouths, a tide of noise
they bury me with louder boys

LET ME SPEAK!
you drown the words right out my throat
LET ME SPEAK!
I'm screaming under every quote
The agenda burns, the silence breaks
I'll tear this table down for all our sakes

They steal the thought right off my tongue
repeat it loud and it gets sung
I choke on every swallowed line
the rage is building, it is mine

No more nodding, no more wait
I break the mute, I break the gate
The hammer falls on every word
you WILL be silent, I WILL be heard

LET ME SPEAK!
you drown the words right out my throat
LET ME SPEAK!
I'm screaming under every quote
The agenda burns, the silence breaks
I'll tear this table down for all our sakes

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Mud on my boots and the main stage gone dark
last can of warm beer, last spark in the spark
The vendors packing canvas, the lights coming down
one more song echoing over the ground

It's the last night, baby, the last night here
tomorrow it's a field and a memory so dear
So play me one more slow one before the morning comes
let me feel it in my chest like the kick of the drums

Strangers turned to family in three days of rain
tomorrow we'll be scattered down the highway again
The headliner's silent, the crowd's drifting home
but a few of us are lingering, can't leave it alone

There's a fire still burning where the late ones stay
got a harmonica crying for the end of the day
We'll carry this weekend like a stone in our shoe
a sweet kind of ache, just me and you

It's the last night, baby, the last night here
tomorrow it's a field and a memory so dear
So play me one more slow one before the morning comes
let me feel it in my chest like the kick of the drums

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Forty years of whistle, forty years of steam
this line built my house and it built my dream
Now the notice on the door says the last shift's done
they're shipping out the future, leaving none

The gates are closing, the lights go low
what happens to a town when the work won't grow
I gave my hands, I gave my years
now I'm clocking out with all my fears

Frank ran the press, Maria ran the floor
we knew each other's children, now we're out the door
The machines sit silent like a heart with no beat
and the rust is gonna swallow up the whole damn street

But you can't shut down the people, you can't close the soul
we'll find another fire to keep us whole
Though the smokestack's cold and the sign comes down
there's a stubborn kind of pride that runs this town

The gates are closing, the lights go low
what happens to a town when the work won't grow
I gave my hands, I gave my years
now I'm clocking out with all my fears

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up on the rooftop with a wire and a prayer
broadcasting freedom on the midnight air
The signal's illegal but the music is true
coming to you live from a room with no view

This is the pirate, turn your dial my way
I'll play you the records that they won't play
They can chase the frequency, they can cut the line
but you can't arrest a song, it's yours and it's mine

The inspector's van is circling the block
so I keep it moving, keep it loose on the clock
A whisper to the lonely, a hymn to the poor
every static crackle is an open door

When the lights go down on the whole grey town
there's a voice in the dark that won't back down
From the attic to the alley, from the dock to the shore
turn it up, turn it up, this is what radio's for

This is the pirate, turn your dial my way
I'll play you the records that they won't play
They can chase the frequency, they can cut the line
but you can't arrest a song, it's yours and it's mine

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the Ford with a hundred proof dreams
back roads at midnight, headlights and screams
Daddy made the recipe, I made the run
thought I knew these hollers better than anyone

But the law was waiting at the bottom of the grade
lights spinning red on the deal that I made
Now the jars are all shattered on the Tennessee clay
and the moonshine river's washing my freedom away

Took the curve too tight where the gravel runs thin
fender kissed the guardrail, felt the night cave in
The smell of cornmash heavy in the cold mountain air
a hundred dollar gamble and a county that don't care

Mama always told me that the still would bring me low
but a poor man's gotta carry what the rich men won't grow
So I'll do my time quiet, let the cell door slam
and I'll dream of that engine and the man that I am

But the law was waiting at the bottom of the grade
lights spinning red on the deal that I made
Now the jars are all shattered on the Tennessee clay
and the moonshine river's washing my freedom away

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night the same thing, Lord, I rise up off the bed
floating past the rooftops with the moon inside my head
I spread my arms like blackbird wings and leave the ground behind
but morning always finds me with the chains back on my mind

I been flying in my dreaming, way up where the eagles roam
then I wake in this old body, in this hard and heavy home
Oh I touched the open heavens, felt the wind beneath my soul
now I'm walking on the pavement with a hunger I can't hold

The doctor say it's nothing, just the workings of the brain
but I swear I feel the freedom and I swear I feel the pain
'Cause something in me's reaching for a sky I cannot keep
and the only place I find it is the falling into sleep

Maybe one day I won't wake up, maybe one day I'll stay high
a shadow on the morning, just a speck against the sky
Till then I'll close my tired eyes and pray the dream comes true
that a man too poor to travel still got wings to carry through

I been flying in my dreaming, way up where the eagles roam
then I wake in this old body, in this hard and heavy home
Oh I touched the open heavens, felt the wind beneath my soul
now I'm walking on the pavement with a hunger I can't hold

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They picked the dress, they picked the man, they picked the day
signed my whole tomorrow in a contract far away
But I packed a little suitcase while the house was asleep
there's a promise to myself that I intend to keep

Me voy, me voy, I'm taking back my name
your perfect little future, baby, that's your game
Dale, the engine's running, the road is mine
I'm leaving the arrangement way behind the line

Abuela's gonna cry and the family's gonna talk
let them fill the empty chair, I'm done with their walk
I loved the people in it but I can't wear that crown
so I'm dancing out the front door, watch me put it down

No ring gonna hold me, no plan gonna chain
I'd rather face the storm than to live in their rain
The bassline is my passport, the night is my friend
this is where their story and my freedom begin

Me voy, me voy, I'm taking back my name
your perfect little future, baby, that's your game
Dale, the engine's running, the road is mine
I'm leaving the arrangement way behind the line

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Lights were low and the room was mine
Everybody hushed at the count of nine
Had the whole crowd leanin' in my way
One more verse and I'd've made my pay

Manager said son you got the spark
Come back Monday, we'll make our mark
Then the rain came down and the wires went black
And that one chance never did come back

Oh I almost made it, Lord I almost shined
Had the door wide open, left the dream behind
So close I could taste it on my tongue
Now I'm singin' to the bottles when the night is young

Still got the suit hangin' on the wall
Still hear the echo of that almost call
Funny how the closest cut the deepest scar
Almost famous, almost a star

Don't you cry for what the river took
Every almost wrote a line in my book
I'll play it slow and I'll play it true
'Cause almost made me, and it might make you

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Door clicks soft, no slam tonight
Just the hum of the fridge and the kitchen light
We said too much, then we said no more
Footsteps fading down the hall to the floor

In the quiet, in the quiet
Words still hangin' in the air, won't deny it
We both tired of the noise, gonna try it
Find our way back through the quiet

Tea gone cold on the windowsill
Clock keeps tickin' while the moment's still
I know your breathing through the open door
Same two hearts that we had before

I'll sit right here till you wanna talk
No need to run, no need to walk
Let the silence do the mending now
We'll find the words somehow, somehow

In the quiet, in the quiet
Love don't leave, it just goes private
Meet me halfway, I won't fight it
Meet me there inside the quiet

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete river, neon glow
Where the gardens used to grow
But there's one green crown standing tall
The last leaf dancer through it all

Shake it tree, shake it down
Last one standing in a paved-over town
Roots in the asphalt, branches in the lights
Dancin' for the city every single night

They poured the sidewalk all around
But couldn't keep your spirit down
Every breeze that comes to play
You throw your shadow on the runaway

So plant your feet and feel the beat
Green against the concrete heat
If you're the last then you're the best
You outshine all the rest

Shake it tree, shake it down
Last one standing in a paved-over town
Roots in the asphalt, branches in the lights
Dancin' for the city every single night

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone felt heavy in my hand
Ten digits I could barely understand
How the years had grown between us wide
But tonight I swallowed all my pride

Mama, it's me, can you hear my voice
I know I left you with no choice
But I'm callin' now, I'm callin' home
Tired of standin' here all alone

She picked up soft on the second ring
Said baby, I been prayin' for this thing
No anger there, just a trembling sigh
And all my reasons started to die

All those years I thought I had to run
From the only one who'd always come
The distance melted in a single word
Sweetest sound I ever heard

Mama, it's me, can you hear my voice
I know I left you with no choice
But I'm callin' now, I'm comin' home
Never gonna leave you all alone

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
Pick up the trumpet, skank to the beat
I'm writin' a letter I'll never complete
To a name on a stone in the cold gray ground
All my anger finally winding down

I forgive you, I forgive you
Even though you're gone and I can't see through
Lay it down, let it go, let the horns ring true
I forgive you, I forgive you

I carried that weight for a thousand days
Grudge like a stone in a dozen ways
But you can't say sorry from the other side
So I'll say it for you, swallow my pride

Upstroke, downstroke, off-beat free
Letting go is the gift to me
Not for you and not for show
Just to finally let it go

I forgive you, I forgive you
Even though you're gone and I can't see through
Lay it down, let it go, let the horns ring true
I forgive you, I forgive you

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They laid my whole life on the table
A name, a house, a wedding band
Wrote my future in their bible
But it wasn't mine to understand

So I'm ridin' out past the chapel doors
Leavin' all their plans on the kitchen floor
The crows can have the life they made
I'm walkin' through the cold to find my own parade

My mother wept and my father swore
A storm rolled in off the moor
The dress hung white like a hollow ghost
The one thing I feared the most

Let the church bells toll for a girl who's dead
For the daughter they invented in their head
I'm somebody new on a darker road
Carryin' nothin' but my own heavy load

So I'm ridin' out past the chapel doors
Leavin' all their plans on the kitchen floor
The crows can have the life they made
I'm walkin' through the cold to find my own parade

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Forty years on a kitchen floor
We danced before, we'll dance once more
The music's fading, the lights gone dim
One last spin on a fragile limb

Hold me close in the breakbeat rain
One more dance to ease the pain
Faster now before the morning comes
Feel the bassline, feel the drums

Your hand still fits inside of mine
Worn like leather, sweet like wine
We move slow while the beat moves fast
Making this moment built to last

Every breath is a snare-drum roll
Every year is a part of the whole
When the track runs out and the room goes still
I'll keep dancin', I always will

Hold me close in the breakbeat rain
One more dance to ease the pain
Faster now before the morning comes
Feel the bassline, feel the drums

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Midnight blue and the screen glows white
Thumb hoverin' over a name tonight
I know better, oh I surely do
But the keys keep spellin' out to you

One little message, one little sin
Let the trouble out, let the trouble in
Swingin' on the edge of a dangerous line
Textin' someone who was never mine

The smart move's to set it down
Walk away before I drown
But the saxophone in my chest plays low
And it whispers everything I shouldn't know

Delete, retype, delete again
Dancin' with a question mark of when
The cursor blinks like a heartbeat slow
Tellin' me to stay, tellin' me to go

One little message, one little sin
Let the trouble out, let the trouble in
Swingin' on the edge of a dangerous line
Textin' someone who was never mine

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
The tether snapped without a sound
No up, no sky, no solid ground
I watch your visor catch the sun
Two small lights where there was one

Drifting, drifting, slow and wide
No current left to turn the tide
I reach for you across the black
Knowing there's no coming back

Your voice goes thin across the line
Static where your hand met mine
The Earth a marble far below
Watching as we let it go

No gravity to hold us near
Just the silence, vast and clear
I'll keep your light inside my eyes
A fading star in endless skies

Drifting, drifting, slow and wide
No current left to turn the tide
I reach for you across the black
Knowing there's no coming back

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Last pew in the back, I slip away
No more words I know how to pray
Grew up here, every hymn, every face
But my heart don't fit inside this place

Me voy callado, no goodbye
Under the steeple, out into the night
Keep your candles, keep your bells
I'm findin' my own heaven, my own hell

The stained glass watched me learn to stand
The holy water on my hand
But somewhere I stopped feelin' it true
So tonight I'm walkin' through

No anger in my chest, no fight
Just a quiet step into the light
The doors close soft, the music fades
This is how a believer's made unmade

Me voy callado, no goodbye
Under the steeple, out into the night
Keep your candles, keep your bells
I'm findin' my own heaven, my own hell

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The sun's gone down on the final day
Glitter on our skin won't wash away
Three days lost in the speakers' roar
And we don't want it anymore

This is the last night, hands in the air
Dust in our hair and we don't even care
Scream it out till the morning light
We were here, we were so alive tonight

Strangers turned to friends for life
Danced together through the night
The headliner takes the stage
We sing along to every page

When the lights come up we'll have to go
Back to lives we used to know
But something here will always stay
The last night never fades away

This is the last night, hands in the air
Dust in our hair and we don't even care
Scream it out till the morning light
We were here, we were so alive tonight

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raise my hand, I clear my throat
The words come out, then someone else's vote
Drowns me out like I wasn't there
Swallowed up in the conference air

I had something, I had something to say
But it died on my tongue and it floated away
Louder, louder, everyone's loud
I'm a quiet voice in a talking crowd

Good idea on a sticky note
Never made it down my throat
They repeat it back an hour late
And everybody thinks it's great

So I'm done bein' polite and small
Gonna write my name across the wall
Turn the amp up, blow the doors
This quiet kid don't whisper anymore

I had something, I had something to say
And I'm sayin' it now, I won't fade away
Louder, louder, hear me now
I'm the loudest voice in the room somehow

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man stands where the water breaks
Speaks to the sea for the wisdom's sake
Step and pause, the foam draws near
He's teachin' the tide what he holds dear

Ven, marea, come and learn
The push, the pull, the slow return
I'll lead you in the dance I know
The ebb, the rise, the come and go

His feet remember a hundred years
Of joys and losses, salt and tears
He shows the water how to sway
The way his lover moved one day

The ocean cannot hold his hand
But still he draws it in the sand
And the tide obeys his patient art
Two old dancers, one old heart

Ven, marea, come and learn
The push, the pull, the slow return
I'll lead you in the dance I know
The ebb, the rise, the come and go

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
You walk in late and the room goes warm
I'm the rain you never feel in the storm
I play it cool with a smoke and a smile
Loving you quiet all the while

You'll never know, you'll never know
How the piano aches when you turn to go
I hold the note that I can't let show
Loving you soft where the shadows grow

You tell me all about someone new
And I laugh along like lovers do
My glass is empty, my heart is full
Of a tide I can't control or pull

Maybe in another song
Where the right notes don't go wrong
You'd hear the thing I cannot say
But the band plays on, you walk away

You'll never know, you'll never know
How the piano aches when you turn to go
I hold the note that I can't let show
Loving you soft where the shadows grow

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night the same neon sky
My feet leave the ground and I learn to fly
No wings, no fear, just the city below
Glowing pink in the afterglow

Up, up, over the chrome and the light
I'm weightless again in the dream tonight
Don't wake me, don't wake me, let me soar
Through the same wide sky I've flown before

The rooftops shrink to a circuit board
Stars like static, a humming chord
I know this place though I've never been
The dream that keeps returning again

Morning pulls me back to the floor
Gravity at my bedroom door
But I close my eyes and I know it's true
Tonight I'll fly the whole sky through

Up, up, over the chrome and the light
I'm weightless again in the dream tonight
Don't wake me, don't wake me, let me soar
Through the same wide sky I've flown before

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Cassette hiss over a frozen lake
My breath draws ghosts the cold won't take
Green runs slow like spilled neon paint
Dripping down a sky too wide to frame
Vinyl crackle, thermos gone lukewarm
Wrapped in a blanket older than the storm
I count each ribbon, lose the count again
The dark just hums and lets the colors in

Slow lights, slow lights
Folding the hours into violet seams
Slow lights, slow lights
Stitching the quiet to the back of my dreams

A dog barks once, then lets it go
Footprints fill behind me in the snow
My phone died hours back, and that's alright
Nobody needs a photo of this light

The radio's just static and a heartbeat
The pines lean in like they don't wanna miss it
I press my palm against the window glass
And let the slow green teach me how to last

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Subzero pulse, the horizon cracks awake
Voltage rivers over a black glass lake
Kick drum thunder in my chest cavity
The sky's a circuit and it's wired through me
Run across the ice with my lungs on fire
Chasing the arc of a fading wire
Strobe of emerald, no time to stall
The whole night's falling and I want it all

Run, run under the burning green
Faster than the dark has ever been
Run, run, let the bassline detonate
The sky's on fire and it will not wait

Snare rolls hard like a heartbeat skipped
Aurora snapping like a whip
No brakes, no ceiling, no closing door
Just the speed of light across the floor

Drop the silence, count it down from four
The stars rip open and the bass hits more
I am a current, I am a livewire
Dancing in the throat of electric fire

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raised my hand in the fluorescent room
Words half-born and swallowed in the noise
They rolled right past me like I wasn't there
Louder voices burying my voice
But I been quiet in a holy way before
Waiting on a morning that I knew would come

My voice gonna rise, my voice gonna rise
Like water through a stone, it's gonna find the sky
They can fill the room, they can drown the air
But the Lord made my breath and my breath is still here
My voice gonna rise

They paved over my sentence with their own
Clapped for echoes of a thing I said first
But I been carried by a quiet hand
That keeps a ledger they could never burst

So let them talk, let the thunder roll
I'm building up a fire underneath my soul
When the room goes silent and they need the truth
They gonna turn around and find it sittin' here in this pew

My voice gonna rise, my voice gonna rise
Like water through a stone, it's gonna find the sky
My voice gonna rise

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the gray from curb to curb
Left one green soldier in the heat
Roots like fingers cracking concrete
Reaching past the dead-end street
Every storm it bowed but stood
Drinking rain from a broken main

Oh stand, last green standing
Lift your branches to the burning day
Oh stand, hands a-raising
They can't pave the praise away

Children carve their names in bark
Lovers leave their promise there
It's the only shade for a city block
The only church that's open air

When the bulldozers come a-humming
We gonna circle round the trunk
Lay our bodies in the gravel
And we'll sing until they're sunk

Oh stand, last green standing
Lift your branches to the burning day
Oh stand, hands a-raising
They can't pave the praise away

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Midnight blue light on my face again
Thumb hovering over a name I deleted twice
I typed it, erased it, typed it once more
And the little gray dots are a roll of the dice

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but my hands won't quit
This phone's a loaded pistol and I'm cleaning it
Lord, send me trouble in a three-word line
I shouldn't be texting, but I'm doing it fine

The wedding ring's a circle, the screen's a square
And somewhere in between is where I lose my way
She answers in a heartbeat like she never slept
And my whole good intention just drove away

Delete the thread, throw the phone in the lake
That's the smart man talking, and I never been him
One more message, one more mistake
Then I'll start being good — tomorrow, on a whim

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but my hands won't quit
This phone's a loaded pistol and I'm cleaning it
Lord, send me trouble in a three-word line
I shouldn't be texting, but I'm doing it fine

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
We laid the rails on a rising chest
Built the courthouse over a slow-drawn breath
Grandpa felt the cobbles roll one spring
Said the ground down there is a living thing

Don't stomp too hard on the valley floor
There's a heartbeat under the hardware store
He's been dreaming since before the flood
This whole town's standing on a giant's blood

The church bell swings when there ain't no wind
The wells run warm and the wells run thin
We planted corn in a shoulder blade
And prayed he likes the noise we made

One day he'll stretch and the steeples'll lean
The river'll climb where the bridge has been
So play it soft on the mandolin
Don't you wake what the dirt keeps in

Don't stomp too hard on the valley floor
There's a heartbeat under the hardware store
He's been dreaming since before the flood
This whole town's standing on a giant's blood

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We shared the same tin can of air
Learned to sleep while spinning through the black
Your voice came crackling through my helmet glass
Now the tether's slack and you're drifting back

And I can't reach you, I can't reach you now
Just two slow planets that forgot their vow
The Earth's a marble we agreed to leave
Now you're a light I'm learning not to grieve

We used to point at constellations same
Name the craters after inside jokes
Now the radio is just your breathing fade
A hundred meters and a thousand strokes

Kick off the hull, I won't follow you
There's no up or down to argue through
We orbit nothing, we are nobody's moon
And the silence between us is a wide, cold room

And I can't reach you, I can't reach you now
Just two slow planets that forgot their vow
The Earth's a marble we agreed to leave
Now you're a light I'm learning not to grieve

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night when the streetlights die
My bedroom ceiling turns to sky
I kick the blanket off the bed
And float right out above my head
Horns kick in and the offbeat skips
I'm doing loops past the rooftop's lip

Up, up, pick it up, I'm gone
Over the chimneys before the dawn
Don't wake me, don't wake me, leave me high
I finally figured out how to fly

The alarm clock's a cruel little man
Drags me down quick as he can
But I keep the secret in my chest
The upstroke guitar knows the rest

Pick it up, brass section, blow
Tell my feet they don't need the floor
Gravity sent me a parking ticket
But I tore it up and flew out the door

Up, up, pick it up, I'm gone
Over the chimneys before the dawn
Don't wake me, don't wake me, leave me high
I finally figured out how to fly

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I light a candle for an empty chair
The bandoneon breathes in the heavy air
You took my anger to a colder bed
And left me dancing with the words unsaid

I forgive you, I forgive you now
Though you can't hear it, though you can't bow
The step is mine to finish alone
I lay the weight of you down like a stone

Your photograph still leads me by the waist
A partner made of memory and lace
I counted every wound a hundred times
Now I trade the count for these quiet lines

The music dips and I let you fall
Not in spite, but to free us both, that's all
The grave is silent, the rose is dry
And I dance the last turn with a dry-eyed goodbye

I forgive you, I forgive you now
Though you can't hear it, though you can't bow
The step is mine to finish alone
I lay the weight of you down like a stone

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They signed my name in my grandmother's hand
A wedding stitched before I learned to walk
A house, a field, a man I never chose
A silver future locked behind a lock

Ay, but I am riding, riding from the gate
The trumpets call me past the garden wall
I tear the lace, I will not meet that date
My own tomorrow is the loudest call

My mother weeps and my father turns away
The priest already practiced every word
But the horizon hums a wilder song
Than any vow this valley ever heard

Forgive me, town, your map was drawn too tight
I'll send a letter from a road with no name
The violins can mourn me all they like
I'd rather burn than live inside a frame

Ay, but I am riding, riding from the gate
The trumpets call me past the garden wall
I tear the lace, I will not meet that date
My own tomorrow is the loudest call

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
(Sha-la-la) I stood by your stone today
(Sha-la-la) with the words I couldn't say
The rain came soft on the marble gray
And I let my anger wash away

I forgive you, darling (oo-wah, oo-wah)
Though you're sleeping now (sleeping now)
I forgive you, darling
I'm taking down the wall somehow

(Sha-la-la) you broke my heart in two
(Sha-la-la) and I carried it for you
But a grudge is a heavy thing to hold
And the night gets awful cold

No more, no more counting what you owe (what you owe)
