I'm letting every dollar of it go (let it go)
The choir of the crickets sings me through
As I make my peace with you

I forgive you, darling (oo-wah, oo-wah)
Though you're sleeping now (sleeping now)
I forgive you, darling
I'm taking down the wall somehow

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Gris en la calle, gris en el cielo
Un árbol solo rompiendo el concreto
Raíz que pelea contra el asfalto
Última sombra pa' todo el barrio
Niños bailando bajo su rama
Mientras la grúa ya lo reclama

No lo tumben, no, déjenlo crecer (déjenlo)
Es lo último verde que queda por ver
Dale, dale, baila alrededor
Un árbol, mil voces, un solo tambor

Lleno de nombres tallados en piel
Cada cicatriz es un amor fiel
El único oxígeno de la avenida
De noche lo cuido, le doy mi vida

Que suene el perreo como protesta
Manos al tronco, esto es nuestra fiesta
Si cae el árbol cae la ciudad
Pero hoy bailamos por su libertad

No lo tumben, no, déjenlo crecer (déjenlo)
Es lo último verde que queda por ver
Dale, dale, baila alrededor
Un árbol, mil voces, un solo tambor

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I got hail on the hood and a map gone soft
A radio screaming where the funnel's at
You asked me to stay, lay the keys on the shelf
But my heart's a barometer dropping flat

I chase the thunder, baby, that's my flaw
Can't sleep in a house with a roof and a wall
When the sky turns green I gotta go
Love me in the lightning or don't love me at all

You set two plates and you lit the wick
But the wind shifted hard from the southwest line
I kissed your forehead and I grabbed my coat
Left you the calm and I took the sign

One day I'll park where the air stays still
Buy a porch and a dog and a quiet hill
But not while the clouds know my first name
Not while the prairie keeps calling my flame

I chase the thunder, baby, that's my flaw
Can't sleep in a house with a roof and a wall
When the sky turns green I gotta go
Love me in the lightning or don't love me at all

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
The diesel hums in a soft minor key
Neon puddles where the truck stop glows
Three in the morning, I dial the old line
Just to hear the house the way it breathes and slows

Pick up, pick up, it's only me
Mile marker lonely on the interstate
I miss the weight of you asleep on my arm
Tell me you're dreaming, tell me you'll wait

The coffee's cold and the dashboard hums
A moth keeps tapping on the windshield glass
You answer slow with your pillow voice
And suddenly the empty miles pass

Don't turn the light on, just stay on the phone
Let me drive these last hours like I'm not alone
The radio's whisper, your sleepy reply
Is the softest samba under a wide dark sky

Pick up, pick up, it's only me
Mile marker lonely on the interstate
I miss the weight of you asleep on my arm
Tell me you're dreaming, tell me you'll wait

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Seven rings and my hand's gone cold
The number's the same as it was back then
I rehearsed an apology in the car
Forgot every word when she said hello again

Hey mama, it's me, it's your boy on the line
It's been too many winters, I know, I know
I let my pride do the talking too long
Now I'm calling to tell you before you go

She didn't ask why and she didn't ask where
Just said your room is the way that you left
And the silence between us went soft as a quilt
All them wasted years, what a fool's bequest

The years I spent angry I can't get them back
But the phone in my hand is a second chance
I hear her old kettle start to sing
And I close my eyes and I almost dance

Hey mama, it's me, it's your boy on the line
It's been too many winters, I know, I know
I let my pride do the talking too long
Now I'm calling to tell you before you go

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Ooh, the sky is dancing, baby (sha-la-la)
Green and purple shining bright
Up above the frozen river
Colors falling through the night

I held your hand beneath the heavens
Watched the ribbons start to glow
Every whisper, every heartbeat
Lit the snow with rainbow snow

Oh, northern lights (northern lights)
Shine your love on me tonight
Dancing high above the sea
Won't you shine for you and me
Oh-oh-oh, my darling

Doo-wop dancing in the starlight
Hand in hand we sway and turn
Every color tells a story
Of the way my heart still burns

If the morning takes the colors
And the sky fades into blue
I will keep this magic moment
Keep it close, my love, with you

Oh, northern lights (northern lights)
Shine your love on me tonight
Dancing high above the sea
Won't you shine for you and me
Sha-la-la-la, forever

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Every night I write your name
In the salt and in the spray
Fold the paper, send it sailing
Watch the dark wash it away

You are deeper than my longing
Wider than the words I know
And I light this lamp to find you
In the place the tides all go

Oh sea, oh sea
Carry my letters home
I am yours and you are mine
In the endless, in the unknown
Oh sea, oh sea
Return to me

Gray horizon, slow and breathing
All my pages turning blue
I have given you my silence
Given all my years to you

No reply but distant thunder
No reply but rolling foam
Still I write, still I wonder
If the waves will bring you home

Oh sea, oh sea
Carry my letters home
I am yours and you are mine
In the endless, in the unknown

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The kettle's cold, the words have fallen
Like the rain on weathered stone
We sit apart in the same small kitchen
Two hearts and yet alone

I hear the clock, I hear your breathing
Louder than the things we said
The candle gutters low and golden
Casting shadows where we tread

Oh, the quiet, oh, the quiet
Softer than a closing door
Reach across the empty table
Love me like you did before

A fiddle hums somewhere outside us
Mournful as the falling night
And I would trade my stubborn silence
Just to hold you in the light

Let the anger turn to ashes
Let the morning take the storm
For the quiet after sorrow
Is the place where we grow warm

Oh, the quiet, oh, the quiet
Softer than a closing door
Reach across the empty table
Love me like you did before

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at six, the city sleepin'
Lights on the ward, I'm wide awake
Family textin' "where you at?"
Got another life to save
Double shift, no holiday
Scrubs still on, I can't complain
Coffee cold, my feet on fire
But I run it back again

Workin' double, workin' double
While the whole world celebrate
I'm the one that's in the trouble
Savin' lives, I can't be late
Workin' double, no rest
Lord, just get me through the day
Got a halo on my chest
While they all up out and play

Monitor beepin', I'm steady movin'
Room to room, I never stop
Missed the dinner, missed the toast
But I'm holdin' down the block
Gloves up, mask on, focused
Somebody's mama needs me now
I'll be home when the sun is risin'
Take my flowers, take my bow

They don't see me, but I'm here
Every holiday, every year
Givin' all I got to give
So somebody else can live

Workin' double, workin' double
While the whole world celebrate
I'm the one that's in the trouble
Savin' lives, I can't be late

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
Well, you stepped on my toes and I stepped on your pride
Now the fiddle is callin' and we can't run and hide
The whole village watching, the clarinet sings
Two enemies tangled in a circle of strings

I grip your hand and you grip mine
Spinning faster, line by line
Hate me later, hate me hard
But tonight we own the yard

Dance, dance, my old foe
Round and round the way we go
Laughing, cursing, knees up high
Two rivals beneath one sky
Oy, oy, spin me again
Maybe enemies, maybe friends

The accordion wheezes, the village claps loud
We whirl till we're dizzy, the proud meets the proud
Your eyes start to soften, my anger turns thin
The music's a thief and it's stealing my grin

Who can stay angry when the bow starts to fly?
Who can keep grudges when the drum reaches high?
Stomp out the bitterness, kick up the dust
The dance makes a bridge out of two who don't trust

Dance, dance, my old foe
Round and round the way we go
Laughing, cursing, knees up high
Two rivals beneath one sky

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this mornin', set the table for two
Then I caught myself, like I always do
One plate too many, one cup of coffee cold
Learnin' how to cook for one is gettin' old

You knew the recipes, you knew the way
Now I burn the bacon every single day
The pot's too big, the kitchen's too wide
Since the Lord came and took you from my side

Cookin' for one, Lord, cookin' for one
Half the supper gone to waste when the day is done
I measure out my heartache by the spoon
Sittin' at this table by the light of the moon

I hear your voice say "add a little salt"
Every little mistake feels just like my fault
But I'm tryin', baby, I'm learnin' slow
Makin' less and less of the meals we used to know

Some night I might just get it right
Set one plate down and turn off the light
And I'll thank you, darlin', for the years you fed
My hungry heart before the words were said

Cookin' for one, Lord, cookin' for one
Half the supper gone to waste when the day is done

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
We sat down Friday (bom-bom-bom)
Just a friendly little game
A dollar here, a dollar there
But nothin' stayed the same

The deck got hot, the night got long
The pile got tall and wide
I bet my watch, I bet my ride
I bet away my pride

Oh, the cards (the cards)
They took me way too far
Lost my money, lost my mind
Underneath the bar-room star
Whoa-oh, deal me out
Before I lose it all

My buddy folded (sha-la-la)
My honey walked out the door
I kept on chasin' that royal flush
Down to the dirty floor

If I could turn the deck back over
I'd fold that first wild hand
But greed's a dealer with a smile
That no fool understands

Oh, the cards (the cards)
They took me way too far
Lost my money, lost my mind
Underneath the bar-room star

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Pick it up, pick it up, here we go
Standing in the spotlight, ready for the show
Everybody waiting, crowd up on their feet
One more chance to make it, feel it in the beat

My hands were shaking, mic was hot
Gave it everything I got
So close I could taste the dream
Dancing on a moonlit beam

Oh, the night I almost made it
Oh, the night I almost flew
Up the mountain, nearly made it
Then the morning came on through
Pick it up, don't let it go
The almost is the part you grow

The trumpet blew, the crowd went wild
Felt just like a dreaming child
Then the curtain came on down
And I was just a kid in town

But almost ain't the same as never
Almost means you touched the sky
I'll lace 'em up and try forever
'Cause the almost taught me why

Oh, the night I almost made it
Oh, the night I almost flew
Up the mountain, nearly made it
Then the morning came on through

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The record's spinning slow tonight
The living room's our dancehall floor
Forty years inside this rhythm
And my heart still wants you more

Your hand still fits inside my hand
Like the day I said "I do"
We've been through the storms and silence
But I'm still in love with you

So give me one more dance, my love
One more turn beneath the light
Let the music hold us closer
Let me love you through the night
One more dance, one more dance
Before the morning comes for us

We were young and now we're golden
Gray and slow but still in tune
Every scar became a story
Every night became a moon

If this is the final measure
If the song must reach its end
I would dance it all over
With my lover and my friend

So give me one more dance, my love
One more turn beneath the light
Let the music hold us closer
Let me love you through the night

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down on a Tuesday morning
I was running for the train
Then a stranger held an umbrella
Saved me from the pouring rain

Shoulder pressed to shoulder, laughing
Strangers in a little dome
One umbrella, two umbrellas
Walking somebody else home

Under one umbrella, hey!
Two strangers in the rain
Pick it up, we're dancing puddles
Down the soggy little lane
Under one umbrella, hey!
Maybe we don't catch that train

Never caught the stranger's name yet
But I caught a little spark
Trumpets in the storm above us
Lighting up the rainy dark

Funny how a bit of weather
Throws two lonely souls together
One small roof against the gray
Turned a stranger's gray to play

Under one umbrella, hey!
Two strangers in the rain
Pick it up, we're dancing puddles
Down the soggy little lane

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every morning I unlock the door
Walk the marble, cross the floor
There she hangs upon the wall
The prettiest painting of them all

I tip my hat, I say good day
Though she never has a word to say
Just a smile in oil and gold
Keeps me company when nights get cold

Oh, painted lady on the wall
You're the reason I don't mind it all
Guardin' beauty, day by day
Don't you ever fade away
One love, one painting, one guard
Watchin' over you, it ain't hard

The tourists come and the tourists go
Snappin' pictures, movin' slow
But none of them can ever see
What that painting means to me

When the lights go down at night
And the moon comes slippin' bright
I swear I see her gently grin
Like she's keepin' watch on him

Oh, painted lady on the wall
You're the reason I don't mind it all
Guardin' beauty, day by day
Don't you ever fade away

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew its final morning
The gates rolled shut for the last time
Three generations gave their hours
To the iron and the grime

My father stood here, so did his
Hands grown rough upon the wheel
Now the furnace fire is dying
And the silence ain't quite real

Oh, the factory's closing down
And the heart goes out of the town
Where will the young ones go
When the river stops its flow
Oh, the factory's closing down

We drank our pints in the evening shadow
Sang our songs and shared our bread
Now the lads are leavin' early
With the future hangin' dead

But the spirit of the workers
Won't be buried in the rust
We will carry on the story
We will rise up from the dust

Oh, the factory's closing down
And the heart goes out of the town
Where will the young ones go
When the river stops its flow

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Pick it up now, slow it down
My old man around the town
Used to lift me to the sky
Now I watch the years go by

Hands that built the whole world strong
Shake a little, takin' long
Still he winks and cracks a joke
Strongest man I ever known

Oh, my father, gettin' gray
Dancin' slower every day
I will hold you when you fall
You're still the tallest of them all
Hey! Hey! My old man
I'll catch you like you taught me to

We put the record on tonight
Moved our feet beneath the light
He forgot a step or two
So I led him slow on through

Time's a thief, but love's a song
And I'll play it all night long
Every wrinkle, every line
Is a verse of yours and mine

Oh, my father, gettin' gray
Dancin' slower every day
I will hold you when you fall
You're still the tallest of them all

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle in my hand since the break of dawn
Threadin' up the dreams of everyone I'm on
Mr. Johnson's suit and the preacher's Sunday best
I stitch a little love in every seam and crest

The widow needs her hemline, the groom he needs his fit
And I press 'em all with pride, oh, every little bit
From the corner to the corner, the whole block walks in style
'Cause the tailor on the avenue is sewin' with a smile

Oh, I dress the whole block, baby
From the young to the old
Every thread a little story
Every jacket made of gold
Oh, I dress the whole block, honey
Make 'em shine when they walk
Give a man his dignity
With a needle and some chalk

The boy who got the job, the girl who got the prom
They come to me in worry, leave a little calm
I measure out their courage, I tailor up their pride
Send 'em down the avenue with somethin' good inside

It ain't just cloth and buttons, no
It's the way you hold your head
When the world don't give you nothin'
I give you gold instead

Oh, I dress the whole block, baby
From the young to the old
Every thread a little story
Every jacket made of gold

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon hummin' on the corner, two a.m.
Coffee's always hot for the lonely men
Truckers, dreamers, broken-hearted souls
Find a little warmth where the river rolls

Waitress knows your order 'fore you sit
Pours a cup of mercy, just a little bit
The grill keeps sizzlin', the jukebox plays
Gettin' folks on through the darkest days

Open all night, Lord, open all night
A little piece of heaven 'neath the buzzin' light
When the world done shut its doors on you
There's a stool and a song and a cup or two
Open all night, open all night

The poet scribbles, the lovers fight and mend
The night-shift workers callin' it the end
Everybody's welcome, everybody's seen
In the warmest little booth you ever been

When the morning finally breaks the dark
And the early sun comes climbin' up the park
We'll keep the coffee brewin' just the same
'Cause somebody's always lookin' for the flame

Open all night, Lord, open all night
A little piece of heaven 'neath the buzzin' light
When the world done shut its doors on you
There's a stool and a song and a cup or two

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The record skips the way it always did
Forty years of dust on the same blue lid
You hold out your hand like it's nineteen-six
My heels still know the floor, the old soft kicks

The lamp is low, the kettle's gone to cold
Your shoulder fits my cheek, both of us old
We sway through the kitchen, no need for the band
A whole marriage folded into one slow hand

So dance me down the hallway, dance me to the door
Dance me to the years we don't get anymore
One more turn, my darling, one more low refrain
Then the needle lifts and we won't dance again

The doctor used a word I won't repeat
So I learned your weight against me, learned your feet
I count them like a metronome too slow
Memorizing every place your hands still go

The song was always longer than the lovers think
We just heard the chorus, never watched it shrink
Now the brushes hush the snare into a sigh
And I'm grateful for the verses, you and I

So dance me down the hallway, dance me to the door
Dance me to the years we don't get anymore
One more turn, my darling, one more low refrain
Then the needle lifts and we won't dance again

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the rails on a ribcage made of stone
Built the courthouse where his heartbeat used to groan
Grandpa said don't drill too deep beneath the square
There's something old and breathing down in there

The church bells ring and the windows hum along
The whole town moves to a low and buried song
We call it settling, the cracks along Main Street
But I swear that's a pulse beneath my feet

O sleep, you giant, sleep beneath our town
Keep your eyes shut tight and don't you turn around
We built our little lives upon your chest
So dream us kindly, giant, take your rest

The river bends the way his shoulder lies
The hills go up and down like he still breathes and sighs
We planted corn between his fingers in the loam
And called this borrowed body home

One day the dust will shake the dishes from the shelf
And he will stand and shrug us off himself
But till that morning cracks the prairie wide
We'll sing him lullabies and stay inside

O sleep, you giant, sleep beneath our town
Keep your eyes shut tight and don't you turn around
We built our little lives upon your chest
So dream us kindly, giant, take your rest

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The peat smoke clings to my only coat
The ferry's at the quay with a low gray throat
My mother's at the window, she won't come down
I'm the last of us to leave this drowning town

The chapel knows my name in the carved oak pew
The hills know the path that my small feet drew
But the work all left on the morning tide
And the young ones follow where the wages ride

Fare thee well, fare thee well, to the hawthorn lane
I'll not walk your green again in the soft gray rain
Keep my name in the churchyard, keep my song in the stone
I am leaving, I am leaving, and I'm leaving alone

There's a girl I never told and now I never will
There's a fiddle on the wall that the dust will fill
The road runs east to a city's iron sound
And I'll not be turning, not be turning round

Oh the heart's a stubborn boat that won't stay moored
But everyone I love is on the leaving shore
So I'll carry the hills inside my chest
And let the salt wind take the rest

Fare thee well, fare thee well, to the hawthorn lane
I'll not walk your green again in the soft gray rain
Keep my name in the churchyard, keep my song in the stone
I am leaving, I am leaving, and I'm leaving alone

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
They carried you out to a minor key
A tuba sobbing low on Rampart Street
The handkerchiefs went up like a flock of doves
And the whole block came to give you all their love

Then somebody hit the snare
And the sorrow caught the air

So cut the body loose and let the brass band play
We'll dance you up to heaven in the bright of day
Grief in the morning, joy by the afternoon
We'll send you home on a second-line tune

The parasols are spinning red and gold
The young ones strut, the story gets retold
Your mama's in the front with a feather in her hand
Laughing through the tears with the marching band

Death ain't the end of the song down here
It's just the key change, loud and clear
We weep on the way and we dance on back
Life on the bright and the grief on the black

So cut the body loose and let the brass band play
We'll dance you up to heaven in the bright of day
Grief in the morning, joy by the afternoon
We'll send you home on a second-line tune

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I still know the hymns by the second line
Still cross myself out of habit, half the time
But I slid out the back while the offering passed
And the heavy oak door clicked shut at last

Pick it up, pick it up, I'm walking out slow
Nobody waving, nobody needs to know
I loved you, steeple, I loved your bell
But I'm trading the pew for the sidewalk swell

The deacon shook my hand, didn't read my face
My mother saved my usual place
I'll send a card at Christmas, keep it light
But I won't be back next Sunday night

It wasn't a fire, it wasn't a fight
Just a slow leak of belief in the night
The horns in my chest started playing a doubt
And a quiet kid finds a quiet way out

No thunder, no banner, no big goodbye
Just me and the parking lot and the sky
Skanking down the steps to a brand new beat
God and I made peace on the empty street

Pick it up, pick it up, I'm walking out slow
Nobody waving, nobody needs to know
I loved you, steeple, I loved your bell
But I'm trading the pew for the sidewalk swell

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Forty years I stand in the same cold hall
My shadow learns the cracks along the wall
But you, señora, in your frame of gold
You never age and I am growing old

They lock the doors and the lights go down
And I am alone with your painted gown
I clap my hands soft so the echo flies
And I swear I see a flicker in your eyes

Ay, mírame, mírame, lady on the wall
You are the only one who hears me at all
The tourists pass, they never see my face
But you have held my heart in your still embrace

The restorers came with their careful light
And I feared they'd take you out of my sight
My hands were shaking as they cleaned your hair
Don't change her, no, just leave her there

When I retire and they walk me to the door
Who will guard you through the night once more?
Who will stamp his heel and clap the dark
And love a woman made of oil and spark?

Ay, mírame, mírame, lady on the wall
You are the only one who hears me at all
The tourists pass, they never see my face
But you have held my heart in your still embrace

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Mr. Okafor at the corner with the chalk and pins
Knows where every shoulder slumps and every hem begins
The deacon's Sunday suit, the bride's first gown
Every seam on the avenue runs through his town

He took my father's jacket, made it new
Let it out for me when my father's father grew
Three generations measured by his tape
The whole block walks in a finer shape

So cut me a line that the angels would wear
Stitch me a Saturday, a swing in the air
He dresses the block, from the cradle to the grave
In the cloth of every dollar that we ever saved

The brass plays low while he threads the bobbin slow
Humming to a tune from forty years ago
His fingers know more secrets than the priest
He's seen us at our worst and at our feast

When they bury him they'll bury us in style
The last suit he sewed will walk the final mile
And every man he fitted on the avenue
Will stand up straight the way he taught us to

So cut me a line that the angels would wear
Stitch me a Saturday, a swing in the air
He dresses the block, from the cradle to the grave
In the cloth of every dollar that we ever saved

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Green light, two a.m., I wasn't gonna go
But my hands took the turn that my heart used to know
Same cracked stoop, same flickering sign
Somebody's curtains where I once hung mine

Drive on, drive on, don't tap the brake
That's not your window for old time's sake
The horns kick in and I roll on by
My old apartment in the rearview sky

There's a light on the third floor, second from the left
Where we burned the toast and we made our best mess
Somebody new is laughing in my kitchen now
And the city doesn't care and it shows me how

I could circle the block like a stubborn ghost
Pour out a verse for the things I miss the most
But the trumpet says move and the offbeat agrees
There's no parking left for memories

The street's still wet from the early rain
And I'm skanking slow through the same old lane
Goodbye to the door I don't have a key
Goodbye to the kid I used to be

Drive on, drive on, don't tap the brake
That's not your window for old time's sake
The horns kick in and I roll on by
My old apartment in the rearview sky

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They offered me a check with a lot of zeros on
Write a jingle for the soda, kill the song
Put my voice in a can, ship it overseas
But my words don't bend on their hands and knees

I won't sell it, I won't sell it, no
Let the bassline carry what the money won't show
Keep the truth on the downbeat, keep it raw, keep it mine
I'd rather starve in rhythm than get rich in a line

The label man is smiling with his shiny pen
Says the underground don't pay the rent again
But every word I sold would be a word I'd lose
And I'd dance to a beat that I didn't choose

Four to the floor and my heart keeps time
The only boss I answer to lives inside the rhyme
Let 'em have the radio, let 'em have the noise
I'll keep the after-hours and the realest voice

I won't sell it, I won't sell it, no
Let the bassline carry what the money won't show
Keep the truth on the downbeat, keep it raw, keep it mine
I'd rather starve in rhythm than get rich in a line

So meet me where the speaker stacks are tall
Where the broke and the beautiful answer the call
I got nothing in the bank but a notebook full
And a heartbeat no man's money's gonna pull

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Cincuenta años, mi vida, en este salón
Donde te di mi nombre y mi corazón
La trompeta llora lo que no diré
Una última vuelta y me despediré

Tus manos en mi cintura, frágiles y fieles
Bailamos entre las sillas y los manteles
El doctor dijo poco, pero yo entendí
Que esta es la canción final para ti

Baila conmigo, amor, una vez más
Antes que la noche se lleve lo demás
Que griten los violines lo que fuimos los dos
Un último baile y luego adiós

Eras la novia con flores en el pelo
Hoy eres la luna que se va hacia el cielo
Pero en mis brazos sigues siendo aquella
La más hermosa, mi única estrella

Que toque el mariachi hasta romper el alba
Que no se note que mi pecho se acaba
Una vuelta, dos vueltas, no quiero parar
Porque parar es dejarte marchar

Baila conmigo, amor, una vez más
Antes que la noche se lleve lo demás
Que griten los violines lo que fuimos los dos
Un último baile y luego adiós

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The pool is gone to glass, the towels gone gray
We lie on the warm cement at the end of the day
Nobody's talking, the cicadas hum
We're listening hard for what's about to come

Hold the light, hold the light a little longer
Before the boxes and the leaving make us stronger
One more hour where nothing has to change
The last warm day before everything's strange

Your car is half packed in the driveway dusk
There's a smell of cut grass and a little rust
We trade our addresses we both know we'll lose
And watch the slow gold drain out of the blue

Tomorrow there's a highway, a dorm, a name they'll mispronounce
Tonight there's just the fireflies and every ounce
Of a summer that we held in our open hands
Dissolving soft as footprints in the sand

Hold the light, hold the light a little longer
Before the boxes and the leaving make us stronger
One more hour where nothing has to change
The last warm day before everything's strange

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Left my mama at the gate, couldn't look her in the eye
One suitcase, one passport, and a whole lot of sky
Plane ticket cost us everything we had
Now I'm chasing a future for my mom and my dad

Across the sea, across the sea
Leaving everything I know to be everything I'll be
Cold new city but my heart stayed home
Building something out of nothing on my own

They can't say my name right at the customs line
New language on my tongue, gotta learn it in time
Send the money back home every other week
Work two jobs, barely sleep, barely speak

Documents, paperwork, a number for a face
Learning how to belong in a borrowed place
But I dream in my own tongue when the lights go low
And I carry every road that I used to know

The ocean don't know how heavy a goodbye is
The ocean don't care whose child this is
But I crossed it anyway with my head held high
Left the ground that I love just to learn how to fly

Across the sea, across the sea
Leaving everything I know to be everything I'll be
Cold new city but my heart stayed home
Building something out of nothing on my own

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The streetlights blur like they're underwater now
I memorized the water tower somehow
Every road just loops back to the same
Gas station glow and my own dead name

Fade me out, fade me out of this town
Let the reverb swallow the whole thing down
I won't turn around, I won't slow the car
Just a wall of sound and a leaving star

The friends I had got married to the rust
The diner closed, the high school turned to dust
I loved you, little town, but you held me tight
Like a hand on my throat in the soft blue night

So drown it in the guitars, let the feedback rise
Till the whole horizon's just a wash of skies
I'm a smear of taillight on the county line
Dissolving into something that was never mine

Fade me out, fade me out of this town
Let the reverb swallow the whole thing down
I won't turn around, I won't slow the car
Just a wall of sound and a leaving star

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole life on a clean white board
The husband, the house, the future stored
A neon road they paved before I was born
But I'm cutting the wire at the edge of dawn

Drive into the violet, leave the plan behind
The future that they wrote for me was never mine to find
Engine in the midnight, heart against the dark
I'm rewriting the ending with a single spark

The ring's in the drawer, the dress on the chair
They'll wake to an empty and a vacant stair
I loved them, I did, but I can't be the shape
That fits the perfect future like a wax-sealed tape

Let the synths rise up like a city of light
A grid I never chose disappearing in the night
No map, no name, just the hum of the lane
And a girl made of static breaking free of the chain

Drive into the violet, leave the plan behind
The future that they wrote for me was never mine to find
Engine in the midnight, heart against the dark
I'm rewriting the ending with a single spark

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The screen lights blue in the dead of the night
Your name in my hand like a forbidden light
My thumb hovers over the words I'll regret
Three dots appearing — you're awake, and we're set

Oh don't send it, don't send it, the heart's a thief
It steals in the silence and it ruins belief
But the candle's near burned and the wine's near gone
And I'm typing to a ghost as the night wears on

There's a ring on my finger and a vow that I gave
There's a quiet good house and a man who is brave
But you knew me before I was anyone's wife
And you're a green hill I left in another life

The fiddle in my chest plays a wandering reel
A tune I shouldn't dance and a thing I shouldn't feel
Delete it, delete it, put the phone face-down
But the message is sent and it can't come around

Oh the loneliest road is the one that we keep
When we whisper to someone while the household's asleep
May the saints forgive me for the want in my hand
And the bridge that I'm burning to a far-off land

Oh don't send it, don't send it, the heart's a thief
It steals in the silence and it ruins belief
But the candle's near burned and the wine's near gone
And I'm typing to a ghost as the night wears on

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Gravel road and a porch light fading
Keys on the table, no note left waiting
I counted these streets like the lines on my hand
Now the county sign's just a grain of sand
Mama waved slow from the kitchen glass
Some roots you keep and some you let pass

I'm leaving, leaving, this time for good
Selling the silence I never could
The water tower spelling out my name
Won't pull me back to the same old same
I'm leaving, leaving, headlights wide
Whole horizon on the other side

First love still works the gas on Main
Waving goodbye through the rolling rain
I loved you all but I had to grow
Where the sidewalk runs further than I know

If the town asks where the boy went to
Tell it the river finally pushed through
I'm not running from, I'm running toward
A door I haven't opened before

I'm leaving, leaving, this time for good
Trading the maybe for understood
The water tower spelling out my name
Won't pull me back to the same old same

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon humming on a two a.m. street
Griddle popping to a backbeat heat
Coffee black as a vinyl groove
Everybody's hips already moving
Waitress spinning with a pot in hand
Clock on the wall don't understand

We stay open, open all night
Grease and the glow of the jukebox light
Come as you are, come as you ain't
We pour the funk and we never go faint
Open, open, til the morning crew
The diner's got a beat for you

Trucker tapping on the chrome bar rail
Lovers splitting one banana split tale
Fry cook drumming with a spatula stick
Syncopated bacon doing the trick

When the rest of the city dims its eyes
We keep the booths a paradise
No last call, no closing sign
Just hash browns and a bass line fine

We stay open, open all night
Grease and the glow of the jukebox light
Come as you are, come as you ain't
We pour the funk and we never go faint

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Tether snapped on the morning side
Watched your visor catch the planet's pride
We spun together for a hundred days
Now we're two dots in the cosmic haze
Groove of the gravity letting us go
Slow disco of the undertow

Drifting, drifting, hand to the dark
You were my orbit, you were my spark
No air to carry the words I'd say
So I funk it out the only way
Drifting, drifting, further and far
Two little moons with no home star

Radio static where your laugh used to be
Bass line lonely in the galaxy
I count your distance in a backbeat sigh
Kiss to the helmet, a satellite goodbye

If the silence swallows what we were
Let the rhythm be the thing you remember
A pocket of soul in the vacuum cold
Two astronauts and a story untold

Drifting, drifting, hand to the dark
You were my orbit, you were my spark
No air to carry the words I'd say
So I funk it out the only way

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Gun in my hand and it screams as I pull
Last strip of tape on a cardboard skull
Empty walls where the posters bled
Dust outlines of the life I led
Floor still warm from the years we burned
Every lesson the hard way learned

Seal it up! Seal it up! Last box down!
Nothing left of me in this town!
Tear the keys from my shaking fist
This is the door, this is the list
Seal it up! Walk it out the frame!
I'm never coming back the same!

Marker squeals out a name that's mine
Fragile, fragile, hold the line
Knees on the concrete, breathing hard
Whole damn history in the yard

No goodbye! No second look!
Just the weight of every page I took!
Light goes off and the echo stays
Buried under these final days!

Seal it up! Seal it up! Last box down!
Nothing left of me in this town!
Tear the keys from my shaking fist
This is the door, this is the list

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Magenta lights on a borrowed stage
Eighteen years and a trembling page
Mic too tall and my voice too thin
Neon buzzing as I drank it in
Three slow claps from a back-row friend
Felt like the world about to begin

Replay, replay the analog night
My first time under the violet light
Every wrong note glowing in chrome
That little room was the road back home
Replay, replay the synthwave glow
The kid I was, the dream I'd grow

Cassette hiss of a memory tape
Nervous hands in a strobe-lit shape
I can still smell the coffee steam
The purple haze of a starter dream

If I could reach through the static glass
Tell the kid the fear would pass
Keep the shake in your singing hand
That's how the lonely ones understand

Replay, replay the analog night
My first time under the violet light
Every wrong note glowing in chrome
That little room was the road back home

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Half an onion in the dimming light
Recipe written in her hand so tight
The pot too big for a single plate
Steam goes up like a question late
I set two forks then I put one back
A small white sound in the kitchen's crack

Cooking for one in the soft blue haze
Learning the math of the rest of my days
The oven hums like a lullaby
Salt of the broth or the salt of my eye
Cooking for one, the windows weep
A meal I'll finish, a promise I'll keep

Her apron still on the rusted hook
I follow each line of her well-worn book
Too much for me but I make it slow
Feeding the ghost that I used to know

They say the body forgets to grieve
When you give it warmth and a reason to leave
The table for two becomes a shore
Where I learn to want my life once more

Cooking for one in the soft blue haze
Learning the math of the rest of my days
The oven hums like a lullaby
Salt of the broth or the salt of my eye

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Thumb hovers over a number I know
Five years of static, five years of no
The dial tone sounds like a held-in breath
Pride is a slow and a quiet death
Then your voice, oh, it cracks the line
Same as it was when the world was mine

Mama, I'm calling, it's been too long
I was too stubborn, I was too wrong
Pick up the silence and make it sound
I'm the lost thing finally found
Mama, I'm calling, hear me through
Every old hurt, I'm bringing to you

You ask if I'm eating, like no time has passed
Love doesn't keep a grudge that lasts
I wanted to win, didn't know the cost
Was every Sunday that the both of us lost

The years don't give themselves back to us
But the line still hums with the love and the fuss
So I'll call you tomorrow and the day after that
Fill up the silence where the distance sat

Mama, I'm calling, it's been too long
I was too stubborn, I was too wrong
Pick up the silence and make it sound
I'm the lost thing finally found

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man stands where the water meets sand
Reading the moon like the back of his hand
Groove in his knees from a hundred years
Teaching the ocean to shift its gears
He taps his cane on the wet and the deep
A rhythm the rolling water keeps

Pull it back, push it slow, that's the way
The elder shows the tide to sway
Don't rush the shore, don't fight the pull
Let the bass of the deep stay full
Pull it back, push it slow, ride the groove
Even the sea's got a heart to move

Salt in his beard and a funk in his stride
The gulls all dancing to the side
He says, young water, you're loud but green
Learn the pocket between the in and between

When he's gone to the foam and the far-off blue
The waves will keep the lessons true
A syncopation in every swell
The ocean's got a story to tell

Pull it back, push it slow, that's the way
The elder shows the tide to sway
Don't rush the shore, don't fight the pull
Let the bass of the deep stay full

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock-in blue and a clock-out gray
Two paychecks that don't quite pay
Morning apron, midnight broom
Rent still creeping across the room
Coffee thin and the bills run wide
I'm always one slow step behind

Falling behind, in a soft swing time
Chasing a dollar like a closing rhyme
The city keeps the lights so high
While I dim mine, baby, by and by
Falling behind, with a tired grin
Two jobs out and the night closing in

Bus seat warm from the soul before
My shoulders carry a little more
The saxophone of the subway moan
Sings the blues of the overgrown loan

They say work hard and the dream comes near
But the dream keeps moving every year
Still I hum a tune in the after-hours cold
Some kind of rich the bank don't hold

Falling behind, in a soft swing time
Chasing a dollar like a closing rhyme
The city keeps the lights so high
While I dim mine, baby, by and by

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Suitcase light but the heart hangs low
Harbor shrinking in the morning glow
The language I know growing thin as foam
Leaving the only word for home
Gray of the water, gray of the sky
A country waving its last goodbye

Across the sea, across the long
Carrying nothing but a half-known song
The waves don't ask where I belong
Across the sea, where the maps go wrong
Across the sea, I drift, I roam
Learning to spell a brand new home

Salt on the rail and the engines hum
A slow horizon yet to come
I fold my past in a paper boat
And set it loose on the things that float

Somewhere ahead is a stranger's door
A cold new shore I'll learn to adore
The sea forgets but the soul will keep
The shape of the place that I lost in the deep

Across the sea, across the long
Carrying nothing but a half-known song
The waves don't ask where I belong
Across the sea, where the maps go wrong

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down on the Lagos street
One umbrella, two pairs of feet
You stepped under like the sky was right
Shoulder to shoulder in the silver light
We didn't talk but the drums agreed
Two strangers planting one small seed

Under the rain, under the same
We never even traded a name
Move your hips, let the water fall
One umbrella covering us all
Under the rain, sweet and slow
Two strangers with nowhere to go

Your perfume mixing with the wet warm air
My heart drumming like it knows you're there
The puddles dancing to a soft amapiano
Two umbrellas would've been one too many, oh

When the clouds break and the sun comes through
Will you walk your way and I walk too?
But for now the rain is a gift from above
A borrowed minute that feels like love

Under the rain, under the same
We never even traded a name
Move your hips, let the water fall
One umbrella covering us all

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Flat tires humming on a Kansas line
Radar bleeding red, I'm doing fine
She asked me to stay through a quiet door
But quiet's the one thing I can't store
Clouds rolling green and the air goes still
I'm gone before the window sill

I chase the storm, I can't slow down
No white picket, no sleepy town
The sky cracks open and it calls my name
Love's a porch light, but I want the flame
I chase the storm, I can't be found
There's a funnel where my heart should pound

Got a glovebox full of motel keys
A dog-eared map of the broken trees
She waters the plants and she waits for spring
While I'm out hunting the dangerous thing

Maybe one day I'll run out of sky
Park the truck and learn to lie
In a bed that doesn't move at dawn
But the thunder calls and then I'm gone

I chase the storm, I can't slow down
No white picket, no sleepy town
The sky cracks open and it calls my name
Love's a porch light, but I want the flame

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Flour on the apron, sun through the screen
Sweetest little kitchen I have ever seen
Cornbread rising and the collards low
Grandmama humming soft and slow
Close my eyes and I'm a child again
Back when love had a cinnamon scent

Oh the smell of that kitchen, take me home
Where I never had to walk alone
Butter and the gospel on the air
Heaven had an address right there
Oh the smell of that kitchen, sweet and warm
A shelter in the middle of the storm

She'd say baby come and bless this bread
Thank the Lord for the lives we've led
Grace went up from the kitchen floor
And the angels lingered at the door

She's gone on up to the higher ground
But the smell still finds me all around
A pot on the stove or a Sunday hymn
And I'm back in the warmth of her again

Oh the smell of that kitchen, take me home
Where I never had to walk alone
Butter and the gospel on the air
Heaven had an address right there

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Luces de la disco, my name on the door
One song from the deal I was waiting for
Everybody screaming like the world was mine
Manager smiling with the pen and the line
Dembow pounding in my chest so loud
King for a night on a paper crowd

La noche que casi, casi llegué
So close to the top that I felt the way
The lights, the bass, the hands in the air
Then morning came and it wasn't there
La noche que casi, almost the one
Gone like the beat when the record's done

The contract folded in a back-room chair
A bigger name walked in from somewhere
They killed the music, they cut the glow
I kept the rhythm but I lost the show

Pero yo sigo, I still got the fire
One almost-night don't kill the desire
Next time the bass gonna shake the town
I was so close, now I'm coming round

La noche que casi, casi llegué
So close to the top that I felt the way
The lights, the bass, the hands in the air
Then morning came and it wasn't there

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Last shift whistle and the gates run cold
Forty years of the steel we sold
Time card punched on the final day
Machines gone quiet, gone away
Fathers, sons on the same old floor
Now the breakbeat echoes through the door

Shut it down, shut it down, the lights go black
Whole town riding on a track with no track
Bass in the basement where the presses ran
Last roll out for the working man
Shut it down, shut it down, the sirens fade
This is the sound of a life remade

Rust on the railing where the sparks once flew
Pension paper and a thank-you too
The city pumps a faster beat
While the silence floods the empty street

But we don't break, we just rearrange
Drop the rhythm in the face of change
From the ashes of the iron and the smoke
A harder pulse in the hearts of broke folk

Shut it down, shut it down, the lights go black
Whole town riding on a track with no track
Bass in the basement where the presses ran
Last roll out for the working man

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I folded up the hymnal, left it on the pew
Wiped my fingerprints off every word I knew
The stained glass kept on coloring the floor
I slipped out through the side, not the velvet-curtain door

Mama's perfume still hangs in the second row
I counted every ceiling fan I'm gonna miss, then let them go
No lightning, no thunder, no angry sky
Just the soft click of a latch and a swallowed-down goodbye

I'm not slamming, I'm just easing out the gate
God can keep the building, I'll keep the weight
Still humming what they taught me under my breath
Walking toward a quiet I'm learning to call rest

The choir robe still smells like cedar and like sweat
I hung my old believing on a hook I won't forget
Somebody locks the doors at half past noon
I'll be three blocks gone, learning a different tune

Nobody asked me where my hands had been
Nobody noticed grace go thin
I left the offering, I kept the song
Turns out you can love a place and still not belong

I'm not slamming, I'm just easing out the gate
God can keep the building, I'll keep the weight
Still humming what they taught me under my breath
Walking toward a quiet I'm learning to call rest

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They poured the streetcar rails across his collarbone
Built a courthouse on his jaw, called it solid stone
Every morning the buses cough across his chest
And the mayor swears the rumble's just the river heading west

Oh, the city sleeps on a body it won't name
Every brick a tooth in a long forgotten game
When he turns over, gonna swallow every light
This whole town's a blanket on a giant, holdin' tight

The banker counts his money on the giant's heavy hand
Drills for oil down deep in the marrow of the land
Grandmama warned me 'fore she went to clay
Don't build your house too heavy on a thing that wants to wake

I felt the sidewalk breathing on a humid August night
A crack ran down Main Street like a long exhale of spite
The steeples leaned a half-inch, then they straightened up again
And the preacher called it settling, and the bartender called it sin

Oh, the city sleeps on a body it won't name
Every brick a tooth in a long forgotten game
When he turns over, gonna swallow every light
This whole town's a blanket on a giant, holdin' tight

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The lamp draws a yellow circle on my page
Coffee gone cold, a small bitter sea
The ceiling fan keeps a lazy, sweet time
And the streetlight hums a samba just for me

Two in the morning, the city's asleep
My pencil and I have a promise to keep
Nobody calling, nobody knows
Just the quiet and the page and the way the night goes

A moth taps the window like it wants to learn too
The refrigerator sighs in the hall
I underline a sentence I'll forget by June
But tonight it's the only soft truth on the wall

If the sun never came I think I'd be alright
Floating here easy on this little raft of light
The world can keep its noon and its crowded gold
Give me this hush and the secret it holds

Two in the morning, the city's asleep
My pencil and I have a promise to keep
Nobody calling, nobody knows
Just the quiet and the page and the way the night goes

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Levanté la mano like a flag in the storm
My voice was a candle, somebody blew warm
They passed the same idea back in a louder coat
And pinned it like a medal on a borrowed throat

Ay, escúchame, I was the first to speak
The trumpet in my chest goes silent and weak
They clap for the echo, they crown the loud man
While my words lie face-down in the sand

I sharpen my sentence on the long drive to work
Rehearse it like a prayer, every comma, every quirk
Then the table starts roaring and I'm small again
A guitar with no strings in a room full of men

But the violins are tuning low behind my teeth
There's a mariachi rising from the quiet underneath
Next time I won't wait for the gap in the noise
I'll bring the whole band, I'll bring all my voice

Ay, escúchame, I was the first to speak
The trumpet in my chest goes silent and weak
No more clapping for the echo, no more crowning the loud
I'm taking back my thunder from the cloud

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
La luna's on my screen at a quarter past two
I'm typing your name like it's something to chew
Delete it, rewrite it, my thumb's a coward and a thief
Three little dots are dancing me to grief

Ay, no debo, but the message is gone
Flew out the window before the dawn
My heart's a guitarrón, too heavy, too low
I sent it, I sent it, now I want it back, oh

There's a ring in a drawer I promised someone else
But your typing-dots ring like little church bells
The trumpets in my blood don't care what is wise
They play for the trouble that lives in your eyes

The phone glows guilty, a coal in the dark
Every buzz is a match, every word is a spark
I know where this river is dragging me to
But I'd drown a hundred times for one word from you

Ay, no debo, but the message is gone
Flew out the window before the dawn
My heart's a guitarrón, too heavy, too low
I sent it, I sent it, now I want it back, oh

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The last whistle cuts the morning like a knife
The gates wear a chain where they used to wear our life
Forty years of oil still shining on the floor
And a clock that nobody will punch anymore

Dance with me, hollow factory, one final turn
The furnace going cold, the ledgers gonna burn
Hold my hand, machine, let the dust be our dress
We waltz through the silence we can't quite confess

María hung her apron on the same rusted nail
The foreman read the letter and his voice went pale
The conveyor belt is frozen mid-embrace
Like a lover left waiting in an empty place

Who will feed the river that ran through our town?
The smokestack was a steeple, now they're tearing it down
We gave it our knuckles, our lungs, our years
And it pays us in echoes and a fistful of tears

Dance with me, hollow factory, one final turn
The furnace going cold, the ledgers gonna burn
Hold my hand, machine, let the dust be our dress
We waltz through the silence we can't quite confess

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I carried the letter to the edge of the lake
Reading it slow for an old wound's sake
The water keeps your name without a sound
The dock breathes under me, half drowned

I forgive you, into the wide gray air
No one to answer, no one's there
The ripples take it out past the reeds
A mercy that no longer has someone who needs

The sky is the color of an unsent reply
A heron stands still where the shallows lie
I set it all down on the cold flat stone
And the wind picks it up and carries it home

Maybe forgiveness was never for you
Maybe it's the rope I cut myself loose into
The fog comes in like a hand on my face
Filling your absence with a softer space

I forgive you, into the wide gray air
No one to answer, no one's there
The ripples take it out past the reeds
A mercy that no longer has someone who needs

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Open bar, neon on the tile, I ain't know your name
Cousin of the bride, you a wildcard in the game
DJ dropped a slow one, then he flipped it to the bass
Now we two strangers spinnin' in a borrowed space

Dance with me, don't gotta know me, baby just sway
Three-course dinner gettin' cold, we ain't tryna stay
Lights low, hennessy slow, your hand on my chain
We forget every face, we don't ask for a name

Grandma in the corner throwin' shade at the moon
Flower girl asleep, balloons floatin' in the room
You laugh at my two-step, I laugh at your shoes
Two people with nothin' but a night left to lose

Midnight, the cake's gone, the speeches are dead
Real world waitin' but it's quiet in my head
Won't get your number, won't text in the day
Just the song, just the floor, just the way you sway

Dance with me, don't gotta know me, baby just sway
Three-course dinner gettin' cold, we ain't tryna stay
Lights low, hennessy slow, your hand on my chain
We forget every face, we don't ask for a name

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
The band plays soft as the candles lean
You asked me to dance with your eyes, not your words
Your shoulder a coast I have never been
We drift past the cake and the white folding chairs

A stranger, a song, and a slow turning floor
I don't know your name and I don't need much more
The night smells of jasmine and somebody's wine
For three little minutes your hand fits in mine

The bride throws her flowers, we don't even look
We sway like two pages from somebody's book
The trumpet goes velvet, the lights go to gold
And a warmth I can't keep is the warmth that I hold

Tomorrow you're a face that the morning will lose
A dance with no ending, a love I won't choose
But the floor keeps us honest, the rhythm keeps us kind
Two strangers, one bossa, and nothing behind

A stranger, a song, and a slow turning floor
I don't know your name and I don't need much more
The night smells of jasmine and somebody's wine
For three little minutes your hand fits in mine

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I seal each letter in a bottle's cold green throat
And send my heart out swaying on a salt-bruised boat
Dear gray and hungry darling, dear my drowning bride
I keep the lamp lit nightly for your restless tide

The sea don't write me back, but I write her all the same
Forty years of ink and oil, she never learned my name
She takes my words and folds them in the undertow
My only love's a thing that swallows all I throw

The gulls have read my secrets off the wind-torn page
My lantern is a wedding ring I light against her rage
The rocks down there are wearing all the rings I gave
Each storm a kind of kiss, each kiss a kind of grave

They'll find me cold some winter with a pen still in my hand
A hundred unsent verses and a heart she'll never land
Bury me with the bottles, let the green glass be my bell
And tell her in the morning that I loved her well

The sea don't write me back, but I write her all the same
Forty years of ink and oil, she never learned my name
She takes my words and folds them in the undertow
My only love's a thing that swallows all I throw

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors close soft but the walls lean in
Forty strangers breathing on my skin
The map on the wall starts to swim and slide
And my heart's a fist trying to get outside

Breathe, baby, breathe, count the lights as they pass
The train's gonna stop, this ain't gonna last
Hold the cold pole, feel it real in your hand
You're a body on a train, you're allowed to just stand

A tunnel swallows all the orange and the sound
My throat's a narrow door, my pulse a hammer pound
A woman reads her book like the world is still fine
And I borrow her calm one slow breath at a time

The lights flicker once and the dark holds its breath
I rehearse all my exits, I rehearse my own death
Then the platform comes glowing like a hand reaching down
And I'm just a wet shirt and a heartbeat in town

Breathe, baby, breathe, count the lights as they pass
The train's gonna stop, this ain't gonna last
Hold the cold pole, feel it real in your hand
You're a body on a train, you're allowed to just stand

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The pool's gone still and the chlorine's gone faint
Sunlight pooling gold on the chipped porch paint
We lay on the hood till the metal turned cool
Counting the hours like a slow-melting jewel

Don't let the evening end, don't let it fold
The whole town's a photograph already going old
Your laugh through the static, your hand in the haze
The last gold hour of the last of our days

The cicadas wind down like a tape running out
Boxes in the hallway I don't talk about
We drove past the high school with the windows wide
Letting the warm wind carry what we couldn't decide

Tomorrow's a highway, a key, an empty room
A stranger's name taped up beside my own soon
But tonight is a wall of soft and rushing sound
And I press my whole life to the fading ground

Don't let the evening end, don't let it fold
The whole town's a photograph already going old
Your laugh through the static, your hand in the haze
The last gold hour of the last of our days

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The rooms all echo now, the walls wear pale square ghosts
Where the pictures used to gather like a choir of hosts
I press the tape down slow on the last cardboard seam
And the empty house is humming an old familiar hymn

Lord, I'm taping up the last of it, I'm carrying it on
Thank You for the kitchen light, thank You it ain't gone
Every scuff a testimony, every nail a name
I'm walking out with gratitude and leaving down the pain

The stairs still know the weight of every Christmas morning's run
The doorframe keeps the pencil marks of how the children come
I lay my hand against the plaster, whisper my amen
For all the ordinary glory that lived in here back then

A house is just a box we fill with mercy and with noise
The walls don't keep the love, no, that travels with the boys
So I'll fold this final flap and lift it to my chest
And trust the road in front of me is somewhere I'll be blessed

Lord, I'm taping up the last of it, I'm carrying it on
Thank You for the kitchen light, thank You it ain't gone
Every scuff a testimony, every nail a name
I'm walking out with gratitude and leaving down the pain

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Underground express and the bass in my chest
Kick drum heartbeat won't give it a rest
Lights doin' the stutter, doin' the strobe
Got a whole brass section freakin' out in my robe

Breathe on the one, baby, breathe on the two
The groove gonna catch you 'fore the panic break through
Grab that pole, let it ground you to the floor
Ride the rhythm of the rails to the next open door

Sweat on the collar, slick funk on my spine
The train hit a tunnel and it's stealin' my line
Clap-track palms and a wah-wah lung
Got a thousand little drummers on the tip of my tongue

Break it down, break it down, the dark's gettin' loud
A whole horn-blast of strangers in a hot pressed crowd
But I find me the pocket, find me the slow
And I let the platform light tell my body let go

Breathe on the one, baby, breathe on the two
The groove gonna catch you 'fore the panic break through
Grab that pole, let it ground you to the floor
Ride the rhythm of the rails to the next open door

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
I still take the long way past your mother's old street
Where we used to count fireflies and learn how to keep
A promise too heavy for two kids to hold
Now your side of the story is a tale someone told

You're the one that got away, the door I didn't run through
I had the whole green light and I stood there in the blue
Now I raise a glass to a ghost in a yellow dress
The one that got away, and the love I didn't confess

They say you got married in a chapel by the bay
I heard it like thunder on a clear blue day
I keep your old letters in a shoebox of regret
The ink's gone faint but the wanting ain't yet

If I could go back to that porch and that rain
I'd say all the words that I swallowed in vain
But time's a one-way river and it don't run uphill
So I love you in the past tense, and I guess I always will

You're the one that got away, the door I didn't run through
I had the whole green light and I stood there in the blue
Now I raise a glass to a ghost in a yellow dress
The one that got away, and the love I didn't confess

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Clock hits two, the world's asleep
Pages open, promises to keep
Eyes are burning, coffee's gone cold
Fighting for a future I was told
Every line a battle in my head
Should've been asleep in my bed

Two in the morning, I won't break
Grinding through the silence for my own sake
Nobody's watching, nobody knows
This is where the quiet warrior grows

Highlighter bleeding through the night
Screaming at the dark to make it right
Alone but I am not afraid
This is the price the dreamers paid

No applause, no crowd, no light
Just me and this relentless fight
I'll bleed the ink until I'm done
A war that's only just begun

Two in the morning, I won't break
Grinding through the silence for my own sake
Nobody's watching, nobody knows
This is where the quiet warrior grows

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
You drift like smoke across my room
A fading light, a sweet perfume
I reach for you but touch the air
You were never really there

And I fall, and I fall
Through the static of it all
You don't hear me when I call
A quiet wave against a wall

Your name dissolves upon my tongue
A song that's left forever unsung
I watch you shine for someone else
While I keep these dreams upon a shelf

Washed in colors, blurred and slow
I loved you more than you will know
Fade to white, fade to gray
You were never mine to stay

And I fall, and I fall
Through the static of it all
You don't hear me when I call
A quiet wave against a wall

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Sun going down on the old front porch
Memories burning like a paper torch
Boxes packed up in the hallway light
Tomorrow I'm chasing something out of sight
Friends on the curb where we used to ride
Holding the summer we can't keep inside

Last day of summer, let it breathe
Golden hour, never wanna leave
Soft like the wind through the open door
Nothing's ever gonna be like before

Fireflies blinking like a slow goodbye
We laugh so we don't have to cry
One more drive down the empty street
Where the past and the future finally meet

Hold it close, let it go
That's the only way we grow

Last day of summer, let it breathe
Golden hour, never wanna leave
Soft like the wind through the open door
Nothing's ever gonna be like before

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Clock is ticking on the concrete throne
King of the pavement, sitting alone
Five bucks a game and he never blinks
Drowns every challenger before he thinks
Pawns are soldiers in his endless war
He's seen ten thousand fall before

Checkmate! The blade comes down
He's the silent king of this broken town
No mercy on the sixty-four
Every move is a march to war

Tourists laugh until the trap is set
He collects the pieces and he collects the debt
Knights are screaming, bishops bleed
He feeds on every reckless greed

He was a master in another life
Now he sharpens kings on the edge of a knife
The board is an altar, the park is a grave
No prisoners taken, no soul to save

Checkmate! The blade comes down
He's the silent king of this broken town
No mercy on the sixty-four
Every move is a march to war

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Gates locked up, the lights went dim
Forty years and they cut him thin
Punch card faded, the line went still
No more whistle on the hill
My daddy bled for that machine
Now it's rust and a broken dream

Factory closing, lights go down
They took the heart right out the town
But we still standing, we still here
Louder than their bottom line of fear

She told the kids it'll be alright
Selling what she can to keep the lights
Main Street ghosting, windows boarded tight
Still we hustle, still we fight

They balance books on broken backs
Leave a town behind the cracks
But you can't close a people down
We are the soul of this town

Factory closing, lights go down
They took the heart right out the town
But we still standing, we still here
Louder than their bottom line of fear

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
One plate set upon the table now
Her recipe card, I don't know how
Measuring spoons that she once held
A quiet kitchen where her laughter swelled

And I learn, slow and small
To make a meal for none at all
Steam rising like a prayer
For the empty kitchen chair

Too much salt and the burner's low
Things she knew that I don't know
I hum the songs she used to sing
While the kettle starts to ring

The portions never come out right
Half a table, half a light
But I'll set the plate and I'll sit and stay
And taste a little more each day

And I learn, slow and small
To make a meal for none at all
Steam rising like a prayer
For the empty kitchen chair

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Lines like rivers on her skin
Every story held within
Flour and gold and a wedding ring
Hands that taught my heart to sing

Grandmother's hands, hold me now
Moving slow, they show me how
Raise them up into the light
Dancing with her through the night

She braided mornings into mine
Wove the years like grapes to wine
Soft and strong and never still
Working love against the will

Feel the rhythm, feel the past
Some things are made to last
Close your eyes and understand
The whole world's in her hand

Grandmother's hands, hold me now
Moving slow, they show me how
Raise them up into the light
Dancing with her through the night

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Dialed the number I still know by heart
Fingers shaking from the very start
Three years gone and the pride got loud
Now I'm standing humble, no more proud
The ringing stops and I hear her breathe
All the words I couldn't leave

Hey mama, it's me, it's me
I'm sorry that I let it be
Pick it up and pick up the phone
I don't wanna be alone

She said baby, I never closed the door
That's what a mother's heart is for
All the silence washed away
In the breaking of the day

Horns up high, let 'em blow
For every word I didn't know
For the love that waited there
Through the silence and the air

Hey mama, it's me, it's me
I'm sorry that I let it be
Pick it up and pick up the phone
I'm finally coming home

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Smoke and gold in the evening haze
Closing out these endless days
A saxophone on the avenue
Playing low like a last adieu

One more night, one more song
Before the summer's gone
Sip it slow, let it sway
Tomorrow takes it all away

Suitcase leaning by the door
Gonna miss this hardwood floor
The city hums a soft refrain
For everything that won't remain

So pour the moonlight in a glass
Raise a toast to the days that pass
We were young and the night was wide
And the summer was on our side

One more night, one more song
Before the summer's gone
Sip it slow, let it sway
Tomorrow takes it all away

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down on a Tuesday street
Two umbrellas turned to one retreat
She was a stranger, I was the same
Neither one of us knew the other's name

One umbrella, two hearts dry
Under the gray of a city sky
Funny how the rain can bring
A stranger and a brand new thing

We walked four blocks that felt like years
Laughing soft through the falling tears
She smiled and the rain felt warm
Two strangers sheltered from the storm

Sometimes love don't make a sound
It's just the rain on solid ground
And a stranger's hand you didn't plan
The day the whole thing began

One umbrella, two hearts dry
Under the gray of a city sky
Funny how the rain can bring
A stranger and a brand new thing

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Every night I light the flame
Whisper soft the ocean's name
Fold a letter, send it down
To the waves that wear no crown

Oh, my darling sea
Won't you write back to me
I'll keep the lantern burning bright
For you across the night

Salt and ink upon my hands
Love that water understands
She takes my words and pulls them deep
A promise that the tides will keep

They say I'm lonely on this stone
But I have never been alone
For the ocean knows my heart
Even when we're worlds apart

Oh, my darling sea
Won't you write back to me
I'll keep the lantern burning bright
For you across the night

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every night when the lights go low
Just me and her in the soft halo
A painted girl with a frozen smile
Keeps me company all the while
I walk the halls, I guard the door
But my heart is on that painted shore

Dame un beso from the frame
Whisper soft my lonely name
You and me until the dawn
Mi amor, you're never gone

They hang you up for the world to see
But at midnight you belong to me
Oil and canvas, eyes so true
All my secrets I tell to you

Morning comes and they fill the room
Never knowing of my secret bloom
But the night returns and the lights go down
And I'm dancing with you in this empty town

Dame un beso from the frame
Whisper soft my lonely name
You and me until the dawn
Mi amor, you're never gone

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Under the lights I sang my heart
So close to playing my biggest part
The crowd leaned in, the moment near
Then it slipped away, mi vida, here

Ay, the night I almost flew
So close to all my dreams come true
The trumpets cried, the moment passed
A glory far too bright to last

One more song, one more chance
One more turn of the fading dance
They called another name, not mine
And I drank the bitter wine

But a man who almost touched the sky
Still remembers how to fly
So I raise my voice and I sing again
For the night I almost, and the dream within

Ay, the night I almost flew
So close to all my dreams come true
The trumpets cried, the moment passed
A glory far too bright to last

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Forty years on this old wood floor
One more dance and then no more
The record's spinning low and slow
We both know it's time to go

So dance with me one final time
Your hand in mine, this heart of mine
The music's fading, the lights grow dim
One last waltz on a quiet whim

We fought and laughed and raised them all
Now it's just the two of us and the wall
Your eyes still shine like that first night
Under that same kitchen light

The needle lifts, the song is through
But I'd live it all again with you
Hold me close, don't let it end
My lover and my oldest friend

So dance with me one final time
Your hand in mine, this heart of mine
The music's fading, the lights grow dim
One last waltz on a quiet whim

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Three gray dots and then they're gone
A screen lit up at the break of dawn
Five cold words, that's all it took
To tear the pages from our book
No goodbye, no face, no sound
Just a phone face down on the ground

Delete me! Erase the years
A text message drowning out the tears
You couldn't even say it to my face
Just a notification taking your place

I read it once, I read it ten
Waiting for you to type again
But the silence screamed and the signal died
And I broke apart inside

Is this all we ever were?
A buzz, a blur, a digital blur
You ended us in black and white
No courage left to even fight

Delete me! Erase the years
A text message drowning out the tears
You couldn't even say it to my face
Just a notification taking your place

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We drove until the asphalt turned to frost
You killed the engine just to hear the cold
A boy who swore he'd never come back home
Now watching green ignite the open road

The radio is static, drowned in haze
Your breath a ghost that fogs the windshield glass
The sky cracks open like a healing wound
And everything we lost begins to pass

Oh the lights, the lights, they're bleeding through the dark
Violet rivers spilling where we are
Hold my hand, I'm dissolving in the glow
We're younger than the silence, don't let go

You said the dead come back as colored air
Maybe that's your mother in the flare
We never learned to say the things we meant
So we just watched the heavens come apart

Oh the lights, the lights, they're bleeding through the dark
Violet rivers spilling where we are
Hold my hand, I'm dissolving in the glow
We're younger than the silence, don't let go

Drown the guitars in a wall of white
Let the reverb carry us all night
If the morning takes this, let it take it slow
We were nothing, then we were the glow

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
You found me reading paperbacks in shade
A stranger with a smile like lemonade
We traded names beside the rolling foam
And somehow neither one of us went home

The afternoons were honey, slow and gold
We danced until the bonfire turned to coal
You taught me how to lose the way I count
The days became a sweet uncertain doubt

Oh, soft tide, take it easy on my heart
You knew from the beginning we'd depart
Still I'd trade a hundred winters, every one
For a single salty kiss beneath the sun

The season folds its towels and goes inside
Your suitcase clicks, the taxi's gonna ride
We promised letters neither one would send
A perfect little summer, perfect end

Oh, soft tide, take it easy on my heart
You knew from the beginning we'd depart
Still I'd trade a hundred winters, every one
For a single salty kiss beneath the sun

No regrets, querida, only sand
That slips like gold dust through an open hand
Somewhere there's an August where we stayed
And nothing, nothing, ever had to fade

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man on the jetty at the break of grey
Knows the moon by name, knows the way it sways
He whispers to the water, low and wise
Watch the rhythm rising, child, open up your eyes

Feel it pull, feel it pull, let the ocean move you
Every wave's a teacher and it's coming through you
In and out, in and out, that's the only law
The elder taught the tide and now the tide is yours

He counts the years in seasons of the salt
Forgives the storm, he never lays the fault
Hands like driftwood, eyes like distant ships
He hums the tempo from his cracking lips

Feel it pull, feel it pull, let the ocean move you
Every wave's a teacher and it's coming through you
In and out, in and out, that's the only law
The elder taught the tide and now the tide is yours

When I'm gone, he said, you keep the beat
The sea remembers everyone it meets
Drop the bass like water on the stone
You're never gonna dance this floor alone

Feel it pull, feel it pull, let the ocean move you
Every wave's a teacher and it's coming through you
In and out, in and out, that's the only law
The elder taught the tide and now the tide is yours

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
The lantern swung above a crooked deal
Four men and whiskey, one of them with steel
I drew an ace I never should have shown
Now there's a debt that I can't pay alone

He bet his daddy's watch, his wedding ring
He bet the kind of things you shouldn't bring
And when the river card came turning red
The table went as quiet as the dead

Lay them down, lay them down, Lord, the game's gone wrong
There's blood between the diamonds where it don't belong
I won the hand but lost my mortal soul
The devil deals in Deadwood and he always takes his toll

He reached beneath the felt, I heard the click
The candle guttered, smoke was burning thick
One shot to settle what the cards had sown
Now I ride alone, I ride alone

Lay them down, lay them down, Lord, the game's gone wrong
There's blood between the diamonds where it don't belong
I won the hand but lost my mortal soul
The devil deals in Deadwood and he always takes his toll
