Bury me beneath the saloon floor
Where the losers go and don't come back no more
Tell my mother that I prayed, I swear
But God don't play at tables that ain't fair

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Ooh, well-a, down on Slumber Street
Where the cobbles hum beneath your feet (sha-la-la)
They built our little town up high
On a giant's chest beneath the sky

He breathes and all the lampposts sway
We whisper so he sleeps till day (doo-wop, doo-wop)
My baby and I, we steal a kiss
On a chest that rises just like this

Sleep on, sleep on, you gentle giant of mine
Keep on dreaming, keep us safe one more time
Don't you wake, don't you wake, hold the world in your hands
We're just lovers on a giant in the sand (sha-la-la-la)

They say one morning he'll arise
And shake the steeples from the skies (bom-bom)
But till that thunder rolls along
I'll hold you close and sing this song

Sleep on, sleep on, you gentle giant of mine
Keep on dreaming, keep us safe one more time
Don't you wake, don't you wake, hold the world in your hands
We're just lovers on a giant in the sand (sha-la-la-la)

And if the ground begins to quake
My darling, for your only sake (ooh-ooh)
I'll sing the lullaby my grandma knew
To keep him dreaming, keep me dreaming too

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
I saw your name light up my phone last night
Five years gone but it still don't feel right
We were Lagos summers, dancefloor and rain
Then I let you walk, oh, what a shame

Na my fault, I no go lie
I watched my baby say goodbye

You be the one that got away, eh
The one I think about every day, eh
I for marry you, I for stay, oh
But I let you slip, now I dey pray, eh
The one that got away, the one that got away

They tell me say you fine somebody new
He dey treat you right, the way I never do
I dey smile for camera, dey die inside
Love wey I throw away, now I no fit hide

You be the one that got away, eh
The one I think about every day, eh
I for marry you, I for stay, oh
But I let you slip, now I dey pray, eh
The one that got away, the one that got away

If I see you for road, I go bow my head
Remember all the sweet things wey we said
Go well, my love, go shine, go grow
You be the lesson wey I learn too slow

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
There's a neon Jesus flickering on the glass
A waitress pouring coffee for the damned that pass
The clock above the counter never moves at all
Just the rain against the window and the lonesome call

A trucker with a secret, a widow in black
A boy who left for somewhere and ain't coming back
We gather at the booths like moths around a flame
Nobody asks your story and nobody asks your name

Keep the lights on, keep the lights on through the dark
For the broken and the haunted with a question in their heart
The coffee's always bitter and the pie is always cold
But the door is never locked against the lost and the old

The cook's got hands like scripture, scarred and worn
He's been frying eggs for sinners since the day that he was born
He says the night don't end here, it just learns to wait
For the ones who come at three a.m. to bargain with their fate

Keep the lights on, keep the lights on through the dark
For the broken and the haunted with a question in their heart
The coffee's always bitter and the pie is always cold
But the door is never locked against the lost and the old

Maybe this is purgatory, maybe this is grace
A halfway house of headlights and a half-forgotten face
When the sun comes up, we vanish like the steam
Just ghosts who stopped for supper in somebody else's dream

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The door is closed but neither of us left
The air still holds the shape of what was said
Your back is turned, the streetlight on the wall
And I can hear my heartbeat, that is all

The glass we didn't break sits on the shelf
I'm learning how to swallow back myself
The silence isn't empty, it's too full
Of every word we sharpened into wool

And I would cross this room if I knew how
I would unmake the storm we're standing in right now
Let the quiet speak the sorry that we can't
Let it grow, let it grow, let it grow into a hand

(instrumental swell, the guitars rising slow)
Here is where the cymbals start to break
Here is where the whole thing learns to ache
No more words, just the long ascending light
The sound of two people trying to make it right

And I would cross this room if I knew how
I would unmake the storm we're standing in right now
Then your fingers find my fingers in the dark
And the silence finally answers, here we are
Here we are
Here we are

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Well we loaded up the crates at half past nine
The captain said the manifest was fine
But the rats jumped ship before we left the bay
And the parrot wouldn't say a word all day

There's a box down in the hold that no one opens
Marked with symbols and a sailor's broken token
The compass spins, the lanterns burn too blue
And the cook swears that the soup is staring too

So swing it, boys, swing it while the swinging's good
We're hauling something wicked in the rotten wood
The sea don't want us, neither does the shore
We got a cursed little cargo and a week or more!

The first mate vanished Tuesday in the fog
Left his boots, his pipe, and one strange log
It said the freight is freedom, freight is doom
Then the ink just up and crawled across the room

So swing it, boys, swing it while the swinging's good
We're hauling something wicked in the rotten wood
The sea don't want us, neither does the shore
We got a cursed little cargo and a week or more!

Now the brass section's playing for the drowned
The whole horn line went sailing, never found
But a band that's doomed is a band that's free
So we'll toot our final chorus out to sea!

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Every night I climb the spiral stair
Light the lamp and whisper to the air
My only lover na the rolling blue
I dey write am letters, every one for you

Dear ocean, dear my darling wave
Na your music keep my soul from grave

Oh sea, oh sea, carry my love away, eh
Every letter that I throw, make e float for you today, eh
I no get nobody but the tide and the moon
So I sing my heart for water, hope you hear my tune, eh

The village say the keeper don go mad
Talking to the foam like e be his lad
But when the storm dey roar and ships dey cry
Na my little light go tell dem no go die

Oh sea, oh sea, carry my love away, eh
Every letter that I throw, make e float for you today, eh
I no get nobody but the tide and the moon
So I sing my heart for water, hope you hear my tune, eh

When I die, no bury me for ground
Throw my body where the waves dey pound
So finally my love and I go meet
And dance forever for the ocean beat

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Friday night, basement, cheap warm beer
Four of us dealing, nothing to fear
Then Danny bet his rent, then bet his car
We should've stopped him, we went too far

Kings on the table, screaming in the dark
Somebody flipped it, lit a spark
Fists in the doorway, glass on the floor
This ain't a game anymore!

GO ALL IN! Burn the whole thing down!
We bet our friendship, lost the crown!
No more chips, no more cards, no more rules
A basement full of broken fools!
GO ALL IN! There's nothing left to fold!
We gambled everything we hold!

Monday morning, Danny's gone
Left a note, the lights still on
Won the pot but lost his name
That's the price of a stupid game

GO ALL IN! Burn the whole thing down!
We bet our friendship, lost the crown!
No more chips, no more cards, no more rules
A basement full of broken fools!

Deal me out, deal me out, I'm done
Nobody wins when the table's a gun
Tear the deck up, throw it in the rain
Swear I'll never play again — (we always play again)

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning, I don't set the alarm
Grew up in those pews, folded hands, the psalm
Mama still believes, I let her think I do
But the pamphlets and the answers, they don't fit me, they don't move

Slip out the side door, no drama, no fight
Just a kid and his questions in the soft morning light
I'm not angry, I'm not bitter, I just grew a different way
Say a quiet little thank-you, and I'm gone before they pray

Learned my kindness in that basement, learned to sing
Learned the names of every saint and what the bells would bring
Keep the good and leave the guilt up on the shelf
God, if you're out there, I'ma find you for myself

Slip out the side door, no drama, no fight
Just a kid and his questions in the soft morning light
I'm not angry, I'm not bitter, I just grew a different way
Say a quiet little thank-you, and I'm gone before they pray

(vinyl crackle, piano loop, rain on the glass)
Maybe faith is just a building I outgrew
Maybe spirit's in the music when I'm passing through
Last look at the steeple in the rearview shine
Leave the door unlatched behind me, the morning's mine

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You stepped on my shoes the day we met
I swore I'd make you pay that debt
Now the judge says partners, side by side
Two old enemies gotta glide

You roll your eyes, I crack my neck
Daring me to make a wreck
But the bassline drops and oh, oh no
My traitor hips just gotta go

We hate it, but we move (we move!)
Locked in a hot little groove (the groove!)
Can't stand your face but baby can't deny
We're the baddest thing beneath the disco sky
Forced together, funky as sin
The more we fight, the more we win!

Spin me out and pull me back
You got rhythm, I'll give you that
The crowd is screaming, can't you see
The two worst rivals dance the best, it's me and thee

We hate it, but we move (we move!)
Locked in a hot little groove (the groove!)
Can't stand your face but baby can't deny
We're the baddest thing beneath the disco sky

Maybe hate's just love that lost the beat
Maybe trouble's sweeter when it's neat
So dip me low and call my bluff
Two rivals dancin' — that's enough!

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
My father picked a husband, picked a date (sha-boom)
Picked a quiet life behind a garden gate
But my heart's a radio that won't tune down
So I'm packing up my suitcase, leaving town

They laid my future out in silver spoons (ooh-wah)
A wedding under somebody else's moon
But I never said a yes that felt like mine
So I'm running for the highway and the open sign

Goodbye, goodbye, to the life they planned for me (so long!)
I'm gonna find a door and find a key
My own two feet, my own true heart, my name
I'd rather lose it all than play their game (sha-la-la)

Mama, don't you cry into your tea (don't cry)
The girl you raised was always meant to be free
I'll send a postcard from a town with neon lights
Where a girl can choose her wrong and choose her rights

Goodbye, goodbye, to the life they planned for me (so long!)
I'm gonna find a door and find a key
My own two feet, my own true heart, my name
I'd rather lose it all than play their game (sha-la-la)

The bus is pulling out at half past four (bom-bom-bom)
I never felt so scared and free before
Whatever's coming, baby, it's all mine
I traded their tomorrow for a borrowed dime

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
Chained at the wrist by a tyrant's decree
You and me, sworn enemies
The court commands a deadly waltz
One wrong step and the blade exalts

Your eyes are fire, mine are stone
We move as one but stand alone
The drums of war become the beat
A battlefield beneath our feet

DANCE! Though we despise the hand we're dealt!
DANCE! Through the rage and fury felt!
Two blades spinning in the dark
Every step a screaming spark
We will not break, we will not fall
We'll dance in defiance of them all!

The crowd expects us both to bleed
To turn our hate to what they need
But something shifts inside the storm
Two rivals find a single form

DANCE! Though we despise the hand we're dealt!
DANCE! Through the rage and fury felt!
Two blades spinning in the dark
Every step a screaming spark
We will not break, we will not fall
We'll dance in defiance of them all!

Then we turn, as one, upon the throne
The puppet strings are overthrown
The dance they forced becomes their end
My enemy is now my friend
RISE! The waltz becomes a war!
We'll dance upon their marble floor!

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Used to lift the whole world on his shoulder blade
Now his coffee shakes a little when the morning's gray
Hands that fixed my bicycle and patched the screen
Moving slow as honey, half the man I seen

Oh, but the groove still hums in the old man's bones
Tap his foot on the porch to a radio tone
Growing old, growing old, but the funk holds on
Daddy keep on shining 'til the daylight's gone

Told me work the downbeat, let the silence ride
Gave me all his rhythm, kept the worry inside
Now I count his breathing like a metronome
Wish that I could carry him the whole way home

Oh, but the groove still hums in the old man's bones
Tap his foot on the porch to a radio tone
Growing old, growing old, but the funk holds on
Daddy keep on shining 'til the daylight's gone

Every wrinkle is a measure, every gray a note
Everything he gave me is the song I wrote
Slow it down, slow it down, let the bassline stay
I'll be your two hands when they fade away

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
A folding chair, a borrowed light
My hands a pair of frightened birds
The microphone breathed back at me
I gave away my unsure words

And the room dissolves to amber now
A hush that holds the younger me
I'd whisper if I could reach back
You're braver than you'll ever see

Six strangers and a flickering bulb
A voice too small to fill the air
But something opened, something stayed
A door I never knew was there

The years go soft, the edges blur
The fear, the floor, the trembling page
Still humming somewhere far below
That first quiet step onto the stage

And the room dissolves to amber now
A hush that holds the younger me
I'd whisper if I could reach back
You're braver than you'll ever see

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Her hands were maps of every winter passed
Knuckles like the roots of an old oak tree
She wrung the necks of chickens, sewed my shirts
And folded both in prayer on bended knee

Those hands, those hands, dark rivers in the skin
They buried more than seeds out in that ground
Those hands that held me, those hands that let me go
Now folded in a box beneath the mound

She kept a pistol underneath the flour
And scripture underneath the kitchen knife
She'd bless you and she'd warn you in one breath
The gentle and the iron of her life

Those hands, those hands, dark rivers in the skin
They buried more than seeds out in that ground
Those hands that held me, those hands that let me go
Now folded in a box beneath the mound

When the crows come walking 'cross the field
And the lamp goes low against the pane
I swear I feel her fingers in my hair
A cold and tender weight against the rain

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
One more turn of the engine, one more turn of the key
The road runs down to the harbor, the harbor runs to the sea
I've outran every copper from Galway to the Tyne
But tonight the fog is rolling and the job is finally mine

Drive, drive, the last of all my driving
Moonlight on the motorway, the boys are still alive
One clean run to the morning and I'll lay these wheels to rest
Sing me down the long road home, I've given it my best

The whistle pierced the silence, the alarm bells start to ring
Three shadows in the mirror and the money in the spring
I feathered her through the village where the church bells used to chime
O Mary, watch the crossroads, just this one last time

There's a cottage by the water that I bought with stolen gold
A fire and a fiddle for a man too tired and old
If the saints are feeling generous and the petrol holds out true
I'll park her in the heather and I'm done with all of you

Drive, drive, the last of all my driving
Moonlight on the motorway, the boys are still alive
One clean run to the morning and I'll lay these wheels to rest
Sing me down the long road home, I've given it my best

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the back end with a hundred proof of fire
Back roads in the black night, dodging every wire
Deputy was waiting where the gravel meets the pine
Now the jars are shattered and the woods are soaked in shine

RUN! Headlights screaming through the trees!
RUN! On my hands and bloody knees!
The whole thing's burning, the still is blown
I'm a dead man crawling, I'm a dead man alone

Flipped her in the ditch where the copperheads all sleep
My cousin's in the river and the water's running deep
Sold us out for nothing, took the marked man's pay
I swear on this whiskey I'll see his judgment day

NO MORE running, no more hiding in the dark!
They lit the whole hollow up, every single spark!
Grandaddy's recipe is ashes in the creek
And I'm choking on the smoke and I can't even speak

RUN! Headlights screaming through the trees!
RUN! On my hands and bloody knees!
The whole thing's burning, the still is blown
I'm a dead man crawling, I'm a dead man alone

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
We parked the car where the cell signal dies
Golden hour painting both your eyes
A cooler full of soda going warm
Last little calm before the storm

One more day, one more sun going down
One more drive through our sleepy town
Tomorrow we're gone but tonight we're seventeen
The best day we never thought would mean a thing

You wrote your number on my hand again
Like we were nine and didn't know the end
The boxes packed, the rooms gone bare
But right now, none of that is there

One more day, one more sun going down
One more drive through our sleepy town
Tomorrow we're gone but tonight we're seventeen
The best day we never thought would mean a thing

We'll grow up, we'll grow apart, I know
Different cities, different snow
But keep this one, this last warm light
Fold it up and hold it tight

One more day, one more sun going down
One more drive through our sleepy town
Tomorrow we're gone but tonight we're seventeen
The best day we never thought would mean a thing

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Smoke hung low like a velvet ceiling
Five of us and the radio low
I was up, I was feeling holy
Didn't know when to let it go

One more hand, baby, one more hand
I bet the rent and I bet the band
Should've folded when my heart said stop
Now I'm watching all my whole world drop

His eyes were calm like a river freezing
Mine were burning with the want to win
Laid my daddy's watch on the table
That's the moment the cold came in

It was never 'bout the money, no
It was something I was trying to prove
Now the chairs are empty, the lights come up
And there's nothing left in me to lose

One more hand, baby, one more hand
I bet the rent and I bet the band
Should've folded when my heart said stop
Now I'm watching all my whole world drop

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the diner when the sky's still black
Clock out, grab the bus, never looking back
Apron in the laundry, name tag on the floor
Second shift is calling, gotta give a little more

I'm running, I'm running, but the ground keeps moving
Two paychecks and I'm still losing
Tell me how the math don't add
Working all my hours and still falling behind

Rent went up again, the milk did too
Counting out the quarters like I always do
Mama says I'm strong, says I'll be alright
But strong gets tired around the middle of the night

I'm running, I'm running, but the ground keeps moving
Two paychecks and I'm still losing
Tell me how the math don't add
Working all my hours and still falling behind

Maybe one day I'll get a little ahead
A little sleep, a little room to breathe instead
Till then I tie my shoes and find the door
And give this tired world a little more

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down like the sky was breaking
Concrete flooding, the whole street shaking
You stepped under, didn't say a word
Closest thing to grace I ever heard

ONE umbrella, two strangers in the storm!
Nobody's saving us but we're staying warm!
Don't know your name and I don't need to
For one black minute the world came through!

Shoulders touching, soaked to the seam
Lightning split the city like a scream
You could've run, you could've left
Instead you stood and shared your breath

This is it, this is all we are
Two dripping souls beneath one star
The light turns green, you walk away
But I'll carry this until my dying day!

ONE umbrella, two strangers in the storm!
Nobody's saving us but we're staying warm!
Don't know your name and I don't need to
For one black minute the world came through!

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Well my grandma's hands could really swing a pan
Flippin' flapjacks faster than a frying man
Snap, snap, snappin' to the kitchen band
Nobody cooked a Sunday like my grandma can

Those hands, those hands, oh they made the whole house sway
Dealin' cards and dishin' love at the break of day
Clap along, clap along to the morning sun
Grandma's hands kept swingin' till the work was done

She'd waltz me 'round the linoleum floor
Apron flyin', laughin' for an hour more
Quick on the buttons, quick on the keys
Playin' boogie-woogie like a summer breeze

Now the radio's quiet and the kitchen's still
But I hear that rhythm and I always will
So I snap my fingers and I tip my hat
To the swingin'-est hands the world ever sat

Those hands, those hands, oh they made the whole house sway
Dealin' cards and dishin' love at the break of day
Clap along, clap along to the morning sun
Grandma's hands kept swingin' till the work was done

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
The striped pole turning slow since 1962
A bell above the door that says he's still here for you
Every skull beneath his blade a story he can tell
The boy who left, the man who stayed, the ones who never well

And the scissors keep on singing through the years
He's cut the joy, he's cut the grief, he's cut away the fears
Every name in every chair a thread he gently holds
The quiet keeper of the town as the whole thing grows old

He knew your father's father by the cowlick in his hair
He held the silence when the news came, didn't say but he was there
A mirror and a leather strap, a hum beneath his breath
He's seen the births, he's swept the floor, he's trimmed them up for death

Slow build, the clippers rising like a tide
The whole town passing through one room and he's the one inside
Louder now, the chorus of a hundred quiet years
The barber and the burden of the things he overhears

And the scissors keep on singing through the years
He's cut the joy, he's cut the grief, he's cut away the fears
Every name in every chair a thread he gently holds
The quiet keeper of the town as the whole thing grows old

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody picked us, no, nobody believed
Last in the table, but we never grieved
One more whistle, one more chance to rise
Look at the fire burning in our eyes

We gonna play, play, play till the final bell
Underdog story that we live to tell
Don't count us out when the pressure come down
Little team gonna shake the whole town

Mud on the jersey, sweat on the brow
Captain say believe and we believe it now
Ninety long minutes and the score still tied
Everything we got, we leave it all inside

Feel the rhythm in the people on the stand
Everybody clapping, everybody hand in hand
Win or lose we walking tall tonight
We came together and we ran the good fight

We gonna play, play, play till the final bell
Underdog story that we live to tell
Don't count us out when the pressure come down
Little team gonna shake the whole town

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Soft light through the colored glass
Falls on a pew where I used to pray
I gather my coat, I touch the wood
And quietly I slip away

No thunder, no goodbye, just the door
Closing gentle on a younger me
The hymns will go on without my voice
And I'll go on, finally free

My mother's hands once held me here
The candle wax, the Sunday shoes
I carry it kindly, I carry it light
A faith I keep but choose to lose

The bell still rings across the square
A sound I'll love but never need
The morning's warm, the river's near
And somewhere quiet I plant my seed

No thunder, no goodbye, just the door
Closing gentle on a younger me
The hymns will go on without my voice
And I'll go on, finally free

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I light the lamp at sundown and I climb the winding stair
I write you in the lantern glow with salt up in my hair
My darling green and restless, you're the only love I've known
Forty years I've kept this light and forty years alone

Oh sea, oh sea, won't you read what I have penned
Every wave's a paragraph and every tide a friend
I seal it in a bottle and I cast it from the stone
My letters to the water and the water is my home

The gulls all know my handwriting, the rocks all know my name
The ships pass in the morning and they never stay the same
But you are always with me, churning blue against the night
I'll love you in the fury and I'll guide you with my light

When at last my lamp goes dark and I can climb no more
Lay me where the breakers run and wash me from the shore
Fold my letters in the foam and carry me out deep
My darling, faithful ocean, take the keeper home to sleep

Oh sea, oh sea, won't you read what I have penned
Every wave's a paragraph and every tide a friend
I seal it in a bottle and I cast it from the stone
My letters to the water and the water is my home

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon humming red at three a.m.
Coffee black as midnight in the cup
The cook's been here since Lord knows when
And the door don't ever lock up

Come on in, baby, the light's still on
We don't close, no, not till the lonely's gone
A booth, a song, a warm hello
The diner that stays open when there's nowhere else to go

The trucker and the dreamer and the heart that just got broke
The waitress knows your order and she knows the things unspoke
She pours a little kindness with the sugar and the cream
A little church of strangers underneath that buzzing beam

When the whole wide world has turned its back
And the streets are cold and gray
There's a window glowing down the block
Gonna chase your blues away

Come on in, baby, the light's still on
We don't close, no, not till the lonely's gone
A booth, a song, a warm hello
The diner that stays open when there's nowhere else to go

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Radar paints a spiral in electric tangerine
I'm doing ninety on a county road nobody's ever seen
Got a duffel full of mornings I forgot to leave behind
And a heartbeat like a siren in the back roads of my mind

I chase the funnel down, down, down
I never learned to put my roots in any sleepy town
You want a porch, a ring, a slowing-down
But baby I'm the kind of weather that won't stick around

Mirrorball of lightning over wheatfields turning blue
Every wall cloud is a lover that I rush to and then through
Motels know me by my restless and the dust upon my shoes
I fall in love at sixty miles, then floor it past the news

I chase the funnel down, down, down
I never learned to put my roots in any sleepy town
You want a porch, a ring, a slowing-down
But baby I'm the kind of weather that won't stick around

They say a man can't outrun what he is
But the sky keeps writing love letters in the wind
So I'll dance until the pressure drops to nothing
And let the open prairie take me in again

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete folded over the meadow like a hand
Leaving one green witness rooted in the sand
A crack in the parking lot, a circle of brown earth
Holding up a memory of what the soil was worth

Stay, stay, oldest breathing thing
Stay through the silence of the hum the wires sing
The light still finds your leaves at the closing of the day
Stay, last green answer, in the gray

Pigeons learn your branches like a half-remembered hymn
Shadows pool around you where the streetlamps grow dim
No one planted you on purpose, no one watered you with care
Yet here you keep on offering your slow and patient air

If they ever come to take you down
The whole forgotten forest in your rings will make a sound
A long low note beneath the town
That says we were here, we were here, we were here

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night I kick the ceiling off my skull
Gravity's a landlord and I'm done paying rent in full
Arms out like a busted antenna catching God
While the alarm clock screams that flying's just a fraud

I fly! I fly!
Rip the seatbelt off the sky!
Wake me up and I'll deny
That down here I even try
I fly! I fly!

Rooftops shrinking into postage stamps of light
My lungs are full of altitude, my fists are full of night
The city's just a circuit board I solder with my feet
Then morning hits the pavement and I'm crawling down the street

I fly! I fly!
Rip the seatbelt off the sky!
Wake me up and I'll deny
That down here I even try
I fly! I fly!

Doctors call it nothing, just a misfire in the dark
But I've got dirt-proof feathers and a runway in my heart
So bury me with windows open wide
So the dream can find a body it can ride

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Forty years of footsteps worn into this floor
Your hand still knows the hollow of my back, like before
The guitar bleeds an old wound, slow and unashamed
And we move like two candles that forgot they shared a flame

Baila, baila, one more time
Before the silence learns to keep the rhythm of goodbye
Your eyes are dark Sevilla, mine are spent
But we'll spend this final compás like it's all we ever meant

The shawl you wore at twenty hangs upon the chair
The years took down the curtains but they left us standing there
Each heel-strike is a sentence we could never say out loud
A proud and trembling thunder underneath a quiet crowd

Don't tell me when the music has to end
Let the last chord shiver in the lamplight, my old friend
One turn, one breath, one final lifted hand
And whatever broke between us, let it sleep in this last stand

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Full moon hung like a hangman over Caldwell County pine
Jars of white lightning sweating in the trunk of '49
I told the Lord I'd quit it once the mortgage came around
But the devil drives a faster car on this old river road

The blue lights bloomed like roses on a grave
And the bridge came up too sudden for a sinner to be saved
Now the corn squeezings burn upon the water like a curse
And the crows recite my eulogy in verse

Deputy was waiting where the gravel turns to clay
I cut the headlights, kissed the cross, and prayed the dark away
But the back wheels found the ditch and then the ditch it found the creek
And the cold came up to take a man too stubborn and too weak

Mama, I can hear the church bells ringing slow
Through the catfish and the kerosene and everything below
Tell 'em I died running from a thing I couldn't name
A thirsty kind of freedom and a slow and gentle flame

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Trunk full of mason jars and a dashboard set to glow
I'm running clear corn liquor down a glittered county road
The radio is preaching and the speedometer's a star
I'm the smoothest little outlaw in the fastest little car

But the sirens hit me boogie, boogie, red and blue
The whole night turned to mirrorball and I spun right out of view
Oh the jars went flying, sparkling like the floor
And the run gone wrong was the funkiest crash I ever saw

I tried to take the curve in a shimmer and a slide
The moon was throwing sequins on the river deep and wide
My getaway was groovy till the asphalt let me down
Now I'm dancing with the deputies on the edge of town

And the sirens hit me boogie, boogie, red and blue
The whole night turned to mirrorball and I spun right out of view
Oh the jars went flying, sparkling like the floor
And the run gone wrong was the funkiest crash I ever saw

Handcuffs catch the streetlight, baby, what a shine
They can book me, they can cook me, but they can't unfunk my mind
So I'll two-step to the cruiser, head held high
Leave a trail of sticky stardust and goodbye

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Highlighter bleeding yellow on a page I can't recall
The ceiling fan's my disco and the textbook is my wall
Coffee gone to cold mud in a chipped and lonely cup
The whole world's gone to sleeping but my brain won't power up

Two a.m., I'm dancing with the answers in my head
Two a.m., the only beat's the heartbeat in my chest
Nobody's watching, so I spin around the room
Me and the lamplight and the glittering of doom

Flashcards on the carpet like a constellation map
I bargain with the chapter for a fifteen-minute nap
The streetlight does the hustle on the ceiling overhead
While formulas go shimmer-shimmer dancing in my dread

Two a.m., I'm dancing with the answers in my head
Two a.m., the only beat's the heartbeat in my chest
Nobody's watching, so I spin around the room
Me and the lamplight and the glittering of doom

If I make it to the morning I'll be made of gold
A disco ball of caffeine and the things that I was told
So turn the music up inside my skull
There's a dancefloor in this all-night, and tonight it's never dull

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Granddaddy's old recipe and a tank just halfway full
A Ford with a tired engine and a heart that's harder still
I loaded up the copper batch when the whippoorwills came near
And lit out for the holler with my pockets full of fear

But the run went wrong on the Cumberland line
Where the law lays low in the kudzu vine
My fiddle-playing freedom hit a tree
And the white lightning poured out over me

Banjo couldn't warn me what the morning fog would hide
A fallen oak across the road on the steep and ragged ride
I swerved to miss the timber and the jars began to sing
A shatter and a holler and the breaking of a string

And the run went wrong on the Cumberland line
Where the law lays low in the kudzu vine
My fiddle-playing freedom hit a tree
And the white lightning poured out over me

Tell my mama I was honest in my way
Just a poor man making music out of corn and clay
The mandolin will mourn me come the spring
And the mountain keeps on humming everything

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Manos en las diez y dos, mira el espejo, mi amor
The parking lot is empty and the sunrise is our floor
You stall it, then you laugh it off, dale, otra vez
My little girl is grown and she's got fire in her chest

Dale suave, dale suave, take the wheel
The whole road's gonna learn how strong you really feel
Green light, mi vida, deja el miedo atrás
I taught you how to ride but now you teach me how to last

Window down, the bass is low, the seatbelt holds you tight
You check your blind spot twice and pull into the morning light
I grip the door, te juro, but I won't let you see
I'm just a passenger now in the life you're gonna lead

Dale suave, dale suave, take the wheel
The whole road's gonna learn how strong you really feel
Green light, mi vida, deja el miedo atrás
I taught you how to ride but now you teach me how to last

Un día vas a manejar muy lejos de mí
Down highways I can't follow, to a place I'll never see
But every curve you take, recuerda, niña, esto
Your papá's in the rhythm of the engine and the road

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Thumb hovers over the number I deleted but still know
Vinyl crackle of the silence, three years deep in undertow
The dial tone's a heartbeat, soft and slow against my ear
Rain on the window doing percussion for my fear

Hey ma, it's me, I know it's late
I rehearsed a hundred sorries and forgot 'em at the gate
Just pick up, just pick up, let the static fade
Let me hear the way you say my name unafraid

Kitchen clock still ticking in the background of my mind
The smell of your café con leche I left far behind
Pride is just a heavy coat I wore through every storm
Now I'm shivering and calling 'cause your voice is somewhere warm

Click. A breath. A pause. And then your hello, frail and true
All the years collapse to nothing and I'm seven, holding you
The beat just loops the moment, soft, no need to rhyme
Ma, I'm sorry. Yeah, I'm home. I had the time, I had the time

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Your tether came undone somewhere past the silver line
I watched you turn to punctuation in the dark behind
The radio went velvet, then it went to grain
And the cold blue marble didn't even know your name

Drift, baby, drift, on a saxophone of light
Two small candles getting smaller in the endless night
I'd reach for you but distance is a thing I can't undo
So I'll play this slow blue nothing just for you

We trained for every failure but they never taught us this
The gentle awful physics of a slowly widening miss
Your helmet caught a sunrise, then it caught the void
A brushstroke and a heartbeat and a love we can't avoid

There's no sound out here to carry what I mean
Just the hush between the stars and the hum of the machine
So let the trumpet swell where my arms can't go
And drift, my fellow traveler, soft and slow

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Got a cracked windshield map and a thermos full of black
Chasing thunder down the panhandle and never looking back
My daddy farmed the same square mile until they laid him low
But I was born with weather in my blood and bound to go

I can't settle, I can't stay
There's a wall cloud calling me a hundred miles away
Love me in the morning, lose me in the gray
I'm a chaser, and the storm's the only home I'll ever lay

She asked me once to build a porch and watch the seasons turn
But my hands get itchy when the prairie skies don't burn
The hail came down like marbles on the hood of my old truck
And I felt more alive than any chapel ever struck

Maybe when I'm older and the funnels let me be
I'll find a quiet doorway where the calm can live with me
But till the last gray anvil rolls across the plain
You'll find me leaning hard into the rain

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
The drums said come closer when I didn't know your name
Fairy lights and aso-ebi turning gold into a flame
You caught my elbow spinning, said you came here for your friend
And the night just cracked wide open like it never had to end

Dance with me, stranger, under borrowed light
We don't owe tomorrow nothing, we just owe it to tonight
Move your waist, move your soul, let the talking drum decide
Two strangers and a wedding and the whole world set aside

Jollof on the table, but I couldn't taste a thing
Watching how you laughed against the music that the speakers sing
The bride threw all her flowers but I only saw your face
A stranger in the rhythm of a sweet and crowded place

Dance with me, stranger, under borrowed light
We don't owe tomorrow nothing, we just owe it to tonight
Move your waist, move your soul, let the talking drum decide
Two strangers and a wedding and the whole world set aside

Maybe we won't text, maybe this is all we get
One perfect song, one stranger's hand, one night I won't forget
So give me one more chorus 'fore the deejay fades the sound
And dance like love can find you when you're not even looking down

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five dollars a game and the clock don't lie
Under the elm trees where the pigeons fly
I got a cardboard sign and a coffee gone cold
And more checkmate stories than my mouth has told

Lay your money down, son, let's see what you got
I been reading this board since before you were taught
The knight's gonna fork you, the bishop's gonna stay
It's just me and the pavement and the games I play

Wall Street suits come and try their luck
Lay their lattes down and they think they're hot stuff
But I traded my fortune for this folding chair
And the only crown I wear is the city air

Lay your money down, son, let's see what you got
I been reading this board since before you were taught
The knight's gonna fork you, the bishop's gonna stay
It's just me and the pavement and the games I play

They ask me why a mind like mine ends up out here
I tell 'em every kingdom's just a board, my dear
I lost a real one once, a wife, a name
But nobody can take the way I love this game

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Attic full of wires and a transmitter humming low
I broadcast to the insomniacs the world won't let me know
The needle finds a frequency the licensed men forgot
And I whisper to the darkness from my little rooftop slot

Can you hear me, can you hear me, out there in the static sea
This is nobody from nowhere sending nothing but it's free
Turn your dial to the edges where the official voices fade
And ride the gentle signal that the lonely ones have made

A song for the night nurse, a song for the man who can't sleep
For the trucker on the interstate the long white lines to keep
I never say my real name, I just let the music bleed
A pirate flag of frequencies for everyone in need

When the authorities come climbing up the stair
They'll find an empty room and just the echo in the air
But somewhere in the wavelength I'll be drifting, soft and wide
A ghost of all the songs I sent into the night

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the pickup when the moon was hangin' low
Mason jars a-clinkin' on a backroad I don't show
Daddy made it copper-clean, a hundred proof and pure
Thought I had it figured out, thought my luck was sure

Oh the run went wrong, sirens in the pines
Red and blue a-flashin' down them old county lines
Shoulda stayed home, shoulda played it slow
Now it's gravel dust and trouble on this lonesome midnight road

Sheriff Tom was waitin' where the river bends around
Spilled it in the ditch and watched it soak into the ground
A hundred dollars gone tonight and freedom right behind
The price of easy money is a heavy thing to find

Oh the run went wrong, sirens in the pines
Red and blue a-flashin' down them old county lines
Shoulda stayed home, shoulda played it slow
Now it's gravel dust and trouble on this lonesome midnight road

Momma always told me that the shortcut's never free
Now I'm learnin' every lesson that she tried to give to me

Oh the run went wrong, sirens in the pines
Now I'm singin' all my sorrows down them old county lines

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The tunnel swallows daylight as the iron carriage groans
A hundred strangers breathing and I'm rattlin' my bones
The walls begin to lean in and the air turns into stone
My heart's a crow that's beating tryin' to break out on its own

Oh the dark comes down, oh the wheels they scream
I'm drownin' on the platform of a black and broken dream
Lord, the train won't stop, and I can't get free
There's a devil in my chest and he's a-rattlin' the keys

The lights flicker like candles in a church that's long since burned
Every face a tombstone for a lesson never learned
I count the breaths like prayers that no one ever hears
And the rails sing low and mournful through the ringing in my ears

Oh the dark comes down, oh the wheels they scream
I'm drownin' on the platform of a black and broken dream
Lord, the train won't stop, and I can't get free
There's a devil in my chest and he's a-rattlin' the keys

Then the doors slide open and the cold air finds my face
And the ghost lets go its grip and leaves an empty hollow space

Oh the dark moves on, oh the wheels grow still
And I'm climbin' toward the streetlight up the long and weary hill

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. and the diesel's idle hum
Fingers on the dial 'cause I miss where I'm from
The phone rings twice, then I hear you breathe
A thousand miles of highway between you and me

Baby I'm callin', callin' home tonight
Under the gas station's lonely light
Tell the kids I love 'em, tell 'em daddy's all right
Just holdin' your voice till the mornin' light

The radio's static, the coffee's gone cold
Nebraska in my mirror lookin' tired and old
But your sleepy little whisper says I'm not alone
You're the only home this old road's ever known

Baby I'm callin', callin' home tonight
Under the gas station's lonely light
Tell the kids I love 'em, tell 'em daddy's all right
Just holdin' your voice till the mornin' light

Four more days, four more sunrise miles
Four more dawns till I see your smile
Keep the porch light burnin', keep my side of the bed
I'm rollin' back to you with these words you said

Baby I'm callin', callin' home tonight
Under the gas station's lonely light
Just holdin' your voice till the mornin' light

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Dust on the windows where the lights used to glow
Silence on the line where the river used to flow
Fathers and mothers with their hands gone still
Ghosts in the smoke stack on the edge of the hill

Fade, fade, the whistle won't blow
Fade into the morning, into the snow
We were the heartbeat, we were the sound
Now we're just echoes in a slowing-down town

The gates are all rusted and the parking lot's bare
A cathedral of metal and the cold empty air
Thirty years of mornings turning gray and dissolved
A whole world of meaning that no one could solve

Fade, fade, the whistle won't blow
Fade into the morning, into the snow
We were the heartbeat, we were the sound
Now we're just echoes in a slowing-down town

And the light comes soft through the broken glass
Golden on the shadows of the days that pass
Something beautiful in the letting go
Drifting like the dust in the afternoon glow

Fade, fade, into the snow
Fade, fade, where the dreamers go

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Flour on her knuckles and a worn gold ring
Those hands could mend a heartache, those hands could make you sing
Seventy winters carved into the lines
A map of all the love she left behind

Grandmother's hands, soft as the dawn
Holding all the stories of the ones who've gone
Grandmother's hands, steady and true
Everything I am, I learned from you

She braided up my sister's hair and dried my mother's tears
Those hands held the family through the hard and holy years
Quilts upon the bedposts that she stitched in candlelight
Warm against the winter and the long and lonesome night

Grandmother's hands, soft as the dawn
Holding all the stories of the ones who've gone
Grandmother's hands, steady and true
Everything I am, I learned from you

Now they rest so quiet on the old front porch swing
Folded like a hymn that the choir used to sing
But I feel them when the morning light comes through
Everything I am, I owe to you

Grandmother's hands, soft as the dawn
Holding all the love of the ones who've gone

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Soft blue glow on a quiet afternoon
Your name on my screen like a slow July moon
I shouldn't, I know it, but my thumb finds the line
Three little dots and a heart that's not mine

Ooh, I shouldn't be typing tonight
Ooh, but the wrong feels so right
Waves on the sand and a secret to keep
Falling so gently, so quiet, so deep

The window is open, the city exhales
A glass of cold wine and a story that fails
You send me a smile and the room starts to sway
I'll delete it tomorrow, but not today

Ooh, I shouldn't be typing tonight
Ooh, but the wrong feels so right
Waves on the sand and a secret to keep
Falling so gently, so quiet, so deep

Samba of the silence, a sin in the breeze
My heart keeps a rhythm that nobody sees
One more little message, then I'll let it go
The sweetest little trouble that I'll never show

Ooh, I shouldn't be typing tonight
Ooh, but the wrong feels so right

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Clock is tickin', cat's got a grin
Five dollars a game and he's always gonna win
Pigeons on the table, autumn in the air
King of the corner with the silver in his hair

Checkmate, baby, lay your money down
He's the smoothest little hustler in the whole downtown
Slide that bishop, watch the suckers fall
He's been playin' these squares since before you could crawl

Quarter past noon and the tourists roll through
He smiles like a fox sayin' 'whatcha gonna do'
A flick of the wrist and the queen takes flight
Another poor fish learnin' wrong from right

Checkmate, baby, lay your money down
He's the smoothest little hustler in the whole downtown
Slide that bishop, watch the suckers fall
He's been playin' these squares since before you could crawl

But the streetlights flicker and the players go home
And the old man sits with his board alone
He ain't never lost but he ain't never free
Just the king and the pawns and the company

Checkmate, baby, the night comes down
He's the loneliest little hustler in the whole downtown

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Headlights on the gravel, engine running low
Glass jars in the trunk and a hundred miles to go
The pines stand like sentinels, the river breathing slow
We were chasing something fast that we would never know

And it all comes crashing down
The quiet of the holler turns to sound
Every dream we carried scattered on the ground
We were running toward the light and now there's no way down

The tires lost the corner where the cold creek ran
Metal kissed the timber, the way these stories ran
Silence after thunder, smoke into the dawn
The jars all broke to pieces and the moonshine's gone

And it all comes crashing down
The quiet of the holler turns to sound
Every dream we carried scattered on the ground
We were running toward the light and now there's no way down

Rise, rise, the morning finds the wreck
Gold light on the water, nothing left to wreck
Rise, rise, the holler holds its breath
And the long road keeps on going past the edge of death

And it all comes crashing down
We were running toward the light and now we're homeward bound

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Gallery seven and the lights are low
Same four walls and the same old glow
She's been hanging there since 1888
And I clock in every morning at a quarter past eight

And I'm falling for a girl made of oil and dust
A hundred years of longing and a frame full of rust
She'll never see me standing in my polyester blue
But I'd give all my Tuesdays just to look at you

The tourists come and snap and walk away
They never stop to hear the things you'd say
But I know every brushstroke, every shade of your face
The loneliest romance in the quietest place

And I'm falling for a girl made of oil and dust
A hundred years of longing and a frame full of rust
She'll never see me standing in my polyester blue
But I'd give all my Tuesdays just to look at you

When the doors are locked and the building sleeps
I swear I see you move in the shadows it keeps
Maybe I'm just tired, maybe I'm just through
Or maybe I'm in love with a ghost in a museum room

And I'm falling for a girl made of oil and dust
But I'd give all my Tuesdays just to look at you

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Night shift, flashlight, walkin' through the hall
Mona on the canvas got me frozen by the wall
Gold frame, cold game, hundred million on the wall
I'm the only one that's watchin' when the curtains finally fall

She don't move, she don't blink (she don't blink)
Worth more than the whole city, what you think? (what you think?)
I'm the guard, I'm the lock, I'm the key (yeah)
Me and the masterpiece, just the masterpiece and me

Marble floor, echo loud, badge up on my chest
Two a.m., empty room, I don't ever get no rest
Every brushstroke talkin' like she tellin' me a secret
Four hundred years of silence and I'm the one to keep it

She don't move, she don't blink (she don't blink)
Worth more than the whole city, what you think? (what you think?)
I'm the guard, I'm the lock, I'm the key (yeah)
Me and the masterpiece, just the masterpiece and me

Lights down low, alarm armed tight
Just a man and a legend in the dead of night
They'll never know what I see when nobody's around
The richest kind of lonely that I ever found

She don't move, she don't blink (she don't blink)
Me and the masterpiece, just the masterpiece and me

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Five in the basement and the deck's gone cold
Stakes on the table that we never should've sold
Whiskey in the glasses, hate behind the eyes
Every hand we're dealing is another set of lies

WE WENT TOO FAR! THE TABLE'S TURNED!
EVERY BRIDGE WE BUILT IS A BRIDGE WE BURNED!
DEAL THE CARDS! THERE'S NO WAY BACK!
BROTHERS TURNED TO STRANGERS IN AN ALL-IN ATTACK!

He pushed his chips, I pushed back hard
Friendship for a pot and a folded card
The fists came up when the cash ran dry
And nobody walks away with their hands held high

WE WENT TOO FAR! THE TABLE'S TURNED!
EVERY BRIDGE WE BUILT IS A BRIDGE WE BURNED!
DEAL THE CARDS! THERE'S NO WAY BACK!
BROTHERS TURNED TO STRANGERS IN AN ALL-IN ATTACK!

Was it worth it? Was it worth it?
For a pile of paper and a wound you can't unearth it?
Was it worth it? Tell me now
We lost it all and I don't even know how

WE WENT TOO FAR! THE TABLE'S TURNED!
EVERY BRIDGE WE BUILT IS A BRIDGE WE BURNED!

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Breathe in, the walls are coming close
Breathe out, the lights begin to fold
A hundred faces blur into the gray
And I am very far away

Hold on, hold on, the tunnel ends
Hold on, hold on, the dark unbends
Every wave will rise and then it goes
Breathe in, breathe out, the river slows

The iron hum, a heartbeat in the dark
The humming light, a slow electric spark
I count to ten and ten again and ten
Until I find the surface once again

Hold on, hold on, the tunnel ends
Hold on, hold on, the dark unbends
Every wave will rise and then it goes
Breathe in, breathe out, the river slows

And the doors slide open soft and slow
And the cold clean air begins to flow
Nothing here can hold me anymore
I'm a single quiet breath upon the floor

Hold on, hold on, the tunnel ends
Breathe in, breathe out, the dark unbends

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Saturday night and the deck's on fire
Neon on the felt and the stakes climbing higher
Diamonds in her eyes and an ace up her sleeve
Nobody at the table's gonna get up and leave

We played too far, under the lights
Gambled all our hearts on a Saturday night
Spin the wheel, deal it down, don't you stop
We're dancing on the edge till the whole thing drops

Gold chains glowin' and the chips piled high
Everybody's bluffin' and nobody's shy
The music keeps poundin' as the fortunes change hands
And love's just another bet that nobody understands

We played too far, under the lights
Gambled all our hearts on a Saturday night
Spin the wheel, deal it down, don't you stop
We're dancing on the edge till the whole thing drops

When the morning comes and the music dies
Nothin' left but smoke and a pair of lonely eyes
We bet it all and we lost it all the same
But oh, what a beautiful, beautiful game

We played too far, under the lights
We're dancing on the edge till the morning light

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
A trembling hand on a borrowed guitar
A crowd of seven and a smoky little bar
I was nineteen with a song and a fear
And a voice that shook like a glass of cheap beer

Oh, that first night, that fragile first light
When I gave them my heart in the dim yellow light
I remember the silence, I remember the fall
The night that I gave them my everything, my all

The spotlight was burning, the mic was too tall
I forgot the second verse and I dropped the pick to fall
But somewhere in the stumble, somewhere in the shame
A flame began burning that would never be the same

Oh, that first night, that fragile first light
When I gave them my heart in the dim yellow light
I remember the silence, I remember the fall
The night that I gave them my everything, my all

Now the halls are bigger and the lights are bright
But I'd trade them all for that terrible night
For the boy who was nothing but a song and a prayer
Standing in the spotlight with his soul laid bare

Oh, that first night, that fragile first light
I gave them my heart in the dim yellow light

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning, empty pew, I'm walkin' out the door
Grew up on these hymns but they don't move me anymore
Momma's in the front row with her hands up to the sky
And I'm slippin' out the back, didn't say goodbye

Quiet, quiet, I'm lettin' it go (lettin' it go)
Everything they taught me that I don't know no more (no more)
Stained glass shinin' but it don't feel like home (like home)
Walkin' out the chapel, gotta find my own (my own)

Twenty years of Sundays in that wooden room
Candles and the choir and the organ's lonely tune
I still believe in somethin', just not the way they say
So I'm closin' up this chapter and I'm walkin' away

Quiet, quiet, I'm lettin' it go (lettin' it go)
Everything they taught me that I don't know no more (no more)
Stained glass shinin' but it don't feel like home (like home)
Walkin' out the chapel, gotta find my own (my own)

No anger, no thunder, no big goodbye
Just a quiet kind of leavin' and a tear in my eye
I'll keep the love they gave me, leave the rest behind
Walkin' toward a faith that I can finally call mine

Quiet, quiet, I'm lettin' it go (lettin' it go)
Walkin' out the chapel, gotta find my own (my own)

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Cada noche cierro los ojos y subo
sobre los tejados de mi pueblo dormido
las trompetas suenan donde nacen las nubes
y el viento me conoce por mi nombre antiguo

Veo el río de plata, la plaza vacía
mi abuela en la ventana me saluda y se ríe
yo extiendo los brazos como un ala perdida
y el cielo me sostiene aunque nadie lo crea

Vuelo, vuelo, sobre la sierra dorada
vuelo, vuelo, donde no llega la mañana
y si despierto y caigo a mi cama de nuevo
guardo el sueño en el pecho hasta el próximo vuelo

Las guitarras me empujan más alto que el monte
paso entre las estrellas como un jinete sin nombre
y abajo queda el miedo, los días de cobre
y todo lo pesado que el día me esconde

Dicen que volar es solo para los muertos
pero yo vuelvo cada noche y regreso despierto
con polvo de luna pegado en los dedos
y la certeza de que el cielo es mi pueblo

Vuelo, vuelo, sobre la sierra dorada
vuelo, vuelo, donde no llega la mañana
y si despierto y caigo a mi cama de nuevo
guardo el sueño en el pecho hasta el próximo vuelo

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Got a message from a friend up in Tromsø
Said the sky was on fire, green and slow
I booked a flight on a feeling, no plan
Left Lagos heat for the cold northern land

Green light dancing, oh the sky dey move
Northern light wahala, got nothing to prove
We dey under the heaven, we dey lose control
Aurora on my body, aurora in my soul

Snow up to my ankles, breath turning to smoke
Local man laughing at my too-thin coat
But when the curtain open up over the bay
Every word I knew just floated away

Green light dancing, oh the sky dey move
Northern light wahala, got nothing to prove
We dey under the heaven, we dey lose control
Aurora on my body, aurora in my soul

I called my mama, said look at this light
She say God dey paint when the world go quiet
No drum, no dancer, just the sky and me
Most alive I ever felt, most free

Green light dancing, oh the sky dey move
Northern light wahala, got nothing to prove
We dey under the heaven, we dey lose control
Aurora on my body, aurora in my soul

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Took the long way home from the highway tonight
Wound up on Fourth Street under the streetlight
There's your old window, second floor, on the right
Still got a curtain but it ain't yours, that's right

Oh I drove on by, didn't slow, didn't stop
Just let the banjo carry what my heart forgot
The light was on, somebody else was home
And I was just a stranger driving alone

We used to dance in that kitchen too small
Radio crackling, your shadow on the wall
Now some young couple's hanging pictures in the hall
And the porch where we kissed don't remember at all

My headlights swept across that fire escape
Where you'd sit and smoke and tell me to wait
I waited so long that the waiting got late
Now I just drive by and let the past evaporate

Funny how a building can hold a whole year
Brick don't cry but I do, right here
Foot on the gas and I'm gone, disappear
Some roads you just take to remember, my dear

Oh I drove on by, didn't slow, didn't stop
Just let the banjo carry what my heart forgot
The light was on, somebody else was home
And I was just a stranger driving alone

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Midnight engine, jars in the trunk
Backroad rhythm, headlights drunk
Uncle's recipe, ninety proof
Moon up high like a warning truth

Run, run, run through the holler tonight
Run, run, run but the law's got a light
Four-on-the-floor and the bass won't quit
Run, run, run — oh we lost it, we lost it

Took the curve too fast on the gravel bend
Glass on the dashboard, that's where it ends
Siren coming up like a kick drum loud
Smoke in the engine, jars on the ground

Run, run, run through the holler tonight
Run, run, run but the law's got a light
Four-on-the-floor and the bass won't quit
Run, run, run — oh we lost it, we lost it

Standing in the cuffs with the river so still
Every dollar gone down the side of the hill
But the beat's still pumping somewhere in my chest
One more run and I swear it'd be the best

Run, run, run through the holler tonight
Run, run, run but the law's got a light
Four-on-the-floor and the bass won't quit
Run, run, run — oh we lost it, we lost it

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They came with contracts soft as rain
Wanted my grief in a brand-new frame
Said sand it down, make it shine
put a price on every line

but I keep the static, keep the haze
keep the words that no one pays
let the feedback swallow the sound
I'd rather drown than sell it down

fluorescent men in glassy rooms
offered me a thousand moons
for the poem I bled to write
they'd hang it pretty, kill the light

but I keep the static, keep the haze
keep the words that no one pays
let the feedback swallow the sound
I'd rather drown than sell it down

so the guitars wash over me
louder than their currency
blurred and honest, dim and free
the only wealth that stays with me

so I keep the static, keep the haze
keep the words that no one pays
let the feedback swallow the sound
I'd rather drown than sell it down

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They slid me a check with a smile on the side
Said soften the message, let the truth subside
Put your name on a billboard, sell a sneaker or two
I said nah, I write what's real, that's the only thing I do
Grew up on the corner where the metaphors live
Words are all I own and they're not mine to give
They want a clean version, radio glow
but the realest of my verses they don't ever let go

I won't sell it, won't trade it, won't water it down
Keep my pen to the people, keep my feet on the ground
They can offer me a mansion, offer me a crown
I'm a poet not a product, I'ma hold my ground

Manager's calling, label wanna sign
Said drop the political, just give us the shine
I seen the ones who folded, seen 'em lose the spark
Trade a soul for a single, now they rhyme in the dark
I'd rather pack a back room than a sold-out lie
I'd rather stay broke and real till the day I die

Mama said son, don't you trade your voice
Money comes and goes but the truth's a choice
So I'm choosing the hard road, ink and a spine
Every word that I spit is a brick in the line

I won't sell it, won't trade it, won't water it down
Keep my pen to the people, keep my feet on the ground
They can offer me a mansion, offer me a crown
I'm a poet not a product, I'ma hold my ground

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Dem come from the city with a pen and a deal
Say write us a jingle, make di people feel
But nah, mi words dem belong to di street
Not to di dollar, not to di beat dem cheat

I and I nah sell out, no no
Keep di truth inna mi throat, let it grow
Babylon a offer gold an' a crown
But mi rhyme fi di people, mi nah water it down

Dem want mi smile pretty pon di TV screen
Forget all di words 'bout di hungry an' di mean
But mi grandmother teach mi, di tongue is a fire
You cyaan put a price pon di soul's desire

I and I nah sell out, no no
Keep di truth inna mi throat, let it grow
Babylon a offer gold an' a crown
But mi rhyme fi di people, mi nah water it down

Under di palm tree mi write inna di shade
No amount a money mek di message fade
Let dem keep dem riches, mi keep mi voice clean
Louder dan di silver, sweeter dan di green

I and I nah sell out, no no
Keep di truth inna mi throat, let it grow
Babylon a offer gold an' a crown
But mi rhyme fi di people, mi nah water it down

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Down on the corner where the needle meets the thread
Mister Lee got a measuring tape hanging round his neck
He stitch you a suit make the whole street stop
Finest cut of cloth from the bottom to the top

He dress the whole block, sharp from head to toe
He dress the whole block, watch the people glow
Lapel so clean, crease so right
Everybody shining on a Saturday night

The mailman struttin' in a three-piece groove
The barber got a vest that make the ladies move
Grandma's church hat and the deacon's coat
All come from the shop with the velvet note

He dress the whole block, sharp from head to toe
He dress the whole block, watch the people glow
Lapel so clean, crease so right
Everybody shining on a Saturday night

He don't charge the kids when they come for prom
Just say wear it proud and keep your manners on
Gold scissors flashing in the window light
Mister Lee make the whole block tight

He dress the whole block, sharp from head to toe
He dress the whole block, watch the people glow
Lapel so clean, crease so right
Everybody shining on a Saturday night

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
We been friends since the seventh grade lunch line
You the first text in the morning, you the last on my mind
I know your coffee order, know you hate the rain
Know the way you laugh right before you explain
But I'm stuck in a corner that I built myself
Watching you fall for everybody else
I play it cool, like a brother, like a friend
While I rehearse confessions that I never gonna send

And I love you, but you don't see
The whole world in front of you is standing here as me
I'm right here, I'm right here
Loving you loud in a silence you can't hear

You call me up crying 'bout some dude that ain't right
I'm holding the phone, holding back all night
Give you the advice that send you back to his arms
Then I hang up alone with my own little harm
They say speak your truth but the truth got a cost
If I say it out loud then I know what I lost

So I carry it quiet, this weight in my chest
Glad to be near you, I guess that's the best
Maybe one day the timing, maybe one day you'll know
Till then I'm just smiling and learning to let go

And I love you, but you don't see
The whole world in front of you is standing here as me
I'm right here, I'm right here
Loving you loud in a silence you can't hear

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
En la torre de piedra yo escribo de noche
cartas para el agua que nunca responde
mi pluma es la luz que da vueltas al mundo
y el mar es mi novia de pecho profundo

Te escribo, mar, te escribo
cada ola se lleva mi suspiro
guarda mis palabras en tu sal y tu espuma
mi amor en botella bajo la luna

Le cuento del faro, del frío, del viento
de cómo la espero con cada momento
las trompetas resuenan sobre el oleaje
y el barco que pasa no entiende mi mensaje

Te escribo, mar, te escribo
cada ola se lleva mi suspiro
guarda mis palabras en tu sal y tu espuma
mi amor en botella bajo la luna

Dicen que estoy loco, que el mar no me oye
que las olas se ríen y nada me trae
pero al amanecer encuentro en la arena
una concha vacía, su única respuesta serena

Te escribo, mar, te escribo
cada ola se lleva mi suspiro
guarda mis palabras en tu sal y tu espuma
mi amor en botella bajo la luna

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I held the phone for an hour before I dialed
seven numbers I have known since I was a child
the dial tone hums like a distant tide
all the years between us opening wide

and the line goes quiet, then I hear you breathe
softer than I remembered, more than I believe
hello, it's me, after all this time
floating in the silence on the other side of the line

you say my name like it never left your mouth
no blame in your voice, no asking what happened, what about
just the sound of the kettle somewhere in your kitchen
and the hush of a love I thought I'd forbidden

we don't fix it all in a single call
but the wall comes down a little, brick and all
your voice a slow river under everything
a quiet I can finally let in

and the line goes quiet, then I hear you breathe
softer than I remembered, more than I believe
hello, it's me, after all this time
floating in the silence on the other side of the line

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the cobblestones across his chest
built the cathedrals where he takes his rest
the streetlights flicker with his every dream
and the river runs warm from an underground stream

Oh the city sways on a slow heartbeat
feel the rumble rising up through the street
don't wake him, don't wake him, just let him breathe
we're dancing on a giant in the city beneath

The saxophone bends with his exhale
the trolley cars rattle when his dreams turn pale
the lovers in the alley feel the ground go soft
as the sleeping titan shifts and turns aloft

Oh the city sways on a slow heartbeat
feel the rumble rising up through the street
don't wake him, don't wake him, just let him breathe
we're dancing on a giant in the city beneath

What happens to us all when he opens an eye
when the towers come tumbling and we kiss the sky
but tonight the piano just rolls real low
and we live like he'll sleep forever, you know

Oh the city sways on a slow heartbeat
feel the rumble rising up through the street
don't wake him, don't wake him, just let him breathe
we're dancing on a giant in the city beneath

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
The tether snapped at a quarter past the dark
now I'm watching you shrink like a fading spark
radio crackle, your voice gettin' thin
Lord, there's no current gonna pull us back in

We're drifting, baby, drifting apart
far as the cold black night, far as my heart
no gravity here gonna bring you to me
just two lonely stars in an empty sea

I can still see your visor catch the sun
countin' down the seconds till the talkin's done
battery's dyin', breath gettin' slow
all the words I should've said I can't let go

We're drifting, baby, drifting apart
far as the cold black night, far as my heart
no gravity here gonna bring you to me
just two lonely stars in an empty sea

We trained for the failure, we trained for the fall
but nobody trains you for losing it all
so I'll reach out my glove to the dark one more time
knowin' your hand ain't never gonna find mine

We're drifting, baby, drifting apart
far as the cold black night, far as my heart
no gravity here gonna bring you to me
just two lonely stars in an empty sea

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I sold the old plow and I sold the brown mare
packed my mother's blanket and a lock of her hair
the harbor was crowded, the morning was grey
and the ship took us all from the only home we'd known

So it's farewell to the green and the church on the hill
farewell to the well and the cold morning chill
I'm bound for a country I never have seen
with an ocean of grief and an ocean between

The waves taught us hunger, the waves taught us fear
some went in the water, we said prayers for the year
but a girl played a whistle down low in the hold
and we sang the old songs to keep out the cold

So it's farewell to the green and the church on the hill
farewell to the well and the cold morning chill
I'm bound for a country I never have seen
with an ocean of grief and an ocean between

They tell of the cities all paved in gold
but I'd trade them for one more day in the fold
still I'll plant my children in this foreign ground
and teach them the songs of the home I unwound

So it's farewell to the green and the church on the hill
farewell to the well and the cold morning chill
I'm bound for a country I never have seen
with an ocean of grief and an ocean between

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors slam shut and the tunnel goes black
heart's a snare drum rolling down the track
too many bodies, not enough air
breath getting shallow, hands in my hair

Breathe, breathe, the walls are closing in
breathe, breathe, feel it under my skin
the train won't stop and the lights won't stay
count to ten till it passes away

Stranger's elbow and the screech of the brakes
the whole car shaking with the tremor it makes
tunnel vision narrowing fast
telling myself that it never can last

Breathe, breathe, the walls are closing in
breathe, breathe, feel it under my skin
the train won't stop and the lights won't stay
count to ten till it passes away

Next station, doors open wide
cold platform air and I'm stumbling outside
hands on my knees on the concrete ground
found the surface, found the sound

Breathe, breathe, the walls are letting go
breathe, breathe, feel the panic slow
the train pulls off and I let it depart
still alive with a hammering heart

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
White lights swinging, the band's gone wild
You caught my hand like a feral child
I don't know your name and I don't care
Gripping the bassline, gripping the air
Kick the chairs back, tear the seam
Two loud strangers in somebody's dream

DANCE WITH ME, I'll never see you again
DANCE WITH ME, burn it down to the end
No vows, no rings, just the floor and the noise
Two wrecking balls, we are nobody's choice

Groom's uncle screaming, the cake hits the wall
Your heels are broken, you don't fall at all
Sweat in the streamers, blood on the toast
We are the chaos this party needs most

When the lights come up we'll be ghosts in the lot
Forget every face, forget what we're not
One song, one stranger, one slam of the heart
Then back to the dark where we always start

DANCE WITH ME, I'll never see you again
DANCE WITH ME, burn it down to the end

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Sky cracked open over frozen ground
Green fire screaming without a sound
We drove all night just to stand and stare
At the riot of color tearing the air
Boots in the ice and our breath like steam
God is throwing a fit in a violent dream

BURN, sky, BURN over the pines
Rip the dark open along the lines
We are so small and the heavens so loud
Screaming back at the auroral crowd

Solar wind hammers the iron core
Charged and furious, wanting more
No cathedral built could ever compete
With this light show kicking us off our feet

Let it swallow the night, let it gut the cold
We were never meant to grow tame or old
Up where the particles smash and ignite
We found our religion in northern light

BURN, sky, BURN over the pines
Rip the dark open along the lines

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the Ford at a quarter to three
Jugs in the back where the seats used to be
Mama said son you're a fool for the road
But the still's run dry and we're owed what we're owed

Oh the run went wrong, baby, wrong as can be
Sheriff on my tail down to Tennessee
Swing them hips while the engine's hot
We'll dance to the jail if it's all we got

Hit a county line and the tire blew loud
Corn liquor spilling like a thunder cloud
Deputy laughing in his Sunday best
Cuffs in his hand and a star on his chest

So pour me a glass of the evidence, sweet
Let the trumpet cry and the cymbals beat
If I'm going down then I'm going in style
A crooked grin and a moonshine smile

Oh the run went wrong, baby, wrong as can be
Sheriff on my tail down to Tennessee
Swing them hips while the engine's hot
We'll dance to the jail if it's all we got

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down on a Tuesday gray
You stepped under, didn't run away
One small circle for the two of us
Shoulders touching at the crowded bus

And I don't even know your name
But the city's washing all the same

Under one umbrella, you and me
Strangers in the rain, but lonely's free
Hold it a little closer, stay a while
We could share the storm, we could share a smile

You laughed at the puddle I almost fell in
Told me where your favorite cafe had been
Four blocks of weather, that's all we'd get
But I memorized the way your hair was wet

When the clouds break open and the sun comes through
You'll fold up and vanish, and so will the blue
But for sixty seconds the whole world stood still
Under one umbrella on a rain-soaked hill

Under one umbrella, you and me
Strangers in the rain, but lonely's free
Hold it a little closer, stay a while
We could share the storm, we could share a smile

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Last seed standing, nobody believed
Wrote us off the bracket before we achieved
Four quarters left and the whole town's loud
We play for the doubt of every silent crowd

Turn it up, this is the final night
Underdogs running into the light
Feel the bass like a heartbeat pound
We were never meant to hold this ground

Down by ten with a minute to go
Coach said run it, let the whole place know
Every missed shot, every long bus ride
Comes down to this, with nothing to hide

Go, go, go — let the bassline blow
Go, go, go — never letting go

Clock hits zero and the buzzer screams
We stitched a miracle out of broken seams
They'll write our names where the legends stand
The smallest dream with the biggest hand

Turn it up, this is the final night
Underdogs running into the light
Feel the bass like a heartbeat pound
We were never meant to hold this ground

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Bajo las luces de la boda del pueblo
Tomé tu mano sin saber tu nombre
Las trompetas lloran un vals de deseo
Y el mundo se vuelve dorado y noble

Baila conmigo, extraña, esta noche
Que suene el violín y se rompa el reloj
No sé quién eres, no importa el reproche
Solo importa el giro de nuestra canción

Tu vestido vuela como una bandera
El novio brinda, la abuela sonríe
Dos desconocidos en la primavera
Que el destino enreda y el tiempo desvíe

Cuando termine la última nota
Quizás no te vea jamás otra vez
Pero en este patio de luna rota
Fuimos eternos de pies a la sien

Baila conmigo, extraña, esta noche
Que suene el violín y se rompa el reloj
No sé quién eres, no importa el reproche
Solo importa el giro de nuestra canción

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Slow drum walking down Rampart Street
Black umbrellas in the August heat
We laid him down with a tear and a hymn
Then the trumpet started, and the lights grew dim

Dance him home, oh dance him home
Down where the river and the spirits roam
