Cry on the way there, swing on the way back
New Orleans buries you in white, not black

Grand Marshal twirling that golden cane
Tuba laughing through the grief and pain
Handkerchiefs waving like a flock of doves
Mourning turned to second-line love

Death ain't an ending on this old ground
It's a brass band turning the sorrow around
So lift up your feet and lift up your eyes
The saints go marching through the Delta skies

Dance him home, oh dance him home
Down where the river and the spirits roam
Cry on the way there, swing on the way back
New Orleans buries you in white, not black

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Cada noche el mismo cielo me llama
Cierro los ojos y me suelto del suelo
La guitarra arde como una llama
Mientras subo descalza por el desvelo

Vuelo, vuelo sobre los tejados
Palmas que truenan, mi pecho encendido
Los campos de olivo quedan abajo abandonados
Y el viento conoce mi nombre perdido

Despierto temblando con sal en la cara
Las manos vacías de todo ese viento
Pero el compás de la noche no para
Y el sueño regresa con cada lamento

¡Olé! grita el alma que no tiene jaula
¡Olé! por las alas que el día me roba
Taconeo la tierra que tanto me ata
Pero el cielo me espera, paciente, en mi alcoba

Vuelo, vuelo sobre los tejados
Palmas que truenan, mi pecho encendido
Los campos de olivo quedan abajo abandonados
Y el viento conoce mi nombre perdido

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning, the screen glows blue
My thumb keeps drifting back to you
I typed it out and I let it go
Into the static, into the slow

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but the words just bloom
Like smoke unspooling across the room
Delete it, delete it, but I press it instead
Floating through wires inside my head

Your name's a secret I keep underwater
A hush, a ripple, a soft disorder
The little dots that appear and fade
Keep me suspended in the haze we made

Somewhere a door I was told to close
Keeps breathing open in pale repose
And I am the moth and the message and the flame
Dissolving slow in a glow with no name

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but the words just bloom
Like smoke unspooling across the room
Delete it, delete it, but I press it instead
Floating through wires inside my head

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
En la cantina jugamos hasta el alba
Las cartas calientes, el tequila al ras
Aposté el rancho, la troca y el alma
Y el diablo sonriendo me pidió un poco más

Ay, la baraja me tendió la trampa
Un as escondido bajo su sombrero
Trompetas que lloran lo que el juego me arranca
Perdí hasta la sombra por un mal tablero

Se alzaron las voces, brillaron navajas
El que repartía tenía mala fe
Volaron las sillas, cayeron barajas
Y la suerte se fue por donde no sé

Debí retirarme con la luna temprana
Debí escuchar lo que mi madre rezó
Pero el orgullo es la peor de las ganas
Y en la mesa de fieltro mi vida quedó

Ay, la baraja me tendió la trampa
Un as escondido bajo su sombrero
Trompetas que lloran lo que el juego me arranca
Perdí hasta la sombra por un mal tablero

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors slam shut and the tunnel go dark
Heart dey race like a runaway spark
Too many bodies, no air to find
The walls dey closing inside my mind

Breathe, oh breathe, just hold on tight
The train go move, you go see the light
My chest dey drum but I no go drown
Riding the panic till it settle down

Phone in my hand but I cannot read
Sweat on my collar, my legs dey bleed speed
Stranger beside me no even know
That I dey fighting a silent show

Count the stops, one, two, three
Every station bring a piece of me
The shaking softens, the grip lets go
Ground beneath me starting to show

Breathe, oh breathe, just hold on tight
The train go move, you go see the light
My chest dey drum but I no go drown
Riding the panic till it settle down

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Close my eyes and I'm gone again
Slippin' the gravity, droppin' the chain
Bassline liftin' me off the bed
Groove in my feet and the sky overhead

I'm flyin', flyin', greasy and free
Funk in my wings and the moon winkin' at me
Same dream rollin' every single night
Get down low so I can rise up right

Rooftops shrinkin' to a city of toys
Horns blastin' out a celestial noise
Can't explain it when the morning comes
But my hips still rememberin' those midnight drums

Wake up grounded with my shoes on tight
Nine to five tryna clip my flight
But soon as that pillow catch my head
I'm a soul-train satellite instead

I'm flyin', flyin', greasy and free
Funk in my wings and the moon winkin' at me
Same dream rollin' every single night
Get down low so I can rise up right

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Keys in your hand and your knuckles white
Empty parking lot in the falling light
Mirror, signal, breathe it slow
Foot off the brake and here we go

Run it, run it, hold the line
Heartbeat racing at a hundred and nine
You got it, you got it, ease the wheel
Fastest thing I feel is the way that you heal

Yesterday small enough to hold
Now you're gripping the road like you're brave and bold
Stalled it twice and you laughed it off
Steadier hands than your old man's, soft

Go — green light — let it ride
Go — green light — I'm right beside

One day soon you'll pull away
Taillights shrinking at the end of the day
But tonight it's first gear, second, third
And the proudest silence I never heard

Run it, run it, hold the line
Heartbeat racing at a hundred and nine
You got it, you got it, ease the wheel
Fastest thing I feel is the way that you heal

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Dusty record, needle drop slow
Forty years in the warm kitchen glow
Your hand in mine like it always fit
Vinyl crackle and the lamp half-lit

One more dance before the lights go down
Slow and easy, no need for a crown
We wore the years like a comfortable coat
Humming the tune that we always wrote

Gray in your hair where the brown used to be
Still the same girl that danced with me
Swaying soft on the worn-out floor
Don't need the music to remember the score

Time took plenty but it left us this
The weight of a head and a goodnight kiss
Whatever comes when the song is through
I'd spend it all again next to you

One more dance before the lights go down
Slow and easy, no need for a crown
We wore the years like a comfortable coat
Humming the tune that we always wrote

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Tether snapped on the morning shift
Now I'm watching my partner slowly drift
No banjo plays in the vacuum cold
But I'm pickin' this sorrow, hard and old

Driftin' apart in the endless black
Reachin' out but there ain't no track
Farther you float on the silent tide
The wider this canyon grows inside

We trained together down in Tennessee
Dreamed of the stars over you and me
Now the Earth's a marble, blue and small
And I can't catch you, can't catch you at all

Radio crackles, your voice goes thin
Tellin' me it's alright where you've been
Save your air, friend, save your breath
I'll hum you home through the gates of death

Driftin' apart in the endless black
Reachin' out but there ain't no track
Farther you float on the silent tide
The wider this canyon grows inside

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
The kettle sings for two but pours for one
Steam on the window where her face once was
I measure half a cup and lose the rest
The radio hums a tune we used to love

One egg cracks lonely on the skillet's edge
The salt still sits exactly where she left
I burn the toast the way she'd shake her head
And laugh into the smoke that fills the room

So I cook for one, and the table's wide
Two chairs, one plate, and the empty side
But the onions make me cry the way I need
A reason for these tears to fall and feed

I learned the spice she hid behind the rice
The quiet trick that made the Sunday warm
Now I stir the pot and talk to no one there
And somehow she answers in the steam

They say a man should starve before he tries
But hunger taught my hands to improvise
A little less, a little tender, slow
I'm setting one place now, and learning how to go

So I cook for one, and the table's wide
Two chairs, one plate, and the empty side
But the broth is rich and the bread is mine
And I eat alone, and somehow I'm fine

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Grandmother stands where the foam meets her feet
Silver in her braid and salt on the breeze
She points one finger at the rolling blue
Says come a little closer, let me teach you how to move

Pull, pull, let the moon take the lead
Push, push, give the shoreline what it needs
She's dancing with the ocean under disco-colored skies
Teaching the tide how to rise, rise, rise

Ninety summers folded in her palms
She counts the waves like beads upon a charm
The young ones laugh but the water seems to know
When she lifts her hand the breakers start to flow

Pull, pull, let the moon take the lead
Push, push, give the shoreline what it needs
She's dancing with the ocean under disco-colored skies
Teaching the tide how to rise, rise, rise

Glitter on the foam, mirror ball of stars
She never learned the steps, she just remembered who we are
The sea was her first partner and it never let her down
So she spins it like a record, makes the whole horizon round

Pull, pull, let the moon take the lead
Push, push, give the shoreline what it needs
She's dancing with the ocean under disco-colored skies
Teaching the tide how to rise, rise, rise

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Clock on the wall, two minutes to bleed
Down by a hand and the crowd holds its breath
We came from the bottom of nobody's list
No cameras, no sponsors, just sweat and a fist

This is the night, baby, this is the line
Everything we lost, we get back one more time
They wrote us off, but they never wrote it down
Watch the longshots wear the crown

My knees remember every empty stand
The coach who believed when nobody else can
We practiced in the dark with a half-deflated dream
Now the whole world's watching this impossible team

This is the night, baby, this is the line
Everything we lost, we get back one more time
They wrote us off, but they never wrote it down
Watch the longshots wear the crown

Last possession, my heartbeat's a drum
For every kid they told would never come
I catch the rock and the gym goes still
If you doubted us before, baby, doubt us still

This is the night, baby, this is the line
Everything we lost, we get back one more time
The buzzer's gonna scream and the underdogs stand proud
Watch the longshots wear the crown

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They drew our names from the same gold hat
My enemy across the floor in a crimson suit
The spotlight swung and it landed on us both
Now your hand is on my waist and I can't move

One, two, hate me on the downbeat
Three, four, you can't break this routine
We're spinning out our war beneath the mirror ball
Forced to be the closest thing of all

You stepped on every dream I ever had
Now you're counting out my rhythm in my ear
I'd push you off the stage if it weren't lit
But your timing is the only thing that's clear

One, two, hate me on the downbeat
Three, four, you can't break this routine
We're spinning out our war beneath the mirror ball
Forced to be the closest thing of all

Somewhere in the dip I stopped pretending
The glitter caught your eyes and they were kind
The song that meant to break us kept us bending
Turns out my worst rival kept perfect time with mine

One, two, fall for me on the downbeat
Three, four, never break this routine
We danced our way past every reason to be cold
Two rivals turning, turning into gold

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up the crates at the midnight tide
The captain wouldn't say what was packed inside
The harbor cat ran screaming down the plank
And every gull went silent as we sank into the dark

Pick it up, pick it up, the hold is full of bones
The cargo wants a captain and it won't sail home
Upstroke on the guitar while the ocean turns to wine
We're cursed and we're skanking on the brine

The compass spins a circle and it points at me
The rum turned green and the biscuits learned to breathe
The first mate saw his shadow climb the mast alone
And whispered to the rigging in a voice that wasn't his own

Pick it up, pick it up, the hold is full of bones
The cargo wants a captain and it won't sail home
Upstroke on the guitar while the ocean turns to wine
We're cursed and we're skanking on the brine

Horns up, horns up, blow it to the storm
Dance until the morning that will never come
We traded all our gold for a chest we couldn't lift
Now the only port we'll ever reach is the drift

Pick it up, pick it up, the hold is full of bones
The cargo wants a captain and it won't sail home
Upstroke on the guitar while the ocean turns to wine
We're cursed and we're skanking on the brine

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slid shut like a soft green tide
And the bossa of my breathing lost its time
Fluorescent hum, a hundred strangers' coats
My heart began a samba I never wrote

Breathe slow, breathe low, let the rails carry me
Count the stations like a quiet melody
The train will surface to the sun, I know
Just hold the rhythm soft and let it go

A tunnel swallowed all the city light
My ribs a cage around a frightened bird
The map above me melting into lines
And no one heard the storm without a word

Breathe slow, breathe low, let the rails carry me
Count the stations like a quiet melody
The train will surface to the sun, I know
Just hold the rhythm soft and let it go

A stranger's hand, a paper cup of calm
She hummed a tune that softened every alarm
The wheels became a brush across a drum
And slowly, slowly, I became someone

Breathe slow, breathe low, the rails are carrying me
The doors will open to a gentle balcony of street
The panic was a wave, and waves recede
I stepped into the light and I was free

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
There's a lantern by the highway that never goes dark
Where the coffee's always brewing and the booths hold a spark
Old Maggie keeps the griddle and the door without a key
For the lost ones and the lonely and the souls like you and me

So come in from the cold of the long winding road
There's a seat by the window and a story to be told
The fiddle's in the corner and the kettle starts to sing
This diner never sleeps and the dawn it always brings

The trucker and the widow and the boy who ran away
All gather round the counter where the night gives way to day
A pie cools on the saucer, the jukebox plays a reel
And no one here's a stranger when you're sitting down to a meal

So come in from the cold of the long winding road
There's a seat by the window and a story to be told
The fiddle's in the corner and the kettle starts to sing
This diner never sleeps and the dawn it always brings

When the whole town's gone to dreaming and the church bells lose their chime
There's a light that keeps on burning at the edge of weary time
Maggie pours another cup and pats my tired hand
Says the door is always open for the ones who understand

So come in from the cold of the long winding road
There's a seat by the window and a story to be told
The fiddle's in the corner and the kettle starts to sing
This diner never sleeps and the dawn it always brings

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors slam shut, no air in the car
Walls coming closer, can't tell where you are
Lights overhead like a strobe in my skull
Forty strangers and the silence is full

CAN'T BREATHE in the underground
Heart's a hammer, no way out now
Scream it silent, claw the rail
The tunnel's got me, I will not fail

Sweat on my collar, the platform's a mile
Every second stretching like a hostile trial
My hands are shaking, the map's a blur
I'm drowning standing up in a steel-bolted blur

CAN'T BREATHE in the underground
Heart's a hammer, no way out now
Scream it silent, claw the rail
The tunnel's got me, I will not fail

Ground my feet, I count to four
This is a wave, not a slammed-shut door
It rises, it crests, it tears, it breaks
I'm still standing for my own damn sake

I BREATHE in the underground
Heart still pounding but I hold my ground
Doors fly open, daylight's near
I walked through fire and I'm still here

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Empty kitchen, one cold knife
Half a recipe, the rest of my life
Her apron hangs where it always did
I burn the water 'cause I can't keep the lid

COOK FOR ONE in a house built for two
Every meal's a war I never knew
Smash the plate or learn to feed
This grief is starving, this grief is greed

Freezer full of what we'll never share
I throw it out and the silence is a glare
She taught me nothing 'cause I never asked
Now I'm bleeding garlic, choking on the past

COOK FOR ONE in a house built for two
Every meal's a war I never knew
Smash the plate or learn to feed
This grief is starving, this grief is greed

So I light the stove with a shaking hand
Make the thing she made, try to understand
It tastes like nothing, it tastes like her
I eat the whole damn pot, I don't say a word

I COOK FOR ONE and I keep the flame
Learn her name in every single grain
I will not starve, I will not break
I'll set one plate for both our sakes

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Down in the back room the lantern burned low
I laid my last dollar where the good Lord said no
The deck was a serpent, the table a sin
I bet what I had and I bet it to win

Oh Lord, I went too far
Traded my soul for a queen and a scar
Deal me out, deal me clean
Wash these aces from the hands of a fool I've been

My children were sleeping while I chased the draw
The rent and the ring and my mama's last shawl
The man across from me had eyes like a grave
And I shook hands with everything I never could save

Oh Lord, I went too far
Traded my soul for a queen and a scar
Deal me out, deal me clean
Wash these aces from the hands of a fool I've been

Then I rose from that table with empty pockets and shame
Walked into the morning and I cried out His name
There's a grace that don't gamble, there's a love that won't fold
And it bought back the soul that I sold, that I sold

Oh Lord, I came back home
Laid down the cards and I won't roll the bones
Deal me out, deal me free
There's a mercy at the table that was waiting for me

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
The cookbook's open to the page she dog-eared
Grease on the spine from a hundred shared years
I halve the measurements and lose my place
The microwave clock blinks the wrong time at my face

I'm learning to cook for one
Under a kitchen bulb and a setting sun
Too much pasta, not enough nerve
I'm feeding a ghost that I don't deserve

The grocery cart feels too big and too clean
A single tomato, a sad tangerine
The checkout girl smiles like she doesn't know
That the fridge at home is a museum of slow

I'm learning to cook for one
Under a kitchen bulb and a setting sun
Too much pasta, not enough nerve
I'm feeding a ghost that I don't deserve

And maybe Tuesday I'll invite a friend
Set two plates just to pretend
But tonight it's me and the radio's drone
Learning that one isn't quite the same as alone

I'm learning to cook for one
Under a kitchen bulb and a setting sun
It's burnt and it's quiet and it's mine
And somehow I'm doing fine, I'm doing fine

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Knuckles like riverstone, worn from the years
Threaded a needle through famine and tears
Hands that buried and hands that fed
Hands that held the family by a thread

THESE HANDS built everything you stand on
These hands fought a war you never ran from
Scarred and shaking, never weak
The whole damn lineage in the lines they keep

She scrubbed the floors of a country not her own
Sent every dollar to a distant home
Hands that prayed when the rent came due
Hands that made a fortress out of nothing for you

THESE HANDS built everything you stand on
These hands fought a war you never ran from
Scarred and shaking, never weak
The whole damn lineage in the lines they keep

Now they fold so small in the hospital light
But they squeeze back hard, she's still got the fight
I press my palm to the map of her skin
And swear I'll carry every mile she's been

THESE HANDS built everything I stand on
These hands gave me ground to plant a song on
When she's gone I'll clench my own
And feel her grip in every bone

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Diez años de silencio, the phone in my hand
The trumpet of my heart playing songs I can't stand
I dial the old number I never let go
And the line starts to ring like a slow violin's bow

Mamá, it's me, can you hear through the years
The pride that I swallowed, the river of tears
Forgive the long silence, the words I withheld
I'm calling you home, mamá, calling you home

I left in the heat of a thing I can't name
Too stubborn to whisper, too wounded to claim
Now the guitars are weeping the time that I lost
And I'd pay any bridge for whatever it costs

Mamá, it's me, can you hear through the years
The pride that I swallowed, the river of tears
Forgive the long silence, the words I withheld
I'm calling you home, mamá, calling you home

And she answers so softly, mijo, where have you been
The violins rise and the morning comes in
The years fall away like the rind from the fruit
And love, the old anthem, plays loud and absolute

Mamá, it's me, and I'm coming back home
No more years of silence, no more roads to roam
The mariachi is singing what I should have said
Te quiero, mamá, te quiero, you heard

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights of that city were close as my breath
A handshake away from the dream I had left
The banjo was rolling, the crowd leaned in near
And I tasted the win on the rim of the year

Oh, the night I almost made it
Held the whole sky in my hand
Then the wind turned around and it took it
Like a footprint washed off the sand

The man with the contract, the pen in his coat
The last verse I sang stuck a frog in my throat
A string snapped clean and the moment went cold
And the door that swung open swung shut on the gold

Oh, the night I almost made it
Held the whole sky in my hand
Then the wind turned around and it took it
Like a footprint washed off the sand

Now I play for the porch and the cicada hum
For the children and the river and the days yet to come
And I don't curse the almost, I tip my old hat
There's a grace in the missing, I've made peace with that

Oh, the night I almost made it
Still rings like a string in the dark
I didn't quite take it, but I played it
And the music still lives in my heart

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Met you on the boardwalk, sunscreen and lime
You stole my fries and you stole my time
A polaroid heart in the golden hour glow
Three months of forever we both knew would go

Summer skin and the saltwater kiss
We knew it would end but we wanted it like this
Dance in the dark where the tide meets the town
Love like a sunset, going down, down, down

Neon on the pier and your laugh in the waves
We spent every dollar, kept none that we saved
You wrote your number in the sand with your toe
The ocean came up and it took it to go

Summer skin and the saltwater kiss
We knew it would end but we wanted it like this
Dance in the dark where the tide meets the town
Love like a sunset, going down, down, down

September's gonna take you on a plane far away
And I'll keep the seashell and the things we didn't say
It wasn't supposed to last, it was only supposed to burn
A flame on the water with no return

Summer skin and the saltwater kiss
We knew it would end but we wanted it like this
Dance in the dark where the tide meets the town
Love like a sunset, going down, down, down

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle in the morning, thread between his teeth
Little shop on the corner where the whole block meets
He knows your shoulders better than your mama do
Takes a sad old jacket, makes it brand new

He dress the whole block, stitch by stitch
Make a poor man walk like he rich
Every seam a sermon, every hem a prayer
He put love in the lining, you can feel it there

Sunday suit for the deacon, sharp from head to shoe
Wedding for the youngblood, he'll see him through
He measures more than fabric, he measures the man
Holds the whole neighborhood in the palm of his hand

He dress the whole block, stitch by stitch
Make a poor man walk like he rich
Every seam a sermon, every hem a prayer
He put love in the lining, you can feel it there

When they lay him down they gonna line up at the door
In the suits he gave 'em, sharper than before
Gold thread, golden hands, golden soul
He dressed the whole block, now the block is whole

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors slide shut and the lights flicker low
Heartbeat racing faster than the train can go
Walls coming closer, air getting thin
Stranger's eyes upon me, I can't pull it in

Don't stop, don't stop, gotta breathe again
Underground and spinning, where does it end
Four more stops till the open sky
Hold on, hold on, don't you let it ride

Neon in the tunnel like a warning sign
Every rattle louder, every second a line
Count the lights along the platform wall
One, two, three, I won't let it fall

Don't stop, don't stop, gotta breathe again
Underground and spinning, where does it end
Four more stops till the open sky
Hold on, hold on, don't you let it ride

And the city keeps moving like it never even knew
That I died and came back somewhere between the avenues
Then the doors slide open and the daylight calls my name
I step into the morning, and I'm breathing again

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Sold the house, burned the maps behind
Left the only country I could call mine
Salt in the throat and a one-way fare
No god, no flag, no welcome there

Across the sea, across the sea
They took my name, they won't take me
Every wave a wall they built to break
I'm still standing for my children's sake

Cold steel hull and a thousand strangers' eyes
Mother tongue dying under foreign skies
They want my hands, they don't want my soul
Grind me down but they'll never make me whole

Across the sea, across the sea
They took my name, they won't take me
Every wave a wall they built to break
I'm still standing for my children's sake

TELL ME WHERE I BELONG
WHEN BOTH MY SHORES ARE GONE
I carry home inside my chest
No border crossed it, no man blessed
Across the sea I rise, I rise

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Smoke curls slow in the amber light
Your empty chair across from mine tonight
The band plays soft what we used to know
A melody I let you go

You're the one that got away
The note I never learned to play
In every song I'll hear your name
And love you all the same

The whiskey warms but it can't replace
The quiet way you'd touch my face
I had forever in my hand
And let it run like grains of sand

You're the one that got away
The note I never learned to play
In every song I'll hear your name
And love you all the same

If you walked back through that door tonight
I'd give up every wrong to make it right
But the last train's gone and the hour is late
And love don't wait, no, love don't wait

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Wheels on the highway, sky turning green
Chasing the thunder like a restless dream
You want me to stay, baby, plant my feet
But the wind's in my blood and it won't let me sleep

No me puedo quedar, I'm chasing the storm
Where the lightning is dancing, that's where I belong
Dale, dale, the funnel's coming down
I was never made to settle down

You packed me a home with a picket and a key
But the radar lights up and it's calling to me
Every siren a lover, every cloud a goodbye
I live for the moment the world meets the sky

No me puedo quedar, I'm chasing the storm
Where the lightning is dancing, that's where I belong
Dale, dale, the funnel's coming down
I was never made to settle down

Maybe one day the sky will run dry
And I'll come back home with the dust in my eye
But tonight there's a wall cloud over the plain
And I'm gone, mami, gone in the rain

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Gray on the rocks where the old man stands
Salt-bitten knuckles, the sea in his hands
He came before us, he'll outlast our names
Teaching the young ones the rhythm of the waves

LISTEN TO THE TIDE
IT REMEMBERS, IT DON'T LIE
What he gives he gives for free
The oldest law beneath the sea

You take what you need and you give the rest back
You read the gray water, you learn what it lacks
The young want it now, want to strip it all bare
But the elder just points where the cormorants are

LISTEN TO THE TIDE
IT REMEMBERS, IT DON'T LIE
What he gives he gives for free
The oldest law beneath the sea

WHEN HE'S GONE WHO WILL TEACH
The pull of the moon, the give of the beach
Stand on the shore, put your hand in mine
We are the elders now — hold the line, hold the line

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine humming low in the midnight rain
One more run and I'm done with this game
Three minutes, baby, that's all I need
Then I'm gone where the city can't follow me

Last job, last ride
Keys in the ignition, devil at my side
One foot on the brake, one foot in the grave
Last job, then I'm saved

They come out running and the clock won't wait
Red light spilling like a stroke of fate
I know these alleys like the back of my hand
One clean exit and a brand new man

Last job, last ride
Keys in the ignition, devil at my side
One foot on the brake, one foot in the grave
Last job, then I'm saved

Somewhere a woman keeps a porch light on
She don't know what I done, just that I'll be home by dawn
So I'll drive, drive, into the blue
One last time, then I'm through

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Lord, I told myself this is the final time
Hands on the wheel and a heart full of crime
The city's asleep but my conscience ain't
Driving these sinners, I'm losing my saint

This my last job, oh, this my last ride
Gonna leave all this running far behind
Take me home, take me home to her arms
Where a tired man can lay down his harm

Engine's praying with a holy roll
Three blocks down and I'm saving my soul
They count the money but I count the cost
Every dollar I made is a year that I lost

This my last job, oh, this my last ride
Gonna leave all this running far behind
Take me home, take me home to her arms
Where a tired man can lay down his harm

Oh, when the morning comes and the sirens fade
I'll be a free man, every debt I've paid
No more shadows, no more flight
Just me and the long road into the light

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
Canto a la luna que no me responde
Como tu corazón que de mí se esconde
I bring you roses, I bring you my song
But your window stays dark all night long

Ay, amor que no me quieres
Me muero por tus ojos altaneros
I'll sing till the dawn breaks the sky
For a love that won't love me, ay, ay, ay

The trumpets they cry what my lips cannot say
That you'll marry another come the wedding day
I'll raise up my glass and I'll smile through the pain
Then ride my horse home through the rain

Ay, amor que no me quieres
Me muero por tus ojos altaneros
I'll sing till the dawn breaks the sky
For a love that won't love me, ay, ay, ay

Que viva el amor aunque me mate
I'd rather love you than to never ache
So here in the plaza I'll stand all alone
Singing to a heart that was never my own

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Thirty years gone and I still can feel
Them cold wooden boards and the stage lights' steel
Guitar out of tune and my hands won't quit shakin'
Fifteen years old and my whole heart breakin'

Oh, that first open mic
Scared half to death in the smoky light
Three people clappin' and two walkin' out
But I found what my whole life's about

Forgot the second verse, made up the rest
Voice cracked wide open, did my level best
Old man at the bar gave a knowing nod
Like he'd seen this fool road, this long hard slog

Oh, that first open mic
Scared half to death in the smoky light
Three people clappin' and two walkin' out
But I found what my whole life's about

Now the rooms got bigger but the fear's the same
Every night I climb up and I play this game
And I'd give it all back just to stand once more
A scared little kid on that creaking floor

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Soft waves on the shoreline, the sun hanging low
I carried this picture through the mud and the snow
Now the palm trees are swaying, the boat coming in
And I'm trembling to touch the ones I left then

Coming home, coming home
No more rifles, no more roam
Just the warm Brazilian breeze
And your arms to set me at ease

I buried my brothers in a faraway field
What I saw over there I will never reveal
But the café is open and the music still plays
And maybe in time I'll forget those days

Coming home, coming home
No more rifles, no more roam
Just the warm Brazilian breeze
And your arms to set me at ease

The boys who don't come back, I'll carry them along
In the soft and quiet rhythm of this gentle song
So pour me a coffee, let me sit by the sea
Thank God, thank God, I'm finally free

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The meadow's gone golden, the swallows fly south
And the taste of this summer still sweet in my mouth
Tomorrow I'm leaving for a city of stone
But tonight by the river I'm never alone

Oh, the last day of summer, hold it tight
Before the world comes and steals the light
Lay down in the heather, sing one more time
For the green hills of home that won't always be mine

We danced by the bonfire, we drank to the years
We laughed like the children we were, fighting back tears
My mother's at the window with a light in her hand
And a piece of my heart that will stay on this land

Oh, the last day of summer, hold it tight
Before the world comes and steals the light
Lay down in the heather, sing one more time
For the green hills of home that won't always be mine

When the autumn leaves fall on a stranger's town
I'll close my eyes and lay my head down
And I'll be right back here where the wild rivers run
On the last golden day before everything's done

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Well I gassed up the Ford 'fore the rooster could crow
One last run down that old county road
The boys in the back with a bag full of green
Fastest clean wheels this county's ever seen

It's my last job, pickin' up speed
Lord give me the road and the Lord give me leave
One more mile and I'm hangin' it up
Drive, boys, drive, 'fore our luck runs up

The banjo's a-rollin' like the wheels on the line
Sheriff's behind us but he's too far behind
I know every holler, every creek, every bend
This old dog runs faster when it's near the end

It's my last job, pickin' up speed
Lord give me the road and the Lord give me leave
One more mile and I'm hangin' it up
Drive, boys, drive, 'fore our luck runs up

Gonna buy me a farm where the tall grass grows
Hang up these keys where nobody knows
Trade the fast life for a slow porch swing
But tonight, by God, let these tires sing

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Yeah, late night, scrollin' through your name again
Should've never let you go, now I'm payin' for my sins
Ice on my wrist but it's cold where my heart been
Got the world in my hand but I lost you in the win

You the one that got away (got away)
No amount of racks gonna make you stay (make you stay)
I was chasing all this money, lost you on the way (on the way)
Now I'm rich and I'm alone, what a price to pay

New car, new chain, same empty bed
Replay all them memories playin' in my head
Text you then I delete it 'fore I press send
Pride got me lonely, that's the message that it sends

You the one that got away (got away)
No amount of racks gonna make you stay (make you stay)
I was chasing all this money, lost you on the way (on the way)
Now I'm rich and I'm alone, what a price to pay

If I could go back, yeah, I'd trade it all
The fame, the lights, every empty hall
Just to hold you one more time before you walked
But you gone, you gone, and the line stay locked

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down on a gray downtown street
One broken umbrella, two pairs of wet feet
You said come on under, there's room for us both
And we walked twelve blocks in the cold and the smoke

Just two strangers in the rain
Sharing one umbrella down a nameless lane
Didn't know your story, didn't know your name
But for nine city blocks we were just the same

You told me your troubles, I told you mine
Two lonely rivers running parallel lines
The rain it kept fallin' like it knew our hearts
Kept us walkin' slow so we wouldn't part

Just two strangers in the rain
Sharing one umbrella down a nameless lane
Didn't know your story, didn't know your name
But for nine city blocks we were just the same

Then the rain it stopped and the sun broke through
And you went your way and I lost you
But I still think about that borrowed time
When a stranger's shoulder pressed to mine

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
I ran a wire to the water tower
where the static breathes in violet
Gave a borrowed name to the empty hour
and let the feedback ride it

Three watts of us against the county line
a hum beneath the gauze and glow
Someone in a parked car learning all the words
to songs the daylight doesn't know

We are the signal in the bruise
the wash, the drone, the buried news
Turn me up until I disappear
I'm only here, I'm only here

The van outside is just a rumor now
they never find the tower in the rain
I press my palm against the speaker cloth
and feel the whole town leaning into the pane

We are the signal in the bruise
the wash, the drone, the buried news
Turn me up until I disappear
I'm only here, I'm only here

When they pull the plug I'll go to noise
a soft collapsing star of voice
A last reverb against the dark
a thumbprint, then the carrier spark

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Midnight and the dial goes soft and gold
I'm broadcasting from a bedroom in the cold
A secret frequency the city can't hold
a ribbon of sound around the bend

I dedicate this one to no one and to you
the boy who can't sleep, the girl in the blue
the insomniac choir of a quiet avenue
follow my voice to the end

Find me on the in-between
the channel that they've never seen
I float above the sleeping town
turn the volume up, don't turn me down

The needle's worn, the antenna leans askew
I play the slow ones till the morning comes through
a pirate flag of feedback, pink and new
and no one knows my name

Find me on the in-between
the channel that they've never seen
I float above the sleeping town
turn the volume up, don't turn me down

When the dawn comes pale and breaks the spell
I'll fade into a hum, a wishing well
A frequency you swear you dreamed you heard
a weightless, half-remembered word

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Same cracked sidewalk, same old corner store
you came back from the city for the summer like before
I been here the whole time, never went too far
now you're laughing in the parking lot beside my car

We used to ride the same bus, knew the same dumb songs
funny how a small town keeps the feeling going strong
You point at the diner where we used to waste the day
and something in my chest just blows the dust away

Hey, you're a hometown girl and I'm a hometown fool
fell for you again right outside the school
Pick it up, pick it up, the horns are gonna blow
I loved you back when neither of us know

The water tower's faded but it's still got your name
the whole place stayed exactly, beautifully the same
You say you might stay longer, I say maybe so
and try to play it cooler than the trumpet's gonna show

Hey, you're a hometown girl and I'm a hometown fool
fell for you again right outside the school
Pick it up, pick it up, the horns are gonna blow
I loved you back when neither of us know

Skank it down the avenue, the streetlights coming on
fifteen years of nothing and it's suddenly not gone
If the city calls you back I'll wave you down the road
but tonight you're a hometown girl and I'm not letting go

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Old Brother Davis got a board on the table in the shade
five dollars a game and a smile like he already got paid
He move the pawn like a preacher move a soul
slow and certain, knowing right where it gon' go

They come from the offices, come from the street
think they got him cornered, then they sittin' in defeat
He say son, every move you make is wrote
he tap the clock and laugh and clear his throat

Oh, the King is gonna fall, the King is gonna fall
the mighty and the proud, He gonna humble them all
Lay your pieces down, lay your burden in the square
there's a hustler in the park and there's grace in the air

He lost a daughter, lost a job, lost the keys to his door
but he never lost the patience that the table's waiting for
Every checkmate is a sermon, every win a little psalm
he collect the bills and bless 'em with a calm

Oh, the King is gonna fall, the King is gonna fall
the mighty and the proud, He gonna humble them all
Lay your pieces down, lay your burden in the square
there's a hustler in the park and there's grace in the air

When the sun go down he fold the board and pray
thank You for the players that You sent his way
Every game a testimony, every move a hymn
he been hustled by the world but he still hustles for Him

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
There's a gravel road outside of Tucumcari
where I left the dust of a girl I couldn't carry
She wanted somewhere green, I wanted to roam
so I drove off slow and I called the truck my home

I still got the postcard that I never sent
the ink gone soft from every place I went
Her name like a station fading off the dial
the kind of ache you wear out for a while

She's the one that got away
the summer I was too young to stay
Now the highway's all I know
and she's a porch light in the long ago

I hear she married steady, got a yard and a dog
lives down where the river meets the morning fog
I raise a cup of coffee to the rising sun
and toast the better life she went and won

She's the one that got away
the summer I was too young to stay
Now the highway's all I know
and she's a porch light in the long ago

Maybe in another town, another skin
I'd have learned to let somebody in
But the road don't ask me how I feel
it just keeps turning underneath the wheel

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Downtown deluge, six o'clock, the sky just split
I'm under one umbrella tryna salvage it
She ducked in beside me, said you mind if I share
I said the city's free, baby, so is the air
We walked in sync, matching steps through the flood
shoulders touching, talking over thunder and mud
She told me her name like it wasn't a thing
I held the handle up so the rain couldn't sting

Two strangers, one umbrella, ten blocks in the storm
never met before but somehow we keep warm
Light turns green, but we walking real slow
cause neither of us know which way we wanna go

We passed her train, she didn't even glance
two soaking-wet kids on an accidental dance
She laughed at my joke, I memorized the sound
the puddles throwing neon all over the ground
We reached her corner, the rain started to thin
the worst part of luck is when the dry sets in
She stepped to the curb, said this is me, I guess
and I felt the whole weather of the loneliness

Never got the number, never got the rest
just a borrowed mile and a rain-soaked chest
But every time it pours I look around the street
for a stranger and a corner where two umbrellas meet

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The spotlight found me trembling at the edge of the floor
one step from the door I'd been dreaming of before
The crowd held its breath like a knife in the dark
and I felt the whole city in the strike of a spark

They called out my name and the music began
I moved like the man I had promised I am
So close I could taste the applause on my tongue
so near to the life I'd been chasing so long

Almost, almost, the word like a blade
the closest I came to the man I'd have made
One breath from the glory, one beat out of time
the night I almost climbed

Then the heel caught the seam and the rhythm came undone
the music kept turning but my moment was gone
They clapped to be kind and they looked away slow
and the floor swallowed up everything I didn't know

Almost, almost, the word like a blade
the closest I came to the man I'd have made
One breath from the glory, one beat out of time
the night I almost climbed

I still dance it alone in the dark of my room
the steps that I missed in that ballroom of perfume
And I bow to the silence, I bow to the ghost
of the night I came closest, the night that I lost the most

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Empty parking lot, Sunday, sky like honey
you got both hands on the wheel, ten and two
Knuckles tight, you laughing 'cause you're nervous
and baby, I'm a little nervous too

I remember you barefoot on the dashboard
now your foot's on the brake, easy now
The radio low, the engine humming
look at God, look how you figured it out

Go on, ease it forward, take your time
the whole wide road is yours and mine
I'll be right here in the passenger seat
letting go a little, sweet, so sweet

You check the mirror like I taught you
you signal to a lane that's empty and clear
And I see the woman that you're becoming
in the quiet of the gearshift, year by year

Go on, ease it forward, take your time
the whole wide road is yours and mine
I'll be right here in the passenger seat
letting go a little, sweet, so sweet

One day soon you'll drive away for real
taillights getting small on some far-off hill
But tonight you're mine and the lot is wide
and I'm teaching you to leave with a little pride

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up on the rooftop with a borrowed mic
a bottle of rye and a city at night
I spin the slow ones nobody will play
for the cab drivers running the blue away

No license, no callsign, no boss on the line
just a needle, a flame, and a quarter past nine
I talk to the lonely, I talk to the moon
and somewhere a saxophone answers the tune

This is the station that isn't there
a ghost on the airwave, a smoke in the air
Find me at the bottom of the dial tonight
where the outlaws and the lovers get it right

The signal goes wandering over the town
slipping past the towers where the suits shut it down
A trumpet, a whisper, a story half-told
and the after-hours world turning soft and gold

This is the station that isn't there
a ghost on the airwave, a smoke in the air
Find me at the bottom of the dial tonight
where the outlaws and the lovers get it right

When the sun comes up I'll cut the power down
fold my little riot back into the ground
But for one more chorus the night is mine
a pirate and a piano on the borderline

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
The peat fire's low and the kettle's gone cold
I'm reading three words that are leaving me old
No letter, no doorway, no hand at the gate
just a light on a screen and a love come too late

We danced in the heather when the summer was long
you hummed me the old airs, you taught me the song
But you faded like mist off the loch in the morn
and you left by a wire on a grey winter's dawn

Oh the message came soft and the message came cruel
"I can't do this longer," and I stood like a fool
No banshee, no thunder, no toll of the bell
just a buzz in my pocket to ring my farewell

My grandmother grieved with a wail and a tear
with a wake and a fiddle and the whole village near
But I grieve in the silence with a phone in my hand
for a love that dissolved like a word writ in sand

Oh the message came soft and the message came cruel
"I can't do this longer," and I stood like a fool
No banshee, no thunder, no toll of the bell
just a buzz in my pocket to ring my farewell

So I'll walk to the shore where the cold water turns
and I'll throw in the phone with the last of my yearns
Let the tide take the words and the screen and the blue
and carry to the deep what I never said to you

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors slam shut and the lights go bright
tunnel swallows up the morning light
My collar's too tight and the car's too packed
and my heart's playing drums I can't take back

I count the poles and I count the stops
count the beating till the rhythm drops
Everybody's reading, nobody can see
there's a hurricane happening inside of me

Breathe, two, three, four, hold the rail
ride it like a horn that won't let you fail
The train's gonna stop and the doors gonna part
just swing with the panic, swing with the heart

The stranger beside me taps her foot
and somehow it lines up with my racing root
I catch the beat and I match her time
and the terror turns into a kind of rhyme

Breathe, two, three, four, hold the rail
ride it like a horn that won't let you fail
The train's gonna stop and the doors gonna part
just swing with the panic, swing with the heart

Daylight cracks at the top of the stair
I'm shaking but I'm climbing into the air
The city don't know that I almost broke
but I beat the dark with a four-four stroke

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine idling in a low blue hum
the radio quiet, the meeting come
Three minutes counted on the dashboard glow
this is the last one, the last I'll ever know

Rain on the windshield like a distant sea
the streetlights breathing soft and slow on me
I memorize the corners, I memorize the air
the weightless kind of stillness of a man no longer there

Drive, just drive, let the city dissolve
no names, no faces, nothing to solve
One more empty highway, one more open door
and I'm gone, I'm gone, I won't come back for more

They climb in fast and the doors come down
we pour like water through the sleeping town
No siren follows, no light, no sound
just the long exhale of the wheels on the ground

Somewhere past the bridge I let them out
and I keep on rolling with no need to shout
The dawn is a rumor, the tank is half full
and I drift toward a morning with nothing to pull
Drive, just drive, till the engine goes still
and the last job fades over the last blue hill

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning, the highway is mine
phone to my ear and the diesel running fine
I dial my baby though I know she's asleep
just to hear the voicemail, just a promise to keep

Miles like a river, the night like a song
I been gone so long, girl, I been gone so long
Kiss the children for me, tell them daddy's on the road
bringing home the money, carrying the load

Hello, hello, can you hear me from afar
I'm a moving light beneath a thousand stars
Three a.m. and I'm calling out your name
oh my love, my home, you're the reason that I came

The truck stop neon, the coffee gone cold
but your laugh in my memory is worth more than gold
Two more days of driving and I'll turn down our street
and the engine will rest and our two hearts will meet

Hello, hello, can you hear me from afar
I'm a moving light beneath a thousand stars
Three a.m. and I'm calling out your name
oh my love, my home, you're the reason that I came

Dance for me, baby, when I pull in the yard
we go move our feet though the leaving was hard
Every mile I drive is a mile back to you
three a.m. on the line and my heart's coming through

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Backstage, hands shaking, told 'em I was ready
lights on my face, but my pulse wasn't steady
One shot, one stage, one A&R in the crowd
five years of the basement about to get loud
Hit my first verse, had 'em rocking with me
tasted the future like it's already free
Then the beat cut out and the mic went dead
and the whole room watched all the dreams in my head

So close, so close, had it right in my hand
the night I almost made it, almost touched the promised land
Lights up, walked off, swallowed all the pain
closest that I came, closest that I came

A&R left early, didn't catch the recover
found a tech to fix it but the moment was over
Played the rest perfect to a half-empty floor
the ones who really love me, they was wanting more
Drove home in the dark with the radio low
replaying the silence, replaying the show
One wire, one fuse, one cruel little break
between me and everything I tried to make

But I kept the receipt of that broken night
framed it on the wall where I write
'Cause almost is a fuel and it burns real slow
and next time the lights hit, baby, I won't let go

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The door stopped slamming and the words ran dry
you're in the kitchen and I don't know why
we took the smallest thing and made it a war
now it's quiet, too quiet, on the kitchen floor

The clock on the wall is the loudest sound
two coffee cups cooling, both turned down
I can hear you breathing in the other room
and the silence got heavier than all the boom

In the quiet, in the quiet after the storm
I just want to hold you, want to keep you warm
Pick it up, pick it up, let the offbeat play
somebody come and chase this hush away

I rehearse my sorry but it sounds all wrong
so I put on a record, an easy little song
You peek round the doorway, your eyes still red
and your foot starts tapping to the thing I said

In the quiet, in the quiet after the storm
I just want to hold you, want to keep you warm
Pick it up, pick it up, let the offbeat play
somebody come and chase this hush away

So we dance it out slow in our socks on the tile
no words, just the horns and a half of a smile
The fight's still there but it's losing its grip
and we skank back together, hip to hip

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I walked down to the water where we used to disagree
The morning sun was rising up and shining over me
I carried all your wrongs around like stones inside my chest
But stones get heavy, brother, and a heart was made to rest

So I forgive you, even though you're gone
I lay it down beside the rising dawn
No letter coming back, no word to say
But I set it free and I let it drift away

They told me hold the anger like it kept you somehow near
But all it kept was bitterness and one more salty tear
The man who did me wrong is dust beneath the cotton tree
And holding on to ghosts won't ever set a poor man free

So I forgive you, even though you're gone
I lay it down beside the rising dawn
No letter coming back, no word to say
But I set it free and I let it drift away

Mmm, the wind don't argue, the river just flows
It carries what it carries to the place nobody knows
And somewhere in the silence I can finally breathe
The weight I dropped this morning was the weight of you and me

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The dishes in the sink have stopped their rattle
The kettle's gone cold on the stove
We said our hardest words like rounds of battle
Now there's nothing left but cold

And it's quiet, oh it's quiet
Louder than the things we said
You're in the other room and breathing
I'm awake here in this bed

I hear the floorboard where you almost wander
Then you turn and walk away
A hundred little doors I'd give to open
But I don't know what to say

And it's quiet, oh it's quiet
Louder than the things we said
You're in the other room and breathing
I'm awake here in this bed

If love is just the choosing to come back
Then let me be the first to cross the floor
I'll leave the lamp on burning in the hallway
And I'll leave the latch undone on every door

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Three chords and a borrowed van
Sticky floor and a busted amp
Fourteen kids and a guy from the label
Standing in the back too cool to clap

We almost made it, almost made it
One more inch and we'd have torn the roof off the sky
We almost made it, almost made it
Then the lights came up and the night went by

He gave us a card, said he'd call us soon
Never called, never wrote, never knew our name
But for forty minutes under that one light
We were bigger than the whole damn game

We almost made it, almost made it
One more inch and we'd have torn the roof off the sky
We almost made it, almost made it
Then the lights came up and the night went by

So what, so what, we sold the gear
We got the day jobs, we disappeared
But play that tape and I swear to God
We almost made it, we almost made it
We almost made it that one night

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
The steam rose up like prayers off the pot
Onions and honey, the whole world she'd got
Flour on her apron, a song in her throat
A candle, a challah, a coat on a coat

Oh the smell of her kitchen, it follows me yet
Through the years and the winters I cannot forget
Garlic and cinnamon, brisket and rye
The whole of my childhood in one rising sigh

She'd hum the old country between every stir
A village long burned that still lived inside her
She fed every cousin, every stranger, every guest
And never sat down, never paused, never rest

Oh the smell of her kitchen, it follows me yet
Through the years and the winters I cannot forget
Garlic and cinnamon, brisket and rye
The whole of my childhood in one rising sigh

Now the stove has gone cold and the table is bare
But I open a jar and she's suddenly there
Dance, little fiddle, and cry if you must
For a kitchen of memory, of honey and dust

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Fifty years she stood at that old stove
Now I'm standing where her shadow used to be
The recipe card is written in her hand
But the measurements were always just for three

Lord, teach me how to cook for one
When the bread still rises bigger than my need
Lord, teach me how to live for one
And be thankful for the table You still feed

I burned the rice and I cried into the pan
Not for the rice but for the empty chair
I set two plates from forty years of habit
Then I bowed my head and left them sitting there

Lord, teach me how to cook for one
When the bread still rises bigger than my need
Lord, teach me how to live for one
And be thankful for the table You still feed

She's at a bigger table now, I know
Where the feast goes on and the cup is never dry
So I'll learn to stir this little pot of mine
Till the day You call me home to sit nearby

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Pick it up, pick it up, the clock says half past two
Neon buzzing pink and the coffee's burning through
Trucker in the corner with a story he won't tell
Waitress named Diane keeping time and giving hell

We never close, we never close
The grill stays hot and the jukebox goes
Whoever you are and wherever you've been
There's a stool with your name and we'll let you in

Lovers fresh from fighting come to make it right with pie
Drunks come in for refuge, cops come in for rye
The night shift is a family that no one ever planned
Under fluorescent angels and a sticky countertop band

We never close, we never close
The grill stays hot and the jukebox goes
Whoever you are and wherever you've been
There's a stool with your name and we'll let you in

Pick it up! When the rest of the city goes dark and to bed
We got eggs over easy and a roof overhead
So bring me your lonely, your broke and your blue
The sign says forever and forever is true

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The candle of my lamp burns low and gold
The rain is tapping soft against the pane
My book lies open, every word grown cold
And still I read the selfsame line again

Oh the hour is dark and the house is still
And the world is sleeping over hill and glen
But I'll keep my watch by the windowsill
Till the grey light finds me at my pen

My mother dreams, my father long asleep
The kettle's whisper is my only friend
I trade my rest for promises I'll keep
And count the lonely miles toward the end

Oh the hour is dark and the house is still
And the world is sleeping over hill and glen
But I'll keep my watch by the windowsill
Till the grey light finds me at my pen

There's a kind of holy in the quiet here
Where no one sees the labor that I give
And when the morning rings the chapel clear
I'll know it was the wakeful hours I lived

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up in the attic with a borrowed wire
A hand-built tower and a holy fire
They said our voice was against the law
But I answer to a higher call

We're broadcasting good news on a stolen band
Oh signal travel out across the land
They can jam the airwaves, they can shut the door
But the gospel's gonna ride the static evermore

Midnight hour and the dial is mine
Singing hope to every troubled mind
A shut-in listening, a prisoner too
God don't need a license to get through

We're broadcasting good news on a stolen band
Oh signal travel out across the land
They can jam the airwaves, they can shut the door
But the gospel's gonna ride the static evermore

If they come and take my transmitter down
I'll sing it from the corner, sing it through the town
For the word was never something you can seize
It rides upon the wind and it travels on the breeze
Can I get a witness, turn your radio loud
We're preaching to the lonely and we're preaching to the crowd

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
The brass is weeping low on Rampart Street
A second line of mourners, dragging feet
The parasols turn slow against the grey
We came to carry one of us away

So dance with me, dance with the dead
With the trumpet and the sorrow overhead
We'll cry on the way and we'll laugh on the turn
For the body in the ground and the soul we can't return

The drummer holds a heartbeat, slow and true
Then breaks it like a promise, splitting two
From dirge into the joy of letting go
The sweetest, saddest rhythm that I know

So dance with me, dance with the dead
With the trumpet and the sorrow overhead
We'll cry on the way and we'll laugh on the turn
For the body in the ground and the soul we can't return

When they bury me, don't bury me in tears
Play it sharp and play it sweet across the years
Let the tuba shake the saints out of the stone
And dance me, darling, dance me, dance me home

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Ooh, sha-la-la, turn your dial tonight
Down past the static to my little light
I got a microphone and a heart on fire
Singing through a homemade wire

This is your pirate on the radio (radio)
Playing all the songs they won't let go (let go)
They can't catch me, no they never will
I'm the voice on the midnight hill
Ooh, ooh, on the radio

A dime-store tower and a rented room
Beaming out love through the evening gloom
For the boy in his car and the girl by the phone
So nobody dances all alone

This is your pirate on the radio (radio)
Playing all the songs they won't let go (let go)
They can't catch me, no they never will
I'm the voice on the midnight hill
Ooh, ooh, on the radio

The cops are on the corner (bum, bum, bum)
The signal's getting warmer (bum, bum, bum)
But I'll spin one more slow one for the lovers in the dark
Then I'll vanish like a whisper, like a spark
Ooh, on the radio

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Washed in the wall of a bottomless sound
Faces dissolving, the floor coming round
I was so close I could taste the light
A halo of feedback, a permanent night

We almost made it, drowned in the haze
Lost in the blur of those impossible days
The reverb is fading, the tape running thin
And I'm holding the almost we never got in

Someone was filming, somebody knew
The colors all bleeding from violet to blue
I reached for the moment, it slipped through my hands
Like water, like distance, like half-finished plans

We almost made it, drowned in the haze
Lost in the blur of those impossible days
The reverb is fading, the tape running thin
And I'm holding the almost we never got in

If I close my eyes I'm still standing there
Suspended in noise, suspended in air
The almost goes on, it never quite ends
A wave that keeps breaking and breaking again

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
My daddy's hands were mountains when I was young and small
Now I watch them shaking when he reaches for the wall
The man who carried tractors, the man who never bent
Now I count his slow steps and I wonder where he went

Oh I'm watching my father grow old, Lord, Lord
Watching that strong man fold
The river that was raging now is running cold
And I'm watching my father grow old

He used to know the answer to every single thing
Now he asks me twice the question, holds on to the swing
I button up his collar like he buttoned mine for years
And I turn so he won't catch me when I'm fighting back the tears

Oh I'm watching my father grow old, Lord, Lord
Watching that strong man fold
The river that was raging now is running cold
And I'm watching my father grow old

So I'll sit here on the porch with him and let the evening pass
I'll hold his hand a little longer, I know it cannot last
The sun is going down on the man who raised me right
And I'll carry him the way he carried me into the night

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
I remember being lifted on your shoulders to the sky
You were everything, my hero, you could never say goodbye
Now your voice has gotten softer and your steps have lost their pace
And I see the years like rivers running down your tired face

Oh, I'm watching you grow older, day by day
And I'd give back all my strength to make you stay
You gave me all your seasons, now it's mine to hold you near
Daddy, let me be the shoulder, let me carry you from here

There's a photograph of you, so young, so sure, so proud
Now you sleep in the recliner while the TV plays too loud
I used to want your blessing, now I just want one more day
To tell you that I love you in every kind of way

Oh, I'm watching you grow older, day by day
And I'd give back all my strength to make you stay
You gave me all your seasons, now it's mine to hold you near
Daddy, let me be the shoulder, let me carry you from here

Time is such a thief, oh, it takes a little more each year
But it cannot steal the love, no, it cannot steal you from right here
So I'll memorize your laughter, every line upon your hand
And I'll thank God for the father, the gentle, fading man

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We drove past the last of the streetlights tonight
Where the dark gets so big you can drown
Then the sky pulled a curtain of green into white
And the whole frozen world wore a crown

Oh the northern lights, the northern lights
Like a river of souls overhead
They ribbon and fold through the coldest of nights
And they dance for the living and the dead

My grandmother said they were spirits at play
The old ones still walking the sky
And I half believe her out here where I stay
With my breath rising up like a sigh

Oh the northern lights, the northern lights
Like a river of souls overhead
They ribbon and fold through the coldest of nights
And they dance for the living and the dead

We were so small underneath all that fire
Two specks on a turning blue stone
But for one frozen hour we wanted no higher
Than to stand there, not lonely, not alone

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I slipped out the back while the organ still played
Past the cushions and hymnals where I knelt and I prayed
Nobody noticed the door as it closed
On the faith I was given, the faith I outgrew, I suppose

So I'm walking out easy, no thunder, no rage
Just a quiet goodbye on a soft empty page
I loved you, old steeple, I loved your old song
But the pew got too small and I knew I'd be gone

There's a stained-glass remembrance of light through the years
First communions, the funerals, the weddings, the tears
I'm not closing the book, I'm just laying it down
And I'm finding my own kind of holy in town

So I'm walking out easy, no thunder, no rage
Just a quiet goodbye on a soft empty page
I loved you, old steeple, I loved your old song
But the pew got too small and I knew I'd be gone

Maybe God's in the doorway, maybe God's in the street
Maybe grace doesn't need a stone roof to be sweet
So I'll carry the kindness and leave all the rest
And hum the old melody, soft, to myself, going west

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Three A.M. and the screen lighting blue on my face
Your name in my thumbs like a thing I can't erase
Deleted the thread but I typed it again
Green bubble guilt, hit send, hit send
She asleep in the other room, breathing slow
I'm staring at a contact I swore I'd let go
Little gray dots, they pulse and they wait
I know this is wrong, I'm still up too late

Shouldn't, shouldn't, but my hand keep moving
Delete it, delete it, but the damage I'm proving
One text turn to ten, ten turn to a sin
Lock the phone, light it up, do it again

Receipts in the cloud like a ghost in the wire
Every word a match and I'm feeding the fire
Told myself once, told myself twice
Numbers don't lie and the heart pay the price
Vibrate on the nightstand, I flip it face down
Love on my left, temptation across town

If I throw this phone in the river tonight
Would the wanting still glow in the dark, burning bright?
I know how it ends, seen the movie before
Still I'm watching the door, still I'm wanting more

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Back room of a bar that don't exist no more
Sticky pint glass sweating on the floor
Nine names on a clipboard, mine scratched in pen
Knees like a engine I couldn't rev down then
A mic that smelled like every mouth before mine
Feedback screaming, red light, count the time

Go, go, the beat won't wait
Four bodies in the crowd and an open gate
I was terrified, alive, a wire pulled tight
First time I ever felt the size of the night

Forgot my second verse, hummed through the gap
Somebody clapped before I could collapse
Walked off the stage on a pair of borrowed legs
Heart going double like a kick drum begs
Rain on the bus glass, headphones, replay
A voice I half-knew finding its way

Now the rooms are bigger and the lights don't blind
But I'd trade the whole thing for that first kind
That scared, that raw, that nothing-to-lose
The night I picked the mic and couldn't refuse

Go, go, the beat won't wait
Four bodies in the crowd and an open gate

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
Knelt on a floor that gave me nothing back
Prayers like bottles thrown off a freight track
The steeple cast a shadow, never warmth
I buried my believing in the dirt of the north
Screamed at a ceiling that refused to crack
Walked out the doors and I never looked back

I lost it! I lost it in the cold!
Every word they sold me, every lie they told!
But something in the wreckage kept a pulse, kept a beat
Dragged me back to standing on my own two feet

Years in the static, years in the gray
No god in the gutter where I learned to pray
Then a stranger pulled a kid from a burning frame
And I felt the whole sky say a different name
Not the church, not the book, not the threat of the flame
Just the raw human fire that I couldn't tame

It wasn't a thunderclap, wasn't a sign
Just a hand in the dark that reached for mine
I don't need the building, don't need the crown
Found my faith in the people who don't let you down

I found it! I found it in the breaking!
Not the thing they gave me, but the thing worth taking!

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Gravel and the green sky pulling me west
Doppler hum and a coffee gone cold in my chest
She asked would I stay, I said maybe next spring
But spring is just weather and weather's a wing
The wall cloud lowers like a hand from above
I chase the thing I can't hold, I call it love

Wide open, the highway dissolves into rain
I'm a wheel that won't rust, a name on a plane
Settle down, settle down, the houses all whisper
But the wind is the only thing that ever stayed with her

Mile markers blur into a soft white drone
A wedding I missed for a funnel near Cohne
The siren in the distance is a lullaby now
I'd promise her forever but I don't know how
Dust on the windshield, hail on the hood
Loving the leaving was the best that I could

When the sky goes still and the danger has passed
I feel the quiet like it might be my last
So I point the truck toward another black bloom
And I leave my own heart in an unmade room

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We stood on the ice where the dark never breaks
Your breath a white ribbon, the silence awake
Then the sky tore open in a ribbon of green
The coldest, the slowest, most violent thing seen
I took your gloved hand and I counted the sway
The heavens were dancing, so we danced their way

One, two, the curtain ignites
We turn through the snow under emerald lights
Your heel in the frost, my hand at your spine
The whole arctic burning, and you are mine

Violet bleeds into a scarlet that hums
The stars keep the rhythm like a thousand drums
You tilt back your throat and the colors run wild
I dip you toward heaven like a reckless child
No bandoneon here but the wind plays the part
A wheezing slow ache from the floor of my heart

They say it's just particles, sun on the air
But I felt a hand move us, I swear it was there
When the green finally faded and left us the black
We kept turning slow, and we never looked back

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The boots that I wore wore the dust of a coast
Where boys became names that their mothers would toast
I carry a duffel and a hole in my sleep
The sea sang a hymn that I couldn't keep
My hands knew the rifle, now they don't know rest
They shake at the table, they pound at my chest

¡Ay! The guitar bleeds the road that I crossed
¡Ay! Every brother I buried, every year that I lost
The heel strikes the wood like a shot in the square
I'm home, I'm home, but my home isn't there

My mother lit candles for a son who returned
A stranger with medals and a country that burned
The orange trees blossom like nothing occurred
The whole village dances to a song I once heard
Clap for the living, clap loud for the dead
The rhythm's the only prayer left in my head

Let the palms keep the time that my heartbeat forgot
Let the strings pull the bullet from the place it was shot
I dance till the morning, I dance till I'm free
Till the war in my body lets go of me

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
The clarinet weeps down a Tremé side street
A tuba like thunder finds the drag of our feet
We carry him slow past the shuttered cafés
A shtetl-born sorrow in a Louisiana haze
The handkerchiefs flutter, the black umbrellas tilt
Mourning and marching on the ground that he built

Oy, the second line winds through the heat
Weeping in a minor key, dancing in the street
We cry him to the graveyard, we laugh him to the sky
A freylekhs and a dirge for the sweetest goodbye

The fiddle finds a doina, a long bending moan
Then the snare cracks open and we don't walk alone
Grandmothers in Brooklyn, brass bands in the Ward
Same old grief that we hand to the Lord
Cut loose the body, let the spirit go wild
We bury him a man, we raise him a child

From Odessa to the bayou the song is the same
You weep and you stomp and you holler his name
Then the trumpet kicks high and the whole street ascends
Death takes the body but the music never ends

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Last shift, the time clock punches a sigh
Fluorescent tubes flicker their long goodbye
Thirty years of steel in the grease on my palm
Now the line goes quiet, a mechanical calm
My father stood here, his father did too
Three generations in a coat of blue

The gates gonna lock when the whistle won't blow
A town built on hours got nowhere to go
Dust on the dials, rust on the rail
We were the engine, now we're the tale

The diner across the street already went dark
No lunch-hour crowd, no lighters, no spark
They shipped the machines to a cheaper coast
Left us a parking lot and a fading ghost
I keep my old badge in the glovebox still
A number, a name, on a deadened hill

What do you tell a kid when the work moves on?
That the place that made you can just be gone?
I sweep the last floor in the amber light
And I clock myself out for the final night

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
His hands used to lift me clean over his head
Now I steady his coffee from the side of the bed
The man who hung doors and could carry a beam
Folds slow in a chair by the window's gleam
I see the years gathered soft on his face
The strongest oak bending, losing its place

Oh, Daddy, when did the mountain grow small?
When did I start being the one to catch the fall?
I'd give back every birthday, every gray in your hair
For one more of those mornings when you were just there

He tells me the same story three times tonight
About a Buick, a girl, and a Mississippi light
And I laugh like it's new 'cause his eyes still shine
This beautiful man, this blood that is mine
His voice is a whisper of the thunder it was
And I love him more now, just because

So I'll hold the door open the way that he taught
I'll be strong like he showed me, the lessons he brought
And when the time comes that he has to let go
I'll thank him for everything he made me know

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The kettle's gone cold and the words hang in the air
Two cups on the table, an unbridgeable chair
We said all the daggers, now we sit in the hush
The loudest thing left is the radiator's blush
A streetlamp draws gold on the half-open door
Neither one of us reaches across the floor

In the quiet, oh, the quiet after the storm
The ash of the anger still flickering warm
I'd take back the cruelest if you'd take back the cold
In the quiet we're learning how to grow old

A saxophone sighs from a neighbor's TV
The night does the talking that's too much for me
Your foot finds my foot in the dark, almost shy
A truce written soft where the hard words die
We don't say I'm sorry, we just let it dissolve
Two stubborn old fools and a thing we can't solve

Love isn't the music when the brass section swings
It's the rest between notes, the unspoken things
The space where forgiveness can finally begin
The quiet, the quiet, that lets us back in

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
El trompetista lifts his horn to the gray
We march down the avenue, we carry José
From Jalisco he came to the river's wide mouth
A son of two countries, the north and the south
The vihuela is crying, the guitarrón keeps the tread
We sing him to glory, our beautiful dead

¡Ay, ay, ay! Through the Quarter we go
Grito for the sorrow, grito for the show
The brass and the strings braid a gold and a black
We walk him to heaven, but we won't walk back sad

His widow holds roses the color of flame
The second line dances and they shout out his name
Two flags on the casket, the eagle and the fleur
A man who loved jambalaya and the songs of Guerrero
The cornet meets the trumpet in a holler so high
That the saints lean down from the Louisiana sky

No borders up there where the music ascends
Mariachi and brass band, they're cousins, they're friends
So cut the rope loose, let his spirit run free
From the bayou to the mountains, mi hermano, ¡que sea!
