songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
I drove you up north where the cities go dim
Killed the engine, killed the lights, let the dark roll in
You wrapped in my jacket, your nose going pink
We waited on a wonder at the world's frozen brink
Then the sky started breathing in a slow ribbon glow
And I forgot every cold thing I needed to know

Green on your skin, girl, green in your eyes
The whole sky is melting and so are these ties
Let it pour over us, let it pull us apart
The northern lights got nothing on you in the dark

You whispered so soft like you'd wake up the stars
Traced the aurora with two trembling arms
I pulled you in closer, felt your heartbeat slow
The universe showing off but it's you that I know
Violet and emerald spilling down the night
And none of it touches you, baby, you're the light

We'll chase a lot of beauty in the years that come
Mountains and oceans and the rising sun
But nothing's gonna ruin me the way that you did
Under a sky on fire when we were just kids

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
June came in barefoot on the boardwalk planks
You smiled like an offering, I gave my thanks
Salt on your shoulders and a sun in your laugh
A holy little season cut clean in half
The Lord made the ocean and the Lord made you
And I praised both glories in the burning blue

Oh, hallelujah for the summer she came
Oh, glory in the sand and the call of her name
It couldn't last forever, no, that's not the design
But I thanked the good heavens for the time she was mine

We built no foundation, just a castle of grains
That the tide came and gathered with its silver chains
September was coming with its colder hand
But for three burning weeks we were the promised land
Your bus pulled away in the amber of fall
And I lifted my hands and I blessed it all

Some love is a steeple, built to stand tall
Some love is a seashell, and that's not a fall
Thank You for the fleeting, thank You for the brief
Thank You for the summer that was sweeter than grief

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Your tether snapped soft, just a whisper, a sigh
I watched you grow smaller against the wide sky
My glove reached for nothing where your hand used to be
Two suits full of breathing in the cold gravity
The Earth a blue marble we both used to call home
Now it's you, and it's me, and the long alone

Drifting, drifting, the dark pulls you wide
No up and no down, just the space in between
I'm calling your name on a fading degree
But sound doesn't carry where you're floating from me

We trained for the danger, we trained for the deep
But nobody trains you for the promises you can't keep
Your light on your helmet blinks out one by one
A constellation leaving like a setting sun
I'd burn all my oxygen to pull you back near
But the physics is cruel and it doesn't care here

Maybe orbits come back if you wait long enough
Maybe gravity's gentle under all of its rough
So I'll hang in the silence with my hand open wide
And I'll trust that the dark brings you back to my side

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights of the city were a hand's-breadth away
I could taste the whole future in the dark of that day
One note from the summit, one breath from the crown
Then the wire that I walked on came shivering down
I fell on my knees on the cold studio floor
So close I could hear it knocking on the door

Lord, I almost made it, I almost broke through
With the dream in my fingers and my heart split in two
But You don't waste a stumble, no, You don't waste a fall
So I lifted my voice and I gave You it all

They signed the next singer, sent me home in the rain
A pocket full of nothing and a beautiful pain
But something in the losing turned my eyes to the sky
And I sang in the gutter where the streetlights die
The man at the bus stop, he started to sway
And I knew that the music had found its own way

Maybe making it never was the mountain to climb
Maybe almost was a gift dressed in heartbreak and time
So I'll sing for the one soul, I'll sing for the crowd
The night I almost made it, Lord, I'm singing it loud

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone is heavy in my shaking hand tonight
A hundred words I rehearsed but none feel right
The dial tone hums like an old familiar song
I've been a stranger, mama, for far too long

So pick up the line, let the silence break
I'm sorry for every wrong turn I'd make
The years rolled by like a train in the rain
But I'm calling you home, gonna love you again

I hear you breathing soft on the other side
All of my stubborn pride just up and died
Your voice still sounds like Sunday afternoon
Like kitchen radio and a warm spoon

So pick up the line, let the silence break
I'm sorry for every wrong turn I'd make
The years rolled by like a train in the rain
But I'm calling you home, gonna love you again

No more empty chairs, no more unsaid things
Just the swing of your laugh and the hope it brings

So pick up the line, let the silence break
I'm sorry, dear mama, for the heart I'd ache
The years rolled by but the love remains
And I'm calling you home, gonna love you again

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights were low and the crowd was small
But I sang my heart against that back wall
A man in a suit said he liked my sound
Then the rain came down and he turned around

Oh, I almost made it, almost touched the sky
So close I could taste it, watched it pass me by
One more turn of fortune, one more shot at fate
It was the night, baby, the night I almost made it

I drove home slow with the radio off
Every streetlight whispered, every block was soft
The dream still burning like a coal in my chest
Too proud to quit and too tired to rest

Oh, I almost made it, almost touched the sky
So close I could taste it, watched it pass me by
One more turn of fortune, one more shot at fate
It was the night, baby, the night I almost made it

But almost is a country where the dreamers stay
And I'll be singing till they call my name someday

Oh, I almost made it, Lord, I almost made it
Gonna rise again before the morning takes it

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The lanterns swing low on the final night
Everybody dancing in the fading light
The band plays loud and the band plays free
One more song before the memory

So grab my hand, let's twirl till dawn
The music's playing, don't let it be gone
The last night, baby, the last night of it all
We're spinning in the magic of the festival

The trumpets cry and the cymbals shine
Your laughter mixing with this heart of mine
Tomorrow comes with its packing and goodbyes
But tonight we're golden under summer skies

So grab my hand, let's twirl till dawn
The music's playing, don't let it be gone
The last night, baby, the last night of it all
We're spinning in the magic of the festival

The sun will rise and the tents will fall
But I'll keep this night in my heart through it all

So grab my hand, let's twirl till dawn
The music's playing, don't let it be gone
The last night, baby, the last night of it all
We're dancing in the magic of the festival

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody picked us, nobody believed
Last on the table, what a team we'd be
But we laced our boots and we hit the field
Got a fire in our chests that will never yield

Pick it up, pick it up, hear the whistle blow
We're the underdogs, baby, watch us go
Final game, final whistle, final shot
Give it everything, give it all we've got

The horns are blasting and the crowd is loud
We're the little engine in a giant crowd
Down by one but we never quit
Skank to the rhythm, every drum and hit

Pick it up, pick it up, hear the whistle blow
We're the underdogs, baby, watch us go
Final game, final whistle, final shot
Give it everything, give it all we've got

They wrote us off but they don't understand
A heart that won't break makes the final stand

Pick it up, pick it up, hear the whistle blow
We're the underdogs, baby, watch us go
Final game, final whistle, we won't stop
Give it everything, we're climbing to the top

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Ten and two on the wheel, baby girl, hold it steady
Deep breath now, take your time, no need to be ready
Mirrors check, seatbelt clicked, foot up off the brake
Every little turn you make is a turn for goodness sake
You were small just yesterday in a booster seat
Now you're rolling down the block to your own heartbeat

Drive slow, my baby, the world is yours
Keep your hands on the wheel and your eyes on the course
I'll be right here beside you every mile of the way
Teaching you to drive, teaching you to fly someday

Green means go but you ease it, don't you race
Life's gonna throw you red lights, keep your pace
Parallel park, you nailed it, look at you grin
That's my girl, that's my heart, that's my kin
I remember holding bottles, now I'm letting go
Of the passenger fear as I watch my baby grow

Drive slow, my baby, the world is yours
Keep your hands on the wheel and your eyes on the course
I'll be right here beside you every mile of the way
Teaching you to drive, teaching you to fly someday

One day you'll drive off and I'll wave from the door
But you'll carry every lesson and a whole lot more

Drive slow, my baby, the world is yours
Keep your hands on the wheel and your eyes on the course

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Neon lights are bleeding into purple skies
Last night of the festival, no more goodbyes
The bass is in my chest like a second heart
We're frozen in the moment before it all departs

Dance with me in the electric glow
One more night before we let it go
The lasers cutting through the midnight air
Last night of the festival, we're everywhere

Your silhouette is glowing in the strobe and haze
We're living in a synthwave kind of golden daze
The city hums below us like a dying star
Tonight we are forever, tonight we are

Dance with me in the electric glow
One more night before we let it go
The lasers cutting through the midnight air
Last night of the festival, we're everywhere

When the sun comes up the dream will fade to gray
But the neon in my memory will never go away

Dance with me in the electric glow
One more night before we let it go
The lasers cutting through the midnight air
Last night of the festival, we're everywhere

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Old man Solomon at the table in the shade
Five dollars a game and a fortune to be made
The clock is ticking and his fingers dance so fast
He'll take your king and your wallet, he's a blast from the past

Oy, the clarinet is wailing as the pawns go down
He's the cleverest hustler in the whole of the town
Checkmate, my friend, hand over your pay
The king of the park on a bright Sunday

The pigeons gather round like they know the score
He's beaten generals, beaten kids and beaten more
A wink and a grin and a queen out of nowhere
The violin is laughing in the springtime air

Oy, the clarinet is wailing as the pawns go down
He's the cleverest hustler in the whole of the town
Checkmate, my friend, hand over your pay
The king of the park on a bright Sunday

He learned the game in a village far away
Now he dances on the squares till the end of the day

Oy, the clarinet is wailing as the pawns go down
He's the cleverest hustler in the whole of the town
Checkmate, my friend, hand over your pay
The king of the park on a bright Sunday

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
The neon sign is buzzing, two A.M. glow
The coffee's always hot when you got nowhere to go
Mama at the counter with a smile so wide
Everybody welcome, come on step inside

We never close, we never sleep
The griddle's hot and the rhythm's deep
All night long, come one come all
The diner on the corner, hear it call

Taxi drivers, lovers, dreamers in the booth
Everybody got a story, everybody got a truth
The jukebox playing soft while the city falls asleep
We got eggs and we got laughter and the company we keep

We never close, we never sleep
The griddle's hot and the rhythm's deep
All night long, come one come all
The diner on the corner, hear it call

When the world shuts down and the lonely roam
There's a light still burning, there's a place called home

We never close, we never sleep
The griddle's hot and the rhythm's deep
All night long, come one come all
The diner on the corner, hear it call

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Half a recipe fading on a yellowed card
Cooking for one is the hardest part
The kitchen's too quiet where you used to hum
I measure out the silence, I measure out the numb

And I'm learning to cook for one
Under a dim and distant sun
The table set for only me
In a haze of what used to be

I burned the rice the way you never would
Laughed and cried the way a broken man should
The steam rises up like a ghost in the air
I set out two plates then I leave one bare

And I'm learning to cook for one
Under a dim and distant sun
The table set for only me
In a haze of what used to be

The walls dissolve in the evening light
Your photograph glowing soft and white

And I'm learning to cook for one
Under a dim and distant sun
The table set for only me
In a haze of what used to be

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Every morning the bell on the door goes ring
Miguel's got the clippers and he's the king
Knows your name, knows your story, knows your pain
A fresh cut and some gossip in the afternoon rain

Dale, dale, sit down in the chair
He'll fix you up with style and flair
The barber knows the whole town's heart
Every fade is a work of art

The mayor and the schoolkid in the same old line
He's cutting hair and dropping wisdom all the time
From weddings to the funerals he's been there
The whole town's secrets resting in his chair

Dale, dale, sit down in the chair
He'll fix you up with style and flair
The barber knows the whole town's heart
Every fade is a work of art

When the streets get loud and the world feels cold
There's a man with a razor and a heart of gold

Dale, dale, sit down in the chair
He'll fix you up with style and flair
The barber knows the whole town's heart
Every fade is a work of art

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
I still see your shadow in the dead of night
A ghost of a love that I couldn't fight
We burned like a fire then we turned to ash
Now I'm screaming at the dark with a heart that's smashed

You're the one that got away
The scar that never fades to gray
I'm drowning in this endless pain
Screaming out your name in the pouring rain

The chains of memory drag me down below
To a place where the only thing I know is no
I reached for your hand but you slipped through the void
Now everything I built has been destroyed

You're the one that got away
The scar that never fades to gray
I'm drowning in this endless pain
Screaming out your name in the pouring rain

The demons in my head won't let me rest
You ripped the beating heart right from my chest
Forever I will carry this disease
A broken man down on his knees

You're the one that got away
The scar that never fades to gray
I'm drowning in this endless pain
Screaming out your name in the pouring rain

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
By the old stone bridge where the willows weep
There sits a man with secrets to keep
A board of black and white upon his knee
Come lay your coin and lose to him for free

Oh, the fiddle plays as the bishops fall
He's the cleverest rogue of them all
Lay down your king on the cobbled green
The finest chess hustler the park has seen

He came from across the wild grey sea
With a tale in his eye and a clever knee
The pawns march on like soldiers to the war
And he takes your purse like he's done before

Oh, the fiddle plays as the bishops fall
He's the cleverest rogue of them all
Lay down your king on the cobbled green
The finest chess hustler the park has seen

The whistle and the drum and the springtime air
And a checkmate waiting for all who dare

Oh, the fiddle plays as the bishops fall
He's the cleverest rogue of them all
Lay down your king on the cobbled green
The finest chess hustler the park has seen

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete stretches far as the eye can see
But there in the lot stands a lonely tree
Neon reflections in its dying leaves
The last green heart that the city believes

Stand tall, last tree, in the electric night
The only thing alive in the artificial light
They paved the world but they left you there
Breathing out hope in the poison air

The headlights sweep like a digital tide
And the roots hold on to the world that died
A single bird still sings in your arms
A fading echo of the natural charms

Stand tall, last tree, in the electric night
The only thing alive in the artificial light
They paved the world but they left you there
Breathing out hope in the poison air

Maybe one day the concrete cracks and breaks
And a forest rises from the choice we make

Stand tall, last tree, in the electric night
The only thing alive in the artificial light
They paved the world but they left you there
Breathing out hope in the poison air

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Duffel bag heavy as I step off the train
Same old town but I'm not the same
The streetlights flicker like they always did
I'm a grown up soldier but inside a kid
The dust still settles in the back of my mind
Leaving pieces of myself somewhere behind

I'm coming home, coming home at last
Trying to outrun the shadow of the past
The porch light's on and the door swings wide
But I carry the war somewhere deep inside

Mama's on the steps with tears in her eyes
The smell of her cooking, the old goodbyes
The dog still knows me, he runs to my feet
Funny how the small things make a man complete
I count the quiet like I counted the days
Learning how to live in the peaceful haze

I'm coming home, coming home at last
Trying to outrun the shadow of the past
The porch light's on and the door swings wide
But I carry the war somewhere deep inside

One breath at a time, one step on the floor
Learning who I am now that I'm home from the war

I'm coming home, coming home at last
Trying to outrun the shadow of the past

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The fiddle's been a-singing since the break of day
Now the sun's gone down and we're all gonna play
Last night of the festival under the stars
With a banjo ringing and a jar of ours

Stomp your feet on the old wood floor
One more reel and then one more
Last night, last dance, hold it tight
We'll pick and grin till the morning light

The mandolin is crying a sweet farewell
Got a story in every string and a tale to tell
Tomorrow we go down the dusty road
But tonight we lighten every heavy load

Stomp your feet on the old wood floor
One more reel and then one more
Last night, last dance, hold it tight
We'll pick and grin till the morning light

So raise a glass to the friends we made
And the music that will never fade

Stomp your feet on the old wood floor
One more reel and then one more
Last night, last dance, hold it tight
We'll pick and grin till the morning light

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
The diner sign is buzzing red on Route 49
I got a cup of coffee and a phone that's mine
Three o'clock and the highway's a long gray seam
I dial the number slow so I don't wake the dream

You answer half asleep, you say my name so soft
I tell you 'bout the snow up in the mountain loft
The rig is parked and humming like a tired old hound
And a thousand miles of dark is all that's around

So tell the kids I love them, kiss the dog goodnight
Keep the porch light burning, I'll be home by Friday light
I'm just a voice on a wire in the freezing air
But my heart's already parked in the driveway there

You laugh about the leak beneath the kitchen sink
I close my eyes and I can almost smell the ink
Of the postcards on the fridge from the towns I've crossed
Every white line on the map is a thing I've lost

The engine and the lonesome are an old duet
But your sleepy little hello is the best I'll get
When the sun comes up I'll pull out slow and true
Counting down the exits 'til I'm back to you

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
(Sha-la-la) the lamp is low
(Sha-la-la) two in the morning glow
My textbook's open, my coffee's cold
And the night keeps whisperin' the same old

The numbers dance, they won't stay still
(Ooh-wah) up on Lonely Hill
My pencil taps a steady beat
While the radiator hums down at my feet

Oh oh oh, just me and the page
Oh oh oh, like a bird in a cage
But I'll learn it by the morning light
So I sing myself a little doo-wop tonight

(Bom-bom) the clock won't stop
(Bom-bom) but I'm gonna reach the top
One more chapter, one more line
And this lonesome desk is a friend of mine

When the whole world's sleeping deep and warm
I'm a little candle in the quiet storm
And I'll thank these hours when the bells all ring
For the lonely two a.m. that taught me how to sing

Oh oh oh, just me and the page
Oh oh oh, like a bird in a cage
But I'll learn it by the morning light
So I sing myself a little doo-wop tonight

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Morning break, the sun dey rise
Smoke dey curl, it touch my eyes
Grandma humming by the pot
Every spice she never forgot
Pepper, ginger, palm oil sweet
Dancing barefoot on the heat

Oh, the smell, it carry me home (carry me home)
No matter how far I roam (far I roam)
Mama's mama, hands of gold
Cooking love that never grow old
Oh, the smell, it carry me home

Jollof bubbling, story time
Clap your hands and feel the rhyme
She go call me, "come and eat"
Little plate, big heart, complete
Fufu soft and soup so deep
Memories I always keep

When I close my eyes I'm small again
In the doorway with my cousins and them
The radio low, the kettle high
And the smoke it write our names across the sky

Oh, the smell, it carry me home (carry me home)
No matter how far I roam (far I roam)
Mama's mama, hands of gold
Cooking love that never grow old
Oh, the smell, it carry me home

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Plane touch down, the tarmac shakes
Every step the old fear breaks
Got a duffel and a name they gave
Marching back from the edge of the grave
City lights run fast and wide
But the engine's still alive inside

Run me home, run me home
Through the bassline and the chrome
I survived the screaming night
Now I'm rolling toward the light
Run me home, run me home
I'm not a soldier all alone

Doorbell rings and the silence drops
Mother crying, the whole world stops
I forgot how the quiet sounds
When there's no boots on broken grounds
Heartbeat racing, two-step fast
Learning how to outrun the past

The tempo's high but my hands are still
Learning peace is an uphill
Let the breakbeat carry the weight
Of the men who didn't make the gate

Run me home, run me home
Through the bassline and the chrome
I survived the screaming night
Now I'm rolling toward the light
Run me home, run me home
I'm not a soldier all alone

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Under the spinning pole of red and white
Old Sam's been cutting since the morning light
A pair of scissors and a steady tale
He knows every secret this town could fail

So take a seat, let the warm towel fall
Sam's little shop, he knows us all
A dab of cream and a knowing grin
He holds the whole town's stories in

The mayor came Tuesday with a worried frown
Left with a fade and his troubles down
The widow's boy got his very first trim
And Sam just smiled and he winked at him

The mirror's foggy, the jazz spins low
A saxophone on the radio
He never gossips, he just nods slow
Keeping the things that a barber should know

When the chair is empty and the sign says closed
He sweeps the floor in his Sunday clothes
And every clipping on the worn pine board
Is a piece of the town that he's adored

So take a seat, let the warm towel fall
Sam's little shop, he knows us all
A dab of cream and a knowing grin
He holds the whole town's stories in

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle in the window, lamp burning warm
Mr. Cole been stitching through the city storm
Chalk lines on the fabric, measure twice and cut
Every suit on the corner came out of his gut
The pinstripe preacher, the kid for his prom
He lets out the seams, keeps the whole block calm

Thread it slow, let the spool unwind
Dress the whole block, one of a kind
From the cuff to the collar, he made it fit
Lo-fi tailor, every piece legit

Hummin' to the static of an old AM tune
Folds the brown paper underneath the moon
A tape around the shoulders, a nod and a grin
"Come back Thursday, son, I'll take it in"
The block walks taller when the Sunday comes
In the threads of a quiet man who never runs

Dusty mannequins keep him company
Ghosts of every gentleman he's ever gonna be
The vinyl crackles soft, the iron lets off steam
Stitching up the neighborhood into a dream

Thread it slow, let the spool unwind
Dress the whole block, one of a kind
From the cuff to the collar, he made it fit
Lo-fi tailor, every piece legit

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Soft the morning on the salt-white stone
The keeper writes his letters all alone
Dear blue water, dear restless tide
You are the only love I keep inside

Oh, the sea, the gentle sea
Every wave is a word to me
I fold my heart in a paper boat
And set it sailing, let it float

The lantern turns its slow and golden eye
He sips his coffee as the gulls go by
He seals each letter with a grain of sand
And lays it where the foam can reach the land

No woman waits on the cobbled shore
Just the rhythm of the ocean's door
But he is happy in the morning haze
Writing bossa to the endless blue of days

And when the storms come tall and gray
He whispers low, please don't drift away
The sea just answers with a quiet sigh
And rocks the little lighthouse lullaby

Oh, the sea, the gentle sea
Every wave is a word to me
I fold my heart in a paper boat
And set it sailing, let it float

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Midnight glow on the telephone
I know I should leave it well alone
Her name light up like a warning sign
But my thumb keep moving down the line

Oh no, I shouldn't text you
Oh no, but I do, I do
Every letter is a step too far
Sweet little trouble, here you are

The riddim slow and the night is deep
When the whole house fall to sleep
Three dots dancing, my heart run quick
Playing with a fire I shouldn't pick

Delete it, delete it, that's what they say
But I press send and it's gone away
Guilty feeling, irie and wrong
Caught in the hook of a dangerous song

Morning come and the light is true
I gotta put the phone away from you
Some roads them lead to a heavy fall
So I won't answer, won't answer at all

Oh no, I shouldn't text you
Oh no, but I do, I do
Every letter is a step too far
Sweet little trouble, here you are

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Dust on the field and the sun gone down
Last night burning over the tent town
One more riddim 'fore the morning come
Feel the heartbeat of the big bass drum

Woah, the last night, hold it tight
Dance until the morning light
Woah, we never wanna go
Let the music sweet and slow

Strangers turn to family now
Hands up high, we don't know how
The lights string up between the trees
And the whole crowd sway like the evening breeze

Tomorrow we pack and we drive away
Back to the grind and the working day
But tonight we are one big sound
Barefoot dancers on the holy ground

So don't let the fire die too soon
Sing it loud to the cratered moon
Every face I will not forget
The sweetest night that we've had yet

Woah, the last night, hold it tight
Dance until the morning light
Woah, we never wanna go
Let the music sweet and slow

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine running, neon rain
One more job and I'm off this lane
Clock on the dash says quarter past
They told me this one would be the last

Heart in my throat, foot on the floor
I swear I won't do this anymore

Drive, drive, into the night
Last getaway, leave it all behind
One more mile and I'm finally free
No more running, it's just me
Drive, drive, hold the wheel tight
Last getaway, into the light

They come out fast and the doors slam shut
Sirens screaming through the city cut
I promised her I'd come back clean
A brand new life and a quiet scene

The map is folded on the seat
A little town where the roads all meet
I'll lose the plates and I'll change my name
Nothing left of this dangerous game

Drive, drive, into the night
Last getaway, leave it all behind
One more mile and I'm finally free
No more running, it's just me
Drive, drive, hold the wheel tight
Last getaway, into the light

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five bucks a game on a folding board
He's been sitting here since the sky was poured
Gray in his beard and a fox's grin
Lay your money down and he'll let you in

King me, king me, watch the clock
He owns the corner of the city block
Queen takes bishop, that's checkmate
The hustler in the park, he never waits

Tourists think they got him beat
He lets them dream from the concrete seat
Then the knight comes dancing out of view
And the pawns line up like they always do

Pigeons scatter, the autumn turns
A cigarette in the ashtray burns
He's lost a house and he's lost a wife
But he never lost a game in his life

When the streetlights flicker and the players go
He packs the pieces, walking slow
Home to a room where the kettle sings
A lonely man and his thirty-two kings

King me, king me, watch the clock
He owns the corner of the city block
Queen takes bishop, that's checkmate
The hustler in the park, he never waits

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Late-night light in the corner shop
The needle moves and it don't stop
He knows your shoulders, knows your name
Makes every man look just the same kind of flame

Ooh, he dress the whole block down
Smoothest threads in the whole damn town
From the church to the after-hour glow
Baby, he's the reason that we steal the show

Silk on the rack, velvet on the chair
A little cologne in the evening air
He pins your jacket, spins you round
Turns a regular soul to the best in town

He stitched my daddy's wedding suit
Same gentle hands, same quiet truth
Now I'm standing in the mirror tall
Knowing love went into every wall

Slow it down, let the iron steam
He's tailoring the neighborhood's dream
Every seam is a love letter sewn
To a block that he calls his own

Ooh, he dress the whole block down
Smoothest threads in the whole damn town
From the church to the after-hour glow
Baby, he's the reason that we steal the show

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Boots hit the ground, hey, can you feel it
Duffel on my back and a heart still healin'
Bus pull up on the same old street
Grandmama cookin' something sweet

I'm home, I'm home, get up off the floor
No more marching, no more war
Gimme that bass and a tambourine
Funkiest welcome you ever seen
I'm home, I'm home, let the horns blow
Welcome back the soldier, here we go

Little sister taller than the door
Daddy's pride that he can't ignore
The block lit up with a barbecue
Everybody groovin', the sky so blue

I carried weight that I can't explain
Nights of thunder, nights of rain
But the rhythm hits and the past lets go
My two feet movin' to the radio

For the ones who didn't make it back
I lay one down on the railroad track
Then I lift my head and I find the beat
Livin' loud for the men I couldn't keep

I'm home, I'm home, get up off the floor
No more marching, no more war
Gimme that bass and a tambourine
Funkiest welcome you ever seen
I'm home, I'm home, let the horns blow
Welcome back the soldier, here we go

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Well I came back to the town I knew
Same old streets and the sky so blue
Then I saw you standing by the corner store
And my two feet danced like never before

Oh, I'm falling, falling, swing me round
For a sweetheart found in my hometown
The band strikes up and the night is gold
Falling for a face that I've always known

We grew up two blocks apart
Never knew you'd steal my heart
Now I twirl you under the old streetlight
And the whole main street feels just right

The soda shop where we used to be
Now you're sharing a malt with me
Who'd have thought that the love I'd find
Was right here, baby, all this time

So step in close, let the trumpet call
We'll dance our way down the city hall
Every memory leads to you
My hometown sweetheart, ever true

Oh, I'm falling, falling, swing me round
For a sweetheart found in my hometown
The band strikes up and the night is gold
Falling for a face that I've always known

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Soft and slow in the morning light
The rivers run on her skin so white
Every line is a road she's known
Every callus a seed she's sown

These hands that braided my mother's hair
These hands that taught me how to care
Folding dough in the kitchen warm
Shelter holding me from the storm

Hold on, hold on
To the hands that carried us along
Hold on, hold on
Long after the hands are gone

Now they tremble like a leaf in fall
But they still reach out, they hold us all
I press my palm against her own
And I feel the years in flesh and bone

Rise, rise, let the strings climb high
For the quiet love that does not die
Every wrinkle is a hymn she prayed
Every scar a gift she made

Hold on, hold on
To the hands that carried us along
Hold on, hold on
Long after the hands are gone

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The clock strikes one and my thumb won't quit
Got your name in my phone and I'm lighting it
Every good reason's gone out the door
I know better, baby, but I want some more

Three little dots and my heart does a hop
Swingin' on a wire that I oughta drop
The band in my chest plays a wicked tune
Dancin' with trouble by the light of the moon

Oh I shouldn't, but I'm doin' it again
Typin' out a secret with a guilty grin
Send it, regret it, hit it on the sly
Text me back, sugar, while the night rolls by

My mama raised me to walk the line
But your little message reads so fine
I'm a sinner with a smile and a tappin' shoe
Keepin' time with a love I never should pursue

Delete, delete, then I write it twice
Tell myself I'll quit but I never think thrice
The screen goes dark and I'm lit from within
Ring-a-ding trouble, here we go again

Oh I shouldn't, but I'm doin' it again
Typin' out a secret with a guilty grin
Send it, regret it, hit it on the sly
Text me back, sugar, while the night rolls by

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up where the streetlight flickers and dies
Same four corners, same gray skies
They said son, you ain't going nowhere fast
But I packed up my heartbeat and I floored the gas

Upstroke on the guitar, feet off the ground
Leaving every dead-end echo behind in this town
Mama waved a dishrag from the second floor
Said go on, baby, that's what your legs are for

I made it out, I made it out the block
Pick it up, pick it up, never gonna stop
Horns are blowing, sun is on my back
I made it out and I ain't looking back

Bus fare folded in a sweaty hand
Dreams too big for this little piece of land
They laughed at the kid with the holes in his shoes
Now watch me skank away from these corner-store blues

Pick it up, pick it up, one two three four
Don't you tell me what my future's for
Every brick they threw I built into a road
Now I'm dancing down it, light load, light load

I made it out, I made it out the block
Pick it up, pick it up, never gonna stop
Horns are blowing, sun is on my back
I made it out and I ain't looking back

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The candle's low and my hand is a fool
Writing to the one that breaks every rule
Clarinet crying in the back of my chest
Laughing and weeping, it knows me best

Oy, the moon is a witness, the moon disapproves
Watching me dance in these forbidden shoes
A word, then a word, then a word too far
Following your light like a foolish star

Dance with me, trouble, spin me around
My fingers keep typing what shouldn't be found
A freylekhs of longing, a hora of shame
I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but I send it again

Grandmother's voice in the wail of the strings
Warning me soft of the sorrow it brings
But the violin pulls and the bow won't rest
And I press the screen to my reckless chest

Faster now, faster, the wedding turns wild
The heart is a stubborn, a sweet-talking child
Left foot, right foot, regret and delight
I'll dance with this sin till the morning light

Dance with me, trouble, spin me around
My fingers keep typing what shouldn't be found
A freylekhs of longing, a hora of shame
I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but I send it again

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Smoke in the rafters, a five-dollar door
My knees doing rhythms I'd never played before
Three shaky people and a barman half asleep
A microphone humming with promises to keep

I forgot the second verse, made up the rest
Voice like a sparrow with a hole in its chest
But somewhere in the wrong notes a right thing was born
A kid finding music in the eye of the storm

Oh, that first open mic, that trembling light
I was scared half to nothing and I felt so alive
Look how far, look how far, from that little stage
Still carrying the warmth of that yellowing page

The applause was polite, maybe four hands or five
But to me it was Carnegie, I was barely alive
Walked home in the cold with the streetlamps for a band
Hummin' my own future, a song in my hand

They don't tell you the brave part is just showing up
Pouring out your shaking into a paper cup
I'd give a year of cool to feel that scared again
That holy kind of foolish, that beautiful when

Oh, that first open mic, that trembling light
I was scared half to nothing and I felt so alive
Look how far, look how far, from that little stage
Still carrying the warmth of that yellowing page

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Ten digits I knew before I knew my name
My thumb on the last one, I'm hanging in shame
Three years of stubborn just sitting on my chest
The dial tone humming like it knows the rest

She picks up so soft, like she always believed
That the boy that she lost would come back to be received
"It's me," I say breaking, "it's me, it's your son"
And the silence between us comes undone

Mama, I'm sorry, it's been too long
I let all that pride write a foolish song
The years fell like rain on a roof we both knew
And I'm calling, I'm calling, I'm coming home to you

Her laugh is still cinnamon, her worry still wide
Asking have I been eating, am I warm enough inside
And I'm forty going seven in the cradle of her voice
Learning love was always patient, learning love was the choice

We don't fix it all in one trembling line
But the bridge is still standing across the divide
A saxophone sighs what we can't quite say
The distance gets shorter just by starting today

Mama, I'm sorry, it's been too long
I let all that pride write a foolish song
The years fell like rain on a roof we both knew
And I'm calling, I'm calling, I'm coming home to you

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Same cracked sidewalk, same flickering light
Dreams in my pocket I held onto tight
They told me this corner was as far as I'd go
But something in my chest said, baby, no

Mama worked doubles, I watched the door
Counting all the reasons I wanted more
Every siren a lullaby, every wall too tall
But I climbed it anyway, I rose through it all

Look at me now, I made it out
Through the rain and the doubt, I figured it out
From the dead-end block to the open sky
I told you, I told you, watch me fly

I still see the faces I had to leave behind
Carry them with me, they live in my mind
This ain't about leaving the love that I knew
It's about proving the corner was never the truth

For every boy still sitting on a stoop tonight
Dreaming past the streetlamp, holding to the light
The block don't define you, the block ain't your name
There's a whole world waiting, go and stake your claim

Look at me now, I made it out
Through the rain and the doubt, I figured it out
From the dead-end block to the open sky
I told you, I told you, watch me fly

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Door didn't slam but the silence did
We both said the thing that we wish we hid
Now we're two strangers on the same couch cushion
Love on the table but nobody pushin'
I hear you breathing, you hear me too
Both too proud to say I miss you
The TV's off, the kettle went cold
We're holding the grudge like it's worth more than gold

It's quiet, it's quiet after the storm
The room's still here but it don't feel warm
I'd trade every word that I won the fight
Just to hold you again in the quiet tonight

Replay the argument, frame by frame
Find the exact spot I'm carrying the blame
Funny how the things that we scream the loudest
Come from the soft part we guard from the crowd, it's
Fear dressed up like it's anger and pride
Building a wall we're both stuck inside
I write you a text then I leave it as a draft
Swallow my ego just to hear you laugh

So I cross the cold floor, I sit by your side
No more keeping score, I'm tired of the divide
You turn, I turn, neither of us speaks
Just two heartbeats patching up the leaks

It's quiet, it's quiet after the storm
The room's still here but it don't feel warm
I'd trade every word that I won the fight
Just to hold you again in the quiet tonight

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the diner, clock out, run again
Night shift at the warehouse, where does it end
Two paychecks thin as the soles of my shoes
Still the rent's a monster and I'm bound to lose

Coffee number six and the sun ain't up
Pouring all my hours in a leaking cup
They say work hard, son, and you'll get ahead
But the math don't math and I'm seeing red

Two jobs, still drownin', tell me where's the line
Runnin' twice as fast and still fallin' behind
Pick it up, pick it up, horns blow loud
Dancin' through the debt with my head still proud

Landlord knockin' while the bills pile high
Kid needs new sneakers and I don't know why
A full week of sweat just to tread the same
This ain't living, friend, it's a rigged-up game

But I skank through the struggle, I won't lie down
The upbeat keeps me upright in this sinking town
One two three four, we're all in the same boat
So we sing it loud just to stay afloat

Two jobs, still drownin', tell me where's the line
Runnin' twice as fast and still fallin' behind
Pick it up, pick it up, horns blow loud
Dancin' through the debt with my head still proud

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded the hold under a bruise-colored moon
Something in those crates started humming a tune
The captain wouldn't look at what we sealed below
Said don't ask the question if you don't want to know

Day by day the compass starts to spin
And the crew goes quiet with the dark settling in
A whisper in the timbers, a chill in the brine
Whatever's in that cargo, it is no longer mine

Oh, the sea won't let us, the sea won't let us go
Dragging this curse where the cold currents flow
Groove of the ocean, slow and low
We're carrying something that we'll never let show

Gold? Or bones? Or a sin in disguise
Wrapped up in canvas with the deadest of eyes
The stars rearranged like they're hiding the way
And the horizon keeps backing further away

Lay it down, lay it down in the deep
Some cargo's a debt that the water won't keep
But my hands won't move and the chain holds tight
So we sail, so we sail through another cursed night

Oh, the sea won't let us, the sea won't let us go
Dragging this curse where the cold currents flow
Groove of the ocean, slow and low
We're carrying something that we'll never let show

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The train pulls in at a quarter past four
(Ooh, sha-la-la) I'm home from the war
My duffel bag heavy, my heart even more
But there's my girl waiting by the old front door

The streetlights still glow like they did before
(Ooh, sha-la-la) the same corner store
But I'm not the boy that they sent off to fight
I'm a man full of shadows in the soft evening light

I'm coming home, home, home to you
(Bom-bom-bom) like I promised I'd do
Hold me close, darling, don't let me go
I've been gone so long, sweet baby, you'll never know

Mama in the kitchen, she's frying up the chicken
(Ooh, sha-la-la) my heartbeat's quickenin'
The whole block's lit up like a holiday
For a soldier they'd half given up to pray

There are buddies I left in a faraway field
(Ooh) and a part of me there will never be healed
But tonight I'll hold tight to the ones that I love
And thank the good stars and the heavens above

I'm coming home, home, home to you
(Bom-bom-bom) like I promised I'd do
Hold me close, darling, don't let me go
I've been gone so long, sweet baby, you'll never know

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Raise my hand but they roll right past me
Good idea, watch a louder voice grab it
Sit in the back like my words don't matter
They hear the noise, never hear the data
Mute on my mic but my mind stay loud
Swallowin' genius just to please the crowd
Every time I open up they cut the line
Takin' all my thunder, callin' it their kind

Talked over, talked over, again and again
I'm done bein' quiet for the louder men
Next time I speak, gon' hold the floor
They gon' learn my name, won't talk over me no more

Watch 'em repeat what I said two minutes ago
Now it's brilliant, now they wanna take it slow
I been countin' all the credit that they stole
Writin' down receipts, keepin' track of my role
Confidence quiet but it's loaded up
Tired of the table where they fill my cup
With silence, with sidelines, with second guess
Time to flip the meeting into my address

So I stand up slow, let the room go still
Every word a hammer, every pause a thrill
You had your turn, now it's mine to hold
Watch the quiet one turn the whole thing gold

Talked over, talked over, again and again
I'm done bein' quiet for the louder men
Next time I speak, gon' hold the floor
They gon' learn my name, won't talk over me no more

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two a.m. and the whole world sleeps
Just me and the textbook and the promise I keep
Desk lamp glowin' like a little lighthouse
Whole city dark, just a buzz in the house
Highlighter dry, third cup of the brew
Memorizin' pages that I gotta get through
Nobody clappin', ain't nobody here
Just the grind and the goal and the future I steer

Late night, lamp light, all on my own
Buildin' up a dream in the quiet alone
Nobody sees it but it's gonna show
Two in the morning's where the champions grow

Eyes gettin' heavy but I push through the haze
Thinking 'bout the people that I'm tryna amaze
Mama back home and she's bettin' on me
So I turn another page on my destiny
No party, no posse, no flashing lights
Just a lonely little soldier in the dead of the night
They'll ask how I did it when I'm up on that stage
I'll point to this desk and this lamplight age

Silence is a teacher if you sit with it long
Discipline's the beat and the focus is the song
Nobody's watchin' so it's all on you
What you do in the dark is what carries you through

Late night, lamp light, all on my own
Buildin' up a dream in the quiet alone
Nobody sees it but it's gonna show
Two in the morning's where the champions grow

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man on the shoreline, salt in his beard
Reading the water like a page that he revered
He says, son, come close, let me show you the way
The sea keeps its secrets but it tells them if you stay

He points to the gulls and the color of the foam
The pull of the moon dragging everything home
Hands like driftwood, eyes like the deep
Giving me the lessons that his father couldn't keep

Learn the tide, learn the tide, how it gives and it takes
Learn the patience of the water and the slow turning wakes
He taught me how to listen, taught me how to wait
The elder and the ocean and the kid by the gate

Low tide, high tide, the rhythm of the years
He's been counting them like seasons, like the salt in his tears
Says one day I'll be standing where he's standing right now
Teaching some young stranger the when and the how

And the waves keep coming like they always have done
Under the same patient, indifferent sun
He presses my palm to the cold of the sand
Says the sea was here first, boy, now you understand

Learn the tide, learn the tide, how it gives and it takes
Learn the patience of the water and the slow turning wakes
He taught me how to listen, taught me how to wait
The elder and the ocean and the kid by the gate

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up in the attic with a wire and a dream
Bootleg antenna and a bass so mean
We broadcastin' nasty on a borrowed frequency
The FCC huntin' but they'll never find me
Midnight to morning we be holdin' it down
Slippin' funky signals to the whole sleepy town

Turn it up, turn it up, we run this dial
Pirate on the airwaves with that outlaw style
Can't catch the signal, can't shut it down
We're the ghost in the radio of this funky town

Grandma's old record and a homemade mic
Spinnin' all the jams that the suits don't like
Dedication comin' in from a girl named Renee
She want that deep cut that they took away
We got horns in the corner, we got groove on the breeze
Spreadin' freedom on a stolen 90.3

They raid the wrong building, we already gone
Moved the whole rig 'fore the sun come on
You can't license the funk, you can't tax the soul
We're the people's transmission and we're never gonna fold

Turn it up, turn it up, we run this dial
Pirate on the airwaves with that outlaw style
Can't catch the signal, can't shut it down
We're the ghost in the radio of this funky town

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Neon on the runway, engines winding down
A ghost in a uniform back in my hometown
The city's all electric but I'm frozen inside
Carrying a desert no ocean can hide

Streetlights pulse pink through the rain on the glass
Every passing mile pulls me back to the past
My reflection's a stranger in the window's cold glow
A boy who left laughing, a man who don't know

I'm coming home through the midnight light
Chrome and the static and the long drive home tonight
Hold me in the glow, let the engines fade
I survived the fire, now I'm learning to stay

Her porch light's still burning like she said it would
A small steady promise in the dark like she could
Keep one little ember from the world going dim
A beacon for a soldier who came back from the grim

The synths hum soft like a lullaby
For the friends in the photographs who didn't make it by
I'll carry their faces in the back of the beat
Every time the bassline meets the rain on the street

I'm coming home through the midnight light
Chrome and the static and the long drive home tonight
Hold me in the glow, let the engines fade
I survived the fire, now I'm learning to stay

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Radar bleeds magenta on the dash
I'm doing ninety where the wheat goes flat
Mama left a voicemail, three weeks old
I play it once, then chase the rolling cold

There's a girl in Tulsa with a porch light on
She keeps a drawer for me, but I'll be gone
The wall cloud lowers like a velvet hand
I'd rather kiss the funnel than a wedding band

I run on neon, gasoline and rain
The siren's singing in my hurricane
Don't ask me to stay, I'm wired to the sky
I only feel alive where the rooftops fly

Hail the size of headlights cracks the glass
The county's praying, I just floor the gas
Green horizon humming like a synth
I marry every mile and leave no print

One day the static's gonna swallow me
No stone, no name, just ozone and debris
And maybe that's the only church I've known
A roaring room where no man stays at home

I run on neon, gasoline and rain
The siren's singing in my hurricane
Don't ask me to stay, I'm wired to the sky
I only feel alive where the rooftops fly

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
Te veo en el club con otro, baby
Y yo fumando solo en la pared
Me sé tu risa como mi teléfono
Pero tú no sabes ni mi nombre, ¿ves?

Yo pongo el corazón, tú pones la distancia
Bailo con tu sombra cada madrugada
Dime qué le hago a este querer que sobra
Tú duermes tranquila y yo cargando el alba

Guardé el ticket del cine que no fuimos
El perfume que compré por si acaso
Mis panas me dicen que ya suelte el tema
Pero suelto el vaso antes que tu paso

Yo pongo el corazón, tú pones la distancia
Bailo con tu sombra cada madrugada
Dime qué le hago a este querer que sobra
Tú duermes tranquila y yo cargando el alba

Double check, me dejaste en visto otra vez
Y yo escribiendo cartas que se queman en el screen
Si el amor fuera moneda, yo soy quien la pierde
Tú la gastas en cualquiera menos en mí

Yo pongo el corazón, tú pones la distancia
Bailo con tu sombra cada madrugada
Dime qué le hago a este querer que sobra
Tú duermes tranquila y yo cargando el alba

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They paired us up, the joke's on me
Your hand on my back like a property line
I'd sooner waltz with a swarm of bees
Than share four bars of this borrowed time

So one-two-three, step on my pride
I'll spin you mean and you'll spin me wide
We're trading insults in triple time
But your shoulder fits in the crook of mine

You stole my spotlight back in '42
I swore I'd never forget that night
Now the brass is hot and the floor is new
And somehow your footwork's doing me right

The band don't care who started what
The trumpet laughs at every grudge we got
Your eyes say war but your hips say truce
And I forgot the reason for the noose

So one-two-three, step on my pride
I'll spin you mean and you'll spin me wide
We're trading insults in triple time
But your shoulder fits in the crook of mine

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I quit the church the spring we buried Joe
Told the steeple it could keep its empty song
Drove past it Sundays with the windows down
Daring God to prove me wrong

I drank the question dry in every bar
Found no answer at the bottom of the glass
Just a tired man in a Petersburg motel
Watching every certainty go past

But grace don't knock, it leaks in like the rain
Through the cracks you swore you'd seal
I found my faith again not in the light
But in a stranger's hand at the wheel

A flat tire, midnight, county road 9
A woman stopped who didn't have to stop
She hummed a hymn while she worked the jack
And something frozen in me dropped

Maybe heaven ain't a building made of pine
Maybe it's the kindness we can't explain
I didn't get my brother back that night
But I got down on my knees again

Grace don't knock, it leaks in like the rain
Through the cracks you swore you'd seal
I found my faith again not in the light
But in a stranger's hand at the wheel

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The radiator clicks a slow Morse code
My coffee's cold, a ring upon the page
The hallway hums in fluorescent gray
A library of one, this empty stage

Two a.m., the city's underwater
I float between the highlighter and the dark
Every fact dissolves and reassembles
My pulse the only metronome to mark

A moth taps softly at the window glass
We're both confused by what we think is light
The paragraph repeats itself again
Until the meaning loosens overnight

Somewhere a truck exhales along the bridge
The building settles, breathing in its sleep
I am a small lamp burning in a field
Of people I will never know or keep

Two a.m., the city's underwater
I float between the highlighter and the dark
Every fact dissolves and reassembles
My pulse the only metronome to mark

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Whoa-oh, the phone lights blue
It's quarter past the rules
My thumb keeps spelling out your name
Like a lovestruck, lonely fool

Delete it (delete it), don't send it (don't send it)
But my heart's got a mind of its own tonight
She's asleep in the other room
And I'm typing wrong what feels so right

Shoo-doo-wop, the three dots dance
You're typing something back
My collar's hot, my conscience small
A record stuck in a guilty track

My mama raised a better man
Than this glow against my chin
But every angel on my shoulder
Keeps on humming let her in

Delete it (delete it), don't send it (don't send it)
But my heart's got a mind of its own tonight
She's asleep in the other room
And I'm typing wrong what feels so right

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Arena en tu espalda, sal en el pelo
Un radio chiquito sonando perreo
No me diste apellido, solo tu boca
Dos semanas de junio que el mar se devora

Dale, baby, que el verano se va
Bésame ahora que la ola se va
No me prometas septiembre ni na'
Solo esta noche, la luna y el bar

Tu sombrilla amarilla, mi cerveza tibia
Nos comimos el cielo como mango maduro
Tu número en mi brazo con tinta de sol
Ya medio borrado pa'l carro y el mundo

Sé que el lunes te vas en el ferry temprano
Y yo voy a fingir que ni me importa, mano
Pero guardo la concha que encontraste en la orilla
Como prueba que existió esta maravilla

Dale, baby, que el verano se va
Bésame ahora que la ola se va
No me prometas septiembre ni na'
Solo esta noche, la luna y el bar

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I packed my grandmother into a single case
Her photographs, a thimble, and her name
The harbor smelled of diesel and goodbye
The gulls above us screaming all the same

So here's to the ocean wide as a no
And the new word for home I don't know how to say
I'm trading every street I memorized
For a skyline I will earn another day

My passport's stamped with somebody's permission
The deck below me trembles like a heart
Back there my mother waves a kitchen towel
Until the shoreline pulls our hands apart

What if I forget the way the church bells rang?
What if my children never taste the rain?
I fold the question, slip it in my coat
And promise I'll send money, not just pain

So here's to the ocean wide as a no
And the new word for home I don't know how to say
I'm trading every street I memorized
For a skyline I will earn another day

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
The whole county's watching from the bleachers
Sadie Cole and me, the bracket's cruel
We've fought since fourth grade over fence lines
Now the caller's pairing oil and gravel pool

So swing your partner, swing your foe
Dosido around the thing we won't let go
Your boots are stomping on my last good nerve
But darlin', that's a finer step than I deserve

She sued my daddy over half an acre
I keyed her truck the summer we turned tw19
Now her calloused hand is locked in mine
And the fiddle's playing something in between

The band slows down, the lanterns start to sway
And hate's a heavy thing to hold all day
I catch her grinning, then she looks away
Maybe forty years of feud just lost its way

So swing your partner, swing your foe
Dosido around the thing we won't let go
Your boots are stomping on my last good nerve
But darlin', that's a finer step than I deserve

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The boat come slow into the morning bay
I count the palm trees like they count the dead
My boots still heavy with another country's clay
And all the orders still loud inside my head

I and I a-coming home, coming home
Lay the rifle in the river, let it rust
Mama got the kettle on, the yard is overgrown
And I'm learning how to walk without the dust

The children don't remember my old face
The dog remembers, and he won't let go
The mango tree got taller in my place
And every loud noise lays my body low

They gave me ribbons for the men I couldn't save
I'd trade the lot to hear my brother sing
The sea don't ask me what I had to be
It just keep rolling, washing everything

I and I a-coming home, coming home
Lay the rifle in the river, let it rust
Mama got the kettle on, the yard is overgrown
And I'm learning how to walk without the dust

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Mr. Calloway's been cutting hair since '63
The chair's worn smooth as a river stone
He knows whose boy got into college
And whose old man is dying all alone

Oh, the clippers hum a holy sound
He's the keeper of the whole town's grace
Confession comes with every fade
And mercy in that mirror's silver face

The deacon and the drunkard share his bench
He gives the same warm towel to them both
He slips a twenty in a young man's collar
And never lets him see, and never boasts

When Mrs. Eddie's husband passed away
It was Calloway who drove the whole night through
The Lord don't only live inside the steeple
Sometimes He's got a comb and apron too

Oh, the clippers hum a holy sound
He's the keeper of the whole town's grace
Confession comes with every fade
And mercy in that mirror's silver face

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The tunnel swallowed the last of the light
The car packed tight as herring in a tin
My heart took off like a startled grouse
And the walls came breathing slowly in

Oh, hold me steady, iron and stone
I'm drowning standing up in this crowd
Count the rivets, count the breath
Sing me quiet though my blood is loud

The stranger's elbow, the flickering bulb
The map a tangle of red and blue thread
I grip the pole like a drowning oar
And a low green panic floods my head

My grandmother crossed a hungry sea
With less air than I'm gasping for tonight
So I borrow her spine and her stubborn lungs
And I ride the dark toward the rumored light

Oh, hold me steady, iron and stone
I'm drowning standing up in this crowd
Count the rivets, count the breath
Sing me quiet though my blood is loud

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
The light's still gold in the second-floor pane
Somebody else is cooking where I cried
I slow the car to the speed of memory
And let the engine idle on the side

Lord, I'm driving past my Egypt tonight
Where I learned to pray with the door shut tight
I thank You for the leaving and the lease
For every brick that taught me how to find my peace

The radiator hissed me into sleep
The roaches knew my name, the rent was steep
I was a younger, hungrier kind of soul
Who didn't know the breaking made me whole

I won't go knock, I won't go climb those stairs
The past's a room You've answered all my prayers in
So I tip my head to the window's amber glow
And I bless the girl up there I used to know

Lord, I'm driving past my Egypt tonight
Where I learned to pray with the door shut tight
I thank You for the leaving and the lease
For every brick that taught me how to find my peace

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
She wore a red that stopped the orchestra
I fumbled with my hat, my nerve, my name
I said I'd call her Tuesday, sure as rain
But Tuesday came and went and so the same

Oh, she's the missed beat in my favorite tune
The dropped call I keep dialing in the dark
I had her in my arms one Saturday
And let the band play on without a spark

They say she married money out in Maine
Keeps roses on a porch I'll never see
And here I am still trading dimes for songs
That sound a little too much like her plea

If I could thumb a ride back through the years
I'd grab that telephone and never lie
But time's a one-way trolley out of town
And all I get to keep is the goodbye

Oh, she's the missed beat in my favorite tune
The dropped call I keep dialing in the dark
I had her in my arms one Saturday
And let the band play on without a spark

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Four a.m. and the speakers still glow
Salt on your collarbone, sand in the floor
We don't trade numbers, we trade the night
The tide keeps time and we ask for more

Feel it, feel it, let the bassline rise
Summer's a body we can't keep
Dance me down to the edge of the foam
We'll sleep in September, not in this heat

Neon spills pink on the breaking waves
Your laugh cuts clean through the kick and snare
No last names, no tomorrow's weight
Just strobe and skin and the humid air

When the sun cracks open the gray-blue line
We'll scatter like gulls to separate towns
But right now the loop is a perfect ring
And nobody here is coming down

Feel it, feel it, let the bassline rise
Summer's a body we can't keep
Dance me down to the edge of the foam
We'll sleep in September, not in this heat

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Iron crates beneath the rolling deck
Something breathing in the salt and wreck
We signed the manifest in fading ink
Never asked what made the ocean blink

Fog comes heavy and the lanterns drown
Whispers crawling through the holding down
Every sailor dreams the same gray shore
Knocking soft against the cargo door

Carry it, carry it, far from home
Under the weight of the unknown
The sea won't take it, won't let go
We drift forever, row by row

Compass spinning where the stars should be
Name的written on the cursed debris
Morning never finds the open blue
Just the hum of what we're sailing to

Drowned in static, soft and slow
A tide of voices we don't know
Let the engines fade to gray
We were never meant to dock today

Carry it, carry it, far from home
Under the weight of the unknown
The sea won't take it, won't let go
We drift forever, row by row

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Well I set up my board on a Saturday morning
Five bucks a game and I'm giving you warning
Pawns in a row like a sweet little band
Got a king and a queen and a plan up my hand

You think you're a master, you swagger on through
I'll let you take a knight just to see what you do
The clock's gonna tick and the pigeons all sing
While I tip over your little old king

Checkmate, baby, that's the name of the game
You pay your money and you go home in shame
I'm the king of the park with a grin and a glove
Playing for nickels and playing for love

A bishop slides slick like a Cadillac car
A rook takes a tumble, you don't get too far
The sun's getting low and my pockets are tight
But I'll hustle these squares till the end of the night

So bring me your dollars, your dimes and your pride
There's nowhere on this board you can run and you can hide
The horns start to swing and the brass starts to blow
And I'm three moves ahead, baby, didn't you know

Checkmate, baby, that's the name of the game
You pay your money and you go home in shame
I'm the king of the park with a grin and a glove
Playing for nickels and playing for love

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Basement tower in the dead of night
Antenna screaming with a stolen light
They can't trace us, they can't shut us down
We are the voice of the underground

Frequency bleeding through the censored air
Kick down the silence, we don't play fair
A thousand listeners with their dials turned high
We own the wires and we own the sky

BROADCAST! Burn the signal free
BROADCAST! This is anarchy
No license, no master, no law of the land
The airwaves are ours and we'll make our stand

Government helicopters in the sky
Kill the transmitter, watch the static die
But we rise again from another floor
Louder and harder than we were before

They jam the channel, we cut it back through
A pirate flag where the speakers grew
Turn it to eleven, let the bass collide
There's no surrender on the rebel side

BROADCAST! Burn the signal free
BROADCAST! This is anarchy
No license, no master, no law of the land
The airwaves are ours and we'll make our stand

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone is heavy in my trembling hand
A number I know but don't understand
The years built a wall I was too proud to climb
Now I'm dialing slowly through the dust of time

Your voice on the line like a fading rose
The silence between us only heaven knows
I rehearsed a thousand things to say
But they all dissolve and they drift away

Mama, it's me, after all this time
Forgive the silence, forgive the crime
Dance with me slowly across the years
Let the music wash away the tears

You ask how I am like no time has passed
As if love like ours was built to last
The bandoneón cries what I cannot speak
A proud heart breaking, a strong heart weak

Every step we lost, every word unsaid
Lives in the rhythm, lives in my head
Let me come home before the song is through
Mama, I'm sorry, I always loved you

Mama, it's me, after all this time
Forgive the silence, forgive the crime
Dance with me slowly across the years
Let the music wash away the tears

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Tether snapped in the cold black void
Your helmet light is all that's left to read
I scream your name through a dying comm
But the vacuum eats the words I need

We trained together for a thousand days
Now the distance grows in a silent blaze
Your hand reached out and it touched the dark
A fading ember, a falling spark

DRIFTING! Away from the light
DRIFTING! Into the endless night
No gravity left to pull us home
Two dying stars, forever alone

Oxygen counting the seconds down
The blue of Earth is a distant crown
I watch you shrink to a point of white
Swallowed whole by the infinite night

Reach for me through the frozen black
There's no return and there's no way back
Our orbits broken, our voices gone
Two broken hearts in the cold beyond

DRIFTING! Away from the light
DRIFTING! Into the endless night
No gravity left to pull us home
Two dying stars, forever alone

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night when the city goes still
I rise from my bed against my will
The rooftops shrink to a glowing seam
And I'm dancing high in the same old dream

The wind is a partner that holds me near
We spin through the clouds without any fear
No ground beneath and no chains to bind
Just the endless sky and my restless mind

Fly with me, fly through the velvet air
A tango of stars without a care
Every night I rise and I fall
The sweetest dream of them all

But morning comes like a jealous thief
It steals my wings and it steals belief
I wake to gravity, cold and gray
With feathers of memory drifting away

So I close my eyes and I count to five
Waiting for the moment I come alive
The bandoneón calls and I leave the floor
Rising again like the night before

Fly with me, fly through the velvet air
A tango of stars without a care
Every night I rise and I fall
The sweetest dream of them all

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Close my eyes and I'm back in time
Cinnamon dancing, oh so fine
Flour on the counter, sugar in the air
Grandma's humming with the radio there

Butter melting on a Sunday morn
Warm like the day that I was born
The oven glowing like a disco light
Everything's gonna be alright

Take me back, back to the kitchen glow
Where the love was warm and the heat was low
Spin me round on the linoleum floor
Grandma's cooking, I want some more

Apron tied and her hands so kind
A recipe stored in the back of my mind
Every spice tells a story of home
A place where my heart will never roam

The strings come up and the memories shine
A boogie of basil, a groove of thyme
Close the door but the smell remains
Dancing soft through the windowpanes

Take me back, back to the kitchen glow
Where the love was warm and the heat was low
Spin me round on the linoleum floor
Grandma's cooking, I want some more

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Three little dots on my screen tonight
Dancing and blinking in the neon light
I waited for love but I got a goodbye
Four cold words and I don't know why

We used to glitter on the dancing floor
Now you ghost me and you close the door
No phone call, baby, no face to face
Just a buzzing heartbreak in cyberspace

You texted me goodbye, oh oh oh
Under the mirror ball's lonely glow
Delivered and read but never replied
You broke my heart on the digital tide

I typed and deleted a hundred times
Looking for reason between the lines
The emoji heart that you used to send
Now it's a period, now it's the end

So I dance alone with my telephone
A disco ball and a dial tone
Let the rhythm carry the pain away
Maybe you'll text me another day

You texted me goodbye, oh oh oh
Under the mirror ball's lonely glow
Delivered and read but never replied
You broke my heart on the digital tide

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The sun came up like it always does
But nothing feels the way it was
Your coffee cup still on the shelf
I'm sitting here all by myself

Shoo-doo-wop, the birds still sing
Life goes on, that funny thing
I fold the suit I wore last night
And step into the morning light

Oh my darling, you're gone away
But the world keeps turning anyway
The flowers wilt and the casseroles cool
And I'm just an empty fool

The phone don't ring like yesterday
The black umbrellas put away
I hum the hymn from the chapel pew
A bum-ba-dum just for you

Who knew that grief could feel so plain
No thunder, lightning, wind or rain
Just dishes drying in the sun
And all the things that won't get done

Oh my darling, you're gone away
But the world keeps turning anyway
The flowers wilt and the casseroles cool
And I'm just an empty fool

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The door clicks soft and the echoes fade
We said the things that can't be unmade
Now the kitchen hums in a silver hush
And I'm afraid to even touch

Your shadow leans on the bedroom wall
Neither of us wants to fall
The air is thick like a fog of glass
Waiting for this storm to pass

In the quiet, oh, in the quiet
We drift like clouds we can't deny it
Love still glowing underneath
If we could only learn to breathe

The clock ticks slow in a dreamy haze
Lost in the maze of the things we say
I reach for your hand in the dark somehow
Maybe we can find us now

Soft as a feather, soft as the rain
Let the silence wash the pain
No more words, just let it be
Float back slowly here to me

In the quiet, oh, in the quiet
We drift like clouds we can't deny it
Love still glowing underneath
If we could only learn to breathe

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I walked these aisles since I was small
Learned every hymn, learned every call
But something shifted deep in me
A quiet door, a need to be free

The stained glass colors still feel like home
But my heart is restless, my spirit roams
I'm not angry, I'm just letting go
Walking soft where the candles glow

Farewell, sweet steeple, farewell, old pew
Thank you for everything you grew
I'm closing the door without a sound
Leaving the only home I found

The choir is singing the songs I know
But I'm a stranger in the back row
Faith is a river that changed its way
I cannot stay, I cannot stay

Oh, the bells will ring without me here
And God is bigger than this fear
I carry the light but I leave the walls
Following where the quiet calls

Farewell, sweet steeple, farewell, old pew
Thank you for everything you grew
I'm closing the door without a sound
Leaving the only home I found

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Those hands could knead the morning bread
And smooth the worry from my head
Wrinkled maps of a life well spent
Every line a sacrament

She'd snap the beans on the front porch swing
And hum a tune while the church bells ring
Hands that worked from dusk to dawn
Now the porch is still and she is gone

Grandma's hands, oh, grandma's hands
They built a home in these old lands
Gentle as a mountain stream
Strong as the oak in my fondest dream

She'd hold my face when I would cry
And wipe the tears and teach me why
A needle and thread and a quilt of love
Stitched together from up above

Now I look down at my own two hands
And I see the woman who understands
Every callus, every scar
Tells me, child, of who you are

Grandma's hands, oh, grandma's hands
They built a home in these old lands
Gentle as a mountain stream
Strong as the oak in my fondest dream

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They came with their gold and their silver tongues
Saying lad, you could sing for the wealthy ones
But my words aren't for sale on a velvet shelf
I'd rather be poor and I'd rather be myself

The river still runs and the hills still call
And I'll write my verse on the old stone wall
No crown, no coin, no master's name
Just the wind and the rain and the candle flame

Oh, I won't sell out, I won't bend the knee
My pen belongs to the wild and free
Let the merchants laugh, let the rich men frown
I'll die with my song before I sell it down

The tin whistle cries on the harbor air
A ballad for those who've nothing to spare
They offered me London, they offered me fame
But the muse would weep and I'd hang my shame

So raise up a glass to the words unbought
To the fire of the heart that can't be caught
A fiddle, a story, a road untamed
A poet undying, a soul unchained

Oh, I won't sell out, I won't bend the knee
My pen belongs to the wild and free
Let the merchants laugh, let the rich men frown
I'll die with my song before I sell it down

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The clock strikes two and the coffee's cold
My textbook pages are turning old
The whole world's sleeping but here I am
Cramming for a morning exam

My pencil's dancing a weary beat
Notes scattered down by my tired feet
The lamp light flickers a lonely tune
Just me and the books and the midnight moon

Two in the morning, swinging solo
Nobody here but the afterglow
Flip the page and sip the brew
Gotta make it, gonna push on through

The highlighter squeaks like a saxophone
A one-man band in the study zone
My eyelids droop but I sway and I sway
Dance with the data till the break of day

One more chapter, one more line
The brass section in my head's doing fine
Keep it swinging, keep it light
I'll be a genius by morning light

Two in the morning, swinging solo
Nobody here but the afterglow
Flip the page and sip the brew
Gotta make it, gonna push on through

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
I was nineteen with a borrowed guitar
Sweaty palms in a half-empty bar
Three wrong chords and a voice that shook
Reading lyrics from a wrinkled notebook

The spotlight burned and the mic stand swayed
I forgot every word that I'd ever made
But somebody clapped in the back of the room
And it cut right through the nervous gloom

Oh, that first open mic, that terrible night
My hands were shaking but my heart was bright
I'd give anything to go back there
To that kid who didn't have a care

Now I play to crowds and I know the drill
But nothing beats that first cheap thrill
The fear, the hope, the amateur shine
The night I crossed that starting line

We all begin where the lights are low
With too much heart and nowhere to go
So here's to the kids with the shaking hands
The future of a thousand bands

Oh, that first open mic, that terrible night
My hands were shaking but my heart was bright
I'd give anything to go back there
To that kid who didn't have a care

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Nine to five in a marble hall
A painted girl on the eastern wall
Her blue scarf caught in a wind that died
Two hundred years and she never cried

I learned the freckles, I learned the gold
The way her shadow refused to fold
The tourists pass like a passing rain
I stay behind with the same soft pain
