Don't fade, don't fade on me
You're the only face that waits for me
The lights go low and the doors all close
And you're the only thing that glows
Don't fade, don't fade away
I'll be standing here at the end of day

They say in spring they will sell the floor
A velvet rope and an exit door
I'd buy you back with my empty hands
If love were a thing that the world understands

Dust on the varnish, hum in the wire
My whole slow heart caught in your quiet fire
If you could blink you would see me here
Guarding a ghost for another year

Don't fade, don't fade on me
You're the only face that waits for me
The lights go low and the doors all close
And you're the only thing that glows

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Packed a single bag at the break of grey
Kissed my mother's hands and I walked away
The harbor lights were a string of gold
A ticket in my pocket, a future to hold

The engines hum and the country shrinks
The whole horizon is what you think
Salt on my lips and the deck so wide
Leaving everything but the hope inside

Across the sea, across the sea
To a brand new name they will give to me
Lift your hands if you're far from home
We are not, we are not alone
Across the sea, across the sea
Every wave is carrying me

Strangers dancing in the lower hold
Sharing bread and the songs of old
Different tongues but the same one fear
Same one prayer that the shore is near

Mother don't cry, I will send for you
When the streets are gold and the sky is new
Hold the line, hold the line
The dawn is coming and the dawn is mine

Across the sea, across the sea
To a brand new name they will give to me
Lift your hands if you're far from home
We are not, we are not alone

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete river where the gardens grew
One green crown in a sky of blue
They poured the world into perfect grey
But a single oak wouldn't move away

Kids carve hearts in the stubborn bark
It's the only shade in the parking dark
Roots cracking up through the asphalt seam
Holding on to an older dream

Stand, stand, the last one standing
Reaching up where the light is landing
Every leaf is a flag unfurled
One green heart in a concrete world
Stand, stand, don't let it fall
The last, the last tree of them all

Developers came with a yellow plan
But the whole town circled hand in hand
Said you'll have to take us down as well
There are stories only roots can tell

Breathe in, breathe out, it gives us air
A hundred summers of standing there
Don't pave the last of the living green
It's the truest thing this town has seen

Stand, stand, the last one standing
Reaching up where the light is landing
Every leaf is a flag unfurled
One green heart in a concrete world

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Caught in the downpour on Seventh Street
One broken sky and four wet feet
You held it out and you said come in
A stranger's kindness like a violin

We matched our steps so the rain stayed out
Learned each other's names in the thunder shout
You laughed at nothing, I laughed at you
Two umbrellas would've never do

Stay under, stay under with me
Let the whole sky fall on the empty street
One small circle of dry and warm
Two strangers hiding from the storm
Stay under, stay under tonight
We'll walk forever through the city light

Your corner dripping, my shoulder soaked
Neither of us said a word and broke
The block got shorter with every stride
I didn't want to reach the other side

Give me the long way, give me the rain
Give me the reason to walk again
When the clouds break open and the sun comes through
I'll fold it up but I'll keep you

Stay under, stay under with me
Let the whole sky fall on the empty street
One small circle of dry and warm
Two strangers hiding from the storm

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Well the bulldozers came and they flattened the green
Paved up the prettiest place you've seen
But one old oak said I won't go
Planted my feet and I'm stealing the show

The sidewalk cracks where my roots break free
All the city pigeons come dance with me
Neon and asphalt as far as you see
But the whole town swings 'round the only tree

Swing it, swing it, last one left
Shakin' my leaves like a song's in my breath
Pour all your concrete, do what you do
But baby this oak's gonna outlast you
Swing it, swing it, tall and proud
The last green thing in the grey old crowd

Mister mayor in his fancy car
Stops every morning by the boulevard
Tips his hat to my dancing crown
The last little green in a paved-up town

They can have their towers, their glass, their steel
But nothing alive ever felt so real
Give me a breeze and a patch of sun
And I'll keep on swingin' till the day is done

Swing it, swing it, last one left
Shakin' my leaves like a song's in my breath
Pour all your concrete, do what you do
But baby this oak's gonna outlast you

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I knelt by the candle when I was a boy
And I sang to the saints with a borrowed joy
Then the winter took my mother away
And the words turned to ash and I ceased to pray

I wandered the moors with an empty chest
No heaven above and no hope to rest
The bells in the valley were a cruel old sound
And I cursed the cold and the holy ground

But the river still runs and the green still grows
And the morning comes back though nobody knows
I lost my faith on a barren shore
And I found it again at my own front door

A stranger gave bread when my hands were bare
And a child sang low in the evening air
And something I buried began to stir
Like the first soft thaw on the winter fur

It's not in the steeple, it's not in the crown
It's the kindness that lifts when the world falls down
It's the candle relit by an unknown hand
And I think, after all, I begin to understand

For the river still runs and the green still grows
And the morning comes back though nobody knows
I lost my faith on a barren shore
And I found it again at my own front door

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I was raised on the gospel down a red dirt road
With a worn-out Bible and a heavy load
Then the river took my brother in the spring of nine
And I left the good Lord standing at the county line

I drank up my Sundays and I sold my songs
Told myself the preacher man had got it wrong
But the empty bottle never filled the hole
And the silence got to gnawing at my restless soul

Oh I'm climbing back up that mountain high
Where the morning meets the maker in the open sky
I threw it all away but I'm coming home
On my knees in the field where the wildflowers grow

Well an old man fixed my fences for nothing in return
Said the grace you can't earn is the grace you learn
And I cried like a baby in the tall June hay
And I felt the weight of years just roll away

It ain't in the pew and it ain't in the loud
It's the quiet little mercy in a thankful crowd
It's the seed in the dark that you thought was dead
Rising green and golden where the doubt had spread

Oh I'm climbing back up that mountain high
Where the morning meets the maker in the open sky
I threw it all away but I'm coming home
On my knees in the field where the wildflowers grow

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You stole my parking spot back in ninety-five
I keyed your car and you keyed mine
We been swinging fists down the whole damn block
Till the wedding deejay made the whole thing stop

They paired us up for the partner dance
Nobody gave us a second chance
You grabbed my hand with a look of dread
And the horns kicked in and we moved instead

Step, two, three, we're spinning around
Two old rivals on the dancing ground
I hate your guts but I love this beat
Look at us moving our enemy feet
Step, two, three, can't help but grin
The music's winning so we both give in

You spun me out and you spun me back
Forgot for a sec that we ran the attack
The trumpet laughing at the both of us
Making peace on the dance floor, no big fuss

Maybe the feud was a waste of years
Maybe the brass can dissolve the fears
You're not so bad when you skank in time
Pour me a drink, I'll forget the crime

Step, two, three, we're spinning around
Two old rivals on the dancing ground
I hate your guts but I love this beat
Look at us moving our enemy feet

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They signed me away when the harvest was thin
A man twice my age with a cold tin grin
My mother said hush, it is how it's done
But my heart was a fiddle that longed to run

The wedding was set for the first of frost
A dowry of silver, a daughter the cost
But a klezmer band came through our town
And the clarinet pulled my whole world down

Play it faster, play it wild
I was never meant to be a quiet child
I'll dance through the gate that they built for me
To the road, to the road, where the music is free
Play it faster, play it loud
I'm leaving the chains and the bridal shroud

I left a note on the kitchen sill
Forgive me mother, I love you still
But the life you planned is a borrowed dress
And I'd rather walk barefoot through the wilderness

The horns are crying, the strings are glad
It's the bravest leaving I've ever had
Let the elders mutter, let the matchmaker mourn
A new tomorrow is being born

Play it faster, play it wild
I was never meant to be a quiet child
I'll dance through the gate that they built for me
To the road, to the road, where the music is free

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Seventeen with a borrowed guitar
A coffee shop and a flickering star
My hands were shaking on the second string
I almost ran before I had to sing

Three people clapping and one was my mom
The mic feedback and my voice gone wrong
But somewhere in the middle I forgot to be scared
And for ninety seconds I was finally there

Oh that night, that little spotlight
Where I found out I could fly
A shaky start in a quiet bar
Is the reason for who you are
Oh that night, I still hold it tight
The first time I touched the sky

Now the rooms are bigger and they know my name
But nothing's ever felt quite the same
As that scared kid with the trembling sound
Who stood right up when the whole world frowned

If I could go back I would tell her this
Don't you change a thing, don't fix the miss
Every wrong note was a stepping stone
To the stage where you'd finally feel at home

Oh that night, that little spotlight
Where I found out I could fly
A shaky start in a quiet bar
Is the reason for who you are

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
Got a letter in my drawer that I never sent
All the angry years and the rage I spent
You were never there for a single game
Left my mother holding all the blame
I built a wall so high out of all that hurt
Put your name in the dirt, dragged it through the dirt
Then the phone call came on a Tuesday cold
You were gone before the truth got told

So I'm letting it go, letting it go
Talking to a stone where the flowers grow
I forgive you now though you'll never know
It's the only way my heart can grow
Letting it go, letting it go
To the empty chair in the front row

Funny how it's easy to hate a ghost
Harder when you miss the thing you needed most
I found your old jacket in the basement gray
Smelled like the man who walked away
But I can't carry this another mile
So I'm setting it down with a broken smile
Not for you, no, this one's for me
Forgiveness is the key to set me free

Maybe you were broken by your own old man
Doing the worst with the worst you ran
I don't excuse it but I understand
And I'm laying these flowers from a steady hand

So I'm letting it go, letting it go
Talking to a stone where the flowers grow
I forgive you now though you'll never know
It's the only way my heart can grow

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
It's a quarter past midnight and the bottle's low
There's a name in my phone that I shouldn't know
We ended it clean in the cold of May
But my thumb keeps drifting to the words to say

The cursor's blinking like a warning light
The whole house sleeping in the dead of night
My wife's wedding ring on the nightstand glows
And I'm typing a message that nobody knows

Don't hit send, don't hit send
It's a road you don't want to walk again
There's a good life sleeping down the hall
Don't throw it away for nothing at all
Don't hit send, put it down
Before the morning comes around

We were young and stupid and a long time gone
But some old fires never quite move on
I could ruin everything with three little lines
Trade the truth I built for the old red signs

So I delete it slow, every letter back
Pour the whiskey out and I cut the slack
Walk down the hall to the breathing dark
And I hold the one who holds my heart

Don't hit send, don't hit send
It's a road you don't want to walk again
There's a good life sleeping down the hall
Don't throw it away for nothing at all

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the foundations on a curving spine
Not knowing the hills were a body's line
The towers rose on a shoulder blade
And the rivers ran where the breathing stayed

We drink from wells in a giant's palm
And the slow tide hums like a distant psalm
No one remembers, no one was told
The ground is alive and impossibly old

Sleep on, sleep on beneath the stone
We built our lives on your dreaming bone
Don't wake, don't wake, just breathe us through
The city is only a coat on you
Sleep on, sleep on, so far below
The softest secret the streets will never know

Sometimes the towers will gently sway
And the old ones whisper it's just the bay
But the tremor follows a heartbeat slow
And the children feel what the grown-ups know

What dreams move under the marble square
What ancient patience is sleeping there
We are the dust on a giant's brow
And we pray it keeps on dreaming now

Sleep on, sleep on beneath the stone
We built our lives on your dreaming bone
Don't wake, don't wake, just breathe us through
The city is only a coat on you

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon flicker on the rain-slick glass
Coffee getting cold as the hours pass
Booth in the corner where the night owls land
A waitress humming with a pot in her hand

Truckers and dreamers and the brokenhearted
End up here when the bars departed
Two bucks bottomless and a quiet ear
Every lonely soul finds a home right here

Keep the lights on, keep the lights on low
For the ones with no place left to go
Three a.m. and the grill still warm
A little yellow window in the storm
Keep the lights on, let it glow
The diner that never closes the door

Old Ray's been cooking since sixty-two
Knows your order before you do
The jukebox plays what the sad hearts need
And nobody rushes you to leave

When the whole world's sleeping and you can't unwind
There's a stool and a smile and a slice of pie kind
No questions asked and no clock to beat
Just the hum of the fridge and the warm vinyl seat

Keep the lights on, keep the lights on low
For the ones with no place left to go
Three a.m. and the grill still warm
A little yellow window in the storm

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I memorize the way you say my name
Like it's nothing, like it's all the same
You laugh at my jokes and you hold my arm
Never knowing that you do me harm

You call me at two when your heart is sore
Cry on my shoulder 'bout the one you adore
And I wipe your tears with a steady hand
Wishing you could only understand

'Cause I love you, I love you, but you don't see
There's a whole ocean here inside of me
I'm the safest harbor that you'll never sail
Loving you quiet is a lonely jail
I love you, I love you, and it's killing me slow
To be the best friend that you'll never know

You ask me for advice on your latest flame
And I smile and I help and I hide the same
The truth that's been burning since the day we met
The one confession I can't make yet

Maybe in some other life we'd be
The kind of love I only get to dream
But I'd rather have you near and ache this way
Than lose your laughter to the words I'd say

'Cause I love you, I love you, but you don't see
There's a whole ocean here inside of me
I'm the safest harbor that you'll never sail
Loving you quiet is a lonely jail

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The screen door settled back against the frame
The kettle ticked, too tired to call your name
We said the kind of things you can't unsay
Then let the silence carry them away

Your coffee's going cold there on the sill
I hear your breathing slow, the house gone still
The dog won't pick a side, he never does
He just lays down between us like a truce

It's quiet now, the kind that hums
The kind that comes when the shouting's done
I'd take your hand if you'd let me try
Love ain't the war, it's the quiet after
The quiet after we don't say goodbye

The radio's just static down the dial
I catch the corner of your tired smile
We're both too proud and both too worn to leave
So we just sit and let the evening breathe

Maybe sorry's just a porch light left on
Maybe staying's how we say it all along
The storm rolled through and took the worst of us
And left this hush we're learning how to trust

It's quiet now, the kind that mends
The kind that comes when the anger ends
I'd take your hand if you'd let me try
Love ain't the war, it's the quiet after
The quiet after we don't say goodbye

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Old Sam been cutting hair on Macon Street
Since before my daddy had a beard to keep
The little bell rings, the leather chair leans back
And the whole town's history's hanging on that rack

He knows who's getting married, who's been laid to rest
Who's running for the council, who's gonna lose the bet
He never says a name but he hears it all
The mirror holds the secrets on that barbershop wall

Snip, snip, the scissors sing
Sam knows everybody, knows everything
From the cradle to the corner where the old men stand
The whole town's heartbeat in his steady hand

He lined up little Jordan for his very first day
And shaved his grandfather before they sent him away
Same striped pole still turning in the morning sun
Same warm hello for every mother's son

When the streetlights flicker and he locks the door
He sweeps the day's confessions off the checkered floor
A keeper of the stories nobody else will hold
A quiet kind of preacher with a heart of gold

Snip, snip, the scissors sing
Sam knows everybody, knows everything
From the cradle to the corner where the old men stand
The whole town's heartbeat in his steady hand

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Concrete and chrome on a chest that breathes
Skyscrapers swaying in the evening breeze
We paved the boulevard right over his spine
And we party all night like it's borrowed time

Don't wake him up, don't wake him up
Keep the bassline low, fill another cup
The giant's dreaming and the city grooves
Every time we dance, the whole earth moves
Don't wake him up, no no

Subway rumble like a heartbeat slow
The streetlights flicker when he rolls below
A tremor in the dancefloor, a shimmy in the wall
We just call it rhythm and we have a ball

The mayor's on the rooftop, the band's gettin' loose
Got a giant for a basement and there's no excuse
We stack the speakers higher, let the funk go down
A hundred stories rockin' on a sleeping crown

One day he gonna stretch and roll over slow
And the whole skyline gonna have to let go
But tonight we got the pocket, got the horns, got the strut
So keep it greasy, keep it sweaty, don't you ever stop

Don't wake him up, don't wake him up
Keep the bassline low, fill another cup
The giant's dreaming and the city grooves
Every time we dance, the whole earth moves

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Got the truck bed loaded and the tank near full
Watched the sun come up over the water tower's hull
Mama's on the porch with a dishrag and a wave
This town raised me up but it ain't where I'll be saved

The same three streets, the same old Friday game
Everybody knows me, nobody knows my name
I counted every mile marker I could find
Now I'm gonna count 'em going the other kind

Goodbye, little town, I'm gone for good
I loved you more than leaving ever should
But there's a road out past the county line
And it's calling out a future that I gotta make mine
Goodbye, little town, goodbye

The diner where I waited tables after school
The river where I learned that breaking hearts is cruel
I'm taking all of it inside my chest
And leaving all the rest to find the rest

Maybe I'll come back when my hair's turned gray
Maybe I'll just send a postcard on the way
But a part of me is buried in that red clay ground
And a bigger part is finally outward bound

Goodbye, little town, I'm gone for good
I loved you more than leaving ever should
But there's a road out past the county line
And it's calling out a future that I gotta make mine

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Ooh, the room got still (so still)
After all the words we spilled
We let our tempers run
Now look what we have done (shoo-bop)

In the quiet, quiet after the storm
I just wanna hold you, keep you warm
Don't go to bed with a frown, my dear
Let me whisper sweet and clear (shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
That I'm right here, right here

We danced and now we fell (we fell)
Rang the angry old doorbell
But anger fades to blue
And all that's left is you (woah-oh)

So take my hand, let's start again
Forget the where, forget the when
The silence isn't goodbye, my love
It's just the calm we're dreaming of

In the quiet, quiet after the storm
I just wanna hold you, keep you warm
Don't go to bed with a frown, my dear
Let me whisper sweet and clear (shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
That I'm right here, right here

The radio plays low (so low)
And I think you ought to know
That every fight we weather
Just glues us back together (shoo-bop, sha-na-na)

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle through the neon, midnight on the seam
Every thread a heartbeat, every stitch a dream
Mr. Okafor in the window light
Cutting cloth for kings on a Tuesday night

He dress the whole block, sharp on the run
From the morning hustle to the setting sun
Faster, faster, the bassline rolls
He sew the city's body, he sew its soul
He dress the whole block, the whole block

DJ at the corner, beat against the glass
He measure up the future, he tailor up the past
Gold thread, fine thread, lining of the brave
Everybody fly when they step out his way

Breakbeat shuffle on the cutting room floor
A hundred hungry tickets pinned up by the door
He never miss a fitting, never drop a stitch
Makes the broke man regal, makes the poor man rich

Rolling, rolling, the snare runs deep
He stitch through the hours when the city's asleep
Every suit a promise, every hem a vow
The whole block sharp and it's all because of how

He dress the whole block, sharp on the run
From the morning hustle to the setting sun
Faster, faster, the bassline rolls
He sew the city's body, he sew its soul

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Back room smoke, low light hum
Four of us deep when the dealer come
Stacked up paper on the felt so green
Biggest pot that the table ever seen
I was up early, shoulda walked away
But greed got a grip and it told me stay
King on the river, my heart hit the floor
Now the stakes ain't money no more

Went too far, went too far
Shoulda folded when I saw who they are
Chips on the table, hand on the steel
Nobody bluffin' when the danger's real
Went too far, too far this time
All in on a hand I can't unwind

He flipped his cards then he flipped the script
Reached for his waist and the whole room tipped
Door's too far and the window's barred
Learned the hard way how they guard the yard
Ace up the sleeve ain't a smart idea
When the man you cheatin' got nothing to fear
Clock on the wall going tick, tick, tick
Last hand dealt and it's making me sick

Mama told me gambling rot the bone
Shoulda stayed my broke self safe at home
Now I'm counting exits, counting breath
Playing the only game that ends in death

Went too far, went too far
Shoulda folded when I saw who they are
Chips on the table, hand on the steel
Nobody bluffin' when the danger's real

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Well I climbed up the holler to the barber's old chair
Where Earl's been a-cuttin' since I don't know where
His razor strop slappin' and his banjo on the wall
That man knows the business of one and of all

Snip go the scissors, hum go the clippers
Earl knows every soul from the saints to the sinners
Who's courtin', who's quittin', who's down on his luck
The whole town's a-spillin' in the back of his truck

He shaved my old grandpa 'fore the good Lord called
And he trimmed up my cowlick when I was real small
He don't carry tales but he carries the load
Of every little secret up and down this old road

The fiddle's a-sawin' and the porch light's a-glow
Folks gather round Earl just to hear what he know
But he winks and he whistles and he keeps it inside
With a grin and a comb and a barbershop pride

When they lay old Earl down 'neath the sycamore tree
The whole town'll come for the memory
For the man with the razor knew us all by heart
And he kept us together when we drifted apart

Snip go the scissors, hum go the clippers
Earl knows every soul from the saints to the sinners
Who's courtin', who's quittin', who's down on his luck
The whole town's a-spillin' in the back of his truck

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slid shut and the lights went thin
A thousand strangers and the walls closing in
My chest is a drum that I can't slow down
We're under the river, under the town

Count the rivets, count the stops
Count the breaths till the feeling drops
The tunnel screams and the handrail sweats
My own heartbeat is the loudest threat

Breathe, just breathe, the train rolls on
This isn't dying, it only feels that long
Hold the silence, hold the light
We'll surface soon into the night
Breathe, just breathe

Nobody sees me come apart
Folding in half around my heart
The map's a tangle of colored veins
And I am drowning in the lanes

And then the swell, the rising tide
Guitars like dawn on the other side
The ground comes up to meet my feet
The doors slide open onto the street
The sky, the sky, the open sky
I didn't die, I didn't die

Breathe, just breathe, the train rolls on
This isn't dying, it only feels that long
Hold the silence, hold the light
We surfaced into the open night

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Luna llena, motor caliente
Trunk full of fire, gotta move it for the gente
Back roads only, lights turned low
Moonshine running where the rivers don't go
Dale, dale, foot on the gas
Thought this little run was gonna be a pass

Ay, salió mal, salió mal
The siren in the mirror like a wild animal
Bottles in the back going clink, clink, clink
Faster than my racing heart can think
Ay, salió mal esta noche
Moonshine running in a borrowed coche

Sharp turn, gravel, smoke in the air
Glass everywhere and the law don't care
Killed the headlights, ran into the trees
Whiskey on my hands and blood on my knees
Primo on the phone saying "where you at?"
No answer good enough to answer that

Mama warned me 'bout the easy dollar
Now I'm in the ditch with a torn-up collar
The still keeps burning but the run is dead
Shoulda kept the bottles and kept my head

Ay, salió mal, salió mal
The siren in the mirror like a wild animal
Bottles in the back going clink, clink, clink
Faster than my racing heart can think

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every morning at a quarter to nine
I take my post by the velvet line
There's a painting on the wall I've come to know
A field of gold in the evening glow

The crowds come through and they barely see
But that light on the canvas, it speaks to me
Like the hand that made it left a little prayer
For a tired old guard standing right there

Oh, glory, glory in a wooden frame
The artist gone but the light's the same
I guard the beauty, but the beauty guards me
A window to the Maker that I can't quite see
Glory, glory, hallelujah

When the doors are locked and the halls go dim
I stand alone and I hum a hymn
Just me and the field and the painted sun
Two quiet keepers when the day is done

They pay me to watch but I came to pray
For the grace that a brush can give away
If heaven's a color, it's hanging right here
And it holds me steady, year after year

Oh, glory, glory in a wooden frame
The artist gone but the light's the same
I guard the beauty, but the beauty guards me
A window to the Maker that I can't quite see

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
I wasn't gonna dance, just came to toast the bride
Stood against the wall with a drink down by my side
Then the band slowed down and you crossed the floor
Held out your hand like I'd done this before

Dancing with a stranger, swaying in the light
Don't know your name but I know tonight
The whole room's spinning and the world fell away
Two strangers learning what the heart wants to say
Dancing with a stranger, oh

Your cheek against my shoulder, the saxophone cried
We moved like we'd been keeping this rhythm inside
The DJ, the cousins, the cake, the rings
All faded to the slow sweet song the singer sings

Maybe when the morning comes you're gone
Maybe this is just one perfect song
But for three sweet minutes you were mine, all mine
And I held a little forever in four-four time

Dancing with a stranger, swaying in the light
Don't know your name but I know tonight
The whole room's spinning and the world fell away
Two strangers learning what the heart wants to say

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raise my hand, I clear my throat
Got a brilliant little thing I wrote
But the words barely leave my chair
Before somebody's talking over the air

Same idea I said an hour ago
Comes back from Dave like a radio show
Suddenly it's gold, suddenly it's smart
Funny how it sounded better from the start

Talked over, talked under, talked clean off the map
My two cents disappearing in the gap
I'm a muted little horn in a too-loud band
Playing solos that nobody planned
Talked over again, mm-hmm

The coffee's gone cold and the clock won't move
Everybody's got a point they gotta prove
I'll save my breath for the elevator ride
Where the only one listening is me, myself, inside

But one of these mornings I'll find my key
Hit a note so blue they got to let me be
Swing it slow, then swing it loud and free
And the whole room finally turns to me

Talked over, talked under, talked clean off the map
My two cents disappearing in the gap
I'm a muted little horn in a too-loud band
Playing solos that nobody planned

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
There's a fella down on Bleecker Street (ooh-wah)
Writes his verses where the misfits meet
They offered him a dollar and a fancy name
But he won't trade his soul for the hall of fame (sha-boom)

He won't sell out, no no no
Keeps it honest in the soft lamp glow
They can keep their gold and their neon shout
My poet, my poet, he won't sell out (shoo-bop)

The big men came in a long black car (long black car)
Said "write us a jingle, you'll be a star"
But he just smiled and he shook his head
And wrote a little truth for the heart instead (woah-oh)

They laughed at his coat with the elbows worn
But his words outlive every dollar born
He's richer than the richest man in town
'Cause he'd rather be true than wear the crown

He won't sell out, no no no
Keeps it honest in the soft lamp glow
They can keep their gold and their neon shout
My poet, my poet, he won't sell out

So here's to the dreamer with the empty purse (empty purse)
Who gave us the gift of an honest verse
Long may he scribble by the candle's light (sha-na-na)
The poet who kept his heart all right

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Neon rivers down a chrome divide
A million windows on a giant's side
We built our towers on a beating chest
And dance in the glow while the titan rests

Under the lights, under the hum
The city breathes to a borrowed drum
Don't wake the giant, keep it slow
We live on the heart of the thing below
Under the neon, under the night

The monorail hums on a sleeping spine
The skyline trembles to a perfect time
Synth in the static, pulse in the deep
A whole electric world on a giant's sleep

Purple horizon, magenta haze
We map his dreaming in our cathode rays
Every streetlight a careful tone
A lullaby of glass and chrome and bone

One distant day he'll open his eyes
And swallow the towers and the painted skies
But tonight we cruise on the warm machine
Through the most beautiful sleep we've ever seen

Under the lights, under the hum
The city breathes to a borrowed drum
Don't wake the giant, keep it slow
We live on the heart of the thing below

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The dishes still sit where you left them
Water gone cold in the sink
We said the words we can't unspeak
Now the kitchen won't let me think

There's a clock on the wall keeping score
Of every minute we don't talk
Your shoes are turned toward the door
But neither one of us will walk

Lord, send me the quiet, the holy kind
Not the silence that splits us apart
Lay a balm on this stubborn mind
And unclench the fist in my heart

I hear you breathing down the hall
Slow as a hymn nobody starts
Grace is a stone too heavy to roll
Till mercy comes and does its part

Who will be first to cross the floor
Who will be first to lay it down
I'll meet you halfway and a little more
If love can still be found

Lord, send me the quiet, the healing kind
The peace that comes after the storm
Lay a balm on this stubborn mind
And lead me back to your arms

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the diner when the rooster's still asleep
Clock out, clock in at the warehouse, loading boxes I can't keep
My hands are two old dollar bills, folded soft and thin
I work till the moon comes hunting, then I do it all again

Two jobs, one shadow, and a stack of red
The rent keeps climbing higher than the prayers above my bed
I'm running on a treadmill, daddy, sweating in the rain
Work all day, work all night, and still I fall behind again

The landlord left a paper taped right across my name
The power company calling, they don't ask me how I came
I count my change on the counter, nickel, dime, and shame
The more I pour into the bucket, the more it drinks the same

My mama said hard work would lift me
Lord, I'd like to know just when
The ladder keeps on losing rungs
Every time I climb again

Two jobs, one shadow, and a stack of red
The rent keeps climbing higher than the prayers above my bed
I'm running on a treadmill, baby, sweating in the rain
Work all day, work all night, and still I fall behind again

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Gold light spilling on a rented floor
Somebody's cousin sang a little flat
You caught my wrist by the open door
Said the band's too good to waste like that

So we sway, no names, just the bassline low
Two strangers borrowing the night
Your palm warm against the small of my soul
Let the chandelier do all the talking with its light

You smell like champagne and somebody's perfume
A stolen napkin folded in your hand
We orbit slow around the cake and the bloom
The deejay reads us like he understands

I don't need your number, don't need your town
Just three more songs before the bouquet flies
Let the saxophone melt the ceiling down
And keep the morning out of both our eyes

So we sway, no names, just the bassline low
Two strangers borrowing the night
Your palm warm against the small of my soul
Let the chandelier do all the talking with its light

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Last night, last light, dust on the wristband glow
Mud on my Jordans, I been three days on the low
Speakers like thunder rolling under my chest
Sun went down on the field but we ain't tryna rest

Last night, turn it up, we don't go home
Fireworks crack where the festival roam
Grab somebody hand 'fore the lights go gone
Last night, last night, ride it till the dawn

Gate close at midnight but the bass say no
Flask in my sock and a stamp on my wrist bone
Met a whole city in a circle of strangers
Tomorrow we ghosts, tonight we the angels

I'ma miss this when the tents come down
When the field's just a field and a quiet brown
So I burn it in my memory, frame by frame
Nobody's a stranger when we screaming the same

Last night, turn it up, we don't go home
Fireworks crack where the festival roam
Grab somebody hand 'fore the lights go gone
Last night, last night, ride it till the dawn

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The scout sat third row, hat on his knee
Lined up my notes like a row of dressed-up dimes
I played that bridge like it could set me free
And the room held its breath in all the right times

So close I could taste the brass on my tongue
The marquee was spelling my name in my head
One wrong reed in a song half-sung
And the morning came up empty instead

He shook my hand, said maybe next spring
Left his card on the lip of the stage
I watched the door swallow everything
While the bartender flipped to the closing page

Funny how almost has a sound
Half a beat behind the snare
I carried that almost all over town
Like smoke that still remembers the air

So close I could taste the brass on my tongue
The marquee was spelling my name in my head
One wrong reed in a song half-sung
And I played to the chairs after everyone fled

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The candle's burned down to a stubborn blue thread
Your chair pushed back, but you haven't gone far
The samovar's cold and the words that we said
Hang in the room like a cracked little star

Oy, let the clarinet cry what we won't
Let the fiddle go where our voices can't
The quiet's a dance that the heart learns slow
Step toward me, love, in this trembling chant

Grandmother's clock keeps a stubborn old time
The table between us as wide as the sea
I'd cross it for a kopek, I'd cross it for a dime
If you'd only look up and cross it to me

We danced at our wedding till the floorboards complained
Now we stand on the same wood and don't make a sound
Let the old melody come in like rain
And loosen the silence wrapped tight all around

Oy, let the clarinet cry what we won't
Let the fiddle go where our voices can't
The quiet's a dance that the heart learns slow
Step toward me, love, in this trembling chant

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man Marcel on the gray morning sand
(shoo-doo, shoo-doo-wah)
A boy and a pail held tight in his hand
He says watch how she breathes, son, in and away
The sea's been a teacher since the first gray day

In and out, in and out, that's how the water sings
She takes what she takes and she gives what she brings
Be like the tide, little one, learn to let go
(ooh-wah) come in slow, go out slow

He pointed his cane at the foam on the stone
(shoo-doo, shoo-doo-wah)
Said she never once hurried to make the shore home
She'll wear down a mountain and never raise a fist
That's the patience, my boy, that the strong ones miss

The boy is a man now with sand in his hair
He walks the same shoreline, Marcel isn't there
But he hears the old voice in the in and the out
In and out, in and out, that's what life's about

In and out, in and out, that's how the water sings
She takes what she takes and she gives what she brings
Be like the tide, little one, learn to let go
(ooh-wah) come in slow, go out slow

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
They put Joe in a box but the box couldn't hold
The trumpet got loud and the crowd got bold
Dirge down Rampart, slow as the heat
Then the snare hit double and we found our feet

Second line, second line, dance him to the gate
Death came early but he can't make us wait
Kick up the gutter, raise a paper fan
We bury you loud in New Orleans, man

Umbrellas spinning over sweat and brass
We stomped out the grief in the broken glass
Tuba like a heartbeat refusing to quit
The preacher gave up and just danced through it

No crying in straight time, no, not here
We cry in a riff and we drink the beer
Joe would've hated a quiet goodbye
So we tore up the street and we made it fly

Second line, second line, dance him to the gate
Death came early but he can't make us wait
Kick up the gutter, raise a paper fan
We bury you loud in New Orleans, man

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Sixteen, shaky, crumpled page in my fist
Three people clapping and a list I almost missed
Mic smelled like pennies, my voice came out thin
But I said my little something and I let it begin

Look how far, look how far, from that coffee shop floor
Now the speakers shake the city, can't fit through the door
But I still hear that kid with the trembling hand
First open mic, that's where I learned to stand

Forgot my second verse, just froze in the light
A stranger in the back said keep going, you alright
Five crumpled dollars in the tip jar that night
Felt like a record deal, felt like taking flight

I been to the arenas, seen my name up in chrome
But nothing hit harder than that walk back home
Whispering my bars to the empty bus seat
Dreaming up the bassline under my feet

Look how far, look how far, from that coffee shop floor
Now the speakers shake the city, can't fit through the door
But I still hear that kid with the trembling hand
First open mic, that's where I learned to stand

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Granddad walked me down to the bay 'fore the sun
Boots in the wet where the saltgrass leans
Said son, the water's the only clock I've run
It'll teach you more than a man your age means

She comes in, she goes out, she don't ask your name
The moon does the pulling and the tide does the same
Learn to read her rhythm and she'll carry you home
That's the only lesson worth the salt and the foam

He baited a hook with his weathered old thumb
Watched the line where the heron stood still
Said don't fight the current, just feel where it's from
A patient man eats while the hurried go hungry on the hill

He's been gone three winters, the dock's gone gray
But I bring my own boy to the edge of the day
And I point at the water doing what water's done
And I say the same words like the rising sun

She comes in, she goes out, she don't ask your name
The moon does the pulling and the tide does the same
Learn to read her rhythm and she'll carry you home
That's the only lesson worth the salt and the foam

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
You were three lockers down and a thousand years ago
Now you're pouring coffee where the old arcade used to glow
Same chipped water tower, same dead-end summer street
But you laugh a little different and it knocks me off my feet

I came back to bury something, not to start
But you've got the whole town glued back to my heart
Funny how the place I swore I'd leave
Is the only place I finally believe

We drove past the high school, you named every ghost
The parking lot we cried in, the bridge we loved the most
I moved to find a bigger sky, came home with empty hands
And found you reading paperbacks where the river bends and bends

Maybe what I ran from wasn't here at all
Maybe it was me, too scared to fall
Now the streetlights flicker on like they remember my name
And you're standing in the orange light, glad I came

I came back to bury something, not to start
But you've got the whole town glued back to my heart
Funny how the place I swore I'd leave
Is the only place I finally believe

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Fireworks outside but I'm under the ward light
Scrubs on, badge on, eighteenth hour tonight
My children on a video call, blowing me a kiss
Mama working, mama working, somebody gotta do this

Holiday, holiday, but the heart still beats
So I dance in my sneakers down the empty corridor streets
Give me the rhythm, give me the night
I'm tired in my bones but my hands hold tight

Bed seven needs water, bed nine needs a prayer
The doctor went home but you'll always find me there
They count the new year while I count a pulse
Sweet small life in my palm, that's the only result

When the sun come up gold over the parking lot
I'll drive home slow to the love I got
Save me a plate, save me a song
Mama coming home where she belong

Holiday, holiday, but the heart still beats
So I dance in my sneakers down the empty corridor streets
Give me the rhythm, give me the night
I'm tired in my bones but my hands hold tight

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I dialed the number I never let myself forget
It rang like a tide pulling back from the shore
Ten digits I'd buried under all my regret
And the hush before hello cracked open a door

Mom, it's me, it's been so long
The years went soft and pale and wrong
I'm sorry the silence grew so wide
Can I come back in from the cold outside

Her voice came through like light through a curtain drawn
Thinner than I left it, but warm as the kitchen used to be
We talked about the weather while the real words yawned
Then she said your room's still painted blue, come see

All the things I rehearsed dissolved like fog
There was only the breathing, only the line
Love doesn't keep a ledger, doesn't keep a log
It just opens its arms one more time

Mom, it's me, it's been so long
The years went soft and pale and wrong
I'm sorry the silence grew so wide
Can I come back in from the cold outside

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five dollars a game under the old oak shade
Clock slapping fast, watch the moves I made
They call me Pastor 'cause I'll send your king to pray
Set 'em up, knock 'em down, all day every day

Come and test me, come and test me on the board
Queen do the dancing while you reaching for your sword
Time ticking, time ticking, pay the man his due
The park is my kingdom and the pawns belong to you

Tourist sit down smiling with his fancy little watch
Four moves later I'm collecting from his pocket
I learned this on a milk crate when I was a kid
Now I read your whole future in the way your fingers slid

They think it's just hustle, but it's prophecy and art
Thirty years of patience folded into the start
The pigeons know my rhythm, the benches know my name
Life is just a longer, slower version of the game

Come and test me, come and test me on the board
Queen do the dancing while you reaching for your sword
Time ticking, time ticking, pay the man his due
The park is my kingdom and the pawns belong to you

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Soft was the night and the jars in the trunk
Glass kissing glass on the old river road
The moon laid a silver coin down where I drove
And I hummed to the curve of my dangerous load

Oh, the run went wrong, so wrong, my dear
Blue lights blooming in the rearview glow
The sweetest little melody turned to fear
With a hundred proof secret and nowhere to go

I took the low bridge where the willows lean down
Killed the headlamps and prayed to the fog
But the sheriff knew the back roads better than the town
And the jars sang soprano as I hit the log

Now I tap out the rhythm on a cold county bunk
The samba still swaying somewhere in my chest
They poured my whole summer in the ditch with a clunk
But the night was so pretty, I confess, I confess

Oh, the run went wrong, so wrong, my dear
Blue lights blooming in the rearview glow
The sweetest little melody turned to fear
With a hundred proof secret and nowhere to go

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Ink on my hands, I'm scribbling in the dark
Neon city humming, every line a spark
They offered me the gold, the lights, the easy way
But I keep my pen honest 'til the break of day
They want a jingle, want a hook to sell
I'd rather stay broke than buy myself a cell

I won't sell out, I won't fold
My words ain't for sale, they can't be sold
Keep it real, keep it true
Every verse I write is straight from the soul

Coffee going cold beside a notebook page
Turning all my struggle into something sage
They say conform, they say just play the game
But I'd rather keep my soul than chase the fame

So I lean back in the static and the rain
Let the vinyl crackle ease away the pain
No I won't trade my heart for a paycheck line
Every word I bleed is gonna stay all mine

I won't sell out, I won't fold
My words ain't for sale, they can't be sold
Keep it real, keep it true
Every verse I write is straight from the soul

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
There's a little neon sign down on the county road
Burnin' through the midnight where the lonely souls unload
Coffee's always brewin' and the grill is always hot
It's the only light for miles when the rest of town forgot

Oh the diner stays open all night long
Where the weary find a place where they belong
Pour another cup, let the jukebox play
We'll be open 'til the dawn brings back the day

Truckers and the dreamers come a-rollin' through the door
Waitress knows your order 'fore your boots hit the floor
Apple pie and stories that we've all heard a hundred times
But they taste a little sweeter under flickerin' lights

When your whole world's closin' and you got nowhere to go
There's a stool that's waitin' and a friend that you might know

Oh the diner stays open all night long
Where the weary find a place where they belong
Pour another cup, let the jukebox play
We'll be open 'til the dawn brings back the day

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone is heavy in my trembling hand tonight
A hundred miles of silence and a fading light
I rehearsed the words a thousand times before
But my heart forgets them when you're on the other shore

So I'm calling you home, mother of mine
Across the cold water and the long passing time
Forgive me the silence, forgive me the years
Let the sound of your voice wash away all my fears

The hills remember running where I used to roam
And the candle in the window of our little stone home
I carried all my anger like a stone in my chest
But I'm tired of the carrying, I'm laying it to rest

The ring, the ring, the waiting on the line
And then your gentle hello, oh that voice of mine

So I'm calling you home, mother of mine
Across the cold water and the long passing time
Forgive me the silence, forgive me the years
Let the sound of your voice wash away all my fears

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Ten and two on the wheel, baby keep it steady
Heart racin' faster than the engine already
You stalled it on the hill but I ain't even mad
Look at you growin' up, makin' your old man glad
Mirror, signal, check your blind spot, slow it down
We just cruisin' through the empty lot around the town

Drive, baby drive, keep your hands on the wheel
I'm right here beside you, tell me how it feel
You the future in the front seat, I'm so proud
Go on hit the gas a little, say it loud

Yesterday I held you, now you holdin' the keys
Tryna act all calm but I'm sweatin' through the breeze
Green light, you press it, and we lurch into the lane
You laughin' at your daddy grippin' tight to stay sane

One day you gon' drive away and I'll watch them taillights
But for now you're learnin', and I treasure these nights

Drive, baby drive, keep your hands on the wheel
I'm right here beside you, tell me how it feel
You the future in the front seat, I'm so proud
Go on hit the gas a little, say it loud

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Glitter on your eyelids, fading like the day
The last song is playing and we don't wanna stray
Fairy lights are swaying in a sky of velvet blue
Every color blurring into me and into you

Don't let the morning come, don't let it end
Hold me in the haze, my dizzy friend
One more song, one more glow
This last night is all we know

Dust and golden ribbons floating through the air
Strangers turned to family with flowers in their hair
The bass is in my chest like a slow and sleepy tide
And I never want to leave this feeling deep inside

When the sun comes creeping over hills of fading sound
We'll carry all this magic as we scatter on the ground

Don't let the morning come, don't let it end
Hold me in the haze, my dizzy friend
One more song, one more glow
This last night is all we know

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
I wasn't gonna stay, just here to see a friend
Then the band started playing and you caught my eye again
You held out your hand, said come on let's just try
Now we're spinning on the floor underneath the string lights high

Dancing with a stranger, lost in the song
Never met before but it don't feel wrong
Hold me a little closer, sway with me slow
A stranger tonight but who's to know

The cake and the speeches, they all fade away
It's just your eyes and mine and the music's gentle sway
I don't know your name yet but I know how this feels
Like the start of something real beneath the spinning wheels

Maybe in the morning we'll be miles apart
But tonight you're the rhythm beating in my heart

Dancing with a stranger, lost in the song
Never met before but it don't feel wrong
Hold me a little closer, sway with me slow
A stranger tonight but who's to know

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The clock strikes two, the world is dead and gray
A mountain of pages, no end to the fray
My eyes are burning embers, my coffee's gone cold
But I'll fight through this darkness, I'll break through the hold

Alone in the night, I am the storm
Knowledge is the fire keeping me warm
No surrender, no retreat, no sleep
Through the silence and the pain I'll keep

The whispers of doubt try to drag me below
But I sharpen my mind like a blade in the snow
Every formula a battle, every word a war
I'll conquer this mountain, that's what I'm fighting for

When the dawn finally bleeds through the cracks in the wall
I'll be standing victorious, I will not fall

Alone in the night, I am the storm
Knowledge is the fire keeping me warm
No surrender, no retreat, no sleep
Through the silence and the pain I'll keep

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Three little dots and then they disappeared
Everything I worried 'bout, everything I feared
A paragraph of goodbye on a glowing screen
The coldest little ending that I've ever seen

You ended us with a text message
No goodbye, just a final message
Typed away everything we had
Now I'm staring at my phone so sad

I read it ten times over like it'd somehow change
But the words stayed the same, distant and so strange
No call and no closure, just a buzz and a tone
And suddenly I'm sitting here completely alone

Delivered, read, and then you went away
A whole love undone in a screen's cold gray

You ended us with a text message
No goodbye, just a final message
Typed away everything we had
Now I'm staring at my phone so sad

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Every night I climb the spiral stair alone
With a candle and a pen and a heart turned to stone
I write to you, my ocean, my cruel and only love
With the salt upon my fingers and the stars up above

Oh the sea, oh the sea, you are mine
I send my burning letters across the foaming brine
You never write me back but still I love you so
My beautiful, my endless, my cold and restless flow

The waves they take my pages and they tear them all apart
But still I keep on writing every secret of my heart
The light it sweeps the darkness, a lover's endless gaze
Watching for your answer through the long and lonely days

Dance with me, you waters, in the moon's silver fire
You are my devotion, you are my one desire

Oh the sea, oh the sea, you are mine
I send my burning letters across the foaming brine
You never write me back but still I love you so
My beautiful, my endless, my cold and restless flow

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete everywhere you look, gray as far as the eye
But there's one green soldier standing tall against the sky
They poured the asphalt over every field and every stream
But that old tree keeps swaying like a stubborn little dream

Last tree standing, get on down
The only bit of green in this paved-over town
Roots in the rubble but it won't lay down
Groovin' to the rhythm of the underground

Kids come and gather in its little patch of shade
The only place in town where the heat begins to fade
Birds got nowhere else to go so they all sing its name
While the city keeps on pourin' more of that concrete shame

So plant another, plant a hundred, let 'em grow
Bring the funky green back, let the whole town know

Last tree standing, get on down
The only bit of green in this paved-over town
Roots in the rubble but it won't lay down
Groovin' to the rhythm of the underground

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They put us in a circle and they pushed us face to face
My worst enemy, my rival, in this stupid little place
The music started blaring and they shoved us in the ring
Now I gotta dance with you, of all the rotten things

One, two, three, four, hate you to the core
But we're dancing on this dirty floor
Step on my toes, I'll step on yours
Two rivals dancing in this war

You grab my hand, I grit my teeth, we spin around the room
Everybody's watching like they're waiting for the doom
But somethin' kinda funny in your stupid angry eyes
Maybe we ain't different, what a rotten little surprise

Fist to fist, now hand to hand, the rhythm's takin' hold
Maybe we'll be friends before this stupid song gets old

One, two, three, four, hate you to the core
But we're dancing on this dirty floor
Step on my toes, I'll step on yours
Two rivals dancing in this war

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Las luces de la feria brillan una vez más
Esta es la última noche, no se quiere acabar
Los violines lloran, las trompetas van a sonar
Bajo el cielo estrellado vamos todos a bailar

���Ay, ay, ay, la última noche!
Canta conmigo, no dejes que termine
La fiesta, la música, el amor
Que viva esta noche con todo su color

Las flores en tu pelo, el vino en la canción
Giramos en la plaza con todo el corazón
Mañana volveremos a la vida y su rumor
Pero esta noche es nuestra, llena de calor

Que suenen las guitarras hasta el amanecer
Esta noche es eterna, no la quiero perder

¡Ay, ay, ay, la última noche!
Canta conmigo, no dejes que termine
La fiesta, la música, el amor
Que viva esta noche con todo su color

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raise my hand and clear my throat, I've got something to say
But the words get swallowed up before they find their way
Another voice cuts in louder, takes the room with ease
And I'm left there in the corner with my unheard pleas

Talked over in every meeting, fading in the noise
Swallowing my sentences, losing all my voice
But I'm gonna find my thunder, gonna make 'em hear
One day I'll speak so loud they can't ignore me here

They took my good idea and they called it their own
While I sat there in silence feeling small and alone
But a quiet kind of fire's been a-building in my chest
And I'm done with being silent, done with second best

So I'll stand up a little taller, let my words ring true
There's a power in my quiet that's about to break through

Talked over in every meeting, fading in the noise
Swallowing my sentences, losing all my voice
But I'm gonna find my thunder, gonna make 'em hear
One day I'll speak so loud they can't ignore me here

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The sun came up this morning like it didn't even know
That we laid you down so gently where the quiet rivers flow
The house is full of silence and the kettle's on the stove
And I'm wearing all your memories like a warm and heavy robe

Morning after, the sun still rise
Tears still falling from my tired eyes
But you're dancing somewhere in the sky
So I'll keep living, I won't say goodbye

The flowers from the service starting wilting on the floor
And the neighbors brought us food but I can't eat no more
Yet a little bird is singing on the window in the light
Like you're telling me to carry on and everything's alright

So I'll dance the way you taught me, feel the rhythm in my soul
You are gone but you are with me, you have made my spirit whole

Morning after, the sun still rise
Tears still falling from my tired eyes
But you're dancing somewhere in the sky
So I'll keep living, I won't say goodbye

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They built their shining towers on a back that's made of stone
A giant lies beneath us and they think it's theirs alone
The subways and the skyscrapers, the lights that never sleep
All resting on a monster buried hundreds of feet deep

Wake up, wake up, the giant's gonna rise
Open up your sleepy, ancient eyes
Shake the city off your back, let it fall
The giant's gonna stand and crush 'em all

They paved over his shoulders, drove their nails into his spine
Drilling for their profit, crossing every single line
But a rumble's getting louder in the cracks beneath the street
And the day is fast approaching when the giant's gonna meet

Tremble, tremble, feel the ground begin to roar
The sleeping giant's waking, he won't sleep no more

Wake up, wake up, the giant's gonna rise
Open up your sleepy, ancient eyes
Shake the city off your back, let it fall
The giant's gonna stand and crush 'em all

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
She walks the gray line where the foam goes thin
Salt in her collar, salt on her chin
She tells the water what the water forgot
Come when I call you, leave when I'm not

Her hands are maps of every storm she crossed
She counts the herons, names the ones she lost
Low tide, low light, the mussels in the rocks
She winds the ocean like a sleeping clock

Go out, come back, go out, come back
Learn the long pull of the moon and the black
I taught you breathing, I taught you to stay
Now take what you're given and carry it away

The young sea argues, pulls against her wrist
She lets it, smiling through the rising mist

One day the shore will hold me where I lie
And you will rise without me, and not ask why
That's all I wanted, water, all along
To leave you fluent in a leaving song

Go out, come back, go out, come back
Learn the long pull of the moon and the black
I taught you breathing, I taught you to stay
Now take what you're given and carry it away

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The boxes by the door, all taped and labeled clean
Mom's making too much coffee, you're seventeen at the seams
We drove down to the reservoir with the radio loud
Spread a blanket on the hood and watched the whole town crowd

One more night, hands in the air
One more song before we're somewhere out there
Don't turn the lights up, don't say goodbye
Just dance with me under this last August sky

Kayla's leaving Tuesday, Marcus left at dawn
We pretend we'll all be the same when summer's gone
Glitter on the dashboard, sneakers in the grass
Let the speakers do the talking, make the moment last

One more night, hands in the air
One more song before we're somewhere out there
Don't turn the lights up, don't say goodbye
Just dance with me under this last August sky

Tomorrow it's a highway and a stranger for a room
But tonight it's just this parking lot and everyone in bloom
So spin me, spin me, let the cheap lights shine
We were gold for a season and the season was mine

One more night, hands in the air
One more song before we're somewhere out there
Don't turn the lights up, don't say goodbye
Just dance with me under this last August sky

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
First chair, Saturday, the line is out the door
Mr. Femi got the clippers humming since '94
He knows your father's father, knows your secret name
Knows who's getting married, knows who left in shame

Sit down, sit down, let the man begin
He'll cut your worries off and keep your business in
Everyone passes through that golden chair
Femi knows the whole town, every story, every prayer

Fade on the left side, lining up the crown
He's the radio, the gossip, and the mayor of the town
A fresh cut for the funeral, a fresh cut for the bride
He sweeps a little quiet when he turns the chair aside

Sit down, sit down, let the man begin
He'll cut your worries off and keep your business in
Everyone passes through that golden chair
Femi knows the whole town, every story, every prayer

Mirror tells the truth that no one else will say
He shapes you for the future while he sweeps the past away
Clippers like a heartbeat, scissors like a song
The whole town in his pocket and he carries it along

Sit down, sit down, let the man begin
He'll cut your worries off and keep your business in
Everyone passes through that golden chair
Femi knows the whole town, every story, every prayer

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Flatbed loaded heavy, jars wrapped up in hay
Midnight on the county road, twelve miles to the bay
Clyde said easy money, said the law's asleep
But the curve at Dempsey Hollow runs too dark and deep

Oh the liquor's in the river now
And the blue lights paint the trees somehow
Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
The whole sweet plan was a three-minute song

Headlight in the mirror like a rising sun
Clyde just laughed and floored it, said a deal's a deal once done
We took the bridge too fast and the boards began to moan
A hundred dollars shattered on the rocks below us, thrown

Now Clyde's in the county and I'm sleeping in the reeds
Learned that quick and crooked never pays you what you need
Moon up on the water where our fortune came apart
A broken jar, a broken man, a slow and sobered heart

Oh the liquor's in the river now
And the blue lights paint the trees somehow
Run gone wrong, run gone wrong
The whole sweet plan was a three-minute song

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Grandaddy on the jetty, salt up in his beard
Forty years a fisherman, ain't a wave he ever feared
He point at the water, say boy watch how it move
The ocean got a rhythm and it ain't got nothin' to prove

In and out, in and out, that's the law
Moon pull the water and the water never fall
Elder teachin' tide, tide teachin' me
Everything that's leavin' comes back from the sea

He say patience is a current you can't rush or fake
Learn to read the surface for the deep that's underneath the break
Hands cracked like the seawall, eyes like the deep
He whisperin' to the swells like they're his own to keep

When they put him in the ground I'ma walk back to the shore
Throw his hat into the foam where the gulls and the breakers roar
He ain't really gone, he just changed his address
He out there in the rhythm, in the in and out, the rest

In and out, in and out, that's the law
Moon pull the water and the water never fall
Elder teachin' tide, tide teachin' me
Everything that's leavin' comes back from the sea

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The kettle's gone cold for the third time tonight
My notes are a country I can't read by this light
The streetlamp's the only one awake on the lane
And the radiator ticks like a slow-moving train

Two in the morning and the whole world's asleep
Just me and my candle and the promises I keep
If I make it to the dawn I'll have made it through alone
With a head full of facts and a heart like a stone

The exam's on the table like a door I can't find
I underline a sentence that I left behind
A train somewhere distant, a dog and its chain
And the small certain comfort that I'm trying again

Nobody will know how I sat in this chair
How I fought off the sleep and the soft easy scare
But I'll know, I'll carry it, this quiet little war
The two-o'clock courage that I didn't have before

Two in the morning and the whole world's asleep
Just me and my candle and the promises I keep
If I make it to the dawn I'll have made it through alone
With a head full of facts and a heart like a stone

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
The light in 2B is somebody else's now
A stranger's curtains hanging where we used to fight and bow
I didn't mean to take this road, it took me on its own
Past the parking space, the dumpster, and the years that I have grown

Drive on, drive on, don't you tap the brake
There's a ghost in that window and she's still awake
The engine knows the way that the heart should not go
Past the place that I buried a long time ago

The oak tree's gotten taller, the railing's painted gray
I can almost see us smoking on the steps at break of day
The dead don't haunt the houses, they haunt the men who leave
And I left a little something there I never could retrieve

Lord, it's just a building, it's just brick and rusted screen
But it holds a younger version of the worst that I have been
So I roll the window up and I let the dark roll in
And I drive until the memory is a coal beneath the skin

Drive on, drive on, don't you tap the brake
There's a ghost in that window and she's still awake
The engine knows the way that the heart should not go
Past the place that I buried a long time ago

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning, a phone booth of light
The diesel still ticking in the cold Kansas night
I dial the old number, I count every ring
A thousand miles of asphalt in the silence between

Answer me, answer me, love, from your bed
I'm a voice on a wire and a road in my head
The dashboard's my candle, the white line's my floor
Dance with me, querida, through the static once more

You whisper you're tired, that the baby's asleep
I tell you I'm two days out, it's a promise I'll keep
The rain on the windshield keeps time with my chest
A tango for the lonely and the eastbound and the pressed

We sway through the receiver, two shadows apart
Your breath and my engine, one rhythm, one heart
Then the quarters run empty and the dial tone hums
And I'm back to the highway and the place that I come from

Answer me, answer me, love, from your bed
I'm a voice on a wire and a road in my head
The dashboard's my candle, the white line's my floor
Dance with me, querida, through the static once more

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Empty Sunday parking lot, the church bells long gone still
She's got both hands on the wheel and a white-knuckle will
I say ease off the clutch now, give it gas real slow
And we lurch like a rabbit 'cross the lines of the snow

Go on, baby, go on, keep it between the lines
The whole road's in front of you and most of it is fine
I'll ride here beside you long as you'll let me stay
Then one day you'll be backing down this very driveway

She killed it twice at the stop sign, cussed just like her mom
Then she found it, found the rhythm, and her shoulders went calm
I saw her at sixteen and I saw her at five
And somewhere in that mirror I saw the day she'd drive

There's a lump in my throat that I won't let her see
'Cause every mile she's learning is a mile away from me
But that's the whole job, ain't it, you teach 'em how to leave
So I check her blind spot one more time and I learn how to grieve

Go on, baby, go on, keep it between the lines
The whole road's in front of you and most of it is fine
I'll ride here beside you long as you'll let me stay
Then one day you'll be backing down this very driveway

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
She keeps a bag packed by the door in spring
A radio crackling with the news the clouds will bring
The sky turns green over Oklahoma wheat
And her heart starts running on its own two feet

She can't stay, she can't stay
The wind keeps calling her away
She loves a man who wants a home
But she was built to chase the storm alone

Hotel curtains, a dawn the color of a bruise
She drives toward the danger she can never lose
A wall cloud spinning like a slow gray prayer
And she's never more at peace than when she's there

He asked her once to stay until the leaves came down
She kissed him in the doorway and she drove out of town
The quiet rooms, the soft and folded life
Feel more like a tempest than the eye of any strife

She can't stay, she can't stay
The wind keeps calling her away
She loves a man who wants a home
But she was built to chase the storm alone

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Suitcase in the hallway, sun going down
We've got eight more hours till they scatter us around
Text the group chat, meet me at the lot
Let's burn this final evening for the good times that we got

Hands up if you don't wanna go
Hands up, let the bassline grow
Last day of summer, hold it tight
We're forever in the strobe of this one perfect night

Kick drum heartbeat under purple sky
Nobody's saying goodbye 'cause nobody knows how to try
Deja's flying Sunday, Theo's gone by noon
So we throw our arms around the speakers and the moon

Hands up if you don't wanna go
Hands up, let the bassline grow
Last day of summer, hold it tight
We're forever in the strobe of this one perfect night

When the sun comes up we'll all be somewhere new
Different cities, different rooms, a different point of view
But the loop keeps spinning and the night holds on
Let it play, let it play, till the summer's gone

Hands up if you don't wanna go
Hands up, let the bassline grow
Last day of summer, hold it tight
We're forever in the strobe of this one perfect night

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine idling cold in the alley's gray throat
Gloves on the wheel and the radio low
They said one more clean run and I'm done for good
A cabin by the lake like I always said I would

Wait for the signal, wait for the door
This is the last time, never anymore
Foot on the pedal, heart on the floor
Drive toward the morning I've been waiting for

Four minutes turns to six and the alarm starts to scream
Two men running wrong, this ain't the way it should've been
I count down the seconds like a man counts his sins
The sirens find the silence where the last job begins

The city pulls past in a smear of wet light
And somewhere a cabin sits empty tonight
I was almost there, I was almost free
Just the road and the rearview and whatever's left of me

Wait for the signal, wait for the door
This is the last time, never anymore
Foot on the pedal, heart on the floor
Drive toward the morning I've been waiting for

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
His hands that once could fix the world now tremble with the cup
The man who taught me how to stand now needs me to help him up
We dance a slower measure than the one he danced before
A half-step in the kitchen toward the light beneath the door

Lean on me, Papá, like I leaned on you
We'll move through this evening the way the old ones do
Your eyes still hold the music though your feet have lost the floor
I'll carry the rhythm now, I'll carry it for sure

He tells the same three stories and I let them all run on
Each time a little softer, like a record nearly gone
The strong arm of my childhood is a thin and folded thing
And I hold it like a treasure, like the gold of a dead king

There's a tango in the photographs, his hair as black as ink
A young man at the dance hall on the dangerous bright brink
And I see him there still spinning, though he's fading from the room
My father, oh my father, in the slow and certain gloom

Lean on me, Papá, like I leaned on you
We'll move through this evening the way the old ones do
Your eyes still hold the music though your feet have lost the floor
I'll carry the rhythm now, I'll carry it for sure

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Open up the screen door, hear it slap behind
Sofrito on the stove top knock you back in time
Garlic and the sweet rice and the coffee on to brew
Grandma in her apron got a plate for me and you

That smell, that smell, takes me right back home
To the little yellow kitchen where I never felt alone
Up on the offbeat, dancing by the sink
Grandma's pots are playing faster than a heart can think

Cinnamon and onion, oregano and lime
She never used a recipe, she cooked it all by time
A pinch of this, a story, a spoon held to my lips
The whole of my childhood in those fingertips

She's gone now but I swear when I crack the garlic clean
The kitchen fills with sunlight and the years between
Go skanking down the hallway, pick me off the floor
And Grandma's at the stovetop, humming, one time more

That smell, that smell, takes me right back home
To the little yellow kitchen where I never felt alone
Up on the offbeat, dancing by the sink
Grandma's pots are playing faster than a heart can think

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
They loaded it at midnight and they wouldn't say the name
A crate that sweats in winter and a hold that smells like flame
The captain stopped his praying on the second blackened day
And the compass spins in circles pointing every wrong way

WE SHOULD NEVER HAVE SAILED
WITH THE THING IN THE HOLD
EVERY MAN ON THIS DECK
WILL BE BURIED IN THE COLD
DROP IT IN THE OCEAN
LET IT SINK, LET IT DROWN
BUT THE CHAINS WON'T BREAK
AND THE SHIP WON'T TURN AROUND

First the cook went silent, then the crew went thin
Something scratching at the bulkhead from the dark within
We threw it to the breakers but it crawled back up the side
There's no port that will take us and no grave wide

Forty souls and counting, dragged below the line
The sea remembers all of us, it keeps a careful sign
If you ever find this logbook in a bottle on the shore
BURN THE CARGO, BURN THE SHIP, AND SAIL OUT NO MORE

WE SHOULD NEVER HAVE SAILED
WITH THE THING IN THE HOLD
EVERY MAN ON THIS DECK
WILL BE BURIED IN THE COLD
DROP IT IN THE OCEAN
LET IT SINK, LET IT DROWN
BUT THE CHAINS WON'T BREAK
AND THE SHIP WON'T TURN AROUND

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Black dress hanging on the door
Sun came up like nothing's wrong
Coffee going cold again
The radio still playing your song
They all went home around midnight
Left the casseroles and the wine
Now it's just the kitchen light
And a Tuesday I have to define

So I dance, I dance in the empty room
Gonna shake this off or I'll come undone
You would hate to see me cry too soon
So I move, I move till the grief is gone
Keep the rhythm, keep the light
Dance me through this lonely morning bright

Found your jacket in the hall
Still smells like the man you were
I pressed it to my face and laughed
At how the whole damn world's a blur
Gonna play the records loud
Like you taught me when I was young
Grief can wear a sequined gown
And still know how to come undone

So I dance, I dance in the empty room
Gonna shake this off or I'll come undone
You would hate to see me cry too soon
So I move, I move till the grief is gone
Keep the rhythm, keep the light
Dance me through this lonely morning bright

Mirror ball of an ordinary sky
Throwing little pieces of you on the wall
I'm not okay but I'm gonna try
To love the living and to honor it all

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man stood where the gray rocks meet the foam
Called me down before the gulls awoke
Said the sea has been here longer than our names
And it keeps no record of the words we spoke
He pointed to the line upon the sand
Where the water draws and lets the morning go
Said you cannot hold the ocean in your hand
But you can learn the rhythm if you're slow

Learn the tide, learn the tide
