She will leave and she'll return
Don't go chasing what recedes
There's a patience you must learn
In and out, in and out
Like the breathing of the world
Learn the tide, my child
And you'll never be unmoored

He was eighty if he was a single year
Hands like driftwood, eyes the color of rain
Said he'd buried friends beneath this very pier
And the water carried off the worst of pain
Now I stand here older than he was
Telling children what the old man told to me
That the tide will take you somewhere if you trust
That the leaving is a part of being free

Learn the tide, learn the tide
She will leave and she'll return
Don't go chasing what recedes
There's a patience you must learn
In and out, in and out
Like the breathing of the world
Learn the tide, my child
And you'll never be unmoored

And the moon pulls on the water
Like a memory pulls on me
Every father, every daughter
Learning how to let things be

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Raised my hand at the long oak table
Had the answer on the tip of my tongue
But a louder voice came rolling over
And my sentence died before it begun
Wrote my notes in the quiet margins
Swallowed everything I meant to say
And they thanked the man who said it after
Took my thunder and they walked away

But I got a fire, I got a fire down in my chest
And one day I'm gonna let it burn
You can talk over me, talk over me
But you're gonna have to wait your turn
I got something, I got something worth the silence
And I'm tired of being unheard
So listen now, listen now
I'm gonna have the final word

Counted all the times they interrupted
Like a clock that only ran one way
Smiled so polite while I corrupted
Everything I wanted them to say
But a quiet woman's been collecting
Every slight into a steady flame
And the day is coming for correcting
When they finally have to learn my name

But I got a fire, I got a fire down in my chest
And one day I'm gonna let it burn
You can talk over me, talk over me
But you're gonna have to wait your turn
I got something, I got something worth the silence
And I'm tired of being unheard
So listen now, listen now
I'm gonna have the final word

No more, no more shrinking small
No more letting good ideas fall
I was here, I had it all along
Now watch me, watch me prove you wrong

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Neon on the boardwalk, salt up in the air
Met you at the bonfire with the sand in your hair
Three weeks and a heartbeat, the city far behind
We were running on the moonlight and a borrowed kind of time
Every night a fever, every wave a yes
Never asked for forever, didn't want it any less

Go, go, go, don't let the summer end
Feel it in the bassline, feel it in your chest
Go, go, go, around the bend again
Just you and me and the ocean and the rest
Faster now, faster now, the night is ours to spend
Faster now, faster now, before it has to end

Tan lines and the engine of a stolen afternoon
Kissed you in the shallows underneath a bleeding moon
We knew it had an ending like the tide knows the shore
But we burned it all the brighter cause we couldn't have it more
Numbers that we never saved, names we never wrote
Just the rhythm and the water and a heart inside my throat

Go, go, go, don't let the summer end
Feel it in the bassline, feel it in your chest
Go, go, go, around the bend again
Just you and me and the ocean and the rest
Faster now, faster now, the night is ours to spend
Faster now, faster now, before it has to end

September's gonna take you, the city's gonna call
But tonight we're still electric, we don't fade, we don't fall
One more drop, one more rush, one more run along the sand
Hold the summer in the dark and never let it leave your hand

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Gravel road behind the water tower
That's where my old man taught me too
You got the seat too close, you're gripping the wheel
Like it's gonna get away from you
Ease off the brake now, let her roll
No, you don't gotta steer so hard
We got all afternoon, sweetheart
Just the dust and the dying yard

Go on and drive, drive, drive
Keep your eyes down the road ahead
I'll be right here by your side
Till there's nothing left to dread
You were just a girl in a booster seat
Now your hands are on the wheel
Go on and drive, my darling
This is how the letting go feels

You stalled it twice at the county line
And you cussed like your mama would
Laughed so hard I forgot for a second
How fast you grew up for good
Soon enough you'll take this same old highway
Somewhere I can't follow you
So I'm teaching you to leave me, baby
It's the bravest thing a father does

Go on and drive, drive, drive
Keep your eyes down the road ahead
I'll be right here by your side
Till there's nothing left to dread
You were just a girl in a booster seat
Now your hands are on the wheel
Go on and drive, my darling
This is how the letting go feels

One day the porch light's gonna catch you
Pulling out and pulling free
And I'll stand there in the doorway
Proud as any man could be

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
He used to lift the whole sky on his back
Now his hands are shaking pouring tea
The man who taught me how to stand my ground
Forgets the road that brings him home to me
Time is a machine that never slows
Dragging us all faster than we know

Slow down, slow down, but the years won't wait
The bassline keeps on running like a heart that's running late
Slow down, slow down, I'm holding on to you
But the rhythm of the leaving is the only thing that's true
Faster now, faster now, I can't make it stay
Faster now, faster now, watching him fade away

Found the photographs of who he was
Tall and laughing, young before the gray
Now I cut his food and tie his shoes
And I swallow all the things I cannot say
The son becomes the father in the end
And the strongest man I knew becomes a friend

Slow down, slow down, but the years won't wait
The bassline keeps on running like a heart that's running late
Slow down, slow down, I'm holding on to you
But the rhythm of the leaving is the only thing that's true
Faster now, faster now, I can't make it stay
Faster now, faster now, watching him fade away

Dad, I see you in the mirror now
Every line you wore I'm wearing too
And I'll carry every lesson loud
Long after the drums are through

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. and the neon's humming low
Vinyl booth and a cup that won't run dry
Deloris knows my order, knows my name
Knows the reason I don't wanna say goodbye
The city's sleeping but the griddle's warm
And the jukebox plays a slow and lonesome tune
This is church for the ones who can't go home
Underneath a buzzing fluorescent moon

Keep it open, keep it open all night
Where the broken come to sit a while
Under that flickering light
Keep it open, keep it open all night
Coffee black and a quiet kind of grace
Making everybody's wrongs feel right

There's a cabbie and a girl who's been out crying
There's a poet writing words he'll never send
We don't ask each other why we're here
We just nod across the counter like a friend
Something holy in the smell of bacon grease
In the way the morning waits outside the glass
For a few more hours of borrowed peace
Before we have to face whatever comes to pass

Keep it open, keep it open all night
Where the broken come to sit a while
Under that flickering light
Keep it open, keep it open all night
Coffee black and a quiet kind of grace
Making everybody's wrongs feel right

When the world don't want you, baby
When the door's been closed on you
There's a little room of strangers
And a light that's shining through

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the stones on the giant's chest
Built the towers where he lays his head
Every morning when the people rise
They forget there's a god beneath the bed
Market trading on the ribs of him
Children playing on his folded hand
And the elders say one day he'll wake
And remember who first held the land

Sleeping giant, sleeping giant
Don't you stir beneath the town
We are dancing on your shoulders
Lord, don't lay the city down
Sleeping giant, sleeping giant
Dream us gentle, dream us slow
We are only borrowing the ground
We forget but you still know

The rich man builds his palace tall
Never asking what he's standing on
But the poor man feels the giant breathe
In the tremble of the early dawn
There's a warning in the steady hum
In the way the valley starts to shake
All the gold you stack and all you take
Means nothing when the giant wakes

Sleeping giant, sleeping giant
Don't you stir beneath the town
We are dancing on your shoulders
Lord, don't lay the city down
Sleeping giant, sleeping giant
Dream us gentle, dream us slow
We are only borrowing the ground
We forget but you still know

So live easy on the mountain
Walk humble on the stone
For the ground you call your kingdom
Was never yours to own

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Last tank of gas and a glovebox full of nothing
Parking lot empty where we used to waste the night
Tomorrow it's boxes and a highway pulling everyone
In a thousand directions out of sight
This was our kingdom, these cracked and burning streets
Now the clock's run out and I can feel it in my teeth

Scream it out, this is the last one
Burn the whole day to the ground
We were everything and nothing
Now there's no one left around
Don't you tell me that it's over
Don't you tell me to grow up
This is the last day of forever
And I won't, I won't give it up

We made promises we already know we'll break
Swore we'd call, swore we'd stay the way we are
But I see it in your eyes, there's a distance gonna take
Everything we built and scatter it too far
So let's tear it open, let's go out in flame
Cause after tonight nothing's ever gonna be the same

Scream it out, this is the last one
Burn the whole day to the ground
We were everything and nothing
Now there's no one left around
Don't you tell me that it's over
Don't you tell me to grow up
This is the last day of forever
And I won't, I won't give it up

So here's to the ones who I'm leaving
Here's to the kids that we were
We'll be strangers by the springtime
But tonight we still feel sure

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night the same, the floor lets go of me
Roofs become the river and I'm rising through the dark
No wings and no engine, just a forgetting of the weight
And the city lights are scattered like a spark
I know this feeling, I have been here in my sleep
Something in me knows the sky and knows it deep

Up, up, up, I'm leaving the ground
The bassline lifts me, there's no falling down
Up, up, up, the only place I'm free
In the dream of the flying that keeps finding me
Higher now, higher now, past the breaking dawn
Higher now, higher now, before the dream is gone

Then I always wake up tangled in the white
With the ache of something massive I can't keep
The ceiling and the gravity and morning at the door
And a body that remembers it could leap
What is it in the sleeping that the waking cannot hold
A bird I used to be before the world got cold

Up, up, up, I'm leaving the ground
The bassline lifts me, there's no falling down
Up, up, up, the only place I'm free
In the dream of the flying that keeps finding me
Higher now, higher now, past the breaking dawn
Higher now, higher now, before the dream is gone

Maybe one night I won't come back down
Maybe the dream is the realest thing I've found
Close my eyes and the horizon calls my name
And I rise, and I rise, and I'm never quite the same

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Cinnamon drifting through the screen door
Flour on her apron, sun across the floor
A pot that's been simmering since I was three years old
And a story in the steam that's never told
Her hands knew a music that the recipes forgot
A little pinch of something that you couldn't measure out

Oh, that kitchen, oh, that smell
Takes me back to where I'm whole
Brown sugar and a hymn she'd hum
Warming up my weary soul
Grandma's kitchen, golden light
Everything was gonna be alright
In the cinnamon and the candlelight
Oh, that kitchen, oh, that smell

She's been gone now nearly twenty winters
But I caught it in a bakery downtown
And I had to stop and lean against the window
While the whole of my childhood came around
Funny how a scent can be a doorway in the air
And I'm small again and safe again and she is standing there

Oh, that kitchen, oh, that smell
Takes me back to where I'm whole
Brown sugar and a hymn she'd hum
Warming up my weary soul
Grandma's kitchen, golden light
Everything was gonna be alright
In the cinnamon and the candlelight
Oh, that kitchen, oh, that smell

Now I'm stirring up her pot
Though I never wrote it down
And my own grandchildren gather
In the only kind of heaven I have found

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I slipped out the back where the candles still were burning
Left my name in the register, the last time I would write
The organ was a heartbeat I had loved since I was learning
But something in me couldn't kneel another night
The stained glass kept its colors and the saints kept their eyes
And I walked into the cold beneath a wide and honest sky

So play, play the leaving song
A little sad and a little free
The place I belonged for so long
Is a door that's closing soft behind me
Dance me out, dance me slow
Through the grief and through the doubt
This is how the faithful go
When the faith has finally run out

I don't curse it, I don't hate it, it held me when I fell
My mother lit her hopes there, my father said his prayers
But a man must walk his own road even out of heaven's gate
And carry all the love of it but leave behind the chains
The fiddle plays a question that the answer cannot keep
And I'm laughing and I'm crying as I wander down the street

So play, play the leaving song
A little sad and a little free
The place I belonged for so long
Is a door that's closing soft behind me
Dance me out, dance me slow
Through the grief and through the doubt
This is how the faithful go
When the faith has finally run out

I'll keep the songs, I'll keep the kindness
I'll keep the way they taught me grace
But I'm trading all their certainty
For the whole and open space

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Tether snapped in the quiet of the black
I watched your visor turning into gold
No gravity to pull you ever back
Just the slow and silent letting go
We trained together, breathed the same recycled air
Now there's a widening of nothing in between
I'm reaching out my hand but you're not there
Just a shrinking little light against the scene

Drifting, drifting, further every beat
No sound out here to carry what I feel
Drifting, drifting, the pulse beneath my feet
Is the only thing that's left of something real
Reach for me, reach for me, across the dark
Reach for me, reach for me, but we're falling apart

The mission said that one of us would stay
The other one would tumble toward the stars
And I keep the channel open anyway
Though I know your voice can't cross this far
The Earth is just a marble far below
And I'm spinning with a memory of your face
Funny how the closest people go
The widest distances in empty space

Drifting, drifting, further every beat
No sound out here to carry what I feel
Drifting, drifting, the pulse beneath my feet
Is the only thing that's left of something real
Reach for me, reach for me, across the dark
Reach for me, reach for me, but we're falling apart

Let the rhythm be my orbit
Let the bass become my breath
I'll keep dancing in the silence
Till there's nothing of you left

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We floated once like petals in a jar
Your hand in mine and Earth a sleeping eye
Now you're a soft and disappearing star
A slow goodbye written across the sky
The radio is static, sweet and low
And everything is moving like a dream
I watch the silver tether let you go
And nothing here is ever what it seems

Fade, fade, into the blue
I'm losing all the colors that were you
Fade, fade, the gentle dark
Is carrying you somewhere I can't park
Don't go, don't go, but the cosmos pulls you wide
Don't go, don't go, into the rising tide

The cold is soft, the silence is a song
And you are just a shimmer now, a haze
I can't recall how long we've been this long
Time has come unbuttoned in the haze
I hum the tune we wrote when we were near
And let it dissolve into the velvet space
Love is just an echo I can hear
Long after I have lost you without trace

Fade, fade, into the blue
I'm losing all the colors that were you
Fade, fade, the gentle dark
Is carrying you somewhere I can't park
Don't go, don't go, but the cosmos pulls you wide
Don't go, don't go, into the rising tide

If the universe is endless
Then you're never really gone
Just a wavelength, just a whisper
That I'll drift toward all along

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Spotlight hot and the crowd was almost mine
One more song and the whole world would have known
I could taste it like a sugar on my tongue
The closest I had ever come to home
They were chanting, they were swaying in the dark
And I swore that this would be the turning night
Everything I bled for in a single spark
Almost, almost, almost in the light

So close, so close, I could feel it in my hands
The dream that I had carried since a kid
So close, so close, the almost-promised land
And I'd give it all to live the night I did
Even if I didn't, even if I fell
It was beautiful, it was beautiful as hell
The night I almost made it, I can tell

Then the lights came up and the moment slipped away
The call that never came, the door that closed
But I still remember how it felt to nearly fly
With the whole horizon glowing where it goes
Maybe almost is a kind of holy ground
Maybe getting close is half of being brave
I was never bigger than that almost sound
And it's a memory that no one's gonna take

So close, so close, I could feel it in my hands
The dream that I had carried since a kid
So close, so close, the almost-promised land
And I'd give it all to live the night I did
Even if I didn't, even if I fell
It was beautiful, it was beautiful as hell
The night I almost made it, I can tell

And if it never comes again
I'll keep that crowd inside my chest
The night I almost made it
Was as close to gold as best

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Suitcase tied with my grandmother's twine
Dock lights bleeding gold across the brine
I counted every coin against the glass
Kissed the harbor, watched the old town pass

Dance me out past the breakwater wall
Salt on my collar, I'm answering the call
Mirror ball moon spinning over the sea
New shore waiting, make a place for me

Mother's letter folded in my shoe
Engine humming a hymn I never knew
Strangers swaying in the steerage light
We traded countries for a single night

Dance me out past the breakwater wall
Salt on my collar, I'm answering the call
Mirror ball moon spinning over the sea
New shore waiting, make a place for me

No turning back to the gate I closed
Just the rhythm and the road that the water chose
Every wave a drumbeat, every star a name
I'll arrive a stranger, leave the old in flame

Dance me out past the breakwater wall
Salt on my collar, I'm answering the call
Mirror ball moon spinning over the sea
New shore waiting, make a place for me

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The last shift drags its boots across the floor
Dust hangs gold where the welders used to roar
A clock still ticking on a wall of rust
We gave it forty years, it gave us dust

Fade the lights, let the turbines drown
We're the echo of a factory town
Guitars like sirens going under slow
Nobody left to tell the gates to close

My father's locker still has his name in chalk
The parking lot's a sea of empty talk
The river by the dock forgets our shoes
The whistle blows a note it cannot lose

Fade the lights, let the turbines drown
We're the echo of a factory town
Guitars like sirens going under slow
Nobody left to tell the gates to close

Washed in feedback, swallowed in the haze
A thousand lunches, ghosts of all those days
The ceiling rains a soft fluorescent snow
We stay until the very last light goes

Fade the lights, let the turbines drown
We're the echo of a factory town
Guitars like sirens going under slow
Nobody left to tell the gates to close

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Water tower spelled a name I outgrew
Gravel roads still humming what they knew
I packed my mama's records and my pride
Left the porch light burning on the other side

I'm gone, baby, gone like the morning train
Left my footprints sleeping in the lane
Love this town too much to let it hold
My whole life folded in a dream of gold

The diner clock still froze at half past nine
Every face a chapter, every street a line
I kissed the river, told it I was free
The town I love was never meant for me

I'm gone, baby, gone like the morning train
Left my footprints sleeping in the lane
Love this town too much to let it hold
My whole life folded in a dream of gold

Don't cry for me at the welcome sign
I carry every Sunday in this spine
What I leave behind still leaves with me
A small town heartbeat in a bigger key

I'm gone, baby, gone like the morning train
Left my footprints sleeping in the lane
Love this town too much to let it hold
My whole life folded in a dream of gold

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
His hands that built the barn now shake the cup
The truck he loved sits rusting, won't crank up
He tells the same three stories, I let them ride
The man who taught me fences can't decide

Oh the years, they wear a man down slow
Like a river takes the stone it learns to know
I'd give my steady hands to give him back his own
Watching my father walk the road toward home

He used to lift the world up off the ground
Now I hold the door and slow my stride down
His eyes still hold the prairie and the rain
The strongest thing I knew now learning pain

Oh the years, they wear a man down slow
Like a river takes the stone it learns to know
I'd give my steady hands to give him back his own
Watching my father walk the road toward home

We sat out on the tailgate, watched the wheat
He called me by my brother's name, I let it keep
There's nothing in the toolbox fixes time
Just me and him and one last hill to climb

Oh the years, they wear a man down slow
Like a river takes the stone it learns to know
I'd give my steady hands to give him back his own
Watching my father walk the road toward home

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Found an old photograph, you're holding me high
Summer in your shoulders, summer in your eyes
Now I match your footsteps, slow them down to wait
Funny how the strongest hands just learn to hesitate

Wasn't ready for the day you'd need me too
All the years you carried, now I carry you
Don't you go, don't you go too soon
Dance with me one more time around the room

You still hum the radio you sang to in the car
Forget my name some mornings but you know just who we are
I'm learning how to be the calm you used to be
The river's getting wider, you taught me how to see

Wasn't ready for the day you'd need me too
All the years you carried, now I carry you
Don't you go, don't you go too soon
Dance with me one more time around the room

Every wrinkle is a map of where we've been
Every silence says it louder than it's ever been
So I'll hold the door, I'll hold the line
I'll hold you, Dad, for a little more time

Wasn't ready for the day you'd need me too
All the years you carried, now I carry you
Don't you go, don't you go too soon
Dance with me one more time around the room

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Concrete cradle where the dreams run dry
Streetlight buzzing like it's asking why
Mama said the world is wider than this gate
I tied my hunger to a plate and a date

From the block to the light, I'm moving now
No looking back, gon' show 'em how
Dance for the boy that the corner forgot
We made it out, we made it out the lot

Every no was building muscle in my chest
Kept my sneakers clean while the city was a test
Now the bassline carry every name I knew
I bring the whole block when I'm breaking through

From the block to the light, I'm moving now
No looking back, gon' show 'em how
Dance for the boy that the corner forgot
We made it out, we made it out the lot

Pour a little for the ones who couldn't leave
I carry your names sewn into my sleeve
The drum say go, the drum say rise
Watch a dead-end open into open skies

From the block to the light, I'm moving now
No looking back, gon' show 'em how
Dance for the boy that the corner forgot
We made it out, we made it out the lot

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete swallowed every field we knew
One oak standing where the orchard grew
Roots cracking sidewalks like it's fighting back
Last green soldier on a world gone black

Last tree standing, hold the line
They poured the city but they can't pour mine
Branches in the smog still reaching for the sun
When they cut you down, where do the birds run

Developers came with a yellow machine
Tied a ribbon round the only thing green
Kids carved their names where the bark grew slow
Now there's a parking lot eating the row

Last tree standing, hold the line
They poured the city but they can't pour mine
Branches in the smog still reaching for the sun
When they cut you down, where do the birds run

I put my hand on the trunk, felt it hum
A hundred summers in a town gone numb
They measure the lot, they don't measure the loss
A crown of leaves on a concrete cross

Last tree standing, hold the line
They poured the city but they can't pour mine
Branches in the smog still reaching for the sun
When they cut you down, where do the birds run

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Mile marker ghosts in the diesel rain
Three a.m. static on the interstate lane
Dial the number burned into my hand
While the white lines stutter like a drumbeat band

Pick up, pick up, I'm a voice in the dark
Eighteen wheels and a beating heart
Tell the kids I'll be home by the weekend light
Keep the porch lamp running through the night

Coffee gone cold in the cupholder gloom
Your sleepy hello is my favorite tune
Radio fading out the towns I crossed
I count the exits like the days I lost

Pick up, pick up, I'm a voice in the dark
Eighteen wheels and a beating heart
Tell the kids I'll be home by the weekend light
Keep the porch lamp running through the night

Bassline rolling like the tires on tar
Every breath of yours is my north star
Don't hang up yet, let the silence stay
I'll drive your whisper all the way to day

Pick up, pick up, I'm a voice in the dark
Eighteen wheels and a beating heart
Tell the kids I'll be home by the weekend light
Keep the porch lamp running through the night

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock out at the diner, clock in at the store
My feet know the bus stop better than my door
Counting tips in the dark like they're falling stars
Still the numbers come up shorter than they are

I'm running, I'm running, but the ground keeps moving
Two paychecks and I'm still proving
That love and rent don't speak the same tongue
Tired down to the marrow, but I'm not done

Baby's drawing pictures of a house with a yard
I tape them to the fridge, say we're not far
My hands smell like coffee and the warehouse cold
I'm twenty-nine going on a hundred years old

I'm running, I'm running, but the ground keeps moving
Two paychecks and I'm still proving
That love and rent don't speak the same tongue
Tired down to the marrow, but I'm not done

Don't tell me to dream when I can't afford the sleep
The promises they sold me were too cheap to keep
But I'll set my alarm and I'll rise again
Someday the math is gonna let me in

I'm running, I'm running, but the ground keeps moving
Two paychecks and I'm still proving
That love and rent don't speak the same tongue
Tired down to the marrow, but I'm not done

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Old Sal's been cutting since nineteen-sixty-two
Knows your daddy's secrets and your grandma's too
Scissors like a heartbeat, chair that never lies
A confessional with a buzz and bloodshot eyes

Sal knows everything, Sal knows your name
Sal knows who started the courthouse flame
Four walls, one mirror, and a barber pole
He's the only therapist this town can hold

Mayor gets a trim and he spills the deal
The priest comes Friday, says he's keeping it real
Sal just nods and sweeps the floor of hair
Holding half the town like nobody's there

Sal knows everything, Sal knows your name
Sal knows who started the courthouse flame
Four walls, one mirror, and a barber pole
He's the only therapist this town can hold

When Sal retires gonna be a riot in the street
No one left to tell us who to trust or beat
So cut it short, old man, and tell it true
The whole town's heartbeat's running through you

Sal knows everything, Sal knows your name
Sal knows who started the courthouse flame
Four walls, one mirror, and a barber pole
He's the only therapist this town can hold

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Slow march down on Rampart Street
Trombone weeping in the heavy heat
White handkerchiefs and a tambourine
We lay him down where the saints have been

Oh when the band turns the corner round
Grief gonna lift right off the ground
Dance him home, let the trumpets cry
We bury the body but the soul gon' fly

Sister in the second line, raise your knee
Umbrella spinning like a soul set free
The preacher say weep but the brass say rise
Heaven's gonna meet us in the cymbal skies

Oh when the band turns the corner round
Grief gonna lift right off the ground
Dance him home, let the trumpets cry
We bury the body but the soul gon' fly

From a dirge to a strut in a single breath
This is how the river laughs at death
Clap your hands, let the sorrow break
There's joy in the morning for the mourner's sake

Oh when the band turns the corner round
Grief gonna lift right off the ground
Dance him home, let the trumpets cry
We bury the body but the soul gon' fly

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Tether snapped above the silver blue
I watched the station shrinking, so did you
Radio crackle saying don't let go
But the dark between us only seems to grow

We're spinning, we're spinning, two satellites of love
Losing gravity in the black above
Reach for my glove one final time
Before the silence claims what once was mine

Your visor caught the sun and turned to gold
A waltz in zero with no hand to hold
I count your breathing fading on the line
Two dancers drifting on a thread so fine

We're spinning, we're spinning, two satellites of love
Losing gravity in the black above
Reach for my glove one final time
Before the silence claims what once was mine

No down, no up, just the endless glide
The stars keep dancing while we lose the tide
I'll send my heartbeat on a beam of light
Maybe it finds you in the longest night

We're spinning, we're spinning, two satellites of love
Losing gravity in the black above
Reach for my glove one final time
Before the silence claims what once was mine

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Seven years of dial tone in my chest
Pride was a stone I couldn't lay to rest
Tonight I press the numbers, hands all damp
Under the kitchen glow of the old lamp

Mama, it's me, can you hear me now
I been a stranger but I'm coming home somehow
The years were heavy but the love stayed warm
Mama, forgive me, weather any storm

She answers soft like the morning rain
No word of anger for the years of pain
Just hum a tune that she sang to me
When I was small as a mango seed

Mama, it's me, can you hear me now
I been a stranger but I'm coming home somehow
The years were heavy but the love stayed warm
Mama, forgive me, weather any storm

Generations in a single line
Roots run deeper than a fool like mine
Gonna catch the next bus down the coast
Hold the woman that I missed the most

Mama, it's me, can you hear me now
I been a stranger but I'm coming home somehow
The years were heavy but the love stayed warm
Mama, forgive me, weather any storm

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old Reb Yankel on the cold gray shore
Teaching the ocean what it knew before
Clarinet crying to the rolling foam
Every wave a wanderer he's calling home

Teach the tide, old man, teach the tide
Tell it where the lost ones hide
Up and down like a freylekhs played
The sea remembers every prayer we made

He counts the breakers like a string of beads
Names every fish in the language of his deeds
The water leans in like a stubborn child
Learning patience from a man gone wild

Teach the tide, old man, teach the tide
Tell it where the lost ones hide
Up and down like a freylekhs played
The sea remembers every prayer we made

When he's gone the gulls will carry on the song
The waves will keep the rhythm all night long
A fiddle in the spray, a doina in the surf
The ocean was his student and the shore his church

Teach the tide, old man, teach the tide
Tell it where the lost ones hide
Up and down like a freylekhs played
The sea remembers every prayer we made

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the wagon when the moon was thin
Jars of lightning sloshing in the tin
Fiddler riding shotgun with a nervous grin
The constable was waiting where the woods begin

Oy, the run went wrong, run went wrong
Clarinet wailing out a getaway song
Spilled the silver liquor on the frozen ground
Dance now, brother, 'fore they turn around

Horse bolted left when the lantern flared
Mottel lost his hat but the bottles spared
We ditched the whole lot in the frozen creek
Laughing like fools though our knees were weak

Oy, the run went wrong, run went wrong
Clarinet wailing out a getaway song
Spilled the silver liquor on the frozen ground
Dance now, brother, 'fore they turn around

We'll toast with water and a borrowed tune
Lost a season's wages to a stubborn moon
But the band's still standing and the night is young
There's a wedding in the village to be sung

Oy, the run went wrong, run went wrong
Clarinet wailing out a getaway song
Spilled the silver liquor on the frozen ground
Dance now, brother, 'fore they turn around

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Morning light through the window pane
Cinnamon dancing in the air again
Flour on her apron, hands so worn
This is where my soul was born
Butter melting on the old cast iron
Memories rising higher and higher

Oh, the smell of her kitchen
Takes me back, takes me home
Every spice tells a story
In this house I'm never alone
Grandma's love in every measure
A recipe I'll always treasure

Sunday gravy on the stove all day
Humming gospel in her gentle way
Nothing fancy, just the simple things
But it's the kind of love that healing brings

Now she's gone but the scent remains
In my heart through the joy and pain
Close my eyes and I'm right there
Wrapped up in her loving care

Oh, the smell of her kitchen
Takes me back, takes me home
Every spice tells a story
In this house I'm never alone

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The road stretches out before me now
Empty highway, distant glow
Everything I knew is fading slow
In the rearview, letting go

Goodbye to the streets I know so well
Goodbye to the stories I won't tell
I'm drifting away, away, away
Nothing left to make me stay

Dust on the porch and an empty chair
The silence hangs heavy in the air
One last look at the morning sky
No more reasons left to cry

Floating into the unknown
Finally finding my way home
The town gets smaller, fades to gray
And I am drifting far away

Goodbye to the streets I know so well
Goodbye to the stories I won't tell
I'm drifting away, away, away
Nothing left to make me stay

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Packed my bag and I'm hittin' the door
Ain't gonna live like this no more
Same old faces, same old grind
Leavin' all this dust behind
Got my groove and I'm feelin' free
This little town can't hold me

I'm gone, gone, gettin' on down the line
Shakin' off the past, I'm feelin' so fine
Goodbye to the small town blues
Got my dancin' shoes, nothin' left to lose

Mama said I'd never make it out
But I got rhythm, I got clout
Watch me strut right down Main Street
Movin' to my own heartbeat

Whoa, can't you feel it now
Breakin' free somehow
Funkin' down the open road
Droppin' off this heavy load

I'm gone, gone, gettin' on down the line
Shakin' off the past, I'm feelin' so fine
Goodbye to the small town blues
Got my dancin' shoes, nothin' left to lose

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Fourth quarter, clock windin' down
Nobody thought we'd wear the crown
Counted us out from the very start
But we got that fire in the heart
Underdogs, yeah we came to play
Watch us take it all today

We ball, we ball, against the odds
Provin' all them doubters wrong
We ball, we ball, we never fall
This the moment, we been here all along

Sweat drippin' down, we grindin' hard
Every point, we playin' our card
The crowd goin' wild, feel the energy
This our time, this our legacy

Buzzer 'bout to sound, we hold the line
Underdog story, this our time
From the bottom now we rise
Victory right before our eyes

We ball, we ball, against the odds
Provin' all them doubters wrong
We ball, we ball, we never fall
This the moment, we been here all along

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine running in the pale moonlight (shoo-bop)
One more job and I'll be alright (shoo-bop)
Keys in my hand and my heart beating fast
This is the night, gonna make it my last

Drive, drive, drive away (drive away)
Gonna leave this life today (today)
One more turn and I'll be free
No more running, just you and me

Tires screaming down the avenue (shoo-bop)
Sirens fading, almost through (shoo-bop)
Got my baby waiting by the shore
After tonight I won't drive no more

Woah-oh, the open road
Woah-oh, drop this heavy load
One last ride into the dawn
Then the getaway is gone

Drive, drive, drive away (drive away)
Gonna leave this life today (today)
One more turn and I'll be free
No more running, just you and me

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. and my phone's lighting up
Know I should stop but I can't get enough
Your name on the screen, my heart skips a beat
This forbidden feeling tastes so sweet

I shouldn't be texting you
But I can't help the things I do
Up all night, the screen glows blue
I shouldn't, shouldn't, but I do

My fingers typing words I can't take back
Riding this feeling down a dangerous track
The rhythm of my heart against the beat
Falling deeper with each word we repeat

Delete, delete, but I press send
Know this road's gonna end
One more message in the dark
Lighting up this secret spark

I shouldn't be texting you
But I can't help the things I do
Up all night, the screen glows blue
I shouldn't, shouldn't, but I do

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Fairy lights are hanging in the trees
Your hand finds mine so easily
We don't even know each other's names
But nothing here will be the same

Dance with me under the stars
Don't care whoever you are
For tonight you're all I see
A stranger and a melody

The music swells and time slows down
Your eyes the only sound around
A borrowed moment, soft and bright
Two strangers floating through the night

Maybe we'll forget by morning light
But for now we hold on tight
Spinning slow in golden haze
Lost inside this perfect daze

Dance with me under the stars
Don't care whoever you are
For tonight you're all I see
A stranger and a melody

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The screen glows soft in the silent dark
Each quiet message leaves a mark
I know I shouldn't, but I stay
Watching the words just drift away

Don't, don't, but I can't let go
Drifting on this secret flow
Fading in and fading out
Filling up this empty doubt

No one knows, the night is still
Against my better will
The hum of silence, soft and low
A place that no one else will know

Floating through the midnight air
Knowing that I shouldn't care
But the glow keeps pulling me
Into where I shouldn't be

Don't, don't, but I can't let go
Drifting on this secret flow
Fading in and fading out
Filling up this empty doubt

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Empty halls and the lights are low
Footsteps echo, soft and slow
Every night I stand and stare
At the woman painted there

She never speaks but I understand
Frozen forever by an artist's hand
In the silence we remain
A quiet love no one explains

The city sleeps beyond the glass
While centuries around me pass
Her painted eyes still hold the light
My only company tonight

Dawn will come and the doors will open
Words between us left unspoken
But in the dark we share the room
A guard, a frame, a soft perfume

She never speaks but I understand
Frozen forever by an artist's hand
In the silence we remain
A quiet love no one explains

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Up on the rocks where the waters roll (shoo-bop)
I write my heart and I lose my soul (shoo-bop)
Every letter I send to the tide
The ocean is my only bride

Oh sea, oh sea, my love so blue (so blue)
I'm writing all my dreams to you (to you)
The waves carry my words away
I'll love you 'til my dying day

The lantern burns through the foggy night (shoo-bop)
Guiding the ships with my faithful light (shoo-bop)
But my heart belongs to the deep
It's the promise that I keep

Woah-oh, the salt and spray
Woah-oh, you wash my words away
But still I write upon the shore
Forever yours and nothing more

Oh sea, oh sea, my love so blue (so blue)
I'm writing all my dreams to you (to you)
The waves carry my words away
I'll love you 'til my dying day

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
Rainy window, headphones on
Thinking 'bout you when you're gone
You don't see me the way I see you
Stuck in this feeling, what can I do
Vinyl crackle, mellow beat
Love that I will never meet

You'll never know how I feel
This quiet love is so real
Faded photos, faded dreams
Nothing's ever what it seems

I write your name then erase it slow
These are the things you'll never know
Maybe in another life somehow
But it's just me and the silence now

Lo-fi nights and lonely days
Lost inside this gentle haze
Letting go but holding on
To a love that's never gonna dawn

You'll never know how I feel
This quiet love is so real
Faded photos, faded dreams
Nothing's ever what it seems

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Neon fading in the evening light
We move together one last night
Thirty years in a single song
This is where we both belong
The record spins, the memories glow
Now it's time to let it go

One last dance, hold me tight
Under the electric light
Everything we used to be
Fading like a memory

Your hand in mine like the old days
Moving through the purple haze
We built a life, we built a home
Now we dance here all alone

The synths are crying soft and low
For all the love we used to know
One more turn before the end
My lover and my oldest friend

One last dance, hold me tight
Under the electric light
Everything we used to be
Fading like a memory

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I lost my way on a lonesome road (oh yeah)
Carried 'round such a heavy load
The lights went out and I couldn't see
Wondered who was watching over me

But I'm swingin' back to the light
Found my faith again tonight
Clap your hands and stomp your feet
Got that rhythm, got that beat
Hallelujah, I believe
In the love I can't conceive

Down in the valley, low and blue (so blue)
Didn't know what I was gonna do
Then a little spark began to grow
And it set my weary soul aglow

Oh, the doubt it tried to win
But the light came flooding in
Now I'm dancin' through the door
Believin' like I did before

So I'm swingin' back to the light
Found my faith again tonight
Clap your hands and stomp your feet
Got that rhythm, got that beat

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Packed my suitcase, kissed the town goodbye (so long)
Got a one-way ticket and a clear blue sky
Main Street fading in the morning sun
My small town days are finally done

I'm swingin' out, I'm movin' on
By tomorrow I'll be gone
Tip my hat and catch the train
Never comin' back again

The diner, the church, and the old courthouse (mm-hmm)
Leavin' it all, gonna shake it out
Got big dreams and a dancin' heart
This is where my story starts

Whistle blowin', wheels they turn
Got so many bridges left to burn
Wavin' to the fields of gold
Leavin' this old town for good

I'm swingin' out, I'm movin' on
By tomorrow I'll be gone
Tip my hat and catch the train
Never comin' back again

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Clock on the wall says it's quarter to two
Coffee gone cold and I'm halfway through
Textbook open, my eyes are red
Should've been asleep in bed
Pick it up, pick up the pace
Gotta ace this stupid race

Up all night, gonna make it through
Studyin' hard like I always do
Skankin' to the rhythm of my racing brain
Two a.m. and I'm goin' insane

Highlighter squeaking across the page
Mind is foggy, I'm losing my rage
One more chapter, one more cup
Can't give in, gotta keep it up

Pick it up, pick it up now
Gonna pass this test somehow
Dance away the sleepy blues
Nothing left for me to lose

Up all night, gonna make it through
Studyin' hard like I always do
Skankin' to the rhythm of my racing brain
Two a.m. and I'm goin' insane

songs.subject: unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I memorized the way you say my name
like weather no one bothers to explain
You orbit rooms I learned to stand inside
close enough to drown, too far to confide

I built a quiet city out of maybes
streets I only walk when you're not there
Every green light turns to one more reason
to pretend that almost is a kind of care

And I am the harbor and you are the sail
I hold the whole ocean and still you set out
The waves keep arriving with nothing to tell
I love you in silence, I love you in doubt

The crescendo always breaks before the word
the one I rehearse and never let you hear
So I let the guitars say it instead
a flood of sound to fill the empty year

If wanting were enough to bend the light
you'd have turned around a thousand nights ago
But wanting is just weather in my chest
a storm that only I will ever know

And I am the harbor and you are the sail
I hold the whole ocean and still you set out
The waves keep arriving with nothing to tell
I love you in silence, I love you in doubt

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Flour on her knuckles, rings she never takes off
Sunday morning kitchen and the radio soft
Those hands been working since before I was born
shelling peas at midnight, up again at dawn

Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
lay the whole world out like a deck of cards
Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
built this family from the backyard

She can braid a story while she braids my hair
press a dollar in my palm like it ain't even there
Slap the table laughing, point a finger when she's right
then pull you to her chest and hold you through the night

Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
lay the whole world out like a deck of cards
Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
built this family from the backyard

Now the joints move slower and the grip ain't tight
but they fold in prayer the same way every night
So I take them gently, hold them while I can
'cause everything I am came through those hands

Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
lay the whole world out like a deck of cards
Grandma's hands, grandma's hands
built this family from the backyard

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Nineteen and shaking in a borrowed coat
three chords and a tremble in my throat
The mic was crooked and the lights too low
but baby look at me, I had a show

Oh, that first open mic, that first open mic
fumbled every word and I played it like a knife
The crowd was just my mother and a guy asleep at the bar
but I walked out of that bar room feeling like a star

I forgot the second verse, made up a rhyme
tripped right off the stage on a quarter to nine
The bartender clapped 'cause it's all he could do
and somewhere in that mess I found something true

Oh, that first open mic, that first open mic
fumbled every word and I played it like a knife
The crowd was just my mother and a guy asleep at the bar
but I walked out of that bar room feeling like a star

Now the rooms are bigger and the bands are tight
but nothing beats the terror of that first wild night
When I had nothing in my pockets and the world to lose
and a head full of dreaming and a beat-up pair of shoes

Oh, that first open mic, that first open mic
fumbled every word and I played it like a knife
Still I tip my hat to the kid that I was then
oh, I'd do it all again

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Well I measured out the rice for two
then I caught myself, the way I always do
Fifty years she stirred the pot
now it's just me and what she taught

Ooh, table for one, ooh, table for one
the kettle still whistles when the day is done
She left me her recipes written in blue
so I cook for myself and I cook for you

I burned the eggs the first three days
then her voice came back in a hundred ways
Low flame, honey, give it time
and the kitchen smelled like nineteen sixty-nine

Ooh, table for one, ooh, table for one
the kettle still whistles when the day is done
She left me her recipes written in blue
so I cook for myself and I cook for you

I set two plates and put one away
but I leave her chair just where it lay
And I say the grace we always said
then I eat the supper that her ghost helped spread

Ooh, table for one, ooh, table for one
but the love that she left me is never undone
I'll learn every dish that she ever knew
so I cook for myself and I cook for you

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Old Eli's got a board on a folding card table
five dollars a game if you think that you're able
He'll yawn and he'll stretch like he don't even care
then he'll mate you in six and he'll lean back in his chair

Run, run, the rook and the pawn
the sun's coming up and your money's all gone
He smiles like a preacher and he plays like a thief
old Eli in the park'll bring a young man grief

A suit came down from the offices high
said I play at the club, gonna teach you, old guy
Eli took his queen and he took all his pride
then he bought the man coffee and he sat by his side

Run, run, the rook and the pawn
the sun's coming up and your money's all gone
He smiles like a preacher and he plays like a thief
old Eli in the park'll bring a young man grief

They say he played for money in a tournament town
till the bottle and the bad luck brought a champion down
Now the pigeons are his witness and the bench is his throne
and he'd rather rule the park than play a king all alone

Run, run, the rook and the pawn
the sun's coming up and your money's all gone
He smiles like a preacher and he plays like a thief
old Eli in the park'll bring a young man grief

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Grandma's hands, callused but they soft when they hold me
raised six kids on a prayer and the words that she told me
Work-worn fingers counting out the change in the jar
she ain't never had a lot but she got us this far
Scars on her knuckles from the years and the grind
peeling potatoes, leaving worry behind
I made it out the struggle, put a ring on her hand
now she don't lift a finger, that's the whole entire plan

Grandma's hands (hands), built me from the ground (ground)
every dollar that I'm getting, I'm a send it on down (down)
Those hands held me crying, those hands wiped me clean
now I'm holding them gently, queen, you know what I mean
Grandma's hands (hands), grandma's hands (hands)
the softest, the strongest, in the whole of the land

She pressed a folded twenty in my palm when I left
said don't you come back broke and don't you come back theft
Those hands know hunger, those hands know the cold
those hands know the Bible better than I'm told
Now the joints are aching and the skin's getting thin
so I rub the ointment in like she rubbed it on my skin

If I make a million, if I lose it all the same
those hands gon' be the reason that I made it through the rain
So I hold 'em, I hold 'em, while I still got the chance
every beat in my chest came from grandma's hands

Grandma's hands (hands), built me from the ground (ground)
every dollar that I'm getting, I'm a send it on down (down)
Those hands held me crying, those hands wiped me clean
now I'm holding them gently, queen, you know what I mean

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Down on the corner where the awning hangs green
there's a man with a needle and a sewing machine
He took my busted jacket and he made it brand new
pin in his mouth saying I got you, I got you

He's the tailor, the tailor, he dresses the block
from the deacon to the dealer, everybody walk the walk
Give him a Tuesday and a yard of good cloth
and the whole neighborhood is looking sharp

He stitched the bride a gown and the groom a fine vest
fixed the postman's trousers when he split the back seam
He don't charge the kids for the patch on the knee
says a sharp-dressed child is a sharp-dressed dream

He's the tailor, the tailor, he dresses the block
from the deacon to the dealer, everybody walk the walk
Give him a Tuesday and a yard of good cloth
and the whole neighborhood is looking sharp

Upright bass and a horn line jump
when Saturday comes the whole street struts
in a suit that he cut, in a hem that he turned
paying back the love and the respect that he earned

He's the tailor, the tailor, he dresses the block
from the deacon to the dealer, everybody walk the walk
Give him a Tuesday and a yard of good cloth
and the whole neighborhood is looking sharp

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The rooms are louder now they're empty
echo where the sofa used to be
I run the tape across the cardboard
one long sound that says it's done with me

There's a square of brighter paint
where a photograph hung for years
The light comes in like it always did
for someone else's morning, not for here

So I press it down, press it down
the last seam closing on the floor
Everything I was inside these walls
fits in a box by the door

I keep the key out of habit
though it opens nothing of mine
The building exhales, the hallway waits
for a future that was never going to be mine

The guitars rise where the words run out
because there's nothing left to say
Just the shape of a life pulled up by the roots
and the long slow letting it drift away

So I press it down, press it down
the last seam closing on the floor
Everything I was inside these walls
fits in a box by the door

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
She called me at the altar said the sky's turning green
there's a wall cloud forming over Abilene
I left the cake, I left the ring
you can't marry down a man who loves a dangerous thing

Go, go, chase the funnel down the plain
horns up high in the hammering rain
I can't settle, I can't stay
the radar's lighting up and I'm a hundred miles away

My mama says a man needs a roof and a wife
but I keep one foot out the door my whole life
The sirens are my lullaby, the hail's my applause
I'm married to the weather and the weather makes the laws

Go, go, chase the funnel down the plain
horns up high in the hammering rain
I can't settle, I can't stay
the radar's lighting up and I'm a hundred miles away

Maybe one day the sky'll go quiet and blue
and I'll find me a porch and I'll find me a you
but tonight there's a supercell turning out west
and a restless little engine pounding here in my chest

Go, go, chase the funnel down the plain
horns up high in the hammering rain
I can't settle, I can't stay
the radar's lighting up and I'm a hundred miles away

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Met you in July where the warm water meets the sand
saltwater in your hair and a cold drink in your hand
We didn't talk tomorrow, didn't talk about the fall
just the sun going down and the tide and that's all

Summer love, easy like the breeze
barefoot in the evening underneath the palm trees
Summer love, we both understand
when September comes you're writing in the sand

We danced on the boardwalk to a radio song
you taught me your language, I sang it all wrong
No promises, no future, just a here and a now
and a kiss that the ocean took out somehow

Summer love, easy like the breeze
barefoot in the evening underneath the palm trees
Summer love, we both understand
when September comes you're writing in the sand

So I won't hold the sunset, won't hold the sea
won't hold a single moment that wasn't meant for me
I'll keep you like a seashell, pressed against my ear
the sound of one bright summer when you were here

Summer love, easy like the breeze
barefoot in the evening underneath the palm trees
Summer love, we both understand
when September comes you're writing in the sand

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Morning at the kitchen, midnight at the bar
my hands know the dishes and they know the guitar
Two shifts in the city and the rent still calls
I count out the coins against the cracking walls

Ay, the candle burns at both of its ends
still the dark keeps coming and it never bends
I work and I work till my fingers are worn
and I'm further behind than the day I was born

My mother sewed for pennies, my father broke his back
now I walk the same road on the same cracked track
The clock is a master and the debt is a chain
but I stamp out my anger like the fire takes the rain

Ay, the candle burns at both of its ends
still the dark keeps coming and it never bends
I work and I work till my fingers are worn
and I'm further behind than the day I was born

But listen to the heels strike hard on the floor
that's the sound of a soul that won't be poor
They can take my whole day, they can take my whole year
but the pride in this music, they will never take here

Ay, the candle burns at both of its ends
still the dark keeps coming and it never bends
I work and I work till my fingers are worn
but I rise with the rhythm and I'm proud and I'm torn

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They put us in a square at the harvest hall
the two who hate each other most of all
The caller called a do-si-do
and my worst enemy had to take me by the elbow

Swing your partner, hate in your eyes
feet keep moving while the fiddle flies
Round and round on the old pine floor
two old rivals and a barn room door

He stole my acre and I stole his hound
we ain't spoke a word since the courthouse downtown
but the banjo don't care about a grudge or a fight
it'll spin you and your enemy clean through the night

Swing your partner, hate in your eyes
feet keep moving while the fiddle flies
Round and round on the old pine floor
two old rivals and a barn room door

Somewhere in the turning, somewhere in the reel
I forgot for a minute how I'm supposed to feel
He laughed when I stumbled, I caught him when he spun
and the music made us neighbors 'fore the dance was done

Swing your partner, let the old grudge fall
the fiddle's the boss of the harvest hall
Round and round on the old pine floor
maybe we ain't rivals anymore

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every night at a quarter past nine
I walk the marble hall and I stop at the line
She's been hanging there a hundred years
oil and canvas and a face that disappears

Mona, oh, my painted girl
you're the only one I love in the whole museum world
They lock the doors and the lights go low
and I tell you all my troubles 'fore the morning show

The tourists come and the tourists go
they snap their little pictures and they never know
that the man in the corner with the flashlight beam
is in love with a lady in a gilded dream

Mona, oh, my painted girl
you're the only one I love in the whole museum world
They lock the doors and the lights go low
and I tell you all my troubles 'fore the morning show

I know you can't answer, I know you can't stay
they'll crate you up and ship you clean away
but for now you're my secret in the dark and the hush
a half-smile painted in a single brush

Mona, oh, my painted girl
you're the only one I love in the whole museum world
When the night watch ends and I turn to go
you keep on smiling at a man you'll never know

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The city's lit for Christmas but the ward is gray
I tied my shoes at dawn, gonna work through the day
My children at the window watching me drive
but there's mothers down this hallway just trying to survive

So I'll work, Lord, I'll work
lay my own holiday down
There's a healing in these hands
and I'll wear it like a crown
Work, Lord, I'll work
till the morning comes around

Room four needs water, room six needs prayer
the new mother's crying and I'm gonna be there
I missed my own dinner, I'll miss my own tree
but the least of these brothers, that's the Lord through me

So I'll work, Lord, I'll work
lay my own holiday down
There's a healing in these hands
and I'll wear it like a crown
Work, Lord, I'll work
till the morning comes around

When the shift is over and my feet can't stand
I feel a glory rising, feel it close at hand
'cause whatever I gave up, I gave it for love
and love keeps an account in the books up above

So I'll work, Lord, I'll work
lay my own holiday down
There's a healing in these hands
and I'll wear it like a crown
Work, Lord, I'll work
till the morning comes around

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
I got a tower in the attic and a wire on the roof
spinning records they don't want as living proof
The man's gonna find me, gonna shut me down
but tonight I'm the only free voice in this town

Pirate radio, ninety-nine point three
the only honest signal that you ever gonna see
They can trace me, they can chase me through the dead of night
but you can't kill a frequency, you can't kill the light

Got the blues at midnight and the news that they hide
playing it for everybody on the wrong side of the tide
A truck with an antenna creeping up the road
so I move the whole station 'fore the secret gets told

Pirate radio, ninety-nine point three
the only honest signal that you ever gonna see
They can trace me, they can chase me through the dead of night
but you can't kill a frequency, you can't kill the light

When they kick in my door and they pull out the plug
there'll be a hundred more towers they ain't never gonna dig
'cause the song's in the air and the air ain't theirs to own
and the truth's gonna travel on a wave all its own

Pirate radio, ninety-nine point three
the only honest signal that you ever gonna see
They can trace me, they can chase me through the dead of night
but you can't kill a frequency, you can't kill the light

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Seventeen and shaking in a borrowed pair of boots
Three chords and a notebook full of half-truths
The mic smelled like every kid before me
I screamed into the feedback like it owed me a story

Nobody clapped, the bartender just stared
But I felt the floor move and I didn't care
I gave it everything I didn't know I had
Walked off the stage half proud and half mad

Look back, look back, I was nothing but noise
A fist in the dark and a cracking voice
But I'd do it again, every shaking line
That broken first night still feels like mine

Now the rooms are bigger and the sound is clean
But I miss the kid who didn't know what he'd mean
The stakes were zero and the heart was full
No plan, no net, just the pull

If you're standing in the back with your hands in your sleeves
Get up, get loud, this is all you need
The worst show you'll play is the one you'll keep
The rest is just the echo of the leap

Look back, look back, I was nothing but noise
A fist in the dark and a cracking voice
But I'd do it again, every shaking line
That broken first night still feels like mine

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
La lámpara tiembla sobre el escritorio gris
My coffee gone cold and the page won't end
The trumpets in my chest play a tired refrain
While the whole sleeping city forgets my name

Las dos de la mañana, solo yo y el reloj
My eyes trace the lines till the letters all blur
A candle of want in a window of doubt
I carry tomorrow on the back of this night

Ay, ay, ay, canta el corazón cansado
I'll learn what I have to, I'll earn what I dreamed
With violins of will and a guitar of pride
Que viva el que estudia cuando el mundo durmió

Mi madre allá lejos no sabe que velo
I write down her sacrifice in every note
The lonely fire burns but it keeps me warm
Manaña the dawn will repay what I gave

No hay mariachi tan fiel como el libro de noche
No crowd and no plaza, just me and the lamp
But the song is the same one my abuelo would sing
Keep going, mijo, the morning will ring

Ay, ay, ay, canta el corazón cansado
I'll learn what I have to, I'll earn what I dreamed
With violins of will and a guitar of pride
Que viva el que estudia cuando el mundo durmió

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The screen door settled and the truck stopped running
You're in the kitchen and I'm out on the step
We said our worst and now there's nothing coming
Just the porch light buzzing over words we can't forget

The radio's off, even the dog's gone still
There's a half-drunk coffee going cold on the rail
The hardest sound in the world's no sound at all
When the people who love you have run out of words to tell

It's the quiet after, the long slow ache
When the door's not slammed but it's hard to face
I'd rather you yell than sit there like stone
This kind of silence is the loudest I've known

I know I was wrong but I'm too proud to cross it
That little stretch of floor between your chair and mine
We built a whole life and I won't go and lose it
Over something we'll laugh about in better time

So I'll set down my anger like a glass on the shelf
Walk in soft and quiet and forgive my own self
Reach for your hand in the hush of the room
Let the quiet turn warm in the kitchen by noon

It's the quiet after, the long slow ache
When the door's not slammed but it's hard to face
I'd rather you yell than sit there like stone
This kind of silence is the loudest I've known

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up where the streetlights flickered out at nine
Same cracked corner, same old yellow line
Mama said baby keep your head to the sky
There's a whole bright city just waiting to fly

Now I'm stepping in the light with my collar turned up
Left the dead-end dust in a golden cup
The bassline carries me out of the gray
Every mirror ball night washing yesterday away

I made it out, I made it out, watch me shine
From the dead-end block to the dancing line
Spin me round, feel the freedom in my feet
The whole world's open on a glittering street

They said you won't leave, you'll be back, you'll see
But the rhythm in my heart had a different plan for me
I packed up my hunger and a pair of secondhand shoes
Now the whole floor's moving and I can't lose

For everybody stuck at the end of a road
With a fire in your chest and a heavy load
There's a beat that'll carry you, just hold on tight
Your corner ain't the end, it's the edge of the night

I made it out, I made it out, watch me shine
From the dead-end block to the dancing line
Spin me round, feel the freedom in my feet
The whole world's open on a glittering street

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Dos cuerpos en la órbita, perdiendo gravedad
Tu mano me soltó y ya no vuelve atrás
El cable se rompió en la oscuridad
Y el silencio nos traga, no hay nada más

Nos vamos separando, baby, lento en el vacío
Tu luz se hace pequeña y yo me muero de frío
Gritando tu nombre pero no hay sonido
Dos astronautas que el espacio dividió

Te veo hacerte estrella en el fondo del azul
Cada segundo lejos y más solo estoy yo
No hay timón, no hay tierra, no hay vuelta a la luz
Solo el eco perdido de lo que se acabó

Quería anclarte el corazón pero el universo es cruel
Nos dimos todo el cielo y aún no fue suficiente
Flotando entre planetas, sin gas y sin papel
Te escribo en las cometas que no sienten

Si me oyes en la radio de una galaxia gris
Recuerda que te amé con todo lo que fui
No hay distancia tan grande pa' olvidar lo que vivimos
Dos astronautas que una vez compartimos

Nos vamos separando, baby, lento en el vacío
Tu luz se hace pequeña y yo me muero de frío
Gritando tu nombre pero no hay sonido
Dos astronautas que el espacio dividió

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at six, clock out at noon
Second shift starts way too soon
My paycheck's gone before it's spent
Half to the man and half to the rent

They call it hustle, they call it grind
I call it drowning one dime at a time
Two name tags hanging on a nail by the door
And I'm still coming up short, still coming up poor

I work, I work, and I fall behind
Breaking my back for a dollar and a dime
They sold me a ladder with the bottom rung gone
So I'm running in place while they're laughing on

Coffee for blood and a debt that won't quit
The American dream is a counterfeit
My boss got a boat and my landlord's got three
And they built every plank off the back of me

But I ain't alone, there's a million like me
Sweating the floor of this economy
So turn it up loud, let the whole block hear
We're tired, we're broke, and we're done being scared

I work, I work, and I fall behind
Breaking my back for a dollar and a dime
They sold me a ladder with the bottom rung gone
So I'm running in place while they're laughing on

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Fifty years of mornings, fifty years of grace
Now the music's playing soft and I can see your face
The hands that I have held through every storm and tide
Reach for me one more time, won't you stay by my side

Oh, dance with me, my darling, while the lamplight's low
The Lord gave us this evening and it's ours to hold
Every step we ever took was leading us right here
One last waltz together, hallelujah, my dear

We buried and we welcomed, we wept and we praised
Through the valley and the mountain, through the count of all our days
Your hair has turned to silver but your eyes still shine the same
And I'd marry you tomorrow, I would do it all again

When the angels come to call me home up yonder
Save a place for me upon that golden floor
For a love like ours don't end, it only wanders
To a ballroom in the sky forevermore

Oh, dance with me, my darling, while the lamplight's low
The Lord gave us this evening and it's ours to hold
Every step we ever took was leading us right here
One last waltz together, hallelujah, my dear

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Neon on the night bus, badge still on my chest
Dropped one uniform, put on the next
The city never sleeps and neither do I
Chasing down a number that keeps climbing high

Two jobs, no sleep, and I'm still falling behind
Feel the kick drum carry what's left of my mind
Work all day, work all night, but the rent keeps the beat
So I lose myself in the lights and the heat

Four walls and a fridge that I can barely fill
Every hour that I sell still don't pay the bill
But the bassline finds me on the warehouse floor
For a moment I'm not tired, not broke, not poor

Let it build, let it build, let it rise and release
For one night the weight slips off of me
Hands up if you're grinding and it's never enough
We dance till the sunrise 'cause the world is too rough

Two jobs, no sleep, and I'm still falling behind
Feel the kick drum carry what's left of my mind
Work all day, work all night, but the rent keeps the beat
So I lose myself in the lights and the heat

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Zarpamos en la noche con un peso en la bodega
El capitán no duerme, dice que algo nos persigue
La brújula da vueltas, el mar se pone ciego
Y el cofre allá en el fondo nunca para, sigue, sigue

Carga maldita, no me dejes naufragar
El viento gime un nombre que no quiero pronunciar
Dale, dale, marinero, reza fuerte hasta llegar
Que lo que va en la bodega no se puede tirar

Tres hombres ya cayeron con la fiebre en la mirada
El oro que cargamos tiene sangre, tiene historia
La luna roja sale y la tripulación callada
Reza por una orilla, reza por una victoria

Dicen que en cada puerto la encontraron y la huyeron
Que el que toca la caja ya no vuelve a ver su casa
Pero el oro es el oro y por él nos arriesgamos
Mientras el océano negro lentamente nos abraza

Si llegamos al amanecer la quemo en la arena
Le devuelvo al diablo lo que el diablo me prestó
No hay tesoro que valga una vida en cadena
Que se lleve la marea lo que el miedo me dejó

Carga maldita, no me dejes naufragar
El viento gime un nombre que no quiero pronunciar
Dale, dale, marinero, reza fuerte hasta llegar
Que lo que va en la bodega no se puede tirar

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Three gray dots on a cracked-up screen
In a parking lot somewhere in between
The gas station hum and the engine off
Waiting on the words that would call it off

Four years folded into a single line
No I love you, no goodbye, no this is hard for me
Just a little blue bubble cutting clean
The quietest end I have ever seen

You let it go with the tap of a thumb
No front porch words, no settling sun
I'd have driven all night just to hear you say it slow
But you ended us in a message and you let the silence grow

I read it twelve times in the dashboard light
Like the letters might rearrange if I tried
But the truth was plain in the cold blue glow
Some doors just close with nowhere to go

There's an old highway out past the county line
Where I used to think you'd be mine all the time
Now I drive it alone with the windows down
And I bury that little screen in the sound

You let it go with the tap of a thumb
No front porch words, no settling sun
I'd have driven all night just to hear you say it slow
But you ended us in a message and you let the silence grow

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
We grew up on the same dead streets
Different porches, the same heartbeat
I knew your name before I knew my own
Now you're standing in the kitchen like you've always been home

Funny how you ride past somebody for years
Then one autumn it all becomes clear
The diner, the river, the water tower hill
Every place I used to know just got realer still

I'm falling for the girl from the end of my road
The one I should've seen back when we were both young and broke
Now the whole town looks golden, every corner I roam
'Cause I fell in love with someone from my own hometown

My daddy knows your daddy from the mill
Our mamas trade their casseroles still
And I used to think that I had to leave to find
Something better than this little patch of mine

But the prettiest thing was here the whole time
Walking the same sidewalks, breathing the same pine
Why chase a stranger across some distant state
When love was just waiting at the corner store gate

I'm falling for the girl from the end of my road
The one I should've seen back when we were both young and broke
Now the whole town looks golden, every corner I roam
'Cause I fell in love with someone from my own hometown

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I threw my Bible in the river back in '09
God wasn't answering and I was tired of the line
Buried my mother in the cold hard clay
And I cursed the empty heaven that just looked away

Walked through ten years like a man with no ground
Drank up my Sundays, never made a sound
The preacher used to wave but I'd just look down
A lost old sinner in a faithless town

But I found it again in a stranger's hand
In the rain that fell on the dry dead land
When I had nothing left, no place to stand
Something caught me falling, Lord, I understand

It wasn't in the steeple or the offering plate
It was a poor man sharing when he had nothing on his plate
A candle in a window on the coldest night
That little bit of mercy brought me back to the light

I don't have it all figured, I still got my doubt
But I quit demanding answers and I started looking out
And grace don't shout, no, grace comes low
Like a slow morning sun on a field of snow

So I found it again in a stranger's hand
In the rain that fell on the dry dead land
When I had nothing left, no place to stand
Something caught me falling, Lord, I understand

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The ceiling fan is turning slow above our heads
We lie like distant islands on the same warm bed
The words have all gone quiet, the harsh ones we said
Now there's only the rain and the things left unsaid

A softer kind of silence than the slamming of doors
Your shoulder turned away on the cool cotton shores
The ocean of the evening laps against the floor
And I'm counting all the reasons not to fight anymore

So hush now, my love, let the quiet be kind
Let the night fold around us and unwind the mind
The ache will dissolve like the foam on the sea
Come back, soft and slow, come back to me

I'll trace a little circle on the small of your spine
A wordless apology, no reason, no line
The candle burns lower and the shadows grow long
And somewhere in the breathing we find our way home

Why do we wound the very ones we'd never lose
With sharp little arrows we never meant to use
Let the bossa play softly, let the tide turn around
Love is the calm that comes after the sound

So hush now, my love, let the quiet be kind
Let the night fold around us and unwind the mind
The ache will dissolve like the foam on the sea
Come back, soft and slow, come back to me

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The fiddle was singing and the whole hall was warm
My name on the poster, my heart in a storm
One song from the prize and the crowd on its feet
So close I could taste it, so near and so sweet

The last note came trembling, my hand it did shake
And the string it went silent for an old fool's sake
The judges they faltered, the moment it passed
The glory I reached for slipped out of my grasp

Oh, the night I almost made it still haunts the cold air
With the wind off the harbour and the gas lamp's glare
So near to the doorway of all that I dreamed
But almost is a country where the lonely ones gleam

I walked the long quay with my coat collar high
The stars over Galway gave a watery sigh
Not bitter, not broken, just a strange kind of ache
For the road I could see and the one I would take

But tell me, what's living without one near miss
A story to carry, a scar you can kiss
For the ones who arrive never know the sweet pain
Of the night you near touched it and rose up again

Oh, the night I almost made it still haunts the cold air
With the wind off the harbour and the gas lamp's glare
So near to the doorway of all that I dreamed
But almost is a country where the lonely ones gleam

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Met you by the boardwalk where the seagulls scream
You had salt in your hair and a wicked little gleam
We danced on the sand to a transistor song
Knew it wouldn't last but it felt so strong
