Pick it up, pick it up, summer's gonna fly
Kiss me by the ocean 'neath the cotton-candy sky
It's a fling, it's a flash, it's a horn-section thing
We'll burn out by September but tonight we're gonna swing

Ice cream melting down your wrist by two
Upstroke guitar and a sky so blue
We rode the Ferris wheel up over the bay
And swore that we'd remember every wave and ray

You said you'd go back to the city in the fall
But the trumpet kept playing like it heard nothing at all
So we skanked on the pier till the lifeguards went home
Two summer-time strangers who were done being alone

Won't pretend that it's forever, won't pretend that it's deep
Just a sunburned little memory that the both of us will keep
When the leaves start to turn and the beach towns close
I'll still hear the horns and the way the breeze blows

Pick it up, pick it up, summer's gonna fly
Kiss me by the ocean 'neath the cotton-candy sky
It's a fling, it's a flash, it's a horn-section thing
We'll burn out by September but tonight we're gonna swing

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They came with silver buttons and a contract in a case
Wanting all my verses for a perfume and a face
The trumpets in the plaza did not learn to count their coins
They learned to split the evening where the dark and music joins

My abuela sold tamales, never once a single word
She said the throat that's bought, mijo, sings the prettiest and least heard
So I keep my pen in vinegar, my notebook in the rain
And the river of the marketplace can't wash away the stain

Ay, let them keep the gold, let them keep the neon name
I will die with empty pockets and a heart they cannot tame
The guitarrón still answers when I knock against the night
A poet with no buyer is a poet still alright

The radio men whisper that my songs are out of style
That hunger makes a singer walk the last and longest mile
But I have walked it barefoot down the cobbles of my town
And every door I passed by knew the man who wouldn't bow down

Violins, do not weep for me, I drink the cheaper wine
The moon does not send invoices for the silver that is mine
And when they ask my widow what her foolish husband sold
She'll say: only words to strangers, and he never once sold those

Ay, let them keep the gold, let them keep the neon name
I will die with empty pockets and a heart they cannot tame
The guitarrón still answers when I knock against the night
A poet with no buyer is a poet still alright

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Third-floor window still got that yellow shade
Somebody else is paying what we never paid
I cut my headlights two blocks back like a thief
Rolling slow past a building full of borrowed grief

There's the fire escape where you used to smoke
The rust on that railing the same color as a joke
I can almost see your robe against the glass
But that's just a stranger and the years that had to pass

I'm driving past your old apartment at night
Forty in a thirty with the radio low and the city out of sight
They changed the locks, they changed the curtains, changed the name on the bell
But they can't change the corner where I learned to love and learned to give it hell

Used to climb those stairs two at a time
Now I won't even slow down for the stop sign
The deli's still open, the dog still barks
Same busted streetlight throwing the same crooked sparks

I ain't gonna park, I ain't gonna knock
I ain't the kind of fool to make the whole street talk
I just need to see it one more time tonight
Then I'll turn at the river and I'll put it out of sight

I'm driving past your old apartment at night
Forty in a thirty with my hands too tight
They changed the locks, they changed the curtains, changed the name on the bell
But they can't change the corner where I learned to love and learned to give it hell

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody printed our name on the banner downtown
The paper had us losing before we put the jerseys on
Forty kids in a county nobody can pronounce
With a coach who drives the school bus and a field that's half-mowed brown

Clock on the scoreboard, two minutes and change
Everybody's screaming and the whole world rearranged

This is the night, this is the only one we get
No trophy room, no sponsor, just the sweat we haven't spent
Throw it to the kid they said would never make the team
We're the long shot, we're the maybe, we're the underside of a dream
And we're playing like the morning's never coming

Grandma's in the bleachers with a homemade sign
The other side's got buses, we got pickup trucks in a line
They're laughing in their warmups, in their matching gold and white
But nobody told them what we practiced every night

Clock on the scoreboard, one heartbeat to go
The ball is in the air and now the whole town holds it slow

This is the night, this is the only one we get
No trophy room, no sponsor, just the sweat we haven't spent
Throw it to the kid they said would never make the team
We're the long shot, we're the maybe, we're the underside of a dream
And we're playing like the morning's never coming

Win or lose, they'll remember our name
Win or lose, we showed up to the game
Win or lose, we were never the same after tonight

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Thirty years I've walked this marble floor
Keys on my hip and a flashlight, nothing more
They hung her in the east wing back in seventy-nine
A woman in a black dress and the paint gone amber with time

The placard says her name's been lost to dust
A portrait of a stranger and a sitter's silent trust
But at a quarter past midnight when the alarms are set
I swear her eyes have followed me to corners we haven't met

Oh, I'm the keeper of a woman made of oil and lead
A hundred years of widows and the things they never said
The building creaks like coffin wood, the moon comes through the glass
And I tip my cap to a lady who will never let me pass

I talk to her in winter when the heating pipes go cold
Tell her how my wife has left and how I'm getting old
She never says a word but Lord, she listens like a stone
And a man can love a painting when he's all the museum's own

They'll retire me come April with a watch and a cake
And they'll move her to the basement for the new wing's sake
But I'll come back as a draft beneath the door
And guard her in the dark forever more

Oh, I'm the keeper of a woman made of oil and lead
A hundred years of widows and the things they never said
The building creaks like coffin wood, the moon comes through the glass
And I tip my cap to a lady who will never let me pass

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Her recipe cards are still taped to the cabinet door
Her handwriting telling me a half a cup, maybe a little more
Forty years she fed me and I never learned a thing
Now I'm standing at the stovetop with a pot and a wedding ring

I bought the smallest skillet that the hardware store had
The checkout girl just smiled at me, didn't know it made me sad
There's no measuring a heartache by the servings on the box
Everything comes in family size and a single set of forks

I'm learning how to cook for one
Scraping half of it in the trash when the day is done
The kitchen window's dark by five, the chair across is bare
And I set two plates from habit, then I put one back with care

Burned the cornbread Tuesday, set the towel near aflame
She'd have laughed until she cried and called me by my middle name
I eat it standing up sometimes, can't face that empty room
With the radio for company and her apron still in bloom

But last night I got the gravy right, the way she liked it thin
And I said it out loud to nobody, I said honey, I'm getting in
Maybe living's just a recipe you ruin till you don't
And one of these cold evenings I'll forgive the meals I won't

I'm learning how to cook for one
Under the only light still on when the day is done
The kitchen window's dark by five, the chair across is bare
But I'm still here, and I'm still hungry, and that's gotta count for something somewhere

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I wrote it all down on the back of your name
Every sharp little thing, every shard of the blame
Drove to the cemetery with the bass in my chest
A letter in my pocket I could finally address

The wind through the headstones, the hum of the wire
The city behind me a low orange fire

I forgive you, I forgive you, into the rolling dark
The break keeps breaking and it's tearing me apart
You're not here to hear it but I needed it to land
Letting go of a grudge I can't put in your hand
Gone, gone, but the anger's gone too
Gone, gone, and I'm lighter than you

They lower the volume when the dead get involved
But my whole life was a question that you left unsolved
Now there's nobody to argue and nobody to win
Just me and the silence and the trouble I'm in

Bpm climbing like the years that we lost
I'm paying the debt and I'm counting the cost

I forgive you, I forgive you, into the rolling dark
The break keeps breaking and it's tearing me apart
You're not here to hear it but I needed it to land
Letting go of a grudge I can't put in your hand

Forgiveness for the gone is a door with no room
You open it anyway and you breathe in the gloom
It was never about you, it was always about me
Walking back to the car and finally free

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
You hum a little tune you don't know that I know
From the time you sang it walking through the early snow
I carry it like water in a cup with no bottom or brim
For a love that only lives in the songs I keep him in

We share the morning bus and a half a word or two
You ask me how I slept and I say fine, how about you
And a whole and tender country falls between the question marks
A continent of wanting in the space before it starks

Oh I love you like the rain loves a window it can't come through
Pressed against the glass of you, never getting through
And I'll go home to my candle and my book and my one warm chair
Loving you completely in the loving that you'll never share

You told me about her with your eyes all lit and wide
And I poured us both more coffee just to have a place to hide
I am happy that you're happy, that's the cruelest thing of all
To wish a man the very thing that is your own downfall

Some loves are meant for keeping and some are meant to hold
Like a coal you never light against the coming of the cold
It warms me just to carry it, it asks for nothing back
A quiet little flame on a long and lonesome track

Oh I love you like the rain loves a window it can't come through
Pressed against the glass of you, never getting through
And I'll go home to my candle and my book and my one warm chair
Loving you completely in the loving that you'll never share

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The tents are coming down on the trampled summer grass
The lights string low and golden and we know it's gonna pass
Three days of strangers singing till our voices wore away
Now the moon hangs like an offering on the closing of the day

Somebody's mother dancing with her shoes held in her hand
Somebody's grief got lifted by a kindness and a band
We came in here as separate stones and leave here as a wall
The last night of the gathering is the holiest of all

Oh lift it up, lift it up, one more time before we go
The morning's gonna scatter us to roads we'll never know
But tonight the field's a chapel and the sky's a stained-glass dome
So sing it like a people who have finally found a home

The headliner's gone quiet, now it's just the crowd and grace
A thousand throats together making heaven of this place
The vendor packs his lanterns but he's humming every word
The last hosanna of the night's the sweetest one I've heard

We will carry it on Monday to the office and the line
A little of this fire kept burning, a little of this shine
And when the world gets heavy and the joy is hard to find
We'll remember one last evening when we left the dark behind

Oh lift it up, lift it up, one more time before we go
The morning's gonna scatter us to roads we'll never know
But tonight the field's a chapel and the sky's a stained-glass dome
So sing it like a people who have finally found a home

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The bus let me off where the gravel meets the rye
Under a wash of static and a low cathedral sky
My mother's at the screen door, smaller than I knew
And the kitchen smells of something that I forgot was true

The town hung up a banner with my name across the square
But I can't hear the marching band for the ringing that I wear
Everything's the same here and everything has moved
A stranger in a body that the desert disapproved

I'm home, I'm home, but the home keeps drifting back
Underneath the swell of all this haze and feedback
Hold me where the porch light bleeds into the lawn
And tell me who I have to be now that the war is gone

My old bed feels enormous, my old room feels like a tomb
The medals in a sock drawer where the moonlight finds the gloom
I flinch at slamming doorways, I count the exits twice
And I'm learning that survival has a long and quiet price

Wash it over, wash it over, let the guitars drown the years
Let the wall of sound be softer than the things I can't unhear
Maybe healing's just a tide that takes its time to come
The ocean of the living pulling back the things I've done

I'm home, I'm home, but the home keeps drifting back
Underneath the swell of all this haze and feedback
Hold me where the porch light bleeds into the lawn
And tell me who I have to be now that the war is gone

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Nine to five I'm a shadow in a navy uniform
Keeping watch on a gallery, keeping out of the storm
But there's a girl in a blue dress that they painted long ago
And she's the only company this lonely heart will know

The tourists never see her, they just snap and walk away
Got their eyes on the famous ones, the headline on display
But I linger in her corner when the crowd has gone to lunch
And I swear the way she's smiling, baby, she's got a hunch

Oh, she don't age and she don't leave and she don't ask me why
She just hangs there in the half-light underneath the skylight sky
And it's foolish, Lord, I know it, loving someone made of paint
But she's patient as a Sunday and she's gentle as a saint

I hum a little Marvin while I'm dusting off the frame
Tell her bout my troubles, and she takes them just the same
There's a kind of love that's quiet, that don't need a thing returned
A candle in the corner of a heart that's gently burned

Maybe I'm the painting and she's the one who's free
Maybe she's been guarding all these years, watching over me
When they cut the lights at closing and the marble starts to hum
I tell her, see you Monday, girl, and morning always comes

Oh, she don't age and she don't leave and she don't ask me why
She just hangs there in the half-light underneath the skylight sky
And it's foolish, Lord, I know it, loving someone made of paint
But she's patient as a Sunday and she's gentle as a saint

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
The clarinet is crying in the kitchen where she stood
Where she rolled the dough on Fridays like her mother said she should
Now the table's set for one and the candles ask me why
An old man and a soup pot and a memory too dry

She measured with her hands, she never trusted a spoon
A pinch of this, a song of that, and supper by the moon
I try to make her brisket but it comes out wrong and thin
And the violin keeps laughing at the state that I am in

Oy, cook for one, the saddest little number in the world
The dumplings for a houseful and the napkins still unfurled
But play, play, klezmer, turn my weeping to a dance
For even a lonely kitchen deserves a second chance

The neighbors bring me casseroles and pity in their eyes
But a man must learn the burner or a man just sits and dies
So I chop the onions slowly and I let them make me cry
It's the only honest weeping that the doctor won't deny

And when the broth is simmering, I feel her at my back
Guiding my old fingers down a warm and salty track
The accordion is swelling and I'm dancing by the stove
A widower, a wooden spoon, and fifty years of love

Oy, cook for one, the saddest little number in the world
The dumplings for a houseful and the napkins still unfurled
But play, play, klezmer, turn my weeping to a dance
For even a lonely kitchen deserves a second chance

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I memorized the verses before I learned to read
Knelt on the same hard pew where they taught me how to bleed
Thirty years of swallowing the words I couldn't say
And nobody noticed the morning I walked away

No letter, no announcement, no scene out in the lot
Just an empty space on Sunday in the back where I forgot
They'll ask my mother where I went, she'll say he's feeling sick
And the silence does the lying that I couldn't make stick

I'm leaving quiet, I'm leaving clean
No door slammed shut, no burning bridge between
I just stopped showing up to the faith I couldn't keep
Walked out through the parking lot while the whole town was asleep
This is not a war cry, this is just goodbye

They said the questions were the devil sitting in my chest
Said doubt was just a weakness I could pray into the rest
But I prayed until my knees went numb and nothing ever came
Just the echo of a building and the weight of my own name

I don't hate you, I just outgrew the size of your god
The walls got small around me and the ceiling felt like sod
There's a bigger sky out here than the one above the steeple
And I'm done performing belief for the comfort of you people

I'm leaving quiet, I'm leaving clean
No door slammed shut, no burning bridge between
I just stopped showing up to the faith I couldn't keep
Walked out through the parking lot while the whole town was asleep

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Fireworks are cracking gold above the parking deck
I'm sixteen hours into it with a stethoscope round my neck
The city's out there dancing and the city's gettin' loud
But the seventh floor needs somebody, so I'm clocking out the crowd

Bed four needs his water, bed nine just needs my hand
Nobody's home to hold them so I'm holding where I stand
The holiday don't reach here past the tinsel on the cart
But the rhythm in the monitors is keeping time with my heart

So I dance down the hallway with a chart and a smile
Keep the disco in my sneakers for another little while
Nobody's gonna clap for me, nobody's gonna know
But the night shift's got a heartbeat and I'm letting it glow
Ooh, I'm working through the fireworks, lighting up the row

The vending machine's my dinner and the break room's got a tree
A paper crown from a patient who said merry one to me
I'll catch a cab at sunrise when the holiday is dead
And sleep right through the leftovers and the carols in my head

But right now somebody's breathing 'cause I chose to stay
And that's a better feeling than a parade or a buffet
So turn the IV music up and let the morning come
I'm the angel in the polyester, dancing for someone

So I dance down the hallway with a chart and a smile
Keep the disco in my sneakers for another little while
Nobody's gonna clap for me, nobody's gonna know
But the night shift's got a heartbeat and I'm letting it glow

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Steel jaws snapping in the fluorescent hum
Forty years of stubble, forty years of scum
The chair is a confessional, the blade is a crown
And I am the keeper of the secrets of this town

The mayor's bald spot, the preacher's lie
The kid who left for war and came back with a thousand-mile eye
They all sit down before me and they spill it on the floor
With the hair and the silence and the blood from the war

I know who's dying, I know who's in debt
I know every sin this town will never forget
Sharpen the razor, let the clippers scream
The barber is the gravekeeper of every buried dream
Kneel in the chair, I'll cut you to the bone
Nobody leaves my shop the same as they came alone

The widow's new lover, the banker's hidden gun
The things they tell a stranger underneath the setting sun
Mirror to mirror, an infinite hall
I've watched them grow and watched them rot, I've watched them rise and fall

And when I die they'll bury me beneath the barber pole
The red and white still spinning like the spiral of a soul
A thousand whispered secrets will go screaming to the ground
The last man who remembered every face in this whole town

I know who's dying, I know who's in debt
I know every sin this town will never forget
Sharpen the razor, let the clippers scream
The barber is the gravekeeper of every buried dream

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The lawyers signed it Friday but we kept the records out
Forty years of vinyl and the things we never doubt
The living room's a ballroom now, the boxes pushed aside
One more spin around the rug before we let it slide

You still know where to put your hand, I still know where to lean
The same old song from sixty-three, the best we've ever been
We're not angry anymore, we're just quieter and worn
Two dancers in the half-dark of a love that's gently torn

So give me the last dance, the slow one, the long goodbye
Under the mirror ball of every year that we let by
The music's gonna end and then we'll each go out the door
But hold me like you held me on the first dance, just once more
Ooh, one last turn across the floor

The kids don't know we're doing this, they think we're cold and done
But you can love a person and still know your time has come
The needle's getting closer to the silence at the end
And I'm grateful for the dancing, grateful you were my friend

We were good at this, weren't we, when the lights were soft and low
We were good at almost everything except for letting go
So before the song runs out and before the morning's true
Let me memorize the feeling of forever dancing you

So give me the last dance, the slow one, the long goodbye
Under the mirror ball of every year that we let by
The music's gonna end and then we'll each go out the door
But hold me like you held me on the first dance, just once more

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my road in candle wax and gold
A name beside my name before I'm old
The guitar weeps the things I cannot say
My father's promise pulls the night away

The shawl they chose still hangs upon the door
But my two feet won't dance on borrowed floor
The river of my blood begins to turn
There are some bridges that a heart must burn

Olé, I let the future fall
Olé, I will not heed the call
The heels I stamp upon the stone
This broken road is mine alone

The roses in the courtyard close their eyes
I trade their promises for open skies
My mother's tears are silver on the wall
But freedom is the loudest song of all

No crown, no cage, no vow I did not choose
I'd rather walk with nothing left to lose
The palmas fade behind me in the dark
I carry forward only my own spark

Olé, I let the future fall
Olé, I will not heed the call
The heels I stamp upon the stone
This broken road is mine alone

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Daddy bought the acres and he bought the plan
Said one day soon you'll be a married man
There's a ring in the drawer and a date on the wall
But I hear the highway and I hear it call

Mama sewed a future out of someone else's thread
A quiet little life with the words already said
I loved her like the morning, I loved this dusty land
But a man can't build a home from another's hand

So I'm leaving the future they laid out for me
Gonna find out who I'm supposed to be
With a half a tank of gas and a worn-out song
Down a road that nobody told me I belong

The church bells ring for a wedding I won't see
The whole town whispered what I ought to be
But the dust on my boots knows the truth I keep
Some promises are easier to break than keep

Maybe I'll be sorry, maybe I'll be free
Maybe both are just the same out here to me
The sun's coming up on a life I never planned
And for the first time it feels like it's mine to hold

So I'm leaving the future they laid out for me
Gonna find out who I'm supposed to be
With a half a tank of gas and a worn-out song
Down a road that nobody told me I belong

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Beneath the cobbled streets a heartbeat slow
A giant dreams of fire down below
We build our towers on his silent chest
And dance upon the place he lies at rest

The fountains sing, the lanterns burn so bright
We never speak of what could wake the night
My guitar trembles when the church bells ring
For every stone remembers everything

Duerme, gigante, duerme bajo el sol
We sing so soft to keep your dreaming whole
The city lives upon a borrowed breath
A waltz between the cradle and the death

The old men whisper legends in the square
Of eyes that opened once and burned the air
We pour the wine, we stamp the heel, we pray
That he may sleep for one more golden day

What if he turns? What if he finally stands?
And shakes our castles loose from trembling hands
Still we will dance until the very end
Upon the chest of our enormous friend

Duerme, gigante, duerme bajo el sol
We sing so soft to keep your dreaming whole
The city lives upon a borrowed breath
A waltz between the cradle and the death

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Gather round the field where the green grass grows
The lads from the valley nobody knows
They said we'd never make it, they laughed at our name
But here we stand together for the final game

The fiddle and the whistle carry on the wind
The whole town came walking, every kith and kin
Our boots are worn and muddy, our jerseys are torn
But the heart of a village in our chests is borne

So raise up your voices, let the anthem ring
For the underdogs running, for the songs that we sing
Win or lose we'll remember this day on the green
The bravest little team that this town's ever seen

The whistle blows sharp and the clock starts to run
The favorites are mighty but we've only begun
For every fallen father who once kicked this ball
We'll give them the glory or we'll give them our all

When the final horn sounds and the dust settles down
They'll tell of this evening in every pub in town
No trophy can measure what we found here today
A family of fighters who refused to give way

So raise up your voices, let the anthem ring
For the underdogs running, for the songs that we sing
Win or lose we'll remember this day on the green
The bravest little team that this town's ever seen

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
There's a map of every year upon her skin
Rivers where the wedding ring wore thin
She'd peel the apples slow beneath the light
And hold my fevered forehead through the night

Those hands could fix a hem or fix a heart
They held our broken family apart
Now they fold like paper in her lap
A quiet little prayer, a folded map

Oh, her hands, her hands
They built a world I'm only now starting to understand
Oh, her hands, her hands
I'm holding on to everything I can

She taught me how to knead the morning bread
The little songs she hummed inside my head
The garden dirt still living in her lines
A history no photograph defines

I wish I'd memorized the way they moved
The small and ordinary love they proved
Now mine are growing older just the same
And in my palms I see her whole again

Oh, her hands, her hands
They built a world I'm only now starting to understand
Oh, her hands, her hands
I'm holding on to everything I can

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The echo of an empty room
Afternoon dissolving into blue
One last box upon the floor
The tape pulls slow across the door

Seal it up, let it go
Everything I used to know
Folded down and packed away
Nothing left for me to say

The shape of where the couch once stood
A paler square of softer wood
My footsteps louder than before
In rooms that aren't mine anymore

Who was I inside these walls
The laughter down the narrow halls
It lingers like a fading light
Then quietly it says goodnight

Seal it up, let it go
Everything I used to know
Folded down and packed away
Nothing left for me to say

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I buried my Bible in the cold black clay
The night the good Lord turned and walked away
The preacher's words were ash inside my mouth
And every prayer I sent kept drifting south

I walked through years of whiskey, dust, and doubt
With every candle in me burning out
The devil knew my name, he knew it well
I made my crooked bed somewhere near hell

But grace came crawling back on broken knees
Like morning through the dead and frozen trees
I lost the Lord and found Him in the dark
A stubborn ember of a dying spark

It wasn't in the steeple or the choir
But in the ditch beside a dying fire
A stranger gave me water, gave me bread
And something in my chest came back from dead

Faith ain't the thing you carry when it's light
It's what you claw for in the deepest night
I cursed the sky until my voice was gone
Then heard it whisper, child, just carry on

So grace came crawling back on broken knees
Like morning through the dead and frozen trees
I lost the Lord and found Him in the dark
A stubborn ember of a dying spark

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Grandma's hands, weathered like an old guitar
Worked three jobs, drove that rusted-out car
Flour on her fingers, Sunday morning grits
Held the family steady through the lows and the hits
Those palms told stories that her mouth never spoke
Cracked from the cold and the labor and the smoke
But soft when she touched me, said baby you'll be fine
Planted every seed of this dream of mine

Grandma's hands, building something out of nothing
Grandma's hands, always giving, never bluffing
Now I'm grown and I can still feel the warmth
Everything I am, she put it in these arms

She'd braid my cousin's hair beneath the kitchen light
Count the pennies twice, made a dollar out of dimes at night
Clasped in prayer, she carried all our weight
Kept the wolves of this world standing outside the gate
Now her hands are still, folded in the frame
But I write every bar to keep alive her name

If I ever made it, it was built on her grind
The blueprints of a queen that she left behind
So I raise up my hands, same shape as her own
And I thank her for the throne that I'm sitting on

Grandma's hands, building something out of nothing
Grandma's hands, always giving, never bluffing
Now I'm grown and I can still feel the warmth
Everything I am, she put it in these arms

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Cinnamon and morning rain
The screen door singing the same refrain
Steam upon the window glass
A quiet world that could not last

Bread is rising, slow and warm
A shelter from the coming storm
The radio hums an older tune
Gold light spilling through the afternoon

And it all comes flooding back to me
The smell of everything I used to be
A doorway I can never walk again
But I can almost feel it now and then

Build it slow, let it rise
The ache of love behind my eyes
Let the whole thing crash and swell
The sound of all I cannot tell

The table set for one less chair
Her apron still hangs on the stair
I close my eyes and I am small
And she is here, and that is all

And it all comes flooding back to me
The smell of everything I used to be
A doorway I can never walk again
But I can almost feel it now and then

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Broken glass and a busted streetlight
Same four corners every single night
They said this block is all you'll ever know
But I got gasoline and nowhere to go

Mama working doubles, rent is past due
Cops on the corner got nothing to do
I'm sick of the sirens, sick of the rot
Gonna burn through these chains or die on the spot

Get me out, get me out of here
I've been dying in this town for years
Dead-end block, dead-end name
I refuse to go out the same

They built us a cage out of concrete and lies
A ceiling so low you forget there's a sky
But I taped up my fists and I learned how to run
This block ain't a grave, it's a starting gun

Woah-oh! Tear the whole thing down!
Woah-oh! I'm leaving this town!
Every kid on the block with a fire inside
We ain't gonna stay here, we ain't gonna hide

Get me out, get me out of here
I've been dying in this town for years
Dead-end block, dead-end name
I refuse to go out the same

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I slipped out the back while the organ still played
Past the pews where my whole childhood prayed
The stained glass watched me with its colored eyes
And I left my amen hanging in the rafters' lies

They baptized me young in the river so cold
Gave me a story and called it gold
But the hymns turned heavy, the steeple turned grey
And a quiet little voice told me walk away

So I'm leaving the church with my hat in my hand
No lightning, no thunder, no almighty command
Just the creak of the door and the gravel outside
And a God I can't find no matter how hard I've tried

The collection plate empty, the candles burned low
The faces I loved that I'm leaving below
I don't slam the door, I don't curse the name
I just can't keep kneeling and feeling the same

Maybe there's a heaven I'll never get to see
Maybe the silence is all that's left for me
But I'd rather walk honest in the cold autumn air
Than whisper a prayer that was never quite there

So I'm leaving the church with my hat in my hand
No lightning, no thunder, no almighty command
Just the creak of the door and the gravel outside
And a God I can't find no matter how hard I've tried

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the concrete over everything green
The widest gray river you ever have seen
But down on the corner where the old church stood
There's one lonely oak still remembering wood

The children don't climb it, they hurry on by
It holds up its branches like arms to the sky
It remembers the meadow, it remembers the stream
The last living keeper of an old town's dream

Oh, stand tall, last tree, stand tall
With your roots in the cracks of the parking lot wall
You're the green in the gray, you're the song in the stone
Don't you give up, you are not alone

A developer measured it, shook his head slow
Said this whole thing's coming down tomorrow, you know
But the old folks remember the shade that it gave
And a few of them gathered to sing and to save

There's a sermon in the branches, a hymn in the leaves
A prophecy hung in the wind that it weaves
That the world we paved over still wants to grow
If we just leave it room and we just let it show

Oh, stand tall, last tree, stand tall
With your roots in the cracks of the parking lot wall
You're the green in the gray, you're the song in the stone
Don't you give up, you are not alone

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down like the end of the world
Bus was late and the wind unfurled
You stepped under my busted umbrella
A total stranger, some soaking-wet fella

We didn't say much, just laughed at the storm
Two broke kids trying to stay warm
Your shoulder on mine and the city ablaze
In the neon and the puddles and the downpour haze

One umbrella, two strangers in the rain
Don't know your name and I won't again
But for three city blocks we were the same
Two lonely people the weather brought near

The light turned green and you ran for your train
I never even caught your name
But something cracked open inside my chest
The smallest kindness, the briefest rest

Woah! Maybe we're all just out in the storm!
Woah! Holding something to keep someone warm!
For one wet minute we weren't alone
Then the rain stopped and we both went home

One umbrella, two strangers in the rain
Don't know your name and I won't again
But for three city blocks we were the same
Two lonely people the weather brought near

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Last night of summer and the streetlights glow
(Sha la la la la)
Got the whole gang here but tomorrow we go
(Sha la la la la)
My suitcase is packed by the front porch door
Won't be a kid on this block anymore

Oh, one more dance, one more song
(One more night)
Before the summer's gone
(Hold me tight)
We'll never be this young again, my dear
So hold my hand while the stars are still here

Mary's leaving Monday and Johnny leaves Tuesday too
(Sha la la la la)
We carved our names in the old oak, me and you
(Sha la la la la)
The radio's playing our favorite tune
Underneath that big September moon

Where do the summers go when they go?
(Where do they go?)
Will you remember me, will you know?
(Will you know?)
We were golden, we were free
Now we're history

Oh, one more dance, one more song
(One more night)
Before the summer's gone
(Hold me tight)
We'll never be this young again, my dear
So hold my hand while the stars are still here

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The morning sun is soft on the square
The baker waves, the church bells in the air
I fold my last goodbye into my hand
And walk away from this familiar land

The river that I knew since I was small
Reflects the boy who heard the city call
The sleepy little streets, the painted door
I won't be coming back here anymore

So long, little town, so long
You gave me every word inside my song
The samba of the leaving and the free
My quiet heart is sailing out to sea

The café where we used to sit and dream
The slow and golden afternoon, it seems
Is fading like a photograph in light
As I step softly toward the coming night

No bitter tears, no anger in my chest
Just gratitude for all the town gave best
A gentle wave, a smile, a soft adieu
The wide and open world is calling true

So long, little town, so long
You gave me every word inside my song
The samba of the leaving and the free
My quiet heart is sailing out to sea

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The record's spinning slow tonight
The kitchen floor, our old spotlight
Fifty years in these two hands
Still remember how we'd stand
Ooh, sha-la-la, the same old song
We swayed to all along

One more dance, my darling dear
Before the morning takes you near
Hold me close, don't let me go
Ooh-wah-ooh, just one more slow

Your hair went silver, mine went gray
We danced through every bill we'd pay
Through the babies, through the rain
Through the joy and through the pain
Sha-boom, my love, you're still the one
Beneath this setting sun

One more dance, my darling dear
Before the morning takes you near
Hold me close, don't let me go
Ooh-wah-ooh, just one more slow

If the music ever ends
I'll still hear it 'round the bends
Every step you ever made
Is the prettiest sound I've played

One more dance, before we part
You're the rhythm in my heart
Hold me close, sha-la-la-lay
Ooh-wah-ooh, don't fade away

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Five a.m. the alarm is screaming
Clock in bleeding, no time for dreaming
Second shift before the first one ends
Gas tank empty, breaking again
Punch the card and feed the machine
Nothing left in the in-between

I work, I bleed, I never gain
Two jobs deep and drowned in chains
The numbers rise, my back gives way
Still falling behind, still falling behind

Rent went up while the wage stood still
Collectors calling, eat your fill
Callused hands on a steering wheel
Grinding teeth on a rotten deal
Overtime is a broken promise
Sweat and steel and nothing honest

I work, I bleed, I never gain
Two jobs deep and drowned in chains
The numbers rise, my back gives way
Still falling behind, still falling behind

Tell me where the bottom is
I'll dig until I find it
A man can only break so far
Before the silence binds it

I work, I bleed, I never gain
Two jobs deep and drowned in chains
The system feeds, I am the prey
Still falling behind, still falling behind

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Woke up to the same light, different weight
Coffee going cold on a paper plate
Black suit on the chair where I left it last night
Dust in the sunbeam, slow and quiet
Phone full of names I don't wanna answer
Grief moves soft, it ain't no dancer

Morning came anyway, morning came anyway
Sun don't ask permission to rise on a gray day
I'm still here, you're still gone
Learning how to carry on

Folded up the casserole, can't eat
Neighbors' flowers wilting on the seat
Clock keeps ticking like it don't know
Left my keys in the door, real slow
Everybody's normal, that's the strange part
World keeps spinning with this hole in my heart

Morning came anyway, morning came anyway
Sun don't ask permission to rise on a gray day
I'm still here, you're still gone
Learning how to carry on

Maybe tomorrow I'll wash the cup
Maybe tomorrow I'll get back up
For now I'll sit in the quiet hum
And let the morning come

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Tether snapped at oh-nine-hundred
Your voice in my helmet, static and thunder
We trained for years to share one sky
Now I watch you shrink against the night
No gravity to hold us down
Just two specks and no more ground

We're drifting, drifting, can't reach your hand
No up, no down, no solid land
Scream into the vacuum, nobody hears
Drifting apart through the endless years

Oxygen's a countdown clock
Your silhouette against the dark
We said forever like fools in love
Now there's nothing left to push against
I reach but space don't care a bit
The void just opens, swallows it

We're drifting, drifting, can't reach your hand
No up, no down, no solid land
Scream into the vacuum, nobody hears
Drifting apart through the endless years

One last burn, one last try
But the math don't lie, the math don't lie
Let go the line, let go the past
Some orbits aren't built to last

We're drifting, drifting, gone for good
Couldn't fix it even if we could
Wave goodbye through the frozen glass
Two dead stars and a drifting past

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Boots on the same street, different man
Flag on the porch, blood on my hands
They threw a parade I didn't want
Clapping for a ghost up front
Mom made my room just like before
But I don't fit through that door

I came home, but home moved on
The kid they sent is dead and gone
Same four walls, a stranger's bed
The war's still loud inside my head

Fourth of July sounds like a round
I hit the floor at the smallest sound
Buddy of mine ain't coming back
Folded a flag, that's all they pack
Thank you for your service, they all say
Then they look the other way

I came home, but home moved on
The kid they sent is dead and gone
Same four walls, a stranger's bed
The war's still loud inside my head

Peel the uniform off my skin
There's a whole other war within
No medal for the nights I fight
Just me and the ceiling 'til the light

I came home, but home's a lie
A hollow word, a thousand-yard sky
Lay me down in my childhood bed
The war's still loud inside my head

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five bucks a game, step right up, my friend
Clock is ticking, watch your bishop end
I been playing here since '93
The pigeons know my strategy
Pick up the pace, don't think too long
Slap that clock and sing my song

King me, sting me, take your shot
I'll have your queen before you're hot
Five on the board and a smile on me
Welcome to the park, you owe a fee

Wall Street suits think they're so slick
I fork the rook and watch 'em tick
Blitz it fast, I never blink
Give 'em coffee, watch 'em sink
The board's my office, the bench my throne
Best seat in the city that I own

King me, sting me, take your shot
I'll have your queen before you're hot
Five on the board and a smile on me
Welcome to the park, you owe a fee

Pick up, horns up, skank in time
Every gambit's got a rhyme
Win or lose I'll shake your hand
Then take your wallet, understand?

King me, sting me, checkmate, son
Game's over, paid in one
Rack 'em up, who's next in line?
The park is mine, the park is mine

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Flour on the counter, dawn through the lace
Butter in a skillet, warmth on my face
She hummed an old hymn I never could name
While the bread rose slow in the iron pan
Cinnamon and woodsmoke, coffee on the stove
That little yellow kitchen was the whole world I'd know

Oh, I'd give every dollar that I've made
To stand in that doorway in the cool of the shade
And breathe in the morning, the cloves and the cream
In my grandmother's kitchen in a half-remembered dream

Apples on the windowsill, jars on the shelf
She'd slip me a biscuit when I'd help her myself
Her apron smelled of garden and rain
Her hands knew the recipe without any name
The radio crackled an old country tune
While the light grew gold in the afternoon

Oh, I'd give every dollar that I've made
To stand in that doorway in the cool of the shade
And breathe in the morning, the cloves and the cream
In my grandmother's kitchen in a half-remembered dream

They sold the house, they cleared the land
But I carry that scent in the cup of my hand
And sometimes a stranger will bake something sweet
And I'm six years old again, swinging my feet

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Sealed in the hold, don't open the crate (no)
Captain told us, man, it's already too late
Thirty days at sea and the compass spin
Something breathing in the dark, let it in
Gold on the manifest, blood on the deck
Every man who touched it got a curse on his neck
Waves get higher, the moon turn red
Half the crew already talkin' to the dead

Cursed cargo, ridin' low, ridin' low
We can't dock and we can't let it go
Devil in the freight, sea won't let us pass
Long voyage, prayin' that we make it past

Night watch whispers that the crate said his name
Morning come, his bunk empty, ain't the same
We throwin' salt but the salt turn to ash
Wind died down and the silence hit fast
I signed up for money, didn't sign for this
Every knot we tie just tightens the abyss

Cursed cargo, ridin' low, ridin' low
We can't dock and we can't let it go
Devil in the freight, sea won't let us pass
Long voyage, prayin' that we make it past

Throw it overboard, watch it float right back
Followin' the hull, leavin' a black track
No port gonna take us, no shore in sight
Just me and the cargo and the endless night

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
I didn't know a soul at table nine
Third glass of someone else's wine
The band played something I half-knew
Then the lights went low and there was you
You held your hand out, said let's pretend
We've got the whole song, we've got no end

So we danced like nobody knew our names
Two strangers caught in the same warm flame
No tomorrow, no who-we-are
Just a borrowed song and a falling star

You told me lies about your life
A fake career, a made-up wife
We laughed too loud at the back of the floor
While the bride and groom went out the door
The DJ packed his speakers down
And we kept spinning 'round and 'round

So we danced like nobody knew our names
Two strangers caught in the same warm flame
No tomorrow, no who-we-are
Just a borrowed song and a falling star

They turned the houselights up too soon
Your face went pale beneath the moon
You kissed my cheek, you slipped away
Never asked your name, and that's okay

But we danced like nobody knew our names
Two strangers caught in the same warm flame
Somewhere out there you're a falling star
And I still hum that song, wherever you are

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors close slow, the car's too packed
Heartbeat racin' down a one-way track
Fluorescent buzz and the tunnel dark
Can't catch a breath, can't make a mark
Everybody's faces start to blur
Hands go numb, the world's a slur

Just breathe, just breathe, count the stops
Four more stations 'til it stops
I'm okay, I'm okay, it's a wave
It'll pass, it'll pass, just be brave

Grip the pole like it's holdin' me here
Sweat on my collar, swallowin' fear
Nobody knows that I'm comin' apart
Quiet little earthquake inside my heart
Reflection in the glass don't look like me
Count the tiles 'til I'm finally free

Just breathe, just breathe, count the stops
Four more stations 'til it stops
I'm okay, I'm okay, it's a wave
It'll pass, it'll pass, just be brave

Ground beneath my feet, name five things I see
A backpack, a poster, the door, the floor, and me
The brakes start to hiss, the platform appears
I step into the air and I let go of the fears

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The lamp burns low, the city sleeps
My coffee's cold, the silence creeps
A mountain of pages, ink and dread
The clock strikes two inside my head
Every word a heavy step
A dance with all the promises I kept

Dance with me, midnight, one more page
Lead me slowly through this cage
Hold the rhythm, hold the line
Just the lamp, the books, and time

My pen, my partner, sharp and slow
We turn together, to and fro
The morning's coming like a debt
But I'm not finished, not done yet
Every sentence, a careful glide
With only ambition at my side

Dance with me, midnight, one more page
Lead me slowly through this cage
Hold the rhythm, hold the line
Just the lamp, the books, and time

The dawn will judge me when it comes
The quiet exam, the steady drums
But here at two I am alone
In this fierce and lonely zone

Dance with me, midnight, til I'm through
One more chapter, see it through
When the sun climbs up the wall
I'll have answered, after all

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They called our names, put our hands in a knot
The one I can't stand, the one I forgot
Music started up, no place to run
My oldest enemy, and we're number one
Your eyes said war, mine said the same
But the spotlight don't know either name

So we move, oh we move, against our will
Two proud hearts and a bitter pill
But the rhythm don't care who's wrong or right
We gotta dance together tonight

Every step a little surrender
You spun me slow, I felt you tremble
All those years we spent at odds
Melting down beneath the lights
I hate to say your hand fit mine
Like we were drawn on the same line

So we move, oh we move, against our will
Two proud hearts and a bitter pill
But the rhythm don't care who's wrong or right
We gotta dance together tonight

Maybe the music knows something we don't
Maybe forgiveness is a step we won't
But for three sweet minutes the feud stood still
And I almost, almost, lost my will

So we moved, oh we moved, and the crowd went wild
Two old rivals reconciled
When the song let go we stepped apart
But you took a little piece of my stubborn heart

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole life on a paper scroll
A name, a house, a borrowed role
Mama smiling at a man I don't know
A future picked out long ago
But something in my chest won't sign
This pretty life that isn't mine

So I'm walking out the door they built
Leaving all the gold and the guilt
I don't know where this road will lead
But it's mine, it's mine, it's mine to need

The dowry sits there, polished bright
All my no's I swallowed every night
I love them, God, I love them still
But love ain't supposed to bend your will
The veil they sewed don't fit my face
I need my own uncertain space

So I'm walking out the door they built
Leaving all the gold and the guilt
I don't know where this road will lead
But it's mine, it's mine, it's mine to need

Maybe one day they'll understand
Why I let go of the planned-out hand
Maybe they won't, and I'll still grieve
But a caged-up bird has gotta leave

So I'm walking, finally walking free
Into a life that's only me
No map, no name they wrote in stone
Just the quiet brave of going alone

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I burn for you like the noonday sun
And you don't know what you've begun
My hands clap loud, my heart claps louder
You pass me by and I grow prouder
Every night I dance alone
My passion turning into stone

Ay, you'll never love me, never love me so
But I'll dance this fire 'til the embers go
Stamp my feet against the floor
For a love that walks right out the door

The guitar weeps the words I hide
You are the ocean, I'm the tide
Pulled to a shore that won't hold me back
I give you crimson, you give me black
My ruffles spin, my sorrow sings
This is the cruelest of all things

Ay, you'll never love me, never love me so
But I'll dance this fire 'til the embers go
Stamp my feet against the floor
For a love that walks right out the door

Let them watch me bleed in red
Let them hear the things unsaid
My heels will speak what lips can't say
A wound that dances, night and day

Ay, you'll never love me, and I'll still burn
A flame for a love that won't return
One last stamp, one last cry
I'll dance your name until I die

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Last strip of tape on the last cardboard square
Echo in a room that's suddenly bare
Nail holes in the wall where the pictures hung
Five years of a life, and now it's done
Keys on the counter, gonna leave 'em behind
Last walkthrough, leave the memories signed
Dust on the floor where the couch used to be
Funny how an empty room can hold so much me

Tape up the last box, turn off the light
Say goodbye to the place I called mine tonight
Everything I am in a stack by the door
This was home, but it ain't no more

Kitchen where we argued, hallway where we laughed
Window where I watched the seasons pass
Marker on the doorframe measurin' growth
Now it's just a wall and a moving-out oath
Sharpie on the boxes: FRAGILE, KITCHEN, BOOKS
Can't pack up the way this old place looks
One deep breath in the quiet and the gray
Then I lift the last box and I carry it away

They'll paint over us, rent it again
New names, new boxes, new where-they-been
But for a second I just stand and stare
At the ghost of a home that's still hangin' in the air

Tape up the last box, turn off the light
Say goodbye to the place I called mine tonight
Everything I am in a stack by the door
This was home, but it ain't no more

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
There's a story in the lines of her palm
Written soft as the rain on the moor
Every scar is a winter she weathered alone
Every callus a door she held sure

She could braid the wild barley to bread
She could mend what the storm tore apart
With a thimble and thread and a hymn in her head
She stitched all our names to her heart

Oh her hands, her hands, like the roots of the rowan
Holding fast to the family tree
Though the years took her dancing, the music kept going
In the hands that once cradled me

Now the rings hang loose on her fingers
And the kettle grows heavy to lift
But she reaches for mine like she did long ago
And I feel the old weight of the gift

When the candle burns low in the gloaming
And the last of the embers go grey
I will fold up these hands that she gave to my blood
And I'll carry her warmth where I stay

Oh her hands, her hands, like the roots of the rowan
Holding fast to the family tree
Though the years took her dancing, the music kept going
In the hands that once cradled me

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Under the buzzing red sign
The coffee runs black as the night
A city of strangers comes in from the cold
To sit in the merciless light

The waitress she knows every face
The trucker, the saint, and the thief
She pours out forgiveness in chipped china cups
And serves it with bread and with grief

Olé, the door never closes
Olé, the griddle stays warm
While the moon walks the gutter and counts out the hours
We gather to weather the storm
Olé, when the world turns its back on you
There's a stool and a plate and a name
In the diner that burns like a heart in the dark
We are all the same flame

The guitar of the dishwasher rattles
The clock has forgotten to chime
A lover writes sorry on damp paper napkins
And folds away one more goodbye

Dawn is a rumor, a blush on the glass
The last cigarette burns to its end
But nobody here is in any hurry to leave
The night is our oldest friend

Olé, the door never closes
Olé, the griddle stays warm
While the moon walks the gutter and counts out the hours
We gather to weather the storm

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Same dream comes a-knocking 'round three
The ceiling pulls back like a wave
And these two heavy boots that I drag through the day
Go light as the wind off the plains

I rise over wheat fields and water towers
The interstate thin as a thread
All the worries I packed in the bed of my truck
Fall away from the sky overhead

I'm flying, flying, no engine, no wing
Just the want in my chest pulling true
And I swear when I wake with the dust in my eyes
For a second I still think I do

My father he never went farther than town
He said dreaming was money you burn
But I figure the Lord gave us shoulders for more
Than to carry the weight and not turn

Maybe flying's just falling that forgot to be afraid
Maybe leaving's just loving the long way back home
But I'll take it, I'll take it, however it comes
Than to stand on the ground all alone

I'm flying, flying, no engine, no wing
Just the want in my chest pulling true
And I swear when I wake with the dust in my eyes
For a second I still think I do

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I let the church bells drown in static
I let the candles gutter out
Everything holy went dim and dramatic
A wash of white noise and of doubt

I prayed to a ceiling that swallowed the sound
I waited for something to break
But the silence just hummed like a feedback around
And I lost the whole shape of my ache

Then low, so low, like a guitar underwater
A light came up under the gray
Not a voice, not a sign, just a warmth in the noise
That refused to be drowned out away

I don't know what I call it these mornings
A tremble, a chord that won't end
It blurs at the edges, it carries no warnings
It just holds me again and again

Let it ring, let it ring, let it bleed through the haze
I'm done trying to name what I lost
If it sounds like forever dissolved in the reverb
Then I'll pay it whatever the cost

Then low, so low, like a guitar underwater
A light came up under the gray
Not a voice, not a sign, just a warmth in the noise
That refused to be drowned out away

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Empty lot on a Sunday morning
Frost still white on the hood
You grip the wheel like it's gonna fight back
And I tell you you're doing good

Ten and two, ease off the clutch now
The whole car lurches and stalls
And you laugh that laugh that you had as a baby
While I pray to the dashboard and walls

Go slow, go slow, little engine
The road's gonna stretch on for years
I'm just here for the start of it, riding the brake
With a pride that's too close to my tears

First gear, second, you're rolling
Down to the end of the lane
And I see in the mirror the day you won't need me
To sit in the passenger lane

There's a hundred goodbyes in a learner's permit
There's a leaving in every green light
But I'd teach you a thousand more turns of the wheel
Just to keep you in reach for tonight

Go slow, go slow, little engine
The road's gonna stretch on for years
I'm just here for the start of it, riding the brake
With a pride that's too close to my tears

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Pick up the rhythm, the clippers are humming
The pole's spinning red, white and blue
Every man in this town's got a seat with his name on it
Waiting his turn for the chew

He knows who's been cheating, who's quietly grieving
Who's getting married in spring
He trims off the gossip and tucks in the secrets
And never repeats a thing

Snip snap, take a seat in the chair
Lay your troubles down on the floor
Walk in a stranger, walk out a brother
He's the heart of the whole corner store
Snip snap, the radio's blasting
A little bit closer to clean
The barber, the barber, he knows the whole town
And he's seen everything in between

He shaped up my grandpa, he buzzed my first haircut
He'll likely outlast the whole street
With a mirror, a comb, and a Saturday horn section
Keeping the whole place on beat

When the shop finally closes and somebody else moves in
With their fancy and their fade
We'll remember the man who could cut you a story
And never once charged for the trade

Snip snap, take a seat in the chair
Lay your troubles down on the floor
Walk in a stranger, walk out a brother
He's the heart of the whole corner store

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Tear of the tape, it's the last one
Echo bounces off the bare wall
The room that held everything's nothing but corners
And light through the curtainless hall

Marker squeaks fragile across cardboard
Kitchen, then bedroom, then me
Five years compressed into stackable boxes
And a key I'm not allowed to keep

Seal it up, lift it up, carry it out
The pulse of the city says go
Everything's moving, the bassline keeps proving
There's nothing that stays, don't you know
Seal it up, run it down, load it all in
Let the rhythm dissolve into rain
The last box is taped and the door's standing open
And nothing here knows me by name

Dust where the sofa once anchored us
Ghost of a frame on the paint
I photograph nothing, I just stand there breathing
The quiet of all that it ain't

Faster now, faster, the engine is idling
The future's a flicker of speed
Don't look at the window, don't look at the garden
Just ride out the break, take the lead

Seal it up, lift it up, carry it out
The pulse of the city says go
Everything's moving, the bassline keeps proving
There's nothing that stays, don't you know

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Desk lamp low, the house is asleep
Highlighter bleeding through the page
Cold coffee ghost in a cup I forgot
And a clock that won't act its age

My phone's face down 'cause I know who's not texting
The city's a hum out the glass
It's just me and these pages and everything riding
On whether tomorrow I pass

Two in the morning, just me and the quiet
Nobody to see me try
But I'll learn it, I'll earn it, I'll carry it slow
Through the dark till the dawn comes by
Two in the morning, the world doesn't know it
But this is where I get made
In the hush and the hour nobody's watching
I'm laying the bricks that I laid

I could call it a night, let it slide, let it go
But the dream's gotta cost me a price
So I steady my breath and I open it back up
And I read the whole thing again twice

Nobody claps for the lonely and quiet
Nobody films the slow climb
But the future's just made of these nights stacked together
One lamplit and stubborn at a time

Two in the morning, just me and the quiet
Nobody to see me try
But I'll learn it, I'll earn it, I'll carry it slow
Through the dark till the dawn comes by

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
One suitcase, two passports, a photograph faded
My mother's address sewn inside
The harbor gets small as the engine starts pulling
And the country I knew starts to hide

Salt on the railing, a language I'm leaving
The gulls speak a goodbye I get
Everything I am fits in carry-on weight
And a debt I have not paid yet

Across the sea, across the sea
Where the new sky won't know my name
I'll plant my old roots in the unfamiliar
And grow toward a different rain
Across the sea, across the sea
Keep the home in my chest like a stone
A little bit lost and a little bit braver
In a world I'll have to call my own

First winter strange, the bread tastes different
The coins feel light in my hand
I write to them slow that I'm doing okay now
From a phone booth in nobody's land

Some nights I dream in the streets of my childhood
And wake to the hum of the train
Two halves of a heart on two sides of an ocean
Learning to beat through the strain

Across the sea, across the sea
Where the new sky won't know my name
I'll plant my old roots in the unfamiliar
And grow toward a different rain

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew its last on Friday
The gates chained up with a notice and a lock
Thirty years my father gave the line, now
The clock on the wall has forgotten to talk

They shipped the work to somewhere cheaper
Left the rust and the men with the skill
The parking lot's empty where the shift change once thundered
And the town holds its breath on the hill

But we rise up, rise up, we ain't broken yet
Though the smokestack stands cold in the rain
What they built with their hands, they can carry it onward
No company can lock up our name
Rise up, rise up, sing it loud through the valley
The factory falls but we stand
The heart of the people don't run on a paycheck
It runs on the strength of the land

Miss Johnson she's starting a garden
On the lot where the loading dock stood
And the young ones are talking of building anew
From the ashes of all that we could

They can take the machines and the orders
They can sell off the steel and the floor
But they cannot reposess the way we look out for each other
When the wolf comes a-knocking the door

But we rise up, rise up, we ain't broken yet
Though the smokestack stands cold in the rain
What they built with their hands, they can carry it onward
No company can lock up our name

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine's purring sweet outside the bank now
Gloves on tight and the radio low
They said one more run and I'm out of the racket
One more and I'm free to go, daddy-o

Got a girl in a town where they don't ask questions
Got a ring and a ticket out west
So I'm watching the door with my foot on the pedal
And a heart doing double its best

Come on boys, the meter is running
Three minutes, then we disappear
One last ride, then I'm hanging the keys up
And I'm kissing this life so long, my dear
Step on it, swing it, the saxophone's wailing
The city flies by in a blur
Last job, last job, then I'm gone like the morning
And nobody's faster than her

Here they come with the bags and the sirens
Drop the clutch and we're gone with the wind
Through the alley, the avenue, dodging the streetlights
A getaway grin on my chin

Funny thing about one-more-times, baby
They got a way of becoming your last
But I'll roll the dice on a clean horizon
And outrun the whole of my past

Come on boys, the meter is running
Three minutes, then we disappear
One last ride, then I'm hanging the keys up
And I'm kissing this life so long, my dear

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
He used to split the cordwood with one swing
He used to lift the world off of the ground
Now he calls me to open the jar on the table
And he laughs but he won't meet me down

His hands shake a little at breakfast
His stories run back more than on
The man who was tall as the barn in my childhood
Is soft as the light before dawn

Oh time, you old thief, you came slow as the river
And took him a grain at a time
Now I hold the door and I slow down my walking
For the father who once carried mine
Oh time, oh time, won't you ease on him gentle
Let him keep his old songs and his name
For the strongest of oaks in the field of my growing
Is bending at last to the rain

We drove out to look at the old place
Where the fences he built have gone gray
And he told me again how he cleared every acre
And I told him I'd do it his way

One day I'll be the tall one, the steady
And a child of my own holds my arm
So I'm learning the patience he's teaching me slowly
In the dusk of the family farm

Oh time, you old thief, you came slow as the river
And took him a grain at a time
Now I hold the door and I slow down my walking
For the father who once carried mine

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Twelve hours down and twelve to climb
The fluorescent hum, the bleeding clock
The whole world's home by a fire tonight
While I'm holding the line at the dock of the lost

Code in room nine, no time to feel it
A child's small hand wrapped around my thumb
The holiday lights blink red in the window
And I swallow the ache and I come

I am the wall that the darkness breaks on
I am the hand in the dead of the night
No cape, no glory, no song in my honor
Just scrubs and a war I will fight
Double shift burning, the monitors screaming
I'll stand till the morning comes 'round
I am the keeper of breath and of heartbeat
The last one to lay the dead down

My coffee's cold and my feet are on fire
My own kids asleep without me
But someone's mother will wake up tomorrow
Because I refused to break free

Let the bells ring out for the soldiers and saviors
They'll never once ring out for me
But I'll carry the weight of a thousand small mercies
Through the long holy dark, let it be

I am the wall that the darkness breaks on
I am the hand in the dead of the night
No cape, no glory, no song in my honor
Just scrubs and a war I will fight

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
The sky turns green and my blood runs faster
The sirens wail and the cattle run
While everyone flees I am flooring the engine
Straight into the eye of the gun

They beg me to stay, build a house, find a quiet
A wife and a porch and a name
But my heart only beats in the roar of the supercell
My home is the heart of the flame

Chase it, chase it, into the maelstrom
Where the wall cloud devours the plain
I was born for the thunder, I'll die in the funnel
I can't love what won't kill me again
Chase it, chase it, the wind is my lover
The lightning the only thing true
I tried to lie still but the calm nearly broke me
So I run where the monsters break through

The motel beds blur into highways
The radar's the only thing home
Every town is a maybe I'm gone from by morning
A chaser is meant to roam

Maybe somewhere there's peace in a stillness
Maybe somewhere a slower man
But the storm's in my marrow, it calls and I answer
I'll outrun the quiet while I can

Chase it, chase it, into the maelstrom
Where the wall cloud devours the plain
I was born for the thunder, I'll die in the funnel
I can't love what won't kill me again

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The building sleeps, just one window burning
A single lamp against the dark
The pages turn slow as the hours dissolving
And somewhere a lone dog barks

No one to witness the quiet endurance
The slow accumulation of will
Just the scratch of a pen and the weight of a future
Held up by a hand that's gone still

And it builds, and it builds, like a tide coming inward
From a whisper to a roar in the chest
Every silent surrender of sleep to the trying
Is a wave breaking slow on the crest

The coffee's gone cold and the eyes start to wander
The doubt creeps in soft as the frost
But I lift up my head and I find the next sentence
And I count what I'd lose if I lost

Let it rise, let it crash, let the whole quiet shatter
Into something too big for the room
The lonely and small and unwitnessed and patient
Will bloom into more than this gloom
Let it swell, let it break, let the morning come find me
Still here, still bent to the page
The whole world will wake to the work that I forged here
In the silence, alone, in the cage

And it builds, and it builds, like a tide coming inward
From a whisper to a roar in the chest
Every silent surrender of sleep to the trying
Is a wave breaking slow on the crest

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Gravel lot and a borrowed glove,
Numbers peeling off the back we love,
They laughed at us when the season starts,
Nine cracked benches and nine spare hearts.

Scoreboard buzzing like a tired bee,
Down by one in the bottom three,
Coach just whispers, "Boys, hold the line,"
Dust on our cleats turning gold in the shine.

Ooh, last out, last call,
Sha-na-na, catch it all,
We were nobody's chosen few,
But the sky stayed wide and the grass stayed blue.

Pitcher winds like the world might end,
I swing for every borrowed friend,
Leather cracks, oh the bleachers rise,
A whole tired town in our hand-me-down eyes.

Whether we win, whether we fall,
Nobody owns the dark or the wall,
We played the game they said we'd lose,
In squeaking shoes and a borrowed bruise.

Ooh, last out, last call,
Sha-na-na, catch it all,
We were nobody's chosen few,
But the night sang back and the night sang true.

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
His hands used to swallow mine whole,
Now I help him button up his coat,
The truck he could fix with a wink and a wire
Sits cold in the yard where the thistles grow tall.

He forgets where he put the keys,
Then remembers the dog from nineteen-eighty-three,
Laughs at a joke from a long-gone friend,
And I laugh too, so the laughing won't end.

Time took the loud out of his stride,
Left the kindness sitting there in his eyes,
I'm catching the man before he goes gray as the dawn,
Learning his face so I'll have it when he's gone.

We drive the back road past the mill,
He points to the church on the buttonwood hill,
"Married your mama right there," he'll say,
Though he told me that one already today.

Funny how the strong ones lean,
How the river runs slow when it's known where it's been,
I'll hold his arm down the gravel and stone,
The boy now the road, and the father the home.

Time took the loud out of his stride,
Left the kindness sitting there in his eyes,
I'm catching the man before he goes gray as the dawn,
Learning his face so I'll have it when he's gone.

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Mud on the canvas, glitter on the skin,
Last of the batteries flickering thin,
The field is a heartbeat we borrowed for free,
One more sunrise owed to you and me.

Don't let the speakers go quiet,
Hold the bass like a pulse in the dark,
We burn the last hour like a riot,
Dawn's a rumor, we're still in the spark.

Strangers turned family under one strobe,
Names we'll forget but the feeling won't go,
Tents folding down like a closing eye,
We dance on the seam where the night meets the sky.

Go — feel it run, low and fast,
Hands to the haze, make the moment last,
Every kick is a clock we refuse to read,
Every breath is a city we planted as seed.

When the gates open and spill us back home,
We'll carry this hum in the marrow and bone,
A loop in the blood that the morning can't break,
The last night a thing we'll forever retake.

Don't let the speakers go quiet,
Hold the bass like a pulse in the dark,
We burn the last hour like a riot,
Dawn's a rumor, we're still in the spark.

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Tinsel on the nurses' station glows,
Vending-machine coffee, the long hall's froze,
My shift folded over itself like a wave,
Sixteen hours of the small and the brave.

And the monitors hum a lullaby blue,
While somewhere a family is missing me too,
But room twelve is breathing, and that's holy enough,
A holiday made out of cheap paper cuffs.

A grandmother hums an old carol off-key,
I hold her cool hand till the morphine sets free,
The city outside wears its window-light gold,
In here we just trade the warm for the cold.

So pour me the dark of a four-a.m. sky,
I'll dance with the gurney, I'll waltz with the IV,
There's grace in the graveyard, a swing in the strain,
A hymn in the beep of the ones who remain.

And the monitors hum a lullaby blue,
While somewhere a family is missing me too,
But room twelve is breathing, and that's holy enough,
A holiday made out of cheap paper cuffs.

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
Apagué las velas, dejé de rezar,
The church bells rang but I couldn't stay,
Nights got loud and the prayers got thin,
I traded the light for the noise within.

Pero vuelve, vuelve like the tide on the shore,
Something in the dark knocking soft on my door,
Dale, dale, I was lost in the gray,
But the fire I buried came back today.

A stranger on the corner shared his bread,
No sermon, just a hand where the hunger was fed,
I felt it move, a low quiet drum,
The God I had cursed in the warmth he'd become.

Baila, baila, with the doubt in my chest,
The heart is a barrio, never at rest,
I don't need the steeple, the gold, or the throne,
Just the proof that I'm walking and never alone.

From the bottom I called and the bottom called back,
Luz en la grieta where the world had gone black,
No tengo respuestas, solo this beat,
Fe in my ribs and the ground at my feet.

Pero vuelve, vuelve like the tide on the shore,
Something in the dark knocking soft on my door,
Dale, dale, I was lost in the gray,
But the fire I buried came back today.

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Clock says midnight, my feet say more,
I've danced this linoleum corridor floor,
Two shifts buttoned up in one starched white,
King of the ward on a holiday night.

So swing me, ward eleven, swing me slow,
There's punch in the breakroom and nowhere to go,
I'll tango the trays and I'll jitterbug pills,
Love on a holiday, paying the bills.

Mrs. Delgado wants one more song,
I hum her a tune and I shuffle along,
The pager's a trumpet, the gurney's a band,
And grace keeps the beat in the squeeze of a hand.

My own table's empty, the gravy's gone cold,
But here's a whole houseful of frightened and old,
So cut me a rug down the fluorescent mile,
I'll punch the next double with a crooked half-smile.

So swing me, ward eleven, swing me slow,
There's punch in the breakroom and nowhere to go,
I'll tango the trays and I'll jitterbug pills,
Love on a holiday, paying the bills.

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors slid shut on the downtown line,
The walls leaned in and they swallowed the time,
My heart kicked loud like a runaway drum,
And the bright tunnel-dark told me trouble would come.

Breathe, oh-oh, count the rail's little song,
Sha-doo, hold on, it won't be long,
The station's a star at the end of the black,
Just breathe, oh-oh, and the day will come back.

Hands wouldn't listen, my knees turned to foam,
A car full of strangers, and none of them home,
The map on the wall was a tangle of veins,
And I rattled along with the runaway trains.

Then a kid with a grin offered half of his seat,
And the floor found my shoes and my shoes found the beat,
The rumble grew gentle, the lights stopped their hum,
And I rode the steel river till the fear was done.

Breathe, oh-oh, count the rail's little song,
Sha-doo, hold on, it won't be long,
The station's a star at the end of the black,
Just breathe, oh-oh, and the day will come back.

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Lamp swinging low in the back of the bar,
Five men leaning over the green felt scar,
Started for matches, then it started for pay,
Then it started for things you can't give away.

Oh, lay it down, brother, lay it down slow,
Some debts grow teeth that you don't wanna know,
The deck don't forgive and the deck don't forget,
Walk away easy 'fore you owe more than you get.

Gold watch gone and the deed's on the pile,
Duppy in the corner with a crocodile smile,
Every hand he raised it, I followed him in,
Chasing a sunrise that I couldn't win.

Mama said gambling's a thirst with no cup,
The more that you pour, the more empties you sup,
Now the rooster a-crow and my pockets are bare,
I bet my whole name on a king that wasn't there.

Oh, lay it down, brother, lay it down slow,
Some debts grow teeth that you don't wanna know,
The deck don't forgive and the deck don't forget,
Walk away easy 'fore you owe more than you get.

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night when the city sleeps,
My shoulders grow what the daylight keeps,
Rooftops shrink to a string of beads,
