And I'm chasing the moon over Lagos seas.

I dey fly, fly, no rope fit hold me,
Wind for my back like my mama told me,
Up where the trouble can't follow me there,
I dey fly, fly, free as the air.

The market noise turn to a hum below,
The red dust roads where I used to go,
My worries drop like a heavy coat,
And the sky open wide like an answered note.

Then morning come knock with the rooster's call,
Gravity catch me before I fall,
But I keep the wings folded under my chest,
And I wait for the night to give back the rest.

I dey fly, fly, no rope fit hold me,
Wind for my back like my mama told me,
Up where the trouble can't follow me there,
I dey fly, fly, free as the air.

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon buzzing in the two-a.m. rain,
Coffee black as the freight-yard train,
Booths full of insomniacs and ghosts,
The griddle keeps time for the sleepless coast.

We don't close, we don't close,
While the rest of the city decomposes,
Run the lights, run the beat through the glass,
Every lonely hour we let it pass.

Trucker, dreamer, the heartbroke, the wired,
The waitress who's somehow never tired,
Pie under glass like a small lit moon,
And the jukebox bleaking a low broken tune.

Faster — the kettle, the clatter, the chase,
The whole night humming in this one bright place,
Bass in the booths where the lost ones meet,
Dawn just a stranger out there on the street.

When the sun comes up we'll still be here,
Serving the brave and the disappeared,
No lock on the door, no end to the song,
Just the all-night glow keeping everyone strong.

We don't close, we don't close,
While the rest of the city decomposes,
Run the lights, run the beat through the glass,
Every lonely hour we let it pass.

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Last tank of gas at the Sunoco sign,
Water tower shrinking in the rearview line,
I packed every almost into one cardboard box,
Left the key on the table, didn't bother the locks.

I'm going, I'm going, where the streetlights don't know my name,
Where nobody measures me by who I became,
Feel the highway open like a door swinging wide,
Let the bass carry everything I left inside.

The diner, the bleachers, the one stoplight,
The rumor that follows you home every night,
I loved it, I hated it, I memorized the dust,
Now the engine's a heartbeat and the road is a must.

Goodbye to the river, goodbye to the field,
Goodbye to the version of me that they sealed,
The city's a frequency calling me home,
To a self I can finally call my own.

I'm going, I'm going, where the streetlights don't know my name,
Where nobody measures me by who I became,
Feel the highway open like a door swinging wide,
Let the bass carry everything I left inside.

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Old man Ruiz with a chalk behind his ear,
Been patching this neighborhood for forty-some years,
A needle for the preacher, a hem for the bride,
He knows every shoulder and every man's pride.

He'll stitch you together when you're coming apart,
Measure your sorrow and tailor your heart,
From the funeral suit to the prom-night frock,
There's a little of Ruiz on the whole of the block.

The banker, the busboy, they all climb his stair,
Leave taller and straighter than when they got there,
He lets the boy pay him in apples and time,
Sends the widow home pressed without taking a dime.

His hands are a map of the lives that he's mended,
The seams of a street that would've unended,
When he finally hangs up that worn measuring tape,
The whole block will wear it, this town he reshaped.

He'll stitch you together when you're coming apart,
Measure your sorrow and tailor your heart,
From the funeral suit to the prom-night frock,
There's a little of Ruiz on the whole of the block.

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The lamp makes an island of gold on the page,
The rest of the room is a soft purple haze,
Highlighter humming a fluorescent hymn,
The whole sleeping building gone watery and dim.

Two a.m., and the world is a blur,
My thoughts drift like snow, like the things I'm not sure,
Coffee gone cold and the future so far,
Just me and the hum and the rain on the car.

Words start to swim like fish in the dark,
The clock is a moth, and the moth is a spark,
I underline sentences I'll never recall,
While the radiator ticks down the hall.

Somewhere a streetlight is keeping me company,
Somewhere the morning is learning to be,
I'm a small lit window in a sleeping town,
Holding the quiet before it comes down.

Two a.m., and the world is a blur,
My thoughts drift like snow, like the things I'm not sure,
Coffee gone cold and the future so far,
Just me and the hum and the rain on the car.

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Got off the bus where the depot still stands,
Dust on my boots and a war in my hands,
The maple's grown tall and the porch leans low,
And the dog don't bark at the man they don't know.

Lord, I'm home, but home moved on,
The house is the same but the boy is gone,
I carry a country no map ever drew,
In the dark behind my eyes, every night, breaking through.

Mama set a plate that I couldn't quite eat,
Fireworks pop and I'm down in the street,
She holds my shaking like she held it before,
But her boy left something out there in the war.

The river still runs where we swam as kids,
It don't know the things that these two hands did,
So I sit on the bank and I let the cold flow,
And I pray it can wash what the daylight won't show.

Lord, I'm home, but home moved on,
The house is the same but the boy is gone,
I carry a country no map ever drew,
In the dark behind my eyes, every night, breaking through.

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Luces de la disco still spinning in my head,
The last thing you said and the things left unsaid,
We had the whole summer like a coin in my hand,
Then you slipped through my fingers like the tide through the sand.

La que se me fue, the one I let go,
I hear your name in every radio,
Dale, baby, in another life maybe,
But tonight you're a ghost on the floor dancing slowly with me.

We were fire on the rooftop, gasoline and rum,
I thought we had forever, I was young and dumb,
Now I scroll through the photos at the edge of the bed,
With the bachata of all of the words I never said.

Maybe in the barrio, maybe by the sea,
There's a version of us that was meant to be,
But the song always ends and the lights always rise,
And I'm dancing alone with the moon in your eyes.

La que se me fue, the one I let go,
I hear your name in every radio,
Dale, baby, in another life maybe,
But tonight you're a ghost on the floor dancing slowly with me.

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Five a.m. the alarm, otra vez de pie
Clock in at the warehouse, gotta move the freight
Noon I'm in the kitchen, grease up on my shirt
Counting every dollar but the dollar never works
Rent went up again, la luz, el internet
Two jobs in my pocket and I'm still in debt
Mami says descansa but I cannot stop
The more that I am running, more I'm at the bottom

Dos trabajos, no money, dale
Running every day but I'm falling, dale
Sweat on my face, never stalling, dale
Still behind, still behind, oh
Dos trabajos, no money, dale
Give it all I got and I'm crawling, dale
God can you hear me when I'm calling, dale
Still behind, still behind, oh

Overtime, no time, my body's a machine
Tired in my bones but the bills don't let me dream
Bus is running late and the boss don't wanna hear
Another week is gone and the future ain't clear

Uno, dos, the hours disappear
Work until I break but I'm staying right here
For my familia I keep the fire alive
Falling behind but I refuse to die

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Soft on the dial at a quarter past two
A whisper of static, a melody true
I broadcast from rooftops the city forgot
With a hum and a microphone, that's all I've got
The frequency drifts like a boat on the bay
Forbidden and gentle, I'm sailing away

Oh, pirate of sound, tuning low
Singing to the night where the lonely ones go
No license, no permission, just the breeze and the sea
This little stolen station belongs to you and me

The samba slips quiet through wires in the dark
A bossa for lovers in cars in the park
They can't trace the signal, it floats like the foam
A secret of starlight that carries you home

If the towers come hunting, I'll vanish like rain
Then find a new corner and start it again
For music wants freedom, it cannot be owned
So I drift through the airwaves, beautifully alone

Oh, pirate of sound, tuning low
Singing to the night where the lonely ones go
No license, no permission, just the breeze and the sea
This little stolen station belongs to you and me

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Under the concrete a heartbeat is slow
We dance on the shoulders of something below
Towers of glass where the old stories sleep
A giant is breathing, his dreaming runs deep
Mama told me child, don't stamp on the ground
The one who is sleeping can wake to the sound

Eh, the city on the giant, oya move your body
We dance, we dance till the morning, everybody
He sleeps and we shine, oh, light up the sky
Don't wake him up now, just let the night ride
Eh, the city on the giant, oya move your body
We dance, we dance, we dance, everybody

The markets are loud and the lights never die
A million small footsteps, a lullaby high
We build and we build but we never look down
The giant turns slowly beneath the whole town

One day he will rise and the rivers will run
The rooftops will tremble, the day will be done
But tonight we keep dancing, we keep him asleep
With rhythm so gentle, with secrets we keep

Eh, the city on the giant, oya move your body
We dance, we dance till the morning, everybody
He sleeps and we shine, oh, light up the sky
Don't wake him up now, just let the night ride

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon buzzing red at the edge of the world
Coffee gone cold and the booths all unfurled
Three in the morning and nobody sleeps
This is where the broken and the restless all meet
Grease on the griddle, a knife and a fork
The city's gone silent but the diner still talks

WE NEVER CLOSE! WE NEVER CLOSE!
The lights stay on for the ones nobody knows
WE NEVER CLOSE! WE NEVER CLOSE!
Pour the last cup for the road, here we go!

The trucker, the drunk, and the runaway kid
All find a table for the things that they did
No judgment, no clock, just the hum of the fan
A refuge of chrome where the lonely ones stand

When the whole damn town turns its back on you
There's a light in the window that's burning still true
Sit down, eat up, you're not alone
This broken little diner is everybody's home

WE NEVER CLOSE! WE NEVER CLOSE!
The lights stay on for the ones nobody knows
WE NEVER CLOSE! WE NEVER CLOSE!
Pour the last cup for the road, here we go!

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Fourth quarter, down by ten, clock is bleeding out
Nobody believed us, that's what they wrote about
From the bottom of the league to the brightest of lights
We practiced in the dark for these forty-eight minutes tonight
Coach said leave it all out on the floor
We been counted out before, we don't count no more
Every missed shot, every loss, every doubt
Led us right here, no time for no doubt

This is it, the final whistle blows
Underdogs rising up, everybody knows
Leave it all, leave it all, on the line
Write our names in the lights one time
This is it, no tomorrow, no maybe
We play for the city, we play for the brave, see
Leave it all, leave it all, on the line
Write our names in the lights one time

Sweat on the hardwood, hearts beating loud
Momma in the stands screaming proud in the crowd
One possession left, give the ball to me
This the moment that defines our destiny

They said we'd never make it this far
Now the buzzer's about to tell us who we are
No fear in my chest, just the love of the game
Win or lose tonight, we'll never be the same

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Caught your eye across the floor, table number nine
Neither of us know the bride, but baby that's just fine
The band hit a groove and you reached for my hand
Now we're moving like we planned it, like a one-night band
Get down, get down, the horns are blowing sweet
Two strangers and a rhythm and a beat beneath our feet

Dance with me, stranger, under the lights
We don't need a reason, we just need tonight
Shake it to the left, baby, shake it to the right
Two hearts and a horn section feeling so right
Dance with me, stranger, the groove is alive
We'll forget our names but remember the vibe

Don't tell me your last name, just keep it on the beat
The bassline got us tangled, got me weak up in my feet
Uncle's doing the worm and the cake is half gone
But you and me, we're locked in till the very last song

Maybe we won't meet again when the morning comes
But tonight we own the dance floor and the rhythm of the drums
So spin me one more time before the lights come up
Two strangers, one groove, baby, that's enough

Dance with me, stranger, under the lights
We don't need a reason, we just need tonight
Shake it to the left, baby, shake it to the right
Two hearts and a horn section feeling so right

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
We sat at the table when the sun went down
Five friends and a deck and a stack on the ground
It started for fun, just the laughter and the beer
But the money kept rising and it changed the air in here
Oga raised the stakes and the room went cold
Now the game has a hunger and it won't let go

We played too far, oh, we played too far
No turning back now, no matter who you are
One more hand, one more hand, na so it dey go
We played too far, eh, we reaped what we sow
We played too far, oh, we played too far
The cards don't lie, oh, see who we are

My brother put his car on a queen and a jack
The table held its breath, there was no going back
The fan kept spinning but nobody could breathe
When the cards hit the wood, somebody had to leave

We came as friends but we left as ghosts
The ones who lost the most lost the ones they loved the most
Let this be a lesson when the night gets long
A game is just a game till the game goes wrong

We played too far, oh, we played too far
No turning back now, no matter who you are
One more hand, one more hand, na so it dey go
We played too far, eh, we reaped what we sow

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Hop in the front seat, baby, buckle up tight
Hands on the wheel now, ten and two, that's right
You're shaking like a leaf but your eyes are so bright
Daddy's gonna teach you how to drive tonight
Ease off the brake, let her roll real slow
Down the empty parking lot, away we go

Drive, baby, drive, keep it between the lines
We got nowhere to be and we got all the time
You'll stall and you'll stutter but you'll get it just fine
Drive, baby, drive, you're a daughter of mine

You clipped a little curb and you started to cry
I laughed and I told you that's how all of us try
My old man taught me on a road just like this
Now I'm passing it down with a wave and a kiss

Soon you'll be gone down a highway alone
Miles between us and a long way from home
But I'll always remember you here by my side
The first time I taught my little girl to drive

Drive, baby, drive, keep it between the lines
We got nowhere to be and we got all the time
You'll stall and you'll stutter but you'll get it just fine
Drive, baby, drive, you're a daughter of mine

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I wrote you a letter but you'll never read
A garden of words for a seed you won't see
Years that I carried the weight of your name
Now I'm laying it down at the foot of your grave
Mama said anger is a stone in the chest
So I'm setting it down, I am laying it to rest

I forgive you, even though you're gone
I forgive you, so I can carry on
The stars hold your name where the river runs free
I forgive you, oh, now you can set me free
I forgive you, even though you're gone
Let the wind take the hurt where it belongs

The things that you broke I can't fix anymore
But I won't let the bitterness live at my door
I talk to your shadow when the moon is up high
And I whisper goodbye with a tear in my eye

Forgiveness no be for you, e be for me
So my own heart fit finally be free
Wherever you dey now, may you find your rest
I release you with love, oh, I've done my best

I forgive you, even though you're gone
I forgive you, so I can carry on
The stars hold your name where the river runs free
I forgive you, oh, now you can set me free

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Sube a la camioneta, mija, no tengas temor
With the trumpets in my heart and a father's love
Las manos en el volante, hold it steady and slow
The whole pueblo is watching as away we go
Tu abuelo me enseñó on a dusty old road
Now I pass it to you, all the love that I hold

Maneja, mija, maneja, with your head held high
Under the wide open Mexican sky
Ay, ay, ay, you're learning to fly
Maneja, mija, with a tear in my eye

You pressed on the gas and we jumped like a goat
The rosary swinging, I grabbed at my coat
Pero te reíste, and your laugh filled the air
Mi corazón bursting, with the wind in your hair

Un día the road will take you far from my side
A woman so brave on a long, lonely ride
Pero siempre recuerda este día and this song
The day that I taught you to drive, to be strong

Maneja, mija, maneja, with your head held high
Under the wide open Mexican sky
Ay, ay, ay, you're learning to fly
Maneja, mija, with a tear in my eye

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We set our sails at the break of the dawn
With a hold full of cargo that we should have left alone
The captain made a bargain that he never should have made
Now the ocean's growing angry and the crew is sore afraid
Lord, have mercy on these sinful souls
We carry a curse where the dark water rolls

Oh, deliver us, deliver us, Lord
Cast the curse from our cargo, throw it overboard
We are sinking, we are sinking in the deep
Won't you save these weary sailors that You keep
Oh, deliver us, deliver us, Lord
Let Your mercy be the lighthouse on the shore

The storm came rolling on the seventh night
The lanterns all flickered and went out their light
We fell to our knees on the trembling deck
And we cried out to heaven from the coming wreck

Then a voice on the water said lay down your greed
Throw the cursed thing over and I'll give you what you need
So we cast it to the waves and the sea went calm
And we sailed into the morning with a healing balm

Oh, deliver us, deliver us, Lord
Cast the curse from our cargo, throw it overboard
Now we're singing, now we're singing, we are free
Glory hallelujah on the calming sea

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Down in the holler by the old mill stream
Four men sat playing in the lantern's gleam
Whiskey on the table and the cards held tight
They didn't mean to gamble all their lives away that night
The fiddle's gone quiet, the banjo's gone still
When the stakes got higher than a man could fill

They played too far, Lord, they played too long
Dealt themselves a hand that was bound to go wrong
Lay your money down, boys, but mind what you sow
They played too far and they reaped the woe
They played too far, Lord, they played too long
Now it's nothing but a sad old bluegrass song

Old Joe wagered the deed to his land
The whole farm riding on a four-card hand
When he laid 'em down and the cards came up cold
He lost the only home that his family ever known

So if you sit to play in the dead of night
Know when to walk away from the lantern light
For a friendly little game in a smoky room
Can turn a man's whole future into ruin and gloom

They played too far, Lord, they played too long
Dealt themselves a hand that was bound to go wrong
Lay your money down, boys, but mind what you sow
They played too far and they reaped the woe

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Diesel veins on the interstate, the white lines never end
Eighteen wheels of thunder, screaming round the bend
Three a.m. and the world's asleep but my mind is wide awake
I pull off at the rest stop with a heart about to break
Grab the phone with trembling hands, dial the number home
Let it ring into the darkness, so damn far and so alone

CAN YOU HEAR ME FROM THE HIGHWAY?
A THOUSAND MILES AWAY!
I'M CALLING OUT YOUR NAME TONIGHT
AT THREE O'CLOCK, THE DEAD OF NIGHT!
I'M COMING HOME, I SWEAR TO YOU
THROUGH THE FIRE AND THE RAIN I'LL BREAK ON THROUGH!

My little girl is sleeping but my wife picks up the line
Her voice is soft and tired but to me it's so divine
The loneliness is crushing like a weight upon my chest
A road that never ends and a man who needs his rest

The asphalt is my prison and the cab is my cell
A wandering soul condemned to roam this rolling steel hell
But your voice across the wire is the light that guides me back
I'll keep on driving through the night down this endless black

CAN YOU HEAR ME FROM THE HIGHWAY?
A THOUSAND MILES AWAY!
I'M CALLING OUT YOUR NAME TONIGHT
AT THREE O'CLOCK, THE DEAD OF NIGHT!

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Faded amber light through a haze in the room
I was nineteen and trembling in the smoke and the gloom
Three chords and a whisper and a notebook of dreams
The microphone was bigger than I ever could seem
My hands were shaking, my voice was so thin
But somewhere in the feedback, a life would begin

And it all washes over me now
Like a wave of the past, somehow
That first little stage, that first little sound
Drifting away, drifting away, drifting away

The crowd was just shadows and a couple of friends
A broken guitar string and a song with no end
I close my eyes and I'm standing there again
In the warm dissolving glow of who I was back then

The years are a blur like the reverb tail
A distant ringing note, a half-remembered trail
I'd give anything to feel that fear once more
That trembling open heart on the open mic floor

And it all washes over me now
Like a wave of the past, somehow
That first little stage, that first little sound
Drifting away, drifting away, drifting away

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew me a map before I could walk
A husband, a house, and the way I should talk
The dress in the closet, the ring on the shelf
A whole life decided by everyone else
I smiled at the dinner and I nodded along
But a quiet little voice said this all feels wrong

So I'm leaving, I'm leaving the future they made
With a bag and a heartbeat and a debt left unpaid
I don't know where I'm going but I know it's not here
I'm trading their certainty for my own kind of fear
I'm leaving, I'm leaving, and it's mine to decide
For the first time I'm taking my own life for a ride

The phone keeps on ringing with my mother's regret
The weight of a name that I can't carry yet
I loved them, I did, but I couldn't stay small
In a life built by hands that were never my own at all

The train pulls away and the city gets thin
A terrifying freedom and the wind on my skin
Maybe I'll fail, maybe I'll fall
But at least it'll be mine, my mistake, that is all

So I'm leaving, I'm leaving the future they made
With a bag and a heartbeat and a debt left unpaid
I don't know where I'm going but I know it's not here
I'm trading their certainty for my own kind of fear

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Smoke curls slow above a downtown bar
He's got ink-stained cuffs and a borrowed guitar
They offered him gold for a softer line
Said sand off the edges, you'll do just fine

But the truth don't fold to fit a frame
And a watered word ain't worth the shame
He folded the contract into a crane
Let it ride out the door in the falling rain

No, he won't sell out, won't trade the ache
For a brighter room and a bigger take
Let the spotlight find a sweeter name
He'd rather burn slow than die in the flame

The piano knows the things he meant
Every nickel of the rent unspent
He reads to twelve and an empty chair
And swears the dust can feel him there

Maybe the gold was never the prize
Maybe it's the look in a stranger's eyes
When a line lands true and the room goes still
That's the only buyer he ever will

No, he won't sell out, won't trade the ache
For a brighter room and a bigger take
Let the spotlight find a sweeter name
He'd rather burn slow than die in the flame

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Gold on the cul-de-sac, sun going down
Boxes in the hallway, we leave tomorrow town
You painted your nails on my mama's back steps
We counted all the things we ain't done yet

Hold on, hold on, just a little more light
The whole world's waiting but it's still tonight
Don't let the morning come too soon
Keep me under this falling-apart moon

We drove with the windows down, nowhere to be
Split a warm soda by the cherry tree
You said you'd write, I said I would too
Both of us knowing what time gon' do

Everything changes, that's the deal we made
When we signed our names to growing up unafraid
But tonight is ours and the crickets agree
Stay in the in-between with me

Hold on, hold on, just a little more light
The whole world's waiting but it's still tonight
Don't let the morning come too soon
Keep me under this falling-apart moon

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone weighs heavy as a loaded gun
Been seven winters since the leaving was done
I dial the number that I never erased
And the dial tone hums like a hymn out of place

The crows on the wire know my every sin
The dust on the line where her voice comes in
She says my name like it never left
Like the silence between us was something I kept

Mama, I'm calling from the edge of the dark
With a coward's hand and a hopeful heart
I buried our years in a shallow ground
Forgive me slow, but don't put the phone down

The clock on the mantel still keeps her time
The whiskey I left and the bitter and brine
She coughs and she laughs and she starts to cry
And the wire holds us both 'til the well runs dry

All them prideful nights I let the embers go cold
Now her voice sounds older than the stories she told
There's a grave with my name if I wait too long
So I'm singing this sorry like an old church song

Mama, I'm calling from the edge of the dark
With a coward's hand and a hopeful heart
I buried our years in a shallow ground
Forgive me slow, but don't put the phone down

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine screaming like a wounded god
Rubber on the rain and the asphalt scarred
They said one more run and the debt is clean
The meanest ride this town has ever seen

Three in the bank and the sirens wake
Doors slam hard for the devil's sake
I count the seconds like a hangman's rope
Foot to the floor, there ain't no slope

Drive! Through the fire and the blinding light
One last run into the throat of night
No brakes, no breaks, no looking back
The last job ends where the road goes black

Bag in the seat and a bullet in the glass
Gotta outrun every ghost in the past
The map's gone red and the bridge is high
Tonight either freedom or I die

They promised me out, they promised me clean
But promises rot like a gasoline dream
The tunnel ahead is a screaming mouth
I floor it and pray as the world goes south

Drive! Through the fire and the blinding light
One last run into the throat of night
No brakes, no breaks, no looking back
The last job ends where the road goes black

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Took the long way home on the interstate
Ended up downtown a little too late
There's the window where we used to fight
Somebody else got the kitchen light

I'm just driving past, that's all it is
A red light memory, a faded kiss
I won't slow down, I won't pull in
Just let me look at where we've been

New curtains hanging where the cactus died
New name on the buzzer there on the side
I swear I can smell the coffee you made
In the cold blue dark of the colonnade

Funny how a building holds a life
Like a pocket holds a folded knife
The street don't know we ever lived
The street's got nothing left to give

I'm just driving past, that's all it is
A red light memory, a faded kiss
I won't slow down, I won't pull in
Just let me look at where we've been

The light turns green and I let it go
Four years gone in a single block
The radio plays an old slow song
And I'm already three streets gone

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
The tether snapped in the silver cold
Your hand reached out but the dark took hold
I sang your name to the empty black
But the stars don't give an echo back

Drift, my love, drift, into the deep
Where the engines die and the comets sleep
I strum this grief on a broken string
For the gravity that won't bring you in

Your visor shrinks to a fading coal
A dying ember, a parting soul
I beat the rhythm on my own chest
A heartbeat lost in the cosmic west

We trained for fire, we trained for flame
But no one trains for the silent same
The radio whispers and then it's gone
And still I clap to carry on

Drift, my love, drift, into the deep
Where the engines die and the comets sleep
I strum this grief on a broken string
For the gravity that won't bring you in

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Soft on the morning the harbor breathes
A ticket folded in my coat sleeves
They planned my wedding and my whole life through
But the tide is leaving and so am I too

Goodbye to the future they drew in chalk
Goodbye to the quiet, obedient walk
I'll find my own warm and easy day
And let the gentle ocean carry me away

My mother's pearls in a velvet case
A polished name and a borrowed grace
I leave them folded on the kitchen chair
With a kiss and a note in the salt-sweet air

Nobody's angry, nobody's wrong
It's just not the rhythm of my own song
So I hum a little, I sway a while
And I let the leaving feel like a smile

Goodbye to the future they drew in chalk
Goodbye to the quiet, obedient walk
I'll find my own warm and easy day
And let the gentle ocean carry me away

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
The band struck up an old slow reel
I didn't know how the night would feel
Then you held your hand out, plain and true
And I had no good reason not to

So we danced, two strangers in the gold
Neither of us twenty, neither of us old
We don't know each other's names
But the fiddle don't care and the night's the same

You stepped on my shoe and we laughed it off
The cake was cut and the toasts were soft
Someone's cousin caught the falling flowers
And we spun away the borrowed hours

Tomorrow we'll be back in separate towns
Different buses, different downs
But for one waltz under paper stars
The whole wide world was only ours

So we danced, two strangers in the gold
Neither of us twenty, neither of us old
We don't know each other's names
But the fiddle don't care and the night's the same

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Old Earl's got a chair by the window light
Been cutting this town since '69
He knows who's dying and who's in love
Knows the secrets the church don't speak of

Sit down, son, let the clippers hum
Tell me where you've been and where you're from
I've shaved every jaw in this whole town
And I'll keep your stories when they put me down

The mayor sat here when he lost the race
The widow's boy with the tear-streaked face
The striped pole turning, the slow town clock
A whole life passing on the avenue block

He cut my daddy's hair and his daddy's too
Three generations through and through
The mirror behind holds a hundred years
Of weddings, funerals, and small-town fears

When Earl retires they'll close the door
And nobody'll know this town no more
The scissors quiet, the chair gone cold
And a thousand stories left untold

Sit down, son, let the clippers hum
Tell me where you've been and where you're from
I've shaved every jaw in this whole town
And I'll keep your stories when they put me down

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down like it had a plan
I ducked the awning and there you ran
One little umbrella, two pairs of feet
Groovin' down a flooded city street

We were sharing one umbrella in the pourin' down
Two strangers makin' rhythm through the soaking town
Shoulder to shoulder in the falling rain
Get a little closer, say it again

You laughed at my shoes all squishin' wet
I didn't even catch your name just yet
But the bassline of the gutter and the splash and the spray
Had us dancing seven blocks the long way

The storm could pour all night for all I care
Long as you keep walking under there
A accidental meeting in a downpour groove
Baby look what one umbrella made us do

We were sharing one umbrella in the pourin' down
Two strangers makin' rhythm through the soaking town
Shoulder to shoulder in the falling rain
Get a little closer, say it again

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors slide shut and the lights flick white
Too many shoulders, too little light
My heart's a drum that won't slow down
Underneath the breathing town

Breathe, just breathe, count the stops to four
Hold the cold steel of the sliding door
It's only a tunnel, it's only the dark
It's only my body sounding the alarm

The map on the wall starts to bend and swim
A stranger's elbow, the air grows thin
I grip my phone like a falling rope
And whisper a small electric hope

Nobody sees the war in my chest
The quiet flood, the failing rest
But the next stop's mine and the doors will part
And I'll carry out my pounding heart

Breathe, just breathe, count the stops to four
Hold the cold steel of the sliding door
It's only a tunnel, it's only the dark
It's only my body sounding the alarm

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew for the very last time
The iron gates closed on the assembly line
Forty years of my father's hands
Now rust and silence where the engine stands

They're closing it down, the heart of the town
The smokestack falls and the gates come down
Clap for the workers, clap for the dead
For the bread we earned and the prayers we said

Maria swept the floor for thirty years
Now she sweeps her own salt tears
The foreman cried when he turned the key
And the dust danced slow in the factory

Where will the morning men go now
With calloused hands and an empty vow
The machines go cold and the wages cease
And the only thing left is a bitter peace

They're closing it down, the heart of the town
The smokestack falls and the gates come down
Clap for the workers, clap for the dead
For the bread we earned and the prayers we said

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning, I slipped out the back
Left my hymnal on the old oak rack
The organ swelled and I didn't stay
Just tipped my hat and walked away

I'm steppin' out light, I'm steppin' out clean
No big goodbye, no burning the scene
Just a quiet little exit, soft on the floor
A boy who don't believe like before

I loved them pews and the stained-glass blue
The coffee hour and the kindly crew
But the words stopped fitting in my chest
So I'm tippin' my way toward something else

No thunder cracked, no lightning fell
No angry preacher wishing me well
Just a Sunday breeze and an open gate
And a swinging heart that couldn't wait

I'm steppin' out light, I'm steppin' out clean
No big goodbye, no burning the scene
Just a quiet little exit, soft on the floor
A boy who don't believe like before

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They called your name and they called my own
Two proud fires on a single stone
We hated each other across the floor
Now the guitar demands we settle the score

Dance, you devil, match my heel
Let the whole world see the way we feel
Every hatred in a stamp and a turn
We're chained together while the candles burn

Your eyes are blades and mine are too
But my hand must rise to meet with you
The crowd holds breath at the bitter grace
Of two old rivals in a forced embrace

And somewhere deep in the pounding beat
The war dissolves in the moving feet
I hate you still, but my God you're fire
And the dance pulls truth from the bitter mire

Dance, you devil, match my heel
Let the whole world see the way we feel
Every hatred in a stamp and a turn
We're chained together while the candles burn

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Key in the ignition, hands at ten and two
Slow down, baby girl, the road belongs to you
Mirror, signal, breathe out easy now
Daddy's right beside you, we gon' figure out how

Drive easy, drive slow, let the engine hum low
The whole wide world is a place you can go
I taught you to walk and I taught you to run
Now I'm teaching you driving in the morning sun

You stalled it at the corner and you started to cry
I said laugh it off, sweetheart, give it one more try
The clutch caught smooth and you rolled on through
And I saw the little girl growing up too

One day soon you'll drive away for good
Down some highway out the neighborhood
But every time you turn that wheel
Remember how your daddy made you feel

Drive easy, drive slow, let the engine hum low
The whole wide world is a place you can go
I taught you to walk and I taught you to run
Now I'm teaching you driving in the morning sun

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
I been standin' in this gallery since a quarter after eight
Watchin' folks walk by her, never knowin' how they're late
She's got a blue dress fadin', oil cracked across her face
And the rope between us tremble like it's holdin' back a chase

My boots wore down the marble where I shuffle every day
The morning light comes through her like she's tryin' hard to stay
I memorized her shadows, every brushstroke, every sigh
She never said a word to me but Lord, she catch my eye

Oh she don't grow older, and I do every night
Lockin' up the darkness, turnin' off the light
Me and that old painting, two ghosts behind the glass
Watchin' all the livin' come and go and pass

They gonna sell this building, move her somewhere far
Gonna roll her up in canvas, load her on a railroad car
And I'll go home to nothin', to a kitchen and a chair
While somebody else stand guard and breathe her painted air

So I tip my cap at closin', say a little prayer
For a woman made of color who was never really there

Oh she don't grow older, and I do every night
Lockin' up the darkness, turnin' off the light
Me and that old painting, two ghosts behind the glass
Watchin' all the livin' come and go and pass

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Pick up your lunch pail, the whistle won't blow
Foreman's got a letter and he's readin' it slow
Thirty years of welding, thirty years of steam
Now they're sellin' off the building like it's somebody's dream

The factory's closing, pack it up, pack it down
Last one out, turn the lights off in this town
Upbeat horns but the heart's gone cold
We're a punchline now in a story they sold

My old man worked here, his old man too
Now the only thing hiring is the parking lot view
They shipped it all overseas on a Tuesday in the rain
Left us holdin' time cards and a permanent stain

The factory's closing, pack it up, pack it down
Last one out, turn the lights off in this town
Upbeat horns but the heart's gone cold
We're a punchline now in a story they sold

So skank it on the picket line, dance while we still can
A dyin' kind of music for a dyin' working man
One two three four, kick the gravel, raise a glass
To every weldin' spark that ever lit and didn't last

The factory's closing, pack it up, pack it down
Last one out, turn the lights off in this town
Upbeat horns but the heart's gone cold
We're a punchline now in a story they sold

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Four in the morning, the floor still breathing
Lights through the smoke and I'm barely believing
They said my name, said come up to the booth
Closest I ever been to the truth

Almost, almost, fingertips on the dream
Almost, almost, closer than it seemed
The whole room moving like it was mine
The night I almost crossed the line

Demo in my pocket, heart in my throat
A hand on my shoulder, a whispered quote
They'd call me Monday, they'd make it real
I danced till sunrise just to feel the feel

Almost, almost, fingertips on the dream
Almost, almost, closer than it seemed
The whole room moving like it was mine
The night I almost crossed the line

Monday never called, the number went dead
But I keep that bassline alive in my head
Almost is a country I still call home
Where the lights never lower and I'm never alone

Almost, almost, fingertips on the dream
Almost, almost, closer than it seemed
The whole room moving like it was mine
The night I almost crossed the line

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
The slow turn of a kettle, steam against the glass
His hands a little quieter than they were in the past
Light moves across the table where he used to stand so tall
Now he counts the staircase, one breath for every wall

And I am holding still, holding still
Watching weather change across a hill
The man who carried me grows light as air
And I just want to stay, to stay right there

He forgets the middle, remembers the start
A boyhood river running clean through his heart
I sit and let him tell it, the same one twice
And I am learning patience is a kind of paradise

Fold the years like linen, soft and worn and warm
Keep him in the quiet, keep him from the storm

And I am holding still, holding still
Watching weather change across a hill
The man who carried me grows light as air
And I just want to stay, to stay right there

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew the map before I learned to walk
A house, a name, a season, all the talk
I traced the lines they gave me with my thumb
Then set it down and watched the morning come

I am the open door, the empty chair
The future folded, left it on the stair
No one waiting where they said I'd be
The quiet now belongs to me

Mother's hands still shaping who I'd been
A garden walled and lovely, locked within
I loved them as I lifted up the latch
And stepped into a field with nothing to match

Forgive the silence where the wedding bells would ring
Forgive the daughter learning how to sing

I am the open door, the empty chair
The future folded, left it on the stair
No one waiting where they said I'd be
The quiet now belongs to me

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The tape gun screams in an empty room
Echo where the sofa used to bloom
One more flap and I'll seal it tight
The last brown box in the fading light

So long, little kitchen, so long, creaking floor
So long to the shadow behind the door
I'm taping up the years with a careless hand
Moving on to somewhere I don't understand

Found a wine ring ghost on the windowsill
A pencil mark for a height standing still
The radiator clanks me a last refrain
Like the building's tryin' to ask me to remain

Keys on the counter, catch the streetlight's gold
A whole life packed and ready to be told
Somewhere else, somewhere new, somewhere strange
Everything's a doorway, everything's a change

So long, little kitchen, so long, creaking floor
So long to the shadow behind the door
I'm taping up the years with a careless hand
Moving on to somewhere I don't understand

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Close my eyes and I'm back on that linoleum floor
Cornbread risin', screen door slammin' by the store
Grease in the cast iron, prayers in the steam
Granny hummin' gospel like it stitched up a dream
Greens on the stove been cookin' since dawn
Smell hit the porch before the morning was gone
She ain't measure nothin', just a pinch and a feel
Love was the seasoning, that's what made it real

That smell, that smell, take me back, take me home
Before I knew loss, before I learned to roam
Flour on her apron, sweet tea in the jar
Granny's little kitchen, that's the realest thing we are

Neighborhood changed, that house been sold
But I caught that scent on a stranger's stove, I froze
Sweet potato pie liftin' up through the years
And a grown man stood on the sidewalk in tears
She taught me that fullness ain't just in the gut
It's a table that's open and a door never shut

So I cook it for my kids now, slow and with care
Try to bottle up that morning, that Sunday, that air
She's gone but the kitchen still warm in my chest
Every meal I make is a way to say blessed

That smell, that smell, take me back, take me home
Before I knew loss, before I learned to roam
Flour on her apron, sweet tea in the jar
Granny's little kitchen, that's the realest thing we are

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They paved the avenues over his chest
Built the cathedrals where the heart should rest
A million windows blink and gleam
On the slow rise and fall of a giant's dream

Don't wake him, don't wake him, keep the rhythm low
Dance soft on the shoulders of the sleeping below
The city's just a heartbeat, the bassline of his breath
We're living on a giant, between his sleep and death

The subway hums through his ancient bones
We call it home, these borrowed stones
The fountains run where his river bled
And we toast the skyline above his head

Don't wake him, don't wake him, keep the rhythm low
Dance soft on the shoulders of the sleeping below
The city's just a heartbeat, the bassline of his breath
We're living on a giant, between his sleep and death

One day he'll roll over, towers turn to sand
All our shining glory just a tremble in his hand
So dance now, dance now, while the ground holds still
Dance on the giant, dance until

Don't wake him, don't wake him, keep the rhythm low
Dance soft on the shoulders of the sleeping below
The city's just a heartbeat, the bassline of his breath
We're living on a giant, between his sleep and death

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Met her in June where the boardwalk ends
Salt in her hair and a pocket full of friends
We ran down the dunes when the tide pulled low
And I knew right then I'd be sorry to go

Oh summer girl, summer girl, dance in the foam
You're a song I'll be singin' all the long winter home
The banjo's runnin' fast as the days disappear
Gone like a wave when September draws near

We ate boiled peanuts and we drank from the spring
Carved our two names where the gulls love to sing
She laughed at my fiddle, she stole my old hat
And nothin' since then ever felt quite like that

Oh summer girl, summer girl, dance in the foam
You're a song I'll be singin' all the long winter home
The banjo's runnin' fast as the days disappear
Gone like a wave when September draws near

She caught a bus northbound on the first cold day
And the ocean kept on rollin' like it had nothin' to say
But I still taste the saltwater, still hear her name
In every summer after, never quite the same

Oh summer girl, summer girl, dance in the foam
You're a song I'll be singin' all the long winter home
The banjo's runnin' fast as the days disappear
Gone like a wave when September draws near

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The big tent's leaning, the beer's gone warm
String lights swayin' like they're weatherin' a storm
A thousand strangers we'll never see again
Singing every word like it's nine and not ten

One more song, one more round, one more turn in the dust
Hold the night a little longer, boys, before it goes to rust
Tomorrow we're just license plates on a long gray road
But tonight we're a family the music almost showed

The headliner's done but nobody leaves
We're trading old harmonies under the eaves
A fiddle by the firepit, a kid asleep in a coat
And the whole tired meadow hangin' on a note

They'll tear down the stages when the sun comes around
Load the dream in a trailer, haul it out of this town
But something stays planted in the trampled-down field
A seed of who we were when our hearts were unsealed

One more song, one more round, one more turn in the dust
Hold the night a little longer, boys, before it goes to rust
Tomorrow we're just license plates on a long gray road
But tonight we're a family the music almost showed

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
There's a barstool ghost in a low-lit room
Where I first sang my sorrow into the gloom
My hands were shaking on the borrowed strings
And the devil in the corner clapped for cheaper things

Oh the first night I sang, I was younger than sin
With a coffin-black guitar and a graveyard grin
They didn't even listen, but the song got loose
And it's been hangin' round my neck like a slow-pulled noose

Three people and a bartender wiping down the bar
A candle and a busted amp and one falling star
I played four crooked verses 'bout a love gone cold
And I aged a hundred years before the night got old

That boy is buried under everything I've done
But sometimes in the feedback I can hear him strum
Reachin' through the smoke from that first cursed stage
Singing come and join me, turn another page

Oh the first night I sang, I was younger than sin
With a coffin-black guitar and a graveyard grin
They didn't even listen, but the song got loose
And it's been hangin' round my neck like a slow-pulled noose

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded her up in the dead of the dark
A crate without papers, a hold without spark
The captain said don't ask what's sealed below
Just keep the engines burning, keep the rivers slow

Oh this cargo's cursed, baby, and the sea knows my name
Every wave's a whisper, every star's a flame
Seven nights to harbor but the harbor moves away
Carrying a curse, love, that I can't lay down today

The crew started dreaming the very same dream
A woman in the water with a silver scream
The compass spun in circles, the salt turned to rust
And whatever's in that crate, baby, I don't trust

I'd throw it overboard but it calls me by name
Whispers all my sins like it's playing a game
The shore's just a rumor, the moon's gone red
And I can't tell the living now from the dead

Oh this cargo's cursed, baby, and the sea knows my name
Every wave's a whisper, every star's a flame
Seven nights to harbor but the harbor moves away
Carrying a curse, love, that I can't lay down today

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
FLUORESCENT HUM, TWELVE-HOUR SHIFT
DEAD-EYED STARE AT A FRAME THAT WON'T LIFT
THEY PAY ME TO STAND, THEY PAY ME TO ROT
GUARDING SOMEBODY'S GENIUS THAT I AIN'T GOT

DON'T TOUCH THE PAINTING! DON'T CROSS THE LINE!
I'M SCREAMING AT THE SILENCE, THIS PRISON IS MINE
SHE STARES BACK FOREVER AND I'LL NEVER BE FREE
A MAN MADE OF NOTHING GUARDING ETERNITY

ROPE AROUND MY NECK LIKE THE ROPE AROUND HER
VELVET AND A BADGE AND A LIFE THAT'S A BLUR
SCHOOL KIDS SPIT GUM, RICH MEN DON'T SEE
THE GHOST IN THE UNIFORM IS SCREAMING IT'S ME

TEAR IT DOWN! TEAR IT DOWN!
EVERY GILDED FRAME IN THIS GODFORSAKEN TOWN!
I GAVE HER MY YEARS, SHE GAVE ME A WALL
SO BURN THE WHOLE MUSEUM, LET IT FALL, LET IT FALL

DON'T TOUCH THE PAINTING! DON'T CROSS THE LINE!
I'M SCREAMING AT THE SILENCE, THIS PRISON IS MINE
SHE STARES BACK FOREVER AND I'LL NEVER BE FREE
A MAN MADE OF NOTHING GUARDING ETERNITY

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They set a table for a life I never chose
Name cards and a future buttoned up in rows
I slipped out the side door, coat over my arm
Traded all their certainty for a little bit of harm

So here's to the runaway, glass raised in smoke
Leaving the blueprint as the last cruel joke
The band plays a number nobody arranged
And I'm beautifully, terribly, gloriously strange

The groom was a stranger, the ring was a cage
The whole gilded chapter on somebody's page
I walked through the city with my heels in my hand
Writing my own ending that they'll never understand

Let the relatives whisper, let the gossip turn cold
I'm a verse improvised, I won't be controlled
The saxophone knows it, that low bending cry
The sound of a woman saying goodbye, goodbye

So here's to the runaway, glass raised in smoke
Leaving the blueprint as the last cruel joke
The band plays a number nobody arranged
And I'm beautifully, terribly, gloriously strange

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
The light's still on in the second-floor room
Where I used to lie awake and watch the gloom
Somebody's curtains, somebody's chair
Living the life I left up there

And I just drive on by, I don't slow down
Just a stranger's headlights moving through the town
But my hands know the turn and my chest knows the ache
Of every quiet promise we couldn't keep or break

There's the crack in the sidewalk, the same broken gate
The diner on the corner that closes too late
I could draw every doorway with my eyes shut tight
Though I haven't lived here in a thousand nights

Funny how a building can hold all that you were
A younger, softer version, everything a blur
I tap the brake then let it go, the red light turning green
And I leave it in the mirror like a half-remembered dream

And I just drive on by, I don't slow down
Just a stranger's headlights moving through the town
But my hands know the turn and my chest knows the ache
Of every quiet promise we couldn't keep or break

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raise my little hand above the long oak table
The clarinet of my voice begins, but never able
To finish half a sentence before the bigger horn
Comes blowing through my ending like a coat that's being worn

I brought a folder full of numbers, neat in rows
The fiddle of my logic plucked, then nobody knows
The chairman taps his pen, the doorman of the air
And hands my open mouth to someone in a louder chair

Oy, talk over me, talk over me
The whole brass section drowns my plea
I am the rest between two notes nobody waits to hear
A freylekhs played to empty rooms, an echo and a cheer

The accordion of patience wheezes in my chest
I swallow my whole argument and call it being blessed
But underneath the table both my polished shoes are kicking
In three-four time, a hidden dance, my quiet little ticking

So let them have the meeting, let them have the floor
I'll take my crooked melody and walk right out the door
And in the stairwell, finally, I sing the missing part
The whole agenda dancing in the freylekhs of my heart

Oy, talk over me, talk over me
One day the silence will be free
And everything I never said will spin around the room
A wedding tune for one lone voice that bloomed out of the gloom

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
The neon hums a flat blue note above the rain-slicked street
A waitress folds the menus where the midnight strangers meet
The coffee keeps its low brown groove inside a chipped white cup
The clock has lost its argument, it simply gave it up

A cabbie reads the racing page, a poet reads the wall
The griddle sings a hissing tune that answers to them all
The pie spins slow inside its case, a moon that never sets
And everyone in here tonight is paying off their debts

We stay open, baby, when the city locks its doors
We stay open for the lonely and the lost ones and the more
There's a stool that's always waiting and a light that won't go down
We're the last warm yellow window in a cold and sleeping town

The trumpet of the kettle whistles softly to the booth
Where two old friends are sorting out the difference and the truth
The sugar in the dispenser glitters like a small white sea
And nobody asks anybody what they're running from, or free

Three a.m. the bass walks in on weary rubber soles
A shift of tired waitresses, a counter full of holes
But the bell above the door still rings its little brass amen
And whoever needs a refuge gets the corner booth again

We stay open, baby, till the morning paints the glass
We stay open for the night birds and we let the slow hours pass
There's a melody in the syrup and a rhythm in the steam
We're the only place still dreaming when the rest stop having dreams

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slammed shut behind me on the downtown number nine
My heart took off without me, left my body two stops behind
The lights began to flicker like they's flickering just for me
And the floor it started leaning like a drunk and rolling sea

Oh the tunnel got no daylight, and the air got no more room
My lungs are two flat tires in a rattling iron tomb
Don't nobody look up from their phones to see me come undone
I'm dying in a crowded car and dying here alone

I count the dirty stations, I count 'em one through nine
I grip the silver pole so hard it leaves a brand on mine
A cold sweat down my collar and a roaring in my ears
And thirty years of running caught me up in thirty fears

Lord the train don't care about me, it just hammers down the line
The screech of steel on iron is the only prayer I find
But somewhere up ahead there's a platform painted gold
Where the doors will finally open and let go this trembling hold

Oh the tunnel found its daylight, and my breath came crawling back
I stumbled to the platform on a pair of jellied tracks
And I sat down on a bench like a man returned from war
Alive and shaking, blessed and broken, on the cold tile floor

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
My boots came off the airplane two whole sizes for the man
I used to be when I was loaded on a bus with my brass band
The horns are kicking upstroke and the town put up a sign
But the welcome's a bit louder than the quiet that I find

Pick it up, pick it up, but my hands don't wanna dance
The trumpets and the trombones give my ghosts a second chance
I'm marching to the offbeat now, a soldier with no war
Just trying to find the rhythm of the kid I was before

The garden's overgrown, the dog forgot my smell
The kettle's where I left it but I'm somebody else as well
My mother sets a place for me, my sister cracks a joke
And the skank keeps on rolling like a curl of chimney smoke

The nightmares wear my old fatigues and march around my bed
But morning brings the horn line dancing circles in my head
I lace my civvy sneakers and I step out in the sun
The war is somewhere far away, and I am almost done

So play it fast, play it bright, play it loud enough to drown
The sound of distant thunder still rolling through this town
And when the chorus lifts me, just an inch above the floor
I'm lighter than I've been since they first carried me to war

Pick it up, pick it up, now my hands begin to dance
The trumpets and the trombones gave my heart a second chance
I'm marching to the offbeat home, a soldier home at last
Letting all that brass and sunshine burn away the past

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The boxes by the doorway and the tan lines on my arm
The lawn sprinkler still ticking out its sleepy little charm
We got one more tank of gasoline and nowhere we should be
So we drove down to the reservoir to swim ourselves to free

Upstroke on the last day, the offbeat of goodbye
We're skanking on the blacktop underneath a peeling sky
Tomorrow I'll be somebody who answers to a name
But today we are the summer and the summer is a flame

The trombone of the ice cream truck goes melting down the block
We steal the final daylight off a slow and stubborn clock
My best friend's leaving Tuesday and I'm leaving on the train
And nobody is saying that it won't be all the same

We wrote our names in marker on the underside of the slide
A time capsule of nothing that the grown-ups never find
The horns keep climbing higher as the streetlights flicker on
And we dance like we can stop the morning swallowing the dawn

So pick it up one more time before the autumn takes the lead
Before we're scattered cities and a thousand miles of need
The offbeat keeps on holding us a half-step off the ground
The last warm night of being kids, the sweetest, fastest sound

Upstroke on the last day, the offbeat of goodbye
We're skanking on the blacktop till there's nothing left of sky
Tomorrow we grow older, but the trumpets still proclaim
That we were once the summer, and the summer was a flame

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
There's a hill behind my sleeping where the long grass learns to bend
And I run there every midnight till the running has to end
Then my heels forget the meadow and my shoulders find the air
And I lift above the orchard with my hands held in a prayer

And I'm flying, I'm flying, over chimney over stream
With the cold pond down below me like a coin inside a dream
Don't wake me, don't wake me, let the bedroom stay the sky
I have spent my waking hours just learning how to fly

I have seen my childhood rooftop, I have seen the schoolhouse small
I have watched the river ribbon where my grandfather would call
The weathervane below me turns to point me toward the dawn
And I bank above the steeple and the night is almost gone

But the secret of the flying is you must not look too long
At the ground that wants to claim you with its old familiar song
The moment that I doubt it is the moment that I fall
Through the ceiling of my certainty and wake against the wall

So each dusk I climb the staircase to the country of the air
And I pray the dream will find me with my arms already bare
For I'm heaviest at morning when the floor comes up to meet
The ordinary mercy of two ordinary feet

And I'm flying, I'm flying, over chimney over stream
With the whole sleeping village like a candle and its gleam
Don't wake me, don't wake me, but I know that morning's near
For the higher that I'm soaring, the more it's gravity I hear

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Eight hours on a folding stool in gallery thirteen
A uniform that doesn't fit, a badge nobody's seen
They pay me less than minimum to stand against the wall
But she's been hanging here three hundred years and seen it all

Hey, painted girl in the golden frame, do you ever wanna scream?
We're both just stuck here doing time inside somebody's dream
They lock the doors at closing, kill the lights and walk away
And it's you and me, you and me, till the break of day

The tourists snap their photos and they never really look
They read your name off the little card like a chapter in a book
Then they're gone to the gift shop for a fridge magnet of your face
While I memorize the cracks in you, this quiet sacred place

My radio is crackling and my feet have turned to stone
The richest people in the world own you, but you're alone
So I tell you all my secrets when the cameras start to nod
A minimum-wage confession to a four-hundred-year-old god

They say don't touch, they say stand back, they say respect the rope
But you and me are both just paint on canvas, dust and hope
One day they'll throw me out of here, one day the frame will rot
So let's burn down this museum with the only love we've got

Hey, painted girl in the golden frame, tonight we both break free
No alarm, no velvet rope, no security
We'll walk right out the exit while the city's fast asleep
Two runaways the history books were never gonna keep

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Última noche, suitcase by the door
Dembow en la bocina, one summer more
La playa got our footprints, the moon got our names
Mañana somos otra cosa, never the same

Dale, dale, baila till the sunrise hits
Esta noche no termina, no, we don't quit
Tomorrow I'm a freshman in a city far from here
Pero esta noche es nuestra, the last one of the year

We filled the cooler, parked it down by the shore
La bocina shaking sand off the old pickup floor
Mi gente all around me, every face I'll miss
So I freeze it like a photo, every laugh, every kiss

December feels like never, August feels like now
We made a hundred promises, I don't know how
We'll keep 'em in the distance, en otra ciudad
But the perreo's in my blood now, won't ever fade

Uno, dos, las manos up, conteo final
The streetlights and the sea breeze and the bass on the rail
No llores, mami, smile for me, give me one more round
Before the autumn comes and pulls us out of this town

Dale, dale, baila till the sunrise hits
Esta noche no termina, no, we don't quit
Tomorrow I'm a freshman in a city far from here
Pero esta noche es nuestra, the last one of the year

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
The candle went out slowly, not a storm, just a sigh
And the ceiling of the chapel was an ordinary sky
I knelt down out of habit and I heard the silence hum
No voice came down to meet me, no kingdom, no come

So I walked into the wide dark with my empty open hands
Where the questions had no answers and the answers had no plans
And I floated through the nothing like a feather through the deep
Learning how to lose the one thing I had sworn that I would keep

The months were grey as water under bridges I had burned
Every prayer I used to lean on was a language I unlearned
I traded all my certainty for honest empty rooms
And I slept beneath a window full of indifferent moons

But a thing began to shimmer at the bottom of the well
Not the god I lost in childhood, not a heaven, not a hell
Just a warmth without a reason, just a yes without a why
Like the first grey of the morning long before there is a sky

So I walked back from the wide dark with my still and open hands
And the faith I found was smaller, but it finally understands
That it doesn't need an answer, doesn't need a steeple's keep
Just the breathing in the silence and the love beneath the deep

The candle's lit again now, though I light it for myself
And I keep it on the windowsill, not high upon a shelf
It isn't proof of anything, it's just a little flame
But I tend it every evening, and the tending is the name

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning rolling and the windows breathing slow
Allspice on the counter where the brown stew used to go
Granny at the iron pot, her headwrap tied up high
And the ginger and the thyme go drifting past the open sky

Oh, the smell of granny kitchen carry me back home
Through the cinnamon and coconut wherever I may roam
No matter how the city try to take the country out of me
One breath of nutmeg morning set my weary spirit free

The dumpling in the water and the saltfish in the pan
She feeding the whole island from a little pot of plan
The scotch bonnet a-whisper and the callaloo a-green
The sweetest little kitchen that my eyes have ever seen

Now granny gone to glory and the house belong to time
But I cook the old recipes and every bubbling line
Brings her standing right beside me with her wooden spoon and grace
And the steam upon my glasses is the kissing of her face

So I teach it to my children, let the riddim never end
The pimento and the patience that a granny used to blend
And when the pot start singing on a lazy Sunday noon
The whole of my ancestors come dancing to the tune

Oh, the smell of granny kitchen carry me back home
Through the cinnamon and coconut wherever I may roam
No matter how the city try to take the country out of me
One breath of nutmeg morning set my weary spirit free

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Got a dashboard full of dust and a radar in my chest
Every time the sky turns green is the only time I rest
My woman packed her suitcase, said a wall cloud's all you love
And she's right as Kansas thunder rolling charcoal up above

I chase the storm, I chase the storm, down every empty road
Where the wheat lies flat and praying and the heavens come unowed
I can't sit still in a kitchen, can't sleep beneath a roof
The only home I ever knew is a funnel's spinning proof

The hail come down like dollar coins and dent the rented hood
My hands are steady on the wheel the way a sane man's never could
I seen a barn go inside out, I seen the river climb
And I outran a mile-wide monster just to do it one more time

They got a name for what I'm missing, got a word for what I lack
But a porch and a quiet supper always send me racing back
To the warm front and the cold front where they argue in the dark
And the lightning writes my letters in a long electric arc

Lord I know it's gonna kill me, some grey afternoon out west
Where the supercell come hunting and I'm too slow to outguess
But bury me out flat country where the wind can find my stone
So even dead and settled, I won't have to stay alone

I chase the storm, I chase the storm, it's the only church I keep
Where the thunder is the sermon and the rain is how I weep
I can't sit still in a kitchen, can't love a thing that stays
So I'll spend my whole tomorrow chasing down my yesterdays

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
These hands that used to lift me up above the morning sun
Now tremble on the coffee cup before the day's begun
The man who carried mountains can't recall my mother's tune
And I watch the giant of my childhood fading in the room

But bless him, Lord, oh bless him, every silver in his hair
The weight he carried all those years he set in You with care
And if his memory's leaving like a river to the sea
Then let the love he poured in me come flooding back to thee

He taught me how to whistle, how to tie a Windsor knot
How to stand up straight in trouble and be grateful for my lot
Now I'm tying his own necktie for the doctor's Tuesday call
And I'm catching him so gently every time I see him fall

The roles have come full circle like the seasons in Your hand
The boy is now the father and the father can't quite stand
But there's glory in the stooping and there's grace inside the slow
For I'm holding up the one who held me up so long ago

When the chariot comes swinging low across that golden floor
Let me walk him to the river, let me carry him no more
And I'll thank You for the burden, Lord, the privilege and the years
Of loving down my father through the laughter and the tears

So bless him, Lord, oh bless him, till You call his name above
And let me be the vessel of his long and steady love
For the strongest man I ever knew is leaning now on me
And that leaning is the sweetest weight You ever gave to be

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Three little dots dey blink, blink for my screen
Then the message come and tear apart the dream
No call, no face, just the blue light cold
You ended all we built in a line, untold

You texted me goodbye, baby, on a Tuesday night
No warning and no reason, just the little screen light
I dey dance but my chest dey heavy, log dey on the floor
How you fit love somebody and then love them no more?

The drums dey play outside but my room dey quiet
I read am ten times over like a small riot
Delivered, then read, then you went offline
Left me here re-typing all these words of mine

We planned Lagos summers and a December owambe
Now your name na notification wey I no fit see
My thumb dey hover over the conversation gone
The sweetest love I carry turn to dial tone

So I go dance am out, let the gbedu carry pain
Let the bassline wash your message off my brain
If you fit type goodbye while you no fit say my name
Then the love wey end so easy was never quite the same

You texted me goodbye, baby, on a Tuesday night
No warning and no reason, just the little screen light
I go rise up from this heartbreak, gonna shine some more
'Cause a love that dies by text was never love before

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Four a.m. and the city's quiet, neon buzzing on the glass
Found a booth beneath a flicker where the lonely hours pass
Got a coffee and a heartbeat and a kick drum in my chest
This the only place still breathing, this the only place to rest

Keep the door open, keep the lights on low
We don't ever close, baby, we don't ever go
From the dusk until the daylight, every soul that lost its way
Finds a stool and finds a rhythm in the breaking of the day

The waitress know my order, she don't ever ask my name
Just a refill and a nod and we are dancing all the same
To the hum of the refrigerator, to the sizzle of the grill
While the dancers from the club come down to ride the after-thrill

Move, move, the morning's coming slow
Move, move, but we don't wanna go
Just the bassline and the coffee and the strangers and the steam
Lost together, found together, in the diner of the dream

When the sun come through the window and it gild the chrome and tile
We'll be sitting in the glory of the night that took a while
And nobody got to leave here, nobody got to be alone
The griddle keeps on singing and the diner is our home

Keep the door open, keep the lights on low
We don't ever close, baby, we don't ever go
From the dusk until the daylight, every soul that lost its way
Finds a stool and finds a rhythm in the breaking of the day

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The roll of tape it screams across the cardboard one last time
My whole life in a corner stack, this room that used to rhyme
With your footsteps in the morning and your jacket on the chair
Now it's echoes and a Sharpie writing KITCHEN in the air

And I'm taping up the last box, sealing up the years
Folding all the laughter in with all the silent tears
The walls are bare and honest where our pictures used to be
And the last box that I'm closing is the one that's holding me

Found a hairpin in the floorboards and your note behind the door
A grocery list from springtime when you still lived here before
I almost called your number just to read it to you slow
But some boxes you don't open and some numbers you don't know

The keys feel strange and heavy in the flat of my own palm
The quiet in the hallway is a loud and aching calm
I'll hand them to the landlord and I'll step into the street
With everything I owned reduced to what my arms can meet

So here's to the apartment and the people that we were
The burned dinners, the slow dances, all that beautiful blur
I'm taping up the ending but I'm carrying the start
There's no box big enough, baby, to hold this folded heart

And I'm taping up the last box, sealing up the years
Folding all the laughter in with all the silent tears
The walls are bare and honest where our pictures used to be
And the last box that I'm closing is the one that's setting me free

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning and my thumbs won't quit
Your name on the screen, oh I can't handle it
I know it's a line that I shouldn't cross
But the upstroke guitar makes me forget the cost

Delete it, retype it, then send it anyway
The horns kick in and I got nothing to say
My phone's lighting up like a neon sign
Trouble in a bubble and the bubble says it's mine

Pick it up, pick it up, pick it up now
Texting you again, don't know why, don't know how
Green light, red flag, dancing on the wire
Every little message is a little bit of fire

My friends all told me put the phone away
But the rhythm section won't let me obey
I'm skanking 'round the kitchen with my heart on read
Should be asleep but I'm typing instead

One more text, just one more text
Never knowing what is coming next
Up and down and up and down we go
My fingers move faster than my common sense, oh no

Pick it up, pick it up, pick it up now
Texting you again, don't know why, don't know how
Green light, red flag, dancing on the wire
Every little message is a little bit of fire

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five dollars on the table in the morning light
Old man laughing, says you'll lose tonight
He moves the pawns like he's reading your soul
Washington Square where the hustlers roll

He's got a velvet voice and a steady hand
Clock ticking down like a one-man band
Knights in the shadows, bishops in the sun
You don't even know that the game's been won

Checkmate, baby, lay your money down
He's the king of the corner in a paper crown
Every move a story, every story true
The board don't lie and neither do you

Pigeons scatter when the queen comes through
He's seen a thousand faces just like you
Smiling while he takes your final piece
Then he tips his hat and he holds his peace

Oh, he played the streets when the streets were rough
Learned that a king ain't ever enough
Wisdom in his fingers, sorrow in his eyes
Winning every game but he lost the prize

Checkmate, baby, lay your money down
He's the king of the corner in a paper crown
Every move a story, every story true
The board don't lie and neither do you

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Groove was tight the night you walked on by
Bassline thumping but you said goodbye
I shoulda made my move on the dance floor
Now you're a memory and nothing more

Ooh, the one that got away
Slippin' through my fingers like the funk won't stay
Ooh, I let you slide
Now I'm dancing solo with my foolish pride

Clavinet crying in the back of my mind
Sweetest little something that I left behind
Every Friday night I can hear your name
In the pocket of the rhythm, in the heat of the flame

I got the moves but I lost the muse
Walking 'round the city with the lonely blues
Horn section wailing what my heart can't say
Thinking 'bout the one, yeah, the one that got away

Give me one more chance to get it right
Under the disco ball, under the light
Spin me around, bring you back to me
Funk so deep it could set us free

Ooh, the one that got away
Slippin' through my fingers like the funk won't stay
Ooh, I let you slide
Now I'm dancing solo with my foolish pride

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Under the arctic sky tonight
Colors dancing, pure delight
Feel the bassline in your chest
Nature putting on her best

Northern lights, take me higher
Green and violet, hearts on fire
Feel the rhythm, feel the glow
Let the colors take control

Hands up reaching for the sky
Watch the aurora rolling by
Four on the floor and the stars align
Everything is gonna be fine

Let it move you, let it flow
Watch the heavens put on a show

Close your eyes and feel the night
Dancing in the borealis light
No more darkness, no more cold
Just the story the sky has told

We are infinite, we are free
Dancing where the sky meets the sea
Every heartbeat, every breath
Glowing brighter, nothing left

Northern lights, take me higher
Green and violet, hearts on fire
Feel the rhythm, feel the glow
Let the colors take control

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete stretches mile on mile
One green leaf in the rank and file
She stands alone where the gardens grew
Holding up a sky of blue

Brushes on the snare so soft and low
A piano plays where the rivers used to flow
They paved the meadow, they paved the lane
But the last old tree still feels the rain

One lonely tree in a city of stone
Singing to the moon all on her own
Roots holding tight to a memory
The last of the wild, the last to be free

A saxophone sighs through the afternoon
Shadows lengthen far too soon
Children passing never look up high
At the last green thing beneath the sky

Oh, what we lose, we never see
Until there's only one left, one lonely tree
Upright bass walks slow and blue
Whispering all that we never knew

One lonely tree in a city of stone
Singing to the moon all on her own
Roots holding tight to a memory
The last of the wild, the last to be free

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent hum and the doors won't part
A thousand hammers in my chest, my heart
The tunnel swallows up the light
I'm holding on with all my might

Breathe, just breathe, the walls are wide
There's an ocean somewhere deep inside
Floating, falling, far away
Waiting for the swell to fade away
