Strangers blur into a wash of gray
Reverb swallows every word they say
The rails are singing somewhere down below
A shimmer in the dark, a fading glow

My hands are ghosts upon the cold steel pole
The train is moving but I've lost control
Synths like water over my closed eyes
I count the stations 'til the feeling dies

And it passes, like it always does
A quiet after all the noise it was
Daylight waiting at the final stop
The wave recedes, the hammers stop

Breathe, just breathe, the walls are wide
There's an ocean somewhere deep inside
Floating, falling, far away
Waiting for the swell to fade away

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Highway humming under restless wheels
Chasing the thunder, you know how it feels
A piano trickles like the coming rain
Never gonna stay, never gonna stay the same

Clouds rolling green on the open plain
She loves the lightning more than she loves the man
Muted trumpet crying down the road
Carrying a heart that she'll never unload

She can't settle, she can't slow
Wherever the wild winds blow she'll go
Love me at the eye of the storm
Then she's gone before the morning's warm

A brush, a cymbal, a low standing bass
No white picket fence could ever hold her place
She's married to the funnel and the falling sky
Kissing me goodbye with the thunder rolling by

Oh, I asked her once to lay her engine down
Build a little life in a little town
But she just smiled and watched the radar glow
Some hearts were born to chase, not know

She can't settle, she can't slow
Wherever the wild winds blow she'll go
Love me at the eye of the storm
Then she's gone before the morning's warm

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Four little words on a glowing screen
Coldest goodbye that I've ever seen
No phone call, baby, no front door
Just a message saying you don't love me no more

You ended us in a text, oh
Left me reading it again, oh
What we had deserved a voice
But you gave me pixels, gave me no choice

Rhodes piano weeping soft and slow
I keep scrolling up to the long time ago
When your words were warm and the nights were long
Now it's read receipts and a sad sad song

I typed a hundred answers, sent you none
Watched the little dots, then the dots were done
How do you delete what the heart still keeps
A love laid down where the data sleeps

Maybe it was easy from behind the glass
To close the door on everything we had
But I deserved the weight of your goodbye
Not a notification and a reason why

You ended us in a text, oh
Left me reading it again, oh
What we had deserved a voice
But you gave me pixels, gave me no choice

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The train pulls in at the break of day
Same old station, but I'm not the same way
Mama's on the platform with tears in her eyes
Thank the Lord above for these tired goodbyes

I'm coming home, coming home at last
Leaving all the thunder in the past
Lay my rifle down, lay my burden too
Coming home, sweet home, to you

The fields look smaller than they did before
But the porch light's burning like it did in the war
In my dreams at night I can still hear the guns
But I'm walking up the road where the river runs

Organ swells like a healing prayer
Running my hands through my baby's hair
The medals on my chest don't mean a thing
Next to the song that the morning birds sing

Lord, I've seen the things a man can't unsee
Carry them quiet, carry them with me
But here in these arms I am whole again
Home is the soldier, home from the wind

I'm coming home, coming home at last
Leaving all the thunder in the past
Lay my rifle down, lay my burden too
Coming home, sweet home, to you

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Bajo el cielo del norte voy cantando
Where the heavens dance in green and gold
The trumpets cry to the colors glowing
A story that the ancient skies have told

Ay, las luces del norte
Dancing for my love tonight
Ay, mi corazón
Burning like the borealis light

The violins weep beneath the aurora
Guitarrón steady like my beating heart
I'd cross the cold and the frozen rivers
Just to watch the heavens come apart

Vihuela strumming to the silver moon
My sombrero tipped to the painted sky
The colors swirl like a wild fandango
And I sing to the lights as they roll on by

Que viva, que viva la noche
Where the green fire kisses the snow
I'll dance until the dawn comes breaking
With the northern lights all aglow

Ay, las luces del norte
Dancing for my love tonight
Ay, mi corazón
Burning like the borealis light

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Packed my bag in the morning sun
This little town and me are done
Same old faces, same old street
Same old rhythm, same old beat

I'm leaving, leaving for good
Gonna find what I always knew I would
Goodbye to the town that I outgrew
Gonna shine like the morning dew

The offbeat guitar plays me down the road
Lighter steps and a lighter load
Mama said child you better stay
But my heart was always far away

The one stop light turns green for me
Never felt this kind of free
Dust on the road and the sky so wide
Got a brand new world on the other side

Don't cry for me when I'm gone
This is where I don't belong
Every river got to reach the sea
And this little town can't hold me

I'm leaving, leaving for good
Gonna find what I always knew I would
Goodbye to the town that I outgrew
Gonna shine like the morning dew

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The last whistle blows in the empty air
Dust on the windows, no one there
Machines fall silent one by one
Forty years and now it's done

The lights go out, the gates are closed
Where the river of our living flowed
Echo of the hands that built it all
Fading down the empty hall

Rust on the railings, weeds in the lot
Memories of everything we got
The hum that filled the morning gray
Drifting like a ghost away

Where will they go, the ones who stayed
The lives that all these walls had made
A quiet settles on the town
As the great machine winds down

Footsteps fading on the factory floor
Nobody's coming through that door
Silence where the engines roared
A chapter closed, a life restored

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Clock on the wall says a quarter past two
Coffee gone cold and I'm still not through
Bassline pumping just to keep me awake
One more chapter for my future's sake

Grindin' at two in the morning
Books and the funk and the day is dawning
Nobody here but the beat and me
Studyin' my way to where I wanna be

Highlighter yellow and my eyes are red
Shoulda been hours and hours in bed
Clavinet talking to my weary brain
Gotta pass this test, gotta ease this pain

The wah-wah cries like my tired soul
Flashcards stacked in a paper roll
Dance a little break just to shake the stress
Then back to the grind, I confess

One day this'll all be worth the fight
Burnin' that midnight oil so bright
Keep on pushin', don't you quit
The funk and the future are a perfect fit

Grindin' at two in the morning
Books and the funk and the day is dawning
Nobody here but the beat and me
Studyin' my way to where I wanna be

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up on the rooftop with a wire and a dream
Broadcasting love on a stolen beam
They can't catch me on the open air
Music for the people everywhere

Pirate radio, turn it up loud
Voice of the streets, voice of the crowd
They can't shut us down tonight
Spinning the truth on a frequency bright

Offbeat skanking through the midnight hour
Dub and the bass got the people power
The signal travels over hill and town
Can't keep the rhythm of the people down

The man come knocking but we're already gone
Moved the antenna and we carry on
Every little record is a freedom song
Playing for the millions all night long

From the tower to the tenement wall
This little station gonna reach them all
No license, no master, just the beat so true
Playing revolution just for you

Pirate radio, turn it up loud
Voice of the streets, voice of the crowd
They can't shut us down tonight
Spinning the truth on a frequency bright

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The sun comes up like it always will
Black suit hanging, the house so still
Empty chairs where the family sat
And I don't know what to do with that

Morning after, the tears run dry
We laid you down 'neath the open sky
Life goes on but it don't feel right
Walking through the morning light

The offbeat guitar plays soft and low
Got a coffee cup but nowhere to go
The flowers wilting on the kitchen table
Trying to be strong as much as I'm able

The birds still sing in the morning air
How can the world act like you're still there
A cool breeze blowing through the open door
Carrying the things I'm grieving for

Jah knows the weight that the heart can hold
The young grow weary and the brave grow old
But love don't end when the body's gone
It's a melody that carries on

Morning after, the tears run dry
We laid you down 'neath the open sky
Life goes on but it don't feel right
Walking through the morning light

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Finger on the digits, seven years gone cold
Dial tone humming like a story left untold
I been chasing money, chasing every neon light
Forgot the only number that could hold me through the night

Mama pick up, pick up the phone
I'm sorry it took silence to know I'm not grown
Mama pick up, the line is open wide
Let the funk in my heartbeat carry me inside

Groove on the bassline of a guilt I can't unhear
Every missed September stacking up another year
She says "baby, baby" before I say a word
And the whole room shaking with the truth I never heard

Mama pick up, pick up the phone
I'm sorry it took silence to know I'm not grown
Mama pick up, the line is open wide
Let the funk in my heartbeat carry me inside

Clap on the two and four, forgiveness in the pocket
She never changed the locks, she never closed the socket
I thought pride was a crown, turns out it was a chain
Now I'm dancing down the hallway, calling out her name

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Gravel and the railroad split the town clean in two
Boarded-up diner where my daddy's daddy grew
I packed a guitar and a duffel full of nerve
Last bus out of nowhere taking every bend and curve

I made it past the water tower, past the county line
Left my name carved in the oak but the rest of me is mine
So long to the dead-end, the dust and the gray
I'm a long gone holler on the highway today

Momma waved a dishrag from the porch she'll never leave
Said "go on, get gone, son, don't you cry on your sleeve"
The fields turned to billboards, the billboards turned to sky
And the only thing behind me was a reason not to die

I made it past the water tower, past the county line
Left my name carved in the oak but the rest of me is mine
So long to the dead-end, the dust and the gray
I'm a long gone holler on the highway today

They said no one ever leaves, they said the block's a grave
But I counted every crack and learned the way they cave
Now the mile markers rising like a hymn I never sang
And the dead-end's just a story 'bout the door I learned to hang

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Underground and the doors slammed shut
Fluorescent buzzing on a city gut
I'm a country boy in a steel-wheel cage
Heart kicking like a bronco off the page

Can't breathe in the tunnel, can't find the light
Everybody's a stranger and they're holding on tight
Lord, just stop this train or stop my chest
Give a drowning man a window and a minute to rest

Sweat on my collar, hands won't hold still
The map's a tangle and the air's gone shrill
I count the stations like I'd count the stars
Back home on a tailgate, not these iron bars

Can't breathe in the tunnel, can't find the light
Everybody's a stranger and they're holding on tight
Lord, just stop this train or stop my chest
Give a drowning man a window and a minute to rest

Then a stranger caught my eye, said "breathe with me now"
Four seconds in, hold it, let it out somehow
The platform rose up golden, the doors slid open slow
And I walked into the daylight learning how to let it go

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Flour on her knuckles, thread between her teeth
A whole village folded in the lines beneath
Those hands crossed an ocean with a candle and a name
Lit the Friday shadows into a steady flame

Oy, those hands, those weathered holy hands
They knead and they bless and they carry the lands
Clap for the wedding, wring for the dead
Every braid of the challah is a prayer she's read

Clarinet is laughing where her fingers used to fly
Buttons on my coat she sewed beneath a weeping sky
She never spoke the war but her palms remembered well
And the violin still carries every story they could tell

Oy, those hands, those weathered holy hands
They knead and they bless and they carry the lands
Clap for the wedding, wring for the dead
Every braid of the challah is a prayer she's read

Now my own hands are shaking as I light the wick alone
But I feel her fingers guiding, I am not on my own
Dance, little circle, let the freylekhs rise
Her hands are in my hands and they will never close their eyes

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Don Refugio threads his needle at the break of gold
His little shop on the corner where the stories get sold
A quinceañera's ruffles, a widower's black suit
He measures every shoulder like he's measuring the truth

¡Ay, el sastre, el sastre del barrio!
He dresses the whole block in his pride and his pano
From the baby to the abuelo, every stitch is a song
Trumpets for the tailor who keeps the neighborhood strong

He hemmed the groom on Sunday, mended Tuesday's torn flag
Gave a gift to the busboy who could not fill his bag
Nobody walks naked, nobody walks ashamed
When Don Refugio's chalk lines call you out by name

¡Ay, el sastre, el sastre del barrio!
He dresses the whole block in his pride and his pano
From the baby to the abuelo, every stitch is a song
Trumpets for the tailor who keeps the neighborhood strong

When they bury Don Refugio the whole street will arrive
In the suits and the dresses that he kept their dreams alive
And the violins will tremble for the man with the thread
Who clothed us all in dignity from the cradle to the bed

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Salt on your shoulders, July in your eyes
A week of red wine under bottomless skies
Your name like a wave that I cannot hold down
We danced on the sand till the moon turned to brown

¡Ay, mi amor de verano, you burn and you flee!
Like the foam on the tide you belong to the sea
Clap your hands, stamp your heel, let the guitar cry
A love made of August is a love born to die

We never spoke promises, only spoke now
Your kiss tasted of olives and salt on your brow
The town band was playing where the harbor lights swing
And I knew with the autumn you'd be gone on the wing

¡Ay, mi amor de verano, you burn and you flee!
Like the foam on the tide you belong to the sea
Clap your hands, stamp your heel, let the guitar cry
A love made of August is a love born to die

The deck chairs are folded, the season is through
But my heels still remember the rhythm of you
The last train is leaving and I won't say goodbye
Just a fistful of summer and a flamenco-stained sky

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
From a rooftop antenna held with wire and a prayer
We send out the corridos through the midnight air
The government can't find us, we move with the moon
Ninety-three point illegal, here comes the tune

¡Radio pirata! We sing for the poor
They can jam all the towers, we'll just build us some more
Turn it up, vecinos, let the trumpet break free
The voice of the barrio belongs to you and me

They banned all our music, said the people should be still
So we broadcast the heartbeat from the top of the hill
A grandmother's request, a striker's lonely call
The whole town is listening behind a stolen wall

¡Radio pirata! We sing for the poor
They can jam all the towers, we'll just build us some more
Turn it up, vecinos, let the trumpet break free
The voice of the barrio belongs to you and me

The vans are in the alley, the signal's running thin
But a hundred other rooftops just chimed back in
You can silence one trumpet, you can cut one wire
But you'll never catch a melody that's already on fire

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We drove past the last streetlight
Into the throat of the silent white
Nothing on the radio but static and breath
The whole sky holding still as death

And then it opened, green and slow
A river of fire we could not know
No words left, only the glow
The heavens learning how to flow

We killed the engine, we left the doors
Footprints fading on the frozen floors
The curtains rippled violet and gold
A story older than anything told

And then it opened, green and slow
A river of fire we could not know
No words left, only the glow
The heavens learning how to flow

We stood so small beneath the burning sky
And for once we did not ask it why
The drums come crashing, the strings ascend
A light that promises the dark will end
And we are nothing, and we are whole
The aurora pouring straight through the soul

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded the crates when the moon was thin
The old man warned us 'bout the thing within
Gold from a temple no hand should hold
But the captain only listened to the shine of gold

Eh, the cargo dey curse us, oya turn the boat
The sea no go let us, we no fit float
Drop it in the water, let the spirit go free
Before the wave wey dey hungry come and swallow me

Day three the compass start to spin and lie
The cook saw a woman walking dry across the sky
We dance on the deck just to chase the fear
But the drum in the hold beat loud and clear

Eh, the cargo dey curse us, oya turn the boat
The sea no go let us, we no fit float
Drop it in the water, let the spirit go free
Before the wave wey dey hungry come and swallow me

Captain held the gold like it could save his name
But the storm called his bluff and the lightning came
We threw it overboard as the dawn broke red
And the sea went quiet like it never said
Now we sail for home with empty hands and breath
No treasure worth the wage you pay in death

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew its last note Friday morning gray
Forty years of iron and they sent us all away
Clock card in my pocket but there's nothing left to clock
Just a chain across the gate and a padlock on the dock

The factory's closing down, Lord, the factory's closing down
They're shipping out the future and they're leaving us this town
My hands still know the motion but the line don't move no more
And the rust is creeping slow across the killing floor

My daddy bled this furnace, his daddy did the same
Now the foreman's crying too 'cause he carries all the blame
The diner's gonna empty, the church bell's gonna fade
When the only thing this town had built was everything we made

The factory's closing down, Lord, the factory's closing down
They're shipping out the future and they're leaving us this town
My hands still know the motion but the line don't move no more
And the rust is creeping slow across the killing floor

What does a man become when his trade is hauled away
When the dignity he earned just dies a little every day
I'll teach my boy the guitar 'stead of standing at the press
And pray he sings these blues someday for nothing but the rest

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Gray light over the cul-de-sac
A single suitcase, no looking back
The hum of a streetlamp letting go
Morning so quiet it barely shows

Drifting out, drifting slow
Past the only world I'll ever know
No sound but the breathing of the dawn
And the soft erasing of where I'm from

Doorways exhaling the years I spent
Every shadow knows where I went
The block dissolves into a haze
A fading map of childhood days

Drifting out, drifting slow
Past the only world I'll ever know
No sound but the breathing of the dawn
And the soft erasing of where I'm from

No crescendo, no triumphant horn
Just a tide that carries something newly born
The dead-end opens like a held breath freed
Into an ocean of the air I need

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Packed the whole band in a beat-up van
Drove up north on a half-baked plan
The trumpet's froze and the bassist's broke
But we chased the sky like it was a joke

Pick it up, pick it up, the lights are green!
Dancing skanking like you've never seen
The aurora's bouncing on the offbeat too
Up on the snow doing the northern boogaloo

The drummer slipped on a patch of ice
The saxophone honked like it asked us twice
But the curtains came down in purple and lime
And we played the upstroke right in time

Pick it up, pick it up, the lights are green!
Dancing skanking like you've never seen
The aurora's bouncing on the offbeat too
Up on the snow doing the northern boogaloo

Who needs a stage when the sky's a show
Flashing every color on the field of snow
Porkpie hats tipped back to see
The whole horizon dancing right along with me
So pick it up one more time tonight
We're a ska band burning underneath the light

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
The band struck soft and the floor went wide
They put us together, no place to hide
You stepped on my toe like you meant it, dear
And I smiled the smile that I keep for fear

So we sway, we sway, my enemy mine
To a gentle guitar and a glass of wine
Your hand on my back like a quiet truce
A bossa nova made of every old excuse

We traded our barbs in a whispered tone
Two proud little kings on a borrowed throne
But the rhythm kept folding us into one
Like the tide doesn't care who started or won

So we sway, we sway, my enemy mine
To a gentle guitar and a glass of wine
Your hand on my back like a quiet truce
A bossa nova made of every old excuse

Somewhere past the second verse I forgot to be cold
Your breath at my ear made the grudges grow old
Maybe rivals are just dancers who never learned the song
And the saddest little bossa is the one we sang too long

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Dusty vinyl crackle, rain against the pane
Close my eyes and I'm a kid on that linoleum again
Cinnamon and onions, something low and slow
A pot that's been forgiving since the long time ago

That smell, that smell, it pulls me back home
Where I never felt hungry and never felt alone
Grandma at the stove humming under her breath
A kitchen full of love that outlives even death

Boom-bap soft as slippers on the kitchen tile
She'd save me the burnt edge, hit me with that smile
Flour-dusted apron, radio turned low
Sugar in the cabinet and stories that she'd sow

That smell, that smell, it pulls me back home
Where I never felt hungry and never felt alone
Grandma at the stove humming under her breath
A kitchen full of love that outlives even death

The house got sold, the recipe got lost
But some Sunday afternoons it drifts back, no cost
A stranger's open window, a spice on the air
And just like that, for a second, she's still standing there

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Seventeen and shaking in a coffee-stained light
Four chords and a notebook on a Tuesday night
The mic was too tall and my voice was too thin
But I closed my eyes and I let the whole room in

Oh, I was so afraid, so impossibly young
With a galaxy of stage fright caught right on my tongue
Now it shimmers like a photograph left out in the sun
That trembling first song, that beautiful undone

Three strangers and a barista who forgot to clap
Reverb like a halo on a broken little map
I forgot the second verse, just hummed and let it ride
And something cracked wide open that I never could hide

Oh, I was so afraid, so impossibly young
With a galaxy of stage fright caught right on my tongue
Now it shimmers like a photograph left out in the sun
That trembling first song, that beautiful undone

If I could float back through the haze and the hush
I'd tell that shaking kid to love the rush
The fear was just the door, the song was on the other side
And everything I am began the night I tried

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You put your hand in mine like a loaded gun
I count the steps the way I'd count the ways you've won
The room is drowning in a wash of violet light
We move like enemies pretending it's alright

So turn me, turn me, hold the hate up close
We're spinning slow through everything we chose
I hate the way you fit against my chest
And hate that hating you is what I do the best

The strings dissolve into a hum I can't outrun
Your breath against my collar like a setting sun
I swore I'd never let you see me come undone
But here we are, two losers and there is no one won

So turn me, turn me, hold the hate up close
We're spinning slow through everything we chose
I hate the way you fit against my chest
And hate that hating you is what I do the best

If the music stops we go back to the war
But god the music's louder than it was before
So don't let go, don't let go, don't let the silence in
Let's lose like this until we can't tell lose from win

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Every evening when the gulls go gray
I fold the paper, watch it sway
I write your name in salt and ink
More than the careful clocks would think

Oh sea, my slow blue letter never answered
Oh sea, you take my pages, never sign
Still I light the lamp and dance us through the dark
Pretending all this distance could be mine

A bottle bobs and floats away
With all the things I couldn't say
The tide comes in like easy brass
And lays my longing on the glass

Oh sea, my slow blue letter never answered
Oh sea, you take my pages, never sign
Still I light the lamp and dance us through the dark
Pretending all this distance could be mine

The foghorn moans a minor key
A lover's note from me to thee
And if I drown, well, let me drown
In every word I sent you down

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I've got a desk and a forty-watt bulb
And a stack of envelopes nobody calls
I write you letters every single night
Then burn them in the beam of the light

Hey ocean, I'm yours, I'm the man on the rock
Counting your waves like the hands of a clock
You never write back but you never leave
And that's more than I got from anyone, believe

The radio crackles, the coastguard's bored
They think I'm out here keeping score
But I'm just a guy with a salt-stained heart
In love with the thing that keeps us apart

Hey ocean, I'm yours, I'm the man on the rock
Counting your waves like the hands of a clock
You never write back but you never leave
And that's more than I got from anyone, believe

Maybe one day a boat won't come
And I'll fold myself into the spray
Dear sea, I'll mail myself at last
And finally hear what you've been trying to say

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Soft neon humming, two in the morning
Coffee like velvet, the city is yawning
A waitress is swaying to nothing at all
While rain writes its name down the glass on the wall

Stay open, stay open, my little gold room
The night is so wide and the moon is in bloom
We've got pie and we've got time and a slow ceiling fan
Come in from the dark, love, as soft as you can

A trucker, a poet, a girl who can't sleep
The jukebox is playing a promise to keep
The griddle is singing a low samba hush
And nobody's leaving, there's nowhere to rush

Stay open, stay open, my little gold room
The night is so wide and the moon is in bloom
We've got pie and we've got time and a slow ceiling fan
Come in from the dark, love, as soft as you can

When the sun comes to find us we'll blink and we'll smile
We borrowed the darkness, we kept it a while
So pour me another, the dawn can just wait
There's bossa in the booth and a song on my plate

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Eight hours a day in a marble hall
I stand by the rope, I lean on the wall
But every shift my eyes come back to you
A woman in oil and a sky painted blue

Oh, you can't hold me, but baby you do
The whole museum sleeps and it's me and it's you
They lock up the door, turn the lights down low
And I swear that you smile 'fore I have to go

The tourists walk by and they snap and they leave
They don't see the thing that I can't quite believe
A hundred years gone and you're still holding on
To a look that you gave to somebody long gone

Oh, you can't hold me, but baby you do
The whole museum sleeps and it's me and it's you
They lock up the door, turn the lights down low
And I swear that you smile 'fore I have to go

Someday they'll move you to a finer wall
And I'll work my last and they won't even call
But I'll keep your face like a song I once knew
The loneliest love that a man ever drew

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Iron hull groaning under blackened skies
Something in the hold that breathes and never dies
The captain signed the manifest in blood he didn't read
Now every soul aboard will pay the debt of greed

Drag it across the ocean, the curse beneath the deck
A thousand miles of nothing and a crew that's bound to wreck
We carry what we cannot name, we feed it through the night
The cargo of the damned will never see the light

The compass spins in circles, the stars have turned to rust
The rations turn to ashes, the prayers all turn to dust
We hear it in the timbers, a heartbeat in the wood
We should have burned the manifest, we should have, but we could

Drag it across the ocean, the curse beneath the deck
A thousand miles of nothing and a crew that's bound to wreck
We carry what we cannot name, we feed it through the night
The cargo of the damned will never see the light

Throw it overboard! the bosun screamed and died
The chains rose up like serpents from the cold and churning tide
No port will ever take us, no grave will hold us down
We sail until the world ends, cursed cargo, let us drown

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
We sat down Friday with a bottle and a deck
Four honest men with nothing much to wreck
The lamp burned low and the pot grew tall
And somewhere in the night we lost it all

Lay 'em down, lay 'em down, the dawn's gonna come
With more than we wagered and less than some
I bet my father's watch and the keys to the door
It started as a game but it went too far

Old Tom put his farm on a pair of red queens
And Danny put up everything he means
The cards don't care for a hungry man's plea
And the river turned cruel as a river can be

Lay 'em down, lay 'em down, the dawn's gonna come
With more than we wagered and less than some
I bet my father's watch and the keys to the door
It started as a game but it went too far

There's a chair that stays empty at the table tonight
And a debt that won't settle by morning light
We don't deal those cards in this town anymore
Some games you stop playing 'fore they go too far

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
I took the long way home again
Though home don't mean the same
I rolled on past your building, girl
And whispered out your name

There's a light in the window that isn't for me
Somebody else living where we used to be
I keep my hands steady, I keep the wheels slow
Driving past the place I can't let go

The streetlight still flickers the way that it did
When you'd lean on the railing, a kiss that we hid
I could swear I still see you out there in the rain
But it's just an old shadow and the same old pain

There's a light in the window that isn't for me
Somebody else living where we used to be
I keep my hands steady, I keep the wheels slow
Driving past the place I can't let go

I should turn at the corner, I should give it a rest
But the heart wants the wound that it loves the best
So I'll circle the block one more time tonight
And let your old window keep me up with its light

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
The hill is green where they laid you down
The wind comes cold from the harbor town
I carried this anger like a stone in my coat
Through every long winter, through every choked throat

So I forgive you, I forgive you at last
Though the words come too late and the years have all passed
Let the heather remember, let the gray water know
I set down the grudge that I carried so slow

You hurt me in ways that I never could say
And you slipped from the world 'fore I learned how to pray
Now there's no door to knock on, no hand left to hold
Just a name on a stone and a story grown old

So I forgive you, I forgive you at last
Though the words come too late and the years have all passed
Let the heather remember, let the gray water know
I set down the grudge that I carried so slow

The fiddle plays low and the fire burns down
And I lay a white flower on the cold of the ground
Forgiveness ain't for you, love, it's a gift to my own
A mercy I give to the both of us, alone

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Mile marker nothing on a road with no name
The dashboard glows green and the night looks the same
I dial the old number, I let it ring twice
Your voice in the dark is the only thing nice

It's three in the morning, I'm sorry I called
The coffee's gone cold and the radio stalled
Just tell me the kids are okay in their beds
And I'll drive through the dark with your voice in my head

There's eight hundred miles between me and the door
And a load I gotta drop by a quarter to four
The white lines keep coming, they never let up
So I talk to you slow with a half-empty cup

It's three in the morning, I'm sorry I called
The coffee's gone cold and the radio stalled
Just tell me the kids are okay in their beds
And I'll drive through the dark with your voice in my head

Go back to sleep now, I'll be home by the weekend
I'll fix the back fence and I'll mean what I say
Don't hang up just yet, let me hear you breathing
It's the closest to home that I get on the way

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The screen lights up the ceiling in a cold electric blue
My thumb is on the line I swore I'd never cross to you
The house is full of breathing of the life I built so slow
And one word in the dark could be the match that lets it go

Don't send it, don't send it, just let the cursor blink
The whole world's on a wire and it's thinner than you think
But my hand has its own hunger and the night is wide and deep
And I'm typing what I shouldn't while the honest people sleep

Three dots and then they vanish, then they rise up like a tide
There's a door I keep on opening to everything I hide
I know exactly where this goes, I've seen the wreck before
Still I'm standing at the threshold with my hand against the door

Delete it, delete it, let the silence be the proof
That I loved the ones I promised more than I loved the truth
But the message glows like morning and I cannot look away
So forgive me, forgive me, for the things I didn't say

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Me ofrecen el dinero, dale firma aquí
Cambia tus palabras por un beat de MP3
Pero yo escribo verdad, no escribo pa' vender
Mi pluma no se compra, eso lo van a aprender

No, no, no me vendo, mi voz no tiene precio
No, no, no me rindo, prefiero ser el necio
Que baile la calle con la rima de verdad
Un poeta no se calla por la publicidad

Luces de la disco, contratos en la mano
Me dicen suaviza, sé un poco más liviano
Pero el barrio me escucha, el barrio sabe quién soy
Y al barrio no le miento por la fama de hoy

No, no, no me vendo, mi voz no tiene precio
No, no, no me rindo, prefiero ser el necio
Que baile la calle con la rima de verdad
Un poeta no se calla por la publicidad

Puedo ser pobre pero mi verso es millonario
Cada línea es mi bandera, cada estrofa mi rosario
Que se queden con el oro, yo me quedo con la voz
Un poeta sin cadenas, ese soy, ese soy yo

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
We came in laughing on a Saturday night
Upstroke guitars and the table set tight
Nickels and dimes turned to twenties and more
The horns hit the downbeat, somebody kept score

Pick it up, pick it up, the stakes climbing higher
Everybody's bluffing, the whole room's on fire
I went all in on a two-step heart attack
It started as a game but there's no turning back

The trumpet was sweating, the bassline got mean
Freddie bet his scooter and the rent in between
The dealer just grinned with a cigarette glow
And the pot got too big for a joke, don't you know

Pick it up, pick it up, the stakes climbing higher
Everybody's bluffing, the whole room's on fire
I went all in on a two-step heart attack
It started as a game but there's no turning back

Now the band's playing slower, the room's gone quiet and tense
There's a fist on the table and it don't make sense
So I cashed out my chips and I skanked for the door
Some games ain't worth winning when they go too far

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I found your number in a drawer of old receipts
It took me twenty minutes just to leave my seat
The phone felt heavy like a thing I shouldn't hold
And all the words I rehearsed just turned to cold

Hi Mom, it's me, I know it's been a while
I hear you catch your breath and I can almost feel you smile
I'm sorry for the silence, I'm sorry for the years
It only took a dial tone to bring me back to tears

There's so much I rehearsed and none of it came out
Just the sound of two people learning how to talk it out
You ask about the weather, I ask about your knee
And somewhere in the small talk there's the kid I used to be

Hi Mom, it's me, I know it's been a while
I hear you catch your breath and I can almost feel you smile
I'm sorry for the silence, I'm sorry for the years
It only took a dial tone to bring me back to tears

Maybe we don't fix it in a single afternoon
Maybe pride's a canyon that we cross a little soon
But I'll call you next Sunday, that's a promise I can keep
And we'll fill up all the silence one slow word at a time

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The lawyers signed the papers, but the band's still in the hall
They're playing our old favorite, the one from the start of it all
You reach across the distance, you say one more for the road
And I take your hand like always, though we're letting the whole thing go

So dance with me one final time
Forty years on a fading line
We were good before we were through
One last dance, just me and you

I know every step you'll take before you take it, love
The way you hum the bridge part, the way you fit my glove
We couldn't make it work but God, we made it long
And here at the ending, we still know all the words to the song

So dance with me one final time
Forty years on a fading line
We were good before we were through
One last dance, just me and you

When the music stops we'll walk to separate cars
Under the same old patient stars
But for now there's a floor and a slow guitar
And I remember exactly who we are

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Forty years of linoleum, my shoes still know the squeak
You kept the radio dial right where it landed Tuesday week
We wore a circle in the kitchen, two old planets in a groove
Nothin' left to prove tonight, just one more way to move

So turn it up, turn it down low
We got one more spin before we let it go
Hand on my hip like a quarter in a jar
Dance me to the doorway, baby, just as you are

The lawyer's got his paperwork, the boxes got our names
But the bassline don't read contracts and the snare don't take the blame
Your gray came in like silver thread, mine fell out by the door
Gimme this, the last one, then I won't ask no more

So turn it up, turn it down low
We got one more spin before we let it go
Hand on my hip like a quarter in a jar
Dance me to the doorway, baby, just as you are

The clock can keep its ticking, let it tick against the wall
We were never good at endings, so we won't end at all
Just a fade out, not a stop
One foot, then the other, till the needle lifts on top

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
The oil is too hot, it spits like it's angry
Half an onion sweats on the board, the other half I throw
Your pan still hangs where your hand always reached
I cook for a ghost who won't come when I call

One plate, one fork, one chair pulled to the table
Salt for two hands, but only mine remain
Clap once for the living, clap twice for the gone
I'll learn to taste the bitter and call it my own

You measured with your wrist and never with a cup
I measure with my grief and the pot boils over
The bread goes stale before I finish a loaf
So I feed the pigeons your half on the sill

One plate, one fork, one chair pulled to the table
Salt for two hands, but only mine remain
Clap once for the living, clap twice for the gone
I'll learn to taste the bitter and call it my own

The guitar in my chest keeps the rhythm of the knife
Faster, faster, then a sudden held breath
I set the second plate down out of habit, out of love
And I eat across from no one, and I call it enough

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the gray around my feet one summer I forgot
The parking lines run under me like ribs beneath the lot
I hold a single sparrow and a square of borrowed sky
The crosswalk hums a current where the river used to lie

Still green, still here
A flag nobody planted, a clock with no hands near
The wind remembers forests when it combs through my one crown
The last lung of a swallowed town

A child once carved initials, now the bark has closed the wound
The streetlight is my false moon and it never lets me swoon
The roots go searching downward, finding pipe and cable hum
Drinking what the rain forgot, waiting for the green to come

Louder now, the engines, then a silence none expect
When the headlights finally sleep and the meters all collect
I drop one leaf at midnight on the asphalt like a coin
For whoever's left to notice where the wild and concrete join

Still green, still here
A flag nobody planted, a clock with no hands near
The wind remembers forests when it combs through my one crown
The last lung of a swallowed town

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew its softest note, a question, not a call
The time cards hang like dried-up leaves against the painted wall
Maria sweeps a floor that won't need sweeping after this
The machines exhale their last warm breath and settle into mist

Slow, so slow, the gates are swinging
No more morning, no more bell
The river keeps on moving, gentle
We wave goodbye, we wish it well

The coffee's gone cold in the cup that thirty years have held
The foreman folds his glasses and his shoulders softly meld
The forklift rests its yellow arms, the conveyor learns to sleep
We gathered crumbs of paychecks here, and that was ours to keep

Somewhere there's a samba and the lights are coming on
We'll find another rhythm when this quiet song is done
But for now we lock the door so soft it doesn't make a sound
And carry home the silence of a factory closing down

Slow, so slow, the gates are swinging
No more morning, no more bell
The river keeps on moving, gentle
We wave goodbye, we wish it well

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five dollars, set the clock, I'll spot you both my rooks
You read a thousand games, but son, I read a thousand looks
Pigeons walk the marble like they paid for the position
I been mayor of this table since before your intuition
Knight to f3, sip my coffee gone lukewarm
Your confidence a sandcastle, here come my little storm
The board's a city block and every pawn pays me rent
You flick the clock so cocky, but you don't know where it went

Clock tick, piece click, money on the slate
King falls, crowd calls, never met a man named fate
I don't play for trophies, I play to eat tonight
Five dollars and the daylight, and the moves come out right

Got a regular named Walter, lost to me since ninety-nine
He brings me cigarettes and stories, says the loss is worth his time
I sacrifice a bishop just to watch your eyes get wide
Then the queen come down the diagonal, nowhere left to hide

Winter thins the players out, but I'm there with my gloves cut
Blowin' warmth into my hands while the cold tries to shut
The park down, but I'm staying, 'cause the kings don't take a day
And as long as someone's brave enough, I got a game to play

Clock tick, piece click, money on the slate
King falls, crowd calls, never met a man named fate
I don't play for trophies, I play to eat tonight
Five dollars and the daylight, and the moves come out right

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Same red dust on both our shoes, same auntie sold us bread
You used to chase me down the lane, now you living in my head
We drank from the same tap water, prayed beneath the same tin roof
I traveled far to find a love, and the love was always proof

Girl, you from my street, my street
Where the generator hums and the night is sweet
I been around the world and the world had nothing
You were home all along, you were home all along

The market man remembers when we both could barely walk
Now he winks and packs the mango when he hears the way we talk
Your mother knows my mother, so the whole town's gonna know
By the morning it's a wedding, by the evening it's a show

Girl, you from my street, my street
Where the generator hums and the night is sweet
I been around the world and the world had nothing
You were home all along, you were home all along

Same moon over the rooftops that we counted as a child
Same song from the same speaker, and it's driving me wild
Why I cross the whole ocean when the treasure dey for here
Dance with me on the same ground, let the whole town hear

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors close, no air, the tunnel swallows light
My heartbeat doing double-time, the bassline of a fright
Forty strangers' shoulders and not one of them can tell
That the man between the poles is ringing his own private bell
Breathe in, can't find it, the count of four won't land
My hands forget they're hands, my plan forgets the plan
The map above the window melts to colors with no name
And the express skips my station like the world skips my name

Hold the rail, hold the rail, count the lights as they go by
It's a wave, not a wall, gonna crest and then it'll die
Nobody's looking, nobody knows the war
Between this breath and the next one, between the floor and the floor

Kid across is gaming, got his earbuds in deep
A woman reads her novel, an old man's half asleep
And I'm a thunderstorm folded into a winter coat
With a scream stuck sideways lodged inside my throat

Then the lights of the platform, then the cool of the air
Then my name comes back to me like it never wasn't there
I step onto the concrete and I let the train depart
And nobody applauds the quiet landing of my heart

Hold the rail, hold the rail, count the lights as they go by
It's a wave, not a wall, gonna crest and then it'll die
Nobody's looking, nobody knows the war
Between this breath and the next one, between the floor and the floor

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I folded my whole life into a single canvas case
My mother's voice on the dock got smaller than her face
The water turned to glass then turned to glass that wouldn't end
And every wave behind me was a letter I can't send

Across the sea, across the sea
I'm carrying my country in the marrow, deep in me
They'll spell my name wrong, but they'll learn it slow
I'm planting something new in the only soil I know

The harbor lights ahead of me are strangers wearing gold
I got my grandfather's prayer and a coat against the cold
The salt got in my eyes, or maybe that was something else
I traded all my mirrors for a chance at someone else

What I left, I left it whole, I didn't break a thing
Just loosened all my fingers from the only home they cling
And when the gangway lowers and the new world says my name
I'll answer in two languages, and neither one's the same

Across the sea, across the sea
I'm carrying my country in the marrow, deep in me
They'll spell my name wrong, but they'll learn it slow
I'm planting something new in the only soil I know

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
The pew still fits the shape my knees once memorized
The stained glass throws its colors where I used to close my eyes
I know which floorboard sighs, I know the hymn before it starts
I'm leaving through the side door of a building in my heart

No slamming, no thunder, just a hand off the rail
The candle keeps its burning where my footsteps grow pale
I'm not angry at the morning light through the colored air
I'm just learning how to carry what I'll leave back there

The baptism water's somewhere underneath my given name
The steeple watched me growing, and it watched me grow ashamed
I fold my hands from habit, then I let them come apart
And the silence isn't empty, it's the start of a new start

The bell will ring on Sunday and I won't be in the sound
But something of the quiet will still hold me to the ground
I close the heavy door so soft it barely makes a seam
And I walk into the open like a body leaving a dream

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded crates at midnight that the captain wouldn't name
The priest refused to bless them, and the ocean knew our shame
The compass spins like a dancer who has had too much to drink
And the gold inside the hold is heavier than we think

Ay, the cargo, ay, the curse
Every mile we sail, the silence gets worse
The trumpets cry like seagulls over water black as wine
We carry someone's sorrow across the salt and brine

The cook says he saw faces in the foam along the side
The youngest sailor's hair turned white the second night we tried
We cannot throw it overboard, the chains have grown to bone
Whatever's in those wooden ribs has claimed us as its own

Violins against the storm, we play to stay alive
If the music never stops, then maybe some of us survive
The harbor lights are mocking us, so close and yet so far
We'll dock this ghost in the morning if we live to see the star

Ay, the cargo, ay, the curse
Every mile we sail, the silence gets worse
The trumpets cry like seagulls over water black as wine
We carry someone's sorrow across the salt and brine

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Grandfather sits where the water meets the worn-down stair
He talks to the ocean low, like it's family there
He says, come in slow today, the children dey for play
And somehow the waves obey, somehow they know his way

Old man and the water, old man and the moon
He been keeping the rhythm since before any tune
Teach the tide, teach the tide, what the ancestors said
The sea got a memory, and it lives in his head

He read the foam like letters, he name each kind of swell
He got a scar for every storm he learned to ride and tell
The young ones laugh and doubt him till the weather proves him right
Then they sit beside the elder when the fishing boats take flight

One day the chair go empty and the water go ask why
The tide go look for the old man underneath the morning sky
But he poured himself in the children, every lesson, every song
So the ocean still get a teacher, and the rhythm carry on

Old man and the water, old man and the moon
He been keeping the rhythm since before any tune
Teach the tide, teach the tide, what the ancestors said
The sea got a memory, and it lives in his head

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
We grew up six blocks distant on the same forgetful street
Never once collided till a rainy night downtown we'd meet
You ordered what my mother made, you hummed what my dad would play
And the whole geography of me rearranged that day

Imagine that, imagine that
The long way 'round the world to the corner where I'm at
Same cracked sidewalk, same diner neon glow
You were the one I should have met a lifetime ago

You know the bridge they almost built, the river's other name
We trade the small mythologies that only locals claim
The piano takes its time tonight, the brushes whisper low
And I'm falling for the accent that's the same as my own

Funny how a person can be standing close as breath
For twenty years of seasons, twenty winters' little death
Then the trumpet hits a blue note and the room goes soft and gold
And the girl from down the avenue is mine to have and hold

Imagine that, imagine that
The long way 'round the world to the corner where I'm at
Same cracked sidewalk, same diner neon glow
You were the one I should have met a lifetime ago

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
We carry him slow through the Tremé heat
A dirge in the brass and a drag in our feet
The handkerchiefs lifting, the trombone weeps low
The casket rides high where the live oaks grow
Then somebody whispers, he wouldn't want tears
He wanted the rhythm to outrun the years
The snare starts to stutter, the tempo takes flight
And grief breaks to dancing somewhere past the light

Second line, second line, lift him on the sound
We bury the body but the spirit won't drown
Faster, faster, let the trumpets ignite
We cry in the morning and we dance through the night

The parasols spinning like wheels in the sun
The bassline goes double, the mourning's undone
The city pours out from the porches and bars
To follow a soul on its way to the stars

Breakdown, hold it, just the kick and a moan
Then the whole street erupts and nobody's alone
The drums hit like rain on a tin-covered town
We send him off flying, we don't lay him down

Second line, second line, lift him on the sound
We bury the body but the spirit won't drown
Faster, faster, let the trumpets ignite
We cry in the morning and we dance through the night

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They slid a contract 'cross the bar, said sign your tongue away
Put the teeth back in your verses, make it smile and make it pay
I tore it into snowfall and I watched it hit the floor
My words ain't for the auction, they're the lock and not the door

NO! You can't buy the fire in my chest!
NO! I'll starve with the truth before I feast on the rest!
Every line is a knuckle, every page is a fist
I'd rather be forgotten than be something on your list

The gallery men in linen want a softer kind of rage
A pretty little protest they can frame behind the glass
But my notebook smells like gasoline and basements after rain
And I'd sooner eat the paper than I'd water down the pain

They ask me what's my number, what's the price to make me kneel
I laugh until it's bleeding, 'cause the answer isn't real
There's no zero with enough zeroes, there's no check that I can't burn
I've got nothing and I've got everything they'll never learn

NO! You can't buy the fire in my chest!
NO! I'll starve with the truth before I feast on the rest!
Every line is a knuckle, every page is a fist
I'd rather be forgotten than be something on your list

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The casseroles are crowding every shelf inside the fridge
The black dress on the doorknob is a flag I cannot ditch
The coffee's brewing normal like the world forgot to stop
And the sunlight through the curtain doesn't know that he's not

Well, the band plays on, the band plays on
The milkman came at seven though the reason that I wept is gone
So I'll butter up my toast and I'll put my stockings on
And I'll swing through this morning 'cause the morning carries on

The phone keeps ringing kindness till the kindness wears me thin
I'll thank them all for nothing 'cause there's nothing they can bring
His razor's by the basin and his slippers by the bed
And I make the coffee for two before I count to ten instead

There's a strange and clumsy lightness in the having-made-it-through
Like the band hit the wrong chord but they're playing it for you
I'll laugh before I mean to and I'll cry inside the sink
And the day will be a dance step that's much harder than I think

Well, the band plays on, the band plays on
The milkman came at seven though the reason that I wept is gone
So I'll butter up my toast and I'll put my stockings on
And I'll swing through this morning 'cause the morning carries on

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Neon fading on the open field
Your hand in mine, this moment's real
The music slowing, the lights go low
I don't wanna be the one to let it go

Dust on our shoes from the days gone by
A thousand strangers under one sky
The speakers humming their final song
We knew this feeling couldn't last that long

One more night, one more sound
Hold me close before the lights come down
Don't say goodbye, just stay with me
The last night of the festival, baby, let it be

Morning's coming but I'm not ready
Your heartbeat keeping mine so steady
Tents are folding, the crowd grows thin
I'd give it all to do it all again

One more night, one more sound
Hold me close before the lights come down
Don't say goodbye, just stay with me
The last night of the festival, baby, let it be

When the sun comes up and we drive away
I'll keep this fire from fading away
Every beat, every breath we shared
Proof that for a moment, we were really there

One more night, one more sound
Hold me close before the lights come down
Don't say goodbye, just stay with me
The last night of the festival, baby, let it be

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They put us in the center of the hall
Your eyes like daggers up against the wall
The fiddle started and we had no choice
Two old enemies, one trembling voice

Your hand found mine like a reluctant friend
I swore I'd never let this fire mend
But the music turned and the floorboards spun
And somewhere in the turning we were one

So dance with me, my enemy
Let the old wounds fall away
The fiddle don't care who's wrong or right
We'll settle it another day

The crowd was clapping, the lanterns swayed
Forgot the war that we had made
Your stubborn grin breaking through the cold
A truce was struck in the steps of old

So dance with me, my enemy
Let the old wounds fall away
The fiddle don't care who's wrong or right
We'll settle it another day

Maybe rivals are just friends afraid
Of the kindness that they might have made
When the song is done we'll part our ways
But I'll remember this for all my days

So dance with me, my enemy
Let the old wounds fall away
The fiddle don't care who's wrong or right
We'll settle it another day

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine running in the dead of night
Dust on the dashboard, hands gripped tight
One more run and then I'm through
Gonna leave this life and start anew

The radio's crackling, the boys are inside
I promised her this was my last ride
The money's clean, the road is long
Just gotta hold it till the deal is done

Last job, last mile
Wheels keep turning down the line
I'm driving home to where you are
Under that old Texas star

Sirens screaming in the rearview glass
Foot to the floor, I'm flying fast
Thought about the ring I bought for you
Gotta make it back, gotta see it through

Last job, last mile
Wheels keep turning down the line
I'm driving home to where you are
Under that old Texas star

They say a man can't outrun his past
But I'm betting on this engine to make it last
One clean break and a county line
And a girl who'll say that I'm still hers and mine

Last job, last mile
Wheels keep turning down the line
I'm driving home to where you are
Under that old Texas star

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
El humo de la estufa, café por la mañana
Abuela en la cocina, la luz por la ventana
Arroz con habichuela, el sazón en el aire
Un olor que me lleva a un tiempo sin pesares

Ese olor, ese olor (ey)
Me devuelve a mi hogar
La cocina de abuela, no hay nada igual
Ese olor, ese olor (ja)
Me hace volver a empezar
La cocina de abuela, mi lugar

El sofrito sonando, la olla cantando
Las manos de mi vida siempre cocinando
Un beso en la frente, un plato pa' llenarme
Abuela en la cocina, nunca vas a faltarme

Ese olor, ese olor (ey)
Me devuelve a mi hogar
La cocina de abuela, no hay nada igual
Ese olor, ese olor (ja)
Me hace volver a empezar
La cocina de abuela, mi lugar

Y aunque ya no estás, te siento en el aroma
En cada platillo tu amor se asoma
Cierro los ojos y vuelvo a ese lugar
La cocina de abuela, mi altar

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Walked in the parlor with a dollar and a grin
The smoke was thick and the night was thin
Dealt me a hand and I felt so alive
Little did I know I wouldn't make it out by five

Oh the cards, they keep on falling
And the stakes keep climbing higher
I bet my watch, I bet my coat
Now I'm playing with the fire

The ace up my sleeve and a queen on the felt
Never felt a sweat the way I felt
The pot was growing, the boys leaned in
A gamble that no honest man could win

Oh the cards, they keep on falling
And the stakes keep climbing higher
I bet my watch, I bet my coat
Now I'm playing with the fire

Should've folded when I had the chance
But a fool in love with a losing dance
The sun came up and my pockets bare
Learned my lesson in that smoky air

Oh the cards, they keep on falling
And the stakes keep climbing higher
I bet it all, I lost it all
Now I'm walking through the fire

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I knelt down empty on a Sunday floor
The words I used to say don't mean it anymore
The ceiling cracked and the light went gray
And everything I trusted slipped away

Walked through the winter with a hollow chest
Questioning the things I once confessed
The silence louder than a thousand prayers
Nobody answered, nobody was there

But I'm still here, I'm still breathing
Something in me won't stop believing
I lost it all and I found it slow
In the cracks where the small lights grow

Found a little grace in a stranger's hand
A kindness I could never understand
Maybe God's not in the steeple high
But in the quiet way we get by

But I'm still here, I'm still breathing
Something in me won't stop believing
I lost it all and I found it slow
In the cracks where the small lights grow

It wasn't thunder, it wasn't fire
Just a flicker lifting me higher
Faith ain't certain, faith ain't loud
It's the candle burning through the doubt

And I'm still here, I'm still breathing
Something in me won't stop believing
I lost it all and I found it slow
In the cracks where the small lights grow

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Bajo la tierra el tren me lleva
El corazón se me acelera
Las luces tiemblan, el aire se va
Y no encuentro la salida ya

Golpea el pecho como un tambor
Manos de hielo, frente de sudor
La gente pasa, nadie me ve
Ahogado en un mar que yo no crucé

¡Ay, respira, respira, corazón!
Que el miedo no es tu dueño, no
Entre las sombras hay una luz
Que te saca del túnel, que eres tú

El vagón se cierra, no hay donde ir
Mil pensamientos quieren huir
Cuento los pasos, cuento hasta diez
Y el suelo vuelve bajo mis pies

¡Ay, respira, respira, corazón!
Que el miedo no es tu dueño, no
Entre las sombras hay una luz
Que te saca del túnel, que eres tú

La estación se acerca, abre la puerta
El alma rota despierta
No estoy solo aunque lo parezca
Mañana este miedo se va y amanezca

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
En el metro, sin aire, no puedo respirar
El corazón a mil, no lo puedo parar
Las paredes se cierran, todo da vueltas ya
Mil personas alrededor pero solo estoy yo na' má'

Respira, respira (oh-oh)
Que esto pasa, no es pa' siempre (no-no)
Respira, respira (ja)
Vas a salir, créeme, créeme

Sudor en la frente, las manos temblando
El vagón en movimiento y yo me estoy ahogando
Cuento los segundos pa' llegar a la estación
Dile a mi mente que apague esta alarma sin razón

Respira, respira (oh-oh)
Que esto pasa, no es pa' siempre (no-no)
Respira, respira (ja)
Vas a salir, créeme, créeme

Abre la puerta, sube la escalera
El aire de la calle ya me espera
No soy mi miedo, soy más que esta noche
Mañana brillo como el sol que se asoma

Respira, respira (oh-oh)
Que esto pasa, no es pa' siempre (no-no)
Respira, respira (ja)
Vas a salir, créeme, créeme

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Sun going down on the old hometown
Sand in my pocket, no need to frown
Tomorrow I pack up and drive away
But tonight, oh tonight, we gonna stay

One love, one last summer day
Don't let the good times fade away
We'll carry this feeling wherever we go
The last day of summer, take it slow

Friends on the beach with a fire glowing
Waves keep rolling, the breeze keeps blowing
New roads calling but my heart stays here
Hold on to the moment, hold it near

One love, one last summer day
Don't let the good times fade away
We'll carry this feeling wherever we go
The last day of summer, take it slow

We grew up under this golden light
Now we're chasing dreams into the night
But every wave gonna whisper my name
And nothing between us gonna stay the same

One love, one last summer day
Don't let the good times fade away
We'll carry this feeling wherever we go
The last day of summer, take it slow

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Two plates in the cupboard but I only need one
The kitchen's awful quiet now that you're gone
You always did the cooking, I just set the table
Now I'm burning toast as best as I am able

But I'm learning, baby, learning how to live
One cup of coffee and a little left to give
The recipe you left me's getting me along
Cooking for one but humming our old song

Measured out the flour like you showed me how
Talking to your picture, I can hear you now
"A pinch of salt, my darling, and a little love"
I swear I feel you smiling from somewhere up above

But I'm learning, baby, learning how to live
One cup of coffee and a little left to give
The recipe you left me's getting me along
Cooking for one but humming our old song

The table's set for one but my heart's set for two
Every little supper is a love note to you
I'll keep on stirring this pot till the day I'm through
And I'll save you a seat at the table, I do

So I'm learning, baby, learning how to live
One cup of coffee and a little left to give
The recipe you left me's getting me along
Cooking for one but humming our old song

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Softly the silence between our two hands
Floating away over endless black sands
Your tether unwinding, a thread in the dark
Two little embers, each fading their spark

The Earth is a marble so blue and so small
We whispered forever but said nothing at all
The stars don't reply and the radio's gray
And slowly, so slowly, you're drifting away

Don't let go, don't let go
But the cold space between us only grows
Reach for my hand in the velvet of night
Two drifting astronauts losing the light

I count every breath in my fogging glass dome
Watching you shrink into nowhere, no home
A waltz with no music, a kiss with no air
Gravity gone and there's nothing to share

Don't let go, don't let go
But the cold space between us only grows
Reach for my hand in the velvet of night
Two drifting astronauts losing the light

Maybe in orbit we'll meet once again
Two lonely comets remembering when
For now I'll just float in the quiet and blue
And send all my silence as starlight to you

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Last roll of tape and the room's all bare
Dust on the floor where the couch used to be there
Echo off the walls of a life I once knew
Sealing up the memories, me and you

Tape it up, close it slow
One more box and then I go
Empty room, open door
Won't be living here no more

Found a ticket stub from a night back when
Never thought I'd have to pack it up again
Keys on the counter, the light bulb's dim
Funny how a home becomes a place I've been

Tape it up, close it slow
One more box and then I go
Empty room, open door
Won't be living here no more

Walls remember every laugh and tear
I'll take the love but I'll leave the fear
Deep breath in at the threshold line
New chapter waiting on the other side

Tape it up, close it slow
One more box and then I go
Empty room, open door
Gonna find a brand new shore

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights were close enough to touch
The roar of the crowd, it meant so much
One more step and the dream was mine
Standing on the edge of the finish line

My hands were shaking, my heart was loud
Everything I wanted in that swelling sound
So close I could taste the morning sun
The night I almost, almost won

And it slipped away, slipped away
Like water through my open hands
But I was there, I was alive
In the moment where the almost stands

The silence after when the lights went black
The long quiet drive on the road back
But something in me started to rise
A fire still burning behind my eyes

Maybe almost is its own kind of grace
A glimpse of the summit, a taste of the place
I'll climb again when the morning breaks
For the almost is just the road it takes

And it slipped away, slipped away
Like water through my open hands
But I was there, I was alive
In the moment where the almost stands

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The lanterns swing on the village square
The clarinet cries in the cooling air
One last night before we part
Dance with me, you'll hold my heart

Oh, play it loud, play it sweet
Stamp the dust beneath our feet
Tomorrow we go our separate ways
But tonight we sing for all our days

The fiddler's bow is flying fast
We spin in circles holding the past
Grandmother claps and the children cheer
The whole world gathered, the whole world here

Oh, play it loud, play it sweet
Stamp the dust beneath our feet
Tomorrow we go our separate ways
But tonight we sing for all our days

When the candles burn down to the ground
And the last sweet echo of the sound
We'll carry this joy through the cold and far
A festival fire, our own little star

So play it loud, play it sweet
Stamp the dust beneath our feet
Tomorrow we go our separate ways
But tonight we sing for all our days

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Son las tres de la mañana y te escribo otra vez
Sé que está mal pero te quiero ver
El teléfono prendido, tu nombre en la pantalla
Un mensaje peligroso que mi corazón estalla

No debería, pero te escribo (te escribo)
Borro el mensaje y lo vuelvo a enviar (a enviar)
No debería, pero contigo (contigo)
Se me olvida todo lo demás

Las burbujitas suben, tú estás escribiendo
Mi pecho acelerado, ya me estoy perdiendo
Lo prohibido sabe dulce en la oscuridad
Un secreto en la pantalla, pura tentación, na' má'

No debería, pero te escribo (te escribo)
Borro el mensaje y lo vuelvo a enviar (a enviar)
No debería, pero contigo (contigo)
Se me olvida todo lo demás

Manana me arrepiento, esta noche no
Tu nombre en mis dedos como una tentación
Apago la pantalla pero la vuelvo a prender
Lo que no debo hacer es lo que quiero hacer

No debería, pero te escribo (te escribo)
Borro el mensaje y lo vuelvo a enviar (a enviar)
No debería, pero contigo (contigo)
Se me olvida todo lo demás

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Clock punched in for thirty years
My daddy's gloves still on the rack
Foreman read it off a paper
Lights go dark, ain't comin' back
The loading dock is quiet now
No forklift hum, no whistle blow
We greased these gears with all we had
Now they're tellin' us to go

Last shift, last groove, last paycheck slow
Factory's closin', where do we go
We built this town one bolt, one beam
Now they're sellin' off the American dream
Last shift, last groove, turn the power down
Funk's still standin' when they bury the town

Maria worked the second line
Singing Marvin to the steel
Now she's countin' out her tips
At the diner off the hill
We poured concrete, we raised the kids
We never asked for much at all
Just a wage and a Friday night
And somethin' steady through it all

Gate's chained, weeds in the lot
Thirty summers in this spot
But you can't padlock how we move
We carry the rhythm, we keep the groove

Last shift, last groove, last paycheck slow
Factory's closin', where do we go
We built this town one bolt, one beam
Now they're sellin' off the American dream

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the gravel, jars packed in the trunk
Hundred proof and headlights off, the back roads running drunk
My brother's at the wheel, his knuckles bone and white
We sold our souls to copper coils and ran them through the night

Run, run, the law's behind
Devil's in the carburetor screaming down the line
Glass and fire, gasoline
The last run that we ever made was nineteen seventeen

The county car came roaring with a siren full of teeth
We took the curve at ninety with the river underneath
The guardrail screamed like Judgment, the world went end on end
The jars went up like Hellfire and I lost my only friend

I crawled out of the wreckage to the smell of burning rye
His name was on my lips and there was smoke against the sky
No confession, no amen, just whiskey and the flame
The mountain keeps its silence and it never speaks his name

Run, run, the law's behind
Devil's in the carburetor screaming down the line
Glass and fire, gasoline
The last run that we ever made was nineteen seventeen

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Sunlight on the casseroles
Black dress on the bedroom floor
The house is full of empty chairs
And no one's knocking anymore
I made the coffee out of habit
Two cups standing by the sink
The city's waking up outside
And I don't know what to think

Morning comes, morning comes
The light still finds me anyway
Morning comes, morning comes
And I'm still breathing, here, today
Hold the silence, let it move
Let the kick drum hold me through
Morning comes, morning comes
I'm gonna carry you, carry you

I played the song you used to play
And let it fill the empty hall
The neighbors heard it through the walls
And no one minded it at all
Grief's a tempo, slow and steady
Four on the floor of who I am
I'm dancing 'cause you taught me to
And that's the only way I can

Raise your hands if you have lost somebody
Raise your hands and let the morning in
This room is full of everybody's somebody
And love don't ever really end

Morning comes, morning comes
The light still finds me anyway
Morning comes, morning comes
And I'm still breathing, here, today

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The screen lights blue at half past one
My wife asleep, the deed half done
Three little dots that come and go
A fire I lit, a thing I know
The ring's still cold against my hand
I type a word I can't unsend
Your name's a country I once crossed
A border bought, a husband lost

Delete, delete, but never quite
My thumbs are sinners in the night
One more message, one more lie
Dance with me before we die
Delete, delete, the dots appear
The devil whispers, draw me near

I know the ending of this song
I've hummed this danger far too long
A stolen rhythm, hip to hip
A secret kept beneath the lip
The phone is hot, the morning near
And everything I love is here
And everything I want is there
And I'm a coward in the glare

One step forward, one step back
The tango of the thing we lack
I press send and close my eyes
And let the dancer improvise

Delete, delete, but never quite
My thumbs are sinners in the night
One more message, one more lie
Dance with me before we die

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They poured the concrete on its chest
Built the towers, named the streets
Nobody told the children here
There's something breathing under their feet
The trains run on its heartbeat slow
The power grid drinks from its veins
We pave the dreams it's dreaming now
And call the tremors growing pains

WAKE UP! It's stirring underneath
WAKE UP! The city grinds its teeth
We built our lives on borrowed sleep
A giant's grave too shallow, too deep
WAKE UP! Before the rumble comes
WAKE UP! And feel the waking drums

The mayor cuts another ribbon
While the bedrock starts to crack
The faultline is a spine that's bending
And it wants its kingdom back
We are tenants on a titan
We are ants upon a god
And every footstep that we hammer
Is a prayer it never nods

It opens one enormous eye
The skyline shivers, glass and steel
We were never kings of anything
We were dust upon the wheel

WAKE UP! It's stirring underneath
WAKE UP! The city grinds its teeth
We built our lives on borrowed sleep
A giant's grave too shallow, too deep

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
He used to lift the whole garage door
With one hand and a cigarette
Now he asks me twice my middle name
And I pretend I don't forget
The recliner's got a shape of him
The remote slips from his grip
I drive the route I used to hate
Just to take another trip

Getting smaller in the doorway
Getting quiet in his chair
The man who taught me how to throw
Now needs me on the stair
And I'm not ready, I'm not ready
For the morning he's not there
Getting smaller in the doorway
With his thinning silver hair

We watched the game and didn't talk
The way we never learned to do
He said son, you turned out alright
And I had to leave the room
His hands still know the engine block
But the words come out delayed
I'm memorizing all of him
Before the colors fade

I'll be the one who lifts the door
I'll be the steady on the stair
The boy you carried carries you
It's only fair, it's only fair

Getting smaller in the doorway
Getting quiet in his chair
The man who taught me how to throw
Now needs me on the stair

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The Ferris wheel is slowing down
The funnel cake stand's closing up
We're passing 'round the warm last beer
In somebody's plastic cup
The headliner just left the stage
The field is mud and folding chairs
And you got hay stuck in your hair
And I don't think I even care

One more song, one more dance
One more shot at a summer romance
The lights go out at half past one
Don't tell me that the night is done
One more song before we go
Back to the lives we used to know
Hold my hand and don't let go
On the last night of the show

We met in line on Friday night
Now it's Sunday and I'm yours
We traded numbers, traded names
And half a dozen choruses
Tomorrow you go back to Tulsa
And I'm bound for Tennessee
But for tonight the band's still playing
And you're still here next to me

The stagehands roll the cables up
The stars come out to take the stage
We'll always have this muddy field
This dog-eared festival page

One more song, one more dance
One more shot at a summer romance
The lights go out at half past one
Don't tell me that the night is done

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Came back for the weekend, nothing more
Same cracked pavement, same corner store
Then you walked in like you never left
And took the air right out my chest
We used to ride these streets at night
Now you're standing in the strobe light
Everything I ran away from
Is the reason I keep coming

Pull me under, pull me down
Falling for the girl from my hometown
The bass is shaking through the floor
I didn't think I'd want this anymore
Faster, faster, here we go
To the only place I'll ever know
Pull me under, pull me down
Falling for the girl from my hometown

The city told me to forget it
Climb the ladder, never settle
But you remember who I was
Before I learned to fake it just because
Two a.m. and the rave's still going
Your eyes are the only thing I'm knowing
