The drum machine, the broken streetlight
Feels like home for the first time tonight

We could leave or we could stay
The break drops out and fades away
Silence, then the snare comes back
And I'm all in, no looking back

Pull me under, pull me down
Falling for the girl from my hometown
The bass is shaking through the floor
I didn't think I'd want this anymore

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the Chevy with the good stuff in the back
Cousin Earl was driving and the moon was painted black
We had a date in Dothan with a fella name of Ray
But the sheriff's deputy was parked along the way
Pick it up! Horns up! Watch the rearview now
We gotta lose this lawman and I don't quite know how

Run the shine, run the shine
Down the county border line
Everything was going fine
Till we spilled the contraband moonshine
Up-stroke, up-stroke, skank it down the road
Dropped the whole entire load

Earl took a corner like a fool without a brain
The trunk popped open and it started raining grain
Jars went bouncing, mason lids went flying through the air
The deputy just stood there with his mouth caught in a stare
Fifty gallons gurgling down the gutter and the ditch
The whole town smelled like Saturday, son, ain't that a glitch

Now we're broke and we're laughing and we're soaking in the still
Earl says try again next weekend, and I reckon that we will
No money and no liquor but at least we're not in jail
Pick the horns back up, boys, we got another tale

Run the shine, run the shine
Down the county border line
Everything was going fine
Till we spilled the contraband moonshine

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
She asked me stay, I watched the sky
A wall cloud rising in the west
The ring was waiting on the dash
The radio screamed out the rest
I love you like a falling glass
But the funnel calls my name
I chase the thing that wants me dead
And I dance it like a flame

I can't settle, I can't stay
The storm and I, we waltz away
Two steps into the hurricane
My heart's a barometer of pain
I can't settle, I can't stand
A quiet life, a steady hand
Give me thunder, give me rain
And the lonesome highway again

She packed her bags in Amarillo
Said a woman needs a home
I kissed her once and chased a supercell
Across the plains alone
The sky's the only partner
That will never ask me why
I press against the violence
And I tango with the sky

Maybe one day in the eye of it
Where everything goes still
I'll think of her and what I left
On some forgotten hill

I can't settle, I can't stay
The storm and I, we waltz away
Two steps into the hurricane
My heart's a barometer of pain

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Concrete jungle on a beating heart
Thirty stories, never knew the start
They told us bedrock, told us stone
But something's breathing under my home
Elevator shaking, ain't the wind
Feel the rumble deep within
We stacked the bricks, we stacked the bread
On a monster's sleeping head

Giant underneath, yeah, giant underground (yeah)
Whole city sittin' on a ticking sound (boom)
Don't wake it up, don't make a sound (shh)
We dancing on a fault, we get down (we get down)
Giant underneath, giant gonna rise (rise)
Skyline shaking, watch it touch the sky (woo)

My momma told me build it high
But never asked the reason why
The ground stay warm in middle of December
Elders gone, but they remember
A breath, a pulse, a buried king
We just dust on everything
Count my blessings, count the cracks
It's gonna want its kingdom back

It's stirring, it's stirring, feel the tremor in the floor
It's turning, it's turning, somebody lock the door
We built it all on borrowed time
A empire on a fault line, a empire on a fault line

Giant underneath, yeah, giant underground (yeah)
Whole city sittin' on a ticking sound (boom)
Don't wake it up, don't make a sound (shh)
We dancing on a fault, we get down (we get down)

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We trained together by the salt-flat lake
Two names stitched on the same blue coat
Now the tether snapped above the world
And I'm watching you grow small and remote
The Earth turns slow beneath our boots
The radio is just your breath
And the distance opens like a hand
Between your heartbeat and my chest

Drifting, drifting, further from your light
Two little candles in the endless night
I reach for you, you reach for me
Across a cold and starry sea
Drifting, drifting, let the silence come
We were two and now we're slowly one by one

You said you'd see me back at home
We'd plant a garden by the door
But the thruster's spent and the line is cut
And I can't reach you anymore
I memorize your final words
The way they crackle and then fade
You're a constellation now to me
A promise that the dark unmade

If you find a planet warm and green
Name a river after me
And I'll be circling somewhere blue
In the quiet you can't see

Drifting, drifting, further from your light
Two little candles in the endless night
I reach for you, you reach for me
Across a cold and starry sea

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Red light, empty street, I take the long way home
Second floor, third window, that's the one we used to own
The curtains different now, somebody's lamp is on
Funny how a building holds a person who is gone
I roll the window down, the same old summer air
The corner store, the broken step, the ghost of you still there
I don't slow down too much, just enough to feel the weight
Of every little ordinary day I made you wait

Just driving past, just driving past
The places that we thought would last
The engine hums, the streetlight glows
I'm fine, I'm fine, but nobody knows
Just driving past, just driving slow
Through every version of the years ago

The radio is low, some song we used to play
I keep both hands on the wheel and let it drift away
New tenant's got a bike chained where I used to park
A stranger's living happy in our half-remembered dark
I'm not here to knock, I'm not here to stay
Just to let the memory wash over me and fade
Green light, I move along, the past stays where it's at
A window getting smaller in the rearview, just like that

Maybe in another life I stop and ring the bell
Maybe in another life we both turned out just well
But tonight I keep on driving, let the city take me back
To wherever I belong now, down some other quiet track

Just driving past, just driving past
The places that we thought would last
The engine hums, the streetlight glows
I'm fine, I'm fine, but nobody knows

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
She came up from the tide like something drowned and blessed
Salt upon her collarbone, a locket on her chest
We burned the August evenings down to driftwood and to bone
And she warned me with a smile she wasn't ever going home
The motel sign was flickering a sickly shade of red
And I should've heard the warning in the gospel that she said

Summer love is a shallow grave
Dug in the sand by the hungry wave
She kissed me cold, she kissed me deep
Made a promise that the sea would keep
Summer love, oh summer love
There's a dark thing crawling up above

By Labor Day the boardwalk emptied, gray and hollow-eyed
I looked for her at every dusk along the falling tide
They found her shawl, they found her shoes, they never found the girl
Just her name carved in the pier and a single oyster pearl
Now the locals cross themselves when the summer people come
And the tide rolls in like organ notes, low and sick and numb

Sometimes on a moonless night I hear her in the foam
Calling soft and calling sweet, come on, baby, come on home
And I stand there at the water's edge with sand up to my knees
And I swear I almost answer to the woman in the seas

Summer love is a shallow grave
Dug in the sand by the hungry wave
She kissed me cold, she kissed me deep
Made a promise that the sea would keep

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I hung my Sunday collar on a nail behind the door
Stopped believing in the things I used to kneel down for
The steeple was just lumber and the hymn was just a sound
And God had left the building, or so I thought I'd found
I drank the smoky midnights and I argued with the rain
And every empty morning wore the color of my pain

But grace is a slow piano in an empty room
A blue note in the darkness, a candle in the gloom
I lost it on a Tuesday and I found it on a train
The quiet little miracle of feeling whole again

A stranger bought my coffee and she didn't want my name
A child laughed in a doorway in the cold November rain
The saxophone was crying something tender, something true
And somewhere in the bridge of it, I felt the old light through
It wasn't in the thunder and it wasn't in the law
It was in the simple kindness of the smallest things I saw

Maybe faith ain't certainty, it's swinging in the dark
Trusting that the rhythm section's gonna hold the spark
I don't need the answers, I just need the song to play
And the gentle hand of mercy that don't ever turn away

For grace is a slow piano in an empty room
A blue note in the darkness, a candle in the gloom
I lost it on a Tuesday and I found it on a train
The quiet little miracle of feeling whole again

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Thumb on the screen, I been staring at your name
Five whole years, ain't a thing about me changed
Dial it, hang it up, dial it back again
Pride been a prison, momma, lemme out and in
I was running from the mirror, running from the truth
Left the porch light burning, never told you where I moved
Now my hands is shaking like the boy you used to hold
Got a grown man's money, got a little kid's soul

Pick up, pick up, it's been too long
I know I did you wrong, I know I did you wrong
Momma it's me, your baby on the line
Forgive me for the silence, give me one more time

Heard your voice say hello and my whole chest caved
Every word I rehearsed went straight to the grave
Said I'm sorry like a flood, like the dam done broke
You ain't even mad, you just glad I spoke
Told me supper's still warm if I ever come back
I'ma drive through the night, leave it all on the map

I ain't too cold, I ain't too proud
I'm calling you now, I'm calling you now
Every mile that I ran was a mile from home
Don't let me lose you on the dial tone

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Neon mile markers bleeding past the glass
Diesel hum and a radio that won't last
Three a.m. and the highway's a black river
I dial your number just to stop the shiver

Pick up the phone, I'm a ghost in the dark
A thousand miles from the beat of your heart
Tell me you're dreaming, tell me you're warm
I'm chasing the dawn through the edge of a storm

Dashboard glow on my tired hands
Coffee gone cold in the cupholder cans
You whisper my name like a soft little prayer
And suddenly home is the static we share

Pick up the phone, I'm a ghost in the dark
A thousand miles from the beat of your heart
Tell me you're dreaming, tell me you're warm
I'm chasing the dawn through the edge of a storm

The white lines run like a heartbeat monitor
The night is electric, the city's a blur
Keep the light on, I'm an hour from gone
Just hold the line till the morning comes on

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Donde antes corría el viento entre las hojas
ahora solo el cemento canta su canción
un árbol solitario levanta sus ramas
guardián olvidado de un viejo corazón

Árbol valiente, no te dejes caer
las trompetas lloran lo que fue nuestro ayer
canta conmigo, raíz de la ciudad
eres la última sombra, la última verdad

Los pájaros buscan tu abrazo de madera
un niño descalzo te toca con amor
las calles de piedra no saben de primavera
pero tú floreces contra todo dolor

Árbol valiente, no te dejes caer
las trompetas lloran lo que fue nuestro ayer
canta conmigo, raíz de la ciudad
eres la última sombra, la última verdad

Que suenen las guitarras, que llore el violín
por cada hoja verde que no tiene fin
si te cortan mañana, yo te cantaré
y en mi pecho de tierra te sembraré

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Salt in your hair and a cigarette glow
We found a town that the maps didn't know
You borrowed my jacket, I lost it that night
Traded my summer for one strand of light

Oh, it was July, it was never gonna last
We were a wave that was breaking too fast
I knew it, you knew it, we kissed anyway
Two kids and a bonfire that burned out by May

We stole a canoe and we never gave it back
Wrote both our names in the sand on the black
The tide came and took them, you laughed at the loss
Said nothing worth keeping was meant to outlast

Oh, it was July, it was never gonna last
We were a wave that was breaking too fast
I knew it, you knew it, we kissed anyway
Two kids and a bonfire that burned out by May

And I don't know your number, I don't know your town
But every September comes pulling me down
To the boardwalk, the streetlights, the radio sound
Of a summer I lost and I never quite found

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Green light, my hands on the wheel
Took the long way home just to see how it feels
Your window's still third from the top
Different curtains now, but my heartbeat don't stop

I'm driving past, I'm driving past
Pretending that the past is the past
But the streetlight hits the same as it did
And I'm seventeen again, falling for a kid
I'm driving past, I'm driving past

Used to climb up that fire escape
You'd be waiting with a record and a coffee gone late
Now somebody else gets the light in that room
And I get the ghost and the gasoline fumes

I'm driving past, I'm driving past
Pretending that the past is the past
But the streetlight hits the same as it did
And I'm seventeen again, falling for a kid
I'm driving past, I'm driving past

One more loop around the block
Then I swear that I'll let it go, let it lock
But the engine keeps idling, slow
Some doors you drive by but you never let go

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the diner when the sun's barely up
Clock out, clock in at the warehouse, fill the cup
My mother used to tell me hard work pays
But the bills keep climbing while I'm counting the days

Lord, I'm working, working
Two hands and a tired back
I'm working, working
But the ground keeps falling out from under my track
How you fall behind when you're running so hard?

The rent went up while the wages stood still
Got a stack on the table that I'm fighting to kill
My babies need shoes and the heater's gone cold
I'm rich in the spirit but the world wants gold

Lord, I'm working, working
Two hands and a tired back
I'm working, working
But the ground keeps falling out from under my track
How you fall behind when you're running so hard?

But I won't break, no, I won't bend
Gonna make a way where there ain't no end
If I gotta carry this whole town on my back
I'll keep on stepping down this broken track

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We drove till the radio gave up the ghost
Past the last gas station, the last lonely post
You said look up, and I swear my breath stalled
The sky was on fire and we were so small

Green rivers running over our heads
We left the heater and the warmth of the bed
For a light that don't care if we live or we die
The whole world is electric tonight in the sky

No phone gets a signal this far from the noise
Just you and the cold and the crackle and poise
Of a billion-year curtain that dances and bends
A color with no name where the atmosphere ends

Green rivers running over our heads
We left the heater and the warmth of the bed
For a light that don't care if we live or we die
The whole world is electric tonight in the sky

And I wanted to bottle it, hold it, to keep
But some things you witness, you don't get to keep
So I held your cold hand and I let the sky burn
A gift that you take but you never return

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Entre piedras grises se levanta su voz
un árbol que sangra la sombra de Dios
le cortaron los hermanos, le robaron el rio
y aún florece en el centro de este pueblo vacío

¡Ay, mi árbol, ay, mi pena!
raíz que rompe la cadena
baila el viento en tu madera
grita el alma, ¡que no muera!

Las palmas suenan duro contra el cemento cruel
y tus hojas son cartas de un olvidado papel
un gitano se sienta a llorar a tu pie
y le cuentas la historia de todo lo que se fue

¡Ay, mi árbol, ay, mi pena!
raíz que rompe la cadena
baila el viento en tu madera
grita el alma, ¡que no muera!

Que zapateen los muertos, que tiemble la ciudad
por el último verde que defiende su verdad
si te arrancan del suelo, compañero, lo juré
en el compás de mi pecho yo te resembraré

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Grandfather walks with his feet in the foam
Salt in his beard, says the ocean's our home
He points to the water, he says watch how it breathes
It takes and it gives, child, it never just leaves

Learn the tide, learn the tide
Let it carry what you cannot hold inside
It'll rise, it'll fall, it'll come back around
There's a rhythm in the losing, there's a faith in the ground
Oh, learn the tide

He's eighty-some winters and steady as stone
Got a hand on my shoulder so I don't feel alone
He says the moon pulls the water like grief pulls a man
You don't fight what's coming, you just learn how to stand

Learn the tide, learn the tide
Let it carry what you cannot hold inside
It'll rise, it'll fall, it'll come back around
There's a rhythm in the losing, there's a faith in the ground
Oh, learn the tide

And when he's gone under the cold and the deep
I'll come to the shore where the old promises sleep
I'll put my own feet in the foam and the cold
And I'll teach my own children the truth that he told

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I drove to the graveyard with whiskey and rain
Knelt in the mud by your cold marble name
All the words I'd been sharpening like a knife in my chest
Fell out of my mouth and they laid down to rest

I forgive you, I forgive you
Though you can't hear a word that I say
I carried this hatred like a stone in my shoe
And I'm leaving it here on your grave

You were a hard man, a thunderhead, a fist
Left a boy full of bruises and a long, ugly list
I swore I'd never say it, swore I'd dance on your stone
But hate's a heavy coffin to be carrying alone

I forgive you, I forgive you
Though you can't hear a word that I say
I carried this hatred like a stone in my shoe
And I'm leaving it here on your grave

The crows don't answer, the wind just moans
Forgiveness ain't for the dead in the stones
It's for the living who are tired of the weight
So I lay it down gently, and I walk through the gate

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up in the attic with a wire and a prayer
A borrowed transmitter and the cold midnight air
They won't give us a license, won't give us a name
So we steal back the airwaves and we light up the flame

This is your captain on the frequency free
Broadcasting low from the back of the sea
They can't catch a signal that belongs to us all
So turn up your radio and answer the call

We spin the forbidden, the songs that they banned
The truth and the trouble from a hidden command
Grandma's old record and the kid down the street
Every voice in this town gets a turn on the beat

This is your captain on the frequency free
Broadcasting low from the back of the sea
They can't catch a signal that belongs to us all
So turn up your radio and answer the call

When the van comes a-hunting with the antenna spinning round
We'll pack up the tower and we'll move underground
You can't kill a signal that lives in the bone
We'll be back by tomorrow on a frequency unknown

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Woke up to the sunlight like it had no right to shine
Your coffee cup still sitting where you left it last time
The black dress on the floor and the casseroles in the hall
And the silence in the kitchen is the loudest of all

The morning after, the morning after
How's the world still turning, where's the laughter?
I made it through the flowers and the words that they said
But nobody told me how to wake in this bed
The morning after you're gone

The phone keeps on buzzing with the are-you-okays
I don't have an answer, I just stare at the days
Your toothbrush, your jacket, your smell in the air
Grief ain't the funeral, it's the after, the where

The morning after, the morning after
How's the world still turning, where's the laughter?
I made it through the flowers and the words that they said
But nobody told me how to wake in this bed
The morning after you're gone

So I'll pour two cups and I'll drink them both slow
And I'll talk to the quiet till I learn how to go
One breath, then another, that's all I can do
The morning after a morning without you

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Well I came back home for the summertime (shoo-bop)
And I saw you standing in the malt shop line (shoo-bop)
The girl from the corner of Maple and Main
My heart did a flip and it ain't been the same

Oh, hometown honey, hometown sweet
You were always just down the street
How'd I never see it, how'd I never know? (oh-oh-oh)
You were my whole world this whole time and I let it go

We used to ride bikes by the old county fair (shoo-bop)
Now you got a ribbon tied up in your hair (shoo-bop)
I'm walking you home like I'm sixteen again
And I'm praying this summer don't ever end

Oh, hometown honey, hometown sweet
You were always just down the street
How'd I never see it, how'd I never know? (oh-oh-oh)
You were my whole world this whole time and I let it go

So I'm staying right here, I ain't leaving this town (bop-bop)
Where the church bells ring and the sun's going down
Gonna marry that girl from the malt shop line
My hometown honey, gonna make you mine

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Clock says midnight, the ward's still bright
Got my scrubs and my coffee and my second wind tight
Everybody's home with the turkey and the tree
But room 304 needs his medicine from me

I'm working the double, the double, y'all
Holiday hustle down the hospital hall
My feet hurt, my back's about to break in two
But somebody's gotta do the work, so the somebody's me, it's true
Groovin' on the graveyard, baby, pushing on through

Mrs. Lee in the corner, she got nobody near
So I sit and I sing her a little holiday cheer
The machines keep the beat like a funky little band
And I'm dancing with a clipboard and an IV in my hand

I'm working the double, the double, y'all
Holiday hustle down the hospital hall
My feet hurt, my back's about to break in two
But somebody's gotta do the work, so the somebody's me, it's true
Groovin' on the graveyard, baby, pushing on through

When the sun comes up I'll drive home slow
Miss the whole holiday, but you know, but you know
There's a fever broke at four and a baby born at two
And that's a better kind of present than a bow on a shoe

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The train let me off where the cottonwoods lean
Same crooked steeple, same dry creek between
But the boy that they sent off don't live here no more
They buried him somewhere on a far-off shore

I'm coming home, I'm coming home
With a ghost in my boots and a stone for a soul
Don't you run out to meet me with that look in your eye
I left my heart in the desert where the young men die

Mama set a plate but my hands wouldn't still
Every slam of the screen door's a shot on the hill
The dog don't know me, the porch swing creaks low
And the medals in my pocket weigh more than the snow

I'm coming home, I'm coming home
With a ghost in my boots and a stone for a soul
Don't you run out to meet me with that look in your eye
I left my heart in the desert where the young men die

So lay me in the field where the tall grass waves
Let me learn how to live among the living again
Maybe one day the thunder won't sound like the guns
And I'll be more than the war that I outran

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew at six o'clock for thirty-seven years
My daddy punched that same time card 'til the paint wore off the gears
Now there's a paper on the door and a padlock on the gate
And the parking lot's just gravel dust and a closing-down date

I learned to drive a forklift here before I learned to shave
Met my wife at the loading dock, she waved from bay number eight
The diner across the highway's got an empty corner booth
Where the second shift would split their fries and lie about the news

They're shutting down the only thing this little town has got
Gonna haul the iron off to Mexico and leave us in the lot
My hands still smell like motor oil, my back still knows the line
But the only thing they're hiring now is the church and the county sign

Frank's been crying in his truck, he won't let nobody see
He's fifty-one with two kids' college and a bank that won't agree
They gave us coffee and a speech and a hat that says goodbye
And a number we can call if we get lonesome by and by

So here's to the rust on the water tower, here's to the dent in the floor
Here's to the men who built this county and won't build no more

They're shutting down the only thing this little town has got
Gonna haul the iron off to Mexico and leave us in the lot
My hands still smell like motor oil, my back still knows the line
But the only thing they're hiring now is the church and the county sign

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
There's a Polaroid taped to the dash of my truck
A marquee with my name spelled wrong but spelled
The man from the label had whiskey on his breath
Said kid, you're a phone call from everything else

I played like the floor was about to give way
Every string was a wire I was walking across
Somewhere a tape machine caught it all down
Then somebody's lawyer decided the cost

I almost made it, almost is a town
With a water tower and a road leading out
I got close enough to read the welcome sign
Then the headlights swung the other way that night

The deal fell apart on a Tuesday in spring
When the money got scared and the suits flew home
Now I tune guitars in a shop on Eighth
And I tell the young ones don't go it alone

Sometimes at the register I'll hear my old song
Playing low on a radio nobody owns
And I'll mouth every word like it still belongs to me
While the customer waits for his change and his keys

I almost made it, almost is a town
With a water tower and a road leading out
I got close enough to read the welcome sign
Then the headlights swung the other way that night

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Neon bled across the ceiling like a tide
We were holy in the smoke and the strobe
Somebody whispered yes against my ear
And the whole room turned to liquid gold

I could feel it in the static of my skin
A door about to open in the air
The future hummed a single perfect note
And I floated up the staircase to the glare

Almost, almost, the word dissolves like sugar
On a tongue too slow to swallow what it knew
We were one breath from the other side of forever
And the morning came in soft and washed it blue

Now it lives behind a curtain made of rain
A reel of light I play against my eyes
The yes became a maybe became a hush
Like a comet you can't follow when it dies

If I close my eyes I'm there again
Suspended in the half-second of yes
Where nothing ever lands and nothing breaks
And I am beautiful and weightless and undressed

Almost, almost, the word dissolves like sugar
On a tongue too slow to swallow what it knew
We were one breath from the other side of forever
And the morning came in soft and washed it blue

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
The striped pole turns like it's been turning since I was a boy
He knows my fade before I sit, knows my mama, knows my joy
He heard about the funeral before the family made the calls
There's a whole town's worth of secrets sleeping in these mirror walls

Mister Earl, Mister Earl, with the clippers and the towel
You could run for mayor on a Saturday right now
Every confession finds your chair, every wound, every win
You cut the grief right off our heads and you let the new light in

He gave my granddad his last shave, gave my son his very first
There's a Polaroid for every chair, the best ones and the worst
He loans out twenties, holds the keys, he knows who's stepping out
And he never says a word too loud, that's what the trust's about

When they tore down half the avenue he kept his lights on still
A lighthouse with a barber's pole at the bottom of the hill
And every man who walks back out walks taller than he came
Feeling seen, feeling sharp, feeling somebody knows his name

Mister Earl, Mister Earl, with the clippers and the towel
You could run for mayor on a Saturday right now
Every confession finds your chair, every wound, every win
You cut the grief right off our heads and you let the new light in

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Gravel road and a low red moon, just the two of us and the dust
Her hands on the wheel like a hymn she's afraid to trust
I said keep your eyes where the headlights die, not on the painted line
The dark out here will take you, girl, if you give it too much time

Her mother's in the cold ground now past the chapel and the gate
I taught her on the same dirt mile, the same hour, the same eight
So I buckle in beside my child and I bite back what I know
That every road I ever loved was the one that watched me go

Go easy on the gas, baby, go easy on the brake
The world is full of ditches that the careless ones will take
I'd give my crooked years to you, my every wrong turn made
Just keep two hands upon the wheel and don't be afraid of shade

A deer steps out, she slams it hard, we shudder to a stop
Her breath comes quick, her knuckles white, I feel my old heart drop
I said that's the lesson, that's the prayer, the night gives nothing free
Then she laughed so loud the crows took flight and something rose in me

One day she'll drive away from here with everything I gave
Past the water tower, past the church, past her mother's grave
And I'll stand in the porch light small as the taillights leave my sight
And I'll whisper to the gravel road, you bring her home tonight

Go easy on the gas, baby, go easy on the brake
The world is full of ditches that the careless ones will take
I'd give my crooked years to you, my every wrong turn made
Just keep two hands upon the wheel and don't be afraid of shade

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Thumb above the green button, three years on the line
Heartbeat playing drum patterns, sweat in the moonshine
I rehearsed a hundred speeches in the back of the bus
But the second that she answered all the grammar turned to dust

Mama pick up, mama pick up, I been gone too long
The pride I carried like a crown was heavy and it's wrong
Mama pick up, mama pick up, hear me through the static
Let me come back to your cooking and your laughter automatic

She said son where you been hiding, your plate's been on the table
I dey wait by this window every night I'm able
No lecture, no shouting, just her voice like warm rain
And the years fell off my shoulders like I never left again

I could hear the radio playing the song she used to hum
The spoon against the pot, the gate, the neighbor and the drum
All the home I tried to bury came alive inside my chest
And I cried into the speaker like a child against her breast

No distance, no silence, no money I could send
Is worth the simple miracle of your voice on the other end
I'm booking the next flight home, I'm done running from your love
Kill the fattest yam, mama, your boy is coming up

Mama pick up, mama pick up, I been gone too long
The pride I carried like a crown was heavy and it's wrong
Mama pick up, mama pick up, hear me through the static
Let me come back to your cooking and your laughter automatic

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
The city's all chrome and a heartbeat, the dashboard glows electric blue
I took the long way home on purpose, the way that runs right past you
Third floor window, same old curtain, somebody new's got the light on tonight
And the streetlamp throws a halo where we used to dance til the morning light

Driving past, driving past, with the radio up too loud
Feeling everything I buried come rising through the crowd
I won't stop, I won't slow down, I just let the engine cry
Driving past your old apartment underneath the disco sky

The mirror ball of every traffic light is spinning on my hood
I swear I see us on the fire escape laughing like we always would
The bassline's in my chest like a pulse I can't outrun
So I circle round the block one time, just one, just one, just one

I'm not going up those stairs again, I'm not gonna ring that bell
I just need to feel the ghost of us before I tell myself farewell
Let the four-on-the-floor keep moving, let the tires hum the tune
Let me grieve us at a hundred beats beneath the strawberry moon

Driving past, driving past, with the radio up too loud
Feeling everything I buried come rising through the crowd
I won't stop, I won't slow down, I just let the engine cry
Driving past your old apartment underneath the disco sky

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. at a phone booth glowing, somewhere off the interstate
(Sha-la-la) the rain is falling, my baby's gonna think I'm late
I dropped my dime in the slot and I'm praying, prayin' that she's still awake
A thousand miles of yellow lines between this heart and the love I make

Hello darling, it's your driver, calling from a world away
(Doo-wop, doo-wop) I'll be home by Sunday, kiss the kids and hear them pray
Don't you cry now, don't you worry, I can hear you through the line
The diesel hums a lullaby and your voice is the morning shine

The truckstop neon buzzes pink and the coffee's gone stone cold
(Sha-la-la) I miss your pillow, I miss your hand for me to hold
There's a photograph above my wheel getting faded, getting old
But the sound of you at three a.m. is worth more than this load of gold

The wind across the prairie sounds like the boys in their best harmony
(Ooh, ooh) singing me on down the highway, back to where I'm meant to be
So I'll drive into the sunrise with your whisper in my ear
My good night and my good morning, my faraway and my so near

Hello darling, it's your driver, calling from a world away
(Doo-wop, doo-wop) I'll be home by Sunday, kiss the kids and hear them pray
Don't you cry now, don't you worry, I can hear you through the line
The diesel hums a lullaby and your voice is the morning shine

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Under the elm where the pigeons hold court there's a man with a foldout board
Five dollars a game and the clock starts to tick, he'll beat you and never get bored
He wears a wool cap in the middle of June and a grin like a card up a sleeve
He's taken more kings than a guillotine blade and he never once asks you to leave

Slap the clock, drop a knight, son, the morning is long
Every pawn's got a story and most of them wrong
He's the king of the corner where the maple leaves fall
And he'll teach you for nothing what he charges you all

He came up from nothing on Nostrand and Vine, learned the board in a cell block of stone
Thirty-one years he's been holding this square, it's the closest he's got to a home
He lets the kids win just enough to come back, takes the bankers for all that they've got
And he hums to himself an old gambit in time, en passant in a quick little trot

They offered him money to teach at a school, in a building with carpet and lights
He just laughed and he set up the pieces again under elms in the cool autumn nights
Said the board doesn't care what your bank account reads, doesn't care if you're famous or poor
Sixty-four squares and the truth in between, and a five-dollar bill at the door

Slap the clock, drop a knight, son, the morning is long
Every pawn's got a story and most of them wrong
He's the king of the corner where the maple leaves fall
And he'll teach you for nothing what he charges you all

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Boots in the doorway, dust of a faraway land
Mama drop the calabash, she no fit understand
The boy that left don turn a man with thunder in him eyes
But the drum dey call am softly back to where the river lies

I dey come home, I dey come home, lay the rifle down
Dance me through the compound, put the flowers on my crown
I dey come home, I dey come home, the war no follow me
Wash the red off of my hands inside the family sea

The children no remember, they was small when I was gone
Now they pull me to the courtyard where the speakers carry on
The uncle pour the palm wine, the auntie sing my name
And somewhere in the rhythm the young soldier dies in shame

I carry men I couldn't save, I carry nights of fire
But the lead guitar dey calling and it lifts my spirit higher
Let the talking drum confess what the mouth no fit to say
Let the people dance the war out of my body till the day

So beat am, beat am, beat am, let the bassline hold my chest
Let the auntie them surround me, let the village do the rest
I no be the same again but I be home, I be alive
And the song go carry pieces that I thought no fit survive

I dey come home, I dey come home, lay the rifle down
Dance me through the compound, put the flowers on my crown
I dey come home, I dey come home, the war no follow me
Wash the red off of my hands inside the family sea

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Woke up Sunday morning, phone face-down on the floor
Woke up Sunday morning, phone face-down on the floor
Three little gray words waiting, and they shut the cellar door

She didn't even call me, didn't knock upon my chest
No, she didn't even call me, didn't lay it on my chest
Just a buzz and a blue bubble and the cold end of the rest

Thirteen years, baby, deleted in a single line
Thirteen years, oh baby, gone in a single line
Now I'm reading it and reading it like the words might change with time

I typed and I deleted, watched the dots come and then go
Lord I typed and I deleted, watched them dots come and then go
The cruelest kind of silence is the kind that's typing slow

Used to be a letter, used to be a slammin' screen door
Used to be a woman crying, telling you it ain't no more
Now they end you with a thumbprint, leave you sitting on the floor
With a glowing little gravestone for the love you're grieving for

Thirteen years, baby, deleted in a single line
Thirteen years, oh baby, gone in a single line
Now I'm reading it and reading it like the words might change with time

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Slow drum down on Rampart, the tuba bending low
We lay the box on shoulders, we taking it real slow
Mama in her white dress, handkerchief and gold
We cry the way the river cry, then we let the spirit go

Then we second line, second line, dance him to the sky
Umbrella spinning rainbow, tell the grief goodbye
We second line, second line, the saints go marching in
Grief and joy together is the way we honor him

The afro on the backbeat with the brass up in the air
Feet that mourned a minute now are kicking up the square
The whole of Tremé pouring out the doorways into sun
We bury you with rhythm cause you taught us how it's done

Grandma snap the tambourine, the children find the groove
Death came to the corner but death don't get to move
We dance until our shoes give out, we dance until we sweat
A man this loved don't really leave, the city won't forget

So pour a little out for him, sprinkle on the ground
Let the snare and the kalimba carry up the holy sound
We'll meet him on the far bank where the band plays through the night
Till then we shake the sorrow loose and turn it into light

Then we second line, second line, dance him to the sky
Umbrella spinning rainbow, tell the grief goodbye
We second line, second line, the saints go marching in
Grief and joy together is the way we honor him

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Forty years and the record's still spinning, needle dragging through the dust
Two kids gone and a house full of nothing but the photographs and rust
She put on the dress from the wedding, I can't button the suit no more
We stand in the wreck of the living room, our shadows on the floor

ONE LAST DANCE before the papers, one last dance before we break
Hold me like you used to mean it, for the dead and living's sake
WE BURNED IT DOWN, WE BUILT IT BACK, we did it all and now it ends
One last dance, then we walk out the door as something less than friends

The song's the one from the kitchen, the one we slow-burned to at night
Before the bills and the silence, before we learned to fight
I feel her breathing against me, I feel the war and the truce
Forty years of holding on, and the brutal grace of letting loose

WHO ARE WE NOW, WHO ARE WE NOW with the music almost gone
TWO STRANGERS WHO BUILT A WHOLE LIFE and couldn't carry it on
Scream it, scream it, we loved like a fire and we choke on the smoke of it all
One more turn around the room, then we let the curtain fall

ONE LAST DANCE before the papers, one last dance before we break
Hold me like you used to mean it, for the dead and living's sake
WE BURNED IT DOWN, WE BUILT IT BACK, we did it all and now it ends
One last dance, then we walk out the door as something less than friends

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Glitter in the grass and the speakers winding down
Fairy lights still humming over everybody's crown
The headliner's gone but nobody wants to leave
We're holding the last hour like a card up every sleeve

Don't turn the lights on yet, oh, let the night stay blue
There's a whole year of Monday waiting on the other side of you
Keep the bassline simmering low, keep my hand inside your hand
The last night of the festival is the realest thing I've planned

We met on a Thursday by the lemonade and smoke
Now it's Sunday and I know you like the inside of a joke
The field smells like rain and beer and somebody's perfume
And I'm scared of the parking lot and the version of me it'll resume

A kid with a saxophone plays soft to the dawn
The Rhodes from the side stage keeps the magic going on
We lie back on a blanket counting satellites for stars
Pretending Monday's a rumor and the world out there ain't ours

When the sun comes up it's gonna take all this away
The wristbands and the strangers who became my family today
So let me memorize your laugh, let me bottle up this air
Let the last note hang forever in the gold electric there

Don't turn the lights on yet, oh, let the night stay blue
There's a whole year of Monday waiting on the other side of you
Keep the bassline simmering low, keep my hand inside your hand
The last night of the festival is the realest thing I've planned

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
The clarinet remembers what my knees forgot
A basement on a Tuesday and the courage that I bought
With a beer I couldn't finish and a song I'd never sung
Oh the freylekhs of the terror when a fool is brave and young

Dance, dance, the little ghost of who I used to be
Shaking at the microphone with the whole world watching me
Three people and a bartender and a dog beneath a chair
But I played like the Messiah might be sitting somewhere there

The accordion comes wheezing like my grandfather's advice
Go on, go up, you'll sweat, you'll shake, you'll do it once or twice
The fiddle saws the memory of my voice that cracked and broke
And the laughter that I feared became the warmest thing they spoke

Now I've played a thousand rooms with the doina and the reel
But none of them could ever match how holy that felt to feel
The first time you stand naked with a melody and a prayer
And offer it to nobody and they catch it in the air

So here's to the trembling, here's to the off-key start
Here's to the bulgar tempo of a terrified young heart
The klezmer says you carry every room you ever played
And the first one hums beneath them all, the bravest one you made

Dance, dance, the little ghost of who I used to be
Shaking at the microphone with the whole world watching me
Three people and a bartender and a dog beneath a chair
But I played like the Messiah might be sitting somewhere there

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The door still trembles in its frame
The echo of your screaming name
We burned the bridge with words like fire
Now silence hangs on every wire

I count the heartbeats in the dark
Each one a hollow, broken spark
The walls remember what we said
The quiet louder than the dread

In the silence after war
I don't know you anymore
But I'd bleed to hear you breathe
In the quiet, don't you leave
Scream it out, then let it die
In the stillness, so do I

The storm has passed but left us cold
Two soldiers far too young and old
The ashes settle on the floor
I reach for what was there before

In the silence after war
I don't know you anymore
But I'd bleed to hear you breathe
In the quiet, don't you leave

No thunder now, just the empty hum
The aftermath of what we've become
If love survives the things we throw
Then hold my hand and let it show

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nadie creía en nosotros, mira ahora
Desde abajo subimos, esta es nuestra hora
No teníamos nada, solo el corazón
Hoy escribimos la historia, esta es la canción

Dale, dale, este es el final
Los de abajo vamos pa' arriba, somos virales
Suena la bocina, baby, no hay marcha atrás
Los underdogs ganamos, ya no hay na' que perder, dale, dale

Nos dijeron que no, lo volvimos un sí
Cada gota de sudor nos trajo hasta aquí
La grada está temblando, se siente el calor
Un minuto en el reloj, el ultimo amor

Dale, dale, este es el final
Los de abajo vamos pa' arriba, somos virales
Suena la bocina, baby, no hay marcha atrás
Los underdogs ganamos, ya no hay na' que perder

Manos arriba si nunca te rendiste
Manos arriba por to' lo que sufriste
Hoy el milagro lo hacemos realidad
Los olvidados ahora somos la verdad

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
The lights came down like falling stars
A crowded room of empty hearts
You took my hand without a word
The loudest silence I have heard

We spun beneath the chandelier
Two strangers, neither one was here
For anyone but this one song
A moment that won't last too long

Dance with me, the only fire
In this hall of borrowed desire
We don't know each other's names
But we burn the same, we burn the same
Hold me close before the dawn
By tomorrow we'll be gone

The vows belong to someone else
We found a world all to ourselves
A stolen turn upon the floor
A door that opens, then no more

Dance with me, the only fire
In this hall of borrowed desire
We don't know each other's names
But we burn the same, we burn the same

When the music dies we'll disappear
Two ghosts who briefly wandered here
But for one song the world stood still
And it always, always will

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
En un café de humo y de neón
escribo versos que no tienen precio
me ofrecen oro por mi corazón
pero el silencio me parece necio

Las calles saben mi nombre olvidado
camino solo con mi verso fiel
no vendo el alma que Dios me ha prestado
aunque me cueste la sangre y la piel

No, no me compras, no me vendo
mi palabra es mi bandera
bailo el tango del que arde sin rendirse
prefiero pobre y verdadero que rico y prisionero

El mundo gira buscando el aplauso
yo busco el verso que nadie escuchó
me quedo firme, no hago una pausa
aunque la fama por mi puerta pasó

No, no me compras, no me vendo
mi palabra es mi bandera
bailo el tango del que arde sin rendirse
prefiero pobre y verdadero que rico y prisionero

Que caiga la lluvia sobre mi papel
y borre todo menos mi verdad
moriré con la pluma siendo fiel
a la única dueña: mi libertad

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Headlights bleeding into rain
One more turn and we're free again
The engine hums a lullaby
This is the last time, last goodbye

Neon smears across the glass
Every red light slipping past
My hands are steady on the wheel
Nothing left for me to feel

Drive away, drive away
Into the static and the grey
One more mile and I am gone
Fading out before the dawn
Drive away, drive away

The sirens drown beneath the noise
A distant choir, a fading voice
I promised this would be the end
No more running, no pretend

Drive away, drive away
Into the static and the grey
One more mile and I am gone
Fading out before the dawn

Dissolve into the morning light
A blur of motion, out of sight
The road forgets, the road forgives
This is the only way one lives

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the highway, baby move it slow
Engine in the city, only I dey know
One last job and then I'm free, oh
Money in the bag, we dey go, we dey go

Drive am, drive am, last time o
No look back, just make we go
Drive am, drive am, we dey fly
Under the moon, under the sky, eh eh

Wheels dey spinning, heart dey racing fast
This na the night that the running go pass
Tell my baby say I dey come home
No more hustle, no more roam, oh oh

Drive am, drive am, last time o
No look back, just make we go
Drive am, drive am, we dey fly
Under the moon, under the sky, eh eh

When the sun go rise I go change my name
Leave the streets behind, no more the game
One more corner and the story end
Freedom dey waiting round the bend

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Last box on the floor, yeah I'm taping it tight
Empty room echo, turn off the last light
Memories packed up, stacked up to the door
This ain't home no more, nah, not anymore

Tape it up, tape it up, that's the last one (last one)
Look around the empty room, now the past's done (past's done)
Keys on the counter, I'm out, I'm gone
Close the door behind me, gotta move on, move on

Shadows on the wall where the pictures used to hang
Every little dent tell a story, every bang
Fold the cardboard flaps, run the tape across
Count up every memory, count up every loss

Tape it up, tape it up, that's the last one (last one)
Look around the empty room, now the past's done (past's done)
Keys on the counter, I'm out, I'm gone
Close the door behind me, gotta move on, move on

New city, new start, but it's heavy on my chest
Leave the keys, leave the ghost, leave it all to rest
One last look, one last breath, then I let it go
Everything I was is in the boxes down below

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
En la esquina del barrio tengo mi taller
hilo y tijera, yo sé lo que hacer
visto a to' el bloque de pies a cabeza
cada puntada es pura realeza

El sastre del barrio, dale, dale
te pongo elegante pa' que brilles en la calle
de la cabeza a los pies, mira como te ves
yo visto al bloque entero, baby, otra vez

Doña Carmen, don José, todos vienen a mí
cada traje una historia, así vivo feliz
la aguja no descansa, el barrio es mi pasarela
cada cliente que sale como estrella que vuela

El sastre del barrio, dale, dale
te pongo elegante pa' que brilles en la calle
de la cabeza a los pies, mira como te ves
yo visto al bloque entero, baby, otra vez

No tengo diamantes pero tengo mi don
visto con amor a toda mi nación
de tela y de sueños yo construyo el lugar
donde el barrio entero aprende a brillar

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The water tower fading in my mirror
The gravel road that raised me from a boy
Every year the edges getting clearer
This little town of sorrow and of joy

I know the names on every weathered headstone
I know the church bell's slow and tired song
But something in me always felt like dead stone
And I have known for years I don't belong

So goodbye, goodbye to the only home I've known
The fields where I was planted, but I never fully grown
I'm taking just my guitar and the dust upon my shoes
Goodbye to the small town, I've got nothing left to lose

My mother waves from underneath the awning
My father's hands too proud to say a word
The highway stretches out toward the dawning
The freest I have ever felt or heard

So goodbye, goodbye to the only home I've known
The fields where I was planted, but I never fully grown
I'm taking just my guitar and the dust upon my shoes
Goodbye to the small town, I've got nothing left to lose

Maybe one day I'll come back through here older
With stories that this place could never hold
But for now the road is on my shoulder
And the morning's turning all the gray to gold

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle through the quiet morning
Thread the light through every seam
Hands that move without a warning
Stitching up the whole block's dream

Every jacket holds a story
Every hem a life undone
In this small and dusty glory
Dressing daughters, dressing sons

And we rise, and we rise
In the cloth that someone gave us
And we rise, and we rise
Woven into something brave

From the wedding to the burial
Every thread he laid by hand
In the fabric of the ordinary
He was here, he made us grand

Slow build, the strings are climbing
The whole street wearing his design
No words now, just the timing
Of a needle and a line

And we rise, and we rise
In the cloth that someone gave us
And we rise, and we rise
Woven into something brave

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Those hands could roll a biscuit thin
And wipe the tears from off my chin
They held the Bible Sunday morning
And held me close without a warning

They planted rows of summer beans
The hardest worker that I've seen
A wedding band worn soft and gold
Those hands that never felt too old

Oh grandma's hands, they raised me right
They rocked the cradle through the night
They taught me how to love and pray
Those gentle hands have passed away
But I still feel them holding mine
Across the years, across the time

The last time that I saw her there
Her hands were folded in the chair
Still soft as cotton, worn with grace
A lifetime written on her face

Oh grandma's hands, they raised me right
They rocked the cradle through the night
They taught me how to love and pray
Those gentle hands have passed away

Now I've got children of my own
And when I hold them, I am shown
That love just passes hand to hand
Now finally I understand

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Three wrong chords and a borrowed guitar
A basement bar where the nobodies are
My voice was shaking, the lights were too bright
Seventeen and scared on that first open night

The crowd was thin, just a handful of souls
Nursing their beers and their own little holes
But something inside me just had to be free
So I sang my heart out for nobody to see

And oh, how far I've come from that stage
A kid with a song and a heart in a cage
I'd give anything now just to stand there again
Back at the start, back where it all began

These days the rooms are a whole lot bigger
But nothing compares to that hand on the trigger
That first taste of fear and the thrill of it all
The night I first answered the call

And oh, how far I've come from that stage
A kid with a song and a heart in a cage
I'd give anything now just to stand there again
Back at the start, back where it all began

If I could go back I would tell that kid
You won't believe the things that you did
So don't you stop, don't you put it down
There's a whole world waiting outside this town

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raised my hand into the haze
A whisper lost inside the noise
The louder voices fill the space
And swallow up my quiet voice

I had the words, I had the thought
They drifted out and came to naught
A ghost inside the conference room
Dissolving in the static gloom

Can you hear me, can you hear me now
I'm fading out somehow, somehow
Every word I try to say
Gets washed away, gets washed away

The table turns, they never see
The shape of what I tried to be
I fold back into muffled grey
Another thing I didn't say

Can you hear me, can you hear me now
I'm fading out somehow, somehow
Every word I try to say
Gets washed away, gets washed away

Maybe one day I'll be loud
A breaking wave above the crowd
But for now I drift and drown
In a room that talks me down

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete sky and a poisoned ground
They paved the world, not a leaf around
One last root in the cracking stone
Standing tall and standing alone

They took the forest, took the green
Built their towers, cold machine
But you still reach for the dying sun
The final witness, the only one

Stand! The last one standing
Stand! Against their demanding
They can't cut down what won't break
Every root for the planet's sake
Stand! The last tree breathing
Stand! While the world is bleeding

The bulldozers wait at the edge of dawn
But we chain our hands, we won't be gone
For every branch that they tore away
We scream defiance, we're here to stay

Stand! The last one standing
Stand! Against their demanding
They can't cut down what won't break
Every root for the planet's sake

Burn it down, their gray cathedral
From the ashes, something equal
Plant a seed in the broken lot
This is the war that can't be bought

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Me acuerdo de esa noche, micrófono en la mano
dieciséis años, el corazón en vano
temblando en la tarima, las luces me cegaban
canté pa' cuatro gatos pero todos me miraban

Dale, mira pa' atrás, cómo he crecido
de aquella esquinita donde nadie me ha oído
lo daría todo por volver a ese lugar
donde todo empezó, donde aprendí a soñar

La primera vez que el mundo me escuchó
un barcito vacío pero el alma se llenó
ahora suenan las bocinas, llenamos el lugar
pero nunca olvido donde tuve que empezar

Dale, mira pa' atrás, cómo he crecido
de aquella esquinita donde nadie me ha oído
lo daría todo por volver a ese lugar
donde todo empezó, donde aprendí a soñar

Si pudiera hablarle a ese nene con temor
le diría: tranquilo, esto se pone mejor
no sueltes el micro, no dejes de cantar
que to' lo que soñaste lo vas a lograr

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
It's a quarter past midnight, the house is asleep
There's a name on my phone that I swore I would keep
In a folder, in a drawer, in the back of my mind
But my thumb's got a habit of crossing the line

Three dots and they vanish, then they show up again
A wedding ring's heavy when you're typing like that
I know where this river runs, I've drowned here before
Still I'm knocking soft knuckles on a closed-up door

Delete, delete, but I never hit send to the dark
Just the little gray screen lighting up half my heart
She's asleep down the hall and I'm playing with fire
Texting someone I shouldn't on a thin copper wire

Morning's an honest man, it don't lie like the night
I'll erase every word in the cold gray light
But the truth is a coward, it hides in my hand
Love like this is a slow burn through everything I planned

One more message, one more match struck and dropped
One more promise to myself that tomorrow I'll stop
But tomorrow keeps texting back, lord, what do I do
When the wrong one feels righter than anything true

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I sold the bed, I sold the chair, I kept my mother's ring
The harbor smelled of diesel and the last familiar thing
They stamped a date into my life and waved me to the rail
The whole town shrinking backward like a story growing pale

And I'm an ocean wide from everyone I knew
Trading every gray-stone street for somewhere painted new
Carry me, carry me, salt water and steel
I'm learning what a goodbye on a dock can really feel

The phone cards and the postage, the accent I can't lose
A different coin, a different word for ordinary news
I dream in my old kitchen and I wake up to the gulls
Between two flags I'm hanging like a sail with no pulls

And I'm an ocean wide from everyone I knew
Trading every gray-stone street for somewhere painted new
Carry me, carry me, salt water and steel
I'm learning what a goodbye on a dock can really feel

They ask me where I'm from and I don't know what to say
Half my heart's in the harbor, half is here in the bay
But the kids will speak the new tongue and they'll never know the cold
Of the country that I folded up and carried in the hold

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Iron skillet roaring black, a furnace in the wall
Garlic burning incense in the temple of the small
Flour ghosts and rendered fat, the smoke that never dies
A cathedral built of cinnamon and woodsmoke in my eyes

It rises, it rises, the breath of the dead
The kitchen of my grandmother lives on inside my head
Bread and blood and rosemary, the altar and the flame
I breathe and I am six years old and screaming out her name

The stove is cold and gutted now, the house is sold for parts
But the scent comes hunting through the dark to detonate my heart
A phantom of paprika, a war-drum of the boil
The whole forgotten kingdom rising up out of the soil

They can take the bricks, they can take the land
They can scatter all the dishes that she held inside her hand
But one whiff of the simmer and the grave gives up its hold
And I am in her doorway and I'm never growing old

It rises, it rises, the breath of the dead
The kitchen of my grandmother lives on inside my head
Bread and blood and rosemary, the altar and the flame
I breathe and I am six years old and screaming out her name

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Phone buzz on the nightstand got me sweating in the groove
Wrong name, right feeling, baby watch me make a move
Thumb on the keyboard doing things it shouldn't do
Midnight got a rhythm and the rhythm points to you

Text I shouldn't send, but I send it anyway
Bass line in my chest got the better of the brain today
Uh, slippery, slippery, down the wrong street
Typing with the devil to a syncopated beat

Got a little gray bubble doing somersaults and flips
Got a guilty little smile creeping up onto my lips
Should delete it, should delete it, but the funk won't let me quit
One more message in the pocket and I'm down into the pit

My conscience playing tambourine, way off in the back
While the horns are screaming yes and the truth is turning black
Lay it down, lay it down, on the one and on the three
The wrongest thing I'm doing is the funkiest thing I see

Text I shouldn't send, but I send it anyway
Bass line in my chest got the better of the brain today
Uh, slippery, slippery, down the wrong street
Typing with the devil to a syncopated beat

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
There's one green thing on Concrete Avenue
A crooked little oak the bulldozers outgrew
They poured the world in asphalt, gray from coast to coast
But the tree threw up two fingers like a stubborn little ghost

Pick it up, pick it up, the last leaf in town
Upstroke on the guitar while the sky comes down
Dance around the only roots they couldn't pull free
The whole town's a parking lot but here's our tree

The pigeons hold a meeting on the only living branch
The mayor wants a Walmart and a brand new avalanche
Of cars and beige and money, but the kids all gather round
The last bit of forest in a permanently paved-over town

They can ticket where we're standing, they can fine us where we sway
But we'll skank around these branches till they tow our bones away
Horns up! one more chorus for the leaves and for the shade
The greenest little protest that a town has ever made

Pick it up, pick it up, the last leaf in town
Upstroke on the guitar while the sky comes down
Dance around the only roots they couldn't pull free
The whole town's a parking lot but here's our tree

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The screen door's done its slamming and the dishes all are still
We said our cruelest sentences and now we've had our fill
There's a silence in the kitchen you could lay down like a quilt
And both of us are sitting in the ashes and the guilt

Oh the quiet after, lord, the quiet after
When the storm has rolled on over and there's nothing left to say
Just the clock and the breathing and the long slow rafter
Of a love that's still standing at the ending of the day

I'd give up being right to hear you humming once again
This hush is heavier than any holler ever been
The banjo of my heart is broke, the fiddle's hanging low
And somewhere 'cross the table is the only place to go

So I reach across the silence and I find your tired hand
Don't need to win the argument, just need you to understand
The quiet ain't the end of us, it's where the mending starts
Two people too stubborn and too soft inside their hearts

Oh the quiet after, lord, the quiet after
When the storm has rolled on over and there's nothing left to say
Just the clock and the breathing and the long slow rafter
Of a love that's still standing at the ending of the day

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Thirty years behind the chair, the buzz that never stops
He's cut the mayor and the killer and the kid who runs the shop
Every secret in this town goes down that drain with the hair
The priest don't hear confessions like the man who holds the shears

SIT DOWN! tell him everything!
He already knows your name and what your daddy did last spring
The whole town in his mirror, the whole town in his hands
The only honest history this rotten city stands

He knows who's getting buried and he knows who's getting born
He knew your marriage failed before the ring was even worn
He never wrote a word of it, he carries it like stone
The loudest silent witness in a town that's never known

When he dies the truth dies with him, every name and every grave
A library of bloodlines that nobody thought to save
So I sit down in his chair and I let the clippers run
And I tell him all my sins like he's the only honest one

SIT DOWN! tell him everything!
He already knows your name and what your daddy did last spring
The whole town in his mirror, the whole town in his hands
The only honest history this rotten city stands

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the cobblestones across his slow stone chest
Built their steeples on his shoulders, told the children he could rest
The river is his breathing and the hills are where he lies
And the church bells are the only thing that keep him from his eyes

Don't wake him, don't wake him, the giant down below
We dance upon his sleeping bones and pray he never knows
The city of the living on a grave that still can rise
We built our whole tomorrow on a thing that someday wakes

My grandfather could feel him when the cellar floor would hum
A heartbeat in the bedrock like a slow funeral drum
The preachers say it's thunder and the rich men say it's trains
But I've felt the city shiver when the old one shifts his chains

And one cold day he'll stretch his arms and shrug the spires away
The banks and bones and bridges in a single rolling day
So light a candle, kiss your love, and step a little light
We're only borrowed tenants on the back of his long night

Don't wake him, don't wake him, the giant down below
We dance upon his sleeping bones and pray he never knows
The city of the living on a grave that still can rise
We built our whole tomorrow on a thing that someday wakes

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
String lights and a borrowed song, the last slow one tonight
You held your hand out, I don't know your name
We turned in the warm dark, two strangers in the light
And nothing in the whole world stayed the same

Just this once, just this turn
Two people the night will never learn
Close as breathing, gone by dawn
A dance, a hush, and then you're gone

The bride was someone's cousin, the band was almost done
You smelled like rain and someone else's wine
We didn't speak a sentence, we just moved as one
A stranger I will keep but never find

Somewhere there's a photograph where we're a blur of gold
Two guests who never met again, a story never told
And if I close my eyes I'm there, the floor beneath us slow
A love that lasted one whole song, then let each other go

Just this once, just this turn
Two people the night will never learn
Close as breathing, gone by dawn
A dance, a hush, and then you're gone

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights of the city were close as my hand
The demo was burning a hole in the dash
One call from the man with the contract and band
And a dream you could finally turn into cash

Oh the night I almost made it, lord, I felt it on my skin
The door was standing open and I never made it in
The rain came down like static on a song that never played
And I drove back to the nothing on the night I almost made it

The phone never rang and the morning came gray
The man found another, the moment went cold
I hung up the guitar and I put it away
And I aged forty years on a road getting old

Sometimes at the diner when the jukebox runs dry
I can hear the whole life that went sailing on by
The stages, the cities, the name up in lights
All buried out back of one almost night

Oh the night I almost made it, lord, I felt it on my skin
The door was standing open and I never made it in
The rain came down like static on a song that never played
And I drove back to the nothing on the night I almost made it

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Two a.m. and the world shut down, but the neon's still on
Vinyl booth, bottomless cup, every heartbreak's gone
The waitress knows my order and she knows my name
Nobody's a stranger here, we all came the same

Meet me at the all-night diner, baby, lights never low
Where the broken and the dreamers and the lonely all go
Coffee's always pouring and the grill never sleeps
It's the one little corner of the world that still keeps
A seat, a song, a midnight glow
Meet me where the all-night people go

First-date jitters by the window, trucker in the back
Kid who missed the last bus home with a heart half-cracked
We don't need an answer, we just need a place
Where the morning's still a rumor and there's room for every face

When your whole life's falling and you can't sleep at all
There's a light on the corner and it answers when you call
No questions and no closing time, just bacon and the rain
The diner keeps the lonely from going quite insane

Meet me at the all-night diner, baby, lights never low
Where the broken and the dreamers and the lonely all go
Coffee's always pouring and the grill never sleeps
It's the one little corner of the world that still keeps
A seat, a song, a midnight glow
Meet me where the all-night people go

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I rehearsed all the words I would say to your face
Kept 'em folded in my chest like a debt
Then the phone call came in and it took all that space
Now there's nobody left to forget

So I forgive you, I forgive you
To the photographs, to the empty chair
I forgive you, I forgive you
To the wind, to the ceiling, to the nobody there

You never said sorry and I never let go
We both thought we had time on the shelf
Now I'm laying it down 'cause I finally know
That the holding was hurting myself

It ain't for you, baby, it's for me
A weight I been carrying that I'm setting free
You can't hear it where you are, but I'll say it just the same
Forgiveness with no audience, just my own quiet name

So I forgive you, I forgive you
To the photographs, to the empty chair
I forgive you, I forgive you
To the wind, to the ceiling, to the nobody there

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
We grew up six streets over, never said a word
You were just a face in the yearbook, a name I half heard
Then I came back for the summer and there you were at the store
And the whole town felt like a love song I never heard before

Falling for you in the place that I'm from
Same old streets but my heart's coming undone
Every corner's got a memory and now they all got your face
Falling for my hometown all over in your embrace

We drove past the high school, past the field and the creek
You knew all my stories before I could speak
There's a shorthand in your laughter, there's a map in your eyes
Loving you is loving every road that I despised

They said get out, get gone, don't look behind
But everything I needed was the thing I left behind
Now the streetlights look like halos and the old town feels brand new
'Cause I never knew my home until I came home to you

Falling for you in the place that I'm from
Same old streets but my heart's coming undone
Every corner's got a memory and now they all got your face
Falling for my hometown all over in your embrace

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Dusty vinyl crackle, rain on the window pane
Back in my hometown, same bus, same lane
Saw you at the gas station, hood up, half asleep
Knew your face from the lunchroom, feelings buried deep
Now they surfacing slow like steam off the street
Same zip code heart with a brand new beat

Yeah, falling, falling, down these old roads
Somebody from the same place I was trying to outgrow
Low tape hiss, your number in my phone
Falling for the one who never left home

We talk on the porch where we both used to play
You remember my brother, you remember the day
The water tower's rusting, the diner's still the same
But I'm seeing it in color now I'm saying your name
Mellow piano loop, just a sample and you
Nostalgia and a future blending into the blue

Funny how the place I swore that I would leave
Got the only thing I never knew that I'd need
Slow it down, half speed, let the moment hold
Love in a small town never gets old

Yeah, falling, falling, down these old roads
Somebody from the same place I was trying to outgrow
Low tape hiss, your number in my phone
Falling for the one who never left home

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Oh, there's one little tree on the corner of Main
(Sha-la-la, the last one standing)
Where the sidewalk and the parking lot took over the lane
(Doo-wop, where the green's been landing)
My baby and I used to kiss in its shade
The last bit of summer this gray town has made

Last tree, last tree, won't you stay for me
(Sha-la-la-la-la)
The whole world's concrete but you're my green
(Doo-doo-wop)
Last tree, last tree, the prettiest thing I've seen
The only little leaf in this old machine

They planted a meter where the garden once grew
(Sha-la-la, the coins keep falling)
But they left me one branch and a sky-patch of blue
(Doo-wop, hear the robins calling)
So I carved out our names where the bark meets the air
A promise that something still grows over there

And when they come to cut you, oh, I'll stand in the way
(Sha-la-la-la-la)
The last little tree on the last green day
My darling and I and your branches above
The last bit of nature, the last bit of love

Last tree, last tree, won't you stay for me
(Sha-la-la-la-la)
The whole world's concrete but you're my green
(Doo-doo-wop)
Last tree, last tree, the prettiest thing I've seen
The only little leaf in this old machine

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down like it had somewhere to be
You stepped under, didn't ask, just looked at me
One small circle keeping two coats dry
Shoulder to shoulder, strangers passing by

Hold it a little closer, baby, don't let go
We got the same gray sky and the same slow road
Didn't know your name 'til a minute ago
Now I'm in no hurry for this rain to slow

Your sleeve against mine, half of me still wet
Laughing 'bout a bus that we both gonna miss yet
You smell like coffee and a little bit of smoke
Never met a stranger feel less like a stranger spoke

Hold it a little closer, baby, don't let go
We got the same gray sky and the same slow road
Didn't know your name 'til a minute ago
Now I'm in no hurry for this rain to slow

Two blocks left and the corner's coming fast
Funny how the shortest walks are the ones that last
If the clouds wanna break, let 'em wait a while
Keep me dry a little longer, give me one more mile

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Soft on the gas, let the engine hum low
Green of the morning on the streets of Rio glow
One more turn and the money's in the seat
Then I'm done with the running, done with the heat

Just one more job, one easy night
The city sways and the moon hangs right
Keep it smooth, keep it slow, let the avenue glide
Last time my hands hold the wheel and the tide

She's waiting at a café with a cup gone cold
Said leave it all behind 'fore you're too old
Mirrors stay empty, no siren, no fear
A quiet getaway and the coast is near

Just one more job, one easy night
The city sways and the moon hangs right
Keep it smooth, keep it slow, let the avenue glide
Last time my hands hold the wheel and the tide

Samba on the radio, low and warm
I'll trade the open road for her open arms
Foot off the pedal where the palm trees lean
Last job behind me like a fading scene

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We left the same launchpad, same red Texas dirt
Two tin cans rising, same dust on our shirt
You took the north tether, I drifted to the south
Now there's a quiet ocean of dark between our mouths

We're drifting, darling, slow and far
Two little lights pretending we're still one star
I'd radio I love you but the signal won't hold
Just static and the distance and the cold

Remember the cornfield, how we'd lie and count the sky
Never thought we'd be the ones up there saying goodbye
Your window's getting smaller every time I turn around
Gravity's a memory of solid Tennessee ground

We're drifting, darling, slow and far
Two little lights pretending we're still one star
I'd radio I love you but the signal won't hold
Just static and the distance and the cold

If you can hear me, hum that old refrain
The one we sang on the porch in the summer rain
I'll keep it playing 'til my air runs thin
Two astronauts and the dark we're drifting in

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Forty winters worn into this floor
The fiddle plays a hymn we can't ignore
Your hand in mine like a withered vine
One more turn 'fore the falling of the line

Dance me down, dance me low
Where the lamplight dies and the cold winds blow
We gave it all, every ash and ember
Last dance, love, so the dark remembers

The ring's gone thin as a wedding bell rust
We buried our children, our quarrels, our trust
Still your eyes hold the boy I knew
Under all this gray there's a sky still blue

Dance me down, dance me low
Where the lamplight dies and the cold winds blow
We gave it all, every ash and ember
Last dance, love, so the dark remembers

When the parson comes and the candles drown
Lay me beside you in the cold churchground
One grave, one stone, one tangled root
Dancing still in our Sunday suit

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the gray right up to my roots
Killed every garden, every shade, every fruit
I'm the last green thing on a dead concrete street
Birds still come for the only place to meet

Oh Lord, I'm the last tree standing
Where the whole world turned to stone
They paved the ground and the river and the meadow
Left me here aching and alone

Used to be a forest where the children would run
Now it's all asphalt cooking in the sun
A bulldozer's idling with my name in its jaw
Last leaf trembling 'gainst the company law

Oh Lord, I'm the last tree standing
