Where the whole world turned to stone
They paved the ground and the river and the meadow
Left me here aching and alone

But a seed slipped down through a crack in the lot
Gonna grow up green in this forgotten spot
You can pave me over, cut me to the ground
There's a root that runs under your whole damn town

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Morning shift, evening shift, never see my bed
Counting little money in the back of my head
Landlord knocking, rent dey climb the wall
Work like a lion still I'm bound to fall

Two jobs, no rest, eh, still I'm behind
Sweat on my collar but the bills don't mind
We dey hustle, we dey grind under heavy skies
Tell me when the morning gonna meet my eyes

Bus to the city when the rooster crow
Count every naira, watch it come and go
Mama say be patient, God go provide
But the price of everything just multiply

Two jobs, no rest, eh, still I'm behind
Sweat on my collar but the bills don't mind
We dey hustle, we dey grind under heavy skies
Tell me when the morning gonna meet my eyes

But I dey dance small even when it pain
Drum dey beat louder than the falling rain
One day this hustle go turn to gold
Keep my head up high till the story told

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle in the window, light on all night
Every seam I'm stitching gonna fit you right
Bring me your old coat, bring me your dream
I'll cut you a Friday at the edge of the seam

I dress the whole block, baby, head to toe
Every thread a heartbeat on the radio
Step out shining when the bassline drops
The whole street dancing in the clothes I sewed

Mrs. Brown's Sunday, the boy's first suit
The bride down the hall in a borrowed boot
Measure by the streetlight, hum to the kick
Give the corner a swagger, give the block a trick

I dress the whole block, baby, head to toe
Every thread a heartbeat on the radio
Step out shining when the bassline drops
The whole street dancing in the clothes I sewed

Spin, spin, let the fabric fly
Every body beautiful beneath the sky
From the corner store to the rooftop lights
I stitched this city into Saturday nights

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We rose together on a pillar of flame
Two souls reaching for the Maker's name
Now the tether's loose and you're floating wide
Lord, keep my brother on the other side

Though we drift apart in the endless night
There's a hand that holds us in the holy light
No dark so deep, no space so wide
That His mercy can't reach across the divide

I watch you shrinking like a setting sun
Can't pull you back, what's begun is begun
But I lift my voice through the silent black
God of the heavens, bring my brother back

Though we drift apart in the endless night
There's a hand that holds us in the holy light
No dark so deep, no space so wide
That His mercy can't reach across the divide

When the air runs low and the stars go dim
We'll both come home on a wing and a hymn
Glory, glory, the tether may break
But the love of the Lord no distance can shake

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole life in a careful hand
A house, a name, a wedding band
I watched it shimmer behind the glass
Then turned my shoulder, let it pass

I'm fading out of the life they planned
Dissolving slow like wet in sand
Goodbye to the future with my face erased
I'm walking into open space

Mother's tears in a folded letter
Father said I'd know no better
But the room was small and the walls were drawn
And I was a song they'd already sung

I'm fading out of the life they planned
Dissolving slow like wet in sand
Goodbye to the future with my face erased
I'm walking into open space

The noise it swallows every name they gave
I'm drowning sweet in the static wave
Whatever comes I won't be told
I'd rather be lost than be controlled

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The lanterns gutter on the trampled green
Last night of the fair, the longest I've seen
The carousel's slowing, the fiddler's half gone
Dawn's a gravedigger and it's coming on

One more turn 'fore the lights go black
One more song 'fore the road takes us back
The festival's dying like a candle in the rain
Kiss me in the ashes 'fore it's morning again

The fortune teller folded up her tent
Took all my coins and the warnings she'd lent
The ferris wheel ghost against a bruise of sky
Everything bright is just learning to die

One more turn 'fore the lights go black
One more song 'fore the road takes us back
The festival's dying like a candle in the rain
Kiss me in the ashes 'fore it's morning again

When the last booth's empty and the field is bare
I'll keep your ribbon and the smell of your hair
The music's a ghost in the cold wet ground
Last night of the fair, and I'm festival-bound

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
We ran out of words about an hour ago
Now it's just the fridge and the radio low
You're on your side, I'm staring at the wall
The loudest thing in the house is nothing at all

It's so quiet now, so quiet now
We burned all the matches, don't know how
To say the small thing that fixes it somehow
So we sit in the quiet now

I could cross the carpet, it's only ten feet
Feels like a country I can't cross in this heat
Your breathing's slowing, maybe you're asleep
Maybe you're awake in the same hole, just as deep

It's so quiet now, so quiet now
We burned all the matches, don't know how
To say the small thing that fixes it somehow
So we sit in the quiet now

Then your foot finds mine under the cold blue sheet
A white flag waving where the borders meet
We don't say sorry, we just let it land
The quiet goes soft, and you take my hand

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Forty years we riddim on this floor
Same old kitchen, same screen door
Gray in your locks but you still move slow and sweet
One more sway, baby, to the same heartbeat

Dance with me one more time, my love
Under the porch light and the moon above
We weathered every storm and the rent and the rain
Last dance, last dance, 'fore we part again

We raised up the children, we buried the rest
You were the calm in my hurricane chest
The band's grown quiet but the bass still hums
Hold me steady now while the evening comes

Dance with me one more time, my love
Under the porch light and the moon above
We weathered every storm and the rent and the rain
Last dance, last dance, 'fore we part again

Jah know the road was never paved so smooth
But every step we found a way to move
So turn me slow till the lantern's done
Forty years of dancing into one

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Raise my hand in the corner of the screen
Half a sentence out 'fore I'm cut from the scene
Got a good idea but it dies in my throat
Somebody louder steals the words that I wrote

Talk right over me, like I ain't even here
Mute on my mic and a fog in my ear
I had the answer but it faded to gray
Another room where I just drift away

They repeat my point like they thought of it first
Nod to the loud one, leave me with the worst
Rain on the window, lo-fi crackle hum
Counting the minutes till the meeting is done

Talk right over me, like I ain't even here
Mute on my mic and a fog in my ear
I had the answer but it faded to gray
Another room where I just drift away

But I'm writing it down, gonna find my own beat
A quiet little track where the verse is complete
When the loud ones fade I'll still press play
Got a voice in the headphones either way

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete in the morning, concrete at noon
They killed the whole forest by the end of June
One tree left standing in a parking lot crack
And the suits got a permit to take it back

Last tree standing, won't go down quiet
Chain yourself up, start a green riot
They paved paradise for a paycheck and a sign
Not this one, not this root, not this time

Developer smiling with a clipboard grin
Bulldozer revving, gonna cave it in
We got our boots and we got our spray
Drew a circle 'round the trunk, said you ain't taking it today

Last tree standing, won't go down quiet
Chain yourself up, start a green riot
They paved paradise for a paycheck and a sign
Not this one, not this root, not this time

Smash the gray, let the green break through
One tree is a fuse and the spark is you
They can cut it down but the seeds are out
There's a wild thing screaming under your town

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Subimos la montaña en la noche fría
Verde en el cielo, baby, qué maravilla
Luces bailando como tú en la pista
Nunca vi un color que se me resista

Mira el cielo, las luces del norte
Dale, mami, que la noche es fuerte
Verde, morado, encima de ti
Baila conmigo bajo el cielo así

Frío en la piel pero el fuego por dentro
Tu mano en la mía, perdemos el tiempo
El cielo es un beat que no para de sonar
Aurora encendida, vámonos a bailar

Mira el cielo, las luces del norte
Dale, mami, que la noche es fuerte
Verde, morado, encima de ti
Baila conmigo bajo el cielo así

Que se apague el mundo, que quede la luz
Tú, yo, y el norte, nadie más que tú
La aurora nos llama, sube el perreo
Bajo mil colores yo solo te veo

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lamp swung low on the plaza floor
Sweat in my collar, dust on the door
They clapped on the two and they leaned on the three
I played for the man who could carry me

My hands were two birds that had learned to bleed
The strings drank the blood of everything I'd done
He nodded once, then he lit a cigarette
And I knew the river had already run

So close I could taste the copper of the crown
So close the night leaned down to kiss my brow
Then the rooster cracked the morning open wide
And the almost stayed an almost, and I cried

A card in my pocket I never could call
A name like a key that fit no door at all
The heels still echo in my sleeping ears
A city of footsteps, a staircase of years

Don't pity the man who came one note short
The fire that I gave you was real, was real
The crown was a rumor, the night was the truth
And the truth keeps dancing on a worn-down heel

So close I could taste the copper of the crown
So close the night leaned down to kiss my brow
Now I play for the dust and the swinging light
For the almost, for the almost, for the night

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The candles are lit in some warmer room
While the hallway hums its fluorescent tune
My feet are two stones in a borrowed shoe
Bed nine needs water, bed four needs you

The clarinet wails from a phone left on
Some cousin's table, some living-room song
I sway to it once by the linen cart
Then the monitor calls and it pulls my heart

Oh play it, play it, let the reed cry out
For the double I'm holding when the day runs out
For the brisket I'm missing, the wine left to pour
I'm dancing alone on the seventh floor

Mrs. Adler is frightened of the dark
So I leave her a hallway, a sliver, a spark
Her hand in my hand is a folded dove
We count out the hours, we ration the love

Somewhere they're breaking the bread in two
Somewhere a child asks where is Sue
Tell them I'm holding the night in my arms
Tell them the holy is wearing alarms

Oh play it, play it, let the reed cry out
For the double I'm holding when the day runs out
When the sun comes up I'll go home and weep
Then I'll set my alarm and I'll fall asleep

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every third night the ceiling forgets me
The blanket lets go of its grip on my chest
I rise past the rooftops, the chimneys, the cables
The town is a tablecloth, neatly pressed

The fiddle below me keeps playing the village
The baker, the cobbler, the goat in the lane
And I am a kite with no string and no master
Laughing out loud at the shape of the rain

Up, up, where the reed-pipe is thin and silver
Up where my grandfather waves from a star
Don't wake me, don't wake me, the wind is a doorway
I finally know who and where they are

But morning's a hand that reels in the kite-string
I land in my body, a coat that's too small
The coffee, the bus, the gray of the pavement
And nobody's flying, no, nobody at all

My mother once told me her mother could fly
That the women of our line all rise in their sleep
We carry the wings like a folded letter
A promise too heavy, a secret to keep

Up, up, where the reed-pipe is thin and silver
Up where my grandfather waves from a star
Tonight when I close these two stubborn windows
I'll meet you, I'll meet you, wherever you are

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I drove out to the cemetery gate
With the words I'd been chewing for fourteen years
The granite don't answer, the granite don't blink
It just took my whiskey and it took my tears

You never said sorry while the saying was cheap
Now it costs me a Sunday and a tank of gas
I'm talking to grass that don't know my name
And a stone that was poured when the anger had passed

I forgive you, old man, though it's late as the moon
I forgive you to nobody, I forgive you to air
The weight didn't lift but it shifted its hold
And I drove back home in the lighter despair

They say let it go like it's setting down stone
But my hands had grown shapes from the carrying so long
I dropped it right here where the dandelions grow
And I stood there feeling so empty and so strong

Maybe forgiving's a thing that I do for me
A door that I close so the cold can't get through
You won't hear a word of it under that ground
But I needed to say it, I needed it true

I forgive you, old man, though it's late as the moon
I forgive you to nobody, I forgive you to air
The grass keeps on growing right over your name
And I'm finally, finally, finally not there

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Antenna on the water tower, bolted in the dark
Transmitter in a lunchbox throwing static like a spark
Frequency they never licensed, ninety-one point stray
I'm the ghost between the channels and I never gave a name

We broadcast from the basement, signal climbing through the floor
Knock-knock on the dial, baby, who's that at your door
They can't trace what they can't catch, I move it every night
Pirate on the airwaves, turning down the city's light

Kids in the projects got my station on the low
Spinning records that the bigwigs would never let them know
FCC van circling, but the block already knew
Flip the kill-switch, hit the alley, leave 'em chasing static blue

We broadcast from the basement, signal climbing through the floor
Knock-knock on the dial, baby, who's that at your door
They can't trace what they can't catch, I move it every night
Pirate on the airwaves, turning down the city's light

They own the towers, own the news, own the words you read
But they don't own the half a watt I bleed into your bed
One mic, one dream, one wire wrapped around a vent
The people's frequency don't pay no rent

Midnight I'm a preacher, three a.m. I'm a thief
Giving away the songs that bring the neighborhood relief
When they finally bust the door it'll be empty, just a note:
The signal's in the people now, you can't unhear what I wrote

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
I taped up the boxes with my father's old tape
Left the porch light on for nobody to see
The diner still serves the same coffee at six
But it won't pour another for me

The water tower's got my initials up high
From a summer when leaving was only a word
Now the highway's unrolling its long gray tongue
And I'm taking the dare that I never thought I heard

Goodbye to the grain silo, goodbye to the creek
Goodbye to the Friday-night lights and the field
I loved you too long and too quiet to stay
So I'm trading the known for whatever's revealed

My mother just nodded and folded a shirt
She knew before I did the road in my chest
The church bell, the cornfield, the names on the stones
They'll keep without me, they'll manage the rest

It isn't the people, it isn't the place
It's the size of the sky when you've memorized it all
There's a version of me that the town never met
And she's waiting somewhere past the county's last wall

Goodbye to the grain silo, goodbye to the creek
Goodbye to the Friday-night lights and the field
I loved you too long and too quiet to stay
So I'm trading the known for whatever's revealed

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
She walks the gray edge where the salt meets her knees
Hair like spun static, a halo of foam
She whispers to water the way you'd teach children
Go out now, come back now, the moon calls you home

And the tide leans in like it's learning to listen
Silver and slow in her papery hands
She's been here a hundred soft-spoken mornings
Writing her lessons in disappearing sand

The young ones don't see her, they hurry their phones
Past the woman who keeps the appointment of waves
But the ocean remembers a much older lesson
The pull and the patience, the way that it stays

And the tide leans in like it's learning to listen
Silver and slow in her papery hands
She's been here a hundred soft-spoken mornings
Writing her lessons in disappearing sand

When she's gone the water will keep the routine
In and then out like a breath through a shell
Maybe that's all that we ever can teach it
To leave and return, and to do it so well

So I'll stand where she stood when the gulls start to scatter
And practice the syllables under my breath
Go out now, come back now, the moon is still calling
And nothing she taught it was ever quite death

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Maps of a country no atlas has drawn
Rivers of vein under tissue-thin dawn
Knuckles like river stones, worn into round
Quiet machinery, making no sound

These are the hands, these are the hands
That braided the morning and buttoned the cold
These are the hands that held everything once
And loosened their grip as the loosening told

Flour still ghosts in the lines of her palm
A ring gone so thin it's a thread of a thread
She folds them at rest like two tired doves
On the white winter field of the hospital bed

I take them in mine and they're lighter than air
All of the lifting now lifted away
A lifetime of stirring and stitching and prayer
Settling soft as the end of a day

These are the hands, these are the hands
That braided the morning and buttoned the cold
These are the hands that held everything once
And loosened their grip as the loosening told

Somewhere a dough rises, somewhere a seam
Holds because she once taught it to hold
The hands go to sleep but the work stays awake
In the warm of the bread and the weave of the cold

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They came with a contract and a velvet pen
A cage made of gold for the words I bled
Sign on the dotted, become a brand
Let the marketing carve out the things you said

I tore it in half by the river of ink
Where the truth runs black and it owes no man
My verses are teeth and they won't be filed
Into jingles to sell what the merchants planned

I won't sell out, I won't lie down
My pen is a blade and it answers to none
Burn the advance, let the empire frown
The poem belongs to the fire, not the crown

They'll starve me out, let the silence rot
They own every stage and they own every light
But the word in my throat is a loaded thing
And it screams the louder the deeper the night

What is a poet who trades in his spine
For a softer chair and a quieter death
I'd rather howl in a roofless room
Than whisper their slogans with my final breath

I won't sell out, I won't lie down
My pen is a blade and it answers to none
Burn the advance, let the empire frown
The poem belongs to the fire, not the crown

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raised up my hand like a sapling in spring
And the room rolled right over the shoot
Three words in I'm a wave that breaks early
While a louder man borrows my fruit

He said it back slower, they nodded like bells
The very same notion I'd offered for free
Funny how genius needs a deeper voice
Funny how no one was hearing it from me

So I write it all down in a little black book
Every sentence they snatched from the air
One day I'll read it aloud to the table
With nobody left who can talk over there

My grandmother carried the same closed mouth
Through kitchens and councils and pews
A lifetime of swallowing half-spoken truths
And the bruise that you learn not to show is a bruise

Maybe the meek will not get the earth
Maybe they'll just get the minutes to take
But I'm done being furniture, done being air
There's a thunder I've saved for the table to shake

So I write it all down in a little black book
Every sentence they snatched from the air
One day I'll read it aloud to the table
With nobody left who can talk over there

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Woke up in the black suit, still tied to my neck
Casseroles cold on the counter, a house full of wreck
Flowers already wilting, the cards in a stack
Everybody went home and they ain't coming back

Morning after, sun don't care that you're gone
Morning after, world had the nerve to come on
I'm pouring two cups out of habit, just one
Morning after a funeral, weight of a ton

Phone still got your number sitting top of the list
Thumb hovering over a call that don't exist
Knees on the kitchen tile, I ain't even cried
It's the quiet that gets you, the after, the wide

Morning after, sun don't care that you're gone
Morning after, world had the nerve to come on
I'm pouring two cups out of habit, just one
Morning after a funeral, weight of a ton

They say grief come in waves but they lied, it's a tide
It don't crash, it just rises, you live on the side
I'll learn how to swim in it, slow, day by day
But today I just float and I let it stay gray

Fold the suit, hang it back, close the closet door
Step outside where the birds didn't get the news
World keep spinning, got nowhere to be
But I carry you with me in everything I do

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They had it all mapped out before I could walk
The ring and the firm and the house on the hill
A bride I never met and a life pre-approved
Signed in my name against my own will

I'm tearing up the blueprint they drew in my crib
Burning down the future that was never my own
They can keep the gold cage and the family name
I'd rather be lost than be somebody's clone

My father's face cracked like a dropped china plate
My mother held a prayer she'd been saving for years
I packed up one bag and the weight of their hopes
Walked out through a doorway of decades of fears

I'm tearing up the blueprint they drew in my crib
Burning down the future that was never my own
They can keep the gold cage and the family name
I'd rather be lost than be somebody's clone

Maybe one day they forgive me, maybe they won't
Maybe the empty chair haunts every feast
But I'd be a ghost in the life that they built
A suit full of nothing, a smile on a beast

So I'm out on the road with no script in my hand
Every turn is a question I get to ask why
It's terrifying freedom, it's cold and it's mine
And for the first time I'm steering, first time alive

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Forty hours a week on a marble floor
Watching folks shuffle past gallery four
But I stand by the one with the girl in the blue
And I've memorized everything that she'd do

Oh, she's painted in stillness, she'll never grow old
While my knees are complaining and my coffee's gone cold
But we keep each other through the long winter days
The guard and the girl in her gilt-frame haze

The scholars come arguing what the brushstrokes mean
The tourists just want a quick shot of the scene
But I know the way that the light hits her hair
At four in the afternoon, nobody there

They think that I'm guarding her, but it's the reverse
She holds up my hours, she lifts off the curse
Of a life spent in waiting by a velvet rope
She's two hundred years of unspeakable hope

When they cut the budget and they let me go
Who'll tell her good morning, who'll dim the bright glow
I'll buy me a postcard, the closest I'll get
And I'll prop her up nightly where the table is set

Oh, she's painted in stillness, she'll never grow old
While my knees are complaining and my coffee's gone cold
But we kept each other through the long winter days
The guard and the girl in her gilt-frame haze

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Close my eyes and I'm right back at five years old
Screen door slapping, the heat and the gold
Cinnamon climbing the walls like a vine
Grease in the iron skillet, oh that smell was mine

Take me back, take me back to that kitchen of hers
Where the okra was frying and the whole world was warm
Nutmeg and butter and the low gospel hum
Grandma's perfume was a Sunday-morning storm

Flour on her apron, sweat on her brow
Stirring something holy that I can't make now
I bought all the spices, I followed the steps
But it never smells right, it just never quite reps

They say smell is the sense that's wired to the past
One whiff and a decade comes flooding on back
So I light a small candle that says home on the side
And I let it pretend for an hour I'm a child

Take me back, take me back to that kitchen of hers
Where the okra was frying and the whole world was warm
Nutmeg and butter and the low gospel hum
Grandma's perfume was a Sunday-morning storm

Now I cook for my own and I leave the door wide
So the cinnamon climbs and it carries outside
Maybe somebody small breathing deep in the air
Will keep me forever the way I keep her there

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
The trumpet walks slow down a wet cobbled street
A black-feathered river of hats and of grief
My heels strike the dirge on the Spanish guitar
While the brass weeps a sorrow too wide for belief

We carry him low past the iron-laced balconies
The candles, the saints in their plaster-white frames
My hands clap the cross of two worlds in the dark
Andalusia and Tremé, both crying his name

Then the snare turns the corner, the tempo takes fire
The dirge becomes dancing, the dead become free
Strum it up, strum it up, let the duende rise
We bury the body and we set loose the glee

The parasols open, the handkerchiefs fly
The widow is laughing with tears on her chin
My compás is the heartbeat the brass band found
The joy and the mourning are kissing like kin

For death is a partner that everyone dances
Better to meet it with heels and with horn
Than to sit in the silence and let the dark win
So we second-line wild till the breaking of morn

Then the snare turns the corner, the tempo takes fire
The dirge becomes dancing, the dead become free
Strum it up, strum it up, let the duende rise
We bury the body and we set loose the glee

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The record spins on a dust-soft floor
Forty winters behind that door
Your hand finds mine like it always knew
The shape of me, the shape of you

The lamplight bends across your face
Time has worn a softer grace
We sway so slow the world stands still
A quiet song, a fading hill

So dance with me one final time
Let the silence hold the rhyme
Every year we've ever known
Folds into this hush we've grown
Dance with me, the lights grow dim
Love was always more than him or her or them

And the strings will rise like morning
With no words left to say
Just the weight of all our seasons
Drifting, drifting away

So dance with me one final time
Let the silence hold the rhyme
Every year we've ever known
Folds into this hush we've grown

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the gray where the cornfields grew
Put a parking lot where the river flew
But out by the diner on Maple Street
Stands one old oak in the asphalt heat

My daddy climbed it when he was small
Carved our name in the bark so tall
Now the bulldozers hum their tune
But she's still reaching for the moon

Oh the last tree standing, roots so deep
Guarding all the memories this town can't keep
They can pave the whole world gray and gone
But that old tree keeps holding on

The city men with their clipboard plans
Don't understand what a green thing stands

So I'll sit beneath her one more time
Let the shade fall down like an old church chime
And I'll thank the Lord for a patch of brown
In the last green heart of a paved-down town

Oh the last tree standing, roots so deep
Guarding all the memories this town can't keep
They can pave the whole world gray and gone
But that old tree keeps holding on

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
La lluvia cae sobre la avenida
Two strangers caught in the falling rain
One umbrella, two beating hearts
The storm that brought us, the storm that starts

Your shoulder touches mine so close
A trembling trumpet, a fragile rose
We do not speak, we do not know
But something blooms in the afterglow

Baila conmigo bajo la lluvia
Under one sky we are not alone
Two strangers walking, the violins crying
Love found a roof of our very own

The vihuela hums a tender sound
As puddles mirror the streetlight crowned

Ay, ay, ay, the rain will end
But will you stay, my newfound friend?
One umbrella, one chance to know
The stranger's name before you go

Baila conmigo bajo la lluvia
Under one sky we are not alone
Two strangers walking, the violins crying
Love found a roof of our very own

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Over the frozen river wide
The sky is dancing, the colors glide
Green and violet, a silken thread
The heavens spin where the angels tread

The clarinet cries a joyful tear
The fiddle laughs and the night draws near
My grandmother told of lights like these
Souls of the old ones in the trees

Oy, the lights, the northern fire
Dancing higher, ever higher
Lift your hands and turn around
Heaven kisses the frozen ground

The accordion swells like a beating heart
A waltz of wonder, a holy art

So we dance till the morning breaks
For every color the cold sky makes
A hora spinning beneath the glow
Of the painted sky and the silver snow

Oy, the lights, the northern fire
Dancing higher, ever higher
Lift your hands and turn around
Heaven kisses the frozen ground

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
His hands were mountains when I was young
Now they tremble like a hymn half-sung
The oak that shaded my whole life through
Is bending low in the evening blue

I see the river in his eyes run slow
A man I knew a lifetime ago
He asks my name some afternoons
Then hums the old forgotten tunes

Oh Father, Father, the light grows thin
The years roll out and they don't roll in
I'll hold your hand by the window pane
And watch the dust come down like rain

The banjo moans a low black sound
As autumn lays his crown on the ground

There's a long black train down a long dirt road
And a heavy heart and a heavy load
But before you go to that far green shore
Let me be your boy once more

Oh Father, Father, the light grows thin
The years roll out and they don't roll in
I'll hold your hand by the window pane
And watch the dust come down like rain

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Midnight engine, gravel scream
Trunk full of that white lightning dream
Sheriff's lights in the rearview glow
Hit the gas, baby, here we go

Groove so greasy, the bassline drops
Dodging every one of the county cops
Mason jars rattling in the back
Ooh, we're sliding off the track

Moonshine run gone wrong, wrong, wrong
Playing the devil's favorite song
Get on up, get down, hit the floor
The law is knocking at the door

Wheels spinning in the Georgia clay
We took a wrong turn yesterday

Uh! Slap that bass, work that wah
Running faster than the long arm of the law
Sweat on the brow and the horn section blows
Where this night ends, nobody knows

Moonshine run gone wrong, wrong, wrong
Playing the devil's favorite song
Get on up, get down, hit the floor
The law is knocking at the door

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Finger on the screen, heart in my throat
Ten years of silence, never wrote
The dial tone ringing like a beating drum
Mama, it's your prodigal son

Pride built a wall that the years made tall
Now I'm just praying that you answer the call
I rehearsed the words but they all came undone
The second you whispered, "Is that my son?"

Mama, I'm sorry, I let the time slip
Lost in the city on a one-way trip
But I'm calling you now, swallowing pride
Come back to the warmth that I left outside

You said you forgave me before I could speak
And I cried like a child, my voice went weak

All the birthdays I missed, all the Sundays I skipped
All the love that you gave from the day I was gripped
In your arms, in your prayers, every night you stayed up
Mama, I'm coming home, fill my cup

Mama, I'm sorry, I let the time slip
Lost in the city on a one-way trip
But I'm calling you now, swallowing pride
Come back to the warmth that I left outside

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights of the city were close enough to touch
One more step, it wasn't much
The fiddle played a hopeful tune
Beneath a thin and trembling moon

So close, so close, the door was wide
Then the wind blew cold and turned the tide
The clarinet let out a sigh
As the golden hour passed me by

Oh, the night I almost made it through
The sky was gold, the dream was true
But close is just another word
For the saddest song you ever heard

The accordion weeps a bittersweet sound
For the inch of ground I never found

But a dance is a dance even when you fall
And a heart still beats through it all
So lift your feet and spin once more
For the almost knocking at the door

Oh, the night I almost made it through
The sky was gold, the dream was true
But close is just another word
For the saddest song you ever heard

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night I close my eyes
And I am ripped into the skies
Wings of fire on my back
Leaving earth and its endless black

The ground falls down, the chains released
I ride the storm, I am the beast
Thunder roars and the heavens crack
But I always wake and I'm pulled back

I'm flying, flying, breaking the chain
Above the clouds and beyond the pain
But the dawn always drags me down
Back to the dirt and the dreaming ground

Double kick like a beating heart
Tearing the sleeping world apart

Will I ever rise and never fall?
Will I answer the burning call?
The dream returns when the moon is high
And again, again, I touch the sky

I'm flying, flying, breaking the chain
Above the clouds and beyond the pain
But the dawn always drags me down
Back to the dirt and the dreaming ground

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Neon low and the smoke hangs thin
Laid my heart on the table to win
Should've folded when the river ran cold
But I chased that hand, did what I'm told

You dealt me a smile, I dealt you my trust
Now the chips are gone and the velvet's dust
One more bet and I crossed the line
Gambled away what was yours and mine

The game went too far, baby, too far gone
I bet it all and the night went wrong
Should've walked away when I had the chance
But I lost it all in a losing dance

Aces and eights in my trembling hand
A dead man's promise I can't understand

Ooh, I'd give it all back if I could
Every card, every night, every misunderstood
But the dealer don't deal you the past again
So I'm cashing out my heart, my friend

The game went too far, baby, too far gone
I bet it all and the night went wrong
Should've walked away when I had the chance
But I lost it all in a losing dance

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Under the elm where the pigeons mourn
Sits the old man with his collar torn
Five dollars a game and a crooked grin
He'll bury you slow, then he'll do it again

His fingers move like a hangman's rope
Trading you time for a sliver of hope
The city walks by in its Sunday best
While he plays for a dollar and a place to rest

Checkmate, son, in the falling dark
The king is dead in the silent park
He's hustled kings and he's hustled fools
With nothing but pawns and his own cold rules

A pawnshop watch and a paper cup
He never folds and he won't give up

They'll find him cold by the board one day
With the white queen toppled and the crows at play
But the game goes on like a long black hearse
In the haunted square of the universe

Checkmate, son, in the falling dark
The king is dead in the silent park
He's hustled kings and he's hustled fools
With nothing but pawns and his own cold rules

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Headlights sweep the empty street
A quiet hum, a slowing beat
Third floor window, the light is on
But everyone I knew is gone

The city breathes in shades of blue
A fading map of me and you
I slow the car but I don't stop
Just let the memory softly drop

Driving past, driving past
Nothing gold was made to last
The windows glow but the rooms are strange
Everything stays and everything changed

And the streetlights blur like falling stars
Through the rain on the windshield bars
Somewhere in there a younger me
Is still in love and still believes

Driving past, driving past
Nothing gold was made to last
The windows glow but the rooms are strange
Everything stays and everything changed

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete river, glass and stone
One green soul standing all alone
They paved the meadow, paved the stream
But she still holds an emerald dream

Mama used to rest in her cool, soft shade
Before the money men got their way
Now she's the last of a greener time
Reaching up through the gray and grime

Ooh, the last tree, swaying slow
Singing soft what the city don't know
Roots run deep where the soul still grows
In the cracks of the world, she glows

Leaves like velvet on a tired sky
Watching all the busy people hurry by

So don't you cut her down, no
Let her stand where the wildflowers used to grow
She's a hymn, she's a prayer, she's a memory
The last green heart of you and me

Ooh, the last tree, swaying slow
Singing soft what the city don't know
Roots run deep where the soul still grows
In the cracks of the world, she glows

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I walked away from the light one day
Let the cold world steal my prayers away
The drums went quiet, the sky turned gray
But something inside me would not stray

Through the long nights I was on my own
Feeling so far from the road back home
Then I heard the rhythm in my chest
Calling me back to the One who knows best

I lost it, I found it, my faith again
Dancing in the rain, oh, amen, amen
Lift my hands to the morning sky
The fire's back and I'm alive

Log drum rolling like a beating soul
Piece by piece I am becoming whole

Eh, eh, the light never left at all
It was waiting through the rise and fall
So I move my feet and I sing it loud
Found my faith in the dancing crowd

I lost it, I found it, my faith again
Dancing in the rain, oh, amen, amen
Lift my hands to the morning sky
The fire's back and I'm alive

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They offered me gold for a softer tune
Said change your words and you'll own the moon
But my pen don't bend for a diamond chain
I write the truth through the joy and pain

The big men whisper, "Boy, play the game"
Trade your soul for a little fame
But the rhythm I carry, you cannot buy
It's a fire in my bones till the day I die

I won't sell out, no, not for the crown
Keep my words real when they try to drown
Dance to my truth in the morning light
A poet's heart and I won't sell out tonight

Shaker shaking like a restless mind
Leaving all their golden lies behind

Eh, you can keep your shiny throne
I'd rather walk these streets alone
With a verse so pure and a beat so free
The truth is the only currency

I won't sell out, no, not for the crown
Keep my words real when they try to drown
Dance to my truth in the morning light
A poet's heart and I won't sell out tonight

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Diesel hum and a coffee gone cold
Three a.m. and the highway's a grave
I dialed your number from the edge of the world
Just to hear something I couldn't save
The phone rings out like a churchyard bell
While the wipers drag through the rain
I'm a thousand miles from the bed we made
And the dark don't know my name

Pick up, darlin', it's me on the line
A ghost in a cab with a load full of time
The mile markers count like the days that I'm gone
Say you're still there when the dawn comes on

There's a crow on the wire where the streetlights die
And a Bible split open on the seat
I been talking to Jesus and the white dotted line
About everything I couldn't keep
Your voice comes soft like a candle flame
Sayin' baby, just come on home
But the road is a river that won't run dry
And it carries me out alone

Pick up, darlin', it's me on the line
A ghost in a cab with a load full of time
The mile markers count like the days that I'm gone
Say you're still there when the dawn comes on

When the engine quits and the dark gets loud
When they lay me down in the cold red ground
I'll dial you up from the other side
And whisper I loved you the whole damn ride

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Maleta en la mano, ya no miro atrás
Esta calle pequeña no me da na' más
Crecí en la esquina contando los días
Soñando con luces, con otra avenida
La terminal late como un corazón
El bus enciende, suena mi canción
Mamá en la ventana me dice adiós
Pero el mundo me llama con su otra voz

Me voy, me voy, y no vuelvo más
El pueblo se queda, yo busco el compás
Luces de ciudad, llévame ya
Me voy pa' lo grande, no hay vuelta atrás

Los mismos rostros, la misma canción
El reloj de la plaza marcando el reloj
Yo quiero más alto, yo quiero volar
Donde nadie sepa de dónde vengo a llegar
Guardo en el pecho el olor a café
La risa de abuela, lo que dejaré
Pero el alma me empuja, no puedo frenar
Hay un beat en mis venas que me hace bailar

Me voy, me voy, y no vuelvo más
El pueblo se queda, yo busco el compás
Luces de ciudad, llévame ya
Me voy pa' lo grande, no hay vuelta atrás

Y si algún día regreso, será con corona
Con historias de noches que nadie perdona
Pero hoy es la fuga, hoy rompo el cristal
Adiós pueblito, me voy a brillar

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody gave us a chance from the start
Small boys with big fire inside the heart
They laughed at our jerseys, faded and torn
But we been hungry since the morning we were born
Whistle blow, the stadium dey shake
Every single pass na destiny we make
Grandma dey pray for under the moon
We carry the village inside this room

We rise, we rise, when they count us out
Underdog anthem, hear the people shout
Ninety minutes, give them everything
We be the small team wey go be the king

Down by one and the clock dey run
Sweat for my eyes like the setting sun
Coach say believe, no time to fear
The whole town watching on the screen out there
I catch the ball and the world go slow
I hear my mama from the crowd, she know
One more push, one more breath, one more
We kick the door wey them lock before

We rise, we rise, when they count us out
Underdog anthem, hear the people shout
Ninety minutes, give them everything
We be the small team wey go be the king

Final second, the net dey sing
The whole stadium fall, the bells dey ring
From the dust to the gold, we no go bow
The small dream big — and we win am now

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
I loaded the jars in the back 'fore dawn
My daddy's old recipe, my daddy long gone
The county was sleeping, the river was wide
And I thought the good Lord was riding my side
But the blue lights came up on the gravel and clay
And the wheels left the road where the willow trees sway
I hollered out Jesus the second I spun
For the wages of moonshine and the running I done

Lord, carry me over the water tonight
Wash all the sin off and make my soul white
I ran from the law but I'm running to You
Lord, carry me over, see this sinner through

The glass it was broken, the liquor ran free
Like the years I been pouring my life out of me
I crawled from the wreck with my hands in the air
And I felt something holy come down through the prayer
No more midnight roads, no more devil's own brew
I'm trading the jar for a pew and a view
The sheriff stood over me, shook his old head
But I heard angels singing the words that You said

Lord, carry me over the water tonight
Wash all the sin off and make my soul white
I ran from the law but I'm running to You
Lord, carry me over, see this sinner through

They can lock up my body, they can take what I made
But they cannot unmake the new vow that I prayed
From the ditch to the glory, from the cuffs to the choir
I walked out the water and into the fire

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
First chair by the window, been here twenty years
Clippers like a heartbeat, hummin' in your ears
I know every fade from the mayor to the kid
Know secrets in this town that the whole town hid
Monday it's the deacon, Tuesday it's the crew
Wednesday old man Charlie tellin' lies that ain't true
I catch every story in the buzz of the blade
Keep the change and the gossip, that's the bread that I made

Line 'em up, line 'em up, take a seat in my chair
I'm the heart of the block, I been everywhere right here
From the front to the funeral, the wedding to the war
The barber knows the whole town, what you waitin' for

Little man came in nervous 'fore his first day at school
I gave him a fresh cut and a couple of rules
His pops sat in this same chair back in eighty-nine
Now they both come Saturday, regular as time
I seen the corner change, seen the rent go high
Seen the regulars come, seen the regulars die
But the door keeps swinging and the mirror stays clean
I'm a museum and a church and a town magazine

Line 'em up, line 'em up, take a seat in my chair
I'm the heart of the block, I been everywhere right here
From the front to the funeral, the wedding to the war
The barber knows the whole town, what you waitin' for

They could build all the towers, let the new money flow
But they still gotta walk past my window to know
Who they was, who they is, what the neighborhood meant
Every head in this city got a piece of me lent

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Packed my whole life in a battered old case
Kissed my mum goodbye with the salt on her face
The horn on the harbour, the gulls overhead
Leaving the country my grandfather bled
Upbeat and offbeat, my heart's doing time
Skanking on the deck to the rhythm of the brine
Waving at a shoreline that's shrinking away
New world up ahead and I can't make it stay

Over the sea, over the sea
A pocket full of nothing and a head full of free
Pick up your feet, won't you dance with me
We're leaving it behind, over the sea

The brass section blaring like the ship's old crew
Everybody on board got a someone they knew
We share out the bread and we share out the fear
Fifteen days of water 'til the harbour gets near
New language on my tongue gonna twist it around
New job, new street, gonna learn the new sound
But the rhythm in my chest is the one from back home
So wherever I land I'm never alone

Over the sea, over the sea
A pocket full of nothing and a head full of free
Pick up your feet, won't you dance with me
We're leaving it behind, over the sea

Might be years 'til I see that green hill again
Might write a hundred letters with a shaking pen
But tonight I'm gonna dance on the rolling dark blue
And carry the old country inside of the new

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Warm light through the blinds on a slow Sunday morn
The smell of the talcum where the regulars are born
My hands know the curve of every head on the street
Every worry, every wedding, every face I repeat
Slow groove on the radio, the fan turning low
Tell me 'bout your troubles, baby, take it real slow
I been cuttin' since my father gave me clippers and a dream
Now the whole town's reflection lives inside this machine

Sit down, let me hold the weight off your mind
The barber knows the secrets that the city left behind
Every soul in the mirror, every story I keep
I'm the keeper of the town while the whole town sleeps

Got the preacher and the player in the very same chair
Got the boy gettin' brave for the girl over there
I heard the first heartbreak and the last goodbye
Watched a generation grow up right under my eye
The music keeps it honest and the buzz keeps it sweet
There's a love in this labor that's hard to repeat
When they leave a little lighter and they walk out tall
I know I held a piece of every one of y'all

Sit down, let me hold the weight off your mind
The barber knows the secrets that the city left behind
Every soul in the mirror, every story I keep
I'm the keeper of the town while the whole town sleeps

When my hands finally still and the chair turns cold
Somebody read these walls like the stories they hold
Every name, every face, every fade, every tear
The whole town's gonna miss me when I'm not right here

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the table, a glass and a glow
Three little dots that come and they go
All of our forever in a screen lighting blue
Funny how the smallest thing says we're through
The saxophone weeping like it knew all along
That the shortest little message could end such a long song
No door slammed shut, no rain on the pane
Just a soft little buzzing and the start of the rain

You ended it in letters on a cold little screen
The loneliest goodbye that I've ever seen
No whisper, no warning, no kiss in the dark
Just a text in the silence tearing me apart

I rehearsed all the speeches I would never get to say
Watched the cursor stop blinking as you faded away
The piano keeps asking what I'm supposed to do
With a heart full of music and a phone full of you
We could've had the trumpet and the slow last dance
Instead I got punctuation and the end of romance
The band plays on softly as I order one more
Love in the key of a closing door

Somewhere there's a ballad we will never complete
A bridge with no ending and a verse incomplete
So I'll fold up the napkin where I'd written your name
And tip the old piano man who feels the same

You ended it in letters on a cold little screen
The loneliest goodbye that I've ever seen
No whisper, no warning, no kiss in the dark
Just a text in the silence tearing me apart

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Última noche, las luces no paran
Los pies en la arena, las almas se aman
Tres días bailando, no quiero dormir
El sol va a salir y no quiero partir
El bajo retumba dentro del pecho
La gente cantando, el cielo deshecho
Manos arriba, perreo hasta el fin
Que suene el último, baila por mí

Una noche más, una noche más
Que no pare la música, dale, no pares ya
Manos al cielo, vívelo, ven
La última noche, bailemos también

Conocí a alguien cuando cayó el primer beat
Ahora bailamos como un solo latir
No sé tu nombre pero sé tu calor
Esta madrugada es nuestra, mi amor
El DJ levanta la mano final
Y el mundo se enciende en un grito mortal
Mañana es el lunes, la vida real
Pero hoy somos fuego, hoy somos festival

Una noche más, una noche más
Que no pare la música, dale, no pares ya
Manos al cielo, vívelo, ven
La última noche, bailemos también

Cuando se apaguen las luces de neón
Guardaré este beat dentro del corazón
El polvo, el sudor, los amigos, la sal
Nunca se acaba lo que vives de verdad

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The sun hung low on the old front porch
Last day of summer like a fading torch
Boxes by the door and the car packed tight
Tomorrow I drive into a brand new light
Mama held my hand and she started to pray
For the child she's been raising fixing to go away
The cicadas were singing like a choir in the trees
And I felt the whole Lord moving through the breeze

Carry me, carry me, into the new
Everything I been, everything I knew
Lay it at the altar of the road up ahead
Carry me, Father, where my future is led

We ate on the table where I grew up small
My daddy said son, you're gonna stand up tall
The screen door slamming like it always done
But it won't slam for me when tomorrow comes
I walked the old field where I learned to believe
Folded up my childhood and tucked it in my sleeve
Thank You for the summers, thank You for the home
Give me grace and courage for the road I roam

Carry me, carry me, into the new
Everything I been, everything I knew
Lay it at the altar of the road up ahead
Carry me, Father, where my future is led

When the homesick midnight finds me far away
Let me hear the porch and the words they used to say
I'm never really leaving 'cause I carry it inside
The last day of summer and the love that won't subside

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Rainy windshield, vinyl crackle in the night
Jars in the trunk catching pale moonlight
Kept it slow on the backroads, kept the headlights dim
Daddy's old recipe filling to the brim
But the bridge was washed out where the creek runs deep
And I traded all my plans for a promise I can't keep
Smoke off the engine, mist over the hood
Guess the run went wrong like I knew it would

Slow down, slow down, watch it all unwind
Moonlight on the broken glass, leave it all behind
No sirens out here, just the crickets and me
A run gone wrong and a long way to be free

Sat on the bumper rolling smoke in the dark
Watching all the moonshine soak into the bark
Phone got no signal, just a hum and a hiss
Never thought my big break would crash down like this
Lamp on the dashboard flickers tired and gold
Got a heart full of nothing and a story half told
The frogs keep singing like they don't even care
That I lost the whole batch on a wing and a prayer

Maybe that's alright, maybe that's the sign
This ain't no life, all this running and the wine
I'll walk home slow when the rain finally clears
Leave the wreck to the river and the long quiet years

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Soft on the shoreline the lanterns burn low
The last of the music, the last of the glow
We swayed in the sand to a gentle guitar
While the festival faded like a falling star
Your head on my shoulder, the tide coming in
The summer slips quiet through the noise and the din
A whisper of cymbals, a brush and a sigh
The loveliest ending under velvet sky

One more song, let it linger and stay
Before the morning carries the lights away
Hold me gentle in the warm evening breeze
The last night of the festival, slow as you please

The vendors are folding their colors and tents
The perfume of jasmine and the day's last events
We dance with no hurry, we dance with no end
Just the moon and the bossa and you, my friend
The waves keep the time that the drummer let go
And nobody's counting how slow we can go
Tomorrow's a postcard we'll send to ourselves
With the sound of the sea on the bossa nova shelves

When the last light is gone and the stage stands bare
I'll still hear the rhythm of you in the air
We'll carry this softness wherever we roam
The last night of the festival walking us home

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Sixteen years old with a borrowed guitar
Three broken chords and a dream way too far
The mic smelled like beer and my hands wouldn't stop
Four people clapping when the feedback would pop
I screamed out a song that I wrote in my room
About hating my town and impending doom
The sound guy was yawning, the bartender too
But I never felt more like I knew what was true

First open mic, three chords and a scream
A kid on a stage with a stupid little dream
I was terrible, I was loud, I was free
That shaking little nobody made me into me

Blew out the amp on the very last note
Forgot half the words that I painfully wrote
My voice cracked in two and my knees turned to rust
But I left there electric, alive and unjust
Now it's twenty years gone and I'm playing big rooms
But nothing's as loud as those four-person tombs
Where a scared little punk found a reason to yell
And dragged his whole future right out of that hell

First open mic, three chords and a scream
A kid on a stage with a stupid little dream
I was terrible, I was loud, I was free
That shaking little nobody made me into me

So here's to the dives and the half-empty nights
The blown-out PAs and the cheap stinging lights
Every kid with a chord and a fire in their chest
Go be terrible loud — that's how you get the best

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Door stopped slamming, now the silence is worse
Every word that we threw like a loaded curse
I can hear you breathing in the room down the hall
And the quiet is screaming louder than it all
My fists are still shaking but there's no one to hit
Just the wreck of us sitting in the dark of it
I didn't mean the half of the daggers I threw
Now the silence is the only thing left between me and you

ThE QUIET! It's tearing me apart!
Louder than the screaming, it's ripping out my heart!
Say something, say nothing, just don't let it stay!
The quiet after the fight is the loudest decay!

I count every second that you don't walk in
Feel the weight of the silence pressing on my skin
We built this whole love just to burn it for spite
Now we're both just survivors of a senseless fight
The rage burned out fast but the ash is so cold
And I'd trade all my pride to just have you to hold
The walls are too thin and the night is too long
And I don't know how to say that I had it all wrong

Then I hear your footsteps soft across the floor
And the silence finally breaks at the bedroom door
No more screaming, no more war, just a quiet that heals
Your hand finds my hand and the whole world is real

ThE QUIET! It's tearing me apart!
Louder than the screaming, it's ripping out my heart!
Say something, say nothing, just don't let it stay!
The quiet after the fight is the loudest decay!

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Radar lighting red on the dashboard glow
Foot to the floor where the dark clouds grow
They said settle down, get a house, get a wife
But I'm married to the lightning, it's the love of my life
Funnel on the horizon and my pulse hits the floor
Every bone in my body wants to run at the roar
Can't sleep in a bed when the sky's coming down
I'm a engine and a tempest, I can't settle down

Chase it, chase it, into the dark
Where the thunder breaks open and the sky throws a spark
Faster, faster, I won't slow down
I'm a storm in a body that can't settle down

Got a girl back in Kansas with her hands on her hips
Saying baby come home from your dangerous trips
But the pressure keeps dropping and it's pulling my soul
To the green of the heavens where I lose all control
The bass of the thunder is the only heart I know
The break in the clouds is the only place I go
Wind tearing sideways, I am right where I should be
Where the storm and the silence make a maniac free

Chase it, chase it, into the dark
Where the thunder breaks open and the sky throws a spark
Faster, faster, I won't slow down
I'm a storm in a body that can't settle down

One day the big one's gonna swallow me whole
And I'll vanish in the vortex, the price of my soul
But I'll grin at the funnel as it takes me up high
A storm chaser only rests in the eye of the sky

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I been around the world and back, seen every kind of light
But I came home to this old street on a slow December night
There she was on the corner where we used to wait for school
Grown into a woman now, Lord, and I'm the only fool

Same cracked sidewalk, same old bar, same neon buzzin' low
She smiled like she remembered all the things I let go
My heart went down like a hammer, boys, my mouth done lost the keys
Fell for my own hometown again right down on my knees

Fell for somebody from my hometown
Where the river runs slow and the sun goes down
I traveled far just to circle around
And lose my heart to my own hometown

My daddy worked this dusty mill, my mama said stay near
I laughed and packed a suitcase, swore I'd never settle here
Now I'm sweatin' through my collar, askin' her to stay
The road I ran from years ago done brought me back this way

Funny how the thing you leave is the thing that calls you home
Funny how the longest road won't let a man roam
She took my hand, said honey, what took you so long
And I had no answer, just this old, sweet song

Fell for somebody from my hometown
Where the river runs slow and the sun goes down
I traveled far just to circle around
And lose my heart to my own hometown

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
The kettle sings to no one in the dim of evening blue
Two chairs at the table and I only need the two
Your apron on the hook still holds the ghost of rosemary
I strike a match alone now, love, and cook this grief for me

You measured salt by memory, you stirred the pot by heart
I follow lines you never wrote, undoing every part
The onion makes me weep again, a mercy and a lie
I tell the empty kitchen it is only in my eye

One plate, one glass, one candle burning low
The dance goes on for one, my love, the only step I know
I set the table slowly, then I clear it just the same
A tango for the widower who whispers your sweet name

The portions are too generous, I always make for two
I eat my half by lamplight and I leave the rest for you
The radio plays something that we swayed to long ago
I hold the wooden spoon and turn, alone across the floor

They say that time will teach me how to want a little less
To simmer down the longing, to forgive the emptiness
But every careful supper is a letter that I send
To a woman in the kitchen who will never come again

One plate, one glass, one candle burning low
The dance goes on for one, my love, the only step I know
I set the table slowly, then I clear it just the same
A tango for the widower who whispers your sweet name

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine's breathing, low and mean, two minutes on the clock
Radio static cutting through, my hands are on the lock
They said one more and then I'm out, just drive and never look
But the city's got a memory and it's reading from my book

Last job, last light, foot down through the night
Run the red, hold the line, everything's alright
Last job, last run, before the morning comes
Just the road and the rhythm and my heartbeat like a drum

Three doors slam, the bag is in, go go go go go
Sirens stitching up the dark, a blue and screaming glow
I thread the needle, sixth gear, the bridge is dead ahead
This is the dream I drive away from, this is what I dread

Last job, last light, foot down through the night
Run the red, hold the line, everything's alright
Last job, last run, before the morning comes
Just the road and the rhythm and my heartbeat like a drum

There's a house out past the county where nobody knows my face
A quiet kind of nothing and I'm running for that place
If I make it through this corner, if the tires hold the ground
I'll let the engine cool forever, never make another sound

Dawn is cracking open like a promise I can keep
The rearview's finally empty and the road begins to sleep
I ease it off the highway where the morning birds are loud
And I leave the keys inside it, walking off into the crowd

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Neon hums against the dark, a memory in chrome
But underneath the city light I'm driving toward home
Flour in the morning air, the sugar and the spice
A cassette of her humming low, the world was warm and nice

Cinnamon and starlight, drifting through the years
The kitchen of my childhood glowing soft against my fears
I close my eyes, I'm seven, the oven's golden hum
A grandmother's kitchen calling, telling me to come

Vinyl spinning in my chest, the bread upon the rack
If I could fold the decades I would drive the whole way back
Butter on the counter, steam upon the glass
A hologram of yesterday I cannot make it last

Cinnamon and starlight, drifting through the years
The kitchen of my childhood glowing soft against my fears
I close my eyes, I'm seven, the oven's golden hum
A grandmother's kitchen calling, telling me to come

The synth fades into silence and the highway turns to gray
The house is gone, the kitchen's gone, but somehow I still stay
In that smell I cannot capture, in that warmth I can't reclaim
A flicker on the dashboard, just the echo of her name

So I let the engine carry me through midnight and through chrome
And the scent of her forever is the only word for home

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Volví al barrio donde crecí, the same old block, the same old beat
And there you were on the corner, mami, sweeping me right off my feet
We went to school here, same buses, same heat
Now look at you, dios mío, you're the dream on my street

De mi pueblo, de mi gente, you're the one I never saw
Grew up right beside me, now you're breaking every law
La misma calle, same old corner, brand new fire in my chest
Falling for my hometown, baby, and I never would have guessed

Dance with me under the same streetlight where we played as kids
Now the rhythm's got me dizzy and I'm doing what love bids
Your mama knows my mama, todo el mundo knows our names
But the way you move tonight, mami, lights the whole block in flames

De mi pueblo, de mi gente, you're the one I never saw
Grew up right beside me, now you're breaking every law
La misma calle, same old corner, brand new fire in my chest
Falling for my hometown, baby, and I never would have guessed

No tengo que viajar lejos to find what I need
It was here the whole time, baby, in this familiar street
Manos arriba for the love that grew up in plain sight
My hometown got me falling, falling, falling tonight

So we stay right here together where the palm trees touch the moon
And the whole barrio is singing our familiar tune

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up on a dead-end street where the dreams hit a wall
But I had two good shoes and a heart that stood tall
Mama said baby, you gotta swing for the sky
So I tipped my old hat and I told the block goodbye

I'm steppin' out, I'm steppin' high, off the dead-end row
Got a one-way ticket and a band that's ready to go
Snap your fingers, kick your heels, watch a poor boy climb
Makin' it out of the corner, baby, one swingin' dime at a time

Used to count the cracks in the sidewalk all day
Now I'm countin' city lights from a brand new doorway
They said the block would keep me, said I'd never break free
But the rhythm in my pocket had a different plan for me

I'm steppin' out, I'm steppin' high, off the dead-end row
Got a one-way ticket and a band that's ready to go
Snap your fingers, kick your heels, watch a poor boy climb
Makin' it out of the corner, baby, one swingin' dime at a time

For every kid still sittin' where I used to sit
Keep your chin up, keep on swingin', baby, never quit
The wall ain't really there, it's just paint and old talk
There's a whole wide world a-waitin' just past your dead-end block

Now I'm tippin' my hat from the top of the town
And the dead-end street is the place that won't keep me down

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Foot on the pedal, no chrome on the rims, keep it low, keep it plain
They loaded the back, I don't look, I don't ask, I just drive in the rain
One more, that's the deal, then I'm ghost, then I'm gone, no more games
Got a bag and a passport, a name that ain't mine, burn the rest in the flames

Last job, last job, hands on the wheel, heart cold
Last job, last job, get the money and go, get gold
Never lookin' back, never lookin' back at the road
Drive, drive, drive, drive, this the last one I'm told

Three minutes, that's all, that's the window, that's the line that I run
Blue lights in the mirror, I breathe and I drift, I don't reach for a gun
The city behind me a blur and a buzz, a memory under the sun
Quiet life waitin', a porch and a dog, when this final ride's done

Last job, last job, hands on the wheel, heart cold
Last job, last job, get the money and go, get gold
Never lookin' back, never lookin' back at the road
Drive, drive, drive, drive, this the last one I'm told

I did this too long, I was good, maybe best, but the best ones still fall
So I'm walkin' away while I still got the legs, before I give it all
Drop the car in the lake, watch it sink with the past, take the cash, take the call
New sunrise, new state, new face in the glass, never answer when they call

Dawn breaks gold on the dash and I exhale slow
The engine goes quiet, the last job, I let it go

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I wrote it all down on a napkin in spit
Everything you did, every rotten little bit
Was gonna scream it in your face, make you finally hear
But the phone just rang dead and you ain't even here

You're gone, you're gone, and I'm screamin' at the sky
Forgiving a ghost is a hell of a way to cry
I hated you alive, now I'm yellin' at the air
I forgive you, you bastard, but you ain't even there

Spent ten angry years sharpening a knife
For a fight you skipped out on, you just up and left life
Now the rage has got nowhere, nowhere left to land
So I'm holding it out empty in an open hand

You're gone, you're gone, and I'm screamin' at the sky
Forgiving a ghost is a hell of a way to cry
I hated you alive, now I'm yellin' at the air
I forgive you, you bastard, but you ain't even there

This isn't for you, it was never for you
It's the only way left for me to make it through
I lay the whole thing down on a grave with no name
And I walk away lighter from the weight and the blame

So here's to the dead and the words I can't send
I forgive you out loud to the wind in the end

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up on the rooftop where the antenna catches stars
We string the wires through the dark like quiet little scars
No license, no permission, just a frequency and a flame
A hundred sleepless cities catch a signal with no name

Broadcast from the edges of the night
We are the static, we are the light
Nobody owns the air we send
This is our song until the end

The crackle is a heartbeat and the hiss is like the sea
We play the songs they buried, set the silent voices free
Downstairs the world is sleeping, up here the towers hum
We answer to no master and we wait for dawn to come

Somewhere a kid is listening with the volume turned down low
Under a blanket, holding tight to something they can't show
And that is why we do it, that is why we climb so high
To throw a thread of music across the empty sky

Broadcast from the edges of the night
We are the static, we are the light
Nobody owns the air we send
This is our song until the end

When they come to shut us down we'll let the last note ring
A wall of sound rising up, a beautiful broken thing
Then silence on the dial, but the echo carries on
In every kid still listening long after we are gone

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the frozen lake, the sky begins to bloom
Green and silver ribbons dancing slow across the gloom
My collar's turned against the cold, my breath a silver tune
And the heavens start to sway beneath an absent moon

Northern lights, take it slow, brushes on the snare
Northern lights, soft and low, painting up the air
A velvet sky, a saxophone, a hush across the night
The whole world holding still beneath the northern light

They say it's only particles, a science and a name
But tell that to my shaking heart, it isn't quite the same
Like a singer hitting one long note that nobody can hold
The aurora pours her solo out in emerald and gold

No conductor waves a baton, no chart, no written line
Just improvisation in the dark, a melody divine
I stand here in the wings of it, a witness and a fool
While the cosmos plays the bluest blue and breaks the only rule

Northern lights, take it slow, brushes on the snare
Northern lights, soft and low, painting up the air
A velvet sky, a saxophone, a hush across the night
The whole world holding still beneath the northern light

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Raise my hand, got a point, got it locked, got it tight
Open my mouth and somebody else grab the mic
Same idea I just said comes back louder, gets praise
I been swallowin' my voice in these rooms for days
Mute on the screen but I wasn't even muted
Got the data, got the proof, but the credit got rerouted
They say great point but they say it to the dude
Who repeated my words in a deeper attitude

Talked over, talked over, every single time
Waiting for a gap that I never get to find
Got a voice, got a vision, got a seat at the table
But they talk right past me like I'm just a label

Day one I was quiet, thought I'd earn it with the work
Now the work speaks loud but the room still acts berserk
Interrupt, overlap, talk faster, talk fast
I'm the footnote in a meeting where I should've been first
So I started writing emails, put it down in ink
Now they can't unsay it, now they gotta stop and think
Receipts in the thread, timestamp and a name
You can talk over a voice but you can't outrun the frame

I'm not loud, never been, that was never my way
But the quiet ones be carrying the room every day
So I take up my space now, I hold the floor slow
If you cut me off I start again, watch me grow

Talked over, talked over, every single time
Waiting for a gap that I never get to find
Got a voice, got a vision, got a seat at the table
Now I talk right back, and believe me, I'm able

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up the Chevy with the jars beneath the hay
My brother riding shotgun, said we'd make it by the day
The backroads through the holler where the county couldn't see
Just two fools and a fortune and a tank near empty

It all went wrong on Route 9
Blue lights cutting through the pine
We ran the moon but the moon ran us
Left it burning in the dust

A deer came out of nowhere and the wheel went hard left
Glass and corn whiskey and the smell of something cleft
My brother started laughing with the blood up on his chin
Said well at least we won't get caught with what we're sittin' in

It all went wrong on Route 9
Blue lights cutting through the pine
We ran the moon but the moon ran us
Left it burning in the dust

We walked the whole way home that night through the cold October air
Broke and bruised and stupid and we didn't even care
Mama never knew it, Daddy took it to the grave
Just two boys and a story and the foolish chance we gave

Now I drive past Route 9 sometimes and slow down by the bend
Think about my brother and that night that wouldn't end
The jars are long since shattered but the laugh still echoes clear
The moonshine run gone sideways in our nineteenth year

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Sun going down on the last of July
We on the rooftop watching the sky
Tomorrow I'm gone, suitcase by the door
But tonight we dance like never before
My people around me, the speakers up loud
One more for the road, one more for the crowd

Last day of summer, hold it, hold it
Don't let the moment go, oh no
Last day of summer, feel it, feel it
We gon' dance till the morning glow

Growing up fast but I'm holding it slow
Every face on this rooftop I already know
We made it through everything, laughter and rain
Now the future is calling my name like a train
So we move to the rhythm, we move like we're free
One more golden night just for you and for me

Last day of summer, hold it, hold it
Don't let the moment go, oh no
Last day of summer, feel it, feel it
We gon' dance till the morning glow

When the leaves start to fall I'll be far from here
Different city, different room, brand new year
But I'm carrying you in the beat of my chest
This last summer night, oh, it was the best

So the sun dips low and we raise up our hands
Last day of summer, the start of the plans

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
I packed the truck at midnight when the whole town was asleep
The streetlight flickered like a vow that nobody could keep
The church bell hung there silent and the diner sign went dark
I lit one final cigarette and watched it spit a spark

I'm leaving for good, I ain't coming back
Down that black ribbon of a railroad track
Bury my name in the cold red clay
This town is a ghost and I'm walking away

My daddy's in the churchyard, my mama's in the ground
There ain't a single living thing to hold me to this town
The water tower's rusted and the mill's been closed for years
This place has drunk a river out of everybody's tears

I'm leaving for good, I ain't coming back
Down that black ribbon of a railroad track
Bury my name in the cold red clay
This town is a ghost and I'm walking away

They say you carry home with you no matter where you roam
But some homes are a millstone and some homes are a tomb
I'll let the crows have my old house, let the kudzu take the rest
And I'll find me a horizon that ain't sitting on my chest

The sun came up behind me as I crossed the county line
And for the first time in my life that sky was fully mine

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Keys in her hand and her knuckles gone white
Mirrors all crooked but the grin's set right
I said check your mirrors, check the road, check your speed
She said Dad relax, I got everything I need
The horns kick in and we lurch off the curb
My heart in my throat but I won't say a word

Go a little easy on the gas, my dear
Foot off the brake, now shift into gear
Hands at ten and two, eyes on the line
My little girl is driving, and she's doing just fine

Stalled at the light, three times in a row
A fella behind us laying on his horn, oh no
She turned a bit red but she found it again
Pulled off so smooth like she'd done it since ten
I'm gripping the door and I'm trying to play cool
While she takes the corner like she owns the whole school

Go a little easy on the gas, my dear
Foot off the brake, now shift into gear
Hands at ten and two, eyes on the line
My little girl is driving, and she's doing just fine

Wasn't long ago I taught her to walk
Held her little hand around the city block
Now she's pulling away and she's taking the wheel
And I'm learning to let go, oh man, what a feel

She parked it dead straight and she shut off the car
Said how'd I do Dad, and I said you'll go far

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Neon hum on the corner of Vine and Fourth
Clippers buzzing like a beehive going north
He knows your father by the cowlick on his crown
Every secret in the fade, every name in town
Mirror doubles up the room, doubles up the talk
He nods once and the whole street starts to walk

Snip, snip, the city tells him everything
Under the strip light, the gossip's gonna sing
He holds the razor, he holds the whole map down
One chair, one comb, and the heart of the town

Widow Reyes, her grandson's getting wed
The banker's cheating, that's the something left unsaid
He keeps it filed behind a quiet little smile
Pours the talcum, dusts the collar, takes his while
Bassline rolling like the traffic out the door
He's been cutting the same block since '94

Snip, snip, the city tells him everything
Under the strip light, the gossip's gonna sing
He holds the razor, he holds the whole map down
One chair, one comb, and the heart of the town

When the shop goes dark he sweeps the day to dust
All those whispered names, he buries them in trust
The broom keeps the rhythm, the streetlight keeps the beat
A confessional with a barber's pole on the street
