Snip, snip, the city tells him everything
Under the strip light, the gossip's gonna sing
He holds the razor, he holds the whole map down
One chair, one comb, and the heart of the town

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Parking lot's empty, the lights are still up
Reindeer on the dash and a cold paper cup
My phone's buzzing photos of a table set warm
I clock in at six in my second-shift form
The hallway smells like coffee that's been brewing since dawn
Room 12's alarm is the song I put on

Somebody's gotta hold the night together
Somebody's gotta stay
While the rest of the world unwraps the weather
I'm trading my holiday away
But these hands keep a heartbeat
These hands keep the pain at bay
Somebody's gotta hold the night together
so I'll stay, I'll stay

Mrs. Doyle's got nobody coming through the door
so I sit on her bed for a minute, maybe more
Tell her my mama's recipe for sweet potato pie
Watch a little color come back to her eye
Double means double but it don't mean alone
We're all just people far from anyone's home

Somebody's gotta hold the night together
Somebody's gotta stay
While the rest of the world unwraps the weather
I'm trading my holiday away
But these hands keep a heartbeat
These hands keep the pain at bay
Somebody's gotta hold the night together
so I'll stay, I'll stay

Midnight, the monitors are humming low
I save you a plate in my heart for when I get home
Yeah the tinsel can wait, the presents can keep
love is what you give when the whole world's asleep

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
The stone holds the cold of a thousand grey morns
My grandmother's pew where the cushion is worn
I learned every hymn like I learned my own name
Now I sit at the back and they look just the same

I'll go like the tide, no door slamming shut
No thunder, no quarrel, no clean little cut
Just a soft step away down the gravel and rain
Carrying the bell but not staying for the chain

The candles still flicker the way that they did
When I was a boy with my hands folded, hid
but the words don't fit right in my mouth anymore
like a coat that I've grown three sizes before

I'll go like the tide, no door slamming shut
No thunder, no quarrel, no clean little cut
Just a soft step away down the gravel and rain
Carrying the bell but not staying for the chain

I'll keep the green hills and the sound of the choir
The warmth of the wax and the smoke from the fire
I'm not throwing it down, I'm just laying it light
Like a lantern I'm leaving to burn through the night

So bless all the faithful still bowing their heads
I'll pray my own prayers to the river instead

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning, same blue dress
Same three rows, same hand on my chest
I mouthed the words so nobody knew
That the ceiling felt lower than it used to do

No big speech, no breaking news
Just a quiet pair of leaving shoes

I'm slipping out the side door, soft as a sigh
No fireworks, baby, no reason why
Just a girl who outgrew the size of the sky
I'm slipping out the side door, saying goodbye

Mom still saves my seat in the third
I text her a heart and a careful word
It's not that the love wasn't real and warm
I just can't keep folding myself to the form

No big speech, no breaking news
Just a quiet pair of leaving shoes

I'm slipping out the side door, soft as a sigh
No fireworks, baby, no reason why
Just a girl who outgrew the size of the sky
I'm slipping out the side door, saying goodbye

I'll carry the good parts, the singing, the light
The people who held me on the hardest of nights
but the rest I'll set down on the steps and the stone
and walk into morning, finally my own

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Back room, low lamp, smoke curling slow
Four men and a deck and nowhere to go
I was up a month's rent by a quarter past ten
Should've stood up and left, but I sat back down again
The felt was green velvet, the whiskey was brown
and pride is a coat that's too heavy to drown

Oh, I pushed it too far, Lord, I pushed it too far
bet the watch off my wrist and the keys to the car
When you're chasing the loss you can't see who you are
yeah, I pushed it too far

Willie laid down kings, I had nothing but air
but I called it anyway, daring him there
My daddy's gold ring went sliding to the pot
that's the last thing he gave me, the last thing I got

Oh, I pushed it too far, Lord, I pushed it too far
bet the watch off my wrist and the keys to the car
When you're chasing the loss you can't see who you are
yeah, I pushed it too far

Morning came grey through the curtain's thin seam
and I sold my whole house for a queen and a dream
Now I tell every young man with a hand full of fire
there's a difference 'tween wanting and feeding the liar

Oh, I pushed it too far, Lord, I pushed it too far
lost the ring and the room and the man that I are
yeah, I pushed it too far

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine breathing hot in the alley's black throat
Three minutes, he said, like a curse, like a note
My hands on the wheel are two doves that won't fly
and the moon is a coin that the dark threw to die

¡Ay! One more turn of the key, one more night
then I'm gone like the smoke, like a thief into light
The road is a guitar with its six strings of fear
and I'm playing my last song, I'm getting out of here

Footsteps, then shouting, then glass on the ground
He falls in beside me, no time to turn 'round
The city is burning red lights in the rain
and my heart keeps the rhythm of palms on the cajón again

¡Ay! One more turn of the key, one more night
then I'm gone like the smoke, like a thief into light
The road is a guitar with its six strings of fear
and I'm playing my last song, I'm getting out of here

There's a woman in Cádiz with salt in her hair
who swore I'd come home if I'd only dare
so I floor it past sirens, past everything owed
and I bury the gun and the name in the road

No more dancing with devils on tires that scream
I'll wake by the sea and forget this was real

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Five a.m. the rooster and the timeclock both crow
I'm pourin' coffee at the diner 'fore the cold sun shows
Then it's down to the mill where the sawdust flies
two paychecks thin as the smoke from the fryers' goodbyes

I'm runnin' and runnin' and the ground keeps slidin' back
two jobs on my shoulders and a hole in the sack
The rent's a fast river and my boat's full of holes
workin' twice as hard just to stay where I was, Lord, just to stay where I was

The banjo on the porch is the one thing they can't take
I play it for the young'uns 'fore the morning comes to break
My daddy worked one job and he kept the lights on bright
but the dollar got smaller while the bills grew overnight

I'm runnin' and runnin' and the ground keeps slidin' back
two jobs on my shoulders and a hole in the sack
The rent's a fast river and my boat's full of holes
workin' twice as hard just to stay where I was, Lord, just to stay where I was

They say pull up your bootstraps, son, but mine done wore clean through
I've got calluses for medals and an IOU
but I'll fiddle till the dawn and I'll laugh at the moon
'cause a poor man's still rich when his kids are in tune

I'm runnin' and runnin' and the ground keeps slidin' back
but my heart it ain't empty even when the wallet's slack

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They came with their gold in a velvet-lined hand
offered to print every word that I planned
but they wanted the teeth filed down off my tongue
the fire made tame and the wild verses hung

¡No, no! My words are not bread you can buy
they are wind off the mountain, they are hawks in the sky
Let me starve with my truth in this candle-thin room
before I dress up my soul for the merchant's perfume

The café is empty, the wine's nearly gone
but the verses keep coming like the breaking of dawn
I write on the napkins, I write on my skin
the ink is the only rich man I'll let in

¡No, no! My words are not bread you can buy
they are wind off the mountain, they are hawks in the sky
Let me starve with my truth in this candle-thin room
before I dress up my soul for the merchant's perfume

Guitar, cry with me, strike the deep minor string
let the heel hit the floor for the freedom I sing
They'll forget all the poets who knelt for the coin
but the mad ones, the true ones, the dawn will rejoin

So keep your fat contract and your gilded-cage door
I'll die with my verses spread out on the floor

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Mapped like the riverbeds carved into stone
Knuckles like mountains where the years have grown
She kneaded the bread through the war and the cold
those hands held the line when the whole world went old

Scarred but unshaken, weathered but whole
ten weathered soldiers, the grip of a soul

HER HANDS! Built a house out of nothing but will
HER HANDS! Pulled the thorn, held the wound, kept us still
Iron in the wrinkles, thunder in the veins
the softest of weapons against all the pain
HER HANDS!

She buried two sons and she never broke down
fingers like roots digging deep in the ground
She could gut a whole fish, she could braid a girl's hair
those same hands could fight, those same hands could care

HER HANDS! Built a house out of nothing but will
HER HANDS! Pulled the thorn, held the wound, kept us still
Iron in the wrinkles, thunder in the veins
the softest of weapons against all the pain
HER HANDS!

Now they lie folded, finally at rest
a crucifix tangled on a still, quiet chest
but I feel them upon me when the darkness draws near
ten calloused angels whispering: no fear, no fear

HER HANDS! Live on every time that I stand
HER HANDS! I carry the storm in the lines of my hand

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
La sirena no suena, the whistle's gone cold
Gate number seven with a padlock and mold
Mi papá gave it thirty, gave the best of his back
now the parking lot's empty and the future is black
Dembow on the corner where the workers used to line
clocking out forever at a quarter past nine

The lights going down, down, down on the line
the town that we built, ya no es mine
but we move, we move, we don't fall to the ground
oyé, we still dancing when the factory comes down

They shipped all the jobs on a boat 'cross the sea
left the rust and the silence for my family and me
Mami's selling empanadas out the back of the car
telling everybody, mijo, you can still be a star

The lights going down, down, down on the line
the town that we built, ya no es mine
but we move, we move, we don't fall to the ground
oyé, we still dancing when the factory comes down

Boom, the machines go quiet, but the streets, they get loud
we turn the loss to a rhythm, turn the pain to a crowd
you can close all the doors but you can't close the soul
barrio strong, corazón whole

The lights going down, down, down on the line
but the people, mira, we shine, we shine

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Idle by the corner where the streetlight hums low
yellow taxi engine got nowhere to go
This the final run, Jah, this the last of the line
then I'm planting up callaloo, leaving the crime
De radio whisper soft like the wind through de tree
one more wicked turn and de wheel set me free

Drive away, drive away, slow under de moon
last job, last night, I'll be gone by noon
No more running with the shadow on my back
I'n'I find the clean road, never coming back

Dem run from the bank with the bag and the fear
I keep the riddim steady though the sirens draw near
Heart beat like a one-drop, calm in the storm
gonna kiss me pickney 'fore the early light is born

Drive away, drive away, slow under de moon
last job, last night, I'll be gone by noon
No more running with the shadow on my back
I'n'I find the clean road, never coming back

Babylon a-chase but de morning go come
I'll burn the old plate, I'll lay down the gun
Down by the harbor where the fishing boats sway
I'll trade the fast life for an honest day

So this is the last time the tires gonna scream
then I'm free like the gull over Kingston, just a dream

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The rooms echo bigger now the walls are all bare
just a square of clean paint where your photo hung there
The tape screams its zipper down the cardboard's brown spine
one last box on the floor and the rest is goodbye

¡Ay! The last box, the last box, I press down the seam
five years of my life pressed down flat at the seam
The key on the counter catches gold from the sun
the dance is over now, the house and I are done

I found a dry rose in the back of the drawer
and the pencil-marked heights of the kids by the door
The kitchen still smells like the meals that we made
but the table is gone and the laughter has frayed

¡Ay! The last box, the last box, I press down the seam
five years of my life pressed down flat at the seam
The key on the counter catches gold from the sun
the dance is over now, the house and I are done

Guitar, play it slow, let the deep strings complain
for the corners that held us through sunshine and rain
I'll carry these boxes like coffins, like wine
and I'll bury the echo, and the empty is mine

One knock on the door frame, one stamp of the heel
then I lock the whole silence and go learn how to heal

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Empty Sunday lot, the sun coming up gold
You in the driver's seat, twelve going on bold
Hands at ten and two like I showed you, my dear
mirror, signal, breathe — there's nothing to fear
The radio low and the windows come down
first gear, baby girl, we move through the town

Go slow, go slow, but go, my love
the road is yours, baby, like the sky's for the dove
I'll hold the map but your hands hold the wheel
eh, drive, my daughter, show me how you feel

You stalled it twice and you laughed till you cried
then you found the clutch and a smoothness inside
The whole world widened in the rearview glass
my little girl turning the key on her past

Go slow, go slow, but go, my love
the road is yours, baby, like the sky's for the dove
I'll hold the map but your hands hold the wheel
eh, drive, my daughter, show me how you feel

One day this same road, you go drive it alone
and I'll be the voice in your head saying go on
so brake for the bend but accelerate the dream
you be the engine, I'll just watch you gleam

Go slow, go slow, but go, my love
life is the highway and you're rising above

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Two a.m. the bassline finds the place you used to stand
strobe cuts through the smoke like the ghost of your hand
I move through the bodies but they all feel the same
every face in the dark wears the shape of your name

And the kick drum keeps asking where, where, where you go
in the loop of the night, in the pulse, in the glow

You're the one that got away, away, away
I'm dancing alone but I'm holding the day
we last said goodbye, the one that got away
the beat keeps you alive in the way that I sway

We found it on a dancefloor just like this one tonight
two strangers and a four-on-the-floor and the light
then the morning came down and you walked through the door
and the music kept playing but you didn't no more

And the kick drum keeps asking where, where, where you go
in the loop of the night, in the pulse, in the glow

You're the one that got away, away, away
I'm dancing alone but I'm holding the day
we last said goodbye, the one that got away
the beat keeps you alive in the way that I sway

Lift your hands if you lost one too
let the rhythm carry the ache on through
we don't have to forget to be free
just let the night hold the memory

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The Bluebird was packed and my guitar was tuned
A&R in the back booth, neon-lit moon
I played 'em the song that I bled to write
and for one shining minute I owned that whole night

It was the night I almost made it
so close I could taste the gold on the air
but close is a town you can't quite get to
so I drove the long highway from there to nowhere
the night I almost made it

He shook my hand, said "Kid, you got somethin' rare"
took my card and my number and a curl of my prayer
but the phone never rang and the spring turned to fall
and the demo's still gathering dust on the wall

It was the night I almost made it
so close I could taste the gold on the air
but close is a town you can't quite get to
so I drove the long highway from there to nowhere
the night I almost made it

Now I play the same bar on a Friday for tips
and I tell the young pickers with the stars on their lips
that almost ain't failin', it's just a long road
and the song's still worth singin' even nobody knows

so I tune up the same six strings every night
for the love, not the dream, and that's almost alright

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
The fiddle calls us to the floor tonight
Your hand in mine beneath the candlelight
We've traded blows in every village square
Now we must move as one, I do not dare

The drum begins, my heart against my will
Your eyes are fire but your steps are still
The whole town watching, waiting for a fall
But we won't break, we won't break at all

So turn me round, my dearest enemy
The reel will bind what words could never free
We spin and spin until the old wounds mend
My fiercest rival becomes my friend

The pipes grow louder, faster grows the beat
I feel the rhythm rising through our feet
The hate we carried softens in the sound
As round and round and round and round we wound

Who knew a dance could quiet ancient war
Who knew your hand was what I'm reaching for
The music fades, we stand there breathing slow
And neither one of us still wants to go

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up where the streetlights flicker and die
Concrete and broken glass beneath the sky
They said I'd never leave this rotten ground
But I was born to break out, not break down

I'm getting out, I'm getting out alive
This dead-end block will never end my drive
Scream it loud, I'm breaking through the wall
I'm getting out, I will not fall

They laughed at every dream I dared to hold
The block don't care, the block stays gray and cold
But every scar is fuel inside my chest
I'm tearing down the cage, I'll prove the rest

No gods, no kings, no chains on me
My own two hands will set me free
From these streets to the open road
I'm carrying my heavy load

I'm getting out, I'm getting out alive
This dead-end block will never end my drive
Scream it loud, I'm breaking through the wall
I'm getting out, I will not fall

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
The whiskey poured and the cards came down
In a smoky room on the edge of town
I had three kings and a hungry grin
Never thought about what I'd lose to win

Oh the game went too far, too far that night
I bet it all under the barroom light
Lost my truck, my watch, and my daddy's ring
Learned the hard way that pride don't mean a thing

The dealer smiled with a crooked eye
I raised the stakes, didn't say goodbye
To the money saved for my wedding day
Now she's packing bags and she's gone away

One more hand, just one more deal
That's the lie a gambler feels
Now I'm sitting here with empty hands
And a lesson no one understands

Oh the game went too far, too far that night
I bet it all under the barroom light
Lost my truck, my watch, and my daddy's ring
Learned the hard way that pride don't mean a thing

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The room is empty now, just echoes on the wall
My voice is floating soft, the ceiling feels so tall
I run the tape across the final cardboard seam
The whole place fading out like waking from a dream

Last box, last light, I'm letting go tonight
The walls remember everything in pale and silver light
Last box, last breath, I'm drifting out the door
This place was home, this place was home, but it's not anymore

The sunlight through the blinds, it paints the hollow floor
I hear the years we lived behind the closing door
My fingers trace the marks where all our pictures hung
A quiet kind of ending for a song we left unsung

Ooh, the keys feel cold inside my hand
Ooh, somewhere new is where I'll land
Fade away, fade away, fade away slow

Last box, last light, I'm letting go tonight
The walls remember everything in pale and silver light
Last box, last breath, I'm drifting out the door
This place was home, this place was home, but it's not anymore

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Dim light on the dashboard, two a.m. glow
Demo in my pocket, nowhere left to go
They said come back tomorrow, we'll see what we see
So close to the dream that was meant to be me

The night I almost made it, almost touched the sky
So close I could taste it, watched it pass me by
But I'm still here breathing, still here on the grind
The night I almost made it lives inside my mind

Rain on the window, beat tape spinning slow
Every little setback is a seed I throw
One phone call away from the life I designed
Almost ain't never, so I'm staying on time

Yeah, the smoke curls up, the city sleeps
I'm counting all the promises I keep
Almost is a chapter, not the end
I'll be back around this corner again

The night I almost made it, almost touched the sky
So close I could taste it, watched it pass me by
But I'm still here breathing, still here on the grind
The night I almost made it lives inside my mind

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
They carried him slow down the avenue
With a trombone cry and a tuba too
We wore our black but our feet wouldn't stay
The drummer hit a beat and we danced away

So blow that horn, let the saints come down
We're marching him home through the heart of town
Up and down, pick it up, don't you cry
We celebrate the living when we say goodbye

Grief in the morning turned to brass by noon
The whole block swaying to a second line tune
Handkerchiefs waving in the humid air
Death came to visit but joy was there

Pick it up, pick it up, one two three four
The spirit's dancing right out the door
From the cemetery to the river wide
We're sending him off on a rolling tide

So blow that horn, let the saints come down
We're marching him home through the heart of town
Up and down, pick it up, don't you cry
We celebrate the living when we say goodbye

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The lights are coming down across the field
A thousand quiet hearts about to yield
The last bass note still humming in the ground
We gather in the fading, gather round

Don't let it end, don't let it end tonight
The stars are bleeding gentle into light
Hold the moment, hold it close and slow
The last night of the world we used to know

The tents glow soft like lanterns in the dark
The embers of the day, a dying spark
Strangers became family in the sound
Now morning's coming, breaking all around

Breathe in, breathe out, the dawn is near
Everything we felt is still right here
Let it go, let it go, let it stay
In the quiet after we drift away

Don't let it end, don't let it end tonight
The stars are bleeding gentle into light
Hold the moment, hold it close and slow
The last night of the world we used to know

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The water tower's shrinking in my mirror now
I made a quiet promise, I don't know how
This town held all my childhood, every name I knew
But a heart this big needs more than it can do

I'm leaving, oh I'm leaving for good this time
These roads I memorized won't keep me in line
I love you, little town, but I gotta grow
So I'm packing up my soul and I'm letting go

Mama on the porch with a tear she won't show
Daddy says go on, son, it's your time to go
The diner and the church and the field of grain
They're written on my bones, they'll forever remain

Woah, I carry you wherever I roam
Woah, a part of me will always be home
But the highway's calling out my name
And I'll never, no I'll never be the same

I'm leaving, oh I'm leaving for good this time
These roads I memorized won't keep me in line
I love you, little town, but I gotta grow
So I'm packing up my soul and I'm letting go

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I stood above the stone where your name was carved
With all the words I swallowed, all the love I starved
Too many years of anger, too much pride to bend
Now I'm talking to the grass, my long lost friend

I forgive you, I forgive you, though you cannot hear
I'm letting go the burden I have carried all these years
The banjo plays a hymn for the words I should have said
I'm making peace too late with the ones already dead

The mandolin is weeping in the morning cold
For every grudge I cradled till my heart grew old
You hurt me, oh you hurt me, but I hurt you too
So I lay it down forever here in front of you

The river keeps on rolling, the wind keeps moving on
Forgiveness isn't for the ones already gone
It's the chain around my own neck I undo
So I can finally breathe again without you

I forgive you, I forgive you, though you cannot hear
I'm letting go the burden I have carried all these years
The banjo plays a hymn for the words I should have said
I'm making peace too late with the ones already dead

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the highway, I take the long way home
My wheels keep turning down a street I should leave alone
Your window's dark up on the second floor
Someone else is living where I lived before

I'm driving past, I'm driving past tonight
Your old apartment glowing in the city light
I tell myself I'm over it, I tell myself I'm fine
But I keep on driving past that love of mine

The radio is playing what we used to play
I swear I see your silhouette and look away
The engine's running warm but my hands are cold
Some stories never finish, they just grow old

One more block, one more red light
One more memory burning bright
I should turn around, I know I should
But my heart won't leave this neighborhood

I'm driving past, I'm driving past tonight
Your old apartment glowing in the city light
I tell myself I'm over it, I tell myself I'm fine
But I keep on driving past that love of mine

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Radar on the dashboard, lightning in my eyes
Chasing down the funnel where the danger lies
They told me settle down, find a quiet life
But I married the tornado, the storm's my wife

I can't settle down, I'm chasing the storm
Where the sky turns green and the clouds get warm
Running toward the thing that everybody fears
Been chasing the wind for a thousand years

Gas tank full, got the wipers on high
Nothing makes me feel like that howling sky
A woman back home but I never stay
The next big system's pulling me away

Thunder in my chest, it's the only beat I know
Adrenaline addiction, can't let it go
The calm don't suit me, the still feels wrong
I was born inside a hurricane song

I can't settle down, I'm chasing the storm
Where the sky turns green and the clouds get warm
Running toward the thing that everybody fears
Been chasing the wind for a thousand years

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I packed my faith inside a worn-out case
Left the only home I'd ever known
The ocean stretched before my trembling face
But I was not, no I was not alone

Carry me over, carry me across the sea
Lord, lay Your hand upon the water, set my spirit free
To a brand new shore where the morning breaks
I'm trusting in Your promise with every wave it makes

The ship rocked hard through the howling night
I clutched my children, I clutched my prayer
And when the dawn broke gold and bright
I knew that mercy met me there

Glory, glory, the harbor's in my sight
Glory, glory, He brought me through the night
A stranger in a land I do not know
But the Lord will plant me, and the Lord will help me grow

Carry me over, carry me across the sea
Lord, lay Your hand upon the water, set my spirit free
To a brand new shore where the morning breaks
I'm trusting in Your promise with every wave it makes

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Sirens in the distance, neon on the wall
Grew up in the shadow where the buildings fall
They said this block would swallow me whole
But I got fire and lightning down in my soul

Break out, break out, I'm running through the night
Faster than the system, chasing down the light
Dead-end block behind me, never looking back
I'm breaking through the ceiling, I'm jumping off the track

Bass drops heavy like the weight I knew
Every broken window had a different view
I moved through the concrete, I moved through the pain
Now I'm running through the rhythm like rain on rain

Faster, faster, feel the pulse alive
This is how the hungry ones survive
From the bottom to the top of the sound
I was never gonna stay in the ground

Break out, break out, I'm running through the night
Faster than the system, chasing down the light
Dead-end block behind me, never looking back
I'm breaking through the ceiling, I'm jumping off the track

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
The kettle sings for two but I am one
Your empty chair still facing toward the sun
I burned the bread you used to make with ease
And cried into the flour on my knees

One small pot upon the fire tonight
Learning how to live without your light
You taught me love but never taught me this
The quiet of a kitchen that I miss

I measure out the tea the way you would
Though half of it goes cold the way it should
The garden herbs still growing by the door
For a woman who won't taste them anymore

The fiddle in the corner plays so low
A tune you hummed so many years ago
I set one plate, I light one candle dim
And whisper to the room, I'm trying, love, I'm trying

One small pot upon the fire tonight
Learning how to live without your light
You taught me love but never taught me this
The quiet of a kitchen that I miss

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five dollars on the table, set the clock
I run these concrete squares, I own this block
Tourists think they're clever, think they're slick
But I've been playing shadows since I was a kid

Checkmate, checkmate, slam the clock down hard
King of every corner of this city park
You never saw it coming, you never had a prayer
The hustle's in the silence, the kill is in the stare

Knight takes bishop, watch your money burn
Every little blunder is a lesson learned
I smile while I'm taking everything you've got
The pieces are my soldiers and I never stop

Thirty seconds left, I see it all
A pawn becomes a queen against the wall
This board's the only place I'm truly free
Four hundred years of war inside of me

Checkmate, checkmate, slam the clock down hard
King of every corner of this city park
You never saw it coming, you never had a prayer
The hustle's in the silence, the kill is in the stare

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I sold the mule and the rusted plow
Kissed the dirt where my father's down
The preacher waved from the blackened pier
Said the Lord don't sail where I'm going from here
Salt in the wind like a funeral hymn
The whole gray ocean swallowing him

My mother sewed coins in the seam of my coat
Wept like a candle the night I left home
The ship groaned low like a beast in chains
And the coastline drowned in the cold black rain

Carry me over the wide dark water
Far from the graves of every father
I'm a ghost on the deck of a borrowed boat
With a hymn in my chest and a stone in my throat

The new land hummed like a stranger's name
Nothing I loved ever looked the same
I built a house from another man's wood
And I prayed to a God who never could

Carry me over the wide dark water
Far from the graves of every father
I'm a ghost on the deck of a borrowed boat
With a hymn in my chest and a stone in my throat

There's a country buried beneath my tongue
A language for the dead and the young
When they lower me down in this foreign clay
Let the wind carry me back the other way

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent hum at a quarter past four
Fireworks crack outside the ward door
Everybody's home with the lights strung low
I got two shifts welded back to back to go
Paper gown, gloved hands, monitor glow
Veins running fast like the city below

Keep me moving, keep me moving on
Double on a holiday, everybody's gone
Pulse in my ears like a kick and a snare
I'm still standing 'cause somebody's gotta be there
Keep me moving, keep me moving on
Till the morning shift and the rising sun

Bed eleven coding, run the line
Breathe with me, sweetheart, you're gonna be fine
My coffee's gone cold and my legs won't quit
Text from my kid says they saved me a bit

Keep me moving, keep me moving on
Double on a holiday, everybody's gone
Pulse in my ears like a kick and a snare
I'm still standing 'cause somebody's gotta be there

Tick of the clock and the drip going steady
Hold the whole world when the world isn't ready
I'll dance home empty on the first light train
Then do it all over again, again, again

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Last whistle blew at a half past five
Forty years and the floor's gone quiet inside
We danced these aisles when the orders were high
Now the foreman's crying, won't meet my eye

Turn the lights down low on the line tonight
We're gonna dance in the dust in the dying light
They took the jobs but they can't take the groove
One last spin in the room we move
Shine, shine, shine while the gates come down
Last disco in a factory town

Maria from packing brought her old transistor
Clocked out crying, then she hugged her sister
We spun the records on the loading dock
Made a Saturday glow out of broken stock

Turn the lights down low on the line tonight
We're gonna dance in the dust in the dying light
They took the jobs but they can't take the groove
One last spin in the room we move
Shine, shine, shine while the gates come down
Last disco in a factory town

They'll board the windows, sell the steel for scrap
But you can't repossess a backbeat clap
So lift your hands to the flickering glow
And let the whole damn building let go

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Butter on the skillet, four a.m. glow
Grandma humming gospel, radio low
Flour on her apron, cinnamon air
Walk in the door and it's everywhere
Greens on the stove been cooking since noon
Smell hit my chest like a long-lost tune
Yeah, I made it out, got the chains and the ride
But nothing hit harder than her kitchen inside

Take me back, take me back to that smell
Grandma's kitchen, knew me well
Butter and the sugar and the slow-cooked love
Only heaven I knew, sent down from above
Take me back, take me back to that door
Wish I could smell it just once more

Now the house is empty and the stove is cold
I got the recipe but it don't taste old
Tried to cook her cornbread, came out wrong
Guess the secret left when she went on

Take me back, take me back to that smell
Grandma's kitchen, knew me well
Butter and the sugar and the slow-cooked love
Only heaven I knew, sent down from above

I'll bottle up the morning if I could
Every pot she stirred, every prayer she stood
Close my eyes and I'm five again
In the warmest room I've ever been

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Three gray dots and they faded out
The screen went dark, that's what it's about
No door slammed shut, no rain, no scene
Just a glowing rectangle in between

I read it twice in the parking lot haze
Letters dissolving in a wall of grays
The whole thing ended in a buzz and a hum
Love of my life and a thumb gone numb

Fading, fading, you went so quiet
Drowned in the static, nobody tried it
Four little words and the whole sky fell
Washed out, washed out in a soft farewell

I keep the message like a fossil thing
Proof of a winter that swallowed the spring
The reverb hangs where your voice used to be
A tide that pulled the shore out of me

Fading, fading, you went so quiet
Drowned in the static, nobody tried it
Four little words and the whole sky fell
Washed out, washed out in a soft farewell

If I could drown the notification ring
Unhear the silence, unsay the thing
But the signal's gone and the wave rolls on
And I'm just noise where the song has gone

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Soft on the rooftops the morning comes slow
Coffee and clatter in the streets down below
We built our towers on his quiet chest
Walking so gently on a stone giant's rest

The fountains, the lovers, the lazy guitar
The tremble of trams and the hum of a car
He breathes once a century, the old ones say
So we tiptoe through every golden day

Sleep, sweet giant, beneath our feet
We'll dance so soft in the cobbled street
Hold up the sea and the sun and the rain
Dream of us kindly and don't wake again

The girl on the balcony hums to the bay
Her song is a lullaby keeping him swayed
We pour out our music like wine on his brow
And he sighs in his sleep and the church bells bow

Sleep, sweet giant, beneath our feet
We'll dance so soft in the cobbled street
Hold up the sea and the sun and the rain
Dream of us kindly and don't wake again

One day the mountains will shrug and turn
The harbor will tilt and the candles will burn
But tonight there's samba and salt in the air
So sleep, dear giant, and we'll stay right here

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Folded the sky into a paper boat
Everything I am in a duffel coat
The harbor lights were melting gold
My whole life shrinking as the deck grew cold

I waved at a shoreline turning to mist
A country dissolving like a name on a list
The water below me was a velvet hum
Carrying me toward a place I'd become

Over the water, over the foam
Leaving the only word for home
Stars in the wake like a trail of milk
A new life waiting, soft as silk
Over the water, into the blue
Dreaming a self I never knew

The morning broke on a stranger's coast
Where even the sunlight talks like a ghost
I carry my mother in a song I keep
And I hum it low when I can't sleep

Over the water, over the foam
Leaving the only word for home
Stars in the wake like a trail of milk
A new life waiting, soft as silk

Two shores inside me, salt and rain
One is a comfort, one is a pain
I'll learn to love the in-between
The widest blue I've ever seen

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up where the streetlight buzzed and died
Corner store dreams and nowhere to ride
Mama said baby, you gotta get gone
Don't let this block put your fire out, son

I'm getting out, I'm getting up, I'm getting gone
Dead-end block in the rearview, moving on
Groove in my step and the bass in my chest
Left the whole cul-de-sac, gave it my best
Getting out, getting up, watch me go
Funkin' my way off this dead-end road

Bus pass folded in my back pocket tight
Worked the night shift just to see daylight
Every no I caught, I turned to a beat
Danced my troubles right off of the street

I'm getting out, I'm getting up, I'm getting gone
Dead-end block in the rearview, moving on
Groove in my step and the bass in my chest
Left the whole cul-de-sac, gave it my best

Don't forget the names that they put on the wall
The ones who couldn't make it past the fall
I carry every one of 'em into the light
And I funk for the block every single night

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
One plate, one fork, the kitchen too wide
Forty years of two and now it's just my side
Her recipe card got her handwriting still
Half an onion, a pinch, and a quiet little chill

I burned the first three, the smoke alarm cried
Sat on the floor of the kitchen and I just sighed
Learned to make her soup from the stains on the page
Measuring grief by the spoon at my age

Cooking for one in the lamplight low
Learning the things that she used to know
Two chairs at the table, I only need the one
But I set them both anyway when the day is done

Got it right tonight, tastes a little like her
Steam on the window where the memories blur
I talk to the radio, talk to the wall
And I save her the half that she'd want most of all

Cooking for one in the lamplight low
Learning the things that she used to know
Two chairs at the table, I only need the one
But I set them both anyway when the day is done

They say it gets easier, a little at a time
Maybe one day I'll cook without the salt in my eyes
For now I'll stir slow and I'll let it warm through
And I'll eat what I make 'cause she'd want me to

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Dead-end sign at the end of my street
Grew up counting cracks under my feet
No way out, that's what they all said
Now I'm doing laps where they left me for dead
Mama's two jobs and the rent always late
I was sketching exits on the back of my plate
Now the chain hang heavy, the engine purr loud
Look how I came up out the dead-end now

Made it out, made it out the block
They said never, now they watch me from the lot
Dead-end turned to a runway, I'm gone
Look at me now, the kid they slept on
Made it out, made it out the gate
Turned the whole no into one big wait

Same corner store but I pull up new
Bought my mama a house with a different view
Every closed door was a hallway to me
Now the only dead-end's the one I can't see

Made it out, made it out the block
They said never, now they watch me from the lot
Dead-end turned to a runway, I'm gone
Look at me now, the kid they slept on

I still drive through when the night get slow
Roll the window down on the road I know
Pour out a little for the ones who stayed
And I floor it past every grave we made

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The water tower fades in the rearview glass
One traffic light blinking on the edge of the past
Every road I knew folds into one
A single thin line toward the rising sun

I counted the silos, I counted the years
The diner, the church, and the field of my fears
The whole town sleeping as I slip away
Nothing to hold me, nothing to say

Goodbye to the small town, goodbye to the gray
The horizon is wider with each passing day
I'm a single car on an empty road
Carrying everything I'll never hold

The radio fades into static and air
The mountains rise up like they always were there
My hands on the wheel and my heart split in two
Half staying behind, half breaking through

Goodbye to the small town, goodbye to the gray
The horizon is wider with each passing day
I'm a single car on an empty road
Carrying everything I'll never hold

And it builds and it builds like a tide coming in
Every mile is a door and the noise of begin
The whole sky opens, the engine sings
And I am the smallest of leaving things

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone in my hand like a stone in the night
Your number still burned in the back of my sight
Three years of silence, a wall I had built
Every ring is a thread pulling hard on the guilt

The line clicks open and I hear you breathe
The whole of my childhood comes flooding underneath
Madre, it's me, and my voice starts to break
Forgive me the years and the heart that I'd ache

Ay, madre, I'm calling you home
Forgive the long roads that I walked all alone
My pride was a fire that burned down the years
And now I am singing your name through my tears

You cried and you laughed and you called me your own
Said the door never locked, said I always had home
The guitar in my chest beats a furious rain
And it washes the silence away from the pain

Ay, madre, I'm calling you home
Forgive the long roads that I walked all alone
My pride was a fire that burned down the years
And now I am singing your name through my tears

Dance with me, mother, across the long wire
Two hands reunited through years of the fire
I'll come in the spring when the orange trees bloom
And I'll never again let the silence consume

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
Shoo-bop, shoo-bop, every night I dream
Of a girl who don't know what my heart means
She walks on by with her hair so fine
And she'll never ever know that I wish she were mine

I carry her books and I hold the door
But she's got a fella, what's it all for
I dance alone to our favorite song
Loving her quiet, loving her wrong

Why oh why won't you look my way
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop) every single day
I'm the boy in the corner with a heart full of blue
Darling, oh darling, I'm in love with you
(Doo-wop, doo-wop) but you'll never see
How much you really mean to me

I saved up my dimes for a malted treat
Hoping you'd notice me there on the street
But you smiled at him and you walked right past
And I knew that my dreaming was never gonna last

Why oh why won't you look my way
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop) every single day
I'm the boy in the corner with a heart full of blue
Darling, oh darling, I'm in love with you

Maybe in another world we'd dance so slow
Under the streetlight where the soft winds blow
But here I'll just hum you my lonely tune
(Shoo-bop) underneath the same old moon

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Midnight glow on the bedroom wall
Thumb hovering over a name I should call off
Neon rain on the window pane
I typed it, deleted, then I typed it again

The city hums in electric blue
And every wrong road leads back to you
I know the trouble, I know the cost
But the screen lights up and I'm already lost

One more message in the dead of night
Dangerous glow of a screen too bright
I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but my hands won't stop
Falling for the buzz at the top, top, top
One more message and I'm gone again
Chasing a feeling I should've let end

Three dots blinking like a heartbeat slow
A secret highway only we know
The synths come up like a guilty tide
And I ride the wave with my conscience denied

One more message in the dead of night
Dangerous glow of a screen too bright
I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but my hands won't stop
Falling for the buzz at the top, top, top

Delete the thread, throw the phone away
But the morning comes and I'll do it again someday
Neon ghost in a midnight haze
Love like a wire in a dangerous blaze

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The rip of the tape in an empty room
Echo of a life in the afternoon gloom
One last box and the walls go bare
Ghosts of the picture frames hanging in air

The light falls strange where the couch used to be
Dust in the sunbeam, a soft gray sea
I seal up the cardboard, I press it down slow
Everything we were in a box I can't show

Tape it up, tape it up, let the room go quiet
All that we built and the silence beside it
Washed in the gray of a leaving day
Fold up the years and I drift away
Tape it up, tape it up, one final sound
The last little echo as I set it down

I run my hand on the kitchen door
Where we marked our heights on the painted floor
The hum of the empty fills up my chest
A reverb of love laid down to rest

Tape it up, tape it up, let the room go quiet
All that we built and the silence beside it
Washed in the gray of a leaving day
Fold up the years and I drift away

Key on the counter, I close the door
The room keeps the sound of us evermore
And the tape holds tight what I cannot say
As I carry the last of it all away

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night the ceiling melts to sky
I lift before I ask myself how high
The rooftops shrink to coins beneath my feet
The wind learns my name and calls it sweet

I rise, I rise, no wings and no sound
The earth lets go and forgets I was bound
I rise, I rise, the dark turns to glass
And I am the slow light moving past

The city breathes in amber down below
I trail my fingers through the undertow
No falling here, no edge to understand
Just the long blue holding up my hand

Then morning pulls a thread and I descend
The sheets, the lamp, the wall, the same again
But something in my shoulders still remembers
the weightless room, the floating embers

I rise, I rise, no wings and no sound
The earth lets go and forgets I was bound
I rise, I rise, the dark turns to glass
And I am the slow light moving past

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Brass against the thunder, drums against the rain
They carry down St. Claude with a hurricane of pain
A second line of shadows where the dead refuse to lie
The trumpet screams a requiem that tears apart the sky

March him to the graveyard, march him through the fire
Let the saints come screaming from the swamp and from the wire
We bury you in thunder, we raise you in the flame
New Orleans never whispers when it speaks a fallen name

The tuba growls like engines, the snare's a breaking bone
The widow wears the river and she dances all alone
Sweat and salt and gospel boiling under iron skies
The band turns grief to lightning till the casket starts to rise

When the dirge becomes a riot and the slow walk turns to run
We celebrate the leaving underneath a dying sun
Death don't get the last word in this drowned and holy town
We scream you to the heavens before we lay you down

March him to the graveyard, march him through the fire
Let the saints come screaming from the swamp and from the wire
We bury you in thunder, we raise you in the flame
New Orleans never whispers when it speaks a fallen name

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Well I closed my eyes at a quarter past ten
And I'm up off the floor and I'm grinning again
No ladder, no ticket, no runway, no fuss
Just me and the moon on a midnight bus

I'm flyin', I'm flyin', baby watch me go
Kick up off the rooftops, put on a show
Gravity called but I let it ring
Swing me to the ceiling, let me have my swing

The clarinet's laughing, the brass takes the stairs
I loop round the steeple without any cares
The stars throw confetti, the saxophone sighs
And the whole sleepy city floats up in my eyes

Morning comes knocking with coffee and shoes
But I keep one foot dancing, I'll never lose
The secret of altitude, the trick of the air
When I dream it again I'll meet you up there

I'm flyin', I'm flyin', baby watch me go
Kick up off the rooftops, put on a show
Gravity called but I let it ring
Swing me to the ceiling, let me have my swing

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We sealed it in the hold beneath a flag we did not raise
The compass turned to nothing in the gray and salt-bleached haze
No one signed the manifest, no one wrote it down
We only felt it breathing as the harbor light went down

Carry it, carry it, the weight we cannot name
The ocean keeps its silence and the silence keeps the blame
Mile after mile after mile of black
We will reach the other shore but none of us comes back

The lanterns die at midnight, the wood begins to moan
The crew speaks in a language no one ever learned at home
The water has no bottom and the sky has lost its end
We row toward a morning that refuses to begin

Throw it overboard, the boatswain cries, but no one moves a hand
The cargo is the journey and the journey has no land
Somewhere far behind us all the maps dissolve to foam
And the thing inside the crate begins to whisper, take me home

Carry it, carry it, the weight we cannot name
The ocean keeps its silence and the silence keeps the blame
Mile after mile after mile of black
We will reach the other shore but none of us comes back

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Keys in your hand and your knuckles gone white
Empty parking lot at the dead of the night
Mirror, signal, breathe, don't stall
You're sixteen and you own it all

Go, go, hit the gas, kid, go
I'm gripping the door but I won't say no
You'll scrape the curb and you'll grind the brake
But every wreck is a chance you take

You stalled it twice and you cussed out loud
Learned that word from your old man, proud
Green light, go, you lurched ahead
My whole life flashing inside my head

One day soon you'll drive away
Gravel and taillights, nothing to say
So grind those gears, kid, learn the road
I'd rather ride shotgun than let you go alone

Go, go, hit the gas, kid, go
I'm gripping the door but I won't say no
You'll scrape the curb and you'll grind the brake
But every wreck is a chance you take

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Phone lit up at a quarter to two
Three little words and not one of them you
Thumb hit send like a slammin' door
Didn't even spell my name no more

Text me goodbye, oh, text me goodbye
No dial tone, baby, no reason why
You typed me out like a typo, gone
Left me on read with the groove still on

Used to call me up just to hear me laugh
Now I'm a screenshot, I'm a cut-in-half
Little gray bubble where the love used to live
You took the easy road, had nothing to give

Delete, delete, but the funk remains
The bassline mourns and the snare complains
I'll dance it off on the kitchen floor
Who needs a love that don't knock on the door

Text me goodbye, oh, text me goodbye
No dial tone, baby, no reason why
You typed me out like a typo, gone
Left me on read with the groove still on

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Las campanas me llamaron desde niño
I knelt where my grandmother knelt and prayed
The candles knew my name in the morning
but something in me quietly walked away

Adiós to the saints in the windows of gold
Adiós to the hymns that I learned when I was small
I'm not slamming the door, I am closing it slow
God, if you're there, you'll forgive me for it all

No trumpet announces my leaving
I fold up my missal and set down the pew
The Virgin still watches with patience
but the faith that was given just no longer feels true

Maybe heaven is wider than steeples
Maybe mercy don't live behind one painted wall
I carry the love that they taught me
even walking away from the place that taught it all

Adiós to the saints in the windows of gold
Adiós to the hymns that I learned when I was small
I'm not slamming the door, I am closing it slow
God, if you're there, you'll forgive me for it all

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
The clarinet is weeping in a minor, broken key
A little screen lit up and took the whole world out of me
No letter and no candle, no goodbye across the room
Just a buzzing in my pocket like a fiddle full of gloom

Oy, you typed it, oy, you sent it, oy, the dancing stopped
The accordion is sighing where the love we had got dropped
We spun around the table and we laughed beneath the lights
Now a cold blue glowing rectangle is all I hold at night

My mother said be careful of a heart that moves too fast
But I gave you all my klezmer and I thought the song would last
The violin remembers every note we used to play
Now it shivers like a winter when the words just walked away

So play it loud, you fiddlers, let the freylekhs turn to tears
We'll mourn it like the old way, like the grief of all the years
Then we'll dance because we have to, for the living and the gone
A broken heart still has two feet and the music must go on

Oy, you typed it, oy, you sent it, oy, the dancing stopped
The accordion is sighing where the love we had got dropped
We spun around the table and we laughed beneath the lights
Now a cold blue glowing rectangle is all I hold at night

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle in the window since nineteen-eighty-three
Mr. Okafor stitching every dream on the street
Kids come for the prom, the grooms come for the vow
He measures up the future, he's been measuring it now
Chalk line on the shoulder, pin between his teeth
Makes a hand-me-down a crown, makes a thrift store a feast
The block walks taller when he hems up the cuff
A man without a suit ain't never sharp enough

He dress the whole block, every shoulder, every seam
Turn a rough cut kid to a king out a magazine
Thread by thread, he believe in what we'll be
The tailor see the man before the man can ever see

Deacon got married in his Sunday best gray
The shooter's little brother got buried the same way
Same hands, same fabric, same careful repair
He sews up the joy and he sews up the prayer
No logo on the label, just his name in blue
More real than the runway, more us than the new
When the chain stores opened up he never closed the door
The people who he raised up keep coming back for more

Thirty years of pins and not a single complaint
He could've left for money but he chose to stay
Said a neighborhood is only as proud as how it stands
And pride is something cut and measured by a steady pair of hands

He dress the whole block, every shoulder, every seam
Turn a rough cut kid to a king out a magazine
Thread by thread, he believe in what we'll be
The tailor see the man before the man can ever see

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
One suitcase and a photograph, a coat too thin for snow
The harbor swallowed everything I ever used to know
The gulls were singing standards as the engines pulled away
And the only home I ever had got smaller every day

Across the water, blue on blue
I'm carrying my country in a coat and one old shoe
Across the water, far from shore
I'll learn to love a city that don't know my name no more

The piano in the steerage played a tune nobody knew
A dozen tongues all humming to a moon that someone drew
We danced between the lifeboats just to feel a little warm
A raggedy little orchestra against the coming storm

Will they have a word for longing where the streetcars run on time
Will the bread taste like my mother's, will the church bells ever chime
I fold my old language up and tuck it in my chest
And I step onto the gangway like the others, like the rest

Across the water, blue on blue
I'm carrying my country in a coat and one old shoe
Across the water, far from shore
I'll learn to love a city that don't know my name no more

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Lights on the marquee, almost spelled my name
Manager said baby you're one shot from the game
Horns were tight, the crowd was hot
I gave 'em everything that I had got

So close, so close, I could taste the gold
One more groove and the whole world sold
The night I almost, almost made it shine
I keep that almost playing one more time

The label man was nodding, the bass was in the pocket
My heart was a rocket two seconds from the socket
Then the lights went dark and the deal fell through
A phone call Monday said we're passing on you

But I still got the funk down deep in my bones
Don't need a contract to sit up on a throne
The almost made me, the almost set me free
The groove don't quit and neither do me

So close, so close, I could taste the gold
One more groove and the whole world sold
The night I almost, almost made it shine
I keep that almost playing one more time

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The roll of tape screams once across the seam
The last cardboard coffin of a folded-up dream
The rooms stand naked, the echoes are loud
Four years of our laughter packed up in a shroud

One last box, one last sigh
One last turn before goodbye
The tape pulls tight and the past comes loose
We dance one slow step, then I cut us loose

The nail in the wall where the picture used to hang
The scratch on the floor from the night that we sang
I press down the corners, I write down the date
The key on the counter, the click of the gate

Who keeps the lamp and who keeps the years
Who keeps the silence that's louder than tears
The music keeps playing, there's no one to lead
Just me and a marker and one box of need

One last box, one last sigh
One last turn before goodbye
The tape pulls tight and the past comes loose
We dance one slow step, then I cut us loose

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Close my eyes and the bassline drops
Cinnamon rising where the memory stops
Flour on the counter, sugar in the air
I'm seven years old and she's standing right there

Take me back, take me back to the kitchen
Where the love came warm and the clock kept ticking
Butter and bread and the windows steamed
Her hands in the dough is the realest dream

The radio humming an old slow song
She danced while she stirred and she pulled me along
No recipe written, it lived in her hands
A language of feeding that the heart understands

Now the house is sold and the table is gone
But the smell finds me here when the beat carries on
Every 4-4 kick is a door coming loose
And I'm back in her kitchen with nothing to lose

Take me back, take me back to the kitchen
Where the love came warm and the clock kept ticking
Butter and bread and the windows steamed
Her hands in the dough is the realest dream

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Mile marker eighty on a cold Nebraska line
Diesel hum and coffee and a heart a state behind
I pull into the gravel where the payphone ghosts still stand
And I dial the only number that can steady up my hand

It's three a.m., baby, are you sleeping
I just needed to hear you breathing on the line
The road's a long dark river and I'm keeping
A picture of your porch light in my mind

The kids are growing taller in the photos that you send
I memorize their faces at each truck stop, each dead end
The rigs roll past like thunder and the rain comes down like sin
And your sleepy voice is the only roof I'm sleeping under in

Two more days of asphalt, then I'm turning for the south
I'll taste your name like water when I say it in my mouth
Don't wait up, but you waited, and you always somehow do
A thousand miles of nothing and the something at the end is you

It's three a.m., baby, are you sleeping
I just needed to hear you breathing on the line
The road's a long dark river and I'm keeping
A picture of your porch light in my mind

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the gray right up to your roots
Mall where the meadow used to bloom
But you stand in the lot like you're keeping a promise
The last green word in a concrete tomb

Oh, lonely tree, oh, hold on
Everything around you is already gone
They paved the whole world but they left you behind
The last little piece of a kinder time

Kids carve their names in your patient skin
Lovers still find you when the day grows thin
You shade the old man on the bus stop bench
The only sweet air in the diesel and the stench

They say there's a permit, they say there's a plan
A blueprint that needs where you're standing to stand
But I'll chain myself to you before they take you down
You're the last living heartbeat in this paved-over town

Oh, lonely tree, oh, hold on
Everything around you is already gone
They paved the whole world but they left you behind
The last little piece of a kinder time

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nine cracked bats and a borrowed glove
Dust on the diamond we grew up of
The favored crowd in their pressed white sleeves
Laughing soft at our taped-up knees
Clarinet whining low and sweet
Over the stomp of our muddy feet

Oy, swing for the fence, you ragged sons
The scoreboard lies but the heart still runs
Let the fiddle climb and the cymbal crash
We are the spark before the ash

Bottom of the ninth and the moon comes pale
Grandfather's prayer in a coffee can pail
One shot left and the pitcher's grin
We got nothing but the will to win
The accordion gasps like an old man's chest
This is the inning that buries the rest

No trophy waits with our crooked names
But they will whisper this in the frames
How the nobodies danced on the chalk and the lime
And stole one hour back from time

Oy, swing for the fence, you ragged sons
The scoreboard lies but the heart still runs
Let the fiddle climb and the cymbal crash
We are the spark before the ash

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Glass room, twelve chairs, fluorescent hum
I raise my hand and the moment's gone numb
Half a word out and the louder voice steals
My idea reborn in somebody's deals
Mute on my mic but my pulse keeps four
Fingernails counting the seconds I bore

Let me finish, let me finish the line
The floor was mine for a beat, just mine
Turn it up till the talking dissolves
Watch how a quiet room revolves

Sticky note army, agenda I drew
They nod at the man who repeats it as new
Coffee gone cold and the clock won't blink
I swallow the sentence before I can think

But the bassline don't ask for a turn
It just enters the room and it makes the walls burn
So I'll speak like a kick, undeniable, low
The whole table moving before they know

Let me finish, let me finish the line
The floor was mine for a beat, just mine
Turn it up till the talking dissolves
Watch how a quiet room revolves

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
The gravel still remembers your truck
The screen door hangs on its busted luck
I brought you whiskey you'll never drink
And words I rehearsed at the kitchen sink

You left me a temper and a banjo neck
A debt I paid and a folded check
I carried the cold of you up this hill
To set it down by the daffodil

So I forgive you, the stone don't care
I forgive you, the morning air
Not for your sake, you're past my reach
But the river's tired of what I teach

The preacher said grace is a door you walk
Not a debt you collect, not a settled talk
So I'm leaving the anger here in the weeds
With the rusted plow and the spilling seeds

Funny how mercy weighs less than a grudge
Funny how late I finally budge
The wind takes the last of the things I'd say
And carries them somewhere, far away

So I forgive you, the stone don't care
I forgive you, the morning air
Not for your sake, you're past my reach
But the river's tired of what I teach

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five dollar table by the iron gate
He's got a clock and a grin that won't wait
Pigeons clap when the tourists sit down
Thinking they're sharp in this part of town
He lets you lead, lets you feel like a king
Then he hums a little nothing thing

Check, baby, check, he never blinks twice
Velvet hands rolling loaded dice
Knight to the corner, your heart's gonna fall
King of the concrete, owns it all

Hustler's got rent in a paper cup
Swallows your queen and he never looks up
Says kid, you played like you had a chance
That's the price of the New York dance

Under the lamplight the board glows white
Thirty years of these summer nights
He could've been famous, could've been more
But the park's his stage and the pawns adore

Check, baby, check, he never blinks twice
Velvet hands rolling loaded dice
Knight to the corner, your heart's gonna fall
King of the concrete, owns it all

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Door still shaking from the way you went
Walls holding echoes of everything meant
Glass on the counter, water gone warm
We were the weather, now we're the calm after storm
My phone face down, won't light up blue
Silence so loud that it sounds like you

Quiet, too quiet, the house got cold
We said the words that we can't reroll
No bass in the kitchen, no beat in the hall
Just the hum of the fridge and the weight of it all

Two cups of coffee, one of them's mine
The other one waiting like a held redline
I count your footsteps that aren't even there
Love turned to static in the empty air

I'd take the yelling over this hush
At least in the storm I could feel your rush
Now it's 808 heartbeat, slow and alone
Texting three dots then I leave it unknown

Quiet, too quiet, the house got cold
We said the words that we can't reroll
No bass in the kitchen, no beat in the hall
Just the hum of the fridge and the weight of it all

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Sunlight melts on the marble squares
He folds the world into halves and pairs
A halo of smoke where the linden leans
Hands moving slow as a film in dreams

The city dissolves to a velvet blur
His voice a low and a feathered slur
Sit down, he hums, let the hours drown
Watch your little kingdom tumbling down

Float the bishop on a tide of gold
Nothing here is bought or sold
Only time in a soft cascade
And the king he's already made

Reverb pigeons in a lavender sky
The clock ticks soft as a lullaby
You lose so gently you can't be mad
It's the prettiest checkmate you ever had

And when the lamps come humming on
He's a silhouette, then he's gone
Leaving a board still warm with his ghost
The one move you wanted most

Float the bishop on a tide of gold
Nothing here is bought or sold
Only time in a soft cascade
And the king he's already made

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded her down with a black oak chest
The captain laughed, said to hell with the rest
But the gulls turned back at the harbor mouth
And the compass needle wouldn't point us south

Roll, boys, roll, the hold's gone cold
There's something down there older than gold
Fiddle plays fast but the wind plays slow
Lord, let me off where the willows grow

First night out and the lantern died
Second night the bosun cried
He heard a voice in the salted dark
Saying every soul will leave its mark

Three weeks gone and the water's green
The strangest port that we ain't seen
The banjo's the only thing still bright
Keeping the men from the long good night

Don't open the chest, don't say the name
Don't let the moon catch you playing that game
Whatever we carry, we carry to shore
And the curse'll be somebody else's, once more

Roll, boys, roll, the hold's gone cold
There's something down there older than gold
Fiddle plays fast but the wind plays slow
Lord, let me off where the willows grow

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle of Mendel by the corner store
Chalk on his thumb and a bell on the door
He's hemmed every dream on this cobbled row
From the bris to the box, he's the man you go
Accordion wheezes where the streetlamp stands
And the whole block walks in his careful hands

Stitch it, stitch it, Mendel, stitch us whole
A shoulder for the worker, a seam for the soul
Let the clarinet cry as the spool spins free
He dresses the living and the memory

The widow's black and the bridegroom's blue
The boy's first suit that he'll grow into
He presses the wrinkles of all our years
And hides in the lining our quiet fears

His own coat's threadbare, his cuffs gone thin
He gives away more than he takes in
But the block stands taller in Mendel's thread
We wear his kindness long after he's dead

Stitch it, stitch it, Mendel, stitch us whole
A shoulder for the worker, a seam for the soul
Let the clarinet cry as the spool spins free
He dresses the living and the memory

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Thumb on the number I never deleted
Seven years of words half-completed
The dial tone hums like a held-in breath
All the small wars we fought to the death
Midnight kitchen, the tile so cold
Dialing the woman whose hands I'm told are old

Pick up, Mama, pick up the line
Let the bassline carry what I can't define
Four on the floor like a beating heart
Seven years and a brand new start

She answers slow and the room goes wide
That single hello and I'm five inside
No grudge survives in that trembling sound
The whole floor drops and I'm finally found

We don't say sorry, we don't keep score
We just breathe together through the open door
The music swells where the words won't go
Mama, I'm here, and I love you so

Pick up, Mama, pick up the line
Let the bassline carry what I can't define
Four on the floor like a beating heart
Seven years and a brand new start

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up where the streetlight flickered and died
Mama worked doubles, never once cried
Same four corners, same cracked song
Everybody leaving but the leaving took long

I'm gettin' out, I'm gettin' gone
Packed my guitar at the break of dawn
Tell the dead-end block I'm through with you
Gonna find a road that's painted new

Daddy's old truck wouldn't turn over right
Slept in the cab on a summer night
Swore on the dashboard, swore on the moon
I'd be more than a name they forgot too soon

The diner closed and the mill shut down
Ghosts in the windows all over town
But I kept a dollar and I kept a dream
And a tank of gas and a head full of steam

I ain't ashamed of the dirt I'm from
It taught me to run and it taught me to come
back for the ones who couldn't break free
The block made me, now watch what I'll be

I'm gettin' out, I'm gettin' gone
Packed my guitar at the break of dawn
Tell the dead-end block I'm through with you
Gonna find a road that's painted new

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Lluvia cayendo on the avenue
One black umbrella and me and you
Never met before but the storm said stay
Your shoulder to mine in the gray, gray day
Neon puddles where the taxis hiss
Never knew a downpour could feel like this

Baila bajo la lluvia, dale, ven
Una sombrilla, two strangers, again
Drop on your cheek and I lose the plan
Hold a little closer if you can

You laugh at my Spanish, I laugh at your shoes
Soaked to the bone but I can't lose
The light turns green but we don't move
The city's a record stuck in a groove

Maybe the rain knows more than we do
Maybe it dropped us here on cue
When the clouds break up will you still stay
O te vas con la tormenta away

Baila bajo la lluvia, dale, ven
Una sombrilla, two strangers, again
Drop on your cheek and I lose the plan
Hold a little closer if you can

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine idling in the loading dock
Clock on the dash, three minutes to knock
I told her this one's the very last
A clean ticket out of a dirty past
Radio static and a half-smoked light
My hands too steady for an honest night

One more run and I'm done, I swear
Foot on the pedal and a prayer in the air
Don't look back at the flashing red
Drive like the version of me that's already dead

They come out running and the doors slam tight
The whole street folds into the headlight white
I know these alleys like a second skin
The last clean exit I'll ever be in

There's a town up north where nobody knows
The name I'll wear and the beard I'll grow
If I make this corner, if I clear this light
I'm a ghost by morning, gone for good tonight

One more run and I'm done, I swear
Foot on the pedal and a prayer in the air
Don't look back at the flashing red
Drive like the version of me that's already dead

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Shoo-bop, I was seventeen
Knees like jelly and the lights so green
A borrowed guitar and a crack in my voice
Stood at that mic 'cause I made the choice

Oh, that first night, doo-wop-shoo-wah
My heart on the floor of that smoky bar
Three people clapped and the world stood still
I've been chasing that moment, I always will

Forgot the second verse, made one up
Spilled my soul in a paper cup
The waitress smiled and the jukebox died
And somethin' woke up that I couldn't hide

Now the room is bigger and the lights are bright
But I'd trade it all for that trembling night
When nobody knew me and I didn't know
That the kid at the mic had somewhere to go

Oh, that first night, doo-wop-shoo-wah
My heart on the floor of that smoky bar
Three people clapped and the world stood still
I've been chasing that moment, I always will

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Five a.m. alarm like a fist to the chest
Clock in at the warehouse, no time to rest
Forklift humming and my back's on fire
Clock out, change shirts, second job's dire
Mop the late diner till the sun comes up
Count the same tips in the same paper cup
Rent went up and the hours stayed flat
Math don't math, ain't no fixing that

Two jobs deep and I'm still in the hole
Selling my hours, can't sell my soul
They say work harder, I been working three lives
Still the water rises faster than I dive

Kid needs shoes and the heater's broke
Laugh so I don't let the worry choke
Proud man drowning with a smile on tight
Bank says zero by the end of the night

I'm not lazy, I'm not behind by choice
The system's loud and it muted my voice
But I show up daily, I carry the weight
If love paid bills I'd be living straight

Two jobs deep and I'm still in the hole
Selling my hours, can't sell my soul
They say work harder, I been working three lives
Still the water rises faster than I dive

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. and the rest stop glows
Diesel ghost where the cold wind blows
Quarters in the payphone, fingers numb
Eighteen wheels and a heart gone dumb
Mile marker nothing on a road called gone
Talking to a dial tone, holding on

Pick up the phone, I'm a thousand miles wrong
Pick up the phone, I been driving too long
Tell the kids their daddy ain't a ghost
Just a voice on a wire from coast to coast

She answers tired and the line goes thin
I hear the house I'm never in
The dog, the dishes, the life I miss
Traded for asphalt and a goodbye kiss

I'm not running, I'm just gone to provide
Every white line is a piece of my pride
But I'd burn the rig and I'd torch the load
For one more morning that ain't on the road

Pick up the phone, I'm a thousand miles wrong
Pick up the phone, I been driving too long
Tell the kids their daddy ain't a ghost
Just a voice on a wire from coast to coast

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete spreading like a tired gray sea
They took the meadow, took the memory
But there you stand on a corner alone
Roots breaking up through the parking lot stone

Neon signs where the orchard used to grow
Nobody waters you, nobody knows
Still every spring you remember to bloom
A little green in a colorless room

Oh, last tree standing, hold on tight
The only living thing left in sight
They paved it over but they couldn't pave you
Oh, last tree standing, see it through

Kids carve their names in your weathered skin
A single bird makes a home within
The city forgot what your shade was for
But you keep on reaching for something more

Oh, last tree standing, hold on tight
The only living thing left in sight
They paved it over but they couldn't pave you
Oh, last tree standing, see it through

And if they come with their saws someday
Let the wind carry your seeds away
So somewhere far from this empty town
A forest rises where they tore you down

Oh, last tree standing, hold on tight
The only living thing left in sight
They paved it over but they couldn't pave you
Oh, last tree standing, see it through

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
I remember the way that you smiled at me
Like a Sunday morning, like a melody
I let you walk on out that door
Didn't know what I was living for

Now I lift my hands and I close my eyes
Asking heaven why I let love pass me by
Every prayer I send above
Carries the name of a long-lost love

The one that got away, oh Lord
The one I should have stayed for
Forgive my foolish heart today
For letting go, for the one that got away

Mama told me hold on tight to the good
Do right by love the way that I should
But pride is a river and I was the stone
Now I'm standing in the water all alone

The one that got away, oh Lord
The one I should have stayed for
Forgive my foolish heart today
For letting go, for the one that got away

Glory, glory, teach me how to mend
Give me grace to love again
If the doors of heaven open wide
Let me find her on the other side

The one that got away, oh Lord
The one I should have stayed for
Forgive my foolish heart today
For letting go, for the one that got away

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every morning I unlock the door
Walk these marble floors like before
But there's a lady in a golden frame
And every single day she calls my name

The tourists come and the tourists go
But she's the only one I really know
