Painted eyes follow me around
Sweetest love in this quiet town

Oh my lady on the wall, ay
You don't move but you got it all, ay
I dey guard you through the night
You and me till the morning light
Oh my lady on the wall, ay
Sweetest treasure in the hall

They say don't touch, just keep your distance
But you been my whole existence
Four hundred years and you never aged
A timeless beauty behind the glass cage

Oh my lady on the wall, ay
You don't move but you got it all, ay
I dey guard you through the night
You and me till the morning light
Oh my lady on the wall, ay
Sweetest treasure in the hall

When the lights go down low
Just me and you in the afterglow
Dance with your shadow on the floor
Who could ever ask for more

Oh my lady on the wall, ay
You don't move but you got it all, ay
I dey guard you through the night
You and me till the morning light

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slid shut and the air went thin
My heart started racing right under my skin
Walls closing in on the downtown line
Couldn't catch my breath, couldn't find the time

Strangers all around but I felt alone
Fluorescent light on the cold hard chrome
I whispered help in a trembling breath
Felt the shadow of a rolling death

But You met me on the underground
When my whole world came tumbling down
In the tunnel dark You were my light
Lord, You held me through the night

Between the stations I called Your name
And the rushing river slowed its claim
A still small voice said child, breathe
I am with you, only believe

And You met me on the underground
When my whole world came tumbling down
In the tunnel dark You were my light
Lord, You held me through the night

Next stop, glory, next stop, peace
The iron grip began to release
With every breath I found Your grace
A quiet calm in a crowded place

You met me on the underground
When my whole world came tumbling down
In the tunnel dark You were my light
Lord, You held me through the night

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Two plates on the counter, I only need one
The morning light hits where the coffee's begun
Your recipe's open to the page we both loved
But the kitchen feels empty, just me and the stove

Measured for two, now I'm cutting it down
Half an onion, half a heart in this town
The pot's too big for the soup that I make
Learning to live with each meal that I take

Cooking for one, baby, cooking for one
Under the kitchen light when the day is done
I still set your place out of memory
Learning how to feed this lonely in me
Cooking for one

You always said season it slow with love
Now I taste the salt of the tears from above
The table's so quiet, no laughter, no clink
Just the hum of the fridge and the drip in the sink

Cooking for one, baby, cooking for one
Under the kitchen light when the day is done
I still set your place out of memory
Learning how to feed this lonely in me
Cooking for one

Maybe one day I'll cook for two again
Invite a friend, let somebody in
But tonight I'll plate it slow and true
And save the first bite, baby, just for you

Cooking for one, oh, cooking for one
Under the kitchen light when the day is done
Still learning how to live and let it be
Feeding this lonely heart in me

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up on the rooftop with a homemade tower
Broadcasting freedom in the midnight hour
The government frequency can't hold me down
I'm the voice of the people in this concrete town

Needle on the record, dial in the static
Rebel transmission coming up from the attic
They try to jam it but the signal stay strong
Every little soul singing along

Pirate radio, turn it up loud
Music for the people, music for the crowd
They can't catch the rhythm, can't catch the sound
We keep the riddim going all around
Pirate radio, set the people free
This is the frequency of liberty

Ded of the night when the city is sleeping
A promise to the listeners I'm always keeping
A dub plate spinning and a message clear
Love and unity for all to hear

Pirate radio, turn it up loud
Music for the people, music for the crowd
They can't catch the rhythm, can't catch the sound
We keep the riddim going all around
Pirate radio, set the people free
This is the frequency of liberty

When the van comes rolling I pack up and run
But tomorrow night the broadcast carry on
You can't arrest a melody, can't jail a song
The airwaves belong to where we belong

Pirate radio, turn it up loud
Music for the people, music for the crowd
They can't catch the rhythm, can't catch the sound
We keep the riddim going all around

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Wooden pews and the stained-glass light
The hymns I memorized every Sunday night
I know the cracks in the ceiling by heart
But something inside me is falling apart

I stood for the prayers and I sang every line
Believing the words would someday be mine
The steeple still rises against the gray
But quietly, quietly, I walk away

No slamming doors, no angry goodbye
Just an empty seat where I used to sit high
The bells still ring but they ring without me
Quietly leaving, quietly free

The people I loved will not understand
Why I let go of the steadying hand
I carry the kindness, I leave the fear
And the road keeps rising, the sky comes clear

No slamming doors, no angry goodbye
Just an empty seat where I used to sit high
The bells still ring but they ring without me
Quietly leaving, quietly free

Let the choir swell and the organ rise
I'll find my faith under open skies
Not in the building, not in the creed
But somewhere out there in the wind and the seed

No slamming doors, no angry goodbye
Just an empty seat where I used to sit high
The bells still ring but they ring without me
Quietly leaving, quietly free

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We floated out beyond the blue
Just me, the stars, and you
Tethered tight in the endless black
But the cord is fraying, no way back

Your voice gets fainter on the radio
Drifting slow where the comets go
I reach my hand but I touch the void
Two little satellites, unemployed

Drifting apart in the gravity
Further from you, further from me
No sound out here but the beating heart
Two astronauts drifting apart

We used to orbit the same warm sun
Now we're spinning, undone
The Earth's a marble, small and far
And I can't remember which light's your star

Drifting apart in the gravity
Further from you, further from me
No sound out here but the beating heart
Two astronauts drifting apart

If I let go will you feel it too
A silent ache where the air once flew
Weightless, helpless, soft and slow
Watching the only love I know

Drifting apart in the gravity
Further from you, further from me
No sound out here but the beating heart
Two astronauts drifting apart

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Out on the headland where the cold wind blows
A little tin shack that nobody knows
With a wire and a whistle and a flickering flame
I send out the music, I won't say my name

The fishermen listen on the rolling tide
And the lasses in the village keep the dial by their side
The crown sent its men but they searched in vain
For the voice on the airwaves over the plain

So raise up the aerial, raise up the song
The whole of the county can sing along
They'll never silence what the wild wind carries
The pirate of the airwaves, the song never tarries

A fiddle, a bodhrán, a reel and a tune
Broadcast in the dark by the light of the moon
When the soldiers come marching I'll vanish like mist
And tomorrow the music will rise from the schist

So raise up the aerial, raise up the song
The whole of the county can sing along
They'll never silence what the wild wind carries
The pirate of the airwaves, the song never tarries

From the cliffs to the glen, from the bog to the sea
The sound of the people, forever to be free
No law of the land and no king on the throne
Can capture a melody that flies on its own

So raise up the aerial, raise up the song
The whole of the county can sing along
They'll never silence what the wild wind carries
The pirate of the airwaves, the song never tarries

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
The stands are full and the lights burn white
We were never the ones they picked to win tonight
Mud on the jersey, scars on the knee
Nobody believed but we believed in we

Forty minutes left and the score's behind
But something is rising in the back of the mind
We carry the town on our tired backs
Every missed practice, every lonely track

This is the final, this is the call
Give it everything, give it your all
Whether we rise or whether we fall
We leave our hearts here, one and all

The whistle screams and the clock runs down
The loudest silence in this little town
One last play, one last chance
One breath, one heartbeat, one final dance

This is the final, this is the call
Give it everything, give it your all
Whether we rise or whether we fall
We leave our hearts here, one and all

And if the scoreboard doesn't show our name
We'll know we played a different kind of game
The ones who fought when no one thought we could
Underdogs forever, and forever good

This is the final, this is the call
Give it everything, give it your all
Whether we rise or whether we fall
We leave our hearts here, one and all

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Fifty winters we have shared, my love
Under the same old roof and the stars above
Now the fire burns low and the fiddle plays slow
One last turn before we have to go

Your hands are weathered, mine are too
But they still remember the steps we knew
Through the lean years and the harvest gold
We danced together, never growing cold

So take my hand for the last waltz, dear
One more turn while the lamp burns clear
The years were a song and we sang them true
One last dance, just me and you

The children grown and the village changed
But the love between us never rearranged
A reel of joy and a slow lament
Every step a year well spent

So take my hand for the last waltz, dear
One more turn while the lamp burns clear
The years were a song and we sang them true
One last dance, just me and you

And when the music finally ends
And the long road comes round the bend
I'll hold you close as the candle dies
And dance you home to the morning skies

So take my hand for the last waltz, dear
One more turn while the lamp burns clear
The years were a song and we sang them true
One last dance, just me and you

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Well the skillet's too big and the table's too wide
There's an empty old chair right here by my side
She cooked every supper for forty-two years
Now I'm peelin' these taters through a curtain of tears

Got her recipe book all dog-eared and stained
But a pinch and a handful was all she explained
So I'm guessin' at gravy and burnin' the bread
Talkin' to her picture and the things that she said

I'm cookin' for one in a kitchen for two
Lord, I'm learnin' to do what I never could do
She fed me with love every day of her life
Now I'm cookin' for one without my sweet wife

The biscuits come hard and the coffee's too strong
But I sit down and bless it and I carry on
A single tin plate where there once were a pair
And the smell of her cornbread still hangs in the air

I'm cookin' for one in a kitchen for two
Lord, I'm learnin' to do what I never could do
She fed me with love every day of her life
Now I'm cookin' for one without my sweet wife

Maybe someday I'll get the seasonin' right
And I'll set just one plate without losin' the fight
But I'll save her the window where the mornin' sun shone
And I'll never quite get used to cookin' alone

I'm cookin' for one in a kitchen for two
Lord, I'm learnin' to do what I never could do
She fed me with love every day of her life
Now I'm cookin' for one without my sweet wife

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Soft is the evening, the candle burns low
The old record spinning, so gentle and slow
Take my hand, querida, let the world fade away
One final dance at the end of the day

The years passed like waves on a warm summer shore
We stumbled, we swayed, and we always found more
Now the music is softer, our footsteps are light
Two silhouettes glowing in the amber night

Dance with me slow, one last time
The rhythm of love, the bossa of time
Your cheek next to mine in the fading glow
One final turn before we let go

The sea breeze is humming a bittersweet tune
The stars on the water, the silver of the moon
We gave all our seasons, our laughter, our tears
And we danced through the beauty of all of these years

Dance with me slow, one last time
The rhythm of love, the bossa of time
Your cheek next to mine in the fading glow
One final turn before we let go

No sorrow tonight, only soft melody
Just the warmth of your arms and the saudade in me
Let the song carry on as our shadows entwine
Forever and always, your hand here in mine

Dance with me slow, one last time
The rhythm of love, the bossa of time
Your cheek next to mine in the fading glow
One final turn before we let go

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the foundations on a beating chest
Built their towers high where the titan takes its rest
We drink our coffee on a rising ribcage
Never read the warning on the ancient page

The streetlights flicker when it shifts in its dreams
And the subway trembles with its muffled screams
We call it an earthquake, we call it the wind
But something enormous is sleeping within

This city's built on a sleeping giant
Go about your day, stay quiet, stay quiet
Don't wake the thing that holds us all
Or the whole of the world is gonna fall
This city's built on a sleeping giant

The mayor cuts a ribbon on a brand new mall
Right above a heartbeat slow and tall
We pave the meadows and we raise the rent
On the back of a god that the heavens sent

This city's built on a sleeping giant
Go about your day, stay quiet, stay quiet
Don't wake the thing that holds us all
Or the whole of the world is gonna fall
This city's built on a sleeping giant

And one day it'll open up its ancient eyes
And shake the glass towers from the smoggy skies
Maybe it'll wonder what we built and why
Then roll back over with a mountainous sigh

This city's built on a sleeping giant
Go about your day, stay quiet, stay quiet
Don't wake the thing that holds us all
Or the whole of the world is gonna fall

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning, the suit's on the floor
Black tie still hanging on the bedroom door
The casseroles stacked up in the fridge so high
And a strange kind of sunshine in the bright blue sky

The phone stopped ringing, the guests all went home
First cup of coffee that I drink alone
Life keeps on moving like it doesn't know
That yesterday we laid somebody low

It's the morning after, pick up the horns
The day comes anyway, a new one's born
We danced through the sorrow, we'll dance through the pain
Put one foot forward, do it again
It's the morning after, play it loud
Life's gotta carry on, sing it proud

The flowers are wilting on the kitchen sink
Got a thousand little memories that make me think
But the trumpet keeps blasting that upbeat sound
Lifting me up off the cold hard ground

It's the morning after, pick up the horns
The day comes anyway, a new one's born
We danced through the sorrow, we'll dance through the pain
Put one foot forward, do it again
It's the morning after, play it loud
Life's gotta carry on, sing it proud

So pour out a drink for the ones who've gone
They'd want us laughing, they'd want us strong
Raise up the offbeat, raise up the skank
For every soul that we've got to thank

It's the morning after, pick up the horns
The day comes anyway, a new one's born
We danced through the sorrow, we'll dance through the pain
Put one foot forward, do it again

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Thunder splits the concrete sky
Two silhouettes refuse to die
You pulled me under steel and rain
A stranger's shelter, shared like pain

We never traded any names
Just held the dark against the flames
The sky came down in iron sheets
We stood our ground on drowning streets

One umbrella, two unknowns
Flesh and lightning, blood and bone
Hold the line against the flood
Strangers bound in storm and mud

Your knuckles white around the spine
Of this thin roof that's yours and mine
The gutters scream, the heavens roar
We will not break, we will not fall

One umbrella, two unknowns
Flesh and lightning, blood and bone
Hold the line against the flood
Strangers bound in storm and mud

When the light turns green we part
Two strangers, one electric heart
The storm forgets but I will not
The shelter that two strangers fought

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
String lights fading, tents come down
Last night humming through the ground
Paper cups and trampled grass
Knowing this one couldn't last

Last night, slow light
Hold the warm before the cold
Last night, fade right
Into stories getting old

Vinyl crackle in the air
Someone's laughing over there
We trade numbers we won't call
Still it meant the most of all

Morning's coming, breaking soft
Folding memory aloft
We were here, we were enough
Dust and music, light and love

Last night, slow light
Hold the warm before the cold
Last night, fade right
Into stories getting old

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Loaded in the dead of night
Crates that hum with hidden light
Captain said don't ask what's there
Just keep moving, breathe the air

We carry, we carry the weight of the deep
We carry, we carry the secrets we keep
Don't open, don't open, just ride through the night
The cargo, the cargo will burn in the light

Compass spinning, no control
Something whispers through the hull
Every wave a warning sign
Fifty days and out of time

We carry, we carry the weight of the deep
We carry, we carry the secrets we keep
Don't open, don't open, just ride through the night
The cargo, the cargo will burn in the light

Feel the bass beneath the deck
Feel the curse around your neck
Dance it off until we land
Salt and shadow, sea and sand

We carry, we carry the weight of the deep
We carry, we carry the secrets we keep

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
La luz amarilla, the lamp burning low
The pages keep turning, I've nowhere to go
My fingers are tapping the rhythm of fear
The clock strikes the two and the dawn is not near

Solo, solo, en la madrugada
Alone with my book and my heart like a blade
Solo, solo, the silence is dancing
I clap out the hours that I have not made

The coffee is bitter, my eyes are like stone
The whole world is sleeping and I am alone
Each word is a footstep on hot Spanish ground
My mind is the guitar, I strum without sound

¡Olé! to the lonely, the late, and the wise
¡Olé! to the burning behind my own eyes
The morning will catch me still bent to the page
A dancer of knowledge alone on the stage

Solo, solo, en la madrugada
Alone with my book and my heart like a blade

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slid shut, the lights went white
My chest became a fist too tight
The tunnel swallowed all the air
A hundred strangers, no one there

And I can't breathe on the downtown line
The walls are close and they're not mine
Count the stops, count to ten
Get me to the surface again

The handrail sweating in my grip
The whole car tilting like a ship
My heartbeat louder than the wheels
Nobody knows the way it feels

And I can't breathe on the downtown line
The walls are close and they're not mine
Count the stops, count to ten
Get me to the surface again

Then daylight cracked the closing dark
A stranger's eyes, a small remark
You're okay, you're almost home
You're not as alone as you've known

And I can breathe on the downtown line
The walls pull back and the world is mine

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Desk lamp glowing, soft and warm
Highlighter weather, paper storm
Mug gone cold an hour ago
Brain on quiet, taking it slow

Two a.m. and the city sleeps
Just me and the notes that I keep
Low beats humming, page by page
Writing my way out of this cage

Rain against the window pane
Lofi looping through my brain
One more chapter, then I'll rest
Telling lies but doing my best

Nobody up but me and the moon
Dawn's gonna find me here soon
Every word a little stone
Building something all alone

Two a.m. and the city sleeps
Just me and the notes that I keep
Low beats humming, page by page
Writing my way out of this cage

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The bus rolled in at half past nine
Same old water tower, same town line
I carried boots that crossed a sea
And ghosts that wouldn't let me be

I'm coming home, I'm coming home
Down the gravel road I've always known
The war is over but it stays
I'm learning how to count the days

My mother's porch, the same screen door
The dog don't know me anymore
The fields are gold, the church bell rings
I'm a stranger to these simple things

I'm coming home, I'm coming home
Down the gravel road I've always known
The war is over but it stays
I'm learning how to count the days

There's a name carved in the county stone
A boy I left and came back alone
Lord let the harvest heal what's torn
Let me be more than what I've borne

I'm coming home, I'm coming home
Down the gravel road I've always known

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Flour on her fingers, gold around her wrist
Every line a story, every scar a kiss
She danced in nineteen-seventy under mirror light
Those hands still keep the rhythm of a younger night

Grandmother's hands, keep on turning
Grandmother's hands, lamp still burning
They held the whole world soft and grand
Everything I am is in her hands

She braided all my summers, sewed my Sunday clothes
Clapped along the kitchen to the radio
Now they shake a little reaching for the cup
But when the music's playing, baby they light up

Grandmother's hands, keep on turning
Grandmother's hands, lamp still burning
They held the whole world soft and grand
Everything I am is in her hands

Spin me on the linoleum, queen beneath the disco ball
Those hands taught me to rise each time I fall
So let the strings keep soaring, let the brass band
Play forever for my grandmother's hands

Grandmother's hands, keep on turning
Grandmother's hands, lamp still burning

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The clarinet is shaking like the train upon the track
My breath is running forward but my heart is running back
The crowd sways like a wedding but there's nobody to hold
The tunnel's getting tighter and the iron's getting cold

Oy, the wheels they cry, the wheels they moan
A dancing fear that I can't outrun alone
Play it minor, play it fast
Pray this trembling will not last

The fiddle in my ribcage saws a frantic little tune
The fluorescent lights are spinning like a pale electric moon
I grip the pole and count the stops, I daven to the dark
The accordion of panic wheezing wild beneath my heart

Then an old man hums a melody his mother used to know
And the freylekhs of the living starts to ease the undertow
Breathe in two, breathe out three, the doors will open wide
The whole car is a shtetl and we're dancing side by side

Oy, the wheels they cry, the wheels they moan
But I am not, I am not, I am not alone

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Duffel bag and a cracked windshield
Chasing thunder cross the open field
Never stayed in one place long
Wind's the only home I've known

I chase the storm, I can't slow down
Never learned to stay in town
Funnel cloud and a Doppler hum
Always leaving, never come

Motel keys and a coffee stain
Following the radar's grain
She asked me once if I would stay
The sky lit up, I drove away

Maybe peace is just a place
I keep outrunning, leave no trace
One day the wind might let me be
Till then the storm is all of me

I chase the storm, I can't slow down
Never learned to stay in town
Funnel cloud and a Doppler hum
Always leaving, never come

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Wheels on the boulevard, low and slow
Past the only window I used to know
The light's still amber, somebody's there
Living my old life like I was never there

Driving past, midnight glide
Ghost of me on the other side
Foot on the gas but the heart's in park
Rolling past your window in the dark

The bassline thumping like my younger days
The streetlamp catching all the old ways
I could pull over, I could ring the bell
But some sweet stories you don't retell

Driving past, midnight glide
Ghost of me on the other side
Foot on the gas but the heart's in park
Rolling past your window in the dark

Green light, go, let the past stay past
Some grooves are too good and they don't last
I tip my hat to the second floor
And let the funk carry me out the door

Driving past, midnight glide
Ghost of me on the other side

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I knelt in empty churches with a hollow in my chest
The prayers fell off my tongue and died like embers in the west
The steeple lost its meaning and the hymns became a noise
I walked out into winter and I gave away my voice

The silence grew, the silence grew
Until I couldn't feel the truth
But something kept on breathing under everything I knew

The years rolled like a glacier over everything I'd been
I looked up at the mountains and the light came pouring in
Not a thunder, not a banner, just a quiet open door
The thing I thought I'd buried was alive at my core

And it builds, and it builds, and it rises like a tide
Every note a little louder than the doubt I tried to hide
The guitars are climbing higher than the dark I left behind
What I lost was never gone, it was waiting for my mind

The light came through, the light came through
And I could finally feel the truth
The thing I lost was leading me right back to something new

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We sailed from Galway in the gray of dawn
With a hold full of crates and a captain gone wrong
The priest would not bless us, the harbor went still
And the old women whispered of sorrow and ill

Heave away, heave away, on the cold black tide
With a cursed cargo bound to the other side
The sea wants its payment, the wind sings the toll
God have mercy on every poor soul

The fiddle plays low when the fog rolls in deep
And no man aboard her has stomach to sleep
A candle keeps lighting itself in the dark
And a wail in the timbers, a tooth and a mark

Heave away, heave away, on the cold black tide
With a cursed cargo bound to the other side
The sea wants its payment, the wind sings the toll
God have mercy on every poor soul

We'll bury the cargo where no cross is found
And pray that the curse goes down with the ground
But the tide it remembers and the tide it will keep
The debt that we owe to the merciless deep

Heave away, heave away, on the cold black tide
With a cursed cargo bound to the other side

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Neon bleeding on the rain
Strangers running, missed the train
You pulled me under, breathless, fast
One small shelter in the blast

Under one umbrella, racing through the night
Two hearts hammer faster than the strobing light
Don't know your name but I know your pace
Running through the downpour, face to face

Puddles flashing back the sky
Feet in rhythm, you and I
The city pulsing, wet and bright
Two unknowns against the night

Under one umbrella, racing through the night
Two hearts hammer faster than the strobing light
Don't know your name but I know your pace
Running through the downpour, face to face

Drop hits hard and the world dissolves
Into bass and breath and unspoken vows
We'll part at the corner where the signal turns
But tonight the whole rain-soaked city burns

Under one umbrella, racing through the night
Two hearts hammer faster than the strobing light

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I light the lamp at evening when the gulls have gone to rest
And I write you in the logbook all the words I love you best
My dearest dark Atlantic, my forever and my foe
I've no other love to speak of in this lonely place I know

So I'll send my letters to the sea
Folded in the foam where the wild waves be
I have loved you longer than the tide has rolled
My salt-and-silver darling, my gray and my gold

The winter storms have torn me but I never turned away
I kept the windows burning through the worst the night could say
They think me mad for staying when the village lights below
But you are all the company a keeper needs to know

When I am old and failing and the lamp is in new hands
Scatter me where the breakers fall and pull me from the sands
For every letter that I wrote was just a way to say
I'll love you past my dying and my keeping fade away

So I'll send my letters to the sea
Folded in the foam where the wild waves be
I have loved you longer than the tide has rolled
My salt-and-silver darling, my gray and my gold

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Three mason jars wrapped up in flannel shirts
Riding shotgun where my brother used to sit
The county line was twelve more miles of dirt
And the gauge said empty, but I swore she'd make it yet

Deputy Hollis had a porch light and a gun
Knew my daddy's still by the smell of copper smoke
I killed the headlamps, let the engine run
Prayed to a God I hadn't talked to since I broke

Oh the creek runs clear but the liquor runs blind
I traded my granddad's name for a fifty-dollar shine
Now there's blue in the mirror and white on the road
And a jar in the ditch where the still water flowed

Hit the cattle gate doing sixty in the rain
Glass and corn whiskey spilling down the hill
I walked home barefoot dragging half my name
While the embers of the truck lit the bottom of the mill

Momma never asked where the money came
She just sewed the holes and never said his name
Now I sew my own and I drink the rest
With a scar on my jaw where the windshield kissed my chest

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Back room of the Laredo, ceiling fan half dead
Four men and a bottle and a debt I should have paid
I laid my truck keys flat against the felt instead
And Tucker grinned the way a copperhead is made

We were playing for matchsticks at a quarter to ten
We were betting our houses by a quarter to three
Somebody flipped a queen and somebody reached again
And the only thing folded was the man next to me

I had jacks over sevens and a steady enough hand
Tucker had a derringer he kept beneath his hat
When the river came up empty he stood and made his stand
And the deal went sideways where the lamplight fell flat

They say luck is a lady but she never learned my street
She just counts the cards and leaves you cold
I walked out owing nothing but the boots upon my feet
And a story I'm too sober now to hold

We were playing for matchsticks at a quarter to ten
We were burying a friend by the first gray light
Somebody flipped a queen and won't flip one again
And the chips are still sitting where he left them last night

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Downhill from the mountain in the cool of the day
Jar of white lightning in a basket of cane
Babylon a-watch from the corner cafe
Me heart beat a riddim in the warm island rain

Run come, run go, but the run gone wrong
Spill the sweet fire on the gravel and the stone
What me carry in the basket couldn't carry me home
Now me walking the long road, walking alone

Police truck idle by the old banyan tree
Smell the proof a-rising like a ghost on the breeze
Me drop the whole basket, let it shatter, let it free
And the river took the liquor down between the trees

Granny used to tell me, son, the still it is a snake
It feed you in the dry time but it bite you for the take
Me no listen to the warning, now me know what it mean
The sweetest little money buy the bitterest routine

Run come, run go, but the run gone wrong
Me trade a week of freedom for a sip and a song
What me carry in the basket couldn't carry me home
So me singing this riddim till the trouble move along

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They offered me a podium, a check with too many ohs
Wanted forty stanzas for a jingle about shoes
I thanked them for the coffee and the cold from the doors
And I walked back to the bus stop in my one good pair of blues

I'd rather write for nobody on the back of a receipt
Than trade my crooked verses for a clean and quiet street
They can buy the printing press, they can buy the whole machine
But they can't read the line I keep beneath the floorboard, clean

The landlord wants his envelope, the editor wants blood
The critics want a younger man with prettier despair
I keep a stub of pencil and a notebook in the mud
And the pigeons get my readings in the gray December air

My father sold his shoulders to a foreman with a clock
Came home each night a little less, a little more unwound
So if I starve a poet, at least the door's not locked
And the words I didn't sell still know the sound of solid ground

I'd rather write for nobody on the back of a receipt
Than rent my open vowels to the marketplace and bleat
They can buy the printing press, they can buy the whole machine
But they'll never own the silence where the honest lines convene

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Slick man rolled up with a contract in his teeth
Gold chain swinging like a metronome of greed
Said baby write me something that'll move a little merch
I said baby keep your bass, I got a different kind of beat

I won't sell it, won't sell it, no
My verses got a backbone and my backbone got a flow
You can shake the whole building, you can light the whole row
But the truth in my pocket ain't a thing that's for show

They want it clean and easy, they want it twenty-two and dumb
Want a hook you can swallow like a stick of bubblegum
I got grit in my consonants, I got static in my thumb
And I'm dropping every syllable as heavy as a drum

Momma worked the night shift, kept the rhythm in the house
Taught me money talks but soul will sing it down and out
So I'm laying in the pocket where the cowards never go
With a rhyme they couldn't buy if they emptied the whole row

I won't sell it, won't sell it, no
My verses got a backbone and my backbone got a flow
You can offer me a kingdom, you can offer me the throne
But the truth in my pocket is the only thing I own

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
Four in the morning, empty church of the dance
I came in hollow with my hands hanging low
Lost the thing I prayed to in a parking lot trance
Somewhere between the steeple and the radio

Then the kick drum found me like a heartbeat in the dark
And I lifted up my hands, lifted up my hands
Every stranger in the room was a little burning spark
And I believed again, oh I believed again

The preacher said the body was a temple made of sin
So I left it on the corner of the avenue and shame
But the bassline came and gathered all the pieces back in
And a thousand sweating sinners sang a holier name

No collection plate, no fear of the flame
Just the low end shaking loose the doubt
I don't need a name for the thing I reclaim
When the lights go down and the love comes out

Then the kick drum found me like a heartbeat in the dark
And I lifted up my hands, lifted up my hands
Every stranger in the room was a little burning spark
And I believed again, oh I believed again

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Seventeen and shaking with a borrowed guitar
Twelve folding chairs and a flickering sign
I forgot the second verse, lost it under the bar
But a girl in the back row mouthed the line

Turn it up, turn it back, to the night I first stepped out
To the mic and the hum and the beautiful doubt
I was nothing, I was nobody, I was loud as the rain
And I'd give the whole city to be that scared again

The owner only paid me in a basket of fries
The sound man fell asleep on the board halfway through
But I felt the room turn toward me like a sunrise
And I knew somewhere inside me that the rumor was true

Now I fill the bigger rooms, now they know my name
But nothing hits the chest like the very first flame
So press play on the memory, let the synth wash it down
To a kid in a coffee shop on the edge of the town

Turn it up, turn it back, to the night I first stepped out
To the mic and the hum and the beautiful doubt
I was nothing, I was nobody, I was loud as the rain
And I'd give the whole city to be that scared again

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew at noon and it never blew again
Forty years of iron and the doors went still
Reb Mendel hung his apron on the rusted oxygen
And the clarinet was crying on the windowsill

Dance, you tired hands, one more turn round the floor
The foreman's locked the gate but the song is still ours
Laugh so the weeping won't break down the door
And we'll spin like the dust in the dying afternoon hours

My grandfather riveted the beams of this town
My father swept the floor where the molten rivers ran
Now the buyers from the city come and tear the chimney down
And they pave a quiet parking lot where the union began

A freylekhs for the firemen, a tear for the line
The fiddle takes a high note and it holds and it holds
We were never just the wages, we were never just the time
We were music in the marrow that the ledger never told

Dance, you tired hands, one more turn round the floor
The foreman's locked the gate but the song is still ours
Laugh so the weeping won't break down the door
And we'll spin like the dust in the dying afternoon hours

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
The streetlight on the corner only worked on the odd nights
We counted passing trains instead of counting sheep
The map of our whole world was four cracked stop signs
And a promise that we whispered but were too scared to keep

But the sky split open at the edge of the lot
And the road learned my name, and the road learned my name
Everything I left was everything I'd got
And I burned like the morning, and I'll never be the same

Danny stayed behind with the engine and the rust
Maria caught a Greyhound with a duffel and a prayer
I walked out at sunrise through the diesel and the dust
And the block grew small behind me in the rearview glare

No words now, just the rising of the strings
The drums find the pulse of a runaway heart
This is the sound of a body that the concrete couldn't keep
This is the swell where the rest of the life can start

And the sky split open at the edge of the lot
And the road learned my name, and the road learned my name
Everything I left was everything I'd got
And I burned like the morning, and I'll never be the same

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I slid out the side door past the offering plate
My Sunday shoes still squeaking on the laminate floor
Nobody saw me go, it was already late
And the hymn kept rising as I touched the door

Quiet, keep it quiet, let the organ swallow the sound
Of a kid losing heaven on the way to the ground
I'm running through the rain where the streetlights drown
And the bassline's the only amen I have found

They'll notice the empty space in the third left pew
Maybe Tuesday, maybe never, when they call the roll
I memorized the verses and I wore them through
Now the breakbeat's doing surgery on the shape of my soul

No lightning came, no thunder, no hand reached down
Just the hush of the carpet and the cold front door
I carry all the kindness, I leave the crown
And the rhythm keeps the faith I couldn't keep anymore

Quiet, keep it quiet, let the organ swallow the sound
Of a kid losing heaven on the way to the ground
I'm running through the rain where the streetlights drown
And the bassline's the only amen I have found

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Same hour every night I lift up off the bed
The ceiling turns to open and the open turns to sky
My grandmother's laundry line goes streaming overhead
And the whole tired city shrinks beneath my eye

And I rise, oh I rise, with my arms thrown wide
Over rooftops, over rivers, over everything I tried
Don't wake me, don't wake me, let me stay up here tonight
Where the weight I carry daylight turns to light

Down there is the foreman and the clock I have to punch
Down there is the rent and the letter I won't read
Up here there's just the wind making music of my lungs
And a freedom that the morning's always quick to take from me

My mother says a flying dream means trouble's letting go
My preacher says it's angels giving rest
I don't need a meaning for the only place I know
Where the chains slide off my shoulders and the silence does the rest

And I rise, oh I rise, with my arms thrown wide
Over rooftops, over rivers, over everything I tried
Don't wake me, don't wake me, let me stay up here tonight
Where the weight I carry daylight turns to light

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They poured the foundations on the curve of his spine
Laid the subway like a vein through the hollow of his chest
We sip our morning coffee on a fault that's half divine
And we call the tremors traffic, and we go back to rest

Sleep, sleep, giant underneath the avenue
We built our whole religion on the rhythm of you
One day you'll roll your shoulder and the towers will bow
But tonight we are dancing on the slow rise and fall of your brow

The old men in the barbershop can feel him in their knees
The dogs go quiet early on the nights before he stirs
We gilded all his fingertips with parking lots and trees
And we never said thank you to the ground that was his

Maybe he's dreaming us, maybe we're the dream
Maybe the river is the sweat of his sleep
Either way the streetlights flicker like a seam
On the edge of a promise that we're too small to keep

Sleep, sleep, giant underneath the avenue
We built our whole religion on the rhythm of you
One day you'll roll your shoulder and the towers will bow
But tonight we are dancing on the slow rise and fall of your brow

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock out from the warehouse, clock in at the bar
The second shift starts where the first one ends
I keep my eyes from closing with the radio and a car
That's running on a prayer and the kindness of friends

Two jobs, one heart, and the math never lands
The rent climbs a ladder that I'm holding with both hands
I'm running through molasses just to stay where I stand
And the dollar slips like water through the cracks in my plans

The baby's got a fever and the heater's nearly gone
The foreman cut my hours but he never cut the speed
I count the change in coffee cups to make it to the dawn
And I dream of one slow Sunday I will probably never need

They told me work was holy, told me sweat was the way
But nobody mentioned how the ladder grows a rung
For every step I climb at the closing of the day
So I sing this to my kids while their version's still young

Two jobs, one heart, and the math never lands
The rent climbs a ladder that I'm holding with both hands
I'm running through molasses just to stay where I stand
And the dollar slips like water through the cracks in my plans

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
The sign hums pink at the edge of the interstate
Coffee black as the highway, pie under glass
Lou's been pouring refills since they paved the eight
And the clock on the wall lets the lonely hours pass

We're open all night, we're open all night
Under the buzz of the fluorescent light
Whatever you're running from, whatever's not right
There's a booth and a bottomless cup till the morning's in sight

The trucker with the photograph he won't put away
The nurse coming down from a twelve-hour war
The kid who can't go home and the man with nothing to say
We're all just regulars knocking on the same all-night door

Three a.m. is honest in a way the daylight ain't
The jukebox plays the songs that nobody requests
And Lou wipes the counter like she's polishing a saint
While the lost get a little bit of warm, and a little bit of rest

We're open all night, we're open all night
Under the buzz of the fluorescent light
Whatever you're running from, whatever's not right
There's a booth and a bottomless cup till the morning's in sight

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
The big shots came a-calling in their two-tone shoes
Said kid we'll make you famous if you soften up the tune
Write us something pretty that the masses cannot lose
I tipped my hat and told them I'd be busy until June

No sir, no sir, I won't water down the wine
I'd rather swing for nickels than get rich on a lie
You can keep your shiny office and your dotted-line design
I'm a verse with a horn section and I'm doing fine

They wanted thirty-two bars about a girl named Lou
Who'd sell a million records if she never said a thing
I gave them all my pencil shavings, told them what to do
And I danced out to the alley where the honest fellas sing

The trumpet knows a secret that the contract never learned
That a true note can't be bought at any price
So I'm spending all my rhythm on the freedom that I've earned
And I'll take my standing ovation in a roll of the dice

No sir, no sir, I won't water down the wine
I'd rather swing for nickels than get rich on a lie
You can keep your shiny office and your dotted-line design
I'm a verse with a horn section and I'm doing fine

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Neon dust on a borrowed floor
I don't know your name, don't need it anymore
The band's a blur, the lights all bleed
You took my hand like you read my need

Spin me slow in the haze, oh
A stranger in the swirl of the strobe
We're nobody here, we're nobody's own
Dancing till the morning's overgrown

Champagne fog and a stranger's eyes
We move like ghosts the song can't recognize
Your shoulder warm against my chin
The walls dissolve and let the static in

Spin me slow in the haze, oh
A stranger in the swirl of the strobe
We're nobody here, we're nobody's own
Dancing till the morning's overgrown

When the last guest fades away
Will you remember how we swayed
A half-lit picture, soft and gone
Two strangers in the wash of dawn

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
We met where the boardwalk meets the tide
Salt in your hair and the sun for a guide
I carved our names in the driftwood grain
Knowing the water would take them again

Oh summer love, you burn so bright
A paper lantern on a warm June night
You'll be gone when the leaves turn brown
But you're mine while the sun stays down

Barefoot dancing on a quiet shore
We swore forever like the kids before
The seagulls laughed at the words we said
With the whole bright ocean overhead

Oh summer love, you burn so bright
A paper lantern on a warm June night
You'll be gone when the leaves turn brown
But you're mine while the sun stays down

September's coming with the cooling rain
And I won't ask you to stay the same
Just let me keep this golden day
Before the tide pulls it away

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Un corazón de papel, mi amor
Se rompió con la luz de un teléfono
No viniste a decirme adiós
Solo un mensaje, frío y veloz

Ay, ay, ay, qué dolor
Una pantalla mató nuestro amor
Las trompetas lloran por ti
Y el mariachi canta así

Guardé las rosas que te di
Marchitas como el sí que no cumpliste
Tres palabras y nada más
Y se fue todo lo que dejas atrás

Ay, ay, ay, qué dolor
Una pantalla mató nuestro amor
Las trompetas lloran por ti
Y el mariachi canta así

Brindo solo con tequila y sal
Por un amor que no supo hablar
Que la noche me cante mejor
Que ese mensaje sin corazón

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Keys in the ignition, heart on the floor
One more run and I'm done for sure
Mirrorball city, the lights all chase
Gotta keep the rhythm, gotta keep the pace

Drive, drive, this is the last ride
Neon in the rearview, nowhere to hide
Four on the floor and the engine sings
Last job, baby, before the morning brings

They said easy money, said in and out
But the sirens got a different route
Foot to the pedal, the bassline pumps
Feel the whole street move in funky jumps

Drive, drive, this is the last ride
Neon in the rearview, nowhere to hide
Four on the floor and the engine sings
Last job, baby, before the morning brings

When the dawn comes I'll be far away
No more running, no more getaway
Just a disco fading in the haze
And the open road for the rest of my days

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle and thread in a small lit room
He stitches the night, he tailors the gloom
Every suit on the avenue
Was cut by his hands, was made by his too

Dance, dance in the cloth he sews
The whole block wears the heart he knows
A tango woven, tight and true
The tailor dresses me and you

The widow's veil and the groom's lapel
He knows our sizes, he knows us well
A fold, a seam, a secret pleat
He dresses the dancers of the street

Dance, dance in the cloth he sews
The whole block wears the heart he knows
A tango woven, tight and true
The tailor dresses me and you

And when he's gone who'll take the chalk
Who'll cut the cloth for the whole block
We'll wear his work till the threads run thin
And dance the tango he sewed us in

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Una baraja sobre la mesa
Dedos temblando, la noche no cesa
Aposté mi anillo, mi orgullo, mi voz
Y el rey de espadas me dijo adiós

Olé, las cartas no perdonan
Las guitarras lloran y las palmas resuenan
Fui demasiado lejos, lo perdí todo
En un juego que ardió como el fuego rojo

El as escondido bajo la manga
La traición que el corazón no aguanta
Golpeé la mesa, grité tu nombre
Y el silencio fue mi único hombre

Olé, las cartas no perdonan
Las guitarras lloran y las palmas resuenan
Fui demasiado lejos, lo perdí todo
En un juego que ardió como el fuego rojo

Manos al cielo, taconeo en el suelo
Perdí la apuesta pero guardo el duelo
Que suene la guitarra, que arda el compás
Un juego de cartas que no vuelve atrás

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Radar on the dashboard, coffee gone cold
Chasing funnel clouds, never doing what I'm told
Green sky on the horizon, that's my only home
Wife says stay but I was born to roam
Thunder in my chest like a 808
Can't sit still, gotta meet my fate
Mileage on the truck, dust on the lens
Every town I leave is a town I won't see again

I chase the storm, I can't slow down
Got a restless heart, can't settle down
Where the wind goes, that's where I'll be
The twister and the road, they're all I need

Mama said you'll never plant your feet
I said the calm scares me more than the heat
Hail on the windshield, sound like applause
I'm married to the chaos, married to the cause

I chase the storm, I can't slow down
Got a restless heart, can't settle down
Where the wind goes, that's where I'll be
The twister and the road, they're all I need

Maybe one day I'll park it in the rain
Let the quiet settle in my veins
But till the last cloud rolls away
I'm chasing, chasing, every day

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down on a Tuesday gray
You had no cover, I had no way
But one umbrella between two coats
And a city full of empty notes

So we walked, we walked, shoulder to shoulder
The sky kept falling but I felt bolder
One umbrella, two strangers' hands
The rain wrote a story neither of us planned

You laughed at the puddles, I learned your name
We took the long way, we played the game
Half of me soaked but I didn't care
There was something electric in the wet gray air

So we walked, we walked, shoulder to shoulder
The sky kept falling but I felt bolder
One umbrella, two strangers' hands
The rain wrote a story neither of us planned

When the clouds broke up and the sun came through
I didn't want to say goodbye to you
So let it pour, let it pour all night
Keep us under this little patch of light

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The door clicked shut and the room went still
Words like broken glass on the windowsill
I hear you breathing through the wall
The loudest silence of them all

In the hush, in the haze, after it all
I'm reaching for you through the static wall
The quiet hums where the shouting was
And I still love you, just because

The clock is ticking, the kettle's cold
We said the things we can't unfold
But underneath the ringing tone
I don't want to be alone

In the hush, in the haze, after it all
I'm reaching for you through the static wall
The quiet hums where the shouting was
And I still love you, just because

Come lie beside me, don't say a word
Let the quiet undo what the anger blurred
The storm has passed, the air is thin
Let the soft and the sorry back in

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete river where the meadow used to flow
One green soldier standing, won't let go
They paved the garden, paved the stream
But the last tree's holding on to the dream

Oh, last tree, keep on standing tall
Roots beneath the city, gonna outlast them all
Sing it, last tree, in the gray and the gray
Don't let them take your green away

Birds got nowhere left to land
But they rest on the branches of the promised land
Children never seen a leaf turn gold
Gather round the story that the old tree told

Oh, last tree, keep on standing tall
Roots beneath the city, gonna outlast them all
Sing it, last tree, in the gray and the gray
Don't let them take your green away

Jah plant a seed and the seed gonna grow
Crack the pavement, let the people know
One tree standing can wake a town
Green gonna rise when the gray comes down

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Lights were low and the band was hot
I gave it everything I got
One more number, one more chance
The whole room caught up in the dance

Oh, it was the night I almost made it
So close I could taste it, baby, I could taste it
The spotlight winked and the crowd stood tall
Then the curtain fell and that was all

A scout in the back with a fancy pen
Said kid, you're good, but not just then
I tipped my hat and I took the blow
And swung right back into the show

Oh, it was the night I almost made it
So close I could taste it, baby, I could taste it
The spotlight winked and the crowd stood tall
Then the curtain fell and that was all

But I'd do it again, every beat, every bar
For that one shining moment I touched a star
Almost ain't nothing, almost ain't shame
I almost, I almost, I almost made my name

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Soft as the rain on a Rio street
You slipped away on quiet feet
I hum the song we used to know
While the ocean breathes so slow

You're the one, the one that got away
A gentle wave that couldn't stay
And I sway, and I sway in the warm night air
Missing the love that was never there

Green of the sea and the gold of your eyes
A samba whisper, a soft goodbye
I should have held you, should have known
But the tide took you all alone

You're the one, the one that got away
A gentle wave that couldn't stay
And I sway, and I sway in the warm night air
Missing the love that was never there

Maybe somewhere you still dance slow
To a quiet song from long ago
And if the breeze should carry my name
Know I'd love you all over again

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Well I packed up my bags and I hit the road
With a camera and a heart that won't be slowed
The sky turns green and I start to grin
Here comes the twister, let the chase begin

I'm a storm chaser, baby, I can't sit still
Got wind in my shoes and a restless thrill
Don't ask me to stay, don't ask me to slow
When the thunder calls, well, I gotta go

They say settle down, find a quiet life
Get a little house and a steady wife
But a quiet sky makes me come undone
Give me clouds and the roll of a rising sun

I'm a storm chaser, baby, I can't sit still
Got wind in my shoes and a restless thrill
Don't ask me to stay, don't ask me to slow
When the thunder calls, well, I gotta go

Swing me round in the howling gale
Dance with me down the tornado trail
Maybe love's just another front rolling through
But till it lands I'll keep chasing the blue

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Dejé mi tierra con la luna en la maleta
Un boleto, una promesa, una silueta
El mar es grande pero grande es mi fe
Cruzando las olas pa' un mañana que no sé

Me voy, me voy, cruzando el mar
Con el corazón pesa'o pero sin parar
Dame, dame fuerza pa' llegar
Al otro lado donde pueda respirar

Mami no llores que yo vuelvo, te lo juro
Construyo un camino sobre el agua y el futuro
La salida es dura pero el sueño es real
Un barco, una estrella, y un final

Me voy, me voy, cruzando el mar
Con el corazón pesa'o pero sin parar
Dame, dame fuerza pa' llegar
Al otro lado donde pueda respirar

De noche el horizonte me habla bajito
Dice sigue, sigue, ya casi, poquito
Tierra nueva, mismo cielo, misma sal
Llevo mi bandera en lo más hondo, na' me pal'

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Same old streetlights, same old sky
You were always there, I just walked on by
Now your hand finds mine on the road we know
And the whole town glows in a softer glow

Falling, falling, right back home
With the one I never knew I'd known
The stars are the same but they shine brand new
Falling in my hometown, falling for you

We drove past the diner where we both grew up
Spilled our hearts in a paper cup
The summer hums in the warm porch light
And everything ordinary feels right

Falling, falling, right back home
With the one I never knew I'd known
The stars are the same but they shine brand new
Falling in my hometown, falling for you

Funny how forever was always near
Just a backroad away, just a quiet year
No need to wander, no need to roam
Love was waiting for me back home

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Woke before the thunder, packed my boots and ran
Girl you held my collar but I slipped your hand
Lagos to the lightning, I don follow rain
Every time you call me I'm a road again

I dey chase the storm, storm, storm
Cannot stay where the sky stay calm
If the wind no blow I no fit be born
Baby I'm a cloud, I no get a home

You build me a bedroom, paint am soft and blue
But the radar humming and I'm gone by two
Dust upon the dashboard, prayer upon my tongue
I was made for moving since the day I'm young

I dey chase the storm, storm, storm
Cannot stay where the sky stay calm
If the wind no blow I no fit be born
Baby I'm a cloud, I no get a home

Maybe one day rain go fall and I go rest
Lay my tired thunder right upon your chest
But tonight the heavens dey crack open wide
And something in my chest say ride, ride, ride

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
The sky cracked open on a Tuesday street
You pulled me close and we shared the heat
One little canopy, two pairs of shoes
Dancing through the puddles with the downtown blues

Under one umbrella, just you and me
Strangers in the storm and the city's our balcony
Swing me through the raindrops, spin me through the gray
Under one umbrella we'll waltz the rain away

I don't know your name and you don't know mine
But your laugh's a saxophone, smooth and fine
A taxi splashed a melody across our feet
And suddenly the puddles got a heartbeat

Under one umbrella, just you and me
Strangers in the storm and the city's our balcony
Swing me through the raindrops, spin me through the gray
Under one umbrella we'll waltz the rain away

The shower's gonna stop and the sun's gonna shine
And you'll go your way and I'll go mine
But for three minutes in the falling weather
Two perfect strangers stayed dry together

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Two in the morning and my thumb won't quit
Screen glowing blue in the dark of it
I know your name's a door I locked
But here I am still picking the lock

Delete it, delete it, don't press send
This is a road with a burning end
But the message is typed and my heart's on fire
One more text and I cross the wire

Three little dots and they pulse like blood
I'm standing at the edge of a coming flood
You said don't, you said stop, you said leave it alone
But the phone's in my hand like a heart of stone

Delete it, delete it, don't press send
This is a road with a burning end
But the message is typed and my heart's on fire
One more text and I cross the wire

The bass drops out and it's only me
And the choice I make in the gravity
Screen goes dark, then it lights again
God help me, I just pressed send

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the bricks on a beating chest
Never knew what slept beneath the rest
Every streetlight, every prayer, every song
Humming on a heart a mile long

Oh, the city sleeps on a giant's bones
We build our love on his quiet groans
Don't wake him now, let him dream below
While we dance on the streets that he'll never know

Mama warned me in a lullaby
Don't stomp too loud or the ground might sigh
We pour the concrete, we ring the bells
Over a sleeper and the stories he tells

Oh, the city sleeps on a giant's bones
We build our love on his quiet groans
Don't wake him now, let him dream below
While we dance on the streets that he'll never know

What happens, baby, when the giant turns?
When the deep heart wakes and the deep heart yearns?
We'll fall like leaves, we'll scatter like rain
So love me tonight before he breathes again

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Saw your face in a crowd that wasn't you
Same perfume on a stranger walking through
I had your number, never made the call
Now I'm replaying every detail, every all

You're the one, the one that got away
The yes I turned into a maybe someday
I held the door but I let you fall
And now I'm reaching for nothing at all

We were young and I thought we had the time
Like forever was a thing I could climb
But you packed your patience and you walked out slow
And I was too proud to say don't go

You're the one, the one that got away
The yes I turned into a maybe someday
I held the door but I let you fall
And now I'm reaching for nothing at all

If I could rewind to that rainy day
I'd grab your hand, I'd find a way
But time only moves the one direction
And you're a ghost in my reflection

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Dialed the number I still know by heart
Seven years of silence pulling us apart
The phone rang out like a tide coming in
And I forgot where to even begin

Hello, it's me, it's been so long
I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I was wrong
Your voice through the static, soft and small
Ma, I'm still your kid after all

I built a wall out of pride and pain
Let the years pile up like falling rain
You never stopped leaving the porch light on
Waiting up for a boy long gone

Hello, it's me, it's been so long
I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I was wrong
Your voice through the static, soft and small
Ma, I'm still your kid after all

The walls dissolve in a wash of sound
Everything I buried coming unwound
We don't say much, we just breathe the line
Two hearts learning how to be fine

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Sunlight came in like it didn't know
That the world had ended hours ago
The kettle whistled, the birds still sang
While my whole chest was an empty hang

How does the morning dare to come
With you gone and the day undone
The coffee's warm and the sky is blue
And everything's wrong without you

Folded the black clothes, put them away
Found your handwriting on a tray
A grocery list for a week you'd never see
Milk and bread and a note to me

How does the morning dare to come
With you gone and the day undone
The coffee's warm and the sky is blue
And everything's wrong without you

Somewhere a clock just keeps on ticking
Somewhere a flower keeps on living
And I float through the rooms like smoke and lace
Learning the shape of an empty space

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
Well the band struck up and they pushed us out
Me and the girl I can't stand, no doubt
She stepped on my toe and I called her a name
But the music kept playing just the same

Sha-la-la, we're dancing, dancing
Two old rivals slowly advancing
I hate your guts but I love this song
So hold me close while the night goes long

She rolled her eyes and I cracked a grin
My hand on her waist and her chin up in
The whole gym watching us spin around
Two sworn enemies, lost and found

Sha-la-la, we're dancing, dancing
Two old rivals slowly advancing
I hate your guts but I love this song
So hold me close while the night goes long

Funny how the music makes you soften
Funny how I'm wishing this song goes often
Maybe hate was just a clumsy start
Sha-la-la, you stole my heart

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
The harbor swallowed the only home I knew
Mother's hand let go as the whistle blew
A suitcase full of a hundred years
And a coat too thin for the road of tears

Across the water, a name I can't pronounce
Across the water, where I don't count
I'll build a life from the wreck of the wave
And send back light from the world I crave

The ocean rolled like a turning page
Gray on gray in a salt-soaked cage
I memorized the shape of the leaving land
Till it sank like a coin in the ocean's hand

Across the water, a name I can't pronounce
Across the water, where I don't count
I'll build a life from the wreck of the wave
And send back light from the world I crave

And the sky cracks open, and the strings rise high
And the whole gray heaven begins to cry
I am nobody yet, I am everything still
A single light on a distant hill

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Los tambores marchando down Rampart Street
A coffin up high to a heavy beat
We cried in the church but we dance in the road
Letting go of the soul, letting go of the load

Dale, dale, the saints go marching
Brass in the air and the whole block starching
We bury you sad but we send you proud
Baila, mi amor, in the second line crowd

Umbrella up white in the August sun
Grief turning gold when the song's begun
Trumpet crying sweet over Tremé stone
Nobody walks to the grave alone

Dale, dale, the saints go marching
Brass in the air and the whole block starching
We bury you sad but we send you proud
Baila, mi amor, in the second line crowd

Death, you don't win on a day like this
We answer your silence with a horn and a kiss
Sweat on the snare and the bass drum's boom
Life throwing a party right over the tomb

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
The tether snapped on a silent night
You floating slow toward the morning light
I reached my glove but the dark was wide
And the stars just hummed as we both did slide

Oy, my love, you're a fading spark
Dancing away in the velvet dark
The clarinet cries where the engines moan
Two little candles drifting alone

We danced in orbit, a wedding round
No up, no down, and no solid ground
Now the radio crackles your fading tune
Waltzing me past the broken moon

Oy, my love, you're a fading spark
Dancing away in the velvet dark
The clarinet cries where the engines moan
Two little candles drifting alone

Freylekh and bitter, the fiddle spins
This is how a goodbye begins
Kick up your heels in the airless deep
Dance, my darling, into the sleep

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We were swinging through the Milky Way
Clipped together at the break of day
Then a little jolt and the line went slack
And baby, there's no swinging back

Drifting, drifting, cheek to the stars
You going to Venus, me past Mars
Give me one last spin in the cosmic blue
Before the distance swallows you

Your helmet glints like a chandelier
Farther every second, less and less near
I'd cross the void for one more dance
But physics won't give us the chance

Drifting, drifting, cheek to the stars
You going to Venus, me past Mars
Give me one last spin in the cosmic blue
Before the distance swallows you

So I'll tip my hat to the fading light
And jitterbug alone tonight
The band plays on though the floor's the sky
Swing me sweet, baby, and say goodbye

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Born on a street where the streetlights die
Boarded-up churches and a bruise-black sky
Mama said son, there's a gate in the fence
Climb it tonight 'fore you lose your sense

I'm getting out, I'm getting gone
Off this block before the dawn
With a busted bag and a borrowed prayer
Lord, I'm crawling out of here

Buried my brother by the old rail line
Left a bottle and a cross of pine
I won't be the next stone in that row
So I'm walking where the cold winds blow

I'm getting out, I'm getting gone
Off this block before the dawn
With a busted bag and a borrowed prayer
Lord, I'm crawling out of here

The crows on the wire all watched me leave
Thorns tore a promise in my ragged sleeve
Don't look back at the burning door
That dead-end block won't hold me no more

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Got my baby girl behind the wheel
Hands at ten and two, learning how it feel
She stalled it twice on the gravel road
Grippin' that stick like a heavy load

Ease off the clutch now, give it some gas
Don't ride my nerves and don't ride that grass
You're growing up faster than this old Ford
Drive, baby, drive, with the good Lord aboard

Same seat I held her car seat tight
Now she's checking the mirror, getting it right
My little girl with her foot on the brake
Lord, my heart's a fool for her sake

Ease off the clutch now, give it some gas
Don't ride my nerves and don't ride that grass
You're growing up faster than this old Ford
Drive, baby, drive, with the good Lord aboard

One day soon she'll drive on out of town
Taillights fading where the sun goes down
But tonight she's mine on this dusty mile
Learning the road with her daddy's smile

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
I came alone to my cousin's day
Didn't know a soul, gonna slip away
Then a hand reached out from across the floor
And a smile I'd never seen before

Dance with me, stranger, under the lights
The Lord sends joy on the sweetest nights
We don't know names but we know this song
So praise His name and dance along

The band struck up an old hymn sweet
And the whole room rose to its dancing feet
Your palm in mine and we spun like grace
Two lonely hearts in a holy place

Dance with me, stranger, under the lights
The Lord sends joy on the sweetest nights
We don't know names but we know this song
So praise His name and dance along

Maybe Heaven planned this all along
Two empty chairs and a wedding song
Glory, glory, I came in alone
But I won't leave this night on my own

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew, the gangway dropped, my duffel on my back
Three long years of mud and smoke behind me on that track
I tipped my hat to the harbor lights all shining in a row
There's a girl beneath a lamppost and she's waiting, don't you know

So swing me round, the band's in town, I'm marching to a different drum
No more bugle in the morning, baby, look at what I've become
I traded boots for dancing shoes, I'm light as I can be
Got my two feet on home soil and my best girl next to me

The trumpet's hot, the bass is walking, brushes on the snare
I count the steps I dreamed about in foxholes over there
My mother's got the kettle on, my pop's got tears to hide
And I'm spinning like a record on the sweet side of the tide

There were nights I thought the morning was a promise I'd have lost
Now the only fire I'm under is the bandstand and the cost
Is a dollar at the door and every ache I left behind
Lord, I'm home, I'm home, I'm home, and the rhythm's been so kind

So swing me round, the band's in town, I'm marching to a different drum
No more bugle in the morning, baby, look at what I've become
I traded boots for dancing shoes, I'm light as I can be
Got my two feet on home soil and my best girl next to me

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
There's a bell above the door on Maple Street
A red and white pole spinning in the heat
Frank's been cutting hair since '93
He knows your daddy's name, he knows your family tree

Every secret in this little town
Gets a trim and lays its burden down

Frank, oh Frank, with the scissors in your hand
You hold the whole town's stories like a map across the land
Who's in love, who's in trouble, who's been crying in the chair
Frank, oh Frank, the wisest man in town is cutting hair

The mayor sits beside the boy who mows the lawn
They both spill everything before the cape comes on
He never tells a soul what's said inside
Just nods and clips and sweeps it with the morning tide

Every worry walking through that door
Leaves it lighter than it was before

Frank, oh Frank, with the scissors in your hand
You hold the whole town's stories like a map across the land
Who's in love, who's in trouble, who's been crying in the chair
Frank, oh Frank, the wisest man in town is cutting hair

When Frank retires they'll close the street to say goodbye
The whole parade of haircuts standing teary-eyed
A hundred quiet keepers of a hundred quiet things
Waving at the keeper of the secrets that he keeps

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the avenue, the engine humming low
Took a wrong turn on purpose to a place I used to know
Third floor window, curtains new, somebody else's light
I'm just a stranger rolling slow through somebody else's night

Drive past, don't stop, let the city pull me on
Every streetlight that I pass is another year that's gone
Feel the bassline in my chest, feel the pavement underneath
I'm driving past the door I closed and I can finally breathe

Let it go, let it go, let it go tonight
Let it go, let it go, let it go

There's a ghost on the balcony where we used to stand and smoke
Laughing at the nothing in the half of words we spoke
The light turns green, my foot comes down, the memory lets me leave
I keep the window halfway open just to feel the city breathe

Drive past, don't stop, let the city pull me on
Every streetlight that I pass is another year that's gone
Feel the bassline in my chest, feel the pavement underneath
I'm driving past the door I closed and I can finally breathe

Four-on-the-floor and the night is wide
Whatever I left up there, I left it for a reason
Four-on-the-floor and the road is mine
Goodbye to the window, goodbye to the season

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
When the candle gutters low and the peat fire fades to grey
I close my weary eyes and I am carried far away
Over heather, over hill, over loch and crumbling stone
With the night wind in my collar I am never quite alone

And I rise, and I rise, like a curlew on the gale
Over every dry stone wall and every silver shining vale
Though I wake with the dawn and my two feet on the floor
Every night the sky remembers and it lifts me up once more

There's no harness and no feather, only longing in my breast
And the green earth falling under like a mother gone to rest
I have flown above my grandfather, I've flown above the sea
And I wonder if the flying is the truest part of me

And I rise, and I rise, like a curlew on the gale
Over every dry stone wall and every silver shining vale
Though I wake with the dawn and my two feet on the floor
Every night the sky remembers and it lifts me up once more

They say a dream of flying is a soul that wants to roam
But I think it's just my spirit finding one more way back home
So lay me down at evening when the last red embers die
And I'll meet you in the morning with the heather and the sky

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
(Sha-la-la) The bus rolls in at quarter past nine
(Sha-la-la) One ticket and a heart on the line
Main Street's sleeping, the diner's dark
I'm leaving footprints in the park

Goodbye, goodbye, my little hometown
Goodbye to the water tower looking down
(Doo-wop, doo-wop) I gotta go where the bright lights are
(Doo-wop) Chasing something near and far
Goodbye, goodbye, my little hometown

(Sha-la-la) Mama's hankie waving slow
(Sha-la-la) Daddy nods, he lets me go
The steeple bell, the schoolyard gate
They're all I've known and I can't be late

Goodbye, goodbye, my little hometown
Goodbye to the water tower looking down
(Doo-wop, doo-wop) I gotta go where the bright lights are
(Doo-wop) Chasing something near and far
Goodbye, goodbye, my little hometown

I'll come back someday, I swear it's true (it's true, it's true)
With a story and a dollar or two (or two, or two)
But tonight the road is calling my name
And this one-horse town won't be the same

Goodbye, goodbye, my little hometown
Goodbye to the water tower looking down
(Doo-wop, doo-wop) I gotta go where the bright lights are
Goodbye, goodbye, so long

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The lamp hums yellow on a page I've read four times
The letters swimming sideways into blurry tired lines
The building's holding its breath, the radiators sigh
And somewhere far away a train is dragging through the sky

Two in the morning and the world dissolves to sound
I'm a faded photograph that nobody around
Will ever quite develop, I'm a hum beneath the floor
Two in the morning and I don't know what I'm here for

My coffee's gone to ice, my pen has given up
There's a ringing in my ears like the bottom of a cup
The streetlight through the blinds is laying bars across my hands
And the textbook keeps on talking but it's nothing I understand

Two in the morning and the world dissolves to sound
I'm a faded photograph that nobody around
Will ever quite develop, I'm a hum beneath the floor
Two in the morning and I don't know what I'm here for

Wash it out, wash it out, let the white noise take the weight
I'll remember none of this, just the color and the late
Wash it out, wash it out, till the morning comes to stay
And the letters on the page just fade away

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
Ey, ey
Pa' ti, donde quiera que estés

Leí tu nombre en una calle que cruzamos
Y el corazón se me apretó como en los años
Te fuiste sin un adiós, sin explicación
Y yo cargué tu sombra como una condición

Pero hoy solté lo que pesaba
Hoy entendí que el rencor me mataba

Te perdono aunque ya no estés
Te perdono y por fin puedo ver
Que guardarte el dolor no te trae otra vez
Te suelto en el viento, mi amor, te perdoné
Te perdono aunque ya no estés (no, no)
Te perdono y por fin puedo ser

Me robaste las noches contando el porqué
Buscando respuestas en un cuarto sin él
Pero el odio es cadena y yo quiero correr
Así que abro la mano y te dejo caer

Te perdono aunque ya no estés
Te perdono y por fin puedo ver
Que guardarte el dolor no te trae otra vez
Te suelto en el viento, mi amor, te perdoné

Donde quiera que estés, que la paz te alcance
Yo ya no peleo con tu fantasma esta tarde
Lo que fue, fue, y lo dejo en el ayer
Libre tú, libre yo, por fin sé querer

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Key in the ignition, three minutes on the clock
Engine running ragged in the alley off the block
They said one more, just one more, and then I'm walking free
But the last job's got a way of getting hold of me

Floor it! Floor it! Don't look in the back
Rubber on the asphalt, smoke and a crack
This is my last ride, this is my last shot
Everybody runs, you run or you get caught

Doors slam, here they come, a duffel and a gun
