Sirens bleeding red and blue, the city come undone
I promised her I'd quit, I'd quit when this was done
But the only thing I'm good at is the getting up and run

Floor it! Floor it! Don't look in the back
Rubber on the asphalt, smoke and a crack
This is my last ride, this is my last shot
Everybody runs, you run or you get caught

Three blocks, two blocks, the river's coming near
The wheel is all I've ever loved, the only thing that's clear
If I make it past the bridge then I'm a free man tonight
And if I don't, well, tell her that I drove off into the light

Floor it! Floor it! One more, one more time!

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Dead-end street and a flickering sign
Same four chords and the same flat line
Everyone here's got a same-old plan
Marry the girl, get a factory hand

I'm gone, I'm gone, I'm out the door
This nowhere town don't own me anymore
Tear up the map, I'm burning the lease
Goodbye to the quiet, goodbye to the peace
I'm gone, I'm gone, don't wait up for me

Water tower with my last name sprayed
Debts to the dead and the promises made
The diner closes, the church bell rings
I'm allergic to all of these small-town things

I'm gone, I'm gone, I'm out the door
This nowhere town don't own me anymore
Tear up the map, I'm burning the lease
Goodbye to the quiet, goodbye to the peace
I'm gone, I'm gone, don't wait up for me

They'll say I'll come crawling back in a year
They'll keep my barstool waiting right here
But I'd rather starve under city rain
Than rot in this town with a screen door slam

Gone! Gone! Don't wait up for me!

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Midnight, mason jars rattling in the trunk
Back roads, no headlights, riding on a hunch
Granddaddy's recipe, copper still in the pines
Hundred proof lightning, crossing county lines
Flatbed Ford with the suspension sitting low
Gotta make the dropoff 'fore the rooster start to crow
But a deputy's cruiser sitting quiet by the creek
And my palms getting sweaty and my brakes start to squeak

Run, run, the shine in the back
Blue lights flashing on a red dirt track
I shoulda stayed home, shoulda stayed in bed
Now it's gravel and gunsmoke and the road up ahead
Run, run, it all went wrong
One wrong turn and the night got long

Killed the lights, cut the wheel into the brush
Heart going double-time, telling myself hush
A jar broke open, now the whole car reek
Like a Saturday sin on a Sunday-morning street
They know my cousin, they been watching the road
Shoulda listened to mama 'bout the company I hold
Now I'm running through the kudzu with a debt I can't pay
And the still's still burning at the break of the day

Every dollar got a shadow, every shortcut got a price
Thought I'd do it once or twice, thought I'd play it nice
But the bottle don't forgive and the law don't forget
And the run that you survive is the one you regret

Run, run, the shine in the back
Blue lights flashing on a red dirt track
I shoulda stayed home, shoulda stayed in bed
Now it's gravel and gunsmoke and the road up ahead

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
La casa está llena pero vacía
Son las seis de la mañana todavía

Las flores en la mesa ya empezando a caer
El café se enfría, no lo puedo beber
Ayer fue el entierro, hoy no sé qué hacer
La cama de tu lado se quedó sin querer

Y el mundo sigue afuera como si nada
Pero adentro yo amanecí sin tu mirada

La mañana después no perdona
El silencio en la sala me ahoga
Guardo tu ropa, guardo tu voz
Dios, dame fuerza, dame un poco de sol
La mañana después, qué dolor
Aprendiendo a vivir sin tu amor

Los vecinos trajeron comida que no probé
Me abrazaron callados, yo solo asentí con la fe
Que algún día el peso se vuelva más leve
Que la primera mañana no dure por siempre

La mañana después no perdona
El silencio en la sala me ahoga
Guardo tu ropa, guardo tu voz
Dios, dame fuerza, dame un poco de sol

Abro la ventana, dejo entrar el aire
Vivir es seguir aunque duela quedarme
Un día a la vez, un café a la vez
Te llevo conmigo en cada amanecer

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Oy, the block where I was born had a gutter for a stream
And a fire escape that rattled out the rhythm of a dream
The pushcarts and the shouting and the tenement so tall
A boy with empty pockets and his back against the wall

But the clarinet is laughing, the clarinet is free
And it's playing me a doorway where a wall used to be
Dance, dance, lift your weary feet from the stone
I'm climbing up the melody and I am going home

My mother sewed the buttons, my father pressed the seams
They poured into a fiddle every one of their schemes
They said, go, little one, go where the road is wide
Don't you weep for the alley, let the music be your guide

But the clarinet is laughing, the clarinet is free
And it's playing me a doorway where a wall used to be
Dance, dance, lift your weary feet from the stone
I'm climbing up the melody and I am going home

Faster now, the freylekhs, faster spin the years
From the dead-end block I rose on a fiddle made of tears
And I'll carry every cobblestone wherever I may roam
For the alley made the music and the music made the home

Oy, dance, dance, the wall came down at last
A poor boy and a clarinet outrunning all the past

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Neon bleeding in the puddles on the avenue
Rain came sudden and it caught the both of us through
You stepped under, I stepped over, no one said a word
Just the hum of the city and the thunder that we heard

One umbrella, two strangers in the glow
Shoulders touching in the downpour, nowhere to go
Hold the handle, hold the moment, let it stay
Two strangers and a streetlight in the rain tonight
Don't let it wash away

Your perfume and the petrichor, the magenta light
We walked slow though the bus had come and gone into the night
I don't know your name and you don't know mine at all
But we're synchronized in footsteps down the boulevard so tall

One umbrella, two strangers in the glow
Shoulders touching in the downpour, nowhere to go
Hold the handle, hold the moment, let it stay
Two strangers and a streetlight in the rain tonight
Don't let it wash away

The rain will stop, the spell will break, you'll turn and disappear
Into a thousand other lives that brought you almost here
But for one electric chorus underneath the falling sky
We were not alone, we were not alone, you and I

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
Softly I watch you cross the room
A gentle wave, a borrowed afternoon
You smile at me the way you smile at rain
Kindly, lightly, never quite the same

And I love you in the quiet, in the corners of the day
In the songs I never sing and the words I never say
You will never know the ocean that I carry just for you
So I hum it to the window and the morning and the blue

You speak his name and I rearrange my face
Into something like a friend, into something like grace
The bossa plays so easy, so I keep my sorrow light
A samba for the longing that I fold away each night

And I love you in the quiet, in the corners of the day
In the songs I never sing and the words I never say
You will never know the ocean that I carry just for you
So I hum it to the window and the morning and the blue

Maybe in another summer, in a softer kind of time
The melody would turn and you'd be mine, you'd be mine
But the guitar only knows the chords it's given here to play
So I love you, and I let you, gently, gently walk away

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
(Bum-bum-bum) Hands at ten and two, my dear
(Bum-bum-bum) Check your mirror, shift the gear
The empty lot on Sunday morn
The day my baby girl is born to roam

Drive, drive, little darling of mine
Easy on the gas, you're doing just fine
(Doo-wop) The wheel in your hands and the world up ahead
(Doo-wop) Remember all the words your old man said
Drive, drive, my darling, you're free
Just always find your way back home to me

(Bum-bum-bum) She kills it twice and starts to cry
(Bum-bum-bum) I say there, there, just give it a try
The lurch, the laugh, the second gear
My little girl is growing up this year

Drive, drive, little darling of mine
Easy on the gas, you're doing just fine
(Doo-wop) The wheel in your hands and the world up ahead
(Doo-wop) Remember all the words your old man said
Drive, drive, my darling, you're free
Just always find your way back home to me

One day soon you'll drive away (drive away)
To a college or a wedding day (wedding day)
And I'll stand in the drive and I'll wave at the car
And be proud of the woman and the driver you are

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Bolted-down table where the green paint peels
Plastic king with a chip in his crown
He licks his thumb, slaps the clock like it squeals
Ten bucks a game and he never sits down

This ain't checkers, kid, put your money where it bleeds
Queen to rook one, watch the suit concede
Pigeons keep the score on the cracked concrete
Hustle in the park where the broke kings meet

Lunch-break lawyer thinks his tie makes him smart
Mate in four moves and his Tuesday's done
Twenty for the lesson that the schools never start
He pockets the sandwich and the afternoon sun

This ain't checkers, kid, put your money where it bleeds
Queen to rook one, watch the suit concede
Pigeons keep the score on the cracked concrete
Hustle in the park where the broke kings meet

Squad car rolls slow but they let him be
He's owned that bench since nineteen-eighty-three
Knock the pieces over, send 'em rolling wide
He's already racked 'em on the other side

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I drove out past the gate with your old cassette
Talking to a stone like you'd talk back to me
Carried this weight so long my shoulders forget
What it ever felt like to be free

So I forgive you, I forgive you
Even though you'll never hear
Laid it down in the wet spring grass
Let the bitterness disappear

Thirteen winters I rehearsed what I'd say
Every cruel line polished sharp and clean
Now the only door is sealed and gray
And you're a name carved between

So I forgive you, I forgive you
Even though you'll never hear
Laid it down in the wet spring grass
Let the bitterness disappear

Not for you, no, this one's for me
So I can sleep without your ghost
The mercy I held back like rent unpaid
I'm spending it on the one I owe the most

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Buttermilk biscuits in a black iron pan
Apple butter bubbling slow on the stove
Flour on her apron, flour on her hands
Morning light gold through the kitchen window

Oh that woodsmoke and cinnamon air
Follows me down every road I roam
Close my eyes and I'm standing there
In the only thing I ever called home

Coffee in a percolator, steam on the glass
Bacon grease saved in a coffee can lid
She'd hum an old hymn while the hours passed
Never once asked for the things that she did

Oh that woodsmoke and cinnamon air
Follows me down every road I roam
Close my eyes and I'm standing there
In the only thing I ever called home

They sold the house and they tore down the shed
But a stranger's kitchen on a cold fall day
Lit one match and the whole thing flooded my head
And for one breath she hadn't gone away

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I sewed a copper coin in the seam of my coat
Kissed the green hill goodbye in the gray of dawn
Stood at the rail of a coal-bellied boat
Watching everything I knew get gone

So carry me over, carry me wide
Salt in my beard and a fire in my chest
Far from the churchyard where my people lie
Chasing a rumor of work in the west

Forty days of herring and a rationed sup
A fiddle in steerage kept the dark away
Babies were born and old men gave up
And we buried two souls in the gray of the spray

So carry me over, carry me wide
Salt in my beard and a fire in my chest
Far from the churchyard where my people lie
Chasing a rumor of work in the west

When the green line of a new shore rose
Grown men wept who hadn't wept in years
I pressed that coin through my coat with froze
Numb fingers, and I let go of the fear

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the floor and the bassline starts to swell
Got a photograph of you I never threw away
Lights cut red to blue, I know this rhythm well
Everything I held I'm gonna leave here when it fades

Let it go, let it go tonight
You're not coming back, I know
Hands up in the strobe and light
I forgive you, let it go

Four to the floor like a heartbeat won't stop
Spinning out the anger in a circle on the tiles
Sweat and the smoke machine kissing the top
Dancing off the distance of a thousand silent miles

Let it go, let it go tonight
You're not coming back, I know
Hands up in the strobe and light
I forgive you, let it go

The drop comes down like a weight off my spine
No more words to send, no more lines to a phone
The room breathes as one and for a second it's fine
To dance in a crowd and finally let you go alone

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The roll of packing tape screams like it knows
This is the last seam I'll ever close in here
Four walls echo back where the sofa used to go
And a square of clean paint where your face hung clear

So I tape up the last box, kill the kitchen light
Set the keys on the counter, they ring like a bell
Nothing left to carry but the weight of tonight
And a apartment that's empty as a shell

Found a hair tie of yours behind the radiator pipe
A receipt from a diner that has since shut down
Funny what survives when you box up a life
Funny what you leave for the next ones in town

So I tape up the last box, kill the kitchen light
Set the keys on the counter, they ring like a bell
Nothing left to carry but the weight of tonight
And a apartment that's empty as a shell

I stand in the doorway one more breath, one more look
Every dent in the floor is a year I can't unsay
Then I pull the door shut like the cover of a book
And the hallway light hums me away

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Thumb on the number I deleted twice
Kitchen of a sublet, beer gone warm
Seven years of pride and it all melts like ice
When the ringtone drags me back to the storm

Ma, it's me, don't hang up the line
I know what I said, I know what I did
My hands are shaking and I'm out of time
I'm forty years old and I'm still your kid

She picks up slow like she don't believe
Says my name like it's been holding its breath
All the rehearsed apologies up my sleeve
Fall apart at the sound of her voice in my chest

Ma, it's me, don't hang up the line
I know what I said, I know what I did
My hands are shaking and I'm out of time
I'm forty years old and I'm still your kid

We don't fix it all in one cracked call
But she laughs at a joke from when I was nine
And I press the phone to the kitchen wall
And cry like a drain for the first time in a long, long time

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Soft green sky over Kansas wheat
The radio hums and the warm rain calls
You laid out plates and a table set sweet
But I watch the horizon where the pressure falls

Forgive me love, I can't stay
The thunder is a song I have to chase
The still of a porch makes me drift away
I only feel home in a falling place

Your hand on my cheek is the gentlest gale
But my pulse keeps time with the distant grey
I kiss your wrist and I check the trail
Of a supercell turning a county away

Forgive me love, I can't stay
The thunder is a song I have to chase
The still of a porch makes me drift away
I only feel home in a falling place

Maybe one day the sky will run dry
And I'll learn the slow art of a quiet room
But tonight there's a wall cloud low in the sky
And my heart spins out like the funnel's plume

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
The hands that once spun me up to the sun
Now tremble a little to open a jar
He laughs it off, says the day is done
And reaches slow for a glass of water near

Oh papa, walk with me slow
Down the same street we always knew
The sun's still warm and the breeze still blows
And I'll match every step you do

He tells the same story he told last spring
And I listen close like I never had
The little gold watch and his wedding ring
Loose now on the fingers of my dad

Oh papa, walk with me slow
Down the same street we always knew
The sun's still warm and the breeze still blows
And I'll match every step you do

There's a photograph of him young and bold
Leaning on a car with the whole world wide
Funny how the strong ones grow soft and old
And how the boy I was still wants to hide in his side

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
The pole turns red and white on the corner of the square
And the bell on the door has a song of its own
Fifty years of necks have leaned back in that chair
And he's heard every secret this old town has known

Oh the razor sings low and the gossip runs free
Weddings and wakes in the lather and steam
Who's courting and who's quarreling, who crossed the sea
The whole town's a-tangle in the barber's dream

He trimmed the priest and he trimmed the thief
The groom on his morning and the boy on his first
He gave the widower's word its grief
And cooled the young hothead before he could burst

Oh the razor sings low and the gossip runs free
Weddings and wakes in the lather and steam
Who's courting and who's quarreling, who crossed the sea
The whole town's a-tangle in the barber's dream

When they lay him down they'll come from the hills
The whole parish hushed in their Sunday best
For he kept the town's story better than the bells
And he carried its sorrows on his striped white vest

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Flash of cinnamon, gone in a breath
A stranger's stove and I'm nine years old
Dragged back screaming through the years and the death
To a warm yellow room in the dead of the cold

YOU CAN'T GO BACK
BUT IT BURNS IN YOUR CHEST
The smoke and the bread and the hands on your head
EVERYTHING GONE BUT THE SMELL WON'T REST

They paved the lot where the house used to stand
Sold the iron pan at a yard sale for a dime
But one whiff of woodsmoke and I'm grabbing her hand
And I'm choking on twenty lost years of time

YOU CAN'T GO BACK
BUT IT BURNS IN YOUR CHEST
The smoke and the bread and the hands on your head
EVERYTHING GONE BUT THE SMELL WON'T REST

I'd give every year I have left to crawl
Under that table on the cracked linoleum floor
And hear her hum low through the kitchen wall
ONE MORE TIME — give me one breath more

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Mud to the ankles, throat scraped raw
Four days of nothing but the wall of sound
Last band, last song, no tomorrow at all
As the whole field roars and the bass shakes the ground

ONE MORE NIGHT — leave it all on the field
Scream til it bleeds, til there's nothing held back
Strangers turned brothers in the circle we sealed
THE LAST NIGHT BURNS and we don't want it back

Lost my shoe in the pit, lost my voice on day two
Got a stranger's blood and my own on my shirt
We carried each other when the heatstroke flew
And we caught every body that the crowd-surf hurt

ONE MORE NIGHT — leave it all on the field
Scream til it bleeds, til there's nothing held back
Strangers turned brothers in the circle we sealed
THE LAST NIGHT BURNS and we don't want it back

Then the lights cut hard and the silence drops in
Ten thousand of us blinking at the dawn
Trudging to the gates with the dust on our skin
Knowing nothing this real ever lasts past the song

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
You bought a little house with a porch and a swing
Painted the door a hopeful kind of blue
But I'm watching the radar light up like a ring
And my keys are already turning in my shoe

I chase the lightning, I follow the grey
I'm a heart in a hurry the storms steal away
I love you, I do, but I just can't stay
When the sky starts to spin I'm a thousand miles away

You say settle down, baby, plant something deep
And I want to, I swear, but the thunder won't let
There's a hum in my blood that won't let me sleep
When the pressure drops low and the whole world gets wet

I chase the lightning, I follow the grey
I'm a heart in a hurry the storms steal away
I love you, I do, but I just can't stay
When the sky starts to spin I'm a thousand miles away

Maybe the calm is its own kind of brave
Maybe forever is a sky that stays clear
But tonight there's a funnel and a road that I crave
So I'm gone with the wind and I'm wishing you here

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Antena on the rooftop, wires in the rain
Transmitter in a shoebox that I built from the scrap
No license, no boss, no corporate refrain
Just the barrio bumping to my underground trap

Pon la radio, sube el volumen
Ninety-two point nine, we don't ask permission
From the projects to the corner, the whole block consumin'
Pirata en la noche on a stolen transmission

The vans come hunting with the signal-tracker light
We move the antenna and we're back on the air
The dembow knocks til the windows shake tight
And the kids on the corner throw their hands in the air

Pon la radio, sube el volumen
Ninety-two point nine, we don't ask permission
From the projects to the corner, the whole block consumin'
Pirata en la noche on a stolen transmission

They can jam every tower, they can cut every wire
But the voice of the block, you can't shut it down
We'll be back next week with the same desire
The loudest little signal in a silenced town

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Came down sudden on the corner of Vine
And you ducked 'neath my umbrella with a laugh and a nod
Two wet strangers and a thin black line
Between us and the gray of the road that we trod

So we walked through the rain, shoulder to shoulder
Under one bent umbrella for a block and a half
Neither one warmer but neither one colder
Just the drum of the downpour and a stranger's soft laugh

You told me your bus stop, I told you my name
We argued the weather and the price of the bread
For four hundred steps we were glad that it came
This ordinary rain over both of our heads

So we walked through the rain, shoulder to shoulder
Under one bent umbrella for a block and a half
Neither one warmer but neither one colder
Just the drum of the downpour and a stranger's soft laugh

Then the sky cleared up and you went on your way
Never traded a number, never met you again
But I think of you sometimes on a gray rainy day
And that small bit of dry that we shared in the rain

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They came with their silver, they came with their gold
Said sign on the line, son, do what you're told
But my pen was a gift that the river put in
And I won't trade my soul for a number-one spin

They said water it down till it's easy to sell
Till it fits in a jingle, till it rings like a bell
But the truth ain't for rent and the word ain't for hire
I was lit by a flame they could never buy higher

I won't sell out, no, I won't sell out
What the Lord put in me is what I'm singing about
You can keep all your money, keep your high-rise crown
I'd rather be broke than to let my spirit down

So I write in the morning, I write in the rain
Every line is a prayer, every verse is a chain
That binds me to glory and frees me from greed
The only investment is the gospel I bleed

Oh, they offered me everything under the sun
But a heart that is honest can never be won
Let 'em laugh, let 'em leave me, let 'em close every door
I'll be singing His truth on a hardwood floor

I won't sell out, no, I won't sell out
What the Lord put in me is what I'm singing about
You can keep all your money, keep your high-rise crown
I'd rather be broke than to let my spirit down

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I been writing you letters by the lantern's dying glow
Folding up my heartache, watch it sink down below
Every word I'm sending out across that midnight foam
Darling salt-and-sorrow, won't you carry my words home

My hands are cracked and weathered from this old stone tower wall
I light the lamp at sundown and I answer when you call
But you never write me back, no, you just roll and you moan
Forty years of loving you and still I'm here alone

Oh sea, oh sea, you're the only love I know
You take my pen, you take my breath, you take me where I go
I'd drown a thousand times just to hold you for a day
My love letters to the ocean, washing all away

The gulls done read my secrets, the tide done stole my name
Every wave's a woman that done loved me just the same
But you're cold and you're cruel and you swallow all you take
Still I light this lamp for you till my old heart breaks

When I'm gone they'll find the bottles stacked up by the door
A hundred years of letters that you never answered for
But I'd do it all again, yeah, I'd write you every line
Lighthouse keeper loving you, the sea that won't be mine

Oh sea, oh sea, you're the only love I know
You take my pen, you take my breath, you take me where I go

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Gasoline sunset bleeding on the shore
You kicked off your shoes and you ran for the roar
Saltwater fury in your wild electric eyes
We burned like a wildfire underneath the dying skies

Skin against the bonfire, screaming at the moon
Knew this couldn't last, knew it'd end too soon
A season of lightning, a heartbeat in the sand
Gripping at forever with a fistful of your hand

SUMMER! Burning out of control!
You set a fire in the depths of my soul!
GONE! Like the tide pulling free!
A fling in the wreckage of the screaming sea!

September came marching like an executioner's drum
The waves washed it under and the cold winds come
But I still taste the salt and I still feel the flame
A ghost in the dunes, screaming out your name

No regrets! No goodbyes!
Just the thunder and the fire in your eyes!
We were doomed from the start, but we burned so bright!
Two souls in the dark on a beach in the night!

SUMMER! Burning out of control!
You set a fire in the depths of my soul!
GONE! Like the tide pulling free!
A fling in the wreckage of the screaming sea!

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Mira, papá, tus manos no tiemblan igual
The hands that lifted mountains now they move so slow
I remember when you carried the whole world for me
Now I hold the door open, watching how you go

El tiempo no perdona, it takes a little more
Gray in your beard where the black used to shine
You taught me how to walk, now I walk you to the chair
Papá, dame tu mano, let me give you mine

No te vayas todavía, stay a little while
I'm not ready for the day you say goodbye
Dance with me slow to the song that you raised me on
Papá, eres mi rey, even as the years roll by

Fotos en la pared of a younger stronger man
You were my superhero, you were ten feet tall
Now I see you smaller but you're bigger than before
Because love it only grows when the body starts to fall

Dale, una más, one more song before you rest
Let me memorize your laugh, let me hold your chest
Everything I am is a piece of what you gave
Y te lo prometo, papá, I'll carry you brave

No te vayas todavía, stay a little while
I'm not ready for the day you say goodbye
Dance with me slow to the song that you raised me on
Papá, eres mi rey, even as the years roll by

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
The brass began to weep on Rampart Street
A slow procession in the heavy heat
White handkerchiefs against the heavy sky
We came to lay you down, we came to cry

The tuba held the heartbeat of the dead
A trumpet spoke the words we never said
Umbrellas turning slow against the gray
We walked you home the only honest way

And when the second line begins to rise
We'll dance the sorrow out beneath the skies
From grief to glory, from the dark to gold
We'll set you free the way it's always told

The snare drum cracked and something broke apart
The dirge dissolved into a beating heart
The mourners lifted, feet began to fly
You'd want us laughing as we said goodbye

Let the cymbals crash like waves upon the shore
Let the whole street shake the way it did before
Death ain't an ending here, it's just a song
That the city plays to carry you along

And when the second line begins to rise
We'll dance the sorrow out beneath the skies
From grief to glory, from the dark to gold
We'll set you free the way it's always told

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I memorize the way you say my name
Like it's nothing, like it's just the same
As everybody else you talk to every day
But I replay it on a loop when you walk away

You tell me all about the one you love
And I just smile and say they're good enough
While I'm dying every time you laugh at me
Like a friend, like that's all I'll ever be

And I love you, I love you, but you'll never know
I'm holding my breath every time you go
You're the wish that I make but it won't come true
'Cause my whole heart is screaming and it's screaming you

Maybe in another life I had a chance
Maybe you would've asked me to that dance
But I'm stuck in the corner of your busy mind
The kind of love you leave a step behind

So I'll keep my secret like a stone I swallow whole
Watch you fall for someone else and lose a little soul
But I'd rather have you near me as a friend than not at all
So I'll catch you every time, even when I'm the one to fall

And I love you, I love you, but you'll never know
I'm holding my breath every time you go
You're the wish that I make but it won't come true
'Cause my whole heart is screaming and it's screaming you

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Well I met my baby where the ocean meets the sand
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
She had a transistor radio and an ice cream in her hand
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
The boardwalk lights were shining and the night was young and free
Oh that summertime sweetheart, she was made for me

We danced beneath the moonlight to a slow and dreamy tune
(Doo-wop, doo-wop)
I knew that I would love her from July until the moon
(Doo-wop, doo-wop)
Her laughter like the seagulls, her kiss like salty wine
For one golden season, oh that girl was mine

Summer love, summer love, by the rolling sea
Summer love, summer love, you were good to me
But the leaves are gonna turn and you'll be gone, I know
Summer love, summer love, why'd you have to go

September came a-calling and she packed her things to leave
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
She wrote her name in lipstick on my old torn shirtsleeve
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
Now the beach is cold and empty and the radio plays low
But I'll dream of her forever in that summertime glow

Whoa-oh-oh, every grain of sand
Whoa-oh-oh, still remembers her hand
And I'm wishing on a star above the bay
That my summer love comes back to me someday

Summer love, summer love, by the rolling sea
Summer love, summer love, you were good to me

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
I didn't come here looking for a thing
Just a glass of wine and somebody's ring
But you crossed the room in the candlelight
And the band slowed down on a Saturday night

You held out your hand, said you don't even know my name
I said baby that's alright, let's play this game
Two strangers swaying where the others can't see
For three little minutes you belonged to me

Dance with me, stranger, under the lights
Don't tell me your story, just hold me tonight
We'll let the song carry us somewhere unknown
Two perfect strangers but we're not alone

Your cologne and the bass and the champagne air
The whole room melted and I didn't care
Maybe tomorrow you'll be just a face
But right now you're my whole entire place

When the music stops we'll go our separate ways
Different cities, different nights, different days
But I'll keep this dance like a photograph
A stranger, a wedding, and a moment that lasts

Dance with me, stranger, under the lights
Don't tell me your story, just hold me tonight
We'll let the song carry us somewhere unknown
Two perfect strangers but we're not alone

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Got a busted-up transmitter in my granddaddy's barn
A coat-hanger antenna and a whole lotta charm
The FCC don't like me but the people tune in
Ninety-five point nothing, where the outlaws begin

I play the songs they won't play on the corporate dial
Real steel guitar and a honky-tonk style
From midnight till the rooster, I'm the voice in the dark
A pirate on the airwaves lighting up the spark

Turn it up, turn it loud, let the signal ride
We're broadcasting free on the back-road side
They can chase me, they can trace me, but they'll never shut me down
I'm the last free station in this one-horse town

Sheriff's at the door but I'm a county away
Moved the rig to the holler before the break of day
Got truckers and dreamers and the lonely tuned in tight
To the only honest station playing through the night

So if you're out there listening on a lonesome road
With a heavy heart and a heavy load
Just know I'm out here spinning every song you need
A pirate flag flying where the airwaves bleed

Turn it up, turn it loud, let the signal ride
We're broadcasting free on the back-road side
They can chase me, they can trace me, but they'll never shut me down
I'm the last free station in this one-horse town

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
You and me, we never get along
Every party, we been singing different songs
But the DJ call our names tonight
Now we moving in the same low light

I don't like you, you don't like me
But your body moving like the rolling sea
The drum dey talk and the bass dey pull
Two enemies on the dancefloor full

Move your body closer, oya let it go
We no gree before but the rhythm say so
Enemy tonight, but we dancing as one
Under the lights till the rivalry done

Step for step and we matching the beat
Fire in your eyes, fire in my feet
The whole crowd watching, them dey shout our name
Funny how the music turn the war to a game

Maybe we no different after all
Same heart beating when the rhythm call
Drop the grudge, baby, let it fall
Let the afrobeat dissolve the wall

Move your body closer, oya let it go
We no gree before but the rhythm say so
Enemy tonight, but we dancing as one
Under the lights till the rivalry done

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Golden hour, your skin in the sun
We knew from the start that this wouldn't run
Past the season, past the sand and the tide
But I gave you the summer and you gave me the ride

Saltwater kisses and a radio low
Laying in the dunes where the warm winds blow
No promises, baby, no future, no past
Just the heat of right now and a love built to last
Until the leaves start to fall

Summer skin, summer love, hold me close tonight
We don't need forever in the fading light
Just you and me and the ocean blue
A summer kind of love, baby, me and you

The boardwalk glowing as the day gives in
Your hand in my hand and the night begins
I know come September you'll be miles away
But tonight you're my reason, so let's stay

When the cold comes calling and the beach goes gray
I'll remember the way that you used to sway
A season ain't forever but it felt so right
My summer, my baby, my warm beach night

Summer skin, summer love, hold me close tonight
We don't need forever in the fading light
Just you and me and the ocean blue
A summer kind of love, baby, me and you

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Well I strung me a wire from the top of the pine
Got a radio humming and the signal's mine
Up in the holler where the law can't see
Playing mountain music on a frequency free

Banjo in the morning, fiddle through the night
I'm a-broadcasting moonshine by the lantern light
The government man, he come a-knocking 'round
But I packed up the gear 'fore he run me down

Roll on, radio, roll on through the trees
Singing to the hills on a mountain breeze
They can't catch a signal that they cannot find
Pirate of the airwaves and I'm losing my mind

Got a preacher and a drunkard and a lonesome soul
All tuned in close where the rivers roll
I'll spin 'em a ballad of the days gone by
Till the deputy's headlights come a-rolling nigh

From Tennessee timber to the Kentucky line
My little station's putting out a signal divine
No sponsor, no license, no boss, no plan
Just an old mountain picker and a coffee can

Roll on, radio, roll on through the trees
Singing to the hills on a mountain breeze
They can't catch a signal that they cannot find
Pirate of the airwaves and I'm losing my mind

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The coffee's gone cold and the clock's blinking red
Got a mountain of books where I oughta be in bed
Two a.m. and the whole world's asleep
Just me and these pages and the promises I keep

Momma always said that the hard road's worth it
So I'm burning this candle, gonna be sure I learned it
Highlighter running dry and my eyes going blurry
But I'll get there somehow, ain't in no hurry

It's a lonesome kind of light at two in the morning
Chasing down a dream while the dawn ain't dawning
Nobody sees the work that it takes to climb
Just me and these books and the ticking of time

Got a picture on the desk of the day I'll walk that stage
Keeps me turning every single weary page
The town's gone quiet and the streetlight hums
I'll keep on going till the morning comes

They don't hand you nothing in this life for free
So I'll trade a little sleep for the man I'll be
One more chapter, one more line
Then the sun comes up and the world is mine

It's a lonesome kind of light at two in the morning
Chasing down a dream while the dawn ain't dawning
Nobody sees the work that it takes to climb
Just me and these books and the ticking of time

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I dialed up the number that I knew by heart
(Sha-la-la-la)
Three long years since we fell apart
(Sha-la-la-la)
My finger on the button and my heart in my throat
Praying that you'd answer, praying that you'd quote

The phone, it rang twice and I heard you say hello
(Doo-doo-wop)
And all my foolish pride just had to go
(Doo-doo-wop)
I said Mama, it's your baby, I been gone too long
Can you ever forgive me, was I that wrong

Mama, oh Mama, I'm coming back home
I'm sorry for the years that I left you alone
The silence is over, can you hear me cry
Mama, oh Mama, let's say hello not goodbye

You cried on the line and you called my name
(Sha-la-la-la)
Said the door's always open, you felt the same
(Sha-la-la-la)
All those wasted seasons just melted away
In the sound of your voice on a Sunday

Whoa-oh, every night I prayed
Whoa-oh, for the call that I'd delayed
Now I'm driving down that old familiar street
With my heart wide open and my mama to meet

Mama, oh Mama, I'm coming back home
I'm sorry for the years that I left you alone
The silence is over, can you hear me cry
Mama, oh Mama, let's say hello not goodbye

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
We drove out to the water with the windows rolled down
Last tank of gas and the lights of the town
Fading in the mirror like the years that we knew
One more golden evening, just me and you

Gonna pack up my whole life in a duffel bag soon
Leave this little nothing town beneath the August moon
But tonight we got bonfires and the songs that we sang
And the echo of the summer in the way it all rang

So here's to the last day, the end of it all
The summer that raised us before the fall
We'll scatter like leaves on the highway wide
But I'll carry this evening for the rest of my life

You're going east and I'm going west
Different dreams in our restless chests
We swore that we'd write, swore we'd stay the same
But we both know that nothing stays once it came

So let the radio play one more time
Let the river keep moving and the streetlights shine
We were kids in the dust of a small-town street
Now we're standing at the edge where the roads all meet

So here's to the last day, the end of it all
The summer that raised us before the fall
We'll scatter like leaves on the highway wide
But I'll carry this evening for the rest of my life

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Carl wrote his lines on the backs of receipts
In a one-room walk-up above Delancey Street
The ad men came with a contract and a pen
Said put our jingle where your verses been

They offered him a car and a beach in Maine
A byline on a billboard, a corporate name
He poured them coffee, said the words ain't yours
Then showed them gently to the screen-door

I won't sell out, I won't sign my name away
I'll keep the truth I bled for, come what may
You can keep your silver, keep your golden mile
I'd rather starve with meaning than feast on a lie

The rent came due and the heat went cold
But the lines he kept were worth more than gold
A young girl found his book in a bin downtown
And carried every word from town to town

They'll forget the men who bought and sold
But not the ones who wouldn't fold
The river keeps the stones it won't let go
And Carl is in the verses, soft and slow

I won't sell out, I won't sign my name away
I'll keep the truth I bled for, come what may
You can keep your silver, keep your golden mile
I'd rather starve with meaning than feast on a lie

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The A&R guy stood at the back of the bar
Nodding his head like we'd already gone far
We played the set tighter than we'd ever done
Sweat on the strings under a dying sun

He said give me your number, we'll talk in a week
Kissed the drummer's cheek, called the bass line unique
We drove home screaming with the windows down
Kings for a night of a nothing town

We almost made it, almost touched the wire
One inch from the lightning, one breath from the fire
The phone never rang and the summer went grey
But for one electric night we almost got away

The number was real but the week never came
Somebody bigger had a better same
We still play Tuesdays to a half-empty floor
Chasing the ghost of that knock on the door

If you'd have called I'd have given my life
Traded the quiet, the kids and the wife
But almost is a country with no flag, no name
And we're its only citizens, half-proud, half-ashamed

We almost made it, almost touched the wire
One inch from the lightning, one breath from the fire
The phone never rang and the summer went grey
But for one electric night we almost got away

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon humming on the boulevard
Rosa flips the sign, never locks the door
Two a.m. and the cabbies roll in
Vinyl booths and the jukebox spin

All night long at the Starlight Grill
The coffee's hot and the booth is yours to fill
Dance with a stranger by the pie-case glow
We never close, baby, we never close

The nurse off shift and the boy in love
The dreamer staring at the stars above
Everyone's welcome when the city's asleep
Rosa's got a stool and a song to keep

All night long at the Starlight Grill
The coffee's hot and the booth is yours to fill
Dance with a stranger by the pie-case glow
We never close, baby, we never close

When the world shuts down and the lonely arrive
This little diner keeps the whole town alive
Syrup and starlight, a hash-brown crown
The last lit window in a sleeping town

All night long at the Starlight Grill
The coffee's hot and the booth is yours to fill
Dance with a stranger by the pie-case glow
We never close, baby, we never close

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Slow the umbrellas down Rampart line
A trumpet weeping soft and fine
We lay you down where the cypress lean
In a velvet hush, a sway between

The river keeps its easy time
Mourning gentle, almost rhyme
White handkerchiefs in the humid air
Grief so soft it's almost prayer

And we'll dance you home, dance you home
Where the second line and the angels roam
Sorrow turns to a softer sound
We'll dance you gently in the ground

Then the snare picks up a brighter beat
Feet remember the sunlit street
Death walks out and the joy walks in
A bossa swing where the tears had been

Saudade in the saxophone
You are gone but never gone
The city holds you, soft and warm
A quiet rhythm through the storm

And we'll dance you home, dance you home
Where the second line and the angels roam
Sorrow turns to a softer sound
We'll dance you gently in the ground

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The county fair and a borrowed guitar
A judge from the city who'd come from afar
He leaned on the rail when I sang my song
And I swear that he listened the whole night long

He said son, you've got something rare and true
Come see me in Nashville, I'll make room for you
He wrote on a napkin an address and a name
And I held it like fire on the road that I came

Oh I almost made it, almost flew
With a napkin promise and a sky so blue
But the rain washed the ink and the years rolled by
And I sing to the cornfields under an empty sky

I bussed into Nashville with my heart in my hand
The address was a parking lot, not what I'd planned
No name on the buzzer, no judge, no door
Just a kid with a guitar and a song to be poor

Maybe he meant it, maybe he forgot
Maybe the dream was all that I got
But I still feel the rail where his hand came to rest
The night I came closest, the night I sang best

Oh I almost made it, almost flew
With a napkin promise and a sky so blue
But the rain washed the ink and the years rolled by
And I sing to the cornfields under an empty sky

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Under a sky of green and rose
We lay where the cold pine river flows
Your hand in mine, the world gone still
Light like a whisper over the hill

Ribbons unspooling, soft and slow
The heavens breathing, ebb and flow
A gentle samba in the snow
The quiet places that lovers go

Dance with me beneath the lights
The aurora swaying through the nights
Green on your shoulders, violet hair
A bossa drifting on the frozen air

We spoke in murmurs, half in dreams
The sky a curtain of liquid streams
No need for words where the colors fall
The Arctic sings and we hear it all

Saudade soft as the falling frost
For all the warmth that we never lost
The lights will fade with the coming day
But the swaying stays when you're far away

Dance with me beneath the lights
The aurora swaying through the nights
Green on your shoulders, violet hair
A bossa drifting on the frozen air

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Black umbrellas on the Treme street
But the bass drum's kicking a four-four beat
We laid him down then we turned around
And the whole damn block found the funky sound

Dance him up to heaven now, hey
Glitter on the casket in the second-line sway
Grief on the downbeat, joy on the up
We're sending him home with the city lit up

The brass section caught a disco fire
Tambourine ringing, the spirits rise higher
Mama in sequins by the streetlight glow
Clapping for a soul that she had to let go

Dance him up to heaven now, hey
Glitter on the casket in the second-line sway
Grief on the downbeat, joy on the up
We're sending him home with the city lit up

No crying in the corner when the horns come loud
We bury the sorrow in a dancing crowd
From Rampart to glory on a mirror-ball ray
Death in New Orleans is a holiday

Dance him up to heaven now, hey
Glitter on the casket in the second-line sway
Grief on the downbeat, joy on the up
We're sending him home with the city lit up

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
The hymnbook still smells like my childhood hands
Light through the glass in colored bands
I stand in the pew where my mother prayed
And feel the soft pull of a faith that frayed

I don't slam the door, I don't make a sound
I just set the kneeler gently down
Nobody noticed, nobody knew
The morning I stopped believing it was true

I'm leaving so quietly, fading like dust
No thunder, no anger, just losing the trust
The steeple gets smaller in the back of my eye
I'm slipping out softly, no need for goodbye

The organ still hums in the back of my chest
I carry the kindness, I leave the rest
God, if you're listening, I bear you no blame
I just can't keep singing the words of your name

There's grace in the leaving, grace in the door
Grace in the girl I won't be anymore
The candles still flicker, the choir still climbs
But I'm out in the rain in my own private rhymes

I'm leaving so quietly, fading like dust
No thunder, no anger, just losing the trust
The steeple gets smaller in the back of my eye
I'm slipping out softly, no need for goodbye

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I bring you coffee, I learn your songs
I memorize the place where you belong
You tell me 'bout the one who broke your heart
And I'm the shoulder, baby, never the start

But I love you, oh, I love you still
From across the table, against my will
You'll never know how deep it goes
I'm the secret only midnight knows

You call me your best friend, your steady ground
The safest place when your world spins round
And every time you say somebody's name
It's never mine and it's never the same

But I love you, oh, I love you still
From across the table, against my will
You'll never know how deep it goes
I'm the secret only midnight knows

Lord, give me the strength to let it be
To love you free and to set me free
I'd rather have you as a friend than not at all
So I'll catch you, darling, every time you fall

But I love you, oh, I love you still
From across the table, against my will
You'll never know how deep it goes
I'm the secret only midnight knows

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raise up my hand in the conference room
Got a good idea start to bloom
Before the words can leave my chest
Big man cut me, take the rest

Every meeting, same old song
They talk me over all day long
But I and I know what I know
One day soon the truth gonna show

Then a louder voice repeat my line
Now the whole room say it's mighty fine
They clap for him, they pass him praise
Same idea, a different face

Every meeting, same old song
They talk me over all day long
But I and I know what I know
One day soon the truth gonna show

Patience like the river to the sea
They cannot drown the truth in me
Mute my mic but not my mind
Jah give justice to the quiet kind

Every meeting, same old song
They talk me over all day long
But I and I know what I know
One day soon the truth gonna show

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slid shut on the downtown train
And the world got small and the lights went strange
My heart took off like a frightened bird
And I couldn't make a sound, couldn't say a word

The tunnel swallowed the daylight whole
A cold black hand reached into my soul
I gripped the pole till my knuckles turned white
Counting the stations, holding on tight

Breathe in, breathe out, the ground is still there
The wave's gonna pass like it's only air
I am not dying, I'm just afraid
And the fear is a storm that the morning will fade

A stranger looked up from across the car
Said easy now, friend, you don't have far
She counted with me, four in, four out
Till the terror loosened its grip of doubt

Nobody saw how I nearly broke
Under the weight of the underground smoke
But a kind voice found me in the dark
And carried me up to the daylight park

Breathe in, breathe out, the ground is still there
The wave's gonna pass like it's only air
I am not dying, I'm just afraid
And the fear is a storm that the morning will fade

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Sit me down at the table, tell me speak my mind
Then you bulldoze every sentence that I leave behind
Raise my hand, get a wall of noise
Drowned beneath the same loud boys

Talk over me, talk over me
You never let me finish, never let me be
I'm done being polite in your little machine
I'll scream my idea till the windows scream

Steal my words then you wear my crown
Say it louder, shout me down
Thirty minutes and I said not one
Now the meeting's adjourned and the work ain't done

Talk over me, talk over me
You never let me finish, never let me be
I'm done being polite in your little machine
I'll scream my idea till the windows scream

No more nodding, no more nice
No more swallowing your advice
I'll flip the table, kill the chart
And say the thing that's in my heart

Talk over me, talk over me
You never let me finish, never let me be
I'm done being polite in your little machine
I'll scream my idea till the windows scream

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Old man Abram with a chalk and thread
A tape round his neck and pins in his head
He's been on the corner since the war came home
Stitching the neighborhood, never alone

He cuffed the suit that buried Joe
Hemmed the dress for the bride's first glow
Let out the seams as the children grew
Abram knew us inside and through

He dresses the whole block, button and seam
Holds every wedding and funeral dream
In the cloth of the living, the cloth of the gone
Abram's needle keeps stitching us on

The banker's coat and the busboy's vest
The same kind hands made each their best
No one's too poor and no one's too proud
For a measure of care from the man who bowed

When they buried Abram in his finest grey
The whole block came in the clothes he'd made
A quilt of a lifetime walking slow
The tailor still dressing the ones below

He dresses the whole block, button and seam
Holds every wedding and funeral dream
In the cloth of the living, the cloth of the gone
Abram's needle keeps stitching us on

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Daddy shook hands on the day I was born
Signed my whole life to the Calloway corn
A ring already picked, a man twice my years
A future laid out like a row of steers

They planned out my wedding, the church and the lace
The farm I'd inherit, the smile on my face
But nobody asked what the girl wanted most
So I packed up my truck while they all slept close

I'm leaving the future they built without me
Driving down highways to who I might be
They'll call me ungrateful, they'll call me a fool
But I'd rather be lost than be somebody's tool

I left a note on the kitchen door
Said I love you all but I can't anymore
The dust in my mirror's the only goodbye
To a life that was chosen before I could try

Maybe I'll fail, maybe I'll fall
But at least it'll be my own name on the wall
No arranged tomorrow, no borrowed design
Whatever's out there, at least it'll be mine

I'm leaving the future they built without me
Driving down highways to who I might be
They'll call me ungrateful, they'll call me a fool
But I'd rather be lost than be somebody's tool

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
Drew your name from the same gold hat
My worst enemy, imagine that
The floor goes dark and the bassline drops
We lock our eyes and the talking stops

You took my title, I took your crown
Now the spotlight says we can't back down
Hand on my waist like a loaded threat
The coldest partner I ever met

Move, move, against my will
Two enemies dancing, going in for the kill
The rhythm don't care who's wrong or right
We're locked in the breakbeat all through the night

Step for step we refuse to yield
A battlefield turned to a dancing field
But the snare rolls fast and the bass goes deep
And a fire ignites that we can't keep

Somewhere in the spin we forgot to hate
The drum and the bass rewriting our fate
Rivals at the door, but the music don't lie
We found something true in each other's eye

Move, move, against my will
Two enemies dancing, going in for the kill
The rhythm don't care who's wrong or right
We're locked in the breakbeat all through the night

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Clock says midnight, hat says cheer
Got a paper crown and a pot of coffee here
Everybody's home with the tree all lit
I'm pushing down the hall in my rubber-soled kit

Double shift, double time, pick it up and go
Double shift, double time, the holiday glow
Nobody clapping but the monitor beep
I'll dance through the ward 'til I finally sleep

Room four needs a blanket, room six needs a smile
I skank to the pharmacy, brand-new style
The turkey's getting cold on the break-room plate
but a heartbeat's worth the running late

Double shift, double time, pick it up and go
Double shift, double time, the holiday glow
Nobody clapping but the monitor beep
I'll dance through the ward 'til I finally sleep

Horns up, scrubs on, here we go again
Two lives saved before the morning when
the sun comes up like a tip I'll never see
but somebody's grandma gets home 'cause of me

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
They gathered in the back of the Crooked Hen
four quiet men and a bottle of gin
the candle burned low and the deck was cold
and Tomas wagered the ring of old

Deal them down, deal them low
the luck of the night is a tide and a flow
but a man who bets what he cannot lose
will walk home barefoot, gone his shoes

The knave fell first, then the queen of spades
the coins all gathered in someone's trade
he staked the farm, then he staked his name
and the river card was a quiet flame

Deal them down, deal them low
the luck of the night is a tide and a flow
but a man who bets what he cannot lose
will walk home barefoot, gone his shoes

The morning found him on the frosted road
no ring, no farm, just the debt he owed
and the fiddles still play in the Crooked Hen
for the fool who'll wager it all again

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Walk in the door and the past comes alive
cinnamon swinging like nineteen-five
flour on the counter, butter on the air
Grandma's apron tied with a bow somewhere

Oh that smell, take me back, take me down
to the warmest little kitchen in town
sugar and the kettle and the radio low
that's the sweetest perfume I'll ever know

The bread's in the oven doing its thing
the whole house humming like a brass band swing
she taps her foot and she stirs the pot
and the years melt down like the chocolate she's got

Oh that smell, take me back, take me down
to the warmest little kitchen in town
sugar and the kettle and the radio low
that's the sweetest perfume I'll ever know

Now the house is empty and the stove is cold
but I bake her recipe to keep the gold
and when the cinnamon rises I can swear I see
Grandma in the doorway, swinging back to me

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Got a tank half full and a sky gone green
fastest little Ford that you ever seen
Mama said marry, Daddy said stay
but the wind comes calling and I drive away

Chasing the thunder, chasing the rain
I was born with a twister in my brain
A quiet little porch ain't no home to me
I'm married to the storm and the storm runs free

Saw a wall cloud spinning over Cherokee
dropped a funnel down like it knew my name
folks all running, I am running toward
the roar of the freight train, the trembling board

Chasing the thunder, chasing the rain
I was born with a twister in my brain
A quiet little porch ain't no home to me
I'm married to the storm and the storm runs free

One day the sky is gonna swallow me whole
bury my boots but it can't take my soul
string up a fiddle on the highway line
and play for the chaser who ran out of time

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete river, headlights run
not a patch of green beneath the sun
but down on the corner of Fifth and Grey
one stubborn root won't go away

Last one standing in the glass and stone
the last green heart that the city's known
break, drop low, let the bassline pound
for the last living tree in a paved-over town

They came with the saws and they came with the maps
measured the shadow for the future gaps
but the kids linked hands in a circle round
and the roots held tight to the buried ground

Last one standing in the glass and stone
the last green heart that the city's known
break, drop low, let the bassline pound
for the last living tree in a paved-over town

Leaves in the subwoofer, wind in the wire
photosynthesis against the fire
breathe in, breathe out, it's the only sound
that keeps a little oxygen around

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Neon bleeding on the wet glass street
fourth gear humming to a midnight beat
I didn't plan it but the wheel turned slow
down the avenue from years ago

Your window's dark, somebody new is there
but the streetlight's painting our old air
I just drive, I don't slow down
ghost of us in a sleeping town

Third floor left, where the curtains hung
where we were stupid and we were young
the radio plays what we used to play
and the dashboard glows a cold cassette blue-gray

Your window's dark, somebody new is there
but the streetlight's painting our old air
I just drive, I don't slow down
ghost of us in a sleeping town

Green light, go, don't look in the glass
some doors you only drive on past
the synth swells up and the city blurs
and I let the engine take what's hers

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Hands at ten and two, mi corazón
the engine trembles like a violin's song
you turn the key and the world holds its breath
I grip the door between life and death

Easy on the clutch, slow on the brake
this dance is yours now, for heaven's sake
I taught you to walk, I'll teach you to ride
then I'll let go and step aside

We glide through the lot in a stuttering sway
stall and recover, you laugh and you pray
your mother's same eyes in the rearview glass
and I feel all the years go racing past

Easy on the clutch, slow on the brake
this dance is yours now, for heaven's sake
I taught you to walk, I'll teach you to ride
then I'll let go and step aside

One day you'll drive to a life of your own
down some long road where I can't be shown
so turn the wheel, niña, take the lead
I'll be the rhythm beneath your speed

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every night at a quarter to ten
I walk the marble hall again
but I always stop at the same gold frame
where a painted girl don't know my name

You don't move, you don't speak
but you've owned my heart for a hundred weeks
oil and canvas, light and shade
falling for a love that a master made

The tourists come and the tourists go
they snap their pictures, they never know
that when the lights go down to a velvet blue
the loneliest guard is in love with you

You don't move, you don't speak
but you've owned my heart for a hundred weeks
oil and canvas, light and shade
falling for a love that a master made

They'll ship you off to a show downtown
and I'll guard an empty space on the wall
but I'll keep your eyes in the back of my mind
the truest thing in this whole damn hall

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon cross on a humming sign
coffee black and the booth is mine
when the whole world's locked and the streets are bare
there's a light still burning, somebody there

The doors stay open, the doors stay wide
come on in from the cold outside
no matter the hour, no matter the sin
there's a seat at the counter, come on in

The trucker, the drunk, and the broken-hearted
find their way where the night got started
Miss Ruby pours and she calls you 'baby'
like grace itself in a uniform, maybe

The doors stay open, the doors stay wide
come on in from the cold outside
no matter the hour, no matter the sin
there's a seat at the counter, come on in

Oh hallelujah for the all-night light
for the bottomless cup in the dead of night
when the churches are sleeping and the steeples are dim
there's a holy little diner taking sinners in

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the hold with a heavy crate
the captain said boys we can't be late
but the compass spun and the lanterns shook
and the cook went pale at the thing we took

Cursed cargo, get it off the boat
something down below that don't wanna float
funk so heavy it'll sink the deck
got a hex in the hull and a chill in the neck

The gulls turned back at the harbor mouth
the wind blew north when we sailed south
the crate it groans in the dead of three
and whatever's inside has its eyes on me

Cursed cargo, get it off the boat
something down below that don't wanna float
funk so heavy it'll sink the deck
got a hex in the hull and a chill in the neck

Drop it in the deep, let the bassline drown
let the wicked little freight go straight on down
slap that string like a prayer to the sea
cut the cursed cargo and set us free

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Dos años lejos, now the wheels touch down
duffel on my shoulder walking through the town
the same old corner, the same old breeze
but my hands still shake from the memories

Volví, volví, I'm coming home tonight
mama leave the porch with the yellow light
dembow in my chest where the fear used to be
put your arms around the soldier, set me free

Mi gente on the block, they call my name
but a part of me did not come home the same
still I'll dance in the street 'til the morning sun
'cause I made it back when the war was done

Volví, volví, I'm coming home tonight
mama leave the porch with the yellow light
dembow in my chest where the fear used to be
put your arms around the soldier, set me free

For the ones who stayed in the foreign ground
I'll carry your names through this hometown sound
levanta las manos, one time for the fallen
one more for the living and the future calling

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man sits where the water meets the sand
been reading these waves since the world began
he says boy, the ocean got a lot to say
if you sit real still at the break of day

Low tide, high tide, the moon pulls the line
the old man's teaching me to read the brine
I got salt in my hair and time on my side
learning all the secrets of the rolling tide

He lost his wife to a winter squall
but he don't curse the sea at all
he says she comes back in the morning spray
and the tide takes nothing it don't repay

Low tide, high tide, the moon pulls the line
the old man's teaching me to read the brine
I got salt in my hair and time on my side
learning all the secrets of the rolling tide

Now he's gone where the deep water rolls
but I sit on his rock and I read the shoals
and I'll teach my own son when he's grown enough
how the tide gives gentle and the tide gives rough

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent hum on a silent floor
midnight badge swipe through the steel door
the city sleeps in a frosted glow
but the ward lights burn and I'm on the go

Double shift under neon white
holding the dark through the holiday night
monitors pulse like a synth machine
I'm the only warm thing on the screen

Sixteen hours and the coffee's cold
gauze and saline and a hand to hold
out the window the snow comes down
on a sleeping, glittering, empty town

Double shift under neon white
holding the dark through the holiday night
monitors pulse like a synth machine
I'm the only warm thing on the screen

Drive home at dawn in the violet haze
engine humming through the after-days
nobody saw what the night-shift gave
but the morning's here and a life was saved

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Kitchen table and a flickering bulb
highlighter dried and the coffee dull
textbook open to a page I've read
ten times over inside my head

Two in the morning, the whole town's asleep
just me and the dreams that I'm trying to keep
every word I underline is a brick in the wall
of the someday me who'll have it all

The radiator clanks and the cat walks by
there's a half-moon hung in the windowsill sky
Mama's working doubles so I gotta come through
first in the family to see it through

Two in the morning, the whole town's asleep
just me and the dreams that I'm trying to keep
every word I underline is a brick in the wall
of the someday me who'll have it all

One more chapter, one more pot
of bitter black to keep what I got
the sun'll come up on a tired old me
but a little bit closer to who I'll be

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I threw my prayers in the kitchen trash
burned my Sunday in a holy crash
said God if you're real then where'd you go
left me dancing in the dark alone

Lost it, lost it, the groove went cold
traded my spirit for a bag of gold
but something in the bassline started to call
and brought me back to the light after all

Walked through the years with an empty chest
putting every easy faith to the test
then a stranger on a corner played a tambourine
and I felt a little something I ain't never seen

Found it, found it, the groove came home
ain't gotta wander these streets alone
the spirit's in the rhythm and the rhythm's in me
clap your hands, hallelujah, I can finally see

It wasn't in the steeple, wasn't in the book
it was down in the funk where I forgot to look
so shake it, shake it, let the doubt unwind
I lost my faith and then the faith found mine

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They chained our wrists, said move in time
Your heartbeat slamming against mine
I hate the way you count the steps
I hate that mine forget the rest

Gym floor sweat and broken light
We trade a glare instead of a stride
Every turn a thrown elbow
Every dip a place to let go

LEFT FOOT, RIGHT FOOT, swing me into the wall
I'd rather break than fall
But your grip won't let me fall
LEFT FOOT, RIGHT FOOT, hate is a kind of glue
I'm only this alive near you

The crowd wants blood, the song says spin
So we let the war back in
You drag, I shove, we keep the beat
Two enemies, one set of feet

What if the rhythm is the truce
What if the cage was always loose
I stop. You stop. The bass stops too.
I move first. So do you.

LEFT FOOT, RIGHT FOOT, swing me into the wall
We didn't break. We didn't fall.
We didn't break at all.

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Debajo de las calles duerme un corazón
Las campanas tiemblan con su respiración
We paved his shoulders into a plaza of stone
And sold him postcards of a home he never owned

The fountains spit where his breathing cracks the tile
Grandmothers cross themselves and walk another mile
We danced on his ribcage, we married on his spine
We poured the bells a hundred years of wine

Ay, gigante, sigue soñando, no despiertes hoy
The trumpets hold the city up, the city he carries slow
Ay, gigante, one yawn and the towers go
Sleep, sleep, sleep below

The mayor reads the tremors like a love letter unsigned
The vendors weigh their oranges against his closing eyes
We built our cathedrals where his pulse runs underground
And named our children after the thunder of his sound

Quizás él sueña con nosotros, pequeños y feroces
Maybe we are the lullaby that keeps the giant his
If he should turn, we'd be a rumor in the dust
So play, mariachi, play — he stays asleep for us

Ay, gigante, sigue soñando, no despiertes hoy
The trumpets hold the city up, the city he carries slow

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Four minutes on the clock and the giants up by ten
The whole town in the bleachers holding what we couldn't spend
We came in dollar jerseys, we came in borrowed shoes
But something in the rafters said we got nothing to lose

Oh, run the lane, oh, lift your eyes
The last shall be the first when the buzzer flies
We were counted out, we were left behind
Now watch what mercy does in the final time
Hallelujah, run the lane!

The coach got hands like scripture, the bench is on its feet
Every missed and broken season folded into this heat
We pass it like a promise, we move like answered prayer
The scoreboard ain't the gospel — the gospel's getting there

Oh, run the lane, oh, lift your eyes
The last shall be the first when the buzzer flies
We were counted out, we were left behind
Now watch what mercy does in the final time

Down by two, the ball is mine
Mama on her knees by the sideline
I don't need the win to know I'm saved
But Lord, just this once — let it fall, let it fall

Oh, run the lane, oh, lift your eyes
The last shall be the first when the buzzer flies
Hallelujah — it fell, it fell, it fell

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Green room smelled like spilled beer and ambition
Manager's text said tonight's the audition
Gold chain borrowed, confidence rented
Three hundred faces and a deal half-cemented
I spit the verse I wrote at fourteen on the stairs
Felt the room lean in like the city finally cared
A&R in the back nodding to my cadence
One yes away from rewriting my whole basement

So close I could taste the lights
So close I could spend the nights
I almost, I almost, I almost made it
Then the morning came and it faded
I almost, I almost — and I'll never hate it

He shook my hand, said "kid, we'll be in touch"
That phrase a coffin, I just trusted it too much
Waited by the phone like it owed me a future
Watched another rapper wear the deal like couture
But I remember how the bass felt in my chest
That one perfect night I was better than the rest
Didn't sign the paper but I signed it in my bones
That I was almost somebody before I came back home

They ask why I still grind at thirty-four
It's 'cause I touched the ceiling, then they shut the door
And a man who's felt the height don't fear the floor

So close I could taste the lights
So close I could spend the nights
I almost, I almost, I almost made it
Then the morning came and it faded

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Las puertas se cerraron y el aire se fue
The tunnel swallows the platform light
Mi corazón is a horse that won't obey
Kicking at my ribs in the underground night

The stranger's shoulder, the flicker of the sign
I count the poles like a rosary of steel
The trumpet in my chest plays a wounded line
Nobody sees the way I cannot feel

Ay, respira, respira, the train will reach the sky
This tunnel has an ending though I cannot see it now
Ay, respira, respira, I am not going to die
The mariachi in my blood plays loud, plays loud, plays loud

Tengo veinte estaciones hasta el sol
Twenty stops of breathing through my sleeve
I hum a song my abuela used to know
And grip the cold rail like it asked me to believe

Que suenen las trompetas en mi pecho asustado
Let the violins outrun the screaming wheels
I am a plaza at noon, I am not a closed door
And this body, this body — eventually it heals

Ay, respira, respira, the train will reach the sky
Doors open, sunlight, the giant lets me by

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
We split a corner store sandwich in two thousand nine
You drew a map of leaving on the back of my mind
Said you'd take the coast and I should take the call
I took the studio time and watched you take it all
You were rent money, you were a reason to wake
You were the only true thing in a city full of fake
Now your number's a museum I won't let myself tour
And I rap about everything except what it's for

You're the one that got away
The verse I wrote a hundred ways
Every hook is your name in a different key
The one that got, the one that got away from me

Seen you in a photo with a ring that ain't mine
Dude looks decent, guess that's the worst kind
I hit the booth and turned the heartbreak to a check
Bought a chain that weighs less than the words I can't collect
They say success is sweet but it tastes like your perfume
The night you packed your records and walked out of the room
I got the platinum plaque, I got the crowd that screams
I'd trade the whole top floor for the basement and your dreams

Maybe in another mix, another master, another take
We didn't make the choices that a younger me would make
But I press play on us when the building's finally still
And I let the silence answer 'cause I always will

You're the one that got away
The verse I wrote a hundred ways

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
La sirena sonó por última vez
The gates wore a chain like a funeral dress
Forty years my father fed that iron mouth
Now the smokestack's cold and the work moved south

The time clock froze at a quarter past goodbye
The lockers still hold a coat, a photo, a why
We built the trucks that built the road we leave on
Now the parking lot's a field with the lines all gone

Ay, fábrica, fábrica, who carries the men
When the whistle don't whistle and won't again
The trumpets play slow for the hands that made
The whole town's bread and the whole town's shade
Ay, fábrica, fábrica, fade, fade, fade

Los jóvenes empacan, the elders just stand
Reading the padlock like a line in the sand
My mother sold tamales at the five o'clock bell
Now she folds her apron, says, "mijo, all is well"

We are not the rust, we are not the debt
We are the song the machines don't get
Play it for the welder, play it for the line
We'll build something new from this borrowed time

Ay, fábrica, fábrica, who carries the men
We carry each other home again

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Ice on the windshield, sky cracking green
Coldest I been but the cleanest I seen
No signal up here, just the hum of the snow
Phone on three percent, let it go, let it go
Drove past the last gas station an hour ago
North till the asphalt turned into ghost
Sky started bleeding like a bruise gone bright
Green, purple, gold, dripping down the night

Lights, lights, dancing on the black (on the black)
Dancing on the cold like they ain't coming back
Green in the dark, gold on the snow
God left the studio light on, I know, I know
Lights, lights, ribbons in the sky
Never prayed before but I'm praying tonight

Killed the engine, stepped into the still
Breath turned to diamonds, time stood until
The whole sky a speaker and the bass is the silence
No flex out here, just the cold and the science
Aurora on my hood like a chain I can't hold
Worth more than the diamonds, worth more than the gold
Used to chase the lights of a city downtown
Now the only lights I need don't touch the ground

No flash, no flex, no crowd, no name
Just me and the sky and the green cold flame
If I never come back tell 'em where I went
Up where the lights spend everything they spent

Lights, lights, dancing on the black
Dancing on the cold like they ain't coming back

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The coffee's gone cold in the cup that he held
The clock keeps the time that he no longer tells
I buttoned my black dress, I unbuttoned it twice
The mirror shows a widow learning a new price

The casseroles sweat on the counter in rows
The doorbell forgets, the telephone knows
I dance with the broom in the kitchen at dawn
One, two, three — to the silence he's gone

Dance with me, ghost, in the gray of the day
The music's still playing, you've nothing to pay
Your hand on my waist is a draft from the hall
The last tango, my love, is no tango at all

The garden still wants what his hands used to give
The roses don't know they've a reason to live
I carry the chair to the window and weep
Then sweep up the petals he meant to keep

They say grief is a country with terrible weather
I'll learn its hard streets, I will live there forever
But first this slow turn on the cold kitchen floor
One more, mi amor — and then nevermore

Dance with me, ghost, in the gray of the day
The music's still playing, you've nothing to pay

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The radio's playing the songs about home
While I chart the vitals of a man all alone
There's tinsel on the nurses' station, half hung
And a turkey gone dry where the cafeteria's flung
My daughter's recital is three states away
She sang me the solo through the phone yesterday

Sixteen hours and the snow's coming down
