On the only lit windows left in this town
I'll hold the hand that the family can't hold
While the rest of the world goes home from the cold
Holiday, holiday, somebody stays
And it's me in these halls and these fluorescent days

Room four-oh-two doesn't have anyone left
So I sit for a minute and I lend her my breath
I know all the call lights like verses by heart
I know which alarms mean the end's gonna start
The coffee's burnt rubber, my shoes have gone flat
But nobody dies on this floor feeling like that

There's a baby born screaming down the hall on the third
First cry of the morning, the holiest word
I'll be tired tomorrow but I won't be ashamed
Of the lonely few hours where I stayed, where I stayed

Sixteen hours and the snow's coming down
On the only lit windows left in this town

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Rrrip goes the packing tape, one last screech
Empty shelf, dusty square where the photos used to reach
Mug I never washed, sock without its twin
Five years in this apartment fold up and fit right in

Tape it up, tape it up, last box by the door (hey!)
Nail holes in the wall where the memories were before (hey!)
Keys on the counter, give the room one spin
I'm checking out the way I never checked in

The radiator clanked like a drummer out of time
The neighbor played trombone every night at nine
The kitchen smelled like everyone I ever fed
Now it echoes back the lease that I just shed

Marker squeaks "KITCHEN," squeaks "STUFF," squeaks "WHO KNOWS"
Labeled all my chapters in a cardboard prose
The sunset through the blinds I'll never own
Draws stripes on the floor of a place that was home

Up-pick, off-beat, the empty room is loud (hey!)
Footsteps make an echo where the couch once allowed (hey!)
One deep breath, switch the lights to none
The box is sealed — and so the next one's begun

Tape it up, tape it up, last box by the door (hey!)
Goodbye little kingdom on the second floor

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Uh! Sweaty palms on a borrowed guitar, yeah
Sign-up sheet number twenty-two, so far
Coffee shop lights and a microphone lean
Greenest little player that the room's ever seen
Knees doing the rhythm I forgot to play
Voice came out a whisper, what a thing to say

Ooh, look at me now, look at where I stood
Shakin' in my sneakers in that little neighborhood
First open mic, baby, first time on the floor
Didn't know my groove was waiting at the door
Get down, get down, on the night I begun
I was terrible, baby — but I was the one

Dropped a verse, picked it up off the ground
Three polite claps was the sweetest little sound
Barista in the back gave a nod and a wink
First time I believed what I never dared think
The bass in my chest found a pocket and stayed
Walked out ten feet taller than the man who'd been afraid

Now the stage is a stadium, the lights cut deep
But the kid with the sign-up sheet is the company I keep
Every time the spotlight tries to swell my head
I remember twenty-two and what the silence said

Ooh, look at me now, look at where I stood
First open mic, baby — it was good, it was good

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
They carried Joe down Rampart in the noonday heat
The tuba led him slow on his last bare beat
Umbrellas black as river mud, handkerchiefs of white
The dirge came moanin' low like the end of every night

The preacher said his piece and the casket rolled on
The trumpet held a note for the man that's gone
Grandmama wept into a fan from the church
While the second line waited for the music to turn

Then the band cut loose, oh the band cut loose
Death don't get the last word on this avenue
From a cryin' to a struttin', from a grave to a groove
We bury you sad and we dance you through

The snare started skippin' like a stone on the bayou
Feet found a rhythm that the sorrow couldn't chew
Little kids twirlin' where the tears just dried
Joe woulda loved how his whole block cried and then flied

Spanish moss and a saxophone wail
A city that knows how to grieve and set sail
We don't fear the river 'cause the river's our friend
In this town, baby, even endings end and then begin again

So the band cut loose, oh the band cut loose
Death don't get the last word on this avenue

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They picked the man, they picked the date, they picked the dress
Folded up my whole damn life and called it happiness
Gold bangles on my wrists like a beautiful jail
Mama smiling at a future where my own heart fails
Suitcase by the window with the train at half past nine
First decision in my life that's mine, all mine

So I'm gone, baby, gone, on the funk of my own two feet
Gone, baby, gone, down a different city street
They arranged me a life but they forgot to ask
Get up, get out — I'm taking off the mask

Slipped the ring on the table where the tea went cold
Left a note in the language that my grandma told
The groove in my hips ain't a thing you can plan
I'm the author of the woman that I am
Uncle's gonna holler, auntie's gonna pray
But the bassline in my body's gotta lead the way

I'm not the daughter in the photograph
I'm not the price somebody gets to do the math
Love me when I come back as a woman, not a deal
Till then watch me move to the way that I feel

So I'm gone, baby, gone, on the funk of my own two feet
Gone, baby, gone — and the rhythm's finally sweet

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
The tether snapped at 0-4 and nobody's to blame (hey!)
Your helmet light's a firefly now, slowly calling my name
We trained two years on solid ground to never let this be
Now you're a shrinking little star and the star is leaving me

Drift, drift, off the off-beat, into the endless blue (hey!)
I'm reaching with a glove that won't ever reach to you
Up-pick on the radio, your voice goes thin and small
Don't go quiet, don't go quiet — keep the music after all

We used to count the cupolas and joke about the views
Now the silence between heartbeats is the only thing we use
I'd swim the vacuum if I could, I'd skank across the black
But physics plays a cruel horn and there's no rhythm bringing back

Your oxygen's a metronome that's slowing down to none
You tell me look at Earth instead, how it's prettier than the sun
The brass section in my chest cracks a note it shouldn't hold
Two specks in the dark getting colder, getting old

So sing it, sing it, while the signal's still alive (hey!)
A little upstroke melody so one of us survives
I'll carry every offbeat that we ever played, I swear
Drift sweet, drift slow — I'll meet you in the air

Drift, drift, off the off-beat, into the endless blue
The band plays on without you — and I'll play it too

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The record's spinning slow tonight, the band has packed away
But we've got one more rhythm left, one more song to sway
Forty years of off-beat hearts, upstrokes in the dark
Your hand still finds my shoulder like the very first remark

So dance with me, one final time
Let the horns ring soft and low
We were never quite in time
But we never let it go
Dance with me, one final time

The trombone hums a lullaby for everything we built
The creaking floor, the wedding dress, the quilt we never quilt
Your laughter is the only beat I ever cared to keep
We're tired now and the night is long and morning's getting deep

So dance with me, one final time
Let the horns ring soft and low
We were never quite in time
But we never let it go
Dance with me, one final time

Pick it up, pick it up, the way we used to do
One more skank across the floor, just me and you
When the music finally fades I'll still be holding you

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The sun is going down on the final night
Dust in the air and the speakers ignite
Strangers became my family by dawn
Tomorrow we leave but the feeling lives on

One more time, let the bassline take control
One more night, feel it deep inside your soul
Hands up high, we are never letting go
Last night of forever, let the music flow

The lights are painting colors on the crowd
Every heartbeat pounding, every soul out loud
We danced through the rain and we danced through the heat
This is the moment where the whole world meets

One more time, let the bassline take control
One more night, feel it deep inside your soul
Hands up high, we are never letting go
Last night of forever, let the music flow

When the morning comes we'll carry this away
The memory of the music and the way we sway
Close your eyes and feel it, let the rhythm stay

One more time, let the bassline take control
One more night, feel it deep inside your soul

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Used to lift me on your shoulders, daddy, tall as any tree
Now your hands they shake a little when you pour the tea for me
The years they roll like ocean waves, slow and steady on the shore
And I see the man who raised me isn't quite the same no more

So grow old slow, my father, grow old slow
There's so much that I still need you to know
The sun is setting golden, take it easy, take it slow
I ain't ready yet to let my father go

You taught me how to stand up straight and how to face the rain
Now I'm holding up your elbow walking down the lane
Every wrinkle tells a story, every grey hair is a song
I was always your reflection, now I'm trying to be strong

So grow old slow, my father, grow old slow
There's so much that I still need you to know
The sun is setting golden, take it easy, take it slow
I ain't ready yet to let my father go

Jah knows the time is borrowed, every breath a gift
Sit with me a little longer, let the morning drift

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Debajo de la calle duerme un gigante de piedra
The towers rise up high but the ground remembers
We're dancing on his shoulders, baby, feel the bass
One day he's gonna wake and shake this whole place

La ciudad no duerme, but the giant does
Mueve, mueve, feel the rumble underneath us
Dance on the bones of a sleeping king tonight
Until the morning comes and brings the light

Neon in the windows, corazón on fire
Every beat we drop is climbing higher and higher
They built it all on something that they couldn't see
The giant's gonna rise and set the whole world free

La ciudad no duerme, but the giant does
Mueve, mueve, feel the rumble underneath us
Dance on the bones of a sleeping king tonight
Until the morning comes and brings the light

Siente el temblor, the heartbeat of the deep
We're living on a legend that refuses just to sleep
Dale, dale, dale, hasta el amanecer

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Boots on the tarmac, finally I'm back
The same old streets but my mind's still under attack
Mama at the door with tears in her eyes
I survived the desert but a part of me died
Dog tags heavy, memories heavier still
Every loud noise sends me back over the hill
I carried my brothers, I carried the weight
Now I'm carrying the silence, learning to relate

I'm home, but home don't feel the same
I'm home, but I'm still saying their names
Through the smoke and the fire I made it back alive
Now I'm fighting a new war just to survive

They shake my hand and call me a hero
But at 3am my hope is down to zero
The flashbacks hit like rounds from a gun
Trying to be a father, trying to be someone
Little by little I'm finding my way
Learning how to live another peaceful day

I'm home, but home don't feel the same
I'm home, but I'm still saying their names
Through the smoke and the fire I made it back alive
Now I'm fighting a new war just to survive

One step at a time, I'm reclaiming my mind
Leaving the war zone and the ghosts behind

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I drove out to the cemetery, knelt down in the rain
Said the words I couldn't say while you were still in pain
All the anger that I carried, all the years that I held tight
I'm laying it all down beside you in the fading light

I forgive you, oh, I forgive you now
Even though you're gone I'm letting go somehow
The hardest thing I've ever done is set this burden free
I forgive you, and in forgiving, I forgive me

There's no letter you can answer, there's no call you'll ever take
But I needed you to hear me for my own sweet sake
The bitterness was poison and it only hurt my soul
So I'm handing it to heaven, finally feeling whole

I forgive you, oh, I forgive you now
Even though you're gone I'm letting go somehow
The hardest thing I've ever done is set this burden free
I forgive you, and in forgiving, I forgive me

Maybe you can hear me where the angels softly sing
Maybe peace is something that the morning's gonna bring
I release it, I release you, let it rise on broken wings

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the streets above him, never knew he was there
A giant in his slumber breathing slow beneath the square
I feel him when I'm walking, feel him in my chest
This whole city's resting on a heart that's never rest

We're living on a giant, baby, can you feel him breathe?
Every streetlight flicker, every shiver underneath
Don't wake him, don't wake him, let him dream his ancient dream
We're dancing on the surface of a love beneath the seam

The rivers are his memories, the towers are his crown
A thousand years of sleeping while the world built up and down
I press my hand against the ground and whisper to the deep
Keep us safe and keep us steady in your everlasting sleep

We're living on a giant, baby, can you feel him breathe?
Every streetlight flicker, every shiver underneath
Don't wake him, don't wake him, let him dream his ancient dream
We're dancing on the surface of a love beneath the seam

If he ever opens up his eyes and rises from the stone
I hope he knows we loved this place we built and called our home

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody believed us when we started out
Last place on the table, full of fear and doubt
Now it's the final whistle minutes from the end
Everything we fought for coming round the bend

This is it, the final game, the final chance
Feel the pulse, feel the rhythm, feel the crowd advance
We were never meant to make it but we're here tonight
Underdogs forever, rising to the fight

Sweat and blood and broken bones, we gave it all
Every time they knocked us down we answered the call
The clock is ticking faster and the bassline drops
This is our moment and it never stops

This is it, the final game, the final chance
Feel the pulse, feel the rhythm, feel the crowd advance
We were never meant to make it but we're here tonight
Underdogs forever, rising to the fight

Break it down, break it down, the moment's now
We'll find a way to win it, we don't care how
Rise up, rise up, take a final bow

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
The stadium lights are burning like a funeral pyre tonight
A ragged band of nobodies come crawling for the fight
The preacher man done left us, and the devil took the rest
But we've got one last quarter left to put us to the test

We're the last ones standing on this cold and bitter ground
The whole world wrote our story but they never heard the sound
Of men who got no business winning, swinging for the grave
One final game, oh Lord, and we ain't praying to be saved

My daddy died a loser and they say I'll do the same
But there's fire in this graveyard and there's thunder in my name
The scoreboard reads our sentence and the clock is running thin
But underdogs and dead men, we got nothing left to win

We're the last ones standing on this cold and bitter ground
The whole world wrote our story but they never heard the sound
Of men who got no business winning, swinging for the grave
One final game, oh Lord, and we ain't praying to be saved

So ring the bell and dig the hole and let the crows come down
We'll either leave as legends or we'll never leave this town

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Saltwater kisses and a sunset glow
We met in July when the tide was low
You wore a smile like a summer song
And I knew right then it wouldn't last long

Oh, summer love, by the sea
Fleeting as the foam, wild and free
We danced barefoot on the shore
Knowing we'd never need much more

The boardwalk lights and the cool night air
Your hand in mine without a care
We knew September would call your name
But for now the moonlight set us aflame

Oh, summer love, by the sea
Fleeting as the foam, wild and free
We danced barefoot on the shore
Knowing we'd never need much more

Now the leaves are turning and you're far away
But I still taste the salt of that golden day
A summer fling, a memory sweet
Where the ocean and the sky would meet

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning, the highway hums low
Your voice on the line through the radio glow
A thousand miles of midnight, the white lines drift
I'm calling you softly, my only gift

Are you sleeping, are you dreaming of me?
The diesel and the starlight, the long lonely sea
Of asphalt and shadow, I'm coming back home
Don't hang up the phone, don't leave me alone

The coffee's gone cold and the cab is so wide
Your breathing's a lullaby keeping me alive
The gas stations flicker like ghosts in the dark
Your love is the compass, your love is the spark

Are you sleeping, are you dreaming of me?
The diesel and the starlight, the long lonely sea
Of asphalt and shadow, I'm coming back home
Don't hang up the phone, don't leave me alone

Floating on the freeway, dissolving into blue
Every mile I'm driving is a mile back to you

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Floating in the silence, just you and the void
The tether came loose and there's nothing to avoid
I watch you getting smaller, a light in the black
Reaching out my hand but there's no pulling back

Drifting, drifting, slow and far
Two lonely souls beneath the same dead star
No gravity to hold us, no ground to land
Just the cold and quiet and your fading hand

The oxygen is humming, the radio's gone dead
All the words we never said are floating in my head
The Earth is just a marble, a memory of blue
And I'm spinning through the darkness still thinking of you

Drifting, drifting, slow and far
Two lonely souls beneath the same dead star
No gravity to hold us, no ground to land
Just the cold and quiet and your fading hand

Maybe in a million years we'll cross the same sky
Two bits of stardust waving goodbye

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I came back to the old town where the river runs slow
Never thought I'd find you in a place I used to know
We grew up on the same streets but I never saw your face
Now you're all that I'm thinking of in this familiar place

Hometown girl, you set my heart on fire
Everything I ran from is everything I desire
I traveled all around the world just to find
The love I needed all along was right here all this time

The diner where we're sitting still smells just the same
And every time you laugh you set my soul aflame
These small town nights got bigger with you by my side
The road I always ran from feels like home tonight

Hometown girl, you set my heart on fire
Everything I ran from is everything I desire
I traveled all around the world just to find
The love I needed all along was right here all this time

Maybe home was never just a place to leave behind
Maybe home was just the love I needed time to find

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
We stood on opposite sides of every line they drew
Your fire against my thunder, my old against your new
But the music started playing and they pushed us to the floor
Two enemies now holding what we swore we'd both ignore

So we move, we move, against our will
Two storms colliding, suddenly still
Every step a battle, every turn a truce
In the space between us something breaks loose

Your hand is cold and rigid, mine is shaking just the same
We count the beats like soldiers, too proud to say a name
But somewhere in the turning, somewhere in the sway
The war we thought we needed slowly fades away

So we move, we move, against our will
Two storms colliding, suddenly still
Every step a battle, every turn a truce
In the space between us something breaks loose

Louder now, the silence breaks, the swelling tide
Two rivals turning rivers, dancing side by side
And when the music rises we forget to hide

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
Neon crosses flicker in the rain-soaked night
I prayed to empty heavens till I lost the light
Driving through the darkness with a hollow chest
Every star had fallen, I was put to the test

But I'm rising, rising, through the electric haze
Found a spark of something in these broken days
I lost my faith in the cold and the chrome
Now a light is calling and it's leading me home

The city hummed its sermons in a synthesized choir
I was numb and empty, a circuit without fire
Then a voice in the static, then a warmth in the cold
Something bigger than the silence took ahold

But I'm rising, rising, through the electric haze
Found a spark of something in these broken days
I lost my faith in the cold and the chrome
Now a light is calling and it's leading me home

Through the midnight and the neon, through the doubt and the fear
I believe again, I feel it, something holy's drawing near

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
We used to dance the floor down at the Roseland Hall
You'd spin me till the saxophone would call
Now my phone lights up a quarter after two
Three little words and not a one was you

No perfume on a letter, no rose left at my door
Just a buzzing little screen down on the floor
"It's over," lowercase, you didn't even sign
Four years of slow piano in a single dotted line

Oh, you texted me goodbye, baby, texted me goodbye
Didn't tip your hat or even meet my eye
The band plays on without you, the brass still hits it sweet
But I'm dancing with a phone now in the middle of the street

I tried to type you something with a little bit of swing
Delete, delete, delete — I couldn't make it sing

Somewhere there's a clarinet still holding out a note
Somewhere there's the cufflink that you left inside my coat
But the message has been read and the message won't reply
So I'll snap my fingers, darling, and I'll waltz on by

Oh, you texted me goodbye, baby, texted me goodbye
The needle's on the record and the night is rolling by
The band plays on without you, the floor is mine to keep
So I'll two-step through the heartbreak till I'm tired enough to sleep

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
I got one suitcase and a tank that's running low
The sign says Hartsville, twelve more miles to go
I counted every streetlight, I know them all by name
But a man can drown out here in all this same and same

I'm leaving, leaving, leaving this town behind
Leaving the dead-end and the worried-down kind
My mama's on the porch and she won't watch me go
But the highway's calling, Lord, and I got to follow that road

The diner where I broke my heart, the church where I was saved
The river took my brother and it's slow and it's gray
There ain't no work but waiting and the waiting wears you thin
So I'm gonna kiss this gravel and I won't come back again

They'll say he thought he's better, they'll say he run away
But you don't know the weight of it until you can't stay
Goodbye to the water tower with my old initials carved
Goodbye to being hungry in a place that kept me starved

I'm leaving, leaving, leaving this town behind
Gonna find me a city where nobody knows my mind
The rearview's getting smaller and the engine's running true
Lord, I love you, little Hartsville, but I'm done loving you

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Three dots on the screen, then they disappear
Four in the morning and you not here
Last text said "we good" with a broken heart
Now you read receipts but you won't reply, that's the hardest part
I'm in the Wraith, foot down, ridin' through the rain
Screen lightin' up my face, but it's never your name

You left me on read, you left me on read
Said forever, then you typed it all dead
No callback, no closure, just a gray little line
You ended the whole world on a six-inch design
You left me on read, you left me on read
I loved you in person, you killed it in text instead

Used to wake up to good morning, hearts and a sun
Now it's typing, then nothing, now the typing is done
I screenshot the last one, I stare till it blur
Delete the whole thread, then I download her

Swear I felt it in my chest like a notification buzz
You ain't even say it loud, you just left it 'cause
It's easier to ghost than to look in my eyes
So the love of my life became a thread I despise

You left me on read, you left me on read
Said forever, then you typed it all dead
No callback, no closure, just a gray little line
You ended the whole world on a six-inch design

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Grandpa had hands like the rope on the dock
Weathered and steady, slow as the clock
He'd walk me to the water before the sun was high
Said, "Watch how she breathes now, learn her by and by"

In, out, the ocean keeps the time
He'd count it on his fingers like a nursery rhyme

He taught me the tide, he taught me to wait
That everything leaving comes back if you're patient and late
Pull of the moon, push of the shore
He taught me the tide, and oh, I don't fear it anymore

Now his boots are empty by the kitchen door
And I walk down alone where we walked before
But the water still rises right on the cue
Like it kept all the lessons that he gave to me and you

Low tide, I miss him, the sand stretching wide
High tide, I feel him come back with the tide
He never said "goodbye," he just said "see the sea"
And every twelve hours, he comes home to me

He taught me the tide, he taught me to wait
That everything leaving comes back if you're patient and late
Pull of the moon, push of the shore
He taught me the tide, and oh, I don't fear it anymore

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The casseroles are sweating on the counter in a row
Somebody's car is leaving, I can hear the gravel go
I slept in yesterday's black shirt, the collar still undone
And the curtain's full of nothing but a watered-down sun

Wash it over, wash it over, let the white noise hold me down
Everybody's gone home now to the rest of their own towns
The coffee's going cold and I forgot to make a sound
Wash it over, wash it over, I'm the only one around

There's a thumbprint on the photo where they held it up to cry
There's a casket-spray of lilies turning brown beside the sky
The phone won't stop its buzzing with the sorry-for-your-loss
And I'm standing in the doorway with my arms across

The house still smells like Sunday, like the kitchen and the prayer
I keep waiting for the footsteps on the landing of the stair
Reverb in the hallway, just the fridge and just my breath
This is what they never tell you, the long morning after death

Wash it over, wash it over, let the guitars take the weight
Everybody's gone home now and they couldn't make me wait
I'll dissolve into the wallpaper, I'll float above the plate
Wash it over, wash it over, it's too early and too late

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Neon on the felt, two a.m. in the back
The dealer's wearing mirrors, I can't read his track
I came in with a paycheck, I came in with a grin
Then the ante kept on climbing and I kept doubling in

Deal me one more, deal me one more card
The city's electric and the playing it hard
I bet the keys, I bet the years, I bet the rising sun
Deal me one more, deal me one more — I can't fold what I've begun

The chips became the title, the title became the deed
His eyes lit up magenta at the smell of my need
The clock above the table running backwards on the wall
And I pushed it all to center, every chip and every all

A queen of broken neon, a jack with no face
The whole machine was humming as I bet my place
I saw it in his sunglasses — the cards were already turned
But the chrome was so beautiful I let the whole thing burn

Deal me one more, deal me one more card
The city's electric and the playing it hard
I lost the keys, I lost the years, I lost the rising sun
Deal me one more, deal me one more — there's nothing left to run

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Clock on the dashboard says a quarter to free
Me and the crew got the whole day, can't you see
Gas tank empty but the speakers still bump
Last lake, last sun, last hometown jump

One more day, one more day in the sun
Before the boxes and the dorms and the growing-up's begun
Turn it up, turn it loose, let the bassline run
It's the last day of summer, baby, give me one more one

Darnell brought the grill and Renee brought the rhythm
We danced on the hood till the neighbors couldn't fit 'em
September's at the door with a suitcase and a tie
But the funk don't quit and neither do I

Slap the bass, kiss your friends, watch the streetlights come on
Tomorrow we're a hundred different highways gone
So hold the groove a second, let it stretch and let it shine
We'll never be this young and this together one more time

One more day, one more day in the sun
Before the boxes and the dorms and the growing-up's begun
Turn it up, turn it loose, let the bassline run
It's the last day of summer, baby, give me one more one

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I sold my daddy's table and my mama's wedding ring
Took a third-class ticket and the clothes that I could bring
The harbor's full of weeping and the gulls all cry my name
And the land that bore me's burning, but the leaving's still a shame

Cross the water, cross the water, where the green don't grow
I'm a stranger going stranger to a place I'll never know
My heart's still on the dock back home, my body's on the bow
Cross the water, cross the water, ain't no turning now

The captain says three weeks of gray if the weather treats us kind
I got a brother in the hold and a sister left behind
The ocean's wide as sorrow and it's deep as what I owe
And I sing to keep from sinking as I watch the homeland go

They'll change my name on paper when I reach that other shore
They'll call me by a stranger's word I never heard before
But I'll keep my mother's language like a coal beneath my tongue
And I'll teach it to the children of the country I'm becoming

Cross the water, cross the water, where the green don't grow
I'm a stranger going stranger to a place I'll never know
My heart's still on the dock back home, my body's on the bow
Cross the water, cross the water, ain't no turning now

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
We drove out to the quarry where we used to waste the heat
Your car's got my initials carved in the back of the seat
The radio plays static and a song we half-recall
And tomorrow's got a moving truck parked outside the hall

So don't say it, don't say goodbye just yet
Let the sun go down slow on the things we won't forget
We were never gonna last but we were never gonna end
The last day of summer with my oldest, dumbest friend

You're going east and I'm going somewhere with a name
We both swear that the texting's gonna keep us all the same
But we know how it goes, man, we've watched the others leave
The phone calls getting shorter till there's nothing to retrieve

Throw a rock into the water, watch it sink the way we will
Into separate September, into rooms we have to fill
But for one more hour, the light's still gold and low
And nobody's a stranger yet, so please don't make me go

So don't say it, don't say goodbye just yet
Let the sun go down slow on the things we won't forget
We were never gonna last but we were never gonna end
The last day of summer with my oldest, dumbest friend

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man on the seawall, fist against the spray
Seventy-five winters and he won't move out the way
They built a wall of concrete, said the water's gonna rise
He just laughed at the council with the salt all in his eyes

He teaches the tide! He teaches the tide!
You can't sell the ocean and you can't make it hide!
In and out, push and pull, you can't legislate the moon!
He teaches the tide and the tide's coming soon!

Kids come down to mock him, then they sit down in a ring
He shows them how she breathes, how she takes and how she brings
No permit and no textbook, just his hands and just the foam
The meanest, oldest punk rock teacher's making the sea his home

They can pave the whole damn coastline, build it taller, build it proud
He just points out at the water and he says it good and loud:
She was here before your fathers, she'll be here when you're gone
So learn her now or drown, kid — the tide keeps rolling on!

He teaches the tide! He teaches the tide!
You can't sell the ocean and you can't make it hide!
In and out, push and pull, you can't legislate the moon!
He teaches the tide and the tide's coming soon!

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The rooms all echo different when there's nothing on the floor
Found a crayon in the closet and a scuff behind the door
The tape gun screams its last one as I fold the cardboard tight
Twelve years in this kitchen folded down to one last night

Tape up the last box, write the word KITCHEN on the side
Load it in the truck bed with the things I couldn't hide
The key's gonna fit a lock that isn't mine no more
Tape up the last box and I'll close the kitchen door

There's a pencil line of heights climbing up the pantry frame
I couldn't bring myself to wash it, so I left it with my name
The new ones move in Sunday with their own kind of light
But tonight the house is mine still, in the bare-bulb amber night

It's funny how a home is just a box you live inside
Till the day you box the box up and you take it for a ride
I'll miss the way the morning came in crooked through the glass
But you can't tape up a window and you can't tape up the past

Tape up the last box, write the word KITCHEN on the side
Load it in the truck bed with the things I couldn't hide
The key's gonna fit a lock that isn't mine no more
Tape up the last box and I'll close the kitchen door

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Gallery seven, the lights go low
Fifteen years and the same girl in the gold
She's oil on canvas, she's three hundred years old
But she looks back at me like she's breaking the mold

Just me and her when the doors all close
The alarm hums low and the city slows
She never speaks but I swear she knows
The guard and the painting where nobody goes

Footsteps echo down the marble hall
My radio crackles, my shadow walks the wall
They'd think I'm crazy if I told them all
That the only one who waits for me is hung up on the wall

Midnight rolls and the bassline runs
Past every frame and the sleeping suns
I clock my round but I always come
Back to gallery seven where it all begun

They'll retire me out in the coming spring
Give me a watch and a handshake thing
But who's gonna stand in the dark and sing
To a girl in a frame who can't feel a thing?

Just me and her when the doors all close
The alarm hums low and the city slows
She never speaks but I swear she knows
The guard and the painting where nobody goes

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Volví al pueblo donde me crié, to the dust and the bell
The plaza where the fountain and the old stories dwell
And there you were, Rosa, with your basket and your shawl
The girl I never noticed when we both were too small

¡Ay, ay, ay! I came home to remember
And I found you in September like a slow-burning ember
The trumpets, they cry it, the whole town can tell
I fell for my hometown when I fell for you as well

They said the city changed me, said I'd never return
But my heart had a lesson that it waited to learn
You pour the coffee strong, girl, in your mother's café
And I'd cross every border just to sit there all day

The guitarrón is beating like the road back to here
The violins are weeping and they're weeping for years
I chased the whole world over for a love I could find
In the cobblestone and church bells I had left behind

¡Ay, ay, ay! I came home to remember
And I found you in September like a slow-burning ember
The trumpets, they cry it, the whole town can tell
I fell for my hometown when I fell for you as well

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The tinsel's on the nurse's station, drooping low and gray
It's Christmas in the ICU and I'm working through the day
My shift bled into another, sixteen hours on my feet
And the monitors are singing in a slow electric beat

Lord, hold the line for me, I'm holding it for them
The dying and the barely and the ones who won't again
The whole world's home and feasting while the snow comes down like lead
And I'm wiping down a fever in a cold and narrow bed

Room four lost her husband and she's got no one to call
So I sat and held her shaking in the holy hollow hall
My children's gifts are waiting and they're wrapped and they're alone
But somebody's got to answer when the call light's all they own

There's blood beneath my fingernails and salt beneath my eyes
I've seen more last breaths leaving than I've seen the sun to rise
They'll never write a carol for the ones who work the night
But the angels in the hallway wear a tired plastic white

Lord, hold the line for me, I'm holding it for them
The dying and the barely and the ones who won't again
The whole world's home and feasting while the snow comes down like lead
And I'm wiping down a fever in a cold and narrow bed

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Young Tam took the back road with a barrel in the cart
The mountain dew was strong that night and so was Tammy's heart
His father ran it years before and never lost a drop
But the moon was bright as morning and the law was at the top

Run, boys, run, down the glen and through the trees
The still is in the hollow and the whiskey's on the breeze
But the river's running higher than the river ought to run
And the moonshine took young Tammy 'fore the night was done

The wheel it caught a boulder where the ford had washed away
The horse went down a-screaming and the barrels rolled astray
He grabbed for one in darkness as the water filled his boots
The glen don't give back what it takes among the tangled roots

They found the cart by daylight but they never found the lad
Just a broken jug of liquor and the only horse he had
So if you walk the hollow when the harvest moon is high
You'll hear young Tammy whistling where the cold black waters lie

Run, boys, run, down the glen and through the trees
The still is in the hollow and the whiskey's on the breeze
But the river's running higher than the river ought to run
And the moonshine took young Tammy 'fore the night was done

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up the hold at midnight, never asked them what was inside
The crate was nailed and groaning, and the captain wouldn't meet my eyes
The sea was calm as glass that morning, by the noon it turned to lead
And the cook he started whispering 'bout the names of all the dead

Oh this cargo's cursed, Lord, it's dragging us down
Gonna ride this freight to the bottom, gonna ride this freight and drown
Every wave's a warning, every mile's a debt I owe
Got a cursed cargo in the belly and a thousand miles to go

The compass spins like a drunkard, and the stars all moved their place
There's a shadow in the rigging wearing somebody's stolen face
The rats went over the railing, they knew something we did not
Now the water's tasting bitter and the timbers all turned hot

Oh this cargo's cursed, Lord, it's dragging us down
Gonna ride this freight to the bottom, gonna ride this freight and drown
Every wave's a warning, every mile's a debt I owe
Got a cursed cargo in the belly and a thousand miles to go

I'd throw it overboard at dawn but the chains won't come undone
It wants to make the harbor, it ain't finished what it's begun
So I'll steer her through the darkness with my hands around the wheel
And pray the shore forgets us 'fore it learns the way we feel

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody picked us, son, they laughed when we walked in
Cheap shoes on the hardwood and the odds against our skin
We practiced in the gravel, we practiced in the rain
Now it's down to forty minutes and the whole town knows our name

We're the long shot, baby, with our backs against the wall
Gonna leave it on the floor tonight, gonna give it our all
They can keep their trophies, they can keep their crown
But they'll remember the night the little guys wouldn't lie down

The clock's a hungry monster, it's eating up the years
My knees done told me stories that I told 'em through my tears
One more inbound pass, one more shot to fall
One more chance to prove we were ever here at all

We're the long shot, baby, with our backs against the wall
Gonna leave it on the floor tonight, gonna give it our all
They can keep their trophies, they can keep their crown
But they'll remember the night the little guys wouldn't lie down

Don't tell me 'bout the scoreboard, don't tell me 'bout the spread
Tell me 'bout the fire still burning in this old gray head
Win or lose the buzzer, we already won the war
We showed up when nobody thought we'd walk through the door

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Shoo-bop, shoo-bop, the harbor's growing small
I'm waving to my mama and the gray old garden wall
A cardboard suitcase, a photo and a prayer
A new world waiting somewhere in the salt and ocean air

Oh oh oh, across the sea
A brand new life is calling out to me
Farewell my sweetheart, don't you cry
I'll send for you, my darling, by and by (by and by)

The steamer hums a love song to the rolling midnight foam
I keep your handkerchief tucked close, it smells a little like home
Fifteen days of water, fifteen nights of stars
Dreaming of a little house and lamplight in the dark

Oh oh oh, across the sea
A brand new life is calling out to me
Farewell my sweetheart, don't you cry
I'll send for you, my darling, by and by (by and by)

Sha-la-la, I see the lights, the city's drawing near
My heart is half a mile behind, my hope is standing here
Whatever waits on that far shore, I'll build it brick by brick
With your name upon my lips and a dollar in my fist

Oh oh oh, across the sea
A brand new life is calling out to me
Farewell my sweetheart, don't you cry
I'll send for you, my darling, by and by (by and by)

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The city lights are dancing while I'm pinned beneath these halls
The pager beats a rhythm and the monitors all call
My coffee's gone to ashes, the clock has lost its grace
While somewhere there's a table set without me in my place

Dance with me, fluorescent night, one more hour, one more round
My feet still keep the tempo though my heart is on the ground
Another chart, another door, another fevered hand
I'm the partner no one chose tonight, and still I take this stand

The holiday is glowing in a window far away
The children open presents while the dying ones still pray
I hold a stranger's fingers as the midnight bells resound
And that's the closest thing to home that I have ever found

Dance with me, fluorescent night, one more hour, one more round
My feet still keep the tempo though my heart is on the ground
Another chart, another door, another fevered hand
I'm the partner no one chose tonight, and still I take this stand

So lead me, weary morning, with your pale and trembling light
I gave away my Christmas to the keeping of the night
And if no one ever thanks me, let the silence be my song
I was here, I held the line, while the whole world danced along

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Neon on the dashboard, your hands at ten and two
The parking lot's an ocean and the whole night's stretched for you
I remember holding bottles, now I'm holding back my fear
The engine's humming purple and the future's drawing near

Drive, just drive, into the violet glow
I'll let go of the wheel, little girl, but I won't let you go
Check your mirrors, feel the road, the city's all aflame
Every streetlight says your name, every streetlight says your name

You stall it at the crossing and you laugh until you cry
A thousand glowing windows and a tape-deck lullaby
I taught you how to tie your shoes, I taught you how to fall
Now I'm teaching you to leave me, and that's the hardest one of all

Drive, just drive, into the violet glow
I'll let go of the wheel, little girl, but I won't let you go
Check your mirrors, feel the road, the city's all aflame
Every streetlight says your name, every streetlight says your name

One day you'll take this highway and I won't be in the seat
Just a voice inside the engine and a memory of heat
So signal, breathe, and merge, my love, into the endless blue
The road belongs to you now, and the road belongs to you

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
My daddy made a promise to a man across the hill
A wedding and a fortune and a life against my will
They measured me for satin, they counted out the land
But they never thought to ask me for the wanting in my hand

So I'm running down the holler with the morning on my heels
Gonna trade their golden cages for whatever freedom feels
Let 'em keep the family name and the deed they had it signed
I'm leaving that arranged tomorrow and the whole of it behind

The fiddle in my satchel and a biscuit in my coat
The river's gonna carry every promise that they wrote
My mama waved a hankie, half a curse and half a blessing
And the road don't care for fortunes, son, the road just keeps on stretching

So I'm running down the holler with the morning on my heels
Gonna trade their golden cages for whatever freedom feels
Let 'em keep the family name and the deed they had it signed
I'm leaving that arranged tomorrow and the whole of it behind

Maybe I'll be hungry, maybe I'll be poor
But every step's my own step out the only open door
I'd rather pick the banjo cold and sleep beneath the pine
Than wear a borrowed future that was never gonna be mine

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the diner 'fore the sun comes up to shine
Clock out and I'm driving to the second of the line
My hands smell like the fryer and the warehouse and the rain
And the numbers in the checkbook never seem to come out plain

Two jobs, no sleep, and the bills keep coming round
I'm running just as fast as I can run to stay on solid ground
They say the Lord helps those who help themselves, well I been trying, Lord
But I'm working twice as hard for half of what I can't afford

My little girl asked Daddy why you're never really here
I said I'm chasing something, baby, getting nowhere near
The rent went up in springtime, the truck broke down in fall
And the harder that I'm grinding, the more I seem to crawl

Two jobs, no sleep, and the bills keep coming round
I'm running just as fast as I can run to stay on solid ground
They say the Lord helps those who help themselves, well I been trying, Lord
But I'm working twice as hard for half of what I can't afford

I ain't asking for a mansion, I ain't asking for a throne
Just a quiet Sunday morning and a roof to call my own
So I'll tie my boots tomorrow and I'll do it all again
And pray that someday catching up ain't only just pretend

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Tether snapped at zero-three, your voice is going thin
I watch the station shrinking with the silence closing in
We trained for every failure, every drill, every code
But no one wrote the manual for letting someone go

We're drifting, drifting, two lights in the dark
The distance keeps on doubling and it's tearing us apart
Reach for me, reach for me, across the widening black
I'm falling up forever and there's no way back

Your signal's breaking sideways, your oxygen runs low
I'm screaming through the static at a star that used to glow
The Earth is just a marble now, a blue and spinning eye
And we are two reflections that the cold forgot to tie

We're drifting, drifting, two lights in the dark
The distance keeps on doubling and it's tearing us apart
Reach for me, reach for me, across the widening black
I'm falling up forever and there's no way back

If I could fold the vacuum, if I could bend the night
I'd pull your orbit back to mine and hold you in the light
But physics has no mercy and the dark has no remorse
So I'll love you at the speed of light along this endless course

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slam like a verdict and the tunnel swallows whole
The lights begin their flicker as the panic takes control
My heartbeat finds a tempo that my lungs cannot obey
And the strangers all start spinning in a cruel and breathless sway

Dance with me, you trembling dark, this fever in my chest
The rails are screaming rhythm and my ribs won't let me rest
Hold the pole, count the breath, the platform's miles away
I'm dancing with my terror on the underground today

The ceiling leans in closer and the air turns into stone
A hundred faces near me and I've never been so alone
My hands are white as candle wax, my vision starts to fray
And the city overhead don't know I'm slipping clean away

Dance with me, you trembling dark, this fever in my chest
The rails are screaming rhythm and my ribs won't let me rest
Hold the pole, count the breath, the platform's miles away
I'm dancing with my terror on the underground today

Then slowly comes the station and the doors release their hold
The daylight at the top of the stairs is worth its weight in gold
I'll climb back to the living with my heartbeat in my throat
A survivor of the darkness in my crumpled winter coat

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Alone upon the granite where the black waves come to break
I carve my devotion in the salt for your insatiable sake
A hundred nights of burning, my lantern is my heart
A beacon for the lover that has torn my world apart

Oh sea, my cruel beloved, take these letters that I drown
Each word a drop of marrow, each line a sinking crown
I'll love you till the granite breaks, I'll love you till I'm bone
The keeper of your fury, forever and alone

You took my father's vessel, you took my brother whole
And still I climb the spiral stairs to feed your endless soul
The storm is your confession, the foam is your reply
I write you in the lightning as the drowning seabirds cry

Oh sea, my cruel beloved, take these letters that I drown
Each word a drop of marrow, each line a sinking crown
I'll love you till the granite breaks, I'll love you till I'm bone
The keeper of your fury, forever and alone

Let the tower fall to ruin, let my fingers turn to rust
I'll keep the flame against your wrath until my lungs are dust
And when at last you claim me and you drag me to your floor
I'll write one final letter on the silence of your shore

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
There's a corner booth that's empty where we used to share the wine
And the jukebox plays our song sometimes, it cuts me every time
I had you in my arms one night, I let you slip on through
Now every woman that I meet just turns into you

You're the one that got away, the ache that won't lie down
The sweetest ghost in this old town, the prettiest dress in brown
I'd trade my hands, I'd trade my name, I'd trade the morning sun
Just to hold the love I lost, just to hold the one

I heard you married steady, got a house out on the hill
A man who never stumbled, a man who never will
And I'm here in this barroom with my whiskey and my pride
Telling everybody stories 'bout the love I let slide

You're the one that got away, the ache that won't lie down
The sweetest ghost in this old town, the prettiest dress in brown
I'd trade my hands, I'd trade my name, I'd trade the morning sun
Just to hold the love I lost, just to hold the one

They say that time's a healer but it's been a thousand days
And you're still in the rain on the window, you're still in the morning haze
I'd write you all my sorrows but I never learned to spell
The name of what I threw away, the love I knew so well

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Lord, put your hand on this old wheel beside my trembling child
The road is long and crooked and the world is running wild
She's got her foot upon the gas and faith inside her chest
So guide her through the valley, Lord, and carry all the rest

Drive on, drive on, by the grace that goes before
There's an angel on the dashboard and a savior at the door
I can't go where she's going but I trust the One who can
So I'm letting go and giving her into His mighty hand

I taught her how to bow her head, I taught her how to pray
Now I'm teaching her to merge into the rivers of the day
My heart is in the passenger seat, my Lord is in the sky
And somewhere between the two of them my baby's learning to fly

Drive on, drive on, by the grace that goes before
There's an angel on the dashboard and a savior at the door
I can't go where she's going but I trust the One who can
So I'm letting go and giving her into His mighty hand

One day the road will take her past the reaches of my sight
But I know the Shepherd's watching every turn through every night
So go on, precious daughter, with His mercy as your guide
The Lord will be your highway and forever by your side

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Gray beard like the driftwood, feet dug in the sand
Reads the moon like scripture, got the ocean in his hand
Says listen, little grandson, ain't no rushing what is true
The tide don't ask permission, it just does what it's gon' do
He点s a finger at the water, says that's a hundred years of me
Every wave that ever broke is still alive inside the sea
Patience is a current, boy, it pulls beneath the loud
The quiet do the carving while the foolish chase the crowd

Teach the tide, old man, teach the tide
Everything you buried's coming back up with the rise
Moon pull, slow grind, salt in the lungs
The ocean's been here longer than the songs we ever sung

He never owned a dollar but he owned the break of dawn
Knew the names of all the fishes and the storms before they're born
Said the young want it fast, want it loud, want it now
But the deepest things take seasons and the tide'll show you how
Now his footprints washing under and his voice is in the spray
But I hear him in the undertow at the closing of the day

Teach the tide, old man, teach the tide
Everything you buried's coming back up with the rise
Moon pull, slow grind, salt in the lungs
The ocean's been here longer than the songs we ever sung

So I stand where he was standing with my own grandson in tow
Point a finger at the water, tell him everything I know
The tide keeps on returning, that's the lesson and the proof
We don't inherit the ocean, son, we borrow it from youth

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent hum and a coffee gone cold
The neon outside flickers stories untold
A waitress with eyes like a fading cassette
Pouring out hours she'd rather forget

Stay open, stay open, all through the night
A washed-out cathedral of secondhand light
We're the lost and the leaving, the wired and the worn
Drowning in static till the gray of the morn

The trucker, the dreamer, the heart on the run
We blur in the window like a watercolor sun
The jukebox is sighing a half-remembered tune
And the booths hold the shapes of the people who'll come soon

Stay open, stay open, all through the night
A washed-out cathedral of secondhand light
We're the lost and the leaving, the wired and the worn
Drowning in static till the gray of the morn

Somewhere the city is folding to sleep
But here the clock melts and the silence runs deep
Refill my cup, let me dissolve in the glow
A ghost in the chrome with no place left to go

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Forty years I've walked these marble floors at night
With my flashlight and my whistle and the low emergency light
But there's one room where I linger, where I always seem to stay
With a girl inside a gold frame who has never looked away

She's the prettiest thing in oil and paint, she's been here a hundred years
And I tell her all my troubles and she dries my lonesome tears
The world walks by in daylight, takes a photo, never sees
But at midnight she's my darling and she keeps her eyes on me

They'll retire me come autumn, hand me a watch and a card
And somebody new will wander through this echoing old yard
But who'll know how she's smiling when the moon comes through the glass?
Who'll talk to her till sunrise when the final tour has passed?

She's the prettiest thing in oil and paint, she's been here a hundred years
And I tell her all my troubles and she dries my lonesome tears
The world walks by in daylight, takes a photo, never sees
But at midnight she's my darling and she keeps her eyes on me

So paint me in the corner, girl, just a shadow on the wall
A gray old guard who loved you best of anyone at all
And when they dim the lights for good and lock the heavy door
I'll find a way to keep my watch beside you evermore

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Doce de la noche y la freidora sigue viva
El letrero parpadea, una letra fugitiva
Mesera con tatuajes sirve café que no se acaba
Taxistas y borrachos, todo el mundo se sentaba
Grasa en el cristal, la luna en el asfalto
La rocola escupe un tema, sube el ritmo, dale alto

Abierto toda la noche, nunca cierra la puerta
Ven, que aquí nadie pregunta por qué sigues despierta
Huevos y tortillas pa' los corazones rotos
El diner es la iglesia de los que andamos solos

Las tres de la mañana, llega el chico del turno
Manos en el bolsillo, ojos de insomnio nocturno
Una moneda en la mesa, propina de centavos
Pero aquí no hay desprecio, todos somos esclavos
Del reloj que no avanza, del plato que humea
Del neón que nos pinta de rojo cualquier idea

Cuando salga el sol la cuenta se hace humo
Pero hasta que amanezca, baila con el zumo
Del jukebox y el cilantro, del azúcar y el sudor
El diner sigue abierto, no le teme al reloj

Abierto toda la noche, nunca cierra la puerta
Ven, que aquí nadie pregunta por qué sigues despierta
Huevos y tortillas pa' los corazones rotos
El diner es la iglesia de los que andamos solos

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Cada noche en la sala número siete
Vigilo a una mujer que un pincel encerró
Lleva un vestido azul de un siglo que se fue
Y en sus ojos de aceite todavía hay un sol
Las botas me lastiman, el reloj no perdona
Pero ella nunca envejece, nunca me abandona

Ay, dama del marco dorado
Te canto aunque no puedas oír
Guardián de tu sueño pintado
Me quedo contigo hasta el fin

Los turistas te miran tres segundos y se van
No saben que de noche tú y yo conversamos
Les cuento del pueblo, del río, del pan
Y el silencio del museo se vuelve dos manos
La linterna te roza la mejilla de barniz
Y juro que sonríes cuando nadie está aquí

Si un día me jubilan y otro guarda viene a estar
No dejes que te mire como objeto a cuidar
Mírame en su lugar, recuerda mi canción
Dama del marco dorado, te dejo el corazón

Ay, dama del marco dorado
Te canto aunque no puedas oír
Guardián de tu sueño pintado
Me quedo contigo hasta el fin

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Paso despacio por la calle que fue mía
El segundo balcón aún cuelga la maceta vacía
La luz del tercero está encendida, no es la tuya
Otra vida cocina en la cocina que era nuestra

Ay, ventana, ventana cerrada
No me devuelves ni una mirada
Giré la esquina mil veces de día
De noche es la única vez que sería
Capaz de mirar lo que ya no es mío

El semáforo en rojo me obliga a quedarme
Frente al portal donde aprendí a quemarme
La farola te pinta de un cobre que miente
Como mienten los muertos que parecen presentes

Acelero, que el verde ya manda seguir
Dejo el barrio en el espejo, lo veo morir
Un compás en el pecho, palmas de adiós
La carretera me traga, te quedas tú y Dios

Ay, ventana, ventana cerrada
No me devuelves ni una mirada
Giré la esquina mil veces de día
De noche es la única vez que sería
Capaz de mirar lo que ya no es mío

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Born on Halsted where the asphalt cracks like a hand
My daddy worked the rail yard till the rail yard left the land
I counted every bullet hole in the corner liquor sign
Swore I'd take that northbound bus before I turned out like mine

I made it off the block, Lord, I made it off the block
Left my key under the mat and I never turned the lock
But the dust of that old corner, it still rides inside my shoe
You can take the boy off Halsted, can't take Halsted out of you

Momma waved a dishtowel from a window painted shut
Said go on, baby, go on, don't you look back at this rut
I sold my brother's bicycle just to buy my one-way fare
And I rode past the cemetery where my whole bloodline is there

Now I got a key that fits a door across the river wide
But some nights I wake up choking on the men who stayed and died
So I send a little money and I light a candle blue
For every soul still standing where the dead-end still comes through

I made it off the block, Lord, I made it off the block
Left my key under the mat and I never turned the lock
But the dust of that old corner, it still rides inside my shoe
You can take the boy off Halsted, can't take Halsted out of you

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Mile marker ninety, Nebraska in the dark
Dashboard glowing green, coffee cold and stark
I thumb your name and let it ring
Three a.m. and the highway hums and sings

Pick up, pick up, I'm calling from the line
Forty tons of steel and a heart that's still mine
Say you're sleeping good, say the kids are alright
Keep me on the road, keep me through the night

You answer half awake, your voice a warm motel
I tell you 'bout the rain, the things I cannot sell
The radio is static, the white lines never end
But your breathing on the speaker is the only road I tend

Drop the beat down low, let the engine and the bassline merge
Four days till I'm home, riding on this lonesome surge
Don't hang up yet, just leave the line alive
Let the silence and the diesel help me drive

Pick up, pick up, I'm calling from the line
Forty tons of steel and a heart that's still mine
Say you're sleeping good, say the kids are alright
Keep me on the road, keep me through the night

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the warehouse when the frost is on the truck
Clock out, drive across town, change my shirt and press my luck
The second register's drawer don't balance with my pay
And the rent goes up in April like it does most every day

Two jobs, two sets of keys, two badges on the wall
Still the mailbox keeps on humming with the red ink of it all
I'm not lazy, I'm not foolish, I just can't outrun the math
Working double, falling backward on this dollar-bleeding path

My daughter drew a picture of me sleeping in the chair
Shoes still on, the TV blue, a halo of my hair
She wrote 'my dad is tired' in a crayon I can't blame
And I taped it to the fridge beside the bills I cannot tame

They say pull up your bootstraps but my boots are coming apart
Glue them every Sunday, same as I keep gluing my heart
If hard work was a ladder I'd be standing on the moon
Instead I'm on the bottom rung still humming this old tune

Two jobs, two sets of keys, two badges on the wall
Still the mailbox keeps on humming with the red ink of it all
I'm not lazy, I'm not foolish, I just can't outrun the math
Working double, falling backward on this dollar-bleeding path

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
We grew up two streets over by the chapel and the weir
You were just the baker's daughter, flour-dusted, disappear
I left for twenty winters, chased a fortune cross the sea
Came home to bury father and you poured the tea for me

Oh the hills they haven't moved an inch and neither has my heart
I knew your face the moment that the kettle made you start
There's a fiddle in the corner and a story in your eyes
I fell for my own hometown wearing your surprise

We walked the muddy boreen where the hawthorn used to bend
You knew the names of all the dead, each cousin, every friend
The rain came soft as memory, the church bell counted slow
And I forgot the cities and the reasons I would go

They say you can't go back again, the old road washes thin
But the same stone bridge still carries me to where I have always been
Give me peat smoke in the evening and your hand inside my own
Let the wandering man lie down at last and call this place his home

Oh the hills they haven't moved an inch and neither has my heart
I knew your face the moment that the kettle made you start
There's a fiddle in the corner and a story in your eyes
I fell for my own hometown wearing your surprise

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The screen lights up the bedroom at a quarter after two
My thumb is hovering over the worst thing I could do
Three dots appear and vanish, like a tide that won't commit
And the ring on my left hand catches every bit of it

I shouldn't, but I'm typing; I should sleep, but I am here
Delete, delete, delete it, then I send it anyway, my dear
There's a name I keep on hiding underneath a coffee cup
And a phone that knows the secrets that I keep on giving up

I told myself in winter that the habit died in spring
But a song came on the radio and started everything
We only trade in weather and the books we used to read
But weather is a doorway and I'm planting that old seed

Downstairs my whole life is sleeping, trusting, breathing slow
And up here glows a window into somewhere I can't go
One more message, then I'll stop, I lie the way I lie
The phone goes dark, my face stays lit, and morning's far off in the sky

I shouldn't, but I'm typing; I should sleep, but I am here
Delete, delete, delete it, then I send it anyway, my dear
There's a name I keep on hiding underneath a coffee cup
And a phone that knows the secrets that I keep on giving up

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Seven years of static on a number I still know
Thumb against the cracked glass, every digit like a blow
The slammed door is a scar that never learned to close
Now my hands are shaking harder than the day I chose to go

RING — let it ring through the wreckage of the years
RING — louder than the shouting, louder than the fears
Mother, it's your ghost calling, the son you thought was stone
Pick up the receiver, I don't want to die alone

I rehearsed a thousand speeches in the mirror's cruel light
Every word a sharpened blade I swore that I would fight
But the dial tone keeps screaming and my armor turns to rust
All my rage was just a child who needed someone he could trust

Click — and a breath — and a voice gone thin and grey
'I kept your room exactly, I knew you'd call someday'
The walls I built come down in a detonating roar
I'm on my knees in the kitchen of a house I left before

RING — let it ring through the wreckage of the years
RING — louder than the shouting, louder than the fears
Mother, it's your ghost calling, the son you thought was stone
Pick up the receiver, I don't want to die alone

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
En la plaza de cemento queda un árbol nada más
Un ombú que los obreros olvidaron arrancar
Sus raíces levantan las baldosas como un grito
Y a su sombra ya no baila ni el más viejo, ni el más fino

Bailá conmigo, árbol solo, bajo el farol y el adiós
Sos el último que sabe cómo era el verde, cómo era Dios
Las grúas afilan la noche, te miran con su metal
Pero esta noche es un tango, y el tango no sabe acabar

Las palomas ya se han ido, las bancas son de aluminio
Los niños juegan adentro, detrás de un cristal sin trino
Vos guardás en cada anillo un invierno que se fue
Una calle de tierra, un caballo, un primer café

Mañana viene la máquina con su orden y su sierra
Te cortarán como cortan lo que recuerda la tierra
Pero yo vendré de noche y enterraré tu semilla
Donde el asfalto se rompa, donde la ciudad no vigila

Bailá conmigo, árbol solo, bajo el farol y el adiós
Sos el último que sabe cómo era el verde, cómo era Dios
Las grúas afilan la noche, te miran con su metal
Pero esta noche es un tango, y el tango no sabe acabar

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Downpour on the avenue, the bus done left the stop
You held a little shelter and you watched the heavens drop
I ducked beneath your canopy, a stranger soaked and shy
You shifted just an inch, my friend, to keep the both of us dry

One umbrella, two strangers, jah send the rain to teach
That kindness is a roof, my love, the simplest thing to reach
Shoulder touch a shoulder while the gutters overflow
We never traded names but oh, we shared the way to go

We talked about the weather, then we talked about our days
The puddles caught the traffic lights in red and golden blaze
You said you missed your island, I said I missed my own
Two little boats of memory in one cloth, not alone

The rain it eased to silver and the sun begin to climb
You folded up the canvas, said the shelter's done its time
We walked our separate corners but I carry it still
A roof we made of nothing on a grey and dripping hill

One umbrella, two strangers, jah send the rain to teach
That kindness is a roof, my love, the simplest thing to reach
Shoulder touch a shoulder while the gutters overflow
We never traded names but oh, we shared the way to go

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
A basement bar, eleven souls, a flickering red bulb
A borrowed amp that buzzed like wasps, a strap too long to hold
My lyrics scrawled on pizza box, my knuckles bone-white pale
The sound guy yawned, the bartender just rinsed another ale

That first night, that first scream into the dark
Four wrong notes and a heartbeat loud enough to leave a mark
They didn't clap, they didn't care, the mic stand nearly fell
But I climbed down a different beast — I'd walked through my own hell

I shook for forty seconds, then the chorus took my fear
And swallowed it and spat it out as something fierce and clear
The feedback howled an answer that the silence couldn't kill
And somewhere in that ugly noise I found my reckless will

Now I headline the cathedrals where the pyro paints the night
Ten thousand fists are pounding back the fury and the light
But I'd trade one stadium for that basement and its dread
Where a terrified kid with shaking hands first raised the dead

That first night, that first scream into the dark
Four wrong notes and a heartbeat loud enough to leave a mark
They didn't clap, they didn't care, the mic stand nearly fell
But I climbed down a different beast — I'd walked through my own hell

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Neon on the kitchen tile, the clock hums VHS
Your hands that fixed my bicycle now fumble with a dress
The man who lifted engines asks me twice what day it is
And the cassette of who you were keeps skipping on its hiss

Fade to violet, fade to slow
The synth still plays the songs you used to know
I'm watching you grow distant like a city in the rain
Dad, don't dissolve into the static, stay in frame

We drive the old coast highway where you taught me how to steer
The ocean's still electric but your gaze is somewhere near
A summer twenty years behind, a radio, a road
You hum a half-remembered tune and shoulder half the load

The future that we dreamed in chrome arrived without a sound
And took you piece by gentle piece, no sirens, no rebound
So I'll record your laughter, every flicker, every glow
And play it back forever in the magenta afterglow

Fade to violet, fade to slow
The synth still plays the songs you used to know
I'm watching you grow distant like a city in the rain
Dad, don't dissolve into the static, stay in frame

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Five a.m. alarm, the night shift bleeds into the day
Badge one on the lanyard, badge two folded in the grey
The bus is always early when my legs are always late
And the overdraft is breathing down the back of every plate

Run, run, run, but the floor keeps moving back
Two wages in my pocket and a hole I cannot pack
Faster, faster, faster, but the rent just learns to fly
Working double, sinking single, watch the numbers multiply

Mop the second floor at midnight, stock the shelves at break of dawn
My phone screen cracked in autumn and the cracks just carry on
They call me a survivor like it's something I should wear
I'd trade the medal gladly for an hour I could spare

Drop it — let the bassline be the engine in my chest
No rest for the relentless, there's no payday in the rest
But I'm still on my feet when the sun comes through the blind
Two jobs deep and breathing, leaving sleep so far behind

Run, run, run, but the floor keeps moving back
Two wages in my pocket and a hole I cannot pack
Faster, faster, faster, but the rent just learns to fly
Working double, sinking single, watch the numbers multiply

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We climbed the frozen headland past the last of the stone walls
The sea below was breathing slow, the gulls had ceased their calls
Then green came creeping over us, a curtain pulled by hand
And all the cold of all my years stood silent on the land

Dance, aurora, dance for the fishermen and me
Ribbons of the spirit world come down to touch the sea
My grandmother once told me they're the souls who went before
Waving down their lanterns from the far Hebridean shore

The rose and violet folded through the rafters of the night
A loom of light above the loch, no candle burned so bright
We did not speak a single word, there was no word to give
Just the breathing of the heavens showing wanderers how to live

When I am old and grey of beard and the peat fire burns down low
I'll close my eyes and find again that emerald overflow
And if the souls of all the dead are waving from up high
Then save a lantern, save a dance, for when it's me they fly

Dance, aurora, dance for the fishermen and me
Ribbons of the spirit world come down to touch the sea
My grandmother once told me they're the souls who went before
Waving down their lanterns from the far Hebridean shore

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Got my boots in the back and the radio loud
Chasing the static where it spins in the cloud
Mama said honey come home for a while
But the sky's calling me with a thunderhead smile

I can't settle down, no I can't settle down
When the funnel comes spinning I'm leaving this town
Groove in my chest like a hurricane sound
Baby I'm wild and I can't settle down

Sugar laid a ring on the kitchen table
Said stay if you're willing, leave if you're able
The pressure dropped low and the wind started to moan
So I kissed her goodbye and I rolled out alone

I can't settle down, no I can't settle down
When the funnel comes spinning I'm leaving this town
Groove in my chest like a hurricane sound
Baby I'm wild and I can't settle down

Lightning in my veins, gasoline and rain
Every quiet morning feels a little like a chain
So I follow the warning where the green sky frowns
That's the only place this heart of mine slows down

I can't settle down, no I can't settle down
When the funnel comes spinning I'm leaving this town
Groove in my chest like a hurricane sound
Baby I'm wild and I can't settle down

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The sun came up like it always does
Though my heart still aches with a heavy because
Empty chair at the kitchen door
But grace walked in where there was grief before

Morning came, oh morning came
And I called on the mercy of His holy name
The tears may fall but I won't fall down
There's a crown of glory where they laid you down

Folded the dress that the choir wore
Swept up the petals from the chapel floor
The casseroles cold on the counter top
But the love in this house, it will never stop

Morning came, oh morning came
And I called on the mercy of His holy name
The tears may fall but I won't fall down
There's a crown of glory where they laid you down

Weeping may endure for a night
But joy comes running in the morning light
I'll see you again on that golden shore
Where goodbye don't live anymore

Morning came, oh morning came
And I called on the mercy of His holy name
The tears may fall but I won't fall down
There's a crown of glory where they laid you down

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Cinnamon hangs in the amber light
Flour on the counter, soft and white
Steam on the window, a slow lullaby
Butter and sugar drifting by

Take me back, take me back
To the warm gold haze of the afternoon
Her hands in the dough, her humming tune
Take me back, take me back so soon

Apron of roses faded thin
The screen door creaking, letting summer in
A pot on the stove with a quiet hiss
Nothing ever felt as safe as this

Take me back, take me back
To the warm gold haze of the afternoon
Her hands in the dough, her humming tune
Take me back, take me back so soon

I can still taste the honey on the bread
All the gentle words she said
The kitchen's quiet, the years have flown
But I carry that warmth wherever I've grown

Take me back, take me back
To the warm gold haze of the afternoon
Her hands in the dough, her humming tune
Take me back, take me back so soon

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Headlights low on a gravel vein
Jars in the trunk and a coming rain
The moon hung pale like a watchful eye
We were ghosts on the road, you and I

Run, run through the silver haze
The siren's bleeding into the maze
We were never gonna make it home
Lost on the backroads all alone

The county line shimmered, almost near
Then the blue light bloomed in the rearview mirror
Glass on the asphalt, sweet and slow
The river took everything we used to know

Run, run through the silver haze
The siren's bleeding into the maze
We were never gonna make it home
Lost on the backroads all alone

Somewhere the corn fields whisper your name
The smoke and the moonlight all the same
I still taste the copper on my tongue
From the night we thought we'd stay forever young

Run, run through the silver haze
The siren's bleeding into the maze
We were never gonna make it home
Lost on the backroads all alone

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Lamp light low and the city's asleep
Pages and coffee, promises to keep
Clock on the wall says it's quarter past two
Just me and the silence and the work I gotta do

Two in the morning, I'm wide awake
One more chapter for my future's sake
Feel the beat in my chest, I won't stop now
Gonna make it, gonna make it somehow

Highlighter glow on a sea of white
Fighting off the heavy in my tired eyes tonight
Dreams in my notebook, fire in my veins
One more push through the growing pains

Two in the morning, I'm wide awake
One more chapter for my future's sake
Feel the beat in my chest, I won't stop now
Gonna make it, gonna make it somehow

The world goes quiet but the dream stays loud
I'll make these lonely hours proud
When the sun comes up I'll still be here
Closing in on everything I hold dear

Two in the morning, I'm wide awake
One more chapter for my future's sake
Feel the beat in my chest, I won't stop now
Gonna make it, gonna make it somehow
