songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Engine screaming down the avenue
Neon bleeding every shade of you
Third floor window, the light's still on
But the love we buried here is long since gone

I'm driving past the wreckage of us
Ghosts in the gasoline and rust
The city burns but I feel nothing now
I tore it down, I don't know how

Same cracked sidewalk, same dying tree
Same iron railing where you held to me
The shadows scream what we used to be
A tomb of memory I can't unsee

I'm driving past the wreckage of us
Ghosts in the gasoline and rust
The city burns but I feel nothing now
I tore it down, I don't know how

Red light, dead light, choking on the past
Nothing built on lies was ever gonna last
I hit the gas and let the demons drown
Never coming back to this poisoned town

I'm driving past the wreckage of us
Ghosts in the gasoline and rust
The city burns but I feel nothing now
I tore it down, I don't know how

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
You laugh at his jokes in the coffee shop line
And I memorize the curve of your smile every time
I'm a footnote written in invisible ink
Closer than you'd ever think

And I love you, but you'll never know
I'm the quiet in the radio
A song you'll never learn to sing
I feel everything, you feel nothing

We talk about your heartbreaks on my old front porch
And I hold the umbrella while I'm holding the torch
You borrow my sweater, you give it back
Still warm, still soft, still off the track

And I love you, but you'll never know
I'm the quiet in the radio
A song you'll never learn to sing
I feel everything, you feel nothing

Maybe in another life I got the nerve
To say the words I never deserve
But here I'll stay, a half a step behind
Loving you quietly in the back of my mind

And I love you, but you'll never know
I'm the quiet in the radio
A song you'll never learn to sing
I feel everything, you feel nothing

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Brass in the heat and a slow black march
Umbrellas bloom under a fevered arch
The second line sways down Rampart Street
Grief and the groove in a single beat

Dance me home, dance me home
Down the river where the spirits roam
Cry for the leaving, sing for the soul
Let the trumpet make the broken whole

White handkerchiefs in the amber air
Magnolia heavy, sweet despair
The drummer's heartbeat, the tuba's moan
We lay you down but you're never alone

Dance me home, dance me home
Down the river where the spirits roam
Cry for the leaving, sing for the soul
Let the trumpet make the broken whole

When the saints come marching, take my hand
Through the haze of the promised land
The mourning turns to a golden hymn
And the streetlights blur as the day grows dim

Dance me home, dance me home
Down the river where the spirits roam
Cry for the leaving, sing for the soul
Let the trumpet make the broken whole

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at six, clock out at ten
Come home just to do it all again
Stack up the hours like I stack the dishes high
Still the bills keep asking why

Two jobs and I'm still running low
Giving everything I got to give and got nothing to show
Lord, I'm tired but I'm holding on
Keep on pushing till the breaking's gone

Name tag faded, feet on fire
Dreams on layaway, rent keeps climbing higher
Count the change on the kitchen sink
Too broke to rest, too tired to think

Two jobs and I'm still running low
Giving everything I got to give and got nothing to show
Lord, I'm tired but I'm holding on
Keep on pushing till the breaking's gone

But I look at my babies, fast asleep
And I find a little reason in the love I keep
Maybe tomorrow gonna cut me some slack
Till then I carry the weight on my back

Two jobs and I'm still running low
Giving everything I got to give and got nothing to show
Lord, I'm tired but I'm holding on
Keep on pushing till the breaking's gone

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Cracked concrete and a faded dream
Grew up where the streetlights scream
They said this corner's all I'd ever be
But I saw a whole wide world in front of me

I'm making it out, making it out
Leaving all the doubt in the rearview now
Watch me rise, watch me shine
This dead-end block won't define what's mine

Mama worked her fingers down to the bone
So I'd never have to do it all alone
I carry her hope in my beating chest
Gonna give this city all my best

I'm making it out, making it out
Leaving all the doubt in the rearview now
Watch me rise, watch me shine
This dead-end block won't define what's mine

Every closed door was a lesson learned
Every cold night was a bridge I burned
Now the sky's the only line I see
This was never the end of me

I'm making it out, making it out
Leaving all the doubt in the rearview now
Watch me rise, watch me shine
This dead-end block won't define what's mine

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle and thread under neon glow
Everybody comes to the man they know
A velvet lapel, a perfect seam
He stitches you into a brand new dream

Dress you up, dress you down
He's the sharpest hands in the whole damn town
From the corner store to the disco floor
Everybody's looking good, can't ask for more

Mrs. Jones in her Sunday red
The preacher's suit, the words he said
The kid getting married, the prom-night gown
He's the golden thread holding up the town

Dress you up, dress you down
He's the sharpest hands in the whole damn town
From the corner store to the disco floor
Everybody's looking good, can't ask for more

Mirror, mirror, spin around
Feel the shimmer, hear the sound
He don't make money, he makes you proud
A little magic for the whole block crowd

Dress you up, dress you down
He's the sharpest hands in the whole damn town
From the corner store to the disco floor
Everybody's looking good, can't ask for more

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Same cracked streets where we used to run
Same dead-end summers, same setting sun
I swore I'd leave and never look back
Then you walked in and broke the track

You're the only thing this town got right
Burning in my chest tonight
I came back broken, I came back torn
Fell for you where I was born

Gas station lights and a beating heart
We were always close but worlds apart
Now I'm screaming every word I hid
Loving you the way I always did

You're the only thing this town got right
Burning in my chest tonight
I came back broken, I came back torn
Fell for you where I was born

Tear down the walls, tear down the past
Something this real was built to last
No more running, no more lies
I see forever in your eyes

You're the only thing this town got right
Burning in my chest tonight
I came back broken, I came back torn
Fell for you where I was born

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Ooh, do-wop, do-wop
I still remember that summer day
The way you smiled before you walked away
I should have told you, should have stayed
Now you're the one that got away

My darling, my darling, where did you go
The one I loved and let go, oh oh oh
Every night I dream of your face
No one ever could take your place

We danced so close to the radio sound
Under the stars on the edge of town
I was a fool with too much pride
Let the best thing slip from my side

My darling, my darling, where did you go
The one I loved and let go, oh oh oh
Every night I dream of your face
No one ever could take your place

If I could turn back the hands of time
I'd make you mine, I'd make you mine
But all I've got is yesterday
And the one that got away

My darling, my darling, where did you go
The one I loved and let go, oh oh oh
Every night I dream of your face
No one ever could take your place

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up where the sirens sing all night
Where the corner boys be chasing the light
Mama said baby keep your head to the sky
This block ain't the place where your dreams gotta die

I'm gonna make it out, make it out somehow
Feel the weight of the whole world but I'm rising now
From the cracks in the concrete a flower still grows
I'm making it out, only heaven knows

Laid my soul on these city streets
Found a rhythm in the broken beats
Every scar got a story to tell
Turned my pieces into something I could sell

I'm gonna make it out, make it out somehow
Feel the weight of the whole world but I'm rising now
From the cracks in the concrete a flower still grows
I'm making it out, only heaven knows

I ain't forget where I came from, no
Gonna send it all back to the ones below
Light a candle for the dreams that died
Carry my people on the other side

I'm gonna make it out, make it out somehow
Feel the weight of the whole world but I'm rising now
From the cracks in the concrete a flower still grows
I'm making it out, only heaven knows

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every morning by the gallery door
Same old uniform, same marble floor
But there's a woman in a frame of gold
With a secret in her eyes that never gets old

Oh, she never says a word to me
But she's the only one who sets me free
A hundred years of dust and rain
And I'm in love with a painting again

The tourists come and the tourists go
They snap their pictures and they never know
That when the lights go down at half past nine
I swear that painted smile is mine

Oh, she never says a word to me
But she's the only one who sets me free
A hundred years of dust and rain
And I'm in love with a painting again

They'll retire me someday, hang my coat
And I'll leave with a lump in my throat
But I'll remember till the day I'm gone
The quiet love that kept me holding on

Oh, she never says a word to me
But she's the only one who sets me free
A hundred years of dust and rain
And I'm in love with a painting again

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The fiddle's warming up beside the woodstove glow
Fifty winters carved into these floorboards down below
Your hand still fits in mine the way it did at twenty-two
Though the silver crept up slow and stole the brown from me and you

So turn me one more time, let the banjo ring it clear
We'll wear a circle in the pine before the dawn gets here
I knew your step before I knew my own
One more dance and then I'll let you go back home

The doctor said the spring won't bring you back to me
So I rolled the rug aside and asked you formally
You laughed that laugh that built a barn and raised a row of girls
And we swayed beneath the kerosene like it was all the world

So turn me one more time, let the banjo ring it clear
We'll wear a circle in the pine before the dawn gets here
I knew your step before I knew my own
One more dance and then I'll let you go back home

No preacher and no fancy band, just crickets keeping time
Your heartbeat slowing on my chest, still somehow stays in rhyme

When the fiddle finally quits I'll fold you to a chair
And thank the Lord for every step and every greying hair

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Three A.M. and the coffee tastes like battery acid grace
Neon buzzing OPEN like it's spitting in night's face
Vinyl booth's been duct-taped since the year that I was born
Grease on every window keeping out the coming morn

We don't close! We don't close!
While the city locks its doors and the decent people doze
We got cracked ceramic mugs and a jukebox full of ghosts
This is church for everyone the daylight never chose

Trucker, nurse, and runaway all share the counter stool
Fry cook with a switchblade smile breaking every rule
Clock above the register been stuck at half past dead
Nobody's keeping time in here, we keep the lights instead

We don't close! We don't close!
While the city locks its doors and the decent people doze
We got cracked ceramic mugs and a jukebox full of ghosts
This is church for everyone the daylight never chose

They can build their shiny towers, they can price us off the map
We'll be frying eggs in here while the whole damn thing collapse

We don't close! We don't close!
Pour another bottomless and damn whatever follows

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
You were always at the edges of the photographs I kept
A hallway I walked past for years without a single step
Now the streetlight on Maple bleeds a halo through the rain
And I'm seventeen and thirty all at once again

Fade into the static of the town that made us both
Your name a tide that pulls beneath the reverb and the smoke
I fell the way the snow falls on the gravel by the lake
Slow and then all at once and far too late

We shared a math class, shared a county, shared the same gray sky
Never knew your gravity until you said goodbye
The water tower rusting with our initials worn away
Yet here you are, here I am, in the same old fading day

Fade into the static of the town that made us both
Your name a tide that pulls beneath the reverb and the smoke
I fell the way the snow falls on the gravel by the lake
Slow and then all at once and far too late

Drown the guitars, let them wash the years between
Every wall a wave and you the only thing I've seen

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Mama Yemi got the needle, got the thread that never breaks
Measuring tape around her neck like gold the morning makes
From the wedding to the funeral, every fit comes through her door
She knows your shoulders better than you knew them years before

She dress the whole block, sew the city in her seams
Every stitch a heartbeat, every fabric full of dreams
Dance for Mama Yemi, let the ankara catch the light
She make the ordinary man look like a king tonight

Scissors cutting rhythm to the radio outside
Gele for the auntie wrapped with elevated pride
The shy boy came in slouching, left him walking ten feet tall
Her little shop the heartbeat keeping color in it all

She dress the whole block, sew the city in her seams
Every stitch a heartbeat, every fabric full of dreams
Dance for Mama Yemi, let the ankara catch the light
She make the ordinary man look like a king tonight

Thread up, thread up, let the bobbin spin around
When the whole street's looking sharp you hear the whole street sound

So when they lay me down to rest dress me in her finest blue
The woman dressed the living, let her dress the leaving too

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
We pushed the couch against the wall like we were twenty-five
The record skips the way it did the summer we arrived
Thirty years of grocery lists and arguments and rain
And one cheap speaker crackling out our old familiar refrain

So dance with me in the kitchen light before the lawyers call
Before the boxes and the keys and splitting up the hall
I don't want the ending clean, I want it slow and loud
One more song to be the only people in the crowd

We already signed the papers, they're face-down on the chair
But your hand found my hand again like neither of us cared
The dog asleep, the kids grown gone, the house already sold
Just two ghosts in a doorway swaying, terrified of cold

So dance with me in the kitchen light before the lawyers call
Before the boxes and the keys and splitting up the hall
I don't want the ending clean, I want it slow and loud
One more song to be the only people in the crowd

We were never good at staying, never good at goodbye
So let the needle ride the groove until the morning sky

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Suitcase full of photographs and one mother's old gold ring
Dock lights strobing on the water like the future flickering
Engine in my ribcage, salt spray on my tongue
Leaving everything I named back where I was young

Go, go, the horizon's pulling fast
A country in the wake of me, a country in the past
Run the wire of the current, let the bassline be my prayer
I'm a signal crossing water to a self that's almost there

No papers for the heartbreak, just a stamp and one-way fare
The coastline of my childhood thinning out to thin gray air
Gulls become the static, then the static becomes night
And a city I can't picture pulls me forward like a light

Go, go, the horizon's pulling fast
A country in the wake of me, a country in the past
Run the wire of the current, let the bassline be my prayer
I'm a signal crossing water to a self that's almost there

Break it down to the breath, to the hum of the hull
Everything I was is luggage now, and the luggage is full

Go, go, the horizon's pulling fast
The drop is just the moment that the old shore lets go last

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I slipped out the side door while the choir hit the high
No announcement, no goodbye, just the parking lot and sky
These pews held my grandmother, held the man I used to be
But the hymn don't fit my mouth no more, it isn't meant for me

So I'm walking, walking soft so the stained glass doesn't break
Carrying the love but leaving everything they say I have to take
Lord, if you are out there, you know I tried to stay
But I had to find my own amen, and it led me away

I still hum the old songs driving down the avenue
Still say grace from habit over food I'm chewing through
They'll notice come the offering, an empty seat in three
But some faith is just a building you outgrow eventually

So I'm walking, walking soft so the stained glass doesn't break
Carrying the love but leaving everything they say I have to take
Lord, if you are out there, you know I tried to stay
But I had to find my own amen, and it led me away

It ain't anger, ain't a sin, it's just a quiet door
Closing gentle on a child who doesn't live there anymore

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
The engine hums a softer note along the avenue
Third floor window glowing gold, but the gold is not for you
Someone new is making coffee where you used to sleep at dawn
And the streetlight keeps the same old time though everything is gone

So I drive a little slower past the place we used to be
The city wears your perfume in the warm humidity
I don't stop, I never stop, I let the green light pull me through
But every red light is a window I once climbed up to you

The corner cafe's closed for years, the awning faded blue
A stray cat on the fire escape recalling something true
I could swear I see your shadow leaning out to catch the air
But shadows live in everyone, and you were never there

So I drive a little slower past the place we used to be
The city wears your perfume in the warm humidity
I don't stop, I never stop, I let the green light pull me through
But every red light is a window I once climbed up to you

Funny how a building keeps the things a heart lets go
The brick remembers everything, and brick was always slow

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The desk lamp is the only sun this whole apartment knows
Cold coffee in a chipped white mug, the radiator's low
My eyes are tracing sentences that swim like tired fish
And the highlighter's bleeding through a page I half-way wish

It's two in the morning and the world is fast asleep
But I got a promise to my future I intend to keep
So light the lonely lamp again and turn another page
This quiet is a furnace and I'm burning into shape

My mother worked the graveyard shift so I could sit right here
Her tired hands inside my hands, her whisper in my ear
The building's dead, the heater clicks, the streetlight's amber hum
And I'm building me a doorway to the man I will become

It's two in the morning and the world is fast asleep
But I got a promise to my future I intend to keep
So light the lonely lamp again and turn another page
This quiet is a furnace and I'm burning into shape

Nobody claps for the hours that nobody ever sees
But the dawn don't come for free, no, the dawn don't come for free

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded crates of midnight from a port that has no name
The harbor master crossed himself, then vanished in the rain
The captain laughed at omens while the compass spun like fire
And the cargo hummed a heartbeat through the hull and through the wire

Don't open the hold, don't open the hold tonight
The disco ball is spinning but the moon ain't acting right
We're dancing on the ocean of a debt we cannot pay
The cargo's gonna call our names before the break of day

The deckhands started dreaming in a language none could speak
The stars rearranged themselves and pointed somewhere bleak
Still the band kept playing, still the strobe lights cut the dark
As the water turned to mirrors and the gulls fell from the arc

Don't open the hold, don't open the hold tonight
The disco ball is spinning but the moon ain't acting right
We're dancing on the ocean of a debt we cannot pay
The cargo's gonna call our names before the break of day

Four-on-the-floor and the floor's gone soft as silk
The latch is coming loose and the sea has turned to milk

Don't open the hold, but the hold is opening slow
Keep dancing, keep dancing, it's the only way to go

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They called our numbers, paired us up, the cruelest joke they had
Your hand on my waist like a debt, your jaw set hard and mad
We've traded insults, traded crowns, for half a bitter year
Now the spotlight's got us tangled and there's nowhere left to steer

So move with me, hate me close, give the crowd a show
Your breath against my collarbone, the one thing they don't know
We're matching every step like we rehearsed it all our lives
Funny how the body tells the truth the mouth denies

You spin me and I catch the scent of everything I swore
I didn't want, I didn't need, I wouldn't fall for more
The judges scribble verdicts but the music's got a plan
And I forgot to hate you somewhere in the second bar's command

So move with me, hate me close, give the crowd a show
Your breath against my collarbone, the one thing they don't know
We're matching every step like we rehearsed it all our lives
Funny how the body tells the truth the mouth denies

When the song ends we go back to being enemies
But for these four minutes, baby, we're a dangerous melody

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Gas tank on a quarter, city forty miles ahead
Welcome sign behind me, population in the red
Same three streets that raised me, same three streets that held me down
Waving at the rearview, I'm a stranger to this town

I'm gone, I'm gone, ain't coming back this time
Left my name carved in the bleachers and a debt I left behind
Momma said go make it, don't you cry on this here road
So I'm flying past the water tower, lightening my load

Diner where I bussed the tables, lot where we got high
Graveyard with my granddad and the dreams that didn't fly
I love it and I hate it like a scar I can't erase
But a small town's just a waiting room you outgrow the space

I'm gone, I'm gone, ain't coming back this time
Left my name carved in the bleachers and a debt I left behind
Momma said go make it, don't you cry on this here road
So I'm flying past the water tower, lightening my load

They gon' say I think I'm better, say I turned my back
Nah, I just refused to die here, that's a different kind of fact
One bag, one shot, one engine running low
The only way to love this place was finally letting go

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I dialed the number I still knew the way I know my hands
Four rings and then your voice came through like rain on summer sand
The pride had built a wall so high I lost you in its shade
And seven years of birthdays sat unspoken and unpaid

It's me, Mama, it's me, can you hear me on the line
I've been carrying this silence and it's heavier than time
I don't have the perfect words, I don't have an excuse
Just a son who finally learned that being right is no use

You didn't ask me where I've been, you just said you're glad I'm well
And somewhere in your gentle voice I felt the breaking spell
The kettle's on, you tell me, like a Tuesday years ago
As if no day had passed at all, as if I'd never go

It's me, Mama, it's me, can you hear me on the line
I've been carrying this silence and it's heavier than time
I don't have the perfect words, I don't have an excuse
Just a son who finally learned that being right is no use

The phone don't weigh a thing but Lord it took me years to lift
And your laughing through the receiver is the only mercy's gift

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down like static on the corner of the block
You stepped under my umbrella, didn't ask, the light was locked
Shoulder pressed to shoulder, two umbrellas worth of cold
Reduced to one small circle and a story still untold

Just you and me and the downpour, strangers in the storm
One umbrella between us keeping half a city warm
Don't know your name, don't know your block, don't know where you been
But the rain don't care 'bout strangers when it's pouring you on in

Traffic lights were bleeding red and gold across the street
Your perfume mixed with petrichor, the puddles at our feet
We didn't speak, we didn't need, the thunder filled the space
Just two dots beneath a nylon sky in this drowning place

Just you and me and the downpour, strangers in the storm
One umbrella between us keeping half a city warm
Don't know your name, don't know your block, don't know where you been
But the rain don't care 'bout strangers when it's pouring you on in

Light turned green, you stepped away, the rain swallowed your face
Never got your number but I kept the empty space
Funny how a stranger fits a hole you didn't know
Then vanishes the way the rain vanish into snow

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole life on a map before I learned to walk
A husband and a ledger and a script for how to talk
The dress was hung, the date was set, the dowry counted twice
But nobody asked the woman if her heart would pay the price

So I'm walking out the gate, Jah, walking to my own
A future they don't own, a road I'll roam alone
No gold can buy my morning, no plan can chain my feet
I'd rather know the hunger than a cage that's looking sweet

My mother did the same as hers, my grandmother before
A chain of borrowed sorrow handed down from door to door
I kissed them at the threshold, said forgive me, I must go
There's a self they never met in me that's been waiting years to grow

So I'm walking out the gate, Jah, walking to my own
A future they don't own, a road I'll roam alone
No gold can buy my morning, no plan can chain my feet
I'd rather know the hunger than a cage that's looking sweet

Let them keep the silver, let them keep the name
Freedom taste like rain to me, and I'll never be the same

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Neon bleeding on the windshield glass
One more run and then I'm letting the past
Fade into the rearview, soft and slow
Engine humming low, the only thing I know

She said come home when the money's clean
But the city's painting everything in between
A velvet midnight, a dashboard glow
My hands on the wheel, my heart letting go

One last drive into the haze
Let the headlights blur the days
I'll disappear where no one knows my name
One last turn and I'm free again

The sirens sing like a faded dream
Nothing is ever quite what it seems
I'm chasing a horizon made of smoke
Every promise that I ever broke

One last drive into the haze
Let the headlights blur the days
I'll disappear where no one knows my name
One last turn and I'm free again

Floating, drifting, gone
The road keeps rolling on
Maybe I was never really here
Just a ghost behind the gears

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Soft like the morning, worn like the years
Hands that have wiped away rivers of tears
Kneading the dough in the amber light
Holding the whole world together tight

Lines like a map of the places she's been
Gold on her finger, grace on her skin
She hums a melody older than time
While the kettle keeps singing in three-quarter rhyme

Oh, those hands, those gentle hands
They taught me how to understand
The love that lingers, the love that stays
In the soft brown warmth of her grandmother's hands

She braided my hair and she mended my soul
She stitched up the pieces and made me whole
A touch on my cheek, a squeeze on my palm
In the chaos of living, she kept me calm

Oh, those hands, those gentle hands
They taught me how to understand
The love that lingers, the love that stays
In the soft brown warmth of her grandmother's hands

Now they rest folded, quiet and still
But I feel them with me, I always will
Every time I reach to help someone stand
I carry the grace of my grandmother's hands

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Clippers buzzin', chair spinnin' round
I'm the man, I know the whole town
Mayor came through, got a fade last week
Told me all his secrets, said don't speak

Fresh cut Friday, line out the door
Everybody talkin' 'bout the city's score
I hold the scissors, I hold the news
Whole neighborhood walkin' in my shoes

They come to me, they tell me everything
King of the chair, I don't need a ring
Fade so clean, edges so sharp
I know this town from the heart to the dark

Teacher, the dealer, the preacher too
All sit down and I see 'em through
Mirror don't lie and neither do I
I keep it a hundred, watch the years go by

They come to me, they tell me everything
King of the chair, I don't need a ring
Fade so clean, edges so sharp
I know this town from the heart to the dark

Thirty years of these floors I swept
Every story this old shop kept
Generations sat right here
I cut 'em young, I cut 'em through the years

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We set out from the harbor with the devil in the hold
A crate that no man opened, sealed in iron cold
The captain swore it's silver, but the silver never shined
And the wind began to whisper that we'd left our God behind

The first mate went to madness on the seventh night at sea
He spoke in tongues of fire 'bout the thing that's set to be
The water turned to ink beneath a moon as red as sin
And something in the cargo started scratching from within

Lord, oh Lord, this ship is bound for hell
We're carrying a curse no priest can quell
The sails are black, the sea is wide
And there ain't no shore on the other side

One by one the crew went down, the fever took them all
I heard them praying softly in the shadow of the squall
I nailed the crate with crosses but the nails just turned to rust
And the voice inside kept laughing, said you'll join us when you must

Lord, oh Lord, this ship is bound for hell
We're carrying a curse no priest can quell
The sails are black, the sea is wide
And there ain't no shore on the other side

If you find this ship a-drifting with no soul upon the deck
Don't you open up that cargo, don't you tempt the wretched wreck
Let it sink into the deep where the drowned men softly moan
Some things are meant to stay forever lost and all alone

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Boots on the tarmac, eyes to the ground
The same old streets but a different sound
They gave me a medal, they gave me a flag
But nobody gave me my brothers back

The nightmares follow me into the day
The ghosts in my head got nothin' to say
They just stand and stare with their hollow eyes
While the whole town smiles and the whole town lies

I CAME HOME BUT I'M STILL AT WAR
DON'T KNOW WHAT I'M FIGHTING FOR
FLESH AND BLOOD BUT MY SOUL IS GONE
I CAME HOME BUT THE WAR LIVES ON

They thank me for service, they shake my hand
But they'll never know, they don't understand
I left a piece of me in the sand and the smoke
And the man who came back is a different bloke

I CAME HOME BUT I'M STILL AT WAR
DON'T KNOW WHAT I'M FIGHTING FOR
FLESH AND BLOOD BUT MY SOUL IS GONE
I CAME HOME BUT THE WAR LIVES ON

Tear it down, tear it all down
This hero's welcome, this empty crown
I don't want your parade, I don't want your pity
Just give me back the man I used to be

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The room's still humming with the words we threw
Now there's nothing left but the space and you
Your back is turned but I feel you near
In the heavy silence, baby, I'm still here

The coffee's gone cold on the kitchen table
I wanna reach out but I'm not quite able
We said some things that we can't take back
Now we're lying in the dark on opposite tracks

In the quiet, in the quiet after
Where's the love, where's the laughter
I don't wanna win, I just wanna stay
In the quiet, let me find a way

Your breathing slows but you're not asleep
And these little wounds, baby, they cut deep
But underneath the anger there's a softer truth
I'd rather lose the fight than lose you

In the quiet, in the quiet after
Where's the love, where's the laughter
I don't wanna win, I just wanna stay
In the quiet, let me find a way

So I'll reach across the cold divide
Find your hand in the dark and hold it tight
We don't gotta say a single word
The quiet says everything that needs to be heard

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I prayed to a ceiling, I prayed to the rain
But the silence came back with nothing but pain
The candles burned out and the stained glass went grey
And I packed up my heaven and walked away

For years I was drifting through hollow and haze
Counting the empty unanswered days
The sky was just sky and the stars were just dust
And I lost every reason to hope or to trust

But the light, oh the light came slow
Like a tide that I didn't know
I was falling but I floated instead
And I found it again in the quiet ahead

It wasn't a thunder, it wasn't a sign
Just a warmth in the morning, a softening line
A stranger's small kindness, a child's open hand
And somehow the broken began to mend

And the light, oh the light came slow
Like a tide that I didn't know
I was falling but I floated instead
And I found it again in the quiet ahead

Maybe faith isn't certainty, maybe it's this
The leaning toward something I can't quite miss
I don't have the answers, I don't need to know
I just feel it returning, soft and slow

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Packed up the duffel, one last look around
Dust on the dashboard, leavin' this town
Water tower fadin' in the rearview glass
Everything I knew slippin' into the past

Mama on the porch with a wave and a tear
Said go chase your dreams, ain't nothin' for you here
The diner, the church, the field where we'd run
Goodbye to it all under the morning sun

I'm gone, I'm gone, down the highway line
Leavin' this small town, leavin' it behind
Gotta find myself in a bigger sky
So I'm sayin' so long, sayin' goodbye

The radio crackles an old familiar song
And I wonder if leavin' is right or wrong
But the road keeps callin', the city keeps glowin'
I don't know where I'm headed but I gotta keep goin'

I'm gone, I'm gone, down the highway line
Leavin' this small town, leavin' it behind
Gotta find myself in a bigger sky
So I'm sayin' so long, sayin' goodbye

Maybe one day I'll come back through
Walk these streets that once I knew
But for now I'm chasin' somethin' new
Small town, I'll always carry you

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Flour on the counter, garlic in the air
Plastic-covered couches and a rocking chair
The radiator clanking, the windows steamed up tight
Grandma's in the kitchen on a Sunday night

Sauce been simmering since the break of dawn
Same stained apron that she always had on
Yelling that you're too skinny, shove another plate
Nobody leaves hungry, nobody's ever late

I CAN STILL SMELL IT, I CAN STILL SMELL IT
That kitchen, that warmth, I can't help but yell it
The whole world was burning but I never knew
Long as that kitchen smelled like you

Now the house got sold and the stove's gone cold
But I catch it on a street corner, makes me feel old
A whiff of oregano, a stranger's open door
And I'm seven years old on her kitchen floor

I CAN STILL SMELL IT, I CAN STILL SMELL IT
That kitchen, that warmth, I can't help but yell it
The whole world was burning but I never knew
Long as that kitchen smelled like you

They can take the house, they can take the years
They can fill my eyes with stupid tears
But they'll never take that Sunday smell
That little crowded kitchen heaven, that little crowded hell

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Chalk on his fingers, a tape round his neck
Needle and thread and a measuring respect
He's been on the corner since before I was born
Stitching up the Sunday suits and the Friday torn

The groom and the mourner both come through his door
He knows all their shoulders, he knows all their scores
He lets out the waistline, he takes in the seam
He dresses the whole block in their borrowed dreams

He's the keeper of the cloth, the keeper of the thread
Dressing all the living, dressing all the dead
Every wedding, every wake, every job to find
He measures out our lives one stitch at a time

He pinned my first jacket when I was a kid
He knew who I'd become before I ever did
There's a whole town's history hanging on his rack
The tailor on the corner who dresses the block

He's the keeper of the cloth, the keeper of the thread
Dressing all the living, dressing all the dead
Every wedding, every wake, every job to find
He measures out our lives one stitch at a time

When he finally hangs his shears up on the wall
Who's gonna mend the seams, who's gonna catch us when we fall
There's a fading kind of magic in those hands
The quiet kind of grace nobody understands

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Well I raised up my hand, Lord, I cleared out my throat
But before I could speak there's another man spoke
Said the very same thing that I tried to say
And they all nodded yes and they sent me away

I got the good idea, I got it down pat
But my voice in that room don't amount to that
They roll right over me like a freight train song
And I'm sitting in the corner just singing along

Talked over, talked over again
In a room full of louder men
Got the answer on the tip of my tongue
But the meeting's over before I've begun

Mmm, I wrote it in the email, I said it out loud
But I disappear somewhere in that crowd
They steal my little notion and they call it their own
And I'm left in that boardroom feeling all alone

Talked over, talked over again
In a room full of louder men
Got the answer on the tip of my tongue
But the meeting's over before I've begun

One of these days I'm gonna slam my fist down
Gonna make 'em all hush in that conference town
Gonna say my piece 'til the very last word
And for once in my life, Lord, I'm gonna be heard

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Well I loaded up the Ford in the dead of night
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
Jugs of white lightning shining moonlight bright
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop)
Daddy said be careful, son, them roads ain't safe
But I kissed my baby and I drove away

The county line was waiting with a flashing red
(Doo-wop, doo-wop)
A siren and a deputy, a feeling of dread
(Doo-wop, doo-wop)
I hit the gas a-runnin' down that gravel grade
With a trunk full of trouble and a debt unpaid

Oh no, oh no, the run went wrong tonight
(Bom-bom-bom)
Moonshine spillin' in the pale headlight
Oh no, oh no, I'm prayin' I'll be free
(Bom-bom-bom)
Sweet baby, won't you wait for me

The wheels they started slidin' on the river bend
(Sha-la-la)
And I knew right then it was the bitter end
(Sha-la-la)
The jugs went a-shatterin', the night went white
And the moonshine washed away into the night

Oh no, oh no, the run went wrong tonight
(Bom-bom-bom)
Moonshine spillin' in the pale headlight
Oh no, oh no, I'm prayin' I'll be free
(Bom-bom-bom)
Sweet baby, won't you wait for me

So if you're drivin' liquor down a back-road line
(Shoo-bop)
Remember this poor fool and his moonshine
(Shoo-bop)
Some runs you don't come back from, that's the cost
Of a midnight dollar and a soul that's lost

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The candles are spent and the mourners have gone
The kettle is cold but the morning comes on
I walk through the kitchen still heavy with grief
And the silence is louder than any belief

The black coats are folded, the whiskey is dry
The last of the hymns has long faded by
The fields are still green and the river still runs
Though the world lost its colour, the morning still comes

And oh, the morning after
When the wake has lost its laughter
The birds still sing in the rowan tree
Though you're not here, though you're not here with me

I'll gather the glasses, I'll sweep up the floor
I'll open the windows and unlatch the door
The living must carry the weight of the day
Though my heart wants to follow you, follow your way

And oh, the morning after
When the wake has lost its laughter
The birds still sing in the rowan tree
Though you're not here, though you're not here with me

So I'll lift up my chin to the grey of the sky
And I'll thank you for everything, kiss you goodbye
The morning is cruel but the morning is kind
For it carries me forward and leaves you behind

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Used to be a giant, used to fill the room
Hands that could break anything, voice like a sonic boom
Now he holds the railing, now he takes it slow
When did the mountain turn to melting snow

He can't read the small print, can't recall my name
The man who built the whole world ain't quite the same
I'm catching him from falling, I'm cutting up his food
And it's tearing me apart, man, it's bitter, it's cruel

YOU WERE NEVER SUPPOSED TO GET OLD
YOU WERE STEEL, YOU WERE STONE, YOU WERE BOLD
NOW I'M HOLDING YOUR HAND AT THE DOOR
AND I DON'T KNOW WHO WE'RE FIGHTING FOR

I was scared of you once, now I'm scared for you
The roles got reversed and I don't know what to do
Little boy still in me wants his dad back strong
But the clock keeps on ticking and the time's near gone

YOU WERE NEVER SUPPOSED TO GET OLD
YOU WERE STEEL, YOU WERE STONE, YOU WERE BOLD
NOW I'M HOLDING YOUR HAND AT THE DOOR
AND I DON'T KNOW WHO WE'RE FIGHTING FOR

So I'll be your giant now, I'll fill the room
I'll be the steady hands, I'll chase away the gloom
Everything you gave me I'm giving back
Old man, I got you, I won't let you crack

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Ten and two on the wheel, baby, easy on the gas
The whole world is waiting but there ain't no need to rush
Your hands are kinda shaking and your eyes are open wide
But I'm right here beside you for the ride

Wasn't it just yesterday I taught you how to walk
Now you're checking mirrors while we make our little talk
The engine's softly humming like a slow and easy tune
And you're driving down the avenue beneath the afternoon

So take it slow, sweetheart, take it slow
There's a whole life of roads where you're gonna go
I'll let go of the wheel but I won't let go of you
Just take it slow, sweetheart, take it slow

You eased around the corner and you flashed that nervous grin
And I saw the little girl and the woman you've become within
My hands they wanna grab it but I keep 'em on my knee
'Cause the hardest part of loving is to set somebody free

So take it slow, sweetheart, take it slow
There's a whole life of roads where you're gonna go
I'll let go of the wheel but I won't let go of you
Just take it slow, sweetheart, take it slow

One day you'll be driving down a highway far from here
And I hope you'll hear me whisper through the rolling atmosphere
Keep your eyes upon the road and keep a little love in view
And know your daddy's always in the seat right next to you

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Clock on the wall say a quarter past two
Kettle gone cold but I'm pushing it through
Highlighter dry and my eyes start to swim
Streetlight outside humming low and dim
Mama's asleep down the hall in her bed
Got a whole world of pages still left to be read

One more chapter, one more cup
Long before the morning come I won't give it up
Jah give me patience in the still of the night
Study by the lamp till the city see light

No riddim in the room but the fan and my pen
Tomorrow they testing what I'm learning again
I write down the answer then I write it once more
Till the knowledge sink deep like the tide on the shore

One more chapter, one more cup
Long before the morning come I won't give it up
Jah give me patience in the still of the night
Study by the lamp till the city see light

Somebody dreaming, somebody gone
But the slow and the steady is the ones carry on
When the rooster crow I'll be ready to stand
With the whole of the lesson living right in my hand

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Two plates in the cupboard, I reach for one
The kettle still thinks there are two
The recipe card in your faded hand
Says simmer the way that you do
Steam on the window, a softening dark
The onion goes gold and goes slow
I measure for halves I will never serve
And quietly let the rest go

A table for one in the lamplight
A chair pulled out for the air
I am learning the salt, I am learning the flame
Learning to sit with you there

I burned the first week, I cried in the smoke
The smoke alarm sang like a hymn
Now the broth knows my name and the bread understands
The long quiet hours within

Somewhere the spoon finds the rhythm again
Somewhere a window goes warm
I set down one bowl in the hush of the house
And I keep a small light from the storm

A table for one in the lamplight
A chair pulled out for the air
I am learning the salt, I am learning the flame
Learning to sit with you there

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I fold the page in the lantern glow
And send it down where the cold tides go
Dear restless one, dear silver skin
I write your name where the waves come in
The gulls take flight in a blur of white
My ink runs blue in the salt of night

Oh sea, oh sea, will you read me tonight
I signal you slow with my turning light
No stamp, no shore, just the foam and the moon
My love is a lighthouse, I'll find you soon

You never answer, you only rise
A hush, a roar, a hundred eyes
I seal the bottle and let it drown
A paper heart in a sinking town

If you should swallow my every word
Let the deep keep them safe, let them never be heard
I am yours till the last bulb dies
A flickering kiss to the endless skies

Oh sea, oh sea, will you read me tonight
I signal you slow with my turning light
No stamp, no shore, just the foam and the moon
My love is a lighthouse, I'll find you soon

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Packed my mother's recipes and a photograph
A suitcase full of summers and a nervous laugh
The harbor took the morning, took the only home I knew
And the engine started humming and the gray gulls flew

So I'm crossing, crossing over the wide and the deep
With a promise in my pocket I intend to keep
I'ma plant my name in a foreign ground
And grow something tender where the tide come down

They speak a language faster than my tongue can hold
But my grandmother's courage runs warm against the cold
I count the coins, I learn the streets, I memorize the rain
And I write back home in letters that I won't explain

Ooh, the salt in the air taste a little like tears
but a little like freedom after all these years
Whatever I lose, whatever I find
I carry the old country deep in my mind

So I'm crossing, crossing over the wide and the deep
With a promise in my pocket I intend to keep
I'ma plant my name in a foreign ground
And grow something tender where the tide come down

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Tres puntos en la pantalla y se borraron
Tus palabras frías como el aire acondicionado
No llamada, no abrazo, solo un mensajito
Mataste lo nuestro con un solo escrito
Leí mil veces ese gris globito
Y el corazón se me quedó frío y solito

Por un texto, por un texto se acabó
Ni siquiera la voz, ni siquiera un adiós
Vibró el teléfono y se cayó el amor
Por un texto, mami, me partiste en dos

Guardé las fotos pero borré el dolor
Baila la calle aunque sangre el interior
Tú escribiendo punto, yo perdiendo el control
Un renglón apagó todo mi sol

No me escribas más, ya leí lo que sentías
Un mensaje no merece todas mis días
Apago la pantalla, prendo la ciudad
Y bailo sola con mi libertad

Por un texto, por un texto se acabó
Ni siquiera la voz, ni siquiera un adiós
Vibró el teléfono y se cayó el amor
Por un texto, mami, me partiste en dos

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They put us in a circle and the drum start to roll
You the thorn in my morning, you the stone in my soul
The elders say we partner till the festival done
Two fighting roosters underneath the same sun
I step, you step, neither giving no ground
But the riddim keep turning us around and around

Dance with your enemy, sway with your foe
The drum don't care who win or who lose, you know
One love in the rhythm even rivals must find
We dancing the war right out of we mind

Your hand meet my hand and it tremble like a leaf
All the years of the grudge start to crumble underneath
The crowd start to clapping, the bassline take hold
And the cold in between us turning warm into gold

Maybe we were never so far apart
Same fire in the feet, same drum in the heart
When the song reach the end and we letting it go
We nod like two brothers the music help us know

Dance with your enemy, sway with your foe
The drum don't care who win or who lose, you know
One love in the rhythm even rivals must find
We dancing the war right out of we mind

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
They carry him slow down Rampart Street
A black umbrella and a tambourine beat
The horns hang low and the dirge come first
A river of mourning, a quenchless thirst
Miss Marie in her veil, she lean on her cane
And the trombone weep like a falling rain

Then the saints come marching, let the gold notes rise
We cry him to heaven, we dance him to the skies
Sorrow in the morning but the evening bring the brass
In New Orleans, baby, even grief gonna pass

The snare it rattle and the tuba groan
We don't lay our people in the ground alone
When the second line shuffle and the handkerchief wave
We celebrate the living right on top the grave

Oh, let me go home on a current of song
Let the city of music carry me along
No silence for the soul that the Lord set free
Just a hot horn blowin' over the levee and the sea

Then the saints come marching, let the gold notes rise
We cry him to heaven, we dance him to the skies
Sorrow in the morning but the evening bring the brass
In New Orleans, baby, even grief gonna pass

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Abro la boca y ya están hablando
Mi idea en el aire, otro se la va robando
Levanto la mano, nadie está mirando
En cada reunión me siguen ignorando
Tengo la data, tengo el flow, tengo el plan
Pero el del traje gris se lleva el aplauso, ¡qué afán!

Escúchame, escúchame, déjame hablar
No me pises la voz cuando voy a explicar
Uno, dos, tres, dame el micrófono ya
Que lo que yo digo también va a brillar

Interrumpe el jefe, interrumpe el de al lado
Mi propuesta de oro y el crédito robado
Pero subo el volumen como bajo pesado
Hoy la sala completa me deja sentado

Se acabó el silencio, prende el altavoz
Voy a repetirlo con mi propia voz
Que retumbe el cuarto, que tiemble el café
Hoy hablo yo primero y todos van a ver

Escúchame, escúchame, déjame hablar
No me pises la voz cuando voy a explicar
Uno, dos, tres, dame el micrófono ya
Que lo que yo digo también va a brillar

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Gray fluorescent prison, the clock is bleeding slow
I raise my voice to speak and they bury it below
A hundred hollow buzzwords, a synergy of lies
They steal the words out of my throat and watch the silence rise

LET ME SPEAK! Tear the table down
Drown out the drowning, choke the corporate sound
I WILL BE HEARD though the suits all scream
A scream from the cubicle, a scream from the machine

Interrupted, gutted, my sentence cut in two
They nod at his reflection of the thing I tried to do
The whiteboard's smeared with carcasses of every plan I made
A graveyard of the voiceless where the brave ideas are laid

No more raising hands
No more standing down
I break the polished surface, I am thunder underground
The mute become the mountain, the meek become the blade
And every word they swallowed comes back as a grenade

LET ME SPEAK! Tear the table down
Drown out the drowning, choke the corporate sound
I WILL BE HEARD though the suits all scream
A scream from the cubicle, a scream from the machine

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Last string of lights on the fairground tonight
The big wheel turning slow, going dim, going quiet
Vendors packing up the sugar and the smoke
And the one tired fiddle play the last little note
Well I spent all my money, I spent all my days
Now I'm watching the field through the trampled-down haze

It's the last night, baby, the last night of the show
The band's gonna pack up and the crowd's gonna go
One more dance in the dust 'fore they cut off the sound
Last night of the festival, sun coming round

Met a girl by the gate when the music was loud
Lost her somewhere in the swell of the crowd
Now the tents coming down and the stage going dark
And I'm holding one ticket and a fading spark

Mister, kill the floodlights, let the morning come slow
Let me stand in the litter where the whole world did go
'Cause tomorrow it's a meadow, just a mud and a moon
But tonight it was heaven, and it ended too soon

It's the last night, baby, the last night of the show
The band's gonna pack up and the crowd's gonna go
One more dance in the dust 'fore they cut off the sound
Last night of the festival, sun coming round

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew its final breath at half past three
Forty years of iron and they hand a man his key
The line goes still, the lights go cold, the great machines all sleep
And the floor that fed the city got a silence way too deep

They're closing down, closing down the gates
Where my daddy poured his shoulders and his faith
What you do with hands that only knew the steel
Lord, tell me what a working man is s'posed to feel

Mary worked the third shift, Joe ran the press
Now they're sweeping out the lockers of a town they won't address
The paychecks built the churches and the school upon the hill
But the men downtown decided that the world don't need us still

But you can't shut down a spirit, no you can't padlock a soul
We'll carry every callus to whatever's down the road
The smokestack may be quiet but the people still stand tall
There's a fire in the family that no closing gonna stall

They're closing down, closing down the gates
Where my daddy poured his shoulders and his faith
What you do with hands that only knew the steel
Lord, tell me what a working man is s'posed to feel

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up in the attic with a wire and a prayer
A secondhand transmitter throwing soul through the air
The man say it's illegal, say they'll come for my sound
But I broadcast from the rooftops where the free folk are found
Midnight to morning, this needle never sleeps
Spinning all the records that the radio won't keep

This is your pirate, coming in on the wire
Ninety-nine point heaven, setting hearts on fire
They can't license a feeling, can't tax a song
So I'll keep on transmitting all night long

Mrs. Brown is dancing in her kitchen alone
The cab drivers cruising with my voice in the zone
When the signal goes wandering through the cracks in the town
We a family of strangers and we won't be shut down

If they kick in my door, if they pull out the plug
I'll be back next evening with a kiss and a hug
For the lonely, the restless, the ones in the dark
I'm the ghost in your speaker, I'm the unlicensed spark

This is your pirate, coming in on the wire
Ninety-nine point heaven, setting hearts on fire
They can't license a feeling, can't tax a song
So I'll keep on transmitting all night long

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the Ford with a hundred quart jars
Daddy's old recipe and a sky full of stars
Down the county backroad with the headlights off low
Where the sheriff and the preacher swear they never gonna go
I was nineteen and certain I could outrun the law
With a tank full of gasoline and a deal with my craw

But the moon ran red on the mountain that night
The blue lights a-flashing in the rearview light
I took the curve too fast on the gravel and the rain
And I left my daddy's whiskey scattered all down the lane

Glass in the ditch and the engine gone still
Smell of corn liquor soaking into the hill
The deputy laughing as he wrote down my name
And the family business ended in the headlights and the shame

Mama don't cry now, I'll be home in a year
The judge wasn't gentle but the lesson's right here
Some roads ain't worth running, some jars ain't worth the gold
I traded all my freedom for a story to be told

And the moon ran red on the mountain that night
The blue lights a-flashing in the rearview light
I took the curve too fast on the gravel and the rain
And I left my daddy's whiskey scattered all down the lane

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night 'bout the same time, baby, my body leave the bed
Arms turn to feathers and the moon get in my head
I rise above the rooftops, I rise above the wire
With the cold wind in my collar and my heart a-catching fire
Then the rooster start to crowing and it all come tumbling down
And I wake up Monday morning with my two feet on the ground

I been flying, flying in my sleep again
Up where the troubles can't follow, up where the sky don't end
But the daylight always catch me, pull me back down low
Lord, I'm a bird in the evening and a stone when the morning show

The rent man, he can't reach me when I'm sailing through the blue
The bossman and the heartache, they ain't got a thing to do
I seen the whole town shining like a handful of dimes
Then I fall back through the ceiling into all these heavy times

One of these mornings, mama, I ain't coming back to land
Gonna keep on rising, rising past the reach of any hand
Till the dream and the daylight finally learn to be the same
And the man down here forgets to call my name

I been flying, flying in my sleep again
Up where the troubles can't follow, up where the sky don't end
But the daylight always catch me, pull me back down low
Lord, I'm a bird in the evening and a stone when the morning show

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Me vistieron el destino desde que nací
Un anillo, una fecha, una vida pa' mí
El novio que escogieron, la casa, el papel
Pero el corazón no firma lo que escriben en él
Esta noche hago maletas, salgo por el portón
Con el alma latiendo a mi propio son

Me voy, me voy, no me esperen atrás
El futuro que pintaron ya no me da paz
Rompo la promesa que yo nunca firmé
Y bailo en la calle con mi propio porqué

Perdón, mamá, perdón, pero esta no soy yo
Una jaula de oro sigue siendo prisión
Guardo tu cariño pero suelto tu plan
Voy a escribir mi historia donde nadie la dan

Que digan lo que digan en el pueblo de ayer
Yo prefiero perderme que vivir sin querer
Con dos manos vacías y el cielo por hogar
Me llevo mi libertad, no me la pueden quitar

Me voy, me voy, no me esperen atrás
El futuro que pintaron ya no me da paz
Rompo la promesa que yo nunca firmé
Y bailo en la calle con mi propio porqué

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights were low at the corner bar
Folks said tonight you'd be a star
A man in a suit said he liked my sound
Then the morning came and he weren't around

I had the crowd up on their feet
Played every note slow and sweet
Felt the door crack open wide
Then it shut on the other side

Oh I almost made it, almost touched the sky
So close I could taste it, never knew why
The train it left the station, I was one step behind
Almost made it, baby, lost it on the line

Now I play the same old room
Under the same old yellow gloom
Dream of a name up there in lights
That slipped away in the back of the night

Maybe almost is all I get
A pocket full of close and regret
But I'll strap this guitar on once more
And knock again on that golden door

Oh I almost made it, almost touched the sky
So close I could taste it, never knew why
The train it left the station, I was one step behind
Almost made it, baby, lost it on the line

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Dishes in the sink, neither of us moves
Clock on the wall counting down our grooves
Words already said, hanging in the air
You on the couch, me on the stair
Vinyl crackle low, static in my chest
Said too much, now I'm second-guessed
Lamp light humming soft and gold
Room feels twice as wide, twice as cold

It's quiet now, just the rain on glass
Waiting for the heavy in my heart to pass
No more shouting, just the hum and the hush
Love still here underneath the dust

I hear you breathing two rooms away
Rehearse the sorry I don't know how to say
Kettle clicks off, tea gone cold
Pride's an anchor, heavy to hold

Maybe I cross the hall, maybe I knock
Maybe we let the silence talk
Maybe forgiveness is a quiet thing
A hand on a shoulder, no need to sing

It's quiet now, just the rain on glass
Waiting for the heavy in my heart to pass
No more shouting, just the hum and the hush
Love still here underneath the dust

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete shining under neon rain
Not a leaf left growing in the lane
But on the corner where the parking ends
One green dancer in the wind still bends

Last tree standing, shake your leaves tonight
Under the streetlamp, catch the silver light
Everybody paved it, but you're still around
Last tree dancing in a paved-over town

They poured the gray from wall to wall
Forgot you growing by the shopping mall
Roots crack the sidewalk, push on through
The whole disco city owes its breath to you

Last tree standing, shake your leaves tonight
Under the streetlamp, catch the silver light
Everybody paved it, but you're still around
Last tree dancing in a paved-over town

So plant a little seed beneath the floor
We don't have to live this gray no more
Mirror ball spinning like a summer sun
Let the green come back for everyone

Last tree standing, shake your leaves tonight
Under the streetlamp, catch the silver light
Everybody paved it, but you're still around
Last tree dancing in a paved-over town

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
My thumb been hovering over your name
For longer than I'd care to say
All that pride and all that shame
Kept the words so far away

The phone rings once, it rings twice more
My heart's a drum against my chest
Then I hear your voice like before
And all the years just come to rest

Mama, it's me, I'm calling home
I'm sorry it's been so long
I never should have let you alone
Mama, I'm still your own

You don't ask me where I've been
You just say baby, are you fed
And every wall I built within
Comes tumbling down instead

All the time we let go by
All the words we left unsaid
Let me hold them while I cry
Let me love you on ahead

Mama, it's me, I'm calling home
I'm sorry it's been so long
I never should have let you alone
Mama, I'm still your own

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning and the weight was light
Something let go in the middle of the night
You been gone now for a year and a day
But I held the anger anyway

So I forgive you, pick it up, horns go loud
Forgive you, throw the heavy in the crowd
You can't hear me but I'll say it just the same
I forgive you, I let go of the blame

Upstroke guitar and the trumpet's on the run
Dancing off the grudge I carried like a gun
No more letters that I'll never send
Just a skank for an old lost friend

So I forgive you, pick it up, horns go loud
Forgive you, throw the heavy in the crowd
You can't hear me but I'll say it just the same
I forgive you, I let go of the blame

Didn't do it for you, did it for me
Funny how the letting go can set you free
Wherever you are, hope you're dancing too
Nothing left between us but the rhythm coming through

So I forgive you, pick it up, horns go loud
Forgive you, throw the heavy in the crowd
You can't hear me but I'll say it just the same
I forgive you, I let go of the blame

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They picked the man and they picked the day
Stitched my name to a life of gray
A house on the hill and a ring of gold
A story written before I was old

But the crows out the window call to me
Of a darker road and a wilder sea
I packed my bag by the candle's moan
Left the wedding dress on the bed alone

I'm leaving the future they carved in stone
Walking the night down a road of my own
Let the church bells ring for an empty pew
I'd rather be lost than belong to you

My mother weeps and my father swears
The preacher prays on the chapel stairs
But the moon hangs low like a hanging tree
And it's calling out my name to be free

Maybe I'll wander, maybe I'll fall
Maybe the dark will swallow me whole
But a borrowed life is a kind of grave
And I'd rather be ruined than be their slave

I'm leaving the future they carved in stone
Walking the night down a road of my own
Let the church bells ring for an empty pew
I'd rather be lost than belong to you

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Forty years pressed into one slow room
The needle drops, the speakers bloom
Your hand finds mine like it always knew
One more rhythm before we're through

The papers signed and laid to the side
But tonight we move, we set them aside
Bass drops low and the kick comes fast
Dancing on the wreckage of a love that lasts

One last dance before the lights go down
Spin me through the silence, lift me off the ground
We broke but we're moving, we lost but we're here
One last dance, my dear, one last dance, my dear

The break comes in like a flood released
All the years pour out, the anger, the peace
Sweat on your brow, tears in my eyes
Letting go at a hundred and seventy-five

When the drop hits, I'll remember you young
The songs we played and the life we sung
The beat won't break though our hands let go
Fade us out nice and slow

One last dance before the lights go down
Spin me through the silence, lift me off the ground
We broke but we're moving, we lost but we're here
One last dance, my dear, one last dance, my dear

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Midnight blue and the screen glows bright
Three little dots in the velvet night
I know better, oh I surely do
But my thumb keeps spelling out the word for you

Shouldn't be texting you, baby, but I am
Pouring out my trouble like the rain on the glass
Every little message is a match in the dark
Shouldn't be texting you, but here goes my heart

The saxophone's crying down the hall
I delete it twice then I send it all
Dangerous as honey, sweet as sin
One more message and I let you in

There's a wedding ring in a drawer somewhere
There's a promise hanging in the smoky air
But the night is long and the wine is low
And I'm typing things I shouldn't know

Shouldn't be texting you, baby, but I am
Pouring out my trouble like the rain on the glass
Every little message is a match in the dark
Shouldn't be texting you, but here goes my heart

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
The tether snapped without a sound
No up, no under, no more ground
I watch your visor catch the sun
We were two and now we're one and one

Drifting, drifting through the black
No hand can reach, no voice comes back
Still I see you, small and far
Two slow lights between the stars

Your radio fades to a whispering hush
The distance grows in the silent rush
I raise my glove to a frozen wave
Across the dark that nothing saves

We will fall in different arcs
Two quiet sparks in endless dark
No sorrow loud enough to send
Just the soft forever of the end

Drifting, drifting through the black
No hand can reach, no voice comes back
Still I see you, small and far
Two slow lights between the stars

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Domingo en la mañana, but I'm not in the pew
The bells they keep ringing, I'm walking on through
Grew up on the hymns and the incense smoke
But something in my chest finally broke

Me voy callado, no slamming the door
I just can't believe like I did before
No grito, no fight, just a quiet goodbye
Leaving the church under an open sky

Mi abuela in the front row, hands to the light
I used to feel it, now it don't feel right
Love for the people, love for the song
But the certainty's a place I don't belong

No soy malo, I'm just being true
Found my own faith in the morning dew
Keep the kindness, leave the rest behind
Walk out gentle, peace of mind

Me voy callado, no slamming the door
I just can't believe like I did before
No grito, no fight, just a quiet goodbye
Leaving the church under an open sky

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Glow sticks fading in the morning haze
Three days of sun in a golden daze
The main stage humming for one more song
We sang so loud and we sang so long

Don't wanna go, don't wanna leave
Got grass on my shoes and your hand on my sleeve

Last night of the festival, hold it while it's here
Confetti in your hair and the lights so clear
We'll be strangers by Monday but we're forever tonight
Last night of the festival, hands to the sky, hold tight

Tents coming down with the rising sun
Numbers swapped and the goodbyes begun
But the bassline's beating like it'll never end
Dancing in the dust with a brand new friend

When we're old and gray we'll remember this
The summer, the stage, the goodbye kiss
One more chorus before it's gone
Keep the festival lights on

Last night of the festival, hold it while it's here
Confetti in your hair and the lights so clear
We'll be strangers by Monday but we're forever tonight
Last night of the festival, hands to the sky, hold tight

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raise my hand and the room goes loud
Swallowed up by the louder crowd
Half a sentence then somebody cuts
I'm a ghost in a shirt, I'm a closing shut

I had the answer, had it good
Said it quiet like I always would
Then he says it back an hour late
And they all nod like it's something great

Talk over me, talk over me
Like I'm furniture, like I'm scenery
One day I'll stand and I'll let it ring
But today I just watch the meeting

Glass conference room and a gray flat sky
My good idea just floating by
I write it down in a notebook spine
Little paper graveyard, all of it mine

Maybe tomorrow I'll talk so loud
Cut clean through that talking crowd
Or maybe I'll just walk on out
Find a quieter room to figure it out

Talk over me, talk over me
Like I'm furniture, like I'm scenery
One day I'll stand and I'll let it ring
But today I just watch the meeting

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man walks where the water meets the stone
Every evening, every dusk, alone
He lifts his cane and the gulls draw near
Teaching the tide what it needs to hear

Ven, marea, come and go
The old man knows the steps to show
In and out like a dancer's breath
He leads the sea through life and death

Pull, he says, and the water pulls
Push, he says, and the great wave rolls
A bandoneón sighs from the boardwalk light
As he counts the rhythm of the night

Who taught the ocean how to sway
Before the old man passed this way
Maybe the tide was teaching him
This slow dura nce, this ebb, this hymn

Ven, marea, come and go
The old man knows the steps to show
In and out like a dancer's breath
He leads the sea through life and death

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
You walk in late and the room turns gold
A story in your eyes that won't be told
I pour my heart in a minor key
But you never look across at me

I love you in the spaces you don't see
A melody you'll never sing to me
You're the chord I'm always reaching for
The open and the always-closing door

You talk of someone else and I just smile
Play it cool for a little while
The trumpet aches what I can't say
And you go home the other way

Maybe in another smoky room
Another life, another tune
You'd hear the way my heart keeps time
And somehow know that you were mine

I love you in the spaces you don't see
A melody you'll never sing to me
You're the chord I'm always reaching for
The open and the always-closing door

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Neon midnight, eyes fall closed
The city shrinks below my toes
Same as last night, same as before
My feet lift off the bedroom floor

I'm flying, flying through the violet light
Over the skyline every night
Arms wide open, chrome and chrome
In the dream I'm finally home

Grid of streetlamps, pink and blue
The horizon hums a song I knew
No one chasing, nothing wrong
Just the engine of the night so long

Then the alarm and the gravity
Pulls me back to the ground from the sea
But I close my eyes on the morning train
And I'm rising over the world again

I'm flying, flying through the violet light
Over the skyline every night
Arms wide open, chrome and chrome
In the dream I'm finally home

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Vinyl crackle, window fogged with my own breath
Thermos going cold while I'm chasing the depth
Green ribbon spilling over the spruce line
slow as a thought I can't quite design
Dashboard humming a beat that won't keep time
snow on the antenna like sugar on a rind

Let it bleed across the dark, let it bleed
purple over white, that's all I need
no hook, no rush, just the hum of the cold
the sky writing letters too quiet to hold

I left the city's orange behind the hill
found a frequency where the world goes still
tape deck wobble and a heater that ticks
stars getting buried under aurora bricks

No replay button on a sky like this
shutter too slow for the way it shifts
so I pocket the cold and I let it go
memory grainy as the radio's glow

Let it bleed across the dark, let it bleed
green over black, that's all I need
no hook, no rush, just the hum of the cold
the sky writing letters too quiet to hold

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Kick the door of the night wide open
latitude high and the lid's been broken
sixty-eight degrees and the dark gets loud
green voltage tearing through the cloud
Run the ridge with my pulse on double
sky cracks open, here comes the trouble
photons drum on the back of my eyes
bassline buried in the polar skies

Faster, faster, the curtain's alive
one seventy-four and the heavens dive
ribbon snap, snare on the snow
run til the green won't let me go

Magnetar wind off a far-flung sun
slams the atmosphere, the rollers run
strobe of emerald, violet flare
feet off the ground and I don't care

Drop—
silence on the tundra, just my breath in white
then the whole horizon ignites the night
count it in, four, the sky reloads
green artillery down the cold dark roads

Faster, faster, the curtain's alive
one seventy-four and the heavens dive
ribbon snap, snare on the snow
run til the green won't let me go

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raised my hand at the long oak table
opened my mouth but the room rolled over
like a river that don't know my name
ten voices louder, mine the same
I wrote it down so I wouldn't lose it
folded the paper, learned not to use it

But I got a voice, Lord, I got a voice
you put a fire in my throat by choice
let the table go quiet, let the loud ones cease
I'm gonna speak my piece, I'm gonna speak my peace

They clapped for the echo of what I'd said
when another man wore it on his own head
I swallowed the bitter, I held the line
the word was mine, the word was mine

There's a meeting up higher where the small get heard
where the last shall carry the first whole word
so I'm building my courage one breath at a time
the morning is coming and the floor is mine

I got a voice, Lord, I got a voice
you put a fire in my throat by choice
let the table go quiet, let the loud ones cease
I'm gonna speak my piece, I'm gonna speak my peace

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the gray from curb to curb
buried the meadow without a word
but one old oak in a crack stood tall
roots like fists through the parking wall
Delivery trucks make a ring around her
shade for a stray and a tired commuter

Stand, old tree, stand in the heat
last green crown over the concrete
they forgot to cut you, glory be
stand and remember the world we used to be

Kids leave ribbons on the lowest limb
an old man prays in the shade of him
seeds come down like a quiet choir
falling on stone that won't catch fire

Lord let the cracks keep widening wide
let a forest hide on the other side
for every paving stone they lay
you keep one root that won't give way

Stand, old tree, stand in the heat
last green crown over the concrete
they forgot to cut you, glory be
stand and remember the world we used to be

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Clock says two and the house is asleep
phone in my hand glowin' blue and cheap
I typed your name then I rubbed it out
then I typed it again, that's what this is about
Three gray dots, they come and they go
you know better, baby, and Lord knows I know

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but my thumb won't quit
every word I send is a match to the wick
Delete don't undo what the heart already sent
I'm spendin' on trouble I can't represent

Ring's on the dresser catchin' the streetlight
she's breathin' easy on her own good side
but the screen keeps pullin' me down in the dark
like a moth with a death wish circlin' the spark

One more message, just one, I swear
that's the lie I been tellin' the empty air
the little gray bubble, it floats and it stays
guiltiest light in the worst of ways

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but my thumb won't quit
every word I send is a match to the wick
Delete don't undo what the heart already sent
I'm spendin' on trouble I can't represent

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
We laid the brick where his shoulder lies
built a steeple up between his eyes
The streetcar runs on his collarbone
folks been livin' here like the ground's their own
My granddad swore he felt the hill breathe
set down his fiddle, told us all to leave

Sleep on, sleep on, you old stone king
don't you wake to the church bells ring
we built our whole world on your chest
so hush now, giant, take your rest

The river's just the sweat off his brow
we dammed it up and we plow his plow
When the quarry blasts he turns in his dream
and the whole town shivers at the seam

One day his eyelid's gonna lift like a door
and the courthouse tumbles to the canyon floor
so play it sweet and play it low
lullaby for the bones below

Sleep on, sleep on, you old stone king
don't you wake to the church bells ring
we built our whole world on your chest
so hush now, giant, take your rest

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We shared a tin can spinning past the moon
you hummed the only song we knew
Now your tether's frayed at the steel
and I can't reach the words to feel
The checklist says to cut the line
mission says your air's not mine

Don't let go, don't let go
we're just two lights in the undertow
but the dark keeps pulling, the radio's thin
and the cold gets in, the cold gets in

You're a slow blue dot getting small
still waving back through the comms and all
I count your breaths in the static hiss
fourteen years and it comes to this

If there's a window where the orbits cross
