I'll be there counting every loss
but the math is honest, the math is mean
you're a memory burning on a screen

Don't let go, don't let go
we're just two lights in the undertow
but the dark keeps pulling, the radio's thin
and the cold gets in, the cold gets in

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night the same old trick
feet leave the floor and the ceiling lifts
horns come up and the offbeat skanks
I'm over the rooftops, over the banks
Kick off the blanket, kick off the day
my bedroom window just blew away

Up, up, pick it up, I'm flying
over the chimney pots, no use trying
to hold me down with your gravity rules
I left them sleeping with the morning fools

The alarm clock's a saxophone scream
but I'm still riding the upstroke beam
telephone wires like a music staff
and I'm the note on the upstroke laugh

Then the pillow pulls me back to the bed
the trumpet fades to the clock instead
but tomorrow night when the lights go down
I'll pick it up again over the town

Up, up, pick it up, I'm flying
over the chimney pots, no use trying
to hold me down with your gravity rules
I left them sleeping with the morning fools

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
The lamp throws shadows on an empty chair
I dance the steps with the salt of the air
You took the apology I never gave
folded it under the marble of your grave
The bandoneon sighs what my mouth would not
the whole bitter year coming undone in a knot

I forgive you now, though the night won't hear
I lay it down where you used to be near
Too late, too late, and the strings agree
but I set you loose so you'd set me free

One step forward, the past steps back
the floor remembers the things we lack
I hold the silence the way one holds a hand
leading a partner that the dead understand

Let the candle gutter, let the violin moan
forgiveness given to a name on stone
is half for you and the rest for me
a lock I open with a missing key

I forgive you now, though the night won't hear
I lay it down where you used to be near
Too late, too late, and the strings agree
but I set you loose so you'd set me free

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Mi padre shook hands on a life for me
a house, a name, and a locked-up sea
The dress hangs white on the closet door
but my boots are pointed toward something more
Trumpets call from the dusty square
and my heart's a horse that won't stay there

Ay, I'm gone before the bells can ring
let the violins cry, let the whole town sing
I gave back the ring and I kept my name
better alone than a tame, sad flame

Mamá, don't weep at the open gate
I'd rather hunger than swallow that fate
The road is mine and the dawn is wide
no arranged tomorrow gonna ride at my side

The guitarrón thumps like a leaving train
the dust on my shoulders, the salt of the rain
I'll send you a song from a far-off town
where nobody hands me a borrowed crown

Ay, I'm gone before the bells can ring
let the violins cry, let the whole town sing
I gave back the ring and I kept my name
better alone than a tame, sad flame

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
Ooh, the corner where we used to stand
(sha-la-la) you'd hold my hand
Now I'm talkin' to the evening star
tellin' it softly wherever you are
I was holdin' a grudge like a worn-out coat
but tonight I'm settin' adrift that boat

I forgive you, darlin', I forgive you now
(doo-doo-wop) somehow, somehow
You're a name on the wind and the wind is kind
so I'm leavin' the hurtin' all behind

The jukebox plays our favorite tune
(sha-la-la) under the moon
I close my eyes and I let it go
the way you taught me, sweet and slow

No more cryin' on the front porch swing
(ooh-wah) let the angels sing
I made my peace with a fadin' ghost
the one I loved and lost the most

I forgive you, darlin', I forgive you now
(doo-doo-wop) somehow, somehow
You're a name on the wind and the wind is kind
so I'm leavin' the hurtin' all behind

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Dale, una sola ceiba en el cemento gris
rodeada de luces y antenas así
Los carros la rodean, no le dan na'
pero ella resiste, no se va a quebrar
Graffiti en el tronco, corazón y un nombre
la única sombra pa' un barrio sin hombre

Árbol, árbol, no te caigas, no
Último verde en este bloque que ardió
Sube la raíz, rompe la acera
baila conmigo la última primavera

La abuela le reza, los niños la trepan
las palomas en la copa no se despegan
Dicen que el lunes viene la excavadora
pero esta noche bailamos hasta la aurora

Si la cortan, planto cien en su lugar
una por cada cuadra de este malecón sin mar
Que retumbe el bajo, que tiemble el bloque
que el verde regrese y a nadie le choque

Árbol, árbol, no te caigas, no
Último verde en este bloque que ardió
Sube la raíz, rompe la acera
baila conmigo la última primavera

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
You packed the truck before the coffee cooled
radar lit up and your heart got schooled
in the language of the funnel and the falling glass
baby, I'm just weather that you're letting pass
You kiss me quick at the kitchen door
then you chase what the prairie's roaring for

You can't settle down, no, you can't settle down
you love a sky that's about to come down
I'm the porch light burning, the steady ground
but you only love what spins around

Wall cloud rising like a debt unpaid
you run toward the dark that I'm afraid
Green in the air and a freight-train sound
that's the only home where your heart is found

One day the storm's gonna keep you, love
no calm to crawl back into from above
but till that hail comes down like a closing hand
I'll keep your dinner warm in the promised land

You can't settle down, no, you can't settle down
you love a sky that's about to come down
I'm the porch light burning, the steady ground
but you only love what spins around

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning, the dashboard glows green
mile marker lonely, the smoothest I've been
I dial the kitchen where you leave the light
soft as the rain on the windshield tonight
The diesel's purring a slow samba beat
white lines drifting under my feet

Pick up, querida, it's only me
half the country between you and me
Hum me to sleep through the little black phone
so I don't drive these hours alone

The radio whispers a Rio guitar
rain on the truck stop, a far yellow star
You yawn that you love me, you murmur my name
and the long road softens, the night feels tame

Don't hang up yet, let it breathe on the line
your quiet beside me's the only sign
that somewhere there's morning and coffee and you
waiting past Tulsa where the sky turns blue

Pick up, querida, it's only me
half the country between you and me
Hum me to sleep through the little black phone
so I don't drive these hours alone

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I been holdin' this number like a stone in my chest
ten years of pride and a long-distance test
The dial tone hums like a hornet in June
and my thumb's too heavy for the afternoon
Last word I gave her was sharp as a blade
now I'm prayin' the line ain't already frayed

Mama, it's me, it's your boy come home
through the wire, through the years, through the long alone
I know I left you in the cold so long
pick up the phone, Mama, tell me where I went wrong

She answers slow like the years made her small
same kitchen clock tickin' down the hall
My throat closes up on the word I owe
ten years of sorry I don't know how to show

The silence between us is a river wide
but her voice still carries me to the other side
"Baby," she says, "I never moved the chair"
and the whole house breaks like an answered prayer

Mama, it's me, it's your boy come home
through the wire, through the years, through the long alone
I know I left you in the cold so long
pick up the phone, Mama, tell me where I went wrong

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Ooh, the sky is dancing green tonight
Whoa-oh, beneath those northern lights
I hold you close, the cold won't bite
Underneath the colors burning bright

Northern lights, shoo-bop, shoo-bop
Shining down on you and me
Northern lights, shoo-bop, shoo-bop
Love as endless as I see

The ribbons fold across the dark
Every wish becomes a spark
Your hand in mine, a perfect start
The heavens painting on my heart

Northern lights, shoo-bop, shoo-bop
Shining down on you and me
Northern lights, shoo-bop, shoo-bop
Love as endless as I see

Whoa-oh-oh, the colors call my name
Whoa-oh-oh, and nothing's quite the same
Darling hold me, don't let go
Let the midnight heavens glow

Northern lights, shoo-bop, shoo-bop
Shining down eternally
Northern lights, shoo-bop, shoo-bop
Forever you and me

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Every night I light the flame
And whisper out your endless name
The waves they carry what I write
My paper folded into white

Oh sea, oh sea, I write to you
In ink as dark as midnight blue
You never answer, still I stay
A keeper drifting in the gray

The salt has stained my trembling hand
I send my words across the sand
The tide will read them one by one
Beneath the slow and sinking sun

Oh sea, oh sea, I write to you
In ink as dark as midnight blue
You never answer, still I stay
A keeper drifting in the gray

Fold the letter, let it go
Into the deep where currents flow
Maybe one day you'll reply
In the breaking of the sky

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The kettle hums, the words have fled
The things we shouted left unsaid
The fire is low, the room is still
And silence sits upon the sill

Oh the quiet, oh the quiet
After all the storm has passed
In the quiet, in the quiet
May our gentle love hold fast

Your shadow waits across the floor
I hear you breathing by the door
No more the anger, only ache
The tender peace we have to make

Oh the quiet, oh the quiet
After all the storm has passed
In the quiet, in the quiet
May our gentle love hold fast

Reach for me across the night
Let the morning set it right
Forgiveness whispered, soft and true
In the hush I turn to you

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Scrubs on, double shift, it's a holiday (yeah)
Everyone home but I'm still on the way (on the way)
Monitors beepin', I'm runnin' the floor
Family textin', I can't answer no more

Workin' the double, no time to rest (no rest)
Savin' the lives, I'm givin' my best (my best)
Holiday lights but I'm under the glow
Of the fluorescent, here we go, here we go

IV drip, I'm checkin' the charts (charts)
Heavy on my feet but I'm carryin' hearts
Midnight strikes and I'm still on the clock
Everyone else got the day off the block

Workin' the double, no time to rest (no rest)
Savin' the lives, I'm givin' my best (my best)
Holiday lights but I'm under the glow
Of the fluorescent, here we go, here we go

They don't see the grind, nah (nah)
They don't feel the time, nah (nah)
But I show up every day
In the gown, in the fray

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
We never could agree on much
Now palm to palm and touch to touch
The fiddle calls, we can't refuse
Two bitter foes with nothing to lose

Dance, oy, dance, around we go
The clarinet won't let us slow
Enemies spinning cheek to cheek
Too proud to laugh, too proud to speak

The circle pulls us, hand in hand
A reluctant, swaying band
Your eyes still sharp, your grip still tight
But feet forgive us through the night

Dance, oy, dance, around we go
The clarinet won't let us slow
Enemies spinning cheek to cheek
Too proud to laugh, too proud to speak

Faster now, the room is bright
Maybe rivals turn to light
Who knew the music held the key
To set two stubborn spirits free

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
I got one plate sittin' on the table tonight
Used to be two by the kitchen light
The recipe calls for a family of four
But it's only me behind this door

Cookin' for one, Lord, cookin' for one
Since my baby's gone the suppers ain't fun
I burn the toast and I salt the tears
Learnin' to live through these lonesome years

Her apron's hangin' where she left it hung
I hum the songs that she once sung
The stove is cold and the pot's too wide
There's an empty chair right by my side

Cookin' for one, Lord, cookin' for one
Since my baby's gone the suppers ain't fun
I burn the toast and I salt the tears
Learnin' to live through these lonesome years

Maybe tomorrow I'll get it right
A little less lonely by candlelight
She taught me well, I'll carry on
Even cookin' for one

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
We gathered round the table late
Dealin' out a hand of fate
The stakes got high, the night got long
Whoa-oh, somethin' went all wrong

Too far, too far, shoo-doo-wop
We couldn't make the betting stop
Too far, too far, shoo-doo-wop
Lost it all on one last drop

A royal flush, a wild bluff
Nobody knew when we'd had enough
The clock struck three, the chips were gone
Whoa-oh, we kept on playin' on

Too far, too far, shoo-doo-wop
We couldn't make the betting stop
Too far, too far, shoo-doo-wop
Lost it all on one last drop

Whoa-oh-oh, I'd give it back
Whoa-oh-oh, the whole darn stack
Fold my cards and walk away
Learned my lesson here today

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Pick it up, pick it up, here we go
Standing at the edge of the show
Lights were bright and the crowd was loud
I was one step out of the crowd

The night I almost made it, whoa
So close I could taste it, whoa
Up on my toes, the horns all blow
The night I almost made it, go go go

One more chance and I'm on the stage
Reaching out to turn the page
Then the curtain fell too fast
Good times never seem to last

The night I almost made it, whoa
So close I could taste it, whoa
Up on my toes, the horns all blow
The night I almost made it, go go go

But I'll be back, I won't stay down
Gonna take this whole dang town
Pick it up, the beat won't quit
Next time I'm gonna make it

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The record's spinning soft and low
Thirty years and one more go
Your hand in mine, we sway so slow
One final dance before we go

One last dance, baby, hold me tight
Under the dim and golden light
We've come so far, we've come so true
One last dance, just me and you

The wrinkles tell the tales we made
Every promise that we paid
Your eyes still shine the way they did
When we were young and love was lit

One last dance, baby, hold me tight
Under the dim and golden light
We've come so far, we've come so true
One last dance, just me and you

If the music has to end
Let me hold you, my best friend
Every step and every turn
Was a love that still will burn

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Pick it up now, rain comes down
Two strangers waiting in the town
One umbrella, one small space
A shy and sudden meeting place

Under the umbrella, you and me
Dodging every raindrop, can't you see
Never met before but here we are
Sharing one small shelter, near and far

Your shoulder bumps against my own
Neither one of us alone
The puddles jump, the horns all play
We laugh the gray and rain away

Under the umbrella, you and me
Dodging every raindrop, can't you see
Never met before but here we are
Sharing one small shelter, near and far

When the storm has come and gone
Maybe we will carry on
Two strangers turning into more
Than the rain we're standing for

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every morning I unlock the hall
She's been hanging there upon the wall
Painted eyes that follow where I go
The only love a guard will ever know

Oh, sweet painting on the wall
You never speak, you never call
But I'll protect you all night long
My silent muse, my quiet song

The tourists come and then they leave
But me and you, we still believe
In the lamplight soft and low
I watch you, lady, in your glow

Oh, sweet painting on the wall
You never speak, you never call
But I'll protect you all night long
My silent muse, my quiet song

If they ever take you down
To some other distant town
I would follow, I would stay
Guarding you, come what may

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew its final morn
The iron gates stand cold and worn
The hands that built this town for years
Now walk away through silent tears

The factory's closing down, my love
No more smoke in the sky above
The town that worked and bled and grew
Must find a way to start anew

My father gave it all his days
My own two hands learned all its ways
Now the machines have stopped their song
And everything we knew is gone

The factory's closing down, my love
No more smoke in the sky above
The town that worked and bled and grew
Must find a way to start anew

But we are more than steel and stone
This ground will never leave us alone
We'll raise our children, raise our song
And carry on, and carry on

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Pick it up, the years roll by
Lines now gathered round his eye
Hands that lifted me up high
Move a little slower, my oh my

Watching my father grow old, oh no
The strongest man I'll ever know
Time keeps marching, on it goes
Watching my father grow old

He used to run and now he rests
He gave this family all his best
I hold his arm across the street
The rhythm slower in his feet

Watching my father grow old, oh no
The strongest man I'll ever know
Time keeps marching, on it goes
Watching my father grow old

So I'll be there to hold him now
Return the love somehow, somehow
Every step that he once gave
I'll walk beside him, strong and brave

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle and thread in my hand
Dressing every woman and man
From the corner to the avenue
I stitch a dream in every hue

I'm the tailor, oh yeah
Dressing up the whole block, sha-la
From your collar to your seam
I sew the people their finest dream

The preacher's suit, the bride's white lace
A perfect fit for every grace
They come to me when times are tight
And leave my door all dressed in light

I'm the tailor, oh yeah
Dressing up the whole block, sha-la
From your collar to your seam
I sew the people their finest dream

Every stitch a little love
Measured careful, fits like a glove
The whole block walks a little proud
In the threads I sewed for the crowd

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
The neon's buzzin' on the door
Coffee's hot at half past four
The lonely come, the lonely go
Under that old diner glow

Open all night, Lord, open all night
A little warmth and a little light
For the broken hearts and the weary souls
This old diner takes us all

The waitress knows my name by now
She pours the cup and asks me how
The truckers talk, the jukebox plays
We pass away the lonesome days

Open all night, Lord, open all night
A little warmth and a little light
For the broken hearts and the weary souls
This old diner takes us all

When the whole world's gone to sleep
This is where the night owls keep
A stool, a song, a friendly face
This all-night, sacred place

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The band is packing up their brass
but leave the needle on, my dear
Forty winters in this glass
and still your hand is warm and near

We danced before the children came
we danced when the rent was high
Now the doctor knows your name
and we sway to say goodbye

One more turn around the room
one more spin beneath the light
Let the bass go slow and blue
let me hold you through the night

Your shoes don't fit the way they did
my knees complain in minor key
but nobody ever taught us how to quit
so we move like used to be

If the song should end at dawn
if the floor should turn to stone
I have loved you all along
I have never danced alone

One more turn around the room
one more spin beneath the light
Let the bass go slow and blue
let me hold you through the night

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the foundation on a heartbeat
Thirty stories tall on a chest that breathes
Grandpa swore he heard it under Main Street
a rumble in the roots of the old oak trees

We pave our hopes on shoulders made of mountain
We drink our coffee where the giant dreams
Nobody talks about the underground fountain
or the way the river runs against the seams

Oh, sleep on, sleep on, you stone-boned king
let our porch lights flicker, let the church bells ring
We built our whole town on the bones of a thing
that could wake up tomorrow and take everything

The mayor cut a ribbon on a brand new highway
right across the place where the heartbeat slows
My daughter asks me, Daddy, is it our way
to live on something nobody really knows

Maybe faith is just a city on a sigh
Maybe love is what you build and hope it holds
We sing a little softer when the dust goes by
and we pray the giant likes the stories we've told

Oh, sleep on, sleep on, you stone-boned king
let our porch lights flicker, let the church bells ring
We built our whole town on the bones of a thing
that could wake up tomorrow and take everything

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The harbour wall remembers every name
the rain has carved into the grey
My mother's window holds a candle flame
but the ferry will not wait for clay

I walked the lane where the blackthorn grows
past the school and the broken pier
I kissed the dog and the door that knows
the weight of every leaving year

Fare thee well, little town by the sea
you were never quite a home to me
but I'll carry your stones in the back of my chest
as I sail toward the west, as I sail toward the west

The fiddle plays in the pub tonight
for a man they'll forget by spring
I'll send no letter, I'll write no line
just the wind and the gulls that sing

There's a part of me buried in that churchyard ground
there's a part that was always at sea
and the tide that takes me will not turn around
for a girl who was longing to leave

Fare thee well, little town by the sea
you were never quite a home to me
but I'll carry your stones in the back of my chest
as I sail toward the west, as I sail toward the west

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
They carried you slow down Rampart Street
black umbrellas and a snare drum beat
Mama was crying in her Sunday best
then the trumpet said it's time to dance, not rest

So we turned the grief into a golden sound
picked our feet up off the holy ground

Second line, second line, wave your handkerchief high
we don't say a cold goodbye
We send you home with a saxophone
dancing down the avenue, you're never gone

You loved the city and the city loved back
hot beignet sugar on a midnight track
Now the tubas swell and the cymbals crash
turning all our sorrow into brass

So we turned the grief into a golden sound
picked our feet up off the holy ground

Second line, second line, wave your handkerchief high
we don't say a cold goodbye
We send you home with a saxophone
dancing down the avenue, you're never gone

When it's my turn underneath that summer sky
play it loud enough to make the angels cry
Don't you wear no frown, just paint the whole town
dancing me down, dancing me down

Second line, second line, wave your handkerchief high
we don't say a cold goodbye
We send you home with a saxophone
dancing down the avenue, you're never gone

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I knew the hymns before I knew my name
pew number nine, the Sunday morning game
The pastor's handshake, the offering plate
I smiled along until I learned to wait

I'm slipping out the side door, oh
no big announcement, no big show
Keep the casserole, keep the choir tune
I'll be gone before the afternoon
pick it up, pick it up, I'm walking away
found my own kind of grace today

They asked me Tuesday why I missed the prayer
I said I'm fine and left it hanging there
No thunder cracked, no lightning struck me down
just the upbeat skank of leaving town

I'm slipping out the side door, oh
no big announcement, no big show
Keep the casserole, keep the choir tune
I'll be gone before the afternoon
pick it up, pick it up, I'm walking away
found my own kind of grace today

I don't hate the steeple, I don't curse the bell
some of those people loved me well
but the suit don't fit and the seat's too small
so I'm dancing out into it all

I'm slipping out the side door, oh
no big announcement, no big show
Keep the casserole, keep the choir tune
I'll be gone before the afternoon

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every morning I unlock the gallery doors
and she is waiting on the wall
A woman painted three hundred years before
with eyes that hold me, hold me, hold me in their fall

The tourists pass like water through the room
they never see the way she breathes
But in the empty hush of afternoon
I swear her painted shoulder gently heaves

Ay, mi corazón, behind the velvet rope
I guard a love I'll never hold
Clap your hands for a man and his impossible hope
for a heart that's framed in gold, framed in gold

The restorers came to clean her face one spring
I could not breathe until they left
What is a guardian but a foolish king
of a kingdom that is beautiful and bereft

When they dim the lights and lock the iron gate
I whisper buenas noches to the frame
She cannot answer, that is my estate
to love a silence that will never speak my name

Ay, mi corazón, behind the velvet rope
I guard a love I'll never hold
Clap your hands for a man and his impossible hope
for a heart that's framed in gold, framed in gold

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Old Mr. Soto on the corner of Vine
has a needle that knows everybody's spine
The banker, the busker, the bride in white
they all come knocking when the seams aren't right

He measures the city shoulder to shoulder
stitches the dreams as the nights grow colder
A little chalk line, a little blue thread
and the whole block walks with their chin up, head
held high in a suit that finally fits
that's the magic of the man with the scissors and wit

The widow's black dress, the boy's first tux
he presses the future into the cuffs
He hums an old standard through a mouthful of pins
that's where the story of the avenue begins

He never charges what the work is worth
he says good cloth is the kindest thing on earth
So when the trumpet sighs at the end of the day
the whole street's dressed in the same warm gray

Someday they'll fold his shutters down for good
and wonder who'll mend the neighborhood
but every lapel that he ever creased
still carries a little of his quiet peace

He measures the city shoulder to shoulder
stitches the dreams as the nights grow colder
A little chalk line, a little blue thread
and the whole block walks with their chin up, head

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Third gear down on Delancey Lane
the streetlight buzzing the same old refrain
There's a light in the window that used to be mine
somebody's living inside the design

Driving past, driving past, gonna keep on rolling
Driving past, driving past, but the wheel keeps slowing
That balcony, that broken screen
the best and the worst that I've ever been
pick it up now, foot on the gas
I'm only ever driving past

New curtains hanging where we used to fight
new bikes chained up in the hallway light
I bet they don't know the floorboard creaks
or how the radiator hissed for weeks

Driving past, driving past, gonna keep on rolling
Driving past, driving past, but the wheel keeps slowing
That balcony, that broken screen
the best and the worst that I've ever been
pick it up now, foot on the gas
I'm only ever driving past

I could circle the block just one more time
relive a love that's no longer mine
but the horns kick in and the green light glows
so I let it go, let it go, let it go

Driving past, driving past, gonna keep on rolling
Driving past, driving past, but the wheel keeps slowing

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Neon offers on a velvet phone
they want my voice but they want it cloned
Sign on the line, baby, smile for the brand
put your truth in a logo, shake the hand

But I won't, I won't, I won't sell the words
let the bassline carry what the silence heard
I won't, I won't, I won't trade the soul
for a number on a screen, for a hit, for a role

Keep the lights, keep the lie, keep the platinum crown
I'll be writing in the dark when the deal falls down
four on the floor and a pen in my hand
dancing on the line that I'll never brand

They said poetry doesn't pay the rent
but my every syllable is heaven-sent
I'd rather burn in an honest fire
than glow in the glass of a liar's choir

But I won't, I won't, I won't sell the words
let the bassline carry what the silence heard
I won't, I won't, I won't trade the soul
for a number on a screen, for a hit, for a role

Let the kick drum preach what the boardroom fears
that a free voice echoes for a thousand years
Raise your hands if you'd rather be true
the floor is a page and the page is you

Keep the lights, keep the lie, keep the platinum crown
I'll be writing in the dark when the deal falls down

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Cincuenta años bailando, mi amor
from the plaza where I asked for your hand
The trumpets remember the color of your dress
and the way that I trembled to stand

Now your hair is a river of plata and snow
and my boots are too tired to spin
but the violins call like they called long ago
so once more, let the dancing begin

Un último baile, corazón
before the candles are gone
Hold me close while the guitarrón sings
our whole life in the sound of its strings
ay, ay, ay, one more turn in the light
mi vida, mi todo, tonight

We buried our fathers, we raised up our sons
we survived every drought and the rain
and the same fierce heart that I gave you at dawn
still beats for you here in the pain

If God should call one of us home before day
let it find us still cheek against cheek
for a love this loud never fades all the way
it just learns a softer way to speak

Un último baile, corazón
before the candles are gone
Hold me close while the guitarrón sings
our whole life in the sound of its strings
ay, ay, ay, one more turn in the light
mi vida, mi todo, tonight

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The pool is closing, the light goes long
shadows stretching across the lawn
We lie on the hood till the engine's cold
and pretend that we'll never grow old

Hold the day, hold the day
let it melt slow away
One more hour of golden air
before the morning takes us there

Boxes packed in the upstairs hall
posters peeled from the bedroom wall
The cicadas hum a fading tone
for a room that won't be mine alone

We don't make promises, we just breathe
watching the last of the heat-light leave
Tomorrow is a highway, a stranger's door
tonight is the porch and nothing more

Somewhere a screen door softly swings
somewhere the future quietly sings
and all of the summers we ever knew
dissolve in a single drop of blue

Hold the day, hold the day
let it melt slow away
One more hour of golden air
before the morning takes us there

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Mama packed a Bible and a photograph
sold the family land for half
Thirty in a boat that was built for ten
salt on my lips, I won't see home again
Stars overhead like a broken map
phone in a baggie, no signal, no app
Prayed to the water, prayed to the moon
prayed that the sunrise was coming soon

Across the sea, across the sea
left everything I knew for a chance to be free
New name, new town, new ground for my feet
but I carry the old one deep in the beat
Across the sea, across the sea
whatever I find, it's gotta find me

Got a cousin in a city I can't pronounce
said the rent is a beast and it pounce for pounce
but I'll wash every dish, I'll sweep every floor
turn a no into a yes, turn a key in a door
They look at my papers, they look at my skin
but they don't see the ocean I'm holding in

Across the sea, across the sea
left everything I knew for a chance to be free
New name, new town, new ground for my feet
but I carry the old one deep in the beat

One day I'll send for you, mama, I swear
gonna build us a roof out of nothing but prayer
The sea took plenty but it gave me a road
and I'm walking it now with the weight of the load

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Washed in static the water tower glows
a rusted halo no one knows
I traced my name in the gravel lot
then the wind erased the little I've got

Fade me out of this place
blur the streetlights, blur your face
I'm a ghost on the interstate
dissolving slow, too far, too late

The diner hums in a wall of sound
last cup of coffee I'll drink in this town
Everything sweet and everything gray
drowned in the reverb of going away

My mother waves from a wash of light
a shape getting smaller in the rearview night
I press the gas till the feeling's gone
let the noise carry, carry me on

Maybe I'll miss it, maybe I won't
maybe the difference just floats and floats
into a hum that I can't unhear
the sound of a place disappearing here

Fade me out of this place
blur the streetlights, blur your face
I'm a ghost on the interstate
dissolving slow, too far, too late

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole life on a clean white board
the wedding, the title, the company car
A future arranged like a velvet cord
but I'm chasing the hum of a distant star

I'm driving out past the neon gate
tonight I rewrite the date
No more blueprint, no more crown
I'm burning the contract down
running on chrome and a midnight sky
the life they planned waves goodbye

The ring in the box and the name on the deed
a perfect machine I was built to feed
but something electric is calling my blood
a voice in the synth says go, says flood

I'm driving out past the neon gate
tonight I rewrite the date
No more blueprint, no more crown
I'm burning the contract down
running on chrome and a midnight sky
the life they planned waves goodbye

Let the arpeggios carry me far
past every door that they left ajar
I'd rather be lost on an open road
than safe in a future I never chose

I'm driving out past the neon gate
tonight I rewrite the date
No more blueprint, no more crown
I'm burning the contract down

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The candle's low and the wine is gone
and my thumb keeps drifting where it shouldn't roam
There's a name in the glow of this little screen
a door I swore I had locked at home

Oh, don't press send, don't press send
let the message die in the dark, my friend
There's a vow on my hand and a ghost in my chest
and a word that would ruin all the rest
oh, don't press send, don't press send

We danced one summer by the river Lee
before the rings and the rooms and the rain
and the fiddle still plays that melody
every time I hear your name again

It's only a hello, it's only a line
that's the lie I tell the midnight air
but a hello from a heart like yours and mine
is a road that leads somewhere

So I'll close the lid and I'll blow out the flame
and I'll let the longing turn to stone
for some loves are kinder left unnamed
and some doors are best left alone

Oh, don't press send, don't press send
let the message die in the dark, my friend
There's a vow on my hand and a ghost in my chest
and a word that would ruin all the rest
oh, don't press send, don't press send

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Packed my whole life in the backseat of a Chevy
Momma's porch light getting smaller in the glass
Every streetlight that I'm passing knows my middle name
But a town this small can't hold a heart this fast
I've been counting all the reasons I should stay
But they all sound like excuses now to me

So I'm gone, I'm gone
Leaving every wrong turn that I memorized
I'm gone, I'm gone
With the morning in my mirror and the future in my eyes
Don't you wait up, don't you call
I was always gonna outgrow these four walls

There's a diner where I broke my mother's curfew
There's a church where I first learned how to lie
I could draw this place blindfolded in my sleep now
That's exactly why I gotta say goodbye
Love you anyway, I always will
But a heart that's standing still is standing still

So I'm gone, I'm gone
Leaving every wrong turn that I memorized
I'm gone, I'm gone
With the morning in my mirror and the future in my eyes
Don't you wait up, don't you call
I was always gonna outgrow these four walls

Maybe someday I'll come back and wave
Maybe someday this'll feel like home again
But today the road is open and the tank is full
And I finally got the nerve to let it end

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon buzzing like a bumblebee
Two in the morning, pour another for me
Greasy spoon got a heartbeat too
Bacon on the griddle doing the boogaloo
Waitress named Ruby calling everybody hon
The coffee never sleeps and neither does the sun

We stay open all night, all night
Keep the kitchen sizzling under the light
We stay open all night, all night
If your soul needs feeding then you came to the right
Diner on the corner where the lonely come to dance
Give the midnight a reason, give the lonely a chance

Trucker and a poet and a girl who can't sleep
Sharing the same booth and the same cheap
Slice of pie that tastes like a second chance
Jukebox spinning every reason to dance
Clock on the wall don't mean a thing
When the bassline's cooking you can feel it ring

We stay open all night, all night
Keep the kitchen sizzling under the light
We stay open all night, all night
If your soul needs feeding then you came to the right
Diner on the corner where the lonely come to dance
Give the midnight a reason, give the lonely a chance

Clap your hands by the sugar and the cream
This is where the city comes to dream
Nobody's a stranger past the closing time
When the closing time never comes, it's fine

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We were tethered in the silence of the black
Two little heartbeats and no way back
You floated left and I floated right
Now you're just a blinking on the edge of night
Groove keeps moving but the cable came undone
Gravity forgot us, baby, one by one

Drifting, drifting, slow and far
Two astronauts and a dying star
Drifting, drifting, can't hold tight
Letting go of you in the funky weightless night
I'll wave at you across the empty miles
We drift apart in slow motion smiles

Radio crackling with the last of your voice
Low on the oxygen, low on the choice
We danced in the cabin when the mission was young
Now the only music is the song unsung
I reach for your hand but the distance is loud
You're a fading rhythm in a silver cloud

Drifting, drifting, slow and far
Two astronauts and a dying star
Drifting, drifting, can't hold tight
Letting go of you in the funky weightless night
I'll wave at you across the empty miles
We drift apart in slow motion smiles

One more groove before the signal's gone
One more reason to keep floating on
The universe is funky, cold and wide
And I'll carry your bassline till I reach the other side

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Gun the tape across the cardboard seam
Rip it loud, it screams like a dying dream
Empty walls where the photos used to hang
Nail holes staring back, let 'em all hang
This is it, the last one, seal it down
Drag my whole life screaming out of town

TAPE IT UP, TAPE IT DOWN
Last box standing in an empty town
TAPE IT UP, NEVER COMING BACK
Everything I was is in the back of a packed
Truck and I'm gone, I'm gone, I'm gone
No forwarding address, no holding on

Floorboards creak where the couch used to sit
Keys on the counter, I'm done with it
No more echoes, no more name on the lease
Burn the threshold, let me find some peace
Scream into the hollow of the rooms gone bare
Nobody's listening, nobody's there

TAPE IT UP, TAPE IT DOWN
Last box standing in an empty town
TAPE IT UP, NEVER COMING BACK
Everything I was is in the back of a packed
Truck and I'm gone, I'm gone, I'm gone
No forwarding address, no holding on

One last look, then I slam the door
What I leave behind ain't mine no more
LET IT GO, LET IT GO
The last box is taped and the truck says GO

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Cheap guitar and a borrowed amp
Neon flickering, the room was damp
Seven people and a busted mic
Nineteen years old in the violet light
My hands were shaking on the metal strings
But something in the static made my whole heart sing

First open mic in the magenta glow
Kid with a dream that he didn't know
First open mic, the synthesizers hum
Looking back at everything I would become
The night was electric, the night was young
I can still taste the first song that I sung

A stranger clapped and I felt alive
Midnight engine in a chrome-plated drive
The city was a circuit, I was the spark
A scared little voice cutting through the dark
I didn't know the road that was up ahead
Just the neon promises inside my head

First open mic in the magenta glow
Kid with a dream that he didn't know
First open mic, the synthesizers hum
Looking back at everything I would become
The night was electric, the night was young
I can still taste the first song that I sung

If I could drive back through that violet door
I'd tell that kid he's worth so much more
Keep your hands shaking, keep your heart loud
One day you'll thank that nervous little crowd

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Half an onion in a bowl too big
The recipe still says it serves for two
Steam on the window writes her name in fog
I wipe it slow, I don't know what to do
The kettle sings a song that we both knew

Cooking for one in the soft blue light
Learning how to season through the quiet of the night
Cooking for one, I set a single plate
And I leave the kitchen radio on for the company of the late
Oh, the salt, the steam, the haze
I'm learning how to love the empty days

She always told me less is more
Now there's so much less than there was before
A simmer and a hush and a folding chair
I talk to the saucepan like she's still there
The garlic and the rosemary fill the air

Cooking for one in the soft blue light
Learning how to season through the quiet of the night
Cooking for one, I set a single plate
And I leave the kitchen radio on for the company of the late
Oh, the salt, the steam, the haze
I'm learning how to love the empty days

Tonight I made it just the way she liked
And I ate it slow beneath the dimming light
Maybe one is not the loneliest sound
Maybe she's still here, just floating around

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
My thumb's been hovering over your name
For a hundred nights, ain't that a shame
Three years of pride built a wall so high
But tonight I'm tearing it down brick by brick, I'll try
The dial tone is louder than my heart
God, I don't even know where to start

So I'm calling you, momma, calling you
After all this silence, what's a son to do
Calling you, momma, pick up the line
Let me hear you say that everything's fine
I know I stayed away too long
But your voice was always home, your voice was the song

You answer soft like you'd been waiting too
No anger in it, just a quiet "is that you?"
And all the words I planned just fall apart
All that's left is the beating of a broken heart
I'm sorry, momma, for the years I lost
I counted every grudge but I never counted the cost

So I'm calling you, momma, calling you
After all this silence, what's a son to do
Calling you, momma, pick up the line
Let me hear you say that everything's fine
I know I stayed away too long
But your voice was always home, your voice was the song

We both start crying, we both start to laugh
Three years of distance cut clean in half
You say come home, baby, the door's still wide
And I'm already crying as I step outside

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man down by the silver shore
Feet in the foam like a hundred years before
He say, listen to the rhythm, child, the water keeps the beat
The ocean's got a groove from her head down to her feet
In and out, she breathing slow
That's the oldest funk the world will ever know

Teach the tide, teach the tide
Let the moon and the bassline coincide
Teach the tide, let it roll
The elder and the ocean got the same old soul
In, out, low and high
Grandpa's teaching rhythm to the tide

He clap his hands and the waves clap back
Salt in his beard and the groove on track
Say everything that moves gotta move in time
The pelican, the current, and this old rhyme
Don't fight the water, let it carry you
That's the lesson, baby, that the deep blue knew

Teach the tide, teach the tide
Let the moon and the bassline coincide
Teach the tide, let it roll
The elder and the ocean got the same old soul
In, out, low and high
Grandpa's teaching rhythm to the tide

When he's gone the waves will still come in
Keeping the time that he kept for them
And a child on the shore one distant day
Will hear the old man's funk in the spray

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at six and I clock out at noon
Clock back in beneath a tired moon
My shoes know the pavement, my back knows the strain
Two paychecks dancing down a leaky drain
The rent's a horn that won't stop blowing
And I'm always running, never knowing

Two jobs, baby, and I'm still behind
Chasing a dollar that I never find
The city's a saxophone, blue and slow
And I'm working, working, with no place to go
Falling behind, falling behind
With a tired heart and a heavy mind

The waitress smiles though her feet are sore
The night shift janitor wants a little more
We're all in this rhythm section, out of breath
Playing the same blue notes to death
The boss man counts his easy time
While we count the pennies, dime by dime

Two jobs, baby, and I'm still behind
Chasing a dollar that I never find
The city's a saxophone, blue and slow
And I'm working, working, with no place to go
Falling behind, falling behind
With a tired heart and a heavy mind

But I hum a little tune on the midnight train
And the melody softens the ache and the pain
Maybe tomorrow swings a different way
Till then I'll keep playing for a better day

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
The harbor fades into a line of gray
Everything I knew is drifting away
A suitcase and a folded photograph
The last of my old language, the last laugh
The water hums a hymn I can't translate
And I lean on the railing of the new and the late

Across the sea, across the sea
Carrying the ghost of who I used to be
Across the sea, the slow horizon glows
Toward a shore whose language nobody I know knows
The waves keep breathing, soft and wide
And I'm a small light on the other side

Salt on my lips and a stranger's prayer
The stars are the same but they hang elsewhere
I fold my mother's voice into my chest
And I let the open water do the rest
The engine hums beneath the endless blue
Carrying the old me toward the new

Across the sea, across the sea
Carrying the ghost of who I used to be
Across the sea, the slow horizon glows
Toward a shore whose language nobody I know knows
The waves keep breathing, soft and wide
And I'm a small light on the other side

When the dawn arrives I'll learn to say hello
In a tongue my children's children soon will know
The sea forgets but the heart will keep
The shape of home in the quiet deep

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Lagos rain coming down in sheets
I'm running for the bus on the flooded streets
You pull me under your little dome of blue
Two strangers laughing, what else can we do
Your shoulder's wet but you say it's fine
One umbrella, baby, your side and mine

Under one umbrella in the falling rain
Two strangers dancing down the lane
Under one umbrella, feel the rhythm grow
We don't even know each other but we move so slow
Rain dey fall, oh, rain dey fall
But under this umbrella we got room for all

You tell me your name and I tell you mine
The puddles are mirrors, the city shines
The drummer in the sky won't quit the beat
And our shoes are soaked but our hearts are sweet
We split a little roof against the storm
And somehow, baby, I am warm

Under one umbrella in the falling rain
Two strangers dancing down the lane
Under one umbrella, feel the rhythm grow
We don't even know each other but we move so slow
Rain dey fall, oh, rain dey fall
But under this umbrella we got room for all

When the rain stops we don't want it to end
A stranger this morning, now a friend
So let it keep falling, let the sky pour down
Keep us dancing slow across the town

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I got a radio crackling on the dash
Chasing the green where the thunderheads clash
A girl back home said baby, stay
But the sky turned violet and I drove away
I never could sleep when the air goes still
I'm only alive on a windswept hill

I'm a storm chaser, can't settle down
Following the funnel out of every town
I'm a storm chaser, married to the wind
I leave before the calm can settle in
Gimme the thunder, gimme the rain
A quiet little life would drive me insane

She framed a picture of a house and a yard
But a house with a yard always hit me hard
I love her like the lightning loves the ground
In a flash and a roar, then I'm not around
The wall cloud's turning and it's calling my name
And I know that I'll never be the same

I'm a storm chaser, can't settle down
Following the funnel out of every town
I'm a storm chaser, married to the wind
I leave before the calm can settle in
Gimme the thunder, gimme the rain
A quiet little life would drive me insane

Maybe one day the sky will run dry
And I'll have nothing left to chase but why
Till then I'm gone where the dark clouds spin
Chasing the only home I've ever been

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Cornbread rising in the early morning glow
Grandma humming hymns soft and low
Flour on her apron, sugar in the air
The whole house breathing like a Sunday prayer
I was just a child but I knew it then
Heaven had a kitchen and I'd been in

Oh, the smell of grandma's kitchen, hallelujah
Butter on the biscuits and the spirit moving through ya
Oh, the smell of grandma's kitchen, can't you see
That the love of the Lord was the meal she made for me
Glory, glory, in the pots and pans
God was working through her gentle hands

Greens on the stove and the gravy poured
Everybody welcome at her humble board
She'd say grace so long the food went cold
But every word was worth its weight in gold
Now she's gone on home to that brighter shore
But I smell her kitchen at the heaven's door

Oh, the smell of grandma's kitchen, hallelujah
Butter on the biscuits and the spirit moving through ya
Oh, the smell of grandma's kitchen, can't you see
That the love of the Lord was the meal she made for me
Glory, glory, in the pots and pans
God was working through her gentle hands

When I close my eyes I can still be fed
By the warmth of her stove and her daily bread
And I know one day at the welcome table
She'll be cooking up glory, willing and able

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Luces de la disco, sweat on my skin
One demo in my pocket, tonight I'm gonna win
The promoter said maybe, the crowd said yeah
I felt the whole world leaning in to hear
Manos arriba, the beat dropped low
I was one step away from the studio

La noche que casi lo logré
So close to the top, I could taste it, ay
The night I almost made it, almost shined
One phone call away from leaving it all behind
Dale, dale, the bass in my chest
One almost away from the very best

He took my number, said he'd call me soon
I danced till the morning underneath the moon
Three days of waiting by the silent phone
Learning how it feels to be so close, alone
But I don't regret how the night caught fire
It lit something in me that won't expire

La noche que casi lo logré
So close to the top, I could taste it, ay
The night I almost made it, almost shined
One phone call away from leaving it all behind
Dale, dale, the bass in my chest
One almost away from the very best

Maybe almost is just not yet
I'll be back on that floor, you can bet
Next time the call comes I'll be ready, mami
The night I almost made it made me

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Gates chained shut at the end of the line
Thirty years of steel and it all falls behind
The whistle blows once, won't blow again
Clock-out for the last of the factory men
Machines gone silent, the floor goes cold
A whole town's future getting bought and sold

Factory's closing down, down, down
The heartbeat stopping in a one-mill town
Factory's closing down, the lights go black
They took the jobs and they're never coming back
Run the breakbeat fast through the empty halls
Echo of the workers off the concrete walls

My father's hands were shaped by this place
Now there's nothing but the dust and the empty space
The bassline rolls like the trucks that left
A city full of pride and a city bereft
We built the world but the world moved on
Now the lunch bell rings for a crew that's gone

Factory's closing down, down, down
The heartbeat stopping in a one-mill town
Factory's closing down, the lights go black
They took the jobs and they're never coming back
Run the breakbeat fast through the empty halls
Echo of the workers off the concrete walls

But we are not the rust they left to rot
We are the fire, we are all we got
Turn the drum up loud in the dying light
The town ain't dead, the town's gonna fight

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I fold the hymnal closed and let the cover settle
Slide out the back pew while the offering plate is full
The stained glass throws its colors on the carpet
Like it always did, like nothing here is dull

Mama's voice still carries on the third row
Daddy's name is carved into a brick outside
I learned to count my pennies by the candle
Learned to swallow questions, learned to hide

So I'm leaving with my shoes still in my hand
No slammed door, no thunder, no demand
Just a girl who loved the singing more than the law
Walking out before the final amen call

I don't hate the steeple or the water in the basin
I just outgrew the answers they kept handing me to hold
There's a God somewhere I haven't met in that building
Maybe out here in the morning, in the cold

Don't tell them I cried in the parking lot
Don't tell them I still hum the songs at night
Some leaving isn't burning, it's just turning
Toward a quieter kind of light

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They poured the concrete over something old and breathing
Laid the streetcar tracks across his collarbone
The mayor cut a ribbon where the heartbeat shows at midnight
And called it progress, called it brick and stone

Oh, this city's sleeping on a giant's chest
Every tower trembles when he turns to rest
We built our whole lives on a someday-no
Slow blues humming where the deep things go

The river bends around a shoulder we don't talk about
The church bells ring a little flat down on the square
Old men in the diner say they felt him sigh in '49
Shook the coffee cups and rattled every chair

I keep my boots beside the bed and one eye on the ceiling
I married a woman who can sleep through anything
But I lie awake and listen to the foundation creaking
Waiting for the day the giant wants to stand

Let him sleep, Lord, let him sleep a little longer
We got rent to pay and gardens still to grow
But every city's built on something it can't hold down
And someday, baby, someday he will know

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The lamp makes a little island on the page
Coffee gone cold in a porcelain cup
The city's gone quiet, the buses have stopped
And it's only the night and me, awake

Two in the morning, soft as the rain
The words start to swim and they swim back again
Nobody's calling, nobody's near
Just the hum of the fridge and the year

I underline a sentence I won't remember
I draw a small bird in the white of the margin
The pencil keeps moving, the heater keeps sighing
And somewhere a clock lets the minutes go thin

My neighbor's window finally went dark an hour ago
The streetlight paints a yellow square across the floor
I tell myself one chapter, then I'll let the morning take me
Then I read it twice and start the chapter more

There's a sweetness in the loneliness of learning
A quiet little kingdom none can see
When the world's asleep and the lamp is still burning
The knowledge and the night belong to me

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raise my hand and the room keeps moving
My voice goes under like a stone in the stream
They finish my sentence with the wrong word
And nod at the echo of somebody's dream

Ay, but I carry a trumpet inside my chest
A whole brass band they will not let rest
Talk over me, talk over me all you please
The song in my throat brings the giants to their knees

Good morning, good idea, sat dying on my tongue
A man repeats it louder and they call the man a star
I smile like the saint above the doorway
And write down every place that they are wrong

My abuela never waited for permission
She sang across the market till the vendors turned to hear
I inherited her lungs and not her courage
But I'm learning, little by little, how to clear

So tomorrow when the table starts to drown me
I'll stand and let the mariachi rise
No more swallowing the verses that they owe me
I'll sing it loud enough to fill their eyes

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The phone glows blue at a quarter past midnight
Your name is a bell I should never have rung
My thumb hovers over a door I should bolt shut
But the message is already halfway sung

Ay, corazón, you traitor, you thief
Typing the words that will bring me to grief
Delete, delete, but I press send instead
And the trumpets play loud in my reckless head

There's a ring on my hand and a vow in the closet
There's a woman asleep with her back to the wall
And here in the dark I'm a fool with a candle
Writing your name on the edge of it all

Three dots are dancing, you're typing me back now
My whole chest is a plaza on fiesta night
God forgive me, the guitars are all laughing
At a man who knows wrong but can't let go of the light

Tomorrow I'll swear that I'll throw the thing in the river
Tomorrow I'll kneel and I'll promise to mend
But tonight there's a screen and a name and a fever
And a sin that I'm willing to send

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew its last at the end of the shift
The gates exhale a rust-colored sigh
Forty years of iron going quiet at once
And the men file out without meeting an eye

Dance with me once on the oil-stained floor
Where the great machines will turn no more
Hold me close while the lights go gray
We built this city, now it's taken away

María sweeps the locker room one final time
The lockers gape like mouths with nothing to say
The foreman pockets a single steel bolt
A souvenir of the thing they threw away

The smokestack stood like a saint over the rooftops
Now it's a finger pointing at an empty sky
We learned to love the rhythm of the hammers
And nobody told us the rhythm could die

So play the bandoneón slow and bitter
Let the bellows weep what the men cannot
We'll waltz through the dust of our own undoing
And leave our shadows where the work forgot

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I say your name into an empty kitchen
The morning light is doing all your chores
The kettle clicks, the dust turns in the window
And I'm forgiving you across the floor

I lay it down, I lay it down at last
The weight I carried longer than the past
There's no one here to take it from my hands
So I give it to the air, and the air understands

The things you broke have softened into stories
The sharp parts dulled like sea glass on the shore
I used to keep your wrong inside a locked room
But I don't need the key for that door anymore

Wherever you are, or wherever you aren't
I'm unclenching a fist I held for years
Not for you, not for you, but for the quiet
That finally has more room than my tears

The candle burns down past the things I can't tell you
The smoke goes up the way a prayer might go
I forgive you, I forgive you, I forgive you
And the room gets lighter, slow

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Open bar, bow tie loose, I don't know your name
DJ spinning something old, the lights are low and flame
You caught me by the cake table, pulled me to the floor
Never met you in my life but I'm not asking for more

Spin, spin, stranger in the strobe
Two lost guests at the edge of the globe
We don't know the bride, we don't know the groom
But we own every inch of this borrowed room

Gold rings on the table, somebody else's vow
We're just two extra plates and we're living in the now
Your heels in your left hand, my jacket on a chair
Last name a mystery, don't care, don't care

The slow songs come and go, we don't slow down
Confetti in your hair like a falling crown
Midnight, the cake's gone, the uncles are asleep
And you and me are dancing on a promise we won't keep

When the lights come up we'll vanish like the bass
No number, no goodbye, just a name I'll never trace
But for one borrowed night under somebody's chandelier
A stranger and a stranger made a whole world right here

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
The garden lights are strung between the trees
The band is soft, the wine is almost gone
You offered me your hand without a reason
And I took it, and the evening carried on

Dance with me, stranger, slow as the tide
I don't know your name and I don't need a guide
The bride throws her flowers, the night opens wide
And we sway like the sea on the warm garden side

Your jacket smells of cedar and of candle
My shoes are in my hand beneath the chair
We move like two leaves that the breeze has gathered
And nobody is watching, nobody to care

You tell me half a story, then you let it
Dissolve like sugar in the evening air
The stars come out above the folding tables
And I rest my cheek against your collar there

When the music stops we'll bow and we'll be strangers
Go home to lives that never overlap again
But the garden and the saxophone will keep it
This one slow dance, this gentle little when

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Forty winters I have climbed these iron stairs
Trimmed the wick and watched the black come down
I write to you each night by the light I keep for sailors
And I drop the pages where the gulls won't drown

Dear sea, dear cold and hungry bride
I've loved you longer than the men who died
Take my words, take my lamp, take my bones at the end
You're the only wife this rock will ever send

The town below forgot my name a decade back
The supply boat comes when the weather lets it through
But every wave that breaks against the foundation
Is a letter coming back to me from you

I signed my name in salt across the logbook
I dream you in the green beneath the foam
My heart is a buoy that the storms keep ringing
And this tower full of light is the closest thing to home

When they find me cold beside the burning glass
When the keeper's chair is empty and the door swings wide
Don't bury me in churchyard dirt and granite
Just open up the window, let me ride the tide

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slide shut and the dark comes rushing
The map on the wall starts to blur and bend
My heart's a drummer who forgot the tempo
And the tunnel doesn't seem to have an end

Breathe, just breathe, count the rivets on the ceiling
Breathe, just breathe, name the colors that you see
The whole car's swaying and nobody's noticing
That the floor is falling out from under me

A man reads his paper like the world's not ending
A kid kicks his sneakers, keeping perfect time
I grip the cold pole till my knuckles whiten
And I beg the next station to arrive

There's an ocean in my ears, there's a fist around my throat
The ad above the seats says I deserve the best
I count four stops, I count my own two hands
I tell my racing chest, please, please rest

And then the platform, and the doors are open
And the air comes in like mercy, like the sun
I step out shaking onto solid concrete
Still alive, still here, the wave is done

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The pool's already drained behind the Hendersons'
The ice cream truck went silent down the street
We lay across the hood till the metal cooled beneath us
And watched the heat lift slowly off the heat

This is the last one, the last one, the last
The whole gold summer collapsing into past
Tomorrow we're scattered like seeds on the wind
So stay on the rooftop, don't let it begin

Your cassette is warbling something we'll forget
The cul-de-sac is glowing like a coal
We carved our names in the wet cement years ago
And now the sidewalk's holding what we stole

The streetlights buzz awake like they're surprised
The moths are writing circles in the glare
We pretend the dawn is something we can argue with
And talk until there's nothing left to spare

Don't say the word, don't say goodbye, just let it
Dissolve into the reverb and the haze
I'll keep this one, the last warm night of nothing
Louder than all the colder, faster days

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The rooms ring hollow now the walls are bare
My footsteps echo where the couch had stood
I press the tape across the final carton
And I thank this house the way I know I should

One more box, Lord, one more box and I'm gone
Thank You for the table where we leaned and carried on
Thank You for the doorway where the babies learned to walk
I'm taping up the years and I can hear the old house talk

There's a pencil line that marked my children growing
A crayon sun some small hand left to stay
I run my palm along the kitchen counter
And I bless the bread we broke here, day by day

The keys feel heavy in my open hand now
The light comes naked through the curtainless glass
Every empty room is humming hallelujah
For the ordinary glory that came to pass

So I'll carry it forward, all of it, the laughter
The arguments forgiven, the long Sunday rest
A house is just a box that holds the loving
And I'm taping up the last and the best

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Clack-clack on the rail and my pulse hit double
Sweatin' through my collar in the underground rumble
The lights start strobin' and my knees go to jelly
Got a marching band stompin' right here in my belly

Whoa, the train keeps rollin' but my brain jumped the track
Gimme one good breath, gimme my body back
The bassline's my heartbeat goin' way too quick
Hold on, hold on, gonna ride through this kick

Groove's got a fever and the fever's got me
The poster says relax but I cannot, can't be
A stranger taps his foot to a song I can't hear
While the walls do the cha-cha and they close in near

Four stops, four stops, I can count to the door
Got my feet on the funk of the rattlin' floor
In through the nose, baby, out through the mouth
Gonna James-Brown my way to the platform south

Then the doors hit open and the cool air slide
Like a horn section bringin' me back to my stride
I step out shakin' but I'm steady, I'm whole
The groove never broke me, it just rattled my soul

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
I saw your coat on someone else this morning
Same shade of red, and my whole chest went still
Fifteen years and a couple cities later
And you're living on a ridge of my windowsill

You're the one, you're the one that got away
The letter I rewrote but never could say
I married kind, I built a quiet life
But your name still hums beneath the everyday strife

We were too young or too scared or too stubborn
We let a small fight grow into a wall
You caught the train and I let the platform empty
And I've replayed the not-running every fall

I hope he's good to you, I hope you're laughing
I'm not asking for the past to come back open
I just carry you down every memory street

Some loves are houses and some loves are weather
You were the storm that I never learned to name
And if I had one honest hour to spend you
I'd just say thank you, and I'd say I'm still the same

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Clock on the wall says I'm out of time
Coffee gone cold, ink running dry
Four walls closing in on me
Pages I read but cannot see

Two in the morning, I'm still awake!
Grinding it out for my own sake!
Nobody's coming, I'm on my own!
Light in the window, I burn alone!

Eyes are burning, my hands won't stop
Climb this mountain straight to the top
Every sacrifice that I make
Is a piece of me I won't take back

Two in the morning, I'm still awake!
Grinding it out for my own sake!
Nobody's coming, I'm on my own!
Light in the window, I burn alone!

Silence screaming in my head
Words on the page like the living dead
But I won't break, I won't cave in
This is the war that I have to win!

Two in the morning, I'm still awake!
Grinding it out for my own sake!
Nobody's coming, I'm on my own!
Light in the window, I burn alone!

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I watch you fade into the gray
A color I can't hold
You never knew my name to say
Yet still I feel the cold

And I drown in the sound of you
A wave I can't get through
You'll never feel the way I do
So I dissolve in blue

The distance hums beneath my skin
A frequency of want
I keep the silence you live in
A ghost I let me haunt

And I drown in the sound of you
A wave I can't get through
You'll never feel the way I do
So I dissolve in blue

Reverb of a heart that breaks so slow
Echoes where the feeling used to go
I fade, I fade, I fade away
Into the noise where I will stay

And I drown in the sound of you
A wave I can't get through
You'll never feel the way I do
So I dissolve in blue

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Last light on the avenue, golden and slow
Packing up boxes, soon I gotta go
Friends on the corner where we used to chill
Memories frozen, time standing still

One more sunset before I'm gone
Humming the beat of this summer song
Hold it close 'cause it won't last long
Last day of summer, then I move on

Bike tires hissing on the warm concrete
Echo of laughter down the empty street
Tomorrow's a stranger in a brand new town
Tonight I'ma let the soft beat wind down

One more sunset before I'm gone
Humming the beat of this summer song
Hold it close 'cause it won't last long
Last day of summer, then I move on

Vinyl crackle, streetlight glow
Everything changes, deep down I know
But for tonight I let it ride
The last warm wave of the summertide

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Under the oak where the pigeons fly
A fold-up board and a hungry eye
Five dollars says you cannot win
The clock is ticking, let the game begin

King of the park, lord of the square!
He'll take your money, he doesn't care!
Checkmate coming, you never saw it fall!
The pawn becomes the master of them all!

He's seen a thousand challengers fall
Queen takes bishop, he conquers all
A general of the sixty-four
Every move a bloody holy war

King of the park, lord of the square!
He'll take your money, he doesn't care!
Checkmate coming, you never saw it fall!
The pawn becomes the master of them all!

No crown, no throne, just a wooden board
His only kingdom, his only sword
But every soldier knows his name
The silent killer of the endgame!

King of the park, lord of the square!
He'll take your money, he doesn't care!
Checkmate coming, you never saw it fall!
The pawn becomes the master of them all!

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Gates locked up, the whistle won't blow
Thirty years of sweat, now there's nowhere to go
My daddy worked these floors 'til his hands turned gray
Now they're shipping all the jobs a world away

Lights out on the factory line
This was yours and this was mine
We built this town with our own two hands
Now we're ghosts in the promised land

Empty lunchpails, rusty machines
Dust on the dreams of the working teens
Mortgage due and the cupboard's bare
Nobody's coming, nobody's there

Lights out on the factory line
This was yours and this was mine
We built this town with our own two hands
Now we're ghosts in the promised land

But we ain't broken, we ain't done
We rise together, every daughter and son
They can take the steel but they can't take the soul
Of a working heart that's still made whole

Lights out on the factory line
This was yours and this was mine
We built this town with our own two hands
Now we're ghosts in the promised land

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Two plates on the shelf, I only need one now
Her recipe card in a faded hand
I measure the salt the way she showed me how
In a kitchen too quiet to understand

So I cook for one tonight
Under the kitchen light
The table set for the life I knew
And every bite tastes like you

The kettle still sings the song she loved
Steam on the window where her name once was
I'm learning the language of letting go
In the simmer and the silence, soft and slow

So I cook for one tonight
Under the kitchen light
The table set for the life I knew
And every bite tastes like you

And the crescendo rises, the strings climb high
A meal, a memory, a long goodbye
But I'll set the table, I'll light the flame
And whisper soft into the dark your name

So I cook for one tonight
Under the kitchen light
The table set for the life I knew
And every bite tastes like you

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Lines like a map of the years gone by
Holding my world when I used to cry
Flour and gold and the softest touch
Those hands, those hands, I miss so much

Grandmother's hands, lift me higher
Grandmother's hands, set my soul on fire
Every line tells a story true
Grandmother's hands, I dance for you

She braided my hair in the morning light
Working the garden 'til the fall of night
Never a word of the weight she bore
Just love, just love, forevermore

Grandmother's hands, lift me higher
Grandmother's hands, set my soul on fire
Every line tells a story true
Grandmother's hands, I dance for you

Feel the rhythm, feel the beat
Those same hands moved me to my feet
Now I raise mine to the sky
Her love still living, never dies

Grandmother's hands, lift me higher
Grandmother's hands, set my soul on fire
Every line tells a story true
Grandmother's hands, I dance for you

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Dialing the number I know by heart
Sweaty palms, where do I even start?
Three rings, four rings, then I hear her voice
And suddenly I'm a kid with no choice

Hey Mama, it's me on the line!
I'm sorry it took such a long, long time!
Pick up the phone, let the trumpets blow!
There's so much love that I never showed!

Years of pride built a wall so tall
But a mother's love can break through it all
She says my name like no time has passed
And the bitter years melt away at last

Hey Mama, it's me on the line!
I'm sorry it took such a long, long time!
Pick up the phone, let the trumpets blow!
There's so much love that I never showed!

Pick it up, pick it up, one more time!
Forgiveness ringing like a Sunday chime!
The horns are skanking, the past is gone
Mama I'm coming, I'm coming home!

Hey Mama, it's me on the line!
I'm sorry it took such a long, long time!
Pick up the phone, let the trumpets blow!
There's so much love that I never showed!

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The streetlights hum a tired tune
August melting into the moon
Suitcase open on my bed
All the words I never said

One last summer night for me
Before I sail across the sea
Soft piano, fading light
Goodbye summer, hello night

We danced barefoot on the boulevard
Letting go was always hard
A saxophone bends low and blue
For the kid I'm about to lose

One last summer night for me
Before I sail across the sea
Soft piano, fading light
Goodbye summer, hello night

The brushes whisper on the snare
A velvet smoke fills up the air
Tomorrow's just a turning page
Tonight I take the moonlit stage

One last summer night for me
Before I sail across the sea
Soft piano, fading light
Goodbye summer, hello night

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down on Main Street, I was running late
You stepped up beside me at the crossing gate
One little umbrella, two strangers underneath
A quiet kind of magic in the falling sheets

Under one umbrella, just you and me
Two hearts getting caught in the same sweet rain
Didn't know your name but somehow I could tell
We were two lost strangers finding home as well

We walked a couple blocks and we talked a little while
You had a way of laughing, you had a crooked smile
The rain it kept on falling, neither one would run
Two umbrellas needed, but we shared the one

Under one umbrella, just you and me
Two hearts getting caught in the same sweet rain
Didn't know your name but somehow I could tell
We were two lost strangers finding home as well

The storm moved on and the sky turned gold
I didn't want to let your hand go
Funny how a downpour and a stranger's grace
Can turn a rainy sidewalk to a holy place

Under one umbrella, just you and me
Two hearts getting caught in the same sweet rain
Didn't know your name but somehow I could tell
We were two lost strangers finding home as well

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Every night I light the flame for you
My salt-kissed lover, deep and blue
I write my words on the foam and tide
And send them out where the seagulls glide

Oh, my love letters to the sea
Will you ever write them back to me?
I'll keep the light burning evermore
For the love that crashes on my shore

The waves they answer in a language old
A whispered secret no one's ever told
You take my heart with the morning tide
And leave me here on the lonely side

Oh, my love letters to the sea
Will you ever write them back to me?
I'll keep the light burning evermore
For the love that crashes on my shore

Forty years I've stood upon this stone
The truest love a man has ever known
And when I'm gone the light will shine
For the endless ocean, forever mine

Oh, my love letters to the sea
Will you ever write them back to me?
I'll keep the light burning evermore
For the love that crashes on my shore

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Midnight in the gallery, just me and you
A woman in a frame with eyes so blue
Every night I walk these marble halls
But I only stop where your beauty calls

Baila, baila, painting on the wall
You're the only one I love at all
Dame tu mirada, give me one more night
You and me alone in the soft moonlight

They painted you a hundred years ago
But you smile at me like you really know
The velvet rope keeps us apart
But nobody can rope off my heart

Baila, baila, painting on the wall
You're the only one I love at all
Dame tu mirada, give me one more night
You and me alone in the soft moonlight

Dun-dun-dun, the rhythm in the dark
Every brushstroke leaves a spark
When the morning comes they'll never see
That a painted love came alive with me

Baila, baila, painting on the wall
You're the only one I love at all
Dame tu mirada, give me one more night
You and me alone in the soft moonlight

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Under the lights I sang my heart so true
One note away from a dream come true
The crowd was silent, the moment near
So close I could almost taste it, dear

Ay, the night I almost flew!
