The night the dream was almost true!
The trumpets cried, the guitars rang
For the brightest song I ever sang!

They called another name, not mine
And the spotlight crossed the cruel line
But I held my head up to the moon
And swore that I'll be back here soon

Ay, the night I almost flew!
The night the dream was almost true!
The trumpets cried, the guitars rang
For the brightest song I ever sang!

Gritos in the warm night air
A broken dream but I don't care
For the man who almost touched the sky
Will keep on singing till he flies!

Ay, the night I almost flew!
The night the dream was almost true!
The trumpets cried, the guitars rang
For the brightest song I ever sang!

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Forty years of mornings, forty years of rain
We danced through all the sunshine and we danced right through the pain
Now the record's spinning slow, the needle's almost done
One more turn around the floor before the setting sun

So dance with me one last time, my dear
Let me hold you while the music's here
Your hand in mine like the very first day
One last dance before you fade away

Your hair has turned to silver, mine has turned to gray
But you're still that pretty girl from a long-lost summer day
The candle's burning low now, the room is soft and dim
We sway here in the shadows like we're young again

So dance with me one last time, my dear
Let me hold you while the music's here
Your hand in mine like the very first day
One last dance before you fade away

Lord, I'd give my whole tomorrow
For one more yesterday
With the only love I've ever known
Before she slips away

So dance with me one last time, my dear
Let me hold you while the music's here
Your hand in mine like the very first day
One last dance before you fade away

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Three little words on a glowing screen
Tore apart everything we'd ever been
No goodbye, no final call
Just a notification and the end of it all

You ended us in a text message!
A lifetime gone in a single message!
I gave you everything I had!
And you typed it out so cold, so bad!

I read it over a hundred times
Looking for the love between the lines
But there was nothing, only gray
The cruelest words you'd ever say

You ended us in a text message!
A lifetime gone in a single message!
I gave you everything I had!
And you typed it out so cold, so bad!

Delete the thread, but it stays in my head
Every letter of the things you said
I won't beg, I won't break down
I'll burn this phone into the ground!

You ended us in a text message!
A lifetime gone in a single message!
I gave you everything I had!
And you typed it out so cold, so bad!

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We drove past the edge of the radio town
With the heater coughing and the windows down
You said wait, then the dark caught fire
Green as a wave that the sky calls higher

I lost your hand in the static glow
A hush so loud I forgot the cold
Reverb of color, no sound at all
Just the slow collapse of a curtain wall

Let the light wash over, let it drown the noise
We are small and shimmering, no name and no voice
Fade me into auroras, bleed me into the night
I was nothing till you showed me the northern lights

Snow held its breath on the frozen lake
Mirrored the heaven we couldn't fake
You whispered something the wind erased
And I kept it anyway, that ghost of a face

Let the light wash over, let it drown the noise
We are small and shimmering, no name and no voice
Fade me into auroras, bleed me into the night
I was nothing till you showed me the northern lights

If the sky can forgive itself in color
Maybe I can too, maybe we recover
Under the hum of a dying star
I finally saw you for who you are

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
You were sunlight spilled on the morning sand
A paper cup of coffee in your hand
We traded names like seashells by the shore
And I forgot the world I had before

The waves kept time like a soft guitar
We didn't ask how far the evenings are
Your laughter drifting on the salty breeze
A little summer god put me at ease

Oh, just for a season, you were mine
Sun on the water, cheap red wine
No promises heavier than the tide
A summer fling and the ocean wide

We knew September wore an honest face
It came and folded up our little place
You kissed my shoulder, packed your faded blue
And left me humming what I never knew

Oh, just for a season, you were mine
Sun on the water, cheap red wine
No promises heavier than the tide
A summer fling and the ocean wide

The sand still keeps the shape of where we lay
The tide will come and softly take it away
And that's alright, that's the bossa way
We loved in tune and let the rest decay

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Grandmother walks where the water meets the stone
Feet in the foam, she is never alone
She hums a number the moon understands
And the sea leans in to learn from her hands

She teaches the tide, she teaches the tide
Pull when I tell you, then pull back inside
Feel the bass beneath, let the rhythm decide
The elder is patient, she teaches the tide

Four to the floor like the heartbeat of the deep
Lessons the careless ocean tries to keep
In, out, breathe, the saltwater obeys
The oldest groove from the oldest of days

She teaches the tide, she teaches the tide
Pull when I tell you, then pull back inside
Feel the bass beneath, let the rhythm decide
The elder is patient, she teaches the tide

Raise your hands, the water rises too
Everything ancient learns something new
When she finally rests on the shoreline floor
The tide will remember and dance evermore

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Lamplight low in the back of Delaney's place
Four men sweating with the devil's own grace
I laid my deed on the splintered pine
Said one more hand and the farm is mine

The stranger smiled with a gold-toothed grin
Dealt from a deck that was paper-thin
The aces fell where the shadows lay
And something cold took my breath away

Oh I bet the house and I bet my name
The Lord won't save you in a crooked game
Blood on the felt where the lantern burned
I learned too late and there's no return

Clyde drew a pistol when the kings came down
Thunder and powder shook the sleeping town
The stranger vanished with my soul in tow
Left me a debt that I'll always owe

Oh I bet the house and I bet my name
The Lord won't save you in a crooked game
Blood on the felt where the lantern burned
I learned too late and there's no return

They buried Clyde in the cold March clay
I dig his grave a little deeper each day
And every night I deal one hand alone
To a chair that's empty, to a debt I own

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Well the streetlights hum on Cumberland row
(Sha-la-la, don't wake him now)
We built our houses where the giant lies low
(Sha-la-la, don't wake him now)
Grandpa told me when I was a kid
There's a heart that beats beneath the city grid

So we tiptoe, baby, tiptoe slow
On the chest of a giant down below
We dance so soft and we sing so sweet
To the lullaby beneath our feet

The church bells ring but they ring real light
(Sha-la-la, hold him in his dream)
And lovers whisper on a summer night
(Sha-la-la, hold him in his dream)
If the towers tremble, if the windows sway
That's just the giant rolling over halfway

So we tiptoe, baby, tiptoe slow
On the chest of a giant down below
We dance so soft and we sing so sweet
To the lullaby beneath our feet

One day he'll wake and the city will fall
But tonight, my darling, we have it all
So kiss me quiet under neon glow
And hush, my love, don't wake him, no

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
You were Lagos rain on a Friday night
Vanished like the morning, out of my sight
I still hear your name in the danfo crowd
Girl I should have told you, should have said it loud

You're the one that got away, oh oh
Dancing in my memory every day, oh oh
I let the good thing slip away, ay
The one that got away, away

Now they play our song and I lose my mind
Every girl I meet, you're the face behind
I was young and foolish, thought the world would wait
Now I'm at your wedding and I'm three years late

You're the one that got away, oh oh
Dancing in my memory every day, oh oh
I let the good thing slip away, ay
The one that got away, away

If I could rewind, baby, I would stay
Hold you through the morning, never run away
But the drum keeps beating and the night gets cold
And you're a sweet story that I should have told

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
There's a light on Route 9 that never goes dark
A coffee-stained altar past the edge of the park
Myrtle's been pouring since nineteen-fifty-three
She knows every sinner the night brings to me

The truckers and widows and the men on the run
Come in from the highway like moths to the sun
The jukebox plays low and the bacon goes cold
And everybody's haunted, everybody's old

Keep the door unlocked, keep the burner lit
For the lost and the wicked and the ones who quit
There's salvation in a booth and a bottomless cup
Where the diner stays open and the sun won't come up

A girl came in bleeding at a quarter to four
Myrtle just nodded and locked the back door
Some stories don't end and some debts don't get paid
But the eggs are still frying and the table is laid

Keep the door unlocked, keep the burner lit
For the lost and the wicked and the ones who quit
There's salvation in a booth and a bottomless cup
Where the diner stays open and the sun won't come up

They say Myrtle died in the spring of last year
But the light's always on and the coffee's still here
So pull off the highway, come rest your tired bones
In the only church left for the ones with no homes

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The door stopped shaking an hour ago
Now there's only the clock and the streetlight's glow
We sit in the wreckage of words we threw
Me on the cold floor, the dark and you

The silence is louder than anything said
It fills up the hallway, it fills up the bed
I hear you breathing across the divide
Two strangers who used to have nothing to hide

And the quiet comes crashing in
Louder than where we've been
Reach for your hand in the dark
Find the shape of who we are

Slowly the tension begins to unwind
You turn your head and our eyes realign
No words, just the weight of a held-out hand
A bridge built of nothing across no man's land

And the quiet comes crashing in
Louder than where we've been
Reach for your hand in the dark
Find the shape of who we are

Let it build, let it rise, let it break like a wave
All the things we couldn't say, all the love we couldn't save
Until the noise becomes a hum, becomes a light, becomes the dawn
And we are still here, and we carry on

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded her up in a midnight haze
A crate marked danger and a captain's daze
The old man warned us, don't you peek inside
But the devil's curious on a long, long ride

Swing low, sail high, we got a cursed little cargo
Doo-bee-doo, the compass spins, nobody knows where we go
Dance on the deck while the ocean moans
We're carrying trouble, we're carrying bones

The first mate opened it on the seventh day
Now his hair's gone white and his eyes won't stay
The gulls all left and the wind went cold
And the cargo whispers like it's centuries old

Swing low, sail high, we got a cursed little cargo
Doo-bee-doo, the compass spins, nobody knows where we go
Dance on the deck while the ocean moans
We're carrying trouble, we're carrying bones

So we tap our toes and we don't look back
Keep the brass a-blowing, keep the rhythm on track
If we're going down, then we're going in style
With a horn and a hi-hat and a crooked smile

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Every evening I climb the winding stair
Light the lantern, leave a letter there
Fold it in a bottle, let the current take
All the words I write for my ocean's sake

Dear sea, dear sea, you're the only one for me
I'm writing in the moonlight, sending love across the sea
Dance with my letters, baby, on the waves you free
Dear sea, dear sea, come and carry me

Forty years of keeping this old flame alive
No wife, no children, just the tide and I
The village thinks I'm lonely on my rock of stone
But the water answers, so I'm never alone

Dear sea, dear sea, you're the only one for me
I'm writing in the moonlight, sending love across the sea
Dance with my letters, baby, on the waves you free
Dear sea, dear sea, come and carry me

When my hands grow weak and the light grows dim
I'll walk to the shoreline and I'll wade right in
And the sea will read every word I gave
And hold me forever in a moonlit wave

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Basement of the squat, three a.m. light
Warm beer, bad blood, and a fixed-up fight
Dealt me a hand and I called your bluff
Nobody's leaving when it gets this rough

ALL IN! Flip the table, kick the door
We don't play for money anymore
Go too far, go too far, never knew when to stop
Now the cards are flying and the windows pop

You bet your jacket, I bet my name
Somebody's bleeding, who's to blame
Ace up the sleeve and a knife in the boot
The whole game's rigged and the point is moot

ALL IN! Flip the table, kick the door
We don't play for money anymore
Go too far, go too far, never knew when to stop
Now the cards are flying and the windows pop

No rules, no refs, no taking it back
We lit the match and we burned the pack
Laugh in the wreckage, spit out a tooth
That's what you get for the ugly truth

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning, same old wooden pew
Stained glass colors that I once thought true
Mama's hymn book, pages soft and worn
I been pretending since the day I was born

I slip out early while the choir sings
Fold my doubt up in my coat with my keys and things
No big announcement, no dramatic scene
Just a quiet boy and the space in between

I'm letting go, soft and slow
The steeple shrinks in the rearview, yeah I know
No hard feelings, just a closing door
I loved you once but I can't anymore

The gravel crunches and the bell still rings
For everybody else and their golden wings
I keep the lessons, I just drop the chains
Walk into a morning where the question remains

Maybe God's still out there in the open sky
Maybe peace is just a long goodbye
I'm not angry, I'm just finally free
The church kept the faith but it lost me

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
The judge said partner up or you're both disqualified
Me and my nemesis, side by side
I hate your guts and you hate mine too
But the band's too tight and we got nothing to do

So we move, get down, can't deny the groove
Two rivals on the floor with something left to prove
You hate me, I hate you, but the bass don't lie
We're locked in the pocket and we both know why

Your hand on my shoulder feels like a knife
But you spin me clean, gotta give you your life
The crowd's going crazy, the horns hit hard
We're talking smack with every funky bar

So we move, get down, can't deny the groove
Two rivals on the floor with something left to prove
You hate me, I hate you, but the bass don't lie
We're locked in the pocket and we both know why

Funny how the rhythm makes the hatred fade
Forty thousand watts and the truce we made
Maybe when the song ends we go back to war
But right now, baby, let me hear that horn once more

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
Mama picked a husband and a house on the hill
(Bom-bom-bom, but I won't, no I won't)
A ring and a calendar against my will
(Bom-bom-bom, but I won't, no I won't)
They planned every Sunday till the day that I die
But I packed a little suitcase and I'm saying goodbye

I'm gonna run, run, run down the avenue
Find a life that's mine and a heart that's true
They wrote my whole story but they got it wrong
So I'm singing my own, my own sweet song

The boy they chose was kind, I'll give him that
(Bom-bom-bom, but I won't, no I won't)
But my heart's not a deal you can haggle at
(Bom-bom-bom, but I won't, no I won't)
There's a whole wide world past the porch and the gate
And I won't sit pretty while I'm tempting fate

I'm gonna run, run, run down the avenue
Find a life that's mine and a heart that's true
They wrote my whole story but they got it wrong
So I'm singing my own, my own sweet song

Forgive me, Mama, for the dreams I chase
I see the hurt and love all over your face
But a borrowed future is a gilded cage
And I'm tearing up the script and the wedding page

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
Chained at the wrist by a tyrant's decree
Dance for the king or you'll both cease to be
My sworn enemy stands across from me
Blood in our eyes and no mercy to plea

The drums of war become a waltz of the damned
Forced to move as one by an iron hand

DANCE! In the fire, in the hate, in the flame
Two rivals bound by a murderous game
Step for step we descend into hell
Moving as one as the kingdom we fell

Every turn is a threat, every step is a fight
Your grip is a vice in the merciless light
But the rhythm takes hold and the chains start to gleam
And we share the same hatred, we share the same scream

DANCE! In the fire, in the hate, in the flame
Two rivals bound by a murderous game
Step for step we descend into hell
Moving as one as the kingdom we fell

Then our eyes meet and a truth is revealed
The enemy's the crown, not the partner we steeled
So we turn as one toward the throne up high
And the dance becomes a war cry

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
He used to lift the engine block with just his two bare hands
Now he asks me twice the time and I don't understand
The gray came in like static on a TV left alone
The king of every Sunday now just whispers on the phone

Oh, the slow groove of getting older
The weight comes down on a strong man's shoulder
I'm watching my father fade in time
The same blood beating, the same heart, the same line

His hands still tap the table when the radio comes on
The rhythm never left him even after strength was gone
I give him back the music that he handed down to me
We sway there in the kitchen, two men and a memory

Oh, the slow groove of getting older
The weight comes down on a strong man's shoulder
I'm watching my father fade in time
The same blood beating, the same heart, the same line

Don't you worry, Pop, I got the groove now
I'll carry every note you never told me how
The funk don't die, it just changes hands
From an old man's heart to a young man's plans

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
The room was small, the lights were low and blue
My hands were shaking on the strings I barely knew
A microphone, a folding chair, a half-filled cup of wine
And seven strangers waiting in a quiet little line

And it floats back to me now
Like smoke that never settled, like a half-remembered vow
The first time I let the silence break
The first small sound I ever dared to make

I don't recall the song, I just recall the air
The way it held its breath like it already knew I'd dare
The years have softened every edge, the faces all gone dim
But something stayed, a glowing thread, a hum beneath the din

And it floats back to me now
Like smoke that never settled, like a half-remembered vow
The first time I let the silence break
The first small sound I ever dared to make

I'm still that kid, somewhere inside the haze
Standing in the quiet glow of those forgotten days

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
They were carved from the holler and the cold red clay
Folded over a Bible at the close of the day
Knuckles like the knots in an old oak tree
Those hands raised the dead and they raised up me

Grandmother's hands, grandmother's hands
Kneading the dough and the will of the land
A cross on the wall and a knife in the drawer
Grandmother's hands don't fear no more

She wrung the necks of the chickens in the snow
She held my fever down when the doctor wouldn't go
They shook like a willow when the preacher passed by
But they never once trembled when it came time to die

Grandmother's hands, grandmother's hands
Kneading the dough and the will of the land
A cross on the wall and a knife in the drawer
Grandmother's hands don't fear no more

They lowered her down where the cedars lean
And I swear those hands still reach through the green
Every time I fold my own to pray
I feel her holler grip and hold me where I lay

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
The fog came down on the harbor road as the church bells tolled at three
My engine warm and my conscience cold, one last run and I'd be free
The lads went in with their collars up, I counted every breath
The sea wind whistled a funeral hymn through the cobbled streets of death

Drive, drive, for the morning light
One more turn and I'll leave it all behind tonight
The wheel in my hands and the green hills near
One last job and I'm gone from here

The shots rang out like a fiddle string snapped clean across the bow
Two got in but the third went down, I can't go back for him now
The peelers came like the howling wind down every twisting lane
And the rain washed red through the gutter cracks of the town that knew my name

Drive, drive, for the morning light
One more turn and I'll leave it all behind tonight
The wheel in my hands and the green hills near
One last job and I'm gone from here

They'll find the car by the cliffside burned, no driver in the seat
Just a set of prints in the morning frost walking toward the sea so deep

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the trunk at the break of dawn
Glass jars rattling, headlights gone
Dirt road screaming under bald tires
State line burning with the county on fire

RUN! The sirens at my back
RUN! No turning off this track
White lightning spilling on the floor
This is the last run, there ain't no more

Hit the curve too hard at a hundred ten
Met the ditch and the devil again
Glass and copper and gasoline
Worst damn night that I've ever seen

RUN! The sirens at my back
RUN! No turning off this track
White lightning spilling on the floor
This is the last run, there ain't no more

Cuffs on my wrists in the kerosene dawn
Everything I built is gone, gone, gone
Tell my daddy that I drove it true
There was nothing left, nothing left to do

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
We drove down to the water with the windows rolled all the way
Your playlist on the speaker, gonna make this whole thing stay
Boxes in my bedroom, half a life all taped and sealed
But right now there's just sunlight and the way the whole world feels

So hold on to the last of it, the gold of it, the glow
One more night before the both of us let go
We're young and we're forever for a minute, don't you cry
This is the last day of summer, say goodbye

We wrote our names in marker on the bridge above the train
Made a promise we'd come back here even if it's not the same
You're going east, I'm going west, we'll text until we don't
But tonight we're gonna swear that we'll remember when we won't

So hold on to the last of it, the gold of it, the glow
One more night before the both of us let go
We're young and we're forever for a minute, don't you cry
This is the last day of summer, say goodbye

And the streetlights flicker on like they always used to do
And I'll keep this little summer in my pocket, and I'll keep you

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Smoke curling slow over the green felt table
You had that look like you weren't quite stable
I raised, you called, the night went deeper
Every hand made the stakes get steeper

Oh, we let it go too far
Laid our whole hearts on the bar
Folded everything we are
Over a king and a falling star

You bet the ring that your mama gave you
I said baby, no card's gonna save you
The dealer flipped and the whole room got quiet
Love on the line and we just let it ride it

Oh, we let it go too far
Laid our whole hearts on the bar
Folded everything we are
Over a king and a falling star

Now the cards are on the floor and you walked out the door
And I'm sitting with a pair of empty hands
The game don't mean a thing, I'd give back everything
For a chance to deal it over, understand

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Five a.m. alarm and I'm already gone
Clock in, clock out, then I do it all again
Apron in the morning, name tag after dark
Counting up my hours like they're miles to the end

And I'm running, running, running just to stay in place
Two jobs deep and I still can't win the race
The rent goes up, the tank runs low, the numbers never climb
I'm working all my life away just to borrow back my time

Mama said hard work would always pay
But the bills keep singing louder every day
I smile for the tips, I smile through the strain
And I save up all my dreaming for the bus ride home again

And I'm running, running, running just to stay in place
Two jobs deep and I still can't win the race
The rent goes up, the tank runs low, the numbers never climb
I'm working all my life away just to borrow back my time

But I'm still standing, still got fight
Gonna catch a break one of these nights
And when I do, oh, when I do
I'll buy back every hour that this city ever took from you

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Concrete flooding, sky split wide
You pulled me under, no time to decide
Didn't know your name, didn't know your face
Just two soaked ghosts in the same gray place

ONE umbrella, two strangers, one storm
Holding the line just to stay warm
We don't speak but we both understand
The whole world drowns but we still stand

Shoulder to shoulder in the screaming rain
Sharing a breath and a six-inch lane
The city tried to wash us down the drain
But we held the ground, we held the frame

ONE umbrella, two strangers, one storm
Holding the line just to stay warm
We don't speak but we both understand
The whole world drowns but we still stand

Then the light turned green and you walked away
Never got your name, didn't have to say
But for ninety seconds in the pouring dark
Two strangers were a shelter, two strangers left a mark

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Well she'd snap her fingers to the radio's tune
Flour on her apron in the kitchen at noon
Those hands could sew a button, those hands could deal a deck
And pull you up to dancing with a kiss upon your neck

Grandma's hands, oh how they'd swing
Clap on the two and the four, let 'em ring
Soft as a feather, quick as a band
There ain't nothing in the world like my grandmother's hands

She'd jitterbug the linoleum till the kettle gave a whine
Hands like a metronome keeping perfect time
They wiped away my crying, they waved me out the door
And every time the brass kicks in, I hear her clap once more

Grandma's hands, oh how they'd swing
Clap on the two and the four, let 'em ring
Soft as a feather, quick as a band
There ain't nothing in the world like my grandmother's hands

Now the band plays on and the floor is mine
And I clap on the two and I keep her time
So take your partner, give the room a chance
And dance it out the way my grandmother's hands

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
The bell above the door has rung for forty years the same
The striped pole turning slow, he greets you all by name
A pair of scissors singing, a mirror full of ghosts
He's heard the whole town's secrets between the morning toasts

And the years pile up like hair on the floor
Every boy he cut now walks back through the door
He holds the town in the palm of his hand
The quiet keeper of a quiet little land

He knew your father's father, he knew the way you'd grow
He cut you for your wedding and for places you would go
The radio static murmurs, the clippers softly hum
And everyone in town will pass that chair before they're done

And the years pile up like hair on the floor
Every boy he cut now walks back through the door
He holds the town in the palm of his hand
The quiet keeper of a quiet little land

The pole still turns, the bell still rings
Long after the barber's gone
And the whole town carries something of him
Quietly, quietly on

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody gave us a fighting chance at all
Last place rebels with a beat-up ball
But we lace up tight and we hold our ground
Gonna play this final like the whole world's around

Rise up, rise up, this is our day
Nobody believed but we found a way
Underdogs running with the sun on our backs
We never had nothing but we never looked back

The whistle blow and the pressure climb
We down by one with no more time
Then little Joseph from the back row
Sends it sailing where the big dogs can't go

Rise up, rise up, this is our day
Nobody believed but we found a way
Underdogs running with the sun on our backs
We never had nothing but we never looked back

Win or lose, we stood up tall
Gave our hearts and we gave our all
And the whole town singing in the cooling night
The smallest team with the biggest light

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Soft light through the colored glass, the hymn I used to know
I mouth the words I no longer mean and quietly let them go
The pews still hold my childhood, the air still smells the same
But something slipped away from me I can no longer name

So I'll slip out gently, no goodbye
No storm, no anger, just a softer sky
I carry the kindness, I leave the rest
And close the door against my chest

My mother's in the second row, she does not see me leave
The candles flicker quietly on everything I believe
It isn't that I'm broken, it isn't that I'm cold
I'm just a river finding new and quieter ground to hold

So I'll slip out gently, no goodbye
No storm, no anger, just a softer sky
I carry the kindness, I leave the rest
And close the door against my chest

The morning sun is warm outside, the bells are far behind
And I keep a little stained-glass light somewhere in my mind

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Well I climb these stairs each evening with a lantern and a pen
Write my words upon the water, then I write them all again
There ain't no girl in town for me, no sweetheart on the shore
Just the gray and rolling ocean that I've come to love and more

Oh sea, oh sea, my only bride
I keep your light and I keep your tide
I fold my letters into the spray
And watch you carry my heart away

The fishermen all pity me, they think I'm half-insane
A man who weds the salt and storm and dances in the rain
But every wave's a letter back, every gull a kiss
There ain't a wife in all the world could love me half like this

Oh sea, oh sea, my only bride
I keep your light and I keep your tide
I fold my letters into the spray
And watch you carry my heart away

And when I'm gone they'll find a stack of papers in the tower
A thousand love notes salt and worn from many a midnight hour
And the sea will keep on rolling, faithful as a vow
The only love that ever stayed, the only love I'll allow

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon humming red and gold against the empty street
A pot of coffee always on, a booth, a vinyl seat
The night shift comes in weary and the lonely come in slow
There's a light that's always burning where the broken hearts can go

The diner stays open all night long
Where the jukebox plays a tired old song
No matter who you are or where you been
There's a door that's always letting you in

The waitress knows your order, she knows your name and more
She's seen a thousand stories walk on through that swinging door
A cab driver, a poet, a couple who can't sleep
All the city's quiet sorrows that the morning's gonna keep

The diner stays open all night long
Where the jukebox plays a tired old song
No matter who you are or where you been
There's a door that's always letting you in

So pour me one more cup before the sun comes climbing through
This little glowing window's seen me halfway broken too
And when the dawn comes rolling and the night begins to fade
I'll thank the lord for diners and the light that someone made

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Doppler bleeding green across the dash
I taped a photo of you to the windshield glass
Mile marker forty, the funnel drops its hand
I choose the cloud, I always choose the cloud, not land

Spin me, spin me, supercell tonight
I'm a needle on a barometer, swinging into white
You wanted Sunday mornings, slippers by the door
But I was born to follow what the wind is reaching for

Motel keys and a radar app gone red
Gas station coffee, a roadmap on the bed
You said come home before the season ends
I sent a postcard from a county with no friends

Spin me, spin me, supercell tonight
I'm a needle on a barometer, swinging into white
You wanted Sunday mornings, slippers by the door
But I was born to follow what the wind is reaching for

Mirrorball hail on a Kansas county line
I dance with the downdraft, I call the chaos mine
Love is a porch light, steady and small
But I keep driving toward the thing that swallows it all

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the gray around my only root
A parking line runs straight beneath my fruit
The meters hum a frequency of coins
While something old inside my heartwood joins

I am the green inside the gray
The held breath the concrete couldn't take away
Wind remembers, wind remembers my name
When every other leaf became a flame of asphalt rain

A sparrow keeps a single morning chord
A child once carved initials, now ignored
The streetlight thinks it is the moon at night
I drink the rain that slips between the white

No forest left to answer when I sway
Just echoes off the bank across the way
Still I unfold one stubborn April vein
A cathedral standing in a lot of rain

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Alarm clock screaming, ceiling pressing down
Nine to five is a leash that drags me round
But every night the gravity lets go
And I'm above the rooftops nobody owns

I flew again, I flew again last night
Kicked off the pavement, cleared the streetlight light
Don't tell me how the morning's gonna land
I had the whole sky burning in my hands

Three minutes free before the buzzer hits
My body's chained to a desk that never quits
But I remember tilting past the wires
Laughing at the asphalt and the liars

I flew again, I flew again last night
Kicked off the pavement, cleared the streetlight light
Don't tell me how the morning's gonna land
I had the whole sky burning in my hands

They wake you up, they pin you to the floor
They tell you feet were made for nothing more
But I keep the runway folded in my chest
And I take off the second I shut my eyes to rest

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Forty winters worn into these hands
Your heel still finds the rhythm where it lands
The guitar weeps a key we learned by heart
We move together now to move apart

Una vez mas, one final compas
The shawl across your shoulders, the years across the glass
Clap for the silence after we are done
Two shadows turning slow against the setting sun

Your wedding ring, a coin worn smooth and pale
My boot heel keeps the count, my old heart frail
The room holds breath the way a church holds smoke
Every turn a vow, every stomp a word we spoke

No more castanets to call the morning back
Just the tremble in the strings, the worn black track
We bow, we hold, the last note will not stay
But god, mi amor, what a long and burning way

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
The jars were singing under burlap shrouds
My daddy's recipe and a sky of clouds
The county road wore fog like a funeral veil
And headlights came up cold behind the rail

Lord, the still ran dry and the river ran red
I buried the bottle where the willows bled
The revenue man got a story to tell
And I got a curve that I remember too well

Gravel like teeth in the bottom of the dark
A blown front tire and the dogwood spark
The brake went soft like a promise breaks
And the corn liquor caught what the hard turn takes

Somewhere a mother sets a plate for two
The second one's cold, the way these things do
I walk the ditch with the smell of fire and rye
And a moon that won't ever look me in the eye

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Trunk full of jars and the city lights ahead
Bassline thumping like the engine in my chest
I greased the highway, I had a getaway plan
With a mirrorball moon and a fake ID tan

Run, run, the bottles gonna roll tonight
Blue and red are flashing under strobe-light white
I thought I'd dance the cargo all the way to dawn
But the law caught the rhythm and the deal was gone

High heels on the gas, sequins on the seat
A checkpoint shining where the off-ramp meets
I smiled like Saturday, I played it cool
But the cork popped loud and I broke the rule

Run, run, the bottles gonna roll tonight
Blue and red are flashing under strobe-light white
I thought I'd dance the cargo all the way to dawn
But the law caught the rhythm and the deal was gone

Glitter on the asphalt, glass across the lane
The disco ball is spinning in the cruiser's rain
I'll do my time in a beat I understand
Still clapping out the offbeat with a cuffed-up hand

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Desk lamp humming like a tiny sun
Highlighter bleeding through page two-oh-one
The building's sleeping but my pulse won't quit
Got a dancefloor heartbeat and a textbook to hit

Two in the morning, just me and the groove
Flashcards spinning, the only ones that move
Nobody's watching, so I let it ride
A one-person discotheque on the quiet side

Cold coffee rings make a vinyl on the wood
I conjugate the verbs the way a dancer would
The vending machine glows like a city block
And my pencil keeps the four-on-the-floor o'clock

Two in the morning, just me and the groove
Flashcards spinning, the only ones that move
Nobody's watching, so I let it ride
A one-person discotheque on the quiet side

Sunrise is a deadline dressed in gold
I'll learn the whole chapter before it's told
Spin the chorus softly, don't wake the hall
Me and the midnight, acing it all

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the Ford 'fore the rooster crowed
Mason jars rattling down the holler road
Banjo in my mind and the dew on the pine
Carrying my granddaddy's white lightning shine

Run, boys, run, but the run went wrong
The sheriff knew the words to my running song
Fiddle on the dashboard, prayer on my lips
And a busted axle where the deal slips

Forded the creek where the cottonwoods lean
The water took the jars and the cargo clean
Now I'm standing in the shallows counting loss
With a soaking pair of boots and a hard-won cross

Mama always said don't follow that trade
But a poor man's pocket is a debt unpaid
So I'll pick my banjo and I'll tell it true
How the river drank the corn and the morning, too

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Manos en las diez y las dos, mija
Empty parking lot, Sunday, no prisa
You kill the engine twice and you laugh out loud
Little girl gone tall, behind the wheel now proud

Dale suave, suave, easy on the brake
Every green light's a future you gonna take
I'm in the passenger seat letting go, letting go
My whole heart riding shotgun, watching you grow

Mirror, signal, breathe, you got the flow
First time on the avenue, radio low
Your knuckles white, then your shoulders drop
And the city opens up at the four-way stop

Dale suave, suave, easy on the brake
Every green light's a future you gonna take
I'm in the passenger seat letting go, letting go
My whole heart riding shotgun, watching you grow

One day pretty soon you gonna drive away
Taillights down a road I cannot stay
But tonight you park it perfect by the curb
And I clap like you just conquered the whole earth, palabra

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Thumb above the green, been hovering for days
Four years of static in a hundred different ways
The dial tone's a heartbeat I forgot the sound
Vinyl crackle morning, rain coming down

Hey ma, it's me, it's been a minute, I know
The line went quiet but the love didn't go
Put the kettle on, I'm finally on the phone
A long way from sorry, still finding my way home

She picks up slow like she knew it all along
Same breath, same pause, the start of the same song
I count the years out loud and they don't weigh as much
When her voice still folds around me like a touch

No big apology, just coffee-cup small talk
The weather, the cousins, the cat, the morning walk
And somewhere in the nothing we begin again
Two people learning how to be a we, not when

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Tether came loose somewhere past the moon
Your voice in my helmet a half-step out of tune
The Earth's a blue coin spinning down the dark
And the distance keeps its own slow walking arc

Drift, baby, drift, the silence in between
A minor key of starlight, a love we cannot lean
I reach, you reach, our gloved hands miss the bar
Two metronomes of breathing, growing far

Radio thins to a whisper, then a hiss
I hum the standard that we used to kiss
The oxygen counts down in lazy blue
And I improvise a goodbye made for two

No gravity to hold the note in place
Just the swing of two bodies losing space
Play it soft, brushes on the snare of night
We fade to black on the downbeat of the light

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I sold the truck twice and I bought it back
There's a hailstone dent for every Texas track
My wedding suit still smells of diesel rain
And the wheat goes flat when I come down the lane

I can't stay, darlin', when the sky turns wrong
There's a green-black promise calling me along
You built us a kitchen, you hung us a door
But I love the open road that wants me more

Grain elevator ghosts in the rearview gold
A county fair radio playing something old
I chase the anvil clouds till the daylight's gone
And I sleep where the thunder writes its song

Maybe when the season finally breaks
I'll learn the weight a quiet morning takes
But the wind's got my name in a low refrain
And I answer it again, and again, and again

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Gold lights swinging on the reception floor
Never met you 'fore the DJ opened the door
You caught my rhythm in the chorus of the night
Two strangers spinning in the auntie spotlight

Wine for me, baby, move your waist so slow
Don't know your name but the body already know
The drum say closer, the bass say stay
We dance like the morning is a lifetime away

The couple cuts the cake, but we cut the air
Confetti in your braids, jollof in the air
You laugh and the speakers catch the sound
We own a little circle of the holy ground

Wine for me, baby, move your waist so slow
Don't know your name but the body already know
The drum say closer, the bass say stay
We dance like the morning is a lifetime away

Maybe we forget by the morning bus
Maybe this whole wedding was a song for us
But for three minutes under the disco palm
You were my wedding, you were my calm

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five bucks a game on a fold-out card
Pigeons keep the time in the courthouse yard
My clock got a crack and my king got grit
Sit down, young blood, let me lighten your kit

Knight to the corner, take my queen if you dare
I been losing on purpose since you sat in that chair
The city walks by but it don't understand
There's a whole working life in the back of my hand

Winter in my gloves with the fingertips gone
I been playing this square since the streetlights came
A banker, a busker, a kid skipping school
They all learn the price of a confident fool

They tore down the diner, they raised up the rent
But the board's still free and the hours well spent
Checkmate, friend, now ante up again
The park is my office and the pawns are my pen

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Antenna lashed to a water tower's bone
Midnight hum where the signal lives alone
I press the key and the static parts like sea
This is nowhere station, broadcasting to be

Can you hear me out there past the licensed light
A frequency of strangers in the open night
No call sign, no permission, just a tone
A lantern made of airwaves for the ones alone

A trucker on the interstate, a sleepless nurse
We share a borrowed channel of the universe
I spin the records nobody else will play
And let the dead air breathe till the break of gray

They triangulate the towers, close the door
I move the rig to a rooftop one block more
As long as one receiver glows in the dark
The pirate keeps the flame, the ghost keeps the spark

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Cada noche cierro los ojos y subo
sobre los tejados de mi pueblo dormido
las trompetas suenan como un viento que llama
y mis brazos se abren como alas de paloma

Vuelo sobre el río donde aprendí a llorar
sobre la plaza vieja, sobre el campanar
la luna me saluda con su pañuelo blanco
y no tengo miedo, solo tengo el canto

Ay, vuelo, vuelo, no me despierten todavía
que en el cielo soy más libre que de día
ay, vuelo, vuelo, sobre el agave y la sierra
soy un hombre con el cielo por bandera

Pero siempre amanece y me caigo a la cama
con el eco de violines y una vieja llama
busco mis alas debajo de la almohada
y solo encuentro el polvo de la madrugada

Dicen que soñar volando es querer escapar
yo solo quiero el cielo para poder cantar
que si una noche no regreso al suelo
me encontrarán riendo en mitad del vuelo

Ay, vuelo, vuelo, no me despierten todavía
que en el cielo soy más libre que de día
ay, vuelo, vuelo, sobre el agave y la sierra
soy un hombre con el cielo por bandera

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Cold air on my skin but my heart dey burn
Stood on the ice waiting for my turn
Mama said the sky go dance one day
Now I see am sway, green and far away

Northern light, carry me go
Paint the night, make the whole world glow
Northern light, oh slow, slow, slow
Dancing in the sky where the cold wind blow

Ribbon for the heaven, purple turn to gold
Never seen this kind of magic since I be small
I dey move my body underneath the flame
Whisper to the stars, call them by their name

Northern light, carry me go
Paint the night, make the whole world glow
Northern light, oh slow, slow, slow
Dancing in the sky where the cold wind blow

From Lagos to the snow, my spirit no go freeze
The heaven dey perform just to put my soul at ease
If na dream, no wake me, let me stay
Under the green river till the break of day

Northern light, carry me go
Paint the night, make the whole world glow
Northern light, oh slow, slow, slow
Dancing in the sky where the cold wind blow

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Took the long way home through the part of town
Where the streetlights flicker and the rent was down
There's a window on the second floor still burns
But it ain't for me, that lesson's one I learned

And I drive on past, I don't even slow
Though my hands they wanna turn that wheel and go
Up the same old stairs to the door I used to know
But I drive on past, I drive on past and let it go

There's a porch light buzzing where we used to sit
With a banjo and a bottle and a heart half-lit
Now somebody else is laughing in our kitchen chair
And the curtains that you sewed ain't hanging there

And I drive on past, I don't even slow
Though my hands they wanna turn that wheel and go
Up the same old stairs to the door I used to know
But I drive on past, I drive on past and let it go

Maybe one of these nights I'll quit this road
Find another way to carry this load
But the tires keep rolling and the memory keeps time
Down the avenue of yours that used to be mine

And I drive on past, I don't even slow
Though my hands they wanna turn that wheel and go
Up the same old stairs to the door I used to know
But I drive on past, I drive on past and let it go

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Midnight engine running hot
Jars of fire in the trunk we got
Backroad headlights cutting through
Thought we had it, me and you

Run, run, the bottle and the night
Run, run, but we're losing the light
Something went wrong on the moonshine line
Now we're praying to the dashboard, out of time

Siren red in the rearview glass
Foot to the floor but it's too fast
Spilled the silver on the county road
Lost the whole damn precious load

Run, run, the bottle and the night
Run, run, but we're losing the light
Something went wrong on the moonshine line
Now we're praying to the dashboard, out of time

Kick drum heartbeat, gravel and the dust
Everything we built turned to rust
Let the bassline carry what we couldn't keep
Dance it off before we fall asleep

Run, run, the bottle and the night
Run, run, but we're losing the light
Something went wrong on the moonshine line
Now we're praying to the dashboard, out of time

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They offered me a window full of gold
If I'd just say the lines the way they're sold
But every word I wrote was bleeding true
And I won't dress it up for me or you

Let it blur, let it ring, let it drown
In the haze I'd rather burn than wear the crown
I keep my ink, I keep my quiet doubt
I'm a poet and I'm never selling out

The billboards hum a language I forgot
They want the soul but only sell the plot
I fade into the feedback and the gray
Where nobody can buy the things I say

Let it blur, let it ring, let it drown
In the haze I'd rather burn than wear the crown
I keep my ink, I keep my quiet doubt
I'm a poet and I'm never selling out

Drown the chorus in a wall of light
No one hears the meaning but it's right
If I disappear inside the sound
At least the truth of me is still around

Let it blur, let it ring, let it drown
In the haze I'd rather burn than wear the crown
I keep my ink, I keep my quiet doubt
I'm a poet and I'm never selling out

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Label slid a contract, gold pen on the table
Said switch up the message, make it radio-stable
I laughed, folded paper into a paper plane
Let it glide past the suits, watched it sail off the frame
I write for the corner where the streetlight flickers
Not the boardroom vultures counting up the figures
My verses got a spine, won't bend for a check
I'd rather eat my pride than wear a leash on my neck

Nah, I won't sell it, won't water it down
Keep my pen honest while they're handing out crowns
They can keep the chains, keep the platinum route
I'm a poet to the bone and I'm not selling out

They said nobody listens to the truth no more
But I seen the kids reciting every bar I wrote
That's the only chart that I'm tryna top
A notebook full of fire that I'll never drop
You can buy the beat but you can't buy the why
The reason that I bleed every time I write

Nah, I won't sell it, won't water it down
Keep my pen honest while they're handing out crowns
They can keep the chains, keep the platinum route
I'm a poet to the bone and I'm not selling out

What's a man with a million if his words are a lie?
I'd rather be broke with the fire in my eye
They can name a price, I just shake my head
Some things ain't for sale, like the truth that I said

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Dem come with the money, dem come with the deal
Say change up your message to something dem feel
But I and I writing what the spirit reveal
No amount of silver gonna buy what is real

I nah sell out, no no, I nah trade my soul
The words are a fire that dem cannot control
I nah sell out, no no, I a stand on the truth
A poor man rich with the rhyme in him mouth

Dem build a big stage with a velvet rope line
But the message get lost when dem put up the sign
I sing for the people in the valley below
Not the man with the contract telling I where to go

I nah sell out, no no, I nah trade my soul
The words are a fire that dem cannot control
I nah sell out, no no, I a stand on the truth
A poor man rich with the rhyme in him mouth

Jah give I the riddim, Jah give I the pen
I write for the children, write again and again
When the money done fade and the gold turn to rust
The poet and the truth, dem the only ones to trust

I nah sell out, no no, I nah trade my soul
The words are a fire that dem cannot control
I nah sell out, no no, I a stand on the truth
A poor man rich with the rhyme in him mouth

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle in his hand and a swagger in his walk
Mister Sam the tailor, he dress the whole block
From the deacon to the dealer, from the bride to the band
Get a suit so clean that it make the corner stand

Stitch it up, cut it sharp, let the fabric talk
Everybody steppin' fly down on the block
From the collar to the cuff, he the talk of the town
Sam gonna lay you out the freshest threads around

Velvet for the preacher, satin for the queen
Pinstripe for the hustler keeping all his money clean
He measure up your shoulders and he measure up your soul
Send you out the door looking ten feet tall

Stitch it up, cut it sharp, let the fabric talk
Everybody steppin' fly down on the block
From the collar to the cuff, he the talk of the town
Sam gonna lay you out the freshest threads around

Groove on the chalk line, scissors keeping time
Every thread a story, every seam a rhyme
He don't just sew a jacket, he sew a little pride
Into every body walking down the avenue with style

Stitch it up, cut it sharp, let the fabric talk
Everybody steppin' fly down on the block
From the collar to the cuff, he the talk of the town
Sam gonna lay you out the freshest threads around

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I got your name living rent-free in my head
Replaying conversations that we never even said
You see me as a homie, I see you as the sky
Every time you laugh with him a little of me die
I hold the door, I hold the secret, hold the line
Pretending that I'm cool while I'm dying inside
Text you good morning like it's casual and light
But I rewrote that message fifteen times last night

And you don't even know, you don't even know
That I'd catch a falling star just to watch your face glow
But I'm stuck in the corner of your camera roll
Loving you in silence is a lonely kind of cold

I memorized your coffee order, your favorite song
Been carrying a torch for you so patient, for so long
But a torch just burns the hand that holds it tight
And I'm tired of pretending that I'm fine, alright
You deserve the love you give, I hope he treats you gold
Even if it's not my hand that's yours to hold

And you don't even know, you don't even know
That I'd catch a falling star just to watch your face glow
But I'm stuck in the corner of your camera roll
Loving you in silence is a lonely kind of cold

Maybe in another life the timing lines up right
Maybe I stop writing songs about you every night
But for now I'll keep this love behind my teeth
And let you be happy even if it's not with me

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
En la torre de piedra donde el viento me canta
escribo cada noche con la pluma en la mano
cartas para el mar, para la ola que se levanta
mi amante de espuma, mi amor más lejano

Le cuento de la luz que enciendo en su honor
de los barcos que salvo cuando ruge el dolor
ella nunca responde, solo besa la arena
pero sé que me escucha en cada marea llena

Ay, mar, mi señora de azul y de sal
recibe mis cartas, mi amor sin final
ay, mar, aunque nunca me puedas amar
seré tu guardián, tu faro, tu hogar

Envío mis palabras dobladas en botellas
flotan hacia el horizonte bajo las estrellas
y si un día la luz de mi torre se apaga
búscame en el fondo, donde el agua me abraza

Los años me han vuelto de cal y de viento
pero el corazón sigue latiendo a su tiempo
porque amar a quien nunca te puede abrazar
es el destino sagrado de quien cuida el mar

Ay, mar, mi señora de azul y de sal
recibe mis cartas, mi amor sin final
ay, mar, aunque nunca me puedas amar
seré tu guardián, tu faro, tu hogar

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The dial tone hums like a held breath
I memorized this number through the years of nothing said
My thumb above the call, the silence stretched so wide
A decade folded quiet in the space we let it hide

So I'm calling, calling, after all this time
Let the ringing carry what I never could in rhyme
Mama, are you there, in the soft and fading light
I'm calling, I'm calling, won't you answer me tonight

Three rings then a voice that I'd know in any dark
Thinner than I left it, but it lands inside my heart
The words dissolve to breathing, just the both of us afloat
On an ocean made of everything we never wrote

No speeches, no apology can hold a thing this deep
Just the static and the quiet and the promises we keep
The years don't disappear but somehow they grow light
When a mother says your name in the middle of the night

So I'm calling, calling, after all this time
Let the ringing carry what I never could in rhyme
Mama, are you there, in the soft and fading light
I'm calling, I'm calling, and you answered me tonight

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They paved the avenues across his shoulder blade
Hung the neon over where the giant's dreaming laid
We sip our coffee easy on a chest that slowly heaves
A million little windows on a sleeper no one believes

So hush now, baby, walk the city soft and low
There's a giant down below us and you never really know
When the rumble of the subway is a yawn beneath the stone
We're dancing on a dreamer who is sleeping all alone

The saxophone is bending where the boulevards collide
The bartenders are pouring on his slow and breathing side
The streetlights start to shimmer when he turns inside his rest
And the whole town holds its martini against his rising chest

What if one gray morning he decides to open eyes
Shake the towers off his back, let the bridges all capsize
Till then we'll keep on swinging, keep the rhythm nice and sly
A lullaby of taxicabs to keep the giant's eyes

So hush now, baby, walk the city soft and low
There's a giant down below us and you never really know
When the rumble of the subway is a yawn beneath the stone
We're dancing on a dreamer who is sleeping all alone

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
The tether snapped at oh-four-hundred, baby, in the black
I watched you getting smaller and I knew you weren't coming back
No gravity to hold us, no ground beneath our feet
Just two lonely sparks of fire and the cold, cold dark between

We're drifting, drifting, further every breath
Two astronauts a-falling through a silence cold as death
I can see your little light still blinking in my eye
But the distance keeps on growing, baby, all I can do is cry

We trained for every danger but they never trained us for
The slow and quiet heartbreak of a love you can't reach for
My radio is crackling but your voice is fading thin
The stars don't care a nickel for the shape that we are in

If I could fire one thruster I would turn this ship around
Chase you through the heavens till my oxygen ran down
But some things drift forever once they're loosed into the night
So I'll love you 'cross the darkness till I lose your blinking light

We're drifting, drifting, further every breath
Two astronauts a-falling through a silence cold as death
I can see your little light still blinking in my eye
But the distance keeps on growing, baby, all I can do is cry

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I sold my father's table and I sold the old guitar
Kissed the cold stone doorway where my people always are
The harbor smelled of salt and tears, the ship was gray and tall
And the country that I loved the most grew small, and then grew small

So carry me over, carry me wide
Across the gray water on the turning tide
I'm trading the known for a name on a shore
And a home I will love but will see nevermore

The nights were full of strangers all dreaming the same dream
Of streets that might be kinder than the ones beside the stream
We shared our bread and stories in the belly of the boat
With a homeland in our pockets and a hymn caught in our throat

I'll plant my mother's flowers in a strange and distant ground
And teach my children words from a village they'll never found
The sea took half my heart and the new land takes the rest
But a wanderer learns to carry his country in his chest

So carry me over, carry me wide
Across the gray water on the turning tide
I'm trading the known for a name on a shore
And a home I will love but will see nevermore

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors slam shut and the tunnel swallows light
Walls are pressing in, every breath a fight
Heartbeat slamming faster than the rolling wheels
Can't remember how a normal moment feels

Breathe, just breathe, count the stations down
Don't let the dark of the tunnel pull you down
Hold on, hold on, till the doors slide wide
There's an open sky waiting on the other side

Strangers' faces blur in the flickering glare
Grip the metal pole like it's the only air
The bassline pounding right behind my eyes
Telling me a story full of beautiful lies

Breathe, just breathe, count the stations down
Don't let the dark of the tunnel pull you down
Hold on, hold on, till the doors slide wide
There's an open sky waiting on the other side

It's a wave, just a wave, and a wave's gotta break
It is loud, it is huge, but it's not gonna take
More than I can carry, more than I can hold
The train is gonna stop and the doors gonna fold

Breathe, just breathe, count the stations down
Don't let the dark of the tunnel pull you down
Hold on, hold on, till the doors slide wide
There's an open sky waiting on the other side

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Rented suit and a borrowed tie
Don't know the bride, don't know why
Caught your eye across the floor
Two strangers slamming through a open door

Dance with me, I don't know your name
This whole room is gonna feel the same
Hands in the air, let the speakers blow
Two strangers and a song we don't know

Cake on the table, blood in the bass
You grabbed my collar, screamed in my face
Not a love song, not a slow one too
Just the violence of me and you

Dance with me, I don't know your name
This whole room is gonna feel the same
Hands in the air, let the speakers blow
Two strangers and a song we don't know

Last call coming, lights gonna rise
Never see you again, no goodbyes
One more circle, one more break
Give me everything this floor can take

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Frozen ground beneath my boots
Sky on fire, no excuse
Green ribbons tearing through the black
A thousand miles, no way back

Burn the sky, let it come
The cold can't kill what we've become
Scream it loud into the white
We are the storm, we are the light

No god up there, just charged-up air
But I fell to my knees, I swear
Electric veins across the dome
A dead man finding his way home

Burn the sky, let it come
The cold can't kill what we've become
Scream it loud into the white
We are the storm, we are the light

Let it bleed, let it crack
The night gave everything back
Hold the line, hold the flame
Nothing here will be the same

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Loaded up the Ford at half past ten
Jugs in the back, riding low again
Sheriff's at the bend with a grin so wide
Nowhere left for a boy to hide

Oh the moon was high and the road was mean
Fastest little run this county's seen
Spilled my shine in the river bed
Lucky I ain't counting myself dead

Hit a pothole, took a spin
Glass went flying, took it on the chin
Whiskey perfume in the morning air
Deputy laughing, I don't care

Oh the moon was high and the road was mean
Fastest little run this county's seen
Spilled my shine in the river bed
Lucky I ain't counting myself dead

Daddy used to say keep it slow
Love a fast wheel more than I should know
Now I'm walking home in muddy shoes
Singing out my moonshine blues

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Caught in the downpour on Seventh and Main
You laughed at the sky like you welcomed the rain
One little umbrella, you held out your hand
A stranger, a downpour, a city, a plan

Under the same little sky tonight
You and me and the city lights
Half of me's wet but I don't even care
Sharing the rain with a stranger out there

We walked twenty blocks just to make it last
Talking 'bout futures and laughing 'bout past
You said you'd remember the way that it rained
I knew right then I would never be the same

Under the same little sky tonight
You and me and the city lights
Half of me's wet but I don't even care
Sharing the rain with a stranger out there

The storm's gonna stop and you'll go your way
But give me this corner, this hour, this day
Oh let it pour, let it pour
I never want sunshine anymore

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody picked us, nobody knew
Fifteen down with a point to prove
Last in the standings, first in the heart
This is the moment we tear it apart

Turn it up, we're not done yet
Last ten seconds, no regret
Feel it rising, feel it loud
Underdogs in a screaming crowd

Buzzer breathing, hands on knees
One more shot through the heavy breeze
Everybody told us to quit
Watch us light up the whole damn pit

Turn it up, we're not done yet
Last ten seconds, no regret
Feel it rising, feel it loud
Underdogs in a screaming crowd

Let the beat drop, let it ride
We carried the whole town's pride
Win or lose we burned so bright
Underdogs own the night

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Bajo las luces de la fiesta
Vi tu sonrisa en la pista
No sé tu nombre, no sé tu historia
Pero esta noche eres mi gloria

Baila conmigo, desconocida
Trompetas cantan toda la vida
Gira y gira bajo el cielo
Dos extraños en un mismo vuelo

La novia ríe, el vino corre
El corazón ya no se esconde
Tu mano en la mía, un compás
No preguntes lo que vendrá después

Baila conmigo, desconocida
Trompetas cantan toda la vida
Gira y gira bajo el cielo
Dos extraños en un mismo vuelo

Mañana el sol nos separa
Pero esta noche nadie nos para
Un violín, un suspiro, un adiós
Gracias por el baile de los dos

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Slow drum walking down Rampart Street
Black umbrellas, dragging feet
Trombone crying for a soul set free
Lay him down by the cypress tree

When the saints go marching slow
Then they turn it up and let it go
Cry on the way, dance on the way back
New Orleans never wears just black

Preacher said his work is done
Now the brass band's just begun
Handkerchiefs to a second line
Grief and joy on the same old time

When the saints go marching slow
Then they turn it up and let it go
Cry on the way, dance on the way back
New Orleans never wears just black

Spin that parasol to the sky
We don't only come to cry
Clap your hands for a life well spent
Death's just a song that the river sent

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Cada noche cierro los ojos
Y la tierra me suelta los cerrojos
Manos de viento bajo mi pecho
Subo por encima del techo

Vuelo, vuelo, no me despiertes
Que el aire me ha vuelto más fuerte
Guitarra rota, palmas al cielo
Nací con alas en este vuelo

Las montañas se hacen pequeñas
El río dibuja viejas señas
Nadie me alcanza, nadie me ata
Solo el latido de la guitarra

Vuelo, vuelo, no me despiertes
Que el aire me ha vuelto más fuerte
Guitarra rota, palmas al cielo
Nací con alas en este vuelo

Pero el alba siempre me llama
Y caigo de nuevo en mi cama
Con los pies clavados al suelo
Rezo por la noche y su vuelo

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning, screen glowing blue
My thumb knows the way back to you
Everything's quiet, everything's slow
A message I shouldn't, but I let it go

Delete it, delete it, I never do
Floating somewhere in the dark with you
Wrong little word in a soft little light
I'll regret it tomorrow but not tonight

Typing and stopping, the dots fade away
A hundred things that I shouldn't say
The whole room's underwater and dim
Drowning so slowly, I let myself swim

Delete it, delete it, I never do
Floating somewhere in the dark with you
Wrong little word in a soft little light
I'll regret it tomorrow but not tonight

The morning will come like it always does
And I'll pretend it never was
But the screen's still warm in my open hand
Somewhere you're awake and you understand

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
En la cantina de Don Ramón
Cinco barajas y un acordeón
Puse en la mesa mi reloj de plata
La suerte esta noche se me desbarata

Una carta más, una carta más
El diablo reparte, no mira atrás
Perdí mi caballo, perdí mi razón
Apostando el alma en esta canción

El tequila me nubla la vista
Subió la apuesta, no hay quien resista
Mi enemigo sonríe, baraja en mano
Saqué la pistola como buen hermano

Una carta más, una carta más
El diablo reparte, no mira atrás
Perdí mi caballo, perdí mi razón
Apostando el alma en esta canción

Ahora corro bajo la luna llena
Dejé la cantina llena de pena
Que aprendan los hombres de mi gran error
Nunca apuestes todo, ni siquiera el honor

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors close quick and the air go thin
Heartbeat louder than the train within
Everybody moving, I dey freeze
Hands cold shaking, can't find my breath please

Breathe, oh breathe, just hold on slow
Next station coming, just let it go
The walls dey close but they no fit win
Count to ten and I rise again

Tunnel dark and the lights dey flash
Mind dey racing like a thunder crash
Stranger look me, ask if I dey fine
Nodded soft but I no dey lie this time

Breathe, oh breathe, just hold on slow
Next station coming, just let it go
The walls dey close but they no fit win
Count to ten and I rise again

Step on the platform, feel the ground
Sweetest cold air all around
I no go let am hold my name
Tomorrow I go ride again the same

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Close my eyes and the bed lets go
Floating up where the cool winds blow
Gravity quit on me last night
Bass line lifting me out of sight

Fly, fly, get up off the ground
Groove so heavy but it don't weigh down
Every night I get my wings
Funk me up to higher things

Roofs go by like a record spin
Moon throwing down a confident grin
Slap that string and I rise some more
Never touch that bedroom floor

Fly, fly, get up off the ground
Groove so heavy but it don't weigh down
Every night I get my wings
Funk me up to higher things

Then the alarm clock starts to scream
Drop me right back out the dream
But my feet remember the air
All day long I'm still up there

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Keys in your hand and your knuckles white
Empty parking lot, fading light
Mirror, signal, breathe it slow
I'm right beside you, here we go

Faster now, but hold the line
Every turn is yours not mine
Foot on the gas, heart in my chest
Little girl, you got the rest

Stalled it twice and you wanted to cry
Then you laughed and you gave it one more try
Engine humming, you found the beat
Hands at ten and two, fast feet

Faster now, but hold the line
Every turn is yours not mine
Foot on the gas, heart in my chest
Little girl, you got the rest

One day soon you'll drive away
Taillights gone at the break of day
So I'll teach you everything I know
Then I'll be the one to let you go

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Vinyl crackle, low and warm
Forty years through every storm
We move slow in the kitchen light
One more record, one more night

Dance with me one final time
Your hand in mine, the old soft rhyme
Dusty needle, fading tune
Dance with me beneath the moon

Gray in your hair where the gold once shined
Still the only hand that fits in mine
The doctor's words don't matter now
Just this rhythm, just this vow

Dance with me one final time
Your hand in mine, the old soft rhyme
Dusty needle, fading tune
Dance with me beneath the moon

When the song runs out we'll stand real still
Quiet kitchen, window sill
Whatever comes when the music's gone
We had the dance, we had the song

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Tether snapped on the dark side of the moon
Reachin' for your hand but I'm comin' loose too soon
Radio crackle, your voice gettin' thin
Slow little drift that we're never comin' in

Fare thee well across the empty black
No silver rope gonna pull us back
Stars don't cry and the void don't care
Just two souls driftin' on the cold night air

Banjo of the engines gone quiet and dead
Nothin' but the hummin' of the stars overhead
You told me 'bout the farm and the fields back home
Now you're just a light, and I drift alone

Fare thee well across the empty black
No silver rope gonna pull us back
Stars don't cry and the void don't care
Just two souls driftin' on the cold night air

When they find us in a hundred years
Frozen in the dark with no more tears
Tell 'em that we held on tight
Till the void took two friends out of sight

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
One onion on the cutting board, the kettle hums alone
I measure half the rice I poured for fifty years at home
Your apron's on the same brass hook, it smells of thyme and you
I'm learning every page that took you all those quiet evenings through

Table set for one tonight, candle flickering low
I plate it like you would have liked, then I let the silence go
Salt it slow, taste it twice, your voice still in my hands
Table set for one tonight, oh, I'm starting to understand

The burner clicks, the butter foams, I almost call your name
A single egg, a single spoon, the kitchen's not the same
I burned the toast on Tuesday and I laughed until I cried
Then ate it black and bittersweet with you here at my side

They say a man can't feed himself once half of him is gone
But I keep the radio low and let the saxophone play on
Thirty years you fed me, now I finally learn the art
Of simmering a little soup to warm a stubborn heart

Table set for one tonight, two cups, I only fill the one
But the kettle sings the way it did, and somehow we go on
Salt it slow, taste it twice, the steam fogs up the glass
Table set for one tonight, and I'm letting the old grief pass

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Grandmother walks the shoreline where the silver foam unrolls
She's older than the lighthouse and she knows the ocean's soul
She taps her cane upon the sand and whispers to the swell
Come in, come in, my restless one, I've got a thing to tell

Shine, shine, roll with the moon
She's teaching the tide a brand new tune
In and out, the steady glow
The elder leads and the waters know
Dance, dance, all through the night
The sea learns rhythm from the old one's light

She counts the herons one by one, she names each falling star
She tells the water where it's been and how it's come this far
The young waves crash and tumble, all impatient to be free
But wisdom hums a slower beat across the velvet sea

Every century she's stood right here in salt and silver hair
The currents come to learn from her the patience of the air
Don't rush the shore, my darling foam, don't break before your time
The moon will pull you back again in perfect, glowing rhyme

Shine, shine, roll with the moon
She's teaching the tide a brand new tune
In and out, the steady glow
The elder leads and the waters know

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Four seconds on the clock, sweat dripping off my brow
Nobody picked us, baby, but they're all watching now
We came up from the bottom with our jerseys stitched by hand
No cameras, no contracts, just a gym and a stand

This is our night, this is our floor
Everything we bled for is what we're playing for
Down by one, hearts on fire
Watch the underdog rise a little higher
This is our night, can't hold us down
We're bringing it home to a no-name town

Mama in the bleachers with her hands clasped tight in prayer
Coach who never quit on us is pacing over there
They wrote us off in autumn, said we'd never see the spring
But here we stand in April, and we're built for everything

If we fall, we fall together, arm in arm we'll walk away
But something in my bones tonight says we were born for today
Pass it, cut it, set the screen, let the buzzer be our song
We were always good enough, the world just took too long

This is our night, this is our floor
Everything we bled for is what we're playing for
Shot's in the air, the whole crowd stalls
Underdogs forever as the moment falls

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They called our numbers, drew them blind, and put us palm to palm
The one I swore I'd never touch, now breathing through my calm
The spotlight hits, the strings come up, there's nowhere left to hide
My enemy, my mirror, stepping closer to my side

Spin me, you traitor, under the glittering ball
We hate it, we hate it, but we can't stop at all
Step for step and toe to toe, the floor won't let us part
Two rivals on the dance floor with one furious heart

You move and I must answer, every grudge becomes a turn
The years of cold and bitterness are burning where we burn
The crowd can't tell our war apart from passion's perfect glow
And God forgive me, somewhere here, I'm losing all my no

Your hand against my spine like fire, my breath caught in your name
We spent so long as opposites we never knew we're flame
The music doesn't care who's right, the music only sways
And I have never felt so seen as in your blazing gaze

Spin me, you traitor, under the glittering ball
We hate it, we hate it, but we can't stop at all
Dip me down, the lights go gold, the whole room starts to spark
Two rivals on the dance floor with one furious heart

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up the crates at dawn, the captain wouldn't look
The manifest was burned to ash, no name was in the book
The parrot wouldn't sing no more, the rats jumped overboard
And something in the cargo hold was knocking on the floorboard

Pick it up, pick it up, blow the horns and run
This cursed little cargo's gonna sink us one by one
Skank to the left, skank to the right, pray the morning comes
We're sailing with a devil and a deck of empty drums

The compass spun like a carousel, the stars all changed their place
A cold wind whistled through the ropes and laughed up in my face
The cook went pale on Thursday, said the stew had stared right back
We're forty days from any port and ten miles off the track

Throw it in the water, boys! No, the water threw it back!
The waves spit out the cursed crate and set us on the rack
We'll dance until the trumpets break, we'll horn until we drown
This cargo's got a heartbeat and it won't let us go down

Pick it up, pick it up, blow the horns and run
This cursed little cargo's gonna sink us one by one
Skank to the left, skank to the right, pray the morning comes
We're sailing with a devil and a deck of empty drums

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slide shut so softly, but my chest forgets to breathe
The tunnel lights go streaming and I grip the silver seat
A hundred faces blur to fog, the rails begin to hum
My heart's a frightened drummer playing faster than the drum

Breathe, slow, count the lights
Hold the rail and ride it through the night
In through the nose and out through the fear
The station's coming, almost here
