The map above is melting, all the colored lines run wild
I'm thirty years of grown-up turning back into a child
The woman reading quietly has no idea I'm falling
Through a floor that isn't there to somewhere far and small

But the wheels keep their soft samba on the curving underground
And I borrow back my rhythm from that gentle rocking sound
Four more stops, just four more stops, the air will find my lungs
I'll step into the daylight where the panic comes undone

Breathe, slow, count the lights
Hold the rail and ride it through the night
In through the nose and out through the fear
The station's coming, I'm still here

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
There's a lantern by the highway where the weary always come
A kettle and a counter and a clock that's never done
The rain can lash the windows and the cold can stalk the door
But the griddle's always glowing on that worn and friendly floor

Keep the light on, keep the coffee warm
There's a roof for every wanderer caught out in the storm
Fiddle up the morning, hold the dark at bay
The little diner never sleeps till night has bled to day

The trucker and the widow and the boy who lost his way
They gather round the booths like ghosts who've nowhere left to stay
Maggie pours the chowder and she calls them all by name
And no one who walks through that door is ever quite the same

For here the lonely hours grow a little less unkind
And a stranger sharing toast with you can ease a troubled mind
The stars wheel slow above the hills, the kettle starts to sing
And somewhere past the midnight comes the comfort morning brings

Keep the light on, keep the coffee warm
There's a roof for every wanderer caught out in the storm
Fiddle up the morning, hold the dark at bay
The little diner never sleeps till night has bled to day

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors slam shut, no air, no out
Walls closing in, I want to shout
Heart's a hammer breaking ribs
The lights strobe white, the whole car tips

CAN'T BREATHE, CAN'T BREATHE on the underground line
Everybody's normal and I'm losing my mind
GRIP THE RAIL, GRIP THE RAIL, swallow down the scream
Trapped inside a body that's coming apart at the seams

Sweat cold, hands shake, vision narrow
A freight train fear shot like an arrow
Nobody sees me dying here
A silent war, a screaming gear

Count the bolts, count the floor, count the seconds down
Three more stops till the doors give back the open ground
IT'S NOT REAL, IT'S NOT REAL, it's chemicals and dread
The monster's in the wiring of my overloaded head

CAN'T BREATHE, CAN'T BREATHE on the underground line
Everybody's normal and I'm losing my mind
STATION LIGHT, STATION LIGHT, the doors are opening wide
I crawl into the daylight and I'm finally alive

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Empty chair across the table
Half a meal I'm barely able
Knife shakes hard against the board
Forty years and now ignored

ONE PLATE, ONE FORK, one ruined goddamn night
Learning how to feed the grief and keep the burner lit
YOUR RECIPE, YOUR HANDS, I copy what I can
Becoming in this kitchen a completely broken man

Burned it black, threw it out
Screamed your name, no one about
Rice for two still in my head
I cook for ghosts, I feed the dead

But I will not starve in your honor, I will learn to stand
I'll chop the onion through the tears with my unsteady hand
ONE MORE DAY, ONE MORE MEAL, I drag myself to live
The smallest act of staying is the most that I can give

ONE PLATE, ONE FORK, one stubborn beating heart
Learning how to live again though every cell's apart
YOUR RECIPE, YOUR HANDS, I'll carry what you knew
Each quiet supper that I make, I'm still in love with you

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
I sat down at the table with a dollar and a grin
The devil dealt the bottom and I let temptation in
One hand turned to twenty, then I bet the family land
Lord, I gambled all the morning light away with one weak hand

Have mercy, have mercy, I went too far
I laid down all my blessings for a wicked, losing card
Reach down, reach down, pull me from the table
Lift this sinner up to grace, Lord, if you are able

The whiskey told me lies and the dealer wore a smile
I bet away my Sunday and I bet away my child
The chips were stacked like tombstones, every one a thing I'd lost
And I never stopped to reckon with the soul-deep awful cost

But the church bell rang at midnight and it cut me to the bone
I threw my cards upon the floor and stumbled toward my home
Oh I fell down on my knees right there and begged the morning sky
To wash the gambler out of me before the well runs dry

Have mercy, have mercy, I went too far
I laid down all my blessings for a wicked, losing card
Reach down, reach down, pull me from the table
Lift this sinner up to grace, Lord, I know that you are able

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
The cookbook's got your handwriting in the margins, faded blue
A little note that says add more, the way you always knew
I stand here in the kitchen light at quarter after nine
Reheating half a casserole that used to be ours, now mine

And I'm learning how to halve it, every recipe of two
Measuring the quiet out in cups I never knew
It tastes a little wrong tonight, it tastes a little new
I'm cooking for the one of me that's left here loving you

I bought a smaller saucepan and it felt like giving up
The single egg, the single bowl, the lonely coffee cup
But Tuesday I made soup from scratch and didn't burn the pot
And sat down at the table with the small life that I've got

They say grief lives in the ordinary, in the things you do alone
The folding of one napkin, the soft hum of the phone
But somewhere between the chopping and the simmer and the steam
I'm finding I can hold the ache and still go on, it seems

And I'm learning how to halve it, every recipe of two
Measuring the quiet out in cups I never knew
It tastes a little wrong tonight, it tastes a little true
I'm cooking for the one of me that's left here loving you

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Knuckles carved from eighty winters
Skin like paper, will like timber
Those hands that pulled a family through
War and hunger, breaking new

HER HANDS, HER HANDS built everything I am
Scrubbed the floors and held the line, the strongest in the land
HER HANDS, HER HANDS, I kiss them now they're still
Everything I'll ever be was forged inside her will

Sewed our clothes from nothing, fed us
Fist to God when no one led us
Those veins like roots beneath the ground
The quietest power I ever found

They shook the last time that I held them, light as autumn leaves
But I felt a hundred years of fight inside those fragile sleeves
DON'T YOU TELL ME, DON'T YOU TELL ME weakness wears a dress
The toughest soul I ever knew wore an apron and a cross

HER HANDS, HER HANDS built everything I am
Scrubbed the floors and held the line, the strongest in the land
HER HANDS, HER HANDS, I carry on the fight
Her calloused holy fingerprints are pressed into my life

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
Diez años sin tu voz, ten years of empty space
I hold the phone like a coward, your number on the case
My thumb hangs over the calling and my eyes begin to burn
Madre, I left like a fool, and now I'm aching to return

Ay, contesta, mamá, before the silence wins
Forgive me all my running and let me in again
The trumpets cry my sorrow, the guitar weeps in my chest
I'm calling home, I'm calling home, to the one who loved me best

I thought my pride was armor but it only made me cold
The years went by like a river and I watched myself grow old
The ringtone sounds like heartbeats, one, two, three, then a sigh
And then I hear you whisper my name and I break down and cry

Mija, mijo, you came back, no anger in her tone
Just the warmth of a thousand suppers and a love that I'd outgrown
The shame falls off my shoulders like a coat I never need
And the boy I was comes running home across the years to feed

Ay, contesta, mamá, the silence finally breaks
Forgive me all my running for my foolish young mistakes
The trumpets cry my sorrow, the guitar sings me to rest
I called you home, I called you home, to the one who loved me best

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights of Nashville flickered like a promise in the dark
I had a guitar on my back and fire inside my heart
They called my name at midnight on a stage I'd dreamed for years
And for one shining holy hour the whole world cleared

Oh I almost, almost made it, had it right there in my hand
The crowd was on their feet and I was finally the man
Then the morning came too early and the offer fell apart
But I'll never lose the memory of that night burned in my heart

The man in the expensive suit said son you've got the spark
We'll cut the record Monday, you'll be lighting up the charts
I called my mama crying, said your boy is gonna fly
Then the label folded Sunday and I watched the dream go by

Some folks never get the mountaintop, they live down in the low
But I stood up there one evening where the lucky get to go
And maybe that's enough to hold when these old hands turn gray
I was almost, almost somebody, for one unforgotten day

Oh I almost, almost made it, had it right there in my hand
The crowd was on their feet and I was finally the man
The dream slipped through my fingers but it left this much for me
The night I touched the edge of all I ever hoped to be

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Met you on the boardwalk where the neon kissed the sea
Salt was in your laughter and your eyes were on me
We ran into the ocean with our sneakers in our hands
Writing names we'd soon forget across the golden sand

Just a summer, just a fling, just a heartbeat in the sun
Dancing till the streetlights and the morning's almost come
We don't talk about September, we don't talk about goodbye
Just your hand and my hand and the fireworks in the sky

Ice cream melting faster than the promises we made
Radio kept playing as the daylight slowly frayed
You kissed me on the ferris wheel a hundred feet up high
And I knew this wouldn't last but baby, did I even try

Maybe in the autumn we'll be strangers once again
Different towns, different lives, just a postcard now and then
But for now the waves are warm and your shoulder's on my own
And I'd trade a hundred winters for this summer that we've grown

Just a summer, just a fling, just a heartbeat in the sun
Dancing till the streetlights and the morning's almost come
We don't talk about September, we don't talk about goodbye
Just your hand and my hand and the fireworks in the sky

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle in the lamplight, thread between my teeth
I been measuring shoulders since I learned to breathe
Mr. Carter's Sunday suit, the kid's first prom
I stitch a little dignity, I keep the whole block calm

Chalk line on the fabric, soft hum on the radio
Every seam a promise that I'm never gonna let go
Widows in their velvet, grooms in muted gray
I cut the cloth of memory and give the block a way

Oh, I dress the whole block, button up the night
Turn a tired Tuesday into something bright
From the corner to the corner, I'ma make it fit
Threaded love, threaded love, you can wear all of it

Press the iron easy, steam against my palm
Lay a little armor on the folks who came up wrong
They walk a little taller when the hemline lands just right
And I close up shop and thank the needle and the night

What I sew ain't only cotton, it's the way you feel inside
A second skin of tenderness, a place where you can hide
So bring me all your broken, bring me all your worn
I'll mend you something beautiful before the morning's born

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent lights are flashing on the downtown line
My heartbeat's doing double-time, it's racing out of time
The tunnel keeps on roaring and the walls start to close
I'm holding to the handrail while the panic overflows

Breathe, oh, just breathe in the strobe
Don't let the rhythm shake you, don't let it take control
Dance it out, dance it out, till the doors slide wide
The city's gonna catch you on the other side

Strangers in the mirror windows, faces blurring past
I'm counting every station, praying this one is the last
My chest is a disco ball, every shard reflecting fear
But the beat keeps on insisting that the morning's almost here

Breathe, oh, just breathe in the strobe
Don't let the rhythm shake you, don't let it take control
Dance it out, dance it out, till the doors slide wide
The city's gonna catch you on the other side

Four, three, two, the platform's coming near
I feel the rush release me, I feel the air get clear
Step into the daylight, leave the trembling underground
My feet remember how to move to a steady, golden sound

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Salt in the wound, a suitcase made of bone
Traded every street I knew for a horizon all alone
The coastline's screaming go, the engines start to roar
I'm burning down the bridges to a country I'm not sure

ACROSS THE SEA, across the sea
I left my name to drown so I could finally be free
No flag, no home, just a fist against the foam
I'll build a wall of nothing into something I call home

My mother's voice a static on a line that's almost dead
I carry all her warnings like a crown upon my head
The waves don't speak my language, the gulls all scream the same
A stranger in the water with a half-forgotten name

ACROSS THE SEA, across the sea
I left my name to drown so I could finally be free
No flag, no home, just a fist against the foam
I'll build a wall of nothing into something I call home

TEAR IT DOWN — the borders in my chest
TEAR IT DOWN — I won't go back to rest
Every mile of ocean is a scar I chose to wear
I'm landing on tomorrow and I'm DARING it to care

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Smoke curls slow above the keys, a quarter after two
The corner booth remembers how the lamplight fell on you
I ordered up your favorite but the chair stayed empty wide
And the bass walks down so lonely like the years I tried to hide

You're the one that got away, the chord I never played
A melody I hum at night in shades of blue and gray
If I could turn the tempo back, I'd hold you in the phrase
But you're the one that got away, the one that got away

The saxophone's confessing what my pride could never say
That I let the sweetest thing I knew just softly drift away
Now every rainy avenue still whispers of your name
And the city plays our standard but it never sounds the same

Maybe in another set, another smoky room
We'd find the bridge we lost somewhere between the dusk and gloom
But the band is packing up and the lights are coming on
And I'm left here with the silence of a love that's come and gone

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Luces en el cielo, I'm driving toward the gray
The thunder is my novia and she never wants to stay
I chase the spinning funnel where the danger meets the rain
Can't settle in your arms, mami, I'm married to the strain

Dale, dale, the storm is calling me
I run into the wind where no quiet man would be
You want a house, a ring, a calmer kind of love
But I belong out there where the lightning lives above

Your pictures on the dashboard, your voice inside the phone
But every quiet evening feels too much like a stone
The pressure drops, my heart jumps, the radar starts to glow
And I'm gone before the sunrise to the places thunder goes

Dale, dale, the storm is calling me
I run into the wind where no quiet man would be
You want a house, a ring, a calmer kind of love
But I belong out there where the lightning lives above

One day the sky'll go silent and I'll have nowhere to run
No funnel on the highway, no chasing down the sun
Maybe then I'll find you, if you're waiting by the door
But tonight the storm is screaming and I love it more, I love it more

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old hands in the saltwater, knuckles carved from stone
He taught me how the ocean breathes, he taught me not alone
The tide don't ask permission, the tide just comes and goes
And the wisdom in his silence is the only thing he shows

HOLD THE LINE like the elder taught
Every wave a lesson that the young ones never sought
PULL — against the current, PUSH — against the years
The tide will take your body but it can't take what he hears

He counts the moons like rosaries, he reads the moving foam
A hundred winters in his spine, the shoreline is his home
He says the sea forgives you if you learn to bend and stay
Then he laughs into the breakers and he turns and walks away

HOLD THE LINE like the elder taught
Every wave a lesson that the young ones never sought
PULL — against the current, PUSH — against the years
The tide will take your body but it can't take what he hears

WHEN HE'S GONE — the water still remembers
WHEN HE'S GONE — I'll feed the dying embers
I'll stand where he was standing and I'll teach another soul
The tide goes out forever but the lesson makes us whole

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine idling soft beneath a flickering streetlight glow
The meter of my heartbeat keeps a rhythm soft and low
One more run, one final score, then I'm hanging up the keys
The city hums a minor chord on this warm and reckless breeze

Just one more getaway, baby, then I'm gone
Foot upon the velvet pedal, gliding into dawn
The horns are blowing midnight as the sirens start to wail
My last job, my last solo, riding down the rail

Three minutes on the clock and the brass is climbing high
Smoke rings in the rearview, neon bleeding through the sky
They climb in slick and breathless, I just touch the gas and sway
A cool and quiet getaway in a slow, illegal way

If this is how it ends, let the saxophone decide
A fugitive in four-four time, the night my only guide
The road unspools like ribbon and the bassline lets me fly
One last beautiful escape underneath a velvet sky

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
I been running these dark streets since I was seventeen
Wheel man for the desperate, the meanest you've ever seen
But my baby's home and waiting and she's praying I come clean
So this is my last ride, Lord, the final getaway scene

One more time, one more time around
Then I'm laying down these keys, gonna plant my feet on solid ground
Drive me home, sweet mercy, let the engine sing my prayer
One last getaway, baby, then I swear I'll meet you there

The money's in the duffel, the boys are climbing in
My hands are shaking gospel for the man I've never been
Every red light a temptation, every green a second chance
I'm dancing with the devil in one final desperate dance

Whoa, I can see her face
In the windshield, in the headlights, in the warm and holy space
If I make it through this midnight I will never run again
Lay me down in her forgiveness, let redemption finally begin

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
Canto bajo tu ventana with a heart that's torn in two
The trumpets cry my sorrow for a love that won't come true
I bring you all my roses but you only see the thorns
And my serenade keeps playing till the cold and lonely morn

Ay, ay, ay, you'll never love me so
But I'll sing beneath the moonlight till the violins let go
Mi corazón is bleeding in this old and golden tune
Unrequited, unrequited, underneath the silver moon

You dance with someone else now while I strum this broken string
The guitarrón is weeping every sad and stubborn thing
I'd cross a thousand deserts just to kiss your distant name
But you smile and turn away and leave me burning in the flame

The mariachis gather close, they know my tired song
They've played it for a hundred fools who loved someone too long
So raise the trumpets one more time and let the whole world hear
The ballad of a foolish heart that loves and disappears

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
I was nineteen, knees a-knocking, in a barroom thick with smoke
Held that secondhand guitar like it might be the last hope I'd ever stroke
Three chords and a trembling voice, a crowd that didn't care
But something caught me by the collar and it pinned me standing there

First open mic, oh, first open mic
My hands were shaking but my soul was finally taking flight
They didn't clap too loud that night, Lord, they barely turned their heads
But I found the only church I'd need on that little wooden stage instead

Thirty years and crooked fingers, I can play it in my sleep
But I'd trade these worn-out calluses for that fear so raw and deep
The terror and the wonder when you don't know how it ends
When the song's the only friend you got and the silence is your friend

That boy is still inside me with his cheap and broken strings
Still chasing down the feeling that a first time always brings
So I close my eyes on this old stool and I'm back there once again
Nineteen and afraid and free, before I knew just where I'd been

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Soft the morning on the harbor, gentle as a sigh
I'm walking off the gangway underneath a healing sky
The waves are humming slowly what my heart can't quite believe
That the guns have finally quieted and I'm finally free to leave

Home, sweet and slow, the bossa carries me
Down the sunlit avenue, beside the whispering sea
Lay your head upon my shoulder, let the soft guitar repair
All the years I left behind me in the smoke and in the air

The café still is open and the coffee's warm and brown
The children chase the pigeons through my quiet seaside town
I carry ghosts inside me but the breeze begins to ease
The weight of all that thunder with a melody of peace

Maybe I'll forget in time the things I can't unsee
The sand, the sound, the silence where my brothers used to be
But for now I'll sip the morning and I'll let the rhythm sway
And thank the gentle ocean that it brought me home today

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The heather's gone to amber on the hill above the bay
And we lie beneath the fading sun on the last warm August day
Tomorrow brings the highway and a town I've never known
But tonight we've got the fireflies and a field to call our own

Oh, sing me one more summer, before the morning calls
Before the leaves go tumbling and the early autumn falls
We'll dance the reel of childhood till the stars begin to fade
The last day of the summer, the last we'll ever wade

My mother packed the photographs, my father holds his tongue
There's a fiddle on the hillside and a song that's barely sung
We carved our names in driftwood where the river meets the stone
And promised through the changing tide we'd never be alone

The whistle and the bodhrán play a slow and aching air
For the boys and girls of summer who are scattering to the air
So hold my hand a moment more before the dawn comes gray
And we step into the rest of life on the last warm August day

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Well I greased up the old Ford and I checked the rolling wheels
Got the boys all loaded heavy and you know just how it feels
One more run down the holler, then I'm hanging up my crime
Gonna marry sweet Loretta if I make it out this time

Run, boys, run, it's my final getaway
Banjo rolling faster than the law can drive today
One last job, one last holler, then I'm done with all the sin
Lord, just let this old jalopy bring me home to her again

The fiddle's sawing wildly as we tear up Highway Nine
The sheriff's on our bumper but the moon is doing fine
My daddy was a runner and his daddy was the same
But I'm the last one driving and I'm done with this here game

Throw the money in the river, throw the pistol in the creek
I'm tired of always running, tired of living hide-and-seek
So pick that banjo, brother, play me one redeeming sound
My last job is behind me and I'm finally homeward bound

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Yeah, late nights, I'm scrolling through the photos that we made
Shoulda held you closer but I chose the chains and chased the blade
Now the ice don't keep me warm and the bottles taste like pain
You the one that got away, girl, I can't do it again

The one that got away, ayy, the one that got away
I had the world but lost the only thing I shoulda saved
Text you at 3 a.m. but I know you never stay
You the one that got away, ayy, the one that got away

Used to ride beside me when a young boy had a dream
Now the diamonds and the foreign cars don't mean a single thing
I traded all your love for a number and a name
Now I'm rich up in this penthouse but I'm drowning in the shame

Money can't rewind it, no, the time don't come for free
I'd give back every dollar just to have you here with me
Lights low, heart cold, whispering your name
You the one that got away and I'm the only one to blame

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
The rain came down on Main Street like it had a debt to pay
And you stepped beneath my umbrella in that ordinary way
We didn't trade our names at first, just shoulders and a smile
Two strangers in a downpour gonna walk a little while

Under one umbrella, just you and the rain and me
Two roads that crossed by accident, the way they're meant to be
We don't know where we're going but the storm don't seem to care
Under one umbrella we'll go anywhere from there

You told me 'bout your mama and the town you left behind
I told you 'bout the highway and the peace I couldn't find
The gutters ran like rivers and the streetlights bled to gold
And somewhere in the middle of it the night stopped feeling cold

Maybe when the rain lets up we'll go our separate ways
Or maybe this is something that's gonna last us all our days
But for now there's just the drumming and the warm and crowded air
Two strangers and one umbrella and a love we didn't plan to share

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up in the attic where the antenna sways
I bleed a frequency into the haze
Nobody licensed this, nobody knows
just the static breathing where the signal goes

I press the needle to a borrowed song
whisper my call sign, hope you sing along
the walls are humming and the dial is warm
broadcasting low beneath the coming storm

Find me in the white noise, find me in the gray
I'm a ghost on the wire that won't fade away
turn it up til the feedback drowns the room
I'm yours til the dawn comes and swallows the tune

They sent a van to triangulate my heart
but I dissolve before they get a part
the reverb swallows where I used to be
just a wash of wanting on a stolen sea

If they pull the plug I'll still be in your ears
a soft collapsing wall of all these years
don't tune away, don't tune away from me
I'm the static teaching you how to be free

Find me in the white noise, find me in the gray
I'm a ghost on the wire that won't fade away

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Midnight blue and the transmitter glows
a pastel hum where the city slows
I float my voice through the velvet air
for anyone awake and anyone there

No permission, just a hidden light
a little heartbeat in the satellite night
I spin the records that nobody plays
and watch the meter shimmer through the haze

This is your station of the soft and the lost
drifting on a signal that we never paid the cost
close your eyes and let the melody stay
I'll keep the channel open til the gray of the day

Somewhere a stranger turns the dial and sighs
finds my little world between the lullabies
we never met but we are not alone
floating on a frequency we call our own

They can chase the tower, they can cut the line
but the song lives soft in the back of the mind
so dream with me, dream with me, slow
in the dark where the secret frequencies go

This is your station of the soft and the lost
drifting on a signal that we never paid the cost

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Same old corner, same old bus stop sign
but you walked up and the whole town shined
I knew your brother, I knew your street
never knew my heart could skip a beat

We grew up six blocks from each other's door
walked the same halls, hated the same store
funny how it took me all these years
to see the girl behind the souvenirs

Hometown heart, you knock me off my feet
pick it up, pick it up on Main Street
I've been around the block a thousand times
but you're the only reason left to stay behind

We drove the loop where we used to be bored
past the diner that we both adored
now every landmark's wearing your name
this tired old town will never look the same

Maybe I left and maybe I came back
following a feeling down a familiar track
so skank with me underneath the water tower
falling for you in our one-stoplight hour

Hometown heart, you knock me off my feet
pick it up, pick it up on Main Street

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Old man Reuben on the corner stone
five bucks a game and he plays alone
but every move is a sermon sung
the King is patient and the night is young

He took my money and he took my pride
then he leaned in close with his hands open wide
said son, the board is just the way I pray
watch how grace can come and take it all away

Oh, checkmate, hallelujah on the square
the Bishop's moving and the Lord is there
lay your kings down, lay your money too
the hustler in the park gonna see you through

Pigeons gathered like a hungry choir
as he set 'em up with a holy fire
he never lost and he never gloat
just hummed a hymn with a gravel throat

Every pawn's a soul that's gotta climb
promoted up at the end of time
so play me, brother, play me clean
there's mercy in the moves that you ain't seen

Oh, checkmate, hallelujah on the square
the Bishop's moving and the Lord is there

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
There's a two-lane highway out past the mill
where we used to park when the world was still
you had a suitcase and a head full of west
I had a ring I never got to confess

Now I drive that road when the harvest fades
counting all the promises that summer made
you wanted cities, I wanted the farm
so I let you slip right out of my arms

You're the one that got away
the taillights I still see at the break of day
I married steady, I married kind
but you're the song that's always in the back of my mind

They say there's somebody out in Santa Fe
who hums your laugh in a softer way
I hope he's gentle, I hope he stays
I hope he never lets the feeling fray

If I could fold the map and make it touch
the place I left and the one I loved too much
I'd tell you sorry, I'd tell you stay
but the wind just carries those words away

You're the one that got away
the taillights I still see at the break of day

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Downtown deluge, I'm caught in the gray
broken forecast turned my whole day sideways
then a stranger steps up with a black umbrella
said which way you headed, I'll walk you, fella
We move in sync 'cause the rain don't quit
elbows touching, neither of us wanna split
she talk about her sister, I talk about my job
two umbrellas worth of life under one little knob

One umbrella, two strangers in the storm
shoulder to shoulder just to stay warm
the city's crying but we found a dry inch
strangers for a minute, friends in a pinch

We hit her corner and the rain start to fade
funny how a downpour's the best plan I made
she say good luck, I say same to you
never got her name but I felt it come through
Sometimes the universe just hands you a sign
folded up small in a six-foot line

Never saw her again but I think of her still
that random little warmth on a cold uphill
life's just strangers passing under the spray
holding up the sky for each other a day

One umbrella, two strangers in the storm
shoulder to shoulder just to stay warm

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights were low and the room held its breath
you were one step from outrunning death
the deal half-signed and the door ajar
so close to becoming the thing you are

A single phone call, a single name
and the whole of your fortune would change its game
you wore your best and you played it cool
you danced like a king, not a desperate fool

Almost, almost, the night you almost won
the music swelled and then it came undone
one beat too early, one card too late
you kissed the edge of a beautiful fate

The bandoneon wept as the moment slid
past every hope that your trembling hid
the partner you held simply turned away
and the floor went cold in the break of day

But oh, how you moved when the world was near
one perfect night with no room for fear
and even in losing you blazed so bright
that the dark still remembers your name tonight

Almost, almost, the night you almost won
the music swelled and then it came undone

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Empty parking lot on a Sunday morning
you in the seat with no warning
hands at ten and two, baby breathe slow
this is the day that I gotta let go

You stalled it twice and you laughed so free
looked over scared like you needed me
I said ease off the brake, let it roll, let it glide
funny how my heart's the thing that won't ride

Go on and drive, baby, drive
I'll be right here, you'll be alright
keep your eyes on the road, keep your soul on the light
I taught you to walk, now I'm teaching you flight

You found the gas and you found your grace
a little smile spreading over your face
I remember the day that you couldn't reach the sink
now you're merging on the highway quicker than I think

Every mile you take is a mile from me
but that's the whole point of being free
so check your mirrors and trust your hands
I'll be the home where the journey lands

Go on and drive, baby, drive
I'll be right here, you'll be alright

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning and the tubes are warm
I'm spinning bebop through the velvet storm
no license, baby, just a borrowed band
and a microphone tremblin' in my hand

The G-men cruising with their dials wide
but I'm a phantom on the lower side
I cut to a solo, I let it ride
while the city sleeps and the rules subside

This is your midnight, your outlaw sound
broadcasting low where the lost are found
pour a little whiskey, lean in real near
the pirate's on the air and the coast is clear

A saxophone moans through the static blue
a love letter mailed to the likes of you
I don't know your name and you don't know mine
but we meet every night on this contraband line

They can trace the tower, they can kill the light
but the swing lives on in the bones of the night
so snap your fingers and forget the law
the sweetest sound is the sound they never saw

This is your midnight, your outlaw sound
broadcasting low where the lost are found

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
The rain came soft on the harbor town
when the little gray screen brought my whole world down
no candle lit and no parting song
just a handful of words said I'd done you wrong

We crossed the moors and we swore the oath
by the standing stones we promised both
but the modern wind blew cold and thin
and a buzzing pocket let the ending in

Oh, the fiddle plays for the love that's gone
faded on a screen at the break of dawn
no final dance and no whispered name
just a cold blue light and a flickering flame

I walked the cliffs where we used to roam
reading seven words on the long way home
you could've come and said it to my face
but you left me a ghost in an empty space

Let the gulls cry out what I cannot say
let the tide take the message far away
for a thousand years lovers grieved in song
now I grieve to a phone that buzzed along

Oh, the fiddle plays for the love that's gone
faded on a screen at the break of dawn

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slammed shut and the lights went bright
too many faces in the underground night
my collar's tight and the air gets thin
the walls lean close and the room caves in

My heart's a snare drum gone too fast
counting all the stations till the feeling's passed
I grip the rail and I try to swing
but my chest's a cage and I can't find the spring

Breathe two-three-four, let it sway, let it go
the train keeps movin' even when you're slow
find the rhythm, baby, find the beat
you'll be back on solid feet down the street

A stranger smiled and she tapped her toe
hummed a little tune that I used to know
the panic loosened like a knot come free
and the rattle of the rails was a melody

So when the dark comes down and it grabs your spine
just let the wheels keep time, keep time
you're not alone in the rush and the roar
there's a downbeat waiting at the open door

Breathe two-three-four, let it sway, let it go
the train keeps movin' even when you're slow

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine idling in the empty blue
the city humming low, the dawn near due
this is the last one, this is the end
no more running for me, my friend

The meter glows and the radio hush
I count the seconds in the pre-dawn rush
three minutes, two, and the door swings wide
then we vanish in the morning tide

Drive, just drive, into the fading gray
let the long road wash the years away
one last engine in the quiet light
then I disappear into the night

No siren yet, just the tires' long sigh
the streetlamps dimming in a violet sky
I always swore that the next would be mine
the one clean exit past the city line

Somewhere a house with an open door
a life that doesn't need to run anymore
so ease off the gas, let the heartbeat slow
this is the last place I'll ever go

Drive, just drive, into the fading gray
let the long road wash the years away

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning, mile marker glow
phone to my ear on the long lonely road
baby pick up, I just wanna hear
the sound of my home when the highway's near

Diesel and starlight, the rumble and hum
two thousand miles til the morning come
tell the little ones daddy's on his way
bringing back the sun at the end of May

Hello, hello, can you hear me now
I'm calling you home, I'm making a vow
the road is long but my heart is near
three a.m. love and the line is clear

You whisper soft so the kids stay asleep
tell me 'bout the dreams that you're trying to keep
I press the gas and I feel you close
on this endless ribbon where the cold wind blows

Miles between us but we move as one
me and the moon and the rising sun
so keep the porch light on for me
I'm driving home to my family

Hello, hello, can you hear me now
I'm calling you home, I'm making a vow

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Studio cold, three a.m. on the clock
label on the phone, finger frozen on the lock
they said sign here, kid, you about to blow
one signature away from the life I know
beat was knockin', whole room held its breath
so close to the top I could taste the success
but the deal flipped sour when the lawyer read it twice
fine print colder than a January ice

Almost made it, almost touched the sky
so close to the dream I could see it fly
one more move, one more roll of the dice
the night I almost paid the price

Walked out the building with my head still high
empty hands but a fire in my eye
'cause almost ain't never, almost ain't the end
I'll be back at the door, I'll knock again
they'll remember the night that they let me walk
turn the almost-story to the talk of the block

Close don't count but it count to me
it showed me exactly who I'm tryna be
so I bottle up the ache and I load it in the chamber
next time I make it, no almost, no stranger

Almost made it, almost touched the sky
so close to the dream I could see it fly

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Door stopped slamming and the words ran dry
just the kitchen clock and a heavy sigh
you on the couch and me on the stair
a whole lotta silence in the in-between air

We said the things that we can't unsay
now the room's too loud in a quiet way
I hear you breathing, I know you're near
but the foot of space feels a hundred years

In the quiet, oh the quiet after
where's the upbeat, where's the laughter
pick it up, pick it up, mend the seam
come back to me in the in-between

Then your hand creeps over and it finds my own
and the silence cracks like a thawing stone
no trumpet blaring, just a soft offbeat
of two stubborn hearts learning to retreat

We don't need the horns to know it's true
the loudest love is the making-up too
so skank it slow in the cooling night
we fought in the dark but we'll dance to the light

In the quiet, oh the quiet after
where's the upbeat, where's the laughter

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I carried your shadow long after you left this earth
A stone in my chest, weighing more than it was worth
The words I never said now they crowd up in my throat
Like letters I keep writing to an address with no road

I forgive you, I forgive you, though you cannot hear the sound
Let the river take the anger, let it lay the burden down
I forgive you, I forgive you, and the morning sun comes round
What I free up in the heavens, I am freeing in the ground

They say the dead don't listen but my heart it disagrees
I feel you in the trade wind moving easy through the trees
The debt between us settled, no more ledger left to keep
I'm done with hauling boulders, I am ready now to sleep

I forgive you, I forgive you, though you cannot hear the sound
Let the river take the anger, let it lay the burden down
I forgive you, I forgive you, and the morning sun comes round
What I free up in the heavens, I am freeing in the ground

Maybe forgiveness was never for you at all
Maybe it's the rope I cut to stop my own free fall
So I light a little candle and I let the smoke go free
I forgive you, I forgive you, mostly I forgive me

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The kettle's gone cold on the stove again
Two cups on the counter, neither one has been
The clock on the wall says a quarter past ten
And the loudest thing here is the not-talking-then

You're folding the laundry too carefully now
I'm reading a page that I'm not reading, somehow
We traded our thunder for this heavy hush
Where even the floorboards are scared to be touched

Oh the quiet, the quiet, it's worse than the storm
It's a house full of distance and a bed gone warm
I'd take back the words if the words would come round
But the hardest of fighting is making no sound

Your shoulder's a country I used to call home
Now there's a border I'm crossing alone
I rehearse my apology under my breath
And swallow it down with the rest of the dread

Oh the quiet, the quiet, it's worse than the storm
It's a house full of distance and a bed gone warm
I'd take back the words if the words would come round
But the hardest of fighting is making no sound

Then you reach for my hand in the dark like before
No speech, no surrender, just skin and no war
And the quiet turns gentle, the kind that we keep
And we hold the whole silence and finally sleep

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Three chords and a borrowed van, gas light blinking red
Fifty kids in a basement and a price upon our heads
The demo in my pocket like a loaded little gun
We were one good break away from being someone

We almost made it, almost made it, almost touched the sky
Close enough to taste it, close enough to lie
We almost made it, almost made it, screamed it til we bled
Then the morning came and stepped on every word we said

The guy from the label never showed up to the show
We played like it mattered, like it was the only road
Sold our shirts for beer money, slept beneath the stage
Young and dumb and certain we'd be famous by some age

We almost made it, almost made it, almost touched the sky
Close enough to taste it, close enough to lie
We almost made it, almost made it, screamed it til we bled
Then the morning came and stepped on every word we said

And I'd do it all again, every dead-end town
Every flat tire highway, every letdown
'Cause almost is a country and we lived there for a while
And nobody can take that one electric mile

We almost made it, almost made it, almost touched the sky
Close enough to taste it, close enough to lie
We almost made it, almost made it, screamed it til we bled
Still I hear those fifty voices every night inside my head

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Onion and schmaltz on a Friday at dusk
The windows all sweating, the bread turning brown
Her hands knew the dough like they knew an old song
And the whole crooked kitchen would tremble and sound

Oh the cinnamon, the cinnamon, the steam on the glass
The garlic and the honey of the years that have passed
I close up my eyes and I'm five years again
In the warm of her kitchen where the world couldn't end

The clarinet of the kettle, it whistled and cried
While she hummed in a language half-Yiddish, half-tears
A pinch of this, a fistful of that, never measured
Just the recipe written in eighty-odd years

Oh the cinnamon, the cinnamon, the steam on the glass
The garlic and the honey of the years that have passed
I close up my eyes and I'm five years again
In the warm of her kitchen where the world couldn't end

Now the apron is folded, the stove has gone cold
But I strike up a match and the memory unfolds
So dance, little fiddle, kick up your heels
For the dead live on in the smell of the meals

Oh the cinnamon, the cinnamon, the steam on the glass
The garlic and the honey of the years that have passed
I close up my eyes and she is humming again
In the warm of her kitchen where the love couldn't end

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Forty years she fed me, every blessed night
Now the table's half a table in the kitchen light
I'm reaching for two plates and I set one back down
Lord, teach my hands to cook for one in this too-quiet town

But You set a table even in the lonesome hour
You take the salt of weeping and You make it flour
I'll learn to feed this body though my heart cooks slow
For a man is never one when the good Lord's in the room, I know

Her recipes in pencil, her hand upon the card
I burn the rice, I cry a while, the simple is so hard
But grace it comes in measures, a teaspoon at a time
And the smell of supper rising is a kind of climbing

But You set a table even in the lonesome hour
You take the salt of weeping and You make it flour
I'll learn to feed this body though my heart cooks slow
For a man is never one when the good Lord's in the room, I know

Glory, glory, one chair filled and one chair bare
But I bow my head and bless it and I feel her sitting there
So I'll cook a little extra and I'll leave the porch light on
For the day He calls me homeward to the table where she's gone

Oh You set a table even in the lonesome hour
You take the salt of weeping and You make it flour
I'm learning, Lord, I'm learning, and the supper's almost done
And a man is never one, no, a man is never one

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon buzzing pink at a quarter to three
Vinyl booth still warm where the last soul used to be
Grill never sleeps and the coffee never quits
Where the lonesome and the restless come to gather up their wits

Pick it up, pick it up, the door's always swingin'
Jukebox in the corner got the whole night singin'
Waffles at four and the world feels alright
At the diner, at the diner that stays open all night

Cab drivers and poets and a girl who couldn't sleep
The cook knows your order and the secrets that you keep
Midnight philosophers debating over pie
While the bottomless cup keeps the morning from goodbye

Pick it up, pick it up, the door's always swingin'
Jukebox in the corner got the whole night singin'
Waffles at four and the world feels alright
At the diner, at the diner that stays open all night

When your apartment's too quiet and your phone's gone dead
When you can't face the ceiling spinning over your bed
There's a stool with your name and a light that won't dim
Come on in off the cold, brother, come on in

Pick it up, pick it up, the door's always swingin'
Jukebox in the corner got the whole night singin'
Waffles at four and the world feels alright
At the diner, at the diner that stays open all night

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The candle of the lamp burns low in my room
The town's gone to sleeping, there's a hush in the gloom
My book lies before me like a long, winding glen
And I'll walk every word of it and walk it again

At two in the morning the world is my own
Just the rain on the window and the lamp's amber glow
The whole sleeping country and me wide and awake
Learning hard in the silence for the morning to break

The kettle's my companion, the tea's gone to cold
My eyes are two embers, my courage grows old
But the page turns to gold when the meaning comes clear
Like a fiddle far off that only I can hear

At two in the morning the world is my own
Just the rain on the window and the lamp's amber glow
The whole sleeping country and me wide and awake
Learning hard in the silence for the morning to break

O the dawn it will find me with ink on my hand
And the knowledge I gathered like seed in the land
Though no soul may see it, this lonely long night
Is the root of the tree that will grow toward the light

At two in the morning the world is my own
Just the rain on the window and the lamp's amber glow
The whole sleeping country and me wide and awake
Learning hard in the silence for the morning to break

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up in the attic with a wire and a prayer
Sending out the good word on a frequency they didn't share
They said we couldn't preach here, said our signal was a sin
But the airwaves belong to the Spirit, let the message in

We're broadcasting glory from a rooftop antenna
Oh can you hear me, can you hear me, on the air
The powers tried to silence but the gospel's gonna travel
To every lonesome radio out there

Grandmama's hymnbook and a microphone that hums
We play it for the prisoner and the lost and the ones
Who turn the dial at midnight looking for a light
And we are that light flickering, holy and unlicensed tonight

We're broadcasting glory from a rooftop antenna
Oh can you hear me, can you hear me, on the air
The powers tried to silence but the gospel's gonna travel
To every lonesome radio out there

Let them trace the signal, let them knock down the door
The word's already flying where it never flew before
You can't arrest a frequency, you can't jail a song
And the choir of the air keeps rolling on

We're broadcasting glory from a rooftop antenna
Oh can you hear me, can you hear me, on the air
The powers tried to silence but the gospel's gonna travel
To every lonesome radio out there

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
The brass band gathers slow in the heavy summer air
A coffin and a second line and sorrow everywhere
The trumpet weeps a dirge as we carry him along
But underneath the weeping there's the seed of a song

So dance me to the graveside, dance me through the tears
We bury all the grieving and we celebrate the years
The drum will turn to thunder when the dirge is finally done
In New Orleans we mourn you, then we dance you to the sun

The handkerchiefs are waving and the parasols unfold
The sorrow's getting lighter as the story's being told
A stranger joins the marching, then a hundred, then a street
And grief becomes a rhythm in the shuffle of our feet

So dance me to the graveside, dance me through the tears
We bury all the grieving and we celebrate the years
The drum will turn to thunder when the dirge is finally done
In New Orleans we mourn you, then we dance you to the sun

Farewell, old friend, we'll meet you in the morning
Where the river meets the gold and there's no more mourning
But till that day arrives we will play your name out loud
And cut the heavy ribbon of this slow and shining crowd

So dance me to the graveside, dance me through the tears
We bury all the grieving and we celebrate the years
The drum will turn to thunder when the dirge is finally done
In New Orleans we mourn you, then we dance you to the sun

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
(Ooh, sha-la-la) I built a little tower out of dreams and copper wire
Up above the city where the rooftops touch the night
I spin the records nobody else will ever play
And send them out to lovers till the breaking of the day

This is my station, my pirate radio
(Bom-bom-bom) playin' for the dreamers down below
They can't shut me down, no, they can't make me go
I'm your midnight DJ on the pirate radio

(Ooh, sha-la-la) There's a girl who's listening from a window down the lane
I dedicate a slow one just to ease her lonely pain
The authorities are knocking but the music's in the air
A melody is something that you never can ensnare

This is my station, my pirate radio
(Bom-bom-bom) playin' for the dreamers down below
They can't shut me down, no, they can't make me go
I'm your midnight DJ on the pirate radio

So if you're feelin' lonesome, baby, turn your dial my way
I got a song that says the things you're too afraid to say
(Sha-la-la-la-la)

This is my station, my pirate radio
(Bom-bom-bom) playin' for the dreamers down below
They can't shut me down, no, they can't make me go
I'm your midnight DJ on the pirate radio

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Washed in the haze of a stage light gone violet and slow
The room was an ocean of faces I'd never quite know
I sang through the static, my voice a soft blur in the wall
And for one ringing moment I was bigger than it all

We almost, we almost, the word dissolves in feedback and rain
So close, so close, drowning sweetly in the reverb again
I held the whole sky in my hands for a breath and a beat
Then it fell through my fingers like the snow on the street

The tape never sold and the man never called back at all
The dream went to whisper, then to hum, then to nothing at all
But somewhere a cassette is still spinning that night into gold
A glittering blur of a story that never got told

We almost, we almost, the word dissolves in feedback and rain
So close, so close, drowning sweetly in the reverb again
I held the whole sky in my hands for a breath and a beat
Then it fell through my fingers like the snow on the street

If I close up my eyes I can stand there once more
In the wall of the sound, in the swell and the roar
Where almost was endless and the night never broke
And I'm still on that stage in the warm violet smoke

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
My daddy's hands were mountains when I was a boy so small
Now I watch him shake a little reaching for his cane in the hall
The man who carried the whole world, he leans on me to stand
Lord, it's a hard, hard sorrow watching time take a strong man's hand

Oh the years, they came like rivers, wore my mountain down to stone
Now I see my father fading and I feel so all alone
He taught me how to be a man, taught me to stand up tall
Now I'm learning how to catch him, learning how to break his fall

He used to fix the engine, used to swing that heavy door
Now he forgets my children's names and asks me twice and more
The strongest tree in all the woods, the wind's begun to lean
And I'd give my own two shoulders just to keep him like he's been

Oh the years, they came like rivers, wore my mountain down to stone
Now I see my father fading and I feel so all alone
He taught me how to be a man, taught me to stand up tall
Now I'm learning how to catch him, learning how to break his fall

So I'll sit beside him slowly in the porch-light of the day
And I'll hold his hand of paper as the daylight slips away
For the boy still loves the mountain even worn down to the ground
And I'll be there, I'll be there, when the evening comes around

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
I remember on your shoulders, I could see the whole parade
You were six foot two of thunder, you were never once afraid
Now your voice has gone to gravel and your steps have gotten slow
And I'm standing in the doorway of the man I used to know

So I'll hold you, oh I'll hold you, like you held me way back then
Turn the years around, daddy, let me be the strong one now and then
Time is slipping, time is slipping through these hands we used to share
But I'm here, oh I'm right here, and I'm never going nowhere

You still try to give me money, slip a twenty in my hand
Still tell me to be careful like I'm ten and can't yet stand
And I take it, yeah I take it, 'cause I know what it's about
It's the only way you've got left to keep on watching out

So I'll hold you, oh I'll hold you, like you held me way back then
Turn the years around, daddy, let me be the strong one now and then
Time is slipping, time is slipping through these hands we used to share
But I'm here, oh I'm right here, and I'm never going nowhere

Every wrinkle is a story, every grey hair is a year
Every silence in the kitchen is a love I hold so dear
So go slow, baby, go slow, let me memorize your face
There is no one in this whole wide world gonna ever take your place

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We drove past the last of the lamplight, left the town to the dark
Where the cold pressed its face to the windows and the engine went quiet and stark
Then you grabbed at my sleeve and you pointed and the whole of the sky was on fire
Green rivers were spilling above us, climbing higher and higher and higher

Oh the northern lights, the northern lights, they don't ask for a thing
Just a curtain of color in the silence, the song that the heavens can sing
And we stood very small in the snowfield with our breath hanging white in the air
And we knew we would never deserve it but the beautiful didn't care

There were colors I had no good name for, there were shapes that I couldn't hold
And the night that I thought was just emptiness was velvet and emerald and gold
No camera could carry it homeward, no story could measure it right
Just two of us, hushed in the vastness, beneath the unspeakable light

Oh the northern lights, the northern lights, they don't ask for a thing
Just a curtain of color in the silence, the song that the heavens can sing
And we stood very small in the snowfield with our breath hanging white in the air
And we knew we would never deserve it but the beautiful didn't care

And I think of it now in the gray of the cities, the rush and the gnaw of the days
And I close up my eyes and the green comes back rolling, the wild and the wordless ablaze
We are smaller than dust, we are here for a moment, then gone like the frost in the sun
But we saw the sky dancing, my love, and that dancing can never be undone

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I slipped out the back while the organ still played
No slamming of doors, no big speech that I made
Just the creak of the pew and the cool of the air
And a God I no longer was certain was there

So I'm leaving you softly, the way that you came
No thunder, no lightning, no curse on the name
Just a boy in his Sunday best learning to doubt
Gonna walk down the steps and I'm quietly out

They'll ask where I went and they won't understand
That I loved every hymn that I held in my hand
But the answers stopped fitting the size of my heart
And you can't stay forever where you've fallen apart

So I'm leaving you softly, the way that you came
No thunder, no lightning, no curse on the name
Just a boy in his Sunday best learning to doubt
Gonna walk down the steps and I'm quietly out

Maybe somewhere down the road I'll find a stranger kind of grace
In a sunset or a stranger's or my own uncertain face
But I'll always keep the candle and the choir and the bell
And I'll thank you, and I'll grieve you, and I'll wish your steeple well

So I'm leaving you softly, the way that you came
No thunder, no lightning, no curse on the name
Just a boy who's grown older with a question for a prayer
Walking out through the morning into wide and open air

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. and the screen lights blue on my face
Thumb hovers over a name I deleted but saved
Got her under "Don't," still I open the thread
Typing, erasing, typing again in my head
Phone face-down on the nightstand, buzzin' like a wasp
Every message I send is a bridge that I burn as I cross
Somebody sleepin' beside me, breathin' slow and warm
While I'm out in the rain of a text-message storm

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but the keys keep movin'
Gray bubble turn to blue and I know what I'm doin'
Delete it for the morning, send it for the night
Wrong number, right feeling, blue glow, no light

Read receipt hit me harder than a closed fist
Little gray "seen" and the years that I dismissed
I keep my ringer off so the guilt don't ring loud
Whisperin' secrets to a screen in a crowd
She say "you up?" and the whole room go quiet
My better judgment lost the vote in the riot

Lock screen, lock jaw, I should put it away
But the dark know my name and it learns what I say
One more, just one more, then I'm done for the night
That's the lie that I text by the cellphone light

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Cheap mic taped to a stand that leaned to the left
Guitar out of tune and a shake in my chest
Four strangers and a barman who wasn't even there
Neon "OPEN" buzzing, smoke heavy in the air
Forgot the second verse, hummed it through
Sweated through a shirt that I borrowed off you

Roll the tape back, let the breakbeat run
Back to the boy with a half-finished song
Faster than my heartbeat, louder than my doubt
That's the night the quiet kid first figured it out

Clapping like rain that was scared to fall
Name misspelled in chalk on the wall
Walked home electric down an empty street
Every streetlight keeping time with my feet

Now the rooms are bigger, now the sound is clean
But I'd trade it for that broken little scene
Spin it up double-time, let the snare collide
With the kid who was terrified and tried

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I burned the book I used to read by candlelight
Knelt at an empty altar and screamed into the night
Where were you when the floor gave out beneath my feet?
I prayed into a phone line nobody would meet
Dust on the pew, rust on the cross
I counted up my prayers and I tallied up the loss

I tore it down, every word, every wall
Then I found you in the rubble after all
Not in the steeple, not in the light
But the hand that pulled me up in the middle of the night

Found it in a stranger who split his bread with me
Found it in the morning that I wasn't built to see
Not the god of thunder, not the god of fear
But the small quiet voice that said "I'm still here"

I don't need the gold, I don't need the throne
I just need to know I'm not bleeding alone
Broke it all apart so I could finally see
The faith I lost was the chain that was holding me

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Dashboard glowing green in a Kansas dark
Hail on the windshield like a thousand little sparks
She loved me in the calm but I lived for the wind
Kissed her in the doorway then I drove off again
Wall cloud rotating like a slow gray hymn
I chase the thing that swallows everything whole and grim

I can't stay, I can't stay
There's a funnel on the field at the end of the day
While you build a quiet house with a light in the hall
I'm out where the sky's about to fall

Four walls feel like a held-down breath
I fall in love with anything that moves like death
The radio crackles, the pressure drops low
And something in my blood just says "go, go, go"

Maybe one day the air will go still
And I'll park in your driveway over the hill
But the clouds are turning green in the west
And I never could mistake the storm for rest

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We stood on the ice where the sea forgets the sun
Two coats and one breath shared between the frozen ones
Then the sky cracked open in a ribbon of green
The wildest dance that the dark had ever seen
It bent like a body, it swayed like your hips
A silent accordion pressed to my lips

Dance with me under the curtain of fire
Green into violet, higher and higher
The heavens are leading, we follow the light
Two small silhouettes in the arctic night

No orchestra played, yet we kept the beat
Snow for a ballroom under our feet
The color poured down like a slow falling silk
And your eyes held it all in a shimmer of milk

When the lights finally faded and the cold took the floor
You whispered, "one more, just one dance more"
So we turned in the dark with no music to hear
The sky was the partner, and you were the year

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The train spat me out on a platform of dust
My boots full of countries, my rifle gone to rust
My mother's old shawl on a chair by the door
But her hands that once mended don't mend anymore
I counted the tiles I had counted as a boy
Each one a brother the powder destroyed

¡Ay! the guitar knows what the mouth cannot say
Six strings for the ones who could not come away
I dance on the stone where my shadow used to play
Alive, alive, and ashamed of the day

The village clapped slow when they saw my face
But their eyes asked questions I could not erase
I brought home my body, I left home my sleep
And a field full of friends that the olives now keep

So strike the wood hard, let the heel hit the ground
Let the grief be a rhythm, let the loss be a sound
I am home, I am home, and the home is a wound
That the dancing won't close but the dancing makes room

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Clarinet climbing like smoke from a flame
We carried old Avram down Rampart by name
A tuba from Odessa, a trumpet from the bay
Two worlds in one second line marching today
Black umbrellas turning slow in the sun
Weeping on the way there, grinning when it's done

Oy, play it slow, then play it for joy
A freylekh for the man, for the laughing old boy
We cry to the cemetery, we dance our way back
Death took the body but it can't take the track

A fiddle and a snare drum learning each other's tongue
Grief in a minor key, then suddenly young
Grandmothers shaking handkerchiefs white
Trading their tears for a stomp in the light

Mazel to the soul on the streetcar of air
May your name be a melody somebody still wears
Spin the parasol, spin it up high
We bury you laughing so you'll know how to fly

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Last whistle blew at a quarter past four
Dad hung his hard hat on the back of the door
Thirty years of the line, now the line's gone cold
Vending machine still hummin' to a room that's been sold
Rust on the gates where the shift used to pour
Nobody clockin' in to the cracked tile floor

Lights off, slow fade, the town holds its breath
Machines goin' quiet like a long slow death
We were built on the rhythm of the steel and the steam
Now we're loopin' the memory of the company dream

Mom packs the lunch he won't carry no more
Diner half-empty where the workers ate before
Kids grow up and they catch the first bus out of here
Leavin' boarded-up windows and a parking lot of fear

Dust on the timecards, weeds in the lot
A whole way of living that the world just forgot
But the river still runs and the porch lights still glow
And somehow this town gotta learn to let go

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Hands that could lift me clean up to the sky
Now they shake a little pourin' the tea by the light
The man who taught me how to throw a right hook
Needs me to read him the small print in the book
He used to be the mountain I learned how to climb
Now I slow my whole walk down to keep his time

Oh, the strongest man I ever knew
Folding up gentle the way the old folks do
I'll be the arm now, lean your weight on me
You carried me first, Daddy, now I'll carry thee

He forgets where he put his keys and his years
But he remembers Mama and it brings up the tears
The stories come twice now, sometimes three
And I let every one of 'em wash over me

Don't you go yet, there's still songs left to sing
Still a porch and a sunset and a slow-turnin' ring
And if your light gotta dim like the end of the day
I'll sit right beside you and I'll watch every ray

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The door didn't slam, it just clicked into place
And the silence sat down in your usual space
Two cups of cold coffee, one chair pushed away
All the things we meant and the things we didn't say
The clock on the wall ticking loud as a drum
Waiting on the other one to finally come

It's the quiet, the quiet, the heavy blue hush
Where the anger lies down and there's nothing to push
We're both in the same room, an ocean between
Saying sorry with our eyes in the spaces unseen

The streetlight comes on and it paints the wall gold
Your shoulder turns slowly, not quite warm, not quite cold
A saxophone somewhere is bleeding through the floor
Playing every soft thing we forgot we adore

Then your hand finds my hand in the dim of the lamp
No words, just the truce of a small open palm
The fight was a stranger that wore both our names
And the quiet's just us, learning love again

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Lloramos por las calles donde el río se duerme
Trumpets from the delta and the heart of Jalisco
We carried Tío Mateo through the heavy gold air
A mariachi from the bayou playing grief like a prayer
Violins and a tuba in the wet summer heat
Following the casket down the cobbled-down street

¡Ay, ay, ay! we sing him to the door
Grito for the man who won't come dancing no more
We weep on the way and we celebrate the soul
Guitarrón and a second line making the broken whole

He loved both the gumbo and his mother's old stew
Spoke in two tongues with a heart split in two
So we bury him bilingual, we mourn him in style
With a sob and a sombrero and a wide weeping smile

Vuela, Mateo, where the saints come to play
Where the trumpets never tire and it's always el día
We send you with mariachi and the parasol's spin
Two cities and one heaven gonna let you on in

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
I drove you north 'til the cities turned black
Blanket in the trunk and no thought of turnin' back
Then the sky started breathin' that electric green
Most beautiful thing that your eyes ever seen
You pulled me close in the cold of the car
Watchin' heaven spill its color like a slow falling star

Baby, look up, the sky's puttin' on a show
Green on the velvet, that aurora glow
I'd cross every cold mile, drive through every night
Just to hold you underneath the northern lights

Your breath made a cloud and I caught it in my hand
Two silhouettes alone in a frozen promised land
No radio playing, just the hum of the heat
And the universe dancing to our two heartbeats

If the lights ever fade and the dark takes the sky
I'll still see that color every time you walk by
So lay your head down, let the cold do its thing
We got our own aurora in the love that we bring

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Met her where the boardwalk meets the foam
Salt in her laughter, sand for a home
June was a Sunday that lasted all week
God in the sunburn and the kiss on my cheek
We ran from the tide with our shoes in our hands
Writing our names where the sea takes the sands

Oh, hallelujah for the summer and the sea
For the girl in the gold light dancing with me
It won't last forever and I know that it's true
But glory, sweet glory, for the summer with you

She smelled like coconut, sunscreen and rain
We rode the last Ferris wheel over and again
The season was short like the flick of a match
A blessing too bright for these two hands to catch

When September comes calling and she boards the train
I'll thank the good Lord for the joy and the pain
For some things are given to teach us to let go
And I praise every wave of that warm undertow

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
Tether came loose on the dark side of blue
Floating in the silence, just me watching you
Your faceplate reflecting the cold and the stars
Your voice in my headset already going to scars
Reach out my glove but the distance won't close
The vacuum keeps pullin' wherever it goes

We're drifting, baby, drifting apart
Two satellites losing the pull of the heart
The radio's static, the oxygen's thin
And there's no gravity left to draw us back in

We trained for the weightless, we never trained for this
The slow kind of leaving, the long fading kiss
Earth's just a marble too small to hold tight
And you're just a blink in the merciless night

If the comms cut out and I drift past the moon
Know I memorized your breathing like a favorite tune
We were never meant for the cold and the black
But I'd burn through the sky just to pull you back

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights were so close I could taste the warm glow
Name on the marquee, front row to front row
Then the phone went silent and the offer fell through
And the dream I had carried just folded in two
I sat in the parking lot, key in the door
With a whole life of almost piled up on the floor

But glory, oh glory, I'm still standing here
The night that I almost was the night I drew near
Didn't get the mountain but I climbed up the stone
And the Lord met me there in the almost, alone

They say close don't count and they may be right
But close kept me prayin' through the rest of the night
I gathered the pieces of the thing I had planned
And I laid every shard in a bigger man's hand

Maybe the missing was the mercy in disguise
Maybe the closed door had a kinder surprise
For I almost made it and I'm here to say
The almost was the bread that I needed that day

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone feels heavy in my shaking hand
Been counting years like grains of sand
The dial tone hums a lonesome tune
Under that same forgiving moon

I rehearsed my lines a thousand ways
Lost in the fog of wasted days
But when you answered soft and slow
All my excuses let me go

So hello mama, it's been too long
Forgive the silence, forgive the wrong
I'm swinging back to where I'm known
Never again gonna leave you alone

The brass kicks up and so does my heart
We're patching up the broken part
Your laughter rings just like back then
When I was small and home again

The band plays on, the years dissolve
With every word my fears resolve
Two cups of coffee, one long call
And love still standing through it all

So hello mama, it's been too long
Forgive the silence, forgive the wrong
I'm swinging back to where I'm known
Never again gonna leave you alone

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights were low, the crowd was right
I had the city in my sight
One more song and the deal was made
Standing in the spotlight shade

My hands were steady on the keys
A hundred dreams up in the breeze
They said tonight could change it all
But life don't always answer the call

Oh, I almost made it
So close I could taste it
The night slipped through my hands
Like water through the sand
Oh, I almost made it
But heaven made me wait

The phone went quiet, the door stayed shut
A dream deferred, a clean deep cut
But something in me wouldn't break
For every fall there's a rise to take

Maybe almost ain't the end
Maybe it's the road's best friend
'Cause the ones who taste defeat
Learn to rise back on their feet

Oh, I almost made it
So close I could taste it
But I'm coming back again
With fire I'll ascend

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The tents are glowing one last time
The band is swinging right on time
Dust on our shoes and stars above
This fading field we've come to love

The strangers turned to lifelong friends
We're dancing where the meadow ends
Grab your partner, hold them near
For morning takes us all from here

One last night, let the trumpets cry
One last dance beneath the sky
We'll remember this 'til we're old and gray
The night we swung the dark away

The fairy lights begin to blur
A summer dream the way things were
The drummer counts us into one
The final chorus, the setting sun

When the morning comes we'll say goodbye
Fold up the stage and let it lie
But every note we played tonight
Will linger like the morning light

One last night, let the trumpets cry
One last dance beneath the sky
We'll remember this 'til we're old and gray
The night we swung the dark away

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody picked us from the start
But we got fire in our heart
Down by ten and the clock won't stop
Little guys climbing to the top

The horns are blaring, pick it up
We're playing for that dented cup
They laughed at us, they doubt our name
But we came here to win this game

Pick it up, pick it up, never gonna quit
Underdogs with a whole lot of grit
One more whistle, one more shot
Give it everything we got

The crowd is jumping to the beat
We won't accept a sure defeat
Skank it left and skank it right
We're taking home the win tonight

When they wrote us off as done
That's exactly when we won
Upstroke guitar and a brass attack
There ain't no holding this team back

Pick it up, pick it up, never gonna quit
Underdogs with a whole lot of grit
Final buzzer, hear it ring
Watch the little guys take the win

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Ten and two, baby, hands on the wheel
Deep breath in, tell me how you feel
Mirror check, now ease off the brake
First little roll, that's all it takes

You were small, now you're reaching the pedals
Growing up fast like the spinning of metals
Green means go but you gotta stay calm
I'm right beside you, I'm your safety, your balm

Drive, baby, drive, take it slow
Daddy's right here, let it go
Every turn is a lesson in trust
Growing up in the city dust
Drive, baby, drive, find your way
I'll be your passenger today

Parallel parking, you nailed it clean
Proudest moment that I've ever seen
From your booster seat to the driver's side
Where'd the time go, where'd it hide

One day soon you'll drive away
Off to chase a brighter day
But every mile you ever roam
Know this car will lead you home

Drive, baby, drive, take it slow
Daddy's right here, let it go
Every turn is a lesson in trust
Growing up in the city dust

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Neon washes over the crowd
Bass is pumping, hearts are loud
Last night here before we're gone
Chasing midnight into dawn

The lasers cut the velvet dark
Every beat a falling spark
Hold my hand beneath the glow
This is all we'll ever know

One more night under electric skies
Watch the sunrise in your eyes
We're alive, we're young, we're free
Dancing through eternity
One more night, don't let it fade
In the neon dream we made

The synths are rising like the tide
Nowhere left for us to hide
The final song begins to play
We'll dance until the break of day

When the lights go dim and the speakers die
We'll carry this electric high
Frozen in a magenta haze
The brightest of our fading days

One more night under electric skies
Watch the sunrise in your eyes
We're alive, we're young, we're free
Dancing through eternity

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Old Moishe sits beneath the trees
A cigarette and a winter breeze
Five dollars says you'll never win
The clock is ticking, let's begin

The clarinet, it bends and cries
Like the twinkle in his crafty eyes
He moves his bishop, sips his tea
A grandmaster of the queen's gambit, see

Checkmate, my friend, the king has fallen
