Hear the violin softly callin'
Pawn by pawn he wins the day
The hustler of the park will play

The tourists come, the tourists fall
He's beaten one and beaten all
He laughs and shrugs, collects his pay
Then sets the board another way

Oy, the wisdom in his hands
The oldest game in all the lands
He dances with the black and white
And hums a freylekh through the night

Checkmate, my friend, the king has fallen
Hear the violin softly callin'
Pawn by pawn he wins the day
The hustler of the park will play

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon buzzing on the corner street
Where the lonely and the dreamers meet
Coffee pouring, never runs dry
Under the city's velvet sky

Mama in the kitchen, frying gold
Serving comfort to the young and old
The jukebox hums a midnight tune
We'll be dancing here 'til noon

All night long, the door stays open
Hearts get mended, dreams get spoken
Come on in from the cold outside
In this little diner we abide
All night long, we move and sway
Dancing till the break of day

The taxi driver, the lonely bride
Find a booth and a place to hide
Nobody's stranger after two
We're family the whole night through

When the world has gone to sleep
This is where the secrets keep
Every soul finds a second home
Nobody here is ever alone

All night long, the door stays open
Hearts get mended, dreams get spoken
Come on in from the cold outside
In this little diner we abide

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
The kitchen's quiet, the kettle sighs
Half a recipe, half goodbyes
One plate set where there used to be two
Learning to cook the way you knew

The spices blur in a wall of sound
Your handwriting on the page I found
A pinch of salt, a fading hum
The softest things are hardest done

Cooking for one in the evening haze
Lost in the fog of better days
I measure love in a smaller cup
Learning slowly to fill it up

The steam it rises, the windows weep
The radio drowning the silence deep
I taste the broth, it tastes like you
The ghost of everything we knew

Maybe one day I'll cook for more
Open up that closed front door
But for tonight it's just my spoon
And your photograph and the kitchen moon

Cooking for one in the evening haze
Lost in the fog of better days
I measure love in a smaller cup
Learning slowly to fill it up

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Silla vieja, el espejo brilla
Every story in my little villa
Fade it up and line it tight
I know the secrets of the night

The mayor sits and spills his heart
The young boy's first cut is an art
Dembow knocking through the door
Everybody knows what I'm here for

El barbero, I know the whole town
From the king to the class clown
Sit down, dale, let me work
Every cut a little perk
El barbero, the chair don't lie
I hear it all as the clippers fly

Love and trouble, win and loss
In my shop I'm the boss
Gossip flowing like the wine
Everybody waits in line

Man comes in feeling low
Leaves out fresh and ready to glow
More than hair, I shape the pride
Of every soul that steps inside

El barbero, I know the whole town
From the king to the class clown
Sit down, dale, let me work
Every cut a little perk

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Thunder rolling through my veins
Memories like burning chains
I see your face inside the fire
A fading ghost, a dead desire

I had you once within my grasp
Now only ashes, only past
The night you left, the heavens cried
A part of me forever died

You're the one that got away
Haunting me both night and day
Screaming through the endless dark
You left a scar, you left your mark
The one that got away

I built a throne upon the pain
And called your name into the rain
The storm inside will never cease
You stole my war, you stole my peace

Forged in sorrow, born in flame
I'll carry on, I'll bear the shame
But every echo of the past
Reminds me love was never built to last

You're the one that got away
Haunting me both night and day
Screaming through the endless dark
You left a scar, you left your mark
The one that got away

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
By the old stone wall where the ivy clings
Sits a clever man and the game he brings
A fiddle plays as the pawns advance
He'll take your coin in a gambler's dance

The morning mist on the cobbled square
He waits for fools with time to spare
A penny whistle, a knowing grin
He sets the board and draws you in

Oh the hustler of the park
Plays his games from dawn to dark
King to bishop, knight to three
No man leaves with his money free

The travelers come from near and far
To test their wits beneath the star
But every move he's seen before
He smiles and sweeps them from the door

The tin whistle cries a lonesome air
For all the souls who challenged there
He's old as stone, he's sharp as thorn
And he'll be playing come the morn

Oh the hustler of the park
Plays his games from dawn to dark
King to bishop, knight to three
No man leaves with his money free

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete stretches mile on mile
Neon hums in a cold dead style
But there it stands against the gray
The last green soul that wouldn't fade

The city swallowed every field
Every garden, every yield
Yet one lone tree beneath the lights
Keeps reaching up through endless nights

The last tree standing in the glow
Roots remember long ago
When the world was wild and free
Hold on, hold on, lonely tree
The last tree standing in the rain
A living heart in a world of chain

The headlights pass, they never see
The quiet strength of this old tree
A synth-pad swells in violet air
A monument to what was there

Maybe one day the concrete cracks
And green comes creeping slowly back
From one lone seed beneath the stone
The wild will rise and reclaim its own

The last tree standing in the glow
Roots remember long ago
When the world was wild and free
Hold on, hold on, lonely tree

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Duffel bag heavy on my back
Walking down the old dirt track
The town looks smaller than before
I'm not the kid who went to war

Vinyl crackle, soft and low
Ghosts of places I won't go
The porch light's on, the same old street
My mama waiting, my heart skips a beat

I'm coming home, coming home
No more nights out there alone
Lay my rifle and my fears
Wash away these wasted years
Coming home, coming home

The coffee's warm, the dog still knows
The creak of the floor, the way it goes
But something in me stayed behind
In the desert sun, in the back of my mind

Slow drums beat like a healing heart
Piece by piece I'll find my start
The war don't leave but neither does love
And that's enough, Lord, that's enough

I'm coming home, coming home
No more nights out there alone
Lay my rifle and my fears
Wash away these wasted years

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The banjo's ringing 'cross the hill
One last night before we're still
The fiddle saws a sweet goodbye
Under that big old country sky

We stomped these fields for three long days
Lost in the music, lost in the haze
Now the lanterns flicker low
It's almost time for us to go

So play it loud, play it long
One more verse of the old-time song
Dance with me 'neath the harvest moon
For morning's coming all too soon
Play it loud, play it long
This is where we all belong

The mandolin and the upright bass
A grin on every weary face
Grab your darling, do-si-do
Before the final embers glow

We'll pack our wagons, hit the road
Carry home this lightened load
But the songs we sang tonight
Will ring on long past morning light

So play it loud, play it long
One more verse of the old-time song
Dance with me 'neath the harvest moon
For morning's coming all too soon

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The clock strikes one and my thumb won't quit
I told myself I was over it
But the screen lights up like a downtown sign
And your name comes rolling down the line

I got a left hand swearing I'll behave
And a right hand digging my own little grave
The band's still playing that low-down tune
While I'm typing trouble by the light of the moon

Oh I shouldn't, but I'm gonna, hit send
Make a midnight mess I can't defend
Every letter's a step I shouldn't take
A sweet little swing for a heart on the make

Three dots dancing, my collar's tight
This is a duet we shouldn't write
But the rhythm says go and the rule says no
And baby you know which one I'll follow

Somewhere a wiser man hangs up the phone
Sleaves well enough and walks on home
But I ain't him and the night's still young
So I'll dance on the edge of my own loose tongue

Oh I shouldn't, but I'm gonna, hit send
Make a midnight mess I can't defend
Every letter's a step I shouldn't take
A sweet little swing for a heart on the make

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Four gray walls and a busted gate
Everyone told me to sit and wait
But the horns kicked in and I caught the beat
Picked up my feet off the dead-end street

Get up, get out, gone
Leaving the same old corner song
Get up, get out, run
First kid on the block to see the sun

Mama packed lunch in a paper sleeve
Said don't come back just because you grieve
I skanked past the boys on the rusted stoop
Traded the circle for an open loop

Get up, get out, gone
Leaving the same old corner song
Get up, get out, run
First kid on the block to see the sun

They said the road ends where the streetlight dies
I said watch me, and I caught the skyline rise
Upbeat, offbeat, every step a door
The block ain't dead, it was just a floor

Get up, get out, gone
Leaving the same old corner song
Get up, get out, run
First kid on the block to see the sun

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The clarinet weeps and so do I
Under a low and a knowing sky
My fingers shake on the little glass screen
Writing to someone I shouldn't have seen

Grandmother's voice in the back of my head
Says leave the dead letters for the dead
But the fiddle climbs and it pulls me near
To a name I promised to disappear

Oy, the trouble in a single word
The sweetest sin I never should have heard
Dance me down where the wise men warn
A freylekhs played on a heart that's torn

The whole village circles, hand in hand
But I step out where I shouldn't stand
Three little dots like a candle flame
Flickering soft around your name

Slow now, minor, the wedding's done
The guests gone home and I'm the only one
Still on the floor with the phone held tight
Dancing alone in the foolish night

Oy, the trouble in a single word
The sweetest sin I never should have heard
Dance me down where the wise men warn
A freylekhs played on a heart that's torn

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
A cramped little room and a crooked stool
A microphone humming, the lights too cruel
My knees were a rhythm I couldn't hide
With a fold of paper and nowhere to hide

The coffee went cold on a corner shelf
I sang for the room and mostly myself
Four polite claps and a stranger's nod
The longest two minutes I ever trod

And I'd go back, I'd go back
To that shaking kid on the warping track
Tell him the fear is the fee you pay
For the music that carried you all the way

I know the chord now I missed back then
I'd play it clean, but I'd lose the when
The beautiful clumsy, the honest start
The first brave crack in a quiet heart

Funny how time turns the dread to gold
The night I survived is the tale I've told
A hundred stages have come and gone
But none rang truer than that first dawn

And I'd go back, I'd go back
To that shaking kid on the warping track
Tell him the fear is the fee you pay
For the music that carried you all the way

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The dial tone hums like a held-down note
A decade of words stuck in my throat
I press the numbers I never forgot
My hands remember what my pride did not

It rings like a question I can't unask
I rehearse an apology, drop the mask
Then a small soft hello breaks the line
And the years fall away in a single sign

Mama, it's me, it's been so long
I buried my voice but I kept the song
No brass too proud, no string too thin
To carry me home to your arms again

She doesn't scold and she doesn't cry
Just asks if I've eaten, asks am I dry
The whole stupid silence, the wasted years
Dissolve in the steam of her gentle fears

How does forgiveness arrive so plain
Like rain that forgets it was ever pain
No lawyer, no ledger, no list of wrong
Just come to the table, you've been gone too long

Mama, it's me, it's been so long
I buried my voice but I kept the song
No brass too proud, no string too thin
To carry me home to your arms again

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Same cracked sidewalk, same flickering light
Same promises whispered and broken by night
I counted the bricks till I knew every one
Swore to myself I'd be gone with the sun

Look at me now, I made it through
Out of the corner the whole world knew
I carry the block in the back of my chest
But my feet found the road and my soul found rest

Mama worked doubles, her hands all worn
Praying her baby outran the storm
I kept her voice like a key in my hand
Unlocked a door they couldn't understand

Look at me now, I made it through
Out of the corner the whole world knew
I carry the block in the back of my chest
But my feet found the road and my soul found rest

I don't forget where the pavement bled
The friends still standing where I used to tread
So I send the light back, I send it home
So no one out there feels they're out there alone

Look at me now, I made it through
Out of the corner the whole world knew
I carry the block in the back of my chest
But my feet found the road and my soul found rest

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Door don't slam but the silence do
Two coffee cups and a point of view
Words got thrown and they hit the floor
Now we breathing in a quiet war
Clock on the wall is the only sound
Both of us proud and both of us bound
I know I was wrong by the fourth line out
But my mouth kept driving the doubt

It's the quiet, the quiet after
No more thunder, no more laughter
Just two hearts in the same dark room
Waiting on who gon' break it soon

I rehearse a sorry I can't deliver
Tongue gets cold and my hands they shiver
Love ain't the absence of a fight
It's who stays standing in the middle of the night
She's on the couch and I'm on the wall
Neither one wants to be the one to fall
But pride is a house that nobody warms
So I reach my hand through the silent storm

One word, just one, could end it now
I swallow my crown and I show her how

It's the quiet, the quiet after
No more thunder, no more laughter
Just two hearts in the same dark room
Waiting on who gon' break it soon

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at six, clock out at nine
Turn around, get back in line
Apron off and the name tag on
Sleep's a rumor that's come and gone

Two jobs, one me, still short
Running a race on a broken court
Fast little horns and a tired heart
Falling behind from the very start

Rent went up and the hours stayed flat
Landlord smiling under his hat
I skank through the day on a coffee prayer
Spending tomorrow I can't spare

Two jobs, one me, still short
Running a race on a broken court
Fast little horns and a tired heart
Falling behind from the very start

But the bassline keeps me on my feet
When the math don't work and I can't eat
So I'll dance through the deficit, dance through the dread
Keep the beat alive even when I'm in the red

Two jobs, one me, still short
Running a race on a broken court
Fast little horns and a tired heart
Falling behind from the very start

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded her low in the dead of the dock
No papers, no prayers, just the turn of a lock
The captain don't speak on what's down in the hold
But the iron's gone quiet and the water's gone cold

Oh, we carry it, carry it down through the deep
A weight that won't rest and a debt we can't keep
The sea knows the secret we swore not to tell
A cursed little cargo and a long, slow bell

The compass spins lazy, the stars all withdraw
The crew trades in whispers and bites at the raw
Something is humming below the waterline
A voice in the cargo that sounds like it's mine

Oh, we carry it, carry it down through the deep
A weight that won't rest and a debt we can't keep
The sea knows the secret we swore not to tell
A cursed little cargo and a long, slow bell

Throw it overboard, the young one cried
But the rope won't loosen, the knots have died
Whatever we're hauling, it's hauling us too
Through the velvet black and the endless blue

Oh, we carry it, carry it down through the deep
A weight that won't rest and a debt we can't keep
The sea knows the secret we swore not to tell
A cursed little cargo and a long, slow bell

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The train pulled in at a quarter past four
(Home, sweet home)
I dropped my bag at the kitchen door
(Home, sweet home)
The porch light burning the way it did
When I was a frightened little kid

I'm home, I'm home, oh sha-la-la
Back in the arms of my ma and pa
The war stayed over the wide blue sea
But a piece of it traveled home with me

My sweetheart waited on the garden gate
(Home, sweet home)
She kept her promise, she didn't wait late
(Home, sweet home)
Her hand in mine like a Sunday hymn
The whole world steady, no longer dim

Some nights I wake to a sound that's gone
The boys I left when the fields turned wan
But morning comes with a kettle's song
And somehow, somehow, I belong

I'm home, I'm home, oh sha-la-la
Back in the arms of my ma and pa
The war stayed over the wide blue sea
But a piece of it traveled home with me

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Raise my hand, watch it sink in the room
Half a sentence, then it's consumed
Somebody louder takes my line
Repeats it back and they call it fine
Mute on my mic but my mind on fire
Good idea drowned in the choir
Nod my head, let the moment pass
Swallowing words like a broken glass

Talk over me, talk over me
Like I ain't here, like I can't speak
But I'm building quiet, I'm building deep
Gon' say it once and the whole room weak

They skip my name on the credit roll
Take my fire and they call it gold
But I keep receipts in the notes app
Every stolen line, every silent gap
Next time the table goes around
I'm a let the silence make the sound
Lean to the mic, no rush, no shake
Say the one thing they can't retake

Volume ain't value, loud ain't right
I been studying rooms in the dead of night

Talk over me, talk over me
Like I ain't here, like I can't speak
But I'm building quiet, I'm building deep
Gon' say it once and the whole room weak

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two a.m. and the lamp still on
Whole world sleeping, I'm holding on
Highlighter dry and my eyes gone red
Reading the same line back in my head
Cold coffee ring on a borrowed desk
Dreams too big for a daytime guess
Nobody clapping, nobody sees
Just me and the silence and the textbook keys

Late light, my fight, alone
Building a future nobody's shown
No crowd, no sound, just me
Planting in the dark what the dawn will see

Momma asleep down the narrow hall
I'm chasing a name on a graduation wall
Every flashcard a brick I lay
For a house I won't live in for many a day
They see the trophy, they don't see this
The lonely grind and the things I miss
But the quiet teaches what the loud can't say
That the win gets built in the unseen way

Clock ticking forward, won't tick back
So I pour the hours on the empty track

Late light, my fight, alone
Building a future nobody's shown
No crowd, no sound, just me
Planting in the dark what the dawn will see

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
She walks the shore where the gray gulls turn
With a hundred years she's been slow to learn
She points to the water, says watch it breathe
It takes what it wants and it gives what you need

Low tide, high tide, the old hands know
The way the moon tells the sea where to go
She taught me to wait, she taught me to read
The pull of the water, the patience I need

Don't fight the current, she said with a grin
Just learn where it ends and you'll know to begin
Her feet in the foam like they grew from the sand
A map of the ocean drawn on her hand

Low tide, high tide, the old hands know
The way the moon tells the sea where to go
She taught me to wait, she taught me to read
The pull of the water, the patience I need

One day the shore will forget her name
But the tide she taught me comes back the same
So I'll stand where she stood when the evening's blue
And teach the next child what she taught me too

Low tide, high tide, the old hands know
The way the moon tells the sea where to go
She taught me to wait, she taught me to read
The pull of the water, the patience I need

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up on the rooftop, antenna in the breeze
Broadcasting groove to whoever I please
No license, no logo, no big-money man
Just a beat-up rig and a master plan

Tune in, turn it up, we're live tonight
Riding a frequency outta sight
They can't shut us down, can't trace the sound
Pirate on the dial, spinning it around

The signal jumps over the sleeping town
To the kid in the corner who's feeling down
I drop a little funk in the dead of the air
Tell 'em somebody's playing, tell 'em somebody's there

Tune in, turn it up, we're live tonight
Riding a frequency outta sight
They can't shut us down, can't trace the sound
Pirate on the dial, spinning it around

The van came knocking but we packed and ran
Set up in the basement of an old tin can
You can jam the tower but you can't jam the soul
The groove gon' travel where it wanna go

Tune in, turn it up, we're live tonight
Riding a frequency outta sight
They can't shut us down, can't trace the sound
Pirate on the dial, spinning it around

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Neon rain on a midnight road
Dropped my rifle, dropped the load
The city hums in electric blue
A skyline humming I once knew

Driving home through the violet haze
Ghosts in the mirror of better days
The engine sings what I can't say
Carry me, carry me far away

Her window glows like a distant star
I count the miles in the dashboard scar
The radio plays a song from before
When I was a boy who'd never seen war

Driving home through the violet haze
Ghosts in the mirror of better days
The engine sings what I can't say
Carry me, carry me far away

The synths rise up like a second dawn
Over the soldier the war turned on
I'm not the same as the one who left
But the city remembers, the city's kept

Driving home through the violet haze
Ghosts in the mirror of better days
The engine sings what I can't say
Carry me, carry me far away

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Keys in the ignition, engine drinking gasoline
Three minutes on the meter, I have done the math
Brick of nervous heartbeat hammering the steering wheel
The alley spits them out and I am gone

One more block, one more red
Drive it like I want to live
One more turn, one more breath
This is everything I have left to give

Sirens fold the city like a paper map on fire
Glass and rearview, faces I will never name
I told them this would be the last one, swore it on the wire
The engine doesn't care about my vow

One more block, one more red
Drive it like I want to live
One more turn, one more breath
This is everything I have left to give

The river bends ahead, the bridge a row of teeth
I count the seconds left before the dark
If I make it to the other side I disappear beneath
A name nobody owns, a car nobody parks

No more block, no more red
I drove it like I wanted to live
Last turn, last breath
That was everything I had to give

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Neon hums above the chair, a violet electric glow
Scissors keeping rhythm to a song from years ago
Every head that tilts back here has whispered something true
I hold the secrets of this street in mirrors painted blue

Under the buzzing sign I cut the dark away
Every face a frequency, every name a wave
I know who's in love, I know who's in debt
I keep the whole town's story in the lather and the set

The mayor and the busboy take the same warm towel and shave
Confessions in the chrome light, half a dollar for the grave
The radio is dreaming in a magnetic tape of rain
I sweep their fallen years up off the linoleum again

Under the buzzing sign I cut the dark away
Every face a frequency, every name a wave
I know who's in love, I know who's in debt
I keep the whole town's story in the lather and the set

Lock the door at midnight, count the silver in the drawer
The streetlights pulse like heartbeats I have memorized before
Every clipping on the floor a little piece of who we were

Under the fading sign I cut the dark away
Every face a frequency, every name a wave

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Salt on your shoulders, cassette in the dash
We spent our whole August like it wouldn't crash
Vinyl crackle of the waves on the shore
Your laugh on a loop I keep coming back for

Just for the summer, just for the sand
Melting like sun on the back of my hand
We knew it would end when the leaves changed their tune
So we lived in the warm static of a borrowed afternoon

Boardwalk lights flicker, the lo-fi of July
You drew a small heart in the salt on my eye
No promises traded, no future to keep
Just two folding chairs and the tide pulling deep

Just for the summer, just for the sand
Melting like sun on the back of my hand
We knew it would end when the leaves changed their tune
So we lived in the warm static of a borrowed afternoon

September came quiet and folded the chairs
Your towel left a shape where the warmth wasn't there
I still hear the ocean in the hiss of the tape

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old man on the shoreline with his feet inside the foam
Telling young ones how the water always carries something home
He say the moon is just a drummer keeping time across the deep
And the ocean only borrows what it eventually will keep

Learn the tide, learn the tide
She will rise and she will hide
Don't you fight the pulling water, let it move you like a song
Learn the tide, learn the tide, she been here all along

He carve the lesson in the sand before the wave can wash it clean
The sea don't read your worry and she don't care what you mean
Bend like the reed, child, and the current be your friend
What she takes out in the morning, she bring back in the end

Learn the tide, learn the tide
She will rise and she will hide
Don't you fight the pulling water, let it move you like a song
Learn the tide, learn the tide, she been here all along

Grandfather of the salt, with the seaweed in his hair
He close his eyes and listen to the rhythm in the air
Now the children walk the wet sand reading water like a book

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
A single bulb above the felt, the smoke a low gray ceiling
Five men and a deck of fate, the slow hours peeling
He pushed the deed across the table like it weighed nothing at all
We watched a quiet man unspool against the wall

Deal it out, deal it slow
We went past the place where reason lets you go
The river card was turning and the room forgot to breathe
Nobody at the table knew the things they couldn't leave

The ante was his wedding ring, then everything behind it
The pot became a hungry thing and dared us all to find it
The clock gave up at four a.m., the chips like little bones
And somewhere in the silence we stopped playing for the loans

No words now, only the shuffle and the breathing
The candle of his fortune slowly leaving
We should have stood and walked into the morning
but the cards kept on falling, falling, falling

Deal it out, deal it slow
We went past the place where reason lets you go
The river card was turning and the room forgot to breathe
Nobody at the table knew the things they couldn't leave

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Empty Sunday parking lot, the world a wall of haze
Your hands at ten and two and trembling through the gauze of grayer days
I hear my father's patience leaking soft out of my mouth
The engine hums a fuzzy chord that pulls the morning south

Easy on the brake, the whole world's slowing down
You are pulling out of childhood and into the open town
I'm fading in the passenger seat, a blur behind the glass
Go slow, go slow, my love, but go, and let the old years pass

Your knuckles white as cotton, you laugh and then you sigh
The windshield holds a wash of light, a smear of summer sky
I taught you how to tie your shoes, now I teach you how to leave
Every lesson is a door I'm holding open in my sleeve

Easy on the brake, the whole world's slowing down
You are pulling out of childhood and into the open town
I'm fading in the passenger seat, a blur behind the glass
Go slow, go slow, my love, but go, and let the old years pass

One day you'll drive away and I'll be smaller in the mirror
A shape of someone waving as the road ahead grows clearer

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Three shifts at the plant and my hands are full of rust
Skipped town in a dying truck because I think I must
North until the asphalt quit and handed me the snow
I came to see the sky do something nobody can own

Green fire, green fire, ripping up the night
Nobody can sell it, nobody owns the light
Screaming colors over the cold, free and loud and wild
The sky's the only riot that never killed a child

No ticket and no fence line, no boss to punch the clock
Just me and forty strangers and the curtains overhead that rock
It doesn't care who voted, doesn't care who prayed
It burns for the bartender and the kid who never paid

Green fire, green fire, ripping up the night
Nobody can sell it, nobody owns the light
Screaming colors over the cold, free and loud and wild
The sky's the only riot that never killed a child

Let it crack the heavens open, let it shame the neon signs
The only honest billboard hangs above the tree lines
Woah-oh, woah-oh, the night is finally mine

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the warehouse, clock out at the grill
Apron in the back seat, gas tank never fill
Rent due on the first and it's already the third
Two checks in my pocket and they both already heard

Grinding, grinding, still I'm falling behind
Two jobs, no sleep, debt still on my mind
Run all day, run all night, never gain no ground
Treadmill of the city, never slow it down

Manager said overtime then cut my hours in half
App keep adding service fees, the late notice in the math
Baby need new sneakers, fridge is humming empty air
I'm working like a engine on a road that goes nowhere

Grinding, grinding, still I'm falling behind
Two jobs, no sleep, debt still on my mind
Run all day, run all night, never gain no ground
Treadmill of the city, never slow it down

They say just work harder like I'm sitting on my hands
I got calluses for fingerprints, they still don't understand
Sun ain't even up yet and I'm lacing up again

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
They carry him down Rampart with a trombone and a tear
The first mile slow as molasses, every footstep heavy here
Black umbrellas in the heat, the hymn a dragging sigh
We walk him to the crossroads where we tell the man goodbye

Dance it, dance it, turn the grief around
When the body's in the ground we lift the spirit off the ground
Cry on the way there, sing on the way back
Joy is just the other side of the same old track

Then the snare drum finds a heartbeat and the tuba starts to grin
The whole street second-lining as the brass come rolling in
Auntie waving handkerchiefs, the children kick the dust
We send him off in color 'cause that's how a soul gets loved

Dance it, dance it, turn the grief around
When the body's in the ground we lift the spirit off the ground
Cry on the way there, sing on the way back
Joy is just the other side of the same old track

Saints come marching, feet won't stay still
Death had its mile and now the music will
We bury the sorrow, we wake up the bones

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Flour in the creases, a wedding band gone thin
These hands have held a century of leaving and of kin
They braided me on Sundays, they pressed my Sunday shirt
They buried two good husbands in the cold Carolina dirt

Oh, those hands, those hands
Soft as the gospel, strong as the land
They rocked the whole family through every storm we ran
Lord, I learned the meaning of love from a grandmother's hands

They snapped the green beans open, they wiped the runny eye
They clapped on the downbeat every time the choir would cry
They never once stopped working, not for money, not for praise
Just folded into prayer at the closing of the days

Oh, those hands, those hands
Soft as the gospel, strong as the land
They rocked the whole family through every storm we ran
Lord, I learned the meaning of love from a grandmother's hands

Now they rest upon the quilt she stitched so long ago
Still warm when I hold them, still telling me to go
I press them to my cheek and feel the river of her years

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The Ferris wheel is slowing, the lanterns burning low
We stole one final dance before the carnival would go
Your ribbon caught the moonlight, the band played soft and blue
And I knew that come the morning the field would say adieu

Last night, last night, hold me close tonight
The summer's almost over but the stars are burning bright
Sha-la-la, my darling, don't you let go of my hand
The last night of the festival is the best we ever had

The cotton candy melted, the prizes all gave out
The trumpet player tipped his hat and walked the long way out
But you and I kept swaying on the trampled summer grass
Pretending that the magic of the midway wouldn't pass

Last night, last night, hold me close tonight
The summer's almost over but the stars are burning bright
Sha-la-la, my darling, don't you let go of my hand
The last night of the festival is the best we ever had

They're folding up the tents now, the lights blink one by one
But I will keep this evening like a coin beneath my tongue

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
There's a station on the river where the morning trains still moan
I watched you board the four-fifteen and ride into unknown
I had the words right in my chest, a ring inside my coat
But courage is a coward and it stuck inside my throat

And you're the one, the one that got away
A harvest I let rot upon the vine of yesterday
I plant your name in winter and it blooms in every spring
The one that got away still wears the ring I didn't bring

They tell me you got married in a town beside the sea
With somebody who said the things I was too scared to be
I poured my whole regret into a bottle and a song
And carried you for thirty years, the wrong I carried long

And you're the one, the one that got away
A harvest I let rot upon the vine of yesterday
I plant your name in winter and it blooms in every spring
The one that got away still wears the ring I didn't bring

If I could ride that four-fifteen back down the rusted line
I'd take your hand at the platform and I'd make you mine

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Pick it up, pick it up, the horns are on the run
Old Pops on the jetty in the early morning sun
He's teaching all the little ones the rhythm of the sea
Stamp your feet on the offbeat like the waves taught me

In and out, in and out, that's the way she goes
The tide it keeps the tempo and the elder, well, he knows
Don't you push the water, let the water push you back
Learn to ride the upbeat and you'll never lose the track

He blows a little trumpet at the gulls along the pier
The ocean is the bandleader, the count is loud and clear
Catch the wave on two and four, fall back on one and three
The old man and the saltwater conduct the symphony

In and out, in and out, that's the way she goes
The tide it keeps the tempo and the elder, well, he knows
Don't you push the water, let the water push you back
Learn to ride the upbeat and you'll never lose the track

Skank along the shoreline, kids, the moon is on the snare
Grandfather of the breakwater is dancing in the air
Pick it up, pick it up, the tide is coming round

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Garlic in the iron pan, the window thick with steam
I cross the threshold of the years into a copper dream
Her apron strings, her humming, the basil on the sill
The kitchen holds her ghost and every shadow standing still

One breath and I am seven, the table set for two
The smell of her tomatoes pulls me dancing back to you
A bittersweet embrace in the doorway of the past
The perfume of her kitchen is a love that had to last

The oregano, the simmer, the bread beneath the cloth
A slow and tender rhythm in the rising kitchen broth
I close my eyes and find her with a wooden spoon in hand
Conducting all the saucepans like an orchestra she planned

One breath and I am seven, the table set for two
The smell of her tomatoes pulls me dancing back to you
A bittersweet embrace in the doorway of the past
The perfume of her kitchen is a love that had to last

Now strangers own the building and the stove has long gone cold
But catch one scent of simmering and I am not so old
She waltzes through the doorway with a plate held in the air

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
A borrowed nylon guitar and a knee that wouldn't quit
The corner of a coffee shop, the one warm yellow lit
I mumbled through three verses to a half-attentive room
A waitress and a sleepy cat were all that heard me bloom

Soft and slow I remember the night
My voice was a feather, my hands not quite right
The microphone trembled, the small crowd was kind
That little open mic still plays in my mind

I played one chord too early and I laughed it all away
The gentle sway of strangers said it's fine, go on and play
The streetlight through the window made a spotlight soft and blue
And somewhere in that nervous hush I found out what was true

Soft and slow I remember the night
My voice was a feather, my hands not quite right
The microphone trembled, the small crowd was kind
That little open mic still plays in my mind

A thousand stages later and the lights are all so bright
but I still hum the melody of that first ordinary night

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The coffee's gone cold on the windowpane
Soft rain on the rooftop, a gentle refrain
I watered the flowers you left by the door
The morning is quiet, you're not here no more

But the sun still rises over the bay
The ocean keeps breathing, carrying it away
I'll sway to the silence, I'll hum your name
The morning after, nothing's the same

Your chair by the table holds the shape of goodbye
A seagull is drifting across the pale sky
The samba is softer, the rhythm runs slow
There's nowhere to be and nowhere to go

But the sun still rises over the bay
The ocean keeps breathing, carrying it away
I'll sway to the silence, I'll hum your name
The morning after, nothing's the same

So I pour two cups out of habit and grace
And I leave one untouched in your usual place
The waves wash the sorrow back into the sea
And the morning, the morning is holding me

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Same old streetlight, same cracked sidewalk glow
Funny how I never saw you years ago
Now you're standing where the corner store used to be
And suddenly this town means everything to me

Falling, falling, right back where I'm from
You're the reason that I never wanna run
Hometown heartbeat, slow and warm and true
I came back for nothing and I found you

We drove past the school where we both used to play
Different hallways, but we ended up the same
Your laugh sounds like summers I forgot I knew
Every road in this city leads me back to you

Falling, falling, right back where I'm from
You're the reason that I never wanna run
Hometown heartbeat, slow and warm and true
I came back for nothing and I found you

Maybe love was waiting on these familiar streets
Maybe home was always more than just concrete
Ooh, baby, take my hand, let's stay a while
This old town never looked so good 'til your smile

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We set sail at midnight under a blood-red moon
The captain said the crates would make us rich real soon
But something in the hold was whispering my name
And nothing on this ocean's ever been the same

Oh, we're carrying a curse across the sea
Every wave is screaming, set the cargo free
We can't turn back now, we're too far from the shore
This cursed cargo's gonna sink us evermore

The compass spins in circles, the stars all disappear
The sailors started praying, I can taste their fear
There's a glow beneath the canvas that should not exist
We're sailing through a darkness no one can resist

Oh, we're carrying a curse across the sea
Every wave is screaming, set the cargo free
We can't turn back now, we're too far from the shore
This cursed cargo's gonna sink us evermore

Throw it overboard, let the ocean take the blame
But the water only echoes back my name
We're ghosts upon the water, we're lost without a sound
The cursed cargo's calling and we're going down

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the back road, headlights burning low
Jars of liquid fire in the trunk below
She danced into the shadows where the lawmen wait
One wrong step, my darling, and we tempt our fate

Dance with me, dance with the danger tonight
The moonshine is running, the sirens ignite
One two three, we spin through the dark
A run gone wrong leaves a permanent mark

The bottle hit the gravel and the whiskey caught aflame
The deputy was laughing as he called out my name
She pulled me through the willows where the river runs cold
A tango of the desperate, a story never told

Dance with me, dance with the danger tonight
The moonshine is running, the sirens ignite
One two three, we spin through the dark
A run gone wrong leaves a permanent mark

So hold me close as the engine starts to scream
We gambled on the moonlight and a foolish dream
The music keeps playing though the night fell apart
A tango and a bullet through a desperate heart

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The door just slammed, now the silence is loud
Words still hanging like a thundercloud
We burned so bright that we burned it all down
Now it's just the echo, just an empty sound

In the quiet, in the quiet after
Where's the sound of our laughter
Break it down, break it down low
In the silence I don't know where to go

Heartbeat pounding like a kick in the dark
Every angry word left a permanent mark
I can hear you breathing in the room next door
We said too much and we meant it all the more

In the quiet, in the quiet after
Where's the sound of our laughter
Break it down, break it down low
In the silence I don't know where to go

Drop the bass on the wreckage of the night
Maybe morning's gonna make it right
I still love you when the noise all fades
In the quiet is the truth that stays

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. on a frequency they can't find
Broadcasting freedom to the open mind
A rusty antenna on a rooftop high
Sending soul and static to the velvet sky

We're the voice they can't turn off
Riding the airwaves, riding it soft
Pirate radio, keep it alive
Tune in, baby, let the signal survive

The authorities are knocking but they're always too slow
We play the songs they don't want you to know
A whisper in the wires, a beat in the night
Underground and golden, we keep burning bright

We're the voice they can't turn off
Riding the airwaves, riding it soft
Pirate radio, keep it alive
Tune in, baby, let the signal survive

So turn your dial to the edge of the band
Where the music is free and the truth's in your hand
They can't silence what the people wanna hear
Pirate radio, loud and clear

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The fireworks are singing in the streets tonight
But I'm under the hospital's fluorescent light
Two shifts on my shoulders, my feet on the floor
While the city is dancing right outside the door

Ay, ay, ay, I'm working through the night
With my hands full of mercy and my heart full of fight
The holiday is calling but my people need me here
So I'll give them all my love and I'll dry every tear

A child holds my hand and I sing him to sleep
The promises of healing are the ones that I keep
My family is waiting with a plate on the table
But I'll stay 'til the morning, as long as I'm able

Ay, ay, ay, I'm working through the night
With my hands full of mercy and my heart full of fight
The holiday is calling but my people need me here
So I'll give them all my love and I'll dry every tear

Viva the nurses who never go home
The quiet little heroes who stand all alone
With trumpets in my spirit and a song in my chest
I'll work through the holiday and give it my best

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
I loaded up the pickup when the moon was hanging low
Mason jars of lightning, more than the law should know
My mama told me, son, don't you run that road tonight
But I needed all that money so I chased the pale moonlight

Oh, the run went wrong, Lord, the run went wrong
The sirens started singing me a sorrowful song
I'm praying to the heavens as I'm flying down the line
Nothing but trouble in this moonshine of mine

The tire blew out on the bridge by the mill
The whiskey hit the river and it's running there still
The sheriff had me cornered with his hand on the door
And I knew that I'd never run that road no more

Oh, the run went wrong, Lord, the run went wrong
The sirens started singing me a sorrowful song
I'm praying to the heavens as I'm flying down the line
Nothing but trouble in this moonshine of mine

Now I'm sitting in a cell with the morning coming through
Thinking 'bout the dollars and the dreams I never knew
Some roads ain't worth the riches, some lessons cut you deep
The moonshine took my freedom while the whole world was asleep

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Neon flickers dim on the assembly line
The machines are silent for the last time
Fathers gather slow at the rusted gate
A whole town holding its breath, holding its fate

The lights go out, the engines die
We watch the smoke fade from the sky
A factory closing, a fading dream
Ghosts in the glow of a midnight machine

Thirty years of metal, thirty years of sweat
A paycheck and a purpose we can't forget
The whistle blows its final, lonely tone
And we walk into a future on our own

The lights go out, the engines die
We watch the smoke fade from the sky
A factory closing, a fading dream
Ghosts in the glow of a midnight machine

Synthesized memories in the cooling dark
Empty parking lots and a dying spark
But we built this city with our hands and pride
And that fire keeps burning deep inside

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up on a corner where the dreams ran dry
Concrete and shadows underneath the sky
But I heard a soft rhythm in the city noise
A quiet little melody, a hopeful voice

I'm making it out, I'm making it out
Leaving behind all the fear and the doubt
Swaying to a new song, soft and free
The dead-end block don't define me

The streetlights are fading in my rearview glass
Every broken promise is a thing of the past
I carry my people in the beat of my heart
A gentle bossa rhythm was my brand new start

I'm making it out, I'm making it out
Leaving behind all the fear and the doubt
Swaying to a new song, soft and free
The dead-end block don't define me

So I'll dance by the ocean where I always belonged
Turned a dead-end corner into a beautiful song
The sun's on my shoulders, the breeze in my soul
I made it, I made it, I'm finally whole

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The whole world's celebrating, lights up on the street
But I'm lacing up my shoes for another sixteen-hour beat
My phone's full of messages, wish you were here
But somebody's gotta hold the hands and calm the fear

So I'll work through the holiday, I'll work through the night
I'm somebody's miracle under hospital light
They don't see my tired eyes, they don't know my name
But I'd do it all over, I'd do it again

Room by room I'm walking with a smile on my face
Every heartbeat matters in this quiet sacred place
My coffee's gone cold but my spirit's still warm
I'm the calm in somebody's holiday storm

So I'll work through the holiday, I'll work through the night
I'm somebody's miracle under hospital light
They don't see my tired eyes, they don't know my name
But I'd do it all over, I'd do it again

When the morning comes and my shift is finally through
I'll know that I gave everything I had to give to you
No confetti, no parade, just a job that I love
Being somebody's angel, sent from up above

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I been carrying this weight for a long, long time
Holding onto anger like it's some kind of crime
But you're already gone, baby, gone from this earth
And I'm learning what forgiveness is really worth

So I let it go, let it go, let it go now
I forgive you, I forgive you, somehow
You can't hear me but I'm saying it loud
Letting all the bitterness fall off like a cloud

There's a groove in my spirit that was locked up tight
Now the bassline's moving and it feels so right
I talk to your picture and I finally smile
Been holding this grudge, baby, all this while

So I let it go, let it go, let it go now
I forgive you, I forgive you, somehow
You can't hear me but I'm saying it loud
Letting all the bitterness fall off like a cloud

Funk it up, set my heavy heart free
Forgiveness ain't for you, it's a gift to me
Dance on the grave of the pain that we knew
I'm finally living, and I'm letting go of you

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
Yeah, I lost it in the trenches, lost it in the dark
Prayers went unanswered, left a hole in my heart
I stopped going to church, stopped saying His name
Walking through the fire, only had myself to blame

But I found it again, yeah, I found it again
Through the pain and the rain, I'm back where I been
Lost my faith but it came right back
God was never gone, I just lost the track

Grinding in the night, no light at the end
Money couldn't fill it, neither could a friend
Then I hit my knees on the bathroom floor
And I felt something open that was closed before

But I found it again, yeah, I found it again
Through the pain and the rain, I'm back where I been
Lost my faith but it came right back
God was never gone, I just lost the track

From the bottom to the top, I was tested in the storm
Now the spirit in my chest keeps me steady and warm
They can't take it from me, no, it's locked in my soul
Lost it, found it, now I'm finally whole

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Driving up north where the cold winds blow
Under a sky doing a cosmic show
Green and purple dancing in the freezing air
Nature dropping funk like nobody's there

The northern lights, they're getting down tonight
Groovin' in the heavens, oh what a sight
Sky on fire with a funky glow
The aurora's putting on a soul-train show

My baby's by the fire and her eyes are bright
Reflecting all the colors of the dancing light
We sway to the rhythm of the silent sky
A universe of funk just rolling on by

The northern lights, they're getting down tonight
Groovin' in the heavens, oh what a sight
Sky on fire with a funky glow
The aurora's putting on a soul-train show

Let the bassline ripple like the lights up high
Get up off your feet underneath that sky
The cosmos got the funk and it's calling your name
Dance beneath the colors, baby, never the same

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
There was a time I stopped believing in anything at all
The lights went out inside me and I watched my whole world fall
I screamed into the silence but the silence never spoke
Every promise that I held onto felt like it was broke

But I'm finding it again, finding it again
Like a light breaking through where the darkness had been
I lost it for a while but it's coming back to me
Faith was always there, I just couldn't see

I wandered through the valley with the weight upon my chest
Thinking I had nothing, thinking I had failed the test
Then a stranger showed me kindness and it cracked me open wide
And I felt a little flicker of the hope I kept inside

But I'm finding it again, finding it again
Like a light breaking through where the darkness had been
I lost it for a while but it's coming back to me
Faith was always there, I just couldn't see

So I'll lift my eyes up to the sky and let it all go
Every fear that held me down, every doubt I used to know
I'm not afraid of falling 'cause I know now I can rise
There's a fire in my soul and a new light in my eyes

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Table number nine, I was watching you arrive
Cousin of the bride, you came alone tonight
The DJ dropped the beat and you caught my eye
No names, no plans, just the bass and the lights

Baila conmigo, stranger, hold me close
The cake is cut but I don't wanna go
One more song before the morning glows
Dance with me, dance with me, nobody knows

Your heels in your hand, my tie on the floor
We spun past the toast and out the side door
The garden lights swayed, the music still poured
Two strangers laughing, who could ask for more

Baila conmigo, stranger, hold me close
The cake is cut but I don't wanna go
One more song before the morning glows
Dance with me, dance with me, nobody knows

Maybe a number, maybe goodbye
Maybe just this, you and I and the night
The band's packing up but my heart's still loud
Found you for a moment in a wedding crowd

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Down the same gray street where I learned to walk
There you were again, and we started to talk
The baker still bakes, the old clock still turns
But something in my chest is starting to burn

Oh, the clarinet cries the way that I feel
Home was just a place till you made it real
Dance with me wild in the cobblestone square
I fell for the girl who was always right there

We used to be children in the very same school
Now your laughter breaks every careful rule
The violin spins and we kick up the dust
All of these years and at last it was us

Oh, the clarinet cries the way that I feel
Home was just a place till you made it real
Dance with me wild in the cobblestone square
I fell for the girl who was always right there

The whole town will whisper, the whole town will sing
They'll dance at our wedding come the warm of the spring
I traveled the world to discover the truth
Love was waiting at home like it waited in youth

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
They folded the dough in the gold of the morning
They braided my hair without any warning
Lined like a map of the years she had crossed
Holding the warmth of the people she lost

Abuela, your hands, how they held up the sky
They wiped every tear, they taught me to fly
They trembled at last but they never let go
Your hands are the dance that I still seem to know

A ring worn so thin from the work and the prayer
A touch on my cheek that is still somehow there
She danced in the kitchen, she swayed to the strings
Those hands were the truest of beautiful things

Abuela, your hands, how they held up the sky
They wiped every tear, they taught me to fly
They trembled at last but they never let go
Your hands are the dance that I still seem to know

Now I knead the same dough in the very same light
And I feel her beside me each Sunday night
The bandoneon sighs and I understand
I carry forever the warmth of her hand

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Dusty corner cafe, fairy lights and rain
Five bucks for the entry and a mic with no name
My hands wouldn't steady, my voice barely came
Three people clapping but I felt the same flame

Yeah, I still hear that night in the back of my mind
The shaky first verse, the kid trying to find
A little bit braver each time that I climbed
That stage was the start, that open mic, that time

Vinyl crackle memory, lo-fi and slow
The espresso machine and the warm purple glow
I forgot half my words but I let myself go
Planted a seed that I needed to grow

Yeah, I still hear that night in the back of my mind
The shaky first verse, the kid trying to find
A little bit braver each time that I climbed
That stage was the start, that open mic, that time

Now the rooms got bigger, the lights got more bright
But I close my eyes and I'm back in that night
Thank you to the kid who was scared but still tried
You're the reason I'm here, you're the reason I ride

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They came with a contract and a shiny new pen
Said son change your words and we'll make you a friend
But I wrote about the dirt and the river and the rain
And I won't trade my truth for a Nashville chain

I'd rather be broke with an honest old song
Than rich singing words that I know to be wrong
They can keep their gold and their bright city lights
I'll keep my pen and my truth and my nights

Small town diner, dollar in the jar
Playing for the farmhands, never gonna be a star
But the words that I sing, they come from my soul
A poet ain't a poet once he's bought and sold

I'd rather be broke with an honest old song
Than rich singing words that I know to be wrong
They can keep their gold and their bright city lights
I'll keep my pen and my truth and my nights

Maybe I'll die with an empty old hat
But the ones who really heard me, they'll remember me for that
The man who wrote it real and never looked away
The poet who refused to sell his soul away

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning and the screen's glowing blue
Thumb hovering over a name I once knew
I typed it and deleted it a hundred times through
I know what is right but I'm reaching for you

Don't hit send, don't hit send, let it fade with the night
This ain't gonna fix what we couldn't make right
But the cursor keeps blinking in the soft city light
Texting someone I shouldn't, knowing it ain't right

Rain on the window, beat slow and low
Memories playing on a loop, soft and slow
You're somebody's now and I gotta let go
But my fingers keep typing what my heart shouldn't show

Don't hit send, don't hit send, let it fade with the night
This ain't gonna fix what we couldn't make right
But the cursor keeps blinking in the soft city light
Texting someone I shouldn't, knowing it ain't right

Finally I closed it, set the phone face down
Let the silence be louder than the wanting to drown
Some doors stay shut for a reason, I know
So I breathe in the dark and I let myself go

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Mud on our boots and the sun going down
Three days of music in this little tent town
The horns are still blaring, the crowd's still alive
Last night of the festival, we're gonna survive

Pick it up, pick it up, one more time tonight
Skank till the morning, hold the moment tight
Tomorrow we go home but right now we're free
Last night of the festival, just you and me

Wristbands all faded, voices all gone
But nobody's leaving till they play the last song
Strangers turned brothers in the kick of the drum
We danced through the chaos and the best part's to come

Pick it up, pick it up, one more time tonight
Skank till the morning, hold the moment tight
Tomorrow we go home but right now we're free
Last night of the festival, just you and me

When the speakers go silent and the lights all die
We'll carry this summer in the back of our minds
So jump one more time before the dawn arrives
Last night of the festival, we're staying alive

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Four cold words on a glowing screen
The cruelest goodbye that I've ever seen
No door slammed shut, no tears in the rain
Just a buzz in my hand and a lifetime of pain

Ay, you ended it all with the tap of a thumb
Years of my heart and now I'm left numb
The guitar it weeps what you couldn't say
You loved me, you left me, in a text, in a day

I stamp on the floor and I clap to the sky
Where is the courage to look in my eye
The palmas are bleeding, the cante is raw
You broke something sacred and walked out the door

Ay, you ended it all with the tap of a thumb
Years of my heart and now I'm left numb
The guitar it weeps what you couldn't say
You loved me, you left me, in a text, in a day

But I'll dance through this fire, I'll burn it all clean
I'll turn all this sorrow to something serene
You deleted me cold but I'll rise from the ash
My heart is not something you delete in a flash

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody picked us, we were last on the list
A roster of kids that the scouts always missed
But we showed up to practice in the cold and the rain
Now it's the final and we're back in the game

Four minutes left and the gym's getting loud
Little team nobody believed in, look at us now
Whatever the score when the buzzer comes down
We already won just by sticking around

Lo-fi heartbeat, slow and steady drum
Coach in the huddle saying look how far we've come
Down by a basket but we play with no fear
Everything we worked for has led us right here

Four minutes left and the gym's getting loud
Little team nobody believed in, look at us now
Whatever the score when the buzzer comes down
We already won just by sticking around

Years from now we'll remember this floor
The squeak of the sneakers, the roar by the door
Win it or lose it, we'll always be proud
The underdogs who finally got loud

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Neon on the dashboard, eighteen wheels rolling
Three in the morning and my heart keeps strolling
Grab the old phone, dial the number I know
Mississippi miles between me and home

Hey baby, it's me, just calling to say
I'm chasing these headlights to get back your way
Keep the porch light burning and the coffee on slow
Three a.m. funky and I'm missing you so

Groove in the diesel, bass in the night
The radio's humming and the gauges glow bright
Kiss the kids for me, tell 'em daddy's en route
Two more days driving and I'm turning about

Hey baby, it's me, just calling to say
I'm chasing these headlights to get back your way
Keep the porch light burning and the coffee on slow
Three a.m. funky and I'm missing you so

The highway is lonely but your voice is my fuel
A little bit of sugar makes the long road cool
I'll be home by Friday with my arms open wide
Till then I keep rolling with you by my side

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Empty parking lot on a Sunday morning
Hands at ten and two, no warning
You turned the key and I held my breath
My little girl, almost grown, no less

Go on and drive, baby, easy and slow
Daddy's right here, I won't let you go
The road's gonna bend and the world's gonna call
But I taught you to steer through it all

First time you stalled it you laughed till you cried
Then you found the gas and you started to glide
Yesterday I rocked you, today you're at the wheel
Lord, you don't know how this moment feel

Go on and drive, baby, easy and slow
Daddy's right here, I won't let you go
The road's gonna bend and the world's gonna call
But I taught you to steer through it all

One day soon you'll be driving away
Down some highway, far from today
But a piece of me rides in that passenger seat
Every mile that you go, every soul that you meet

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Same wooden pew where my grandmother prayed
Same stained glass light where my whole childhood stayed
But something inside me has come to the end
So I slip out the back, not as foe, not as friend

I'm leaving quiet, no anger, no shout
Just a soul that has questions it has to figure out
Thank you for the hymns and the warmth and the bread
But I gotta go find my own God instead

The steeple still rises in the gold evening sun
The bells still are ringing for everyone
I loved this old building, I loved every face
But I can't keep pretending I have found my place

I'm leaving quiet, no anger, no shout
Just a soul that has questions it has to figure out
Thank you for the hymns and the warmth and the bread
But I gotta go find my own God instead

Maybe the spirit's out there in the field
In the wind and the river and the wounds that healed
I'm not losing faith, I'm just changing the door
Walking out gentle to go searching for more

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Keys in your hand and your eyes open wide
Seventeen summers and you're ready to ride
I buckle up next to you, try to stay calm
My baby's the driver and I'm holding on

So drive, drive, take the wheel of your life
I'll be right beside you through the turns and the nights
Green light, go on, the whole world is yours
I taught you to drive, now I'm opening doors

You hit the brakes hard and we both start to laugh
Then smooth down the road on the second half
I remember your stroller, your first little shoes
Now you're checking your mirrors, you got nothing to lose

So drive, drive, take the wheel of your life
I'll be right beside you through the turns and the nights
Green light, go on, the whole world is yours
I taught you to drive, now I'm opening doors

One day the seat next to you will be free
And you'll drive off to chase your own dreams
But every road that you take, every star up above
Know you learned how to steer in a car full of love

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon sign humming on the corner of Main
Coffee always brewing in the cold and the rain
The jukebox is swinging and the grill's never cold
A little bit of heaven for the lonely and the bold

Come on down to the all-night diner, baby
Where the eggs are hot and the band plays maybe
Midnight or morning, we never close the door
Swing on in, there's always room for more

The cab drivers gather and the dreamers all meet
Apple pie waitress with the dancing feet
A trumpet on the radio, a booth in the back
Where the whole tired city comes to find its track

Come on down to the all-night diner, baby
Where the eggs are hot and the band plays maybe
Midnight or morning, we never close the door
Swing on in, there's always room for more

When the rest of the world has turned out the light
We're still serving comfort through the heart of the night
So grab you a stool and we'll dance till the dawn
At the little all-night diner where the music plays on

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night the same, I lift off the ground
The city falls away without a sound
Arms become wings in the velvet of dark
Chasing the moon like a runaway spark

I'm flying, flying, over it all
No gravity, baby, I never will fall
Is it the spirit or is it my soul
That keeps rising up to make me whole

Smooth Rhodes humming in the back of my mind
Leaving the heavy, the worry behind
Rooftops like rivers, the stars within reach
There's a freedom up here that I'm trying to teach

I'm flying, flying, over it all
No gravity, baby, I never will fall
Is it the spirit or is it my soul
That keeps rising up to make me whole

Then I wake to the morning and my feet on the floor
But a part of me's still up there wanting more
Maybe the dream is the truest of me
The one who remembers what it means to be free

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I memorized the cracks in the ceiling tile
Learned the hymns before I learned to doubt
Grandma's perfume and the same tired smile
Nobody noticed me slipping out

I didn't slam the heavy wooden door
Didn't write a letter, didn't make a scene
Just stopped showing up like I did before
Left a space where a boy had been

I'm not angry, I'm just quiet now
I traded amen for the open road
I'll keep the kindness, I'll forget the vow
Lay it down, this weight I never chose to hold

The parking lot still empties out at noon
The steeple holds its shadow on the lawn
They'll sing the same old verses come next June
And never feel the corner where I'm gone

I'm not angry, I'm just quiet now
I traded amen for the open road
I'll keep the kindness, I'll forget the vow
Lay it down, this weight I never chose to hold

Maybe God's still out there in the trees
Maybe she was never in those pews
I found a stranger language for my knees
A prayer that doesn't ask me to choose

I'm not angry, I'm just quiet now
I walk out lighter than I came
I'll keep the kindness, I'll forget the vow
And I won't say your name

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Ten and two, baby, hands on the wheel
Deep breath in, tell me how it feel
Mirror, signal, let the engine hum
We got all night till the morning come

You were five years old on a tricycle
Now you're sixteen, this is physical
Foot off the brake, let it roll real slow
Trust the road and just let it go

Drive, drive, eyes on the line
Everything's gonna be fine
I'm right here, you're doing alright
We'll take the long way home tonight
Drive, drive, into the glow
There's so much I want you to know
But for now just keep it slow
And drive, baby, drive

Green light glowing on your nervous face
You found the rhythm, you found the pace
One day soon you'll be gone from view
So I memorize this, me and you

Drive, drive, eyes on the line
Everything's gonna be fine
I'm right here, you're doing alright
We'll take the long way home tonight

Let the city lights blur into a stream
You're steering straight through somebody's dream
My little girl with the world ahead
Go on, go on, like I always said

Drive, drive, into the glow
There's so much I want you to know
But for now just keep it slow
And drive, baby, drive

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
They hung a paper on the iron gate
The ink still wet, the morning gray
Thirty years I clocked in there
And now they're locking it away

My father pressed the same cold steel
His hands like maps of all the years
He told me, son, this town will hold
But towns can crumble same as years

And the whistle won't blow at the break of day
The smokestack stands like a stone
The river keeps rolling the same old way
But the men, the men have gone home

Delia's diner's closing too
No lunch crowd left to feed
The hardware man, the union hall
All planted in the same dead seed

And the whistle won't blow at the break of day
The smokestack stands like a stone
The river keeps rolling the same old way
But the men, the men have gone home

Where does a working man put his pride
When the work just up and dies
I'll tell my boy a different trade
And I'll teach him not to memorize

So here's to the rust and the empty floor
To the lunch pails set on the shelf
A town's just people, nothing more
And a man's just gotta save himself

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The record turns, the needle sighs
Low lamplight on your tired eyes
Forty winters, here we stand
My palm still fits inside your hand

No music left but what we know
We move the way the old clocks go
Slow, so slow, around the floor
The steps we've taken a thousand times before

Hold me here in the fading light
Let the whole world drift from sight
There's nothing left for us to say
So we let the silence sway
One more turn, one more breath
One last dance before the rest

The house exhales its quiet hum
The years all gather, all become
A single song without a sound
Your heartbeat is the only ground

Hold me here in the fading light
Let the whole world drift from sight
There's nothing left for us to say
So we let the silence sway

If this is how the story ends
Not lovers, no, but something more than friends
Then let me memorize the weight
Of leaning here, of being late

The record stops, we do not move
Still swaying in the empty groove

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Open bar and a borrowed tie
Didn't know a soul, gonna give it a try
Then you grabbed my hand off the folding chair
Said come on slowpoke, the band's right there

Horn section blasting, upbeat skank
I didn't catch your name and the punch I drank
But your shoes were red and your laugh was loud
We cut a hole right through the crowd

Pick it up, pick it up, one two three
You don't know me and I don't know thee
But the trumpet's calling and the floor is free
So dance, sweet stranger, dance with me

Cousin of the bride, friend of the groom
Doesn't matter who in this sweaty room
Up on the offbeat, down on the floor
Then back on our feet for an encore more

Pick it up, pick it up, one two three
You don't know me and I don't know thee
But the trumpet's calling and the floor is free
So dance, sweet stranger, dance with me

Maybe I'll never see you again
Maybe this song's the start of something then
But the bouquet's flying and the cake is cut
So who needs tomorrow, let's dance it up

Pick it up, pick it up, one two three
You don't know me and I don't know thee
But the trumpet's calling and the floor is free
So dance, sweet stranger, dance with me

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Up before the rooster, down past the moon
Clock in at the diner, clock out way too soon
Then over to the warehouse, lift until I ache
Count up all my dollars, count up my mistake

But I lift my hands, I lift my voice
When I got no money I still got a choice
Lord, you see me running, Lord, you see me try
Carry me over, carry me high

Rent went up in April, light bill's overdue
Baby needs her medicine, I don't know what to do
I prayed on it Sunday, I'll pray on it tonight
The ledger keeps on losing but my soul keeps the fight

So I lift my hands, I lift my voice
When I got no money I still got a choice
Lord, you see me running, Lord, you see me try
Carry me over, carry me high

They say the meek gonna inherit
They say the last shall be the first
Well I been last for a long, long while
So let this be the day you quench my thirst

And I lift my hands, I lift my voice
When I got no money I still got a choice
Lord, you see me running, Lord, you see me try
Carry me over, carry me high
Carry me over, before I die

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Monday morning, slap that bass
Punch the clock with a sleepy face
Flip them burgers, mop that floor
Clock out, run, and clock in more

Second shift got a different name tag
Same tired feet in the same old drag
Wallet's thin but the hustle's thick
Groove so deep but the math don't stick

Two jobs, no sleep, still in the hole
Working my fingers down to the bone
Got rhythm in my pocket but no green to show
Falling behind but I won't let go
Get up, get down, keep that hustle
Flexing every dollar muscle

Overtime, baby, no overtime pay
The rent's a monster I can't keep at bay
But the bassline carries what the bank account won't
I'll dance through the trouble even when I don't

Two jobs, no sleep, still in the hole
Working my fingers down to the bone
Got rhythm in my pocket but no green to show
Falling behind but I won't let go

Ow! Funk don't pay the light bill
But funk's the only thing that's mine still
So I'ma ride this nasty groove
Till the day my luck improves

Two jobs, no sleep, still in the hole
Working my fingers down to the bone
Got rhythm in my pocket but no green to show
Falling behind but I won't let go

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
One plate in the cabinet, used to be two
Boiling up a pot like she taught me to
Salt on my fingers, salt on my face
Empty seat across, an empty space

Recipe card in her looping hand
Don't know the measures, don't understand
Too much rice, the pan's too wide
Cooking for a ghost still here inside

Table for one, table for one
Light one candle when the day is done
Used to be we, now it's just me
Learning how to feed a memory
Table for one, table for one
Still set a plate for the missing one

Freezer full of meals the neighbors made
But I gotta learn before the casseroles fade
Her apron on the hook, I tie it slow
Talking to the kitchen, nowhere else to go

Table for one, table for one
Light one candle when the day is done
Used to be we, now it's just me
Learning how to feed a memory

Thirty years she fed me right
Now I burn the eggs and I cry at night
But every onion that I dice
Feels a little like she's giving me advice

So I plate it up, I sit, I pray
Eat alone but I made it today

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody picked us, nobody cared
Gave us no headlines, gave us no prayer
Forty long minutes, the clock running down
Last in the standings of a forgotten town

We practiced in shadows, we played in the rain
Wore secondhand jerseys and swallowed the pain
Now the lights are on us, the whole world can see
The kids they wrote off, the team that should not be

Oh, leave it on the floor, leave it all behind
Every drop of heart, every piece of mind
Win or lose tonight, we already won
The moment we believed, the moment we begun
Leave it on the floor, oh, leave it all

Down by a basket, the seconds tick slow
Coach looks around at the team that he grew
No more tomorrow, no more next year
Just this one shot and a whole life of fear

So leave it on the floor, leave it all behind
Every drop of heart, every piece of mind
Win or lose tonight, we already won
The moment we believed, the moment we begun

They'll forget the final score someday
But they'll never forget the way we played
Give me your hand, give me your soul
This is the story they'll always be told

Leave it on the floor, leave it all behind
Every drop of heart, every piece of mind
Win or lose tonight, we already won
The moment we believed, the moment we begun

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
You order the same coffee, two sugars, no cream
I know it by heart like a recurring dream
You talk about him while I'm bussing your cup
And I nod and I smile and I never speak up

We grew up on the same dirt road
Carried your books, helped you carry your load
You see a good friend when you look at me
And I see the woman I'll never get to be

So I love you in the quiet, I love you from here
Close enough to touch you, too far to be near
You'll marry in the springtime, I'll stand in the back
Loving you forward while I'm holding it back

I rehearse all the words that I'll never say
Then I crumple them up and I throw them away
You hug me goodbye and you don't even know
That you're breaking my heart every time that you go

So I love you in the quiet, I love you from here
Close enough to touch you, too far to be near
You'll marry in the springtime, I'll stand in the back
Loving you forward while I'm holding it back

Maybe in another life, another town
You'd look up and see me before you settled down
But this is the only life I'm given to live
Loving someone who's got nothing to give

So I love you in the quiet, I love you from here
Close enough to touch you, too far to be near

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
My father was a mountain with a voice like the sea
He'd carry me on shoulders through the heather and the rain
Now his hands they shake like leaves on the old oak tree
And the eyes that knew the whole wide world don't know my name

He taught me how to whistle and to mend the wooden gate
He taught me every story that his father told to him
Now I sit beside his window and I help him hold his plate
