And I sing the songs he gave me when the evening light grows dim

Oh the years they turn like rivers to the sea
And the strongest man I knew is growing frail
So I'll hold him like he held me, tenderly
And I'll be his steady boat through the gale

The fire is burning low and the kettle's gone to rust
The collie at his feet is the last friend that remains
And I'll keep his pipe and bonnet when he turns at last to dust
And I'll walk the hills he walked with his blood inside my veins

Oh the years they turn like rivers to the sea
And the strongest man I knew is growing frail
So I'll hold him like he held me, tenderly
And I'll be his steady boat through the gale

Da, do you remember when you carried me so high?
Now it's my turn, my turn, to lift you to the sky

Oh the years they turn like rivers to the sea
And the strongest man I knew is growing frail
So I'll hold him like he held me, tenderly
And I'll be his steady boat through the gale

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Well I came here alone in my Sunday best
Didn't know a soul in the whole damn place
Then you took my hand, said let's leave the rest
And we slow-dragged slow with a knowing grace

The band played low and the lights went blue
I don't know your name, mama, don't know mine to you
But your hand on my shoulder felt like coming home
Two lonesome strangers and we ain't alone

Oh dance with me, dance with me, one more song
We both know this feeling, it can't last long
When the band packs up and the lights come on
You'll be a memory, baby, you'll be gone
So dance with me, dance with me, one more time

The groom kissed the bride and the cake got cut
But we kept on swaying in a world we shut
You laid your head down where my heartbeat plays
And I felt twenty years melt away

Oh dance with me, dance with me, one more song
We both know this feeling, it can't last long
When the band packs up and the lights come on
You'll be a memory, baby, you'll be gone

I got the wedding blues, the bittersweet kind
Found you on the floor, gonna leave you behind
But for these three minutes you are mine, all mine

So dance with me, dance with me, one more song
We both know this feeling, it can't last long

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. and the highway hums
A payphone glow where the cold rain comes
I dial the number I know by heart
Miles of dark keeping us apart

The receiver crackles, you're half asleep
I just wanted to hear you breathe
The diesel's running, the road's still long
I'll be home before the porch light's gone

Can you hear me over the静 distance
Over the wires and the static hiss
I'm a thousand miles from your bedroom door
But I'm holding on, I'm holding on for more

Tell the kids their daddy called
Tell them I saw the mountains tall
I'll bring a postcard from the edge of the night
Keep the kitchen lamp a little light

Can you hear me over the long distance
Over the wires and the static hiss
I'm a thousand miles from your bedroom door
But I'm holding on, I'm holding on for more

The sun will rise on a different state
The white lines blur, the hours grow late
But every mile is a mile toward you
Every mile is a promise I'll get through

So go back to sleep, I'll let you go
I just needed you to know
The road is long but the road bends home
And I am never quite alone

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
I held the phone for a good ten minutes
Watched my thumb just hover over your name
Five years of pride and a wall between us
And nobody left to take the blame

It rang twice, then I heard you breathing
Your voice was older than I remember
You said hello like you weren't believing
Like spring had come back into December

And I said Mama, it's been too long
I'm sorry for the words and the years I was wrong
There's a whole lot of living I want you to see
If there's still a little room left in your heart for me
Mama, it's been too long

You cried a little, said you'd been praying
That the phone would ring and it'd be my call
All the stubborn things we both kept saying
Just melted down to nothing at all

And I said Mama, it's been too long
I'm sorry for the words and the years I was wrong
There's a whole lot of living I want you to see
If there's still a little room left in your heart for me

Don't know how many years we got left
But I don't want to waste another one
So put the coffee on, I'm coming home
Your prodigal, your only son

And I said Mama, it's been too long
I'm sorry for the words and the years I was wrong

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the avenue, windows down low
Took the long way home, the way I used to go
Second floor light, it's burning like before
But it's somebody else behind that door

We used to dance in that little room
Jollof on the stove, your sweet perfume
Now I'm rolling slow with the radio on
Chasing a ghost of a love that's gone

Driving past, driving past your door
The city remembers what we were before
I no fit stop but I no fit go
My heart still parking on your old window
Driving past, oh, driving slow
Somewhere you dey, but I no go know

The streetlight catches the place we'd kiss
Funny how a corner can hold this much
I dey alright, I dey tell myself
But I drove this far just to feel your touch

Driving past, driving past your door
The city remembers what we were before
I no fit stop but I no fit go
My heart still parking on your old window

Maybe you moved on, maybe you stay
Maybe you also drive my way some day
But tonight na me alone wey dey cruise
Through every street wey still get your name

Driving past, driving past your door
The city remembers what we were before
I no fit stop but I no fit go
My heart still parking on your old window

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The candles in the window burn for someone else tonight
While I walk these quiet hallways in the pale fluorescent light
The bells are ringing far away, the snow falls on the town
But here the monitors are singing as I lay my burden down

My children sleep without me and my table sits unset
A double on the holiday, a debt I won't forget
I hold a stranger's trembling hand and whisper that it's fine
Though no one holds this weary heart of mine

Oh carry me, ye northern winds, across the frozen sea
For every soul I'm tending is a prayer come back to me
I'll trade my Christmas morning for the breath in someone's chest
And find my peace in giving when I cannot find my rest

The kettle in the break room is the only warmth I know
A paper crown, a plastic feast, and twelve more hours to go
But when the dawn comes creeping and the ward is finally still
I'll bless the hands that held the line against the bitter chill

So light a candle, light it slow, for those who cannot come
Who mend the world in silence till the rising of the sun

Oh carry me, ye northern winds, across the frozen sea
For every soul I'm tending is a prayer come back to me
I'll trade my Christmas morning for the breath in someone's chest
And find my peace in giving when I cannot find my rest

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
The harbor lights are fading as the morning turns to grey
I kissed my mother's weathered hands and turned my face away
The green hills of my homeland sink beneath the rolling foam
I carry in a wooden chest the only piece of home

Farewell, farewell, my emerald shore, I'll see your face no more
The ocean wide between us now, a thousand miles of war
But every wave that breaks below still sings your ancient song
And in my heart I'll carry you wherever I belong

The sea is cold and hungry and the nights are long and black
There's nothing left behind me and there's no road leading back
A stranger in a foreign land with calluses for gold
I'll build a life from nothing in a country grey and cold

They speak of streets of plenty where the rivers run with wine
But I would trade their cities for one acre that was mine
The fiddle in my memory plays a tune from long ago
Of turf fires and of laughter and the names I used to know

My children's children someday may forget the words I knew
But may they feel the longing in the bones I carry through

Farewell, farewell, my emerald shore, I'll see your face no more
The ocean wide between us now, a thousand miles of war
But every wave that breaks below still sings your ancient song
And in my heart I'll carry you wherever I belong

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Clock on the wall says 2 AM
Coffee gone cold, here I am again
Highlighter dancing 'cross the page
Locked in my room like a bird in a cage

Every word's a blur, but I can't stop now
Gonna make it through, gotta show 'em how

It's just me and the moonlight, me and the grind
Chasing a dream that's stuck in my mind
Nobody's watching, nobody's near
But I'll be somebody, that much is clear
Two in the morning, I'm wide awake
There's a future I'm fighting to make

Phone on silent, the world's asleep
Promises to myself I gotta keep
One more chapter, one more line
This lonely night is gonna make me shine

Eyes getting heavy but my heart says go
There's a fire inside that they'll never know

It's just me and the moonlight, me and the grind
Chasing a dream that's stuck in my mind
Nobody's watching, nobody's near
But I'll be somebody, that much is clear
Two in the morning, I'm wide awake
There's a future I'm fighting to make

When the sun comes up I'll still be standing tall
Every sleepless night was worth it all

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Three little words lit up my phone tonight
But they weren't the three I prayed I'd read
Just "I think we're done" in that cold blue light
And everything we had fell out of my head

No front porch goodbye, no look in your eyes
No tears on a Tennessee road
Just a buzz on the table where my whiskey lies
And a heart that you left in the cold

You ended us in a text message, darlin'
Fourteen years come down to a screen
Didn't even spell my name, just left me starin'
At the loneliest words I've ever seen
Guess that's how love dies in the modern world
With a notification and a goodbye, girl

I read it again like it'd somehow change
Like the letters might rearrange to stay
But the message just sat there, distant and strange
While my whole world slipped away

They say the heart can mend with time
But how do you heal from a single line

You ended us in a text message, darlin'
Fourteen years come down to a screen
Didn't even spell my name, just left me starin'
At the loneliest words I've ever seen

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Close my eyes and the city fades to gold
Feet leave the ground, never doin' what I'm told
Same dream every night, I'm above it all
No gravity, no weight, no walls
Drifting over rooftops, breathing in the clouds
Finally somewhere quiet, far from all the crowds

I'm flying, flying, no reason why
Just me and the moon up in the midnight sky
Wake up to the alarm but I'm still up there
Floating through the dream like I haven't got a care

They say a falling dream means you scared to land
But I'm risin' up with the whole world in my hand
Wingless but I'm soaring, weightless and free
Is the dream just sleep or is it showing me me
Morning comes too soon, pull me back to the ground
But tonight I'll be flying when I lay back down

Maybe the sky is just my mind
Leaving all the heavy stuff behind

I'm flying, flying, no reason why
Just me and the moon up in the midnight sky
Wake up to the alarm but I'm still up there
Floating through the dream like I haven't got a care

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at the dawn, clock out in the dark
A second shift waiting, another fading spark
My hands know the motions, my eyes forget the why
Two jobs and a mountain I can't climb

The bills come like a tide that never turns
I'm pouring out the hours but the lantern barely burns
Every dollar earned is a dollar already spent
I'm running just to stand where I went

And the wheels keep turning, turning, turning
And the fire keeps burning, burning low
I am tired but I am still here
Falling behind but I won't let go

They tell me work is freedom, that the dream is fair
But I'm too tired to dream and there's no one there
The ceiling that I'm chasing only climbs away
Another dawn, another endless day

Rise
Rise
There is something more than this
There has to be something more than this

And the wheels keep turning, turning, turning
And the fire keeps burning, burning low
I am tired but I am still here
Falling behind but I won't let go

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Grew up on the same old street, yeah
Never knew you'd sweep me off my feet, yeah
Same corner store, same setting sun
Now I see you and my heart's undone

Oh, hometown girl, you're my melody
The sweetest thing this town gave me
We danced these roads since we were small
Now I'm falling, falling, falling for it all

We know the same names, the same old song
This is where we both belong
Under the palm tree where we used to play
I'm seeing you in a brand new way

Oh, hometown girl, you're my melody
The sweetest thing this town gave me
We danced these roads since we were small
Now I'm falling, falling, falling for it all

Didn't have to travel far
Love was right here where you are
Every streetlight, every star
Led me right back to where you are

So let the riddim carry us slow
Back to the places only we know
I'll hold you close as the evening calls
In this little town that holds it all

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Mile marker glowing, radio low
Three in the morning, two hundred to go
I dial your number, hope I don't wake the kids
Just need to hear you breathe, baby, that's all it is

The road is a river that won't let me rest
A diesel lullaby singing in my chest
The yellow lines blur into one long prayer
That carries me home to you waiting there

So talk to me, baby, while the highway rolls
Keep me company in these midnight souls
I'm a thousand miles but I'm right by your side
When I hear your voice on this lonesome ride

You whisper you love me, half asleep and warm
And it shelters me through the comin' storm
The cab gets a little less empty now
With you on the line, I'll make it somehow

Don't hang up yet, just stay a while
Let me carry the sound of your tired smile
Through the dark, through the rain, through the breakin' dawn
You're the only reason I keep rollin' on

So talk to me, baby, while the highway rolls
Keep me company in these midnight souls
I'm a thousand miles but I'm right by your side
When I hear your voice on this lonesome ride

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Under the silver moon I drove the dusty road
Jars of fire in the back, a heavy secret load
The night was wide and quiet, the engine ran so sweet
Till the headlights came behind me down the lonely street

Ay, ay, ay, the moon is my witness
Ay, ay, ay, I ran too fast
The trumpets cry for a foolish young man
Whose midnight run could not last

The river bent like a serpent, the tires lost their hold
The jars went crashing, shining, in the canyon dark and cold
I climbed up from the wreckage with the smell of fire and rye
And cursed the greedy spirit that had brought me here to die

The sheriff found my fortune scattered down the hill
The moonshine soaked the desert and it's soaking still
My mother lit a candle, my sweetheart turned away
For the boy who chased the silver moon had thrown his life away

So listen, all you young men, to a tale of foolish pride
The quickest road to ruin is the road you have to hide

Ay, ay, ay, the moon is my witness
Ay, ay, ay, I ran too fast
The trumpets cry for a foolish young man
Whose midnight run could not last

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The rooms all echo empty now, the walls are bare and white
A roll of tape, a final box, beneath the kitchen light
I run my hand along the door where we once marked our years
And seal up all the memories with cardboard and with tears

Oh, this is the last box, the last goodbye
The last little piece of you and I
I'm taping up the love, taping up the pain
Folding up a home that won't come again

That stain upon the carpet from the wine we spilled one June
The window where you used to hum a half-forgotten tune
It's all just dust and shadows now, a key I have to leave
The hardest part of letting go is learning how to grieve

Four walls don't make a home, it's true
A home is just the time I spent with you
And now the time is packed and gone
But somehow love, somehow love lingers on

Oh, this is the last box, the last goodbye
The last little piece of you and I
I'm taping up the love, taping up the pain
Folding up a home that won't come again

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Flour on the counter, biscuits in the pan
Mornin' light come spillin' where the gingham curtains hang
She'd hum an old hymn softly while the coffee start to brew
And that smell of Sunday mornin' is the sweetest thing I knew

Take me back to Grandma's kitchen, cinnamon and grace
Where love was made of buttermilk and a warm and wrinkled face
I'd give back all my years of chasin' just to stand and breathe it in
The smell of Grandma's kitchen and her arms around me again

Fried chicken on a Sunday, peach cobbler in July
She'd slip me extra sugar with a twinkle in her eye
The radio was playin' and the screen door used to creak
And everything was holy in that little kitchen seat

She's been gone for years now, that old house been sold
But sometimes in a bakery I catch that scent of gold
And I'm six years old and barefoot, and the world is soft and small
In the smell of Grandma's kitchen, she's still here through it all

Take me back to Grandma's kitchen, cinnamon and grace
Where love was made of buttermilk and a warm and wrinkled face
I'd give back all my years of chasin' just to stand and breathe it in
The smell of Grandma's kitchen and her arms around me again

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I drove out to the churchyard with the words I never said
Knelt down in the morning dew beside your grassy bed
All the anger that I carried like a stone inside my chest
I came to lay it down at last and finally let it rest

So I forgive you, I forgive you
Though you're too far gone to hear
I'm settin' down this heavy load I carried all these years
It don't change what's broken, it won't bring you back to me
But I forgive you, I forgive you, and at last my heart is free

You hurt me when I was a boy, you weren't the man you should
I spent so long in bitterness when you were gone for good
But hate's a chain that binds the one who's holdin' it the most
So I'm makin' peace this morning with your shadow and your ghost

They say forgiveness is a gift you give yourself
Not for them but for the weight you set down on the shelf
So I'm breathin' out the years of pain
And I'm walkin' out this churchyard light again

So I forgive you, I forgive you
Though you're too far gone to hear
I'm settin' down this heavy load I carried all these years
It don't change what's broken, it won't bring you back to me
But I forgive you, I forgive you, and at last my heart is free

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Little stage, one light, hands shaking in the dark
Fifteen people watching but I came to leave a mark
My voice came out a whisper, then it grew into a flame
From that night I knew that nothing would ever feel the same

Oh, that first night, that first light
I was scared but I was so alive
Look how far, look how far we came
From a little open mic to the whole world know my name
We dey rise, we dey shine, we dey move
That first night gave me something to prove

The mic was old and crackling, the crowd was barely there
But something in that moment lifted me into the air
I think about it sometimes when the stadium go loud
That shaky boy who started it would be so proud

So to anybody dreaming with a beat inside your chest
Just step up to the moment, baby, give it all your best
Every king and every queen had a first and trembling night
Now look at me, oh look at me, I'm standing in the light

Oh, that first night, that first light
I was scared but I was so alive
Look how far, look how far we came
From a little open mic to the whole world know my name

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Green felt table, smoke up in the air
Started out for fun but now there's tension in the chair
Stacks getting bigger, eyes getting cold
Watched a friendship crumble for a pile of fool's gold
He pushed it all in, said he couldn't fold
This ain't about the money now, it's pride takin' hold

We went too far, we went too far
Chasin' every hand like we don't know who we are
Shoulda walked away when the laughin' turned to war
But we played one hand too many, now we went too far

Deed to the car, watch off his wrist
The dealer dealt a fate that nobody could've missed
He lost it all, then he reached for somethin' worse
A full house turned a blessing to a curse
I grabbed his arm, said "man, it's just a game"
But the look in his eyes wasn't ever gonna be the same

Some bets you can't take back
Some bridges burn down to the track
We were brothers 'fore the cards were dealt
Now there's nothing but the silence that we felt

We went too far, we went too far
Chasin' every hand like we don't know who we are
Shoulda walked away when the laughin' turned to war
But we played one hand too many, now we went too far

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Grab your coat and take my hand, the sky's about to dance
Green and gold are swingin' low, c'mon let's take a chance
Up above the frozen pines the colors start to sway
Like a big band in the heavens playin' all the night away

Oh, the northern lights, the northern lights
Swingin' 'cross the velvet nights
Ribbons of emerald, ribbons of rose
Dancin' where the cold wind blows
We'll cut a rug beneath that glow
With the brightest band you'll ever know

The trumpet of the cosmos blows a shimmer 'cross the dark
And every star's a spotlight on this frosty little park
I'll twirl you 'neath the curtain of that flickering parade
The finest show on Earth, my dear, and baby, free of trade

So let 'em flicker, let 'em fly
That jukebox painted in the sky
There's no orchestra below
Like the heavens puttin' on a show

Oh, the northern lights, the northern lights
Swingin' 'cross the velvet nights
Ribbons of emerald, ribbons of rose
Dancin' where the cold wind blows
We'll cut a rug beneath that glow
With the brightest band you'll ever know

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
It's two AM and my thumb's a liar
Scrolling down to your name like a live wire
I deleted you twice, you came back thrice
My phone's a loaded gun and I keep paying the price

Everyone said don't, so of course I did
Typing out the truth like a runaway kid
The screen lights green, my whole face glows guilty
Three little dots and the room gets filthy

Send it, send it, I already hit send
This is how the good intentions end
I know better, I do it anyway
Delete the evidence, do it again someday

My heart's a riot the cops won't break up
You're the bad idea I keep waking up

Throw the phone in the river, I'll just swim
Drag it out and text you on a whim
There's no app for the thing I lack
I hit send and there's no taking it back

Send it, send it, I already hit send
This is how the good intentions end
I know better, I do it anyway
Delete the evidence, do it again someday

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Desk lamp humming, the rest of the house is dark
Cold coffee rings on a textbook left to spark
My pulse is a metronome counting down the dawn
Everyone's asleep but the pressure carries on

Two in the morning, the pages keep turning
Heart in my throat and the candle still burning
Nobody's watching but the clock on the wall
Run it back, run it back, I won't fall

Highlighter bleeding through the margins of my brain
Facts on a loop like a fast-approaching train
I memorize the rhythm, I let the silence drive
Alone in the data and somehow still alive

Breathe in the quiet, let the bassline hold the weight
Morning's a deadline I am racing to outwait
One more page, one more line, one more breath
I'll sleep when the knowledge is the only thing left

Two in the morning, the pages keep turning
Heart in my throat and the candle still burning
Nobody's watching but the clock on the wall
Run it back, run it back, I won't fall

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five dollars a game on a fold-up board
He smiles slow, says baby, can you afford
The pigeons keep time while the tourists lean in
He's already winning before you begin

King me, no, this ain't checkers, my dear
He moves like honey and he plays without fear
Clock on his wrist, gold tooth in the sun
By the time you blink, the hustle's done

Queen takes the corner, his voice stays low
Talking all the while so you don't see it go
He learned every trap on these same green chains
The park is his castle and the bench is his reign

Some men gamble cards, some men gamble cars
He gambles silence underneath the bars
Thirty years deep in this concrete game
Every stranger's a stranger but he knows the same

King me, no, this ain't checkers, my dear
He moves like honey and he plays without fear
Clock on his wrist, gold tooth in the sun
By the time you blink, the hustle's done

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Mile marker blurring into amber haze
Diesel hum and the dashboard's lonely blaze
I dial the number I could dial half asleep
A thousand miles of promises to keep

Hey, it's me, did I wake the kids again
The road keeps rolling but I'm holding to the when
Three in the morning and your voice is all I own
A highway of static carrying me home

The wipers drag a rhythm through the rain
You whisper soft so you don't wake the pain
I tell you 'bout the snow up past the line
You say come back safe and the static answers fine

The distance is a fog I drive straight through
Every exit sign is shaped a lot like you
When the wheels finally stop and the engine's cold
I'll fall asleep inside the voice I hold

Hey, it's me, did I wake the kids again
The road keeps rolling but I'm holding to the when
Three in the morning and your voice is all I own
A highway of static carrying me home

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I wrote it all down in a letter I won't send
To an address that the seasons couldn't mend
There's a chair at the table that I set out of habit
A word I'd take back if I only could grab it

I forgive you, I forgive you
Even if you'll never hear me say
I forgive you, I forgive you
I'm just learning how to let the anger fade away

The phone's full of pictures and the silence is loud
I'm talking to the rain, I'm talking to a cloud
All the things left unfinished, all the doors left ajar
I'm making peace with the shape of who you are

It's not for you now, it's the weight I want to lose
A pair of heavy shoes I'm finally going to lose
Wherever you are, if anywhere at all
I'm letting go before the next leaves fall

I forgive you, I forgive you
Even if you'll never hear me say
I forgive you, I forgive you
I'm just learning how to let the anger fade away

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The black suit's on the floor where it fell
The coffee's gone cold and the room is a shell
Sunlight slices through a crack in the blind
The world kept spinning and it left you behind

Morning after, the silence screams
The living wake up to the dead in their dreams
The casseroles rot and the flowers go brown
Morning after, and the sky comes down

The phone keeps ringing with the voices of strange
Folks who say sorry but nothing will change
I fold up the program, I can't read your name
The ground is still fresh and the grief is a flame

No more black cars, no more bowing heads
Just an empty house and a made-up bed
The ritual's over, now the real war starts
The morning after lives inside our hearts

Morning after, the silence screams
The living wake up to the dead in their dreams
The casseroles rot and the flowers go brown
Morning after, and the sky comes down

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The lights came up and the crowd knew my name
One signature away from the edge of the flame
The manager smiled with a contract in hand
I could taste the whole world like a promised land

So close, so close, I could touch the sky
One broken string and I watched it die
The night I almost held it in my fist
Now it lives in the almost, it lives in the missed

The amps were screaming and the floor was alive
I swear for a second I knew I'd survive
Then the call never came and the dawn turned grey
The legend I was died the very next day

I replay the tape in the dark of my skull
The roar of the almost, the deafening lull
Maybe in some life I signed on the line
But this is the one where I ran out of time

So close, so close, I could touch the sky
One broken string and I watched it die
The night I almost held it in my fist
Now it lives in the almost, it lives in the missed

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Same gas station, same flickering sign
You were always there but you weren't always mine
We grew up two streets and a lifetime apart
Now you're standing here rearranging my heart

Funny how it took me leaving to come back
To see the gold in this old dusty track
I fell for the boy from the end of my road
The one I drove past a thousand times slow

We drink at the diner where our parents once met
You know all my stories, the ones I forget
The whole town is watching but I don't even care
Home was a feeling and it turns out it's you there

I chased the big cities and the bright neon nights
But nothing held a candle to your porch lights
Funny how the map led me right where I began
Falling for the boy with the hometown plan

Funny how it took me leaving to come back
To see the gold in this old dusty track
I fell for the boy from the end of my road
The one I drove past a thousand times slow

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Neon memory on a faded photograph
Flour on her knuckles, the echo of a laugh
Those hands rolled the dough and they braided my hair
Maps of every year written gently there

Grandmother's hands, like rivers and like rings
Holding all the warmth that a lifetime brings
Grandmother's hands, in the violet glow
Teaching me the things that only time can know

Veins like the highways on a midnight drive
Every scar a story that kept us alive
She'd press a coin in my palm at the door
Those hands gave everything and asked for no more

Now the kitchen's quiet and the radio hums low
I catch my own knuckles starting to show
The same soft language, the same gentle plans
I carry the country of my grandmother's hands

Grandmother's hands, like rivers and like rings
Holding all the warmth that a lifetime brings
Grandmother's hands, in the violet glow
Teaching me the things that only time can know

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
It started out friendly, just a kitchen table game
A couple of dollars and nobody to blame
Then the stakes got higher and the laughter went thin
Somebody was losing more than money to win

Deal me out, I went all in too deep
The house always wins and the debt's mine to keep
We played for the fun till the fun turned to scars
Just a card game, just a card game gone too far

He bet on his pride and she bet on her name
By the end of the night nobody felt the same
The cards hit the floor and the friendships went cold
Some things you can't fold and some truths can't be told

The ace up the sleeve was a secret all along
We were playing each other the whole night long
Now I shuffle the deck but I can't deal it out
Learned what the betting was really about

Deal me out, I went all in too deep
The house always wins and the debt's mine to keep
We played for the fun till the fun turned to scars
Just a card game, just a card game gone too far

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
Well I put on my hat and I straightened my tie
Gonna say my goodbyes to a clear blue sky
You stepped out the door 'fore I cleared the air
Now I'm dancing alone with an empty chair

So I forgive you, baby, hear the trumpets blow
Forgive you, baby, even though you had to go
I'm shaking off the heavy and I'm stepping light
Forgive you, baby, gonna swing it out tonight

No more grudges weighing down my two-step shoes
No more carrying around those low-down blues
The band's playing soft and the lights are low
I'm letting go of everything I couldn't show

Wherever you've drifted, on whatever shore
I'm not gonna hold it against you no more
Snap of the fingers, brush of the snare
I release it to the rhythm in the smoky air

So I forgive you, baby, hear the trumpets blow
Forgive you, baby, even though you had to go
I'm shaking off the heavy and I'm stepping light
Forgive you, baby, gonna swing it out tonight

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
The sky cracked open on a Saturday night
Neon was bleeding in the puddle-mirror light
You ducked under cover where I happened to stand
One little umbrella and a stranger's warm hand

Under the rain, under the glow
Two perfect strangers with nowhere to go
Dance in the downpour, spin to the beat
One tiny umbrella on a flooded-out street

You laughed at the thunder and I matched your stride
The city kept pouring but we stayed dry inside
Your shoulder kept brushing mine in the storm
A stranger this morning, but tonight we stay warm

We don't know each other's names but who's keeping score
The rain wrote a number on a coffee shop door
The taxis kept honking but we let them roll by
Dancing in a circle 'neath a borrowed dry sky

Under the rain, under the glow
Two perfect strangers with nowhere to go
Dance in the downpour, spin to the beat
One tiny umbrella on a flooded-out street

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Boxes in the hallway and the AC's broke
We're burning through the last of the summer like smoke
Windows down screaming on the interstate
Tomorrow we scatter but tonight we're awake

Last day of summer, don't you let it end
Gun the engine, hold the hand of every friend
We'll be strangers by September, that's the deal
So blow out the curfew, this is the last we'll feel

Parking lot fireworks and a stolen cheap beer
Nobody saying the goodbye that's so near
We carved our initials in the old wooden pier
Gonna miss it like crazy by this time next year

The whole town shrinking in the rearview glass
Everything golden 'cause it's never gonna last
One more loop around the block, one more song
We were invincible, we just weren't here long

Last day of summer, don't you let it end
Gun the engine, hold the hand of every friend
We'll be strangers by September, that's the deal
So blow out the curfew, this is the last we'll feel

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
I sold the old plow and I kissed the dry ground
Packed all my life in a trunk tied around
Mama stood waving from the edge of the lane
A one-way ticket and a heart full of rain

Fare thee well, my green country shore
I'm crossing the water, won't see you no more
With a fiddle in my bag and a prayer on my tongue
I'm chasing a country where my children stay young

The deck rolled beneath me and the salt stung my eyes
Watched the cliffs of my homeland dissolve in the skies
Four weeks of grey ocean and the cold steerage hold
Dreaming of a harbor paved in stories of gold

I'll write you a letter when the new land is mine
With money for your passage tucked between the lines
Though the sea is a grave for the home that I knew
I carry the old hills in everything I do

Fare thee well, my green country shore
I'm crossing the water, won't see you no more
With a fiddle in my bag and a prayer on my tongue
I'm chasing a country where my children stay young

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Disco ball spinning and my phone's in my hand
One name lighting up that I swore I'd disband
The bassline is thumping but my thumb's got a mind
Typing out the trouble that I left behind

Don't hit send, baby, but I'm gonna anyway
Under the strobe light I got nothing left to say
Midnight confession on a glowing little screen
Texting you trouble like a dancing machine

The mirrorball catches every word that I wrote
My heart's on the dancefloor, there's a lump in my throat
I know that it's reckless, I know that it's wrong
But the beat keeps on calling and I sway along

Delete it, retype it, let it sit in the dark
Three little dots like a runaway spark
The rhythm says do it, the morning says don't
I'll regret it by sunrise but tonight I won't

Don't hit send, baby, but I'm gonna anyway
Under the strobe light I got nothing left to say
Midnight confession on a glowing little screen
Texting you trouble like a dancing machine

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Roll of tape and a screaming gun
Last cardboard coffin under the sun
Walls got holes where the posters bled
Four years gone and the carpet's dead

Kitchen empty, the fridge unplugged
Someone's deposit we'll never get
Stack it up and I drag it out
Slam the door on a kid I met

Tape it up, tape it up, that's the last one
Nothing left but the dust and the past, son
Keys on the counter, I'm already gone
Tape it up, tape it up, and move on

Mattress leaning against the hall
Ghost of a party I can't recall
Echo bounces off naked floor
Won't be coming around here no more

Goodbye nothing, goodbye walls
Goodbye stairwell, goodbye all
Load the van in the pouring rain
Never felt so light again

Tape it up, tape it up, that's the last one
Nothing left but the dust and the past, son
Keys on the counter, I'm already gone
Tape it up, tape it up, and move on

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Met her down where the boardwalk ends
Salt on her shoulders, July on her skin
She said don't ask me what tomorrow brings
Just hold my hand till the morning comes in

We burned like driftwood, low and slow
Under a moon hanging fat and gold
She danced barefoot in the cold black tide
And I left my good sense back on the other side

Oh, summer woman, you came and you went
Like the heat off the sand when the long day's spent
Now the gulls are crying and the season's gone
And I'm still standing where the waves come on

September found me with an empty chair
Her towel still smelling of coconut air
The ocean don't keep what the ocean don't owe
And she rode out east when the tide went low

I got sunburn faded, I got footprints washed away
Got a postcard promise she was never gonna pay
But Lord, for a minute, I was twenty-two and free
With the whole blue water just for her and me

Oh, summer woman, you came and you went
Like the heat off the sand when the long day's spent
Now the gulls are crying and the season's gone
And I'm still standing where the waves come on

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew its very last note
A thousand lunch pails, a thousand coats
They hung the padlock on the chain-link gate
And told us all it was a year too late

My daddy bolted the frames in '64
I swept the same floor till there ain't no floor
The river still runs by the empty docks
Past windows full of the silent clocks

They're closing the doors on Maple and Vine
The smokestack's cold for the first last time
What do you tell a town that was built on a wage
When the only thing left is the turning page

The diner's quiet at the morning shift
No overtime crowd, no paycheck lift
The Help Wanted sign in the hardware glass
Came down the same week the orders passed

We ain't asking for a hand, just a hand to hold
A reason to stay when the nights turn cold
The young ones leaving on the southbound line
And the old ones staring at the factory sign

They're closing the doors on Maple and Vine
The smokestack's cold for the first last time
What do you tell a town that was built on a wage
When the only thing left is the turning page

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I tip my hat when she walks by
A little spin, a little sigh
She's got the whole room on a string
And me? I'm just a backbeat thing

I'd buy her roses by the score
She wouldn't notice one bit more
She laughs at jokes some other guy
Is telling slick beneath the sky

But I keep swingin', swingin' alone
Dancin' to a love that ain't my own
She never calls and she never will
So I tap my feet and I love her still

The band strikes up that favorite tune
I'm waltzing with the wall and the moon
She twirls away in a stranger's arms
Immune to all my two-bit charms

Maybe someday, who's to say
The horns will blow a brighter day
But till that downbeat finally lands
I'm clapping with these empty hands

So I keep swingin', swingin' alone
Dancin' to a love that ain't my own
She never calls and she never will
So I tap my feet and I love her still

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
I packed my mama's worn-out Bible
And a photograph of home
The steeple bell was ringing, ringing
As I walked out on my own

These gravel roads have raised me
Every porch knows my name
But the river's calling, calling
And I won't be back the same

Goodbye, little town, I'm bound for glory
Down a road I've never known
Lay my burden by the crossroads
Lord, I'm leaving, leaving home

The deacon laid his hand upon me
Said son, go on and fly
There's a wider gate of heaven
Underneath a stranger's sky

I'll carry every hymn we sang
In the chambers of my chest
And when the world gets heavy on me
I'll lay it down and rest

Goodbye, little town, I'm bound for glory
Down a road I've never known
Lay my burden by the crossroads
Lord, I'm leaving, leaving home

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Five men circle the smoke and the felt
Iron eyes and a loaded belt
The deck is cut by a trembling hand
Nobody leaves till the dealer's damned

Aces bleeding on the table's edge
A debt sworn out on a razor's pledge
The lamp swings low on a tightening rope
The last man folding has given up hope

Deal the dead man's hand again
Kings and coffins, nine and ten
The stakes are higher than the soul can pay
We went too far and we can't walk away

A pistol cocked where the chips once lay
The river card turns the night to grey
No king of hearts gonna save your hide
When the whole damn table's bound to ride

Greed is a god with an empty face
It drags us down to a darker place
One more bet and the floor falls through
The game was never the death of you

Deal the dead man's hand again
Kings and coffins, nine and ten
The stakes are higher than the soul can pay
We went too far and we can't walk away

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole life in a careful line
A house, a name, a date, a sign
I traced the edges with a quiet thumb
And felt the years before they'd come

There was a dress that was never mine
A borrowed song, a borrowed shrine
The mirror showed me someone planned
A stranger holding my own hand

So I let it go, let it drift like snow
Into a softer, farther glow
Whatever's waiting I don't know
But it's mine to hold, mine to hold

The train pulled slow through a violet haze
Dissolving all my numbered days
I watched the future fall behind
Like curtains parting in my mind

No one's voice but the hush of rain
No one's name in the windowpane
I'm weightless now, I'm coming clean
Into the space between the seen

So I let it go, let it drift like snow
Into a softer, farther glow
Whatever's waiting I don't know
But it's mine to hold, mine to hold

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every night in the marble hall
Old Yankel guards the eastern wall
A painted girl in a faded blue
Has watched him sixty winters through

The tourists come and the tourists go
They never see what the old men know
He hums a tune that his bubbe sang
While the empty echo of his keyring rang

Dance, little brushstroke, dance for me
The clarinet cries in a minor key
She'll never age and he never flies
But they share a secret in painted eyes

He brings her stories of the snow
The price of bread, the war below
She answers nothing, painted still
Yet warms the marble, warms the chill

When morning comes he locks the door
And leaves her hanging as before
But oh, the fiddle and the wail
Of an old man loving a girl in a frame's pale veil

Dance, little brushstroke, dance for me
The clarinet cries in a minor key
She'll never age and he never flies
But they share a secret in painted eyes

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
Folding chairs and a busted PA
Nineteen, nervous, with nothing to say
But a notebook crammed with a thousand nights
And a borrowed fit and a stomach full of fright
The host called my name and the room went blank
Mic in my fist and my whole chest sank
First eight bars came out shaky and thin
Then the beat caught my heart and I locked back in

Look how far, look how far from that room
From the cold coffee and the fluorescent gloom
Still chasing that feeling, that first little spark
When I found my whole life on an open mic in the dark

Three people clapped and one of them left
But one girl nodded like she felt every breath
And that was enough, man, that was the seed
A tiny yes against everything I'd need
Now I play the big rooms, the lights and the noise
But I'm still that kid with the trembling voice
Every time I grab it I'm back at the start
Nineteen and broke with a fire in my heart

They don't tell you the dream starts that small
A cracked linoleum, a water-stained wall
But everybody legendary stood right there
Shaking and praying to the dead static air

Look how far, look how far from that room
From the cold coffee and the fluorescent gloom
Still chasing that feeling, that first little spark
When I found my whole life on an open mic in the dark

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at six and the world is grey
Clock out at two for the second pay
My hands keep moving but the numbers don't
The rent's a tide and I swim, I won't

The bus light flickers on an empty street
I count the days on my aching feet
A paper moon in a paper sky
And bills like birds that won't learn to fly

And I'm running, running in a soft slow dream
Never quite reaching where the lights all gleam
The more I hold the more it slips away
Falling behind in the kindest way

Coffee cold and my eyes half-shut
A second shift in a borrowed rut
The ceiling fan turns a lazy round
While I drift above the solid ground

Maybe I'll wake and the math will bend
Maybe the month will finally end
But the soft machine of the working day
Just hums me under and hums me away

And I'm running, running in a soft slow dream
Never quite reaching where the lights all gleam
The more I hold the more it slips away
Falling behind in the kindest way

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Third gear screaming down the avenue
Ten o'clock and I shouldn't be here
But the wheel turned left like it always knew
To the block where I wasted a year

Your window's lit with a stranger's glow
Different curtains, a different name
I hit the gas but I'm driving slow
Through the wreckage of the same old game

Drive past, don't stop, don't you dare slow down
This ghost-town heart in a one-light town
The engine roars but it can't outrun
Everything we left undone

The corner store and the broken sign
The stairwell where we used to fight
It all comes back on the yellow line
Like a fever burning up the night

I could circle the block a hundred times
Wear the asphalt down to the bone
But the door's locked tight on those old crimes
So I floor it and I drive on home

Drive past, don't stop, don't you dare slow down
This ghost-town heart in a one-light town
The engine roars but it can't outrun
Everything we left undone

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We drove past the edge of the cellphone glow
To a field of breath and frozen snow
You killed the engine, killed the sound
And the whole green sky came rippling down

Like silk unspooling, slow and wide
A secret ocean turned on its side
We didn't speak, there was nothing to say
When heaven is bleeding violet and grey

Oh the lights, the lights, they fold and they fall
A cathedral with no walls at all
Hold my hand in the shivering dark
While the sky tears open, spark by spark

My fingers numb and my eyes gone wide
Something ancient and unafraid inside
The stars stepped back to let it through
A curtain pulled between me and you

We are so small and the night's so vast
A glowing thing that will never last
But for one hour the world was kind
And painted itself across my mind

Oh the lights, the lights, they fold and they fall
A cathedral with no walls at all
Hold my hand in the shivering dark
While the sky tears open, spark by spark

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
A room of pale and dimming light
Your name a hum beneath the night
I hold it soft, I hold it long
A wordless and a fading song

You never knew the weight I kept
The quiet hours, the way I slept
With half a hope against my chest
That dimmed and dimmed and never rest

And I drift, I drift toward you
A tide that never breaks the shore
A loving thing with nowhere to go
That asks and asks and is no more

The air is thick with what's unsaid
A candle burning in my head
You move through rooms I'll never reach
A silence longer than all speech

Slowly, slowly let it go
Like fog dissolving off the snow
The ache becomes a gentle tone
A love that learns to live alone

And I drift, I drift toward you
A tide that never breaks the shore
A loving thing with nowhere to go
That asks and asks and is no more

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Under the chestnut and the noonday sun
Old Ramón sets his pieces, one by one
A cigarette burning, a coin on the board
Come lose your pesos, come lose your accord

The tourist sits down with a smiling face
Thinking his money is safe in this place
But the guitar trembles and the heel comes down
And the king falls quick in the heart of the town

Ay, jaque mate, the hustle is sweet
Queen takes the fool in the dust of the street
Clap your hands for the master of play
He'll take your gold and he'll dance away

He's buried princes and bankers alike
With a flick of the wrist and a pawn on a spike
His fingers are lightning, his eyes never blink
He's three moves ahead before you can think

They say he was somebody, long long ago
A champion lost to the wine and the snow
Now the park is his kingdom, the bench is his throne
And he reigns over strangers, magnificent, alone

Ay, jaque mate, the hustle is sweet
Queen takes the fool in the dust of the street
Clap your hands for the master of play
He'll take your gold and he'll dance away

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
He used to lift me to the sky
Now I steady him going by
Those dancing shoes are gathering dust
And his big strong hands have learned to trust

He taught me how to knot a tie
To look the whole wide world in the eye
Now I tie it slow at the mirror's edge
And help him keep his careful pledge

Oh the years they swing so fast
From the future to the past
Dance with me one more sweet round
Before the music slows on down

His stories loop like a worn-out tune
The same old summer, the same old June
But I lean in close and I let him play
The golden record of his finest day

There'll come a morning he won't rise
With that crooked grin and those laughing eyes
So I'll hold this measure while it's mine
And waltz him gently down the line

Oh the years they swing so fast
From the future to the past
Dance with me one more sweet round
Before the music slows on down

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
There's a story in the lines of her palms
Years of bread and lullaby psalms
Fingers worn like the handle of a door
That opened up a thousand times before

Oh, her hands, her gentle hands
They held me close, they understand
Through the cold and through the years
They wiped away my every tear

She'd braid my hair by the kitchen light
Stitch the seams of the day come night
Knuckles like the roots of an old oak tree
Reaching down to steady me

Oh, her hands, her gentle hands
They held me close, they understand
Through the cold and through the years
They wiped away my every tear

Now I hold them soft as they hold mine
Folded paper, thin as time
And I feel the love they always gave
In every line, in every wave

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Shoo-bop, shoo-bop, do you remember
Cinnamon air in the cold December
Butter on the counter, sugar in the bowl
That sweet little kitchen warmed my soul

The smell of her kitchen (sha-la-la)
Takes me right back home (sha-la-la)
Apple pie and coffee
A boy is never alone

Flour on her apron, humming a tune
Sunlight pourin' in the afternoon
Bread on the windowsill startin' to rise
Love was the secret in her every recipe

The smell of her kitchen (sha-la-la)
Takes me right back home (sha-la-la)
Apple pie and coffee
A boy is never alone

Close my eyes and I'm a child again
Woah-oh, oh, back then
Every little memory so warm and so dear
When I breathe it in, she's still here

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Well I met her where the boardwalk meets the sand
A paper cup of soda in her hand
The band was playin' hot and the sun was sinkin' low
She took my arm and said come on, let's go

We were swingin' in the summer, dancin' by the sea
Just a fling, just a moment, just her and me
Salt up in the air and a rhythm in our feet
Oh that summertime love was sweet

We twirled beneath the lights along the pier
Knew it wouldn't last past the end of the year
But who needs forever when the night's this bright
We danced like fools till the morning light

We were swingin' in the summer, dancin' by the sea
Just a fling, just a moment, just her and me
Salt up in the air and a rhythm in our feet
Oh that summertime love was sweet

The waves rolled in, the season rolled away
But I still hear that brassy band play
A postcard memory I'll always keep
Of a summer fling that swept me off my feet

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night when I close my eyes
I feel my body start to rise
Above the city, above the lights
I'm floating through the velvet night

I'm flying (flying), so high tonight
I'm flying (flying), into the light
No one can hold me down, I'm free
This is the dream that's keepin' me

The rooftops shrink, the stars draw near
There's nothing left to hold or fear
My arms are wings, my heart's a flame
And nothing ever feels the same

I'm flying (flying), so high tonight
I'm flying (flying), into the light
No one can hold me down, I'm free
This is the dream that's keepin' me

But I always wake before I land
Reaching for a sky I can't hold in my hand
So close my eyes and chase it once again
Up where the dreaming never ends

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Four walls and an empty floor
Echo of a closing door
One last box, the tape runs out
This is what it's all about

Tape it up, let it go
Every ending's a door, you know
One last box, one last room
Making space for something new

Marks on the wall where the pictures hung
Memories of when we were young
Keys on the counter, lights go dim
A brand new chapter to begin

Tape it up, let it go
Every ending's a door, you know
One last box, one last room
Making space for something new

Feel the beat, feel it rise
Letting go of old goodbyes
Dance it out into the night
Everything's gonna be alright

Tape it up, let it go
Every ending's a door, you know
One last box, one last room
Making space for something new

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew me a map of a life I never chose
A suit and a title and a road that's already paved
But I'm tearing up the blueprint, watch it fold
Gonna live a little reckless, gonna live a little brave

Leaving it behind, the future that they planned
Walking my own line, my life back in my hands
Slow beat in my chest, smoke fading in the air
I'm choosing my own mess, and I don't even care

They said this is your name, this is who you'll be
Folded up so neat like the corners of a bed
But I'd rather chase the question than the guarantee
Follow all the maybes that are spinning in my head

Leaving it behind, the future that they planned
Walking my own line, my life back in my hands
Slow beat in my chest, smoke fading in the air
I'm choosing my own mess, and I don't even care

Quiet streetlight hum, suitcase by the door
I don't know where I'm headed but I won't be here no more

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
It's 2 AM and my thumb's on the screen
Know I shouldn't but you know what I mean
Three little dots, my heart starts to race
One wrong move and I'm losing my place

I shouldn't be textin' you, baby
But my fingers got a mind of their own
I shouldn't be textin' you, baby
But I hate sleepin' all alone

Got your name saved under something fake
Every message is a sweet mistake
The phone lights up and I lose my breath
Playin' a game I know leads to regret

I shouldn't be textin' you, baby
But my fingers got a mind of their own
I shouldn't be textin' you, baby
But I hate sleepin' all alone

Delete it, type it, send it anyway
Tomorrow I'll regret it but I need you today
Ooh, this is wrong, but it feels so right
So I'm textin' you again tonight

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Gates are chained, the lights went black
Thirty years and they ain't comin' back
The whistle blew its final scream
There goes the town, there goes the dream

Shut it down! Shut it down!
They took the heart right out of town
Shut it down! Shut it down!
Nothin' left but a rusted crown

My old man gave 'em all his years
Got a pink slip and a round of cheers
The suits upstairs, they cashed it in
While we're out here in the wind

Shut it down! Shut it down!
They took the heart right out of town
Shut it down! Shut it down!
Nothin' left but a rusted crown

No more smoke, no more grind
Just a wreck they left behind
But we ain't dead, we ain't done
We'll burn louder than the setting sun

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
I packed my bag at the break of day
Kissed my mama, walked away
Down that road where the willows bend
Leavin' this town, ain't comin' back again

So long, little town, I'm gone
Where the river runs, I'm movin' on
Got a fire in my chest and a song to sing
Gonna chase the morning, gonna spread my wing

Every porch and every name
Everybody knows my game
But a heart can't grow where the fences hold
So I'm tradin' the familiar for the unknown gold

So long, little town, I'm gone
Where the river runs, I'm movin' on
Got a fire in my chest and a song to sing
Gonna chase the morning, gonna spread my wing

The banjo rings as the mile signs pass
Know I'll miss that steeple and the churchyard grass
But some birds are born to fly real far
Followin' the light of a wanderin' star

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
The rain came down on a crowded street
Two lonely souls and one heartbeat
You pulled me under your little sky
A stranger's grace as the world went by

Under one umbrella, just you and me
Two strangers findin' company
The rain keeps fallin' but I don't mind
Found a little shelter, found something kind

We didn't know each other's name
But for a moment we felt the same
Shoulder to shoulder, step in time
Your world for a minute fell into mine

Under one umbrella, just you and me
Two strangers findin' company
The rain keeps fallin' but I don't mind
Found a little shelter, found something kind

Oh, the storm don't last, and neither do we
You'll turn your corner, I'll cross my street
But I'll remember how the rain felt warm
When a stranger held me through the storm

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every night when the doors are sealed
I stand alone in the marble field
And she looks down from her gilded frame
A painted woman without a name

Dance with me, my silent love
Under the moon and the chandelier above
You'll never age and you'll never leave
A love I can only believe

Her eyes follow every step I take
A passion no museum wall can break
I bow to her in the dim blue light
My partner for one more sleepless night

Dance with me, my silent love
Under the moon and the chandelier above
You'll never age and you'll never leave
A love I can only believe

But morning comes and the crowds return
Another heart for her to spurn
So I take my post and I guard her well
The keeper of a beautiful, lonely spell

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Hands at ten and two, you're shaking
Engine humming, morning breaking
I remember when you couldn't reach the floor
Now you're reaching for so much more

Drive, my darling, take the wheel
I'll let go slow, I know it's real
The road ahead is yours to find
Just don't forget I'm right behind

First the parking lot, then the open street
My heart and the dashboard skip a beat
You ease it forward, steady, true
And somehow I'm the one learning too

Drive, my darling, take the wheel
I'll let go slow, I know it's real
The road ahead is yours to find
Just don't forget I'm right behind

The horizon opens, wide and bright
My little girl disappears into the light
And I'm so proud and I'm so afraid
Of every mile that you'll drive away

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Neon waves and a magenta sky
We were young and the night was high
You wore the sunset in your hair
Electric heartbeat in the salty air

Summer fling in the neon tide
You and me on a midnight ride
The ocean glows and the stars ignite
We'll burn forever in this summer night

Chrome reflections on the water's edge
Made a promise we could never keep, a pledge
Your hand in mine as the synths would swell
A cassette of a love we'd never tell

Summer fling in the neon tide
You and me on a midnight ride
The ocean glows and the stars ignite
We'll burn forever in this summer night

The season fades to a distant hum
A pixel sun and the days to come
But in my dreams you're always there
Dancing slow in the violet air

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We drove all night to the edge of the cold
Where the pines stand tall and the dark turns gold
Killed the engine, killed the light
And waited on the edge of the world that night

Oh, the northern lights came dancin' down
Green and gold over frozen ground
Like the hand of God across the sky
We just held each other and watched it fly

You wrapped a blanket around us two
Breath like smoke in the midnight blue
All our troubles felt so small
Beneath that great and shimmering wall

Oh, the northern lights came dancin' down
Green and gold over frozen ground
Like the hand of God across the sky
We just held each other and watched it fly

Some things you can't put into words
Like the silent flight of the winter birds
So we let it wash us clean and bright
Two small souls in the northern light

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They had it all mapped out before I learned to walk
The job, the ring, the title, didn't ask me, didn't talk
A gilded little cage with my name on the door
But I'm cuttin' all the strings, I ain't playin' it no more

I'm leavin' it behind, the life they had designed
Writin' my own future, gonna speak my own mind
No more livin' for the plan that they made
I'm tearin' down the script, watch the picture get remade

They said respect the legacy, respect the family name
But a borrowed dream is heavy and it never feels the same
I love 'em but I gotta go and find out who I am
Not a chess piece on a board, I'm a free man with a plan

I'm leavin' it behind, the life they had designed
Writin' my own future, gonna speak my own mind
No more livin' for the plan that they made
I'm tearin' down the script, watch the picture get remade

One suitcase, one ticket, one heart full of fear
But the second that I left, everything got clear
They'll understand someday, or maybe they won't
But I gotta live my life, this one's my own

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
I string the antenna up over the synagogue roof
My uncle's old tape deck and a microphone for proof
The transmitter coughs like a kettle on the stove
But the freylekhs come through to every coat and every cove

The police van circles slow down the cobbled lane
My mother hums the clarinet to cover up the strain
We broadcast from the cellar where the pickle barrels sleep
A wedding tune for strangers, a promise we can keep

Oh turn your dial to the candle in the dark
We are the static and the spark
No license, no permission, just a doina and a drum
Pirate of the airwaves, let the dancing come

We spin the old recordings that the censors tried to burn
A bulgar for the living, for the dead a slow return
The signal jumps the rooftops, hops the river to the shore
And somebody is laughing who has not laughed before

If they kick the door in I will swallow up the key
The music is already loose, already running free
It lives inside the listeners, it cannot be unsung
A pirate keeps on playing with a fiddle for a tongue

Oh turn your dial to the candle in the dark
We are the static and the spark
No license, no permission, just a doina and a drum
Pirate of the airwaves, let the dancing come

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The gun screams in my fist as the cardboard folds shut
Last roll of the packing tape, a scar across the gut
Empty walls are staring with their pale unfaded squares
Ghosts of every photograph, the weight of all the years

Seal it! Seal it! The final seam is drawn
Everything I ever was is boxed and bagged and gone
The echo of the slamming door is louder than the scream
Tape it shut, tape it shut, on the carcass of a dream

I dragged this kingdom down to one square meter on the floor
The key feels like a bullet that I cannot fire anymore
The radiator ticking like a heart that won't stay still
I press my palm against the wall and feel it going chill

No turning back, the threshold's at my feet
The last box is a coffin and the hallway is the street
I breathe in all the dust of who I used to be
Then I lift it on my shoulder and I carry, and I'm free

Seal it! Seal it! The final seam is drawn
Everything I ever was is boxed and bagged and gone
The echo of the slamming door is louder than the scream
Tape it shut, tape it shut, on the carcass of a dream

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
I was hiding by the cake with a glass I didn't drink
When you held your hand out, baby, faster than I'd think
The band kicked up a number and the floor began to swing
And I forgot I came alone, forgot most everything

One two, off we go, in a borrowed pair of shoes
A stranger and a sweetheart, got nothing left to lose
The trumpet's getting cheeky and the bass is running wild
I'm dancing with somebody who is somebody's child

You told me you're the cousin of the cousin of the bride
I told you a confession that I usually hide
We spun beneath the fairy lights, we dipped beneath the moon
And neither of us wanted that good band to quit too soon

Maybe in the morning we'll be strangers once again
Two names we never traded, two umbrellas in the rain
But tonight the saxophone is begging us to stay
So hold me one more chorus, let tomorrow drift away

One two, off we go, in a borrowed pair of shoes
A stranger and a sweetheart, got nothing left to lose
The trumpet's getting cheeky and the bass is running wild
I'm dancing with somebody who is somebody's child

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
The candle in the chapel had gone quiet, gone cold
I walked out to the water with my questions getting old
The waves kept their rhythm though my prayers had run dry
And the gulls drew their slow circles through an empty paper sky

Soft, soft, the morning comes anyway
Whatever I let go of found its own way back to stay
I lost it like a coin between the cushions of the years
Then I heard it humming low beneath the salt of all my tears

A fisherman was mending up his net along the stone
He smiled like he had never once felt truly alone
He didn't speak of heaven, just the bread and just the sea
And something in his patience left a window open in me

Faith is not a fortress, it's a tide that comes and goes
It slips out with the evening and at dawn it overflows
I stopped my desperate digging and I let the warm light land
And the thing I thought I'd buried put its fingers in my hand

Soft, soft, the morning comes anyway
Whatever I let go of found its own way back to stay
I lost it like a coin between the cushions of the years
Then I heard it humming low beneath the salt of all my tears

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
The screen lit up the bedroom in a cold electric blue
Three little lines of nothing where I used to live in you
No door slammed in the hallway, no rain against the glass
Just the buzz against the table and the silence afterward

Goodbye in a whisper, goodbye in a thumb
The sharpest knife is silence and the cut has left me numb
We danced through every city, now we end inside a phone
Two dancers and one only is allowed to walk back home

I read it like a sentence that a stranger had condemned
The punctuation final, the embrace I can't amend
The accordion is weeping in the room across the wall
And I learn the hardest steps are when there's no one there at all

Give me back the slamming door, the shouting in the street
Give me anything with heat instead of this cold tidy defeat
A love that burned so loudly should not flicker out so small
But the phone goes dark, the music stops, and that is all

Goodbye in a whisper, goodbye in a thumb
The sharpest knife is silence and the cut has left me numb
We danced through every city, now we end inside a phone
Two dancers and one only is allowed to walk back home

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
Every morning I dey wait by the corner where you pass
You wave at me and keep on moving, quick like breaking glass
I memorize your laughter, every song you say you love
Building you a kingdom that you never asking of

But you no see me, baby, you no see me at all
I dey shine my light for you, you dey look over the wall
The drum it beat my heart out but the dance is just for me
Loving you in silence is the only love I see

I hear your name and something in my chest go start to run
You talk about another and I smile like nothing's wrong
The DJ play our song but you no know that it is ours
I dey planting in a garden that will never give me flowers

Maybe in another life you turn and call my name
Maybe the same fire burning, maybe both of us the flame
But tonight I dey go home with the rhythm in my chest
Loving you one-sided, still I love you like the best

But you no see me, baby, you no see me at all
I dey shine my light for you, you dey look over the wall
The drum it beat my heart out but the dance is just for me
Loving you in silence is the only love I see

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole tomorrow on a contract made of stone
A name picked for my children, a road I'd never known
The suit was hung and waiting, the speeches all rehearsed
A gilded little prison and I was the one they cursed

Not your son! Not your plan! I tear the blueprint down!
Burn the crown they carved for me, I'd rather wear the ground
The future that you sold me was a coffin lined in gold
I'm running through the wreckage of the life I wasn't told

The elders speak in thunder of the duty and the line
A thousand generations and they swear that none was mine
I nod and smile politely while I'm sharpening the blade
That cuts the rope that drags me down the path that they have made

Let them keep the photographs, the table set for ten
Let them keep the empty chair and never speak my name again
I choose the open highway and the storm that has no map
A stranger to my bloodline but at last I own my path

Not your son! Not your plan! I tear the blueprint down!
Burn the crown they carved for me, I'd rather wear the ground
The future that you sold me was a coffin lined in gold
I'm running through the wreckage of the life I wasn't told

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Grandmama walks me down to where the water meets the clay
Says the ocean's been her teacher since before she learned to pray
She reads it like a letter, every wrinkle, every fold
The tide it keeps its promises far older than the old

Let it rise, let it fall, child, you can't hold the sea
You just learn to move beside it like your grandmama showed me
The water knows the secret of the leaving and return
The slowest kind of wisdom is the kind you'll never earn

She hums against the morning with the gospel in her chest
Says the moon do all the pulling and the shore just does the rest
Her hands are maps of patience, every line a thing she knew
And she presses all that knowing soft and quiet into you

One day she'll be the water and the water will be her
And I'll come down to the shoreline when my own heart starts to stir
I'll read it like she taught me, every wrinkle, every fold
And I'll teach it to a young one when these hands of mine grow old

Let it rise, let it fall, child, you can't hold the sea
You just learn to move beside it like your grandmama showed me
The water knows the secret of the leaving and return
The slowest kind of wisdom is the kind you'll never earn

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
She used to walk the harbor when the morning sun come up
We'd share a single coffee from a chipped and crooked cup
I told her wait till payday, told her wait until the spring
Now the boat done sail without her and I never gave the ring

Oh the one that got away, oh the one I let go
Thought I had forever but forever moved too slow
Now I sit beside the water where she used to call my name
And the tide keep on returning but my baby never came

I hear she's in the city now, she found another way
Somebody told me she got married on a bright September day
I raise a glass of memory to the love I let grow cold
The richest thing I ever had I traded for the gold

If I could roll the years back like a fisherman his line
I'd cast it out and pull her in and tell her she was mine
But time it only moves the one direction down the stream
And the one that got away she only lives now in a dream

Oh the one that got away, oh the one I let go
Thought I had forever but forever moved too slow
Now I sit beside the water where she used to call my name
And the tide keep on returning but my baby never came

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The words have all been thrown now, they are shattered on the tile
We sit on opposite corners and we haven't spoke in a while
The clock is much too loud now in the ringing of the air
And the space between our shoulders is a desert of despair

Ay, the silence, ay, the silence after all the fire
We burned each other's houses down on nothing but desire
Now the embers they are cooling and I'd give my hands to know
If the love is in the ashes or the love got up to go

I watch your back, your shoulders, the slow rising of your breath
A stranger and a lover all at once across the depth
My pride it is a mountain but my heart it is a stone
That wants to roll across the floor so you don't sit alone

Who will be the brave one, who will cross the broken ground
Who will take the smallest step and make the smallest sound
The guitar holds its breathing, every string pulled taut and tight
Until one of us is human in the quiet of the night

Ay, the silence, ay, the silence after all the fire
We burned each other's houses down on nothing but desire
Now the embers they are cooling and I'd give my hands to know
If the love is in the ashes or the love got up to go

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up the Chevy with a dream and one guitar
The manager said maybe, said we'd nearly be a star
The lights were on the marquee and the crowd began to sway
And for one shining minute it was almost ours that day

So close, so close, the moon was in our hands
We almost touched the velvet of the promise and the plans
Whoa-oh, the night we almost made it, baby, can't you see
It's the almost that I carry, it's the almost haunting me

The record man, he smiled at us and then he looked away
Somebody bigger came along, he had no more to say
We drove back through the darkness with the radio turned low
And the song we should've sung that night had no place left to go

Sometimes I hear it playing on a station late at night
Another voice, another band, a different kind of light
And I think about that almost and I hum it to the stars
The night we held the whole wide world in one old worn guitar

So close, so close, the moon was in our hands
We almost touched the velvet of the promise and the plans
Whoa-oh, the night we almost made it, baby, can't you see
It's the almost that I carry, it's the almost haunting me

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I carried it for years, man, a fist around a stone
The things you never said to me, the seeds you left ungrown
I rehearsed the whole damn speech, every word I'd make you hear
Then the phone rang in the morning and you weren't anywhere

I forgive you! I forgive you! And you'll never even know!
I'm screaming at a headstone, at a name carved in a row
The war is finally over but there's no one left to fight
So I lay it down, I lay it down, alone here in the night

They told me let it go but they don't know the weight I held
The apology I waited for, the bell that never knelled
Now the only one who carries it is standing here is me
So I have to be the one who cuts the rope and sets us free

It's not for you, it's for the kid inside my chest
The one who never got the words, who never got to rest
I dig the anger out of me, I tear it from the root
Forgiveness is the only door, and I walk through, I walk through

I forgive you! I forgive you! And you'll never even know!
I'm screaming at a headstone, at a name carved in a row
The war is finally over but there's no one left to fight
So I lay it down, I lay it down, alone here in the night

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Cinco dólares, mi amigo, come and take the seat
The clock is ticking, papi, and you're already beat
I been playing on this table since the morning was a kid
I seen a thousand kings go down for everything they did

Dale, dale, drop your money on the board
The queen she is my soldier and the bishop is my sword
You think you got me, baby, but I'm three moves up the road
Checkmate in the city, watch the whole park explode

The pigeons know my rhythm and the hustlers know my name
I smell the fear like coffee when you sit down to the game
You study all your openings, you read it in a book
But I learned it on the concrete, take a closer, harder look

The tourists come and snap their photos, think it's just for show
But the board is how I eat, mijo, that's the part you'll never know
Every pawn a little payday, every game a little war
The park is my casino and the king is what it's for

Dale, dale, drop your money on the board
The queen she is my soldier and the bishop is my sword
You think you got me, baby, but I'm three moves up the road
Checkmate in the city, watch the whole park explode

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine running, low and steady, neon on the dash
