They said one final number then I'm gone with all the cash
The city's breathing midnight and the streetlights start to blur
My hands around the wheel like it's the only thing that's sure

One more, one more, then I disappear
Forty seconds on the clock and then I'm out of here
Feel the bass line in the pavement, feel the rhythm in the run
Last job, last job, baby, and the driving's almost done

The doors fly open, bodies in, I drop it into gear
The siren's just a heartbeat and the dawn is getting near
I dreamed about a quiet road, a house beside the sea
Where nobody is chasing and there's no one after me

Don't look back, the mirror's full of everywhere I've been
The pedal and the promise and the life I won't begin
If I make it past the bridge then I'll be nothing but a name
A ghost behind the wheel who never plays this game again

One more, one more, then I disappear
Forty seconds on the clock and then I'm out of here
Feel the bass line in the pavement, feel the rhythm in the run
Last job, last job, baby, and the driving's almost done

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Didn't know a single soul beneath the spinning light
Just a plus-one to a cousin on a humid summer night
Then the beat dropped like a heartbeat and you found me by the bar
Two strangers in the strobe lights, no idea who we are

Dance with me, dance with me, we don't need a name
The floor is full of strangers but it never felt the same
Lift your hands up to the ceiling, let the bass dissolve the room
Dancing with a stranger in the after-midnight bloom

The DJ reads our heartbeat and he never lets it stop
We move like we have always known the rhythm from the top
Your smile beneath the disco glow, a stranger and a friend
And nobody is asking where the night is gonna end

Maybe we'll exchange our numbers, maybe we will not
Maybe this is all there is, just this single perfect shot
But the four-on-the-floor is calling and I never want to leave
So dance with me, you stranger, in the magic of the eve

Dance with me, dance with me, we don't need a name
The floor is full of strangers but it never felt the same
Lift your hands up to the ceiling, let the bass dissolve the room
Dancing with a stranger in the after-midnight bloom

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
The morning leans against the window glass
A paper cup of coffee going cold
I hear your laugh inside a passing breeze
A softer version of the words you told

I kept the anger like a folded note
That yellowed somewhere underneath my chest
But paper tears the longer that you hold it
And I am tired of carrying the weight

So I forgive you, quiet as the tide
The sea does not apologize to shore
I let you go the way the evening does
No bitterness, no knocking at the door

There is no number left for me to call
No street where I could meet you with my eyes
The only room we share is in my memory
Where every grudge eventually dies

Maybe somewhere you already knew
The things I never found the breath to say
And maybe peace is just the slower music
We learn to hum when someone slips away

So I forgive you, soft as falling rain
That asks for nothing as it leaves the cloud
I set you free and free myself the same
And say your name without the old aloud

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded her at midnight, no manifest, no name
The captain signed in candlelight and never looked the same
The crates were sealed in iron, the hold was cold as stone
And something underneath the boards was breathing on its own

Oh the cargo, oh the cargo, it sings a low refrain
A minor seventh whisper in the rigging and the rain
We paid for passage with our luck, we'll pay it back in years
The cargo keeps the ledger and it settles up in fears

The compass spun like trumpet keys, the stars forgot their place
The cook went pale at supper, said he saw a second face
The bass drum of the engine kept a heartbeat in the dark
And every wave that hit us left a fingerprint, a mark

Nobody asks what's in the boxes now
We only ask the moon to let us land
The horn solo bends like a prayer somehow
From a sailor with a tremble in his hand

Oh the cargo, oh the cargo, it follows where we go
A syncopated shadow in the undertow below
We'll dock her in the morning if the morning ever comes
And walk away forever from the curse beneath the drums

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slid shut on Fourteenth Street, the lights began to hum
My heart took off a-running like a banjo on the run
The tunnel swallowed daylight and my breath got thin and tight
A hundred strangers swaying and I'm holding on for life

Oh the train keeps rolling, rolling, but my mind has jumped the rail
My hands are cold as river stones, my courage turning pale
Lord, just let me see the platform, let me feel the open air
The train keeps rolling, rolling, and I'm praying I'm still there

I count the rivets on the wall, I count them one through nine
The fiddle of the wheels below is screaming down the line
A woman reads her paper like the world ain't caving in
While every inch of me is fire underneath my skin

Then a kid looks up and offers me his seat
A small and steady kindness in the roar
And somehow that's the rope I needed thrown
To pull me back to solid ground once more

Oh the train keeps rolling, rolling, into Twenty-Third at last
The doors slide open, daylight pours, the worst of it has passed
I step into the morning with my legs still made of strings
And thank the Lord for stations and the breath that mercy brings

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole life on a paper, signed before I learned to speak
A house, a name, a Sunday table, every day of every week
The dress was hung, the date was chosen, all the candles in their rows
But no one asked the girl who'd wear it where her restless heart would go

So I dance away, I dance away, on heels that strike the floor like thunder
Every step a slammed-shut door, every turn a vow torn under
Let them keep their tidy future, let them keep the ring of gold
I am not a debt to settle, I am not a hand to hold

The bandoneón is weeping in the corner of the night
My mother's eyes are railroads leading where I will not ride
I loved them, oh I loved them, but I cannot be their plan
I'd rather burn an empty stage than bow to someone's hand

One last embrace beneath the streetlamp glow
A stranger's arms that ask for nothing more
Then I collect my shadow and I go
Into the city no one charted for

So I dance away, I dance away, the future cracking at the seams
I trade their certain quiet years for my uncertain, savage dreams
Let them keep their tidy future, let the candles gutter low
The music's mine and so's the door, and so's the road I'll go

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Thirty years on the same old floor, now the lawyers got the keys
Kitchen table where we built it all, now it's boxes to the knees
One more song before the silence, put your hand inside my hand
We ain't lovers, we ain't strangers, we're just two who understand

Last dance, last dance, turn the lights down low
Everything we were is everywhere we gotta let it go
Last dance, last dance, hold me like before
We gon' finish how we started, then I'm walking out the door

Gray up in your temples now, I remember them jet black
We survived the storms and babies, all the years we can't take back
No more shouting through the hallway, no more crying in the car
Just this slow and heavy rhythm of two people who got far

I don't hate you, never could
We just ran out of the road
Kiss my forehead one more time
Before I carry my own load

Last dance, last dance, turn the lights down low
Everything we were is everywhere we gotta let it go
Last dance, last dance, hold me like before
We gon' finish how we started, then I'm walking out the door

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I knelt down as a younger man with answers in my chest
I thought I held the holy thread that tied me to the rest
Then winter took my brother and the prayers fell to the floor
And I stopped believing anyone was listening anymore

So I wandered through the valley with my candle blown to smoke
With a hollow where the hymn had been, a covenant I broke
But the road still met my footsteps and the morning still came round
And grace, it seems, was patient, waiting quiet underground

I found it in a stranger's hand that shared his bread with me
I found it in the stubborn green that split the fallen tree
Not the church of all my childhood, not the thunder and the rod
But something low and ordinary that I learned to call my God

Maybe faith is not a fortress but a field
That burns to black and somehow grows again
Maybe doubt was never something I should kill
But just the fallow season before rain

So I'm walking back toward something, not the way I left before
With a softer kind of certainty, a less demanding door
And the candle's lit by other hands when mine are worn and low
And grace, it seems, was waiting all the years I didn't know

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We took the freight from a one-eyed man on a dock outside of Maine
He said don't open box thirteen no matter sun or rain
The pay was good, the road was long, the diesel hummed us west
And something in the trailer kept us turning in our rest

Oh the cargo's cursed, the cargo's cursed, we feel it in the wheel
A thousand miles of asphalt and a weight that ain't quite real
We can't turn back, we can't unload, we just keep rolling on
With a shadow in the rearview that'll follow till we're gone

The radio caught voices that no station ever played
The coffee turned to ashes and the headlights start to fade
My partner stopped his talking somewhere outside Tennessee
Just stares into the broken white and won't look back at me

We should've read the warning in his eye
We should've left it sitting on the shore
Now the only prayer we've got is just to drive
And drop it off and never haul no more

Oh the cargo's cursed, the cargo's cursed, the dawn won't break it clean
We're hauling something older than the roads we've ever seen
We can't turn back, we can't unload, we just keep rolling on
With a shadow in the rearview that'll follow till we're gone

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew its final time on a cold November day
The line went still, the lights went down, and the foreman walked away
Thirty years I gave these walls, my father's hands here too
Now the gates are chained and rusted and there's nothing left to do

But my soul, oh my soul, it ain't clocked out yet
The Lord don't lay His children off, He don't forget
When the factory closes down and the machines all cease
He's still building in my spirit, He's still working out my peace

The whole town shared one heartbeat in the humming of that floor
Now the diner's half-deserted and the church gets a few more
We pray for one another, pass the basket hand to hand
For every laid-off brother trying hard to understand

I may not know what Monday's gonna bring
No paycheck and no purpose in my name
But I'll lift my weary voice and I will sing
The One who called me's faithful just the same

So my soul, oh my soul, it ain't clocked out yet
The Lord don't lay His children off, He don't forget
When the factory closes down and the silence fills the air
He's still building in my spirit, He's still answering my prayer

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Mama packed the suitcase with the spices and the prayers
Folded up the photographs of everybody there
The boat is waiting early when the morning meets the tide
I'm leaving all I ever knew with hope and fear inside

Across the sea, across the sea, I'm chasing down the sun
Leaving home to find a home, the journey's just begun
Across the sea, across the sea, my heart in either hand
One foot in the motherland, one foot in the promised land

The city on the other side don't know my mother's name
But I'll carry her inside me and I'll light a brand-new flame
The drum still beats inside my chest the rhythm of the shore
I'll send the money, I'll send the love, I'll send for them once more

Don't cry for me, I'm not lost, I'm just far
The ocean's wide but love is even wider
I'll plant my feet beneath a foreign star
And grow a bridge for everyone beside her

Across the sea, across the sea, I'm chasing down the sun
Leaving home to find a home, the journey's just begun
Across the sea, across the sea, my heart in either hand
One foot in the motherland, one foot in the promised land

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
There were maps inside her knuckles, little rivers in her skin
Years of bread and years of mending written soft and deep within
She'd hold my face on Sundays like I was something made of gold
Those hands had carried generations, never once let go

Grandmother's hands, grandmother's hands
The gentlest strength I'll ever understand
They planted gardens, dried my tears, held the whole world where they stand
I'd give anything to feel them one more time, grandmother's hands

She taught me how to fold the dough and how to say a prayer
The way she tucked a stray gray curl behind her silver hair
The rings she wore were worn so thin from sixty years of love
A quiet kind of holiness she never spoke enough

Now her chair is empty by the window in the light
But I swear I feel her fingers brush my shoulder in the night
And when I knead the bread the way she showed me how to do
Those hands are in my hands somehow, and I am holding you

Grandmother's hands, grandmother's hands
The gentlest strength I'll ever understand
They planted gardens, dried my tears, held the whole world where they stand
I'd give anything to feel them one more time, grandmother's hands

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody picked us, nobody cared, they wrote us off in ink
Last place jokes and empty stands, they never let us think
But we showed up every morning when the world was still asleep
With bloody knuckles, busted pride, and promises to keep

This is it, the final whistle, this is everything we are
Forty minutes, one last war, no holding nothing back so far
We came from nothing, we got nothing, so we got nothing to lose
Scream it loud, we're not backing down, this is the fight we choose

The clock is bleeding seconds and my lungs are full of fire
The crowd that doubted's on their feet, the stakes are climbing higher
One more push, one more inch, one more breath inside the storm
This is what we sweat the whole damn year to be reborn

Win or lose they'll never take this back
The night we stood and made the giants bleed
The underdogs who wouldn't break the track
Remember us, remember what we need

This is it, the final whistle, this is everything we are
Forty minutes, one last war, no holding nothing back so far
We came from nothing, we got nothing, so we got nothing to lose
Scream it loud, we're not backing down, this is the fight we choose

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I climb the iron staircase every night to tend the flame
And I write you on the salt-stained paper, calling out your name
No woman ever held me like your gray and endless arms
My dear, my dark, my restless one, my dangerous of charms

So I write to you, I write to you, and send it on the wind
The only love that ever stayed, the love I can't rescind
You crash against my lonely rock, you take and never give
But you're the reason for the light, the reason that I live

The townsfolk think me foolish, talking sweet to wave and foam
But you have been my company on every night alone
I've watched you swallow ships and men and spit them on the sand
And still I trim the wick for you and reach you with my hand

Maybe someday you will take me too
Down into your cold and quiet deep
And I'll stop writing letters, my old blue
And finally lie beside you, finally sleep

So I write to you, I write to you, and seal it with the spray
The only love that ever stayed and never went away
You crash against my lonely rock, you take and never give
But you're the reason for the light, the reason that I live

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
Well the band's still playing, baby, though our song is nearly through
Forty years of stepping lively, me in step with you
The papers say it's over, but the floor remembers when
We were young and full of trouble, so let's take one spin again

One more dance, one more dance, before we say goodnight
Swing me 'round the ballroom, baby, hold me sweet and tight
The music's slowing, so are we, but oh we had our day
One more dance, my darling, then we waltz our separate way

Remember how you spun me till the room became a blur
The wedding and the worry and the way the seasons were
We stepped on toes, we missed our cues, we found the beat somehow
And if it's ending, let it end with you and me right now

No hard feelings, no, not anymore
Just a quiet little ache and a grin
We danced it out, we settled every score
Now kiss me like we're back where we begin

One more dance, one more dance, before we say goodnight
Swing me 'round the ballroom, baby, hold me sweet and tight
The music's slowing, so are we, but oh we had our day
One more dance, my darling, then we waltz our separate way

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
I was seventeen and shaking with a borrowed beat-up guitar
The coffee shop was half asleep, the lights were way too far
My voice cracked on the second line, my hands forgot the chords
But something in me lit right up beneath those wobbly boards

And I played, played, played that night, off-key and unafraid
The worst three minutes of my life and the best I ever made
Pick it up, skank it down, the horns are blowing bright
I'd give a year to be back there on that first open mic night

There were maybe nine people clapping and a barista in the back
A dude who heckled gently and a girl who liked my track
I thought that I had blown it, walked out into the rain
But man, I couldn't wait to climb right back up there again

Now I've played a hundred bigger rooms
With better gear and tighter, cleaner sound
But nothing's ever beat that wild perfume
Of being young and terrified and found

So I played, played, played that night, off-key and unafraid
The worst three minutes of my life and the best I ever made
Pick it up, skank it down, the horns are blowing bright
I'd give a year to be back there on that first open mic night

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
There was a girl in the old quarter with eyes like candle flame
We danced one summer by the river, I still whisper her name
The clarinet was crying as we spun beneath the moon
I thought we had forever, but forever ended soon

Oh she got away, she got away, like smoke beneath the door
The sweetest song my heart still hums, the one I'm aching for
The fiddle plays a freylekhs but my feet are made of stone
For the one that got away from me and left me here alone

Her father had his reasons and the world had other plans
They carried her across the ocean, slipping through my hands
I kept the ribbon from her hair, I kept it all these years
And the music still can find the place where I am keeping tears

So dance, my friends, and let the wine go round
Laugh until the morning takes the night
But every joyful tune has buried sound
A minor key beneath the major light

Oh she got away, she got away, like smoke beneath the door
The sweetest song my heart still hums, the one I'm aching for
The fiddle plays a freylekhs but my feet are made of stone
For the one that got away from me and left me here alone

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning, phone is a small blue moon
Your name on the screen like a thief in the room
I typed it, I cleared it, I typed it again
Fingers like dancers who don't know when to end
Lagos in my chest, the generator hum
My heart doing footwork to a guilty drum

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but my thumb knows your name
Delivered, then read, then I'm playing this game
Don't reply, don't reply — but I'm holding my breath
Texting the one that I promised to forget

She sleeping beside me, soft as the rain
While I water a fire I should let go drain
You send me one word and the whole city sways
Palmwine and shadows, the rest of my days
My conscience a drummer who skips on the beat
My fingers betraying the ground at my feet

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but my thumb knows your name
Delivered, then read, then I'm playing this game
Don't reply, don't reply — but I'm holding my breath
Texting the one that I promised to forget

Delete the whole thread, throw the phone in the Lagoon
Still I'll be humming your message by noon
The link light is blinking, a small green sin
Lord, lock my phone and don't let me back in

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Under the jacaranda, a board made of wood
Viejo with a smile that says nothing is good
Five pesos a game, he will let the boy win
Then the trap closes soft like a door in the wind
Trumpets in the plaza, his fingers don't shake
He sips on his coffee and waits for the take

Ay, jaque mate, the pigeons take flight
He's the king of the corner from morning to night
Guitarrón keeps the time as the pawns fall in line
The hustler in the park makes the whole city his shrine

A banker, a student, a man in a suit
They sit down so proud and they leave without loot
He learned it in prison, he learned it from need
Every move is a prayer, every win is a seed
The clock has no hands but he hears it tick true
He was poor before sunrise, by dusk he is too

Ay, jaque mate, the pigeons take flight
He's the king of the corner from morning to night
Guitarrón keeps the time as the pawns fall in line
The hustler in the park makes the whole city his shrine

When he dies they will bury his knight and his rook
And the bench will stay empty, no five-peso hook
But the boys he once beat will sit down in his place
And teach the next sucker the rules of the chase

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I hung my rosary on a nail by the door
And I danced with the doubt on a cold marble floor
The candles went black, the saints turned their eyes
And I learned every prayer is a beautiful lie
Bandoneón weeping the length of the night
I buried my God in the absence of light

But the city kept turning, the streetlamps still burned
And grace is a tango — you leave and return
One step into shadow, one step into flame
I lost Him in winter and found Him the same

A stranger, an old man, held out his torn hand
And I felt something move that I don't understand
Not thunder, not lightning, no chorus of wings
Just the warmth of the small and forgettable things
The bread and the wine and a child's open face
The quietest doorway I stumbled through grace

But the city kept turning, the streetlamps still burned
And grace is a tango — you leave and return
One step into shadow, one step into flame
I lost Him in winter and found Him the same

So I took down the rosary, kissed the worn beads
Not for fear of the fire, but the names of my needs
The dance never ended, I only looked down
Now I lift up my eyes and I dance through the town

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Abuelo on the seawall when the moon is full grown
Talking to the water like it's flesh of his own
Dembow in the distance from the cars passing slow
But he hears the older rhythm, the pull and the tow
Salt in his beard, sand worn smooth in his hand
Teaching the ocean how to kiss the dry land

Sube, baja, the tide learns to breathe
He whispers to the foam what the foam can't believe
Dale, mar, dale — come close, don't be shy
The elder and the ocean, low moon in the sky

He say to the water, you been here before me
You'll be here when my grandsons are old by the quay
But even the eternal got something to learn
How to leave, how to come, how to wait for your turn
The beat from the bass and the beat from the deep
Both keeping the promise the shoreline must keep

Sube, baja, the tide learns to breathe
He whispers to the foam what the foam can't believe
Dale, mar, dale — come close, don't be shy
The elder and the ocean, low moon in the sky

When he's gone the tide will keep the lesson he gave
Rolling in like a verse, rolling out like a wave
And the children on the seawall, phones glowing bright
Will feel an old man's rhythm in the pull of the night

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
You were August on a fire escape, bare feet on warm steel
A cup of cold coffee and a laugh I can still feel
I had a key to nowhere and a head full of plans
Let your future slip like water through my open hands
Rhodes piano dripping in the back of my mind
Every chord I play is a door I left behind

You're the one that got away, the one I let go
A melody I hum but I never let it show
I see you in the steam off a stranger's coffee cup
The one that got away, and I never woke up

They say I look happy and I guess that I am
Got a quiet kind of love and a steady little plan
But some nights the bassline of an old summer song
Reminds me of the rooftop where I knew it was wrong
Wrong to ever leave you on that fire escape alone
Watching cabs go yellow til the morning came home

You're the one that got away, the one I let go
A melody I hum but I never let it show
I see you in the steam off a stranger's coffee cup
The one that got away, and I never woke up

I hope he holds you gentle, I hope he knows your tea
I hope he never wonders what you wonder about me
And if our paths should cross on some gold afternoon
I'll smile and say hello, then I'll leave the room too soon

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Used to lift me with one arm, now his grip's a soft thing
Hands that built the whole block shake to button a sleeve in spring
He was concrete, he was thunder, he was six foot of law
Now he naps in the recliner with his mouth open, jaw
The man who taught me jumpers can't remember the score
Keeps the same three stories on a loop by the door

Watching my father turn to glass, slow, slow
The strongest man I knew getting harder to hold
I'm steady on the outside, inside I'm a kid
Watching my father, every brave thing he did

He asks me twice the day, I say it twice with no sigh
The way he taught me patience when I scraped my whole knee high
I'm parking in the handicap, walking slow to match his pace
Used to chase his shadow, now I hold his arm in place
There's a photograph of him at twenty-five, lean and proud
I see it on the mantle and I have to leave the crowd

Watching my father turn to glass, slow, slow
The strongest man I knew getting harder to hold
I'm steady on the outside, inside I'm a kid
Watching my father, every brave thing he did

So I'll tell him that I love him while the words can still land
Write it down so I remember how he held my whole hand
And when the day comes that he doesn't know my name
I'll know his, I'll know his, and I'll love him just the same

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I left this town with dust on my boots and fire in my throat
Swore the narrow streets would drown me, swore I'd burn the little boat
Ten years and a thousand cities, then I came back for a wake
And you poured me wine in the same plaza for an old man's sake
Guitar strings like a fever, palmas quick in the heat
And I fell for the girl I ignored on this very same street

¡Ay! the same white walls, the same orange tree
The thing I ran from is the thing that's holding me
Your eyes are the village, your hands are the bell
I crossed the whole wide world to fall back where I fell

You never left, you tend the bar where your mother used to stand
You know the name of every stone, the history of the land
And I thought that I was bigger than this map upon the hill
But your laugh undid my distance and it's undoing me still
The cante rises, broken, like a confession of mine
That home was never walls and roads — it was you all this time

¡Ay! the same white walls, the same orange tree
The thing I ran from is the thing that's holding me
Your eyes are the village, your hands are the bell
I crossed the whole wide world to fall back where I fell

So stamp the heel and let the dust rise up off the ground
I'm not leaving, I'm not leaving, I have finally been found
Let the strangers keep the cities with their towers of glass
I'll grow old in the shadow of the orange tree at last

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Neon bleeding on the wet black street, engine breathing low
One more run and then I'm out, that's the only thing I know
Dashboard glowing crimson, clock counting down the score
Three minutes on the meter and a duffel by the door
Analog heart on a digital night, headlights on the rain
This is the last time I let the city pull my chain

Drive, drive, the last job, the long goodbye
Chrome reflections burning in the corner of my eye
One more mile of midnight, one more red to run
Then I'm gone with the dawn before the rising sun

They come out running, doors slam shut, I feel the city breathe
Sirens fold like origami in the mirror as we leave
She's beside me now, the only thing I'm taking when I'm through
A cassette of better futures and a tank that's almost through
The overpass is glowing like a synthesizer dream
And I floor it past the skyline, past the man I used to be

Drive, drive, the last job, the long goodbye
Chrome reflections burning in the corner of my eye
One more mile of midnight, one more red to run
Then I'm gone with the dawn before the rising sun

Somewhere there's an ocean where the engines never start
A quiet little nothing for a loud and tired heart
The tank light's blinking gold now, the gauge is running thin
But the dawn is coming up — and I'm finally driving in

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Gravel road on the back forty where the fences run long
Got my girl behind the wheel of the truck her granddad drove on
Clutch and gas like a fiddle bow, she's bucking down the lane
Laughing til she's crying, killing dust into the rain
Banjo rolling like the gravel underneath the worn tire
Little hands at ten and two, eyes lit up like fire

Easy on the brake now, darlin', let her roll
The road's gonna teach you what I can't tell your soul
Mind the curve by the river where the willows hang low
I taught you how to drive, now I gotta let you go

She stalls it by the mailbox and she swears just like her ma
I bite my lip from grinning, say there's nothing to it, ha
She finds the gear, the truck rolls smooth, the dust trails like a song
And I see the woman coming where the little girl was, long
The summer light is honey on the dashboard and her face
And I'm holding to this moment 'fore she drives off from this place

Easy on the brake now, darlin', let her roll
The road's gonna teach you what I can't tell your soul
Mind the curve by the river where the willows hang low
I taught you how to drive, now I gotta let you go

There'll come a day she takes that truck clear past the county line
And I'll stand here in the gravel with this old worn heart of mine
But tonight she's still my baby grinding gears in the field
And I'm the proudest man in Tennessee right here beside the wheel

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Up the spiral stairs every night I climb, ink stains on my sleeve
Writing letters to the ocean that I never can believe
The horns are blasting upbeat while the storm is rolling in
I fold the page, I seal the wave, I throw it to the wind
Dear sea, you cold and salty thing, you never write me back
But I'll keep the lantern burning down this lonely watery track

Pick it up, pick it up, the offbeat and the foam
A keeper and the ocean and a light to bring 'em home
I'm dancing on the gallery, my letters in the spray
The sea don't love me back but I'll write her anyway

The trombone slides like a fog horn through the gulls and the gray
I tell her about the sailors that she swallowed yesterday
I tell her she's my only girl, my green and roaring bride
And I dance the brass-band shuffle as I watch the rising tide
The bottles bob like punctuation on a sentence with no end
A love affair with something that will never be my friend

Pick it up, pick it up, the offbeat and the foam
A keeper and the ocean and a light to bring 'em home
I'm dancing on the gallery, my letters in the spray
The sea don't love me back but I'll write her anyway

One day they'll find the lighthouse with the lantern still aglow
And a thousand unsent letters in a chest down below
And the sea will keep on rolling, never knowing what she had
While the brass plays on forever for a keeper, gone but glad

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Oy, the clarinet is laughing as she grinds the second gear
My little bird behind the wheel and me half-dead with fear
We roll down Kestenbaum Street where her bubbe used to walk
And the violin is trembling like my voice when I try to talk
Hands on the wheel, mamele, the world is wide and gray
But I'll sit here in the passenger seat and teach you the way

Drive, drive, may the road rise to meet you
Freilach in the engine, may no red light defeat you
A little to the left now, a little to the right
My daughter and the open road and the trembling violin's light

She stalls before the synagogue, the cantor gives a wave
She laughs and I am crying for the years I cannot save
The accordion is wheezing like her zayde used to do
When he taught me on these same streets in a Buick painted blue
The melody goes minor, then it lifts into a turn
That's the secret of our people: how to dance through what we mourn

Drive, drive, may the road rise to meet you
Freilach in the engine, may no red light defeat you
A little to the left now, a little to the right
My daughter and the open road and the trembling violin's light

So go, go, and carry all the names that we have lost
Drive gentle through the winters, drive brave across the frost
The clarinet will follow you, the door is open wide
And I'll be there, mamele, in the rhythm of your ride

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Desk lamp pooling amber on a hill of unread pages
Cold coffee growing colder while the radiator ages
The city's gone to whispers, just a saxophone next door
And my pencil keeps on walking like a bassline on the floor
Two a.m. is honest, it don't ask you to pretend
Just me and these equations and a night that won't quite end

It's late, it's late, and the streetlight's playing blue
Just me and a notebook and the things I have to do
The brushes on the snare drum of the rain against the glass
Studying alone while the quiet hours pass

Highlighter yellow fading, every word begins to swim
The clock hands doing slow improv on a melody so dim
I dream about the morning when I'll close this heavy book
But for now I'm in the woodshed, taking one more careful look
There's a strange and lonesome beauty in a world that's gone to bed
While you chase a little knowledge through the corners of your head

It's late, it's late, and the streetlight's playing blue
Just me and a notebook and the things I have to do
The brushes on the snare drum of the rain against the glass
Studying alone while the quiet hours pass

Dawn is just a rumor on the far side of the dark
But I'll get there one note at a time, one small and stubborn spark
So pour another cup, let the saxophone next door
Play me through the chapter — just a little bit more

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
Hi-hats rolling triple-time, we stuck on the same floor
My worst enemy in front of me, ain't been this close before
The judges call our numbers, now we partnered for the round
Hate is in the 808, but our footwork lock the ground
She step, I step, mirror in the smoke and the strobe
Two kings of the same block forced to share a single globe

We don't like it, we don't like it, but the beat say move
Enemies in lockstep, nothing left to prove
Spin me, don't trip me, keep it tight, keep it clean
Two rivals on the floor making war look serene

Every grudge between us turning into perfect time
The thing that made us enemies done made us so sublime
I know all your weaknesses, you know every one of mine
So we read each other's body like a threat and like a sign
The bass drop like a gunshot but our hands don't let it land
The crowd don't know we're fighting, all they see is something grand

We don't like it, we don't like it, but the beat say move
Enemies in lockstep, nothing left to prove
Spin me, don't trip me, keep it tight, keep it clean
Two rivals on the floor making war look serene

When the music finally cuts and the lights come up too bright
We step apart like strangers, back to opposite sides of the night
But somethin' shifted in the silence, somethin' neither one can name
We was never gonna say it, but we won that thing the same

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Back room of a venue, smell of beer and cheap perfume
Label guy was in the building, said he loved the way I bloom
I had the whole set ready, every note already won
Three songs from a different life beneath a borrowed sun
I could taste it on my tongue, gold and电 close as my skin
The door was halfway open and I almost walked on in

It was the night I almost made it, almost touched the light
So close I felt it breathing, then it slipped into the night
I don't cry about it often but on certain shades of blue
I live inside the almost, where my other life came true

His phone rang in the middle, said he had to take the call
Never came back to the green room, never heard from him at all
I played the show to no one but I played it like a flame
Then I drove back to the day job and I clocked in just the same
The demo's on a hard drive in a box beneath the stairs
The almost is a country and I'm still a citizen there

It was the night I almost made it, almost touched the light
So close I felt it breathing, then it slipped into the night
I don't cry about it often but on certain shades of blue
I live inside the almost, where my other life came true

But I still got the music and I still got the fire
Maybe almost was a doorway, not a closing on desire
So I'll sing it in the kitchen, sing it loud and sing it free
The night I almost made it made an honest song of me

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The words done fell like broken glass, now the room is holding still
You by the window, me by the door, and the kettle going chill
No riddim in the silence, just the one-drop of the clock
All the things we shouldn't said now sittin' heavy on the block
The fan turn slow, the curtain breathe, neither one will make the sound
That says I'm sorry, come back close, let me put my arms around

In the quiet, in the quiet after the storm
The love still there, just tired and worn
Bassline low like a heartbeat slow
Waiting for the brave one to let the anger go

I hear you breathing different, that little catch you do
When you holding back the crying 'cause you're stubborn just like I do too
The evening turn to honey-gold, the argument gone small
We were never really fighting each other after all
Just two tired hearts colliding 'cause the day was hard and long
And the love beneath the shouting is the only thing still strong

In the quiet, in the quiet after the storm
The love still there, just tired and worn
Bassline low like a heartbeat slow
Waiting for the brave one to let the anger go

So I cross the little distance, lay my hand upon your back
And you melt into the silence, all the hardness going slack
No need for big apology, just the riddim of we two
Breathing in the after-quiet — me and you, me and you

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Moonlight on the backroad, jar of liquid gold
Engine running quiet, story yet untold
Mama said be careful, baby watch the line
But I chased the silver dollar, thought I'd be just fine

Oh, the run went wrong, the run went wrong
Flashing lights behind me singing my own song
Should've stayed at home where the love was strong
Now I'm learning lessons that I had all along

Tires on the gravel, heart up in my throat
Every dollar spent before I ever wrote
The note to say I'm sorry for the road I run
Now the sun is rising on the damage done

Oh, the run went wrong, the run went wrong
Flashing lights behind me singing my own song
Should've stayed at home where the love was strong
Now I'm learning lessons that I had all along

Gonna lay it down, gonna let it go
Every broken promise that the river knows
From the shadows rising into morning light
Gonna find my way back home tonight

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Under the olive moon I ride, fire in my veins
Clay jars in the saddle, dust upon the lanes
My heart it beats like castanets, a warning in the night
But I would not listen, no, I rode against the light

Ay, the run went wrong, the wind it cried my name
Guitar strings are weeping for my pride and for my shame
Dance the floor, stamp the floor, the rhythm of my fate
I sold my soul for silver and I learned the cost too late

The gypsy women warned me, the cards they spoke of rain
The shadows in the valley sang a melody of pain
The bottles shattered on the stones, the gold poured to the ground
And in the broken silence only sorrow could be found

Ay, the run went wrong, the wind it cried my name
Guitar strings are weeping for my pride and for my shame
Dance the floor, stamp the floor, the rhythm of my fate
I sold my soul for silver and I learned the cost too late

Olé, olé, my hands they clap the truth
The fire of my youth is burning down to proof
Let the heels strike the earth, let the passion rise
I'll dance away the morning with the sorrow in my eyes

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Summer in the city, pieces on the board
Old man with a smile that you cannot afford
Five dollars for a lesson, ten to play for real
He moves just like the rivers, baby, that's the deal

King me, queen me, watch the way I play
Every move a story, every game a brand new day
In the park where the pigeons fly, the masters never fall
Checkmate, baby, he's been hustling through it all

Got a velvet voice and fingers quick and clean
He's the smoothest gambler that the block has ever seen
Clock is ticking softly while the city makes its sound
And the bishop slides like honey till the kingdom's coming down

King me, queen me, watch the way I play
Every move a story, every game a brand new day
In the park where the pigeons fly, the masters never fall
Checkmate, baby, he's been hustling through it all

Life is just a chessboard, baby, that's the truth
Every pawn is dreaming of the crown it'll get soon
So he sits and waits, patient as the dawn
The hustler in the park keeps on playing on and on

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I was born beneath a thundercloud, raised on rolling skies
Got the highway in my blood and lightning in my eyes
Mama begged me settle down, find a quiet town
But the wind keeps on calling and I can't lay it down

Chasing storms, chasing storms across the open plain
I'm in love with the danger and I'm married to the rain
They say a man should settle, build a house and stay
But I'll be gone tomorrow when the clouds roll my way

There's a girl back in the valley with a ribbon in her hair
She waits beside the window but I'm never gonna be there
For every funnel on the field is calling out my name
And the road that I keep running is the only love I claim

Chasing storms, chasing storms across the open plain
I'm in love with the danger and I'm married to the rain
They say a man should settle, build a house and stay
But I'll be gone tomorrow when the clouds roll my way

Maybe someday I'll grow weary, maybe someday I'll be still
Maybe someday I'll come home to that house upon the hill
But until the sky stops turning and the wind has had its say
I'll be out there in the thunder, chasing storms away

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Back in my city, same block, same streets
Saw you at the corner store, my heart skipped a beat
Grew up on the same side, knew you way back when
Now you looking like a movie, girl, let's run it again

Hometown, hometown, fell for you again
Same stars, same town, you my day one, not pretend
They don't know our story, baby, where it all began
Hometown love, yeah, you the realest in the land

I been around the world but it ain't feel like this
Every city that I touched I was missing your kiss
Remember on the porch, we was talking 'bout dreams
Now I'm living all of 'em but it's empty, it seems

Hometown, hometown, fell for you again
Same stars, same town, you my day one, not pretend
They don't know our story, baby, where it all began
Hometown love, yeah, you the realest in the land

We been through the struggle, seen it all go down
But I'd trade all the diamonds just to keep you around
No matter where I go, girl, you my home base
Hometown in your eyes, I found my favorite place

songs.subject: the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The lights are coming down on the final song
We danced in the dust and the days felt long
Your hand in my hand and the bass in my chest
This fleeting little summer was the very best

Oh, it's the last night, the last night of it all
We're screaming at the stars, we won't let the curtain fall
Hold on a little longer, don't you let me go
The last night of the festival is all we'll ever know

The tents will come down in the morning grey
And the strangers we loved will all drift away
But tonight we are golden, tonight we are free
Tonight you are everything the world means to me

Oh, it's the last night, the last night of it all
We're screaming at the stars, we won't let the curtain fall
Hold on a little longer, don't you let me go
The last night of the festival is all we'll ever know

Woah, woah, the morning's gonna come
Woah, woah, but right now we are young
So throw your hands up to the fading sky
And we'll remember this until the day we die

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Brass band marching down Saint Charles, slow and low
Umbrella in the air, we put on a show
They laid my brother down but his spirit's still here
So we second-line, wipe away the tear

Down in New Orleans where the trumpets cry
We celebrate the living when the homies say goodbye
Dance for the dead, let the spirit fly
From the sorrow to the joy, we touch the sky

Tuba in the front, snare drum on the side
We mourn in the morning then we take it in stride
From Treme to the Ninth, every soul gonna feel
The rhythm of the city, yeah, the pain is real

Down in New Orleans where the trumpets cry
We celebrate the living when the homies say goodbye
Dance for the dead, let the spirit fly
From the sorrow to the joy, we touch the sky

When I go, don't you cry for me
Just grab a horn and set my spirit free
Let the whole city dance in the street
Where the sorrow and the joy and the heaven all meet

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Every morning I walk the marble halls
Footsteps echo soft against the silent walls
And she's hanging there, the same as yesterday
A woman made of oil who never goes away

And I rise, and I rise, in the quiet of the light
Keeping watch over beauty through the endless night
She will never know my name, she will never turn to see
But I'll guard her till the morning, she's the whole world to me

The crowds they come and go but they never really stay
They snap their little pictures then they drift away
But I have learned her secrets, every shadow, every line
This painted, silent stranger that I've made into mine

And I rise, and I rise, in the quiet of the light
Keeping watch over beauty through the endless night
She will never know my name, she will never turn to see
But I'll guard her till the morning, she's the whole world to me

When the lights go down and the doors are locked
It's just her and me and the ticking clock
And I swear sometimes I see her almost breathe
In the silence of the gallery, she's the only thing I need

songs.subject: the last dance of a long marriage
songs.lyrics:
The record's spinning slow, the lamplight's burning low
Forty years of footprints on this kitchen floor
Your hand is in my hand like the very first time
When you were seventeen and I made you mine

So dance with me, darling, one more time
Before the morning takes what's yours and mine
We've weathered every season, every storm and every fall
So dance with me, darling, the last dance of them all

We raised 'em up and watched 'em go, built a life from nothing slow
Through the good years and the lean, every high and every low
Now your hair is silver and the wrinkles tell our tale
But you're still the prettiest girl who ever lived in this dale

So dance with me, darling, one more time
Before the morning takes what's yours and mine
We've weathered every season, every storm and every fall
So dance with me, darling, the last dance of them all

The song is almost over but our love it lingers on
In the photographs and memories long after we are gone
So hold me close and sway with me beneath this fading light
And we'll dance into forever on this quiet, holy night

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
The cypress trees are weeping in the heavy Delta air
A black umbrella rising and the smell of death is there
They carry down the casket through the cobblestones and rain
While the trumpet plays a hymn to all the sorrow and the pain

Oh, bury me in New Orleans where the dead don't sleep alone
Where the river keeps the secrets and the angels carve the stone
Let the brass band play me homeward through the mist and through the moss
For in this drowning city even joy is born of loss

The candles in the chapel throw their shadows on the wall
And the voodoo queen is whispering a low funereal call
The mourners dressed in tatters dance a slow and crooked tread
While the spirits in the graveyard rise to walk among the dead

Oh, bury me in New Orleans where the dead don't sleep alone
Where the river keeps the secrets and the angels carve the stone
Let the brass band play me homeward through the mist and through the moss
For in this drowning city even joy is born of loss

When the saints come marching for me I'll be ready for the road
With a coin upon my eyelids and a cross to bear my load
So lay me 'neath the willow where the black water flows
And let New Orleans keep me where nobody ever goes

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The clock strikes twelve and the hallway's still
Another cup of coffee and a stack of pills
My family's at the table, the turkey's getting cold
But there's folks here who need me, so I do what I'm told

Working a double on a holiday
While the rest of the world is tucked away
But there's an angel in these scrubs and a light in this room
And I'll hold somebody's hand through the dark and the gloom

Room 304 just lost his wife, room 8 just had a son
I carry all their stories till my weary shift is done
The tinsel's on the nurses' desk, the radio plays low
And I'll be here when the sun comes up over the snow

Working a double on a holiday
While the rest of the world is tucked away
But there's an angel in these scrubs and a light in this room
And I'll hold somebody's hand through the dark and the gloom

My feet are aching, my heart is full
This ain't a job, it's a holy pull
So save my plate, I'll be home by dawn
'Cause somebody's gotta keep the love light on

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The rooms are empty now, just echoes on the floor
A strip of tape, a cardboard box, and one last open door
The sunlight through the window where the table used to be
And every little corner holds a piece of you and me

So I tape up the last box, oh, hey, hey, hey
Dancing with the memories as I'm packing them away
Laugh so you don't cry, my friend, the fiddle starts to play
A whole life in a little box and now I'm on my way

The clarinet is weeping but my feet begin to kick
For every ending's a beginning, that's the oldest trick
The doorframe where we marked the years, the kitchen full of song
I'll carry them inside my heart no matter where I belong

So I tape up the last box, oh, hey, hey, hey
Dancing with the memories as I'm packing them away
Laugh so you don't cry, my friend, the fiddle starts to play
A whole life in a little box and now I'm on my way

Oy, the violin, it spins me round and round
From the laughter to the tears, it's the sweetest, saddest sound
So turn the key and say goodbye, then dance into the night
For the heart it carries everything, and everything's alright

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Midnight in the gallery, the lights are running low
Just me and her and silence in this endless marble row
She's framed in gold and waiting and she never says a word
But every night I feel her like a song that's never heard

Move, move, dancing in the dark
She's a painting on the wall but she's a fire in my heart
Feel the rhythm rising, let the colors come alive
In the museum after midnight, baby, that's where I survive

The beat is in the marble and the bass is in the floor
And I swear that she is moving as I'm pacing past the door
Four on the floor forever, let the painted world ignite
She's the only one who knows me in the silence of the night

Move, move, dancing in the dark
She's a painting on the wall but she's a fire in my heart
Feel the rhythm rising, let the colors come alive
In the museum after midnight, baby, that's where I survive

Let it go, let it go, let the spirit free
She's a masterpiece of color and she's dancing here with me
Raise your hands to the ceiling, feel the painted angels sing
In the museum after midnight, love can be a living thing

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
They put us on the floor, said you better play nice
Your eyes were full of fire and my words were made of ice
We've been at each other's throats since the very start
But the music's getting closer and it's messing with my heart

So we dance, we dance, enemies in time
Your hand upon my waistline, every step a perfect crime
I was born to hate you but the rhythm's got a plan
Now I'm falling for my rival as we move across the sand

One, two, step, and the tension starts to break
Every turn a little softer, every breath a sweet mistake
The spotlight's getting warmer and the war begins to fade
In the heat of the music, oh, a different game is played

So we dance, we dance, enemies in time
Your hand upon my waistline, every step a perfect crime
I was born to hate you but the rhythm's got a plan
Now I'm falling for my rival as we move across the sand

Maybe hate and love are closer than they seem
Maybe you were never my enemy, just the other half my dream
So pull me a little closer, let the old war disappear
And we'll dance until the morning makes it crystal clear

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Neon lights are bleeding soft into the velvet haze
We were only playing, lost inside the smoke and daze
You dealt me out a heartbeat and I bet it all on you
The cards came down like petals and I never had a clue

We played too far, oh, we played too far
Chasing every shimmer like a falling, fading star
The table turned to water and the night dissolved to blue
We played a game of forever and I lost it all to you

The queen of hearts was melting in the slow and silver light
And every chip we gambled was another piece of night
I raised you all my secrets, you called me with your eyes
And somewhere in the shuffle we forgot about the prize

We played too far, oh, we played too far
Chasing every shimmer like a falling, fading star
The table turned to water and the night dissolved to blue
We played a game of forever and I lost it all to you

Fold, fold, the dream is coming down
We spent it all on nothing and now nobody's around
But I'd play it all again, I'd lose it all for true
Just to feel the velvet midnight one more time with you

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Old Earl's been cuttin' hair since nineteen-sixty-three
Got a chair by the window where the whole town can see
He knew your daddy's cowlick and your grandpa's gray
He'll tell you who got married and who up and moved away

He heard about the layoff 'fore the foreman made the call
Kept a jar of penny candy for the kids out in the hall
He don't write nothin' down but he remembers every name
Every scar and every secret, and he never plays a game

Three dollar trim and a dollar for the shave
Earl knows the living and he tends to all the graves
He's the heart of Main Street, the pulse beneath the sign
The barber knows the whole town, one head at a time

When Tommy came back broken from a war across the sea
It was Earl who sat beside him, said, son, you're home with me
He never charged the widows, never told a soul their fears
Just hummed an old church hymn and caught their fallin' tears

Now the mirror's gettin' foggy and his hands have started to shake
But he opens up at seven for the whole town's sake
There's a line out to the sidewalk, folks just come to sit and stay
'Cause when Earl finally hangs the apron, somethin' dies that day

Three dollar trim and a dollar for the shave
Earl knows the living and he tends to all the graves
He's the heart of Main Street, the pulse beneath the sign
The barber knows the whole town, one head at a time

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Llegué al barrio con cien en el bolsillo
La mesa en la esquina, el humo amarillo
Repartió las cartas el viejo Castillo
Y el corazón me latía como un martillo

Gané la primera, gané la segunda
La codicia en mi pecho como ola que inunda
Subí la apuesta, la cadena, el reloj
La noche se puso caliente, perdí la razón y la voz

Una carta más, una carta más (dale)
No sabía cuándo parar, ya no hay vuelta atrás
Lo aposté todo en la mesa fatal
El juego se fue de las manos, terminó muy mal

Perdí las llaves, perdí hasta el carro
El viejo sonriendo encendiendo un cigarro
Saqué lo que no debía, brilló bajo la luz
Y el aire se congeló, ya nadie se rió

Gritos en la calle, sirenas a lo lejos
Me vi en el cristal, no reconocí mis reflejos
Por un mazo de cartas lo perdí todo, mi gente
Que te sirva de lección: sabé cuándo retirarte a tiempo

Una carta más, una carta más (no)
No sabía cuándo parar, ya no hay vuelta atrás
Lo aposté todo en la mesa fatal
El juego se fue de las manos, terminó muy mal

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Flour on the window light
bleeding gold into the white
I was four and barely tall
fingers tracing down the wall

cinnamon and something warm
a slow dissolving thunderstorm
her apron smelled of rain and bread
of every word she never said

and it floods me, it floods me now
that kitchen drowning in the haze
I'd give the years I've burned away
to stand inside one ordinary day

the kettle hum, the clouded glass
the way the afternoons would pass
like honey poured too slow to keep
I fell into that hum like sleep

the house is sold, the stove is cold
the new tenants don't know
that ghosts can live in cinnamon
and never let you go
and never let you go

and it floods me, it floods me now
that kitchen drowning in the haze
I'd give the years I've burned away
to stand inside one ordinary day

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Bajo el plátano viejo monta su altar
una mesa, dos sillas, un reloj que hace temblar
viene el turista orgulloso a desafiar
y él sonríe, ya sabe cómo va a terminar

Mueve el peón como quien tira una flor
esconde la trampa detrás del candor
te deja ganar la torre, te invita a soñar
y en tres movimientos te empieza a desplumar

Jaque, mi amor, jaque al corazón
en el tablero del parque no existe el perdón
diez pesos la partida, la vida es así
rey acorralado, ya no hay para ti

Ganó cien batallas, ninguna en papel
fue campeón de algo que nadie quiso ver
la gloria que pudo, la cambió por pan
pero aquí en su reino es emperador y guardián

Y cuando cae la tarde recoge sus peones
guarda en una bolsa sus pequeñas razones
un maestro sin nombre, sin trofeo, sin luz
que solo entre tilos lleva su cruz

Jaque, mi amor, jaque al corazón
en el tablero del parque no existe el perdón
diez pesos la partida, la vida es así
rey acorralado, ya no hay para ti

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
They carried him slow down Rampart Street
a muffled drum, a draggin' beat
the handkerchiefs were soaked and white
the morning held its breath all tight

but when the trumpet hit, the whole street turned to gold
dance him home, dance him home, let the sorrow go
feet on the pavement, hands up to the sky
we don't say goodbye in this town, we fly

the tuba kicked, the snare let loose
the grief untied its heavy noose
grandmama spun in her Sunday dress
laughin' through the tears, no less

and when the trumpet hit, the whole street turned to gold
dance him home, dance him home, let the sorrow go
feet on the pavement, hands up to the sky
we don't say goodbye in this town, we fly

second line, second line, twirl the parasol
the living and the leaving move as one soul
from dirge into the rapture, from the dark into the light
we bury all our sadness in the rhythm of the night

and when the trumpet hit, the whole street turned to gold
dance him home, dance him home, let the sorrow go
feet on the pavement, hands up to the sky
we don't say goodbye in this town, we fly

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Cada noche cuando cierro los ojos
se me caen del cuerpo todos los cerrojos
me levanto del techo, dejo la ciudad
los edificios chiquitos, pura libertad

Vuelo, vuelo, sobre el malecón
el viento en la cara me cura el corazón
vuelo, vuelo, no me quiero despertar
que la cama me espera pero el cielo es mi lugar

Paso por las nubes como quien pasa el portal
veo a mi abuela en la azotea, me saluda igual
el mar abajo brilla como un mapa de cristal
y por una vez la vida no me sabe a sal

Pero suena el despertador, brutal, sin compasión
y mi cuerpo otra vez pesa como un camión
bajo al tren, a la rutina, al gris del cartón
guardando para esta noche mi única evasión

Dicen que volar es querer escapar
de algo que de día no me atrevo a nombrar
pero prefiero el cielo a quedarme a llorar
así que apago la luz y me dejo elevar

Vuelo, vuelo, sobre el malecón
el viento en la cara me cura el corazón
vuelo, vuelo, no me quiero despertar
que la cama me espera pero el cielo es mi lugar

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
Man in a suit came knockin', briefcase in his hand
said son, we'll make you famous, biggest name in all the land
just water down them verses, give the people what they buy
I looked him in the necktie, and I told that man goodbye

I got holes in my pockets, holes in my shoes
but I ain't never gonna sell these blues
you can keep your shiny dollar, keep your golden crown
this pen don't write for sale, no, this pen won't bow down

My landlord's gettin' restless, the cupboard's lookin' bare
I write by a candle 'cause the power went somewhere
but every word is honest, every line is mine
and a hungry man with truth still beats a fed man tellin' lies

They play the empty music on the radio all day
got a thousand singin' nothin' in the very same gray way
but down here in the gutter where the real ones come to drink
they know a poem costs you everything, and they know what I think

So let 'em build their towers out of plastic and of foam
I'll be writin' in the darkness, makin' language out of bone
and when the towers crumble and the gold turns into rust
these words'll still be standin', risen up out of the dust

I got holes in my pockets, holes in my shoes
but I ain't never gonna sell these blues
you can keep your shiny dollar, keep your golden crown
this pen don't write for sale, no, this pen won't bow down

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
streetlight buzzing on the corner where we stood
counting cracks in concrete like we always would
the same four walls, the same parked cars
the same kids wishing on the same dim stars

but I'm gone, I'm gone, watch the block grow small
in the rearview mirror, in the morning's call
I'm a balloon let loose, I'm a kite cut free
this dead-end street was never meant for me

mama waved her dishtowel from the second floor
said don't you dare come back the way you went before
I packed the silence and a paper bag
and a heart that wouldn't let the old block drag

the bus pulled out at quarter after five
first time in years I felt the thing called alive
the billboards melted into open sky
and I didn't even turn to say goodbye

but I'll send for you, every name I knew
every friend still stuck inside the avenue
'cause making it out don't mean you forget
it means you build a door for the ones not free yet

so I'm gone, I'm gone, watch the block grow small
in the rearview mirror, in the morning's call
I'm a balloon let loose, I'm a kite cut free
this dead-end street was never meant for me

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Nobody picked us, we were last in the line
bottom of the standings, runnin' out of time
coach with a clipboard and a faith we couldn't see
eleven hungry dreamers and a maybe-could-be

turn it up, turn it up, the clock is countin' down
little team from nowhere takin' over the town
put your hands together, watch the long shot fly
underdogs are dancin' underneath the Friday sky

four minutes left and we were down by three
the crowd was on their feet in electric ecstasy
our captain caught the pass like it was made of gold
and the whole arena trembled as the story got told

disco ball was spinnin' on the gymnasium light
sweat and glitter mixin' in the heat of the night
we ran on pure heartbeat, we ran on the sound
of a thousand voices liftin' us up off the ground

buzzer, buzzer, freeze the final frame
nobody remembers who was favored to claim
'cause the ones who weren't supposed to are the ones they'll recall
the smallest in the room dancin' tallest of all

turn it up, turn it up, the clock is countin' down
little team from nowhere takin' over the town
put your hands together, watch the long shot fly
underdogs are dancin' underneath the Friday sky

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
I was making coffee, sun across the floor
phone lit up beside me like a hundred times before
three gray dots were blinking, then they stopped, then came
thirty-seven words and none of them my name

so this is how it ends, not a slammin' door
not a scream, not a reason, just a glowing little four
by five-inch rectangle saying we should not
I read it forty times until the coffee turned to rot

No phone call, no last look, no standing in the rain
just a notification and a quiet kind of pain
I typed and then I deleted, typed and then deleted more
what do you even say to a goodbye through a store-

-bought little screen that I'll be holding all my life
the same glass that you ended it now buzzes blue and white
with ads and stupid memes and someone else's day
like it didn't just deliver everything away

and I keep the message saved, I don't know why I do
scroll up through all the hearts to get to leaving you
from good morning sunshine down to we are through
one thread, two people, and a thousand shades of blue

so this is how it ends, not a slammin' door
not a scream, not a reason, just a glowing little four
by five-inch rectangle saying we should not
I read it forty times until the coffee turned to rot

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
We locked eyes 'cross the floor, been enemies for years
same club, same crew, same battle, same fears
the DJ called our names, said tonight you move as one
two kings of the cipher and there's nowhere left to run

so we dance, we dance, fire against fire
two flames in the dark building something higher
every step a challenge, every turn a truce
we hated 'til the rhythm cut the both of us loose

I threw a move to break him, he came right back harder
the crowd began to roar like we were lighting up a martyr
but somewhere in the bassline something shifted in the room
our war became a language, our rivalry a bloom

his shadow matched my shadow, my heartbeat caught his beat
the floor became a mirror underneath our flying feet
all the years of cold contempt dissolved into the sound
two rivals turned to partners on the holy hollow ground

maybe we were always the same hunger, the same drive
two halves of one obsession just to feel the most alive
so let the bassline hold us, let the strobe erase the line
your pride and my pride melting in four-four time

so we dance, we dance, fire against fire
two flames in the dark building something higher
every step a challenge, every turn a truce
we hated 'til the rhythm cut the both of us loose

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
we drove the long way down to where the lake goes still
the boxes packed and waiting on the windowsill
your hand out the window catching wind like time
the radio playing something we'd never find

and it's the last, last day, gold all over the trees
let's not talk about leaving, let's just feel the breeze
tomorrow we're strangers in a city of new
today we're forever, just me and just you

we wrote our names in sunscreen on each other's backs
watched them fade to nothing in the afternoon cracks
you said do you think we'll change, and I couldn't say
so I held your shoulder as the light slipped away

the ice cream truck went silent down the empty street
the summer pulling slowly out from under our feet
we stayed until the fireflies replaced the sun
pretending the long warm season hadn't come undone

and somewhere there's a version where we never grew
where the lake stays warm and the sky stays blue
where your laugh's still echoing across the bay
and nobody packs the boxes, nobody drives away

but it's the last, last day, gold all over the trees
let's not talk about leaving, let's just feel the breeze
tomorrow we're strangers in a city of new
today we're forever, just me and just you

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Levanto la mano y se apaga mi voz
hablo y me pisan, me cortan veloz
la idea era mía, la dije primero
pero la repite él y ahora es un lucero

¡Ay, que me callan, que me callan otra vez!
grito por dentro y por fuera cortés
la mesa redonda no es redonda pa' mí
todos me atraviesan como si no estoy aquí

Veinte palabras y ninguna llegó
se las llevó el viento de quien más gritó
tengo razones, tengo el fuego en el pecho
pero el turno de hablar nunca me lo han hecho

Apunto en mi cuaderno cada vez que callé
cada buena propuesta que tragué con el café
y por dentro retumba un compás de tacón
un zapateado entero de pura indignación

Pero un día me planto, golpeo la madera
y suelto las palmas como nadie las espera
que suene mi taconeo más fuerte que su no
porque el silencio, señores, hoy se terminó

¡Ya no me callan, ya no me callan, no!
grito por fuera lo que por dentro guardó
la mesa redonda se va a enterar de mí
porque vengo con duende y existo aquí

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
There's a cornbread memory livin' in my mind
collard greens simmerin', take me back in time
the screen door creakin', flour in the air
Grandmama hummin' gospel from her rockin' chair

oh, take me to that kitchen, let me breathe it in
the butter and the cinnamon, the warmth beneath my skin
there's a heaven in that doorway, there's a love I can't replace
oh, that kitchen was a church and grace was on her face

Sunday morning bacon, biscuits risin' high
she'd slip me a sweet potato 'fore the rest came by
her hands knew every secret, every spice and song
and the whole house felt like somewhere I could not go wrong

Now the years done took her, took the stove, the chair
but I swear when I'm cookin' I can feel her there
one whiff of nutmeg risin' and my eyes get wet
'cause some kinds of love a heart will never forget

so I make her sweet potato pie the way she'd do
measurin' with my heart 'cause she never told me to
and the smell fills up my own house, fills it to the brim
and for one golden minute I'm a child again with him

oh, take me to that kitchen, let me breathe it in
the butter and the cinnamon, the warmth beneath my skin
there's a heaven in that doorway, there's a love I can't replace
oh, that kitchen was a church and grace was on her face

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Gates locked up, padlock on the dream
thirty years of steel just vanished off the scene
Daddy worked the line 'fore I could even walk
now the whistle don't blow and the floor don't talk

plant's closin' down, down, lights goin' out
whole town built on it, now they full of doubt
they shipped the jobs overseas for a dime
left us holdin' lunch pails and a clock with no time

Mr. Johnson cryin' in the parking lot
fifty-five years old, that's the only job he got
foreman shook his hand, said there's nothin' he could do
orders from the top floor, men they never knew

Main Street boarded up, diner servin' air
the pharmacy, the hardware, all the lights went bare
we was proud of what we made, put it in your car
now we ghosts in our own town, don't know who we are

but my people don't break, no, my people adapt
mama back in school and my brother flippin' that
we was forged in that furnace, we was tempered in the heat
you can close the factory but you can't close the street

plant's closin' down, down, lights goin' out
whole town built on it, now they full of doubt
they shipped the jobs overseas for a dime
left us holdin' lunch pails and a clock with no time

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
He keeps a window full of morning light
thread the color of a tangerine
the mailman's collar, the widow's hem
every shoulder on the avenue leans clean

Chalk lines drawn on a Tuesday back
a pencil tucked behind a patient ear
he measures the baker, he cuffs the boy
who's gotten taller every single year

Sew me soft, sew me slow
let the seam follow where I go
the whole block wears his quiet hand
a little kingdom, pressed and planned

Buttons in a tin like sleeping bees
scissors humming low against the cloth
he hems the dusk into the sleeve
and sends it dancing where the streetlights cross

No one knows the secret of his ease
the way he folds a worry into pleats
he stitches us together one by one
and we go shining down the gentle streets

Sew me soft, sew me slow
let the seam follow where I go
the whole block wears his quiet hand
a little kingdom, pressed and planned

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They poured the concrete over something old
laid the railroad down across his spine
Momma said don't stomp so hard out there
there's a heartbeat underneath the county line

The courthouse leans a little more each spring
the well runs warm when the summer's dry
old men on the porch say they felt him turn
saw the church bell swing without a wind to try

We built our whole town on his shoulder blade
raised our children in the shadow of his sleep
Lord, let him dream a little longer now
there's a promise in these streets we cannot keep

The diner cups go rattling on the shelf
when the freight comes through, or maybe when he sighs
I married Jolene by the old oak tree
that grows up from the corner of his eyes

One day he'll stretch and shake the steeples down
fold the highways like a Sunday shirt
but until then we'll plant our little rows
and thank the giant for the loaned-out dirt

We built our whole town on his shoulder blade
raised our children in the shadow of his sleep
Lord, let him dream a little longer now
there's a promise in these streets we cannot keep

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Rain on the awning, static off the train
the city hums on something we can't name
foundations breathing slow beneath the floor
you feel the warm come up through the hardware store

Neon flickers in a puddle on his chest
we pay our rent on borrowed time, I guess
grandma said keep your footsteps light and kind
there's a sleeper down below who reads your mind

Don't wake him up, don't wake him up
keep the bassline low, fill the coffee cup
the whole block resting on a giant's breath
we live so soft we never speak of death

Clock on the wall doing nothing slow
dust in the lamplight, nowhere left to go
I press my palm flat to the kitchen tile
and feel him turning over for a while

Maybe we're a dream he's having now
the streetlights, the smoke, the cigarette glow
so let the tape hiss, let the record spin
let's stay this quiet 'til he wakes again

Don't wake him up, don't wake him up
keep the bassline low, fill the coffee cup
the whole block resting on a giant's breath
we live so soft we never speak of death

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Oy, lift your feet on the cobblestone road
the clarinet cries where the old man's stowed
they built the market on his folded knee
and the fiddle plays a tremble none can see

The rabbi blesses what he cannot move
the bride steps light in a borrowed groove
beneath the square a giant turns his head
and the chimney shivers crumbs of crusted bread

Dance, dance, but softly on his brow
the accordion knows what we don't allow
spin the bride beneath the giant's sigh
one false stomp and the whole town learns to fly

The grandmother counts on her thread-worn beads
planting her garden in the giant's creases
the trumpet leaps and the drum runs hot
we celebrate the floor we maybe ought not

If he wakes, let him wake to a song
a minor key we have carried so long
let the strings lean low and the cymbal hush
and cradle the sleeper in our joyful rush

Dance, dance, but softly on his brow
the accordion knows what we don't allow
spin the bride beneath the giant's sigh
one false stomp and the whole town learns to fly

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I open my mouth and the room takes a breath
then swallows my sentence before it is said
your voice is a blade and it cuts to the front
while mine lies down quiet, a coat on the bed

I rehearsed every word like a step on the floor
the turn and the pause and the lean and the snap
but the music belongs to the loudest of men
and I dance with my hand pressed over my map

Talk over me, talk over me
your drumbeat drowning what I came to be
but watch how I hold this silence like a knife
one day the room will lean to hear my life

The table is long and the lamplight is low
I count all the times that my name disappeared
the minutes are taken, the credit is yours
and my best idea wears another man's beard

So I sharpen my patience, I polish my poise
I gather the quiet they think is defeat
there's a tango that ends with the floor going still
and only my heels coming down on the beat

Talk over me, talk over me
your drumbeat drowning what I came to be
but watch how I hold this silence like a knife
one day the room will lean to hear my life

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Gray light through the blinds in horizontal lines
I raise my hand like a flag with no wind
the glass on the table holds a trembling sun
and the sentence I built starts caving in

Louder, louder, the wall of their sound
I am a small light going under the ground
but the quiet is gathering, slow and immense
a tide pulling back from the edge of the fence

Minutes dissolve into somebody's name
the whiteboard fills up with a war that's not mine
I fold my idea like a paper bird
and set it adrift on the conference room line

And it builds
and it builds
the drums find the room
the guitars climb the walls of the afternoon
