every swallowed word coming back as a wave
cresting the table, the chairs, and the grave

Louder, louder, but now it is me
the whole room cracking like ice on a sea
the quiet I carried becomes a great sound
and finally, finally, they turn around

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
It starts in the hallway with rain on the glass
my feet leave the carpet, the ceiling gives way
the town spreads below like a circuit of gold
and I rise through the cold of the end of the day

Up, and the rooftops fall under my chest
the wind a slow chord I have learned how to ride
I have done this a thousand sleeping nights
and woken with morning still caught in my side

The power lines hum as I pass them like birds
the river a ribbon untying its hands
I bank toward a window I cannot quite reach
with somebody's face I can't quite understand

And the strings open wide
and the sky goes to white
the drums are the heartbeat of leaving the ground
everything weightless, everything bright
a roar in the silence, a falling that's found

Up, and the rooftops fall under my chest
the wind a slow chord I have learned how to ride
I have done this a thousand sleeping nights
and I'm never afraid until I open my eyes

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
The payphone glow on a Nebraska night
the clarinet weeps where the diesel sighs
I feed the dark every coin that I've got
to hear you breathe under the parking lot skies

The fiddle runs fast like the white dotted line
the cargo behind me, the miles up ahead
you answer so soft with the sleep in your throat
and I picture the warmth of our crooked old bed

Oy, three in the morning and a thousand miles wide
the accordion breathing the road that I ride
sing me back home through the wire and the rain
till the dawn comes to lift this old weight off the chain

The little ones dreaming, you tell me they're fine
the roof that needs fixing, the dog that won't eat
I laugh and the laughter goes thin as a string
and the trumpet comes up from the cold of the street

Dance with me here on the edge of the dark
just you and the static and the hum of the truck
the band in my chest playing minor and bright
for a man who has nothing but distance and luck

Oy, three in the morning and a thousand miles wide
the accordion breathing the road that I ride
sing me back home through the wire and the rain
till the dawn comes to lift this old weight off the chain

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
The crows came back to the courthouse spire
the same year you came walking down
I swore I had buried this place in the dirt
but you dug up my name from the cold of the town

We drank from the river that drowned my old man
kissed in the graveyard where my mother is laid
you wear the dust of these streets in your hair
and I love you the way that a debt's never paid

Oh, fall for the girl from the back of the church
the one with the hometown sewn into her bones
she knows where the bodies and the lovers are buried
and she'll lead you back home over miles of gray stones

The steeple still leans like it's trying to fall
the willows still whisper your grandmother's hymn
I left and I left and I left and I stayed
and the love grows up dark where the lamplight is dim

There's no leaving a love that was born in the ground
where your first breath and your last name agree
so bury me here in the soil where I'm from
with the one who's as haunted and rooted as me

Oh, fall for the girl from the back of the church
the one with the hometown sewn into her bones
she knows where the bodies and the lovers are buried
and she'll lead you back home over miles of gray stones

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Moonlight resting on the long flat road
eighteen wheels and a heavy load
I pull to the side where the streetlamp hums
dial up the number, feel my heart drum

Call you at three in the morning, my love
stars rolling slow in the dark up above
tell the children daddy's coming with the dawn
keep the porch light burning, keep it shining on

You pick up sleepy and your voice is gold
warmer than the coffee growing cold
rain on the windshield, riddim on the seat
bassline rolling underneath my weary feet

I carry the world so the world can eat
mile after mile down the empty street
but all that I want is your hand on my face
and the smell of the morning in our little place

Distance is heavy but the love is light
it travels the wire through the belly of night
one day I'll park this rig for good
and stay in the yard like I always should

Call you at three in the morning, my love
stars rolling slow in the dark up above
tell the children daddy's coming with the dawn
keep the porch light burning, keep it shining on

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Green light, slow roll, down the avenue
the window still glows the same dusty blue
somebody new hanging curtains up there
in the room where you used to braid my hair

Driving past slow in the cool of the night
our old little window still burning its light
I don't stop, no, I just let it go by
a riddim of memory under the sky

The corner store still got the flickering sign
the stoop where we sat is no longer mine
bass in the speaker keeping time with my chest
rolling on past where I once loved you best

I could've turned in, I could've sat still
but some doors stay shut by an act of the will
the streetlight she paints all the ghosts on the wall
and I keep my foot easy, I don't stall

Let it pass, let it pass like the smoke
like the things in the dark that we never spoke
the wheels keep turning, the city keeps wide
and I leave you behind with the warmth inside

Driving past slow in the cool of the night
our old little window still burning its light
I don't stop, no, I just let it go by
a riddim of memory under the sky

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I was born in the eye where the quiet goes mad
baptized in the green of a Kansas sky
my mother said son you will love what you fear
and I chase down the funnels till the day that I die

There's a woman who waits in a house with a porch
she asks me to stay when the season is through
but the barometer drops and the crows scatter wide
and I leave her again for the bruise-colored blue

I can't settle down where the still air sleeps
I'm married to thunder, I'm wed to the wind
the storm is the only thing wild as my blood
and it leaves me alive where the wreckage begins

I've stood in the roar where the houses take flight
seen the hand of the dark pull a town off the map
and I felt more at home in that terrible noise
than I ever did sleeping in anyone's lap

One day a black wall will swallow me whole
and they'll never recover a bone or a name
but bury me empty, just leave me out there
in the field where I worshipped the lightning and flame

I can't settle down where the still air sleeps
I'm married to thunder, I'm wed to the wind
the storm is the only thing wild as my blood
and it leaves me alive where the wreckage begins

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
The room fills up with a soft white noise
your photograph fading to milk in my hands
I carried this anger so long it went numb
now it slips through my fingers like fistfuls of sand

I forgive you, I forgive you, into the haze
though you can't hear a word from the place that you went
the reverb is washing the bitterness out
and I'm spending the grief that I never had spent

The guitars are an ocean of gauze and of gray
your name a slow chord that dissolves as it rings
I thought I would tell you the day that you came
but you left through a door that goes nowhere it sings

So I say it to no one, I say it to air
I let the wall of the sound carry it far
forgiveness for ghosts is forgiveness for me
for the wound that I held like a held-open jar

I forgive you, I forgive you, into the haze
though you can't hear a word from the place that you went
the reverb is washing the bitterness out
and I'm spending the grief that I never had spent

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
There's a light at the edge of the off-ramp glow
where the coffee is cheap and it's never too late
Marie wipes the counter and hums to the dark
and the door keeps a welcome for the lost and the freight

The trucker, the widow, the kid who can't sleep
the nurse coming home from the long graveyard shift
they gather like moths to the warm yellow hum
where a cup and a booth are a small kind of gift

Keep the light on, keep the griddle warm
for the ones who are out in the cold and the storm
the night runs long but the diner won't close
and it holds all the people that nobody knows

The jukebox still plays for a quarter or two
the pie under glass and the clock running slow
there's a kindness that lives in a three a.m. plate
and a place that won't ask you which way you should go

When the rest of the world locks its doors for the night
and the houses go dark on the hill and the lane
there's a window still burning down by the road
like a lighthouse that's built for the folks in the rain

Keep the light on, keep the griddle warm
for the ones who are out in the cold and the storm
the night runs long but the diner won't close
and it holds all the people that nobody knows

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
It's late and the screen throws a blue on my face
your name in the dark like a match that I struck
I typed it and deleted it twelve times tonight
then sent it anyway, I ran out of luck

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but the thumb finds the send
the wall of the static swallows everything wrong
three dots appearing in the haze and the hum
and I'm drowning so sweetly in the wash of the song

The guitars are a fog rolling under the door
the ceiling dissolves into rivers of gray
I know how it ends, I have ended it twice
but the glow of the phone burns the morning away

Don't answer, don't answer, oh please, just reply
my heart's a feedback that won't let it rest
every reason to stop is a tide pulling out
and the worst thing I want is the thing I want best

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but the thumb finds the send
the wall of the static swallows everything wrong
three dots appearing in the haze and the hum
and I'm drowning so sweetly in the wash of the song

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Chalk lines fading on the worn-out field
We were never the names they came to see
Every whistle blown said we should kneel
But we wrote a different kind of history

Coach said leave it all out on the grass tonight
No scoreboard left to tell us who we are
Underneath the buzzing stadium light
We were small-town kids reaching for a star

This is our final game, our final stand
Grass stains and broken dreams held in our hands
Nobody believed but here we are
Underdogs burning like a falling star

Forty seconds and the crowd goes still
Every heartbeat pounding like a drum
We climbed a long way up this losing hill
And whatever happens now, we've already won

This is our final game, our final stand
Grass stains and broken dreams held in our hands
Nobody believed but here we are
Underdogs burning like a falling star

Oh, the final whistle's gonna blow
Win or lose, this is the only life we know
We carry it forever, every scar
Underdogs forever, here we are

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Down on Main Street there's a spinning pole
Red and white and shining, got that classic soul
Walk on in and take your favorite chair
He knows every story floating in the air

He's the keeper of the secrets in this town
Every wedding, every heartbreak going down
Snip snip rhythm and the gossip flows
Mister Barber, he's the only one who knows

Dance, dance under the neon light
The barber knows the whole town tonight
Groove, groove to the buzzing sound
He keeps the heartbeat of the whole town spinning round

Mayor's whisper and the schoolboy's dream
All get mixed up in that aftershave steam
Fifty years of fade and feathered crowns
He's the smiling soul of this disco town

Dance, dance under the neon light
The barber knows the whole town tonight
Groove, groove to the buzzing sound
He keeps the heartbeat of the whole town spinning round

Mirror shining, scissors gleam
Every face that passes through is part of one big dream
Clap your hands and stomp the ground
The barber's got the rhythm of the whole town

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
The highway hums beneath the silver moon
Diesel and the dust, I'll be home soon
Three in the morning, the payphone rings
Your sleepy voice, the sweetest of things

Trumpets crying in this lonely cab
Guitarrón heartbeat is all that I have
Thousand miles of yellow line ahead
I hear your whisper, mi amor, in my head

Ay, llámame, my love, at three a.m.
The road is long but I'll find my way back again
With every mile my heart cries out your name
Ay, mi corazón, I'm coming home again

The radio plays an old ranchera tune
My coffee's cold beneath this trucker's moon
Tell the children papa's almost there
I smell the kitchen, feel your morning air

Ay, llámame, my love, at three a.m.
The road is long but I'll find my way back again
With every mile my heart cries out your name
Ay, mi corazón, I'm coming home again

Violine weep and the trumpets soar
A few more sunrises, then I drive no more
Keep the porch light burning gold
This lonely trucker's heart is coming home

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
One small pot upon the stove tonight
Two chairs at the table, only one's in the light
Your recipe is faded, written in your hand
I'm learning how to season what I don't understand

The trumpets play a soft and lonely sound
Salt and cumin scattered all around
You always knew the measure, knew the way
Now I cook for one at the end of every day

Ay, mi vida, the kitchen's still warm
I stir the pot and weather every storm
A meal for one but I set the other plate
Mi amor, your memory is the flavor that I taste

The guitar weeps a slow and tender song
I taste the broth and feel that you're not gone
Your apron hanging gently by the door
I'm learning how to live and love once more

Ay, mi vida, the kitchen's still warm
I stir the pot and weather every storm
A meal for one but I set the other plate
Mi amor, your memory is the flavor that I taste

Violins rise in the candlelight
I raise a glass to you tonight
One plate, one heart, one fading flame
But every dish I make still whispers out your name

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Train doors closing, can't catch my breath
Heartbeat racing like I'm running from death
Fluorescent lights flicker, the walls closing in
Sweat on my collar, ice cold on my skin

Strangers all staring but they don't even see
The storm in my chest tryna swallow me
Counting the seconds, counting the stops
Praying to God that the spinning just stops

Can't breathe, can't breathe on the underground
Walls keep shaking but there's no sound
Hold on, hold on till the next stop
Heart in my throat and it won't, it won't stop

Grip on the pole with my knuckles turning white
Tunnel keeps screaming through the endless night
In through the nose, out through the mouth
Tryna find a way to ride this feeling out

Can't breathe, can't breathe on the underground
Walls keep shaking but there's no sound
Hold on, hold on till the next stop
Heart in my throat and it won't, it won't stop

Doors slide open and I stumble to the air
The city's still spinning but I'm finally there
I made it, I made it, one breath at a time
The monster in my chest, I left it on the line

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
Neon reflections on the ballroom floor
Never seen your face in my life before
The band plays slow under violet light
You took my hand and the world felt right

Champagne shimmer and a synthesized beat
Two lonely hearts in a midnight retreat
We don't know the bride, we don't know the groom
But we're falling in love in this borrowed room

Dance with me, stranger, under neon skies
I see forever in your shining eyes
We'll never meet again when the morning comes
But tonight we're electric, tonight we're young

Glitter on your shoulder, stars in your hair
Two silhouettes in the smoke-machine air
The DJ fades into a retro dream
Nothing is real but this moment, it seems

Dance with me, stranger, under neon skies
I see forever in your shining eyes
We'll never meet again when the morning comes
But tonight we're electric, tonight we're young

The clock strikes twelve and the spell will break
A stranger's kiss is all that I'll take
But for one synth-soaked, glittering night
We danced like ghosts in the magenta light

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Midnight engine on a backwoods road
Trunk full of jars and a heavy load
Headlights off, riding low and fast
Knew this kinda luck was never gonna last

Blue lights flashing in my rearview glass
Heart hit the floor as they're gaining fast
Gravel spinning, throwing up the dust
In this old V8 is the only thing I trust

Moonshine run and it all went wrong
Sirens screaming like a devil's song
Down in the holler where the river bends
Pray to God that I make it to the end

Glass jars rattling, the corn liquor spills
Fishtail sliding through the Tennessee hills
Wrong turn taken on a washed-out bridge
Now I'm hanging on the edge of a moonlit ridge

Moonshine run and it all went wrong
Sirens screaming like a devil's song
Down in the holler where the river bends
Pray to God that I make it to the end

Smoke in the engine and the law on my tail
Mama always said this road leads to jail
One last gamble in the dead of night
Moonshine running till I see the light

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Dusty vinyl crackle, rain against the glass
Watching old home movies, ghosts of the past
His hands used to lift me way up to the sky
Now they shake a little when he says goodbye

Gray in his beard where the black used to be
He still calls me kiddo, still worries about me
Time is a river and it don't run slow
Watching my father gently growing old

Slow it down, slow it down, let the moment stay
Don't wanna wake up to find he's faded away
Lazy piano and a soft snare roll
Holding every memory of my father growing old

He naps in the chair where he read me my books
I memorize the lines of his face and his looks
The man who was a mountain now moves like the tide
But he's still my hero, still my guide

Slow it down, slow it down, let the moment stay
Don't wanna wake up to find he's faded away
Lazy piano and a soft snare roll
Holding every memory of my father growing old

Vinyl spinning soft in the amber light
I'll sit a little longer with him tonight
Every wrinkle is a story, every breath is gold
Loving every minute of my father growing old

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Upstroke guitar and the horns hit bright
Grandma in the kitchen in the morning light
Flour on her fingers, rings of worn-down gold
Those hands tell a story that will never get old

She kneaded all the bread and she braided my hair
Wiped away my tears with a tender care
Pickin' up the rhythm, clappin' to the band
There's a whole lotta love in my grandmother's hands

Pick it up, pick it up, let the trumpets play
Grandmother's hands gonna lead the way
Dancin' in the kitchen to a skankin' sound
Those loving hands keep the whole world spinning round

They sewed up my buttons and they held me tight
Lit the candle softly every Friday night
Wrinkled like a roadmap of a life well spent
Every line a blessing, every callus heaven-sent

Pick it up, pick it up, let the trumpets play
Grandmother's hands gonna lead the way
Dancin' in the kitchen to a skankin' sound
Those loving hands keep the whole world spinning round

Now they move a little slower than they did before
But they still reach out to pull me through the door
When I close my eyes I can still understand
The whole of my world in my grandmother's hands

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
Well the band struck up and the floor went still
They put us together against our will
You stepped on my toes and I cut you a glare
Two old rivals in the smoky air

I been hatin' you since the day we met
Now I got my hand on your shoulder, soaking wet
The slide guitar moans a low-down sound
Two proud fools just spinnin' around

Dance with the devil, dance with your foe
The music's got us movin' real slow
I don't wanna like it but Lord help me now
We're dancin' together somehow, somehow

Your eyes met mine and the anger broke
Something funny in the way that you spoke
Maybe all this bad blood was just a song
We been hummin' it together all along

Dance with the devil, dance with your foe
The music's got us movin' real slow
I don't wanna like it but Lord help me now
We're dancin' together somehow, somehow

Harmonica cryin' as the night grows old
Two bitter hearts turnin' soft from cold
Who'd have thought that a twelve-bar tune
Would make two rivals dance beneath the moon

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Smoke in the basement and the vinyl spins low
Four folding chairs and a green felt glow
Started out friendly, just a Friday night thing
Nobody knew what the morning would bring

Chips on the table and the stakes climbing high
Deck getting heavy and we don't know why
Mellow piano but the tension is thick
Somebody's bluffing and the clock starts to tick

Lay 'em down, lay 'em down, but we played too long
What started as a game turned into something wrong
Lazy beat fading in the cigarette haze
We gambled away the rest of our days

Deed to the car and the keys to the door
Nobody folding 'cause we're chasing for more
The dawn creeps in through the dusty blinds
We lost a lot more than the money this time

Lay 'em down, lay 'em down, but we played too long
What started as a game turned into something wrong
Lazy beat fading in the cigarette haze
We gambled away the rest of our days

Snare brush whisper and the record skips
Fortunes traded on the turn of the chips
Some things you can't win back when they're gone
We shoulda walked away before the dawn

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two in the morning and the city's asleep
Books on the table and my promises to keep
Lamplight flicker and the log drum low
Grinding for the future that I'm dreaming to know

Coffee gone cold but my fire still burns
Pages and pages, so much left to learn
The rhythm in my chest keeps me moving on
I'll be standing in the light when the night is gone

Alone, alone but I'm chasing the dream
Two a.m. hustle, it's harder than it seems
Move your body, keep the candle aglow
I'm studying alone but I'm ready to grow

Mama always said that the grind pays off
When the road gets weary and the climb gets rough
Shakers and the groove keep my spirit alive
In the dead of the night is where dreams survive

Alone, alone but I'm chasing the dream
Two a.m. hustle, it's harder than it seems
Move your body, keep the candle aglow
I'm studying alone but I'm ready to grow

Sun gonna rise and I'll still be here
Writing my future, conquering the fear
Every single page is a step on the road
Two in the morning, carrying the load

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
My grandmama's hands, Lord, they worked the land
Soil in the creases, sun-baked tan
They cradled my mama and they cradled me
Those old weathered hands set my whole soul free

Slide guitar moanin' like a memory
She'd hum an old hymn while she'd cook for me
Knuckles like the roots of an old oak tree
Those hands held the weight of a century

Grandmother's hands, oh, they carried the years
Wiped away the sorrow, wiped away the tears
Rough as the river but soft as the sand
There's a whole world of love in my grandmother's hands

She stitched up my britches by the kerosene light
Held onto her Bible through the longest night
When the hard times came she would understand
Lay the trouble to rest with her healin' hands

Grandmother's hands, oh, they carried the years
Wiped away the sorrow, wiped away the tears
Rough as the river but soft as the sand
There's a whole world of love in my grandmother's hands

Now she's restin' easy beneath the willow tree
But I feel those hands every time I'm weak
Harmonica cryin' low and slow
Grandmother's hands won't ever let me go

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Monitor glow in a midnight blue room
Caffeine and the hum of an electric tune
Two a.m. numbers on the digital clock
The whole world sleeping but my mind won't stop

Neon reflections on the open page
A lonely scholar in a digital age
Synths pulsing soft like a distant star
Studying alone is just who we are

Neon nights and the pages turn
Two in the morning and the candle burns
Alone in the glow of the cathode light
Chasing a future through the endless night

Gridlines fading on the bedroom wall
Retro arpeggios begin to call
Every equation is a star to chart
The quiet hum of a beating heart

Neon nights and the pages turn
Two in the morning and the candle burns
Alone in the glow of the cathode light
Chasing a future through the endless night

Dawn is a rumor on the violet sky
One more chapter as the hours fly
In the synthesized hush I will find my way
Studying alone till the break of day

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Suitcase packed in the back of my car
Rearview mirror full of who we are
Water tower fading in the morning haze
Goodbye to the only home I've known my days

Main Street empty as the sun comes up
One last coffee in a paper cup
Everybody knows my name out here
But the world is calling and the road is clear

I'm leaving this small town for good
Doing what I always said I would
With the windows down and the radio loud
Gonna make my mama proud
Leaving this small town for good

Waved to the diner where I used to work
Kissed the old streets, every crack and quirk
My heart is heavy but my dreams are wide
There's a bigger life on the other side

I'm leaving this small town for good
Doing what I always said I would
With the windows down and the radio loud
Gonna make my mama proud
Leaving this small town for good

Maybe one day I'll come driving back
Down this dusty, familiar track
But tonight I'm chasing a brighter star
Leaving this small town for who we are

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Neon hums on the avenue
A single bulb in the window glows
He threads the night in electric blue
Measuring tape and a row of bows
Every hem that the city wears
Started here on his careful chair

He dresses the whole block in light
Stitching the dark into something bright
From the corner store to the midnight train
Everybody walks in his name
He dresses the whole block in light

The boxer's suit and the widow's lace
The busboy's cuffs and the dreamer's coat
He gives each shoulder a sharper grace
And hangs their courage around the throat
Chrome and velvet, the gridlines hum
Waiting for whoever will come

He dresses the whole block in light
Stitching the dark into something bright
From the corner store to the midnight train
Everybody walks in his name
He dresses the whole block in light

When they bury him in his finest seam
The street will shine like a dressed-up dream
And every collar will stand up straight
For the man who made the ordinary great

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Brass on the corner, slow and low
White handkerchiefs in a waving row
Hi-hats rolling like the river tide
Walking my brother to the other side
Umbrella up, gold tooth shine
Second line moving down the line
Tuba breathing under the 808
Death came early but the saints can wait

We march him home, we march him slow
Then we let the body go
Cry on Rampart, dance on Claiborne
Out of the grief a new song's born
We march him home, we march him slow

Grandma rocking on the porch and prayed
Bottles poured for the debts he paid
Flowers wilting in the summer heat
Drums pick up and we shake the street
From the church door to the cemetery gate
We carry love that's heavier than hate

We march him home, we march him slow
Then we let the body go
Cry on Rampart, dance on Claiborne
Out of the grief a new song's born
We march him home, we march him slow

When the snare cuts loose and the trumpet flies
We turn the tears into a sky of light
He ain't gone, he's in the beat
Living in the stomp of the second line feet

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
We paved the streets across his chest
Laid the wires where his ribs would rest
The towers breathe when he turns in sleep
A tremor humming low and deep
Washed in static, washed in haze
We live our small electric days

Don't wake him, don't wake him now
We built our heaven on his brow
Every window, every light
Is a dream he carries through the night
Don't wake him, don't wake him now

The morning fog is his slow breath
The subway shakes like distant death
We planted gardens on his spine
And called the trembling a sign
Guitars dissolve into the gray
A lullaby to keep him laid

Don't wake him, don't wake him now
We built our heaven on his brow
Every window, every light
Is a dream he carries through the night
Don't wake him, don't wake him now

And if one dawn he opens his eyes
The city will fall like falling skies
So sing him soft, so sing him long
A hundred thousand voices strong

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Clippers buzzin' since a quarter past nine
Chair spinning slow, everybody in line
He got the news before the news come out
Knows who's in love and who's got doubt
Hot towel steaming, mirror gleaming bright
He lines you up and he lines up right

He knows the whole town, every name
Every secret, every shame
Sit in his chair, you sit in the truth
He been cutting hair since the days of your youth
He knows the whole town, oh yeah

The mayor come in for a Tuesday trim
Told the barber things he won't tell him
The deacon, the dealer, the boy turning ten
All get the same hands again and again
Groove in the floor where the regulars stand
The whole block beating in the palm of his hand

He knows the whole town, every name
Every secret, every shame
Sit in his chair, you sit in the truth
He been cutting hair since the days of your youth
He knows the whole town, oh yeah

Sweep the floor when the day is done
A hundred stories, he keeps every one
Lock the door, flip the sign to closed
The town's whole heartbeat, only he knows

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
There's a streetlight burned out on my street
Where we counted cracks beneath our feet
Mama worked the double, came home gray
Said get out, son, find another way
The corner held us like a closing fist
But I kept a road map in my wrist

I'm making it out, I'm making it gone
With a borrowed bag and a busted song
Don't look back at the chain-link fence
Freedom costs you everything you spent
I'm making it out, I'm making it gone

Said goodbye to the boys on the stoop
Some of them flew, most of them stooped
The bus pulled off at half past four
I watched the block get small once more
Every mile was a debt I owed
To everyone I left on that road

I'm making it out, I'm making it gone
With a borrowed bag and a busted song
Don't look back at the chain-link fence
Freedom costs you everything you spent
I'm making it out, I'm making it gone

And when I send the money home
They'll know the seed I planted grown
The dead-end block don't end in me
It ends the day a kid breaks free

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors slide shut and the lights go white
My heartbeat running out of time tonight
The tunnel swallows the platform glow
Nowhere up and nowhere low
Four stops left and I can't breathe
Hands on the pole, my knuckles seize

Breathe in, breathe out, let it go
Ride the rhythm, ride it slow
The train keeps moving, so do I
Ground beneath the racing sky
Breathe in, breathe out, let it go

Strangers' faces start to blur and bend
The rails are screaming round the bend
I count the tiles, I count to four
I count until I'm me once more
The bassline finds my flooded chest
And tells my body it can rest

Breathe in, breathe out, let it go
Ride the rhythm, ride it slow
The train keeps moving, so do I
Ground beneath the racing sky
Breathe in, breathe out, let it go

Next stop mine, the doors will part
I'll walk up steady, hold my heart
The city air, the open sky
I made it through, I'm still alive

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Up on the rooftop, antenna high
We steal a little corner of the sky
A hundred watts and a beat-up board
Playing every song they can't afford
Midnight signal, catch it if you can
We're the voice they tried to ban

Turn it up, we're on the air
Broadcasting love from nowhere
No license, no rules, no one to please
Just a frequency on the breeze
Turn it up, we're on the air

Kids in their bedrooms tuning in
Waiting for the song to begin
We take requests on a busted phone
Nobody listening feels alone
The cops keep circling, never find
The little tower in our mind

Turn it up, we're on the air
Broadcasting love from nowhere
No license, no rules, no one to please
Just a frequency on the breeze
Turn it up, we're on the air

And when they trace us, when they come
We'll already be on the run
But the song's already out there free
Floating somewhere over the sea

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Well the rooms are empty, the echoes ring
Got one last box and a roll of string
Tape it up tight with a screech and a tear
Four years of living packed up in here
Dust on the floor where the couch once sat
Say goodbye to the place, just like that

Tape it up, baby, seal it tight
We're rolling out into the night
One last box and the door swings wide
A whole new world on the other side
Tape it up, baby, seal it tight

Found a button under the kitchen door
A photograph and a marble on the floor
Little ghosts of the days we spent
Wrapped in the corners of an old apartment
Hand me the marker, sign the lid
Number thirty, and that's the kid

Tape it up, baby, seal it tight
We're rolling out into the night
One last box and the door swings wide
A whole new world on the other side
Tape it up, baby, seal it tight

Key on the counter, take a last look round
Funny how silence becomes a sound
Lift it up, gonna carry it through
The end of the old is the start of the new

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Stepped off the train in my dusty boots
City lights in their dancing suits
Two long years in a foreign night
Now the mirrorball's catching the light
Mama's crying at the station door
I ain't the boy I was before

I'm coming home, I'm coming home
Never again gonna roam
Spin me round on the shining floor
I don't hear the guns no more
I'm coming home, I'm coming home

The band strikes up and the strings come in
Let the rhythm wash away the sin
Every brother I left behind
Dances somewhere in my mind
So I move for them, I move for me
Under the lights of the living and free

I'm coming home, I'm coming home
Never again gonna roam
Spin me round on the shining floor
I don't hear the guns no more
I'm coming home, I'm coming home

Let the bassline carry the weight
Of everything I couldn't say
Tonight I'm just a man who survived
Grateful, breathing, glad to be alive

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night around three a.m.
I lift right off the bedroom hem
Through the ceiling, past the wires
Over the rooftops and the spires
Arms out wide in the cold blue air
Nobody up here, nobody there

And I fly, I fly, I fly
Over the sleeping town I rise
I never want to come back down
I never want to touch the ground
And I fly, I fly, I fly

The river bends like a silver thread
The streetlights blink, the world's in bed
I know somehow I'll wake again
To gravity and the morning rain
But for a moment I am free
The sky a second skin on me

And I fly, I fly, I fly
Over the sleeping town I rise
I never want to come back down
I never want to touch the ground
And I fly, I fly, I fly

They say a falling dream means fear
But mine keeps lifting year by year
Maybe my body knows it's true
That I was built to break right through

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Slow trombone on a humid day
They carry the box down Basin way
The preacher's done and the clouds hang low
A mournful tune for a soul let go
Clarinet weeping soft and blue
For an old friend the whole street knew

We walk him down, we walk him slow
Under the cypress, soft and low
Then the music turns and the sorrow lifts
Grief and joy are the same two gifts
We walk him down, we walk him slow

The banjo starts to pick up time
The snare drum finds a brighter rhyme
Umbrellas twirl in the dwindling light
The city dances through the night
From a dirge to a swinging refrain
Love outlasting all the pain

We walk him down, we walk him slow
Under the cypress, soft and low
Then the music turns and the sorrow lifts
Grief and joy are the same two gifts
We walk him down, we walk him slow

So pour the brass into the air
Let the trumpet climb the stair
He's gone ahead but the tune remains
Rolling soft through the old French lanes

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Drove all night to the edge of the cold
Killed the engine where the dark unrolled
Green ribbons wake in the velvet black
A neon ocean rolling back
My breath a cloud on the frozen glass
Watching the colors rise and pass

Under the lights, under the lights
The sky's alive on the coldest nights
Magenta, emerald, electric blue
A universe pouring down on you
Under the lights, under the lights

No radio out this far from town
Just the hum of the heavens coming down
The synth of the cosmos overhead
Playing chords that the stars have bled
I feel so small and so awake
Alive for the beauty's holy sake

Under the lights, under the lights
The sky's alive on the coldest nights
Magenta, emerald, electric blue
A universe pouring down on you
Under the lights, under the lights

And when the dawn comes pale and slow
The colors fade but I still glow
I carry a piece of that arctic fire
A wire of wonder that won't expire

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Coffee's cold in a borrowed cup
The casseroles are stacking up
Black suit hanging on the door
The house is quiet like before
Sun comes up like it always does
Indifferent to the way it was

Morning after, the world goes on
The one we loved is up and gone
Wash the dishes, fold the chair
Grief just sitting in the kitchen air
Morning after, the world goes on

The flowers wilting on the stair
His reading glasses, still right there
Nobody knows quite what to say
So we just move through the day
The dog still waits beside the door
For boots that won't come home no more

Morning after, the world goes on
The one we loved is up and gone
Wash the dishes, fold the chair
Grief just sitting in the kitchen air
Morning after, the world goes on

We'll learn to carry what we lost
We'll learn to pay the quiet cost
And one fine day without a warning
We'll wake up lighter in the morning

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Well I been cuttin' hair on this street since '61
Seen the fathers come in, then I cut their sons
My razor knows the story of every man in town
Who's flyin' high and who's been beaten down
Sit in my chair, son, tell me your blues
I heard 'em all and I'll hear 'em through

I know the whole town, every soul
Every secret they can't control
The pretty lies and the ugly truth
I keep 'em locked in this old barber booth
I know the whole town, Lord, I do

The banker owes the gambler down the way
The preacher's got a debt he cannot pay
I hear it all in the squeak of my chair
While the clippers hummin' through your hair
Mirror don't lie and neither do I
But I'll keep your business till the day I die

I know the whole town, every soul
Every secret they can't control
The pretty lies and the ugly truth
I keep 'em locked in this old barber booth
I know the whole town, Lord, I do

When they lay me down in my Sunday best
A hundred secrets goin' to my rest
The town gonna miss me when I'm gone
The only man who knew 'em all along

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
I lit a candle by your name
The clarinet cries out the same
All those years I held the stone
Now I'm talking to a stone alone
The fiddle bends a minor key
For everything you took from me

So I forgive you, late and slow
Though you're already long gone
I lay the anger in the ground
And let the music turn around
I forgive you, late and slow

The dancers circle, hand in hand
A grief too old to understand
I carried hate like a heavy coat
A bitter song stuck in my throat
But you can't hear me, you can't say
The sorry words you owed that day

So I forgive you, late and slow
Though you're already long gone
I lay the anger in the ground
And let the music turn around
I forgive you, late and slow

So play it freylekh, play it bright
Turn the mourning into light
The debt is cleared, the ledger torn
I set us free though you are gone

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The porch light's humming low, the crickets keep the time
Your bike's still on the lawn where we left it last July
We drove down to the water, watched the daylight come undone
Tomorrow I'll be somebody new, but tonight I'm still your son

One more golden hour, let it linger, let it stay
God, I'm not ready for the morning to take this all away
One more song on the radio, one more block around the town
The last day of summer's burning slow, and I don't want to put it down

My mother's at the window, says she folded all my clothes
There's a suitcase by the door that knows where everybody goes
You kissed me on the bottom step like nothing's gonna change
But something in the August air already smells like rain

One more golden hour, let it linger, let it stay
God, I'm not ready for the morning to take this all away
One more song on the radio, one more block around the town
The last day of summer's burning slow, and I don't want to put it down

We were never gonna stay the same, we always knew the deal
But nobody warns you how a fading summer feels
So hold my hand a little longer, let the streetlights blur the years
I'll carry every August with me, salt and gold and tears

One more golden hour, let it linger, let it stay
God, I'm not ready for the morning to take this all away
One more song on the radio, one more block around the town
The last day of summer's burning slow, and I don't want to put it down

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine idling soft, rain on the windshield glass
Three minutes on the clock, watch the second hand pass
They said this is the last one, then I'm clean and I'm gone
bought a little house out west where the quiet lives on
Fingers on the wheel, keep the heater on low
radio static murmur, nowhere left I gotta go
seen too many sunrises through a rearview mirror
every year the road gets longer, every exit gets nearer

Last job, last light, last turn down the street
one more clean getaway and I'll finally get some sleep
slow drums, slow heart, let the city slide by
driving toward a morning where I never have to hide

Door swings, they pile in, breathing hard, say drive
I don't ask what happened, just keep us all alive
foot easy on the pedal, never spike, never stall
the trick was always patience, never rushing at all
drop 'em at the corner, count the bills, say goodbye
fold the key into the river, watch it sink and say bye

funny how it ends not with sirens but with calm
just a man and an old car and the dawn in his palm

Last job, last light, last turn down the street
one more clean getaway and I'll finally get some sleep
slow drums, slow heart, let the city slide by
driving toward a morning where I never have to hide

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Well I raised my hand up high in the corner of the room
but the words came tumbling out and got buried just as soon
there's a fella with a louder voice and a fancier degree
and the table keeps on turning like it's turning without me

Talked over, talked under, talked clean off the floor
I brought a good idea but it walked right out the door
strum it on the banjo what they wouldn't let me say
I'll sing it to the mountain since the meeting won't make way

I had a point worth making and I made it nice and clear
but the room just kept on rolling like there's nobody to hear
then an hour and a minute later somebody says it twice
and they call the man a genius for repeating my advice

Talked over, talked under, talked clean off the floor
I brought a good idea but it walked right out the door
strum it on the banjo what they wouldn't let me say
I'll sing it to the mountain since the meeting won't make way

Maybe I'll just whistle, maybe I'll just grin
let 'em fight about the credit while I quietly turn it in
cause the fiddle don't need permission and the holler always hears
and a good song carries further than a boardroom full of years

Talked over, talked under, talked clean off the floor
I brought a good idea but it walked right out the door
strum it on the banjo what they wouldn't let me say
I'll sing it to the mountain since the meeting won't make way

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two a.m., the whole house sleep, just me and the desk lamp glow
highlighter running dry, got a long way left to go
cold coffee, third cup, eyes red, won't quit
phone face down on the table cause I can't afford to slip
they was out, I stayed in, that's the difference in the end
while they posting in the function I'm rereading it again
flash cards on the floor, formulas on the wall
grinding in the silence cause the silence pays for all

Two in the morning, nobody watching
putting in the hours that they never gon' clock in
lights off in the city, light on in my room
I'ma make it out of here, just give it a little room

Mama working doubles, I can't let her down
turn the music low, let the textbook make the sound
every line I memorize is a brick in the wall
building me a future tall enough to clear it all
they don't see the late nights, only see the win
but the win was made at two a.m. with nobody watching in

yawn, stretch, reset, one more chapter to the dawn
sun gon' catch me studying with the morning coming on
this the part they skip, this the part that counts
lonely little victories in quiet little amounts

Two in the morning, nobody watching
putting in the hours that they never gon' clock in
lights off in the city, light on in my room
I'ma make it out of here, just give it a little room

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
It's late and my thumb's hovering over a name I swore I'd delete
the screen lights up the ceiling, oh, this trouble tastes so sweet
I know exactly where it leads, I've walked this road before
but my heart's a careless burglar slipping back through someone's door

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but the message is already gone
three little dots are dancing and now I can't take it back, it's on
oh, the want don't care what's wise, the want don't care who cries
I'm texting someone I shouldn't underneath the midnight skies

There's a good man fast asleep with his arm around my best intent
and here I am awake and aching for the love that came and went
one word and the past comes rushing like a tide across the years
I delete it, then I send it, then I drown in my own tears

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but the message is already gone
three little dots are dancing and now I can't take it back, it's on
oh, the want don't care what's wise, the want don't care who cries
I'm texting someone I shouldn't underneath the midnight skies

Lord, give me the strength to put this poison phone away
but my hands have got a hunger that my Sunday self can't pray
cause some loves never finish, they just simmer and they wait
for a weak and lonely hour to come knocking at the gate

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but the message is already gone
three little dots are dancing and now I can't take it back, it's on
oh, the want don't care what's wise, the want don't care who cries
I'm texting someone I shouldn't underneath the midnight skies

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Empty walls and a roll of tape, the echo says goodbye
footsteps loud in a hollow room where the sofa used to lie
I press the seal across the top, the final cardboard square
everything I ever was is folded up in there

Tape it up, tape it up, one more box and I'm free
spinning round the empty floor, just the memories and me
shut the lid on yesterday, let the disco ball turn
I'm dancing out the door tonight with bridges left to burn

The marker squeaks out KITCHEN on a life I'm leaving here
seven years inside these rooms gone shimmering and clear
the keys feel light inside my hand, the future's calling loud
so I twirl across the linoleum beneath an invisible crowd

Tape it up, tape it up, one more box and I'm free
spinning round the empty floor, just the memories and me
shut the lid on yesterday, let the disco ball turn
I'm dancing out the door tonight with bridges left to burn

Goodbye to the crack in the ceiling, goodbye to the squeaky stair
goodbye to the ghost of the person I was when I first moved in there
the truck is humming downstairs and the night is wide and bright
so I'll boogie down the hallway one last glittering time tonight

Tape it up, tape it up, one more box and I'm free
spinning round the empty floor, just the memories and me
shut the lid on yesterday, let the disco ball turn
I'm dancing out the door tonight with bridges left to burn

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night I close my eyes and the floorboards fall away
and I'm rising up above the pines where the morning learns to play
the holler shrinks beneath my boots, the river's just a thread
and I'm soaring over Caroline with the clouds above my head

Fly, fly, same old dream, same old open sky
the Lord gave me a pair of wings every time I close my eye
but I always wake at sunup with my feet upon the ground
chasing that old flying dream that never sticks around

I seen the church steeple from the top, I seen the whole wide town
I seen my mama hanging wash and I never once looked down
the wind it sang a mountain tune I knew but couldn't name
and every blessed night I sleep, it calls me back the same

Fly, fly, same old dream, same old open sky
the Lord gave me a pair of wings every time I close my eye
but I always wake at sunup with my feet upon the ground
chasing that old flying dream that never sticks around

Maybe it's a memory from before I came to be
maybe it's a promise of the place I'm meant to see
so I'll lay me down each evening with a hope inside my chest
that one night I keep flying and I never come to rest

Fly, fly, same old dream, same old open sky
the Lord gave me a pair of wings every time I close my eye
but I always wake at sunup with my feet upon the ground
chasing that old flying dream that never sticks around

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
Neon bleeding down the avenue, the rain remembers you
taillights painting crimson on the road we used to cruise
I still hear the engine humming on a summer we let die
you were riding shotgun, now you're just a city light goodbye

You're the one, the one that got away
fading like a signal at the ending of the day
I chase your ghost through midnight on a highway made of light
the one that got away keeps driving through my night

We were eighteen and electric in a world that wouldn't end
cassette deck and a full tank and a heart I couldn't mend
I let you slip through neon when I thought we had the time
now you're just a frequency I can't quite leave behind

You're the one, the one that got away
fading like a signal at the ending of the day
I chase your ghost through midnight on a highway made of light
the one that got away keeps driving through my night

If I could turn the synth back, rewind it to the start
I'd hold the chord a little longer, I'd never lose the part
but the tape runs out, the city sleeps, the dawn dissolves the glow
and the one that got away is just a song from long ago

You're the one, the one that got away
fading like a signal at the ending of the day
I chase your ghost through midnight on a highway made of light
the one that got away keeps driving through my night

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Under the floodlights humming, we are smaller than the night
nobody believed us, nobody but the light
the scoreboard hums a lullaby, the crowd dissolves to haze
and we are floating somewhere in the last of all our days

One more whistle, one more chance, hold the moment soft and slow
we were always the ones they counted out, but look how far we go
feel the field beneath us turning into something like a dream
the final game is glowing and we shimmer as a team

My heartbeat in the silence, the ball suspended high
a thousand tiny galaxies are spinning in the sky
we played the whole year underwater, no one heard us breathe
but here beneath the buzzing lights it's hard to half-believe

One more whistle, one more chance, hold the moment soft and slow
we were always the ones they counted out, but look how far we go
feel the field beneath us turning into something like a dream
the final game is glowing and we shimmer as a team

Win or lose dissolves to mist, it never was the point
it was the running side by side that put the stars in joint
so let the clock run out in gold, let the echoes softly fade
we were never gonna win it all but God, the game we made

One more whistle, one more chance, hold the moment soft and slow
we were always the ones they counted out, but look how far we go
feel the field beneath us turning into something like a dream
the final game is glowing and we shimmer as a team

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
I hung my Sunday collar on a nail behind the door
the year my brother left us, I just couldn't pray no more
I drove past every steeple like they owed me an excuse
and the silence where God used to be was a long and empty noose

I lost it on a backroad, found it in the rain
somewhere between the bottle and the bottom of the pain
I thought that He had left me but He was riding in the bed
of the truck I drove to nowhere with the doubt inside my head

It wasn't in the sermon, it wasn't in the hymn
it was a stranger at the diner who paid my coffee on a whim
it was my daughter's laughter spilling sunlight on the floor
it was the morning coming back the way it always did before

I lost it on a backroad, found it in the rain
somewhere between the bottle and the bottom of the pain
I thought that He had left me but He was riding in the bed
of the truck I drove to nowhere with the doubt inside my head

Maybe faith ain't a fortress, maybe faith's a creaking door
that swings out in the wind and slams and swings out once more
I don't need it all to make sense, I just need to feel the wind
and know that what I lost out there will find its way back in

I lost it on a backroad, found it in the rain
somewhere between the bottle and the bottom of the pain
I thought that He had left me but He was riding in the bed
of the truck I drove to nowhere with the doubt inside my head

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Attic full of wires, antenna on the roof
transmitter humming warm, that's the only proof
that somewhere in the static there's a voice that ain't for sale
broadcasting to the lonely on a frequency off the trail
vinyl crackle, low drum, midnight on the dial
I talk to nobody and everybody for a while
the city's got its towers, its commercials and its noise
but down here in the basement we just spin it for the boys

Pirate on the airwaves, nobody can find me
broadcasting from the shadows with the whole world behind me
turn it down, tune it in, catch me if you can
I'm a ghost on the radio with a record in my hand

They came knocking once or twice with a license and a frown
but you can't arrest a signal, you can't shut a whisper down
I move the dial each evening, never twice the same
leaving little songs for strangers who'll never know my name
request line's a payphone, callers two or three
but that's a packed arena when you broadcast just to be

someday they'll triangulate, kick the attic door
but the music will have traveled where they'll never find the source

Pirate on the airwaves, nobody can find me
broadcasting from the shadows with the whole world behind me
turn it down, tune it in, catch me if you can
I'm a ghost on the radio with a record in my hand

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We launched together, brothers strapped to the same flame
two names on the mission patch, sharing the same aim
but the tether came undone on a routine spacewalk day
and I watched you start to shrink as the void pulled you away
I reached, you reached, our gloved hands missed by a breath
now there's a widening dark between my life and your death
radio still works, your voice comes through calm
"don't waste your fuel," you tell me, with that astronaut's balm

Drifting, drifting, two stars coming apart
same sky, same silence, but a widening dark
I can see you in the window getting smaller every hour
and there's nothing in my reach that's got the power

We trained eight years for this, ran the sims till we were numb
but nobody simulates the moment that has finally come
you tell me about your daughter, how you taught her to ride
I'm crying in a helmet where the tears got no place to hide
the station's getting closer, your voice is getting thin
and the math already told us I can't pull you back in

Thirty seconds left of signal, then the static takes the line
you say my name once gently like it's the last good sign
then it's me and all the cosmos and the engine's quiet hum
and a friend become a satellite of everything to come

Drifting, drifting, two stars coming apart
same sky, same silence, but a widening dark
I can see you in the window getting smaller every hour
and there's nothing in my reach that's got the power

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Midnight in the studio, just a candle and a board
spinning slow jams for the city that the city can't afford
no sponsors, no permission, just a needle and a flame
baby, this transmission doesn't even have a name
I press my lips up to the mic like it's the only one who hears
and I'm sending out this melody across the lonely years

Turn your radio low, let me into your room
I'm an outlaw frequency cutting through the gloom
can't catch me, can't cage me, I'm here and then I'm gone
but I'll love you on the airwaves till the breaking of the dawn

Somewhere there's a woman crying soft beside her bed
and my song slips through her window and it cradles her head
I don't know who's listening but I sing it like I do
every hush and every shiver, baby, that one's just for you
they can scan their little dials, they can hunt me all night long
but you can't put handcuffs on a soul inside a song

They say the law is coming, say they'll silence my band
but a voice this full of longing slips right through their hand
so lay down in the darkness, let the bassline hold you tight
I'm the secret on the spectrum gonna love you through the night

Turn your radio low, let me into your room
I'm an outlaw frequency cutting through the gloom
can't catch me, can't cage me, I'm here and then I'm gone
but I'll love you on the airwaves till the breaking of the dawn

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Boots upon the gravel of a road I used to know
the same old porch is standing but the man who left is gone
I carried back a thousand ghosts inside this hollow chest
they march behind my eyelids and they will not let me rest

I'm home, I'm home, but the war came home with me
the gunfire in the silence is the only thing I see
they hung a yellow ribbon but they can't unsee the dead
I left the battlefield behind but it's living in my head

My mother holds me trembling, says her prayers were finally heard
but I flinch at every backfire, I can't speak a single word
the boy who joined for glory came back grayer than the stone
that marks the friends I buried in a country not my own

I'm home, I'm home, but the war came home with me
the gunfire in the silence is the only thing I see
they hung a yellow ribbon but they can't unsee the dead
I left the battlefield behind but it's living in my head

Tear it down, tear it down, every medal on my coat
they don't know the weight of what's still lodged inside my throat
but I'll fight one final battle in the trenches of my mind
to crawl back to the morning and the peace I left behind

I'm home, I'm home, but the war came home with me
the gunfire in the silence is the only thing I see
they hung a yellow ribbon but they can't unsee the dead
I left the battlefield behind but it's living in my head

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
The hands that built the treehouse shake above the morning tea
and the giant of my childhood folds down smaller next to me
his voice is in a fog now, drifting somewhere soft and slow
and I'm holding back an ocean that I'm not allowed to show

Fade, fade, slow into the gray
the father that I worshipped wearing thinner every day
I'd give it all to freeze the frame, to keep him bright and whole
but time is just a tide that pulls the giant from the shore

He asks me twice my own name in the blur of afternoon
and the man who knew the answers hums a half-forgotten tune
I watch him at the window where the light comes pouring through
and he's beautiful and breaking and there's nothing I can do

Fade, fade, slow into the gray
the father that I worshipped wearing thinner every day
I'd give it all to freeze the frame, to keep him bright and whole
but time is just a tide that pulls the giant from the shore

So I'll sit beside him quiet in the haze of all these years
and I'll memorize his breathing through the static of my tears
let the guitars wash over us, let the whole world blur and bend
I just want a little longer with my father and my friend

Fade, fade, slow into the gray
the father that I worshipped wearing thinner every day
I'd give it all to freeze the frame, to keep him bright and whole
but time is just a tide that pulls the giant from the shore

songs.subject: looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
The stool was crooked, the spotlight too white,
I counted the bottle caps under the light,
My father's old guitar against my chest like a shield,
Six strings and a throat that refused to yield.

A waitress kept pouring, nobody looked up,
I sang to the steam coming off of a cup,
My voice cracked open on the very first line,
And the silence that followed felt longer than time.

Ay, how green I was, how the trumpets would soar
If they'd only been there by that swinging door,
I was nineteen and shaking and sure I would fall,
But I'd give back the years to be nervous and small.

Now the plaza remembers the kid that I was,
The vihuela still hums when the evening applause
Rolls in like a tide that I once couldn't earn,
When I sang to an empty room, waiting to learn.

So here's to the corner, the half-finished beer,
The ghost of a boy who was braver than fear,
I tip my sombrero to that crooked old stool,
Where a coward became, just for three minutes, a fool.

Ay, how green I was, how the trumpets would soar,
Now they carry the song I once sang to the floor,
I was nineteen and shaking and sure I would fall,
But I'd give back the years to be nervous and small.

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Last whistle screamed at a quarter past three,
Gate chained shut on what's left of me,
Forty years of forearms feeding the line,
Traded for a letter, a date, and a sign.

LIGHTS OUT! The press goes cold!
Thirty thousand hands, and not one of them sold!
LIGHTS OUT! The town goes still!
They carved out our heart and they billed us the kill!

My old man bled on this concrete floor,
Now the suits say it doesn't pencil no more,
A spreadsheet decided the name of my street,
A stranger in a tower we will never meet.

LIGHTS OUT! The press goes cold!
Thirty thousand hands, and not one of them sold!
LIGHTS OUT! The town goes still!
They carved out our heart and they billed us the kill!

Rust eats the sign. Weeds split the lot.
This is the funeral nobody bought.
We built the whole world and they handed us debt —
STAND UP! We're not in the ground yet!

So torch up the rumor, pass it hand to hand,
We're the ghost in the gears of a dying land,
They can padlock the doors and salt all the dirt,
But they can't unteach the value of work.

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Dear Atlantic, it's me again, ink running thin,
Wax on the bulb, salt crust on my skin,
I climb the same stairs that my grandfather climbed,
Writing you sonnets that nobody will find.
I seal them in bottles, I toss them off the rail,
My mailbox is a current, my postman a gale,
You never write back but you answer in foam,
The only address that I've ever called home.

I keep the flame so you'll find your way,
Night after night, gray after gray,
You took every sailor, you'll take me one day,
But I'll love you in letters the tide rolls away.

Seventeen winters I've lived on this stone,
Married to a fog horn, in love with the unknown,
I know your moods better than I knew any wife,
You're the cruelest and truest companion of my life.
You swallowed the Henley, the Marsh, and the Crane,
Yet I light you a candle and call it again,
Devotion is showing up after the loss,
A keeper, a beam, and the kelp on the cross.

When they automate me and dim my old light,
When the bulb goes electric and forgets how to fight,
Bury my letters where the breakers come down,
Let the woman I worshipped just hold me and drown.

I keep the flame so you'll find your way,
Night after night, gray after gray,
You took every sailor, you'll take me one day,
But I'll love you in letters the tide rolls away.

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Gas tank empty, phone at nine percent,
Whole crew piled where the whole summer went,
Reservoir water warm as the air,
We ain't talkin' 'bout August, we just sittin' there.
Boxes in the hallway with my name on the tape,
Everybody actin' like nobody escape,
Mari rollin' slow past the school we don't go,
Last lap 'round a town that we already know.

Last day, last day, sun goin' down slow,
Tomorrow I'm a stranger in a city I don't know,
Hold up, hold up, let it stretch, let it last,
We was kings of nothing and it happened too fast.

Drive-thru receipts and a deck full of friends,
Nobody sayin' that's the way that it ends,
Fireworks left over from the Fourth in the trunk,
Lightin' up the parking lot, we feelin' like we drunk
Off the fact that this'll never come again,
Whole different number when I count to ten,
Mama in the window leave the porch light warm,
Last calm before I drive into the storm.

Group chat gon' get quiet, I can already tell,
We gon' promise that we won't but we know that we will,
So I'm soakin' up the cricket and the cul-de-sac hum,
Goodbye to the only home I ever come from.

Last day, last day, sun goin' down slow,
Tomorrow I'm a stranger in a city I don't know,
Hold up, hold up, let it stretch, let it last,
We was kings of nothing and it happened too fast.

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raised my hand at nine-fifteen,
The quietest voice you've ever not heard,
I had the answer, clean and clear,
Then Brian repeated my every word.

And the room said "genius,"
The room said "yes,"
The same idea in a deeper dress.

Talk over me, talk over me,
I'm a melody you keep on cutting short,
But I hum underneath what you think you control,
One day the silence is gonna report
Everything you stole, everything you stole.

I swallow the sentence, I sip the cold tea,
I smile like I'm fine, like I planned to agree,
My name in the minutes spelled halfway wrong,
The footnote that carried the whole song.

Talk over me, talk over me,
I'm a melody you keep on cutting short,
But I hum underneath what you think you control,
One day the silence is gonna report
Everything you stole, everything you stole.

So I'm done waiting for a gap in your voice,
I'ma make the room a verse and leave you no choice,
Next time I open my mouth, baby, hold your breath —
I been rehearsing this quiet to death.

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Alarm didn't ring 'cause I never went to sleep,
Last night of the block was a promise I couldn't keep,
Sun comin' up on the court where we balled,
Net's still torn from the spring, never got it installed.
My suitcase zipped but my chest stay open,
Everything I'm leavin' is the language I'm spoken,
The bodega cat, the hydrant, the heat —
The whole curriculum I learned on this street.

One more morning, let me memorize the block,
Let me freeze the corner store and stop the clock,
I'm grown in the paperwork, a kid on the stoop,
Last day of summer and I'm leavin' the loop.

Moms ironed a shirt that I'ma never wear,
Proud and scared, doin' both in the air,
Little brother actin' hard so he don't cry,
Dap turned to a hug, turned to a held goodbye.
I'ma be the first one to walk through that door,
With the whole neighborhood ridin' shotgun for sure,
They didn't pay tuition but they paid in belief,
I'm a scholarship stitched out of everybody's grief.

So when the campus get cold and I feel like a guest,
I'ma play this block back, the unrest and the rest,
The last day of summer ain't a door that I shut,
It's the root that I'm carryin', dirt and all, uncut.

One more morning, let me memorize the block,
Let me freeze the corner store and stop the clock,
I'm grown in the paperwork, a kid on the stoop,
Last day of summer and I'm leavin' the loop.

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Flour on the knuckles, river in the veins,
Map of every winter, every harvest, every rain,
She folds the dough like she's folding back the years,
Thumbs that pressed the lids on a hundred thousand tears.

Go — hands in the half-light, fast as the beat,
Knead, fold, turn, repeat,
Eighty-six years moving quick on the snare,
Everything she built is in the bread and the air.

Her hands, her hands, keep time, keep time,
Faster than my heart and older than rhyme,
When the bass falls out and the room goes black,
It's her two hands that bring the rhythm back.

She darned the same sock through the strike and the snow,
Held a stranger's baby on a train no one would slow,
Those hands buried two sons and still learned how to wave,
Still braided my hair on the edge of the grave.

Now they shake when they rest but they steady when they care,
Tracing my face like she's reading a prayer,
Speed it up, break it down, let the sub-bass swell —
The softest, fastest hands I ever knew so well.

Her hands, her hands, keep time, keep time,
Faster than my heart and older than rhyme,
When the bass falls out and the room goes black,
It's her two hands that bring the rhythm back.

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
Woke up and the suit's still hangin' on the chair,
Black tie loose like it gave up on the air,
Casseroles sweatin' in the fridge nobody'll eat,
Program from the service crumpled under my feet.
Phone's gone quiet, all the flowers start to brown,
The loudest thing today is the rest of the town
Actin' regular, sellin' coffee, runnin' late,
Like the world didn't just lose a heavyweight.

Morning after, and the sun got the nerve to rise,
Same old sky with a brand new size,
I'm makin' two cups out of habit, not need,
Pourin' one out for the half I can't feed.

Mama in the kitchen but she ain't cookin' nothin',
Just holdin' the pan like she's holdin' on to somethin',
We said all the words but the words don't land,
Grief is a language you learn second-hand.
I keep checkin' my phone for a call that won't come,
Thumb over a contact I'm too scared to delete from,
The house is the same but it echoes too loud,
A seat at the table that's just shaped like a cloud.

They say take it day by day, but who designed the day?
Who made it this long, who made the light this gray?
I'ma fold up the suit, I'ma keep the program,
Learn to carry the missin' like it's part of who I am.

Morning after, and the sun got the nerve to rise,
Same old sky with a brand new size,
I'm makin' two cups out of habit, not need,
Pourin' one out for the half I can't feed.

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
(Ooh, sha-la-la) Grandma's hands,
Soft as the velvet on the family Bible,
(Ooh, sha-la-la) Grandma's hands,
Kept this whole family from a fall.

She'd snap the green beans on the porch at noon,
Hummin' me a low and a lovin' tune,
Those fingers could fix what the world had broke,
Wipe away my tears with a gingham cloak.

Grandma's hands (sha-la-la-la),
Clapped on Sunday in the second row (oh yeah),
Grandma's hands (sha-la-la-la),
Waved me off when it was time to go,
Now they're folded still and I miss them so,
Grandma's hands, don't you let me go.

She rolled the dough and she rolled my hair,
Said "baby, hold your head up, walk with care,"
The rings hung loose 'cause the years grew thin,
But the love in that grip, oh it pulled me in.

(Bom-bom-bom) If I could hold 'em one more time,
(Bom-bom-bom) I'd press my cheek and I'd stop the rhyme,
I'd thank each line and each freckled spot,
For every single thing those hands have got.

Grandma's hands (sha-la-la-la),
Clapped on Sunday in the second row (oh yeah),
Grandma's hands (sha-la-la-la),
Waved me off when it was time to go,
Now they're folded still and I miss them so,
Grandma's hands, don't you let me go.

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Three gray dots, then a quiet so wide,
The screen went dark like a closing tide,
I watched the little word "delivered" glow,
The softest guillotine I'll ever know.

You ended us in lowercase,
No punctuation, no goodbye face,
I'm floating in the blue light haze,
Loving a ghost of a phrase, a phrase.

I typed and deleted a thousand replies,
Each one a galaxy that lived and dies,
The pillow still smells like a Tuesday in June,
When "forever" still fit in a cartoon balloon.

You ended us in lowercase,
No punctuation, no goodbye face,
I'm floating in the blue light haze,
Loving a ghost of a phrase, a phrase.

And the read receipt is a tombstone, small and bright,
Marking the second you turned off the light,
I keep the thread open like an unhealed seam,
Scrolling up to the part where we still were a dream.

Now the notification sound makes me bleed,
A Pavlovian ache for a hope I don't need,
So I muted the world, set the screen face-down,
And let the silence finally pull me down.

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Well I set up my board on a Tuesday in the cold,
Five dollars a game, son, that's how it's sold,
I got pigeons on the table and pigeons in the seat,
Gonna take your whole paycheck on this dirty old street.

I'm the king of the corner, baby, mate in three,
You can't see the trap that's comin' for me,
Clock's tickin', tickin', and your money's all mine,
I been losin' my whole life, but I'm winnin' on the line.

You came in so cocky with your little online rank,
Now your queen's in my pocket and your pride's in the tank,
I ain't got a roof and I ain't got a dime,
But on these sixty-four squares, Lord, I'm beatin' father time.

I'm the king of the corner, baby, mate in three,
You can't see the trap that's comin' for me,
Clock's tickin', tickin', and your money's all mine,
I been losin' my whole life, but I'm winnin' on the line.

Now the streetlight's my chandelier, the bench is my throne,
Every hustler out here's playin' a game all alone,
I traded a future for a hundred quick wins,
That's the bluest little checkmate a man ever sins.

So bring me your students and bring me your fools,
I wrote my own opening, I broke all the rules,
When they fold up my board and they carry me home,
Lay a pawn on my chest, let me sleep on my throne.

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We were tethered to the same bright dream,
Floating in a quiet, a weightless team,
Then the line went slack and I didn't notice when,
You were a satellite I couldn't reach again.

And the radio crackles, your voice gets thin,
A half-second delay where you used to be in.

We're drifting, drifting, two stars in the dark,
Further apart with every spark,
I'd give all my oxygen just to slow it down,
But gravity's gone and there's no more ground,
We're drifting, drifting, hand letting go,
Losing you slow, losing you slow.

We used to count the same stars through the glass,
Now you're a window I keep floating past,
The mission control of my heart's gone dark,
No countdown left, just an empty arc.

We're drifting, drifting, two stars in the dark,
Further apart with every spark,
I'd give all my oxygen just to slow it down,
But gravity's gone and there's no more ground,
We're drifting, drifting, hand letting go,
Losing you slow, losing you slow.

Maybe somewhere the Earth still spins us as one,
Two silhouettes against the same old sun,
But out here it's silent, and I finally see —
Some orbits just weren't built to keep you and me.

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
It started as a joke on a folding card table,
Cheap beer, bad jokes, and a label-less bottle,
Dealt the second hand and the laughing got thin,
When Danny put his car keys and his wedding ring in.

We should've folded, we should've gone home,
Left the kitchen light on, let the dishes alone,
But we doubled and doubled till there was nothing to win,
And the whole damn friendship caved in.

The kitchen clock kept the only honest count,
Three in the morning, raising every amount,
Somebody's marriage in the pot by dawn,
And nobody breathing when the last card was drawn.

We should've folded, we should've gone home,
Left the kitchen light on, let the dishes alone,
But we doubled and doubled till there was nothing to win,
And the whole damn friendship caved in.

Now the deck's in a drawer that I never reopen,
Fifty-two reasons that something stayed broken,
We were boys playing men with a stake we couldn't pay,
And we lost more than money the night we wouldn't walk away.

Danny moved out by the end of the spring,
Nobody talks about the ring, the ring,
I still hear the shuffle when the kitchen goes still,
The sound of a bet that we're all paying still.

songs.subject: a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Neon on the hearse, chrome on the brass,
A second line glowing through the city's last gas,
The trumpet bends purple under the streetlight's hum,
Slow drum, slow drum, here the angels come.

Dance him to the river, dance him to the dark,
A synthesizer sunset and a tuba's spark,
We cry on the dirge, then we glow on the turn,
In this electric city, even the dead can burn.

White handkerchiefs flutter like a pixel parade,
Grief in major key, in a violet shade,
The grandmothers two-step under wires and stars,
Mourning made of laser light and old guitars.

Dance him to the river, dance him to the dark,
A synthesizer sunset and a tuba's spark,
We cry on the dirge, then we glow on the turn,
In this electric city, even the dead can burn.

The arpeggio rises where the sorrow let go,
Up from the slow march, into the strobe,
Death cut the umbilical, the city cut the chord,
Now it's saxophone neon and a joyful reward.

So bury me in synth and a sousaphone moan,
Roll my casket through the quarter, don't leave me alone,
When the bass drops at midnight and the cymbals all shine,
Throw a parade for the end of the line.

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Behind a fogged-up window where the mannequins sleep,
