Old Mr. Okafor lets the measuring tape weep,
He knows your shoulders better than your mirror ever could,
He knows where you're broken and he hems where he should.

He dresses the whole block in a soft golden thread,
Funerals and weddings and the words left unsaid,
Chalk on his fingers like a quiet little prayer,
Stitching us together in the powder-blue air.

He took in my father's suit when the cancer made him small,
He let out my mother's seams when she carried us all,
The drunk and the deacon both stand on his stool,
Equal in the lamplight, hemmed by the same rule.

He dresses the whole block in a soft golden thread,
Funerals and weddings and the words left unsaid,
Chalk on his fingers like a quiet little prayer,
Stitching us together in the powder-blue air.

And the dust floats like snow through the afternoon haze,
A needle keeps time with our comings and our days,
When they fit me for the last suit I'll ever wear,
Let his ghost pin the cuffs and his chalk mark me there.

So the block stays in fashion though the years come undone,
Held up by a man and the threads he has spun,
We wear all his kindness like a coat in the cold,
The softest, the steadiest, the keeper of the fold.

songs.subject: studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
The candle burns down low tonight
My books are open, the world is quiet
Two in the morning, the city sleeps
But I am here with the words I keep

The clarinet of my tired mind
Plays every line I left behind
A cup of tea gone cold and still
I climb this lonely paper hill

Oh, study on, study on, through the candlelight
Dance with the letters in the dead of night
The violin weeps and the moon hangs high
Study on, study on, till the morning comes alive

My grandmother hummed an ancient tune
Now I hum it beneath the moon
The pages turn like falling leaves
A wandering scholar, no one believes

Spin me round, little melody
Carry my heart across the sea
Every answer I cannot find
Lives somewhere in the back of my mind

Oh, study on, study on, through the candlelight
Dance with the letters in the dead of night
The violin weeps and the moon hangs high
Study on, study on, till the morning comes alive

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning, the highway hums
I dial the number, my tired hands numb
A thousand miles between us tonight
But your sleepy voice is my guiding light

I'm calling home, oh I'm calling home
Lord knows I'm tired of the road alone
Keep the porch light burning, keep the faith so strong
I'm calling home, I'm coming home

The diesel rumbles, the white lines fly
A gospel station beneath the sky
I hear my children stir in their sleep
A promise to them is a promise I keep

Well, the angels ride beside my wheel
Through the rain and the steel I feel
Every mile is a prayer I send
Till these wandering roads come to an end

I'm calling home, oh I'm calling home
Lord knows I'm tired of the road alone
Keep the porch light burning, keep the faith so strong
I'm calling home, I'm coming home

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slide shut, the lights go dim
My heart starts racing deep within
The walls are closing, the air grows thin
I'm drowning here in this crowded skin

Breathe, just breathe, gonna make it through
Hold on tight to the part of you
That knows the morning's gonna come
Breathe, just breathe, you're not the only one

A hundred strangers, a hundred eyes
I close my own and I memorize
The rhythm slowing down in time
The steady ground beneath the line

Oh, the tunnel ends in a flash of day
The fear it loosens and floats away
I feel my feet on the solid floor
I'm still standing, I'm something more

Breathe, just breathe, gonna make it through
Hold on tight to the part of you
That knows the morning's gonna come
Breathe, just breathe, you're not the only one

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
Neon hum in the conference glow
I raise my voice but it sinks below
A thousand words I never get to say
They fade to static and drift away

I'm a ghost in the room tonight
A flickering signal, a fading light
But I'm building my fire in the dark
One day they'll hear the spark

The screen reflects a silent face
My ideas lost in empty space
They cut me off at every turn
But deep inside the engines burn

One day the lights will turn to me
And all this silence sets me free
The synths will rise, the bass will roll
They'll finally hear my electric soul

I'm a ghost in the room tonight
A flickering signal, a fading light
But I'm building my fire in the dark
One day they'll hear the spark

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine running, the clock is tight
Neon city in the dead of night
They said one more and then I'm out
No turning back, ain't no doubt

Drive, drive, this is the last ride
City lights blurring on the other side
One more job and I'm finally free
Drive, drive, no one catching me

Mirrors flashing red and blue
Foot on the gas, gotta see it through
Dreamed of a life on a quiet shore
Just one more run and I'm done for sure

Every corner that I take
Every promise that I make
Is riding on these spinning wheels
Nobody knows just how it feels

Drive, drive, this is the last ride
City lights blurring on the other side
One more job and I'm finally free
Drive, drive, no one catching me

songs.subject: a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Sandy toes and a sunset glow
We met where the salty breezes blow
Your cutoff jeans and that easy smile
We danced in the waves for a little while

Summer love on the sand
Your heart in my hand
Gone when the leaves turn brown
But I'll keep this feeling around

A bonfire crackled, the stars came out
We talked about everything, no doubt
A radio playing an old slow song
We knew that it wouldn't last too long

September's coming, I know it's true
You'll drive away and so will I too
But summer keeps a memory warm
A little shelter from the storm

Summer love on the sand
Your heart in my hand
Gone when the leaves turn brown
But I'll keep this feeling around

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Every night I climb the winding stair
My lantern burning in the salty air
I write my letters to the restless tide
My only lover, my faithful bride

Oh sea, my sea, take my words tonight
Dance with me beneath the silver light
You are the heart that I cannot keep
My endless love, my restless deep

The waves they whisper, the waves they sigh
They carry secrets that never die
I fold my sorrow into the foam
The ocean is the only home

Turn, turn, the beam goes round
A lonely waltz without a sound
You never answer, yet still I stay
Writing my heart till the break of day

Oh sea, my sea, take my words tonight
Dance with me beneath the silver light
You are the heart that I cannot keep
My endless love, my restless deep

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Back in town where the streetlights hum
I saw your face and my heart went numb
We grew up on these same old roads
Now neon memories start to glow

You're my hometown heart, my electric dream
Glowing soft in the headlight beam
All these years and I never knew
I was always falling for you

The diner's open, the jukebox plays
A song from our forgotten days
Your laugh still sounds like summer rain
And nothing here will feel the same

Drive with me past the water tower
This midnight is our only hour
The stars hang low on the empty street
Where your heart and my heart finally meet

You're my hometown heart, my electric dream
Glowing soft in the headlight beam
All these years and I never knew
I was always falling for you

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grew up where the sirens never sleep
Promises shallow but the struggle deep
Concrete dreams on a broken street
But I kept marching to my own heartbeat

Made it out, made it out the block
Never let 'em stop my clock
From the bottom to the top I rose
Made it out, that's how it goes

Mama working double, never had a dime
I was writing verses just to pass the time
They said I'd never leave, said I'd never grow
Now look at me shining, stealing the show

Every scar is a story told
Every struggle turned to gold
I carry my block in everything I do
This one's for the ones who never made it through

Made it out, made it out the block
Never let 'em stop my clock
From the bottom to the top I rose
Made it out, that's how it goes

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five dollars a game on the weathered board
Kings and pawns, my only hoard
The city rushing, the clocks tick fast
Every move is built to last

Checkmate, checkmate, the beat drops low
I see the future ten moves to go
Roll the dice on the city block
King of the park, I never stop

Tourists laughing, they think they'll win
Then I spring the trap they're caught within
The bass it rumbles beneath my feet
Another hustle on the concrete street

Faster, faster, the pieces fly
Queen takes bishop, watch 'em die
The rhythm pounding in my chest
I never lose, I'm simply the best

Checkmate, checkmate, the beat drops low
I see the future ten moves to go
Roll the dice on the city block
King of the park, I never stop

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights flicker on the same old road
Carrying dreams that I never showed
Four a.m. and the city sleeps
But a fire in my heart it keeps

I'm breaking out, I'm breaking free
Dancing toward the life I'm meant to be
Feel the rhythm lift me off the ground
Made it out, I'm finally found

Bricks and bars and a fading sign
But the beat in my chest is a lifeline
Everybody said I'd never go
Now watch me rise from the down below

Hands up high, let the bassline roll
Music healing my tired soul
Every wall that they built so tall
I'm dancing right over it all

I'm breaking out, I'm breaking free
Dancing toward the life I'm meant to be
Feel the rhythm lift me off the ground
Made it out, I'm finally found

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
Packed my bags by the morning light
This little town just felt too tight
Upbeat horns and a one-way ticket
Kissed the past, no time to nitpick

I'm gone, I'm gone, down the highway line
Leaving this town in the rearview shine
No more small talk, no more chains
Dancing away on the morning trains

Same old faces, same old bar
Never gonna make it very far
So I picked up my feet and I started to run
Goodbye little town, my time here is done

Pick it up, pick it up, skank to the beat
New horizons beneath my feet
Everything I ever knew
Fading out as the sky turns blue

I'm gone, I'm gone, down the highway line
Leaving this town in the rearview shine
No more small talk, no more chains
Dancing away on the morning trains

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
His hands once strong now tremble slow
The man who taught me how to grow
The guitar weeps a mournful tune
Beneath the dying afternoon

Oh father, father, the years they fly
Like shadows cast across the sky
I hold your hand as the embers fade
The strongest man that God ever made

His eyes still hold the fire inside
Though the body fails, the soul won't hide
I dance the steps that he once knew
My heart on fire for the love so true

Clap the rhythm, stomp the floor
For everything we were before
Time is cruel but love is strong
Father, I will sing your song

Oh father, father, the years they fly
Like shadows cast across the sky
I hold your hand as the embers fade
The strongest man that God ever made

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
In the hall where the voices ring
I raise my own but no one hears a thing
The fiddle plays a lonesome air
For all my words that vanish in the air

But I'll sing, I'll sing my song so true
Though they talk right over everything I do
The whistle and the drum will carry me along
One day they'll listen to my song

They speak and speak and never cease
While I sit waiting, holding peace
My quiet heart it beats so loud
Alone among the talking crowd

Gather round the fire's glow
There's a voice in me they do not know
It rises with the morning tide
A strength I carry deep inside

But I'll sing, I'll sing my song so true
Though they talk right over everything I do
The whistle and the drum will carry me along
One day they'll listen to my song

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon sign in the pouring rain
Coffee's hot and it eases the pain
The jukebox plays a sweet old song
Where the lonely hearts all belong

Oh, the diner stays open all night (all night)
Under that flickering light (shoo-doo-wop)
Where the lonely souls can stay
Till the dark turns into day

The waitress smiles with a knowing grace
A second home, a gentle place
The truckers laugh and the dreamers dream
Over pie and a coffee cream

When the whole world shuts its door
There's a light here forevermore
A little booth where you can hide
With a friendly face by your side

Oh, the diner stays open all night (all night)
Under that flickering light (shoo-doo-wop)
Where the lonely souls can stay
Till the dark turns into day

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
We came up from the gravel and the rust
A dozen names nobody learned to trust
The scoreboard never loved us, never tried
But we kept the engine running on the pride

Fourth quarter and the rain begins to fall
Coach just nods, says give it all or nothing at all
My old man's in the bleachers, gray and worn
He's been waiting on this Friday since I was born

So let 'em call us nothing, let 'em count us low
We're the long-shot boys with nowhere left to go
One more whistle, one more chance to climb
We were never gonna win, so we won it on borrowed time

The clock's a heartbeat slowing in the cold
A story that the favorites never told
We didn't have the money or the name
Just the dirt beneath our nails and one last game

If we lose, we lose like thunder
If we fall, we fall like rain
This little town will pull us under
And raise us up again

So let 'em call us nothing, let 'em count us low
We're the long-shot boys with nowhere left to go
One more whistle, one more chance to climb
We were never gonna win, so we won it on borrowed time

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Yellow linoleum and a humming fridge
A window fogged above the kitchen sink
Flour on her apron like a snowfall
Butter on the counter starting to think

Cinnamon ghosts in the curtain folds
A radio static singing something old
I was seven with my elbows on the table
Watching steam climb up and disappear

And it smells like Sunday, smells like staying
Smells like nobody's leaving here tonight
There's a warm gold light I keep replaying
In a house that burned down years behind

Now the spice rack's boxed in someone's garage
And the recipe is half a paragraph
But I caught the scent on a stranger's coat
And I was small again, and I could float

Funny how a smell can build a room
With walls you swore the world tore down
She's still standing at that humming stove
Stirring something I can almost taste right now

And it smells like Sunday, smells like staying
Smells like nobody's leaving here tonight
There's a warm gold light I keep replaying
In a house that burned down years behind

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Smoke hung low in the back of the bar
Four men deep in the green felt war
Midnight folded into two
And the kitty grew the way that trouble do

Danny laid his watch down on the pile
Then the deed, then the keys, then a crooked smile
The dealer's hands were cool as glass
While every man bet more than he had to lose

One more hand, baby, one more deal
Nobody's leaving till somebody kneels
The aces whisper, the kings ignore
It was just a game till it went too far

A cough, a chair scraped back too quick
A flash of steel and the clock went sick
The chips spilled red across the floor
And nobody won that night at all

Greed's a slow piano line
That plays you sweeter every time
You think you're up, you think you're free
Then the house collects the misery

One more hand, baby, one more deal
Nobody's leaving till somebody kneels
The aces whisper, the kings ignore
It was just a game till it went too far

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Clock says four, the hallway's humming blue
Coffee gone cold, I'm on hour twenty-two
String lights blinking in the break room glow
Everybody's home but the folks I can't let go

Double shift, holiday, candle in the rain
I'll trade my Christmas for somebody's heartbeat staying the same
Tired in my bones but my hands still steady
Hold the line, hold the line, I was already ready

Room 9 squeezed my glove and said don't leave
I said I'm right here, baby, breathe and breathe
My own kids opening gifts on a screen
Waving through the static of a vending machine

They don't see the small saves, quiet and unsung
The whispered it's okay when the night comes undone
No applause, no spotlight, just the beep and the hum
And a stranger's mother making it to the morning sun

Double shift, holiday, candle in the rain
I'll trade my Christmas for somebody's heartbeat staying the same
Tired in my bones but my hands still steady
Hold the line, hold the line, I was already ready

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down like a falling sky
You stepped under, I don't know why
We didn't speak, we didn't need
Just a wobbling dome and the city bleed

Neon puddles, a violet hush
Your shoulder warm in the evening rush
We matched our steps so the cold stayed out
Two silhouettes the streetlights drowned

Stay a little, stay a while
Under this small impossible sky
We're strangers melting in the haze
For the length of a downpour, you were mine

Your stop arrived like a closing door
You turned and smiled and you said no more
The rain went on but the dome was light
A shape of you walking out of the night

I never asked your name
Never caught the color of your eyes
But I keep a little rain inside my chest
For the version of us that never said goodbye

Stay a little, stay a while
Under this small impossible sky
We're strangers melting in the haze
For the length of a downpour, you were mine

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
The doors slam shut and the lights go thin
The tunnel swallows the train and me within
My collar's tight, my pulse a runaway snare
And the strangers' faces dissolve into air

The brass in my chest plays a frightened tune
The walls lean close in the rattling room
Count the poles, count the stops, count the breath
Every station's a small rehearsal for death

Hold on, hold on, the wave will break
The floor is real, it's just the fear that shakes
Breathe the four, hold the seven, let the eight roll by
You are not the storm, you're the calm beneath the sky

A stranger's eyes meet mine and soften slow
She mouths the word easy, like she'd know
The train climbs up to the bridge and the light
And the city exhales me back into the night

Nobody saw the war I fought
Between the handrail and a single thought
But the piano slows, the cymbals fade
And I'm still here, undrowned, unafraid

Hold on, hold on, the wave will break
The floor is real, it's just the fear that shakes
Breathe the four, hold the seven, let the eight roll by
You are not the storm, you're the calm beneath the sky

songs.subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
songs.lyrics:
Vengo a tu tumba con la voz quebrada
The trumpets weep where the headstone stands
I brought the anger I carried for years
And I'm laying it down with these trembling hands

You left before I could say the word
That sat like a stone in the back of my throat
Now the marigolds bend in the morning wind
And I'm singing the pardon you never quite heard

Te perdono, te perdono
Though you can't hear the song I bring
I forgive you, I forgive you
So my own heart learns to sing

The guitar cries soft for the years we lost
For the table set with an empty chair
I was waiting for sorry, I was waiting for you
But peace doesn't wait, so I leave it there

Forgiveness is not a gift for the dead
It's a key I turn in my own locked chest
I set you free so I can be free
And lay this old weight finally to rest

Te perdono, te perdono
Though you can't hear the song I bring
I forgive you, I forgive you
So my own heart learns to sing

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
The sun pours gold on the dusty square
Guitars are crying in the August air
We danced barefoot till the streetlights came
Knowing nothing here would stay the same

Tomorrow the train pulls me far from home
To a city of strangers and rooms of stone
But tonight the heels strike fire on the ground
And the whole town spins to a clapping sound

Último día, last light of June
Dance till the morning steals you too soon
Hold every face, hold every name
After tonight we won't be the same

María's laughing, her shawl in flight
The river of childhood runs out tonight
We carved our names where the old wall bends
A summer that swears it never ends

The palmas slow and the cante swells
Goodbye in the language only the body tells
I take this fire wherever I go
The stamp of a summer the cold won't know

Último día, last light of June
Dance till the morning steals you too soon
Hold every face, hold every name
After tonight we won't be the same

songs.subject: a card game that went too far
songs.lyrics:
Friday night and the table's hot
Deal me in, baby, give it all you got
Bassline thumping while the chips stack high
Everybody grinning, looking sly

Little Ray put his paycheck down
Then the rent, then the ring, then his daddy's crown
The groove kept rolling but the room went cold
When he bet the one thing money can't hold

Too far, too far, you went too far
Chasing that pot like a falling star
The drums keep pounding but the fun is gone
When you wager the morning to win the dawn

The horns blew sharp as the last card flipped
The whole table froze and the silence dripped
Ray just laughed but his laugh was thin
You can't get back what the night takes in

Get down, get down, but know when to fold
The slickest hand still loses its hold
The funk'll carry you all night long
But greed's the one note that plays you wrong

Too far, too far, you went too far
Chasing that pot like a falling star
The drums keep pounding but the fun is gone
When you wager the morning to win the dawn

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole life on a folded page
A name, a ring, a gilded cage
Every door already locked
Every clock already stopped

They said be grateful, said be still
Said your wants are not your will
I swallowed it for twenty years
Choked it down with their applause and fears

NOT MINE! This was never mine!
I'm tearing every dotted line!
You built a future, brick and chain
I'm walking out into the rain!

No blessing, no goodbye at the door
Just the floor I won't kneel on anymore
Let them keep the plan, the prize, the throne
I'd rather be lost than be owned

Burn it down, the whole design
Every expectation, line by line
My life, my breath, my own mistake
This is the chain I choose to break!

NOT MINE! This was never mine!
I'm tearing every dotted line!
You built a future, brick and chain
I'm walking out into the rain!

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the gray over field and root
Till the only green was a stubborn shoot
One crooked oak by the dead motel
Keeping a secret it would never tell

The crows still come like they always done
To the last black branches against the sun
Kids carved hearts in its weathered hide
Before the whole town up and died

Stand, old tree, in the asphalt sea
The last green ghost of what used to be
They paved the prayer but they missed the bone
You're holding the dirt all alone

The wind comes low through the empty lots
Past the rusted swings and the rotting docks
And the oak just bows like a preacher would
Over a grave that no one understood

When the last leaf falls in the headlight glare
Will anybody even be there
To hear the roots crack under the stone
And the town let go of the breath it's blown

Stand, old tree, in the asphalt sea
The last green ghost of what used to be
They paved the prayer but they missed the bone
You're holding the dirt all alone

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The snow's piling up on the windowpane
While I'm chasing the clock down the hall again
Sixteen hours and my feet are sore
But there's somebody calling from behind that door

My supper's a cracker and a paper cup
My babies are home while I'm holding up
A stranger's hand through the longest night
Keeping the dark from her flickering light

Work that double, ring that bell
There's grace in the ward where the weary dwell
No turkey, no tree, no Christmas chair
Just a heartbeat steady and a whispered prayer

The fiddle of the heart monitor plays
A lonesome tune through the holiday haze
But I signed for this and I'd sign again
To be somebody's angel now and then

The morning comes like a banjo roll
Light on the snow and it warms my soul
She opened her eyes and she squeezed my hand
And somehow the long night made me grand

Work that double, ring that bell
There's grace in the ward where the weary dwell
No turkey, no tree, no Christmas chair
Just a heartbeat steady and a whispered prayer

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
The recipe card's in her looping hand
Measurements I never understand
I cut the onions, I let them fall
For two, like always, too much for them all

The kitchen's quiet where she used to hum
The kettle whistles and nobody comes
I set one plate on the table tonight
And the empty chair holds the evening light

Half a portion, half a song
Learning the math of carrying on
Salt to taste, but who's to say
When the one who tasted's gone away

I burned the first three, threw them out
Learned what her patience was about
Now the soup tastes close to how she'd make
And I eat it slow for her own sake

They say grief is a table set for two
That slowly learns to make do with you
I'm clumsy with the years she left behind
But I'm cooking, love, I'm getting by just fine

Half a portion, half a song
Learning the math of carrying on
Salt to taste, but who's to say
When the one who tasted's gone away

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I prayed your name like a Sunday hymn
Low in the pew with the lights gone dim
You never turned, you never knew
How long I knelt at the thought of you

The choir rises, the organ swells
And my heart confesses what it never tells
You love another, that's the truth
And I lay it down in the burning pew

Lord, take this love I cannot give
Teach my heart how it's supposed to live
If she's not mine then let me be
Free, oh free, set this longing free

There's a kind of grace in the love unspun
A blessing hid in the wanting one
I'll wish her well from the back of the room
And let the light pour over my gloom

Every teardrop's a hallelujah too
Even the love that never comes through
So lift me up, oh lift me high
Till I love her freely and say goodbye

Lord, take this love I cannot give
Teach my heart how it's supposed to live
If she's not mine then let me be
Free, oh free, set this longing free

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Same dusty road, same steeple bell
Same river where we learned to swim and yell
But I saw you new in the Sunday sun
And my old heart knew you were the one

We grew up tangled in the same small town
Your mama's porch where the vines hang down
Now you're singing soprano in the second row
And I'm falling slow, oh I'm falling slow

Hallelujah, hometown grace
Found my heaven in a familiar face
Thank you, Lord, for the long way 'round
That led me back to my own hometown

No big city could ever show
What the gravel and the cornfields know
You were always there in the morning light
And I was blind till the church bells made it right

Sometimes the answer's where you stand
The promise written in the home soil sand
I don't need the world, I don't need the sea
The whole of my prayer's been right next to me

Hallelujah, hometown grace
Found my heaven in a familiar face
Thank you, Lord, for the long way 'round
That led me back to my own hometown

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Radar bruise on the dashboard glow
I read the sky like a pulse I owe
Mile markers blur to a held breath
Gravel hum and the smell of wet

They built me a porch and a quiet name
A chair that waits in the window frame
But my hands go numb when the air turns still
I need the green in the western hill

Give me the wall cloud, give me the wire
The county shaking under tire
I am only home in the funnel's eye
Where the still ground learns how to fly
I am only home in the falling sky

She left a note on the kitchen glass
Said storms move on and so do you, fast
I traced the front past three state lines
Chasing a thing that outruns time

The quiet scares me more than the roar
I've never once knocked on a settled door
Somewhere a hailstone holds my name
And melts before it can stay the same

Give me the wall cloud, give me the wire
The county shaking under tire
I am only home in the funnel's eye
Where the still ground learns how to fly

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Last whistle blew at a quarter past four
Grease on my palms I won't need anymore
Thirty years feeding that iron mouth
Now the lights step down and the trucks roll south

What do you do when the machine goes cold
When the line that raised you can't be sold
I gave it my back, I gave it my prime
Now they fold the gates on my best time
Now they fold the gates on my time

Clock card still in my coat from today
Name on a locker they'll paint away
Danny got laid off, took it on the chin
We shook hands slow at the loading bin

My daddy walked these floors before
Now there ain't no floor and there ain't no door
The town's a heartbeat losing its drum
But I'll be standing when the morning comes

What do you do when the machine goes cold
When the line that raised you can't be sold
I gave it my back, I gave it my prime
Now they fold the gates on my time

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came hard on the avenue
I had one cover, you had none too
No word between us, just a tilted shade
And half the storm on my shoulder stayed

Hallelujah for the stranger's hand
For the open arc where two can stand
We were dry on one side, wet on the other
And somewhere in the rain I found a brother
Somewhere in the rain I found a brother

Your bus came first but you let it pass
Waiting with me on the flooded glass
We never traded a single name
But grace don't ask you where you came

Lord, You send the cloud and the cover too
You put a stranger right beside the pew
Of a city street with no roof above
And taught us shelter is a word for love

Hallelujah for the stranger's hand
For the open arc where two can stand
We were dry on one side, wet on the other
And somewhere in the rain I found a brother

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They set my table before I could walk
A ring of someone else's talk
A farm, a name, a wedding band
All measured out by my father's hand

So I'll take the low road past the byre
Past the church and the wedding fire
I'll carry nothing but my own true breath
And marry the morning, not this death
And marry the morning instead

My mother weeps at the garden gate
My promised man can have my plate
The candles burn for a girl long gone
I slipped the latch before the dawn

There's a fiddle playing across the glen
For lives that begin and begin again
The dowry coins go cold in the chest
I'll spend my days as I think is best

So I'll take the low road past the byre
Past the church and the wedding fire
I'll carry nothing but my own true breath
And marry the morning, not this death

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Empty lot on a Sunday haze
Hazard lights and a mellow phase
Seatbelt click, you adjust the glass
Both hands ten and two, take it slow, don't rush past

Easy on the brake, let the engine breathe
Little girl I taught to crawl now learning to leave
Mirror, signal, blind spot, go
Watch you pull away, watch you grow
Watch you pull away and grow

You stalled it twice and you laughed it off
Lamp posts blinking in the parking trough
I remember strollers, now you steer
Dad's just cargo riding shotgun here

One day soon you'll back it down the drive
And I'll wave a hand from the porch alive
The radio low and the windows down
My whole heart cruising out of town

Easy on the brake, let the engine breathe
Little girl I taught to crawl now learning to leave
Mirror, signal, blind spot, go
Watch you pull away and grow

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Two plates out of habit again
I put one back in the cabinet then
The recipe card in her looping hand
Measures for two that I don't understand

Half an onion, half a song
Cooking for one when one feels wrong
The kettle sings to an empty chair
And I season the silence, salt the air
I season the silence with her there

She knew the heat and she knew the wait
I burn the rice and I overstate
The garlic, the time, the love it took
Now I'm a stranger in our own cookbook

Maybe I'll learn to halve the bread
To set one fork, to go to bed
With dishes light and the leftovers small
But God, I miss cooking for them all

Half an onion, half a song
Cooking for one when one feels wrong
The kettle sings to an empty chair
And I season the silence, salt the air

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Same dream hit me round three a.m.
Feet leave concrete, I'm above the hem
Of the city grid like a folded map
Projects shrinking in the rooftop gap
No wings, no jet, just a will to rise
Wind in my collar, salt in my eyes
Every night I learn the lean again
Then wake to a ceiling and a window pane

I fly in my sleep, I fall in the day
Gravity grips what the dream gave away
But my mind keeps the blueprint of the sky
And I swear one morning I won't have to lie
I swear one morning I won't fall, I'll fly

Doctors say it's stress in the REM tide
Grandma say it's spirit taking a ride
I don't argue, I just chase the height
Trade the alarm clock for another flight
Up past the water tower, past the loss
Past every bill and every double-cross

What if the dream is the realest part
And the pavement just a parking lot for the heart
I close my eyes and I leave the ground
The only freedom I've ever found

I fly in my sleep, I fall in the day
Gravity grips what the dream gave away
But my mind keeps the blueprint of the sky
And I swear one morning I won't fall, I'll fly

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Pot too big for the soup I make
Stir the stew for my own sake
Mama of my kids, she cooked for crowds
Now the kitchen quiet, no laughing loud

One plantain, one small fire
Cooking for one, my lonely choir
But I dey learn, I dey try, I dey grow
Feeding my heart slow, slow, slow
Feeding my heart slow, oh

Jollof for one no fit taste the same
I add the pepper, I call her name
The spoon she used still hang by the door
I cook small-small on the kitchen floor

Neighbors bring food but I wave them by
A man must learn before he can fly
From the pot to the plate to the empty room
I light one candle in the kitchen gloom

One plantain, one small fire
Cooking for one, my lonely choir
But I dey learn, I dey try, I dey grow
Feeding my heart slow, slow, slow

songs.subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Up at the dark for the baker's door
Flour on my sleeves by half past four
Home for an hour then the tavern light
I pour the pints till the dead of night

Two wages in and the well runs dry
The landlord's hand and the winter sky
I work the morning, I work the moon
And still the rent comes 'round too soon
And still I'm short by the harvest moon

My boots wear thin on the cobbled lane
My back's a bell that rings with rain
The children sleep when I leave and come
They know my coat but they don't know whom

They told me labour would set me free
But the ledger laughs at the likes of me
Two hands, two jobs, and a hollow purse
A fiddle plays for the working curse

Two wages in and the well runs dry
The landlord's hand and the winter sky
I work the morning, I work the moon
And still the rent comes 'round too soon

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Grey on grey where the harbour ends
I fold my country into my hands
The gulls dissolve in the engine drone
A wake of foam for the leaving home

Far, so far, where the water blurs
I watch the coastline turn to a rumor
Everything I was goes under the wave
And a new pale shore is all I'll have
A new pale shore is all I'll save

The deck hums low and the salt comes through
My mother's voice in the residue
Of a language thinning in the spray
I hold the photographs gone soft and grey

Will they know my name on the other side
Or just the ghost the ocean dyed
I lean on the rail where the two worlds meet
And let the horizon swallow my street

Far, so far, where the water blurs
I watch the coastline turn to a rumor
Everything I was goes under the wave
And a new pale shore is all I'll have

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The train let me off at the edge of town
A duffel bag and a faded brown
The steeple stands where it always stood
But I don't fit in my old neighborhood

I came home but the home stayed there
And I'm a stranger in my mother's chair
The fields are green and the church bell rings
But I left a piece in a field of things
I left a piece of me over there

My sister's grown and my dog has died
The creek runs slow where we used to ride
They set a plate and they smile at me
But they're talking to a man I used to be

The nights are loud when the house is still
I hear the others over the hill
I dig the garden, I mend the gate
And I learn to live with the things I hate

I came home but the home stayed there
And I'm a stranger in my mother's chair
The fields are green and the church bell rings
But I left a piece in a field of things

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Needle in the window of a busted street
Thread the chaos, make the ragged neat
No boss, no brand, just a measuring tape
And a hundred bodies I help reshape

I dress the whole block, hem to collar
For a six-pack smile and a folded dollar
The banker won't but the corner will
I stitch the people the system tries to kill
I stitch the people the suits can't kill

Grandma's funeral coat, the kid's first suit
The drag queen's gown and the boxer's boot
I take their scraps and I make them stand
Like kings and queens of a falling-down land

They wanna gentrify my crooked door
Put a chain shop where my chalk hits the floor
But you can't outsource a needle's pride
I'll cut and sew till the day I died

I dress the whole block, hem to collar
For a six-pack smile and a folded dollar
The banker won't but the corner will
I stitch the people the system tries to kill

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Grey stone tenements, a narrow sky
Where the dreams of boys go to lie and die
I knew the cracks of each cobbled stone
And swore one day I would not grow old alone

I'm away, I'm away from the dead-end row
Where the only road was the road below
I carry the block in my chest like a coal
But I'm walking out with my whole soul
I'm walking out with my own true soul

My father drank what the mill would pay
My mother prayed the rent away
The corner lads with their knives and pride
I loved them all but I would not bide

There's a fiddle keening for the ones who stayed
For the graves they dug and the debts they paid
I'll send back coin and I'll send back word
But I won't come back to be interred

I'm away, I'm away from the dead-end row
Where the only road was the road below
I carry the block in my chest like a coal
But I'm walking out with my whole soul

songs.subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Old Seamus keeps a lamp in the lane
A needle and thread and a window pane
He's cut the cloth for the whole parish round
From the christening gown to the burial shroud

He dresses the block, both high and low
The rich man's coat and the widow's woe
With a thimble worn to a silver shine
He stitches the village, line by line
He stitches us together, line by line

The bride wore lace that his fingers made
The piper's jacket, the soldier's braid
No coin from the poor, just a loaf, a song
And a place by his hearth when the nights grow long

When Seamus goes there'll be no hand
To measure the grief of this old land
So raise a glass to the man of thread
Who clothed us living and clothes us dead

He dresses the block, both high and low
The rich man's coat and the widow's woe
With a thimble worn to a silver shine
He stitches the village, line by line

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Old Maren walks where the kelp lies thrown
Her feet remember each barnacled stone
She reads the moon like a worn-out creed
And teaches the young what the waters need

The tide comes in, the tide goes far
She names each one by an older star
Give to the sea and the sea will give
She's taught us how the shoreline lives
She's taught us how to take and live

Gather the cockles but leave the small
Heed the grey when the gannets call
She lost a husband to the hungry swell
Yet still she loves the bell of the bell

There's a wisdom older than any boat
In the way she steadies the children's float
When Maren's gone to the further shore
The tide will speak what she spoke before

The tide comes in, the tide goes far
She names each one by an older star
Give to the sea and the sea will give
She's taught us how the shoreline lives

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Boots on the runway, the engines fade away
I carried the weight of every single day
The city lights are calling out my name
Nothing here will ever feel the same

I'm coming home, coming home tonight
Feel the rhythm pull me to the light
Lay my burden down upon the floor
I don't have to be a soldier anymore

Mama left the porch light burning low
There are stories in these scars she'll never know
The music's playing soft against my chest
Maybe now my heart can finally rest

I'm coming home, coming home tonight
Feel the rhythm pull me to the light
Lay my burden down upon the floor
I don't have to be a soldier anymore

Every beat a heartbeat, every drop a prayer
The peace I couldn't find is everywhere
Raise your hands and let the silence break
This is the home that no one's gonna take

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Las palabras se cayeron como vidrio en el piso
Now the room is heavy with the things we didn't say
The candle burns between us, a trembling little fire
And silence wears the colors of the dying day

Ay, el silencio, it cuts deeper than the shout
The trumpets in my heart have nothing left to play
Mi amor, mi amor, come back across this empty table
Before the quiet takes my love away

I hear the clock that's ticking where your laughter used to live
The guitar in the corner hangs its weary head
I'd trade a hundred battles for one word that you forgive
For the warmth of all the tender things we said

Ay, el silencio, it cuts deeper than the shout
The trumpets in my heart have nothing left to play
Mi amor, mi amor, come back across this empty table
Before the quiet takes my love away

Violoncello weeping soft against the night
Two hearts in one casa, turned away
Let the morning bring us back into the light
And chase this bitter quiet far away

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Papa used to lift me to the mango tree so high
Now his hands are shaking when he waves the world goodbye
The rhythm of his footsteps slowing on the road
He carried all our dreams, oh, such a heavy load

Oh my father, my father, the years they move so fast
The sun is going down but your love it's gonna last
Dance with me one more time before the evening calls
Oh my father, I will catch you when you fall

Grey is in his beard now where the black used to shine
His stories getting softer, like a slowly fading line
I press my head against him, feel his tired heartbeat go
The man who taught me everything is letting the world slow

Oh my father, my father, the years they move so fast
The sun is going down but your love it's gonna last
Dance with me one more time before the evening calls
Oh my father, I will catch you when you fall

Eh, the talking drum is calling out your name
The ancestors are smiling, they remember just the same
Grow old slow, my father, take your time, take your time
Every wrinkle is a verse in this sweet life of mine

songs.subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
songs.lyrics:
Midnight on the back road, headlights off and low
Jars of liquid lightning in the trunk below
We were laughing like the devil, thought we'd never get caught
But the sirens started screaming everything we forgot

Oh no, the moonshine run gone wrong
We were flying way too fast and now the night is gone
Red and blue behind us, heart pounding like a drum
We were chasing easy money, look what we've become

The river took the bottles, the engine took the blame
My best friend's in the back seat whispering my name
We thought we were invincible, two kids against the law
Now the gravel and the silence is the only thing I saw

Oh no, the moonshine run gone wrong
We were flying way too fast and now the night is gone
Red and blue behind us, heart pounding like a drum
We were chasing easy money, look what we've become

If I could take it back I'd never start the car
We were reaching for the moon but it was way too far
Now I'm counting all the choices underneath the stars
The moonshine run that left us with these scars

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Grab your hat and shine your shoes, the sun is sinking gold
This is the last sweet day before the story's getting old
We danced beside the boardwalk where the saxophones would play
Tomorrow we go separate ways, but baby, not today

One more swing, one more spin around the floor
One more summer night like we've never had before
The trumpets blow, the clock is running fast
Let's make this golden moment last and last and last

The suitcase by the doorway, it's waiting in the hall
But the band is playing sweetly and I'm dancing with you all
The ice cream and the laughter, the radio's soft tune
We're holding onto August underneath the silver moon

One more swing, one more spin around the floor
One more summer night like we've never had before
The trumpets blow, the clock is running fast
Let's make this golden moment last and last and last

We'll write a thousand letters, we'll call across the miles
But nothing beats this jukebox and your summer-fading smiles
So kick your heels and spin me till the morning light is here
The best day of our lives, my dear, the best day of the year

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
The rain came down on Orchard Street, a cold and silver thread
You held a little umbrella above my soaking head
We didn't know each other, two strangers in the storm
But under that small shelter, oh, the world grew strange and warm

Dance with me, dance with me, the clarinet is crying
Under one umbrella, two hearts are slowly flying
The violin is laughing at the rain upon the ground
Two strangers found a melody in all the falling sound

You told me where you came from, a village by the sea
I told you all my secrets that I'd never set free
The puddles caught the streetlights, the accordion played sweet
And time stood still a moment on that rainy little street

Dance with me, dance with me, the clarinet is crying
Under one umbrella, two hearts are slowly flying
The violin is laughing at the rain upon the ground
Two strangers found a melody in all the falling sound

Oy, the rain will stop and maybe we will part
But you will leave a footprint right here upon my heart
So play it once more, fiddle, before the skies grow clear
Two strangers and one umbrella, and a moment we held dear

songs.subject: falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
The streetlights hum the songs we used to know
Familiar shadows on the road where we would go
I saw your face beneath the same old summer sky
And something in me woke that I'd let lie

Falling, falling, soft as evening rain
Back into the place I thought I left in vain
You were always here, beneath the quiet glow
Falling for the someone from my hometown long ago

The river still remembers how we ran
The same old water moving where it all began
I traced the streets that raised me, found you standing there
And all the years dissolved into the air

Falling, falling, soft as evening rain
Back into the place I thought I left in vain
You were always here, beneath the quiet glow
Falling for the someone from my hometown long ago

No city lights could ever shine this true
Every road I wandered led me back to you
The quiet of this town is singing low
Falling, gently falling, in the afterglow

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Concrete where the meadow used to lie
One lonely branch reaching for the sky
They paved the whole world over, gray on gray
But you kept standing tall through every day

You're the last tree standing, the last green in the gray
Roots beneath the city that they couldn't take away
Hold on, hold on, let your branches reach the sun
You're the last green heartbeat when the wild world is gone

Kids forgot the color of a leaf
You whisper to the wind your quiet grief
But every spring you blossom, soft and brave
A little patch of life they couldn't pave

You're the last tree standing, the last green in the gray
Roots beneath the city that they couldn't take away
Hold on, hold on, let your branches reach the sun
You're the last green heartbeat when the wild world is gone

If they cut you down, the sky will start to cry
The birds will lose the only place to fly
So grow, oh grow, against the steel and stone
You are the proof that nature's not alone

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
The fireworks are blooming far outside the window pane
While I am here in white among the fever and the pain
My family is dancing where the festival lights glow
But I am holding hands with strangers, soft and slow

Olé, my weary heart, keep beating through the night
These hallways are my passion, this caring is my fight
The guitar of my courage strums a fierce and tender song
A double on the holiday, I'm where I belong

A child cries for his mother, I become her for a while
I carry all their sorrows and I give away my smile
The clock has stopped its mercy, the coffee's gone cold
But every life I'm holding is worth more than gold

Olé, my weary heart, keep beating through the night
These hallways are my passion, this caring is my fight
The guitar of my courage strums a fierce and tender song
A double on the holiday, I'm where I belong

Clap your hands, clap your hands, for the ones who never rest
The quiet heroes giving every patient of their best
The dawn will find me standing, though my feet are made of stone
The nurse who spent the holiday so no one was alone

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The screen door stopped its slamming, the dust is settling down
Your shadow's in the doorway like a ghost across this town
We said the kind of things that you can never take away
Now the silence sits between us at the dying of the day

Oh the quiet, oh the quiet, it's louder than the storm
The bed's gone cold beside me where you used to keep me warm
I'd rather hear you cursing than this nothing in the air
The quiet after fighting is the heaviest cross to bear

The whiskey's getting warmer and the candle's burning thin
I'm staring at the photographs of who we might have been
The crows are on the power line, they're watching what we've done
Two hearts in one dark cabin, but the talking's come undone

Oh the quiet, oh the quiet, it's louder than the storm
The bed's gone cold beside me where you used to keep me warm
I'd rather hear you cursing than this nothing in the air
The quiet after fighting is the heaviest cross to bear

Lord, let me cross this empty room before the morning breaks
And lay my pride down gently for my foolish heart's mistakes
For the quiet's gonna bury us if no one says a word
The saddest song there ever was is the one that's never heard

songs.subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
El aroma del comal me lleva a mi niñez
The masa on the griddle and the cinnamon and spice
Abuela's hands were dancing in the morning's golden light
And everything she touched she touched with love and sacrifice

Ay, la cocina, the kitchen of my heart
The smell of her tortillas never truly will depart
The trumpets sing of pozole and the candle's gentle flame
Abuela, in that kitchen I still hear you call my name

The pot of beans is simmering, the radio plays low
She hums an old ranchera that her mother used to know
The whole world fit inside those walls of yellow and of clay
And I would give my everything to live one more such day

Ay, la cocina, the kitchen of my heart
The smell of her tortillas never truly will depart
The trumpets sing of pozole and the candle's gentle flame
Abuela, in that kitchen I still hear you call my name

Violins, oh play for her, wherever she may be
The chile and the chocolate are her memory in me
I close my eyes and I am small, the table set for all
And the smell of her kitchen is the sweetest I recall

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m. the screen ignites, a demon in my hand
My thumb is hovering over fire I don't understand
The message that could burn it all, the words I shouldn't send
A loaded gun of pixels pointed straight at the end

Send it, delete it, the war inside my skull
The gravity of wanting and the silence and the pull
I know that this is poison but I drink it anyway
Texting in the darkness for the soul I'll betray

The notification glowing like a beacon in the black
I built a life of stone and now I'm tearing down the stack
One word and all the bridges that I built go up in flame
But my fingers keep on typing out the syllables of shame

Send it, delete it, the war inside my skull
The gravity of wanting and the silence and the pull
I know that this is poison but I drink it anyway
Texting in the darkness for the soul I'll betray

The phone goes dark, my heart is screaming loud
I am drowning in a self-inflicted shroud
Delete, delete, before the morning comes
But the message has been read and now the damage is done

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Upon the rocky headland where the gulls forever cry
I light the lamp at evening as the ships go sailing by
I have no wife to wait for me, no sweetheart on the shore
But the ocean is my darling, and I love her ever more

Oh sea, my love, my restless love, I write to you tonight
The ink runs like your tideline in the flicker of the light
I'll never sail away from you, I'll never close the door
A keeper and his ocean love, forevermore, forevermore

The fiddle and the whistle play the songs of long ago
I seal my words in bottles and I cast them where you flow
The storms may rage and batter at my lonely little tower
But I'll keep the flame a-burning through the darkest midnight hour

Oh sea, my love, my restless love, I write to you tonight
The ink runs like your tideline in the flicker of the light
I'll never sail away from you, I'll never close the door
A keeper and his ocean love, forevermore, forevermore

When I am old and grey and the lamp goes dark and cold
Lay me in your salty arms and keep me in your hold
The waves will read my letters that the years have torn apart
And the sea will keep the lighthouse of this lonely keeper's heart

songs.subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
Neon bleeding through the blinds, the room is cold and blue
The echo of the slamming door still hanging in the room
We ran out of the angry words, now nothing's left to say
Just the hum of the machines and the night that slips away

In the quiet, in the quiet, the static fills the air
I reach across the empty bed and find you're still not there
The city lights are pulsing like a heartbeat out of time
The silence after fighting is the loudest in my mind

The cassette is softly spinning out a song we used to love
The streetlight paints your shadow on the ceiling up above
I'd cross a thousand circuits just to hold your hand tonight
But I'm frozen in the afterglow of this electric fight

In the quiet, in the quiet, the static fills the air
I reach across the empty bed and find you're still not there
The city lights are pulsing like a heartbeat out of time
The silence after fighting is the loudest in my mind

Let the synthesizer carry what my voice could never say
Let the midnight and the moonlight wash the angry words away
Come back into my orbit, let the silence finally break
In the quiet of this neon night, my heart begins to ache

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Two plates upon the counter, but I only need the one
The kitchen that we filled with light is empty now she's gone
Her recipes are bleeding ink, the pages worn and thin
I strike the match and curse the silence I am drowning in

Cooking for one, the loneliest war
The table set for shadows that won't come anymore
I burn the bread, I salt the wound, I scream into the night
Learning how to feed a heart that lost its appetite

She always knew the measurements, the timing and the heat
Now the kitchen is a battlefield of grief beneath my feet
The steam rises like a phantom, her laughter in the air
I cook for one but set for two, forgetting she's not there

Cooking for one, the loneliest war
The table set for shadows that won't come anymore
I burn the bread, I salt the wound, I scream into the night
Learning how to feed a heart that lost its appetite

But she would want me living, want me strong
So I will learn the recipe she sang in every song
One plate, one fork, one candle burning low
I'll cook for one and carry all the love she used to know

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
The stage lights breathing slow against the haze
My name half-spoken in the static air
A single chord still hanging in the maze
Of almost, almost, nearly, almost there

And the night just opens like a door
That closes one breath before I walk through
So close I could taste the floor of more
The almost made it, the almost true

They called another name into the dark
The room exhaled and turned away from me
I held the silence like a fading spark
And watched it drift across the empty sea

No crash, no fall, just quiet letting go
The sound of nothing folding into snow

And the night just opens like a door
That closes one breath before I walk through
So close I could taste the floor of more
The almost made it, the almost true

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Neon hums above the kitchen sink
One plate drying where there used to be two
I measure salt and stop myself to think
How much of this I only learned from you

Cooking for one in the midnight glow
Half a recipe I don't quite know
The pan still warm, the table wide
Learning how to live with you outside

Your handwriting still curls along the card
A pinch of this, a little of your time
I burn the first, the second one is hard
The third one almost tastes like it was mine

The microwave clock blinks a tired blue
I set one fork and pour a glass for two
Then take it back and laugh until I cry

Cooking for one in the midnight glow
Half a recipe I don't quite know
The pan still warm, the table wide
Learning how to live with you outside

songs.subject: making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Ooh, the streetlight on the corner where I grew
Shoo-bop, the same old story every day
But I got a dream, baby, shining bright and new
And these tired bricks ain't gonna make me stay

I'm gonna make it out, out of this block
No more counting cracks on the broken walk
Woah-oh, the road is calling out my name
And I'll never be the same, the same

My mama waved her apron from the door
She said go on, son, don't you look behind
I kissed her cheek and headed for the shore
Of something bigger than this state of mind

Doo-wop, doo-wop, hear the engine start
Doo-wop, doo-wop, with a hopeful heart

I'm gonna make it out, out of this block
No more counting cracks on the broken walk
Woah-oh, the road is calling out my name
And I'll never be the same, the same

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five bucks a game and the clock is ticking fast
I set the board, I let you make the call
You think you're winning but it never lasts
The bishop slides and watch your kingdom fall

Move it, move it, drop the clock
King me, sweep me, round the block
In the park where the pigeons fly
Checkmate baby, say goodbye

The summer sweat, the slap of plastic clocks
A crowd of strangers leaning in to see
I live my whole life six moves out the box
The street's my school, the board belongs to me

Feel the bassline like a winning row
Queen to four, now everybody go
Ten seconds left and the rhythm never stops

Move it, move it, drop the clock
King me, sweep me, round the block
In the park where the pigeons fly
Checkmate baby, say goodbye

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
I drive past your window in the violet rain
The radio bleeding a song you'd never play
You are the chorus I can't quite explain
The one I keep singing the wrong way

You don't even know my name in the dark
But I light up the city for the spark
Unrequited, undefeated, all alone
Loving you across a line I'll never own

I build a future out of borrowed light
A neon ghost of everything you're not
You kiss the world and leave me to the night
The almost-lover that you never sought

And I would burn out every star I know
Just to watch you turn and never go
But you keep walking through the amber glow

You don't even know my name in the dark
But I light up the city for the spark
Unrequited, undefeated, all alone
Loving you across a line I'll never own

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
The last train coughs and shudders at the gate
I hold one suitcase and a folded prayer
This town that loved me, this town that I hate
Will not remember that I once was there

Adiós to the square and the fountain stone
Adiós to the bells that called me home
I'm cutting the thread, I'm walking the line
This dust on my shoes is the last that's mine

The baker waves, the dogs begin to bark
My mother's window glowing soft and gold
I press my hand against the leaving dark
And let the platform swallow what I hold

One step, two step, the rhythm of goodbye
A sharp accordion tearing through the sky
I will not turn, I will not, will not cry

Adiós to the square and the fountain stone
Adiós to the bells that called me home
I'm cutting the thread, I'm walking the line
This dust on my shoes is the last that's mine

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
There's a cast iron skillet that she always swore by
Hangs over the stove like a moon on the wall
I dust off the recipe, give it a try
And the kitchen feels bigger, the ceiling feels tall

Just me and the kettle and the slow Sunday light
Learning to cook for one tonight
Half the green beans and a quarter the bread
And a whole lot of words that I never said

Forty-one years she fed everyone here
Now the table's too long and the chairs are too clean
I salt the potatoes, I pour me a beer
And I talk to the radio in between

She'd laugh at the mess that I made on the stove
She'd hand me a towel and say honey, let's go
So I cook like she's coming, though I know, though I know

Just me and the kettle and the slow Sunday light
Learning to cook for one tonight
Half the green beans and a quarter the bread
And a whole lot of words that I never said

songs.subject: driving past your old apartment at night
songs.lyrics:
Fourth floor light still burning like a wound
I hit the gas but the brakes don't agree
The same cracked curb where I used to be ruined
The ghost of me is screaming to get free

Drive past, don't stop, don't look, don't slow
Everything we were is gone below
Drive past, don't stop, the engine and the night
Burning down the memory of the light

The buzzer name's been painted over twice
Some stranger's curtain hanging in our frame
I paid for everything and called it nice
And left with nothing but the burning shame

Tear it down, tear it down, the block, the bed, the year
Tear it down, tear it down, I was never here
Gun the engine, scream until it's clear

Drive past, don't stop, don't look, don't slow
Everything we were is gone below
Drive past, don't stop, the engine and the night
Burning down the memory of the light

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Pick up, pick up, the pan and the spoon
Whistle while the kettle starts to scream
One egg, one toast, in the kitchen at noon
Learning how to live the half-a-dream

Cooking for one, pick it up, pick it up
One little plate and one little cup
She'd love the way I burned the rice
Up on the upstroke, cooking for one tonight

The horns in my head play her favorite tune
She danced in this room with a wooden spoon
Now I skank alone by the light of the moon
And I salt the soup and I'll see her soon

Don't sink down low, keep the offbeat strong
She'd want me dancing while I cook along
So I pick it up, pick it up, all night long

Cooking for one, pick it up, pick it up
One little plate and one little cup
She'd love the way I burned the rice
Up on the upstroke, cooking for one tonight

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone is warm and heavy in my hand
The dial tone soft as a tide upon the sand
Three years of silence folded in my chest
And a number that my fingers still know best

Hello, mamãe, it's me, it's me
The son who sailed too far across the sea
Forgive the quiet, forgive the years
I'm calling now to wash away the tears

She breathes my name like sugar in the rain
No questions and no edges to the pain
The ocean of the years just slips away
And it's a soft and ordinary day

The bossa of her voice, a gentle sway
The rhythm of the words we couldn't say
Now swaying like a palm tree in the bay

Hello, mamãe, it's me, it's me
The son who sailed too far across the sea
Forgive the quiet, forgive the years
I'm calling now to wash away the tears

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Sixteen hours and the coffee's gone cold
The fireworks crack while the monitors beep
My family's eating without me, I'm told
And the only thing I'm full of is sleep

Working a double on the holiday
Nobody's coming to take it away
Bed forty-two and the call light's on
I'll dance in the break room till the feeling's gone

No turkey, no toast, no tree, no song
Just IV poles and the ones holding on
I hold a stranger's hand till the morning comes
While somewhere out there a party drums

I didn't sign up to be a saint
But I'll be the one here when you faint
So light your sparklers, I'll hold the line

Working a double on the holiday
Nobody's coming to take it away
Bed forty-two and the call light's on
I'll dance in the break room till the feeling's gone

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew its last at half past four
The foreman wiped his eyes and locked the gate
Thirty years of iron on the floor
Now silent as a dance hall closing late

So swing it low, the line is shutting down
The brightest little factory in town
Grab your coat and grab your gal
We'll dance the lights out, pal, oh pal

The punch clock stopped, the conveyor's still
The lunchbox empty, hanging on the nail
They're shipping all our future up the hill
And selling off the story for a sale

But we made the wheels that ran the world
We made the steel, we made it twirl
So raise a glass and let the brass unfurl

So swing it low, the line is shutting down
The brightest little factory in town
Grab your coat and grab your gal
We'll dance the lights out, pal, oh pal

songs.subject: a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Engine idling low on the avenue
My hands on the wheel and my heart on the run
They're inside counting what they think is due
And I swore to myself that this one's the last one

One more turn and I'm gone, I'm gone
One more night and the moon carries on
Meet me where the city lets go
This is my last ride, baby, slow, so slow

The radio whispers a love I can't keep
The sirens are stitching the edge of the dark
I think of her face and I'm in too deep
With a duffel of dreams and a getaway heart

Click of the door, three of them in
I hit the gas and I let it begin
Never again, never again, never again

One more turn and I'm gone, I'm gone
One more night and the moon carries on
Meet me where the city lets go
This is my last ride, baby, slow, so slow

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Those hands could braid the morning into gold
And press a wrinkle out of any day
They carried more than any heart could hold
And folded every worry into pray

Grandmother's hands, oh, lift them high
They worked the earth and touched the sky
Flour and faith and a worn-out ring
Those hands taught my whole soul to sing

They rocked the cradle, wrung the Sunday clothes
They buried husbands, lifted up the choir
And every line that time and labor chose
Was a verse of love that never would expire

Now they rest upon the quilt of years
Still warm, still soft, still holding back my tears
And I feel them when the morning light appears

Grandmother's hands, oh, lift them high
They worked the earth and touched the sky
Flour and faith and a worn-out ring
Those hands taught my whole soul to sing

songs.subject: an unrequited love
songs.lyrics:
The clarinet is laughing while I cry
It bends the note the way you bent my heart
You dance with everyone but never I
And the fiddle plays us further apart

Oy, my love, you'll never know
The way I burn beneath the candle's glow
Spin and spin in the circle of the night
Loving you is my unanswered light

The whole town claps and lifts you in the chair
I hold the corner where the shadows fall
You throw your laughter up into the air
And I catch none of it, I catch it all

The minor key, it knows me like a friend
It rises up and breaks and tries to mend
And plays the song that never finds its end

Oy, my love, you'll never know
The way I burn beneath the candle's glow
Spin and spin in the circle of the night
Loving you is my unanswered light

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night the ceiling opens wide
I step off the edge and I don't fall, I glide
City lights below me turning into stars
I forget my name, I forget the cars

Lift me, lift me, let the bassline take control
I'm a thousand feet above my body and my soul
Lift me, lift me, keep the kick drum beating slow
In the dream I'm flying and I never want to go

Clouds like cotton brushing past my hands
I can read the rivers, I can map the lands
Morning always finds me back inside my bed
With the wind still humming somewhere in my head

Lift me, lift me, let the bassline take control
I'm a thousand feet above my body and my soul
Lift me, lift me, keep the kick drum beating slow
In the dream I'm flying and I never want to go

Close my eyes, count it down, here we go again
Gravity's a rumor I refuse to let me in
Four on the floor and the floor is the sky
Tell me how a sleeping man learned how to fly

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The black suit's on the floor where it fell
The coffee's gone cold and so has the bell
I buried my brother in the cold red clay
And woke to a sun that had nothing to say

It's the morning after, the flowers still smell of rain
The house is full of casseroles and a long thin ache of pain
The crows are on the power line, the preacher's gone away
And I'm learning how the living have to live another day

His boots by the door like he's coming back soon
His dog won't stop howling at the low gray moon
I poured out two whiskeys from his bottle of rye
Drank both of them slow and I wondered why

It's the morning after, the flowers still smell of rain
The house is full of casseroles and a long thin ache of pain
The crows are on the power line, the preacher's gone away
And I'm learning how the living have to live another day

Lord, I don't want your mansion, I don't want your gold
I just want one more evening 'fore the ground gets cold
But the kettle's gonna whistle and the cattle's gotta feed
And grief don't stop the morning that the morning's gonna need

songs.subject: the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
We drove till the streetlights disappeared
Killed the engine, killed our every fear
You said wait, just wait, it's gonna start
And the sky put its hand right on my heart

Green and gold and electric blue
Dancing like they're dancing just for me and you
Oh, the northern lights, the northern lights
Painting all our wishes 'cross the longest night

We wrapped in a blanket, breath like smoke
Neither of us laughed and neither of us spoke
Just two little dots on a frozen shore
Under something bigger than we'd seen before

Green and gold and electric blue
Dancing like they're dancing just for me and you
Oh, the northern lights, the northern lights
Painting all our wishes 'cross the longest night

Whole world's asleep, doesn't even know
That the heavens put on the greatest show
And I'll remember this when we're old and gray
The night the sky came down to play

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
We were never the ones they printed in the news
Hand-me-down jerseys and second-hand shoes
But we showed up Friday with mud on our knees
Nine little nobodies and a town to please

So raise up the banner, boys, win or lose
We came here to play and we paid our dues
No trophy can measure what we already won
The pride of a town in the setting sun

Four runs down in the bottom of the ninth
Coach said sons, just keep it alive
Then Tommy hit a liner past the second base
And the whole town stood with tears on their face

So raise up the banner, boys, win or lose
We came here to play and we paid our dues
No trophy can measure what we already won
The pride of a town in the setting sun

They'll forget the final score in a year or two
But they'll remember how we fought it through
The underdog don't always get the crown
But by God, we never once backed down

songs.subject: the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
Neon on the dashboard, midnight on the dial
You were one phone call from the top of the pile
The deal on the table, the pen in your hand
The whole shining future like a promised land

So close, so close, you could taste the light
The night you almost made it, the almost-perfect night
The city held its breath and the stars stood still
And then it slipped right through you like it always will

They said we'll call you Monday, never made the call
You drove the empty freeway and you watched it fall
The radio kept playing like nothing was wrong
A glittering, electric, almost-victory song

So close, so close, you could taste the light
The night you almost made it, the almost-perfect night
The city held its breath and the stars stood still
And then it slipped right through you like it always will

But almost is a country, and you lived there for a while
With the chrome and the synths and that impossible smile
And somewhere in the static of that long-gone year
You were everything you wanted, and the dream felt near

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
You were summer in a borrowed car
A halo on the dashboard from a falling star
I had every reason and I had the time
But I let you wander out of mine

And you drift like a tide going out, going out
The one that got away, the beautiful doubt
I can still feel the warmth where your shoulder lay
The one, the only one, that got away

Now you're a photograph that won't dissolve
A gentle little ghost I can't resolve
I hear your laughing in a stranger's song
And I'm sorry, oh I'm sorry, it took so long

And you drift like a tide going out, going out
The one that got away, the beautiful doubt
I can still feel the warmth where your shoulder lay
The one, the only one, that got away

Maybe in another, softer life
I was brave enough to ask you to be mine
But here the light is fading, slow and gray
And you're still the one, the one that got away

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Clock in at nine, polished shoes on the marble floor
Same room every day, room two-oh-four
There's a woman on the wall in a faded blue dress
Oil and a smile that the centuries can't undress
Tourists rush past her, snap and they go
But me and her, we got a thing nobody knows
I tell her 'bout my daughter, 'bout the rent and the rain
She just keeps on smiling through her two-hundred-year pain

Just a guard and a painting in the quiet of the hall
She never says a word but she hears it all
When the lights go down and the crowd's all gone
We keep the whole museum company till dawn

They value her at millions, alarm wired tight
But she's worth more to me on a lonely night
I know every brushstroke, every crack in the frame
The little gold plaque don't even spell her name right, what a shame
Retirement's coming, gold watch, goodbye
Who's gonna talk to her, who's gonna stand by?
The new kid won't know her, won't know what I know
So I'll memorize her face before I go

Four hundred years she'll outlive us all
Still gonna hang on that same old wall
But for thirty long years it was her and me
The realest love that nobody could see

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning and the phone is a flame
Your name on the screen like a beautiful shame
My thumb hovers over the words I should erase
But the devil wears your face, ay, the devil wears your face

Ay, I shouldn't, but I'm typing it again
One more message, one more sin
The guitar is crying what my mouth won't say
I'm texting you tonight and I'll burn for it by day

My wedding ring is cold on the bedside light
She's asleep in the dark and I'm awake with the night
Every letter I send is a match to the hay
But I send it anyway, ay, I send it anyway

Ay, I shouldn't, but I'm typing it again
One more message, one more sin
The guitar is crying what my mouth won't say
I'm texting you tonight and I'll burn for it by day

Delete it, delete it, throw the phone in the sea
But my hands don't listen, no, they don't answer to me
Clap once for my conscience, clap twice for the flame
Clap three times for a man with no one but himself to blame

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Down on the corner where the palm trees lean
Got a little red chair and a shop kept clean
Old man Delroy with the scissors and the comb
Every head in the parish find their way back home

He knows the whole town, every secret, every sound
Who's in love, who's leaving, who's coming around
Sit down in the chair, let the worries all unwind
Delroy cut your hair and he ease your troubled mind

The mayor and the fisherman wait in the same line
He don't care 'bout your money, he don't care 'bout your kind
He heard the first cries and he'll see the last days
Holding all the stories in a hundred different ways

He knows the whole town, every secret, every sound
Who's in love, who's leaving, who's coming around
Sit down in the chair, let the worries all unwind
Delroy cut your hair and he ease your troubled mind

When Delroy close his shop, what the town gonna do?
Who gonna keep the secrets, who gonna pull us through?
So long as that radio play and the scissors sing
The whole town keep on turning like a living thing
