songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Fluorescent lights, fourteen hours deep
Family's at the table, I ain't gonna sleep
Monitor screaming down in bed nine
Merry Christmas to me, I'm holding the line

Double shift, no rest, blood on my scrubs
While the whole world's home with the ones they love
I didn't sign up for thanks, I signed up to stay
Somebody's gotta hold the dark away

Code blue, push the cart, count it out loud
Lost one at noon, saved one in the crowd
My coffee's gone cold and my feet are on fire
But the call light keeps ringing and I don't get to tire

Double shift, no rest, blood on my scrubs
While the whole world's home with the ones they love
I didn't sign up for thanks, I signed up to stay
Somebody's gotta hold the dark away

No medal, no parade, no name on a wall
Just a nurse in the night answering the call
So go hug your kids and pour the wine
I got you, I got you — I'm holding the line

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Upon the black rock where the ocean breaks its teeth
Stands the ancient one with the salt-grey beard beneath
A thousand winters carved into his hands
He speaks the old commandments to the shifting sands

Rise! He calls the water, rise and learn the law
Bow! Before the moon and the iron jaw
The elder teaches the tide how to roar
And the tide remembers what it knew before

No king nor empire ever bent the sea
But he whispers to the deep and the deep agrees
The gulls fall silent when he lifts his stave
And the storm lies down like an obedient slave

Rise! He calls the water, rise and learn the law
Bow! Before the moon and the iron jaw
The elder teaches the tide how to roar
And the tide remembers what it knew before

When at last he crumbles into foam and bone
The ocean keeps his lessons carved in stone
Forever and forever the waves will climb
Reciting the teachings of the end of time

songs.subject: the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
There's a hum in the wall where your voice used to be
A wash of guitar over everything I see
You faded like reverb at the end of a song
And I held the last note way too long

You got away, away, into the white
A blur of you dissolving in the light
I reach through the noise but there's nothing to hold
The one that got away in the gold

I keep the cassette though it warps and it bends
A tape full of starts that never had ends
Your name's underwater, I can't make it clear
Just a shape in the static that used to be near

You got away, away, into the white
A blur of you dissolving in the light
I reach through the noise but there's nothing to hold
The one that got away in the gold

Drown me in the feedback, let it swell and break
Every wall of sound is a wall I make
Somewhere in the haze you are turning to stay
But you're already gone, already gray

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Suitcase by the door and the harbor lights aglow
Last look at the city that I'll never get to know
The ship hums electric on the midnight tide
Leaving everything I was on the other side

Across the sea, across the neon dark
Chasing a horizon and a brand new spark
Goodbye to the home that I'm leaving behind
A new world waiting at the end of the line

Mother's on the dock getting smaller and small
I press my hand cold to the porthole wall
The stars are the same but the sky feels strange
Everything familiar rearranged

Across the sea, across the neon dark
Chasing a horizon and a brand new spark
Goodbye to the home that I'm leaving behind
A new world waiting at the end of the line

I'll send you my picture from a faraway street
In a language I'll stumble through and learn to speak
And I'll carry our kitchen and our songs in my chest
Across the wide water to the glittering west

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
El sol salió pero mi alma se quedó
In the black of the dress that my mother wore
We sang her to heaven with the trumpets last night
And I woke with the candles still burning bright

Ay, la mañana, the morning is cruel
It rises so golden, it breaks every rule
The roses are wilting on the cold marble stone
And I face the first day in this house all alone

Her coffee cup waits by the window in the sun
Half the tortillas she made are still undone
The guitar in the corner won't play her song
Grita, corazón, for the one that's gone

Ay, la mañana, the morning is cruel
It rises so golden, it breaks every rule
The roses are wilting on the cold marble stone
And I face the first day in this house all alone

But the mariachi taught me, when you love you must cry loud
So I'll sing to the heavens till my voice fills the cloud
Descansa, mamá, en la luz del Señor
I'll carry your morning in my heart evermore

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Used to be a giant, used to block the sun
Now his hands they shaking when he holds his gun
Used to fix the engine, used to run the block
Now I'm cutting up his pills and watching the clock
Yeah, time don't care who you are
Took the strongest man I knew and left a scar
I remember when he carried me up every stair
Now I carry him and pretend that I don't care

Watch my father growing old, growing old
The story that nobody ever told
The king of my whole world getting slow, getting slow
And I don't know how to let my hero go

He forgets my birthday but he calls me by my name
Sits up in his chair watching old football games
He was never soft but his eyes go wet
Talking 'bout a brother that he can't forget
I keep the truck running, keep the bills paid
Doing all the things that he taught me to be brave
But nobody taught me how to watch him fade
So I'm holding back the river of the tears I made

Dad, if you can hear me through the fog and the gray
Thank you for the man that you made me today
I'll be the giant now, I'll block out the sun
Rest your heavy crown, old man — your work is done

songs.subject: a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
I bought the smaller pan today
The big one stays up on the shelf
Your recipe card's in your hand still
Butter-stained, I read it to myself
Half an onion in the drawer
The other half I'll never use
Funny how a kitchen learns
The shape of who it's gonna lose

I'm setting one plate, one fork, one chair
One pot of coffee nobody will share
The steam fogs up the window like before
I'm learning how to feed a quiet door
One is a number I can't get used to
But tonight I made it warm for two

Salt the water, let it boil
You always said don't crowd the heat
I hear your laugh inside the timer
Dinner for a ghost who couldn't eat
I left the radio on low
So the silence wouldn't win
And I ate it standing at the counter
Where your coffee cup had been

They say it gets a little softer
Like garlic going gold and slow
I'm not good at this yet, darling
But I'm staying at the stove

I'm setting one plate, one fork, one chair
Leaving the porch light on out there
The steam fogs up the window like before
I'm learning how to love a quiet door
One is a number I can't get used to
But tonight I made it warm for two

songs.subject: teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Empty lot at six AM
Frost still biting on the glass
Your knuckles white around the wheel
My heart a hammer on the dash
Clutch and gas, a fragile war
The engine coughs and stalls and dies
But there's a fire behind your jaw
And lightning loaded in your eyes

Drive, daughter, drive
Hands at ten and two and burning
The whole horizon's yours to take
No brake can hold the world from turning
Drive into the open road
I built this armor, now it's yours
Go tear the morning wide awake
And kick the wind through every door

First gear screaming into second
Tires bark against the cold
I taught you fear so you'd respect it
Not so it would make you fold
Mirror, signal, claim the lane
The asphalt thunders, dark and wide
One day this engine won't have me
But it will always have your stride

When the headlights find a curve too fast
And the steel wants to betray
Hold the line, hold the line
And carve the fear away
I'm white-knuckled in the passenger seat
Letting go is half the fight
Go on — burn it down the street
And own the road tonight

Drive, daughter, drive
Hands at ten and two and burning
The whole horizon's yours to take
No brake can hold the world from turning

songs.subject: watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Slower now down the same hallway
The man who carried me on his back
His watch still ticks a steady measure
But his footsteps lost the beat they had
I see the gray come in like morning
Through the blinds we never close
The hands that fixed my broken bicycle
Now shake a little holding mine

And the clock keeps moving, moving, moving
Won't you slow down, slow down, slow down
Every hour you taught me how to dance
Now I'll hold you while the world spins round
Moving, moving, moving
Father, let me slow it down

We play your records in the evening
The needle finds the songs you love
You tap your foot just like you used to
Though the rhythm's softer than it was
Time's a river and we're standing
Where it pulls a little fast
So I'll memorize your laughter
Make the small light moments last

Don't go quiet, don't go gentle
Keep your hand inside my hand
Every wrinkle is a chapter
Every year a place we've been

And the clock keeps moving, moving, moving
Won't you slow down, slow down, slow down
Every hour you taught me how to dance
Now I'll hold you while the world spins round

songs.subject: an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
We were never on the posters
Nobody learned our names
Mud-soaked jerseys, borrowed cleats
And a stadium of empty seats
The scoreboard glowed against the fog
Four minutes left to lose
But something hummed beneath our ribs
A noise we couldn't refuse

Final whistle, hold your breath
We were always meant for this
Drowning in the floodlight haze
One more swing before the end of days
Let it blur, let it roar
We were nothing, now we're more

The rain came down like television static
Grass turned silver in the glare
They said go home, you've already lost it
We said we're already there
Every elbow, every bruise
A hymn we sang without a sound
The last team picked, the never-chosen
Digging heaven from the ground

No trophy waits behind the wall
No headline saves our name
But for one shivering, ringing second
We were larger than the game
And it blurs, and it bleeds
Into everything we'll need

Final whistle, hold your breath
We were always meant for this
Drowning in the floodlight haze
One more swing before the end of days

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We left the blue ball spinning slow
Two little lights in the cold up high
Your tether snapped on a silver morning
Now you're a star I'm waving goodbye
Radio crackle, your voice gone thin
Floating further every day
I reach my glove out through the window
But the dark just pulls you away

Drift, drift, drift apart
Two moons that lost the same sky
I can still hear your heartbeat
In the static, in the night
Drift, drift, oh so far
But I'll keep your light, wherever you are

We used to share one tiny capsule
Counting sunrise sixteen times a day
Now the distance hums between us
Like a song the silence learned to play
Gravity forgot our names here
Nothing holds and nothing stays
Still I send you signals, easy
Love that travels light-year ways

No down, no up, no ground to stand
Just you, a speck, against the stars
If the orbit brings you back round
I'll be right here where we are

Drift, drift, drift apart
Two moons that lost the same sky
I can still hear your heartbeat
In the static, in the night

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
String lights low and the band's half tired
Folding chairs pushed to the wall
You took my hand 'cause the song required
A body to lean on, that's all
Didn't catch your name, just the color of your laugh
Warm like a streetlamp on a rainy avenue
Two plus-ones at a table for the leftovers
Spinning slow in somebody else's view

Dance with me, stranger, just for the song
We don't have to belong
The night's gonna end, the cake's gonna fall
But right now we got it all
Just for the song, stranger
Just for the song

The groom is crying, the aunties dancing
Somebody spilled the champagne gold
You said you hate these things but here you are
With a stranger's hand to hold
We traded little nothing secrets
Footnotes the floor will never keep
A three-minute country we'll abandon
When the deejay says goodnight to sleep

Maybe we won't trade numbers
Maybe the morning takes you home
But the speakers hum a little softer
When you're not dancing alone

Dance with me, stranger, just for the song
We don't have to belong
The night's gonna end, the cake's gonna fall
But right now we got it all

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The tape gun screams like a crow on a fence
Across the last brown lid
The walls wear ghosts of the frames I hung
Pale squares of the life I lived
Dust devils turn in the empty light
Where the couch used to sit and sigh
I sealed your name in cardboard, darling
And I taped it like a goodbye

Last box, last breath in this room
The key's a stone in my hand
Everything I was is folded up
In a stack I can barely stand
Tape it shut, tape it down
I'm a stranger now in my own town

The faucet drips its same old psalm
To a kitchen wiped of us
I swept the corner where the cat would sleep
And I swallowed all the dust
There's a hollow sound a house will make
When the last thing's carried out
Like a coffin with the lid raised open
Waiting on the doubt

I'll leave the porch light burning
For a love that left no note
And the moving truck is idling
Like a hearse with its engine choked

Last box, last breath in this room
The key's a stone in my hand
Everything I was is folded up
In a stack I can barely stand

songs.subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
They poured the gray from edge to edge
Where the meadow used to run
But one old oak stood stubborn yet
With its arms held to the sun
Roots cracked up through the parking lot
Like a fist that won't let go
The last green thing in a concrete world
Singing low and singing slow

Stand, old tree, stand your ground
Where the bulldozers couldn't win
You're the only shade in a sunbaked town
And the only birds left in
Stand, old tree, stand
With your roots in the hardened land

The children climb your weary limbs
While the engines cough and hum
You drop your acorns on the asphalt
For a forest yet to come
They measured you for lumber once
But your shadow changed their mind
Now you're the church the town forgot
The last green prayer they'll find

When the wind comes off the highway
And it carries all that dust
You catch it in your branches
Like you're keeping all of us

Stand, old tree, stand your ground
Where the bulldozers couldn't win
You're the only shade in a sunbaked town
And the only birds left in

songs.subject: a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
The whistle blew its final morning
And the gates locked up for good
Forty years of steel and sawdust
Gone as quiet as it could
My daddy clocked in at that doorway
And his daddy did the same
Now the parking lot grows thistle
And nobody knows our name

Closing down, closing down
The big machines have gone to rust
The heart of this old river town
Lies sleeping in the dust
Closing down, closing down
What'll we do when the work runs out
In a closing-down town

The diner cleared its lunchtime counter
No more boots come through the door
The foreman wept behind his clipboard
There's nothing left to count it for
They shipped the presses overseas, boys
And they left the silence here
A cathedral made of girders
Filling up with empty years

We built the bridges, built the engines
Built the country, beam by beam
Now we're standing in the doorway
Holding nothing but a dream

Closing down, closing down
The big machines have gone to rust
The heart of this old river town
Lies sleeping in the dust

songs.subject: leaving your small town for good
songs.lyrics:
I taped a map across the dashboard
Drew a line through Main and gone
The water tower with our initials
Shrinking smaller in the dawn
Same three streets, same flickering diner
Same gray church and graduating field
I loved you, town, but you're a sweater
Two sizes small, I had to peel

So long to the cul-de-sac dreaming
So long to the porch light and the screen
I'm a comet in a borrowed Honda
Chasing somewhere I have never been
Don't wait up, don't wait up
I'm leaving for good this time

My mother folded sandwiches in wax paper
Like I was nine and not full-grown
Dad just nodded at the driveway
Some goodbyes are said in tone
The radio plays static county
As the cornfields blur to gold
I'll miss the names of every neighbor
But not the way they made me old

Maybe I'll romance you from a distance
In a song I half-believe
But a place can love you backwards
Till the only cure is leave

So long to the cul-de-sac dreaming
So long to the porch light and the screen
I'm a comet in a borrowed Honda
Chasing somewhere I have never been

songs.subject: texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
It's quarter past a bad idea
Thumb hovering over your name
The screen lights up my guilty ceiling
Like a match that won't admit its flame
Three dots blinking, then they vanish
Then they start to dance again
I typed and deleted seven sorries
For a sin I haven't done — but when

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but I'm typing it slow
The phone's a loaded thing I shouldn't hold
Delete, delete, send anyway
Some wrongs just glow too bright to throw away
I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but I do
At midnight everything circles back to you

There's a ring inside a drawer somewhere
And a calendar I should respect
But your message is a lit cigarette
In a house I built to protect
The smart thing's gray and gathering quiet
The stupid thing is warm and near
I know exactly how this ends, love
And I'm pressing send right here

Morning's gonna find the evidence
Glowing in my open hand
A conversation I'll regret
Like footprints in wet sand

I shouldn't, I shouldn't, but I'm typing it slow
The phone's a loaded thing I shouldn't hold
Delete, delete, send anyway
Some wrongs just glow too bright to throw away

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
They laid the stones on a beating heart
A mountain shaped like a man
Ten thousand windows, neon spires
On a slumber none understand
The subway rattles down his ribcage
The foundries burn behind his eyes
We paved our greed across his shoulders
And we never heard him sigh

Wake the giant, feel him turn
The towers tremble, the heavens burn
We built our crown upon his grave
Now nothing left alive to save
Rise, rise, ancient and slow
The city's a candle on a buried throne

The priests said sleep, the kings said deeper
Pour the concrete, raise the steel
But a dreaming god is still a god
And a hunger he can feel
The cracks ran spider through the boulevards
The church bells swung with no command
We danced on his sleeping eyelids
With our cities in our hands

When the first tooth of the morning
Cuts the marrow of the deep
There will be no prayer loud enough
To lull the world back to sleep

Wake the giant, feel him turn
The towers tremble, the heavens burn
We built our crown upon his grave
Now nothing left alive to save

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone weighs heavy in my palm tonight
Your number still the same I knew by heart
Four years of pride between us, mami
Four years I let the silence do its part
The dial tone is a drum that's beating
Louder than the bass downtown
I rehearsed a hundred openings
Now my voice won't make a sound

Pick up, mami, pick up the line
I'm sorry took me all this time
Let me hear you say my name again
Forgive the boy I was back then
Call my mother, swallow the pride
There's a little kid still here inside

I hear you breathe before you answer
And the whole world cracks in two
You don't say angry, you say 'mijo'
Like no time ever flew
The city honks behind my window
But I only hear you cry
Four years dissolving like the sugar
In the coffee you would buy

Mama, I was scared of nothing
Scared of saying I was wrong
But a mother's door stays open
Even when the kid's been gone too long

Pick up, mami, pick up the line
I'm sorry took me all this time
Let me hear you say my name again
Forgive the boy I was back then

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Old Sal's been cutting since the fifties
In a chair that creaks like the church out back
He knows your father by your cowlick
And your sorrows by the way you sat
The mirror's gone a little cloudy
Like the years it's held in line
Every wedding, every funeral
Starts with Sal and his scissors' shine

He knows the whole town by their haircuts
Every secret, every name
The mayor and the man who sweeps the station
In his chair they're all the same
Snip and tell, snip and listen
Half-confessor, half a friend
The whole town passes through his doorway
From the cradle to the end

He held the comb the day they buried
The boy who never came back home
And he cut the curls off newborn babies
For the lockets mothers own
The radio plays the ballgame quiet
The striped pole turns its slow parade
A hundred years of small-town gossip
In the bristles of his blade

When Sal retires they'll close the corner
And the town will lose its thread
The one man who remembered
Every word that no one said

He knows the whole town by their haircuts
Every secret, every name
The mayor and the man who sweeps the station
In his chair they're all the same

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Four gray bubbles on a screen, baby
That's how you chose to set me free
No slow song, no rain-soaked doorway
Just a notification killing me
I read it standing in the kitchen
Where you used to hum off-key
The coffee going cold beside me
And the morning robbing me

You ended us in twelve cold words
Lowercase and clean
No tears could fit between the letters
Of the cruelest thing I've seen
Oh, you texted me goodbye
Like I was nothing, like I was small
After everything we carried
You let a screen say it all

I gave you Sundays, gave you secrets
Gave you keys to my front door
And you typed it out in seconds
While you scrolled to something more
There's a cruelty in convenience
A coward dressed in gray
You couldn't even hear my voice break
When you typed my heart away

If you'd only called me, darling
If you'd let me hear you try
Maybe I could've survived it
But you texted me goodbye

You ended us in twelve cold words
Lowercase and clean
No tears could fit between the letters
Of the cruelest thing I've seen

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
Woke up to the thunder calling out my name
Another town behind me, baby, never the same
I chase the lightning, girl, it's all I know
Can't put down roots when the wind says go

You asked me stay, lay my heart on the ground
But the sky keeps spinning, turning me around
I feel the pressure dropping in my chest
A restless soul that never gets no rest

I'm a storm chaser, can't slow down
Running from the quiet of a sleepy town
Ooh, I love you, but I love the rain
Forgive me baby, I gotta ride again

Every horizon is a brand new high
Funnel clouds and a bruised-up sky
You're the calm I never let me hold
The warmest fire left out in the cold

I'm a storm chaser, can't slow down
Running from the quiet of a sleepy town
Ooh, I love you, but I love the rain
Forgive me baby, I gotta ride again

Maybe one day the winds will let me be
Maybe your love is the eye I need to see
Till then I'm gone where the wild skies roam
Chasing every storm but never coming home

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Close my eyes and the ceiling disappears
Lift right up over all my years
No gravity can hold me when I sleep
Rising through the secrets that I keep

Same dream every night, I spread my arms
Glide above the city and the farms
Everybody small down on the ground
Up here, baby, I don't make a sound

I'm flying, flying, oh so high
Kissing every cloud up in the sky
Don't wake me, no, just let me soar
This is what my heart was made for

Then the morning comes and pulls me down
Back to gravity and solid ground
But I'll be back tonight, you'll see
Where the open sky is calling me

I'm flying, flying, oh so high
Kissing every cloud up in the sky
Don't wake me, no, just let me soar
This is what my heart was made for

Maybe it's a sign of who I'll be
Maybe my soul is finally free
When I close my eyes I understand
The whole wide world is in my hand

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Every night I light the lantern high
Write my words beneath the moonlit sky
Darling ocean, you're my only flame
I whisper sweet and call your salty name

I seal my letters in a bottle green
Toss 'em to the prettiest blue I've seen
You never answer but you never leave
Rolling in the waves I do believe

Oh, sea, my sweet, my swinging tide
Dance with me from this lonely side
I'll keep the light a-burning true
My heart's a sailor, lost in you

The gulls all gossip 'bout my foolish heart
The keeper and the water, worlds apart
But I'll keep writing every single line
To the only love that's ever mine

Oh, sea, my sweet, my swinging tide
Dance with me from this lonely side
I'll keep the light a-burning true
My heart's a sailor, lost in you

So come on waves and take my pen
Carry all my love and back again
I'll be here when the storms roll through
Forever, darling sea, with you

songs.subject: a nurse working a double on a holiday
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this mornin', the streets all still
Everybody home but I got shifts to fill
Got my scrubs on, Lord, my coffee cold
Workin' through the holiday, gettin' old

The lights stay buzzin' in this lonely hall
Answerin' every single bell and call
My feet are achin', been twelve hours straight
Another twelve to go, the bus won't wait

I got the double-shift, holiday blues
Worn-out body and these tired shoes
While the whole world's dancin', I'm holdin' a hand
Doin' my best so somebody can stand

My babies waitin' with the dinner cold
Momma's got a duty, that's what I'm told
I wipe a fever, I ease the pain
Say a quiet prayer and start again

I got the double-shift, holiday blues
Worn-out body and these tired shoes
While the whole world's dancin', I'm holdin' a hand
Doin' my best so somebody can stand

But when that old man smiled at me
Said thank you, darlin', for settin' me free
That's when I knew why I'm standin' here
Through the lonely night, I persevere

songs.subject: a panic attack on the subway
songs.lyrics:
Doors slam shut and the lights go fast
Can't catch my breath, this won't last
Walls are closing, the air's too thin
Heart's a jackhammer pounding under my skin

Everyone's staring but nobody sees
I'm screaming silent, down on my knees
The tunnel's roaring, the rails are loud
Drowning alone in a packed-tight crowd

Get me off! Get me out!
Can't even breathe, can't even shout
Underground and out of my mind
Leave the panic and the rails behind

Next stop, next stop, hold on tight
Sweat on my collar, swallowing the light
Count to ten but the numbers blur
Everything's static, everything's a slur

Get me off! Get me out!
Can't even breathe, can't even shout
Underground and out of my mind
Leave the panic and the rails behind

Then the doors fly open, I hit the air
Gasping on the platform, fingers in my hair
Still alive, still standing here
I beat it down one more time, my dear

songs.subject: a recurring dream of flying
songs.lyrics:
Every night I lay my hat down on the bed
And the same old dream goes runnin' through my head
I'm liftin' off above the cottonwood trees
Floatin' on the warm Oklahoma breeze

No more fences, no more dusty road
No more weight of this heavy load
Just me and the hawks up in the blue
Leavin' all my worries, sailin' through

I'm flyin', flyin' over the plain
Free as a feather, light as the rain
Momma always said I'd find my way
And in my dreams I fly away

Then the rooster crows and I come on down
Boots back on the hard red ground
But I carry that sky inside my chest
Knowin' someday I'll fly to my rest

I'm flyin', flyin' over the plain
Free as a feather, light as the rain
Momma always said I'd find my way
And in my dreams I fly away

Maybe heaven's just a longer flight
Wingin' through the everlasting light
Till then I'll close my eyes and rise
And chase the freedom in the skies

songs.subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
Stuck on a rock with a busted old light
Screaming my heart at the waves every night
Dear stupid ocean, you never write back
Just spit in my face and you swallow the wreck

I scrawl my letters and I burn 'em all
Nail my devotion to the lighthouse wall
You drowned my fathers, you'll drown me too
But I can't stop loving the cold cruel blue

Dear sea! Dear sea! You never care!
I shout my love to the empty air!
Take my letters, take my name!
I'll love you 'til I'm gone in flame!

Salt in my eyes and rust in my lungs
Singing the song that nobody sung
The tide don't answer, the gulls just laugh
Love's a shipwreck split in half

Dear sea! Dear sea! You never care!
I shout my love to the empty air!
Take my letters, take my name!
I'll love you 'til I'm gone in flame!

So crash on, crash on, do your worst
I was lonely long before, and cursed
Keep the light burning, keep the rage
A keeper screaming from his cage

songs.subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
songs.lyrics:
I and I a-follow where the thunder roll
Chasin' down the lightning, it's a-feed my soul
Can't stay still, no, can't settle here
The wind a-call me, and the sky come near

She say come home, baby, lay your head
But the storm a-rumble in my chest instead
From town to town with my feet so free
The restless rain a-living inside of me

Oh, chase the storm, na settle down
Movin' like the breeze from town to town
Jah know my heart belong to the sky
So I and I a-wave the world goodbye

See the dark clouds gather over the bay
I pack my bag and I'm gone away
The sweetest love can't hold me still
I ride the weather over every hill

Oh, chase the storm, na settle down
Movin' like the breeze from town to town
Jah know my heart belong to the sky
So I and I a-wave the world goodbye

One day maybe the rain go cease
And I find me a little piece of peace
But until the calm come find my door
I chase the storm forevermore

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning, I don't ring the bell
Walking soft from the stories they tell
The choir still singing but I'm out the door
My spirit looking for something more

Mama still praying in the second row
I love her but it's my time to go
No loud goodbye, no big scene
Just a quiet boy and the in-between

I'm leaving slow, oh, leaving low
The only home my soul did know
Don't hate me, God, I'm finding my way
Walking gentle from the place I'd pray

The candles flicker, the hymn rolls on
By the time they look up, I'll be gone
Carry the love but I drop the chain
Stepping out into the morning rain

I'm leaving slow, oh, leaving low
The only home my soul did know
Don't hate me, God, I'm finding my way
Walking gentle from the place I'd pray

Maybe the faith still lives in me
Just a different shape, a different key
I close the gate without a sound
And carry heaven on new ground

songs.subject: emigrating across the sea
songs.lyrics:
Oy, the harbor's calling, the ship is gray
Leaving my village before the break of day
A suitcase of memories, a coat too thin
Sailing to a country I have never been

The fiddle plays a tune both glad and sad
For everything I lost and all I had
Mama's shawl and Papa's old prayer
Folded in my heart, I take them there

Across the sea, across the sea
A new tomorrow waiting for me
Dance though my eyes are wet with tears
Leaving behind a thousand years

The waves they rock me like a lullaby
As the old world fades beneath the sky
A stranger's land with a golden door
Where my children's children will dance once more

Across the sea, across the sea
A new tomorrow waiting for me
Dance though my eyes are wet with tears
Leaving behind a thousand years

So play, clarinet, play loud and bright
Carry my sorrow into the light
I'll plant my roots in foreign sand
And build a home with my two hands

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon humming in the velvet dark
Coffee cooling, a quiet spark
The clock don't matter when the lights stay low
We're floating somewhere the lost ones go

Vinyl booths and a sleepy song
The night outside just rolls along
The waitress hums to an empty room
Stirring stardust in the gloom

Open all night, open all night
Under the soft fluorescent light
We never close, we never end
The diner where the dreamers blend

Rain on the window, blurred and slow
The whole world sleeping but here we glow
A cup of warmth and a stranger's eyes
Lost in the hum of the lullabies

Open all night, open all night
Under the soft fluorescent light
We never close, we never end
The diner where the dreamers blend

Time dissolves in the coffee steam
We're just shadows in a waking dream
Stay with me till the morning grey
Where the all-night light won't fade away

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Three a.m., yeah, the spot still lit (still lit)
Neon sign, never quit, never quit (never quit)
Coffee black, pour it up, no sleep (no sleep)
Grindin' all night while the city counts sheep (yeah)

Booth in the back, that's my office, my throne
Waitress know my order, yeah, she leave me alone
Plates keep comin', clock don't stop
Open all night, we don't never close shop

Open all night (all night)
Neon burnin' bright (so bright)
We don't sleep, we just grind (grind)
Diner light on my mind (my mind)

Hash browns sizzle, the bell go ding
Night owls slidin' in, doin' they thing
Everybody welcome when the world go dark
Late-night dreamers leavin' they mark

Open all night (all night)
Neon burnin' bright (so bright)
We don't sleep, we just grind (grind)
Diner light on my mind (my mind)

Sun come up but we still here (still here)
Bottomless cup, no fear, no fear
Twenty-four-seven, that's the life
Glowin' in the dark like a diner light

songs.subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
songs.lyrics:
Rain came down on a crowded street
You stepped under, our eyes did meet
One umbrella, two souls in the storm
Strangers staying dry and warm

We didn't talk, just walked in time
Your shoulder brushing close to mine
The city blurry, the gray turned gold
A quiet magic starting to unfold

Just you and me beneath the rain
Two strangers sharing one small frame
Maybe it's fate, maybe it's chance
A stolen moment, a soft slow dance

The puddles shimmer, the streetlights glow
I don't even know your name, you know
But for these blocks we're intertwined
Sweetest stranger I could find

Just you and me beneath the rain
Two strangers sharing one small frame
Maybe it's fate, maybe it's chance
A stolen moment, a soft slow dance

The rain might stop and you might go
But I'll remember this, I know
One umbrella, one perfect storm
Where two cold strangers got warm

songs.subject: leaving an arranged future behind
songs.lyrics:
They drew my whole life on a page
Name and a future, a gilded cage
Every door already chosen for me
But I was born to be wild and free

The dress was picked, the ring was set
A life I never wanted yet
I looked in the mirror, I couldn't pretend
This is where the story has to end

I'm leaving it all behind tonight
Running toward my own bright light
No more living someone else's dream
I'm tearing up the whole machine

They'll say I'm reckless, they'll say I'm wrong
But I've been silent for far too long
My heart's a compass, it points away
From all the plans they made today

I'm leaving it all behind tonight
Running toward my own bright light
No more living someone else's dream
I'm tearing up the whole machine

So here I go with empty hands
Writing my own and burning their plans
Scared to death but I'm finally me
Walking into who I'm meant to be

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raise my hand but they don't see
Got a good idea but it ain't for free
Start to speak and they cut me off
Roll right over with a fake little cough

Sit at the table but I'm a ghost
The one with the plan they need the most
Every time I open up my mouth
Somebody louder turns me south

Talked over, talked over again
Drowned out by the louder men
But I got the funk, I got the groove
One day, baby, you gonna move

Nod my head and I bite my tongue
Wait for a gap that never come
My two cents stuck up on a shelf
Guess I'll just say it to myself

Talked over, talked over again
Drowned out by the louder men
But I got the funk, I got the groove
One day, baby, you gonna move

So turn the bass up, let it ride
I'll put my power in the groove inside
Next time I speak, gonna bring the heat
And lay it down on a funky beat

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Monday morning on the corner where the pigeons play
Got my board set up, got a dollar bet to lay
Slick in my shades and a grin you can't refuse
Step right up, son, you got nothing left to lose
Queen takes the bishop, watch my fingers move
I been doing this since '82, I'm in the groove

Checkmate, baby, drop that crown
King of the park, ain't nobody taking me down
Five bucks a game and the clock keeps tickin'
Funky little hustle and the money keep clickin'

Suit and tie comes strutting, thinks he knows the deal
I let him take my rook just to see how he feel
Then I spring the trap, oh the board start to sing
Never trust a man who's smiling at your king

They come and they go but the bench stays the same
Thirty years of pawns and I never lost the game
Sun going down, gotta fold up my throne
Count up my winnings, walk it on home

Checkmate, baby, drop that crown
King of the park, ain't nobody taking me down
Five bucks a game and the clock keeps tickin'
Funky little hustle and the money keep clickin'

songs.subject: coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
The train pulled slow through the fields of rye
Four long years beneath a foreign sky
My mother's window, a candle in the night
I carried her face through every fight

Oy, the clarinet is crying
Oy, the fiddle starts to play
I laid my rifle down forever
I am coming home today

The village square where the old men dance
They saw my eyes, they gave me one more chance
The bread still warm and the wine still red
But I hear the names of the friends I left for dead

Dance, dance, though my heart is torn
Dance for the living and the ones we mourn
Lift up your feet, let the sorrow turn to song
The war is over, I am where I belong

Oy, the clarinet is crying
Oy, the fiddle starts to play
I laid my rifle down forever
I am coming home today

songs.subject: the last day of summer before college
songs.lyrics:
Neon dripping off the parking lot
We drove around to every spot we got
Your hand out the window catching August air
Tomorrow we go everywhere and nowhere

One more midnight, one more song
Hold the summer 'til the dawn
We're electric, we're alive
Last day before the rest of our lives

The arcade glow and the ocean spray
We spent every coin and we threw it away
Your laugh on the boardwalk, magenta and chrome
Tomorrow they pack up the car and we're gone

Don't say goodbye, just say goodnight
Let the streetlights blur into white
We were forever for a little while
Keep the engine running, keep that smile

One more midnight, one more song
Hold the summer 'til the dawn
We're electric, we're alive
Last day before the rest of our lives

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
I was on my knees when the phone lit blue
Three little lines and I knew we were through
No amen, no goodbye, no grace
Just a screen where I used to see your face

But I lift it up, I lift it high
Every tear that's falling from my eye
The Lord didn't leave me though you did
I'll find my peace in the love I hid

The choir sang on while my heart broke down
The deacon prayed and I came around
You can leave with a word and never look back
But faith is the road and I'm getting on track

Glory, glory, I'm still standing
Glory, glory, soft landing
A text can break a heart in two
But grace will carry me on through

So I lift it up, I lift it high
Every tear that's falling from my eye
The Lord didn't leave me though you did
I'll find my peace in the love I hid

songs.subject: a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Dusty old fan turning slow in the chair
Clippers humming soft like a hymn in the air
Mr. Earl been cutting since before I was grown
He knows every name, every secret in town

Fade it down low, take your time
Everybody's story on the barber's line
Mirror full of faces coming through the door
He heard it all before, he heard it all before

Who got married, who skipped town last spring
The mayor's bad toupee, that whole thing
He nods and he listens, he never says much
But the whole block trusts his steady touch

Vinyl crackle, neon out the glass
He sweeps up the hair like he's sweeping the past
Close up at six with a tired little grin
Tomorrow the same town walks right back in

Fade it down low, take your time
Everybody's story on the barber's line
Mirror full of faces coming through the door
He heard it all before, he heard it all before

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
A screen in the dark and the cold light burns
Four years of us in a sentence returns
No final scream, no slamming door
Just a notification I can't ignore

DELETE ME, ERASE THE FLAME
You killed it all with a typed-out name
I scream at a phone that will never reply
Love of my life in a line goodbye

The rage in my chest is a furnace tonight
I smash through the silence, I burn through the light
You couldn't even look me in the eye
A coward's exit, a digital lie

Bleed out, black out, let the speakers roar
I won't be the ghost at your door anymore
The ashes are mine and the fire is free
You texted goodbye, now you're dead to me

DELETE ME, ERASE THE FLAME
You killed it all with a typed-out name
I scream at a phone that will never reply
Love of my life in a line goodbye

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
Empty walls where the pictures used to hang
Footsteps echo in a room that used to sang
One roll of tape and a marker running dry
Writing MEMORIES on the side, saying goodbye

So I tape up the last box
Turn the key in the old locks
Everything I was is folded down
Driving out of this town
Tape up the last box

The scratch on the floor from the night that we danced
The height marks on the door, every birthday glance
I thought it would be heavy, thought I'd fall apart
But the cardboard holds it all, even my heart

Last look, lights off, hand on the frame
The place that I'm leaving won't be the same
Four walls of a life I am leaving behind
The best of it's coming, I'll keep it in mind

So I tape up the last box
Turn the key in the old locks
Everything I was is folded down
Driving out of this town
Tape up the last box

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
(Shoo-bop, shoo-bop) on a Saturday
There's a man with a board where the children play
He'll cost you a dollar, he'll cost you your pride
With a grin and a wink and the moves on his side

Oh, checkmate, my darling, checkmate tonight
(Doo-wop, doo-wop) the king's in his sight
He'll take all your money with a tip of his hat
The king of the park, now how about that

The young ones all gather just to watch him play
(Sha-la-la) he never gives the game away
A queen and a knight and a sly little smile
He's hustled this corner for a good long while

When the sun's going down and the bench is bare
He folds up his board with a millionaire's air
(Shoo-bop) tomorrow he'll be back for more
The smoothest old hustler you ever saw

Oh, checkmate, my darling, checkmate tonight
(Doo-wop, doo-wop) the king's in his sight
He'll take all your money with a tip of his hat
The king of the park, now how about that

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
She writes by the window in a haze of blue
Words like water, only ever true
They offered her gold for a softer line
She smiled and she folded it back into time

She won't sell out, she won't sell out
Her voice is a whisper, never a shout
Let the money drift like smoke in the rain
She keeps every word, she keeps every pain

The city kept asking for something to buy
A shimmering version, a marketable sky
But her ink stays honest, her pages stay torn
A candle still burning since the day she was born

Drift, drift, into the fade
Nothing she wrote was ever for trade
The softest rebellion the world never saw
A poet who answers to nothing at all

She won't sell out, she won't sell out
Her voice is a whisper, never a shout
Let the money drift like smoke in the rain
She keeps every word, she keeps every pain

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Las manos de mi abuela, worn and brown
They kneaded the masa, they held the whole town
Every line a story, every scar a year
They wiped away my childhood, every fall and fear

Ay, those hands, those gentle hands
They built a family out of dust and sand
The trumpets cry for everything she gave
Those blessed hands I'll honor to my grave

She braided my hair in the morning light
She lit the candles every Sunday night
The rosary slid through her fingers like rain
Those hands held our joy and they carried our pain

Ahora están quietas, now they are still
Folded so softly on the windowsill
But I feel them upon me wherever I stand
The whole of my life in my grandmother's hands

Ay, those hands, those gentle hands
They built a family out of dust and sand
The trumpets cry for everything she gave
Those blessed hands I'll honor to my grave

songs.subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
songs.lyrics:
The band played slow as the candles burned low
You took my hand and you said let's go
I didn't know your name, didn't know your town
But the saxophone fell and the lights came down

Dance with me, stranger, just for a song
The night is short but the moment is long
I'll never see you when the morning comes
So hold me close 'til the music's done

You spun me around by the wedding cake
Two lonely hearts on the floor we'd make
A borrowed romance in a borrowed room
A flower I'll keep though it's bound to bloom

Who are you, where did you come from tonight
A face in the crowd in the soft golden light
We'll part with a smile and we'll never know
The story that started where the trumpets blow

Dance with me, stranger, just for a song
The night is short but the moment is long
I'll never see you when the morning comes
So hold me close 'til the music's done

songs.subject: calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The dial tone hums in the violet dark
My thumb on the number, my heart a spark
Five years of silence, a wall I built high
Now the phone is ringing under a neon sky

Mama, it's me, after all this time
The line is open, the stars align
Forgive the silence, forgive the years
I'm calling you home through all my fears

I hear her breathing, then I hear my name
Like no time passed, like no one's to blame
The streetlights flicker, the static clears
A mother's voice melting all my years

Drive through the night with the windows down
The glow of the city, the edge of town
Everything broken can mend in a call
The distance is nothing, no distance at all

Mama, it's me, after all this time
The line is open, the stars align
Forgive the silence, forgive the years
I'm calling you home through all my fears

songs.subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Three in the morning, the diesel hum
I-80 empty, my fingers numb
Dialing the house where my babies sleep
Miles on the dash that I can't keep
Yeah, the engine roar but my voice stay low
Don't wanna wake 'em but I miss 'em though

Call home, 3am, I'm still rolling
Headlights cutting through the cold and snowing
Daddy's coming home, just hold on tight
Long road, long night, see you in the light

Voicemail picks up and I hear her breathe
Leave a little message that she'll believe
Gas station coffee and a fading dream
Working for the family, that's the whole scheme

Twelve hundred miles 'til I see your face
The whole world sleeping and I'm holding space
Keep the porch light on, keep the bed warm
I'm driving through the dark and the highway storm

Call home, 3am, I'm still rolling
Headlights cutting through the cold and snowing
Daddy's coming home, just hold on tight
Long road, long night, see you in the light

songs.subject: a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
Neon flickers on the avenue
Coffee's always hot and the booth seats two
The night owls gather where the city don't sleep
Secrets and syrup that the waitress keep

Open all night, the light stays on
For the broken hearts till the break of dawn
Come on in, baby, take a seat
Where the lonely and the dreamers meet

A cabbie, a poet, and a girl in tears
This little diner's held them all for years
She pours another cup with a knowing smile
Everybody's welcome to stay a while

Three a.m. and the rain comes down
Last refuge in a sleeping town
The jukebox hums an old slow tune
And nobody's lonely in this room

Open all night, the light stays on
For the broken hearts till the break of dawn
Come on in, baby, take a seat
Where the lonely and the dreamers meet

songs.subject: the morning after a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The casseroles are cold on the kitchen counter
The black suit's hanging on the bedroom door
The sun came up like it didn't know better
And I don't know what I'm living for

Morning after, the house is too quiet
Your coffee cup still sittin' by the sink
The whole world's turning like nothing has happened
And I'm standing here, can't even think

The folks all left with their kind words spoken
The flowers wilting in the morning chill
I fed the dog and I made the bed up
Life keeps moving but my heart stands still

They say it gets easier, give it some time
But time's just a river and you're not on this side
I'll carry you with me down every back road
Every sunrise from now till I die

Morning after, the house is too quiet
Your coffee cup still sittin' by the sink
The whole world's turning like nothing has happened
And I'm standing here, can't even think

songs.subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
songs.lyrics:
Concrete on a chestbone, towers on a spine
We poured the foundations along a fault in the divine
Every morning traffic hums above a slow held breath
We call it solid ground, we never call it death
The subway shakes a little when he turns inside his dream
We blame it on the engines, we blame it on the steam

Don't wake the giant, keep the bassline low
Keep the sirens humming so he'll never know
We built a million windows on a beating chest
Dance until the tremor, dance until the rest

Architects with blueprints never read the older maps
The rivers are his arteries, the bridges close the gaps
We sell him as a legend to the children on the train
A mountain that was breathing, a heartbeat in the rain

One day he'll roll a shoulder and the skyline turns to dust
Until then we keep dancing 'cause dancing is a must
Low end in the basement, kick drum like a pulse
A lullaby of neon for the body underneath us

Don't wake the giant, keep the bassline low
Keep the sirens humming so he'll never know
We built a million windows on a beating chest
Dance until the tremor, dance until the rest

songs.subject: a love that ended over a text message
songs.lyrics:
Three little dots were dancing on my screen
Thought you'd say you loved me, said the worst I've ever seen
I was skanking in the kitchen with a coffee going cold
When the buzz inside my pocket made my whole heart fold

You broke it with a buzz, broke it with a beep
Forty-seven characters and now I cannot sleep
No phone call, no flowers, no goodbye at the door
Just a little gray bubble and a love that's no more

The horns are playing upbeat but my mood is way down low
The trombone's feeling sorry for a fool who didn't know
You couldn't even spell it right, you typed it 'your the one'
Then sent the wrong one after, said the autocorrect's done

Pick it up, pick it up, pick the pieces off the floor
Delete the conversation, don't go reading anymore
The rude boys all are telling me to dance it out the door

You broke it with a buzz, broke it with a beep
Forty-seven characters and now I cannot sleep
No phone call, no flowers, no goodbye at the door
Just a little gray bubble and a love that's no more

songs.subject: a museum guard and a painting
songs.lyrics:
Nine to five beneath the velvet rope
Watching over canvas, watching over hope
She's been hanging on that wall since 1962
But every time the lights go low she's only dancing for you

Oh she moves, she moves in the gallery glow
A painted girl in motion that the curators don't know
When the doors are locked and the city's gone to sleep
We waltz across the marble, it's a secret we keep

They think I guard the masterpiece, they don't know that I'm in love
With a smile in oil and gold leaf and a sky in pearl above
I hum a little something and I swear I see her sway
Then the morning crowd comes shuffling and she turns the other way

Midnight is the only hour, mirror ball of moon
She steps down from the frame and the floor becomes a tune
Don't tell the day-shift, don't tell a single soul
This quiet love I'm holding keeps the whole museum whole

Oh she moves, she moves in the gallery glow
A painted girl in motion that the curators don't know
When the doors are locked and the city's gone to sleep
We waltz across the marble, it's a secret we keep

songs.subject: two rivals forced to dance together
songs.lyrics:
The plaza lit with lanterns, the band begins to play
They put your hand in my hand though I'd push you far away
For twenty years we've quarreled over fences and a name
Now the trumpets call us forward and we burn with the same flame

Dance with me, enemy, under the moon
My boots against your boots in a borrowed tune
We hate how well we move, we hate that it's true
The whole town claps along while I despise you

Your spurs are bright as anger, my hat is low with pride
The violins are weeping for the war we cannot hide
But step for step we follow like we practiced all our lives
Two roosters in a courtyard where the old resentment thrives

Ay, the guitarrón is laughing at the both of us tonight
Two stubborn men still spinning in the lantern light
Maybe hate is just a dance we never learned to end
Maybe my worst rival was my almost-friend

Dance with me, enemy, under the moon
My boots against your boots in a borrowed tune
We hate how well we move, we hate that it's true
The whole town claps along while I despise you

songs.subject: a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Manos de mi abuela, worn like river stone
Kneading all the masa in a kitchen warm as home
Rings too loose on fingers that once held me when I cried
Every line a story of the years she carried wide

Las manos, las manos, they raised a whole family
Soft on every forehead, hard as any sea
Dale, abuela, dale, let me kiss them one more time
Those hands that built my whole world, those hands divine

She braided all my sisters, she buried all her men
She prayed a thousand rosaries and prayed them all again
Now I hold them gently when the evening starts to fade
And I trace the map of everything those fingers ever made

Perreo slow, perreo soft, a rhythm for the old
For the strength inside a woman that the radio never told
Clap clap for abuela, for the hands that never stopped
From the bottom of the barrio to the rooftop

Las manos, las manos, they raised a whole family
Soft on every forehead, hard as any sea
Dale, abuela, dale, let me kiss them one more time
Those hands that built my whole world, those hands divine

songs.subject: a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
Five dollars says you'll lose before the pigeons take their flight
He sets the wooden soldiers in the soft Sunday light
A paper cup of coffee and a clock that never sleeps
He smiles like the river, slow and warm and deep

Move your little bishop, friend, I've seen it all before
The park bench is my kingdom and the gambit is my shore
Gently, gently, he takes your queen away
Samba in his fingers as he steals another day

The tourists think he's harmless with his cardigan and grin
But every game's a bossa with a trap tucked deep within
He whistles something Brazilian as he sets the final mate
Then pockets all your money and he wishes you were great

Doce, doce, the way he lets you hope
Dangles you a draw then pulls away the rope
The afternoon is golden, the fountain softly sighs
And checkmate comes as gentle as the clouds across the skies

Move your little bishop, friend, I've seen it all before
The park bench is my kingdom and the gambit is my shore
Gently, gently, he takes your queen away
Samba in his fingers as he steals another day

songs.subject: running a pirate radio station
songs.lyrics:
Antenna on the rooftop, taped against the night
A thousand watts of contraband across the city light
They try to find my frequency, they triangulate the sound
But I'm already moving, I am never tracked down

Midnight on the dial, this is the ghost
Broadcasting to the lonely on the neon coast
Turn it up, turn it up, let the static bloom
A pirate in the airwaves cutting through the gloom

A cassette deck, a mixer, and a heart that won't comply
I play the songs they banned us from beneath a purple sky
The cops are circling slowly but the signal's getting strong
Every kid with headphones knows the words to every song

They can take the tower, they can cut the wire
But they'll never kill the frequency, they'll never kill the fire
Driving down the highway with the dial set to me
The last free voice transmitting in the key of being free

Midnight on the dial, this is the ghost
Broadcasting to the lonely on the neon coast
Turn it up, turn it up, let the static bloom
A pirate in the airwaves cutting through the gloom

songs.subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
songs.lyrics:
I didn't slam the heavy door, I let it softly close
No lightning and no thunder, just the ending no one chose
The hymns are in my marrow but the answers never came
I memorized the prayers but I forgot to feel the same

I'm walking out the back, I'm walking out the back
No speech and no rebellion, just a thing I can't take back
They'll notice I'm not singing, they'll notice I'm not there
And I'll carry all the silence like a half-forgotten prayer

They taught me how to kneel before they taught me how to ask
I wore belief like Sunday clothes, a tight and itching mask
I loved the people in those pews, I just stopped loving why
So I'm slipping out the side door, no apology, no goodbye

It's not a scream, it's not a fight, it's quieter than that
It's leaving on a Tuesday and not coming back
No god to curse, no devil's bargain, nothing to defend
Just a kid who stopped believing and a story at its end

I'm walking out the back, I'm walking out the back
No speech and no rebellion, just a thing I can't take back
They'll notice I'm not singing, they'll notice I'm not there
And I'll carry all the silence like a half-forgotten prayer

songs.subject: taping up the last box when you move out
songs.lyrics:
The rooms are full of echoes now, the walls are bare and pale
The kettle's in the carton and the curtains have set sail
I run the tape across the lid, one final screeching sound
And everything I lived here is in cardboard on the ground

So fare thee well, old doorway, fare thee well, old floor
I'm taping up the last box, I won't see you anymore
The whistle and the fiddle play a tune both sweet and sore
For a home that held my growing and will hold it nevermore

There's a mark upon the doorframe where they measured me each year
A scuff beneath the window from the dancing we did here
I'll leave the keys upon the sill and let the new ones in
But I'm taking all the music in the marrow of my skin

The last box always heaviest, though nothing's left inside
Just the weight of every morning and the evenings that have died
The tin whistle keeps on calling like a gull above the foam
For the ordinary magic of a place we once called home

So fare thee well, old doorway, fare thee well, old floor
I'm taping up the last box, I won't see you anymore
The whistle and the fiddle play a tune both sweet and sore
For a home that held my growing and will hold it nevermore

songs.subject: two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
The tether snapped at sunrise over a turning blue
Now I am drifting outward and I'm drifting far from you
My hand still reaches forward through the cold and silent black
But the distance only widens and there is no way back

Adiós, compañero, in the endless night
Watch me grow as small and distant as a fading light
Sing me one last ranchera through the radio's last breath
Two riders of the heavens slowly parted by our death

We trained together, laughed together, dreamed of distant stars
Now the only sky between us is a million empty miles
Your voice is breaking softly through the helmet's dying hum
And the trumpet of my heartbeat plays for all the days to come

Ay, the earth is spinning under us, so green and far and small
And I would trade the cosmos just to feel the ground at all
The stars are cold as silver and the guitar weeps alone
For two sons of the desert who will never make it home

Adiós, compañero, in the endless night
Watch me grow as small and distant as a fading light
Sing me one last ranchera through the radio's last breath
Two riders of the heavens slowly parted by our death

songs.subject: being talked over in every meeting
songs.lyrics:
I raised my voice up softly, had the answer in my throat
Then somebody louder stole it, didn't even read the note
Thirty-eight and seasoned, I have done my time and more
But they look right through me sitting at the table I built before

Talk over me, talk over me, like I'm not even here
My whole truth gets repeated by a man and they all cheer
But I know what I know, and I'm done shrinking small
Next time that I open up, baby, I'ma say it all

Good idea in a minor key, swallowed by the noise
Meeting after meeting full of interrupting boys
I write it in the chat box and they scroll on past the line
Then the boss repeats it later like the thought was never mine

Mmm, watch me find my register, the low and steady kind
The one that don't ask permission, the one they can't rewind
I've been polite for far too long, been waiting for the floor
The groove just changed, sweetheart, I don't wait anymore

Talk over me, talk over me, like I'm not even here
My whole truth gets repeated by a man and they all cheer
But I know what I know, and I'm done shrinking small
Next time that I open up, baby, I'ma say it all

songs.subject: an elder teaching the tide
songs.lyrics:
Abuelo walks the morning sand with sea-glass in his eyes
He speaks to all the water like it's something he can guide
Ven acá, he tells the ocean, come and learn from me
I've watched you eighty winters, now sit still beneath the tree

Enséñale, viejo, teach the tide to stay
Though it laughs and keeps on rolling in its old and stubborn way
The trumpets greet the sunrise and the foam comes rushing through
And the sea just keeps on doing what the sea will always do

He draws a line of seashells where he wants the waves to halt
The ocean steps right over it and tastes it sweet with salt
But still he comes each morning with his patience and his pride
A stubborn old professor for an unrepentant tide

Maybe he's not teaching it, maybe it's teaching him
That some things won't be governed by the will or by the whim
The guitarrón keeps rolling like the rhythm of the deep
And the elder smiles and surrenders to a lesson he can keep

Enséñale, viejo, teach the tide to stay
Though it laughs and keeps on rolling in its old and stubborn way
The trumpets greet the sunrise and the foam comes rushing through
And the sea just keeps on doing what the sea will always do

songs.subject: losing your faith and finding it again
songs.lyrics:
There was a winter in my spirit when the wells all ran dry
I sang the songs of glory but I couldn't tell you why
The heavens turned to silence and the pews felt cold as stone
I knelt down in the darkness and I felt so alone

But I found it, oh I found it, in the breaking of the day
The light I thought had left me had been with me all the way
Lost it in the valley, found it climbing out
My faith came back to find me in the middle of my doubt

I wandered through the questions like a desert with no end
I cursed the empty silence and I called it my friend
Then a stranger showed me kindness with no reason and no name
And something old and holy lit a small and stubborn flame

It wasn't in the thunder, it wasn't in the loud
It was a whisper in the quiet underneath the breaking cloud
Glory, glory, the river runs again
What I lost out in the wilderness came home to me again

And I found it, oh I found it, in the breaking of the day
The light I thought had left me had been with me all the way
Lost it in the valley, found it climbing out
My faith came back to find me in the middle of my doubt

songs.subject: a poet who won't sell out
songs.lyrics:
They offered him the silver and a billboard with his name
Just soften up the verses and we'll fan a little fame
He sipped his bitter coffee and he watched the rain come down
And folded up their contract into a paper crown

Não, não, he says it soft and slow
My words are not for selling, that's the only thing I know
Gently on the ocean, gently on the breeze
A poet keeps his poverty and keeps it with such ease

The agents call him foolish, say the world has moved along
But he writes for one old woman who still hums his oldest song
He trades a verse for supper and a sonnet for the wine
And every line he won't betray is somehow still divine

Suave, suave, the way he turns them down
No penthouse and no statue and no flowers from the town
Just a notebook full of mornings that the market couldn't buy
And a quiet kind of richness in the corner of his eye

Não, não, he says it soft and slow
My words are not for selling, that's the only thing I know
Gently on the ocean, gently on the breeze
A poet keeps his poverty and keeps it with such ease

songs.subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
songs.lyrics:
We loaded up the crates at midnight, no one asked us what
The captain paid in old gold and he told us keep it shut
Now the compass spins in circles and the stars have all gone strange
And something in the cargo hold is starting to derange

Don't open up the cargo, don't break the iron seal
Whatever's in the darkness, boys, it's learning how to feel
Neon on the water, engines running cold
We're carrying a curse across an ocean black and old

The first mate stopped his sleeping, says he hears it call his name
The crew is getting fewer and the rest are not the same
A glow beneath the floorboards in a pulsing violet hum
And every mile we travel makes it stronger than we've come

Throw it overboard, the bosun cries, but no one moves a hand
The gold has made us prisoners to a thing we understand
The horizon's getting closer but the port is not in sight
We'll deliver what we carry or we'll vanish in the night

Don't open up the cargo, don't break the iron seal
Whatever's in the darkness, boys, it's learning how to feel
Neon on the water, engines running cold
We're carrying a curse across an ocean black and old
